## 5034: likeness (id: 5034)

The similarity between objects or persons. Humanity is created in the likeness of God. Jesus Christ possesses a likeness to both God and humanity, which is expressed and safeguarded by the doctrine of the incarnation.

Human beings are created in the likeness of God
-----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26-Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gen5:1), [GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6); [COL 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Col3:9-Col3:10); [JAS 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9)

Redemption includes being conformed to God's likeness
-----------------------------------------------------

### The likeness of God the Father

**[MAT 5:48](https://ref.ly/Matt5:48)** *See also* [EPH 4:20–4:24](https://ref.ly/Eph4:20-Eph4:24)

### The likeness of God the Son

**[ROM 8:29](https://ref.ly/Rom8:29)** *See also* [1CO 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:16); [2CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:18); [EPH 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:13)

Jesus Christ was in the likeness of God and humanity
----------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ is like God

**[COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15)** *While stating that Jesus Christ is like God, Scripture also insists that this likeness is grounded in the fact that Jesus Christ is God.* *See also* [2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:4); [PHP 2:6](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3)

### Jesus Christ is like human beings

**[ROM 8:3](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3)** *While stating that Jesus Christ is like humanity Scripture also insists that this likeness is grounded in the fact that Jesus Christ is human. Yet Christ did not share human sinfulness [2Co 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21); [Heb 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15).* *See also* [PHP 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:26-27; Genesis 5:1; Genesis 9:6; Colossians 3:9-10; James 3:9; Matthew 5:48; Ephesians 4:20-24; Romans 8:29; 1 Corinthians 2:16; 2 Corinthians 3:18; Ephesians 4:11-13; Colossians 1:15; 2 Corinthians 4:4; Philippians 2:6; Hebrews 1:3; Romans 8:3; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Hebrews 4:15; Philippians 2:6-7
* **Associated Articles:** 2018: Jesus Christ, divinity of (ID: `2018@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2033: Jesus Christ, humanity of (ID: `2033@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2218: Jesus Christ, Son of God (ID: `2218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2221: Jesus Christ, Son of Man (ID: `2221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5023: image of God (ID: `5023@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5308: equality (ID: `5308@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5035: mind (id: 5035)

The seat of human consciousness, thought and desires. Scripture stresses that the minds of believers must be shaped by the knowledge and love of God, as their ways of thinking and acting become more like the pattern set out in Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 5036: mind, of God (ID: `5036@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5037: mind, of Christ (ID: `5037@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5038: mind, the human (ID: `5038@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5036: mind, of God (id: 5036)

God's pattern of thought and attitude of heart is holy, righteous and good. It is expressed in his law and his word, and also in the outworking of his mercy, but it is, ultimately, beyond human understanding.

God's mind is expressed in his law and his word
-----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:1–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:1-Exod20:17)** *See also* [DEU 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Deut4:1-Deut4:2), [DEU 6:1](https://ref.ly/Deut6:1), [DEU 6:24–6:25](https://ref.ly/Deut6:24-Deut6:25); [PSA 119:4](https://ref.ly/Ps119:4), [PSA 119:34](https://ref.ly/Ps119:34), [PSA 119:66](https://ref.ly/Ps119:66), [PSA 119:89](https://ref.ly/Ps119:89), [PSA 119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105), [PSA 119:130](https://ref.ly/Ps119:130)

The mind of God is holy, righteous and good
-------------------------------------------

**[ROM 7:7](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7)** *See also* [DEU 4:8](https://ref.ly/Deut4:8); [NUM 23:19](https://ref.ly/Num23:19); [1SA 15:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:29); [2SA 22:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:31) parallel [PSA 18:30](https://ref.ly/Ps18:30); [PRO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Prov30:5); [PSA 19:7–19:10](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7-Ps19:10), [PSA 119:68](https://ref.ly/Ps119:68), [PSA 119:75](https://ref.ly/Ps119:75), [PSA 119:137–119:138](https://ref.ly/Ps119:137-Ps119:138), [PSA 119:142](https://ref.ly/Ps119:142); [JHN 17:17](https://ref.ly/John17:17); [ROM 7:12](https://ref.ly/Rom7:12)

The mind of God is also expressed in his merciful acts
------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Eph2:4-Eph2:5)** *See also* [NEH 9:31](https://ref.ly/Neh9:31); [PSA 123:2](https://ref.ly/Ps123:2); [ISA 55:7](https://ref.ly/Isa55:7); [DAN 9:18](https://ref.ly/Dan9:18); [HOS 11:8](https://ref.ly/Hos11:8); [MIC 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mic7:18); [LUK 1:78](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78); [ROM 9:18](https://ref.ly/Rom9:18), [ROM 11:32](https://ref.ly/Rom11:32), [ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1); [1PE 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3), [1PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:10)

The constancy of the mind of God
--------------------------------

**[NUM 23:19](https://ref.ly/Num23:19)** *See also* [1SA 15:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:29); [PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4); [EZK 24:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:14)

God changes his mind
--------------------

### Drawing back from threatened actions

**[JER 26:13](https://ref.ly/Jer26:13)** *The idea of God “relenting” does not mean a capricious change of mind, but the considered decision on God's part not to proceed with a threatened course of action in response to repentance on the part of individuals or the people.* *See also* [EXO 32:12–32:14](https://ref.ly/Exod32:12-Exod32:14); [JER 18:5–18:10](https://ref.ly/Jer18:5-Jer18:10), [JER 26:3](https://ref.ly/Jer26:3), [JER 26:19](https://ref.ly/Jer26:19); [JOL 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Joel2:13-Joel2:14); [AMO 7:3](https://ref.ly/Amos7:3), [AMO 7:6](https://ref.ly/Amos7:6); [JON 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:9-Jonah3:10), [JON 4:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:2)

### Grieving over past actions

**[1SA 15:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:11)** *See also* [GEN 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Gen6:6-Gen6:7); [1SA 15:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:35)

The mind of God is, ultimately, beyond human understanding
----------------------------------------------------------

### Because of God's greatness

**[ISA 55:8–55:9](https://ref.ly/Isa55:8-Isa55:9)** *See also* [ISA 40:13–40:14](https://ref.ly/Isa40:13-Isa40:14), [ISA 40:28](https://ref.ly/Isa40:28), [ISA 44:6–44:7](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6-Isa44:7); [ROM 11:33–11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:33-Rom11:36); [1CO 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:16)

### Because of human lowliness

**[ECC 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1-Eccl5:2)** *See also* [ECC 7:23–7:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:23-Eccl7:25)

## 5037: mind, of Christ (id: 5037)

The mind of Christ reveals the essence of God's nature. Jesus Christ desires that his followers adopt his attitudes; this comes about through the work of the Holy Spirit.

The mind of Christ discloses the mind of God
--------------------------------------------

### In relation to righteousness, holiness and the law

**[JHN 14:9–14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:9-John14:11)** *See also* [MAT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17); [JHN 15:10](https://ref.ly/John15:10); [1CO 1:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:30)

### In mercy

**[1TI 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:15-1Tim1:16)** *See also* [MAT 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:12-Matt9:13) parallels [MRK 2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:17), [LUK 5:31–5:32](https://ref.ly/Luke5:31-Luke5:32); [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23); [JUD 1:21](https://ref.ly/Jude1:21)

The mind of Christ is made known to believers by the Holy Spirit
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26)** *See also* [JHN 16:12–16:15](https://ref.ly/John16:12-John16:15); [ROM 8:9](https://ref.ly/Rom8:9); [1CO 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:11-1Cor2:13)

### The plans and purposes of Jesus Christ are made known to the first Christians by the Spirit

**[ACT 16:6–16:7](https://ref.ly/Acts16:6-Acts16:7)** *See also* [ACT 8:29](https://ref.ly/Acts8:29), [ACT 10:19](https://ref.ly/Acts10:19), [ACT 11:12](https://ref.ly/Acts11:12), [ACT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Acts11:28), [ACT 13:2](https://ref.ly/Acts13:2), [ACT 15:28](https://ref.ly/Acts15:28)

Knowledge of the mind of Christ leads to holiness
-------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:14-1Cor2:16)** *See also* [PHP 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:11); [1PE 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:1-1Pet4:2)

* **Associated Passages:** John 14:9-11; Matthew 5:17; John 15:10; 1 Corinthians 1:30; 1 Timothy 1:15-16; Matthew 9:12-13; Mark 2:17; Luke 5:31-32; Hosea 6:6; Matthew 23:23; Jude 1:21; John 14:26; John 16:12-15; Romans 8:9; 1 Corinthians 2:11-13; Acts 16:6-7; Acts 8:29; Acts 10:19; Acts 11:12; Acts 11:28; Acts 13:2; Acts 15:28; 1 Corinthians 2:14-16; Philippians 2:5-11; 1 Peter 4:1-2

## 5038: mind, the human (id: 5038)

On account of its inner orientation the fallen human mind is in conflict with the mind of God. It is nevertheless capable of knowing God, and of being changed and renewed by him.

The human mind is the seat of reason and decision\-making
---------------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Job12:1-Job12:3)** *See also* [1CH 17:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:2); [2CH 7:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:11); [NEH 5:7](https://ref.ly/Neh5:7); [PSA 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:14), [PSA 49:3](https://ref.ly/Ps49:3); [PRO 15:28](https://ref.ly/Prov15:28), [PRO 19:21](https://ref.ly/Prov19:21); [ECC 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:3), [ECC 8:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:16); [DAN 4:16](https://ref.ly/Dan4:16), [DAN 5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:12), [DAN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Dan7:1); [ROM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Rom10:10), [ROM 14:5](https://ref.ly/Rom14:5); [1CO 14:14–14:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:14-1Cor14:15); [1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13); [REV 17:9](https://ref.ly/Rev17:9)

The fallen human mind
---------------------

### The human mind is fatally flawed by sin

**[JER 17:9](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9)** *See also* [GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5), [GEN 8:21](https://ref.ly/Gen8:21); [2CH 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:14); [PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9), [PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6), [PSA 73:7](https://ref.ly/Ps73:7); [ISA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Isa32:6); [JER 17:1](https://ref.ly/Jer17:1); [ROM 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5-Rom8:8); [EPH 4:17–4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17-Eph4:18)

### Even the fallen mind can know something of God through creation

**[ROM 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:19-Rom1:20)**

### The fallen mind tends to confuse the Creator with his creation

**[ISA 44:16–44:18](https://ref.ly/Isa44:16-Isa44:18)** *See also* [ROM 1:21–1:25](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21-Rom1:25)

Consequences of the human mind's sinfulness
-------------------------------------------

### Sinful attitudes lead to sinful words and actions

**[MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19)** parallel [MRK 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:22) *See also* [ROM 1:28–1:32](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28-Rom1:32); [EPH 4:17–4:19](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17-Eph4:19); [COL 2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:18); [1TI 6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:5); [2TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:8)

### Sinfulness leads to death

**[ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5)** *See also* [ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23); [COL 1:21](https://ref.ly/Col1:21)

### Doubt and instability

**[JAS 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Jas1:6-Jas1:8)**

God can change the attitudes of the sinful human mind
-----------------------------------------------------

### The mind can be convicted of sin

**[PSA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Ps51:3)** *See also* [1KI 2:44](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:44); [JHN 16:8–16:11](https://ref.ly/John16:8-John16:11)

### The mind can be changed

**[1TH 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:9)** *See also* [2CH 32:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:26); [JOB 42:6](https://ref.ly/Job42:6); [PSA 119:36](https://ref.ly/Ps119:36); [ACT 2:37](https://ref.ly/Acts2:37), [ACT 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:17-Acts26:18)

### It is God who enlightens the mind

**[2CO 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:6)** *See also* [DEU 29:4](https://ref.ly/Deut29:4) *God may withhold understanding*; [1SA 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:9); [JOB 38:36](https://ref.ly/Job38:36); [ISA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Isa32:4); [JER 24:7](https://ref.ly/Jer24:7), [JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33), [JER 32:39](https://ref.ly/Jer32:39); [EZK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:19), [EZK 18:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:31), [EZK 36:26–36:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:26-Ezek36:27); [ROM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rom2:4); [2CO 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:14)

It is God who renews the human mind
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Ps51:10)** *See also* [TIT 3:3–3:7](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3-Titus3:7)

The renewed mind of the believer
--------------------------------

**[ROM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1-Rom12:2)**

### Renewal brings knowledge of God

**[COL 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Col1:9-Col1:10)**

### Renewal brings peace

**[PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7)** *See also* [ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3)

### Renewal leads to obedience

**[ROM 8:5](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5)** *See also* [ROM 7:25](https://ref.ly/Rom7:25), [ROM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1-Rom12:2); [COL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Col1:10), [COL 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Col3:1-Col3:2); [1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13); [HEB 8:10](https://ref.ly/Heb8:10), [HEB 10:16](https://ref.ly/Heb10:16); [JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33)

### The believer's mind needs growth and renewal

**[2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3)** *See also* [ROM 7:22–7:23](https://ref.ly/Rom7:22-Rom7:23); [EPH 1:17](https://ref.ly/Eph1:17); [COL 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 12:1-3; 1 Chronicles 17:2; 2 Chronicles 7:11; Nehemiah 5:7; Psalms 19:14; Psalms 49:3; Proverbs 15:28; Proverbs 19:21; Ecclesiastes 2:3; Ecclesiastes 8:16; Daniel 4:16; Daniel 5:12; Daniel 7:1; Romans 10:10; Romans 14:5; 1 Corinthians 14:14-15; 1 Peter 1:13; Revelation 17:9; Jeremiah 17:9; Genesis 6:5; Genesis 8:21; 2 Chronicles 12:14; Psalms 5:9; Psalms 64:6; Psalms 73:7; Isaiah 32:6; Jeremiah 17:1; Romans 8:5-8; Ephesians 4:17-18; Romans 1:19-20; Isaiah 44:16-18; Romans 1:21-25; Matthew 15:19; Mark 7:21-22; Romans 1:28-32; Ephesians 4:17-19; Colossians 2:18; 1 Timothy 6:5; 2 Timothy 3:8; Romans 2:5; Romans 6:23; Colossians 1:21; James 1:6-8; Psalms 51:3; 1 Kings 2:44; John 16:8-11; 1 Thessalonians 1:9; 2 Chronicles 32:26; Job 42:6; Psalms 119:36; Acts 2:37; Acts 26:17-18; 2 Corinthians 4:6; Deuteronomy 29:4; 1 Samuel 10:9; Job 38:36; Isaiah 32:4; Jeremiah 24:7; Jeremiah 31:33; Jeremiah 32:39; Ezekiel 11:19; Ezekiel 18:31; Ezekiel 36:26-27; Romans 2:4; 2 Corinthians 3:14; Psalms 51:10; Titus 3:3-7; Romans 12:1-2; Colossians 1:9-10; Philippians 4:7; Isaiah 26:3; Romans 8:5; Romans 7:25; Colossians 1:10; Colossians 3:1-2; Hebrews 8:10; Hebrews 10:16; 2 Corinthians 11:3; Romans 7:22-23; Ephesians 1:17; Colossians 1:9
* **Associated Articles:** 1409: dream (ID: `1409@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1441: revelation, necessity of (ID: `1441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5894: intelligence (ID: `5894@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5904: maturity, spiritual (ID: `5904@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6178: hardness of heart (ID: `6178@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6181: ignorance (ID: `6181@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6733: repentance, nature of (ID: `6733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8145: renewal, of people of God (ID: `8145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8408: decision-making (ID: `8408@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8419: enlightenment (ID: `8419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8777: lust (ID: `8777@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5040: murder (id: 5040)

The unlawful killing of individual human beings, forbidden by God and carrying the threat of severe punishment.

Murder forbidden by the law
---------------------------

**[EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13)** parallel [DEU 5:17](https://ref.ly/Deut5:17) *See also* [MAT 5:21](https://ref.ly/Matt5:21), [MAT 19:18](https://ref.ly/Matt19:18) parallels [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19), [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20); [ROM 13:9](https://ref.ly/Rom13:9); [1TI 1:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9); [JAS 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Jas2:11-Jas2:12)

Murder is to be punished
------------------------

### Punishment by death

**[EXO 21:12](https://ref.ly/Exod21:12)** *See also* [GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6); [LEV 24:17](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17), [LEV 24:21](https://ref.ly/Lev24:21); [NUM 35:16–35:21](https://ref.ly/Num35:16-Num35:21), [NUM 35:31](https://ref.ly/Num35:31); [DEU 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:13)

### Punishment by banishing

**[GEN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Gen4:13-Gen4:14)** *See also* [PRO 28:17](https://ref.ly/Prov28:17)

### Punishment by exclusion from the kingdom

[REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8), [REV 22:15](https://ref.ly/Rev22:15)

Unintentional murderers are protected from avengers
---------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 35:11](https://ref.ly/Num35:11)** *See also* [NUM 35:22–35:29](https://ref.ly/Num35:22-Num35:29), [NUM 35:32](https://ref.ly/Num35:32); [JOS 21:13](https://ref.ly/Josh21:13), [JOS 21:21](https://ref.ly/Josh21:21), [JOS 21:27](https://ref.ly/Josh21:27), [JOS 21:32](https://ref.ly/Josh21:32), [JOS 21:38](https://ref.ly/Josh21:38)

Witnesses are required before murder is proven
----------------------------------------------

**[NUM 35:30](https://ref.ly/Num35:30)** *See also* [DEU 17:6](https://ref.ly/Deut17:6), [DEU 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15); [HEB 10:28](https://ref.ly/Heb10:28)

Unpremeditated murder
---------------------

**[NUM 35:22–35:24](https://ref.ly/Num35:22-Num35:24)** *See also* [EXO 21:13](https://ref.ly/Exod21:13); [DEU 4:41–4:44](https://ref.ly/Deut4:41-Deut4:44), [DEU 19:4](https://ref.ly/Deut19:4); [JOS 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:3)

Premeditated motives of murder
------------------------------

### Jealousy

**[GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8)** *See also* [1JN 3:12](https://ref.ly/1John3:12)

### Lust

**[2SA 12:9–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:9-2Sam12:10)** *Many motives contributed to David's plan to remove Uriah. It began with lust.* *See also* [2SA 11:1–11:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:1-2Sam11:17)

### Rebellion and power

**[2KI 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:20)** *See also* [JDG 3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:21); [2KI 9:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:14), [2KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:24), [2KI 19:36–19:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:36-2Kgs19:37)

### To remove opponents

**[1KI 2:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:25)** *See also* [JDG 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Judg9:1-Judg9:6); [2SA 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9-2Sam20:10); [2CH 21:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:4)

### To take revenge

**[2SA 13:28–13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:28-2Sam13:29)** *See also* [EXO 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Exod2:11-Exod2:13); [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27), [2SA 4:1–4:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:1-2Sam4:8)

### Robbery

**[PRO 1:11–1:13](https://ref.ly/Prov1:11-Prov1:13)** *See also* [LUK 10:30](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30)

### Persecution

**[MAT 24:9](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9)** parallel [LUK 21:12–21:17](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12-Luke21:17) *See also* [2CH 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:13); [JOB 24:14](https://ref.ly/Job24:14); [PRO 29:10](https://ref.ly/Prov29:10); [MAT 14:6–14:11](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6-Matt14:11), [MAT 23:30–23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:30-Matt23:37); [MRK 3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:6); [ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23), [ACT 7:54–7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54-Acts7:60); [2TI 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:6)

Murder as an attitude as well as an action
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:21–5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:21-Matt5:22)** *See also* [MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19); [JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44); [1JN 3:15](https://ref.ly/1John3:15)

Murderers can be pardoned
-------------------------

### Unjustly

**[LUK 23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:25)** parallels [MAT 27:15–27:17](https://ref.ly/Matt27:15-Matt27:17), [MRK 15:6–15:11](https://ref.ly/Mark15:6-Mark15:11), [JHN 18:39–18:40](https://ref.ly/John18:39-John18:40) *See also* [ACT 3:14](https://ref.ly/Acts3:14)

### Justly\-\-through God's grace

**[2SA 12:9–12:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:9-2Sam12:13)**; **[ACT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10)**; **[1TI 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:13-1Tim1:15)** *See also* [EXO 2:12–3:12](https://ref.ly/Exod2:12-Exod3:12); [ACT 7:58](https://ref.ly/Acts7:58), [ACT 8:3](https://ref.ly/Acts8:3), [ACT 9:1](https://ref.ly/Acts9:1), [ACT 9:13](https://ref.ly/Acts9:13), [ACT 22:4](https://ref.ly/Acts22:4); [1CO 15:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:9); [EPH 3:8](https://ref.ly/Eph3:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:13; Deuteronomy 5:17; Matthew 5:21; Matthew 19:18; Mark 10:19; Luke 18:20; Romans 13:9; 1 Timothy 1:9; James 2:11-12; Exodus 21:12; Genesis 9:6; Leviticus 24:17; Leviticus 24:21; Numbers 35:16-21; Numbers 35:31; Deuteronomy 19:11-13; Genesis 4:13-14; Proverbs 28:17; Revelation 21:8; Revelation 22:15; Numbers 35:11; Numbers 35:22-29; Numbers 35:32; Joshua 21:13; Joshua 21:21; Joshua 21:27; Joshua 21:32; Joshua 21:38; Numbers 35:30; Deuteronomy 17:6; Deuteronomy 19:15; Hebrews 10:28; Numbers 35:22-24; Exodus 21:13; Deuteronomy 4:41-44; Deuteronomy 19:4; Joshua 20:1-3; Genesis 4:8; 1 John 3:12; 2 Samuel 12:9-10; 2 Samuel 11:1-17; 2 Kings 12:20; Judges 3:21; 2 Kings 9:14; 2 Kings 9:24; 2 Kings 19:36-37; 1 Kings 2:25; Judges 9:1-6; 2 Samuel 20:9-10; 2 Chronicles 21:4; 2 Samuel 13:28-29; Exodus 2:11-13; 2 Samuel 3:27; 2 Samuel 4:1-8; Proverbs 1:11-13; Luke 10:30; Matthew 24:9; Luke 21:12-17; 2 Chronicles 21:13; Job 24:14; Proverbs 29:10; Matthew 14:6-11; Matthew 23:30-37; Mark 3:6; Acts 2:23; Acts 7:54-60; 2 Timothy 4:6; Matthew 5:21-22; Matthew 15:19; John 8:44; 1 John 3:15; Luke 23:25; Matthew 27:15-17; Mark 15:6-11; John 18:39-40; Acts 3:14; 2 Samuel 12:9-13; Acts 26:10; 1 Timothy 1:13-15; Exodus 2:12-3:12; Acts 7:58; Acts 8:3; Acts 9:1; Acts 9:13; Acts 22:4; 1 Corinthians 15:9; Ephesians 3:8

## 5041: name (id: 5041)

The title given to a person or object by one who is in a position of authority or ownership. Names often reflect the characteristics of a person or the aspirations or wishes for that person of the one bestowing the name.

* **Associated Articles:** 5042: name of God, significance of (ID: `5042@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5043: names, significance of (ID: `5043@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5044: names, giving of (ID: `5044@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5042: name of God, significance of (id: 5042)

God's name is synonymous with his person, his presence and his power, and is therefore held in the highest honour.

God is referred to as the Name
------------------------------

**[ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:41)** *See also* [LEV 24:10–24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:10-Lev24:16); [2SA 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:2) parallel [1CH 13:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:6); [3JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/3John1:7)

God's person is revealed in his name
------------------------------------

**[EXO 34:5–34:7](https://ref.ly/Exod34:5-Exod34:7)** *See also* [GEN 14:18–14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18-Gen14:20), [GEN 17:1](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1), [GEN 21:33](https://ref.ly/Gen21:33), [GEN 32:29–32:30](https://ref.ly/Gen32:29-Gen32:30); [EXO 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Exod3:13-Exod3:15), [EXO 6:3](https://ref.ly/Exod6:3), [EXO 33:19](https://ref.ly/Exod33:19); [JDG 13:17–13:18](https://ref.ly/Judg13:17-Judg13:18); [JER 16:21](https://ref.ly/Jer16:21)

God's name is synonymous with his presence
------------------------------------------

**[NUM 6:22–6:27](https://ref.ly/Num6:22-Num6:27)** *See also* [DEU 12:5–12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:5-Deut12:7); [1KI 8:15–8:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:15-1Kgs8:21) parallel [2CH 6:4–6:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:4-2Chr6:11); [NEH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Neh1:9); [PSA 20:1](https://ref.ly/Ps20:1), [PSA 54:1](https://ref.ly/Ps54:1), [PSA 74:7](https://ref.ly/Ps74:7), [PSA 75:1](https://ref.ly/Ps75:1); [ISA 30:27](https://ref.ly/Isa30:27); [JHN 17:11–17:12](https://ref.ly/John17:11-John17:12)

The name of God identifies his people
-------------------------------------

**[DEU 28:10](https://ref.ly/Deut28:10)** *See also* [2CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14); [ISA 43:6–43:7](https://ref.ly/Isa43:6-Isa43:7); [MIC 4:5](https://ref.ly/Mic4:5), [MIC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Mic5:4)

Dishonouring God's name is a serious offence
--------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7)** *See also* [LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12), [LEV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Lev22:2), [LEV 22:32](https://ref.ly/Lev22:32); [PSA 74:10](https://ref.ly/Ps74:10), [PSA 74:18](https://ref.ly/Ps74:18), [PSA 139:20](https://ref.ly/Ps139:20); [EZK 43:7–43:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:7-Ezek43:8); [MAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6)

The righteous honour God's name
-------------------------------

**[PSA 29:2](https://ref.ly/Ps29:2)** *See also* [NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11); [PSA 86:11–86:12](https://ref.ly/Ps86:11-Ps86:12); [ISA 26:13](https://ref.ly/Isa26:13); [MAL 2:5](https://ref.ly/Mal2:5), [MAL 3:16–4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal3:16-Mal4:2)

The name of Jesus Christ
------------------------

### Supplication is to be made in the name of Jesus Christ

**[JHN 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/John14:13-John14:14)** *See also* [MAT 18:19–18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:19-Matt18:20); [JHN 15:16](https://ref.ly/John15:16), [JHN 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/John16:23-John16:24)

### Healing and other miracles performed in the name of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 3:6–3:8](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6-Acts3:8)** *See also* [ACT 3:16](https://ref.ly/Acts3:16), [ACT 4:30](https://ref.ly/Acts4:30)

### Baptism is in Jesus Christ's name

**[ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38)** *See also* [MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19); [ACT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Acts8:12), [ACT 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Acts8:15-Acts8:16), [ACT 10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:48), [ACT 19:5](https://ref.ly/Acts19:5)

### Salvation is in the name of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:12)** *See also* [JHN 14:6](https://ref.ly/John14:6), [JHN 20:31](https://ref.ly/John20:31); [ACT 2:21](https://ref.ly/Acts2:21); [JOL 2:32](https://ref.ly/Joel2:32); [1CO 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:11); [1JN 2:12](https://ref.ly/1John2:12); [PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 5:41; Leviticus 24:10-16; 2 Samuel 6:2; 1 Chronicles 13:6; 3 John 1:7; Exodus 34:5-7; Genesis 14:18-20; Genesis 17:1; Genesis 21:33; Genesis 32:29-30; Exodus 3:13-15; Exodus 6:3; Exodus 33:19; Judges 13:17-18; Jeremiah 16:21; Numbers 6:22-27; Deuteronomy 12:5-7; 1 Kings 8:15-21; 2 Chronicles 6:4-11; Nehemiah 1:9; Psalms 20:1; Psalms 54:1; Psalms 74:7; Psalms 75:1; Isaiah 30:27; John 17:11-12; Deuteronomy 28:10; 2 Chronicles 7:14; Isaiah 43:6-7; Micah 4:5; Micah 5:4; Exodus 20:7; Leviticus 19:12; Leviticus 22:2; Leviticus 22:32; Psalms 74:10; Psalms 74:18; Psalms 139:20; Ezekiel 43:7-8; Malachi 1:6; Psalms 29:2; Nehemiah 1:11; Psalms 86:11-12; Isaiah 26:13; Malachi 2:5; Malachi 3:16-4:2; John 14:13-14; Matthew 18:19-20; John 15:16; John 16:23-24; Acts 3:6-8; Acts 3:16; Acts 4:30; Acts 2:38; Matthew 28:19; Acts 8:12; Acts 8:15-16; Acts 10:48; Acts 19:5; Acts 4:12; John 14:6; John 20:31; Acts 2:21; Joel 2:32; 1 Corinthians 6:11; 1 John 2:12; Philippians 2:9-11
* **Associated Articles:** 1205: God, titles and names of (ID: `1205@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1415: miracles (ID: `1415@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of (ID: `2203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3110: Holy Spirit, titles and names of (ID: `3110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5429: oaths (ID: `5429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5800: blasphemy (ID: `5800@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6510: salvation (ID: `6510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8334: reverence, and God's nature (ID: `8334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8607: prayer, God's promises concerning (ID: `8607@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8807: profanity (ID: `8807@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5043: names, significance of (id: 5043)

Labels that idenitfy individuals. They may also denote aspects of character or reputation.

A name may reflect an individual's nature
-----------------------------------------

**[1SA 25:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:25)** *See also* [GEN 17:5](https://ref.ly/Gen17:5) *“Abraham” means “father of many”*; [GEN 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/Gen32:27-Gen32:29) *“Israel” means “he struggles with God”*; [EXO 34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:14); [MRK 5:9](https://ref.ly/Mark5:9) parallel [LUK 8:30](https://ref.ly/Luke8:30)

### Additional names or nicknames

**[MRK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Mark3:17)** *In NT times an additional name or surname reflecting someone's character was sometimes appended to the given name.* *See also* [LUK 6:15](https://ref.ly/Luke6:15); [JHN 1:42](https://ref.ly/John1:42) *“Cephas” and “Peter” mean “rock” in Aramaic and Greek respectively*; [ACT 4:36](https://ref.ly/Acts4:36)

A name may indicate an individual's reputation and standing in the community
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

### A good name indicates a good reputation

**[PRO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1)** *See also* [PRO 3:4](https://ref.ly/Prov3:4), [PRO 10:7](https://ref.ly/Prov10:7); [ECC 7:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:1); [SNG 1:3](https://ref.ly/Song1:3)

### A bad name indicates a bad reputation

**[NEH 6:13](https://ref.ly/Neh6:13)** *See also* [DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19); [PRO 25:9–25:10](https://ref.ly/Prov25:9-Prov25:10)

Survival of one's name is important
-----------------------------------

**[RUT 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:9-Ruth4:10)** *See also* [GEN 48:15–48:16](https://ref.ly/Gen48:15-Gen48:16); [NUM 27:1–27:4](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:4); [DEU 25:7–25:9](https://ref.ly/Deut25:7-Deut25:9); [2SA 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:18); [JOB 18:17–18:19](https://ref.ly/Job18:17-Job18:19)

### Preservation of a name indicates divine blessing

**[ISA 56:4–56:5](https://ref.ly/Isa56:4-Isa56:5)** *See also* [GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3); [2SA 7:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:9) parallel [1CH 17:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:8); [1KI 1:47](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:47); [ISA 66:22](https://ref.ly/Isa66:22)

Obliteration of a name is associated with death
-----------------------------------------------

**[JER 11:19](https://ref.ly/Jer11:19)** *See also* [DEU 7:24](https://ref.ly/Deut7:24), [DEU 9:14](https://ref.ly/Deut9:14); [2SA 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:7); [2KI 14:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:27)

Obliteration of a name is often linked with divine judgment
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Ps9:5-Ps9:6)** *See also* [DEU 29:20](https://ref.ly/Deut29:20); [PSA 109:13](https://ref.ly/Ps109:13); [ISA 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Isa14:22-Isa14:23), [ISA 48:18–48:19](https://ref.ly/Isa48:18-Isa48:19); [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14)

Bearing someone's name indicates ownership or identification
------------------------------------------------------------

**[REV 22:3–22:4](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3-Rev22:4)** *See also* [EXO 23:21](https://ref.ly/Exod23:21); [2CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14); [DEU 28:10](https://ref.ly/Deut28:10); [ISA 43:6–43:7](https://ref.ly/Isa43:6-Isa43:7); [JER 14:9](https://ref.ly/Jer14:9); [DAN 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/Dan9:18-Dan9:19); [REV 3:12](https://ref.ly/Rev3:12), [REV 14:1](https://ref.ly/Rev14:1), [REV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Rev22:4)

God knows his people by name
----------------------------

**[ISA 43:1](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1)** *See also* [EXO 33:12](https://ref.ly/Exod33:12), [EXO 33:17](https://ref.ly/Exod33:17); [ISA 45:3](https://ref.ly/Isa45:3), [ISA 49:1](https://ref.ly/Isa49:1); [JHN 10:3](https://ref.ly/John10:3)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 25:25; Genesis 17:5; Genesis 32:27-29; Exodus 34:14; Mark 5:9; Luke 8:30; Mark 3:17; Luke 6:15; John 1:42; Acts 4:36; Proverbs 22:1; Proverbs 3:4; Proverbs 10:7; Ecclesiastes 7:1; Song of Songs 1:3; Nehemiah 6:13; Deuteronomy 22:13-19; Proverbs 25:9-10; Ruth 4:9-10; Genesis 48:15-16; Numbers 27:1-4; Deuteronomy 25:7-9; 2 Samuel 18:18; Job 18:17-19; Isaiah 56:4-5; Genesis 12:1-3; 2 Samuel 7:9; 1 Chronicles 17:8; 1 Kings 1:47; Isaiah 66:22; Jeremiah 11:19; Deuteronomy 7:24; Deuteronomy 9:14; 2 Samuel 14:7; 2 Kings 14:27; Psalms 9:5-6; Deuteronomy 29:20; Psalms 109:13; Isaiah 14:22-23; Isaiah 48:18-19; Nahum 1:14; Revelation 22:3-4; Exodus 23:21; 2 Chronicles 7:14; Deuteronomy 28:10; Isaiah 43:6-7; Jeremiah 14:9; Daniel 9:18-19; Revelation 3:12; Revelation 14:1; Revelation 22:4; Isaiah 43:1; Exodus 33:12; Exodus 33:17; Isaiah 45:3; Isaiah 49:1; John 10:3

## 5044: names, giving of (id: 5044)

Scripture contains many examples of the naming of people and places. Personal names, usually given at birth by parents, were sometimes changed at a later date.

Names may reflect personal experiences
--------------------------------------

**[GEN 41:51–41:52](https://ref.ly/Gen41:51-Gen41:52)** *See also* [GEN 29:31–29:35](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31-Gen29:35), [GEN 30:6–30:8](https://ref.ly/Gen30:6-Gen30:8), [GEN 30:18–30:20](https://ref.ly/Gen30:18-Gen30:20), [GEN 30:23–30:24](https://ref.ly/Gen30:23-Gen30:24), [GEN 35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:18); [EXO 2:10](https://ref.ly/Exod2:10), [EXO 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/Exod2:21-Exod2:22), [EXO 18:3–18:4](https://ref.ly/Exod18:3-Exod18:4); [1SA 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:20); [1CH 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:9)

Names may reflect national experiences
--------------------------------------

**[1SA 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:21)** *See also* [GEN 10:25](https://ref.ly/Gen10:25) parallel [1CH 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:19); [ISA 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Isa8:3-Isa8:4); [HOS 1:2–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2-Hos1:11)

Names may indicate future destiny
---------------------------------

**[MAT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:21)** *See also* [LUK 1:31–1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:31-Luke1:33)

The giving of a name reflects the authority of the giver
--------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 1:5–1:10](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5-Gen1:10), [GEN 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Gen2:19-Gen2:20)** *See also* [GEN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23), [GEN 35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:18); [2KI 23:34](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:34) parallel [2CH 36:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:4); [2KI 24:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:17); [LUK 1:62–1:63](https://ref.ly/Luke1:62-Luke1:63)

The changing of names
---------------------

### On divine instruction

**[GEN 17:3–17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:3-Gen17:8)** *See also* [GEN 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/Gen17:15-Gen17:16), [GEN 35:9–35:10](https://ref.ly/Gen35:9-Gen35:10); [JHN 1:42](https://ref.ly/John1:42)

### In relation to salvation

**[ISA 62:2](https://ref.ly/Isa62:2)** *See also* [ISA 65:15](https://ref.ly/Isa65:15); [REV 2:17](https://ref.ly/Rev2:17)

### In the light of past experience

**[JDG 6:32](https://ref.ly/Judg6:32)** *See also* [RUT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:20)

### In the future

**[ZEC 8:3](https://ref.ly/Zech8:3)** *See also* [ISA 1:26](https://ref.ly/Isa1:26), [ISA 44:5](https://ref.ly/Isa44:5), [ISA 58:12](https://ref.ly/Isa58:12), [ISA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Isa62:4), [ISA 62:12](https://ref.ly/Isa62:12); [JER 7:32](https://ref.ly/Jer7:32), [JER 20:3–20:6](https://ref.ly/Jer20:3-Jer20:6); [MAL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mal1:4)

### In a new culture

**[GEN 41:45](https://ref.ly/Gen41:45)** *See also* [DAN 1:7](https://ref.ly/Dan1:7)

### The instance of Paul

[ACT 13:9](https://ref.ly/Acts13:9) *The occasion of and reason for the change from “Saul” to “Paul” are not clear.*

Place\-names may reflect events or experiences
----------------------------------------------

[GEN 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:8-Gen11:9), [GEN 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/Gen16:13-Gen16:14), [GEN 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Gen19:21-Gen19:22), [GEN 21:31](https://ref.ly/Gen21:31), [GEN 26:32–26:33](https://ref.ly/Gen26:32-Gen26:33), [GEN 28:18–28:19](https://ref.ly/Gen28:18-Gen28:19), [GEN 31:45–31:50](https://ref.ly/Gen31:45-Gen31:50), [GEN 32:1–32:2](https://ref.ly/Gen32:1-Gen32:2), [GEN 32:30](https://ref.ly/Gen32:30), [GEN 33:17](https://ref.ly/Gen33:17), [GEN 35:7–35:8](https://ref.ly/Gen35:7-Gen35:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 41:51-52; Genesis 29:31-35; Genesis 30:6-8; Genesis 30:18-20; Genesis 30:23-24; Genesis 35:18; Exodus 2:10; Exodus 2:21-22; Exodus 18:3-4; 1 Samuel 1:20; 1 Chronicles 4:9; 1 Samuel 4:21; Genesis 10:25; 1 Chronicles 1:19; Isaiah 8:3-4; Hosea 1:2-11; Matthew 1:21; Luke 1:31-33; Genesis 1:5-10; Genesis 2:19-20; Genesis 2:23; 2 Kings 23:34; 2 Chronicles 36:4; 2 Kings 24:17; Luke 1:62-63; Genesis 17:3-8; Genesis 17:15-16; Genesis 35:9-10; John 1:42; Isaiah 62:2; Isaiah 65:15; Revelation 2:17; Judges 6:32; Ruth 1:20; Zechariah 8:3; Isaiah 1:26; Isaiah 44:5; Isaiah 58:12; Isaiah 62:4; Isaiah 62:12; Jeremiah 7:32; Jeremiah 20:3-6; Malachi 1:4; Genesis 41:45; Daniel 1:7; Acts 13:9; Genesis 11:8-9; Genesis 16:13-14; Genesis 19:21-22; Genesis 21:31; Genesis 26:32-33; Genesis 28:18-19; Genesis 31:45-50; Genesis 32:1-2; Genesis 32:30; Genesis 33:17; Genesis 35:7-8
* **Associated Articles:** 1065: God, holiness of (ID: `1065@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1105: God, power of (ID: `1105@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1348: covenant, God's with Abraham (ID: `1348@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5655: birth (ID: `5655@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5046: opportunities (id: 5046)

Events or moments at which actions of importance or significance can be achieved. God provides opportune moments both for his people to serve him and to proclaim the gospel and for those outside the church to respond to the gospel.

* **Associated Articles:** 5047: opportunities, in life (ID: `5047@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5048: opportunities, and salvation (ID: `5048@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5047: opportunities, in life (id: 5047)

Scripture views life as divinely ordained to include occasions when certain forms of behaviour are appropriate.

The need to discern opportune moments
-------------------------------------

**[ECC 3:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:1)**

Every possible opportunity should be taken to do good
-----------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10)** *See also* [LUK 10:33](https://ref.ly/Luke10:33); [JHN 9:4](https://ref.ly/John9:4); [EPH 5:16](https://ref.ly/Eph5:16); [COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5); [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)

Examples of individuals who seized opportunities to serve God
-------------------------------------------------------------

Rahab saved the spies and her household: [JOS 2:8–2:14](https://ref.ly/Josh2:8-Josh2:14), [JOS 6:23](https://ref.ly/Josh6:23); [2KI 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:1-2Kgs5:3) *Naaman's wife's maid helped Naaman by telling her mistress about Elisha*; ; [NEH 2:2–2:5](https://ref.ly/Neh2:2-Neh2:5) *Nehemiah gained permission to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.*; [EST 4:14](https://ref.ly/Esth4:14), [EST 7:3–7:6](https://ref.ly/Esth7:3-Esth7:6); [DAN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Dan2:14-Dan2:16) *Daniel offered to interpret Nebuchadnezzar's dream*; ; [ACT 18:24–18:26](https://ref.ly/Acts18:24-Acts18:26) *Priscilla and Aquila taught Apollos.*

Some opportunities should be rejected
-------------------------------------

[1SA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:3-1Sam24:4), [1SA 24:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:10), [1SA 26:6–26:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:6-1Sam26:11)

Opportunities to do evil may have appalling consequences
--------------------------------------------------------

[GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8) *Cain made an opportunity to murder Abel*; ; [GEN 25:29–25:31](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:31) *Jacob took the opportunity to “buy” Esau's birthright.*; [GEN 37:18–37:20](https://ref.ly/Gen37:18-Gen37:20), [GEN 37:27](https://ref.ly/Gen37:27); [DAN 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Dan6:4-Dan6:5) *Daniel's enemies sought and found an opportunity to lay a charge against him.*; [MAT 26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:16) parallels [MRK 14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:11), [LUK 22:6](https://ref.ly/Luke22:6)

Giving others an opportunity to do wrong must be avoided
--------------------------------------------------------

[1TI 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Ecclesiastes 3:1; Galatians 6:10; Luke 10:33; John 9:4; Ephesians 5:16; Colossians 4:5; 2 Timothy 4:2; Joshua 2:8-14; Joshua 6:23; 2 Kings 5:1-3; Nehemiah 2:2-5; Esther 4:14; Esther 7:3-6; Daniel 2:14-16; Acts 18:24-26; 1 Samuel 24:3-4; 1 Samuel 24:10; 1 Samuel 26:6-11; Genesis 4:8; Genesis 25:29-31; Genesis 37:18-20; Genesis 37:27; Daniel 6:4-5; Matthew 26:16; Mark 14:11; Luke 22:6; 1 Timothy 5:14
* **Associated Articles:** 1355: providence (ID: `1355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4903: time (ID: `4903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4918: dawn (ID: `4918@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4921: day (ID: `4921@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4933: evening (ID: `4933@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4954: morning (ID: `4954@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4957: night (ID: `4957@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5299: door (ID: `5299@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5861: favour, human (ID: `5861@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8493: watchfulness, of believers (ID: `8493@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5048: opportunities, and salvation (id: 5048)

God chooses the right moment in his work to save humanity. It is important to seize the opportunities he offers, both in receiving salvation and in working with him to offer it to others.

Israel had opportunities of salvation
-------------------------------------

### At Sinai

[EXO 19:4–19:8](https://ref.ly/Exod19:4-Exod19:8) *God had saved Israel from slavery in Egypt. Now they accepted the opportunity to become his covenant people.*

### At Shechem

[JOS 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Josh24:14-Josh24:15) *After Israel entered Canaan, Joshua challenged them to forsake idolatry and renew the covenant with the LORD.*

The coming of Jesus Christ offers a new and urgent opportunity to respond to God
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Matt3:1-Matt3:2)**; **[GAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4)** *See also* [MAT 10:7](https://ref.ly/Matt10:7); [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [ROM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6)

The message of opportunity continues after the resurrection of Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### For Israel

[ACT 3:24–3:26](https://ref.ly/Acts3:24-Acts3:26)

### For the Gentiles

**[ACT 17:30](https://ref.ly/Acts17:30)** *See also* [ACT 17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:23), [ACT 17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:31)

God in his mercy gives opportunity to repent and believe
--------------------------------------------------------

[ROM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rom2:4); ; [2PE 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:8-2Pet3:9)

The urgency of the need to respond to God's opportunity of salvation
--------------------------------------------------------------------

**[2CO 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:2)**; **[HEB 4:7](https://ref.ly/Heb4:7)** *See also* [PSA 95:7–95:8](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:8); [ISA 49:8](https://ref.ly/Isa49:8), [ISA 55:6–55:7](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6-Isa55:7)

God will judge evil at an opportune time
----------------------------------------

**[REV 14:18](https://ref.ly/Rev14:18)** *See also* [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7)

It is possible to refuse the opportunity of salvation, with sad consequences
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The lesson of Israel

[NUM 14:8–14:10](https://ref.ly/Num14:8-Num14:10); ; [PSA 95:7–95:11](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:11); ; [2CO 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:1-2Cor6:2); ; [HEB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Heb4:3), [HEB 4:7](https://ref.ly/Heb4:7) *Hebrews warns Christians not to miss their chance of blessing.*

### The seriousness of rejecting the good news

[MAT 10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:14) parallels [MRK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:11), [LUK 9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:5); ; [LUK 19:41–19:42](https://ref.ly/Luke19:41-Luke19:42) *Jesus Christ wept over Jerusalem because the city didn't recognise its opportunity*; ; [ACT 13:46–13:51](https://ref.ly/Acts13:46-Acts13:51), [ACT 28:25–28:28](https://ref.ly/Acts28:25-Acts28:28)

Christians should take every opportunity to tell others the good news
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 4:35](https://ref.ly/John4:35)** *See also* [LUK 10:2](https://ref.ly/Luke10:2); [1CO 16:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:9); [COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5); [1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 19:4-8; Joshua 24:14-15; Matthew 3:1-2; Galatians 4:4; Matthew 10:7; Mark 1:15; Romans 5:6; Acts 3:24-26; Acts 17:30; Acts 17:23; Acts 17:31; Romans 2:4; 2 Peter 3:8-9; 2 Corinthians 6:2; Hebrews 4:7; Psalms 95:7-8; Isaiah 49:8; Isaiah 55:6-7; Revelation 14:18; Revelation 14:7; Numbers 14:8-10; Psalms 95:7-11; 2 Corinthians 6:1-2; Hebrews 4:3; Matthew 10:14; Mark 6:11; Luke 9:5; Luke 19:41-42; Acts 13:46-51; Acts 28:25-28; John 4:35; Luke 10:2; 1 Corinthians 16:9; Colossians 4:5; 1 Peter 3:15
* **Associated Articles:** 1055: God, grace and mercy of (ID: `1055@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1095: God, patience of (ID: `1095@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2377: kingdom of God, entry into (ID: `2377@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2426: gospel, responses to (ID: `2426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6232: rejection of God, results of (ID: `6232@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8489: urgency (ID: `8489@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9240: last judgment (ID: `9240@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5050: reason (id: 5050)

The human capacity to think logically and intelligently, thereby discovering truth or persuading others. But human reason is limited and cannot arrive at a true and full knowledge of God without divine assistance and revelation.

The human power of reason
-------------------------

**[1CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:11)**

Human reason is limited
-----------------------

**[JOB 32:11–32:12](https://ref.ly/Job32:11-Job32:12)** *See also* [JOB 32:3](https://ref.ly/Job32:3)

Human reason is far inferior to divine reason
---------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:18–1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18-1Cor1:31)** *See also* [JOB 12:20](https://ref.ly/Job12:20), [JOB 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/Job12:24-Job12:25); [ISA 29:14](https://ref.ly/Isa29:14), [ISA 55:8–55:9](https://ref.ly/Isa55:8-Isa55:9)

Human reason may point people towards God
-----------------------------------------

**[ROM 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Rom2:14-Rom2:15)** *See also* [ISA 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18-Isa1:20); [ACT 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:22-Acts17:23), [ACT 17:27–17:28](https://ref.ly/Acts17:27-Acts17:28); [ROM 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:19-Rom1:20); [HEB 11:17–11:19](https://ref.ly/Heb11:17-Heb11:19)

Paul reasons with unbelievers
-----------------------------

**[ACT 17:2–17:4](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2-Acts17:4)** *See also* [ACT 17:17](https://ref.ly/Acts17:17), [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4), [ACT 18:19](https://ref.ly/Acts18:19)

Believers are to give reasons for their faith
---------------------------------------------

**[1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)**

God reasons with his rebellious people
--------------------------------------

**[ISA 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18-Isa1:19)**

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 13:11; Job 32:11-12; Job 32:3; 1 Corinthians 1:18-31; Job 12:20; Job 12:24-25; Isaiah 29:14; Isaiah 55:8-9; Romans 2:14-15; Isaiah 1:18-20; Acts 17:22-23; Acts 17:27-28; Romans 1:19-20; Hebrews 11:17-19; Acts 17:2-4; Acts 17:17; Acts 18:4; Acts 18:19; 1 Peter 3:15; Isaiah 1:18-19
* **Associated Articles:** 1440: revelation, through creation (ID: `1440@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2054: Jesus Christ, mind of (ID: `2054@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5008: conscience (ID: `5008@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5029: knowledge, of God (ID: `5029@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5038: mind, the human (ID: `5038@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5191: thought (ID: `5191@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5441: philosophy (ID: `5441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5894: intelligence (ID: `5894@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7703: apologetics (ID: `7703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7751: persuasion (ID: `7751@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5051: responsibility (id: 5051)

An obligation, especially to give account of one's actions. Scripture stresses that the responsibilities which believers and the church have arise from their special and privileged relationship to God.

* **Associated Articles:** 5052: responsibility, to God (ID: `5052@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5053: responsibility, for God's world (ID: `5053@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5054: responsibility, examples of (ID: `5054@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5052: responsibility, to God (id: 5052)

Even though God is sovereign, all people everywhere are accountable to him for everything they do, say or think.

People are responsible for their own sins
-----------------------------------------

### God is not responsible for human sin

**[DEU 24:16](https://ref.ly/Deut24:16)**; **[EZK 18:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:4)** *See also* [JOB 19:4](https://ref.ly/Job19:4); [PSA 51:4–51:5](https://ref.ly/Ps51:4-Ps51:5); [JER 31:30](https://ref.ly/Jer31:30); [EZK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:20), [EZK 33:1–33:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:1-Ezek33:20); [ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)

### Death is the result of sin

**[ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12), [ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:7); [ISA 43:27](https://ref.ly/Isa43:27); [EZK 18:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:4); [1CO 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:22)

The law heightens human responsibility for sin
----------------------------------------------

### It brings knowledge of sin

**[ROM 5:20](https://ref.ly/Rom5:20)** *Because the law brings knowledge of sin, it prevents people from claiming ignorance of the nature and seriousness of that sin.* *See also* [ROM 7:7–7:9](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7-Rom7:9); [GAL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gal3:19)

### It applies not only to actions but also to words and thoughts

**[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22), [MAT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28)** *See also* [JER 17:9–17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9-Jer17:10); [MAT 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:18-Matt15:19)

### It brings judgment

**[ECC 12:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:14)**; **[MAT 12:36–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36-Matt12:37)**; **[1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)** *See also* [JOB 15:5–15:6](https://ref.ly/Job15:5-Job15:6); [ROM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Rom2:16), [ROM 14:12](https://ref.ly/Rom14:12); [2CO 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:10); [1PE 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:5); [JUD 1:15](https://ref.ly/Jude1:15)

Ignorance does not remove responsibility
----------------------------------------

**[LEV 5:17](https://ref.ly/Lev5:17)** *See also* [LUK 12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:48); [ACT 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Acts3:17-Acts3:19); [ROM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20)

The responsibility of humanity to repent and believe
----------------------------------------------------

### It is commanded by God

**[ACT 17:30](https://ref.ly/Acts17:30)** *See also* [EZK 18:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:30); [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17); [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15), [MRK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Mark6:12); [LUK 24:46–24:47](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46-Luke24:47)

### Jesus Christ's life and ministry heightens the responsibility to repent

**[JHN 3:18](https://ref.ly/John3:18), [JHN 15:22](https://ref.ly/John15:22)** *See also* [MAT 11:20–11:24](https://ref.ly/Matt11:20-Matt11:24), [MAT 12:41–12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:41-Matt12:42) parallel [LUK 11:31–11:32](https://ref.ly/Luke11:31-Luke11:32); [JHN 12:48](https://ref.ly/John12:48)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 24:16; Ezekiel 18:4; Job 19:4; Psalms 51:4-5; Jeremiah 31:30; Ezekiel 18:20; Ezekiel 33:1-20; Romans 6:23; Romans 5:12; Genesis 3:1-7; Isaiah 43:27; 1 Corinthians 15:21-22; Romans 5:20; Romans 7:7-9; Galatians 3:19; Matthew 5:22; Matthew 5:28; Jeremiah 17:9-10; Matthew 15:18-19; Ecclesiastes 12:14; Matthew 12:36-37; 1 Corinthians 4:5; Job 15:5-6; Romans 2:16; Romans 14:12; 2 Corinthians 5:10; 1 Peter 4:5; Jude 1:15; Leviticus 5:17; Luke 12:48; Acts 3:17-19; Romans 1:20; Acts 17:30; Ezekiel 18:30; Matthew 4:17; Mark 1:15; Mark 6:12; Luke 24:46-47; John 3:18; John 15:22; Matthew 11:20-24; Matthew 12:41-42; Luke 11:31-32; John 12:48
* **Associated Articles:** 5376: law, purpose of (ID: `5376@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5667: children, responsibilities to God (ID: `5667@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6611: adoption, privileges and duties of (ID: `6611@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6641: election, responsibilities of (ID: `6641@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7760: preachers, responsibilities of (ID: `7760@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7967: spiritual gifts, and responsibility (ID: `7967@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8403: commands (ID: `8403@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8495: witnessing (ID: `8495@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5053: responsibility, for God's world (id: 5053)

All humanity has a moral obligation under God to one another, whether as national leaders, ministers, spouses, parents, children, believers or simply as human beings. This moral obligation is shown in the Scriptures to also include the animal kingdom and the environment.

The responsibility of national leaders
--------------------------------------

### To reward good and punish evil

**[ROM 13:3–13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:3-Rom13:4)** *See also* [PRO 16:12](https://ref.ly/Prov16:12)

### To administer justice

**[PRO 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:5), [PRO 31:8–31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:8-Prov31:9)** *See also* [DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19); [PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31), [PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5), [PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4), [PRO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Prov29:14)

The responsibility of Christian leaders
---------------------------------------

### To care for believers

**[1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)** *See also* [JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17); [ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)

### To preach, teach and handle the Scriptures properly

**[2TI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15), [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)** *See also* [EZK 33:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:6)

The responsibility of those who are married
-------------------------------------------

### To love each other

**[EPH 5:25](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25)**; **[TIT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4)**

### To mutually honour each other

**[EPH 5:22](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22)**; **[1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)** *See also* [1PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1)

The responsibility of parents to their children
-----------------------------------------------

**[EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)** *See also* [DEU 6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:7); [PRO 13:24](https://ref.ly/Prov13:24), [PRO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Prov22:6); [1TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:4); [TIT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4)

The responsibility of children to honour their parents
------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3)** *See also* [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16)

The responsibility of every believer to represent Jesus Christ in the world
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:13](https://ref.ly/Matt5:13), [MAT 5:14](https://ref.ly/Matt5:14), [MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20)** *See also* [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)

The responsibility of every human being to love God and others
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 10:27](https://ref.ly/Luke10:27)** parallels [MAT 22:37–22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:37-Matt22:39), [MRK 12:29–12:31](https://ref.ly/Mark12:29-Mark12:31) *See also* [DEU 6:5](https://ref.ly/Deut6:5); [LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18); [1SA 20:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:17); [PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31); [MAT 5:43–5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:43-Matt5:45); [LUK 10:28–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:28-Luke10:37)

The responsibility to exercise dominion over the animal kingdom and creation
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 1:26](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26)**; **[PRO 12:10](https://ref.ly/Prov12:10)** *See also* [GEN 9:2](https://ref.ly/Gen9:2); [EXO 23:5](https://ref.ly/Exod23:5); [DEU 22:4](https://ref.ly/Deut22:4), [DEU 22:6–22:7](https://ref.ly/Deut22:6-Deut22:7), [DEU 25:4](https://ref.ly/Deut25:4); [PSA 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/Ps8:6-Ps8:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 13:3-4; Proverbs 16:12; Proverbs 31:4-5; Proverbs 31:8-9; Deuteronomy 27:19; Proverbs 14:31; Proverbs 17:5; Proverbs 29:4; Proverbs 29:14; 1 Peter 5:2; John 21:15-17; Acts 20:28; 2 Timothy 2:15; 2 Timothy 4:2; Ezekiel 33:6; Ephesians 5:25; Titus 2:4; Ephesians 5:22; 1 Peter 3:7; 1 Peter 3:1; Ephesians 6:4; Deuteronomy 6:7; Proverbs 13:24; Proverbs 22:6; 1 Timothy 3:4; Ephesians 6:1-3; Deuteronomy 5:16; Matthew 5:13; Matthew 5:14; Matthew 28:19-20; Mark 16:15; Acts 1:8; Luke 10:27; Matthew 22:37-39; Mark 12:29-31; Deuteronomy 6:5; Leviticus 19:18; 1 Samuel 20:17; Matthew 5:43-45; Luke 10:28-37; Genesis 1:26; Proverbs 12:10; Genesis 9:2; Exodus 23:5; Deuteronomy 22:4; Deuteronomy 22:6-7; Deuteronomy 25:4; Psalms 8:6-8
* **Associated Articles:** 4005: creation (ID: `4005@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4029: world, human beings in (ID: `4029@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4603: animals (ID: `4603@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5001: human race, the (ID: `5001@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5366: king (ID: `5366@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7939: ministry (ID: `7939@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7948: mission (ID: `7948@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8242: ethics, personal (ID: `8242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8472: respect, for environment (ID: `8472@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5054: responsibility, examples of (id: 5054)

Scripture provides examples of individuals and situations in which responsibility is accepted, delegated or denied.

Examples of responsibility accepted
-----------------------------------

### Noah to build the ark

**[GEN 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Gen6:13-Gen6:14), [GEN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Gen6:22)** *See also* [HEB 11:7](https://ref.ly/Heb11:7)

### Joseph over Potiphar's household

**[GEN 39:4](https://ref.ly/Gen39:4)** *See also* [GEN 39:1–39:6](https://ref.ly/Gen39:1-Gen39:6)

### Joseph over the other prisoners

**[GEN 39:22](https://ref.ly/Gen39:22)** *See also* [GEN 39:20–39:23](https://ref.ly/Gen39:20-Gen39:23)

### Joseph over the whole of Egypt

**[GEN 41:39–41:41](https://ref.ly/Gen41:39-Gen41:41)** *See also* [GEN 41:55–41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:55-Gen41:57), [GEN 45:8–45:9](https://ref.ly/Gen45:8-Gen45:9)

### Levites to be priests

**[NUM 3:5–3:7](https://ref.ly/Num3:5-Num3:7)** *See also* [NUM 8:5–8:26](https://ref.ly/Num8:5-Num8:26)

### Solomon, not David, to build the temple

[1CH 22:2–22:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:2-1Chr22:10)

### Ezekiel to be a watchman

[EZK 3:16–3:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:16-Ezek3:21)

### Jesus Christ in dying on the cross

**[PHP 2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:8)** *See also* [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39); [JHN 10:18](https://ref.ly/John10:18); [ROM 5:19](https://ref.ly/Rom5:19)

Examples of delegation of responsibility
----------------------------------------

### Moses chose leaders to judge

**[EXO 18:24–18:26](https://ref.ly/Exod18:24-Exod18:26)** *See also* [EXO 18:13–18:23](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13-Exod18:23)

### The apostles delegated responsibility to seven men

**[ACT 6:3](https://ref.ly/Acts6:3)** *See also* [ACT 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:6)

### Responsibility delegated to elders

**[ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23)**

Examples of denial of responsibility
------------------------------------

### Adam and Eve in eating the fruit

**[GEN 3:11–3:13](https://ref.ly/Gen3:11-Gen3:13)** *God held them both responsible.*

### Aaron after making the golden calf

*See also* [EXO 32:19–32:24](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19-Exod32:24)

### Saul after not destroying everything belonging to the Amalekites

[1SA 15:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:3), [1SA 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:7-1Sam15:9), [1SA 15:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:15)

### Pilate after ordering Jesus Christ to be crucified

**[MAT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:24)**

### Judas after betraying Jesus Christ

*See also* [MAT 27:3–27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:5)

* **Associated Articles:** 4208: land, as a divine responsibility (ID: `4208@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5216: authority, nature of (ID: `5216@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5668: children, responsibilities to parents (ID: `5668@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5685: fathers, responsibilities of (ID: `5685@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5716: middle age (ID: `5716@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5719: mothers, responsibilities of (ID: `5719@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5851: excuse (ID: `5851@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6115: blame (ID: `6115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6662: freedom, abuse of Christian (ID: `6662@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6710: privileges (ID: `6710@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8446: hospitality, a duty of God's people (ID: `8446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8452: neighbours, duty to (ID: `8452@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5056: rest (id: 5056)

Scripture recognises the need for physical and spiritual relief and stresses that the true resting\-place of humanity lies in God, the Creator and Redeemer.

* **Associated Articles:** 5057: rest, physical (ID: `5057@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5058: rest, spiritual (ID: `5058@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5059: rest, eternal (ID: `5059@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5057: rest, physical (id: 5057)

The need for physical rest is recognised in Scripture, and provision is made for ensuring that people are allowed time for rest.

Jesus Christ needed physical rest
---------------------------------

**[JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6)** *See also* [MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38), [MRK 6:31](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31); [LUK 9:58](https://ref.ly/Luke9:58)

The provision of rest
---------------------

### The need for rest

**[MRK 6:31](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31)** *See also* [PSA 23:2–23:3](https://ref.ly/Ps23:2-Ps23:3); [PRO 17:1](https://ref.ly/Prov17:1); [ECC 4:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:6); [MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30)

### Provision of sleep for rest

**[PSA 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ps4:8)** *See also* [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); [ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12)

### Provision of a Sabbath\-rest

**[EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21)**; **[MRK 2:27](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27)** *See also* [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11) parallel [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15); [EXO 31:15](https://ref.ly/Exod31:15), [EXO 35:2](https://ref.ly/Exod35:2); [LEV 23:3](https://ref.ly/Lev23:3); [NEH 13:15](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15); [ISA 58:13–58:14](https://ref.ly/Isa58:13-Isa58:14)

The purpose of rest
-------------------

### To renew strength

**[LAM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Lam5:5)** *See also* [ISA 40:29–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:29-Isa40:31)

### To know peace

**[MAT 6:25–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:34)** *See also* [JOB 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/Job11:18-Job11:19); [PSA 3:5](https://ref.ly/Ps3:5), [PSA 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ps16:8); [PRO 3:24](https://ref.ly/Prov3:24); [PHP 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6-Phil4:7)

The character of rest
---------------------

### The absence of war

**[JOS 11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:23)** *See also* [JOS 14:15](https://ref.ly/Josh14:15); [1KI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:4); [1CH 22:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:9); [PSA 46:9–46:10](https://ref.ly/Ps46:9-Ps46:10); [PRO 1:33](https://ref.ly/Prov1:33); [ISA 14:3](https://ref.ly/Isa14:3)

### The absence of social strife

**[2CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:11)** *See also* [ECC 10:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:4); [1CO 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:10); [1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11); [HEB 12:14](https://ref.ly/Heb12:14); [JAS 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Jas3:17-Jas3:18); [1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)

### The absence of fear

**[MRK 4:37–4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:37-Mark4:38)** parallels [MAT 8:24–8:25](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24-Matt8:25), [LUK 8:23–8:24](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23-Luke8:24) *See also* [GEN 32:11](https://ref.ly/Gen32:11); [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); [MIC 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:4); [MAT 6:31](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31) parallel [LUK 12:29](https://ref.ly/Luke12:29)

### The absence of anxiety

**[1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)** *See also* [MAT 6:25](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25); [PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6)

### The presence of God

**[EXO 33:14](https://ref.ly/Exod33:14)** *See also* [DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27); [MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28)

### The experience of security

**[DEU 33:12](https://ref.ly/Deut33:12)** *See also* [PRO 19:23](https://ref.ly/Prov19:23)

### The experience of peace in death

**[REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13)** *See also* [DEU 31:16](https://ref.ly/Deut31:16); [JOB 3:13–3:17](https://ref.ly/Job3:13-Job3:17)

The destruction of rest
-----------------------

### By business

**[PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6)** *See also* [GEN 31:40](https://ref.ly/Gen31:40); [ECC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13), [ECC 2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:23), [ECC 8:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:16); [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34)

### By conflict

**[2CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:5)** *See also* [LAM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Lam5:5); [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5), [2CO 11:27](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:27)

### By grief

**[JER 45:3](https://ref.ly/Jer45:3)** *See also* [JOB 3:26](https://ref.ly/Job3:26); [PSA 77:4](https://ref.ly/Ps77:4); [DAN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Dan6:18)

### By unconfessed guilt

**[PSA 32:3–32:5](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:5)** *See also* [GEN 4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:12); [DEU 28:65–28:67](https://ref.ly/Deut28:65-Deut28:67); [ISA 48:22](https://ref.ly/Isa48:22), [ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21); [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:11)

### By sickness

**[JOB 30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:17)** *See also* [JOB 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/Job7:4-Job7:5)

* **Associated Passages:** John 4:6; Mark 4:38; Mark 6:31; Luke 9:58; Psalms 23:2-3; Proverbs 17:1; Ecclesiastes 4:6; Matthew 11:28-30; Psalms 4:8; Psalms 127:2; Ecclesiastes 5:12; Exodus 34:21; Mark 2:27; Exodus 20:8-11; Deuteronomy 5:12-15; Exodus 31:15; Exodus 35:2; Leviticus 23:3; Nehemiah 13:15; Isaiah 58:13-14; Lamentations 5:5; Isaiah 40:29-31; Matthew 6:25-34; Job 11:18-19; Psalms 3:5; Psalms 16:8; Proverbs 3:24; Philippians 4:6-7; Joshua 11:23; Joshua 14:15; 1 Kings 5:4; 1 Chronicles 22:9; Psalms 46:9-10; Proverbs 1:33; Isaiah 14:3; 2 Corinthians 13:11; Ecclesiastes 10:4; 1 Corinthians 1:10; 1 Thessalonians 4:11; Hebrews 12:14; James 3:17-18; 1 Peter 3:8; Mark 4:37-38; Matthew 8:24-25; Luke 8:23-24; Genesis 32:11; Micah 4:4; Matthew 6:31; Luke 12:29; 1 Peter 5:7; Matthew 6:25; Philippians 4:6; Exodus 33:14; Deuteronomy 33:27; Matthew 11:28; Deuteronomy 33:12; Proverbs 19:23; Revelation 14:13; Deuteronomy 31:16; Job 3:13-17; Psalms 39:6; Genesis 31:40; Ecclesiastes 1:13; Ecclesiastes 2:23; Ecclesiastes 8:16; Luke 21:34; 2 Corinthians 7:5; 2 Corinthians 6:5; 2 Corinthians 11:27; Jeremiah 45:3; Job 3:26; Psalms 77:4; Daniel 6:18; Psalms 32:3-5; Genesis 4:12; Deuteronomy 28:65-67; Isaiah 48:22; Isaiah 57:20-21; Revelation 14:11; Job 30:17; Job 7:4-5
