## 5058: rest, spiritual (id: 5058)

To be at rest spiritually is to find peace with God through Jesus Christ. This rest is promised to all who put their trust in God and obey him.

The human desire for spiritual rest
-----------------------------------

**[LAM 5:19–5:22](https://ref.ly/Lam5:19-Lam5:22)** *See also* [PSA 55:4–55:8](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4-Ps55:8); [LAM 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/Lam3:25-Lam3:26); [1TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:2)

Spiritual rest is to be found in God alone
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30)** *See also* [EXO 33:14](https://ref.ly/Exod33:14); [PSA 23:2](https://ref.ly/Ps23:2), [PSA 51:12](https://ref.ly/Ps51:12), [PSA 62:1–62:2](https://ref.ly/Ps62:1-Ps62:2), [PSA 62:5–62:8](https://ref.ly/Ps62:5-Ps62:8), [PSA 91:1–91:13](https://ref.ly/Ps91:1-Ps91:13); [ISA 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3-Isa26:4); [JHN 14:27](https://ref.ly/John14:27); [ROM 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1); [GAL 1:3](https://ref.ly/Gal1:3), [GAL 5:22](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22); [COL 1:20](https://ref.ly/Col1:20)

God promises his people spiritual rest
--------------------------------------

**[HEB 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb4:9-Heb4:11)** *See also* [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3), [PSA 37:7](https://ref.ly/Ps37:7), [PSA 116:7](https://ref.ly/Ps116:7), [PSA 119:165](https://ref.ly/Ps119:165), [PSA 125:1](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1), [PSA 131:1–131:3](https://ref.ly/Ps131:1-Ps131:3); [PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25); [ISA 30:15](https://ref.ly/Isa30:15), [ISA 32:17–32:18](https://ref.ly/Isa32:17-Isa32:18), [ISA 58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:11); [JER 6:16](https://ref.ly/Jer6:16), [JER 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7-Jer17:8); [JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1); [ROM 8:6](https://ref.ly/Rom8:6); [1PE 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:4)

Spiritual rest is not for the wicked
------------------------------------

**[PSA 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Ps1:4-Ps1:5)** *See also* [PSA 112:10](https://ref.ly/Ps112:10); [ECC 2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:23); [ISA 48:22](https://ref.ly/Isa48:22), [ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21)

God's offer of rest rejected by a faithless people
--------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 28:12](https://ref.ly/Isa28:12)**; **[JER 6:16](https://ref.ly/Jer6:16)**

* **Associated Passages:** Lamentations 5:19-22; Psalms 55:4-8; Lamentations 3:25-26; 1 Timothy 2:2; Matthew 11:28-30; Exodus 33:14; Psalms 23:2; Psalms 51:12; Psalms 62:1-2; Psalms 62:5-8; Psalms 91:1-13; Isaiah 26:3-4; John 14:27; Romans 5:1; Galatians 1:3; Galatians 5:22; Colossians 1:20; Hebrews 4:9-11; Psalms 1:3; Psalms 37:7; Psalms 116:7; Psalms 119:165; Psalms 125:1; Psalms 131:1-3; Proverbs 29:25; Isaiah 30:15; Isaiah 32:17-18; Isaiah 58:11; Jeremiah 6:16; Jeremiah 17:7-8; John 14:1; Romans 8:6; 1 Peter 3:4; Psalms 1:4-5; Psalms 112:10; Ecclesiastes 2:23; Isaiah 48:22; Isaiah 57:20-21; Isaiah 28:12
* **Associated Articles:** 3215: Holy Spirit, and peace (ID: `3215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5939: satisfaction (ID: `5939@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6652: forgiveness (ID: `6652@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6700: peace (ID: `6700@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7257: promised land, the (ID: `7257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8030: trust (ID: `8030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8328: quietness (ID: `8328@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5059: rest, eternal (id: 5059)

Eternal rest consists of peace with God, an absence of toil and tribulation and a final deliverance from pain, sorrow and death. It is promised as the inheritance of the people of God.

Eternal rest foreshadowed in the OT
-----------------------------------

### In God's rest after creation

**[HEB 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Heb4:3-Heb4:4)** *See also* [GEN 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Gen2:2-Gen2:3)

### In the rest promised to Joshua

**[HEB 4:8](https://ref.ly/Heb4:8)** *See also* [DEU 31:7](https://ref.ly/Deut31:7); [JOS 22:4](https://ref.ly/Josh22:4); [PSA 95:7–95:11](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:11)

The eternal state described as rest
-----------------------------------

**[HEB 4:9](https://ref.ly/Heb4:9)** *See also* [JOB 3:11–3:19](https://ref.ly/Job3:11-Job3:19); [REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13)

Aspects of eternal rest
-----------------------

### Being in the presence of God

**[LUK 23:43](https://ref.ly/Luke23:43)** *See also* [JHN 14:3](https://ref.ly/John14:3); [PHP 1:23](https://ref.ly/Phil1:23); [1TH 4:17–4:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:17-1Thess4:18); [REV 3:4](https://ref.ly/Rev3:4), [REV 7:15](https://ref.ly/Rev7:15)

### Happiness

**[2CO 5:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:8)** *See also* [MAT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:23); [LUK 13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:29)

### Refreshment and satisfaction

**[REV 7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:17)** *See also* [PSA 16:11](https://ref.ly/Ps16:11), [PSA 17:15](https://ref.ly/Ps17:15); [EZK 47:1–47:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:1-Ezek47:12); [REV 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:2)

### The absence of trouble

**[2TH 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:6-2Thess1:7)** *See also* [REV 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Rev6:10-Rev6:11), [REV 7:14](https://ref.ly/Rev7:14)

### The absence of labour

**[REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13)** *See also* [JOB 3:17](https://ref.ly/Job3:17); [ISA 57:2](https://ref.ly/Isa57:2), [ISA 65:22–65:23](https://ref.ly/Isa65:22-Isa65:23); [HEB 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb4:1-Heb4:11)

### The absence of pain, sorrow and death

**[REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)** *See also* [ISA 25:8](https://ref.ly/Isa25:8), [ISA 65:19](https://ref.ly/Isa65:19); [LUK 20:36](https://ref.ly/Luke20:36); [ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23); [1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24); [REV 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16-Rev7:17)

### The absence of conflict and harm

**[ISA 65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:25)** *See also* [ISA 11:6–11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:6-Isa11:9); [HOS 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hos2:18)

### The absence of the curse

**[REV 22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3)**

Eternal rest is conditional upon faithful obedience
---------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb4:11), [HEB 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:1-Heb4:2)**

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 4:3-4; Genesis 2:2-3; Hebrews 4:8; Deuteronomy 31:7; Joshua 22:4; Psalms 95:7-11; Hebrews 4:9; Job 3:11-19; Revelation 14:13; Luke 23:43; John 14:3; Philippians 1:23; 1 Thessalonians 4:17-18; Revelation 3:4; Revelation 7:15; 2 Corinthians 5:8; Matthew 25:23; Luke 13:29; Revelation 7:17; Psalms 16:11; Psalms 17:15; Ezekiel 47:1-12; Revelation 22:1-2; 2 Thessalonians 1:6-7; Revelation 6:10-11; Revelation 7:14; Job 3:17; Isaiah 57:2; Isaiah 65:22-23; Hebrews 4:1-11; Revelation 21:4; Isaiah 25:8; Isaiah 65:19; Luke 20:36; Romans 8:23; 1 Corinthians 15:24; Revelation 7:16-17; Isaiah 65:25; Isaiah 11:6-9; Hosea 2:18; Revelation 22:3; Hebrews 4:11; Hebrews 4:1-2
* **Associated Articles:** 5498: reward (ID: `5498@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5703: inheritance (ID: `5703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5874: happiness (ID: `5874@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5952: sorrow (ID: `5952@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6644: eternal life (ID: `6644@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8248: faithfulness (ID: `8248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9410: heaven (ID: `9410@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5061: sanctity of life (id: 5061)

Scripture treats human life as a divine gift and something for which humans are responsible. It is thus to be valued and respected. No human being has the right to take the life of another.

The sanctity of all human life
------------------------------

### It proceeds from God

**[GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7)**; **[ACT 17:25](https://ref.ly/Acts17:25)**

### It is precious to God

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Gen4:10-Gen4:11); [2SA 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:12-2Sam18:13); [MAT 6:25–6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:26)

### It is protected by God

**[DEU 22:8](https://ref.ly/Deut22:8)** *See also* [GEN 4:15](https://ref.ly/Gen4:15); [NUM 35:11–35:12](https://ref.ly/Num35:11-Num35:12); [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3)

### Death is by God's appointment

**[HEB 9:27](https://ref.ly/Heb9:27)** *See also* [JOB 14:5](https://ref.ly/Job14:5); [PSA 90:3](https://ref.ly/Ps90:3), [PSA 90:5](https://ref.ly/Ps90:5)

### God prohibits unlawful taking of life

**[EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13)** parallel [DEU 5:17](https://ref.ly/Deut5:17) *See also* [MAT 19:18](https://ref.ly/Matt19:18); [ROM 13:9](https://ref.ly/Rom13:9) *So precious is human life that some situations which required the death penalty in the OT are dealt with differently in the NT (e.g., [Jn 8:5,11](https://ref.ly/John8:5,John8:11)).*

### God punishes unlawful taking of life

**[GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6)** *See also* [EXO 21:14](https://ref.ly/Exod21:14); [2SA 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:9); [2KI 21:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:16)

The sanctity of children's lives
--------------------------------

### Children are planned by God

[GEN 18:10](https://ref.ly/Gen18:10); ; [JDG 13:3–13:4](https://ref.ly/Judg13:3-Judg13:4); ; [1KI 13:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:2); ; [2KI 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:16-2Kgs4:17); ; [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5); ; [LUK 1:13–1:17](https://ref.ly/Luke1:13-Luke1:17), [LUK 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:24-Luke1:25), [LUK 1:31–1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:31-Luke1:33), [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Luke1:35)

### Children are precious and to be treasured

**[PSA 127:3–127:4](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:4)** *See also* [GEN 33:5](https://ref.ly/Gen33:5), [GEN 48:9](https://ref.ly/Gen48:9); [PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9), [PSA 128:3](https://ref.ly/Ps128:3)

### Child sacrifice is opposed

[GEN 22:10–22:12](https://ref.ly/Gen22:10-Gen22:12); ; [LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21); ; [DEU 12:31](https://ref.ly/Deut12:31); ; [2KI 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:27), [2KI 16:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:3) parallel [2CH 28:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:3); ; [PSA 106:38](https://ref.ly/Ps106:38); ; [ISA 57:5](https://ref.ly/Isa57:5); ; [JER 7:30–7:31](https://ref.ly/Jer7:30-Jer7:31), [JER 19:5](https://ref.ly/Jer19:5); ; [EZK 16:20–16:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:20-Ezek16:21)

### Warnings against murder of children

[EXO 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Exod1:16-Exod1:17); ; [ACT 7:19](https://ref.ly/Acts7:19)

### Antenatal life is fully human

[JOB 31:15](https://ref.ly/Job31:15); ; [PSA 22:9](https://ref.ly/Ps22:9), [PSA 119:73](https://ref.ly/Ps119:73), [PSA 139:13–139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13-Ps139:16); ; [ISA 46:3–46:4](https://ref.ly/Isa46:3-Isa46:4), [ISA 49:1](https://ref.ly/Isa49:1), [ISA 49:5](https://ref.ly/Isa49:5); ; [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5); [LUK 1:41](https://ref.ly/Luke1:41), [LUK 1:44](https://ref.ly/Luke1:44)

### Injury to a pregnant woman is serious

[EXO 21:22–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22-Exod21:25); ; [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13)

The sanctity of old age
-----------------------

### The old are to be respected

**[PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)** *See also* [LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32); [JOB 32:6](https://ref.ly/Job32:6); [PSA 71:18](https://ref.ly/Ps71:18); [1TI 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1-1Tim5:2)

### The old are to be cherished

**[1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4)** *See also* [GEN 47:12–47:13](https://ref.ly/Gen47:12-Gen47:13); [1SA 22:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:3); [JHN 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/John19:26-John19:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:7; Acts 17:25; Genesis 1:27; Genesis 4:10-11; 2 Samuel 18:12-13; Matthew 6:25-26; Deuteronomy 22:8; Genesis 4:15; Numbers 35:11-12; Jeremiah 22:3; Hebrews 9:27; Job 14:5; Psalms 90:3; Psalms 90:5; Exodus 20:13; Deuteronomy 5:17; Matthew 19:18; Romans 13:9; John 8:5; John 8:11; Genesis 9:6; Exodus 21:14; 2 Samuel 12:9; 2 Kings 21:16; Genesis 18:10; Judges 13:3-4; 1 Kings 13:2; 2 Kings 4:16-17; Jeremiah 1:5; Luke 1:13-17; Luke 1:24-25; Luke 1:31-33; Luke 1:35; Psalms 127:3-4; Genesis 33:5; Genesis 48:9; Psalms 113:9; Psalms 128:3; Genesis 22:10-12; Leviticus 18:21; Deuteronomy 12:31; 2 Kings 3:27; 2 Kings 16:3; 2 Chronicles 28:3; Psalms 106:38; Isaiah 57:5; Jeremiah 7:30-31; Jeremiah 19:5; Ezekiel 16:20-21; Exodus 1:16-17; Acts 7:19; Job 31:15; Psalms 22:9; Psalms 119:73; Psalms 139:13-16; Isaiah 46:3-4; Isaiah 49:1; Isaiah 49:5; Luke 1:41; Luke 1:44; Exodus 21:22-25; Amos 1:13; Proverbs 23:22; Leviticus 19:32; Job 32:6; Psalms 71:18; 1 Timothy 5:1-2; 1 Timothy 5:4; Genesis 47:12-13; 1 Samuel 22:3; John 19:26-27

## 5062: spirit (id: 5062)

In Scripture the innermost aspect of human nature. The three\-fold division of spirit, soul and body is sometimes used to refer to this distinction between the physical and spiritual aspects of human existence.

* **Associated Articles:** 5063: spirit, nature of (ID: `5063@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5064: spirit, emotional aspects of (ID: `5064@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5065: spirit, fallen and redeemed (ID: `5065@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5063: spirit, nature of (id: 5063)

The life\-giving principle of human nature, to be distinguished from the purely physical aspects of life.

The human spirit as distinct from the body
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41)** parallel [MRK 14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:38)**[1TH 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:23)** *See also* [ROM 8:10](https://ref.ly/Rom8:10); [1CO 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:3-1Cor5:5), [1CO 7:34](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:34); [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1); [COL 2:5](https://ref.ly/Col2:5); [HEB 4:12](https://ref.ly/Heb4:12) *“spirit” and “soul” may be two names for the inner being penetrated by the word of God, or they may distinguish the inner person in relation to God (“spirit”) from their relation to the world (“soul”)*; [JAS 2:26](https://ref.ly/Jas2:26); [REV 18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:13)

The human spirit vitalises the body
-----------------------------------

**[JAS 2:26](https://ref.ly/Jas2:26)** *See also* [GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7); [JOB 34:14](https://ref.ly/Job34:14); [LUK 8:55](https://ref.ly/Luke8:55); [1CO 15:45](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:45); [REV 13:15](https://ref.ly/Rev13:15) *“breath” is the same word as “spirit” in Greek.*

The human spirit as the seat of thought and feeling
---------------------------------------------------

### The seat of perception

[EZK 13:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:3); ; [MRK 2:8](https://ref.ly/Mark2:8); ; [ROM 8:16](https://ref.ly/Rom8:16); ; [1CO 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:11)

### The seat of feeling

[2SA 13:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:39); ; [JOB 32:18](https://ref.ly/Job32:18); ; [ISA 54:6](https://ref.ly/Isa54:6), [ISA 57:15](https://ref.ly/Isa57:15), [ISA 66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2); ; [EZK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:14); ; [DAN 7:15](https://ref.ly/Dan7:15); ; [MAT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3); ; [LUK 1:47](https://ref.ly/Luke1:47); ; [JHN 11:33](https://ref.ly/John11:33), [JHN 13:21](https://ref.ly/John13:21)

### The seat of volition

[MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41) parallel [MRK 14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:38); ; [2CO 12:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:18); ; [PHP 1:27](https://ref.ly/Phil1:27)

The human spirit survives physical death
----------------------------------------

**[2CO 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:1-2Cor5:5)** *See also* [1SA 28:13–28:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:13-1Sam28:14); [MAT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28); [ACT 7:59](https://ref.ly/Acts7:59); [ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23); [HEB 12:23](https://ref.ly/Heb12:23); [1PE 3:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:19), [1PE 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:6)

Jesus Christ had a human spirit
-------------------------------

**[JHN 13:21](https://ref.ly/John13:21)** *See also* [MAT 27:50](https://ref.ly/Matt27:50) parallels [LUK 23:46](https://ref.ly/Luke23:46), [JHN 19:30](https://ref.ly/John19:30); [JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/John12:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 26:41; Mark 14:38; 1 Thessalonians 5:23; Romans 8:10; 1 Corinthians 5:3-5; 1 Corinthians 7:34; 2 Corinthians 7:1; Colossians 2:5; Hebrews 4:12; James 2:26; Revelation 18:13; Genesis 2:7; Job 34:14; Luke 8:55; 1 Corinthians 15:45; Revelation 13:15; Ezekiel 13:3; Mark 2:8; Romans 8:16; 1 Corinthians 2:11; 2 Samuel 13:39; Job 32:18; Isaiah 54:6; Isaiah 57:15; Isaiah 66:2; Ezekiel 3:14; Daniel 7:15; Matthew 5:3; Luke 1:47; John 11:33; John 13:21; 2 Corinthians 12:18; Philippians 1:27; 2 Corinthians 5:1-5; 1 Samuel 28:13-14; Matthew 10:28; Acts 7:59; Romans 8:23; Hebrews 12:23; 1 Peter 3:19; 1 Peter 4:6; Matthew 27:50; Luke 23:46; John 19:30; John 12:27

## 5064: spirit, emotional aspects of (id: 5064)

A human being is made up of a variety of emotions and attitudes. Sometimes one trait is or becomes so dominant as to make it characteristic of the entire personality.

Examples of a bad spirit
------------------------

### Pride

**[PRO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Prov16:18)**

### Jealousy

*See also* [NUM 5:14](https://ref.ly/Num5:14)

### Fear

[2KI 19:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:7) parallel [ISA 37:7](https://ref.ly/Isa37:7)

### Unfaithfulness

[HOS 4:12](https://ref.ly/Hos4:12), [HOS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Hos5:4)

### Despair

[ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3)

### Spiritual dullness

[ROM 11:8](https://ref.ly/Rom11:8); ; [DEU 29:4](https://ref.ly/Deut29:4); ; [ISA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10)

Examples of a good spirit
-------------------------

### Humility

**[PRO 29:23](https://ref.ly/Prov29:23)** *See also* [NUM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Num12:3); [ISA 57:15](https://ref.ly/Isa57:15)

### Submission

**[NUM 14:24](https://ref.ly/Num14:24)** *See also* [NUM 14:6–14:9](https://ref.ly/Num14:6-Num14:9)

### Wisdom

[DEU 34:9](https://ref.ly/Deut34:9); ; [1KI 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:11-1Kgs3:12); ; [JOB 32:8](https://ref.ly/Job32:8); ; [DAN 4:9](https://ref.ly/Dan4:9)

### Steadfastness

[PSA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Ps51:10), [PSA 51:12](https://ref.ly/Ps51:12)

### Strength

[LUK 1:17](https://ref.ly/Luke1:17); ; [MAL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Mal4:5)

### Zeal

[ROM 12:11](https://ref.ly/Rom12:11); ; [JHN 2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:17)

### Gentleness

[1CO 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:21); ; [1PE 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:4)

### Trust

[2CO 4:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:13)

God can effect a change of spirit
---------------------------------

**[EZK 36:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:26)** *See also* [EZK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:19), [EZK 18:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:31); [HAG 1:14](https://ref.ly/Hag1:14); [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10)

## 5065: spirit, fallen and redeemed (id: 5065)

Every aspect of the human personality, including the spirit, was affected by the fall. At regeneration the human personality is transformed by the Holy Spirit.

The human spirit is directly created by God
-------------------------------------------

**[NUM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Num16:22)** *See also* [ISA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Isa42:5); [ZEC 12:1](https://ref.ly/Zech12:1); [HEB 12:9](https://ref.ly/Heb12:9) *“human fathers” is literally “fathers of our flesh (bodies)”, i.e., there is an antithesis between those who are the fathers of people's bodies and God, who is the Father of their spirits. Thus bodies are derived from parents but souls from God.*

The human spirit was affected by the fall
-----------------------------------------

**[JER 17:9](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9)** *See also* [GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5); [PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6), [PSA 78:37](https://ref.ly/Ps78:37); [MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19) parallel [MRK 7:21](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21); [EZK 18:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:31)

The human spirit is renewed by the Holy Spirit at regeneration
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 36:26–36:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:26-Ezek36:27)** *See also* [PSA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Ps51:10); [JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33); [EZK 11:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:19); [JHN 3:6](https://ref.ly/John3:6); [ROM 8:10](https://ref.ly/Rom8:10); [GAL 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22-Gal5:23)

The regenerated human spirit beyond death
-----------------------------------------

### It is perfected in heaven

**[HEB 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/Heb12:22-Heb12:23)** *See also* [2CO 5:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:8)

### It is incomplete without a resurrection body

**[ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23)** *See also* [2CO 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:1-2Cor5:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 16:22; Isaiah 42:5; Zechariah 12:1; Hebrews 12:9; Jeremiah 17:9; Genesis 6:5; Psalms 64:6; Psalms 78:37; Matthew 15:19; Mark 7:21; Ezekiel 18:31; Ezekiel 36:26-27; Psalms 51:10; Jeremiah 31:33; Ezekiel 11:19; John 3:6; Romans 8:10; Galatians 5:22-23; Hebrews 12:22-23; 2 Corinthians 5:8; Romans 8:23; 2 Corinthians 5:1-4
* **Associated Articles:** 3233: Holy Spirit, and sanctification (ID: `3233@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4018: life, spiritual (ID: `4018@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4195: spirits (ID: `4195@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5135: blindness, spiritual (ID: `5135@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6139: deadness, spiritual (ID: `6139@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6154: fall, the (ID: `6154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6626: conversion (ID: `6626@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6644: eternal life (ID: `6644@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6728: regeneration (ID: `6728@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8320: perfection (ID: `8320@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9410: heaven (ID: `9410@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5067: suicide (id: 5067)

The deliberate taking of one's own life, a rare occurrence in Scripture.

Psychological causes of suicide
-------------------------------

### A sense of guilt leads to suicide

**[MAT 27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:5)** *See also* [ACT 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Acts1:18-Acts1:19)

### Defeat leads to suicide

**[1SA 31:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:4)** *See also* [1SA 31:5–31:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:5-1Sam31:6) parallel [1CH 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:4-1Chr10:5); [2SA 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:9), [2SA 17:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:23); [1KI 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:18)

### Defiance leads to suicide

[JDG 16:26–16:30](https://ref.ly/Judg16:26-Judg16:30); ; [2SA 17:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:23)

### Failure leads to attempted suicide

[ACT 16:27](https://ref.ly/Acts16:27)

Desire for death is more frequently expressed in the Bible than actual suicide
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The desire for death in a prophet's depression

**[1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4)** *See also* [JER 15:10](https://ref.ly/Jer15:10), [JER 20:14–20:18](https://ref.ly/Jer20:14-Jer20:18); [JON 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:1-Jonah4:3) *A desire for death does not lead to taking of one's own life, but to entrusting the place and time of one's death to the Lord.*

### The desire for death arising from grief and pain

**[JOB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Job10:1)** *See also* [JOB 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/Job3:1-Job3:4), [JOB 7:15–7:16](https://ref.ly/Job7:15-Job7:16)

### The desire for death because of judgment

**[REV 9:6](https://ref.ly/Rev9:6)** *See also* [HOS 10:8](https://ref.ly/Hos10:8); [REV 6:16](https://ref.ly/Rev6:16)

Life hated because of its apparent futility
-------------------------------------------

**[ECC 2:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17)** *See also* [PSA 90:8–90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:8-Ps90:10); [ECC 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1-Eccl4:3)

Suicide usurps God's sovereignty over life
------------------------------------------

### God is Creator, and thus has authority over his creation

**[JOB 10:12](https://ref.ly/Job10:12)** *See also* [GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7), [GEN 2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:17); [PSA 90:1–90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:1-Ps90:10), [PSA 139:13–139:15](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13-Ps139:15)

### God controls life and death

**[JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21)** *See also* [EXO 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Exod23:25-Exod23:26); [DEU 32:39](https://ref.ly/Deut32:39); [JOB 30:23](https://ref.ly/Job30:23); [ECC 8:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:8); [LUK 12:22–12:25](https://ref.ly/Luke12:22-Luke12:25); [HEB 9:27](https://ref.ly/Heb9:27)

Taking life, including one's own, is sin
----------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13)**

### God's command that believers love themselves is violated by suicide

*See also* [LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18); [MAT 22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:39); [EPH 5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:29)

### God's command that believers should love others is violated by suicide

[MAT 7:12](https://ref.ly/Matt7:12) *Relatives and friends are hurt when a loved one commits suicide.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 27:5; Acts 1:18-19; 1 Samuel 31:4; 1 Samuel 31:5-6; 1 Chronicles 10:4-5; 2 Samuel 1:9; 2 Samuel 17:23; 1 Kings 16:18; Judges 16:26-30; Acts 16:27; 1 Kings 19:3-4; Jeremiah 15:10; Jeremiah 20:14-18; Jonah 4:1-3; Job 10:1; Job 3:1-4; Job 7:15-16; Revelation 9:6; Hosea 10:8; Revelation 6:16; Ecclesiastes 2:17; Psalms 90:8-10; Ecclesiastes 4:1-3; Job 10:12; Genesis 2:7; Genesis 2:17; Psalms 90:1-10; Psalms 139:13-15; Job 1:21; Exodus 23:25-26; Deuteronomy 32:39; Job 30:23; Ecclesiastes 8:8; Luke 12:22-25; Hebrews 9:27; Exodus 20:13; Leviticus 19:18; Matthew 22:39; Ephesians 5:29; Matthew 7:12

## 5071: Aaron (id: 5071)

The elder brother of Moses and Miriam. He served as a spokesman for his brother in the confrontation with Pharoah, which eventually led to the exodus from Egypt. He was later consecrated as high priest.

* **Associated Articles:** 5072: Aaron, spokesman for Moses (ID: `5072@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5073: Aaron, as model priest (ID: `5073@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5072: Aaron, spokesman for Moses (id: 5072)

He was called by God to speak for his brother Moses in the confrontation with Pharaoh and to lead the Israelites out of Egypt. Negligence of the LORD's express command cost him the privilege of entering the promised land.

## 5073: Aaron, as model priest (id: 5073)

Aaron was made high priest and charged with the responsibility of making offerings for the sins of the people and ensuring that the LORD was worshipped in the proper manner. His priesthood was to pass to his descendants, thus ensuring its continuity.

Aaron was high priest
---------------------

### His call to the priesthood

**[NUM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Num3:10)** *See also* [EXO 28:1](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1); [1SA 12:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:6); [PSA 99:6](https://ref.ly/Ps99:6); [HEB 5:4](https://ref.ly/Heb5:4)

### His anointing, ordination and consecration

### His priestly garments

**[EXO 28:2](https://ref.ly/Exod28:2)** *See also* [EXO 28:4](https://ref.ly/Exod28:4), [EXO 28:36](https://ref.ly/Exod28:36), [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42); [LEV 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:7-Lev8:9)

### Skilled craftsmen were employed in the making of the priestly garments

[EXO 31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:6), [EXO 31:10](https://ref.ly/Exod31:10)

### Fellow Levites were to help Aaron in ministry

[NUM 18:2](https://ref.ly/Num18:2)

## 5075: Abraham (id: 5075)

The OT patriarch born in Ur and called by God to live in Canaan. God promised to make his descendants into a great nation, a promise fulfilled initially in Israel, and subsequently in the Christian church. His trust in God is seen as exemplary by NT writers.

* **Associated Articles:** 5076: Abraham, life of (ID: `5076@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5077: Abraham, character of (ID: `5077@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5078: Abraham, significance of (ID: `5078@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5076: Abraham, life of (id: 5076)

God called Abraham to live as a nomad in Canaan and promised the land to his descendants, naming him “father of many”, although he had no heir until his old age.

---------------------------------------------

### The line of descent from Shem to Abraham

[1CH 1:28–1:34](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:28-1Chr1:34)

------------------------------------

### His nomadic way of life in Canaan

**[HEB 11:9](https://ref.ly/Heb11:9)** *See also* [GEN 12:6–12:10](https://ref.ly/Gen12:6-Gen12:10), [GEN 13:1–13:4](https://ref.ly/Gen13:1-Gen13:4)

### God gives Abraham his new name

**[GEN 17:5](https://ref.ly/Gen17:5)** *The giving of a new name signifies a new relationship to God. “Abram” has the sense of “revered father”, whereas “Abraham” means “father of many people”.* *See also* [GEN 17:15](https://ref.ly/Gen17:15)

### Abraham buys a burial site for Sarah

**[GEN 23:3–23:4](https://ref.ly/Gen23:3-Gen23:4)** *This is his only acquisition of land in Canaan.* *See also* [GEN 23:17–23:20](https://ref.ly/Gen23:17-Gen23:20), [GEN 49:29–49:32](https://ref.ly/Gen49:29-Gen49:32)

Abraham's dealings with his nephew, Lot
---------------------------------------

[GEN 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/Gen12:4-Gen12:5) *Abraham is accompanied to Canaan by Lot*; ; [GEN 13:5–13:12](https://ref.ly/Gen13:5-Gen13:12) *Abraham and Lot separate*; ; [GEN 14:8–14:16](https://ref.ly/Gen14:8-Gen14:16) *Abraham rescues Lot from five local kings.*; [GEN 18:20–18:33](https://ref.ly/Gen18:20-Gen18:33), [GEN 19:29](https://ref.ly/Gen19:29)

Significant encounters in Abraham's life
----------------------------------------

With Melchizedek: [GEN 14:18–14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18-Gen14:20); ; [HEB 7:1–7:10](https://ref.ly/Heb7:1-Heb7:10); [GEN 18:1–18:22](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1-Gen18:22) *with three angels*

God's covenant with Abraham
---------------------------

### God makes a covenant with Abraham

**[GEN 15:1–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:1-Gen15:21)** *See also* [ACT 7:5–7:7](https://ref.ly/Acts7:5-Acts7:7)

### God gives Abram the name Abraham, as a sign of the covenant

[GEN 17:1–17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1-Gen17:8)

### God gives Abraham the gift of circumcision as a sign of the covenant

**[GEN 17:9–17:27](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:27)** *See also* [ACT 7:8](https://ref.ly/Acts7:8); [ROM 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rom4:11)

Abraham's family
----------------

### Abraham and Sarah were childless

**[GEN 11:30](https://ref.ly/Gen11:30)** *See also* [GEN 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Gen15:2-Gen15:3), [GEN 17:17](https://ref.ly/Gen17:17), [GEN 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Gen18:11-Gen18:13)

### Abraham's pretence that Sarah is his sister

[GEN 12:10–12:20](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10-Gen12:20), [GEN 20:1–20:18](https://ref.ly/Gen20:1-Gen20:18)

### The birth of Ishmael

[GEN 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:4), [GEN 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/Gen16:15-Gen16:16)

### The birth of Isaac

[GEN 21:1–21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:1-Gen21:7)

### Abraham dismisses Hagar and Ishmael

[GEN 21:8–21:14](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8-Gen21:14)

### God tests Abraham over Isaac

**[GEN 22:1–22:19](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:19)** *See also* [HEB 11:17–11:19](https://ref.ly/Heb11:17-Heb11:19); [JAS 2:21](https://ref.ly/Jas2:21)

### Abraham's wife Sarah dies

[GEN 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Gen23:1-Gen23:2)

### Abraham makes arrangements for a wife for Isaac

[GEN 24:1–24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1-Gen24:9)

### Abraham marries Keturah and has children by her

[GEN 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Gen25:1-Gen25:2) parallel [1CH 1:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:32)

### The death of Abraham

[GEN 25:7–25:11](https://ref.ly/Gen25:7-Gen25:11)

The time span of Abraham's life
-------------------------------

[GEN 12:4](https://ref.ly/Gen12:4) *Abraham leaves Haran at the age of 75\.*; [GEN 16:3](https://ref.ly/Gen16:3), [GEN 16:16](https://ref.ly/Gen16:16); [GEN 17:1](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1) *Abraham renamed and circumcised at the age of 99*; ; [GEN 21:5](https://ref.ly/Gen21:5) *Isaac born when Abraham is 100*; ; [GEN 23:1](https://ref.ly/Gen23:1) *Sarah dies when Abraham is 137\. [Ge 17:17](https://ref.ly/Gen17:17) indicates that Sarah was ten years younger than Abraham*; ; [GEN 25:7](https://ref.ly/Gen25:7) *Abraham dies at the age of 175\.*

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 1:24-27; Genesis 11:29-30; 1 Chronicles 1:28-34; Genesis 11:31-12:5; Joshua 24:2-3; Acts 7:2-4; Hebrews 11:8-9; Hebrews 11:9; Genesis 12:6-10; Genesis 13:1-4; Genesis 17:5; Genesis 17:15; Genesis 23:3-4; Genesis 23:17-20; Genesis 49:29-32; Genesis 12:4-5; Genesis 13:5-12; Genesis 14:8-16; Genesis 18:20-33; Genesis 19:29; Genesis 14:18-20; Hebrews 7:1-10; Genesis 18:1-22; Genesis 15:1-21; Acts 7:5-7; Genesis 17:1-8; Genesis 17:9-27; Acts 7:8; Romans 4:11; Genesis 11:30; Genesis 15:2-3; Genesis 17:17; Genesis 18:11-13; Genesis 12:10-20; Genesis 20:1-18; Genesis 16:1-4; Genesis 16:15-16; Genesis 21:1-7; Genesis 21:8-14; Genesis 22:1-19; Hebrews 11:17-19; James 2:21; Genesis 23:1-2; Genesis 24:1-9; Genesis 25:1-2; 1 Chronicles 1:32; Genesis 25:7-11; Genesis 12:4; Genesis 16:3; Genesis 16:16; Genesis 17:1; Genesis 21:5; Genesis 23:1; Genesis 25:7
* **Associated Articles:** 1335: blessing (ID: `1335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1348: covenant, God's with Abraham (ID: `1348@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4207: land, as a divine gift (ID: `4207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4275: Sodom and Gomorrah (ID: `4275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5094: Jacob (ID: `5094@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5100: Melchizedek (ID: `5100@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5225: barrenness (ID: `5225@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5467: promises, divine (ID: `5467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5590: travel (ID: `5590@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5672: concubines (ID: `5672@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6620: calling (ID: `6620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8832: testing (ID: `8832@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5077: Abraham, character of (id: 5077)

Despite some shortcomings as a husband and father, his obedience, faith, hospitality and effective intercession were exemplary. Such qualities led to his being called “friend of God”.

Abraham, the friend of God
--------------------------

### He was blessed by God

**[GEN 14:18–14:19](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18-Gen14:19)** *See also* [GEN 21:22](https://ref.ly/Gen21:22), [GEN 24:1](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1), [GEN 24:35](https://ref.ly/Gen24:35)

### He worshipped God

**[GEN 21:33](https://ref.ly/Gen21:33)** *See also* [GEN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:8), [GEN 13:4](https://ref.ly/Gen13:4), [GEN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Gen13:18)

### He had faith in God

**[GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6)** *See also* [ROM 4:18–4:22](https://ref.ly/Rom4:18-Rom4:22); [HEB 6:15](https://ref.ly/Heb6:15) *Abraham's faith is seen in the length of time (25 years) that he waited for the realisation of the promise*; [HEB 11:8–11:12](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:12), [HEB 11:17–11:19](https://ref.ly/Heb11:17-Heb11:19)

### He was obedient

**[GEN 22:1–22:19](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:19)** *See also* [GEN 12:4](https://ref.ly/Gen12:4), [GEN 26:5](https://ref.ly/Gen26:5); [JAS 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Jas2:20-Jas2:22)

### He was a prophet and intercessor

**[GEN 20:7](https://ref.ly/Gen20:7)** *See also* [GEN 18:16–18:33](https://ref.ly/Gen18:16-Gen18:33) *As a prophet, God reveals his will to Abraham; as an intercessor, Abraham brings the revelation back to God and pleads for him to change his mind.*

### He was called the friend of God

**[JAS 2:23](https://ref.ly/Jas2:23)** *See also* [GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6); [2CH 20:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:7); [ISA 41:8](https://ref.ly/Isa41:8)

Abraham's weaknesses as head of his household
---------------------------------------------

### He was held responsible by God for his family

**[GEN 18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:19)** *As the following references show, Abraham did not discharge this responsibility well.*

### He exploited his wife in a cowardly way

*See also* [GEN 12:10–12:20](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10-Gen12:20), [GEN 20:1–20:18](https://ref.ly/Gen20:1-Gen20:18)

### He fathered Ishmael as a result of wavering faith

[GEN 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:4)

### He allowed Sarah to mistreat Hagar

[GEN 16:4–16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4-Gen16:6), [GEN 21:9–21:14](https://ref.ly/Gen21:9-Gen21:14)

Abraham's strengths as a member of society
------------------------------------------

### He was well\-respected outside his household

[GEN 21:22](https://ref.ly/Gen21:22), [GEN 23:6](https://ref.ly/Gen23:6)

### He sought to resolve disputes amicably

[GEN 13:5–13:12](https://ref.ly/Gen13:5-Gen13:12), [GEN 21:25–21:31](https://ref.ly/Gen21:25-Gen21:31)

### His wealth was a source of prestige

**[GEN 24:35](https://ref.ly/Gen24:35)** *See also* [GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2), [GEN 24:1](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1)

### He was honoured for his military prowess

[GEN 14:13–14:21](https://ref.ly/Gen14:13-Gen14:21)

### He was concerned to maintain his independence from the Canaanites

**[GEN 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22-Gen14:24)** *See also* [GEN 23:7–23:16](https://ref.ly/Gen23:7-Gen23:16), [GEN 24:1–24:4](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1-Gen24:4)

### He showed hospitality to strangers

**[GEN 18:1–18:8](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1-Gen18:8)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 14:18-19; Genesis 21:22; Genesis 24:1; Genesis 24:35; Genesis 21:33; Genesis 12:8; Genesis 13:4; Genesis 13:18; Genesis 15:6; Romans 4:18-22; Hebrews 6:15; Hebrews 11:8-12; Hebrews 11:17-19; Genesis 22:1-19; Genesis 12:4; Genesis 26:5; James 2:20-22; Genesis 20:7; Genesis 18:16-33; James 2:23; 2 Chronicles 20:7; Isaiah 41:8; Genesis 18:19; Genesis 12:10-20; Genesis 20:1-18; Genesis 16:1-4; Genesis 16:4-6; Genesis 21:9-14; Genesis 23:6; Genesis 13:5-12; Genesis 21:25-31; Genesis 13:2; Genesis 14:13-21; Genesis 14:22-24; Genesis 23:7-16; Genesis 24:1-4; Genesis 18:1-8
* **Associated Articles:** 5714: men (ID: `5714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7772: prophets (ID: `7772@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8122: friendship, with God (ID: `8122@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8445: hospitality (ID: `8445@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8602: prayer (ID: `8602@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5078: Abraham, significance of (id: 5078)

Whereas Jews see Abraham as the father of their nation on account of God's covenant with him, Christians see Abraham as the father of all who believe in Jesus Christ.

The significance of Abraham for nations other than Israel
---------------------------------------------------------

### Abraham was the father of many nations

**[GEN 17:3–17:6](https://ref.ly/Gen17:3-Gen17:6)** *See also* [GEN 17:20](https://ref.ly/Gen17:20), [GEN 25:1–25:4](https://ref.ly/Gen25:1-Gen25:4), [GEN 25:12–25:18](https://ref.ly/Gen25:12-Gen25:18) *Muslim Arabs trace their ancestry through Ishmael to Abraham.*

### Abraham was to be a means of blessing to many nations

**[GEN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:3)** *See also* [GEN 18:18](https://ref.ly/Gen18:18), [GEN 22:17–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:17-Gen22:18); [ACT 3:25](https://ref.ly/Acts3:25)

The significance of Abraham for the people of Israel
----------------------------------------------------

### Israel traces its origins back to God's call of Abraham

**[GEN 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:2)** *See also* [JOS 24:2–24:3](https://ref.ly/Josh24:2-Josh24:3); [ACT 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Acts7:2-Acts7:3); [HEB 11:8–11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:10)

### God promises the land of Canaan to Abraham's descendants

**[NEH 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Neh9:7-Neh9:8)** *See also* [GEN 15:1–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:1-Gen15:21), [GEN 17:1–17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1-Gen17:8); [EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8); [DEU 9:5](https://ref.ly/Deut9:5); [ACT 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/Acts7:4-Acts7:5)

### God is subsequently known in Israel as the God of Abraham

**[1KI 18:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:36)** *See also* [GEN 24:26–24:27](https://ref.ly/Gen24:26-Gen24:27), [GEN 26:24](https://ref.ly/Gen26:24), [GEN 31:53](https://ref.ly/Gen31:53); [PSA 47:9](https://ref.ly/Ps47:9); [ACT 7:32](https://ref.ly/Acts7:32); [EXO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:6)

### Abraham is seen as the father of the Jewish nation

**[JHN 8:39](https://ref.ly/John8:39)** *See also* [PSA 105:5–105:6](https://ref.ly/Ps105:5-Ps105:6); [ISA 41:8](https://ref.ly/Isa41:8), [ISA 51:1–51:2](https://ref.ly/Isa51:1-Isa51:2); [LUK 1:54–1:55](https://ref.ly/Luke1:54-Luke1:55); [JHN 8:33](https://ref.ly/John8:33); [ACT 13:26](https://ref.ly/Acts13:26); [ROM 11:1](https://ref.ly/Rom11:1)

### God deals with Israel on the basis of his covenant with Abraham

**[1CH 16:14–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:14-1Chr16:18)** parallel [PSA 105:7–105:11](https://ref.ly/Ps105:7-Ps105:11) *See also* [EXO 2:23–2:25](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23-Exod2:25); [2KI 13:22–13:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:22-2Kgs13:23); [PSA 105:42–105:45](https://ref.ly/Ps105:42-Ps105:45); [LUK 1:68–1:75](https://ref.ly/Luke1:68-Luke1:75)

### Circumcision was the sign of participation in God's covenant with Abraham

**[GEN 17:9–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:14)** *See also* [LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3); [JHN 7:22](https://ref.ly/John7:22); [ACT 15:1](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1)

The significance of Abraham for Christians
------------------------------------------

### Abraham was an ancestor of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Matt1:1)** parallel [LUK 3:34](https://ref.ly/Luke3:34)

### Jesus Christ attacks Jewish confidence in mere physical descent from Abraham

**[MAT 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:9-Matt3:10)** parallel [LUK 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:8-Luke3:9) *See also* [JHN 8:33–8:41](https://ref.ly/John8:33-John8:41)

### Jesus Christ makes a point of identifying outcasts as children of Abraham

**[LUK 19:7–19:9](https://ref.ly/Luke19:7-Luke19:9)** *See also* [LUK 13:10–13:16](https://ref.ly/Luke13:10-Luke13:16)

### God's promise to Abraham is not inherited by those who obey the law

**[GAL 3:15–3:18](https://ref.ly/Gal3:15-Gal3:18)** *See also* [ROM 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Rom4:13-Rom4:16); [GAL 4:21–4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:21-Gal4:31)

### God's promise to Abraham is fulfilled in Jesus Christ, who is Abraham's seed

**[GAL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gal3:16)** *See also* [GEN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Gen12:7), [GEN 13:15](https://ref.ly/Gen13:15), [GEN 24:7](https://ref.ly/Gen24:7); [GAL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gal3:19), [GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:29)

### Abraham is the father of those who are justified by faith

**[GAL 3:6–3:9](https://ref.ly/Gal3:6-Gal3:9)** *See also* [GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6); [ROM 4:1–4:25](https://ref.ly/Rom4:1-Rom4:25)

### Abraham's obedience to God shows that faith must lead to good works

**[JAS 2:20–2:24](https://ref.ly/Jas2:20-Jas2:24)**

Fellowship with Abraham is a symbol of salvation
------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11-Matt8:12)** *See also* [LUK 13:28–13:29](https://ref.ly/Luke13:28-Luke13:29), [LUK 16:19–16:26](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19-Luke16:26)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 17:3-6; Genesis 17:20; Genesis 25:1-4; Genesis 25:12-18; Genesis 12:3; Genesis 18:18; Genesis 22:17-18; Acts 3:25; Genesis 12:1-2; Joshua 24:2-3; Acts 7:2-3; Hebrews 11:8-10; Nehemiah 9:7-8; Genesis 15:1-21; Genesis 17:1-8; Exodus 6:8; Deuteronomy 9:5; Acts 7:4-5; 1 Kings 18:36; Genesis 24:26-27; Genesis 26:24; Genesis 31:53; Psalms 47:9; Acts 7:32; Exodus 3:6; John 8:39; Psalms 105:5-6; Isaiah 41:8; Isaiah 51:1-2; Luke 1:54-55; John 8:33; Acts 13:26; Romans 11:1; 1 Chronicles 16:14-18; Psalms 105:7-11; Exodus 2:23-25; 2 Kings 13:22-23; Psalms 105:42-45; Luke 1:68-75; Genesis 17:9-14; Leviticus 12:3; John 7:22; Acts 15:1; Matthew 1:1; Luke 3:34; Matthew 3:9-10; Luke 3:8-9; John 8:33-41; Luke 19:7-9; Luke 13:10-16; Galatians 3:15-18; Romans 4:13-16; Galatians 4:21-31; Galatians 3:16; Genesis 12:7; Genesis 13:15; Genesis 24:7; Galatians 3:19; Galatians 3:29; Galatians 3:6-9; Genesis 15:6; Romans 4:1-25; James 2:20-24; Matthew 8:11-12; Luke 13:28-29; Luke 16:19-26

## 5080: Adam (id: 5080)

The Hebrew word “adam” is used both for “man” in general and also as the personal name of the first man that God created. It sounds like the word “adamah”, meaning “ground”.

* **Associated Articles:** 5081: Adam, creation and life of (ID: `5081@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5082: Adam, significance of (ID: `5082@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5083: Adam, and Jesus Christ (ID: `5083@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5081: Adam, creation and life of (id: 5081)

Adam is portrayed as the height of God's creation, made in the image and likeness of God, and entrusted with the stewardship of creation.

The creation of Adam
--------------------

### As a unique event

**[GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7)**

### In the image of God

**[GEN 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26-Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gen5:1); [1CO 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:7); [JAS 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9)

### His commission from God

**[GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28), [GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)** *See also* [GEN 1:26](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26); [PSA 8:4–8:8](https://ref.ly/Ps8:4-Ps8:8), [PSA 115:14–115:16](https://ref.ly/Ps115:14-Ps115:16)

### His incompleteness without Eve

**[GEN 2:18–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18-Gen2:24)** *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27), [GEN 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Gen5:1-Gen5:2); [MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9)

### His position and privileges

**[GEN 1:28–1:29](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28-Gen1:29), [GEN 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Gen2:19-Gen2:20)**

### The limitations of his freedom

**[GEN 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:16-Gen2:17)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:3)

The fall of Adam
----------------

### The nature of his sin

**[GEN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6)** *See also* [GEN 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:16-Gen2:17), [GEN 3:12](https://ref.ly/Gen3:12), [GEN 3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17); [HOS 6:7](https://ref.ly/Hos6:7)

### Immediate results of his sin

**[GEN 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/Gen3:7-Gen3:10)**

### God's curse upon Adam and the earth

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *See also* [GEN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:29); [JOB 7:1](https://ref.ly/Job7:1), [JOB 10:9](https://ref.ly/Job10:9); [PSA 90:3](https://ref.ly/Ps90:3); [ECC 2:17–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17-Eccl2:23), [ECC 3:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:20); [ROM 8:20](https://ref.ly/Rom8:20)

The life of Adam after the fall
-------------------------------

### His expulsion from the garden

[GEN 3:21–3:24](https://ref.ly/Gen3:21-Gen3:24)

### His children

**[GEN 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Gen4:1-Gen4:2)** *See also* [GEN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Gen4:25), [GEN 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Gen5:3-Gen5:4); [1CH 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:1)

### His lifespan and death

**[GEN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gen5:5)**

## 5082: Adam, significance of (id: 5082)

Throughout the NT, Adam is seen as a real historical figure, from whom all humanity is descended and by whose sin all humanity is affected.

Adam as the original member of the human race
---------------------------------------------

**[ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)**; **[1CO 15:45–15:49](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:45-1Cor15:49)** *See also* [GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7); [LUK 3:23–3:38](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23-Luke3:38) *The genealogy of Jesus Christ is traced back to Adam.*

Adam's relationship with Eve
----------------------------

### Adam as one flesh with Eve

**[MAT 19:3–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3-Matt19:6)** parallel [MRK 10:2–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:2-Mark10:9) *See also* [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24); [1CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16); [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

### Adam as the head of Eve

[1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3), [1CO 11:8–11:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:8-1Cor11:12); ; [1TI 2:12–2:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:12-1Tim2:15)

The reality and results of Adam's sin
-------------------------------------

### Sin spread to all the human race

**[ROM 5:19](https://ref.ly/Rom5:19)** *See also* [GEN 4:3–4:16](https://ref.ly/Gen4:3-Gen4:16); [PSA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Ps51:5); [PRO 20:9](https://ref.ly/Prov20:9); [ROM 3:23](https://ref.ly/Rom3:23)

### Death was introduced into the world

**[ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12)** *See also* [ROM 5:14–5:17](https://ref.ly/Rom5:14-Rom5:17), [ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23); [1CO 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:22)

### Condemnation for the human race resulted

**[ROM 5:16](https://ref.ly/Rom5:16), [ROM 5:18](https://ref.ly/Rom5:18)**

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:26; 1 Corinthians 15:45-49; Genesis 2:7; Luke 3:23-38; Matthew 19:3-6; Mark 10:2-9; Genesis 2:24; 1 Corinthians 6:16; Ephesians 5:31; 1 Corinthians 11:3; 1 Corinthians 11:8-12; 1 Timothy 2:12-15; Romans 5:19; Genesis 4:3-16; Psalms 51:5; Proverbs 20:9; Romans 3:23; Romans 5:12; Romans 5:14-17; Romans 6:23; 1 Corinthians 15:21-22; Romans 5:16; Romans 5:18

## 5083: Adam, and Jesus Christ (id: 5083)

Paul presents Jesus Christ as both the last Adam, succeeding where the first Adam failed, and the second man, standing at the head of a new humanity.

The contrast between the first and second Adam
----------------------------------------------

**[ROM 5:14](https://ref.ly/Rom5:14)** *By his sin Adam brought universal misery on humanity. In this he is the foreshadower and prototype of Jesus Christ, who through his righteous act brings the universal hope of redemption.*

The contrast in nature of the first Adam and the second Adam
------------------------------------------------------------

### Adam was a living being; Jesus Christ is a life\-giving being

**[1CO 15:45](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:45)**

### Adam was natural, Jesus Christ is spiritual

**[1CO 15:46](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:46)**

### Adam was earthly, Jesus Christ is heavenly

**[1CO 15:47](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:47)**

The contrast in the consequences of the actions of the first Adam and the second Adam
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Adam brought death, Jesus Christ brings life

**[ROM 5:15](https://ref.ly/Rom5:15), [ROM 5:17](https://ref.ly/Rom5:17)**; **[1CO 15:21–15:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:23)**

### Adam brought judgment, Jesus Christ brings justification

**[ROM 5:16](https://ref.ly/Rom5:16), [ROM 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Rom5:18-Rom5:19)**

### The assurance this doctrine brings

**[1CO 15:48–15:49](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:48-1Cor15:49)** *See also* [PHP 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:20-Phil3:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 5:14; 1 Corinthians 15:45; 1 Corinthians 15:46; 1 Corinthians 15:47; Romans 5:15; Romans 5:17; 1 Corinthians 15:21-23; Romans 5:16; Romans 5:18-19; 1 Corinthians 15:48-49; Philippians 3:20-21
* **Associated Articles:** 1680: types (ID: `1680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4015: life (ID: `4015@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5110: Paul, teaching of (ID: `5110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6124: condemnation (ID: `6124@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9310: resurrection (ID: `9310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9410: heaven (ID: `9410@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5085: David (id: 5085)

The youngest son of Jesse and great\-grandson of Ruth and Boaz, who became Israel's greatest king. Born in the town of Bethlehem, he probably lived c. 1040–970 B.C.

* **Associated Articles:** 5086: David, rise of (ID: `5086@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5087: David, reign of (ID: `5087@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5088: David, character of (ID: `5088@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5089: David, significance of (ID: `5089@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5086: David, rise of (id: 5086)

David rose from humble origins to become Israel's greatest king. His rise to power is seen by OT writers as evidence of God's guiding hand upon both David and Israel.

David was the great\-grandson of Ruth and Boaz
----------------------------------------------

**[RUT 4:13–4:22](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13-Ruth4:22)**

David was anointed king by Samuel
---------------------------------

**[1SA 16:1–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1-1Sam16:13)**

David was a member of Saul's court
----------------------------------

**[1SA 16:17–16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:17-1Sam16:23)** *See also* [1SA 18:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:2), [1SA 18:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:5)

David's defeat of Goliath
-------------------------

**[1SA 17:50–17:51](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:50-1Sam17:51)** *See also* [1SA 17:4–17:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:4-1Sam17:11), [1SA 17:17–17:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17-1Sam17:26), [1SA 17:32–17:54](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:32-1Sam17:54)

David's friendship with Jonathan
--------------------------------

**[1SA 18:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:1)** *See also* [1SA 19:1–19:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:1-1Sam19:7), [1SA 20:1–20:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:1-1Sam20:42), [1SA 23:16–23:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:16-1Sam23:18); [2SA 1:25–1:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:25-2Sam1:27)

**[1SA 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:9-1Sam18:12)** *See also* [1SA 18:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:15), [1SA 18:28–18:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:28-1Sam18:29), [1SA 19:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:1), [1SA 19:9–19:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:17); [PSA 59:1–59:4](https://ref.ly/Ps59:1-Ps59:4)

### His flight from Saul

### His reaction to Saul's death

## 5087: David, reign of (id: 5087)

David reigned over Judah for seven and a half years and over all Israel for 33 years. He extended the kingdom to the boundaries promised by God to Joshua.

David reigned over Judah in Hebron
----------------------------------

**[2SA 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:4)** *See also* [2SA 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:8-2Sam2:11), [2SA 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:1), [2SA 3:12–3:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:12-2Sam3:21)

## 5088: David, character of (id: 5088)

David, although not perfect, is portrayed as a man and a king after God's own heart.

David's appearance and attributes
---------------------------------

**[1SA 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:18)** *See also* [1SA 16:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:12)

David's characteristics
-----------------------

### His dependence upon God

**[1SA 13:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:14)** *See also* [1SA 23:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:2), [1SA 23:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:4), [1SA 23:9–23:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:9-1Sam23:13); [PSA 23:1–23:6](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1-Ps23:6), [PSA 34:1–34:7](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1-Ps34:7), [PSA 54:1–54:4](https://ref.ly/Ps54:1-Ps54:4), [PSA 62:1–62:2](https://ref.ly/Ps62:1-Ps62:2), [PSA 142:1–142:7](https://ref.ly/Ps142:1-Ps142:7)

### His trust in God

**[1SA 17:34–17:37](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:34-1Sam17:37)** *See also* [1SA 17:40–17:51](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:40-1Sam17:51)

### His loyalty to others

**[1SA 20:41–20:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41-1Sam20:42)** *See also* [2SA 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:1), [2SA 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:6-2Sam9:7)

### His respect for those in authority

**[1SA 24:1–24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1-1Sam24:7)** *See also* [1SA 26:1–26:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:1-1Sam26:25)

### His deep humanity is evident in his paternal grief

**[2SA 18:32–18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:32-2Sam18:33)** *See also* [2SA 13:38–13:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:38-2Sam13:39), [2SA 18:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:5), [2SA 19:1–19:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:1-2Sam19:7)

David's abilities
-----------------

### He was a strategist and courageous commander

**[2SA 8:3–8:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:3-2Sam8:6)** *See also* [1SA 17:34–17:47](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:34-1Sam17:47); [2SA 5:6–5:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:10), [2SA 5:17–5:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:17-2Sam5:25), [2SA 8:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:1), [2SA 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:13-2Sam8:14); [1SA 27:5–27:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:5-1Sam27:11)

**[2SA 23:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:1)** [1SA 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:18), [1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23), [1SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10), [1SA 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:10); [2SA 22:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:1) parallel PSA 18:0 *He sang*; ; [1CH 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:7); ; PSA 3:0, PSA 34:0, PSA 51:0, PSA 52:0, PSA 54:0, PSA 63:0

### He was a wise leader

**[2SA 8:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:15)** *See also* [2SA 7:18–7:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:18-2Sam7:29), [2SA 15:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:14), [2SA 15:25–15:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:25-2Sam15:29), [2SA 15:32–15:36](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:32-2Sam15:36), [2SA 16:5–16:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:5-2Sam16:12), [2SA 19:18–19:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:18-2Sam19:29), [2SA 21:1–21:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1-2Sam21:9)

## 5089: David, significance of (id: 5089)

David was instrumental in the establishment of Jerusalem as the centre of Israel's worship. Through David's personal rule, God exercised his own kingship over his people. David thus foreshadowed the Messiah, who would spring from his royal line.

The historical significance of David
------------------------------------

### The establishment of Jerusalem as the centre of worship

[2SA 6:1–6:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:1-2Sam6:11) parallel [1CH 13:1–13:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:1-1Chr13:14); ; [2SA 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:12-2Sam6:15) parallel [1CH 15:25–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:25-1Chr15:28)

### Preparations for building the temple

**[1CH 28:2–28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:2-1Chr28:9)** *See also* [2SA 7:1–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:1-2Sam7:7) parallel [1CH 17:1–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:1-1Chr17:6); [1KI 8:17–8:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:17-1Kgs8:19) parallel [2CH 6:7–6:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:7-2Chr6:9); [1CH 22:1–22:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:1-1Chr22:19), [1CH 23:4–23:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:4-1Chr23:5), [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1), [1CH 26:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:12), [1CH 29:1–29:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1-1Chr29:5)

-----------------------------------

### The son of God and chosen king

**[PSA 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Ps2:6-Ps2:7)**

*See also* [PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4)

## 5091: Deborah (id: 5091)

An outstanding leader in Israel when the nation faced a crisis of resurgent Canaanite power in the time of the judges. Her name means “bee”.

Deborah's calling
-----------------

### As a judge

[JDG 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4-Judg4:5), [JDG 5:7](https://ref.ly/Judg5:7) *She was probably called upon to settle intertribal disputes, “mother in Israel” meaning one who promotes the unity of God's family.*

### As a prophetess

[JDG 4:4](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4), [JDG 4:7](https://ref.ly/Judg4:7), [JDG 4:9](https://ref.ly/Judg4:9), [JDG 4:14](https://ref.ly/Judg4:14)

Deborah's response to the Canaanite threat
------------------------------------------

### She was petitioned by the people

[JDG 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Judg5:11-Judg5:12)

### She appointed Barak commander\-in\-chief

[JDG 4:6](https://ref.ly/Judg4:6)

### She accompanied the army into battle

[JDG 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Judg4:8-Judg4:10)

### She led the people in victory celebration

[JDG 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Judg5:1-Judg5:3), [JDG 5:28–5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:28-Judg5:31) *In this respect she is like Moses and Miriam ([Ex 15:1,20](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1,Exod15:20)).*

Deborah's character
-------------------

### She was wise, decisive, courageous, inspiring and bold

[JDG 4:5–4:9](https://ref.ly/Judg4:5-Judg4:9), [JDG 4:14](https://ref.ly/Judg4:14), [JDG 5:9](https://ref.ly/Judg5:9), [JDG 5:15–5:17](https://ref.ly/Judg5:15-Judg5:17) *Her confidence was in God's word and promise and she did not shrink from rebuking God's people.*

### She gave credit to others

[JDG 5:14–5:15](https://ref.ly/Judg5:14-Judg5:15), [JDG 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Judg5:18-Judg5:19), [JDG 5:24–5:27](https://ref.ly/Judg5:24-Judg5:27)

### She gave glory to God

[JDG 5:20–5:23](https://ref.ly/Judg5:20-Judg5:23) *These verses are a poetic acknowledgment that God has fought on Israel's behalf.*

She was God's person for the hour
---------------------------------

**[JDG 5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:31)** *See also* [EST 4:14](https://ref.ly/Esth4:14)

## 5092: Elijah (id: 5092)

Living in Israel, his ministry as a prophet was to oppose the worship of Baal in Israel, and the sin, injustices and idolatry of Ahab, Jezebel and Ahaziah. He came, probably, from Tishbe in Gilead, but his parentage is not certain. His name means “the LORD is God”.

Elijah's special standing as a prophet
--------------------------------------

### His prophecies and prayers were fulfilled and attested by miracles

Drought prophesied and experienced: [1KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:1), [1KI 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:7), [1KI 18:1–18:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:1-1Kgs18:6); ; [JAS 5:17](https://ref.ly/Jas5:17); [1KI 17:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:14) *provision of flour and oil prophesied*; [1KI 18:41](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:41), [1KI 18:44–18:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:44-1Kgs18:45); [1KI 17:17–17:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:17-1Kgs17:24) *The widow's son is restored to life.*; [1KI 18:36–18:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:36-1Kgs18:38); ; [2KI 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:10), [2KI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:12); [1KI 21:20–21:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:20-1Kgs21:24), [1KI 22:37–22:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:37-1Kgs22:38); ; [2KI 9:30–9:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:30-2Kgs9:37)

### The word of the LORD came to him

**[1KI 19:9–19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:9-1Kgs19:18)** *See also* [1KI 17:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:8), [1KI 18:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:1), [1KI 21:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:17), [1KI 21:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:23), [1KI 21:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:28), [1KI 22:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:38)

Elijah's ministry to Israel
---------------------------

### He confronts Ahab, king of Israel

**[1KI 18:16–18:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:16-1Kgs18:18)** *See also* [1KI 21:20–21:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:20-1Kgs21:22)

### He challenges the people of Israel

**[1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21)**

### He challenges Baal and his prophets

**[1KI 18:20–18:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:20-1Kgs18:29), [1KI 18:36–18:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:36-1Kgs18:38)**

### The prophets of Baal are sentenced to death and executed

*See also* [1KI 18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:40)

### Elijah calls down fire on Ahaziah's messengers

[2KI 1:1–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:1-2Kgs1:14)

### He predicts Ahaziah's death

**[2KI 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:16-2Kgs1:17)**

### He appoints successors to continue the fight against Baal worshippers

**[1KI 19:15–19:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:15-1Kgs19:17)** *See also* [1KI 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19-1Kgs19:21) *Elisha is called*; [2KI 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:13-2Kgs8:15), [2KI 9:1–9:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1-2Kgs9:13) *Jehu is anointed by one of Elisha's assistants.*

He does not die, but is taken up to heaven
------------------------------------------

**[2KI 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:11-2Kgs2:12)**

Elijah's lifestyle and character
--------------------------------

### It was one of simplicity

*See also* [1KI 17:2–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:2-1Kgs17:6), [1KI 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:4-1Kgs19:5); [2KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8)

### He was a man of action and physical endurance

[1KI 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:1), [1KI 17:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:3), [1KI 17:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:9)

Parallels between Elijah and Moses
----------------------------------

**[2KI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:8)** *See also* [EXO 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Exod14:21-Exod14:22); [JOS 3:14–3:17](https://ref.ly/Josh3:14-Josh3:17); [2KI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:14)

Elijah's return is awaited by Israel
------------------------------------

### Some Jews believed John the Baptist was Elijah returned

**[LUK 1:17](https://ref.ly/Luke1:17)** *See also* [MAL 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Mal4:5-Mal4:6)MAT [11:14](https://ref.ly/Mal11:14), [MAT 17:9–17:13](https://ref.ly/Matt17:9-Matt17:13) parallel [MRK 9:9–9:13](https://ref.ly/Mark9:9-Mark9:13)[JHN 1:21](https://ref.ly/John1:21) *John the Baptist denies being Elijah.*[2KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8); [MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) parallel [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6)

Elijah appears at the transfiguration of Jesus Christ

## 5093: Eve (id: 5093)

The wife of Adam and the first woman created. Her significance lies in being the first to be deceived and become disobedient to God. She is only given her name (which resembles the word for “living”) by Adam after the expulsion from Eden.

Eve was created in the image of God
-----------------------------------

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)**

Eve was a companion for Adam in Eden
------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)**

----------------

## 5094: Jacob (id: 5094)

The grandson of Abraham, and one of the two twin sons of Isaac. As the firstborn son of Isaac, Esau was entitled to inherit all of Isaac's property. However, he sold this to his brother Jacob to satisfy his immediate physical hunger. Through subsequent deception, Jacob was able to gain Isaac's blessing, and inherit all that was rightly Esau's. Despite such major faults, Scripture regards Jacob as further confirmation of God's promises to Abraham, and a model of faith and obedience.

* **Associated Articles:** 5095: Jacob, life and character of (ID: `5095@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5096: Jacob, the patriarch (ID: `5096@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5095: Jacob, life and character of (id: 5095)

The grandson of Abraham and son of Isaac, who was chosen by God, despite his personal faults and shortcomings, to be the recipient of the promises made to Abraham. His name means “he grasps the heel”, an allusion to the circumstances of his birth ([Ge 25:25–26](https://ref.ly/Gen25:25-Gen25:26)).

Jacob's birth
-------------

**[GEN 25:21–25:26](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21-Gen25:26)**

Jacob obtains the rights of the firstborn son
---------------------------------------------

**[GEN 27:36](https://ref.ly/Gen27:36)** *See also* [GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34), [GEN 27:1–27:29](https://ref.ly/Gen27:1-Gen27:29); [HEB 12:16–12:17](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16-Heb12:17)

Jacob inherits the promises made to Abraham
-------------------------------------------

**[GEN 28:10–28:15](https://ref.ly/Gen28:10-Gen28:15)** *See also* [GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3), [GEN 15:5](https://ref.ly/Gen15:5), [GEN 27:29](https://ref.ly/Gen27:29), [GEN 28:3–28:4](https://ref.ly/Gen28:3-Gen28:4), [GEN 35:11–35:13](https://ref.ly/Gen35:11-Gen35:13)

Jacob flees to Haran
--------------------

### As the result of Esau's enmity

**[GEN 27:41–27:45](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41-Gen27:45)** *See also* [GEN 28:5](https://ref.ly/Gen28:5)

### He encounters God at Bethel

[GEN 28:10–28:22](https://ref.ly/Gen28:10-Gen28:22) *The encounter with God at Bethel confirms that Jacob has received the promise made to Abraham, and that Jacob has accepted his role of bearer of that promise to future generations.*

### He marries Leah and Rachel

**[GEN 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Gen28:1-Gen28:2)** *See also* [GEN 29:16–29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:16-Gen29:30) *Jacob is deceived by Laban into marrying Leah before Rachel.*

### He accumulates wealth

**[GEN 30:29–30:43](https://ref.ly/Gen30:29-Gen30:43)** *See also* [GEN 31:4–31:9](https://ref.ly/Gen31:4-Gen31:9), [GEN 31:14–31:16](https://ref.ly/Gen31:14-Gen31:16), [GEN 31:36–31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:36-Gen31:42)

Jacob returns to Canaan
-----------------------

### His flight from Haran

**[GEN 31:1–31:3](https://ref.ly/Gen31:1-Gen31:3)** *See also* [GEN 31:10–31:13](https://ref.ly/Gen31:10-Gen31:13), [GEN 31:17–31:24](https://ref.ly/Gen31:17-Gen31:24), [GEN 31:44–31:55](https://ref.ly/Gen31:44-Gen31:55)

### His encounter with God

**[GEN 32:24–32:30](https://ref.ly/Gen32:24-Gen32:30)** *The change of his name from “Jacob” (meaning “he grasps the heel”, figuratively, “he deceives”) to “Israel” (meaning “he struggles with God”) confirms Jacob as the recipient of the Abrahamic blessings.* *See also* [GEN 35:1–35:5](https://ref.ly/Gen35:1-Gen35:5), [GEN 35:9–35:15](https://ref.ly/Gen35:9-Gen35:15)

### His reunion with Esau

**[GEN 33:1–33:17](https://ref.ly/Gen33:1-Gen33:17)**

### His twelve sons

**[GEN 35:22–35:26](https://ref.ly/Gen35:22-Gen35:26)** *See also* [GEN 29:32–29:35](https://ref.ly/Gen29:32-Gen29:35), [GEN 30:3–30:13](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3-Gen30:13), [GEN 35:16–35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:16-Gen35:18)

### Jacob blesses his descendants

**[HEB 11:21](https://ref.ly/Heb11:21)** *See also* [GEN 48:15–48:20](https://ref.ly/Gen48:15-Gen48:20), [GEN 49:1–49:28](https://ref.ly/Gen49:1-Gen49:28)

Jacob's death and burial
------------------------

**[GEN 47:28–47:30](https://ref.ly/Gen47:28-Gen47:30)** *See also* [GEN 49:29–49:33](https://ref.ly/Gen49:29-Gen49:33), [GEN 50:2–50:3](https://ref.ly/Gen50:2-Gen50:3), [GEN 50:12–50:13](https://ref.ly/Gen50:12-Gen50:13)

## 5096: Jacob, the patriarch (id: 5096)

God's covenant promises to Abraham were inherited by Jacob, through his father Isaac, and so it was through his line of descent that the promised Messiah was to come.

Jacob was one of the three patriarchs
-------------------------------------

**[EXO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:6)** *See also* [NUM 32:11](https://ref.ly/Num32:11); [DEU 1:8](https://ref.ly/Deut1:8); [MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11), [MAT 22:32](https://ref.ly/Matt22:32); [ACT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Acts3:13)

Jacob received the Abrahamic promises
-------------------------------------

### God's promise to Abraham

**[GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3)**

### Jacob chosen in preference to Esau

**[ROM 9:11–9:13](https://ref.ly/Rom9:11-Rom9:13)** *See also* [GEN 25:23](https://ref.ly/Gen25:23); [MAL 1:2](https://ref.ly/Mal1:2)

### The promises given to Jacob and his descendants

**[GEN 28:3–28:4](https://ref.ly/Gen28:3-Gen28:4)** *See also* [GEN 28:13–28:15](https://ref.ly/Gen28:13-Gen28:15), [GEN 35:11–35:12](https://ref.ly/Gen35:11-Gen35:12); [LEV 26:42](https://ref.ly/Lev26:42); [1CH 16:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:17) parallel [PSA 105:10](https://ref.ly/Ps105:10); [HEB 11:9](https://ref.ly/Heb11:9)

Jacob's descendants
-------------------

### He fathered the twelve tribes

**[EXO 1:1–1:5](https://ref.ly/Exod1:1-Exod1:5)** *See also* [1KI 18:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:31); [1CH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:1-1Chr2:2)

### He was the father of the nation

**[PSA 22:23](https://ref.ly/Ps22:23)** *See also* [PSA 77:15](https://ref.ly/Ps77:15); [ISA 45:19](https://ref.ly/Isa45:19)

Jacob as a symbol for the nation
--------------------------------

### Jacob and Israel were linked together as equivalent terms for the nation

**[PSA 14:7](https://ref.ly/Ps14:7)** *See also* [NUM 23:7](https://ref.ly/Num23:7), [NUM 24:5](https://ref.ly/Num24:5); [1CH 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:13); [PSA 78:5](https://ref.ly/Ps78:5), [PSA 114:1](https://ref.ly/Ps114:1), [PSA 135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:4); [ISA 9:8](https://ref.ly/Isa9:8), [ISA 43:28](https://ref.ly/Isa43:28)

### The nation is described as the house of Jacob

**[EXO 19:3](https://ref.ly/Exod19:3)** *See also* [ISA 2:6](https://ref.ly/Isa2:6), [ISA 8:17](https://ref.ly/Isa8:17); [JER 5:20](https://ref.ly/Jer5:20); [AMO 9:8](https://ref.ly/Amos9:8); [LUK 1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:33)

### Other descriptions of the nation

[DEU 33:4](https://ref.ly/Deut33:4); ; [PSA 87:2](https://ref.ly/Ps87:2); ; [ISA 27:9](https://ref.ly/Isa27:9), [ISA 59:20](https://ref.ly/Isa59:20), [ISA 41:21](https://ref.ly/Isa41:21); ; [JER 30:18](https://ref.ly/Jer30:18); ; [LAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lam2:2); ; [MAL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mal2:12); ; [ROM 11:26](https://ref.ly/Rom11:26)

### National and patriarchal references merged

**[PSA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Ps46:7)** *See also* [PSA 20:1](https://ref.ly/Ps20:1), [PSA 24:6](https://ref.ly/Ps24:6), [PSA 75:9](https://ref.ly/Ps75:9), [PSA 94:7](https://ref.ly/Ps94:7), [PSA 99:4](https://ref.ly/Ps99:4); [JER 10:16](https://ref.ly/Jer10:16)
