## 5098: John the Baptist (id: 5098)

The forerunner of Jesus Christ, born to elderly parents of the Aaronic line. His public ministry began in the Judean wilderness with a call to repentance and baptism. His ministry is seen as a fulfilment of OT prophecy, in preparation for the ministry of Jesus Christ. He was executed by Herod.

John's birth and early years
----------------------------

### His birth and naming

**[LUK 1:57–1:60](https://ref.ly/Luke1:57-Luke1:60)** *See also* [LUK 1:5–1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5-Luke1:13), [LUK 1:61–1:66](https://ref.ly/Luke1:61-Luke1:66)

### Prophetic words over his life

**[LUK 1:14–1:17](https://ref.ly/Luke1:14-Luke1:17)** *See also* [LUK 1:76–1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:76-Luke1:79)

### He was related to Jesus Christ

[LUK 1:36](https://ref.ly/Luke1:36)

### His early years

[LUK 1:80](https://ref.ly/Luke1:80)

John's ministry
---------------

### His simple lifestyle

[MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) parallel [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6); ; [MAT 11:18](https://ref.ly/Matt11:18) parallel [LUK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Luke7:33); ; [LUK 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Luke7:24-Luke7:25)

### His call for repentance

**[LUK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Luke3:3)** parallels [MAT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Matt3:1), [MRK 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4)

### He fulfilled Isaiah's prophecy

**[MAT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Matt3:3)** parallels [MRK 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Mark1:2-Mark1:3), [LUK 3:4–3:6](https://ref.ly/Luke3:4-Luke3:6) *Just as roads were improved in the ancient world in preparation for the visit of a king, so John calls for people to prepare spiritually and morally for the imminent coming of the Messiah.* *See also* [ISA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3); [JHN 1:23](https://ref.ly/John1:23)

### His practice of baptism

**[MRK 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4-Mark1:5)** parallel [MAT 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Matt3:5-Matt3:6) *See also* [JHN 3:23](https://ref.ly/John3:23), [JHN 10:40](https://ref.ly/John10:40)

### His challenge to established religion

**[MAT 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7-Matt3:10)** parallel [LUK 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:7-Luke3:9) *See also* [LUK 3:10–3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:10-Luke3:14)

### The contrast of his own baptism with that which the Messiah would bring

**[LUK 3:15–3:17](https://ref.ly/Luke3:15-Luke3:17)** parallels [MAT 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11-Matt3:12), [MRK 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Mark1:7-Mark1:8)

### His testimony to Jesus Christ

**[JHN 1:15](https://ref.ly/John1:15)** *See also* [JHN 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/John1:6-John1:8), [JHN 1:21–1:27](https://ref.ly/John1:21-John1:27), [JHN 10:41](https://ref.ly/John10:41)

### He recognises that his own ministry must decrease, while that of Jesus Christ will increase

**[JHN 3:27–3:30](https://ref.ly/John3:27-John3:30)**

John's baptising of Jesus Christ
--------------------------------

*See also* [MAT 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Matt3:13-Matt3:15) parallels [MRK 1:9](https://ref.ly/Mark1:9), [LUK 3:21](https://ref.ly/Luke3:21) *John's reluctance to baptise Jesus Christ lay in his recognition of who Jesus was and the apparent inappropriateness of such a baptism*; [JHN 1:29–1:34](https://ref.ly/John1:29-John1:34)

John and his disciples
----------------------

### John had disciples

[JHN 1:35–1:37](https://ref.ly/John1:35-John1:37), [JHN 3:25](https://ref.ly/John3:25), [JHN 4:1](https://ref.ly/John4:1)

### He taught them to pray

[LUK 11:1](https://ref.ly/Luke11:1)

### His disciples become envious of Jesus Christ

[JHN 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/John3:25-John3:26)

### He sends his disciples to question Jesus Christ

[MAT 11:2–11:6](https://ref.ly/Matt11:2-Matt11:6) parallel [LUK 7:18–7:23](https://ref.ly/Luke7:18-Luke7:23) *Locked up in prison, John needed reassurance that his expectations about Jesus Christ had been well\-founded.*

### John's disciples and the first Christians

[ACT 18:24–19:7](https://ref.ly/Acts18:24-Acts19:7)

John's imprisonment and death
-----------------------------

### His confrontation with Herod leads to his imprisonment

**[LUK 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Luke3:19-Luke3:20)** *See also* [MAT 14:3–14:5](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3-Matt14:5) parallel [MRK 6:17–6:20](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:20)

### His execution

[MAT 14:6–14:12](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6-Matt14:12) parallel [MRK 6:21–6:29](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21-Mark6:29)

Some thought Jesus Christ to be John risen from the dead
--------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 8:27–8:28](https://ref.ly/Mark8:27-Mark8:28)** parallel [MAT 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/Matt16:13-Matt16:14) *See also* [MAT 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Matt14:1-Matt14:2) parallels [MRK 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/Mark6:14-Mark6:16), [LUK 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/Luke9:7-Luke9:9)

Jesus Christ's estimation of John
---------------------------------

### Jesus Christ honours him

**[MAT 11:11](https://ref.ly/Matt11:11)** parallel [LUK 7:28](https://ref.ly/Luke7:28)

### Jesus Christ sees him as marking the end of the old covenant and the arrival of the kingdom

**[LUK 16:16](https://ref.ly/Luke16:16)** *See also* [MAT 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/Matt11:12-Matt11:13)

### Jesus Christ sees him as the promised Elijah

**[MAT 11:14](https://ref.ly/Matt11:14)** *On the basis of Malachi's prophecy ([Mal 4:5](https://ref.ly/Mal4:5)), it was widely accepted that Elijah must come before the Messiah could appear. This identification would have been reinforced by John dressing like Elijah (see [2Ki 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8)).* *See also* [MAT 11:10](https://ref.ly/Matt11:10); [MAL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Mal3:1); [MAT 17:11–17:13](https://ref.ly/Matt17:11-Matt17:13) parallel [MRK 9:11–9:13](https://ref.ly/Mark9:11-Mark9:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:57-60; Luke 1:5-13; Luke 1:61-66; Luke 1:14-17; Luke 1:76-79; Luke 1:36; Luke 1:80; Matthew 3:4; Mark 1:6; Matthew 11:18; Luke 7:33; Luke 7:24-25; Luke 3:3; Matthew 3:1; Mark 1:4; Matthew 3:3; Mark 1:2-3; Luke 3:4-6; Isaiah 40:3; John 1:23; Mark 1:4-5; Matthew 3:5-6; John 3:23; John 10:40; Matthew 3:7-10; Luke 3:7-9; Luke 3:10-14; Luke 3:15-17; Matthew 3:11-12; Mark 1:7-8; John 1:15; John 1:6-8; John 1:21-27; John 10:41; John 3:27-30; Matthew 3:13-15; Mark 1:9; Luke 3:21; John 1:29-34; John 1:35-37; John 3:25; John 4:1; Luke 11:1; John 3:25-26; Matthew 11:2-6; Luke 7:18-23; Acts 18:24-19:7; Luke 3:19-20; Matthew 14:3-5; Mark 6:17-20; Matthew 14:6-12; Mark 6:21-29; Mark 8:27-28; Matthew 16:13-14; Matthew 14:1-2; Mark 6:14-16; Luke 9:7-9; Matthew 11:11; Luke 7:28; Luke 16:16; Matthew 11:12-13; Matthew 11:14; Malachi 4:5; 2 Kings 1:8; Matthew 11:10; Malachi 3:1; Matthew 17:11-13; Mark 9:11-13
* **Associated Articles:** 5092: Elijah (ID: `5092@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1427: prophecy (ID: `1427@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2510: Jesus Christ, baptism of (ID: `2510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3242: Holy Spirit, baptism with (ID: `3242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4230: desert (ID: `4230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5225: barrenness (ID: `5225@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7772: prophets (ID: `7772@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7903: baptism (ID: `7903@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5099: Mary, mother of Jesus Christ (id: 5099)

Mary, the mother of Jesus Christ, provides Christians with an example of obedience to God and trust in Christ. She shared in the sufferings of her son, and took her place alongside the other disciples.

Mary's background
-----------------

### She was from Nazareth

[LUK 1:26](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26), [LUK 2:39](https://ref.ly/Luke2:39), [LUK 2:51](https://ref.ly/Luke2:51)

### She was related to Elizabeth, a descendant of Aaron

[LUK 1:5](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5), [LUK 1:36](https://ref.ly/Luke1:36)

### Possibly descended from David

[LUK 1:32](https://ref.ly/Luke1:32)

### She was betrothed to Joseph

[LUK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Luke1:27)

### She was a virgin

**[LUK 1:34](https://ref.ly/Luke1:34)** *The Greek word translated “virgin” means “young”, “unmarried” and implies virginity. Betrothal might take place as early as 12 years old and lasted usually for a year, being as binding as a marriage contract, though without sexual relations.* *See also* [ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14); [MAT 1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:23)

The events in Mary's life leading up to the birth of Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### The annunciation

**[LUK 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Luke1:30-Luke1:31)**

### Mary's response of submission and obedience

**[LUK 1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:38)**

### The Magnificat expresses Mary's wonder and joy

*See also* [LUK 1:46–1:55](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:55)

### Elizabeth's response to Mary

[LUK 1:42–1:45](https://ref.ly/Luke1:42-Luke1:45)

Mary, the mother
----------------

### The birth of Jesus Christ

[LUK 2:4–2:16](https://ref.ly/Luke2:4-Luke2:16)

### Her memories

[LUK 2:51](https://ref.ly/Luke2:51)

### Jesus Christ's circumcision and presentation

[LUK 2:21–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:21-Luke2:24) *Joseph and Mary were poor and they could not afford to offer a lamb (see [Lev 12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:8)).*

### The flight to Egypt

[MAT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Matt2:14)

### Her other children

[MAT 13:55–13:56](https://ref.ly/Matt13:55-Matt13:56) parallel [MRK 6:3](https://ref.ly/Mark6:3)

### Her anxieties

[LUK 2:48](https://ref.ly/Luke2:48)

### Her doubts about Jesus Christ

[MRK 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20-Mark3:21), [MRK 3:31](https://ref.ly/Mark3:31)

### She could not dominate Jesus Christ

[MRK 3:34–3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:34-Mark3:35) parallels [MAT 12:48–12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:48-Matt12:50), [LUK 8:19–8:21](https://ref.ly/Luke8:19-Luke8:21); ; [JHN 2:4](https://ref.ly/John2:4)

Mary, the disciple
------------------

### Mary acknowledges Jesus Christ's authority

**[JHN 2:5](https://ref.ly/John2:5)** *At the wedding at Cana.*

### Mary at the cross

**[JHN 19:25](https://ref.ly/John19:25)** *Many Christians see this as the poignant fulfilment of Simeon's prophecy in [Lk 2:35](https://ref.ly/Luke2:35).*

### Mary in Jesus Christ's family

**[JHN 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/John19:26-John19:27)**

### Mary praying with other disciples

**[ACT 1:14](https://ref.ly/Acts1:14)**

## 5100: Melchizedek (id: 5100)

“King of Righteousness”: king and priest of Salem, who blessed Abraham. David assumed Melchizedek's role as king and priest of Jersualem for himself and his descendants. Jesus Christ succeeded to this role and became a high priest in the order of Melchizedek.

Melchizedek was both king and priest
------------------------------------

### As a king, Melchizedek entertained Abraham

**[GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18)** *See also* [HEB 7:1](https://ref.ly/Heb7:1)

### As a priest, Melchizedek blessed Abraham

**[GEN 14:18–14:19](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18-Gen14:19)**

### Abraham acknowledged Melchizedek as a priest of the LORD

**[GEN 14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:20), [GEN 14:22](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22)** *Melchizedek here points ahead to Jesus Christ, who is also priest and king.*

The status of Melchizedek
-------------------------

### David appropriates Melchizedek's office and authority for himself and his descendants

**[PSA 110:1–110:2](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1-Ps110:2), [PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4)** *The word “order” here means “in succession to”, meaning that Christ assumes the status and function of Melchizedek.*

### The Davidic Messiah (Christ) inherits the office of Melchizedek

**[MAT 22:41–22:44](https://ref.ly/Matt22:41-Matt22:44)** parallels [MRK 12:35–12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:35-Mark12:37), [LUK 20:41–20:44](https://ref.ly/Luke20:41-Luke20:44) *Jesus Christ was at that time being acclaimed as the “Son of David” and by implication here claims to be the Messiah.* *See also* [MAT 20:30](https://ref.ly/Matt20:30) parallels [MRK 10:47](https://ref.ly/Mark10:47), [LUK 18:35–18:39](https://ref.ly/Luke18:35-Luke18:39); [MAT 21:9](https://ref.ly/Matt21:9) parallel [MRK 11:10](https://ref.ly/Mark11:10); [MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15); [ACT 2:34](https://ref.ly/Acts2:34)

Jesus Christ as high priest after the order of Melchizedek
----------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/Heb5:8-Heb5:10), [HEB 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19-Heb6:20)** *Only the high priest could sacrifice for the sins of the nation in the inner sanctuary behind the curtain.*

### The characteristics of the order of Melchizedek

**[HEB 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Heb7:2-Heb7:3)** *Melchizedek's order of priesthood was one of kingship, peace and righteousness; it did not depend on genealogical descent (unlike the Levitical priesthood); it is eternal, without known beginning or end.* *See also* [PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4); [HEB 5:6](https://ref.ly/Heb5:6), [HEB 6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:20), [HEB 7:21](https://ref.ly/Heb7:21)

### The uniqueness of Jesus Christ's high priesthood in the order of Melchizedek

**[HEB 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Heb7:6-Heb7:7)** *Melchizedek was superior to Abraham and therefore to the Levitical priesthood descended from Abraham*; **[PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4)** *The priesthood is secured by God's oath.* *See also* [HEB 6:17–6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:17-Heb6:20), [HEB 7:16](https://ref.ly/Heb7:16), [HEB 7:20–7:22](https://ref.ly/Heb7:20-Heb7:22), [HEB 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Heb7:26-Heb7:27), [HEB 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Heb8:1-Heb8:2)

### Jesus Christ's high priesthood makes the Levitical priesthood obsolete

**[HEB 8:13](https://ref.ly/Heb8:13)** *See also* [HEB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Heb7:11), [HEB 7:18–7:19](https://ref.ly/Heb7:18-Heb7:19), [HEB 8:7–8:13](https://ref.ly/Heb8:7-Heb8:13)

## 5101: Moses (id: 5101)

One of the most important figures in the OT. Moses was called by God to lead Israel out of captivity in Egypt through the Sinai wilderness to the brink of the promised land. He was the mediator of the old covenant, and in several major respects anticipates the person of Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 5102: Moses, life of (ID: `5102@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5103: Moses, significance of (ID: `5103@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5104: Moses, foreshadower of Jesus Christ (ID: `5104@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5102: Moses, life of (id: 5102)

The life of Moses sets the scene for the great events of the exodus from Egypt, and the showing forth of the power of the LORD, culminating in the giving of the covenant at Sinai and the preparations for entry into the promised land.

The meaning of the name Moses
-----------------------------

**[EXO 2:10](https://ref.ly/Exod2:10)**

Moses' birth and early life
---------------------------

*See also* [EXO 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Exod2:1-Exod2:3), [EXO 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Exod2:5-Exod2:6); [ACT 7:20–7:22](https://ref.ly/Acts7:20-Acts7:22)

Moses kills an Egyptian
-----------------------

**[EXO 3:14](https://ref.ly/Exod3:14)**

### Moses protests his inadequacy

## 5103: Moses, significance of (id: 5103)

Moses performed a number of major roles in the history of Israel. Many of these roles later found their perfection in Jesus Christ.

The roles of Moses
------------------

### As teacher

[EXO 18:20](https://ref.ly/Exod18:20); ; [MAT 23:10](https://ref.ly/Matt23:10); ; [JHN 1:38](https://ref.ly/John1:38)

### As intercessor

[EXO 17:11](https://ref.ly/Exod17:11), [EXO 32:11](https://ref.ly/Exod32:11); ; [NUM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Num12:13), [NUM 21:7](https://ref.ly/Num21:7); ; [PSA 106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:23); ; [JHN 17:9](https://ref.ly/John17:9); ; [HEB 7:25](https://ref.ly/Heb7:25)

### As servant of the LORD

[JOS 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Josh1:1-Josh1:2); ; [2CH 24:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:6); ; [ACT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Acts3:13); ; [HEB 3:5](https://ref.ly/Heb3:5); ; [REV 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3)

### As prophet

[NUM 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Num12:6-Num12:8); ; [DEU 18:15](https://ref.ly/Deut18:15), [DEU 34:10](https://ref.ly/Deut34:10); ; [ACT 3:22](https://ref.ly/Acts3:22)

### As deliverer

[EXO 3:10](https://ref.ly/Exod3:10); ; [ACT 7:35](https://ref.ly/Acts7:35); ; [GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1)

### As judge

[EXO 18:13](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13)

### As writer

[EXO 24:4](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4); ; [DEU 31:9](https://ref.ly/Deut31:9); ; [LUK 24:27](https://ref.ly/Luke24:27); ; [JHN 1:45](https://ref.ly/John1:45), [JHN 5:46](https://ref.ly/John5:46)

### As shepherd

[EXO 3:1](https://ref.ly/Exod3:1); ; [PSA 77:20](https://ref.ly/Ps77:20); ; [JHN 10:11](https://ref.ly/John10:11)

### As the friend of God

**[EXO 33:11](https://ref.ly/Exod33:11)** *See also* [EXO 33:17](https://ref.ly/Exod33:17); [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5); [JHN 5:20](https://ref.ly/John5:20)

### As the mediator of the law and the covenant

[EXO 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5-Exod19:6); ; [DEU 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Deut6:1-Deut6:3), [DEU 31:9](https://ref.ly/Deut31:9); ; [JOS 23:6](https://ref.ly/Josh23:6); ; [2KI 18:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:12); ; [GAL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gal3:19); ; [HEB 9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:15), [HEB 12:24](https://ref.ly/Heb12:24)

Characteristics of Moses
------------------------

### He was a man of faith

[EXO 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/Exod14:13-Exod14:14); ; [HEB 11:24–11:28](https://ref.ly/Heb11:24-Heb11:28)

### He was not eloquent

[EXO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10)

### He understood that the law meant life

**[DEU 32:46–32:47](https://ref.ly/Deut32:46-Deut32:47)** *See also* [MAT 5:17–5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17-Matt5:19), [MAT 19:17](https://ref.ly/Matt19:17); [ACT 7:38](https://ref.ly/Acts7:38)

### He was passionate in his defence of the Lord's holiness

[EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19); ; [NUM 16:30](https://ref.ly/Num16:30); ; [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46)

### He worked miracles that brought glory to God

[EXO 14:15–14:18](https://ref.ly/Exod14:15-Exod14:18); ; [JHN 2:11](https://ref.ly/John2:11)

Institutions established by Moses
---------------------------------

### He fathers, and is identified with, the Judaic legal system

[JOS 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:7-Josh1:8); ; [1KI 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:3); ; [EZR 3:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:2); ; [NEH 13:1](https://ref.ly/Neh13:1); ; [JHN 5:45](https://ref.ly/John5:45), [JHN 7:23](https://ref.ly/John7:23); ; [1CO 9:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:9)

### He inaugurates the ritual slaughter of the Passover lamb

**[EXO 12:21](https://ref.ly/Exod12:21)** *See also* [JHN 1:29](https://ref.ly/John1:29)

### He sprinkles the people with the blood of the covenant

### He orders the atonement sacrifice

[NUM 16:46](https://ref.ly/Num16:46); ; [ROM 3:25](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25)

Moses enters the glory of the LORD
----------------------------------

[EXO 24:17–24:18](https://ref.ly/Exod24:17-Exod24:18), [EXO 34:29](https://ref.ly/Exod34:29); ; [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2); ; [2CO 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:7); ; [REV 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3-Rev15:4)

## 5104: Moses, foreshadower of Jesus Christ (id: 5104)

Moses foreshadows Jesus Christ in his role as a law\-giver, as a mediator between God and his people and as a prophet who declared the will of God. In these matters, Jesus Christ brought to perfection the work begun through Moses.

Parallels between the infancies of Moses and Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------------------

### Their births

**[ACT 7:20](https://ref.ly/Acts7:20)** *See also* [LUK 2:11](https://ref.ly/Luke2:11)

### Moses' mother hid him to escape the slaughter decreed by Pharaoh

[EXO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:22), [EXO 2:2](https://ref.ly/Exod2:2)

### Herod's attempt to slaughter Jesus Christ

[MAT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Matt2:12), [MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16)

### Moses, the Israelite exile in Egypt and Midian

[EXO 2:11](https://ref.ly/Exod2:11), [EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15), [EXO 2:22](https://ref.ly/Exod2:22); ; [DEU 34:4](https://ref.ly/Deut34:4)

### Joseph takes Jesus Christ to Egypt for safety

[MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13), [MAT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:20) *Moses, like Jesus Christ, had no settled home in his own country.*

Supernatural signs confirmed the callings both of Moses and Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------------------------------

[EXO 4:5–4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:5-Exod4:9); ; [MAT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17)

Parallels between Moses and Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------

### As mediators of covenants

**[HEB 9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:15)** *See also* [EXO 19:3–19:9](https://ref.ly/Exod19:3-Exod19:9); [JHN 1:17](https://ref.ly/John1:17); [HEB 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/Heb8:6-Heb8:7)

### As those intimate with God

**[EXO 33:11](https://ref.ly/Exod33:11)**; **[JHN 5:20](https://ref.ly/John5:20)** *See also* [DEU 34:10](https://ref.ly/Deut34:10); [MAT 17:3–17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:3-Matt17:5); [JHN 14:9–14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:9-John14:11)

### As prophets

**[DEU 34:10](https://ref.ly/Deut34:10)**; **[ACT 3:22](https://ref.ly/Acts3:22)**; **[JHN 6:14](https://ref.ly/John6:14)** *See also* [DEU 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/Deut18:15-Deut18:18); [LUK 24:19](https://ref.ly/Luke24:19); [JHN 4:19](https://ref.ly/John4:19); [ACT 7:37](https://ref.ly/Acts7:37)

### As priests

**[HEB 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Heb9:11-Heb9:12)** *See also* [HEB 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Heb10:11-Heb10:12)

### As those who reveal the glory of God

### Moses as servant; Jesus Christ as Son

**[HEB 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Heb3:1-Heb3:6)**

## 5106: Noah (id: 5106)

A figure of major importance in the early chapters of Genesis, with whom God established a covenant, the sign of which was the rainbow. The life of Noah (whose name means “rest” or “comfort”) demonstrates God's ability to save the righteous and destroy the ungodly.

Noah's righteousness
--------------------

### Noah walked with God

**[GEN 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Gen6:8-Gen6:9)** *See also* [EZK 14:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:14), [EZK 14:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:20)

### Noah enjoyed God's protection

**[2PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:5)**

### Noah illustrates God's restoring grace after judgment

**[ISA 54:7–54:10](https://ref.ly/Isa54:7-Isa54:10)**

The history of Noah
-------------------

### Noah's family

*See also* [GEN 5:22–5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:22-Gen5:29); [1CH 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:1-1Chr1:3)

### Noah built the ark at God's command

**[HEB 11:7](https://ref.ly/Heb11:7)** *See also* [GEN 6:11–6:21](https://ref.ly/Gen6:11-Gen6:21)

### Noah had three sons

**[GEN 5:32](https://ref.ly/Gen5:32)** *See also* [GEN 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/Gen9:18-Gen9:19); [1CH 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:4)GEN [10:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:1), [GEN 10:32](https://ref.ly/Gen10:32)

### Noah saved his family and animals from the flood

**[GEN 7:7–7:9](https://ref.ly/Gen7:7-Gen7:9)** *See also* [GEN 8:18–8:19](https://ref.ly/Gen8:18-Gen8:19); [1PE 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:20)

### Noah worshipped the LORD

**[GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20)**

### God established a covenant with Noah, the sign of which is the rainbow

**[GEN 8:21–8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:21-Gen8:22)** *See also* [GEN 9:8–9:9](https://ref.ly/Gen9:8-Gen9:9), [GEN 9:12–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:12-Gen9:17)

### Noah lived to a great age

**[GEN 9:28–9:29](https://ref.ly/Gen9:28-Gen9:29)**

Noah's character
----------------

### Noah was obedient

**[GEN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Gen6:22)** *See also* [GEN 8:15–8:19](https://ref.ly/Gen8:15-Gen8:19)

### Noah was reverent

[GEN 8:20](https://ref.ly/Gen8:20)

### Noah was a man of the soil

**[GEN 9:20](https://ref.ly/Gen9:20)**

### Noah was subject to human frailty

**[GEN 9:21](https://ref.ly/Gen9:21)** *Nakedness was seen as shameful by OT writers.*

### Noah blessed and cursed his sons in a prophetic way

*See also* [GEN 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/Gen9:24-Gen9:27)

Jesus Christ was a direct descendant of Noah
--------------------------------------------

[LUK 3:23](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23), [LUK 3:36](https://ref.ly/Luke3:36)

## 5107: Paul (id: 5107)

The “apostle to the Gentiles”. Converted from Judaism by an encounter with the risen Christ, Paul planted and corresponded with churches through much of the Mediterranean. In his letters, Paul stresses the universality of the gospel, which knows no distinction between Jew and Gentile, and the effects of Christian faith upon life and conduct.

* **Associated Articles:** 5108: Paul, life of (ID: `5108@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5109: Paul, apostle to Gentiles (ID: `5109@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5110: Paul, teaching of (ID: `5110@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5108: Paul, life of (id: 5108)

Paul, previously known as Saul, had been a zealous persecutor of the church, but an encounter with the risen Christ (c. A.D. 35\) led to his becoming the apostle to the Gentiles, with a mission to proclaim the gospel throughout the Gentile world. He was probably martyred during the persecutions of Nero, possibly in A.D. 67\.

-----------------

### His Jewish heritage

**[PHP 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:5)**

### His citizenship

**[ACT 22:27–22:28](https://ref.ly/Acts22:27-Acts22:28)**

### His place of birth and education

**[ACT 22:3](https://ref.ly/Acts22:3)** *See also* [ACT 21:39](https://ref.ly/Acts21:39)

### His trade as a tentmaker

[ACT 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/Acts18:1-Acts18:3)

### His conversion on the road to Damascus and his apostolic calling

[ACT 9:1–9:19](https://ref.ly/Acts9:1-Acts9:19) parallels [ACT 22:3–22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:3-Acts22:16), [ACT 26:9–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:9-Acts26:18)

Paul's early Christian activity
-------------------------------

### His journey to Arabia

**[GAL 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Gal1:15-Gal1:17)** *Acts makes no mention of this trip, but it seems that Paul went to Arabia immediately after he met Ananias and then subsequently returned to preach in Damascus.*

### His return to Damascus

**[2CO 11:32–11:33](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:32-2Cor11:33)** *See also* [ACT 9:19–9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:19-Acts9:25)

### His visit to Jerusalem (c. A.D. 38\)

**[GAL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Gal1:18)** *See also* [ACT 9:26–9:28](https://ref.ly/Acts9:26-Acts9:28)

### His return to his home town of Tarsus

**[ACT 9:29–9:30](https://ref.ly/Acts9:29-Acts9:30)** *See also* [GAL 1:21](https://ref.ly/Gal1:21) *Tarsus was in Cilicia.*

### His stay in Antioch

**[ACT 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/Acts11:25-Acts11:26)**

Paul's first missionary journey and his partnership with Barnabas (c. A.D. 46–48\)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Paul and the Council of Jerusalem (c. A.D. 49\)
-----------------------------------------------

The similarities between Paul and Luke here suggests that both writers are referring to the same events in these passages. Some, however, prefer to equate the meeting recorded in [Gal 2:1–10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:1-Gal2:10) with the journey to Jerusalem mentioned in [Ac 11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:30): [ACT 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1-Acts15:6), [ACT 15:22–15:35](https://ref.ly/Acts15:22-Acts15:35); ; [GAL 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:1-Gal2:10)

-------------------------------------------------

### His disagreement with Barnabas and his new partnership with Silas

[ACT 15:36–15:41](https://ref.ly/Acts15:36-Acts15:41)

### In Asia Minor

### In Macedonia

[ACT 16:9–16:12](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9-Acts16:12), [ACT 17:1](https://ref.ly/Acts17:1), [ACT 17:10](https://ref.ly/Acts17:10)

### In Greece

[ACT 17:15](https://ref.ly/Acts17:15), [ACT 18:1](https://ref.ly/Acts18:1) *It was from Corinth that Paul wrote to the Thessalonians.*

### His return to Antioch

[ACT 18:18–18:22](https://ref.ly/Acts18:18-Acts18:22)

Paul's third missionary journey (c. A.D. 53–57\)
------------------------------------------------

### His general purpose in making his journey

**[ACT 18:23](https://ref.ly/Acts18:23)**

### At Ephesus

*See also* [ACT 19:1–19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:1-Acts19:12); [1CO 15:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:32), [1CO 16:8–16:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:8-1Cor16:9)

### His visit to Corinth

[2CO 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:15-2Cor1:16), [2CO 1:23–2:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:23-2Cor2:4), [2CO 13:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:2) *Most scholars believe that Paul wrote 1Corinthians from Ephesus, and that he also visited Corinth during this period. This visit, however, proved to be such a painful experience for him that he abandoned plans for a third visit, and instead wrote the Corinthians a “tearful” letter, calling them to repent of their sinful behaviour.*

### His journey to Greece

[ACT 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/Acts20:1-Acts20:6); ; [2CO 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:12-2Cor2:13) *Paul had hoped that Titus would bring him news from Corinth. They finally met in Macedonia, and Titus brought good news about the Corinthians' change of heart. Paul then wrote 2Corinthians before visiting Corinth for a third time.*

### His return to Jerusalem with the collection

[ACT 20:6](https://ref.ly/Acts20:6), [ACT 20:13–20:16](https://ref.ly/Acts20:13-Acts20:16), [ACT 21:1–21:8](https://ref.ly/Acts21:1-Acts21:8), [ACT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Acts21:15), [ACT 21:17–21:19](https://ref.ly/Acts21:17-Acts21:19); ; [ROM 15:25–15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:25-Rom15:26) *Paul wrote Romans at this point in his travels. There is a considerable degree of uncertainty as to precisely when Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, Colossians and Philemon were written.*

-----------------

## 5109: Paul, apostle to Gentiles (id: 5109)

Paul saw his encounter with the risen Christ as a call to apostleship, with a special mission to proclaim the gospel to the Gentile world. Paul's complete dedication to Jesus Christ made his own personal weaknesses a vehicle for the power of God.

Paul's calling as an apostle: origins and implications
------------------------------------------------------

### His calling began with his encounter with the risen Christ

**[GAL 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Gal1:15-Gal1:16)** *See also* [ACT 9:3–9:19](https://ref.ly/Acts9:3-Acts9:19) parallels [ACT 22:6–22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:6-Acts22:16), [ACT 26:12–26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:12-Acts26:18); [1CO 9:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:1)

### His calling involved his being sent to the unevangelised Gentile world

**[GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9)** *See also* [ACT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Acts22:21); [ROM 15:18–15:21](https://ref.ly/Rom15:18-Rom15:21); [2CO 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:13-2Cor10:16); [EPH 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7-Eph3:9)

### His calling put him under an obligation to preach the gospel

**[1CO 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:16)** *See also* [ROM 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:14-Rom1:15); [2CO 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:11-2Cor5:14)

Paul's commitment to his apostolic calling
------------------------------------------

### He supported himself in order to present the gospel free of charge

**[1TH 2:6–2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6-1Thess2:9)** *See also* [1CO 9:3–9:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:3-1Cor9:18); [2CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:13)

### He put the gospel before everything else

**[2CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:5)** *See also* [ROM 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/Rom9:1-Rom9:3); [1CO 9:19–9:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:19-1Cor9:23); [PHP 1:12–1:26](https://ref.ly/Phil1:12-Phil1:26)

### He boasted in his weaknesses

**[2CO 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7-2Cor12:10)** *See also* [2CO 4:7–4:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7-2Cor4:10), [2CO 6:3–6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:3-2Cor6:10), [2CO 11:21–11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:21-2Cor11:29)

### He experienced God's power at work through him

**[2CO 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:11-2Cor12:12)** *See also* [ACT 13:6–13:12](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6-Acts13:12), [ACT 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:11-Acts19:12); [ROM 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:18-Rom15:19); [1CO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4-1Cor2:5); [1TH 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:4-1Thess1:5)

### He had a confident attitude to death

**[PHP 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10-Phil3:11)** *See also* [2CO 5:6–5:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:6-2Cor5:10); [PHP 1:20–1:24](https://ref.ly/Phil1:20-Phil1:24); [2TI 4:6–4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:6-2Tim4:8)

Paul and the example of Jesus Christ
------------------------------------

### His appeal to Jesus Christ as an example to the churches

**[2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9)** *Paul here uses the example of Jesus Christ in order to encourage the Corinthians in their giving.* *See also* [ROM 15:1–15:3](https://ref.ly/Rom15:1-Rom15:3); [PHP 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:11)

### His own exemplary imitation of Jesus Christ

**[1CO 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:1)** *See also* [1CO 4:14–4:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:14-1Cor4:17); [PHP 3:12–3:17](https://ref.ly/Phil3:12-Phil3:17), [PHP 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Phil4:8-Phil4:9); [2TH 3:6–3:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:6-2Thess3:9)

### His sharing of Jesus Christ's sufferings

**[COL 1:24](https://ref.ly/Col1:24)** *Paul is not suggesting here that there is any deficiency in Jesus Christ's atoning sacrifice. Rather, there are some aspects of suffering which Christ could not experience because they arose only as a result of people preaching the message of the cross and suffering because of it.* *See also* [2CO 1:5–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:5-2Cor1:9), [2CO 4:7–4:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7-2Cor4:12); [GAL 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gal6:17)

Paul and the churches
---------------------

### His integrity in dealing with the churches

**[2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:12)** *See also* [ACT 20:17–20:21](https://ref.ly/Acts20:17-Acts20:21); [2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17), [2CO 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:1-2Cor4:2); [1TH 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:10-1Thess2:12)

### His love for the churches

**[PHP 1:8](https://ref.ly/Phil1:8)** *See also* [2CO 11:7–11:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:7-2Cor11:11), [2CO 12:14–12:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:14-2Cor12:15); [1TH 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:12)

### His prayers for the churches

**[1TH 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:2)** *See also* [ROM 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/Rom1:8-Rom1:10); [EPH 1:15–1:19](https://ref.ly/Eph1:15-Eph1:19); [2TH 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:11-2Thess1:12)

### His dependence on the prayers of the churches

**[1TH 5:25](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:25)** *See also* [ROM 15:30–15:32](https://ref.ly/Rom15:30-Rom15:32); [EPH 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Eph6:19-Eph6:20); [PHM 1:22](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:22)

Paul's imagery of apostleship
-----------------------------

### As Jesus Christ's ambassador

[2CO 5:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:20); ; [EPH 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Eph6:19-Eph6:20)

### As a builder

[ROM 15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:20); ; [1CO 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:10)

### As a farmer

[1CO 3:5–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:5-1Cor3:9)

### As a father

[1CO 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:15); ; [1TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:11-1Thess2:12)

### As a mother

[GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19); ; [1TH 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6-1Thess2:7)

### As God's captive

[1CO 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:9); ; [2CO 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14-2Cor2:16) *Paul portrays God as a victorious Roman general, with himself as a captive, bringing up the rear of the triumphal procession.*

### As Jesus Christ's slave

The word translated `servant'' in these verses commonly meant`slave'': [ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1); ; [2CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:5); ; [PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1); ; [TIT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Titus1:1)

Paul's successors were to safeguard the truth of the gospel after his death
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[2TI 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:1-2Tim2:2)** *See also* [2TI 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:13-2Tim1:14), [2TI 3:10–4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:10-2Tim4:8)

## 5110: Paul, teaching of (id: 5110)

Paul's understanding of the Christian life was dominated by the importance of Jesus Christ's death and resurrection, by means of which he saw God as having acted decisively to redeem the world. Paul emphasised that Jews and Gentiles alike are justified by faith, and share together in the glorious new era of the Holy Spirit, which is to be consummated at Christ's return.

The redeeming work of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------

### God's work of redemption in Jesus Christ's death and resurrection

**[ROM 3:25](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25)** *See also* [ROM 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3-Rom8:4); [2CO 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:18-2Cor5:19); [COL 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Col1:21-Col1:22)

### Jesus Christ as substitute, exchanges places with the sinner

**[2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21)** *See also* [GAL 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13-Gal3:14), [GAL 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:5)

### Jesus Christ reverses the effects of Adam's sin

**[1CO 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:22)** *See also* [ROM 5:12–5:19](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12-Rom5:19)

The death and resurrection of Jesus Christ mark the beginning of God's new order
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### New life comes through the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ

**[GAL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Gal2:20)** *See also* [2CO 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:16-2Cor5:17); [EPH 2:4–2:7](https://ref.ly/Eph2:4-Eph2:7); [COL 2:13](https://ref.ly/Col2:13)

### Jesus Christ's death and resurrection are the basis for a new moral life

**[ROM 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/Rom6:11-Rom6:13)** *See also* [ROM 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Rom6:1-Rom6:7); [2CO 5:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:15); [COL 2:20–3:11](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col3:11)

### Those who participate in God's new order are \`\`in Christ''

**[1CO 1:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:30)** *See also* [ROM 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Rom8:1-Rom8:2); [EPH 1:3–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:3-Eph1:14) *Paul extends the scope of the phrase “in Christ” to include believers, their activity, and the salvation they have received.*

Both Jews and Gentiles are justified by faith
---------------------------------------------

### Paul stresses that salvation is by grace through faith alone

**[EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9)** *See also* [ROM 3:21–3:26](https://ref.ly/Rom3:21-Rom3:26), [ROM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Rom4:16), [ROM 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1-Rom5:2)

### Jewish works of the law have no role in salvation

**[GAL 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15-Gal2:16)** *See also* [ROM 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Rom4:13-Rom4:15), [ROM 9:30–10:4](https://ref.ly/Rom9:30-Rom10:4); [GAL 3:6–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:6-Gal3:14); [PHP 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:8-Phil3:9)

### Both Jews and Gentiles are united in the one body of Christ

**[1CO 12:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:13)** *See also* [GAL 3:26–3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26-Gal3:28); [EPH 2:14–2:18](https://ref.ly/Eph2:14-Eph2:18), [EPH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:6)

The Holy Spirit
---------------

### His indwelling is an integral part of Christian experience

**[ROM 8:9](https://ref.ly/Rom8:9)** *See also* [ACT 19:1–19:7](https://ref.ly/Acts19:1-Acts19:7); [GAL 3:2–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gal3:2-Gal3:5) *Paul's questions suggest that he identifies receiving the Spirit with becoming a Christian.*

### He equips the church with gifts

**[1CO 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:1-1Cor12:11)** *See also* [1CO 14:1–14:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1-1Cor14:33)

### He enables believers to relate to God as Father

**[GAL 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:6-Gal4:7)** *See also* [ROM 8:14–8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:14-Rom8:17); [EPH 2:18](https://ref.ly/Eph2:18)

### He guides believers into a right understanding of the law

**[ROM 7:6](https://ref.ly/Rom7:6)** *See also* [ROM 2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:29), [ROM 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3-Rom8:4); [2CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:3), [2CO 3:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:6)

### He overcomes believers' sinful human nature

**[ROM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Rom8:13)** *See also* [ROM 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5-Rom8:8); [GAL 5:16–5:25](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16-Gal5:25), [GAL 6:8](https://ref.ly/Gal6:8)

### He is the guarantee of eternal life

**[ROM 8:11](https://ref.ly/Rom8:11)** *See also* [ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23); [2CO 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:4-2Cor5:5); [EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14)

The return of Jesus Christ
--------------------------

### Paul lived in the hope of the forthcoming personal return of the risen Christ

**[PHP 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:20-Phil3:21)** *See also* [1CO 15:22–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:22-1Cor15:28); [1TH 4:14–4:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:14-1Thess4:17); [2TH 1:6–1:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:6-2Thess1:10)

### Believers are to be raised with Christ upon his return

**[1TH 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:14)** *See also* [1CO 15:12–15:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:12-1Cor15:23)

### The whole created order will be renewed at the return of Jesus Christ

**[ROM 8:19–8:21](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19-Rom8:21)** *See also* [EPH 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9-Eph1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 3:25; Romans 8:3-4; 2 Corinthians 5:18-19; Colossians 1:21-22; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Galatians 3:13-14; Galatians 4:4-5; 1 Corinthians 15:21-22; Romans 5:12-19; Galatians 2:20; 2 Corinthians 5:16-17; Ephesians 2:4-7; Colossians 2:13; Romans 6:11-13; Romans 6:1-7; 2 Corinthians 5:15; Colossians 2:20-3:11; 1 Corinthians 1:30; Romans 8:1-2; Ephesians 1:3-14; Ephesians 2:8-9; Romans 3:21-26; Romans 4:16; Romans 5:1-2; Galatians 2:15-16; Romans 4:13-15; Romans 9:30-10:4; Galatians 3:6-14; Philippians 3:8-9; 1 Corinthians 12:13; Galatians 3:26-28; Ephesians 2:14-18; Ephesians 3:6; Romans 8:9; Acts 19:1-7; Galatians 3:2-5; 1 Corinthians 12:1-11; 1 Corinthians 14:1-33; Galatians 4:6-7; Romans 8:14-17; Ephesians 2:18; Romans 7:6; Romans 2:29; 2 Corinthians 3:3; 2 Corinthians 3:6; Romans 8:13; Romans 8:5-8; Galatians 5:16-25; Galatians 6:8; Romans 8:11; Romans 8:23; 2 Corinthians 5:4-5; Ephesians 1:13-14; Philippians 3:20-21; 1 Corinthians 15:22-28; 1 Thessalonians 4:14-17; 2 Thessalonians 1:6-10; 1 Thessalonians 4:14; 1 Corinthians 15:12-23; Romans 8:19-21; Ephesians 1:9-10
* **Associated Articles:** 2321: Jesus Christ, as redeemer (ID: `2321@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2410: cross, the (ID: `2410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2565: Jesus Christ, second coming of (ID: `2565@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3233: Holy Spirit, and sanctification (ID: `3233@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church (ID: `3275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3278: Holy Spirit, indwelling of (ID: `3278@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6666: grace (ID: `6666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7110: body of Christ (ID: `7110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5112: Peter (id: 5112)

The foremost of the disciples of Jesus Christ during his lifetime and of the apostles after the resurrection.

* **Associated Articles:** 5113: Peter, the disciple (ID: `5113@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5114: Peter, the apostle (ID: `5114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5115: Peter, preacher and teacher (ID: `5115@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5113: Peter, the disciple (id: 5113)

The son of Jonas or John, he was called Simon until Jesus Christ gave him the name Peter meaning “rock”.

Peter's background and family
-----------------------------

### He lived in Capernaum

[MAT 8:14](https://ref.ly/Matt8:14) parallels [MRK 1:29](https://ref.ly/Mark1:29), [LUK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Luke4:38); ; [JHN 1:44](https://ref.ly/John1:44) *He originally came from Bethsaida.*

### He was a fisherman by trade

[LUK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10)

Peter as Jesus Christ's disciple
--------------------------------

### He was called by Jesus Christ

[MAT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18-Matt4:20) parallel [MRK 1:16–1:20](https://ref.ly/Mark1:16-Mark1:20); ; [LUK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10); ; [JHN 1:40–1:42](https://ref.ly/John1:40-John1:42)

### He witnessed Jesus Christ perform many miracles

[MAT 8:14–8:15](https://ref.ly/Matt8:14-Matt8:15) parallels [MRK 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Mark1:30-Mark1:31), [LUK 4:38–4:39](https://ref.ly/Luke4:38-Luke4:39); ; [MAT 17:24–17:27](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:27); ; [LUK 5:4–5:8](https://ref.ly/Luke5:4-Luke5:8); ; [JHN 21:2–21:6](https://ref.ly/John21:2-John21:6)

### He was chosen to be with Jesus Christ on certain occasions

[MAT 17:1–17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:1-Matt17:2) parallels [MRK 9:2](https://ref.ly/Mark9:2), [LUK 9:28–9:29](https://ref.ly/Luke9:28-Luke9:29) *at the transfiguration of Jesus Christ*; ; [MAT 26:36–26:37](https://ref.ly/Matt26:36-Matt26:37) parallel [MRK 14:32–14:33](https://ref.ly/Mark14:32-Mark14:33) *in the Garden of Gethsemane*; ; [MRK 5:37](https://ref.ly/Mark5:37) parallel [LUK 8:51](https://ref.ly/Luke8:51)

### He sometimes acted as spokesman for the other disciples

He asked Jesus Christ to teach them: [MAT 15:15](https://ref.ly/Matt15:15), [MAT 18:21](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21); [MAT 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/Matt16:15-Matt16:16) parallels [MRK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Mark8:29), [LUK 9:20](https://ref.ly/Luke9:20); ; [JHN 6:69](https://ref.ly/John6:69); [MAT 17:4](https://ref.ly/Matt17:4) parallels [MRK 9:5](https://ref.ly/Mark9:5), [LUK 9:33](https://ref.ly/Luke9:33) *He asked Jesus Christ to prolong the transfiguration experience*; ; [MAT 19:27](https://ref.ly/Matt19:27) parallels [MRK 10:28](https://ref.ly/Mark10:28), [LUK 18:28](https://ref.ly/Luke18:28); ; [MRK 1:36–1:37](https://ref.ly/Mark1:36-Mark1:37); ; [LUK 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Luke5:4-Luke5:5); ; [JHN 6:68](https://ref.ly/John6:68)

### His impetuous nature interacted with his devotion to Jesus Christ

[MAT 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Matt14:28-Matt14:29) *He wanted to walk to Jesus Christ on the water*; ; [MAT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Matt16:22) parallel [MRK 8:32](https://ref.ly/Mark8:32) *He tried to prevent Jesus Christ from going to his death*; ; [MAT 26:35](https://ref.ly/Matt26:35) parallels [MRK 14:31](https://ref.ly/Mark14:31), [LUK 22:33](https://ref.ly/Luke22:33), [JHN 13:37](https://ref.ly/John13:37) *He vowed that he was willing to die for Jesus Christ*; ; [JHN 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/John13:6-John13:8), [JHN 18:10](https://ref.ly/John18:10)

### He was the subject of both special commendation and rebuke

[MAT 16:17–16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:17-Matt16:19) *He was commended for his confession of faith in Jesus Christ*; ; [MAT 16:23](https://ref.ly/Matt16:23) parallel [MRK 8:33](https://ref.ly/Mark8:33) *He was rebuked for his opposition to Jesus Christ's prediction of his suffering*; ; [MAT 26:34](https://ref.ly/Matt26:34) parallels [MRK 14:30](https://ref.ly/Mark14:30), [LUK 22:34](https://ref.ly/Luke22:34), [JHN 13:38](https://ref.ly/John13:38) *He was warned that he would disown Jesus Christ*; ; [MAT 26:40–26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40-Matt26:41) parallel [MRK 14:37–14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:37-Mark14:38) *He was rebuked for falling asleep in the Garden of Gethsemane*; ; [JHN 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/John18:10-John18:11), [JHN 21:20–21:22](https://ref.ly/John21:20-John21:22)

Peter's denial of Jesus Christ and his reinstatement
----------------------------------------------------

### He denied that he knew Jesus Christ

[MAT 26:69–26:74](https://ref.ly/Matt26:69-Matt26:74) parallels [MRK 14:66–14:71](https://ref.ly/Mark14:66-Mark14:71), [LUK 22:54–22:60](https://ref.ly/Luke22:54-Luke22:60), [JHN 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/John18:15-John18:18), [JHN 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:25-John18:27)

### He repented of his denial

[MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75) parallels [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:72), [LUK 22:61–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:61-Luke22:62)

### The risen Christ appeared to him on his own

[LUK 24:33–24:34](https://ref.ly/Luke24:33-Luke24:34); ; [1CO 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:4-1Cor15:5)

### He was reinstated by Jesus Christ in front of other disciples

[JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 8:14; Mark 1:29; Luke 4:38; John 1:44; Luke 5:10; Matthew 4:18-20; Mark 1:16-20; John 1:40-42; Matthew 8:14-15; Mark 1:30-31; Luke 4:38-39; Matthew 17:24-27; Luke 5:4-8; John 21:2-6; Matthew 17:1-2; Mark 9:2; Luke 9:28-29; Matthew 26:36-37; Mark 14:32-33; Mark 5:37; Luke 8:51; Matthew 15:15; Matthew 18:21; Matthew 16:15-16; Mark 8:29; Luke 9:20; John 6:69; Matthew 17:4; Mark 9:5; Luke 9:33; Matthew 19:27; Mark 10:28; Luke 18:28; Mark 1:36-37; Luke 5:4-5; John 6:68; Matthew 14:28-29; Matthew 16:22; Mark 8:32; Matthew 26:35; Mark 14:31; Luke 22:33; John 13:37; John 13:6-8; John 18:10; Matthew 16:17-19; Matthew 16:23; Mark 8:33; Matthew 26:34; Mark 14:30; Luke 22:34; John 13:38; Matthew 26:40-41; Mark 14:37-38; John 18:10-11; John 21:20-22; Matthew 26:69-74; Mark 14:66-71; Luke 22:54-60; John 18:15-18; John 18:25-27; Matthew 26:75; Mark 14:72; Luke 22:61-62; Luke 24:33-34; 1 Corinthians 15:4-5; John 21:15-17
* **Associated Articles:** 6730: reinstatement (ID: `6730@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7024: church, nature and foundations of (ID: `7024@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7620: disciples (ID: `7620@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7630: Twelve, the (ID: `7630@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (ID: `8712@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5114: Peter, the apostle (id: 5114)

As a result of the coming of the Holy Spirit, Peter was transformed into a dynamic apostle and witness to Jesus Christ.

Peter's credentials as an apostle
---------------------------------

[MAT 10:2](https://ref.ly/Matt10:2); ; [ACT 2:37](https://ref.ly/Acts2:37), [ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29); ; [1PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1); ; [2PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:1)

### Peter as an apostle to the Jews

**[GAL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Gal2:8)**

Peter's leadership
------------------

*See also* [ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22), [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14), [ACT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Acts3:12), [ACT 4:8](https://ref.ly/Acts4:8), [ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29), [ACT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Acts8:20)

Peter and the Gentiles
----------------------

### He was the first to baptise Gentiles who had not been circumcised

[ACT 10:47–10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:47-Acts10:48)

### He defended this action against critics

[ACT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Acts11:17), [ACT 15:7–15:11](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7-Acts15:11)

### He later wavered in his attitude to the Gentiles

[GAL 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:14)

The miraculous power of the Holy Spirit at work in Peter
--------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 3:2–3:7](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2-Acts3:7), [ACT 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Acts5:9-Acts5:10), [ACT 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:15-Acts5:16), [ACT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Acts9:34), [ACT 9:40](https://ref.ly/Acts9:40), [ACT 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Acts12:1-Acts12:11) *Peter himself experienced a miracle when imprisoned by Herod Agrippa.*

Peter's missionary travels
--------------------------

[1CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:12) *He visited Corinth*; ; [1CO 9:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:5) *He took his wife with him*; ; [1PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1) *He visited northern Asia Minor*; ; [1PE 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:13) *One interpretation of this verse is that Peter was writing from Rome and was using the name “Babylon” symbolically to refer to Rome.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 10:2; Acts 2:37; Acts 5:29; 1 Peter 1:1; 2 Peter 1:1; Galatians 2:8; Acts 1:21-22; Acts 2:14; Acts 3:12; Acts 4:8; Acts 8:20; Acts 10:47-48; Acts 11:17; Acts 15:7-11; Galatians 2:11-14; Acts 3:2-7; Acts 5:9-10; Acts 5:15-16; Acts 9:34; Acts 9:40; Acts 12:1-11; 1 Corinthians 1:12; 1 Corinthians 9:5; 1 Peter 5:13
* **Associated Articles:** 7378: high priest, in NT (ID: `7378@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7512: Gentiles, in NT (ID: `7512@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7706: apostles (ID: `7706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7739: missionaries (ID: `7739@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8619: prayer, in the church (ID: `8619@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5115: Peter, preacher and teacher (id: 5115)

Peter preached mainly to the Jews. He taught both through his preaching and his letters.

Peter's commission
------------------

**[JHN 21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:17)**

Peter quoted from the OT in his teaching
----------------------------------------

Referring to the death of Judas: [ACT 1:16](https://ref.ly/Acts1:16), [ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Acts1:20); [ACT 2:16–2:20](https://ref.ly/Acts2:16-Acts2:20), [ACT 2:25–2:28](https://ref.ly/Acts2:25-Acts2:28), [ACT 2:34–2:35](https://ref.ly/Acts2:34-Acts2:35); [ACT 3:22–3:23](https://ref.ly/Acts3:22-Acts3:23), [ACT 4:11](https://ref.ly/Acts4:11); [1PE 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:15-1Pet1:16), [1PE 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:24-1Pet1:25), [1PE 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:6-1Pet2:8), [1PE 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:5-1Pet3:6), [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12), [1PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:14), [1PE 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:20), [1PE 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:18), [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5); ; [2PE 2:5–2:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:5-2Pet2:9)

Peter taught about Jesus Christ
-------------------------------

### About the life and miracles of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 2:22](https://ref.ly/Acts2:22)** *See also* [ACT 10:36–10:39](https://ref.ly/Acts10:36-Acts10:39); [2PE 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:17-2Pet1:18)

### About Jesus Christ's suffering and death

**[ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23)** *See also* [ACT 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Acts3:13-Acts3:15), [ACT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Acts4:10), [ACT 5:30](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30), [ACT 10:39](https://ref.ly/Acts10:39); [1PE 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22-1Pet2:23), [1PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:18)

### About the resurrection of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 2:24](https://ref.ly/Acts2:24)** *See also* [ACT 2:31–2:32](https://ref.ly/Acts2:31-Acts2:32), [ACT 3:15](https://ref.ly/Acts3:15), [ACT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Acts4:10), [ACT 5:30](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30), [ACT 10:40–10:41](https://ref.ly/Acts10:40-Acts10:41); [1PE 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3), [1PE 1:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:21), [1PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:18)

### About the ascension and exaltation of Jesus Christ

**[ACT 5:31](https://ref.ly/Acts5:31)** *See also* [ACT 2:33](https://ref.ly/Acts2:33), [ACT 2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:36), [ACT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Acts3:13); [1PE 1:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:21), [1PE 3:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:22)

### About the second coming of Jesus Christ

**[1PE 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:4)** *See also* [1PE 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:7), [1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13), [1PE 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:13); [2PE 3:10–3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10-2Pet3:13)

Peter taught about salvation through repentance and faith in Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:12)** *See also* [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 3:19](https://ref.ly/Acts3:19), [ACT 10:43](https://ref.ly/Acts10:43)

Peter taught that God cared for all people
------------------------------------------

[ACT 10:34](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34), [ACT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7-Acts15:9)

Peter wrote his first letter to encourage Christians
----------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1), [1PE 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:12)** *See also* [1PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1) *He exhorted them to live holy lives*; [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14) *They must obey authority*; [1PE 3:161](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:161)PE [4:12–4:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:12-1Pet4:16), [1PE 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:19)[1PE 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10) *God will vindicate and save his people.*

Peter wrote his second letter to strengthen Christians who were at risk from false teaching
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[2PE 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:12)** *See also* [2PE 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:16) *He reminded them of the truth of the gospel*; [2PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:3) *He warned them of the judgment awaiting false teachers*; [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13) *They must keep alive their hope in the coming day of the Lord.*

* **Associated Passages:** John 21:17; Acts 1:16; Acts 1:20; Acts 2:16-20; Acts 2:25-28; Acts 2:34-35; Acts 3:22-23; Acts 4:11; 1 Peter 1:15-16; 1 Peter 1:24-25; 1 Peter 2:6-8; 1 Peter 3:5-6; 1 Peter 3:12; 1 Peter 3:14; 1 Peter 3:20; 1 Peter 4:18; 1 Peter 5:5; 2 Peter 2:5-9; Acts 2:22; Acts 10:36-39; 2 Peter 1:17-18; Acts 2:23; Acts 3:13-15; Acts 4:10; Acts 5:30; Acts 10:39; 1 Peter 2:22-23; 1 Peter 3:18; Acts 2:24; Acts 2:31-32; Acts 3:15; Acts 10:40-41; 1 Peter 1:3; 1 Peter 1:21; Acts 5:31; Acts 2:33; Acts 2:36; Acts 3:13; 1 Peter 3:22; 1 Peter 5:4; 1 Peter 1:7; 1 Peter 1:13; 1 Peter 4:13; 2 Peter 3:10-13; Acts 4:12; Acts 2:38; Acts 3:19; Acts 10:43; Acts 10:34; Acts 15:7-9; 1 Peter 1:1; 1 Peter 5:12; 1 Peter 2:1; 1 Peter 2:13-14; 1 Peter 3:161; 1 Peter 4:12-16; 1 Peter 4:19; 1 Peter 5:10; 2 Peter 1:12; 2 Peter 1:16; 2 Peter 2:3; 2 Peter 3:13
* **Associated Articles:** 7754: preaching (ID: `7754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8414: encouragement (ID: `8414@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8749: false teachers (ID: `8749@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5117: Ruth (id: 5117)

A young Moabite widow, who, because of her selfless devotion to her mother\-in\-law Naomi, and to Naomi's God, gained a husband and a child, a place in Jewish history and became the great\-grandmother of David and an ancestor of Jesus Christ.

Ruth faces the problems of widowhood
------------------------------------

**[RUT 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:3-Ruth1:5)** *See also* [RUT 1:6–1:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:6-Ruth1:13)

Ruth commits herself to Naomi
-----------------------------

**[RUT 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:16-Ruth1:17)** *See also* [RUT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:18), [RUT 1:22](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:22), [RUT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:11)

Ruth and Boaz
-------------

### Boaz allows Ruth to glean in his fields

**[RUT 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:8-Ruth2:9)** *See also* [RUT 2:14–2:23](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:14-Ruth2:23)

### Ruth asks Boaz to be her \`\`kinsman\-redeemer''

**[RUT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:9)** *The kinsman\-redeemer was the closest male relative who had responsibility to marry a widow within the family and thereby protect the interests of needy members of the extended family.*

### Boaz negotiates with a nearer relative of Ruth's

*See also* [RUT 4:1–4:8](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:8)

### Boaz marries Ruth

**[RUT 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:9-Ruth4:10)** *See also* [RUT 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:11-Ruth4:13)

### She has a son who is presented to Naomi

**[RUT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13)** *See also* [RUT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:17)

Ruth is included in the genealogy of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Matt1:5-Matt1:6)**

* **Associated Passages:** Ruth 1:3-5; Ruth 1:6-13; Ruth 1:16-17; Ruth 1:18; Ruth 1:22; Ruth 2:11; Ruth 2:8-9; Ruth 2:14-23; Ruth 3:9; Ruth 4:1-8; Ruth 4:9-10; Ruth 4:11-13; Ruth 4:13; Ruth 4:17; Matthew 1:5-6
* **Associated Articles:** 2540: Jesus Christ, genealogy of (ID: `2540@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5086: David, rise of (ID: `5086@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5720: mothers, examples of (ID: `5720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5743: widows (ID: `5743@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7388: kinsman-redeemer (ID: `7388@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5118: Solomon (id: 5118)

The son of David and Bathsheba, who succeeded David as king of Israel. Noted for his great wisdom, Solomon was responsible for the building of the temple of the LORD. In his latter years, influenced by his many foreign wives, he turned to idolatry.

* **Associated Articles:** 5119: Solomon, life of (ID: `5119@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5120: Solomon, character of (ID: `5120@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5119: Solomon, life of (id: 5119)

Solomon succeeded his father David as king and secured Israel's stability through treaties with foreign powers.

Solomon's birth
---------------

**[1CH 3:1–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr3:1-1Chr3:9)** *See also* [2SA 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24-2Sam12:25)

Solomon's accession
-------------------

### Adonijah's rivalry

**[1KI 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:5)** *See also* [1KI 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:11-1Kgs1:14), [1KI 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:24-1Kgs1:25), [1KI 2:13–2:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:13-1Kgs2:25)

### David's approval

**[1KI 1:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:30)** parallel [1CH 29:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:23) *See also* [1KI 1:32–1:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:32-1Kgs1:35), [1KI 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:1-1Kgs2:4)

### The coronation

**[1KI 1:38–1:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:38-1Kgs1:40)** parallel [1CH 29:21–29:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:21-1Chr29:22) *See also* [1KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:12)

----------------------------------

### The temple

## 5120: Solomon, character of (id: 5120)

Solomon was a man of outstanding gifts and abilities. Initially he was approved by God, but the latter years of his reign were marred by idolatry and apostasy.

Solomon's abilities
-------------------

### Wisdom

**[1KI 4:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:29)** *See also* [1KI 3:7–3:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:7-1Kgs3:12) parallel [2CH 1:8–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:8-2Chr1:12); [1KI 3:16–3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:16-1Kgs3:28), [1KI 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:6-1Kgs10:7) parallel [2CH 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:5-2Chr9:6); [MAT 12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:42)

### Political shrewdness

**[1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1)** *See also* [1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21), [1KI 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:13)

### Dealing with individuals

**[1KI 2:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:35)** *See also* [1KI 2:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:24), [1KI 2:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:27)

### Poetry and writing

**[1KI 4:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:32)** *See also* PSA 72:0, PSA 127:0; [PRO 1:1](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1), [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1), [PRO 25:1](https://ref.ly/Prov25:1)

Solomon's righteousness
-----------------------

**[1KI 8:62–8:63](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:62-1Kgs8:63)** parallel [2CH 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:4-2Chr7:5) *See also* [1KI 3:6–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:6-1Kgs3:15), [1KI 8:54–8:61](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:54-1Kgs8:61), [1KI 8:65–8:66](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:65-1Kgs8:66)

Solomon's sin
-------------

**[NEH 13:26](https://ref.ly/Neh13:26)** *See also* [DEU 17:16–17:17](https://ref.ly/Deut17:16-Deut17:17); [1KI 10:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:26), [1KI 10:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:28), [1KI 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1), [1KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4), [1KI 11:9–11:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:9-1Kgs11:13)

Solomon's splendour was renowned
--------------------------------

**[MAT 6:29](https://ref.ly/Matt6:29)** parallel [LUK 12:27](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27)

## 5126: arm (id: 5126)

Used as a symbol of strength and as a means of expressing affection and relationship. Some ornaments are worn on the arm.

Everyday uses of arms
---------------------

*See also* [NUM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:12) *carrying*; [NUM 20:11](https://ref.ly/Num20:11) *hitting*; [2SA 22:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:35) parallel [PSA 18:34](https://ref.ly/Ps18:34) *archery*[2KI 5:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:18), [2KI 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:2), [2KI 7:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:17)PSA [129:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs129:7); [ISA 17:5](https://ref.ly/Isa17:5)[ISA 44:12](https://ref.ly/Isa44:12) *craftsmanship*; [HOS 11:3](https://ref.ly/Hos11:3) *teaching to walk*

The arm as a symbol of human strength
-------------------------------------

### Of physical strength in general

[GEN 49:24](https://ref.ly/Gen49:24); ; [JDG 15:14](https://ref.ly/Judg15:14), [JDG 16:12](https://ref.ly/Judg16:12); ; [PRO 31:17](https://ref.ly/Prov31:17); ; [ISA 44:12](https://ref.ly/Isa44:12)

### Of strength for battle

**[EZK 30:24–30:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:24-Ezek30:25)** *See also* [JOB 35:9](https://ref.ly/Job35:9)

The arm as a symbol of human weakness
-------------------------------------

### A broken arm expresses defeat

**[JER 48:25](https://ref.ly/Jer48:25)** *See also* [JOB 38:15](https://ref.ly/Job38:15); [PSA 10:15](https://ref.ly/Ps10:15); [EZK 30:21–30:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:21-Ezek30:22), [EZK 30:24–30:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:24-Ezek30:25); [ZEC 11:17](https://ref.ly/Zech11:17)

### Restrained arms

[JER 40:4](https://ref.ly/Jer40:4); ; [ACT 12:7](https://ref.ly/Acts12:7)

### Feeble arms need to be strengthened

**[JOB 26:2](https://ref.ly/Job26:2)** *See also* [HEB 12:12](https://ref.ly/Heb12:12)

### Human arms are weak compared with God's strength

**[JOB 40:9](https://ref.ly/Job40:9)** *See also* [2CH 32:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:8)

The arm of God as a symbol
--------------------------

### Of the might of God

**[PSA 77:15](https://ref.ly/Ps77:15)** *See also* [PSA 89:10](https://ref.ly/Ps89:10), [PSA 89:13](https://ref.ly/Ps89:13); [ISA 63:12](https://ref.ly/Isa63:12); [JER 27:5](https://ref.ly/Jer27:5)

### Of the power of God to save

**[NUM 11:23](https://ref.ly/Num11:23)** *See also* [EXO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6); [DEU 9:29](https://ref.ly/Deut9:29), [DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27); [2CH 6:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:32); [ISA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1), [ISA 59:1](https://ref.ly/Isa59:1), [ISA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Isa59:16)

### Of the eternity of God

[DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27)

### Of the holiness of God

[PSA 98:1](https://ref.ly/Ps98:1); ; [ISA 52:10](https://ref.ly/Isa52:10)

Arms as an expression of affection and greeting
-----------------------------------------------

### Used to embrace in greeting

**[GEN 45:14](https://ref.ly/Gen45:14)** *See also* [GEN 29:13](https://ref.ly/Gen29:13), [GEN 33:4](https://ref.ly/Gen33:4), [GEN 46:29](https://ref.ly/Gen46:29), [GEN 48:10](https://ref.ly/Gen48:10); [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20); [ACT 20:37](https://ref.ly/Acts20:37)

### Used to embrace a wife or lover

**[SNG 2:6](https://ref.ly/Song2:6)** *See also* [GEN 16:5](https://ref.ly/Gen16:5); [2SA 12:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:8); [PRO 5:20](https://ref.ly/Prov5:20); [SNG 8:3](https://ref.ly/Song8:3); [MIC 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mic7:5)

### Used to embrace the young

**[MRK 10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:16)** *See also* [NUM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:12); [2SA 12:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:3); [1KI 17:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:19), [1KI 17:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:23); [2KI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:16); [ISA 49:22](https://ref.ly/Isa49:22); [LAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:12); [MRK 9:36](https://ref.ly/Mark9:36); [LUK 2:28](https://ref.ly/Luke2:28); [ACT 20:10](https://ref.ly/Acts20:10)

**[PRO 31:20](https://ref.ly/Prov31:20)**

### Used in phrases expressing the adoption of false gods

## 5127: back (id: 5127)

As the part of the body used for bearing loads it is used both literally and metaphorically.

The human back used for carrying
--------------------------------

### Jesus Christ carried his cross on his back

**[JHN 19:17](https://ref.ly/John19:17)** *See also* [MAT 27:32](https://ref.ly/Matt27:32) parallels [MRK 15:21](https://ref.ly/Mark15:21), [LUK 23:26](https://ref.ly/Luke23:26) *The shape of the cross necessitated it being carried across the shoulders and back. It appears that Jesus Christ started to carry his own cross but Simon of Cyrene took over as Christ weakened.*

### Weapons carried on the back

[1SA 17:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:6)

Animals carry people and goods on their backs
---------------------------------------------

**[ISA 30:6](https://ref.ly/Isa30:6)** *See also* [EXO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1), [EXO 15:21](https://ref.ly/Exod15:21); [2SA 13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:29), [2SA 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:1-2Sam16:2), [2SA 18:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:9), [2SA 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:26); [2KI 18:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:23) parallel [ISA 36:8](https://ref.ly/Isa36:8); [ISA 21:7](https://ref.ly/Isa21:7), [ISA 30:16](https://ref.ly/Isa30:16); [JER 6:23](https://ref.ly/Jer6:23); [HAG 2:22](https://ref.ly/Hag2:22); [REV 9:17](https://ref.ly/Rev9:17)

### Riding on the backs of animals may indicate royalty or privilege

**[ZEC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9)** *See also* [1KI 1:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:33), [1KI 1:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:38), [1KI 1:44](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:44)JDG [5:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:10), [JDG 10:4](https://ref.ly/Judg10:4), [JDG 12:14](https://ref.ly/Judg12:14)REV [6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Judg6:1-Judg6:8), [REV 19:11–19:21](https://ref.ly/Rev19:11-Rev19:21)

### Jesus Christ mounted a donkey for his triumphal entry into Jerusalem

**[MAT 21:7–21:9](https://ref.ly/Matt21:7-Matt21:9)** parallels [MRK 11:7–11:11](https://ref.ly/Mark11:7-Mark11:11), [LUK 19:35–19:38](https://ref.ly/Luke19:35-Luke19:38) *See also* [JHN 12:13–12:15](https://ref.ly/John12:13-John12:15)

Carrying a burden on the back represents bearing suffering and trials
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Ps66:11)** *See also* [PSA 38:4](https://ref.ly/Ps38:4); [ECC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13), [ECC 3:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:10); [MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30)

Injury to the back
------------------

[JDG 3:22](https://ref.ly/Judg3:22); ; [2SA 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:23); ; [JOB 20:24–20:25](https://ref.ly/Job20:24-Job20:25)

### Pain in the back causing anguish

[PSA 38:7](https://ref.ly/Ps38:7)

The back endures punishment
---------------------------

### Bending of the back indicates loss of physical strength

**[PSA 69:23](https://ref.ly/Ps69:23)** *See also* [ROM 11:10](https://ref.ly/Rom11:10)

### Beating on the back is a punishment

[PRO 10:13](https://ref.ly/Prov10:13), [PRO 14:3](https://ref.ly/Prov14:3), [PRO 19:29](https://ref.ly/Prov19:29), [PRO 26:3](https://ref.ly/Prov26:3)

### Beating on the back may be an unjust punishment for God's servants

**[ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6)** *See also* [ISA 53:4–53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4-Isa53:5); [MAT 21:35](https://ref.ly/Matt21:35) parallels [MRK 12:3](https://ref.ly/Mark12:3), [LUK 20:11](https://ref.ly/Luke20:11) *The servants in the parable represent the OT prophets.*

### Jesus Christ endured flogging

**[MAT 27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26)** parallels [MRK 15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15), [JHN 19:1](https://ref.ly/John19:1) *Jesus Christ becomes the suffering servant of Isaiah's prophecies.*

### The apostles endured flogging

**[2CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:25)** *See also* [ACT 5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:40), [ACT 16:22–16:23](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22-Acts16:23), [ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37), [ACT 21:32](https://ref.ly/Acts21:32); [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5), [2CO 6:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:9)

### Jesus Christ's disciples should expect similar flogging

**[MAT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17)** *See also* [MAT 23:34](https://ref.ly/Matt23:34); [MRK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9)

### Whipping on the back used figuratively in a warning

[JOS 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12-Josh23:13)

### Walking over the back used figuratively to indicate defeat

[ISA 51:23](https://ref.ly/Isa51:23)

Turning one's back means desertion
----------------------------------

### God makes the enemies of his people flee

**[EXO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Exod23:27)** *See also* [2SA 22:41](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:41) parallel [PSA 18:40](https://ref.ly/Ps18:40); [PSA 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ps21:12)

### Israel flees from her enemies

**[JOS 7:12](https://ref.ly/Josh7:12)**

### God's people desert him

**[NEH 9:29](https://ref.ly/Neh9:29)** *“To turn one's back”, in these verses and the following verses, also implies a lack of respect and reverence for God and his ways.* *See also* [2CH 29:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:6); [ISA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Isa1:4), [ISA 59:12–59:13](https://ref.ly/Isa59:12-Isa59:13); [JER 2:27](https://ref.ly/Jer2:27), [JER 32:33–32:34](https://ref.ly/Jer32:33-Jer32:34); [EZK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16)

### God's people desert his instruction

[ZEC 7:11](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11); ; [2PE 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:21)

Thrusting behind one's back means discarding
--------------------------------------------

**[ISA 38:17](https://ref.ly/Isa38:17)** *God forgives sins, putting them out of sight*; **[EZK 23:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:35)** *See also* [1KI 14:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:9); [NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26)

God turns his back in his grace
-------------------------------

**[EXO 33:22–33:23](https://ref.ly/Exod33:22-Exod33:23)** *To see God's face would bring death.*

God turns his back against disobedient people
---------------------------------------------

**[JER 18:17](https://ref.ly/Jer18:17)**

* **Associated Passages:** John 19:17; Matthew 27:32; Mark 15:21; Luke 23:26; 1 Samuel 17:6; Isaiah 30:6; Exodus 15:1; Exodus 15:21; 2 Samuel 13:29; 2 Samuel 16:1-2; 2 Samuel 18:9; 2 Samuel 19:26; 2 Kings 18:23; Isaiah 36:8; Isaiah 21:7; Isaiah 30:16; Jeremiah 6:23; Haggai 2:22; Revelation 9:17; Zechariah 9:9; 1 Kings 1:33; 1 Kings 1:38; 1 Kings 1:44; 1 Kings 5:10; Judges 10:4; Judges 12:14; Judges 6:1-8; Revelation 19:11-21; Matthew 21:7-9; Mark 11:7-11; Luke 19:35-38; John 12:13-15; Psalms 66:11; Psalms 38:4; Ecclesiastes 1:13; Ecclesiastes 3:10; Matthew 11:28-30; Judges 3:22; 2 Samuel 2:23; Job 20:24-25; Psalms 38:7; Psalms 69:23; Romans 11:10; Proverbs 10:13; Proverbs 14:3; Proverbs 19:29; Proverbs 26:3; Isaiah 50:6; Isaiah 53:4-5; Matthew 21:35; Mark 12:3; Luke 20:11; Matthew 27:26; Mark 15:15; John 19:1; 2 Corinthians 11:23-25; Acts 5:40; Acts 16:22-23; Acts 16:37; Acts 21:32; 2 Corinthians 6:5; 2 Corinthians 6:9; Matthew 10:17; Matthew 23:34; Mark 13:9; Joshua 23:12-13; Isaiah 51:23; Exodus 23:27; 2 Samuel 22:41; Psalms 18:40; Psalms 21:12; Joshua 7:12; Nehemiah 9:29; 2 Chronicles 29:6; Isaiah 1:4; Isaiah 59:12-13; Jeremiah 2:27; Jeremiah 32:33-34; Ezekiel 8:16; Zechariah 7:11; 2 Peter 2:21; Isaiah 38:17; Ezekiel 23:35; 1 Kings 14:9; Nehemiah 9:26; Exodus 33:22-23; Jeremiah 18:17

## 5128: baldness (id: 5128)

Loss of hair on the head as the result of natural causes or deliberate action. Baldness may be used to signify judgment, mourning or shame.

Instructions regarding baldness within Jewish law
-------------------------------------------------

### Associated with idolatrous practice

*See also* [LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5); [DEU 14:1](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1); [JER 16:6](https://ref.ly/Jer16:6) *In pagan Canaan mourners shaved their foreheads.*

### Regarding sores on bald heads

[LEV 13:40–13:42](https://ref.ly/Lev13:40-Lev13:42)

### Cleansing may involve shaving the head

[LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9); ; [NUM 6:9](https://ref.ly/Num6:9), [NUM 6:18](https://ref.ly/Num6:18); ; [DEU 21:12](https://ref.ly/Deut21:12)

### Vows that involve shaving the head

[JDG 13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:5), [JDG 16:17–16:19](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17-Judg16:19); ; [ACT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Acts18:18) *based on the Nazarite rule recorded in [Nu 6:1–21](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:21)*

Baldness may signify God's judgment
-----------------------------------

[EZK 5:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:1) *Ezekiel instructed to shave his head to symbolise this*

### Judgment on Jerusalem and Judah

[ISA 3:17](https://ref.ly/Isa3:17), [ISA 3:24](https://ref.ly/Isa3:24), [ISA 7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:20)

Baldness as a symbol of mourning
--------------------------------

**[JOB 1:20](https://ref.ly/Job1:20)** *Job here mourns the loss of his children, household and livestock.* *See also* [ISA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2), [ISA 22:12](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12); [JER 48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:37); [EZK 27:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:31); [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10); [MIC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mic1:16)

### Used figuratively to indicate the mourning of a nation

[JER 47:5](https://ref.ly/Jer47:5)

Baldness as a symbol of shame
-----------------------------

[2KI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23); ; [EZK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:18); ; [1CO 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:5-1Cor11:6)

Examples of baldness
--------------------

[EZK 29:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:18) *baldness caused by the rubbing of leather helmets in warfare*; ; [JDG 16:19](https://ref.ly/Judg16:19) *Samson*; ; [2KI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23) *Elisha*

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 21:5; Deuteronomy 14:1; Jeremiah 16:6; Leviticus 13:40-42; Leviticus 14:8-9; Numbers 6:9; Numbers 6:18; Deuteronomy 21:12; Judges 13:5; Judges 16:17-19; Acts 18:18; Numbers 6:1-21; Ezekiel 5:1; Isaiah 3:17; Isaiah 3:24; Isaiah 7:20; Job 1:20; Isaiah 15:2; Isaiah 22:12; Jeremiah 48:37; Ezekiel 27:31; Amos 8:10; Micah 1:16; Jeremiah 47:5; 2 Kings 2:23; Ezekiel 7:18; 1 Corinthians 11:5-6; Ezekiel 29:18; Judges 16:19
* **Associated Articles:** 5154: forehead (ID: `5154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5155: hair (ID: `5155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5157: head (ID: `5157@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5158: head-covering (ID: `5158@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5180: shaving (ID: `5180@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5419: mourning (ID: `5419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5129: bathing (id: 5129)

Bathing as an act of ritual cleansing
-------------------------------------

**[LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9)** *See also* [LEV 15:4–15:11](https://ref.ly/Lev15:4-Lev15:11), [LEV 16:26–16:28](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26-Lev16:28); [NUM 19:7–19:8](https://ref.ly/Num19:7-Num19:8)

Bathing as a means of cleansing or refreshing the body
------------------------------------------------------

Bathing referred to metaphorically
----------------------------------

[DEU 33:24](https://ref.ly/Deut33:24); ; [PSA 58:10](https://ref.ly/Ps58:10); ; [ISA 34:5–34:6](https://ref.ly/Isa34:5-Isa34:6); ; [EZK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:9), [EZK 23:40](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:40)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 14:8-9; Leviticus 15:4-11; Leviticus 16:26-28; Numbers 19:7-8; Exodus 2:5; 2 Samuel 11:2; 1 Kings 22:38; Deuteronomy 33:24; Psalms 58:10; Isaiah 34:5-6; Ezekiel 16:9; Ezekiel 23:40

## 5130: beard (id: 5130)

References to the beard
-----------------------

**[EZK 5:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:1)** *See also* [JDG 16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17); [1SA 21:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:13); [2SA 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9); [PSA 133:2](https://ref.ly/Ps133:2)

Cutting or pulling the beard
----------------------------

### In grief

**[JER 48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:37)** *Moab is in mourning.* *See also* [ISA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2); [JER 41:5](https://ref.ly/Jer41:5)

### In dismay

**[EZR 9:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:3)**

### In the ceremony of cleansing

*See also* [LEV 14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:9)

### As an extreme insult

[2SA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4-2Sam10:5) parallel [1CH 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:4-1Chr19:5); ; [ISA 7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:20), [ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6)

### Cutting the edges of the beard was forbidden

[LEV 19:27](https://ref.ly/Lev19:27) *This is a prohibition still adhered to by some Jews today*; ; [LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 5:1; Judges 16:17; 1 Samuel 21:13; 2 Samuel 20:9; Psalms 133:2; Jeremiah 48:37; Isaiah 15:2; Jeremiah 41:5; Ezra 9:3; Leviticus 14:9; 2 Samuel 10:4-5; 1 Chronicles 19:4-5; Isaiah 7:20; Isaiah 50:6; Leviticus 19:27; Leviticus 21:5
