## 5131: belt (id: 5131)

An item of clothing, worn round the waist, used for gathering clothes and holding weapons or money.

The belt as an item of clothing
-------------------------------

**[1KI 18:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:46)** *See also* [2SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:8); [1KI 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:5); [2KI 4:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:29), [2KI 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1); [PSA 109:19](https://ref.ly/Ps109:19); [ISA 5:27](https://ref.ly/Isa5:27); [JER 13:1–13:11](https://ref.ly/Jer13:1-Jer13:11); [DAN 10:5](https://ref.ly/Dan10:5); [MAT 10:9](https://ref.ly/Matt10:9) parallel [MRK 6:8](https://ref.ly/Mark6:8); [ACT 21:11](https://ref.ly/Acts21:11)

Part of the battledress of a warrior
------------------------------------

**[1SA 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:4)** *See also* [2SA 18:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:11); [EZK 23:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:15)

A leather belt was a sign of rough living
-----------------------------------------

**[2KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8)** *See also* [MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) parallel [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6)

The belt symbolises truth and righteousness
-------------------------------------------

**[ISA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:5)**; **[EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:14)**

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 18:46; 2 Samuel 20:8; 1 Kings 2:5; 2 Kings 4:29; 2 Kings 9:1; Psalms 109:19; Isaiah 5:27; Jeremiah 13:1-11; Daniel 10:5; Matthew 10:9; Mark 6:8; Acts 21:11; 1 Samuel 18:4; 2 Samuel 18:11; Ezekiel 23:15; 2 Kings 1:8; Matthew 3:4; Mark 1:6; Isaiah 11:5; Ephesians 6:14
* **Associated Articles:** 5144: cloak (ID: `5144@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5177: robes (ID: `5177@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5612: weapons (ID: `5612@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8154: righteousness (ID: `8154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8486: spiritual warfare, armour (ID: `8486@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5132: biting (id: 5132)

The use of teeth to harm, threaten or express frustration. It is illustrative of the effect of slander, cold wind, excessive wine or of God's judgment.

Biting used literally
---------------------

### An effect of demon\-possession

*See also* [MRK 9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:18)

### Biting by reptiles and animals

**[NUM 21:6](https://ref.ly/Num21:6)** *See also* [ECC 10:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:8), [ECC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:11); [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24)

### Reptiles and animals prevented from biting

**[DAN 6:21–6:22](https://ref.ly/Dan6:21-Dan6:22)** *See also* [MRK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Mark16:18); [ACT 28:4–28:5](https://ref.ly/Acts28:4-Acts28:5); [HEB 11:33](https://ref.ly/Heb11:33)

Biting used metaphorically
--------------------------

### Of God's judgment on the wicked

**[JER 8:17](https://ref.ly/Jer8:17)** *See also* [JOL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Joel1:6); [AMO 5:19](https://ref.ly/Amos5:19), [AMO 9:3](https://ref.ly/Amos9:3)

### Of the wicked against God's people

**[ACT 7:54](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54)** *See also* [PSA 35:16](https://ref.ly/Ps35:16), [PSA 37:12](https://ref.ly/Ps37:12); [LAM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:16); [1CO 15:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:32) *A fulfilment of the prophecy of [Isa 22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:13), indicating that the devouring of God's people is a part of the greed of the wicked*; [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

### Of Satan and demons

[1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8); ; [REV 9:8](https://ref.ly/Rev9:8)

### Of the frustration of the wicked

**[LUK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Luke13:28)** *See also* [PSA 112:10](https://ref.ly/Ps112:10); [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12), [MAT 13:42](https://ref.ly/Matt13:42), [MAT 22:13](https://ref.ly/Matt22:13), [MAT 24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:51), [MAT 25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:30); [REV 16:10](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10)

### Of quarrelling, slander and treachery

**[GAL 5:15](https://ref.ly/Gal5:15)** *See also* [GEN 49:17](https://ref.ly/Gen49:17); [PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4), [PSA 58:4](https://ref.ly/Ps58:4), [PSA 140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:3); [ROM 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Rom3:13-Rom3:14); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)

### Of a cold wind

**[JOB 37:9–37:10](https://ref.ly/Job37:9-Job37:10)** *See also* [ISA 28:2](https://ref.ly/Isa28:2)

### Of penetrating sarcasm

## 5133: blindness (id: 5133)

The inability on the part of someone to see properly. Blindness is often used in Scripture as a symbol of human sin.

* **Associated Articles:** 5134: blindness, natural (ID: `5134@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5135: blindness, spiritual (ID: `5135@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5134: blindness, natural (id: 5134)

The inability to see properly. The healing of such physical blindness was one of the many miracles performed by Jesus Christ, who restored sight to the blind at several points during his ministry. Scripture often treats blindness as a symbol of the human inability or refusal to realise the importance of God and the gospel. The restoration of sight can thus be treated as a symbol of coming to faith.

The causes of blindness
-----------------------

### A natural affliction

**[EXO 4:11](https://ref.ly/Exod4:11)** *See also* [GEN 27:1](https://ref.ly/Gen27:1), [GEN 29:17](https://ref.ly/Gen29:17); [1SA 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:2), [1SA 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:15); [JHN 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:3); [ACT 22:11](https://ref.ly/Acts22:11); [GAL 4:15](https://ref.ly/Gal4:15)

### A consequence of sin

[GEN 19:11](https://ref.ly/Gen19:11); ; [DEU 28:28–28:29](https://ref.ly/Deut28:28-Deut28:29); ; [2KI 6:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:18); ; [PSA 69:23](https://ref.ly/Ps69:23); ; [ACT 13:9–13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:9-Acts13:11)

### A result of human cruelty

[NUM 16:14](https://ref.ly/Num16:14); ; [JDG 16:21](https://ref.ly/Judg16:21); ; [2KI 25:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:7) parallels [JER 39:7](https://ref.ly/Jer39:7), [JER 52:11](https://ref.ly/Jer52:11)

## 5135: blindness, spiritual (id: 5135)

An inability or unwillingness to perceive the truth of the gospel.

Unbelievers are spiritually blind
---------------------------------

**[1CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:14)** *See also* [PSA 32:9](https://ref.ly/Ps32:9), [PSA 92:6–92:7](https://ref.ly/Ps92:6-Ps92:7); [PRO 4:19](https://ref.ly/Prov4:19)ISA [44:9](https://ref.ly/Prov44:9), [ISA 44:18–44:19](https://ref.ly/Isa44:18-Isa44:19)[EZK 12:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:2); [MIC 4:12](https://ref.ly/Mic4:12)MAT [13:13–13:15](https://ref.ly/Mic13:13-Mic13:15); [LUK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:10); [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10)[MAT 15:16](https://ref.ly/Matt15:16); [LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25); [JHN 1:5](https://ref.ly/John1:5), [JHN 9:40–9:41](https://ref.ly/John9:40-John9:41) *the spiritual blindness of the Pharisees*; [ROM 3:11](https://ref.ly/Rom3:11); [1CO 14:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:23), [1CO 14:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:24)

## 5136: body (id: 5136)

Used in Scripture in both a physical and metaphorical sense, the body is susceptible to sin and suffering, but may be offered to God in worship and will finally be transformed by resurrection.

The body is formed by God
-------------------------

**[PSA 139:15–139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:15-Ps139:16)** *See also* [GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7), [GEN 2:22](https://ref.ly/Gen2:22); [JOB 10:8–10:11](https://ref.ly/Job10:8-Job10:11); [ECC 11:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:5)

---------------------

-------------------

Rituals performed on the body
-----------------------------

Circumcision: [GEN 17:13–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:13-Gen17:14); ; [JER 9:25](https://ref.ly/Jer9:25); ; [EPH 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11); [LEV 15:16](https://ref.ly/Lev15:16); ; [NUM 8:7](https://ref.ly/Num8:7); [LEV 19:28](https://ref.ly/Lev19:28), [LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5)

The believer's body as a temple of the Holy Spirit
--------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19-1Cor6:20)** *See also* [JHN 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/John2:19-John2:22) *Jesus Christ also likens his body to a temple*; [ROM 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Rom8:10-Rom8:11)

Believers are to devote their bodies to God
-------------------------------------------

**[2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1)** *See also* [ROM 6:13](https://ref.ly/Rom6:13), [ROM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Rom8:13), [ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1); [1CO 9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:27); [1TH 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:4), [1TH 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:23); [JAS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Jas3:2)

Sin at work in the body
-----------------------

### The body is subject to sinful desires

**[MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41)** parallel [MRK 14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:38) *See also* [ROM 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rom6:6), [ROM 7:23–7:24](https://ref.ly/Rom7:23-Rom7:24)

### Bodily temptation is to be resisted

**[ROM 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12)** *See also* [ROM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24), [ROM 6:19](https://ref.ly/Rom6:19); [1CO 6:13–6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:13-1Cor6:16), [1CO 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:18); [JUD 1:8](https://ref.ly/Jude1:8)

### Undue concern for the body is to be avoided

**[1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:8)** *See also* [MAT 6:25–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:32) parallel [LUK 12:22–12:30](https://ref.ly/Luke12:22-Luke12:30); [MAT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28) parallel [LUK 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/Luke12:4-Luke12:5)

The resurrection of the body
----------------------------

### Dead bodies restored to this life

[1KI 17:20–17:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:20-1Kgs17:22); ; [2KI 4:32–4:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:32-2Kgs4:36), [2KI 13:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:21); ; [MAT 9:25](https://ref.ly/Matt9:25) parallels [MRK 5:41–5:42](https://ref.ly/Mark5:41-Mark5:42), [LUK 8:54–8:55](https://ref.ly/Luke8:54-Luke8:55); ; [LUK 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Luke7:14-Luke7:15); ; [JHN 11:43–11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:43-John11:44)

### The resurrection body of believers

**[PHP 3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:21)** *See also* [PSA 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Ps16:9-Ps16:10); [ISA 26:19](https://ref.ly/Isa26:19); [ROM 8:11](https://ref.ly/Rom8:11), [ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23); [1CO 15:35–15:54](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:35-1Cor15:54)

Bodies other than human
-----------------------

### Idols of pagan gods

[1SA 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:4); ; [PSA 115:4–115:7](https://ref.ly/Ps115:4-Ps115:7), [PSA 135:15–135:17](https://ref.ly/Ps135:15-Ps135:17)

### Angelic beings

[ISA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Isa6:2); ; [EZK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:11), [EZK 1:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:23), [EZK 10:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:12)

### Heavenly bodies

[MAT 24:29](https://ref.ly/Matt24:29) parallels [MRK 13:25](https://ref.ly/Mark13:25), [LUK 21:26](https://ref.ly/Luke21:26); ; [ACT 7:42](https://ref.ly/Acts7:42); ; [1CO 15:40–15:41](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:40-1Cor15:41)

Body in a metaphorical sense
----------------------------

A body of people: [EZR 8:20](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:20); ; [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14); [DEU 4:8](https://ref.ly/Deut4:8); ; [ROM 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rom2:20)

## 5137: bones (id: 5137)

Associated in Hebrew thought with both death and life. A symbol of the whole person, and also the state of one's well\-being.

Bones represent dead bodies
---------------------------

### Human remains to be treated with respect

**[1SA 31:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:13)** parallel [1CH 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:12) *See also* [2SA 21:12–21:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:12-2Sam21:14); [1KI 13:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:31); [2KI 23:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:18); [EZK 39:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:15)

### Non\-burial as a sign of disrespect

**[AMO 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Amos2:1-Amos2:2)**

### Non\-burial as a sign of God's judgment

**[PSA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Ps53:5)** *See also* [2KI 9:34–9:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:34-2Kgs9:37); [PSA 141:7](https://ref.ly/Ps141:7); [JER 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Jer8:1-Jer8:2); [EZK 24:4–24:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:4-Ezek24:5), [EZK 24:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:10)

Bones were considered to be unclean
-----------------------------------

### Bones made anyone who touched them unclean

**[NUM 19:16](https://ref.ly/Num19:16)** *See also* [NUM 19:18](https://ref.ly/Num19:18); [MAT 23:27](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27)

### Bones used to desecrate objects of pagan worship

**[2KI 23:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:14)** *See also* [1KI 13:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:2); [2KI 23:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:16), [2KI 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:20); [2CH 34:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:5); [EZK 6:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:5)

Bones as a sign of hope
-----------------------

### The hope of God's promise

**[GEN 50:25](https://ref.ly/Gen50:25)** *See also* [EXO 13:19](https://ref.ly/Exod13:19); [JOS 24:32](https://ref.ly/Josh24:32); [HEB 11:22](https://ref.ly/Heb11:22) *The presence of Joseph's bones among the Israelites was a constant reminder of God's promise to Joseph concerning the promised land.*

### The hope of life after death

**[EZK 37:1–37:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:1-Ezek37:14)** *See also* [2KI 13:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:21)

### The hope of the Passover lamb

**[JHN 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36)** *See also* [EXO 12:46](https://ref.ly/Exod12:46); [NUM 9:12](https://ref.ly/Num9:12); [PSA 34:20](https://ref.ly/Ps34:20) *Jesus Christ fulfilled the symbolism of the unbroken bones of the Passover lamb.*

Bones represent living individuals
----------------------------------

### Bones indicate a living body

**[LUK 24:39](https://ref.ly/Luke24:39)**

### Bones represent a whole person

**[PSA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:10)** *“My whole being” is literally “my bones”.* *See also* [JOB 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Job2:4-Job2:5), [JOB 10:11](https://ref.ly/Job10:11); [PSA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2), [PSA 51:7–51:8](https://ref.ly/Ps51:7-Ps51:8); [ISA 66:14](https://ref.ly/Isa66:14)

### Bones represent kinship

**[GEN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23)** [GEN 29:14](https://ref.ly/Gen29:14); ; [JDG 9:2](https://ref.ly/Judg9:2); ; [2SA 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:1) parallel [1CH 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:1); ; [2SA 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:12-2Sam19:13)

Bones represent physical health
-------------------------------

### Bones associated with good health

**[PRO 15:30](https://ref.ly/Prov15:30)** *See also* [PRO 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Prov3:7-Prov3:8), [PRO 16:24](https://ref.ly/Prov16:24)

### Bones symbolise strength and vigour

**[JOB 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/Job21:23-Job21:24)** *See also* [JOB 20:11](https://ref.ly/Job20:11), [JOB 40:18](https://ref.ly/Job40:18); [PRO 25:15](https://ref.ly/Prov25:15)

### Bones associated with God's protection

**[PSA 34:19–34:20](https://ref.ly/Ps34:19-Ps34:20)** *See also* [JHN 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36)

### Mere skin and bone represents bad health

**[JOB 19:20](https://ref.ly/Job19:20)** *See also* [JOB 33:21](https://ref.ly/Job33:21); [PSA 102:5](https://ref.ly/Ps102:5); [LAM 4:8](https://ref.ly/Lam4:8)

### Broken bones symbolise defeat or oppression

**[NUM 24:8](https://ref.ly/Num24:8)** *See also* [ISA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Isa38:13); [JER 50:17](https://ref.ly/Jer50:17); [LAM 3:4](https://ref.ly/Lam3:4); [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24); [MIC 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Mic3:2-Mic3:3)

### Health can be damaged by negative attitudes

**[PRO 14:30](https://ref.ly/Prov14:30)** *See also* [JOB 4:14](https://ref.ly/Job4:14); [PRO 12:4](https://ref.ly/Prov12:4), [PRO 17:22](https://ref.ly/Prov17:22); [HAB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Hab3:16)

### Health can be damaged by sin

**[PSA 38:3](https://ref.ly/Ps38:3)** *See also* [PSA 32:3](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3), [PSA 109:18](https://ref.ly/Ps109:18); [LAM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Lam1:13)

### Repentance brings restoration

**[PSA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2)** *See also* [PSA 51:18](https://ref.ly/Ps51:18)

### Health can be damaged through obedience to God's will

**[JER 23:9](https://ref.ly/Jer23:9)** *See also* [JOB 30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:17), [JOB 33:19](https://ref.ly/Job33:19); [PSA 22:14](https://ref.ly/Ps22:14), [PSA 22:17](https://ref.ly/Ps22:17), [PSA 31:10](https://ref.ly/Ps31:10), [PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10); [JER 20:9](https://ref.ly/Jer20:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 31:13; 1 Chronicles 10:12; 2 Samuel 21:12-14; 1 Kings 13:31; 2 Kings 23:18; Ezekiel 39:15; Amos 2:1-2; Psalms 53:5; 2 Kings 9:34-37; Psalms 141:7; Jeremiah 8:1-2; Ezekiel 24:4-5; Ezekiel 24:10; Numbers 19:16; Numbers 19:18; Matthew 23:27; 2 Kings 23:14; 1 Kings 13:2; 2 Kings 23:16; 2 Kings 23:20; 2 Chronicles 34:5; Ezekiel 6:5; Genesis 50:25; Exodus 13:19; Joshua 24:32; Hebrews 11:22; Ezekiel 37:1-14; 2 Kings 13:21; John 19:36; Exodus 12:46; Numbers 9:12; Psalms 34:20; Luke 24:39; Psalms 35:10; Job 2:4-5; Job 10:11; Psalms 6:2; Psalms 51:7-8; Isaiah 66:14; Genesis 2:23; Genesis 29:14; Judges 9:2; 2 Samuel 5:1; 1 Chronicles 11:1; 2 Samuel 19:12-13; Proverbs 15:30; Proverbs 3:7-8; Proverbs 16:24; Job 21:23-24; Job 20:11; Job 40:18; Proverbs 25:15; Psalms 34:19-20; Job 19:20; Job 33:21; Psalms 102:5; Lamentations 4:8; Numbers 24:8; Isaiah 38:13; Jeremiah 50:17; Lamentations 3:4; Daniel 6:24; Micah 3:2-3; Proverbs 14:30; Job 4:14; Proverbs 12:4; Proverbs 17:22; Habakkuk 3:16; Psalms 38:3; Psalms 32:3; Psalms 109:18; Lamentations 1:13; Psalms 51:18; Jeremiah 23:9; Job 30:17; Job 33:19; Psalms 22:14; Psalms 22:17; Psalms 31:10; Psalms 42:10; Jeremiah 20:9

## 5138: bowing (id: 5138)

An act of submission before something or someone deemed worthy of honour.

Bowing as an act of submission
------------------------------

**[GEN 42:6](https://ref.ly/Gen42:6)**; **[2SA 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:2)**; **[PSA 72:9](https://ref.ly/Ps72:9)** *See also* [GEN 33:3](https://ref.ly/Gen33:3), [GEN 43:26–43:28](https://ref.ly/Gen43:26-Gen43:28), [GEN 49:8](https://ref.ly/Gen49:8); [EXO 11:8](https://ref.ly/Exod11:8), [EXO 18:7](https://ref.ly/Exod18:7); [RUT 2:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:10); [1SA 2:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:36), [1SA 24:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:8), [1SA 25:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:23); [2SA 15:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:5), [2SA 18:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:28); [1KI 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:16); [1CH 21:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:21); [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23)

Bowing before angels
--------------------

**[GEN 18:2](https://ref.ly/Gen18:2)** *See also* [GEN 19:1](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1); [NUM 22:31](https://ref.ly/Num22:31); [LUK 24:5](https://ref.ly/Luke24:5)

Bowing before God
-----------------

**[EXO 4:31](https://ref.ly/Exod4:31)**; **[2CH 20:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:18)**; **[PSA 95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6)** *See also* [GEN 17:3](https://ref.ly/Gen17:3), [GEN 24:26](https://ref.ly/Gen24:26); [EXO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:27), [EXO 34:8](https://ref.ly/Exod34:8); [DEU 26:10](https://ref.ly/Deut26:10), [DEU 33:3](https://ref.ly/Deut33:3); [1KI 1:47](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:47); [2KI 17:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:36); [1CH 29:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:20); [2CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:28); [NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6); [PSA 22:27](https://ref.ly/Ps22:27); [ISA 66:23](https://ref.ly/Isa66:23)

Bowing before the temple
------------------------

**[PSA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Ps5:7)** *The temple represented God's presence.* *See also* [PSA 138:2](https://ref.ly/Ps138:2)

Bowing before idols
-------------------

**[EXO 32:8](https://ref.ly/Exod32:8)**; **[2KI 17:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:16)**; **[ISA 2:8](https://ref.ly/Isa2:8)** *See also* [NUM 25:2](https://ref.ly/Num25:2); [DEU 29:26](https://ref.ly/Deut29:26); [2KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3); [2CH 25:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:14); [ISA 44:17](https://ref.ly/Isa44:17), [ISA 44:19](https://ref.ly/Isa44:19); [EZK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16); [MIC 5:13](https://ref.ly/Mic5:13)

Bowing before Jesus Christ
--------------------------

**[MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11)**; **[PHP 2:10](https://ref.ly/Phil2:10)** *See also* [MAT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2), [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18), [MAT 15:25](https://ref.ly/Matt15:25), [MAT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29); [MRK 5:6](https://ref.ly/Mark5:6), [MRK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17); [LUK 17:16](https://ref.ly/Luke17:16)

Bowing as an expression of sorrow
---------------------------------

**[PSA 35:14](https://ref.ly/Ps35:14), [PSA 145:14](https://ref.ly/Ps145:14)** *See also* [PSA 38:6](https://ref.ly/Ps38:6), [PSA 57:6](https://ref.ly/Ps57:6), [PSA 146:8](https://ref.ly/Ps146:8); [LAM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Lam2:10)

## 5139: bracelet (id: 5139)

An expensive ornamental armlet usually worn by women.

* **Associated Articles:** 4342: jewels (ID: `4342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5126: arm (ID: `5126@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5778: adorning (ID: `5778@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5915: ornaments (ID: `5915@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8808: riches (ID: `8808@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5140: breasts (id: 5140)

On a woman, the bosom; on a man the upper part of the chest. Female breasts are primarily referred to as a means of feeding young and as a figurative description of Israel and God as nursing mothers. Beating of the breast is a sign of remorse. The breast is protected in physical and spiritual warfare.

Breasts as a symbol of fertility
--------------------------------

**[GEN 49:25](https://ref.ly/Gen49:25)**

Breasts as the means of feeding young
-------------------------------------

*See also* [1SA 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:23) *until they are weaned*; [1KI 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:20); [JOB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Job3:12); [PSA 22:9](https://ref.ly/Ps22:9); [SNG 8:1](https://ref.ly/Song8:1); [JOL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Joel2:16); [LUK 11:27](https://ref.ly/Luke11:27) *“Nursing” includes breast\-feeding.*

### Nursing mothers and their children experience acute distress

**[LUK 23:29](https://ref.ly/Luke23:29)** *See also* [JOB 24:9](https://ref.ly/Job24:9); [MAT 24:19](https://ref.ly/Matt24:19) parallels [MRK 13:17](https://ref.ly/Mark13:17), [LUK 21:23](https://ref.ly/Luke21:23) *The implication is that children nursing at their mothers' breasts are completely vulnerable and defenceless.*

### Other creatures breast\-feed their young

**[LAM 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lam4:3)**

Female breasts are attractive
-----------------------------

**[SNG 8:10](https://ref.ly/Song8:10)** *See also* [PRO 5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:19); [SNG 4:5](https://ref.ly/Song4:5), [SNG 7:3](https://ref.ly/Song7:3), [SNG 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Song7:7-Song7:8)

### Breasts are a source of temptation

[PRO 5:20](https://ref.ly/Prov5:20)

### Undeveloped breasts are a sign of youth

[SNG 8:8](https://ref.ly/Song8:8)

The figurative breasts of Israel
--------------------------------

### Jerusalem as a breasted woman

**[EZK 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:7)**

### Jerusalem as a nursing mother

**[ISA 66:10–66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:10-Isa66:12)**

### Jerusalem as a prostitute

**[EZK 23:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:3)** *The two sisters are Samaria and Jerusalem.* *See also* [EZK 23:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:8), [EZK 23:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:21); [HOS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2) *Gomer's unfaithfulness is likened to that of Israel.*

### Dry breasts are a sign of God's punishment

**[HOS 9:14](https://ref.ly/Hos9:14)**

God's care exceeds that of nursing mothers
------------------------------------------

**[ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)** *See also* [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23), [ISA 60:16](https://ref.ly/Isa60:16) *The Gentiles will be used by God to provide nursing for Israel.*

Beating of breasts
------------------

### As a sign of grief

**[NAM 2:7](https://ref.ly/Nah2:7)** *See also* [ISA 32:12–32:13](https://ref.ly/Isa32:12-Isa32:13); [EZK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:12), [EZK 23:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:34); [LUK 23:48](https://ref.ly/Luke23:48)

### As a sign of contrition

**[LUK 18:13](https://ref.ly/Luke18:13)** *See also* [JER 31:19](https://ref.ly/Jer31:19)

Protection of the breast
------------------------

### The high priest's breastpiece

[EXO 25:7](https://ref.ly/Exod25:7), [EXO 28:15–28:30](https://ref.ly/Exod28:15-Exod28:30), [EXO 29:5](https://ref.ly/Exod29:5), [EXO 35:9](https://ref.ly/Exod35:9), [EXO 35:27](https://ref.ly/Exod35:27), [EXO 39:8–39:21](https://ref.ly/Exod39:8-Exod39:21); ; [LEV 8:8](https://ref.ly/Lev8:8)

### Breastplates worn for protection in confrontations

[REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Rev9:9), [REV 9:17](https://ref.ly/Rev9:17)

### Figurative breastplates as spiritual armour

**[EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:14)** *See also* [ISA 59:17](https://ref.ly/Isa59:17); [1TH 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:8)

Animal breasts used in sacrificial offerings
--------------------------------------------

## 5141: cheeks (id: 5141)

Literally, the parts either side of the face. Striking the cheek is an extreme form of insult.

Cheeks as features of beauty
----------------------------

**[SNG 1:10](https://ref.ly/Song1:10)** *See also* [SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13)

Cheeks can bear symbols of grief
--------------------------------

**[LAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Lam1:2)**

Striking the cheek is a form of insult or challenge
---------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 16:10](https://ref.ly/Job16:10)**; **[MAT 5:39](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39)** *See also* [LAM 3:30](https://ref.ly/Lam3:30); [MIC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Mic5:1); [MAT 26:67](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67); [JHN 18:22–18:23](https://ref.ly/John18:22-John18:23); [LUK 6:29](https://ref.ly/Luke6:29)

Plucking the beard from the cheek is a form of insult
-----------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6)** *See also* [2SA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4-2Sam10:5) parallel [1CH 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:4-1Chr19:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Song of Songs 1:10; Song of Songs 5:13; Lamentations 1:2; Job 16:10; Matthew 5:39; Lamentations 3:30; Micah 5:1; Matthew 26:67; John 18:22-23; Luke 6:29; Isaiah 50:6; 2 Samuel 10:4-5; 1 Chronicles 19:4-5
* **Associated Articles:** 2570: Jesus Christ, suffering of (ID: `2570@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5130: beard (ID: `5130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5149: eyes (ID: `5149@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5150: face (ID: `5150@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5865: gestures (ID: `5865@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5893: insults (ID: `5893@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5142: clapping (id: 5142)

Applauding with one's hands, as an expression of delight. Scripture portrays even the creation sharing in this delight at the majesty of its Creator.

------------------------------------

### At a coronation

**[2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)**

### Expressing praise

**[PSA 47:1](https://ref.ly/Ps47:1)**

### Expressing the delight of the creation in its Creator

**[PSA 98:8](https://ref.ly/Ps98:8)** *See also* [ISA 55:12](https://ref.ly/Isa55:12)

Clapping as an act of derision
------------------------------

**[LAM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Lam2:15)** *See also* [JOB 27:21–27:23](https://ref.ly/Job27:21-Job27:23), [JOB 34:37](https://ref.ly/Job34:37); [EZK 25:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:6); [NAM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:19)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 11:12; Psalms 47:1; Psalms 98:8; Isaiah 55:12; Lamentations 2:15; Job 27:21-23; Job 34:37; Ezekiel 25:6; Nahum 3:19
* **Associated Articles:** 5156: hand (ID: `5156@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5830: delight (ID: `5830@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8283: joy (ID: `8283@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

## 5144: cloak (id: 5144)

A protective outer garment, often treated as a symbol of authority.

---------------------------

**[ACT 12:8](https://ref.ly/Acts12:8)** *See also* [GEN 39:12](https://ref.ly/Gen39:12), [GEN 39:18](https://ref.ly/Gen39:18); [1KI 19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:13); [SNG 5:7](https://ref.ly/Song5:7); [MAT 5:40](https://ref.ly/Matt5:40) parallel [LUK 6:29](https://ref.ly/Luke6:29); [MAT 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Matt9:20-Matt9:21) parallels [MRK 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Mark5:27-Mark5:28), [LUK 8:44](https://ref.ly/Luke8:44) *A woman's bleeding is stopped*; [MAT 14:36](https://ref.ly/Matt14:36) parallel [MRK 6:56](https://ref.ly/Mark6:56); [MAT 24:18](https://ref.ly/Matt24:18) parallel [MRK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Mark13:16); [ACT 22:22–22:24](https://ref.ly/Acts22:22-Acts22:24); [2TI 4:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:13)

## 5145: clothing (id: 5145)

Garments were originally provided by God for Adam and Eve. Kings and priests were given special garments. God's people should neither be unduly anxious about how they dress, nor judge others by what they wear. Inner clothing is more important than outer adornment.

The origin of clothing
----------------------

**[GEN 3:21](https://ref.ly/Gen3:21)**

Various regulations regarding clothing
--------------------------------------

*See also* [LEV 15:1–15:27](https://ref.ly/Lev15:1-Lev15:27); [DEU 22:5](https://ref.ly/Deut22:5), [DEU 22:11–22:12](https://ref.ly/Deut22:11-Deut22:12)

Special clothing for priests
----------------------------

[EXO 28:1–28:5](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1-Exod28:5), [EXO 29:5–29:6](https://ref.ly/Exod29:5-Exod29:6), [EXO 39:1](https://ref.ly/Exod39:1); ; [LEV 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:7-Lev8:9); ; [EZK 44:17–44:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:17-Ezek44:19)

Rich clothing a sign of wealth or status
----------------------------------------

[GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42); ; [2SA 13:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:18); ; [EST 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Esth6:8-Esth6:9), [EST 8:15](https://ref.ly/Esth8:15); ; [PSA 45:13–45:14](https://ref.ly/Ps45:13-Ps45:14); ; [DAN 5:7](https://ref.ly/Dan5:7); ; [LUK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19); ; [ACT 12:21](https://ref.ly/Acts12:21)

The Israelites' clothing did not wear out in the desert wanderings

* **Associated Articles:** 4645: fleece (ID: `4645@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4807: colours (ID: `4807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5131: belt (ID: `5131@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5144: cloak (ID: `5144@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5158: head-covering (ID: `5158@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5177: robes (ID: `5177@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5179: sandal (ID: `5179@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5188: tearing of clothes (ID: `5188@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5258: cloth (ID: `5258@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5915: ornaments (ID: `5915@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6742: sackcloth and ashes (ID: `6742@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7478: washing (ID: `7478@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5146: covering (id: 5146)

That which literally or metaphorically hides from the human or divine eye what should not be seen.

Covering for the human body
---------------------------

**[GEN 3:7](https://ref.ly/Gen3:7)**; **[EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8)** *See also* [GEN 9:23](https://ref.ly/Gen9:23); [EXO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26-Exod22:27), [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42); [ISA 47:3](https://ref.ly/Isa47:3), [ISA 57:8](https://ref.ly/Isa57:8); [EZK 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:10); [HOS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hos2:9); [JON 3:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:6); [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)

Covering as a sign of submission or humility
--------------------------------------------

**[ISA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Isa6:2)** *See also* [GEN 24:65](https://ref.ly/Gen24:65); [1CO 11:5–11:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:5-1Cor11:15) *Some suggest that this passage refers either to a woman's modesty or to her dignity considering her new Christian status of equality with man.*

The covering of human sin through atonement
-------------------------------------------

**[PSA 32:1–32:2](https://ref.ly/Ps32:1-Ps32:2)** *See also* [GEN 20:16](https://ref.ly/Gen20:16); [LEV 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lev4:20) *The word translated “atonement” literally means “covering”*; [NEH 4:5](https://ref.ly/Neh4:5); [JOB 14:17](https://ref.ly/Job14:17); [PSA 32:5](https://ref.ly/Ps32:5), [PSA 85:2](https://ref.ly/Ps85:2); [PRO 17:9](https://ref.ly/Prov17:9); [ROM 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Rom4:7-Rom4:8); [JAS 5:20](https://ref.ly/Jas5:20); [1PE 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:8)

Natural phenomena as God's covering
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 18:11](https://ref.ly/Ps18:11)** *See also* [EXO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Exod19:18); [NUM 9:15–9:16](https://ref.ly/Num9:15-Num9:16); [PSA 105:39](https://ref.ly/Ps105:39)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 3:7; Ezekiel 16:8; Genesis 9:23; Exodus 22:26-27; Exodus 28:42; Isaiah 47:3; Isaiah 57:8; Ezekiel 16:10; Hosea 2:9; Jonah 3:6; Revelation 3:18; Isaiah 6:2; Genesis 24:65; 1 Corinthians 11:5-15; Psalms 32:1-2; Genesis 20:16; Leviticus 4:20; Nehemiah 4:5; Job 14:17; Psalms 32:5; Psalms 85:2; Proverbs 17:9; Romans 4:7-8; James 5:20; 1 Peter 4:8; Psalms 18:11; Exodus 19:18; Numbers 9:15-16; Psalms 105:39
* **Associated Articles:** 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5158: head-covering (ID: `5158@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5169: nakedness (ID: `5169@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5195: veil (ID: `5195@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5258: cloth (ID: `5258@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5812: concealment (ID: `5812@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6614: atonement (ID: `6614@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5147: deafness (id: 5147)

The condition of being wholly, or partly, without hearing. It can be expressed in physical or spiritual terms and is often used to depict spiritual rebellion.

God's control of deafness
-------------------------

**[EXO 4:11](https://ref.ly/Exod4:11)** *See also* [PSA 94:9](https://ref.ly/Ps94:9); [PRO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Prov20:12); [LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20), [LUK 1:62](https://ref.ly/Luke1:62)

Physical aspects of deafness
----------------------------

### Compassion for the deaf

**[LEV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Lev19:14)**

### Deafness sometimes caused by Satan

**[MRK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25)**

### Healed by Jesus Christ

**[MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5)** parallel [LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22) *See also* [MRK 7:32–7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32-Mark7:37); [LUK 22:49–22:51](https://ref.ly/Luke22:49-Luke22:51)

Spiritual aspects of deafness
-----------------------------

### God deaf to a rebellious people

**[DEU 1:45](https://ref.ly/Deut1:45)** *See also* [PSA 28:1](https://ref.ly/Ps28:1), [PSA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Ps38:13), [PSA 39:12](https://ref.ly/Ps39:12)

### Rebellious humanity deaf to God

**[JER 6:10](https://ref.ly/Jer6:10)** *See also* [PRO 28:9](https://ref.ly/Prov28:9); [ISA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:10), [ISA 42:19–42:20](https://ref.ly/Isa42:19-Isa42:20); [EZK 33:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:32), [EZK 37:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:4); [MIC 7:16](https://ref.ly/Mic7:16); [ZEC 7:11](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11); [MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15); [MRK 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:9-Mark4:12); [LUK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:10); [JHN 12:40](https://ref.ly/John12:40); [ACT 28:26–28:27](https://ref.ly/Acts28:26-Acts28:27); [2TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:4); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7) *Sin brings spiritual deafness.*

The deafness of idols
---------------------

**[DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28)** *See also* [PSA 115:6](https://ref.ly/Ps115:6), [PSA 135:17](https://ref.ly/Ps135:17); [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23); [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20)

Release from deafness
---------------------

### Through repentance

**[ISA 29:18](https://ref.ly/Isa29:18)** *See also* [ISA 35:5](https://ref.ly/Isa35:5), [ISA 42:18](https://ref.ly/Isa42:18)

### Through the coming of Jesus Christ

**[LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22)** parallel [MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5) *See also* [MRK 7:32–7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32-Mark7:37)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 4:11; Psalms 94:9; Proverbs 20:12; Luke 1:20; Luke 1:62; Leviticus 19:14; Mark 9:25; Matthew 11:5; Luke 7:22; Mark 7:32-37; Luke 22:49-51; Deuteronomy 1:45; Psalms 28:1; Psalms 38:13; Psalms 39:12; Jeremiah 6:10; Proverbs 28:9; Isaiah 6:10; Isaiah 42:19-20; Ezekiel 33:32; Ezekiel 37:4; Micah 7:16; Zechariah 7:11; Matthew 13:15; Mark 4:9-12; Luke 8:10; John 12:40; Acts 28:26-27; 2 Timothy 4:4; Revelation 2:7; Deuteronomy 4:28; Psalms 115:6; Psalms 135:17; Daniel 5:23; Revelation 9:20; Isaiah 29:18; Isaiah 35:5; Isaiah 42:18
* **Associated Articles:** 2351: Jesus Christ, miracles of (ID: `2351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5148: ear (ID: `5148@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5159: hearing (ID: `5159@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5165: listening (ID: `5165@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5168: muteness (ID: `5168@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5806: compassion (ID: `5806@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6178: hardness of heart (ID: `6178@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6222: rebellion, against God (ID: `6222@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8351: teachableness (ID: `8351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5148: ear (id: 5148)

The organ of hearing; the ear is referred to in various OT rites and is a symbol of attention, alertness or readiness to hear.

The ear in the OT rites
-----------------------

### Piercing the ear: a symbol of servanthood

**[PSA 40:6](https://ref.ly/Ps40:6)** *See also* [EXO 21:5–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:5-Exod21:6); [DEU 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Deut15:16-Deut15:17)

### Blood applied to the ear: a symbol of cleansing and ordination

**[LEV 8:23](https://ref.ly/Lev8:23)** *See also* [EXO 29:20](https://ref.ly/Exod29:20); [LEV 14:14](https://ref.ly/Lev14:14)

God's ear as a symbol of his alertness to humanity
--------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 116:1–116:2](https://ref.ly/Ps116:1-Ps116:2)** *See also* [2KI 19:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:16); [2CH 7:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:15); [NEH 1:6](https://ref.ly/Neh1:6)JOB [34:28](https://ref.ly/Neh34:28); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)[PSA 5:1](https://ref.ly/Ps5:1), [PSA 31:2](https://ref.ly/Ps31:2), [PSA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Ps34:15), [PSA 88:2](https://ref.ly/Ps88:2), [PSA 130:1–130:2](https://ref.ly/Ps130:1-Ps130:2); [DAN 9:18](https://ref.ly/Dan9:18)

God's ear may be turned away from humanity
------------------------------------------

**[PSA 28:1](https://ref.ly/Ps28:1)** *The refusal to turn an ear towards someone was a symbol of refusing to listen.* *See also* [DEU 1:45](https://ref.ly/Deut1:45); [LAM 3:56](https://ref.ly/Lam3:56); [EZK 8:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:18) *God's attending ear is partly dependent on human attitudes to him.*

The human ear used symbolically
-------------------------------

### Human ears should be open to God

**[ISA 50:4–50:5](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4-Isa50:5), [ISA 55:3](https://ref.ly/Isa55:3)** *See also* [JOB 42:5](https://ref.ly/Job42:5); [PSA 81:8](https://ref.ly/Ps81:8); [ISA 30:21](https://ref.ly/Isa30:21), [ISA 32:3](https://ref.ly/Isa32:3); [MAT 13:9](https://ref.ly/Matt13:9) parallels [MRK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Mark4:9), [LUK 8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:8) *“He who has ears” implies a potential willingness to listen*; [MAT 13:16](https://ref.ly/Matt13:16); [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7)

### Human ears may be closed against God

**[ZEC 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11-Zech7:13)** *See also* [PSA 58:4–58:5](https://ref.ly/Ps58:4-Ps58:5); [ISA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:10)

### The human ear should also be open to others

[ISA 48:8](https://ref.ly/Isa48:8); ; [JER 6:10](https://ref.ly/Jer6:10); ; [EZK 12:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:2); ; [ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51), [ACT 7:57](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57); ; [2TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:4)**[PRO 21:13](https://ref.ly/Prov21:13)**

A discerning ear brings knowledge and understanding
---------------------------------------------------

*See also* [JOB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Job12:11), [JOB 13:1](https://ref.ly/Job13:1), [JOB 34:3](https://ref.ly/Job34:3); [PRO 18:15](https://ref.ly/Prov18:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 40:6; Exodus 21:5-6; Deuteronomy 15:16-17; Leviticus 8:23; Exodus 29:20; Leviticus 14:14; Psalms 116:1-2; 2 Kings 19:16; 2 Chronicles 7:15; Nehemiah 1:6; Nehemiah 34:28; James 5:4; Psalms 5:1; Psalms 31:2; Psalms 34:15; Psalms 88:2; Psalms 130:1-2; Daniel 9:18; Psalms 28:1; Deuteronomy 1:45; Lamentations 3:56; Ezekiel 8:18; Isaiah 50:4-5; Isaiah 55:3; Job 42:5; Psalms 81:8; Isaiah 30:21; Isaiah 32:3; Matthew 13:9; Mark 4:9; Luke 8:8; Matthew 13:16; Mark 9:7; Revelation 2:7; Zechariah 7:11-13; Psalms 58:4-5; Isaiah 6:10; Isaiah 48:8; Jeremiah 6:10; Ezekiel 12:2; Acts 7:51; Acts 7:57; 2 Timothy 4:4; Proverbs 21:13; Job 12:11; Job 13:1; Job 34:3; Proverbs 18:15
* **Associated Articles:** 1210: God, human descriptions of (ID: `1210@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5147: deafness (ID: `5147@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5159: hearing (ID: `5159@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5165: listening (ID: `5165@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5806: compassion (ID: `5806@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5865: gestures (ID: `5865@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5946: sensitivity (ID: `5946@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7745: ordination (ID: `7745@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8226: discernment (ID: `8226@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5149: eyes (id: 5149)

The part of the body designed for seeing. In Scripture eyes are often associated with light, watchfulness or spiritual understanding, whereas blindness may be a metaphor for darkness, ignorance of God or rebellion against him. The eyes of God watch over all his creation.

Eyes are part of the physical body
----------------------------------

[DEU 28:65](https://ref.ly/Deut28:65); ; [JOB 11:20](https://ref.ly/Job11:20), [JOB 31:16](https://ref.ly/Job31:16); ; [PSA 69:3](https://ref.ly/Ps69:3)

### Eyes may represent the whole person

[LUK 10:23](https://ref.ly/Luke10:23); ; [REV 1:7](https://ref.ly/Rev1:7)

### Eyesight is a vital sense

**[MAT 6:22](https://ref.ly/Matt6:22)** *See also* [PSA 54:7](https://ref.ly/Ps54:7); [PRO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Prov20:12); [LAM 4:17](https://ref.ly/Lam4:17); [MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5) parallel [LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22); [MAT 20:33](https://ref.ly/Matt20:33) parallels [MRK 10:51](https://ref.ly/Mark10:51), [LUK 18:41](https://ref.ly/Luke18:41); [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/Acts28:27); [1CO 12:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:17)

### Eyes may have a powerful emotional effect

**[SNG 4:9](https://ref.ly/Song4:9)**

### Eyes reflect character, feelings and emotions

Malice: [PSA 35:19](https://ref.ly/Ps35:19); ; [PRO 6:13](https://ref.ly/Prov6:13), [PRO 10:10](https://ref.ly/Prov10:10), [PRO 16:30](https://ref.ly/Prov16:30); [PSA 101:5](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5); ; [PRO 21:4](https://ref.ly/Prov21:4); [MRK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mark3:5) *anger*; ; [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21) *love*; [PSA 88:9](https://ref.ly/Ps88:9); ; [JHN 11:33–11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:33-John11:35); [2PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:14); ; [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

God's eyes are upon all his creation
------------------------------------

### God is aware of everything

**[JOB 34:21](https://ref.ly/Job34:21)** *See also* [1SA 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:7); [PSA 94:9](https://ref.ly/Ps94:9), [PSA 139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:16); [PRO 15:3](https://ref.ly/Prov15:3); [AMO 9:8](https://ref.ly/Amos9:8); [HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13); [ZEC 3:9](https://ref.ly/Zech3:9), [ZEC 4:10](https://ref.ly/Zech4:10); [HEB 4:13](https://ref.ly/Heb4:13)

### God watches over his people with care

**[PSA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Ps34:15)** *See also* [DEU 11:12](https://ref.ly/Deut11:12); [1KI 8:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:29), [1KI 9:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:3); [PSA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Ps1:6), [PSA 121:3–121:5](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:5); [PRO 22:12](https://ref.ly/Prov22:12); [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12)

### God's eyes symbolise his assessment of human behaviour

**[GEN 6:8](https://ref.ly/Gen6:8)** *See also* [EXO 15:26](https://ref.ly/Exod15:26); [JDG 2:11](https://ref.ly/Judg2:11); [1KI 15:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:5); [2CO 8:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:21)

Human eyes may symbolise spiritual vision
-----------------------------------------

### God opens eyes in a spiritual sense

**[PSA 119:18](https://ref.ly/Ps119:18)** *The “opening of eyes” is an important biblical metaphor for revelation*; **[EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eph1:18)** *See also* [NUM 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Num24:3-Num24:4); [2KI 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:17); [EZR 9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:8); [ISA 35:5](https://ref.ly/Isa35:5), [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [MAT 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Matt6:22-Matt6:23) parallel [LUK 11:34–11:36](https://ref.ly/Luke11:34-Luke11:36); [ACT 26:18](https://ref.ly/Acts26:18); [HEB 11:27](https://ref.ly/Heb11:27)

### Human eyes should be directed towards God

**[PSA 34:5](https://ref.ly/Ps34:5), [PSA 123:1–123:2](https://ref.ly/Ps123:1-Ps123:2)** *Lifting one's eyes towards God is an important biblical image of prayer.* *See also* [2CH 20:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:12); [PSA 25:15](https://ref.ly/Ps25:15), [PSA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4); [ISA 40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:26); [JHN 17:1](https://ref.ly/John17:1); [ACT 7:55](https://ref.ly/Acts7:55); [2CO 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:16); [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)

Human eyes will ultimately see God
----------------------------------

**[JOB 19:25–19:27](https://ref.ly/Job19:25-Job19:27)** *See also* [ISA 33:17](https://ref.ly/Isa33:17); [JHN 17:24](https://ref.ly/John17:24); [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:12); [1JN 3:2](https://ref.ly/1John3:2); [REV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Rev22:4)

Eyes are a channel of temptation
--------------------------------

**[MAT 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8-Matt4:9)** parallel [LUK 4:5–4:7](https://ref.ly/Luke4:5-Luke4:7) *See also* [GEN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6); [NUM 15:39](https://ref.ly/Num15:39); [JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21); [2SA 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2); [PSA 119:37](https://ref.ly/Ps119:37); [ECC 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:10), [ECC 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:9); [MAT 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28-Matt5:29); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

### Eyes should be guarded against evil

**[JOB 31:1](https://ref.ly/Job31:1)**; **[PRO 4:25](https://ref.ly/Prov4:25)** *See also* [PSA 101:3](https://ref.ly/Ps101:3); [ISA 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:15-Isa33:16) *The direction of human eyes is a matter of choice.*

Closing one's eyes may symbolise indifference to God and his will
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15)** *See also* [ISA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:10); [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27); [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26); [MAT 23:16–23:17](https://ref.ly/Matt23:16-Matt23:17); [JHN 9:41](https://ref.ly/John9:41)

## 5150: face (id: 5150)

The main visual characteristic of a person. Facial features are recognisable as uniquely those of a particular person. The face also indicates mood and attitude. The showing or directing of the face towards someone or something may indicate regard, approval or determination, while hiding or turning away of the face indicates the opposite.

The face is the main recognisable feature of a person
-----------------------------------------------------

### Examples of facial recognition

**[MAT 26:71](https://ref.ly/Matt26:71)** parallels [MRK 14:66–14:67](https://ref.ly/Mark14:66-Mark14:67), [LUK 22:56](https://ref.ly/Luke22:56) *See also* [EST 5:2](https://ref.ly/Esth5:2), [EST 5:9](https://ref.ly/Esth5:9); [MAT 13:55](https://ref.ly/Matt13:55) parallel [MRK 6:3](https://ref.ly/Mark6:3); [MAT 14:35](https://ref.ly/Matt14:35) parallel [MRK 6:54](https://ref.ly/Mark6:54); [JHN 1:36](https://ref.ly/John1:36), [JHN 1:47–1:48](https://ref.ly/John1:47-John1:48)

### Examples of failure to recognise faces

**[GEN 42:8](https://ref.ly/Gen42:8)** *See also* [GEN 38:15](https://ref.ly/Gen38:15); [LUK 24:16](https://ref.ly/Luke24:16); [JHN 20:14](https://ref.ly/John20:14) *There is no evidence to suggest that Jesus Christ's post\-resurrection face was different.*

### The face may be used symbolically to represent the whole person

[DEU 7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:10)

The physical appearance of faces
--------------------------------

### Attractive faces

**[SNG 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Song1:15-Song1:16)** *See also* [1SA 16:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:12); [ECC 8:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:1); [DAN 1:4](https://ref.ly/Dan1:4)

### Disfigured faces

**[ISA 52:14](https://ref.ly/Isa52:14)** *See also* [JOB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Job2:12); [MAT 27:29–27:30](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29-Matt27:30) parallels [MRK 14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:65), [LUK 22:63](https://ref.ly/Luke22:63); [JHN 18:22](https://ref.ly/John18:22)

Facial expressions
------------------

### The face expresses a person's mood

**[GEN 4:5](https://ref.ly/Gen4:5)** *See also* [GEN 21:6](https://ref.ly/Gen21:6); [1SA 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:18); [JOB 29:24](https://ref.ly/Job29:24); [DAN 5:6](https://ref.ly/Dan5:6), [DAN 5:9](https://ref.ly/Dan5:9); [LUK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Luke24:17); [JHN 20:15](https://ref.ly/John20:15)

### Averting the face may express worship, respect, modesty or shame

**[EXO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:6)** *See also* [GEN 24:65](https://ref.ly/Gen24:65); [RUT 2:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:10); [1SA 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:3-1Sam5:4); [1KI 18:42](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:42); [1CH 21:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:21); [JOB 11:15](https://ref.ly/Job11:15); [PSA 83:16](https://ref.ly/Ps83:16); [ISA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Isa6:2); [JER 13:26](https://ref.ly/Jer13:26); [LUK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12)

### Setting the face expresses determination

[LUK 9:51](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51) *the literal translation is “set his face to go”*

Other gestures involving the human face
---------------------------------------

### Hiding the face from someone as a sign of loss of favour

[DEU 31:17–31:18](https://ref.ly/Deut31:17-Deut31:18); ; [JOB 13:24](https://ref.ly/Job13:24)

### Raising the face as a sign of lack of shame

[JOB 11:15](https://ref.ly/Job11:15)

### Slapping someone's face as a challenge or insult

[1KI 22:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:24); ; [JHN 18:22](https://ref.ly/John18:22)

### Spitting in someone's face as an insult

[DEU 25:9](https://ref.ly/Deut25:9); ; [MAT 26:67](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67)

Seeing someone's face
---------------------

### An indicator of acceptance

**[GEN 33:10](https://ref.ly/Gen33:10)** *See also* [GEN 43:3–43:4](https://ref.ly/Gen43:3-Gen43:4), [GEN 44:23–44:26](https://ref.ly/Gen44:23-Gen44:26); [DEU 34:10](https://ref.ly/Deut34:10)

### Indicating confrontation

**[GAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11)** *See also* [2SA 14:24–14:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:24-2Sam14:32); [2KI 14:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:8)

### Not seeing someone's face may indicate rejection

[EXO 10:28](https://ref.ly/Exod10:28)

Examples of supernatural facial appearance
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2)** parallels [MRK 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Mark9:2-Mark9:3), [LUK 9:29](https://ref.ly/Luke9:29) *See also* [EXO 34:30](https://ref.ly/Exod34:30); [MAT 28:3](https://ref.ly/Matt28:3) parallels [MRK 16:5](https://ref.ly/Mark16:5), [LUK 24:4](https://ref.ly/Luke24:4); [ACT 6:15](https://ref.ly/Acts6:15); [2CO 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:7-2Cor3:8), [2CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:18); [REV 1:14](https://ref.ly/Rev1:14), [REV 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rev1:16)

The face of God
---------------

### As a symbol of blessing

**[NUM 6:25–6:26](https://ref.ly/Num6:25-Num6:26)** *See also* [2SA 21:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1); [1CH 16:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:11); [PSA 4:6](https://ref.ly/Ps4:6), [PSA 31:16](https://ref.ly/Ps31:16), [PSA 67:1](https://ref.ly/Ps67:1), [PSA 80:19](https://ref.ly/Ps80:19), [PSA 105:4](https://ref.ly/Ps105:4), [PSA 119:135](https://ref.ly/Ps119:135); [EZK 39:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:29)

### Seeking the LORD's face means seeking his will

[2CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14); ; [PSA 27:8](https://ref.ly/Ps27:8); ; [HOS 5:15](https://ref.ly/Hos5:15)

### God's face may be turned away, hidden or set against people as a sign of opposition or removal from favour

**[DEU 31:17](https://ref.ly/Deut31:17)** *See also* [LEV 17:10](https://ref.ly/Lev17:10); [2CH 30:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:9); [PSA 44:24](https://ref.ly/Ps44:24); [ISA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:15), [ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2), [ISA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Isa64:7); [EZK 14:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:8), [EZK 39:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:23); [MIC 3:4](https://ref.ly/Mic3:4); [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12); [PSA 34:15–34:16](https://ref.ly/Ps34:15-Ps34:16)

### People cannot normally see God's face

**[EXO 33:20](https://ref.ly/Exod33:20)** *See also* [GEN 32:30](https://ref.ly/Gen32:30); [DEU 34:10](https://ref.ly/Deut34:10); [JOB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Job13:15), [JOB 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/Job19:26-Job19:27); [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:12); [REV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Rev22:4) *In the end all human beings will see God face to face.***[ISA 50:7](https://ref.ly/Isa50:7)**

\`\`Face of the earth'' indicates the earth's total area
--------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [GEN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Gen6:7), [GEN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:4); [DEU 6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:15), [DEU 14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:2); [PSA 104:30](https://ref.ly/Ps104:30); [ZEP 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:2-Zeph1:3); [LUK 21:35](https://ref.ly/Luke21:35)

## 5151: feet (id: 5151)

The part of the body associated with moving and with foundation, direction and chosen path. Feet may symbolise the response to God's direction. The feet of God and of Jesus Christ are mentioned symbolically to describe some divine characteristics.

Feet as part of the physical body
---------------------------------

### Human feet

[EXO 30:19–30:21](https://ref.ly/Exod30:19-Exod30:21); ; [LEV 8:23](https://ref.ly/Lev8:23); ; [DEU 8:4](https://ref.ly/Deut8:4); ; [JOS 14:9](https://ref.ly/Josh14:9); ; [2SA 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:4); ; [1KI 15:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:23); ; [SNG 7:1](https://ref.ly/Song7:1); ; [EZK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:11), [EZK 25:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:6); ; [LUK 7:44](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44); ; [ACT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Acts3:7)

### The feet of creatures and objects of worship

[GEN 8:9](https://ref.ly/Gen8:9); ; [EXO 37:3](https://ref.ly/Exod37:3); ; [EZK 1:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:7); ; [DAN 2:33](https://ref.ly/Dan2:33)

A whole person may be represented by the foot
---------------------------------------------

[JOB 28:4](https://ref.ly/Job28:4); ; [PSA 36:11](https://ref.ly/Ps36:11); ; [PRO 6:18](https://ref.ly/Prov6:18); ; [ISA 59:7](https://ref.ly/Isa59:7)

Symbolic references to human feet
---------------------------------

### In relation to guidance, direction and following

**[PSA 17:5](https://ref.ly/Ps17:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:31](https://ref.ly/Ps37:31), [PSA 44:18](https://ref.ly/Ps44:18), [PSA 56:13](https://ref.ly/Ps56:13), [PSA 119:101](https://ref.ly/Ps119:101), [PSA 119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105); [PRO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Prov3:6), [PRO 4:26–4:27](https://ref.ly/Prov4:26-Prov4:27); [LUK 1:79](https://ref.ly/Luke1:79)

### In relation to disobedience and waywardness

**[PSA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1)** *See also* [2KI 21:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:22); [JOB 31:5](https://ref.ly/Job31:5); [PRO 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Prov1:15-Prov1:16), [PRO 4:14](https://ref.ly/Prov4:14), [PRO 6:18](https://ref.ly/Prov6:18); [ISA 59:7](https://ref.ly/Isa59:7); [MAT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Matt18:8) parallel [MRK 9:45](https://ref.ly/Mark9:45)

### In relation to a secure standing with God

**[PSA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Ps40:2)** *See also* [1SA 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9); [2SA 22:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:34) parallel [PSA 18:33](https://ref.ly/Ps18:33); [PSA 31:8](https://ref.ly/Ps31:8), [PSA 66:9](https://ref.ly/Ps66:9); [PRO 4:26](https://ref.ly/Prov4:26); [HAB 3:19](https://ref.ly/Hab3:19); [1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58); [EPH 6:13](https://ref.ly/Eph6:13)

### In relation to bringing the gospel

**[ISA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7)** *See also* [NAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Nah1:15); [ROM 10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:15); [EPH 6:15](https://ref.ly/Eph6:15)

### In relation to expressing attitudes and emotions

[JOS 10:24](https://ref.ly/Josh10:24) *defeat and humiliation*; ; [RUT 3:4–3:8](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:4-Ruth3:8) *requesting marriage and redemption*; ; [1SA 25:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:24) *supplication and homage*; [1SA 25:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:41); ; [LUK 7:44](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44); ; [JHN 13:5](https://ref.ly/John13:5); [2SA 22:39–22:40](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:39-2Sam22:40) parallel [PSA 18:38–18:39](https://ref.ly/Ps18:38-Ps18:39); ; [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17) *mourning*; ; [MAT 10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:14) parallels [MRK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:11), [LUK 9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:5) *scorn and a warning to those who reject God's message*; [ACT 4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:35), [ACT 10:25](https://ref.ly/Acts10:25); [HEB 10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:29) *rejection*

\`\`Jesus' feet'' symbolises the source and place of God's grace
----------------------------------------------------------------

### Worshippers come to Jesus Christ's feet

**[LUK 8:35](https://ref.ly/Luke8:35)** *See also* [MAT 28:9](https://ref.ly/Matt28:9)

### The sinful come to Jesus Christ's feet

**[LUK 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/Luke7:37-Luke7:38)** *See also* [JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3)

### The suffering come to Jesus Christ's feet

**[MAT 15:30](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30)** *See also* [MRK 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22-Mark5:23) parallels [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18), [LUK 8:41](https://ref.ly/Luke8:41); [MRK 7:25](https://ref.ly/Mark7:25) parallel [MAT 15:25](https://ref.ly/Matt15:25); [JHN 11:32](https://ref.ly/John11:32)

### People sit at Jesus Christ's feet to learn

**[LUK 10:39](https://ref.ly/Luke10:39)** *It was common practice for disciples to gather around their teacher in this way.* *See also* [JHN 8:2](https://ref.ly/John8:2)

### Ultimately all will come to Jesus Christ's feet in worship

[ROM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rom14:11); ; [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23); ; [PHP 2:10](https://ref.ly/Phil2:10)

\`\`Feet of God'' symbolises his majestic presence
--------------------------------------------------

**[ZEC 14:4](https://ref.ly/Zech14:4)** *See also* [EXO 24:10](https://ref.ly/Exod24:10); [ISA 66:1](https://ref.ly/Isa66:1); [NAM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Nah1:3); [MAT 5:35](https://ref.ly/Matt5:35); [ACT 7:49](https://ref.ly/Acts7:49); [REV 10:2](https://ref.ly/Rev10:2)

### God's feet are referred to in relation to worship

[PSA 99:5](https://ref.ly/Ps99:5), [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1), [PSA 132:7](https://ref.ly/Ps132:7); ; [REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17)

\`\`Beneath/under the feet'' to denote the defeat and subservience of enemies
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[JOS 10:24](https://ref.ly/Josh10:24); ; [2SA 22:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:39); ; [1KI 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:3); ; [PSA 8:6](https://ref.ly/Ps8:6), [PSA 45:5](https://ref.ly/Ps45:5), [PSA 47:3](https://ref.ly/Ps47:3); ; [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23); ; [LAM 3:34](https://ref.ly/Lam3:34); ; [MAL 4:3](https://ref.ly/Mal4:3); ; [MAT 22:42–22:45](https://ref.ly/Matt22:42-Matt22:45); ; [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1); ; [ACT 2:34–2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:34-Acts2:36); ; [ROM 16:20](https://ref.ly/Rom16:20); ; [1CO 15:25–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:25-1Cor15:28); ; [EPH 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22-Eph1:23); ; [HEB 10:13](https://ref.ly/Heb10:13); ; [REV 3:9](https://ref.ly/Rev3:9)

## 5152: fingers (id: 5152)

Parts of the hand used in various kinds of activity, both good and bad. They were prominent in the OT sacrificial ritual, and were also used as a symbol for authority, accusation and minimum effort.

Regular activities using the fingers
------------------------------------

[PSA 144:1](https://ref.ly/Ps144:1); ; [PRO 31:19](https://ref.ly/Prov31:19); ; [SNG 5:5](https://ref.ly/Song5:5)

Fingers used in sacrificial ritual
----------------------------------

### In the consecration of priests

**[EXO 29:12](https://ref.ly/Exod29:12)** *See also* [LEV 8:15](https://ref.ly/Lev8:15)

### In the ritual for the sin offering

**[LEV 4:6](https://ref.ly/Lev4:6)** *See also* [LEV 4:17](https://ref.ly/Lev4:17), [LEV 4:25](https://ref.ly/Lev4:25), [LEV 4:30](https://ref.ly/Lev4:30), [LEV 4:34](https://ref.ly/Lev4:34), [LEV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Lev9:9)

### In the ritual of the Day of Atonement

[LEV 16:14](https://ref.ly/Lev16:14), [LEV 16:19](https://ref.ly/Lev16:19)

### In cleansing rituals

[LEV 14:16](https://ref.ly/Lev14:16); ; [NUM 19:4](https://ref.ly/Num19:4)

The finger as a unit of measurement
-----------------------------------

[JER 52:21](https://ref.ly/Jer52:21)

Deformed fingers
----------------

[2SA 21:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:20) parallel [1CH 20:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:6)

Jesus Christ's fingers
----------------------

### Jesus Christ heals with his fingers

**[MRK 7:32–7:35](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32-Mark7:35)**

### Jesus Christ writes with his fingers

**[JHN 8:6](https://ref.ly/John8:6)** *See also* [DAN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5)

Proving Jesus Christ's resurrection
-----------------------------------

**[JHN 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25)** *See also* [JHN 20:27](https://ref.ly/John20:27)

Evil done with the fingers
--------------------------

### Idolatrous worship

**[ISA 2:8](https://ref.ly/Isa2:8)** *See also* [ISA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Isa17:8), [ISA 59:3](https://ref.ly/Isa59:3)

### Making accusations

**[ISA 58:9](https://ref.ly/Isa58:9)** *See also* [PRO 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Prov6:12-Prov6:13)

### Doing harm

**[JOB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Job1:12)**

Fingers used in symbolic actions
--------------------------------

### The finger ring as a sign of authority

**[GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42)** *See also* [EST 3:10](https://ref.ly/Esth3:10), [EST 8:2](https://ref.ly/Esth8:2); [LUK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22)

### The finger as a symbol of minimal effort

**[MAT 23:4](https://ref.ly/Matt23:4)** *See also* [1KI 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:10) parallel [2CH 10:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:10) *Rehoboam refusing to lift the burdens placed on the people by his father Solomon*; [LUK 11:46](https://ref.ly/Luke11:46)

### The finger as a visible reminder

**[PRO 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Prov7:2-Prov7:3)** *See also* [DEU 6:8](https://ref.ly/Deut6:8), [DEU 11:18](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18)
