## 5153: foot-washing (id: 5153)

A practice of hospitality used by Jesus Christ to express his servanthood.

Foot\-washing as a sign of courteous hospitality
------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 18:4](https://ref.ly/Gen18:4)** *See also* [GEN 19:2](https://ref.ly/Gen19:2); [JDG 19:21](https://ref.ly/Judg19:21); [1SA 25:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:41); [LUK 7:44](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44)

Jesus Christ washes his disciples' feet
---------------------------------------

**[JHN 13:3–13:5](https://ref.ly/John13:3-John13:5)** *The washing of feet was a lowly task normally performed by a servant.*

### As an example of servanthood for his followers

**[JHN 13:14](https://ref.ly/John13:14)** *See also* [MAT 20:26–20:27](https://ref.ly/Matt20:26-Matt20:27) parallel [MRK 10:44–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:44-Mark10:45); [LUK 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Luke22:26-Luke22:27); [1TI 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:10)

### As a symbol of spiritual cleansing

**[JHN 13:6–13:10](https://ref.ly/John13:6-John13:10)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 18:4; Genesis 19:2; Judges 19:21; 1 Samuel 25:41; Luke 7:44; John 13:3-5; John 13:14; Matthew 20:26-27; Mark 10:44-45; Luke 22:26-27; 1 Timothy 5:10; John 13:6-10
* **Associated Articles:** 2327: Jesus Christ, as servant (ID: `2327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2339: Jesus Christ, example of (ID: `2339@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5151: feet (ID: `5151@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6652: forgiveness (ID: `6652@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7342: cleanliness (ID: `7342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7478: washing (ID: `7478@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8445: hospitality (ID: `8445@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5154: forehead (id: 5154)

The front of the head between the eyes. Often used figuratively in phrases denoting a stubborn or persevering attitude. The forehead is the place where symbols of remembrance or allegiance can be placed. Such symbols may particularly represent allegiance to God or Satan.

Literal use of the forehead
---------------------------

*See also* [LEV 13:41–13:43](https://ref.ly/Lev13:41-Lev13:43); [2CH 26:19–26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19-2Chr26:20)NUM [24:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:17); [1SA 17:49](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:49); [JER 48:45](https://ref.ly/Jer48:45); [EZK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:9)

The forehead used figuratively to denote stubbornness
-----------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 48:4](https://ref.ly/Isa48:4)**

The forehead as a place for symbols which prompt remembrance
------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Exod13:9)** *See also* [EXO 13:16](https://ref.ly/Exod13:16)DEU [6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8), [DEU 11:18](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18)

The forehead as a place for symbols of dedication and allegiance
----------------------------------------------------------------

### To God and his purposes

**[EXO 28:36–28:38](https://ref.ly/Exod28:36-Exod28:38)** *See also* [EZK 9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:4); [REV 7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:3), [REV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Rev9:4), [REV 14:1](https://ref.ly/Rev14:1), [REV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Rev22:4) *The receipt of this symbol of allegiance provides protection in judgment and a reminder of the Passover where God's marked people escaped judgment.*

### To Satan and his ways

[REV 13:16](https://ref.ly/Rev13:16), [REV 14:9](https://ref.ly/Rev14:9), [REV 17:5](https://ref.ly/Rev17:5), [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Rev20:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 13:41-43; 2 Chronicles 26:19-20; 2 Chronicles 24:17; 1 Samuel 17:49; Jeremiah 48:45; Ezekiel 3:9; Isaiah 48:4; Exodus 13:9; Exodus 13:16; Exodus 6:8; Deuteronomy 11:18; Exodus 28:36-38; Ezekiel 9:4; Revelation 7:3; Revelation 9:4; Revelation 14:1; Revelation 22:4; Revelation 13:16; Revelation 14:9; Revelation 17:5; Revelation 20:4
* **Associated Articles:** 5150: face (ID: `5150@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5157: head (ID: `5157@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6245: stubbornness (ID: `6245@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7410: phylactery (ID: `7410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8207: commitment (ID: `8207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8467: reminders (ID: `8467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8670: remembering (ID: `8670@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5155: hair (id: 5155)

Various conditions, both physical and spiritual, are indicated in Scripture by human hair. Animal hair is used for a variety of purposes.

Physical qualities of human hair
--------------------------------

### A woman's hair is attractive

**[SNG 7:5](https://ref.ly/Song7:5)** *See also* [2KI 9:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:30); [SNG 4:1](https://ref.ly/Song4:1); [1CO 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:15)

### A man's hair shows vigour

**[JDG 16:17–16:22](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17-Judg16:22)** *See also* [2SA 14:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:26); [SNG 5:11](https://ref.ly/Song5:11)

### Grey hair shows the glory of old age

**[PRO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Prov16:31)** *See also* [LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32) *“aged” may be translated “grey\-haired”*; [PRO 20:29](https://ref.ly/Prov20:29)

### Greying hair as a sign of declining strength

[HOS 7:9](https://ref.ly/Hos7:9)

### White hair as a sign of age and wisdom

**[REV 1:14](https://ref.ly/Rev1:14)** *See also* [DAN 7:9](https://ref.ly/Dan7:9)

Hair as indicating physical or spiritual conditions
---------------------------------------------------

### Unharmed hair speaks of protection

**[MAT 10:30](https://ref.ly/Matt10:30)** parallel [LUK 12:7](https://ref.ly/Luke12:7) *See also* [1SA 14:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:45); [2SA 14:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:11); [DAN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:27); [LUK 21:18](https://ref.ly/Luke21:18); [ACT 27:34](https://ref.ly/Acts27:34)

### Hair standing on end indicates fear

[JOB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Job4:15)

### Baldness may indicate God's judgment

**[ISA 3:24](https://ref.ly/Isa3:24)** *See also* [ISA 3:17](https://ref.ly/Isa3:17)

### Hair colour may indicate skin disease

**[LEV 13:3](https://ref.ly/Lev13:3)** *See also* [LEV 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Lev13:10-Lev13:11), [LEV 13:26](https://ref.ly/Lev13:26), [LEV 13:30–13:37](https://ref.ly/Lev13:30-Lev13:37), [LEV 13:40–13:45](https://ref.ly/Lev13:40-Lev13:45)

Rituals and customs involving the hair
--------------------------------------

### Rules concerning hair for those under a vow

**[NUM 6:5](https://ref.ly/Num6:5)** *See also* [NUM 6:9](https://ref.ly/Num6:9), [NUM 6:18–6:19](https://ref.ly/Num6:18-Num6:19); [JDG 13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:5), [JDG 16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17); [1SA 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11); [ACT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Acts18:18), [ACT 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/Acts21:23-Acts21:24)

### Rules concerning hair for those being cleansed

[LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9)

### Mourning customs affecting hair

**[MIC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mic1:16)** *See also* [LEV 10:6](https://ref.ly/Lev10:6); [DEU 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Deut21:12-Deut21:13); [JOB 1:20](https://ref.ly/Job1:20); [JER 16:6](https://ref.ly/Jer16:6), [JER 47:5](https://ref.ly/Jer47:5), [JER 48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:37); [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10)

### Grief signified by tearing or cutting one's hair

**[EZR 9:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:3)** *See also* [ISA 22:12](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12); [JER 7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:29)

### Humiliating others by shaving or pulling out the hair

**[2SA 10:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4)** parallel [1CH 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:4) *See also* [NEH 13:25](https://ref.ly/Neh13:25); [ISA 7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:20)

### Pagan customs to be avoided

**[LEV 19:27](https://ref.ly/Lev19:27)** *See also* [DEU 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1-Deut14:2)

### Customs concerning hair and public worship

[1CO 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:6), [1CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:13-1Cor11:15); ; [1TI 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:9); ; [1PE 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:3)

Animal hair
-----------

### Animal hair used in tabernacle furnishings

**[EXO 26:7](https://ref.ly/Exod26:7)** *See also* [EXO 25:4](https://ref.ly/Exod25:4), [EXO 35:26](https://ref.ly/Exod35:26), [EXO 36:14](https://ref.ly/Exod36:14)

### A prophet's clothing made from animal hair

**[2KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:8)** *See also* [ZEC 13:4](https://ref.ly/Zech13:4); [MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) parallel [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6)

### Animal hair as a disguise

**[GEN 27:16](https://ref.ly/Gen27:16)** *See also* [1SA 19:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:13), [1SA 19:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:16)

Hair used to wipe Jesus Christ's feet
-------------------------------------

**[JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3)** *See also* [LUK 7:38](https://ref.ly/Luke7:38), [LUK 7:44](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44); [JHN 11:2](https://ref.ly/John11:2)

Nazirite vows forbade the cutting of hair
-----------------------------------------

### The reason for taking a Nazirite vow, separation to the Lord

[NUM 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:2) *While attention is often focused on Nazirite prohibitions, it is important to note that the primary reason was for separating oneself to God. Both men and women could make the vow*; ; [NUM 6:8](https://ref.ly/Num6:8)

### The requirements of the Nazirite vow

[NUM 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Num6:2-Num6:4) *Nazirites must abstain from all produce of the vine*; ; [NUM 6:5](https://ref.ly/Num6:5) *Nazirites must not cut their hair*; ; [NUM 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Num6:6-Num6:7) *Nazirites must not touch a dead body.*

### The rules for re\-establishing a broken Nazirite vow

[NUM 6:9–6:12](https://ref.ly/Num6:9-Num6:12)

### The regulations for completing the Nazirite vow

[NUM 6:13–6:17](https://ref.ly/Num6:13-Num6:17) *The Nazirite vow is completed, first, through prescribed sacrifices*; ; [NUM 6:18](https://ref.ly/Num6:18) *The Nazirite vow is completed, second, through cutting off one's hair and burning it on the altar*; ; [NUM 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/Num6:19-Num6:20) *The Nazirite vow is completed, third, through ritual acts performed by the priest.*

### Examples of people dedicated to being lifelong Nazirites

Samson was a lifelong Nazirite: [JDG 13:2–13:7](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2-Judg13:7), [JDG 16:1–16:22](https://ref.ly/Judg16:1-Judg16:22); [1SA 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:10-1Sam1:11) *Samuel was a lifelong Nazirite*; ; [LUK 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Luke1:13-Luke1:15) *John the Baptist was possibly a Nazirite.*

### Examples of temporary Nazirite vows

[ACT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Acts18:18), [ACT 21:20–21:26](https://ref.ly/Acts21:20-Acts21:26)

### Nazirites to be seen as gifts from God to his people

[AMO 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Amos2:11-Amos2:12)

Expressions involving hair
--------------------------

[JDG 20:16](https://ref.ly/Judg20:16); ; [REV 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Song of Songs 7:5; 2 Kings 9:30; Song of Songs 4:1; 1 Corinthians 11:15; Judges 16:17-22; 2 Samuel 14:26; Song of Songs 5:11; Proverbs 16:31; Leviticus 19:32; Proverbs 20:29; Hosea 7:9; Revelation 1:14; Daniel 7:9; Matthew 10:30; Luke 12:7; 1 Samuel 14:45; 2 Samuel 14:11; Daniel 3:27; Luke 21:18; Acts 27:34; Job 4:15; Isaiah 3:24; Isaiah 3:17; Leviticus 13:3; Leviticus 13:10-11; Leviticus 13:26; Leviticus 13:30-37; Leviticus 13:40-45; Numbers 6:5; Numbers 6:9; Numbers 6:18-19; Judges 13:5; Judges 16:17; 1 Samuel 1:11; Acts 18:18; Acts 21:23-24; Leviticus 14:8-9; Micah 1:16; Leviticus 10:6; Deuteronomy 21:12-13; Job 1:20; Jeremiah 16:6; Jeremiah 47:5; Jeremiah 48:37; Amos 8:10; Ezra 9:3; Isaiah 22:12; Jeremiah 7:29; 2 Samuel 10:4; 1 Chronicles 19:4; Nehemiah 13:25; Isaiah 7:20; Leviticus 19:27; Deuteronomy 14:1-2; 1 Corinthians 11:6; 1 Corinthians 11:13-15; 1 Timothy 2:9; 1 Peter 3:3; Exodus 26:7; Exodus 25:4; Exodus 35:26; Exodus 36:14; 2 Kings 1:8; Zechariah 13:4; Matthew 3:4; Mark 1:6; Genesis 27:16; 1 Samuel 19:13; 1 Samuel 19:16; John 12:3; Luke 7:38; Luke 7:44; John 11:2; Numbers 6:1-2; Numbers 6:8; Numbers 6:2-4; Numbers 6:6-7; Numbers 6:9-12; Numbers 6:13-17; Numbers 6:18; Numbers 6:19-20; Judges 13:2-7; Judges 16:1-22; 1 Samuel 1:10-11; Luke 1:13-15; Acts 21:20-26; Amos 2:11-12; Judges 20:16; Revelation 6:12
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5098: John the Baptist (ID: `5098@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5128: baldness (ID: `5128@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5130: beard (ID: `5130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5158: head-covering (ID: `5158@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5180: shaving (ID: `5180@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5419: mourning (ID: `5419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5725: old age (ID: `5725@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5741: vows (ID: `5741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5879: humiliation (ID: `5879@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6742: sackcloth and ashes (ID: `6742@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5156: hand (id: 5156)

Associated in Scripture with various activities, the hand symbolises particularly power and strength. Jesus Christ's hands often bring healing and blessing.

The hand as part of the physical body
-------------------------------------

### Hands used for physical work

**[1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11)** *See also* [GEN 31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:42); [EXO 35:25](https://ref.ly/Exod35:25); [DEU 2:7](https://ref.ly/Deut2:7); [JOB 1:10](https://ref.ly/Job1:10); [PSA 90:17](https://ref.ly/Ps90:17); [ECC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:11); [ACT 20:34](https://ref.ly/Acts20:34)

### Hands used for praying

**[EXO 9:29](https://ref.ly/Exod9:29)** *See also* [1KI 8:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:38) parallel [2CH 6:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:29); [2CH 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:12-2Chr6:13); [EZR 9:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:5); [PSA 77:2](https://ref.ly/Ps77:2), [PSA 88:9](https://ref.ly/Ps88:9); [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8)

### Hands used in clapping

**[2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)** [PSA 47:1](https://ref.ly/Ps47:1), [PSA 98:8](https://ref.ly/Ps98:8); ; [ISA 55:12](https://ref.ly/Isa55:12); [JOB 27:23](https://ref.ly/Job27:23); ; [LAM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Lam2:15); ; [EZK 25:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:6); ; [NAM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:19)

### Hands used for carrying weapons

**[JER 21:4](https://ref.ly/Jer21:4)** *See also* [NUM 22:29](https://ref.ly/Num22:29), [NUM 35:17](https://ref.ly/Num35:17); [JOS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Josh5:13); [1SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10); [2KI 11:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:11) parallel [2CH 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:10); [EZK 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:1-Ezek9:2)

### Hands used for writing

**[GAL 6:11](https://ref.ly/Gal6:11)** *See also* [1CH 28:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:19); [DAN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5), [DAN 5:24](https://ref.ly/Dan5:24); [ROM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Rom16:22); [COL 4:18](https://ref.ly/Col4:18); [2TH 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:17); [PHM 1:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:19)

### Hands as a means of assistance

**[MAT 9:25](https://ref.ly/Matt9:25)** parallels [MRK 5:41](https://ref.ly/Mark5:41), [LUK 8:54](https://ref.ly/Luke8:54) *See also* [PSA 109:31](https://ref.ly/Ps109:31); [ISA 63:12](https://ref.ly/Isa63:12); [LAM 4:6](https://ref.ly/Lam4:6); [MRK 9:27](https://ref.ly/Mark9:27); [ACT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Acts3:7), [ACT 9:41](https://ref.ly/Acts9:41)

### Hands used in making idols

**[2CH 32:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:19)** *See also* [DEU 31:29](https://ref.ly/Deut31:29); [2KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:18), [2KI 22:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:17); [2CH 34:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:25); [ACT 7:41](https://ref.ly/Acts7:41)

### Idle hands

**[ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18)** *See also* [PRO 6:10](https://ref.ly/Prov6:10), [PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4), [PRO 21:25](https://ref.ly/Prov21:25), [PRO 24:33](https://ref.ly/Prov24:33); [ECC 4:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:5), [ECC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:6)

Right and left hands
--------------------

### Right hand as the place of honour

**[1KI 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:19)** *See also* [GEN 48:13–48:14](https://ref.ly/Gen48:13-Gen48:14); [1CH 6:39](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:39); [PSA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ps45:9); [JER 22:24](https://ref.ly/Jer22:24); [MAT 25:33–25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:33-Matt25:34)

### Significant uses of the left hand

**[JDG 20:16](https://ref.ly/Judg20:16)** *See also* [GEN 48:13–48:14](https://ref.ly/Gen48:13-Gen48:14); [JDG 3:15](https://ref.ly/Judg3:15), [JDG 3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:21); [1CH 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:2); [EZK 39:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:3); [MAT 6:3](https://ref.ly/Matt6:3)

Symbolic uses of the hand
-------------------------

### The hand as a symbol of power

**[JOB 30:2](https://ref.ly/Job30:2)** *See also* [DEU 32:39–32:40](https://ref.ly/Deut32:39-Deut32:40); [1KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:12); [ISA 47:6](https://ref.ly/Isa47:6); [DAN 9:15](https://ref.ly/Dan9:15)

### The hand as a symbol of violence

**[PSA 58:2](https://ref.ly/Ps58:2)** *See also* [JOB 16:17](https://ref.ly/Job16:17), [JOB 31:21](https://ref.ly/Job31:21); [PRO 6:17](https://ref.ly/Prov6:17); [ISA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:15), [ISA 59:3](https://ref.ly/Isa59:3), [ISA 59:6](https://ref.ly/Isa59:6); [EZK 23:45](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:45)

### Clean hands as a symbol of innocence

**[PSA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3-Ps24:4)** *See also* [DEU 21:6–21:7](https://ref.ly/Deut21:6-Deut21:7); [2SA 22:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:21) parallel [PSA 18:20](https://ref.ly/Ps18:20); [JOB 17:9](https://ref.ly/Job17:9), [JOB 22:30](https://ref.ly/Job22:30); [PSA 26:6](https://ref.ly/Ps26:6), [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13); [MAT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:24); [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8)

### Limp hands as a symbol of paralysing fear

**[EZK 21:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:7)** *See also* [NEH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:9); [JOB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Job4:3); [ISA 13:7](https://ref.ly/Isa13:7), [ISA 35:3](https://ref.ly/Isa35:3); [JER 47:3](https://ref.ly/Jer47:3); [EZK 7:17–7:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:17-Ezek7:18); [ZEP 3:16](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:16)

### Hands as a symbol of temptation

**[MAT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Matt18:8)** *See also* [GEN 3:22](https://ref.ly/Gen3:22); [EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8), [EZK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:17); [MAT 5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:30); [MRK 9:43](https://ref.ly/Mark9:43)

Hands used in making agreements
-------------------------------

### Striking hands in pledge

[EZR 10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:19); ; [PRO 6:1](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1), [PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15), [PRO 17:18](https://ref.ly/Prov17:18), [PRO 22:26](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26)

### Symbolic actions in oath taking

[GEN 14:22](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22), [GEN 24:2](https://ref.ly/Gen24:2), [GEN 24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:9), [GEN 47:29](https://ref.ly/Gen47:29)

### Joining hands in partnership or fellowship

[2KI 10:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:15); ; [ISA 2:6](https://ref.ly/Isa2:6); ; [HOS 7:5](https://ref.ly/Hos7:5); ; [GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9)

Figurative use of hands
-----------------------

**[1SA 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:5)** *See also* [JDG 12:3](https://ref.ly/Judg12:3); [1SA 28:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:21); [JOB 13:14](https://ref.ly/Job13:14); [PSA 119:109](https://ref.ly/Ps119:109); [ISA 2:8](https://ref.ly/Isa2:8)

Hands of Jesus Christ
---------------------

### Jesus Christ's hands used in healing

**[LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40)** *See also* [MAT 8:3](https://ref.ly/Matt8:3) parallels [MRK 1:41](https://ref.ly/Mark1:41), [LUK 5:13](https://ref.ly/Luke5:13); [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18) parallel [MRK 5:23](https://ref.ly/Mark5:23); [MRK 6:5](https://ref.ly/Mark6:5), [MRK 7:32–7:33](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32-Mark7:33); [LUK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Luke13:13)

### Jesus Christ's hands used in blessing

**[MRK 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:16)** parallel [MAT 19:13–19:15](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:15) *See also* [LUK 24:50](https://ref.ly/Luke24:50)

### Jesus Christ's hands were pierced

**[JHN 20:27](https://ref.ly/John20:27)** *See also* [PSA 22:16](https://ref.ly/Ps22:16); [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5); [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10); [JHN 19:37](https://ref.ly/John19:37); [LUK 24:40](https://ref.ly/Luke24:40); [JHN 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25); [ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23)

## 5157: head (id: 5157)

The position of the head often expresses an attitude or emotion. Figuratively head may mean “chief”, “first” or “top”.

The head as part of the body
----------------------------

### Heads of people

[GEN 28:11](https://ref.ly/Gen28:11), [GEN 28:18](https://ref.ly/Gen28:18), [GEN 48:14](https://ref.ly/Gen48:14), [GEN 48:17](https://ref.ly/Gen48:17); ; [JDG 5:26](https://ref.ly/Judg5:26); ; [SNG 2:6](https://ref.ly/Song2:6); ; [JON 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:5)

### Heads of animals

[EXO 12:9](https://ref.ly/Exod12:9); ; [LEV 1:4](https://ref.ly/Lev1:4); ; [2KI 6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25); [EXO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Exod22:1); ; [1KI 4:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:23); ; [2CH 15:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:11), [2CH 35:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:9)

### Heads of idols

[1SA 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:4), [1SA 19:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:13), [1SA 19:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:16)

The head as the uppermost part of the body
------------------------------------------

[LEV 13:12](https://ref.ly/Lev13:12); ; [2SA 14:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:25); ; [JOB 2:7](https://ref.ly/Job2:7); ; [ISA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:6); ; [HAB 3:13](https://ref.ly/Hab3:13); ; [JHN 13:9](https://ref.ly/John13:9)

### A measure of height

[1SA 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:2), [1SA 10:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:23)

### A crown worn on the head

[2SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:10); ; [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17); ; [PSA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Ps21:3); ; [JER 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jer13:18); ; [LAM 5:16](https://ref.ly/Lam5:16); ; [ZEC 6:11](https://ref.ly/Zech6:11)

### A turban worn on the head

[LEV 8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:9); ; [EZK 23:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:15); ; [ZEC 3:5](https://ref.ly/Zech3:5)

### Death and decapitation

[GEN 40:19](https://ref.ly/Gen40:19); ; [1SA 17:46](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:46), [1SA 17:51](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:51); ; [2SA 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:7), [2SA 16:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:9); ; [MAT 14:8–14:11](https://ref.ly/Matt14:8-Matt14:11) parallel [MRK 6:24–6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:24-Mark6:28)

Head used figuratively in various expressions
---------------------------------------------

### \`\`Be it on one's own head'': personal responsibility that is inescapable

[LEV 20:9](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9), [LEV 20:11–20:13](https://ref.ly/Lev20:11-Lev20:13), [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27); ; [JOS 2:19](https://ref.ly/Josh2:19); ; [1SA 25:39](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:39); ; [2SA 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:16); ; [1KI 2:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:33); ; [EZK 9:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:10), [EZK 33:4–33:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:4-Ezek33:5)

### \`\`To come back on one's own head'': a wrongdoer suffering the very wrong he or she has committed

[1KI 8:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:32) parallel [2CH 6:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:23); ; [NEH 4:4](https://ref.ly/Neh4:4); ; [EST 9:25](https://ref.ly/Esth9:25); ; [PSA 7:16](https://ref.ly/Ps7:16), [PSA 140:9](https://ref.ly/Ps140:9); ; [JOL 3:4](https://ref.ly/Joel3:4), [JOL 3:7](https://ref.ly/Joel3:7); ; [OBA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Obad1:15)

### \`\`To heap burning coals on his head'': generosity towards an enemy in the hope of causing a change of heart

[PRO 25:22](https://ref.ly/Prov25:22); ; [ROM 12:20](https://ref.ly/Rom12:20)

### \`\`Not a hair on one's head will be lost'': absolute safety

[1SA 14:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:45); ; [1KI 1:52](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:52); ; [DAN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:27); ; [LUK 12:7](https://ref.ly/Luke12:7); ; [ACT 27:34](https://ref.ly/Acts27:34)

### \`\`To keep one's head'': staying calm

[2TI 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:5)

### \`\`Making it up out of one's head'': imagining something

[NEH 6:8](https://ref.ly/Neh6:8)

### \`\`Blessing resting on your head''

[GEN 49:26](https://ref.ly/Gen49:26); ; [DEU 33:16](https://ref.ly/Deut33:16); ; [PRO 10:6](https://ref.ly/Prov10:6); ; [ISA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:10), [ISA 51:11](https://ref.ly/Isa51:11)

Rituals concerning the head
---------------------------

Cleansing after infectious skin diseases: [LEV 14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:9), [LEV 14:18](https://ref.ly/Lev14:18), [LEV 14:29](https://ref.ly/Lev14:29); [NUM 6:9](https://ref.ly/Num6:9); ; [JDG 13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:5), [JDG 16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17); ; [1SA 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11); [DEU 21:12](https://ref.ly/Deut21:12)

### The anointing of the head

[EXO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Exod29:7); ; [LEV 8:12](https://ref.ly/Lev8:12); ; [MAT 26:7](https://ref.ly/Matt26:7) parallel [MRK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3)

Expressions of grief involving the head
---------------------------------------

### Bowing the head

[PSA 35:14](https://ref.ly/Ps35:14); ; [LAM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Lam2:10)

### Shaving the head

[DEU 14:1](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1); ; [ISA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2); ; [JER 47:5](https://ref.ly/Jer47:5), [JER 48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:37); ; [EZK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:18), [EZK 27:30–27:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:30-Ezek27:31), [EZK 44:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:20); ; [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10); ; [MIC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mic1:16)

### Covering the head

[2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30); ; [EST 6:12](https://ref.ly/Esth6:12); ; [JER 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Jer14:3-Jer14:4)

Various attitudes expressed with the head
-----------------------------------------

### Lifting up the head in joy and expectation

[PSA 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ps3:3), [PSA 27:6](https://ref.ly/Ps27:6), [PSA 110:7](https://ref.ly/Ps110:7); ; [LUK 21:28](https://ref.ly/Luke21:28)

### Bowing the head in humility

[ISA 58:5](https://ref.ly/Isa58:5); ; [LUK 18:13](https://ref.ly/Luke18:13)

### Hanging the head in shame and dejection

[JDG 8:28](https://ref.ly/Judg8:28); ; [JOB 10:15](https://ref.ly/Job10:15)

### Shaking the head in scorn

[2KI 19:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:21) parallel [ISA 37:22](https://ref.ly/Isa37:22); ; [JOB 16:4](https://ref.ly/Job16:4); ; [PSA 109:25](https://ref.ly/Ps109:25); ; [JER 18:16](https://ref.ly/Jer18:16), [JER 48:27](https://ref.ly/Jer48:27); ; [LAM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Lam2:15); ; [MAT 27:39](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39) parallel [MRK 15:29](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29); ; [PSA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7)

### Rearing the head in rebellion

[PSA 83:2](https://ref.ly/Ps83:2)

--------------------------------

### Jesus Christ as the head

[1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3); ; [EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10), [EPH 1:22](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22), [EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23); ; [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18), [COL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:10), [COL 2:19](https://ref.ly/Col2:19)

## 5158: head-covering (id: 5158)

A form of clothing worn on the head, often as a sign of status or rank.

Priestly head\-coverings
------------------------

**[EXO 29:6](https://ref.ly/Exod29:6)** *See also* [EXO 28:4](https://ref.ly/Exod28:4) parallel [LEV 8:9](https://ref.ly/Lev8:9); [LEV 16:4](https://ref.ly/Lev16:4); [ZEC 3:5](https://ref.ly/Zech3:5)

Royal head\-coverings
---------------------

**[EZK 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:25-Ezek21:26)** *See also* [2SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:10); [2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12) parallel [2CH 23:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:11); [EST 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Esth1:10-Esth1:11), [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17); [PSA 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Ps21:1-Ps21:3); [SNG 3:11](https://ref.ly/Song3:11); [REV 14:14](https://ref.ly/Rev14:14)

Military head\-coverings
------------------------

**[1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5)** *See also* [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14); [EZK 23:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:15), [EZK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:24), [EZK 27:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:10), [EZK 38:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:5)

Head\-coverings in mourning
---------------------------

**[2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30)** *See also* [EST 6:12](https://ref.ly/Esth6:12); [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17)

Head\-coverings in worship
--------------------------

**[1CO 11:4–11:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:4-1Cor11:7)** *NIV footnote gives an alternative translation for these verses, taking hair as the head\-covering.*

Metaphorical use of head\-coverings
-----------------------------------

**[JOB 29:14](https://ref.ly/Job29:14)** *See also* [EPH 6:17](https://ref.ly/Eph6:17)

## 5159: hearing (id: 5159)

In Scripture, both physical hearing and spiritual apprehension. God hears the prayers of his people and the cry of the oppressed. People must hear and respond to God's word.

Divine hearing
--------------

### God hears the prayers of his people

**[2SA 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:7)** *See also* [PSA 3:4](https://ref.ly/Ps3:4); [ISA 65:24](https://ref.ly/Isa65:24); [JHN 11:41–11:42](https://ref.ly/John11:41-John11:42); [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12); [PSA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Ps34:15)

### God hears the grumbling of his people

**[NUM 11:1](https://ref.ly/Num11:1)** *See also* [EXO 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/Exod16:7-Exod16:9), [EXO 16:11–16:12](https://ref.ly/Exod16:11-Exod16:12); [NUM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Num12:1-Num12:2), [NUM 14:27](https://ref.ly/Num14:27)

### God hears the cry of the oppressed

**[JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)** *See also* [EXO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7); [PSA 94:9](https://ref.ly/Ps94:9)

### God does not hear the unrighteous

**[ISA 59:1–59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:1-Isa59:2)** *See also* [PSA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Ps66:18); [ISA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:15); [JER 11:11](https://ref.ly/Jer11:11), [JER 11:14](https://ref.ly/Jer11:14); [EZK 8:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:18); [AMO 5:23](https://ref.ly/Amos5:23); [JHN 9:31](https://ref.ly/John9:31)

### Other gods cannot hear

[1KI 18:26–18:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26-1Kgs18:27); ; [PSA 115:6](https://ref.ly/Ps115:6), [PSA 135:17](https://ref.ly/Ps135:17); ; [ISA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Isa46:7)

Hearing the word of God
-----------------------

### The importance of hearing God's word

**[DEU 5:1](https://ref.ly/Deut5:1)**; **[EPH 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13)** *See also* [DEU 4:1](https://ref.ly/Deut4:1); [JER 2:4](https://ref.ly/Jer2:4), [JER 7:2](https://ref.ly/Jer7:2); [MIC 1:2](https://ref.ly/Mic1:2); [MAT 13:16–13:17](https://ref.ly/Matt13:16-Matt13:17) parallel [LUK 10:23–10:24](https://ref.ly/Luke10:23-Luke10:24); [JHN 5:24](https://ref.ly/John5:24); [ROM 10:17](https://ref.ly/Rom10:17)

### Having ears to hear

**[MAT 11:15](https://ref.ly/Matt11:15)** *See also* [MAT 13:9](https://ref.ly/Matt13:9); [MRK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Mark4:9), [MRK 4:23](https://ref.ly/Mark4:23); [LUK 8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:8); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7), [REV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rev2:11), [REV 2:17](https://ref.ly/Rev2:17), [REV 2:29](https://ref.ly/Rev2:29), [REV 3:6](https://ref.ly/Rev3:6), [REV 3:13](https://ref.ly/Rev3:13), [REV 3:22](https://ref.ly/Rev3:22)

### The gospel must not only be heard but accepted and acted on

**[HEB 4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:2)** *See also* [MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27); [MRK 4:14–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:14-Mark4:20) parallels [MAT 13:19–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:19-Matt13:23), [LUK 8:11–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:15); [ROM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Rom2:13); [GAL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Gal3:2), [GAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Gal3:5); [1TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:13); [JAS 1:22–1:24](https://ref.ly/Jas1:22-Jas1:24)

### It is God who grants effective hearing

**[ACT 16:14](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14)** *See also* [PRO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Prov20:12); [ISA 50:5](https://ref.ly/Isa50:5); [2TH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:13-2Thess2:14)

### Some people refuse to hear God's word

**[JER 6:10](https://ref.ly/Jer6:10)** *See also* [ISA 42:18–42:20](https://ref.ly/Isa42:18-Isa42:20); [EZK 12:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:2); [ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51); [2TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:4)

### God denies the effective hearing of his word to those who stubbornly resist him

**[MAT 13:10–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:10-Matt13:15)** parallels [MRK 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:12), [LUK 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:9-Luke8:10) *See also* [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); [JHN 8:43](https://ref.ly/John8:43); [ACT 28:26–28:27](https://ref.ly/Acts28:26-Acts28:27); [ROM 11:8](https://ref.ly/Rom11:8); [DEU 29:4](https://ref.ly/Deut29:4); [ISA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10)

The first Christians witnessed to what they had heard
-----------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 22:14–22:15](https://ref.ly/Acts22:14-Acts22:15)** *See also* [ACT 4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:20); [HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3); [1JN 1:1](https://ref.ly/1John1:1)

Physical hearing was restored by Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

**[MRK 7:35–7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:35-Mark7:37)** *See also* [MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5) parallel [LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22)

The dead will hear the voice of the Son of God
----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 5:25](https://ref.ly/John5:25)** *See also* [JHN 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/John5:28-John5:29), [JHN 11:43–11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:43-John11:44)

-----------------------------------------

--------------------------------------

## 5161: kneeling (id: 5161)

A bodily posture which expresses an attitude of humility, especially in worship, prayer or submission to the authority of another person.

Kneeling in worship
-------------------

**[PSA 95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6)** *See also* [2CH 7:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:3), [2CH 29:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:29)

Kneeling in submission
----------------------

### To God

**[ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23)** *See also* [ROM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rom14:11); [PSA 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ps22:29)

### To Jesus Christ

**[PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11)** *See also* [MAT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29) *kneeling in mock submission*; [MRK 5:6](https://ref.ly/Mark5:6), [MRK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17); [LUK 5:8](https://ref.ly/Luke5:8)

### To other people

**[EST 3:2](https://ref.ly/Esth3:2)** *See also* [1KI 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:16); [2KI 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:13); [MAT 18:26](https://ref.ly/Matt18:26)

Kneeling in prayer
------------------

**[DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10)**; **[MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18)**; **[EPH 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eph3:14)** *See also* [1KI 8:54](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:54); [2CH 6:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:13); [EZR 9:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:5); [MAT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2), [MAT 15:25](https://ref.ly/Matt15:25), [MAT 17:14](https://ref.ly/Matt17:14), [MAT 20:20](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20); [LUK 22:41](https://ref.ly/Luke22:41); [ACT 7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:60), [ACT 9:40](https://ref.ly/Acts9:40), [ACT 20:36](https://ref.ly/Acts20:36), [ACT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Acts21:5)

## 5162: lameness (id: 5162)

A physical condition impairing leg movement.

Causes of lameness
------------------

### Congenital

**[ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2), [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8)**

### Accidental

**[2SA 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:4)** *See also* [2SA 9:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:3), [2SA 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:13)

Care for the lame
-----------------

### General care

**[JOB 29:15](https://ref.ly/Job29:15)** *See also* [JER 31:8](https://ref.ly/Jer31:8); [LUK 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/Luke14:13-Luke14:14)

### Healing

**[MAT 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5-Matt11:6)** parallel [LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22) *See also* [MAT 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23-Matt4:24), [MAT 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/Matt8:6-Matt8:7), [MAT 9:2–9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2-Matt9:8) parallels [MRK 2:1–2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:1-Mark2:12), [LUK 5:17–5:26](https://ref.ly/Luke5:17-Luke5:26); [MAT 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30-Matt15:31), [MAT 21:14](https://ref.ly/Matt21:14); [ACT 3:6–3:10](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6-Acts3:10), [ACT 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Acts8:7-Acts8:8), [ACT 9:33–9:34](https://ref.ly/Acts9:33-Acts9:34), [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8)

--------------------------------------

### Relative importance of lameness

**[MRK 9:45](https://ref.ly/Mark9:45)**

### Parallel to weakness

*See also* [2SA 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:8); [ISA 35:2–35:3](https://ref.ly/Isa35:2-Isa35:3); [HEB 12:12–12:13](https://ref.ly/Heb12:12-Heb12:13)

## 5163: legs (id: 5163)

The legs of an animal were among the choice parts offered in sacrifices. In a human being legs provide strength. Bowing the knee symbolises the submitting of one's human strength to God's control.

Choice parts of a sacrificial animal
------------------------------------

**[EZK 24:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:4)** *See also* [EXO 12:9](https://ref.ly/Exod12:9)EXO [29:17–29:18](https://ref.ly/Exod29:17-Exod29:18); [LEV 1:9](https://ref.ly/Lev1:9), [LEV 1:13](https://ref.ly/Lev1:13), [LEV 8:21](https://ref.ly/Lev8:21), [LEV 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Lev9:13-Lev9:14)[LEV 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Lev4:11-Lev4:12) *burnt outside the camp in the sin offering*

Legs of human beings are objects of beauty
------------------------------------------

[SNG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Song5:15), [SNG 7:1](https://ref.ly/Song7:1)

Legs of human beings symbolise strength
---------------------------------------

**[REV 10:1](https://ref.ly/Rev10:1)** *See also* [1SA 17:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:6); [PSA 147:10](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10); [DAN 2:33](https://ref.ly/Dan2:33), [DAN 2:40](https://ref.ly/Dan2:40), [DAN 10:6](https://ref.ly/Dan10:6)

### God's judgment saps human strength

**[DAN 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5-Dan5:6)** *See also* [EZK 7:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:17), [EZK 21:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:7); [NAM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Nah2:10); [HAB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Hab3:16)

### God's judgment brings pain and disgrace

[DEU 28:35](https://ref.ly/Deut28:35); [ISA 7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:20), [ISA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Isa47:2)

### God gives strength to the weak

**[HEB 12:12–12:13](https://ref.ly/Heb12:12-Heb12:13)** *See also* [JOB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Job4:4); [ISA 35:3](https://ref.ly/Isa35:3)

The legs of Jesus Christ were not broken at his death
-----------------------------------------------------

[JHN 19:31–19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:31-John19:36)

## 5164: lips (id: 5164)

Lips as part of the human body
------------------------------

### As a vehicle of speech

[1SA 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:13); ; [JOB 15:6](https://ref.ly/Job15:6), [JOB 33:3](https://ref.ly/Job33:3)

### As a means of eating and drinking

[SNG 7:9](https://ref.ly/Song7:9); ; [DAN 10:3](https://ref.ly/Dan10:3); ; [JOL 1:5](https://ref.ly/Joel1:5); ; [JHN 19:29](https://ref.ly/John19:29)

### As an object of beauty

[SNG 4:3](https://ref.ly/Song4:3), [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11), [SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13)

Lips attributed to God
----------------------

**[JOB 11:5](https://ref.ly/Job11:5)** *See also* [JOB 23:12](https://ref.ly/Job23:12); [PSA 17:4](https://ref.ly/Ps17:4), [PSA 89:34](https://ref.ly/Ps89:34); [ISA 30:27](https://ref.ly/Isa30:27)

Lips associated with various kinds of speech
--------------------------------------------

### Praising lips

**[PSA 34:1](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1)** *See also* [PSA 51:15](https://ref.ly/Ps51:15), [PSA 63:3](https://ref.ly/Ps63:3), [PSA 63:5](https://ref.ly/Ps63:5), [PSA 119:171](https://ref.ly/Ps119:171); [ISA 57:19](https://ref.ly/Isa57:19); [MAT 21:16](https://ref.ly/Matt21:16); [PSA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ps8:2); [HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)

### Praying lips

**[PSA 21:2](https://ref.ly/Ps21:2)** *See also* [JOB 13:6](https://ref.ly/Job13:6); [PSA 17:1](https://ref.ly/Ps17:1)

### Lips that speak God's word

**[ACT 15:7](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7)** *See also* [EXO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Exod13:9); [LUK 4:22](https://ref.ly/Luke4:22)

### Lips that speak promises and vows

**[PSA 66:14](https://ref.ly/Ps66:14)** *See also* [NUM 30:6](https://ref.ly/Num30:6), [NUM 30:12](https://ref.ly/Num30:12); [DEU 23:23](https://ref.ly/Deut23:23)

### Lips that give testimony

[PSA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9), [PSA 119:13](https://ref.ly/Ps119:13); ; [LUK 22:71](https://ref.ly/Luke22:71)

### Encouraging lips

[JOB 16:5](https://ref.ly/Job16:5); ; [PRO 10:21](https://ref.ly/Prov10:21)

Lips expressing different moods
-------------------------------

### Joyful lips

[JOB 8:21](https://ref.ly/Job8:21); ; [PSA 71:23](https://ref.ly/Ps71:23)

### Lips that express hesitancy

[EXO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Exod6:12), [EXO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Exod6:30)

### Apprehensive lips

[HAB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Hab3:16)

### Lips that speak rashly

[PSA 106:33](https://ref.ly/Ps106:33)

Lips associated with wrong values
---------------------------------

### Perverse and sinful lips

**[JOB 6:30](https://ref.ly/Job6:30)** *See also* [PSA 59:12](https://ref.ly/Ps59:12), [PSA 140:9](https://ref.ly/Ps140:9); [PRO 4:24](https://ref.ly/Prov4:24), [PRO 16:30](https://ref.ly/Prov16:30), [PRO 19:1](https://ref.ly/Prov19:1); [ISA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:5)

### Lips can tempt into sin

**[PRO 5:3](https://ref.ly/Prov5:3)** *See also* [PRO 14:7](https://ref.ly/Prov14:7), [PRO 17:4](https://ref.ly/Prov17:4), [PRO 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:6-Prov18:7)

### Deceitful lips

**[PRO 24:28](https://ref.ly/Prov24:28)** *See also* [PSA 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:18), [PSA 34:13](https://ref.ly/Ps34:13), [PSA 120:2](https://ref.ly/Ps120:2); [PRO 10:18](https://ref.ly/Prov10:18); [ISA 59:3](https://ref.ly/Isa59:3)

### Flattering lips

[PSA 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Ps12:2-Ps12:3)

### Hypocritical lips

[PSA 50:16](https://ref.ly/Ps50:16); ; [PRO 26:23–26:24](https://ref.ly/Prov26:23-Prov26:24); ; [JER 12:2](https://ref.ly/Jer12:2); ; [MAT 15:8](https://ref.ly/Matt15:8) parallel [MRK 7:6](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6); ; [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)

Lips associated with positive values
------------------------------------

### Lips that speak truth

**[PRO 12:19](https://ref.ly/Prov12:19)** *See also* [PRO 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/Prov8:6-Prov8:7), [PRO 16:13](https://ref.ly/Prov16:13), [PRO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Prov23:16); [JER 17:16](https://ref.ly/Jer17:16); [MAL 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6-Mal2:7)

### Lips that speak wisdom

**[PRO 10:32](https://ref.ly/Prov10:32)** *See also* [PSA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9); [PRO 10:13](https://ref.ly/Prov10:13), [PRO 15:7](https://ref.ly/Prov15:7), [PRO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Prov20:15), [PRO 22:17–22:18](https://ref.ly/Prov22:17-Prov22:18)

Wise lips are profitable
------------------------

**[PRO 12:14](https://ref.ly/Prov12:14)** *See also* [PRO 13:2](https://ref.ly/Prov13:2), [PRO 18:20](https://ref.ly/Prov18:20)

Foolish lips are disastrous
---------------------------

**[ECC 10:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12)** *See also* [PRO 14:3](https://ref.ly/Prov14:3), [PRO 18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:7)

Lips must be watched over by God
--------------------------------

### People must guard their own lips

**[PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)** *See also* [EXO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Exod23:13); [PRO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Prov13:3); [1PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:10); [PSA 34:12](https://ref.ly/Ps34:12)

### Lips must be purified

**[ISA 6:7](https://ref.ly/Isa6:7)** *See also* [HOS 2:17](https://ref.ly/Hos2:17); [ZEP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:9); [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8)

### Lips need God's touch

## 5165: listening (id: 5165)

People are called upon to listen attentively to God's word, to his Son and also to words of wisdom. Christians must not only listen to God's word but also put it into practice. Those who refuse to listen to God's word or to correction are condemned.

The importance of listening
---------------------------

### To God's word and his spokesmen

**[1SA 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:10)** *See also* [2CH 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:15); [PSA 81:8](https://ref.ly/Ps81:8); [JER 26:3–26:6](https://ref.ly/Jer26:3-Jer26:6); [MIC 1:2](https://ref.ly/Mic1:2); [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14), [ACT 13:16](https://ref.ly/Acts13:16), [ACT 28:28](https://ref.ly/Acts28:28)

### To Jesus Christ

**[MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5)** parallels [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7), [LUK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Luke9:35)**[JHN 10:27](https://ref.ly/John10:27)** *See also* [MAT 15:10](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10) parallel [MRK 7:14](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14); [LUK 10:16](https://ref.ly/Luke10:16), [LUK 10:39](https://ref.ly/Luke10:39); [JHN 10:8](https://ref.ly/John10:8), [JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:16)

### To words of wisdom

**[PRO 19:20](https://ref.ly/Prov19:20)**; **[JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)** *See also* [PSA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Ps34:11); [PRO 5:7](https://ref.ly/Prov5:7), [PRO 7:24](https://ref.ly/Prov7:24), [PRO 8:32–8:34](https://ref.ly/Prov8:32-Prov8:34), [PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15), [PRO 13:1](https://ref.ly/Prov13:1), [PRO 15:31](https://ref.ly/Prov15:31); [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Luke2:46); [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5)

Believers should not listen to false prophets or ungodly suggestions
--------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 23:16](https://ref.ly/Jer23:16)** *See also* [DEU 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Deut13:1-Deut13:3), [DEU 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6-Deut13:8); [JER 27:9](https://ref.ly/Jer27:9), [JER 27:14–27:17](https://ref.ly/Jer27:14-Jer27:17), [JER 29:8–29:9](https://ref.ly/Jer29:8-Jer29:9); [EZK 13:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:19); [1JN 4:1](https://ref.ly/1John4:1), [1JN 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/1John4:5-1John4:6); [2JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10)

The wicked refuse to listen to God
----------------------------------

**[EZK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:7)** *See also* [EXO 7:13](https://ref.ly/Exod7:13) *Pharaoh's refusal to listen*; [2KI 17:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:14), [2KI 17:40](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:40), [2KI 18:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:12); [NEH 9:17](https://ref.ly/Neh9:17); [PSA 81:11](https://ref.ly/Ps81:11); [ISA 65:12](https://ref.ly/Isa65:12)JER [7:13](https://ref.ly/Isa7:13), [JER 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Jer7:26-Jer7:27), [JER 12:17](https://ref.ly/Jer12:17), [JER 13:11](https://ref.ly/Jer13:11), [JER 17:23](https://ref.ly/Jer17:23), [JER 22:21](https://ref.ly/Jer22:21), [JER 25:3](https://ref.ly/Jer25:3), [JER 25:7](https://ref.ly/Jer25:7), [JER 29:19](https://ref.ly/Jer29:19), [JER 32:33](https://ref.ly/Jer32:33), [JER 34:14](https://ref.ly/Jer34:14), [JER 35:15](https://ref.ly/Jer35:15), [JER 35:17](https://ref.ly/Jer35:17), [JER 37:2](https://ref.ly/Jer37:2), [JER 38:15](https://ref.ly/Jer38:15), [JER 44:5](https://ref.ly/Jer44:5), [JER 44:16](https://ref.ly/Jer44:16)[EZK 20:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:8); [ZEC 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11-Zech7:12); [MAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:2); [LUK 16:29–16:31](https://ref.ly/Luke16:29-Luke16:31)

Those who refuse to listen to correction are condemned
------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:17)**

God's people must not only listen to God's word but also put it into practice
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 11:28](https://ref.ly/Luke11:28)**; **[JAS 1:22–1:24](https://ref.ly/Jas1:22-Jas1:24)** *In Hebrew and Greek the verb “listen” is related by the verb “to obey”.* *See also* [EZK 33:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:32); [MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27) parallel [LUK 6:47–6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:47-Luke6:49); [ROM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Rom2:13)

Examples of people listening
----------------------------

[GEN 18:10](https://ref.ly/Gen18:10) *Sarah at the tent entrance*; ; [GEN 27:5](https://ref.ly/Gen27:5) *Rebekah to Isaac*; ; [1SA 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:9-1Sam3:10) *Samuel to God*; ; [PRO 19:27](https://ref.ly/Prov19:27) *a son to instruction*; ; [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Luke2:46) *Jesus Christ to the teachers of the law*; [LUK 5:1](https://ref.ly/Luke5:1), [LUK 20:45](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45); [LUK 10:39](https://ref.ly/Luke10:39) *Mary to Jesus Christ*; [ACT 16:14](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14), [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25), [ACT 26:29](https://ref.ly/Acts26:29)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 3:10; 2 Chronicles 20:15; Psalms 81:8; Jeremiah 26:3-6; Micah 1:2; Acts 2:14; Acts 13:16; Acts 28:28; Matthew 17:5; Mark 9:7; Luke 9:35; John 10:27; Matthew 15:10; Mark 7:14; Luke 10:16; Luke 10:39; John 10:8; John 10:16; Proverbs 19:20; James 1:19; Psalms 34:11; Proverbs 5:7; Proverbs 7:24; Proverbs 8:32-34; Proverbs 12:15; Proverbs 13:1; Proverbs 15:31; Luke 2:46; James 2:5; Jeremiah 23:16; Deuteronomy 13:1-3; Deuteronomy 13:6-8; Jeremiah 27:9; Jeremiah 27:14-17; Jeremiah 29:8-9; Ezekiel 13:19; 1 John 4:1; 1 John 4:5-6; 2 John 1:10; Ezekiel 3:7; Exodus 7:13; 2 Kings 17:14; 2 Kings 17:40; 2 Kings 18:12; Nehemiah 9:17; Psalms 81:11; Isaiah 65:12; Isaiah 7:13; Jeremiah 7:26-27; Jeremiah 12:17; Jeremiah 13:11; Jeremiah 17:23; Jeremiah 22:21; Jeremiah 25:3; Jeremiah 25:7; Jeremiah 29:19; Jeremiah 32:33; Jeremiah 34:14; Jeremiah 35:15; Jeremiah 35:17; Jeremiah 37:2; Jeremiah 38:15; Jeremiah 44:5; Jeremiah 44:16; Ezekiel 20:8; Zechariah 7:11-12; Malachi 2:2; Luke 16:29-31; Matthew 18:15-17; Luke 11:28; James 1:22-24; Ezekiel 33:32; Matthew 7:24-27; Luke 6:47-49; Romans 2:13; Genesis 18:10; Genesis 27:5; 1 Samuel 3:9-10; Proverbs 19:27; Luke 5:1; Luke 20:45; Acts 16:14; Acts 16:25; Acts 26:29

## 5166: liver and kidneys (id: 5166)

The liver and kidneys as vital internal organs of the body
----------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 7:23](https://ref.ly/Prov7:23)** *See also* [JOB 16:13](https://ref.ly/Job16:13), [JOB 20:24–20:25](https://ref.ly/Job20:24-Job20:25)

The liver and kidneys are symbolic of human emotions and the conscience
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 73:21](https://ref.ly/Ps73:21)** *The Hebrew words for liver and kidneys are occasionally translated as “heart”, “spirit” or “inmost being”.* *See also* [JOB 19:27](https://ref.ly/Job19:27); [PRO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Prov23:16); [JER 12:2](https://ref.ly/Jer12:2); [LAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11), [LAM 3:13](https://ref.ly/Lam3:13)

### God examines the innermost being as symbolised by the kidneys

**[PSA 26:2](https://ref.ly/Ps26:2)** *See also* [PSA 7:9](https://ref.ly/Ps7:9); [JER 11:20](https://ref.ly/Jer11:20), [JER 17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:10), [JER 20:12](https://ref.ly/Jer20:12)

### God is concerned for the innermost being as symbolised by the kidneys

**[PSA 139:13](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13)** *See also* [PSA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ps16:7)

The liver and kidneys as offered in animal sacrifice
----------------------------------------------------

### Liver and kidneys offered in various sacrifices

**[LEV 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Lev3:3-Lev3:5)** *The liver and kidneys are associated with the fat as the choice parts of the animal.* *See also* [LEV 3:9–3:11](https://ref.ly/Lev3:9-Lev3:11), [LEV 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Lev3:14-Lev3:16), [LEV 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Lev4:8-Lev4:10), [LEV 7:3–7:6](https://ref.ly/Lev7:3-Lev7:6)

### Liver and kidneys had a central role in the ordination services for priests

[EXO 29:13](https://ref.ly/Exod29:13), [EXO 29:22–29:25](https://ref.ly/Exod29:22-Exod29:25); ; [LEV 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Lev8:16-Lev8:17), [LEV 8:25–8:28](https://ref.ly/Lev8:25-Lev8:28), [LEV 9:8–9:10](https://ref.ly/Lev9:8-Lev9:10), [LEV 9:19–9:21](https://ref.ly/Lev9:19-Lev9:21)

Divination by inspection of the liver
-------------------------------------

[EZK 21:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:21) *Divination by inspection of the liver (hepatoscopy) was a common practice. The future was predicted from the colour and configuration of the liver of a sacrificial animal, usually a sheep.*

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 7:23; Job 16:13; Job 20:24-25; Psalms 73:21; Job 19:27; Proverbs 23:16; Jeremiah 12:2; Lamentations 2:11; Lamentations 3:13; Psalms 26:2; Psalms 7:9; Jeremiah 11:20; Jeremiah 17:10; Jeremiah 20:12; Psalms 139:13; Psalms 16:7; Leviticus 3:3-5; Leviticus 3:9-11; Leviticus 3:14-16; Leviticus 4:8-10; Leviticus 7:3-6; Exodus 29:13; Exodus 29:22-25; Leviticus 8:16-17; Leviticus 8:25-28; Leviticus 9:8-10; Leviticus 9:19-21; Ezekiel 21:21
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4155: divination (ID: `4155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5008: conscience (ID: `5008@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5014: heart, human (ID: `5014@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5024: inner being (ID: `5024@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5038: mind, the human (ID: `5038@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5172: offal (ID: `5172@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5844: emotions (ID: `5844@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5858: fat (ID: `5858@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5167: mouth (id: 5167)

In addition to its normal physical functions, it has great potential for good and evil. The words of God's mouth are always effective. Mouth is also used in the sense of an entrance or opening.

The words of God's mouth
------------------------

### They are always effective

**[LAM 3:38](https://ref.ly/Lam3:38)** *See also* [ISA 55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:11)

### They are creative and life\-giving

**[DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3)** *See also* [PSA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Ps33:6); [MAT 4:4](https://ref.ly/Matt4:4)

### They bring judgment

**[HOS 6:5](https://ref.ly/Hos6:5)** *See also* [JOB 15:30](https://ref.ly/Job15:30); [2TH 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8); [REV 2:16](https://ref.ly/Rev2:16)

### They are reliable

**[NUM 23:19](https://ref.ly/Num23:19)** *See also* [1KI 8:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:15) parallel [2CH 6:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:4); [1KI 8:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:24) parallel [2CH 6:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:15); [HEB 6:13–6:18](https://ref.ly/Heb6:13-Heb6:18)

### They are to be treasured

**[PSA 119:72](https://ref.ly/Ps119:72)** *See also* [JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8); [JOB 23:12](https://ref.ly/Job23:12); [PSA 119:13–119:14](https://ref.ly/Ps119:13-Ps119:14), [PSA 119:103](https://ref.ly/Ps119:103)

God puts his words into the mouths of his people
------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 18:18](https://ref.ly/Deut18:18)** *See also* [EXO 4:12](https://ref.ly/Exod4:12), [EXO 4:15](https://ref.ly/Exod4:15); [NUM 22:38](https://ref.ly/Num22:38); [1KI 17:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:24); [ISA 49:2](https://ref.ly/Isa49:2); [JER 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jer1:7), [JER 1:9](https://ref.ly/Jer1:9); [EZK 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:1-Ezek3:4); [ACT 4:25](https://ref.ly/Acts4:25), [ACT 4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31)

The mouth used for eating and drinking
--------------------------------------

**[PRO 18:20](https://ref.ly/Prov18:20)** *See also* [JDG 7:5–7:6](https://ref.ly/Judg7:5-Judg7:6); [PSA 78:29–78:30](https://ref.ly/Ps78:29-Ps78:30); [PRO 19:24](https://ref.ly/Prov19:24), [PRO 30:20](https://ref.ly/Prov30:20); [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7); [EZK 4:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:14); [NAM 3:12](https://ref.ly/Nah3:12); [ACT 11:8](https://ref.ly/Acts11:8)

The mouths of animals
---------------------

### The danger they represent

[1SA 17:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:35); ; [JOB 41:14](https://ref.ly/Job41:14), [JOB 41:19](https://ref.ly/Job41:19), [JOB 41:21](https://ref.ly/Job41:21); ; [AMO 3:12](https://ref.ly/Amos3:12)

### Enemies described as dangerous animals

**[PSA 22:13](https://ref.ly/Ps22:13)** *See also* [PSA 22:21](https://ref.ly/Ps22:21); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

### God controls the mouths of dangerous animals

**[DAN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Dan6:22)** *See also* [NUM 22:28–22:30](https://ref.ly/Num22:28-Num22:30); [HEB 11:33](https://ref.ly/Heb11:33); [2PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16); [REV 12:15–12:16](https://ref.ly/Rev12:15-Rev12:16), [REV 16:13](https://ref.ly/Rev16:13)

The mouth used for speaking
---------------------------

### To speak what is good

**[PRO 10:11](https://ref.ly/Prov10:11)** *See also* [JOB 16:5](https://ref.ly/Job16:5); [PSA 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:14); [PRO 8:7](https://ref.ly/Prov8:7)

### To praise

**[PSA 71:8](https://ref.ly/Ps71:8)** *See also* [PSA 51:15](https://ref.ly/Ps51:15), [PSA 63:5](https://ref.ly/Ps63:5), [PSA 89:1](https://ref.ly/Ps89:1); [ROM 15:6](https://ref.ly/Rom15:6)

### To testify about God

**[PSA 71:15](https://ref.ly/Ps71:15)** *See also* [PSA 71:18](https://ref.ly/Ps71:18); [ROM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Rom10:10)

### To teach God's wisdom

**[PSA 49:3](https://ref.ly/Ps49:3)** *See also* [PSA 37:30](https://ref.ly/Ps37:30); [PRO 10:31](https://ref.ly/Prov10:31); [MAL 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6-Mal2:7); [MAT 13:35](https://ref.ly/Matt13:35); [PSA 78:2](https://ref.ly/Ps78:2)

### To speak what is bad

**[PSA 10:7](https://ref.ly/Ps10:7)** *See also* [1SA 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:3); [JOB 20:12](https://ref.ly/Job20:12); [PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9), [PSA 36:3](https://ref.ly/Ps36:3), [PSA 50:19](https://ref.ly/Ps50:19); [PRO 5:3](https://ref.ly/Prov5:3), [PRO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Prov7:21), [PRO 11:9](https://ref.ly/Prov11:9), [PRO 15:28](https://ref.ly/Prov15:28), [PRO 26:28](https://ref.ly/Prov26:28); [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17), [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13); [LUK 6:45](https://ref.ly/Luke6:45); [REV 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Rev13:5-Rev13:6)

Gestures involving the mouth
----------------------------

[JOB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Job3:1) *Opening the mouth signifies declaration*; ; [JOB 21:5](https://ref.ly/Job21:5) *Clapping the hand over the mouth signifies keeping silent from fear or astonishment*; ; [ACT 23:2](https://ref.ly/Acts23:2) *Striking the mouth signifies rebuke.*

God fills mouths
----------------

### With good things

**[PSA 81:10](https://ref.ly/Ps81:10)**

### With joy

**[PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2)** *See also* [EZR 3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:13); [JOB 8:21](https://ref.ly/Job8:21), [JOB 35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:10); [PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3), [PSA 132:16](https://ref.ly/Ps132:16), [PSA 149:6](https://ref.ly/Ps149:6); [ISA 35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6); [LUK 1:64](https://ref.ly/Luke1:64)

### With food

**[PSA 145:15](https://ref.ly/Ps145:15)** *See also* [NEH 9:20](https://ref.ly/Neh9:20); [PSA 78:27–78:30](https://ref.ly/Ps78:27-Ps78:30), [PSA 81:16](https://ref.ly/Ps81:16), [PSA 104:27–104:28](https://ref.ly/Ps104:27-Ps104:28); [LUK 1:53](https://ref.ly/Luke1:53)

---------------------------------

### The mouth of a well

### The mouth of the grave

[PSA 141:7](https://ref.ly/Ps141:7); ; [ISA 5:14](https://ref.ly/Isa5:14) *“the grave” is literally “Sheol”, the shadowy abode of the departed.*

### The mouth of a cave

[JOS 10:18](https://ref.ly/Josh10:18), [JOS 10:22](https://ref.ly/Josh10:22), [JOS 10:27](https://ref.ly/Josh10:27); ; [1KI 19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:13); ; [JER 48:28](https://ref.ly/Jer48:28)

### The mouth of a river

[JOS 15:5](https://ref.ly/Josh15:5), [JOS 18:19](https://ref.ly/Josh18:19); ; [ISA 19:7](https://ref.ly/Isa19:7)

### The mouth of the earth

## 5168: muteness (id: 5168)

The inability to speak or to make sounds comprehensible as speech.

Physical muteness
-----------------

### A disability, sometimes associated with demon\-possession, healed by Jesus Christ

**[MAT 9:32–9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32-Matt9:33)** *See also* [MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22) parallel [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14); [MAT 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30-Matt15:31); [MRK 7:31–7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:31-Mark7:37), [MRK 9:14–9:29](https://ref.ly/Mark9:14-Mark9:29)

### Healing as a Messianic sign

**[MAT 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22-Matt12:23)** *See also* [ISA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Isa32:4), [ISA 35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6); [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23), [MAT 9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:33)

God and muteness
----------------

### God gives and withholds speech

**[EXO 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Exod4:11-Exod4:12)** *See also* [EXO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10)

### God may impose muteness in judgment

**[LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20)** *See also* [LUK 1:64](https://ref.ly/Luke1:64)

### God may silence his prophets

**[EZK 3:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:26)** *See also* [EZK 24:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:27), [EZK 33:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:22)

The muteness of idols
---------------------

**[1CO 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:2)** *See also* [1KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26); [PSA 115:5](https://ref.ly/Ps115:5) parallel [PSA 135:16](https://ref.ly/Ps135:16); [ISA 41:26](https://ref.ly/Isa41:26), [ISA 41:28](https://ref.ly/Isa41:28), [ISA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Isa46:7); [JER 10:5](https://ref.ly/Jer10:5); [HAB 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/Hab2:18-Hab2:19)

Spiritual aspects of muteness and silence
-----------------------------------------

### Evil silenced by God

[JOB 5:16](https://ref.ly/Job5:16); ; [PSA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Ps63:11), [PSA 107:42](https://ref.ly/Ps107:42); ; [EZK 16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:63); ; [MRK 3:4](https://ref.ly/Mark3:4); ; [ROM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rom3:19)

### Evil silenced by godliness

[1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)

### Silence on earth

[HAB 2:20](https://ref.ly/Hab2:20)

### Silence in heaven

[REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rev8:1)

### God opens believers' mouths in praise

[PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3)

Voluntary muteness
------------------

### Of David

**[PSA 38:13–38:14](https://ref.ly/Ps38:13-Ps38:14)** *See also* [PSA 39:1–39:3](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1-Ps39:3), [PSA 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ps39:9)

## 5169: nakedness (id: 5169)

Nakedness was innocent before the fall
--------------------------------------

[GEN 2:25](https://ref.ly/Gen2:25)

The shame of nakedness a result of the fall
-------------------------------------------

[GEN 3:7–3:11](https://ref.ly/Gen3:7-Gen3:11)

Public exposure of nakedness is indicative of shame or humiliation
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)** *See also* [GEN 9:22–9:23](https://ref.ly/Gen9:22-Gen9:23); [EXO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Exod20:26); [ISA 47:3](https://ref.ly/Isa47:3); [EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8), [EZK 16:35–16:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:35-Ezek16:37); [HOS 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Hos2:9-Hos2:10); [AMO 2:16](https://ref.ly/Amos2:16); [NAM 3:5](https://ref.ly/Nah3:5); [HAB 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hab2:15); [REV 17:16](https://ref.ly/Rev17:16)

Exposure of nakedness is symbolic of adultery and prostitution
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 23:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:18)** *See also* [ISA 57:8](https://ref.ly/Isa57:8); [LAM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Lam1:8); [EZK 16:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:36)

Nakedness is symbolic of dire poverty
-------------------------------------

----------------------------------

### Human life is given and sustained through the nostrils

Animals snort and sniff through their noses
-------------------------------------------

The nose and sneezing
---------------------

[2KI 4:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:35)

The nose as an object of beauty
-------------------------------

**[SNG 7:4](https://ref.ly/Song7:4)**

### The nose may be adorned with rings

The nose is vulnerable to attack
--------------------------------

**[PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33)** *See also* [EZK 23:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:25)

Captives are led by rings or hooks in the nose
----------------------------------------------

Contempt is communicated by means of the nose

---------------------------------------------

**[2SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21)** *See also* [GEN 34:30](https://ref.ly/Gen34:30); [EXO 5:21](https://ref.ly/Exod5:21); [2SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:6) parallel [1CH 19:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:6)

**[ISA 65:5](https://ref.ly/Isa65:5)**

-----------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 15:8](https://ref.ly/Exod15:8)** *See also* [2SA 22:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:9) parallel [PSA 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ps18:8); [2SA 22:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:16) parallel [PSA 18:15](https://ref.ly/Ps18:15)

## 5172: offal (id: 5172)

The internal organs of an animal, traditionally regarded as being inferior to meat, and used as a symbol of disgrace.

Offal is symbolic of disgrace
-----------------------------

**[MAL 2:3](https://ref.ly/Mal2:3)** *See also* [NAM 3:6](https://ref.ly/Nah3:6)

In animal sacrifices the offal was removed and burnt outside the camp
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Exod29:14)** *See also* [LEV 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Lev4:11-Lev4:12), [LEV 8:17](https://ref.ly/Lev8:17), [LEV 16:27](https://ref.ly/Lev16:27); [NUM 19:3–19:5](https://ref.ly/Num19:3-Num19:5), [NUM 19:5](https://ref.ly/Num19:5)

Jesus Christ bears the believer's disgrace
------------------------------------------

[HEB 13:11–13:13](https://ref.ly/Heb13:11-Heb13:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Malachi 2:3; Nahum 3:6; Exodus 29:14; Leviticus 4:11-12; Leviticus 8:17; Leviticus 16:27; Numbers 19:3-5; Numbers 19:5; Hebrews 13:11-13
* **Associated Articles:** 2324: Jesus Christ, as Saviour (ID: `2324@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2525: Jesus Christ, cross of (ID: `2525@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5166: liver and kidneys (ID: `5166@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5836: disgrace (ID: `5836@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6027: sin, God's remedy for (ID: `6027@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6752: substitution (ID: `6752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5173: outward appearance (id: 5173)

That which is visible on the outside may reveal the true person, but is sometimes misleading. Judging by it and boasting in it are typical of sinful humanity. God sees the inner reality.

Outward appearance can reveal the truth
---------------------------------------

### Spiritually

**[MAT 7:16–7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:16-Matt7:20)** parallel [LUK 6:43–6:45](https://ref.ly/Luke6:43-Luke6:45) *See also* [MAT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:16), [MAT 12:33–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:33-Matt12:37); [JHN 15:8](https://ref.ly/John15:8); [JAS 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Jas2:17-Jas2:18)

### Emotionally

**[PRO 15:13](https://ref.ly/Prov15:13)** *See also* [1SA 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:16-1Sam1:18); [PRO 17:22](https://ref.ly/Prov17:22); [ECC 8:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:1); [DAN 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5-Dan5:6); [MRK 10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:22)

Outward appearance can be deceptive
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 23:25–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25-Matt23:28)** *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13), [ISA 53:2–53:3](https://ref.ly/Isa53:2-Isa53:3); [MAT 7:15](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15); [LUK 11:39–11:41](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39-Luke11:41); [2CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13-2Cor11:15); [COL 2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:23); [HEB 13:2](https://ref.ly/Heb13:2)

While people may judge by outward appearance, God does not
----------------------------------------------------------

### People take note of outward appearance

**[JHN 7:23–7:24](https://ref.ly/John7:23-John7:24)** *See also* [LUK 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:14-Luke16:15); [JHN 8:15](https://ref.ly/John8:15); [2CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:7); [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4); [REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)

### God looks at the heart

**[1SA 16:6–16:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:6-1Sam16:7)** *See also* [1KI 8:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:39) parallel [2CH 6:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:30); [PSA 139:1–139:2](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:2); [PRO 16:2](https://ref.ly/Prov16:2), [PRO 21:2](https://ref.ly/Prov21:2); [ISA 11:3–11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:3-Isa11:5); [LUK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:15); [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Gal2:6); [REV 2:23](https://ref.ly/Rev2:23)

The folly of boasting in outward appearance
-------------------------------------------

**[LUK 20:45–20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:47)** *See also* [MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2), [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5), [MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16); [2CO 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:12); [GAL 6:12](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12)

What is unseen is more important than outward appearance
--------------------------------------------------------

**[2CO 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:16-2Cor4:18)** *See also* [PSA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Ps51:6), [PSA 147:10–147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10-Ps147:11); [ROM 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:28-Rom2:29)

Beautiful physical appearance
-----------------------------

### Examples of the problems and opportunities caused by beautiful outward appearance

[GEN 12:11–12:15](https://ref.ly/Gen12:11-Gen12:15) *Sarah's beauty led to Abraham's folly*; ; [GEN 39:6–39:7](https://ref.ly/Gen39:6-Gen39:7) *Joseph's handsome appearance led to his being tempted by Potiphar's wife*; ; [1SA 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:2), [1SA 16:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:12), [1SA 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:3); ; [2SA 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:3) *Bathsheba's beauty tempted David*; ; [2SA 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:25-2Sam14:26); ; [EST 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Esth1:10-Esth1:11), [EST 2:7](https://ref.ly/Esth2:7) *Esther's beauty was an opportunity for God.*

### The cultivation of inner beauty encouraged

[1PE 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:3-1Pet3:5); ; [1TI 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:9-1Tim2:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 7:16-20; Luke 6:43-45; Matthew 5:16; Matthew 12:33-37; John 15:8; James 2:17-18; Proverbs 15:13; 1 Samuel 1:16-18; Proverbs 17:22; Ecclesiastes 8:1; Daniel 5:5-6; Mark 10:22; Matthew 23:25-28; Isaiah 29:13; Isaiah 53:2-3; Matthew 7:15; Luke 11:39-41; 2 Corinthians 11:13-15; Colossians 2:23; Hebrews 13:2; John 7:23-24; Luke 16:14-15; John 8:15; 2 Corinthians 10:7; James 2:1-4; Revelation 3:17; 1 Samuel 16:6-7; 1 Kings 8:39; 2 Chronicles 6:30; Psalms 139:1-2; Proverbs 16:2; Proverbs 21:2; Isaiah 11:3-5; Luke 16:15; Galatians 2:6; Revelation 2:23; Luke 20:45-47; Matthew 6:2; Matthew 6:5; Matthew 6:16; 2 Corinthians 5:12; Galatians 6:12; 2 Corinthians 4:16-18; Psalms 51:6; Psalms 147:10-11; Romans 2:28-29; Genesis 12:11-15; Genesis 39:6-7; 1 Samuel 9:2; 1 Samuel 16:12; 1 Samuel 25:3; 2 Samuel 11:2-3; 2 Samuel 14:25-26; Esther 1:10-11; Esther 2:7; 1 Peter 3:3-5; 1 Timothy 2:9-10
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4040: beauty (ID: `4040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5012: heart (ID: `5012@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5024: inner being (ID: `5024@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5840: eagerness (ID: `5840@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8784: nominal religion (ID: `8784@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5174: prostration (id: 5174)

The act of falling down upon one's face in front of someone, as a gesture of fear or reverence before God or a person of high rank.

Prostration before God
----------------------

### In reverence or fear

**[NUM 20:6](https://ref.ly/Num20:6)**; **[1CH 29:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:20)**; **[REV 7:11](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11)** *See also* [GEN 17:3](https://ref.ly/Gen17:3); [LEV 9:24](https://ref.ly/Lev9:24); [NUM 24:4](https://ref.ly/Num24:4); [1SA 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:3); [1KI 18:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:39); [NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6); [EZK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:28); [MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11), [MAT 17:6](https://ref.ly/Matt17:6), [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39); [LUK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12)

### As an expression of grieving over sin

[DEU 9:18](https://ref.ly/Deut9:18), [DEU 9:25](https://ref.ly/Deut9:25); ; [JOS 7:6](https://ref.ly/Josh7:6); ; [ISA 15:3](https://ref.ly/Isa15:3); ; [EZK 9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:8)

Prostration before God's messenger
----------------------------------

**[NUM 22:31](https://ref.ly/Num22:31)** *See also* [JOS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Josh5:14); [JDG 13:20](https://ref.ly/Judg13:20); [1CH 21:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:16); [DAN 8:17](https://ref.ly/Dan8:17); [LUK 24:5](https://ref.ly/Luke24:5)

Prostration before other people
-------------------------------

**[1SA 24:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:8)**; **[ISA 51:23](https://ref.ly/Isa51:23)** *See also* [RUT 2:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:10); [1SA 25:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:23), [1SA 28:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:14); [2SA 14:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:4), [2SA 19:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:18); [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23); [DAN 2:46](https://ref.ly/Dan2:46)

## 5175: reading (id: 5175)

The gaining of information through written documents. The Law was read regularly in the synagogue. Jesus Christ expected the Scriptures to be read. NT letters were read in the churches to which they were sent.

Reading of the Law to Israel
----------------------------

### By Moses

**[EXO 24:7](https://ref.ly/Exod24:7)** *See also* [DEU 31:10–31:11](https://ref.ly/Deut31:10-Deut31:11)

### By Joshua

[JOS 8:34–8:35](https://ref.ly/Josh8:34-Josh8:35)

### By Josiah

[2KI 23:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:2) parallel [2CH 34:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:30)

### In Ezra's time

[NEH 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2-Neh8:3), [NEH 8:18](https://ref.ly/Neh8:18), [NEH 9:3](https://ref.ly/Neh9:3), [NEH 13:1](https://ref.ly/Neh13:1)

Reading of the Law in the synagogue
-----------------------------------

**[ACT 15:21](https://ref.ly/Acts15:21)** *See also* [LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16); [ACT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Acts13:15), [ACT 13:27](https://ref.ly/Acts13:27); [2CO 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:14-2Cor3:15)

Other examples of reading
-------------------------

[DEU 17:18–17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18-Deut17:20); [JER 36:6–36:8](https://ref.ly/Jer36:6-Jer36:8), [JER 51:61](https://ref.ly/Jer51:61); [DAN 9:2](https://ref.ly/Dan9:2) *Daniel foresaw the end of the exile through reading the Scriptures.*

Jesus Christ expected the Scriptures to be read
-----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 12:3–12:5](https://ref.ly/Matt12:3-Matt12:5)** parallels [MRK 2:25–2:26](https://ref.ly/Mark2:25-Mark2:26), [LUK 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Luke6:3-Luke6:4) *See also* [MAT 19:4](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4), [MAT 21:16](https://ref.ly/Matt21:16), [MAT 21:42](https://ref.ly/Matt21:42) parallels [MRK 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:10-Mark12:11), [LUK 20:17](https://ref.ly/Luke20:17); [MAT 22:31](https://ref.ly/Matt22:31), [MAT 24:15](https://ref.ly/Matt24:15) parallel [MRK 13:14](https://ref.ly/Mark13:14)

Reading of NT letters
---------------------

**[COL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Col4:16)** *See also* [ACT 15:31](https://ref.ly/Acts15:31); [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:27)

Reasons for reading the Scriptures
----------------------------------

### They are God\-breathed

[2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16)

### God's word is true

[JHN 17:7](https://ref.ly/John17:7)

### To understand the truth about Jesus Christ

[LUK 24:26–24:27](https://ref.ly/Luke24:26-Luke24:27); ; [EPH 3:4](https://ref.ly/Eph3:4)

### To believe and be saved from God's wrath

[JHN 20:31](https://ref.ly/John20:31); ; [2TI 3:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:15)

### To learn how to serve and please God

[PSA 119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105), [PSA 119:130](https://ref.ly/Ps119:130); ; [2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16); ; [REV 1:3](https://ref.ly/Rev1:3)

### They teach Christians and give them hope

[ROM 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rom15:4); ; [1JN 2:1](https://ref.ly/1John2:1), [1JN 2:12–2:14](https://ref.ly/1John2:12-1John2:14), [1JN 2:21](https://ref.ly/1John2:21)

### They enable Christians to discern truth from error

**[ACT 18:28](https://ref.ly/Acts18:28)** *See also* [ACT 17:11](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11); [GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9); [2PE 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:14-2Pet3:16); [1JN 2:26](https://ref.ly/1John2:26), [1JN 4:1](https://ref.ly/1John4:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 24:7; Deuteronomy 31:10-11; Joshua 8:34-35; 2 Kings 23:2; 2 Chronicles 34:30; Nehemiah 8:2-3; Nehemiah 8:18; Nehemiah 9:3; Nehemiah 13:1; Acts 15:21; Luke 4:16; Acts 13:15; Acts 13:27; 2 Corinthians 3:14-15; Deuteronomy 17:18-20; Jeremiah 36:6-8; Jeremiah 51:61; Daniel 9:2; Matthew 12:3-5; Mark 2:25-26; Luke 6:3-4; Matthew 19:4; Matthew 21:16; Matthew 21:42; Mark 12:10-11; Luke 20:17; Matthew 22:31; Matthew 24:15; Mark 13:14; Colossians 4:16; Acts 15:31; 1 Thessalonians 5:27; 2 Timothy 3:16; John 17:7; Luke 24:26-27; Ephesians 3:4; John 20:31; 2 Timothy 3:15; Psalms 119:105; Psalms 119:130; Revelation 1:3; Romans 15:4; 1 John 2:1; 1 John 2:12-14; 1 John 2:21; Acts 18:28; Acts 17:11; Galatians 1:6-9; 2 Peter 3:14-16; 1 John 2:26; 1 John 4:1

## 5176: ring (id: 5176)

An ornament or a status symbol, sometimes used in sacred furnishings or as a charm.

Rings worn as jewellery
-----------------------

### Gold nose rings, worn especially by women

These were often given by a suitor as a symbol of betrothal: [GEN 24:22](https://ref.ly/Gen24:22), [GEN 24:30](https://ref.ly/Gen24:30), [GEN 24:47](https://ref.ly/Gen24:47); ; [PRO 11:22](https://ref.ly/Prov11:22); ; [ISA 3:21](https://ref.ly/Isa3:21); ; [EZK 16:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:12)

### The signet ring as a sign of authority

[GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42); ; [NUM 31:50](https://ref.ly/Num31:50); ; [EST 3:10](https://ref.ly/Esth3:10), [EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12), [EST 8:8](https://ref.ly/Esth8:8); ; [DAN 6:17](https://ref.ly/Dan6:17); [JER 22:24](https://ref.ly/Jer22:24); ; [HAG 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hag2:23)

### Gold ear\-rings, worn by men and women

Ear\-rings were sometimes used as charms or amulets: [GEN 35:4](https://ref.ly/Gen35:4); ; [EXO 32:2–32:3](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:3), [EXO 35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:22); ; [JDG 8:24–8:25](https://ref.ly/Judg8:24-Judg8:25)

Rings as indications of wealth or status
----------------------------------------

[JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11); ; [HOS 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:13); ; [LUK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22); ; [JAS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2)

Rings used in sacred furnishings
--------------------------------

### On the ark of the covenant

[EXO 25:12–25:15](https://ref.ly/Exod25:12-Exod25:15)

### On the table

[EXO 25:26–25:27](https://ref.ly/Exod25:26-Exod25:27)

### In the tabernacle

[EXO 26:24–26:29](https://ref.ly/Exod26:24-Exod26:29)

### On priestly garments

[EXO 28:23–28:24](https://ref.ly/Exod28:23-Exod28:24)

### For carrying altars

[EXO 27:4–27:7](https://ref.ly/Exod27:4-Exod27:7), [EXO 30:4](https://ref.ly/Exod30:4), [EXO 37:27](https://ref.ly/Exod37:27)

## 5177: robes (id: 5177)

A formal style of clothing, often indicating priesthood, royalty or elevated social status.

Priestly robes
--------------

### Aaron's priestly robes

**[EXO 28:4](https://ref.ly/Exod28:4)** *See also* [EXO 28:31–28:34](https://ref.ly/Exod28:31-Exod28:34), [EXO 29:5](https://ref.ly/Exod29:5), [EXO 39:22–39:26](https://ref.ly/Exod39:22-Exod39:26); [LEV 8:7](https://ref.ly/Lev8:7); [PSA 133:2](https://ref.ly/Ps133:2)

Robes indicating office, favour or wealth
-----------------------------------------

Robes associated with righteousness and purity
----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10)** *See also* [REV 6:11](https://ref.ly/Rev6:11), [REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Rev7:13-Rev7:14), [REV 22:14](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14)

Tearing of robes to show mourning or repentance
-----------------------------------------------

**[JOB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Job2:12)** *See also* [2CH 34:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:27)
