## 5178: running (id: 5178)

Scripture gives pictures of running in both its literal and metaphorical senses, the image of the Christian life as a race being particularly significant.

Running away
------------

### Fleeing in battle

**[EXO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Exod23:27)** *See also* [LEV 26:36–26:37](https://ref.ly/Lev26:36-Lev26:37); [JOS 7:12](https://ref.ly/Josh7:12), [JOS 8:3–8:19](https://ref.ly/Josh8:3-Josh8:19); [1SA 14:20–14:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:20-1Sam14:22)

### Escaping from danger

**[PRO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Prov18:10)** *See also* [GEN 19:12–19:22](https://ref.ly/Gen19:12-Gen19:22); [NEH 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Neh6:10-Neh6:11); [ISA 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:3); [JER 17:16](https://ref.ly/Jer17:16), [JER 48:4–48:6](https://ref.ly/Jer48:4-Jer48:6), [JER 51:6](https://ref.ly/Jer51:6), [JER 51:44–51:45](https://ref.ly/Jer51:44-Jer51:45); [AMO 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Amos2:14-Amos2:16); [JHN 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/John10:12-John10:13)

### Running away from individuals

**[1SA 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:11-1Sam19:12)** *See also* [GEN 16:6–16:8](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6-Gen16:8), [GEN 31:20–31:21](https://ref.ly/Gen31:20-Gen31:21), [GEN 39:11–39:15](https://ref.ly/Gen39:11-Gen39:15); [JHN 10:2–10:5](https://ref.ly/John10:2-John10:5)

### Running away from God

[JON 1:1–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:10)

### Running away from evil

**[2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:22)** *See also* [1CO 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:18), [1CO 10:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:14); [1TI 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11)

Running after people or things
------------------------------

**[PSA 16:4](https://ref.ly/Ps16:4)** *See also* [RUT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:10); [ISA 5:11](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11); [JER 2:23–2:25](https://ref.ly/Jer2:23-Jer2:25); [EZK 16:32–16:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:32-Ezek16:34); [MAT 6:28–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:28-Matt6:34) parallel [LUK 12:27–12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27-Luke12:31)

Running in eagerness
--------------------

**[JHN 20:1–20:4](https://ref.ly/John20:1-John20:4)** *See also* [2CH 23:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:12); [MRK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25); [LUK 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Luke17:22-Luke17:23); [ACT 3:11](https://ref.ly/Acts3:11), [ACT 21:30](https://ref.ly/Acts21:30)

Running swiftly
---------------

**[JOB 9:25](https://ref.ly/Job9:25)** *See also* [JOB 39:13–39:18](https://ref.ly/Job39:13-Job39:18); [ECC 9:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:11); [JER 12:5](https://ref.ly/Jer12:5)

Heralds running ahead of a dignitary
------------------------------------

**[1SA 8:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11)** *See also* [2SA 15:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1); [1KI 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:5), [1KI 18:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:46)

Messages carried by runners
---------------------------

**[2SA 18:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:19)** *See also* [2SA 18:21–18:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:21-2Sam18:32); [2KI 4:25–4:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:25-2Kgs4:31), [2KI 5:20–5:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:20-2Kgs5:23), [2KI 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1-2Kgs9:3); [PSA 147:15](https://ref.ly/Ps147:15); [JER 23:21](https://ref.ly/Jer23:21); [HAB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hab2:2); [ZEC 2:3–2:5](https://ref.ly/Zech2:3-Zech2:5)

The life of the believer likened to running a race
--------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:2)** *See also* [PSA 119:32](https://ref.ly/Ps119:32); [PRO 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Prov4:10-Prov4:12); [ISA 40:30–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:30-Isa40:31); [ACT 20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:24); [1CO 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:27); [GAL 5:7](https://ref.ly/Gal5:7); [PHP 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14-Phil2:16); [2TI 4:6–4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:6-2Tim4:8); [HEB 12:1](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1); [2JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/2John1:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 23:27; Leviticus 26:36-37; Joshua 7:12; Joshua 8:3-19; 1 Samuel 14:20-22; Proverbs 18:10; Genesis 19:12-22; Nehemiah 6:10-11; Isaiah 10:1-3; Jeremiah 17:16; Jeremiah 48:4-6; Jeremiah 51:6; Jeremiah 51:44-45; Amos 2:14-16; John 10:12-13; 1 Samuel 19:11-12; Genesis 16:6-8; Genesis 31:20-21; Genesis 39:11-15; John 10:2-5; Jonah 1:1-10; 2 Timothy 2:22; 1 Corinthians 6:18; 1 Corinthians 10:14; 1 Timothy 6:11; Psalms 16:4; Ruth 3:10; Isaiah 5:11; Jeremiah 2:23-25; Ezekiel 16:32-34; Matthew 6:28-34; Luke 12:27-31; John 20:1-4; 2 Chronicles 23:12; Mark 9:25; Luke 17:22-23; Acts 3:11; Acts 21:30; Job 9:25; Job 39:13-18; Ecclesiastes 9:11; Jeremiah 12:5; 1 Samuel 8:11; 2 Samuel 15:1; 1 Kings 1:5; 1 Kings 18:46; 2 Samuel 18:19; 2 Samuel 18:21-32; 2 Kings 4:25-31; 2 Kings 5:20-23; 2 Kings 9:1-3; Psalms 147:15; Jeremiah 23:21; Habakkuk 2:2; Zechariah 2:3-5; Galatians 2:2; Psalms 119:32; Proverbs 4:10-12; Isaiah 40:30-31; Acts 20:24; 1 Corinthians 9:24-27; Galatians 5:7; Philippians 2:14-16; 2 Timothy 4:6-8; Hebrews 12:1; 2 John 1:9
* **Associated Articles:** 5197: walking (ID: `5197@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5280: crown (ID: `5280@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5319: fugitives (ID: `5319@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5335: herald (ID: `5335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5342: hunting (ID: `5342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5408: messenger (ID: `5408@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8110: athletics (ID: `8110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8140: prize (ID: `8140@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5179: sandal (id: 5179)

A form of open shoe worn in warm climates, and the only form of foot\-covering to be of major importance in Scripture. Both the wearing and the removal of sandals is often seen as symbolic; to go barefoot was often a sign of shame, mourning or poverty.

Sandals were normally worn by an official, soldier or messenger
---------------------------------------------------------------

[1KI 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:5); ; [EPH 6:15](https://ref.ly/Eph6:15)

Untying and carrying sandals was the task of a servant
------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 3:11](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11)** parallels [MRK 1:7](https://ref.ly/Mark1:7), [LUK 3:16](https://ref.ly/Luke3:16), [JHN 1:27](https://ref.ly/John1:27) *John considers himself unworthy even to be a slave of Jesus Christ.* *See also* [ACT 13:25](https://ref.ly/Acts13:25)

The removal of sandals as a gesture
-----------------------------------

### As a mark of respect in the presence of God

**[EXO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Exod3:5)** *See also* [JOS 5:15](https://ref.ly/Josh5:15); [ACT 7:33](https://ref.ly/Acts7:33)

### To show rejection of any claim to property

[DEU 25:9–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:9-Deut25:10); ; [RUT 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:7-Ruth4:8); ; [PSA 60:8](https://ref.ly/Ps60:8) parallel [PSA 108:9](https://ref.ly/Ps108:9)

### To predict the defeat and disgrace of Egypt and Assyria

[ISA 20:2–20:4](https://ref.ly/Isa20:2-Isa20:4)

### As a sign of mourning and repentance

[2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30); ; [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17), [EZK 24:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:23); ; [MIC 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mic1:8)

The symbolic use of sandals
---------------------------

### As symbols of readiness for action

**[EXO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Exod12:11)**

### As symbols of oppression and injustice

*See also* [AMO 2:6](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6), [AMO 8:6](https://ref.ly/Amos8:6)

### Fine sandals as a status symbol

[EZK 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:10); ; [LUK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22)

Sandals as an example of the need for minimum possessions in God's service
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 10:10](https://ref.ly/Matt10:10) parallels [MRK 6:9](https://ref.ly/Mark6:9), [LUK 10:4](https://ref.ly/Luke10:4); ; [LUK 22:35](https://ref.ly/Luke22:35)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 2:5; Ephesians 6:15; Matthew 3:11; Mark 1:7; Luke 3:16; John 1:27; Acts 13:25; Exodus 3:5; Joshua 5:15; Acts 7:33; Deuteronomy 25:9-10; Ruth 4:7-8; Psalms 60:8; Psalms 108:9; Isaiah 20:2-4; 2 Samuel 15:30; Ezekiel 24:17; Ezekiel 24:23; Micah 1:8; Exodus 12:11; Amos 2:6; Amos 8:6; Ezekiel 16:10; Luke 15:22; Matthew 10:10; Mark 6:9; Luke 10:4; Luke 22:35
* **Associated Articles:** 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5151: feet (ID: `5151@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5197: walking (ID: `5197@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5180: shaving (id: 5180)

The removal of hair with a razor or other sharp implement. Both male and female Israelites allowed their hair to grow long. Barbers trimmed, but did not crop, men's hair, so giving special significance to the shaving of the head or whole body. These actions indicated shame or grief.

Personal toilet
---------------

[GEN 41:14](https://ref.ly/Gen41:14); ; [2SA 14:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:26)

Symbolic shaving
----------------

### Shaved head a sign of grief

**[JOB 1:20](https://ref.ly/Job1:20)** *See also* [DEU 21:10–21:14](https://ref.ly/Deut21:10-Deut21:14); [ISA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2); [JER 47:5](https://ref.ly/Jer47:5), [JER 48:36–48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:36-Jer48:37); [EZK 27:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:31); [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10); [MIC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mic1:16); [JER 7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:29), [JER 41:5](https://ref.ly/Jer41:5)

### A shaved head a sign of shame

**[2SA 10:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4)** parallel [1CH 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:4) *See also* [EZR 9:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:3); [ISA 7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:20), [ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6); [JER 2:16](https://ref.ly/Jer2:16); [EZK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:18); [1CO 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:5-1Cor11:6)

Shaving in ritual cleansing
---------------------------

[LEV 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8-Lev14:9); ; [NUM 8:7](https://ref.ly/Num8:7); ; [ACT 21:24](https://ref.ly/Acts21:24)

Shaving as a visual aid
-----------------------

[EZK 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:1-Ezek5:4)

After a period of dedication
----------------------------

[NUM 6:18](https://ref.ly/Num6:18)

Shaving forbidden
-----------------

### As a pagan practice

**[LEV 19:27](https://ref.ly/Lev19:27), [LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5)** *See also* [DEU 14:1](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1) *Pagans shaved the front of their heads as a sign of mourning. God's people should have a different attitude to bereavement from those who have no hope*; [EZK 44:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:20)

### For the sake of a Nazarite vow

**[NUM 6:5](https://ref.ly/Num6:5)** *See also* [JDG 16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17); [1SA 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 41:14; 2 Samuel 14:26; Job 1:20; Deuteronomy 21:10-14; Isaiah 15:2; Jeremiah 47:5; Jeremiah 48:36-37; Ezekiel 27:31; Amos 8:10; Micah 1:16; Jeremiah 7:29; Jeremiah 41:5; 2 Samuel 10:4; 1 Chronicles 19:4; Ezra 9:3; Isaiah 7:20; Isaiah 50:6; Jeremiah 2:16; Ezekiel 7:18; 1 Corinthians 11:5-6; Leviticus 14:8-9; Numbers 8:7; Acts 21:24; Ezekiel 5:1-4; Numbers 6:18; Leviticus 19:27; Leviticus 21:5; Deuteronomy 14:1; Ezekiel 44:20; Numbers 6:5; Judges 16:17; 1 Samuel 1:11
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5128: baldness (ID: `5128@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5155: hair (ID: `5155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5372: knife (ID: `5372@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5836: disgrace (ID: `5836@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5938: sadness (ID: `5938@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5952: sorrow (ID: `5952@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7342: cleanliness (ID: `7342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7424: ritual law (ID: `7424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8218: consecration (ID: `8218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8223: dedication (ID: `8223@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5181: sitting (id: 5181)

A bodily posture associated with resting, eating or instruction. When used of God, the image often conveys the idea of kingly authority over creation.

Sitting to rest
---------------

**[GEN 18:1](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1)**; **[JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6)** *See also* [EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15), [EXO 17:12](https://ref.ly/Exod17:12); [1KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:4); [MAT 26:36](https://ref.ly/Matt26:36)

Sitting to eat
--------------

**[EXO 32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:6)**; **[LUK 17:7](https://ref.ly/Luke17:7)** *See also* [JDG 19:6](https://ref.ly/Judg19:6); [RUT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:14); [1SA 20:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:24); [PRO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Prov23:1); [JER 16:8](https://ref.ly/Jer16:8); [MAT 14:19](https://ref.ly/Matt14:19); [1CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:7)

Sitting to teach or to learn
----------------------------

**[MAT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Matt5:1-Matt5:2)** *See also* [MAT 13:2](https://ref.ly/Matt13:2); [MRK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Mark9:35); [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Luke2:46), [LUK 10:39](https://ref.ly/Luke10:39); [JHN 8:2](https://ref.ly/John8:2)

Sitting for prayer
------------------

**[JDG 21:2](https://ref.ly/Judg21:2)** *See also* [2SA 7:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:18)

Sitting as a sign of authority
------------------------------

### Human authority

**[EXO 11:5](https://ref.ly/Exod11:5)** *See also* [1KI 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:13), [1KI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:6); [2KI 10:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:30); [ZEC 6:13](https://ref.ly/Zech6:13); [MRK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Mark10:37)

### Divine authority

**[PSA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Ps29:10)** *See also* [1KI 22:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:19); [PSA 47:8](https://ref.ly/Ps47:8), [PSA 80:1](https://ref.ly/Ps80:1); [ISA 6:1](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1); [MAT 26:64](https://ref.ly/Matt26:64); [LUK 20:42](https://ref.ly/Luke20:42); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3), [HEB 10:12](https://ref.ly/Heb10:12), [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2); [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21), [REV 4:10](https://ref.ly/Rev4:10), [REV 7:10](https://ref.ly/Rev7:10)

Sitting to judge
----------------

**[EXO 18:13](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13)**; **[MAT 25:31](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31)** *See also* [2SA 19:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:8); [PRO 20:8](https://ref.ly/Prov20:8); [ISA 28:6](https://ref.ly/Isa28:6); [JER 38:7](https://ref.ly/Jer38:7); [DAN 7:26](https://ref.ly/Dan7:26); [JOL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Joel3:12); [MAT 27:19](https://ref.ly/Matt27:19); [LUK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:30); [ACT 23:3](https://ref.ly/Acts23:3)

Sitting as a sign of possession and peace
-----------------------------------------

**[MIC 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:4)** *See also* [ZEC 3:10](https://ref.ly/Zech3:10)

Jesus Christ seated at God's right hand
---------------------------------------

[LUK 22:69](https://ref.ly/Luke22:69); ; [EPH 1:20](https://ref.ly/Eph1:20), [EPH 2:6](https://ref.ly/Eph2:6); ; [COL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Col3:1)

## 5182: skin (id: 5182)

The outer covering of the body. Diseased skin is often subject to pain and regarded as unclean.

Skin as the outer covering of the body
--------------------------------------

**[JOB 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/Job10:10-Job10:11)** *See also* [GEN 3:21](https://ref.ly/Gen3:21), [GEN 27:11](https://ref.ly/Gen27:11); [EZK 37:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:6), [EZK 37:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:8)

Violence against the skin
-------------------------

**[MIC 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Mic3:2-Mic3:3)** *See also* [JOB 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Job2:4-Job2:5)

Skin and bones
--------------

**[PSA 102:5](https://ref.ly/Ps102:5)** *To be reduced to skin and bones is a mark of severe suffering.* *See also* [NUM 4:8–4:14](https://ref.ly/Num4:8-Num4:14) *animal skins used for covering the ark of the Testimony*; [JOB 19:20](https://ref.ly/Job19:20); [LAM 3:4](https://ref.ly/Lam3:4), [LAM 4:8](https://ref.ly/Lam4:8)

Skin diseases
-------------

### Skin diseases are painful

**[JOB 30:30](https://ref.ly/Job30:30)** *See also* [JOB 7:5](https://ref.ly/Job7:5), [JOB 18:13](https://ref.ly/Job18:13); [LAM 5:10](https://ref.ly/Lam5:10)

### They can be defiling

**[NUM 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Num5:2-Num5:3)** *See also* [LEV 13:1–13:11](https://ref.ly/Lev13:1-Lev13:11), [LEV 13:43–13:46](https://ref.ly/Lev13:43-Lev13:46), [LEV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4)

### As God's punishment for sin

[NUM 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Num12:10-Num12:11) *Miriam*; ; [2KI 5:26–5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:26-2Kgs5:27) *Naaman*; ; [2CH 26:19–26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19-2Chr26:20) *Uzziah*

### Healed by Jesus Christ

[MAT 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:3) parallels [MRK 1:40–1:42](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40-Mark1:42), [LUK 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12-Luke5:13); ; [LUK 17:12–17:14](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12-Luke17:14)

Skin cannot be changed
----------------------

**[JER 13:23](https://ref.ly/Jer13:23)**

A person can know God even when the skin has been destroyed
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/Job19:26-Job19:27)**

* **Associated Passages:** Job 10:10-11; Genesis 3:21; Genesis 27:11; Ezekiel 37:6; Ezekiel 37:8; Micah 3:2-3; Job 2:4-5; Psalms 102:5; Numbers 4:8-14; Job 19:20; Lamentations 3:4; Lamentations 4:8; Job 30:30; Job 7:5; Job 18:13; Lamentations 5:10; Numbers 5:2-3; Leviticus 13:1-11; Leviticus 13:43-46; Leviticus 22:4; Numbers 12:10-11; 2 Kings 5:26-27; 2 Chronicles 26:19-20; Matthew 8:2-3; Mark 1:40-42; Luke 5:12-13; Luke 17:12-14; Jeremiah 13:23; Job 19:26-27

## 5183: smell (id: 5183)

An aroma; God is pleased by the aroma of sacrifice, whether a literal sacrifice or the figurative offering of Christian service is a picture of its acceptability to him. Bad smells can indicate physical decay or the results of judgment. To be a stench to someone is to be held in contempt or hated by that person.

The aroma of sacrifice
----------------------

**[GEN 8:21](https://ref.ly/Gen8:21)** *See also* [LEV 1:9](https://ref.ly/Lev1:9), [LEV 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:2), [LEV 3:5](https://ref.ly/Lev3:5), [LEV 4:31](https://ref.ly/Lev4:31), [LEV 6:15](https://ref.ly/Lev6:15), [LEV 6:21](https://ref.ly/Lev6:21), [LEV 8:21](https://ref.ly/Lev8:21), [LEV 23:13](https://ref.ly/Lev23:13), [LEV 23:18](https://ref.ly/Lev23:18); [NUM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Num15:14), [NUM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Num15:24) *offerings to be made for unintentional breaking of the LORD's commandments*; [NUM 18:17](https://ref.ly/Num18:17), [NUM 28:2](https://ref.ly/Num28:2), [NUM 29:2](https://ref.ly/Num29:2)

### Substances to create a pleasing smell

**[EXO 30:7–30:8](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7-Exod30:8)** *See also* [EXO 30:22–30:29](https://ref.ly/Exod30:22-Exod30:29), [EXO 30:34–30:38](https://ref.ly/Exod30:34-Exod30:38), [EXO 35:28](https://ref.ly/Exod35:28), [EXO 37:29](https://ref.ly/Exod37:29), [EXO 40:26–40:27](https://ref.ly/Exod40:26-Exod40:27); [NUM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Num4:16); [2CH 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:4), [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11)

### The aroma of sacrifice is not always pleasing to God

[LEV 26:31](https://ref.ly/Lev26:31); ; [EZK 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:11-Ezek8:13), [EZK 20:27–20:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:27-Ezek20:29)

### Jesus Christ was the supreme fragrant offering

**[EPH 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eph5:2)**

God accepts his people as fragrant incense
------------------------------------------

**[EZK 20:41](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:41)** *See also* [HOS 14:5–14:6](https://ref.ly/Hos14:5-Hos14:6)

A pleasing aroma depicts acceptable Christian service
-----------------------------------------------------

**[2CO 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14-2Cor2:16)** *See also* [PHP 4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:18)

Fragrance associated with the expression of love
------------------------------------------------

**[SNG 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Song1:2-Song1:3), [SNG 4:16–5:1](https://ref.ly/Song4:16-Song5:1)** *The beloved's garden is an image of her body.* *See also* [PSA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8); [SNG 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Song1:12-Song1:13), [SNG 3:6](https://ref.ly/Song3:6), [SNG 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:10-Song4:11), [SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13), [SNG 7:8](https://ref.ly/Song7:8)**[JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3)**

A bad smell
-----------

### Indicating decay

**[JHN 11:39](https://ref.ly/John11:39)** *See also* [EXO 16:20](https://ref.ly/Exod16:20) *Manna soon smelled bad if kept. This meant the Israelites had to trust God afresh for each day's supply.*

### As the result of God's judgment

The first plague on Egypt, which made the Nile smell bad: [EXO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Exod7:18), [EXO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:21); [ISA 34:3](https://ref.ly/Isa34:3); ; [AMO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Amos4:10)

### As a picture of the results of judgment

[ISA 3:24](https://ref.ly/Isa3:24), [ISA 19:6](https://ref.ly/Isa19:6); ; [JOL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Joel2:20)

### As a figurative cause of contempt or hatred

[GEN 34:30](https://ref.ly/Gen34:30); ; [EXO 5:21](https://ref.ly/Exod5:21); [1SA 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:4); ; [2SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:6) parallel [1CH 19:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:6); ; [2SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21)

Idols cannot smell
------------------

**[PSA 115:4–115:6](https://ref.ly/Ps115:4-Ps115:6)** *See also* [DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 8:21; Leviticus 1:9; Leviticus 2:2; Leviticus 3:5; Leviticus 4:31; Leviticus 6:15; Leviticus 6:21; Leviticus 8:21; Leviticus 23:13; Leviticus 23:18; Numbers 15:14; Numbers 15:24; Numbers 18:17; Numbers 28:2; Numbers 29:2; Exodus 30:7-8; Exodus 30:22-29; Exodus 30:34-38; Exodus 35:28; Exodus 37:29; Exodus 40:26-27; Numbers 4:16; 2 Chronicles 2:4; 2 Chronicles 13:11; Leviticus 26:31; Ezekiel 8:11-13; Ezekiel 20:27-29; Ephesians 5:2; Ezekiel 20:41; Hosea 14:5-6; 2 Corinthians 2:14-16; Philippians 4:18; Song of Songs 1:2-3; Song of Songs 4:16-5:1; Psalms 45:8; Song of Songs 1:12-13; Song of Songs 3:6; Song of Songs 4:10-11; Song of Songs 5:13; Song of Songs 7:8; John 12:3; John 11:39; Exodus 16:20; Exodus 7:18; Exodus 7:21; Isaiah 34:3; Amos 4:10; Isaiah 3:24; Isaiah 19:6; Joel 2:20; Genesis 34:30; Exodus 5:21; 1 Samuel 13:4; 2 Samuel 10:6; 1 Chronicles 19:6; 2 Samuel 16:21; Psalms 115:4-6; Deuteronomy 4:28
* **Associated Articles:** 4490: ointment (ID: `4490@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4496: perfume (ID: `4496@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5171: nose (ID: `5171@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6142: decay (ID: `6142@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6206: offence (ID: `6206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7386: incense (ID: `7386@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7434: sacrifice (ID: `7434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5184: standing (id: 5184)

The bodily posture of being in a stationary upright position.

Standing as a sign of authority
-------------------------------

**[ACT 7:55](https://ref.ly/Acts7:55)**

Standing merely to wait or rest
-------------------------------

**[EXO 33:21](https://ref.ly/Exod33:21)** *See also* [JOS 3:8](https://ref.ly/Josh3:8); [1KI 20:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:38); [MAT 12:47](https://ref.ly/Matt12:47), [MAT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Matt20:3), [MAT 26:73](https://ref.ly/Matt26:73); [LUK 19:24](https://ref.ly/Luke19:24); [JHN 8:9](https://ref.ly/John8:9)

Standing ready for action
-------------------------

**[NUM 22:23](https://ref.ly/Num22:23)**; **[ACT 5:23](https://ref.ly/Acts5:23)** *See also* [DEU 10:8](https://ref.ly/Deut10:8); [JOS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Josh5:13); [1SA 17:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:16); [1CH 21:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:16); [LUK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Luke1:11); [ACT 16:9](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9); [REV 7:1](https://ref.ly/Rev7:1)

Standing to show respect or honour
----------------------------------

**[DAN 2:2](https://ref.ly/Dan2:2)** *See also* [1KI 10:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:8), [1KI 22:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:19); [2CH 6:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:3); [NEH 8:4](https://ref.ly/Neh8:4); [JER 36:21](https://ref.ly/Jer36:21); [DAN 10:11](https://ref.ly/Dan10:11)

Standing in the presence of God
-------------------------------

### In heaven

**[LUK 1:19](https://ref.ly/Luke1:19)** *See also* [ZEC 6:5](https://ref.ly/Zech6:5); [REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 8:2](https://ref.ly/Rev8:2)

### On earth

**[GEN 19:27](https://ref.ly/Gen19:27)**; **[EXO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Exod3:5)**; **[NEH 9:5](https://ref.ly/Neh9:5)** *See also* [EXO 33:10](https://ref.ly/Exod33:10); [DEU 4:10](https://ref.ly/Deut4:10), [DEU 29:10](https://ref.ly/Deut29:10); [1SA 1:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:26); [1CH 23:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:30); [2CH 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:9); [PSA 24:3](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3); [JER 28:5](https://ref.ly/Jer28:5); [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5); [MRK 11:25](https://ref.ly/Mark11:25); [LUK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11); [ROM 14:4](https://ref.ly/Rom14:4)

Standing for trial or judgment
------------------------------

**[EXO 18:13](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13)**; **[ROM 14:10](https://ref.ly/Rom14:10)** *See also* [NUM 5:16](https://ref.ly/Num5:16), [NUM 35:12](https://ref.ly/Num35:12); [DEU 19:17](https://ref.ly/Deut19:17); [1SA 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:3), [1SA 12:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:7); [ZEC 3:1](https://ref.ly/Zech3:1); [MAT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11); [LUK 11:32](https://ref.ly/Luke11:32); [ACT 23:6](https://ref.ly/Acts23:6), [ACT 25:10](https://ref.ly/Acts25:10); [REV 20:12](https://ref.ly/Rev20:12)

Standing firm as a believer
---------------------------

**[PRO 10:25](https://ref.ly/Prov10:25)**; **[1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58)** *See also* [LUK 21:28](https://ref.ly/Luke21:28); [ROM 5:2](https://ref.ly/Rom5:2), [ROM 11:20](https://ref.ly/Rom11:20); [1CO 15:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:1), [1CO 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:13); [2CO 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:21), [2CO 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:24); [GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1); [PHP 4:1](https://ref.ly/Phil4:1); [COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12); [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15); [JAS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Jas5:8); [1PE 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:12)

### Against evil and temptation

[1CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:13); ; [EPH 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:11-Eph6:14)

### Against enemies and opposition

**[PSA 94:16](https://ref.ly/Ps94:16)** *See also* [EXO 14:13](https://ref.ly/Exod14:13); [2CH 20:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:17); [ISA 7:9](https://ref.ly/Isa7:9); [JER 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jer1:17); [EPH 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:11-Eph6:14); [PHP 1:27](https://ref.ly/Phil1:27)

Other metaphorical uses of standing
-----------------------------------

### God's purpose will stand

**[ISA 46:10](https://ref.ly/Isa46:10)** *See also* [ISA 14:24](https://ref.ly/Isa14:24)

### Standing in the gap

**[EZK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:30)** *See also* [PSA 106:23](https://ref.ly/Ps106:23)

## 5185: stomach (id: 5185)

As well as the physical stomach, the term is used in association with God's judgment or in a figurative sense.

Stomach as part of the physical body
------------------------------------

**[1CO 6:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:13)** *See also* [JOB 40:16](https://ref.ly/Job40:16); [SNG 7:2](https://ref.ly/Song7:2); [DAN 2:32](https://ref.ly/Dan2:32)

### Jonah in the stomach of a huge fish

**[MAT 12:40](https://ref.ly/Matt12:40)** *See also* [JON 1:17–2:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:17-Jonah2:1)

Ritual laws concerning the stomach
----------------------------------

**[MRK 7:18–7:19](https://ref.ly/Mark7:18-Mark7:19)** parallel [MAT 15:17](https://ref.ly/Matt15:17) *See also* [LEV 11:41–11:45](https://ref.ly/Lev11:41-Lev11:45)

Afflictions of the stomach
--------------------------

[LUK 15:16](https://ref.ly/Luke15:16) *hunger*; ; [1TI 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:23) *illness*

Fatal wounding in the stomach
-----------------------------

[JDG 3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:21); ; [2SA 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:23), [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27), [2SA 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:6), [2SA 20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:10)

The stomach and God's judgment
------------------------------

### Severe stomach pain

**[JER 30:6–30:7](https://ref.ly/Jer30:6-Jer30:7)** *See also* [NUM 5:20–5:22](https://ref.ly/Num5:20-Num5:22), [NUM 5:27](https://ref.ly/Num5:27)

### Crawling on one's stomach as a sign of submission

**[GEN 3:14](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14)** *Part of God's curse upon Satan.* *See also* [PSA 72:9](https://ref.ly/Ps72:9); [ISA 49:23](https://ref.ly/Isa49:23), [ISA 65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:25); [MIC 7:17](https://ref.ly/Mic7:17)

### Hunger as a result of God's judgment

**[MIC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:14)** *See also* [JOB 20:12–20:15](https://ref.ly/Job20:12-Job20:15), [JOB 20:20–20:23](https://ref.ly/Job20:20-Job20:23); [PRO 13:25](https://ref.ly/Prov13:25); [ISA 9:20](https://ref.ly/Isa9:20); [EZK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:19)

### An empty stomach as a warning

**[AMO 4:6](https://ref.ly/Amos4:6)**

The stomach and God's blessing
------------------------------

In the OT a full stomach was one sign of God's blessing: [PSA 34:9–34:10](https://ref.ly/Ps34:9-Ps34:10), [PSA 37:19](https://ref.ly/Ps37:19), [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25); ; [PRO 10:3](https://ref.ly/Prov10:3)

Figurative uses of the stomach
------------------------------

### Stomach as a symbol of inner thoughts

**[EZK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:3)** *See also* [PRO 18:8](https://ref.ly/Prov18:8), [PRO 26:22](https://ref.ly/Prov26:22); [REV 10:9–10:10](https://ref.ly/Rev10:9-Rev10:10)

### Stomach as a symbol of worldliness

**[PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19)** *See also* [ROM 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18)

### Stomach as something to be filled

**[PRO 18:20](https://ref.ly/Prov18:20)** *See also* [JOB 15:2](https://ref.ly/Job15:2); [JER 51:34](https://ref.ly/Jer51:34)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 6:13; Job 40:16; Song of Songs 7:2; Daniel 2:32; Matthew 12:40; Jonah 1:17-2:1; Mark 7:18-19; Matthew 15:17; Leviticus 11:41-45; Luke 15:16; 1 Timothy 5:23; Judges 3:21; 2 Samuel 2:23; 2 Samuel 3:27; 2 Samuel 4:6; 2 Samuel 20:10; Jeremiah 30:6-7; Numbers 5:20-22; Numbers 5:27; Genesis 3:14; Psalms 72:9; Isaiah 49:23; Isaiah 65:25; Micah 7:17; Micah 6:14; Job 20:12-15; Job 20:20-23; Proverbs 13:25; Isaiah 9:20; Ezekiel 7:19; Amos 4:6; Psalms 34:9-10; Psalms 37:19; Psalms 37:25; Proverbs 10:3; Ezekiel 3:3; Proverbs 18:8; Proverbs 26:22; Revelation 10:9-10; Philippians 3:19; Romans 16:18; Proverbs 18:20; Job 15:2; Jeremiah 51:34

## 5186: stumbling (id: 5186)

Faltering or falling in the course of running or walking. The term is also used metaphorically to refer to failure, hesitation or difficulty.

Physical stumbling
------------------

[LEV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Lev19:14); [LEV 26:37](https://ref.ly/Lev26:37); ; [ISA 21:4](https://ref.ly/Isa21:4); [ISA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Isa28:7) *through drunkenness*; [NAM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Nah2:5), [NAM 3:3](https://ref.ly/Nah3:3)

God protects his people from stumbling
--------------------------------------

**[PSA 56:13](https://ref.ly/Ps56:13)** *See also* [1SA 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:4); [JOB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Job4:4); [PSA 37:24](https://ref.ly/Ps37:24), [PSA 116:8](https://ref.ly/Ps116:8)

The enemies of God's people will stumble
----------------------------------------

**[JER 20:11](https://ref.ly/Jer20:11)** *See also* [PSA 9:3](https://ref.ly/Ps9:3), [PSA 27:2](https://ref.ly/Ps27:2)

God's people will stumble if they sin
-------------------------------------

[ISA 8:14](https://ref.ly/Isa8:14); ; [JER 13:16](https://ref.ly/Jer13:16)

Avoiding stumbling by keeping God's law
---------------------------------------

**[PSA 119:165](https://ref.ly/Ps119:165)** *See also* [PRO 3:23](https://ref.ly/Prov3:23), [PRO 3:26](https://ref.ly/Prov3:26), [PRO 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Prov4:11-Prov4:12); [ISA 26:7–26:8](https://ref.ly/Isa26:7-Isa26:8), [ISA 40:30–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:30-Isa40:31)

The wicked stumble because they reject God's way
------------------------------------------------

[PRO 4:19](https://ref.ly/Prov4:19); ; [HOS 14:9](https://ref.ly/Hos14:9)

Causes of stumbling
-------------------

### Dishonesty

[ISA 59:14](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14)

### Arrogance

[JER 50:32](https://ref.ly/Jer50:32); ; [HOS 5:5](https://ref.ly/Hos5:5)

### False teaching by religious leaders

[HOS 4:5](https://ref.ly/Hos4:5); ; [MAL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Mal2:8)

### Idolatry

[JER 18:15](https://ref.ly/Jer18:15)

### Trust in riches

[EZK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:19)

### Trust in secular political alliances

[ISA 31:3](https://ref.ly/Isa31:3)

Stumbling as a consequence of sin and divine judgment
-----------------------------------------------------

### Making one's own stumbling\-block

[EZK 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:3-Ezek14:4), [EZK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:7)

### Stumbling over obstacles sent by God

[JER 6:21](https://ref.ly/Jer6:21), [JER 13:16](https://ref.ly/Jer13:16); ; [EZK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:20)

### Some stumbling is educative or disciplinary

[DAN 11:35](https://ref.ly/Dan11:35); ; [ROM 9:32–9:33](https://ref.ly/Rom9:32-Rom9:33)

Jesus Christ as the light that prevents believers from stumbling
----------------------------------------------------------------

[JHN 9:5](https://ref.ly/John9:5), [JHN 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/John11:9-John11:10)

Jesus Christ as a stumbling\-block for unbelievers
--------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:23)** *See also* [1PE 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:8)

Stumbling and sin
-----------------

### All sin and stumble

[JAS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Jas2:10), [JAS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Jas3:2)

### None should feel superior to others

[PRO 24:17](https://ref.ly/Prov24:17); ; [1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12)

### None should cause others to stumble

[ROM 14:20](https://ref.ly/Rom14:20); ; [1CO 10:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:32)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:14; Leviticus 26:37; Isaiah 21:4; Isaiah 28:7; Nahum 2:5; Nahum 3:3; Psalms 56:13; 1 Samuel 2:4; Job 4:4; Psalms 37:24; Psalms 116:8; Jeremiah 20:11; Psalms 9:3; Psalms 27:2; Isaiah 8:14; Jeremiah 13:16; Psalms 119:165; Proverbs 3:23; Proverbs 3:26; Proverbs 4:11-12; Isaiah 26:7-8; Isaiah 40:30-31; Proverbs 4:19; Hosea 14:9; Isaiah 59:14; Jeremiah 50:32; Hosea 5:5; Hosea 4:5; Malachi 2:8; Jeremiah 18:15; Ezekiel 7:19; Isaiah 31:3; Ezekiel 14:3-4; Ezekiel 14:7; Jeremiah 6:21; Ezekiel 3:20; Daniel 11:35; Romans 9:32-33; John 9:5; John 11:9-10; 1 Corinthians 1:23; 1 Peter 2:8; James 2:10; James 3:2; Proverbs 24:17; 1 Corinthians 10:12; Romans 14:20; 1 Corinthians 10:32
* **Associated Articles:** 2414: cross, centrality of (ID: `2414@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4833: light (ID: `4833@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5133: blindness (ID: `5133@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5480: protection (ID: `5480@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5877: hesitation (ID: `5877@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6206: offence (ID: `6206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6738: rescue (ID: `6738@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8741: failure (ID: `8741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8749: false teachers (ID: `8749@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5187: taste (id: 5187)

The act of tasting is used figuratively of experiencing the goodness of God and his word and of a variety of experiences: misery, sin, wisdom, love, death and blessing.

The taste of food or drink
--------------------------

**[EXO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Exod16:31)** *See also* [GEN 25:28](https://ref.ly/Gen25:28), [GEN 27:4](https://ref.ly/Gen27:4), [GEN 27:7](https://ref.ly/Gen27:7), [GEN 27:9](https://ref.ly/Gen27:9), [GEN 27:14](https://ref.ly/Gen27:14), [GEN 27:17](https://ref.ly/Gen27:17), [GEN 27:31](https://ref.ly/Gen27:31); [NUM 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/Num11:7-Num11:8); [2SA 19:34–19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:34-2Sam19:35); [JHN 2:9](https://ref.ly/John2:9)

Legalistic prohibitions against tasting certain foods are condemned by Paul
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:22)**

Taste used figuratively
-----------------------

### Of the experience of misery

**[JOB 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Job6:6-Job6:7)** *In his complaint against God, Job compares the unacceptability of his lot to inedible food.* *See also* [JOB 27:2](https://ref.ly/Job27:2)

### Of the experience of the goodness of God

**[PSA 34:8](https://ref.ly/Ps34:8)** *See also* [HEB 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:5); [1PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:3)

### Of the experience of the goodness of the word of God

**[PSA 119:103](https://ref.ly/Ps119:103)** *See also* [PSA 19:10](https://ref.ly/Ps19:10); [JER 15:16](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16); [EZK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:3); [REV 10:9–10:10](https://ref.ly/Rev10:9-Rev10:10)

### Of the experience of sin

**[PRO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Prov20:17)**

### Of the experience of wisdom

**[PRO 24:13–24:14](https://ref.ly/Prov24:13-Prov24:14)**

### Of the experience of love

**[SNG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Song2:3)** *See also* [SNG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Song4:11), [SNG 4:16](https://ref.ly/Song4:16)

### Of the effectiveness of Christian influence

**[MAT 5:13](https://ref.ly/Matt5:13)** parallel [LUK 14:34–14:35](https://ref.ly/Luke14:34-Luke14:35)

### Of the experience of death

**[HEB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Heb2:9)** *See also* [MAT 16:28](https://ref.ly/Matt16:28) parallels [MRK 9:1](https://ref.ly/Mark9:1), [LUK 9:27](https://ref.ly/Luke9:27); [JHN 8:52](https://ref.ly/John8:52)

### Of the blessings of the kingdom of God

[LUK 14:24](https://ref.ly/Luke14:24), [LUK 22:29–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:29-Luke22:30)

To determine not to taste food until a task was completed was commonly used as an oath
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[1SA 14:24–14:46](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:24-1Sam14:46); ; [2SA 3:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:35); ; [JON 3:7](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:7); ; [ACT 23:14](https://ref.ly/Acts23:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 16:31; Genesis 25:28; Genesis 27:4; Genesis 27:7; Genesis 27:9; Genesis 27:14; Genesis 27:17; Genesis 27:31; Numbers 11:7-8; 2 Samuel 19:34-35; John 2:9; Colossians 2:20-22; Job 6:6-7; Job 27:2; Psalms 34:8; Hebrews 6:4-5; 1 Peter 2:3; Psalms 119:103; Psalms 19:10; Jeremiah 15:16; Ezekiel 3:3; Revelation 10:9-10; Proverbs 20:17; Proverbs 24:13-14; Song of Songs 2:3; Song of Songs 4:11; Song of Songs 4:16; Matthew 5:13; Luke 14:34-35; Hebrews 2:9; Matthew 16:28; Mark 9:1; Luke 9:27; John 8:52; Luke 14:24; Luke 22:29-30; 1 Samuel 14:24-46; 2 Samuel 3:35; Jonah 3:7; Acts 23:14
* **Associated Articles:** 1050: God, goodness of (ID: `1050@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4357: salt (ID: `4357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4404: food (ID: `4404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4544: wine (ID: `4544@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5167: mouth (ID: `5167@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5284: cupbearer (ID: `5284@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5429: oaths (ID: `5429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5792: appetite (ID: `5792@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5832: desire (ID: `5832@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5852: experience (ID: `5852@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5188: tearing of clothes (id: 5188)

Primarily associated with mourning, such action was an expression of deep sorrow and heartfelt grief. It was also a natural reaction at times of great distress and in cases of sincere repentance.

The tearing of clothes was a sign of mourning
---------------------------------------------

### It was a demonstration of grief

**[2SA 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:11-2Sam1:12)** *See also* [2SA 13:30–13:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:30-2Sam13:31); [2KI 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:11-2Kgs2:12); [JOB 1:20](https://ref.ly/Job1:20), [JOB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Job2:12)

### It was often associated with wearing sackcloth

**[GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34)** *See also* [2SA 3:31–3:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:31-2Sam3:32)

### The high priest was forbidden to tear his clothes in mourning

**[LEV 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:12)** *See also* [LEV 10:1–10:7](https://ref.ly/Lev10:1-Lev10:7)

The tearing of clothes indicated deep distress
----------------------------------------------

**[JDG 11:34–11:35](https://ref.ly/Judg11:34-Judg11:35)** *Jephthah had vowed to sacrifice whatever came out of the door of his house to meet him.* *See also* [GEN 37:29](https://ref.ly/Gen37:29), [GEN 44:12–44:13](https://ref.ly/Gen44:12-Gen44:13); [NUM 14:1–14:9](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:9); [JOS 7:3–7:6](https://ref.ly/Josh7:3-Josh7:6); [2KI 6:26–6:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:26-2Kgs6:30), [2KI 18:28–19:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:28-2Kgs19:4) parallel [ISA 36:13–37:4](https://ref.ly/Isa36:13-Isa37:4); [EST 3:13–4:1](https://ref.ly/Esth3:13-Esth4:1); [ACT 14:8–14:18](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8-Acts14:18)

Repentance was sometimes accompanied by the tearing of clothes
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 21:20–21:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:20-1Kgs21:27)** *See also* [JER 36:21–36:24](https://ref.ly/Jer36:21-Jer36:24) *an indication of the lack of repentance*; [JOL 2:12–2:14](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12-Joel2:14) *God requires that the tearing of clothes should express genuine repentance.*

Other references to the tearing of clothes
------------------------------------------

### As an expression of anger

**[MAT 26:63–26:65](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63-Matt26:65)** parallel [MRK 14:61–14:63](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61-Mark14:63)

### To stop the spread of mildew

*See also* [LEV 13:53–13:58](https://ref.ly/Lev13:53-Lev13:58)

### As a symbol of the dividing of the kingdom

[1SA 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:27-1Sam15:28)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 1:11-12; 2 Samuel 13:30-31; 2 Kings 2:11-12; Job 1:20; Job 2:12; Genesis 37:34; 2 Samuel 3:31-32; Leviticus 21:10-12; Leviticus 10:1-7; Judges 11:34-35; Genesis 37:29; Genesis 44:12-13; Numbers 14:1-9; Joshua 7:3-6; 2 Kings 6:26-30; 2 Kings 18:28-19:4; Isaiah 36:13-37:4; Esther 3:13-4:1; Acts 14:8-18; 1 Kings 21:20-27; Jeremiah 36:21-24; Joel 2:12-14; Matthew 26:63-65; Mark 14:61-63; Leviticus 13:53-58; 1 Samuel 15:27-28
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5177: robes (ID: `5177@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5198: weeping (ID: `5198@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5419: mourning (ID: `5419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5795: bereavement (ID: `5795@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5899: lament (ID: `5899@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6742: sackcloth and ashes (ID: `6742@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7376: high priest (ID: `7376@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8429: fasting (ID: `8429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5189: teeth (id: 5189)

Scripture often uses teeth symbolically, to refer to the power of individuals or to human rage (as in the “gnashing of teeth”).

Teeth in a literal sense
------------------------

### In eating

**[NUM 11:33](https://ref.ly/Num11:33)** *See also* [DAN 7:5](https://ref.ly/Dan7:5), [DAN 7:7](https://ref.ly/Dan7:7), [DAN 7:19](https://ref.ly/Dan7:19); [ZEC 9:7](https://ref.ly/Zech9:7); [REV 9:8](https://ref.ly/Rev9:8)

### As an object of beauty

**[SNG 4:2](https://ref.ly/Song4:2)** *See also* [GEN 49:12](https://ref.ly/Gen49:12); [SNG 6:6](https://ref.ly/Song6:6)

### As an object of fear

**[JOB 41:14](https://ref.ly/Job41:14)** *See also* [DEU 32:24](https://ref.ly/Deut32:24); [JOB 20:16](https://ref.ly/Job20:16); [JOL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Joel1:6)

Teeth used symbolically
-----------------------

### To represent offensive weapons

[PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4); ; [PRO 30:14](https://ref.ly/Prov30:14)

### To symbolise military power

**[ISA 41:15](https://ref.ly/Isa41:15)** *See also* [JOL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Joel1:6); [AMO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3); [HAB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Hab3:12)

### God rescues his people from the teeth of their enemies

**[PSA 124:6](https://ref.ly/Ps124:6)** *See also* [JOB 4:10](https://ref.ly/Job4:10); [DAN 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Dan6:20-Dan6:21)

### Breaking the teeth of the wicked ends their power

**[PSA 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ps3:7)** *See also* [JOB 29:17](https://ref.ly/Job29:17); [PSA 58:6](https://ref.ly/Ps58:6) *Enemies are often described as dangerous animals.*

Gnashing of teeth
-----------------

### In rage

**[ACT 7:54](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54)** *See also* [JOB 16:9](https://ref.ly/Job16:9); [PSA 35:16](https://ref.ly/Ps35:16), [PSA 37:12](https://ref.ly/Ps37:12)

### In frustration

[PSA 112:10](https://ref.ly/Ps112:10)

### In anguish

[MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12), [MAT 13:42](https://ref.ly/Matt13:42), [MAT 13:50](https://ref.ly/Matt13:50), [MAT 22:13](https://ref.ly/Matt22:13), [MAT 24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:51), [MAT 25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:30); ; [LUK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Luke13:28)

### In gloating

[LAM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:16)

### Under demonic influence

[MRK 9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:18)

Proverbial sayings about teeth
------------------------------

[JOB 19:20](https://ref.ly/Job19:20); ; [PRO 10:26](https://ref.ly/Prov10:26); ; [JER 31:29–31:30](https://ref.ly/Jer31:29-Jer31:30); ; [EZK 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 11:33; Daniel 7:5; Daniel 7:7; Daniel 7:19; Zechariah 9:7; Revelation 9:8; Song of Songs 4:2; Genesis 49:12; Song of Songs 6:6; Job 41:14; Deuteronomy 32:24; Job 20:16; Joel 1:6; Psalms 57:4; Proverbs 30:14; Isaiah 41:15; Amos 1:3; Habakkuk 3:12; Psalms 124:6; Job 4:10; Daniel 6:20-21; Psalms 3:7; Job 29:17; Psalms 58:6; Acts 7:54; Job 16:9; Psalms 35:16; Psalms 37:12; Psalms 112:10; Matthew 8:12; Matthew 13:42; Matthew 13:50; Matthew 22:13; Matthew 24:51; Matthew 25:30; Luke 13:28; Lamentations 2:16; Mark 9:18; Job 19:20; Proverbs 10:26; Jeremiah 31:29-30; Ezekiel 18:2
* **Associated Articles:** 4040: beauty (ID: `4040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4508: sickle (ID: `4508@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4522: threshing (ID: `4522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5132: biting (ID: `5132@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5481: proverb (ID: `5481@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5789: anger (ID: `5789@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5190: thigh (id: 5190)

The human thigh
---------------

### A symbol of strength

[JOB 40:17](https://ref.ly/Job40:17); ; [DAN 2:32](https://ref.ly/Dan2:32)

### Weapons strapped to the thigh

[JDG 3:16](https://ref.ly/Judg3:16), [JDG 3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:21)

### The hand is placed under the thigh when taking an oath

[GEN 24:2](https://ref.ly/Gen24:2), [GEN 24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:9), [GEN 47:29](https://ref.ly/Gen47:29)

### The priests' undergarments covered the thigh

**[EXO 28:42–28:43](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42-Exod28:43)** *See also* [EXO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Exod20:26); [LEV 6:10](https://ref.ly/Lev6:10), [LEV 16:3–16:4](https://ref.ly/Lev16:3-Lev16:4); [EZK 44:17–44:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:17-Ezek44:18)

### The wasting of the thigh

The wasting away of the thigh is part of the judgment on an unfaithful wife: [NUM 5:21–5:22](https://ref.ly/Num5:21-Num5:22), [NUM 5:27](https://ref.ly/Num5:27)

### The thigh as a place of identification

[REV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Rev19:16)

The right thighs of animals used in sacrificial offerings
---------------------------------------------------------

[EXO 29:22](https://ref.ly/Exod29:22), [EXO 29:27](https://ref.ly/Exod29:27); ; [LEV 7:32–7:34](https://ref.ly/Lev7:32-Lev7:34), [LEV 8:25–8:26](https://ref.ly/Lev8:25-Lev8:26), [LEV 9:21](https://ref.ly/Lev9:21), [LEV 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Lev10:14-Lev10:15); ; [NUM 6:20](https://ref.ly/Num6:20), [NUM 18:18](https://ref.ly/Num18:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 40:17; Daniel 2:32; Judges 3:16; Judges 3:21; Genesis 24:2; Genesis 24:9; Genesis 47:29; Exodus 28:42-43; Exodus 20:26; Leviticus 6:10; Leviticus 16:3-4; Ezekiel 44:17-18; Numbers 5:21-22; Numbers 5:27; Revelation 19:16; Exodus 29:22; Exodus 29:27; Leviticus 7:32-34; Leviticus 8:25-26; Leviticus 9:21; Leviticus 10:14-15; Numbers 6:20; Numbers 18:18

## 5191: thought (id: 5191)

The human ability to reason and reflect. Believers are called to reflect upon the words and works of God as they are made known through Scripture and to avoid speculation based purely upon human reason.

Human thought and God
---------------------

### God's thoughts are infinitely higher than human thoughts

**[ISA 55:8–55:9](https://ref.ly/Isa55:8-Isa55:9)** *See also* [PSA 92:5](https://ref.ly/Ps92:5), [PSA 139:17–139:18](https://ref.ly/Ps139:17-Ps139:18); [1CO 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:10-1Cor2:11)

### God knows all human thoughts

**[1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9)** *See also* [GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5); [PSA 94:11](https://ref.ly/Ps94:11), [PSA 139:1–139:4](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:4); [MAT 9:4](https://ref.ly/Matt9:4), [MAT 12:25](https://ref.ly/Matt12:25) parallel [LUK 11:17](https://ref.ly/Luke11:17); [LUK 9:47](https://ref.ly/Luke9:47); [HEB 4:12](https://ref.ly/Heb4:12)

### Meditating on God's words

**[PSA 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1-Ps1:2)** *See also* [DEU 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:7), [DEU 11:18](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18); [JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8); [PSA 119:15](https://ref.ly/Ps119:15), [PSA 119:23](https://ref.ly/Ps119:23), [PSA 119:78](https://ref.ly/Ps119:78), [PSA 119:97](https://ref.ly/Ps119:97)

### Meditating on God's works

**[PSA 77:12](https://ref.ly/Ps77:12)** *See also* [1SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:24); [PSA 106:7](https://ref.ly/Ps106:7), [PSA 107:43](https://ref.ly/Ps107:43), [PSA 119:27](https://ref.ly/Ps119:27), [PSA 143:5](https://ref.ly/Ps143:5), [PSA 145:4–145:5](https://ref.ly/Ps145:4-Ps145:5); [ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18)

Warnings against founding faith on speculative thought or human wisdom
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)** *See also* [ISA 29:14](https://ref.ly/Isa29:14); [1CO 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20), [1CO 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6), [1CO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:19-1Cor3:20); [2CO 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:5); [JAS 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Jas3:13-Jas3:15)

Believers are to give thought to others
---------------------------------------

### To think about their needs

**[PHP 2:4](https://ref.ly/Phil2:4)** *See also* [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10); [LUK 10:36–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:36-Luke10:37); [ROM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Rom12:13); [1CO 12:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:26), [1CO 14:11–14:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:11-1Cor14:12); [1TI 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:3); [1JN 3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:17)

### To think about their abilities

**[PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3)** *See also* [1CO 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:24-1Cor12:25), [1CO 14:26–14:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:26-1Cor14:33); [HEB 10:24](https://ref.ly/Heb10:24)

### To think about, and learn from, their shortcomings

**[GAL 6:1](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1)** *See also* [ACT 8:20–8:21](https://ref.ly/Acts8:20-Acts8:21); [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21); [EPH 4:17–4:19](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17-Eph4:19)

Believers are to give thought to themselves
-------------------------------------------

### To think accurately about themselves and their God\-given abilities

**[ROM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rom12:3)** *See also* [1CO 1:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:26), [1CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:18), [1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12), [1CO 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:20), [1CO 14:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:37); [2CO 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:5-2Cor11:6); [GAL 6:3](https://ref.ly/Gal6:3); [PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3); [1TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:4)

### To think about their shortcomings and temptations

**[2CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:12)** *See also* [PRO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6); [MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19); [ROM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Rom2:15); [1CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:11); [2CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:7); [EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:3); [PHP 3:13](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13); [COL 1:21](https://ref.ly/Col1:21)

### To think about their needs

**[PHP 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:7-Phil3:9)** *See also* [MAT 6:31–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31-Matt6:34); [PHP 2:4](https://ref.ly/Phil2:4)

### To think rightly about their plans and ambitions

**[1PE 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13-1Pet1:14)** *See also* [DEU 15:9](https://ref.ly/Deut15:9); [PRO 5:6](https://ref.ly/Prov5:6), [PRO 14:8](https://ref.ly/Prov14:8), [PRO 14:15](https://ref.ly/Prov14:15); [ISA 55:7](https://ref.ly/Isa55:7); [HAG 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hag1:5); [LUK 14:31](https://ref.ly/Luke14:31) *Jesus Christ wanted his followers to consider carefully the cost of being a disciple*; [ACT 5:35](https://ref.ly/Acts5:35); [ROM 13:14](https://ref.ly/Rom13:14); [PHP 4:8](https://ref.ly/Phil4:8); [COL 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Col3:1-Col3:2); [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Heb3:1), [HEB 11:15](https://ref.ly/Heb11:15); [JAS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2); [1PE 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:1); [2PE 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:1-2Pet3:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 55:8-9; Psalms 92:5; Psalms 139:17-18; 1 Corinthians 2:10-11; 1 Chronicles 28:9; Genesis 6:5; Psalms 94:11; Psalms 139:1-4; Matthew 9:4; Matthew 12:25; Luke 11:17; Luke 9:47; Hebrews 4:12; Psalms 1:1-2; Deuteronomy 6:6-7; Deuteronomy 11:18; Joshua 1:8; Psalms 119:15; Psalms 119:23; Psalms 119:78; Psalms 119:97; Psalms 77:12; 1 Samuel 12:24; Psalms 106:7; Psalms 107:43; Psalms 119:27; Psalms 143:5; Psalms 145:4-5; Isaiah 1:18; Colossians 2:8; Isaiah 29:14; 1 Corinthians 1:20; 1 Corinthians 2:6; 1 Corinthians 3:19-20; 2 Corinthians 10:5; James 3:13-15; Philippians 2:4; Zechariah 7:10; Luke 10:36-37; Romans 12:13; 1 Corinthians 12:26; 1 Corinthians 14:11-12; 1 Timothy 5:3; 1 John 3:17; Philippians 2:3; 1 Corinthians 12:24-25; 1 Corinthians 14:26-33; Hebrews 10:24; Galatians 6:1; Acts 8:20-21; Romans 1:21; Ephesians 4:17-19; Romans 12:3; 1 Corinthians 1:26; 1 Corinthians 3:18; 1 Corinthians 10:12; 1 Corinthians 14:20; 1 Corinthians 14:37; 2 Corinthians 11:5-6; Galatians 6:3; 1 Timothy 4:4; 2 Corinthians 10:12; Proverbs 6:6; Matthew 15:19; Romans 2:15; 1 Corinthians 13:11; 2 Corinthians 10:7; Ephesians 2:3; Philippians 3:13; Colossians 1:21; Philippians 3:7-9; Matthew 6:31-34; 1 Peter 1:13-14; Deuteronomy 15:9; Proverbs 5:6; Proverbs 14:8; Proverbs 14:15; Isaiah 55:7; Haggai 1:5; Luke 14:31; Acts 5:35; Romans 13:14; Philippians 4:8; Colossians 3:1-2; Hebrews 3:1; Hebrews 11:15; James 1:2; 1 Peter 4:1; 2 Peter 3:1-2

--------------

## 5193: tongue (id: 5193)

The organ of speech, which Scripture stresses can be the source of both praise and blame. The tongue is to be kept under control, on account of its capacity for evil words.

The tongue as a bodily organ
----------------------------

### As part of the body

**[JAS 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jas3:5)** *See also* [JDG 7:5](https://ref.ly/Judg7:5); [JOB 41:1](https://ref.ly/Job41:1); [MRK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Mark7:33); [LUK 16:24](https://ref.ly/Luke16:24); [REV 16:10](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10)

### Used for eating

[NUM 22:4](https://ref.ly/Num22:4); ; [JOB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Job12:11), [JOB 34:3](https://ref.ly/Job34:3); ; [PSA 68:23](https://ref.ly/Ps68:23)

### Used for drinking

[JDG 7:5](https://ref.ly/Judg7:5)

### Used for licking

[1KI 21:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:19), [1KI 22:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:38); ; [LUK 16:21](https://ref.ly/Luke16:21)

### Parched with thirst

[PSA 22:15](https://ref.ly/Ps22:15), [PSA 137:6](https://ref.ly/Ps137:6); ; [ISA 41:17](https://ref.ly/Isa41:17); ; [LAM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Lam4:4)

The tongue as an organ of speech
--------------------------------

### It is God\-given

**[EXO 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10-Exod4:11)** *See also* [EZK 3:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:26); [LUK 1:20–1:22](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20-Luke1:22), [LUK 1:62–1:64](https://ref.ly/Luke1:62-Luke1:64)

### God is able to loose the tongue of the mute

**[MRK 7:33–7:35](https://ref.ly/Mark7:33-Mark7:35)** *See also* [ISA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Isa32:4), [ISA 35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6); [MAT 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30-Matt15:31); [LUK 1:64](https://ref.ly/Luke1:64)

Reasons for holding one's tongue
--------------------------------

### To restrain sin

**[PRO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19)** *See also* [PRO 11:12](https://ref.ly/Prov11:12), [PRO 17:27–17:28](https://ref.ly/Prov17:27-Prov17:28), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23); [JAS 1:26](https://ref.ly/Jas1:26); [1PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:10)

### As a mark of respect and awe

[JOB 29:9–29:10](https://ref.ly/Job29:9-Job29:10); ; [HAB 2:20](https://ref.ly/Hab2:20)

The tongue's potential for good
-------------------------------

### Right use of the tongue requires an act of the will

**[JOB 27:4](https://ref.ly/Job27:4)** *See also* [PSA 39:1](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1), [PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)

### The tongue can praise God

**[PSA 35:28](https://ref.ly/Ps35:28)** *See also* [PSA 16:9](https://ref.ly/Ps16:9), [PSA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Ps45:1), [PSA 51:14](https://ref.ly/Ps51:14), [PSA 66:17](https://ref.ly/Ps66:17), [PSA 71:24](https://ref.ly/Ps71:24), [PSA 119:172](https://ref.ly/Ps119:172), [PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2); [ACT 2:26](https://ref.ly/Acts2:26); [JAS 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9)

### The tongue can speak God\-given words

**[2SA 23:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:2)** *See also* [PRO 16:1](https://ref.ly/Prov16:1); [ISA 50:4](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4); [MIC 3:8](https://ref.ly/Mic3:8); [LUK 21:14–21:15](https://ref.ly/Luke21:14-Luke21:15)

### Every tongue will acknowledge God

**[ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23)** *See also* [ROM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rom14:11); [PHP 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:10-Phil2:11)

### The tongue can speak wisdom

**[PSA 37:30](https://ref.ly/Ps37:30)** *See also* [PRO 10:19–10:20](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19-Prov10:20), [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18), [PRO 15:23](https://ref.ly/Prov15:23), [PRO 31:26](https://ref.ly/Prov31:26)

### The tongue can bring healing

**[PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4)** *See also* [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18)

The tongue's potential for evil
-------------------------------

### The tongue's power needs to be controlled

**[PRO 18:21](https://ref.ly/Prov18:21)**; **[JAS 3:3–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jas3:3-Jas3:10)** *See also* [JOB 27:4](https://ref.ly/Job27:4); [PSA 34:13](https://ref.ly/Ps34:13); [PRO 11:12](https://ref.ly/Prov11:12); [JAS 1:26](https://ref.ly/Jas1:26)

### The tongue can speak deceitfully

**[PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9)** *See also* [PSA 50:19](https://ref.ly/Ps50:19), [PSA 52:4](https://ref.ly/Ps52:4), [PSA 120:2–120:3](https://ref.ly/Ps120:2-Ps120:3); [PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4), [PRO 17:20](https://ref.ly/Prov17:20); [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8); [MIC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Mic6:12); [ROM 3:13](https://ref.ly/Rom3:13)

### The tongue can speak lies

**[JER 9:3](https://ref.ly/Jer9:3)** *See also* [PRO 6:17](https://ref.ly/Prov6:17), [PRO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Prov21:6), [PRO 26:28](https://ref.ly/Prov26:28)

### Other evil uses of the tongue

**[PSA 10:7](https://ref.ly/Ps10:7)** *See also* [PSA 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Ps12:3-Ps12:4), [PSA 52:2](https://ref.ly/Ps52:2); [PRO 6:24](https://ref.ly/Prov6:24); [ISA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Isa57:4), [ISA 59:3](https://ref.ly/Isa59:3); [JER 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jer18:18)

### The punishment for a perverse tongue

[PRO 10:31](https://ref.ly/Prov10:31)

Tongue in the sense of language
-------------------------------

### As a foreign language

**[ACT 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:8-Acts2:11)** *See also* [EST 1:22](https://ref.ly/Esth1:22); [PSA 114:1](https://ref.ly/Ps114:1); [ISA 28:11](https://ref.ly/Isa28:11), [ISA 33:19](https://ref.ly/Isa33:19), [ISA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Isa66:18); [1CO 14:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:10)

### As a language given by the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:4)** *See also* [MRK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Mark16:17); [ACT 10:46](https://ref.ly/Acts10:46), [ACT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Acts19:6); [1CO 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:10), [1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28), [1CO 12:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:30), [1CO 13:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:1), [1CO 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:8), [1CO 14:2–14:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:2-1Cor14:23)

Metaphors used to describe the tongue
-------------------------------------

### The tongue as a dangerous weapon

[PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4), [PSA 64:3](https://ref.ly/Ps64:3); ; [JER 9:3](https://ref.ly/Jer9:3), [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8)

### Other metaphors

[PSA 140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:3); ; [PRO 10:20](https://ref.ly/Prov10:20); ; [ISA 5:24](https://ref.ly/Isa5:24); ; [ACT 2:3](https://ref.ly/Acts2:3); ; [JAS 3:6](https://ref.ly/Jas3:6)

* **Associated Passages:** James 3:5; Judges 7:5; Job 41:1; Mark 7:33; Luke 16:24; Revelation 16:10; Numbers 22:4; Job 12:11; Job 34:3; Psalms 68:23; 1 Kings 21:19; 1 Kings 22:38; Luke 16:21; Psalms 22:15; Psalms 137:6; Isaiah 41:17; Lamentations 4:4; Exodus 4:10-11; Ezekiel 3:26; Luke 1:20-22; Luke 1:62-64; Mark 7:33-35; Isaiah 32:4; Isaiah 35:6; Matthew 15:30-31; Luke 1:64; Proverbs 10:19; Proverbs 11:12; Proverbs 17:27-28; Proverbs 21:23; James 1:26; 1 Peter 3:10; Job 29:9-10; Habakkuk 2:20; Job 27:4; Psalms 39:1; Psalms 141:3; Psalms 35:28; Psalms 16:9; Psalms 45:1; Psalms 51:14; Psalms 66:17; Psalms 71:24; Psalms 119:172; Psalms 126:2; Acts 2:26; James 3:9; 2 Samuel 23:2; Proverbs 16:1; Isaiah 50:4; Micah 3:8; Luke 21:14-15; Isaiah 45:23; Romans 14:11; Philippians 2:10-11; Psalms 37:30; Proverbs 10:19-20; Proverbs 12:18; Proverbs 15:23; Proverbs 31:26; Proverbs 15:4; Proverbs 18:21; James 3:3-10; Psalms 34:13; Psalms 5:9; Psalms 50:19; Psalms 52:4; Psalms 120:2-3; Proverbs 17:20; Jeremiah 9:8; Micah 6:12; Romans 3:13; Jeremiah 9:3; Proverbs 6:17; Proverbs 21:6; Proverbs 26:28; Psalms 10:7; Psalms 12:3-4; Psalms 52:2; Proverbs 6:24; Isaiah 57:4; Isaiah 59:3; Jeremiah 18:18; Proverbs 10:31; Acts 2:8-11; Esther 1:22; Psalms 114:1; Isaiah 28:11; Isaiah 33:19; Isaiah 66:18; 1 Corinthians 14:10; Acts 2:4; Mark 16:17; Acts 10:46; Acts 19:6; 1 Corinthians 12:10; 1 Corinthians 12:28; 1 Corinthians 12:30; 1 Corinthians 13:1; 1 Corinthians 13:8; 1 Corinthians 14:2-23; Psalms 57:4; Psalms 64:3; Psalms 140:3; Proverbs 10:20; Isaiah 5:24; Acts 2:3; James 3:6
