## 5194: touch (id: 5194)

God prohibits physical contact in certain circumstances and warns of serious consequences of violating the ritual law in this respect. Great sigificance is attached to the touch of God, Jesus Christ and angels and the disciples' touching of the risen Christ.

God's prohibits touch in certain circumstances
----------------------------------------------

### At Mount Sinai

**[EXO 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:12-Exod19:13)** *See also* [HEB 12:18–12:24](https://ref.ly/Heb12:18-Heb12:24)

### With the furnishings and articles of the tabernacle

**[NUM 4:15](https://ref.ly/Num4:15)** *See also* [2SA 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:6-2Sam6:7)

The law concerning touching certain articles
--------------------------------------------

### Anyone or anything touching what is ritually unclean also becomes unclean

**[NUM 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Num19:21-Num19:22)** *See also* [LEV 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Lev5:2-Lev5:3), [LEV 7:19–7:21](https://ref.ly/Lev7:19-Lev7:21), [LEV 11:8](https://ref.ly/Lev11:8), [LEV 11:24–11:40](https://ref.ly/Lev11:24-Lev11:40), [LEV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Lev12:4), [LEV 15:5–15:12](https://ref.ly/Lev15:5-Lev15:12), [LEV 15:19–15:27](https://ref.ly/Lev15:19-Lev15:27), [LEV 22:4–22:6](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4-Lev22:6); [NUM 19:11–19:16](https://ref.ly/Num19:11-Num19:16) *the law concerning dead bodies*; [DEU 14:8](https://ref.ly/Deut14:8) *Pigs are unclean*; [ISA 52:11](https://ref.ly/Isa52:11); [HAG 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Hag2:10-Hag2:14)

### Touching what is purified confers consecration

[EXO 29:37](https://ref.ly/Exod29:37), [EXO 30:29](https://ref.ly/Exod30:29); ; [LEV 6:18](https://ref.ly/Lev6:18), [LEV 6:27](https://ref.ly/Lev6:27)

Man\-made rules against touching certain things are condemned by Paul
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:22)**

The touch of God
----------------

### Upon Jacob, to humble him

**[GEN 32:25](https://ref.ly/Gen32:25), [GEN 32:32](https://ref.ly/Gen32:32)**

### Upon those he calls to be prophets

**[JER 1:9](https://ref.ly/Jer1:9)** *See also* [ISA 6:7](https://ref.ly/Isa6:7); [EZK 2:8–3:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:8-Ezek3:4); [DAN 10:16](https://ref.ly/Dan10:16)

### Upon the world he has made, to display his glory

**[PSA 104:32](https://ref.ly/Ps104:32)** *See also* [PSA 144:5](https://ref.ly/Ps144:5); [AMO 9:5](https://ref.ly/Amos9:5)

The touch of Jesus Christ
-------------------------

### To heal

**[MAT 8:3](https://ref.ly/Matt8:3)** parallels [MRK 1:41–1:42](https://ref.ly/Mark1:41-Mark1:42), [LUK 5:13](https://ref.ly/Luke5:13) *See also* [MAT 9:29–9:30](https://ref.ly/Matt9:29-Matt9:30), [MAT 20:34](https://ref.ly/Matt20:34); [MRK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Mark7:33), [MRK 8:22–8:25](https://ref.ly/Mark8:22-Mark8:25); [LUK 22:51](https://ref.ly/Luke22:51)

### To raise the dead

**[MAT 9:18–9:25](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18-Matt9:25)** parallels [MRK 5:22–5:42](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22-Mark5:42), [LUK 8:41–8:55](https://ref.ly/Luke8:41-Luke8:55) *In touching the dead, Jesus Christ shows his willingness to become unclean so that others can be healed.* *See also* [LUK 7:14](https://ref.ly/Luke7:14)

### To bring reassurance and show acceptance

[MAT 17:6–17:7](https://ref.ly/Matt17:6-Matt17:7); ; [MRK 10:13](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13) parallels [MAT 19:13](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13), [LUK 18:15](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15)

The touch of angels
-------------------

[1KI 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5), [1KI 19:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:7); ; [DAN 8:18](https://ref.ly/Dan8:18), [DAN 10:10](https://ref.ly/Dan10:10), [DAN 10:18](https://ref.ly/Dan10:18)

The reality of Jesus Christ's incarnation and resurrection is confirmed by touch
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1JN 1:1](https://ref.ly/1John1:1)**; **[JHN 20:24–20:27](https://ref.ly/John20:24-John20:27)**

The figurative use of touch
---------------------------

### To mean harming people

**[EZK 9:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:6)** *See also* [GEN 20:6](https://ref.ly/Gen20:6); [JOS 9:19](https://ref.ly/Josh9:19); [1CH 16:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:22) parallel [PSA 105:15](https://ref.ly/Ps105:15); [PRO 6:29](https://ref.ly/Prov6:29); [ZEC 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Zech2:8-Zech2:9); [HEB 11:28](https://ref.ly/Heb11:28)

### To mean separation from sin

**[2CO 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:17)** *See also* [ISA 52:11](https://ref.ly/Isa52:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 19:12-13; Hebrews 12:18-24; Numbers 4:15; 2 Samuel 6:6-7; Numbers 19:21-22; Leviticus 5:2-3; Leviticus 7:19-21; Leviticus 11:8; Leviticus 11:24-40; Leviticus 12:4; Leviticus 15:5-12; Leviticus 15:19-27; Leviticus 22:4-6; Numbers 19:11-16; Deuteronomy 14:8; Isaiah 52:11; Haggai 2:10-14; Exodus 29:37; Exodus 30:29; Leviticus 6:18; Leviticus 6:27; Colossians 2:20-22; Genesis 32:25; Genesis 32:32; Jeremiah 1:9; Isaiah 6:7; Ezekiel 2:8-3:4; Daniel 10:16; Psalms 104:32; Psalms 144:5; Amos 9:5; Matthew 8:3; Mark 1:41-42; Luke 5:13; Matthew 9:29-30; Matthew 20:34; Mark 7:33; Mark 8:22-25; Luke 22:51; Matthew 9:18-25; Mark 5:22-42; Luke 8:41-55; Luke 7:14; Matthew 17:6-7; Mark 10:13; Matthew 19:13; Luke 18:15; 1 Kings 19:5; 1 Kings 19:7; Daniel 8:18; Daniel 10:10; Daniel 10:18; 1 John 1:1; John 20:24-27; Ezekiel 9:6; Genesis 20:6; Joshua 9:19; 1 Chronicles 16:22; Psalms 105:15; Proverbs 6:29; Zechariah 2:8-9; Hebrews 11:28; 2 Corinthians 6:17
* **Associated Articles:** 1265: hand of God (ID: `1265@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2351: Jesus Christ, miracles of (ID: `2351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2515: Jesus Christ, birth of (ID: `2515@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2555: Jesus Christ, resurrection appearances of (ID: `2555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5156: hand (ID: `5156@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7348: defilement (ID: `7348@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7372: hands, laying on of (ID: `7372@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7424: ritual law (ID: `7424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9030: dead bodies (ID: `9030@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5195: veil (id: 5195)

Generally, an item of female dress worn by an unmarried woman in the presence of her future husband and also by a prostitute. Head\-coverings were worn by men and women as a sign of mourning. The term is also used metaphorically to indicate a lack of understanding of God's truth. Scripture uses the imagery of “removing a veil” to refer to God's final self\-revelation at the end of the age, or the making known of God's will and nature through Jesus Christ or other means.

The veil as an item of female dress
-----------------------------------

### Worn by women in general

**[SNG 4:1](https://ref.ly/Song4:1)** *The veil covered the lower part of the face, so focusing attention on the eyes and temples.* *See also* [GEN 24:65](https://ref.ly/Gen24:65) *Rebekah veils herself in the presence of her future husband, Isaac*; [SNG 4:3](https://ref.ly/Song4:3), [SNG 6:7](https://ref.ly/Song6:7)

### Worn by prostitutes

**[GEN 38:14–38:15](https://ref.ly/Gen38:14-Gen38:15)** *See also* [SNG 1:7](https://ref.ly/Song1:7)

### Worn by female sorcerers

[EZK 13:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:18), [EZK 13:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:21)

### Worn as a sign of wealth and status

[ISA 3:19](https://ref.ly/Isa3:19), [ISA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Isa47:2) *Babylon's veil will be removed as a mark of her humiliation.*

Veils worn as a sign of grief
-----------------------------

### Faces covered in sorrow and dismay

**[2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30)** *See also* [LEV 13:45](https://ref.ly/Lev13:45) *A veil worn by the ceremonially unclean signifies grief at separation from God*; [JER 14:4](https://ref.ly/Jer14:4)

### Veils customarily worn by mourners

**[2SA 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:4)** *See also* [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17), [EZK 24:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:22)

### Faces covered as a sign of disgrace

[EST 6:12](https://ref.ly/Esth6:12), [EST 7:8](https://ref.ly/Esth7:8); ; [MIC 3:7](https://ref.ly/Mic3:7)

Head\-coverings in worship
--------------------------

**[1CO 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:4)** *A man's uncovered head in worship signifies submission to his spiritual head, i.e., Jesus Christ*; **[1CO 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:5-1Cor11:6)** *A woman's covered head signifies proper submission to her husband.* *See also* [1CO 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:7), [1CO 11:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:13)

God's sight of humanity is not veiled
-------------------------------------

### The wicked foolishly boast that God does not see them

**[JOB 22:12–22:14](https://ref.ly/Job22:12-Job22:14)** *See also* [PSA 10:11](https://ref.ly/Ps10:11), [PSA 94:7](https://ref.ly/Ps94:7); [ISA 29:15](https://ref.ly/Isa29:15)

### God's people complain that God does not see their suffering

**[PSA 44:24](https://ref.ly/Ps44:24)** *See also* [JOB 13:24](https://ref.ly/Job13:24); [PSA 69:17](https://ref.ly/Ps69:17), [PSA 88:14](https://ref.ly/Ps88:14)

### God sees all humanity

**[JOB 28:24](https://ref.ly/Job28:24)** *See also* [GEN 16:13](https://ref.ly/Gen16:13); [JOB 34:21](https://ref.ly/Job34:21); [PSA 33:13](https://ref.ly/Ps33:13), [PSA 139:1–139:3](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:3)

### People's deeds will be made known

**[1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)** *See also* [JOB 28:11](https://ref.ly/Job28:11); [ISA 26:21](https://ref.ly/Isa26:21); [MAT 10:26](https://ref.ly/Matt10:26) parallel [LUK 12:2](https://ref.ly/Luke12:2); [1CO 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:13)

The truth about God is sometimes veiled
---------------------------------------

### The veil worn by Moses

**[2CO 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:13)** *After speaking with God, Moses' face was radiant; the veil, possibly a loose part of his clothing pulled over his face, prevented the Israelites from seeing that the radiance did not last.* *See also* [EXO 34:29–34:35](https://ref.ly/Exod34:29-Exod34:35)

### Lack of understanding of God's truth

**[2CO 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:14-2Cor3:15)** *See also* [ISA 44:18](https://ref.ly/Isa44:18); [LAM 3:65](https://ref.ly/Lam3:65); [1CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:14); [2CO 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:3); [EPH 4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:18)

The unveiling of God's truth
----------------------------

### God's hidden truth is revealed to believers

**[1CO 2:7–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:7-1Cor2:10)**; **[COL 1:26](https://ref.ly/Col1:26)** *See also* [ISA 64:4](https://ref.ly/Isa64:4); [ROM 16:25–16:26](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25-Rom16:26); [2CO 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:16-2Cor3:18); [EPH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9), [EPH 3:3–3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:3-Eph3:6); [2TI 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:10); [TIT 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Titus1:2-Titus1:3)

### God's self\-revelation at the end of the age

**[DAN 12:2–12:4](https://ref.ly/Dan12:2-Dan12:4)** *See also* [ISA 25:7–25:8](https://ref.ly/Isa25:7-Isa25:8); [ROM 8:19](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19); [1JN 3:2](https://ref.ly/1John3:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Song of Songs 4:1; Genesis 24:65; Song of Songs 4:3; Song of Songs 6:7; Genesis 38:14-15; Song of Songs 1:7; Ezekiel 13:18; Ezekiel 13:21; Isaiah 3:19; Isaiah 47:2; 2 Samuel 15:30; Leviticus 13:45; Jeremiah 14:4; 2 Samuel 19:4; Ezekiel 24:17; Ezekiel 24:22; Esther 6:12; Esther 7:8; Micah 3:7; 1 Corinthians 11:4; 1 Corinthians 11:5-6; 1 Corinthians 11:7; 1 Corinthians 11:13; Job 22:12-14; Psalms 10:11; Psalms 94:7; Isaiah 29:15; Psalms 44:24; Job 13:24; Psalms 69:17; Psalms 88:14; Job 28:24; Genesis 16:13; Job 34:21; Psalms 33:13; Psalms 139:1-3; 1 Corinthians 4:5; Job 28:11; Isaiah 26:21; Matthew 10:26; Luke 12:2; 1 Corinthians 3:13; 2 Corinthians 3:13; Exodus 34:29-35; 2 Corinthians 3:14-15; Isaiah 44:18; Lamentations 3:65; 1 Corinthians 2:14; 2 Corinthians 4:3; Ephesians 4:18; 1 Corinthians 2:7-10; Colossians 1:26; Isaiah 64:4; Romans 16:25-26; 2 Corinthians 3:16-18; Ephesians 1:9; Ephesians 3:3-6; 2 Timothy 1:10; Titus 1:2-3; Daniel 12:2-4; Isaiah 25:7-8; Romans 8:19; 1 John 3:2
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1110: God, present everywhere (ID: `1110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1439: revelation (ID: `1439@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5135: blindness, spiritual (ID: `5135@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5150: face (ID: `5150@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5158: head-covering (ID: `5158@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5419: mourning (ID: `5419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5952: sorrow (ID: `5952@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6694: mystery (ID: `6694@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7344: curtain (ID: `7344@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8419: enlightenment (ID: `8419@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5196: voice (id: 5196)

The organ of speech; someone's voice expresses a person's character, thoughts and emotions, and attracts attention by its tone and volume.

The human voice
---------------

### The sound of familiar voices

**[ACT 12:14](https://ref.ly/Acts12:14)** *See also* [GEN 27:22](https://ref.ly/Gen27:22); [JDG 18:3](https://ref.ly/Judg18:3); [1SA 24:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:16), [1SA 26:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:17); [SNG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Song2:14), [SNG 8:13](https://ref.ly/Song8:13)

### Groups of people speaking in unison

**[EXO 24:3](https://ref.ly/Exod24:3)** *See also* [1SA 10:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:24); [2CH 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13), [2CH 23:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:11); [EZR 10:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:12); [ACT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Acts12:22), [ACT 19:34](https://ref.ly/Acts19:34), [ACT 22:22](https://ref.ly/Acts22:22)

Voices communicate in various ways
----------------------------------

### Voices expressing defiance

**[1SA 17:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:8)** *See also* [2KI 19:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:22) parallel [ISA 37:23](https://ref.ly/Isa37:23)

### Voices that give a battle cry

[1SA 17:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:20); ; [2CH 13:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:15); ; [ISA 8:9](https://ref.ly/Isa8:9); ; [JER 4:16](https://ref.ly/Jer4:16), [JER 4:19](https://ref.ly/Jer4:19)

### Festive voices

[2SA 19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:35); ; [JER 7:34](https://ref.ly/Jer7:34), [JER 16:9](https://ref.ly/Jer16:9), [JER 25:10](https://ref.ly/Jer25:10), [JER 33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:11)

### Voices expressing distress

**[JDG 21:2](https://ref.ly/Judg21:2)** *See also* [GEN 27:34](https://ref.ly/Gen27:34); [NUM 14:1](https://ref.ly/Num14:1); [2SA 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:7) parallel [PSA 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ps18:6); [1KI 22:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:36); [JER 4:31](https://ref.ly/Jer4:31); [EZK 27:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:30); [DAN 6:20](https://ref.ly/Dan6:20); [MAT 2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:18); [JER 31:15](https://ref.ly/Jer31:15)

Voices of varying volume
------------------------

### God's loud voice

**[JOB 37:5](https://ref.ly/Job37:5)** *See also* [DEU 5:22](https://ref.ly/Deut5:22); [ISA 33:3](https://ref.ly/Isa33:3); [EZK 43:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:2); [JHN 12:28–12:29](https://ref.ly/John12:28-John12:29)

### Loud human voices

[1SA 28:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:12); ; [2CH 20:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:19); ; [EZR 10:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:12); ; [ISA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Isa40:9); ; [LUK 4:33](https://ref.ly/Luke4:33); ; [ACT 7:57](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57)

### God's quiet voice

**[1KI 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:12-1Kgs19:13)** *See also* [JOB 26:14](https://ref.ly/Job26:14)

### Quiet human voices

[JOB 4:16](https://ref.ly/Job4:16); ; [ECC 9:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:17); ; [ISA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Isa42:2)

Voices addressed to God
-----------------------

### In praise

**[LUK 17:15](https://ref.ly/Luke17:15)** *See also* [JDG 5:11](https://ref.ly/Judg5:11); [1KI 8:55](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:55); [EZR 3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11); [PSA 47:1](https://ref.ly/Ps47:1), [PSA 100:1–100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:1-Ps100:4); [ISA 24:14](https://ref.ly/Isa24:14); [LUK 19:37](https://ref.ly/Luke19:37)

### In prayer

**[PSA 27:7](https://ref.ly/Ps27:7)** *See also* [DEU 33:7](https://ref.ly/Deut33:7); [PSA 64:1](https://ref.ly/Ps64:1)

### Assurance that God hears his people's voice

**[PSA 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ps5:3)** *See also* [EXO 22:23](https://ref.ly/Exod22:23); [DEU 24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:15); [PSA 55:17](https://ref.ly/Ps55:17); [ISA 19:20](https://ref.ly/Isa19:20)

### God's response to his people's voice

**[NEH 9:9](https://ref.ly/Neh9:9)** *See also* [GEN 21:17](https://ref.ly/Gen21:17); [EXO 2:23–2:25](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23-Exod2:25), [EXO 3:9](https://ref.ly/Exod3:9); [NUM 20:16](https://ref.ly/Num20:16)

### God sometimes refuses to hear his people's voice

**[EZK 8:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:18)** *See also* [ISA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:15), [ISA 59:1–59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:1-Isa59:2); [JER 14:12](https://ref.ly/Jer14:12); [AMO 5:23](https://ref.ly/Amos5:23)

The futility of lifting up one's voice to an idol
-------------------------------------------------

[JDG 10:14](https://ref.ly/Judg10:14); ; [1KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26), [1KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28); ; [JER 11:12](https://ref.ly/Jer11:12)

Voices that proclaim God's word
-------------------------------

**[ROM 10:18](https://ref.ly/Rom10:18)** *The original sense of the voice of the heavens is reapplied to all who proclaim the gospel.* *See also* [ISA 58:1](https://ref.ly/Isa58:1); [MIC 3:8](https://ref.ly/Mic3:8); [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14)

The voice of God
----------------

### God's voice addresses individuals

**[GEN 3:9](https://ref.ly/Gen3:9)** *See also* [EXO 3:4](https://ref.ly/Exod3:4); [1SA 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:4); [ISA 6:8](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8); [JER 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jer1:4); [EZK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:28)

### God's voice is awe\-inspiring

**[PSA 29:3–29:9](https://ref.ly/Ps29:3-Ps29:9)** *See also* [DEU 5:26](https://ref.ly/Deut5:26), [DEU 18:16](https://ref.ly/Deut18:16); [JOB 37:2](https://ref.ly/Job37:2); [PSA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Ps46:6), [PSA 68:33](https://ref.ly/Ps68:33); [ISA 30:30–30:31](https://ref.ly/Isa30:30-Isa30:31), [ISA 42:13](https://ref.ly/Isa42:13); [EZK 1:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:24); [HEB 12:19](https://ref.ly/Heb12:19), [HEB 12:26](https://ref.ly/Heb12:26)

### God's voice demands obedience

[EXO 15:26](https://ref.ly/Exod15:26); ; [DEU 30:20](https://ref.ly/Deut30:20); ; [1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22); ; [ISA 30:21](https://ref.ly/Isa30:21); ; [HEB 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7-Heb3:8)

### God's voice affirms his Son

[MAT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17) parallels [MRK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mark1:11), [LUK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Luke3:22) *at Jesus Christ's baptism*; ; [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) parallels [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7), [LUK 9:35–9:36](https://ref.ly/Luke9:35-Luke9:36) *at the transfiguration*; ; [JHN 12:28](https://ref.ly/John12:28); ; [2PE 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:17-2Pet1:18)

The voice of Jesus Christ
-------------------------

### His voice is recognisable

[JHN 10:1–10:5](https://ref.ly/John10:1-John10:5), [JHN 10:14–10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:14-John10:16), [JHN 10:27](https://ref.ly/John10:27)

### His voice calls people to him

**[JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37)** *See also* [ACT 9:4–9:7](https://ref.ly/Acts9:4-Acts9:7) parallels [ACT 22:7–22:9](https://ref.ly/Acts22:7-Acts22:9), [ACT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Acts26:14-Acts26:16); [REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20)

### His voice raises the dead

[JHN 5:25](https://ref.ly/John5:25), [JHN 5:28](https://ref.ly/John5:28), [JHN 11:43](https://ref.ly/John11:43)

### His voice cries out

[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46) parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34); ; [MAT 27:50](https://ref.ly/Matt27:50) parallel [LUK 23:46](https://ref.ly/Luke23:46)

Other voices
------------

### The voices of angels

[JOB 38:7](https://ref.ly/Job38:7); ; [DAN 10:6](https://ref.ly/Dan10:6); ; [MAT 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20-Matt1:21), [MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13); ; [REV 5:2](https://ref.ly/Rev5:2), [REV 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Rev5:11-Rev5:12), [REV 14:7–14:9](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7-Rev14:9), [REV 18:2](https://ref.ly/Rev18:2)

### The voice of wisdom

[PRO 1:20](https://ref.ly/Prov1:20), [PRO 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/Prov8:1-Prov8:4)

### The voices of the natural world

**[PSA 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:4)** *See also* [JOB 38:41](https://ref.ly/Job38:41); [PSA 93:3](https://ref.ly/Ps93:3); [ROM 10:18](https://ref.ly/Rom10:18)

## 5197: walking (id: 5197)

The action of travelling on foot at a normal pace. The image of walking with God is used in Scripture to represent the behaviour of believers in their relationship with God.

The physical activity of walking
--------------------------------

### For travel

**[GEN 13:17](https://ref.ly/Gen13:17)**; **[DEU 6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:7)** *See also* [JDG 5:10](https://ref.ly/Judg5:10); [1KI 18:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:7); [AMO 3:3](https://ref.ly/Amos3:3); [MAT 4:18](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18); [LUK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Luke24:17)

### As a miracle

**[MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5), [MAT 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/Matt14:25-Matt14:26)** *See also* [ACT 3:6–3:8](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6-Acts3:8), [ACT 14:10](https://ref.ly/Acts14:10)

### God walking

**[LEV 26:12](https://ref.ly/Lev26:12)** *See also* [GEN 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8)

Walking used figuratively of human behaviour
--------------------------------------------

### Walking in sin and darkness

**[1KI 15:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:26)**; **[PRO 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Prov2:12-Prov2:13)** *See also* [PSA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1), [PSA 82:5](https://ref.ly/Ps82:5); [PRO 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Prov2:12-Prov2:13); [1JN 1:6](https://ref.ly/1John1:6)

### Sometimes darkness represents depressing circumstances

**[LAM 3:2](https://ref.ly/Lam3:2)** *See also* [PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4); [ISA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Isa9:2), [ISA 59:9](https://ref.ly/Isa59:9)

### Walking with God

**[GEN 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Gen5:22-Gen5:24), [GEN 17:1](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1)**; **[REV 3:4](https://ref.ly/Rev3:4)** *See also* [GEN 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gen6:9); [DEU 8:6](https://ref.ly/Deut8:6), [DEU 10:12](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12); [1KI 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:3-1Kgs2:4); [ISA 2:3](https://ref.ly/Isa2:3); [JER 7:23](https://ref.ly/Jer7:23); [MIC 4:5](https://ref.ly/Mic4:5), [MIC 6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:8); [ZEC 3:7](https://ref.ly/Zech3:7); [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6)

### Walking in the truth or in the light

**[DEU 5:33](https://ref.ly/Deut5:33)**; **[1JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/1John1:7)** *See also* [PSA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Ps15:2), [PSA 26:2–26:3](https://ref.ly/Ps26:2-Ps26:3), [PSA 56:13](https://ref.ly/Ps56:13), [PSA 86:11](https://ref.ly/Ps86:11), [PSA 89:15](https://ref.ly/Ps89:15); [PRO 4:12](https://ref.ly/Prov4:12); [ISA 2:5](https://ref.ly/Isa2:5); [2JN 1:4–1:6](https://ref.ly/2John1:4-2John1:6); [3JN 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/3John1:3-3John1:4); [REV 21:24](https://ref.ly/Rev21:24)

### Walking as a way of living or behaving

**[EPH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Eph2:1-Eph2:2)** *See also* [ROM 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rom8:1); [2CO 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:7); [EPH 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eph4:1), [EPH 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eph5:2); [COL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Col1:10)

## 5198: weeping (id: 5198)

The physical act of shedding tears as the result of sadness or joy or in recognition of sin.

Weeping in personal grief
-------------------------

**[GEN 23:2](https://ref.ly/Gen23:2)**; **[PSA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Ps42:3)**; **[REV 5:4](https://ref.ly/Rev5:4)** *See also* [GEN 27:38](https://ref.ly/Gen27:38), [GEN 37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:35); [JDG 11:37](https://ref.ly/Judg11:37); [RUT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:9); [1SA 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:8), [1SA 20:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41); [2SA 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:16), [2SA 13:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:19), [2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30), [2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33) *David weeps for Absalom*; [2KI 8:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:11), [2KI 13:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:14), [2KI 20:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:3); [JOB 16:16](https://ref.ly/Job16:16), [JOB 16:20](https://ref.ly/Job16:20); [PSA 39:12](https://ref.ly/Ps39:12), [PSA 56:8](https://ref.ly/Ps56:8), [PSA 102:9](https://ref.ly/Ps102:9); [JHN 11:33](https://ref.ly/John11:33), [JHN 20:11](https://ref.ly/John20:11)

Weeping in shared grief
-----------------------

**[DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8)**; **[PSA 137:1](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1)** *See also* [NUM 14:1](https://ref.ly/Num14:1); [1SA 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:4-1Sam11:5), [1SA 20:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41), [1SA 30:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:4); [2SA 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:12) *David and his men mourn for Saul and Jonathan*; [2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24), [2SA 3:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:34); [JOB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Job2:12); [PSA 80:4–80:5](https://ref.ly/Ps80:4-Ps80:5); [ISA 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2-Isa15:3), [ISA 33:7](https://ref.ly/Isa33:7); [MRK 5:38](https://ref.ly/Mark5:38); [ACT 21:13](https://ref.ly/Acts21:13)

Weeping in personal remorse over failure and sin
------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75)** parallels [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:72), [LUK 22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:62) *See also* [1SA 2:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:33); [LUK 7:38](https://ref.ly/Luke7:38); [HEB 12:17](https://ref.ly/Heb12:17)

Weeping in group response to sin
--------------------------------

**[NEH 8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9)**; **[JER 3:21](https://ref.ly/Jer3:21)**; **[JAS 5:1](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1)** *See also* [NUM 25:6](https://ref.ly/Num25:6); [DEU 1:45](https://ref.ly/Deut1:45); [JDG 20:26](https://ref.ly/Judg20:26), [JDG 21:2](https://ref.ly/Judg21:2); [EZR 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:12-Ezra3:13) *the people's response to the rebuilding of the temple*; [ISA 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12-Isa22:13); [JER 25:34](https://ref.ly/Jer25:34), [JER 31:9](https://ref.ly/Jer31:9), [JER 50:4](https://ref.ly/Jer50:4); [LAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Lam1:2), [LAM 2:18](https://ref.ly/Lam2:18); [JOL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12); [AMO 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/Amos5:16-Amos5:17), [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10); [ZEC 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10-Zech12:11); [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12)

Weeping in response to others' sin and judgment
-----------------------------------------------

## 5199: womb (id: 5199)

The part of the female body where offspring are conceived and developed. A barren womb can be indicative of God's displeasure or a test of faith, while a fruitful womb may be a sign of his favour. It is God who creates within the womb. In ancient Israel, the first offspring of every womb belonged to God.

The womb as the place of conception and gestation
-------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 139:13–139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13-Ps139:16)** *See also* [GEN 25:23–25:24](https://ref.ly/Gen25:23-Gen25:24), [GEN 38:27](https://ref.ly/Gen38:27); [DEU 28:53](https://ref.ly/Deut28:53), [DEU 28:57](https://ref.ly/Deut28:57); [JOB 3:10–3:12](https://ref.ly/Job3:10-Job3:12), [JOB 10:18–10:19](https://ref.ly/Job10:18-Job10:19), [JOB 31:15](https://ref.ly/Job31:15); [PSA 22:9–22:11](https://ref.ly/Ps22:9-Ps22:11); [ECC 11:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:5); [ISA 44:2](https://ref.ly/Isa44:2), [ISA 44:24](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24), [ISA 49:5](https://ref.ly/Isa49:5), [ISA 66:7–66:9](https://ref.ly/Isa66:7-Isa66:9); [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5), [JER 20:14–20:18](https://ref.ly/Jer20:14-Jer20:18); [HOS 12:3](https://ref.ly/Hos12:3); [LUK 1:41–1:45](https://ref.ly/Luke1:41-Luke1:45); [JHN 3:4](https://ref.ly/John3:4); [ROM 4:19–4:21](https://ref.ly/Rom4:19-Rom4:21)

The barren womb
---------------

### Indicating divine displeasure

**[GEN 20:18](https://ref.ly/Gen20:18)** *See also* [GEN 29:31](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31); [HOS 9:11](https://ref.ly/Hos9:11), [HOS 9:14](https://ref.ly/Hos9:14)

### As a test of faith

**[LUK 1:6–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:6-Luke1:25)** *See also* [GEN 30:22–30:23](https://ref.ly/Gen30:22-Gen30:23); [1SA 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:5-1Sam1:6)

### A blessing in times of persecution

**[LUK 23:29–23:31](https://ref.ly/Luke23:29-Luke23:31)** *See also* [MAT 24:19](https://ref.ly/Matt24:19) parallels [MRK 13:17](https://ref.ly/Mark13:17), [LUK 21:23](https://ref.ly/Luke21:23)

A fruitful womb may indicate God's favour
-----------------------------------------

**[DEU 7:13](https://ref.ly/Deut7:13)** *See also* [GEN 29:31](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31), [GEN 30:22–30:23](https://ref.ly/Gen30:22-Gen30:23), [GEN 49:25](https://ref.ly/Gen49:25); [DEU 28:4](https://ref.ly/Deut28:4), [DEU 28:11](https://ref.ly/Deut28:11), [DEU 30:9](https://ref.ly/Deut30:9); [PSA 127:3–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:5), [PSA 128:3–128:4](https://ref.ly/Ps128:3-Ps128:4)

The first offspring of every womb belonged to God
-------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Exod13:1-Exod13:2)** *See also* [EXO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Exod13:12), [EXO 13:15](https://ref.ly/Exod13:15); [NUM 18:15](https://ref.ly/Num18:15)

Proverbial use of the womb
--------------------------

**[JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21)** *See also* [JOB 15:34–15:35](https://ref.ly/Job15:34-Job15:35), [JOB 24:20](https://ref.ly/Job24:20), [JOB 38:8](https://ref.ly/Job38:8), [JOB 38:29](https://ref.ly/Job38:29); [PSA 110:3](https://ref.ly/Ps110:3); [PRO 30:15–30:16](https://ref.ly/Prov30:15-Prov30:16), [PRO 31:2](https://ref.ly/Prov31:2); [ECC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:15); [HOS 13:13](https://ref.ly/Hos13:13)

------------------------------------

## 5201: accusation (id: 5201)

A charge brought against a person in a legal, ecclesiastical or spiritual context.

In legal affairs in general
---------------------------

### In the OT legal systems

**[DEU 19:15–19:21](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15-Deut19:21)** *See also* [DEU 17:2–17:7](https://ref.ly/Deut17:2-Deut17:7); [1KI 21:8–21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:13); [EZR 4:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:6); [NEH 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/Neh5:6-Neh5:7); [DAN 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Dan6:4-Dan6:5)

### In the NT legal systems

**[ACT 19:38–19:41](https://ref.ly/Acts19:38-Acts19:41)** *See also* [ACT 7:1](https://ref.ly/Acts7:1), [ACT 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12-Acts18:13), [ACT 23:23–23:30](https://ref.ly/Acts23:23-Acts23:30), [ACT 24:1–24:9](https://ref.ly/Acts24:1-Acts24:9), [ACT 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts25:2), [ACT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Acts28:19)

In God's covenant dealings with his people
------------------------------------------

**[MIC 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Mic6:1-Mic6:2)** *See also* [JER 2:5–2:9](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5-Jer2:9); [HOS 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:1-Hos4:3), [HOS 11:12–12:6](https://ref.ly/Hos11:12-Hos12:6)

In the earthly experience of Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------

**[JHN 19:6](https://ref.ly/John19:6)** *See also* [MAT 27:12–27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:12-Matt27:14), [MAT 27:37](https://ref.ly/Matt27:37) parallels [MRK 15:26](https://ref.ly/Mark15:26), [LUK 23:38](https://ref.ly/Luke23:38), [JHN 19:19–19:22](https://ref.ly/John19:19-John19:22); [LUK 23:4](https://ref.ly/Luke23:4); [JHN 18:29](https://ref.ly/John18:29), [JHN 19:4](https://ref.ly/John19:4)

In the experience of God's people
---------------------------------

### In the context of justification

**[ROM 8:33](https://ref.ly/Rom8:33)** *See also* [ISA 50:8–50:9](https://ref.ly/Isa50:8-Isa50:9); [COL 1:22](https://ref.ly/Col1:22)

### In their standing in the eyes of the world

**[EPH 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3-Eph5:4)** *See also* [MAT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:16); [1TH 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:13)

In the conduct of church affairs
--------------------------------

### Christian involvement in litigation

**[MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26)** parallel [LUK 12:58–12:59](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58-Luke12:59) *See also* [1CO 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1-1Cor6:8)

### In the qualification for church leaders

**[1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2)** *See also* [TIT 1:6](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6)

### In church disputes

**[1TI 5:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:19)** *See also* [MAT 18:15–18:20](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:20)

In the character and activity of Satan
--------------------------------------

**[ZEC 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Zech3:1-Zech3:2)** *See also* [JOB 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/Job1:9-Job1:11); [PSA 109:6](https://ref.ly/Ps109:6); [REV 12:9–12:10](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9-Rev12:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 19:15-21; Deuteronomy 17:2-7; 1 Kings 21:8-13; Ezra 4:6; Nehemiah 5:6-7; Daniel 6:4-5; Acts 19:38-41; Acts 7:1; Acts 18:12-13; Acts 23:23-30; Acts 24:1-9; Acts 25:1-2; Acts 28:19; Micah 6:1-2; Jeremiah 2:5-9; Hosea 4:1-3; Hosea 11:12-12:6; John 19:6; Matthew 27:12-14; Matthew 27:37; Mark 15:26; Luke 23:38; John 19:19-22; Luke 23:4; John 18:29; John 19:4; Romans 8:33; Isaiah 50:8-9; Colossians 1:22; Ephesians 5:3-4; Matthew 5:16; 1 Thessalonians 3:13; Matthew 5:25-26; Luke 12:58-59; 1 Corinthians 6:1-8; 1 Timothy 3:2; Titus 1:6; 1 Timothy 5:19; Matthew 18:15-20; Zechariah 3:1-2; Job 1:9-11; Psalms 109:6; Revelation 12:9-10
* **Associated Articles:** 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4120: Satan (ID: `4120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5270: court (ID: `5270@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5383: lawsuits (ID: `5383@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5401: madness (ID: `5401@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5472: proof, as evidence (ID: `5472@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5623: witnesses, legal (ID: `5623@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5625: witnesses, false (ID: `5625@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7772: prophets (ID: `7772@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5202: accusation, false (id: 5202)

Scripture expressly forbids making false accusations, but God's people have often been subjected to them and many false accusations were made against Jesus Christ.

False accusation forbidden
--------------------------

**[EXO 23:7](https://ref.ly/Exod23:7)** *See also* [EXO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1); [LEV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Lev19:16); [LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14)

Satan associated with false accusation
--------------------------------------

**[ZEC 3:1](https://ref.ly/Zech3:1)** *Although taken as a proper name in English, “Satan” is in fact simply the Hebrew word meaning “accuser”.* *See also* [JOB 1:6–1:12](https://ref.ly/Job1:6-Job1:12); [REV 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rev12:10)

Examples of false accusation
----------------------------

### In the OT

**[PSA 41:5–41:6](https://ref.ly/Ps41:5-Ps41:6)** *See also* [GEN 39:7–39:18](https://ref.ly/Gen39:7-Gen39:18); [2SA 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:1-2Sam10:3); [1KI 21:7–21:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:7-1Kgs21:14); [EZR 4:6–4:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:6-Ezra4:16); [NEH 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Neh6:5-Neh6:7); [PSA 35:19–35:20](https://ref.ly/Ps35:19-Ps35:20)

### In the experience of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:59–26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:61)** parallel [MRK 14:55–14:59](https://ref.ly/Mark14:55-Mark14:59) *See also* [MAT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Matt9:34), [MAT 12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24) parallels [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22), [LUK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15); [LUK 23:2](https://ref.ly/Luke23:2)

### In the experience of the first Christians

**[ACT 16:19–16:21](https://ref.ly/Acts16:19-Acts16:21)** *See also* [MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12); [ACT 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11-Acts6:14)

## 5203: acquittal (id: 5203)

The declaration of innocence and release from custody. It may be literal, in the context of human courts of justice, or figurative, God being portrayed as the judge of all the earth.

Divine justice
--------------

### Acquittal of the innocent

**[MAT 12:33–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:33-Matt12:37)** *See also* [1KI 8:31–8:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:31-1Kgs8:32) parallel [2CH 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:22-2Chr6:23); [JOB 4:7](https://ref.ly/Job4:7); [PRO 10:16](https://ref.ly/Prov10:16); [DAN 6:16–6:22](https://ref.ly/Dan6:16-Dan6:22); [MAT 12:5](https://ref.ly/Matt12:5)

### Punishment of the guilty

**[EXO 23:6–23:7](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6-Exod23:7)** *See also* [EXO 34:5–34:7](https://ref.ly/Exod34:5-Exod34:7); [JOB 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Job4:8-Job4:9); [MIC 6:9–6:16](https://ref.ly/Mic6:9-Mic6:16); [NAM 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Nah1:1-Nah1:6)

### The acquittal of sinners in justification

**[ROM 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1-Rom5:2)** *See also* [ROM 4:23–4:25](https://ref.ly/Rom4:23-Rom4:25)

Human justice
-------------

**[DEU 25:1–25:3](https://ref.ly/Deut25:1-Deut25:3)** *See also* [EXO 22:2](https://ref.ly/Exod22:2), [EXO 23:7–23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:7-Exod23:8); [DEU 19:15–19:19](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15-Deut19:19); [2KI 10:1–10:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:1-2Kgs10:9); [ISA 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:20-Isa29:21)

Unjust acquittal of the guilty
------------------------------

**[PRO 17:15](https://ref.ly/Prov17:15)** *See also* [PRO 18:5](https://ref.ly/Prov18:5), [PRO 24:23–24:25](https://ref.ly/Prov24:23-Prov24:25); [ISA 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Isa5:22-Isa5:23)

Examples of acquittal of the innocent
-------------------------------------

**[ACT 18:12–18:17](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12-Acts18:17)** *See also* [ACT 16:35–16:39](https://ref.ly/Acts16:35-Acts16:39)

Instances when the innocent were not acquitted
----------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ was not acquitted because of pressure from the chief priests and the elders

**[MAT 27:11–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11-Matt27:26)** parallels [MRK 15:2–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2-Mark15:15), [LUK 23:2–23:3](https://ref.ly/Luke23:2-Luke23:3), [LUK 23:18–23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:18-Luke23:25), [JHN 18:29–19:16](https://ref.ly/John18:29-John19:16)

### Paul was not acquitted because he had appealed to Caesar

**[ACT 26:30–26:32](https://ref.ly/Acts26:30-Acts26:32)** *See also* [ACT 25:1–25:12](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts25:12), [ACT 25:23–26:25](https://ref.ly/Acts25:23-Acts26:25)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 12:33-37; 1 Kings 8:31-32; 2 Chronicles 6:22-23; Job 4:7; Proverbs 10:16; Daniel 6:16-22; Matthew 12:5; Exodus 23:6-7; Exodus 34:5-7; Job 4:8-9; Micah 6:9-16; Nahum 1:1-6; Romans 5:1-2; Romans 4:23-25; Deuteronomy 25:1-3; Exodus 22:2; Exodus 23:7-8; Deuteronomy 19:15-19; 2 Kings 10:1-9; Isaiah 29:20-21; Proverbs 17:15; Proverbs 18:5; Proverbs 24:23-25; Isaiah 5:22-23; Acts 18:12-17; Acts 16:35-39; Matthew 27:11-26; Mark 15:2-15; Luke 23:2-3; Luke 23:18-25; John 18:29-19:16; Acts 26:30-32; Acts 25:1-12; Acts 25:23-26:25

## 5204: age (id: 5204)

The number of years reached or expected to be reached in a human life. The span of life is determined by God and may be divided into distinct phases, each with its own characteristics. The term may also refer to an era of history, particularly in contrasting the present age with the coming age to be inaugurated at Jesus Christ's return.

The span of life
----------------

### In God's hands

**[MAT 6:27](https://ref.ly/Matt6:27)** parallel [LUK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Luke12:25) *See also* [1SA 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:6); [PSA 139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:16); [ECC 8:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:8); [JAS 4:14–4:15](https://ref.ly/Jas4:14-Jas4:15)

### Longevity in the OT

**[GEN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gen5:5)** *See also* [GEN 5:27](https://ref.ly/Gen5:27), [GEN 9:29](https://ref.ly/Gen9:29)GEN [6:3](https://ref.ly/Gen6:3), [GEN 25:7](https://ref.ly/Gen25:7), [GEN 47:28](https://ref.ly/Gen47:28)

### The present\-day span of life

**[PSA 90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:10)** *See also* [2SA 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:4-2Sam5:5) *David lives to be 70*; [2SA 19:34–19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:34-2Sam19:35) *Barzillai, aged 80, expects to die soon*; [ISA 23:15](https://ref.ly/Isa23:15)

### Its apparent brevity

**[PSA 39:4–39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:4-Ps39:6)** *See also* [GEN 47:9](https://ref.ly/Gen47:9); [JOB 7:6](https://ref.ly/Job7:6); [PSA 89:47](https://ref.ly/Ps89:47), [PSA 90:3–90:6](https://ref.ly/Ps90:3-Ps90:6)

### Long life is a blessing from God

**[PRO 10:27](https://ref.ly/Prov10:27)** *See also* [PSA 91:16](https://ref.ly/Ps91:16); [PRO 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Prov3:1-Prov3:2)

Phases of human life
--------------------

### Importance associated with age

[GEN 43:33](https://ref.ly/Gen43:33); ; [1SA 16:6–16:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:6-1Sam16:11); ; [LUK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Luke22:26)

### The age of discretion

**[JHN 9:21–9:23](https://ref.ly/John9:21-John9:23)** *See also* [LUK 2:42](https://ref.ly/Luke2:42)

### The age of maturity: 20 years

**[LEV 27:3](https://ref.ly/Lev27:3)** *Adulthood reckoned from the age of 20*; **[NUM 26:2–26:4](https://ref.ly/Num26:2-Num26:4)** *See also* [NUM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:3), [NUM 1:45](https://ref.ly/Num1:45), [NUM 14:29](https://ref.ly/Num14:29), [NUM 32:11](https://ref.ly/Num32:11); [JOS 5:41](https://ref.ly/Josh5:41)CH [23:24](https://ref.ly/Josh23:24); [2CH 31:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:17); [EZR 3:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8)

### Full maturity reckoned from the age of 30

**[LUK 3:23](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23)** *See also* [NUM 4:3](https://ref.ly/Num4:3) *Levites assumed their duties at the age of 30*; [NUM 8:24](https://ref.ly/Num8:24) *From the ages of 25 to 30, Levites may have served an apprenticeship.*

### Old age

**[LEV 27:7](https://ref.ly/Lev27:7)** [NUM 4:47](https://ref.ly/Num4:47), [NUM 8:25–8:26](https://ref.ly/Num8:25-Num8:26)

Mountains as symbols of age
---------------------------

**[HAB 3:6](https://ref.ly/Hab3:6)** *See also* [GEN 49:26](https://ref.ly/Gen49:26); [JOB 15:7](https://ref.ly/Job15:7); [PSA 90:2](https://ref.ly/Ps90:2); [PRO 8:25](https://ref.ly/Prov8:25)

Ages of history
---------------

### Before Jesus Christ was revealed

**[EPH 3:9](https://ref.ly/Eph3:9)** *See also* [ROM 16:25–16:26](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25-Rom16:26); [1CO 10:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:11); [COL 1:26](https://ref.ly/Col1:26); [HEB 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:2)

### The present evil age

**[GAL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Gal1:4)** *See also* [1CO 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20), [1CO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6-1Cor2:8); [2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:4); [EPH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Eph2:2); [TIT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:12)

### The end of this age

**[MAT 13:49](https://ref.ly/Matt13:49)** *See also* [MAT 13:39–13:40](https://ref.ly/Matt13:39-Matt13:40), [MAT 24:3](https://ref.ly/Matt24:3), [MAT 24:10](https://ref.ly/Matt24:10)

### The age to come

**[MAT 19:28–19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28-Matt19:29)** *See also* [MRK 10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:30) parallel [LUK 18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:30); [LUK 20:34–20:36](https://ref.ly/Luke20:34-Luke20:36); [EPH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Eph2:7); [1TI 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:19)

### Blessing in the present age

**[HEB 6:5](https://ref.ly/Heb6:5)** *See also* [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [EPH 1:21](https://ref.ly/Eph1:21); [HEB 9:26](https://ref.ly/Heb9:26)

## 5205: alliance (id: 5205)

The coalition of two or more parties in order to achieve a particular aim. Political alliances might be formalised in a treaty or sealed by marriage. Human rebellion against God is demonstrated in hostile alliances against his people culminating in the rejection of Christ and the final gathering and overthrow of the nations outside Jerusalem. Because God's people are to depend on him alone, unholy alliances are condemned; however God does work in partnership with human allies and encourages his people to work together.

Political alliances
-------------------

### General examples

[GEN 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/Gen14:1-Gen14:3), [GEN 14:13](https://ref.ly/Gen14:13); ; [1SA 27:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:2)

### Alliances formalised in a treaty

[GEN 21:27](https://ref.ly/Gen21:27), [GEN 26:31](https://ref.ly/Gen26:31); ; [1KI 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:12), [1KI 15:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:19) parallel [2CH 16:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:3)

### Alliances sealed by marriage

[1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1); ; [2CH 18:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:1); ; [DAN 11:6](https://ref.ly/Dan11:6), [DAN 11:17](https://ref.ly/Dan11:17)

Alliances against God and his people
------------------------------------

### Surrounding nations against God's people

**[PSA 83:5–83:8](https://ref.ly/Ps83:5-Ps83:8)** *See also* [JOS 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/Josh10:5-Josh10:6), [JOS 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/Josh11:4-Josh11:5); [2CH 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:1); [ISA 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Isa7:1-Isa7:2); [NEH 4:8](https://ref.ly/Neh4:8)

### The nations against Jesus Christ

**[ACT 4:25–4:27](https://ref.ly/Acts4:25-Acts4:27)** *See also* [PSA 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1-Ps2:2)

### The nations against Jerusalem

[ZEC 14:2](https://ref.ly/Zech14:2); ; [REV 20:8–20:9](https://ref.ly/Rev20:8-Rev20:9)

### The wicked against the righteous

**[PSA 31:13](https://ref.ly/Ps31:13)** *See also* [PSA 56:6](https://ref.ly/Ps56:6), [PSA 59:3](https://ref.ly/Ps59:3), [PSA 71:10](https://ref.ly/Ps71:10)

### The rich and powerful against the weak

**[MIC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:3)** *See also* [PSA 94:20–94:21](https://ref.ly/Ps94:20-Ps94:21)

### God is greater than human alliances

**[PSA 48:4–48:5](https://ref.ly/Ps48:4-Ps48:5)**; **[NAM 1:12](https://ref.ly/Nah1:12)** *See also* [2CH 20:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:22); [ISA 7:4](https://ref.ly/Isa7:4); [EZK 30:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:6), [EZK 31:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:17); [OBA 1:7](https://ref.ly/Obad1:7); [NAM 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Nah3:8-Nah3:10)

Unholy alliances condemned by God
---------------------------------

### God's people forbidden to ally with the unrighteous

**[EXO 34:12](https://ref.ly/Exod34:12)**; **[2CO 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14-2Cor6:16)** *See also* [DEU 23:6](https://ref.ly/Deut23:6); [JOS 9:7](https://ref.ly/Josh9:7), [JOS 23:12](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12)

### Examples of alliances condemned by God

[2KI 16:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7) parallel [2CH 28:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:16) *Ahaz's proposed alliance with Assyria*; ; [2CH 20:35–20:37](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:35-2Chr20:37) *Jehoshaphat's alliance with Ahaziah of Israel*; [ISA 30:1](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1), [ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1); [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1) *Israel's vacillation between Egypt and Assyria*

### The futility of trust in human allies

**[ISA 31:3](https://ref.ly/Isa31:3)** *See also* [JER 13:21](https://ref.ly/Jer13:21), [JER 22:20–22:21](https://ref.ly/Jer22:20-Jer22:21); [LAM 1:19](https://ref.ly/Lam1:19)

God works with human allies
---------------------------

### God uses Cyrus to deliver Israel

**[ISA 48:14](https://ref.ly/Isa48:14)** *See also* [ISA 13:4](https://ref.ly/Isa13:4), [ISA 41:2](https://ref.ly/Isa41:2); [JER 50:9](https://ref.ly/Jer50:9), [JER 51:20–51:23](https://ref.ly/Jer51:20-Jer51:23)

### Fellow\-workers with God

**[2CO 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:1)** *See also* [1CO 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:9); [2CO 5:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:20)

Believers allied in God's service
---------------------------------

### Working together

[EZR 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:2); ; [NEH 4:6](https://ref.ly/Neh4:6); ; [1CO 15:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:10)

### Paul's fellow\-workers

**[PHP 4:3](https://ref.ly/Phil4:3)** *See also* [ROM 16:3](https://ref.ly/Rom16:3), [ROM 16:21](https://ref.ly/Rom16:21); [2CO 8:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:23); [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25); [PHM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:24)

## 5206: archers (id: 5206)

Those armed with arrows, whether for hunting or warfare. Archers were particularly valued on account of their accuracy.

## 5207: architecture (id: 5207)

The design of buildings, for religious or secular use. Building designs were often regarded in Scripture as embodying spiritual truths, and capable of being divinely inspired.

Examples of architecture
------------------------

### The tabernacle

**[HEB 8:5](https://ref.ly/Heb8:5)** *See also* [EXO 25:8–25:9](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8-Exod25:9), [EXO 26:1–26:37](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1-Exod26:37)

### The temple

**[1CH 28:11–28:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:11-1Chr28:12)** *See also* [1KI 6:1–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:1-1Kgs6:10), [1KI 6:15–6:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:15-1Kgs6:38); [1CH 28:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:19); [2CH 3:1–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:1-2Chr3:17), [2CH 4:1–4:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:1-2Chr4:22)

### Altars

**[JOS 8:31](https://ref.ly/Josh8:31)** *See also* [EXO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Exod20:25); [2KI 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10-2Kgs16:11) *Adopting the design of a pagan altar indicated both idolatry and submission to Assyria.*

### Ordinary dwellings

[DEU 22:8](https://ref.ly/Deut22:8)

### Ezekiel's vision of a restored temple

[EZK 40:1–40:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:1-Ezek40:49), [EZK 41:1–41:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:1-Ezek41:26), [EZK 42:1–42:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek42:1-Ezek42:20), [EZK 43:10–43:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:10-Ezek43:17)

### Other structures

[GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4); ; [1KI 7:1–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:1-1Kgs7:12); ; [PRO 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Prov24:3-Prov24:4) *implies that a house must be more than bricks and mortar*; ; [JER 22:13–22:14](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13-Jer22:14)

Divinely dictated design
------------------------

### The ark

[GEN 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/Gen6:14-Gen6:16) *the earliest recorded example of naval architecture*

Building design as a spiritual metaphor
---------------------------------------

### The prophecy of the rejected stone

[PSA 118:22](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22); ; [MAT 21:42](https://ref.ly/Matt21:42) parallels [MRK 12:10](https://ref.ly/Mark12:10), [LUK 20:17](https://ref.ly/Luke20:17); ; [1PE 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:7); ; [MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27) parallel [LUK 6:48–6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:48-Luke6:49) *the two foundations*; ; [1CO 3:10–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:10-1Cor3:13) *the work of Christian ministers*; ; [EPH 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19-Eph2:22) *the church*; ; [1PE 2:4–2:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:4-1Pet2:7); ; [ISA 54:11–54:12](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11-Isa54:12) *the heavenly city*; ; [HEB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:10); ; [REV 21:9–21:22](https://ref.ly/Rev21:9-Rev21:22)

God as the architect of creation
--------------------------------

[JOB 38:4–38:5](https://ref.ly/Job38:4-Job38:5)

## 5208: armies (id: 5208)

An organised military force for the defence or expansion of national borders. Israel's national life was characterised by many battles with foreign nations such as Egypt, Philistia, Assyria and Babylon and by Roman occupation.

Examples of armies in conflict with Israel
------------------------------------------

### The Egyptian army when Israel left Egypt

[EXO 14:5–14:9](https://ref.ly/Exod14:5-Exod14:9), [EXO 14:23–14:25](https://ref.ly/Exod14:23-Exod14:25)

### The inhabitants of Canaan when Israel entered the promised land

[JOS 3:10](https://ref.ly/Josh3:10), [JOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Josh12:1), [JOS 12:7](https://ref.ly/Josh12:7), [JOS 23:9](https://ref.ly/Josh23:9); ; [JDG 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Judg3:1-Judg3:3)

### The Philistines, a constant enemy

[1SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:5), [1SA 14:20–14:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:20-1Sam14:23), [1SA 17:1–17:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:1-1Sam17:3), [1SA 31:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:1), [1SA 31:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:7) parallel [1CH 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:7)

### Assyria, defeating the northern kingdom of Israel

[2KI 15:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:29), [2KI 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:5-2Kgs17:6); ; [ISA 36:1](https://ref.ly/Isa36:1)

### Babylon, defeating the southern kingdom of Judah

[2KI 24:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:1), [2KI 25:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1); ; [JER 39:1](https://ref.ly/Jer39:1) parallel [JER 52:4](https://ref.ly/Jer52:4); ; [2KI 25:10–25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:10-2Kgs25:11) parallel [2CH 36:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:17)

Palestine occupied by the Roman army in NT times
------------------------------------------------

### Roman law in force at the time of Jesus Christ

[LUK 2:1](https://ref.ly/Luke2:1); ; [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/John18:28)

### Roman military personnel in the life of Jesus Christ and in the lives of the first Christians

[MAT 8:5](https://ref.ly/Matt8:5) parallel [LUK 7:3](https://ref.ly/Luke7:3); ; [MAT 27:54](https://ref.ly/Matt27:54) parallel [LUK 23:47](https://ref.ly/Luke23:47); ; [ACT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Acts10:1), [ACT 21:31](https://ref.ly/Acts21:31)

Development of Israel's army from the time of the exodus to the reign of Solomon
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### A military census taken after the exodus

[NUM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:3), [NUM 26:2](https://ref.ly/Num26:2)

### Fighting men to possess the promised land

[DEU 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:2); ; [JOS 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Josh1:10-Josh1:11), [JOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Josh12:1)

### The army as a tribal militia assembled in times of crisis

[JDG 4:1–4:6](https://ref.ly/Judg4:1-Judg4:6)

### Certain tribes gaining reputations for proficiency in particular forms of combat

[JDG 20:16](https://ref.ly/Judg20:16); ; [1CH 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:2), [1CH 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:8), [1CH 12:24–12:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:24-1Chr12:37)

### Saul and David having regular contingents of special forces

[1SA 13:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:2); ; [2SA 15:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:18), [2SA 23:8–23:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:8-2Sam23:12) parallel [1CH 11:10–11:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:10-1Chr11:14)

### Forces divided into twelve battalions, each serving for a month at a time

[1CH 27:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:1)

### Chariot forces hardly known in David's time, but much used by Solomon

[2SA 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:3-2Sam8:4); ; [1KI 4:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:26), [1KI 10:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:26)

God's sovereignty over the armies of Israel and over his spiritual armies
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[JOS 5:13–5:15](https://ref.ly/Josh5:13-Josh5:15); ; [1SA 17:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45); ; [1KI 22:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:19); ; [2KI 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:17)

### In the final battle between good and evil, Jesus Christ appearing as the leader of the armies of heaven

[REV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Rev19:14), [REV 19:19](https://ref.ly/Rev19:19)

## 5209: armour (id: 5209)

Protective covering worn by soldiers to prevent injury in battle. However, those who wore it were not impregnable. Christians are to be equipped with spiritual armour.

Coat of armour
--------------

### The breastplate

**[2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14)** *See also* [1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5), [1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38); [2SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:8); [EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:14)

### The belt

**[2SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:8)** *The belt was an essential item of armour as it held the protective parts of the tunic in place.* *See also* [1SA 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:4); [2SA 18:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:11); [EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:14)

### The helmet

**[1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5)** *See also* [1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38); [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14); [JER 46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:4); [EZK 38:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:5)

### The shield

**[JER 46:3](https://ref.ly/Jer46:3)** *See also* [2CH 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:12), [2CH 32:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:5); [NEH 4:16](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16); [EPH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Eph6:16)

Armour worn by soldiers
-----------------------

**[2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14)** *See also* [NUM 31:3–31:5](https://ref.ly/Num31:3-Num31:5), [NUM 32:20–32:21](https://ref.ly/Num32:20-Num32:21), [NUM 32:27](https://ref.ly/Num32:27); [JOS 4:13](https://ref.ly/Josh4:13); [JER 46:3–46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:3-Jer46:4)

Armour\-bearers
---------------

**[1SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:21)** *Armour\-bearers were sometimes used to carry the shield and additional weapons for a military leader or outstanding warrior.* *See also* [JDG 9:54](https://ref.ly/Judg9:54); [1SA 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:1), [1SA 14:6–14:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:6-1Sam14:14), [1SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:7)

Those wearing armour were not impregnable
-----------------------------------------

**[1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11), [1KI 22:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34)** parallel [2CH 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33) *See also* [1SA 17:48–17:49](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:48-1Sam17:49), [1SA 31:3–31:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:3-1Sam31:4); [LUK 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/Luke11:21-Luke11:22)

Christians are to be equipped with spiritual armour
---------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 6:11](https://ref.ly/Eph6:11)** *See also* [EPH 6:13–6:17](https://ref.ly/Eph6:13-Eph6:17); [ROM 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12); [2CO 6:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:7); [1TH 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:8)

## 5210: arrows (id: 5210)

Pointed pieces of wood, shot from a bow, used in hunting and warfare. In the hands of skilful archers they are accurate and deadly weapons. Figuratively, they represent conviction and judgments from God, or the hurtful words of men and women.

Characteristics of arrows
-------------------------

### Deadly

**[PSA 64:3](https://ref.ly/Ps64:3)** *See also* [DEU 32:42](https://ref.ly/Deut32:42); [PSA 7:13](https://ref.ly/Ps7:13); [EZK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:16)

### Accurate

**[2KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:24)**

### Sharp and piercing

**[PSA 45:5](https://ref.ly/Ps45:5)** *See also* [2KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:24); [JOB 20:24](https://ref.ly/Job20:24); [PSA 120:4](https://ref.ly/Ps120:4); [ISA 5:28](https://ref.ly/Isa5:28)

Arrows used as a sign
---------------------

### To warn David

[1SA 20:19–20:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:19-1Sam20:22)

### Of victory over Aram

[2KI 13:17–13:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:17-2Kgs13:19)

### Of divination by the Babylonians

[EZK 21:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:21)

Arrows referred to figuratively
-------------------------------

### To describe conviction from God

**[PSA 38:2](https://ref.ly/Ps38:2)** *See also* [JOB 6:4](https://ref.ly/Job6:4), [JOB 34:6](https://ref.ly/Job34:6); [LAM 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Lam3:12-Lam3:13)

### To describe judgment from God

**[PSA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Ps64:7)** *See also* [DEU 32:23](https://ref.ly/Deut32:23), [DEU 32:42](https://ref.ly/Deut32:42); [PSA 45:5](https://ref.ly/Ps45:5); [EZK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:16)

### To describe false accusations

**[PSA 64:3–64:4](https://ref.ly/Ps64:3-Ps64:4)** *See also* [PSA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Ps11:2), [PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4)

### To describe false testimony

**[PRO 25:18](https://ref.ly/Prov25:18)** *See also* [PRO 26:18–26:19](https://ref.ly/Prov26:18-Prov26:19); [JER 9:3](https://ref.ly/Jer9:3), [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8)

### To describe attacks of Satan

**[EPH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Eph6:16)**

### As lightning

**[PSA 77:17](https://ref.ly/Ps77:17)** *See also* [2SA 22:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:15); [PSA 144:6](https://ref.ly/Ps144:6); [ZEC 9:14](https://ref.ly/Zech9:14)

### As sons as a blessing from God

**[PSA 127:3–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:5)**

## 5211: art (id: 5211)

Skilful, creative work, carried out using a variety of materials. Purposes included decoration, ornamentation and representation of objects of worship, the last of which Scripture firmly condemns.

Painting
--------

**[EZK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:10)** *See also* [EZK 23:11–23:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:11-Ezek23:15)

Decorative panelling
--------------------

**[2CH 3:5–3:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:5-2Chr3:7)** *See also* [1KI 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:14-1Kgs6:15); [PSA 74:3–74:6](https://ref.ly/Ps74:3-Ps74:6); [JER 22:14](https://ref.ly/Jer22:14)

------------------------------------

**[1KI 6:21–6:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:21-1Kgs6:22)** parallel [2CH 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:4-2Chr3:7) *See also* [1KI 6:31–6:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:31-1Kgs6:35), [1KI 10:14–10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14-1Kgs10:21) parallel [2CH 9:13–9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13-2Chr9:20)

-------------------------------------

**[SNG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Song5:14)** *See also* [1KI 10:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:18) parallel [2CH 9:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:17); [1KI 22:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:39); [PSA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8); [AMO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Amos3:15), [AMO 6:4](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4)

Carving and sculpture
---------------------

**[1KI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:18)** *See also* [1KI 6:29–6:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:29-1Kgs6:35); [2CH 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:7); [PSA 144:12](https://ref.ly/Ps144:12); [EZK 41:15–41:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:15-Ezek41:26)

Engraving
---------

### As an artistic skill

**[1KI 7:34–7:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:34-1Kgs7:37)** *See also* [EXO 28:9–28:12](https://ref.ly/Exod28:9-Exod28:12), [EXO 28:15–28:21](https://ref.ly/Exod28:15-Exod28:21), [EXO 28:36–28:38](https://ref.ly/Exod28:36-Exod28:38), [EXO 39:6–39:7](https://ref.ly/Exod39:6-Exod39:7), [EXO 39:8–39:14](https://ref.ly/Exod39:8-Exod39:14), [EXO 39:30–39:31](https://ref.ly/Exod39:30-Exod39:31); [1KI 7:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:31), [1KI 7:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:36); [2CH 2:5–2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:5-2Chr2:7), [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14)

Art in the tabernacle
---------------------

-----------------

Art associated with idolatry
----------------------------

### Forbidden

**[DEU 27:15](https://ref.ly/Deut27:15)** *See also* [EXO 20:3–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:3-Exod20:6) parallel [DEU 5:7–5:10](https://ref.ly/Deut5:7-Deut5:10); [EXO 20:22–20:23](https://ref.ly/Exod20:22-Exod20:23), [EXO 34:10–34:17](https://ref.ly/Exod34:10-Exod34:17); [LEV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Lev19:4), [LEV 26:1](https://ref.ly/Lev26:1); [DEU 4:16–4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:16-Deut4:19), [DEU 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/Deut4:23-Deut4:24), [DEU 29:16–29:18](https://ref.ly/Deut29:16-Deut29:18)

### Examples of idols and images

**[2KI 21:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:7)** parallel [2CH 33:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:7) *See also* [EXO 32:1–32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:4); [JDG 17:1–17:6](https://ref.ly/Judg17:1-Judg17:6), [JDG 18:11–18:31](https://ref.ly/Judg18:11-Judg18:31); [ISA 46:1–46:2](https://ref.ly/Isa46:1-Isa46:2); [JER 50:1–50:2](https://ref.ly/Jer50:1-Jer50:2); [EZK 8:9–8:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:9-Ezek8:12)

### Condemned

**[HAB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hab2:18)** *See also* [EXO 34:10–34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:10-Exod34:14); [NUM 33:51–33:52](https://ref.ly/Num33:51-Num33:52); [DEU 7:25–7:26](https://ref.ly/Deut7:25-Deut7:26); [2CH 34:1–34:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:1-2Chr34:4); [ISA 44:9–44:20](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9-Isa44:20); [EZK 7:20–7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:20-Ezek7:22); [HOS 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Hos13:1-Hos13:3); [MIC 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/Mic5:13-Mic5:14); [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14)

## 5212: arts and crafts (id: 5212)

Artistic skills, usually practised by craftsmen and passed on from generation to generation, of which a wide variety are mentioned in Scripture.

Metalwork
---------

**[EXO 39:2–39:3](https://ref.ly/Exod39:2-Exod39:3)** *See also* [EXO 31:1–31:5](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:5) parallel [EXO 35:30–35:33](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:33); [1SA 13:19–13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:19-1Sam13:21); [1KI 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:13-1Kgs7:14); [ISA 41:7](https://ref.ly/Isa41:7); [JER 10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:9); [ACT 19:23–19:41](https://ref.ly/Acts19:23-Acts19:41)

Stonework
---------

**[1KI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:17-1Kgs5:18)** *See also* [1KI 6:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:36), [1KI 7:9–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9-1Kgs7:12); [2KI 22:3–22:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:3-2Kgs22:6) parallel [2CH 34:8–34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:8-2Chr34:11); [1CH 22:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:2); [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14); [EZK 40:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:42)

Building
--------

**[1CO 3:10–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:10-1Cor3:15)** *See also* [2CH 34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:11); [EZR 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:10); [ISA 9:8–9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:8-Isa9:10); [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); [HEB 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/Heb3:3-Heb3:4)

Pottery
-------

**[ISA 41:25](https://ref.ly/Isa41:25)** *See also* [2SA 17:27–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:27-2Sam17:29); [1CH 4:21–4:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:21-1Chr4:23); [ISA 29:16](https://ref.ly/Isa29:16), [ISA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9), [ISA 64:8](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8); [JER 18:1–18:10](https://ref.ly/Jer18:1-Jer18:10), [JER 19:1](https://ref.ly/Jer19:1); [ROM 9:19–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:19-Rom9:21)

Carpentry
---------

**[ISA 44:13](https://ref.ly/Isa44:13)** *See also* [GEN 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gen6:14); [2SA 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:11) parallel [1CH 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:1); [1KI 6:31–6:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:31-1Kgs6:33); [2KI 12:10–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:10-2Kgs12:12) parallel [2CH 24:11–24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11-2Chr24:12); [MAT 13:54–13:55](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54-Matt13:55) parallel [MRK 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2-Mark6:3)

---------

## 5213: assembly (id: 5213)

Assemblies of the Israelites
----------------------------

**[LEV 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Lev23:1-Lev23:2)** *See also* [EXO 35:1](https://ref.ly/Exod35:1); [LEV 4:14](https://ref.ly/Lev4:14), [LEV 8:1–8:5](https://ref.ly/Lev8:1-Lev8:5); [NUM 10:1–10:7](https://ref.ly/Num10:1-Num10:7), [NUM 15:35–15:36](https://ref.ly/Num15:35-Num15:36), [NUM 16:45–16:47](https://ref.ly/Num16:45-Num16:47); [1SA 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:5); [1KI 8:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:22); [1CH 15:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:3); [2CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:28); [EZR 3:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:1); [PSA 22:25](https://ref.ly/Ps22:25), [PSA 40:9–40:10](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9-Ps40:10), [PSA 149:1](https://ref.ly/Ps149:1); [AMO 5:21](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21)

Assembling armies for war and groups for self\-protection
---------------------------------------------------------

**[JDG 20:2](https://ref.ly/Judg20:2)** *See also* [JDG 10:17](https://ref.ly/Judg10:17); [1SA 7:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:6), [1SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:5), [1SA 17:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:2); [EST 8:11](https://ref.ly/Esth8:11), [EST 9:16](https://ref.ly/Esth9:16)

Assemblies of the Christian church
----------------------------------

**[ACT 14:27](https://ref.ly/Acts14:27)** *The Greek word for “church” can also be translated “assembly”.* *See also* [MAT 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18); [ACT 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Acts13:1-Acts13:2), [ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23); [1CO 11:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:18), [1CO 14:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:23), [1CO 16:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:19)
