## 5214: attack (id: 5214)

A violent act of aggression often associated with intent to invade, sometimes as an act of retribution and occasionally as a strategic act of self\-defence. Scripture also recognises the possibility of spiritual aggression through attack by evil forces.

Examples of God's people attacking others
-----------------------------------------

[GEN 14:14–14:16](https://ref.ly/Gen14:14-Gen14:16) *Abraham attacks the captors of the king of Sodom*; ; [JDG 7:19–7:21](https://ref.ly/Judg7:19-Judg7:21) *Gideon attacks the Midianites*; ; [1SA 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:13-1Sam14:14) *Jonathan attacks the Philistines*; ; [2SA 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:7) parallel [1CH 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:4-1Chr11:5) *David attacks the Jebusites*; ; [DEU 20:1–20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1-Deut20:20) *Moses gives detailed instruction on going to war.*

### The Israelites attack the inhabitants of Canaan

**[JOS 6:2](https://ref.ly/Josh6:2), [JOS 6:20](https://ref.ly/Josh6:20)** *See also* [JOS 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Josh8:1-Josh8:3), [JOS 8:21–8:22](https://ref.ly/Josh8:21-Josh8:22), [JOS 10:9–10:10](https://ref.ly/Josh10:9-Josh10:10), [JOS 10:29–10:42](https://ref.ly/Josh10:29-Josh10:42), [JOS 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/Josh11:7-Josh11:8), [JOS 11:16–11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:16-Josh11:23)

Examples of God's people attacked by others
-------------------------------------------

### By the Amalekites

**[EXO 17:8–17:13](https://ref.ly/Exod17:8-Exod17:13)**

### By the Midianites

*See also* [JDG 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Judg6:1-Judg6:6)

### By the Ammonites

[JDG 10:9](https://ref.ly/Judg10:9), [JDG 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Judg11:4-Judg11:6); ; [2SA 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:8) parallel [1CH 19:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:9); ; [2CH 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:1)

### By the Philistines

**[1SA 7:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:7)** *See also* [2SA 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:17), [2SA 5:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:22) parallels [1CH 14:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:8), [1CH 14:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:13); [PSA 56:1–56:2](https://ref.ly/Ps56:1-Ps56:2)

### By Syria (Aram)

**[1KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:1)** *See also* [1KI 20:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:26); [2KI 3:21–3:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:21-2Kgs3:27) *the Moabites seek to attack Israel but are routed*; [2KI 5:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:2), [2KI 6:24–6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:24-2Kgs6:25), [2KI 12:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:17); [2CH 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:1-2Chr16:4), [2CH 28:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:5)

### By the Egyptians

[2CH 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:1-2Chr12:4), [2CH 12:7–12:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:7-2Chr12:9)

Attacks on God's people resulting in exile
------------------------------------------

### Israel attacked by the Assyrians

**[2KI 17:3–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:3-2Kgs17:6)** *See also* [2KI 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:9-2Kgs18:12)

### Judah attacked by the Babylonians

**[2CH 36:5–36:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:5-2Chr36:7)** parallel [2KI 24:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:1) *See also* [2KI 24:10–24:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:10-2Kgs24:16), [2KI 25:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1), [2KI 25:8–25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:8-2Kgs25:11) parallel [2CH 36:15–36:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:20); [JER 32:24](https://ref.ly/Jer32:24); [EZK 33:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:21)

God's people attack each other
------------------------------

### Intertribal warfare

[JDG 9:22–9:23](https://ref.ly/Judg9:22-Judg9:23), [JDG 9:39–9:45](https://ref.ly/Judg9:39-Judg9:45), [JDG 20:18–20:48](https://ref.ly/Judg20:18-Judg20:48)

### The north (Israel) and south (Judah) attack each other

**[2KI 14:11–14:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:11-2Kgs14:14)** parallel [2CH 25:20–25:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:20-2Chr25:24) *See also* [2CH 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:1-2Chr13:3)

### Attacks within the church

**[JAS 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1-Jas4:2)** *See also* [PHP 3:2](https://ref.ly/Phil3:2); [1TH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:15); [JAS 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11-Jas4:12); [1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9)

Examples of individuals who were attacked
-----------------------------------------

**[GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8)**; **[ACT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22)** *See also* [EXO 21:12–21:20](https://ref.ly/Exod21:12-Exod21:20); [1SA 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:10), [1SA 24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:7)

The danger of attack by Satan and his agents
--------------------------------------------

**[1PE 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8-1Pet5:9)** *See also* [MAT 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:11) parallel [LUK 4:1–4:13](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:13); [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12); [REV 11:7](https://ref.ly/Rev11:7)

God provides protection against both physical and spiritual attack
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 6:13–6:18](https://ref.ly/Eph6:13-Eph6:18)** *See also* [PSA 7:10](https://ref.ly/Ps7:10), [PSA 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/Ps18:1-Ps18:3), [PSA 138:7](https://ref.ly/Ps138:7); [2TI 4:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 14:14-16; Judges 7:19-21; 1 Samuel 14:13-14; 2 Samuel 5:6-7; 1 Chronicles 11:4-5; Deuteronomy 20:1-20; Joshua 6:2; Joshua 6:20; Joshua 8:1-3; Joshua 8:21-22; Joshua 10:9-10; Joshua 10:29-42; Joshua 11:7-8; Joshua 11:16-23; Exodus 17:8-13; Judges 6:1-6; Judges 10:9; Judges 11:4-6; 2 Samuel 10:8; 1 Chronicles 19:9; 2 Chronicles 20:1; 1 Samuel 7:7; 2 Samuel 5:17; 2 Samuel 5:22; 1 Chronicles 14:8; 1 Chronicles 14:13; Psalms 56:1-2; 1 Kings 20:1; 1 Kings 20:26; 2 Kings 3:21-27; 2 Kings 5:2; 2 Kings 6:24-25; 2 Kings 12:17; 2 Chronicles 16:1-4; 2 Chronicles 28:5; 2 Chronicles 12:1-4; 2 Chronicles 12:7-9; 2 Kings 17:3-6; 2 Kings 18:9-12; 2 Chronicles 36:5-7; 2 Kings 24:1; 2 Kings 24:10-16; 2 Kings 25:1; 2 Kings 25:8-11; 2 Chronicles 36:15-20; Jeremiah 32:24; Ezekiel 33:21; Judges 9:22-23; Judges 9:39-45; Judges 20:18-48; 2 Kings 14:11-14; 2 Chronicles 25:20-24; 2 Chronicles 13:1-3; James 4:1-2; Philippians 3:2; 1 Thessalonians 5:15; James 4:11-12; 1 Peter 3:9; Genesis 4:8; Acts 16:22; Exodus 21:12-20; 1 Samuel 19:9-10; 1 Samuel 24:7; 1 Peter 5:8-9; Matthew 4:1-11; Luke 4:1-13; Ephesians 6:12; Revelation 11:7; Ephesians 6:13-18; Psalms 7:10; Psalms 18:1-3; Psalms 138:7; 2 Timothy 4:18
* **Associated Articles:** 5290: defeat (ID: `5290@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5291: defence (ID: `5291@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5354: invasions (ID: `5354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5527: shield (ID: `5527@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5529: sieges (ID: `5529@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5572: sword (ID: `5572@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5596: victory (ID: `5596@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5612: weapons (ID: `5612@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5814: confrontation (ID: `5814@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5975: violence (ID: `5975@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8482: spiritual warfare (ID: `8482@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5215: authority (id: 5215)

The right to act or speak in certain ways, in accordance with the authorisation of a higher power. All human authority derives from God, and is to be exercised in a responsible manner.

* **Associated Articles:** 5216: authority, nature of (ID: `5216@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5217: authority, in the church (ID: `5217@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5218: authority, in the home (ID: `5218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5219: authority, of human institutions (ID: `5219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5220: authority, abuse of (ID: `5220@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5216: authority, nature of (id: 5216)

The God\-given freedom of action expressed in rightful power to control the actions of others.

Ultimate authority belongs to God, who does as he pleases
---------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 115:3](https://ref.ly/Ps115:3)** *See also* [2CH 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:6); [JOB 9:12](https://ref.ly/Job9:12), [JOB 23:13](https://ref.ly/Job23:13), [JOB 42:2](https://ref.ly/Job42:2); [PSA 135:6](https://ref.ly/Ps135:6); [ISA 14:27](https://ref.ly/Isa14:27), [ISA 43:13](https://ref.ly/Isa43:13), [ISA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9), [ISA 46:10](https://ref.ly/Isa46:10); [DAN 4:35](https://ref.ly/Dan4:35) *Nebuchadnezzar acknowledges God's authority*; [ROM 9:19–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:19-Rom9:21); [EPH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eph1:11) *God's authority is demonstrated in election.*

Human authority
---------------

### All human authority is given and established by God

**[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21), [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17), [DAN 5:18–5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:18-Dan5:21); [JHN 19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:11)

### It involves the rightful freedom to give orders

**[MAT 8:9](https://ref.ly/Matt8:9)** parallel [LUK 7:8](https://ref.ly/Luke7:8) *The centurion to Jesus Christ.* *See also* [GEN 41:35](https://ref.ly/Gen41:35); [NUM 27:20](https://ref.ly/Num27:20); [DEU 1:15](https://ref.ly/Deut1:15) *Moses delegates authority*; [JDG 9:29](https://ref.ly/Judg9:29); [EZR 7:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:24); [NEH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Neh3:7); [EST 9:29](https://ref.ly/Esth9:29); [ISA 22:21](https://ref.ly/Isa22:21); [JER 38:10](https://ref.ly/Jer38:10); [MAT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:23) parallel [LUK 19:17](https://ref.ly/Luke19:17) *the parable of the talents*; [MRK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Mark13:34); [LUK 20:20](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20), [LUK 23:7](https://ref.ly/Luke23:7); [JHN 19:10](https://ref.ly/John19:10)ACT [9:14](https://ref.ly/John9:14), [ACT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10), [ACT 26:12](https://ref.ly/Acts26:12)[1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13)

### It is limited, unlike God's authority

**[JHN 19:10–19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:10-John19:11)**

Responsibilities of those with authority
----------------------------------------

### They are to act as servants, not lording it over people

**[MAT 20:25–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25-Matt20:28)** parallel [MRK 10:42–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:45) *See also* [JHN 13:4–13:15](https://ref.ly/John13:4-John13:15)

### Their authority is to be used for the benefit of others

**[2CO 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:8)** *See also* [2CO 13:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:10)

### It is sometimes necessary to withhold exercising authority for others' good

**[1CO 9:4–9:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:4-1Cor9:18)** *See also* [1CO 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1-1Cor6:7), [1CO 8:8–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:8-1Cor8:13), [1CO 10:23–10:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:23-1Cor10:24)

Examples of the authority of believers
--------------------------------------

### Authority over possessions

**[ACT 5:4](https://ref.ly/Acts5:4)** *Peter to Ananias regarding the land he sold.*

### Authority over the will

**[1CO 7:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:37)** *Paul on marriage.*

### Authority to become children of God

**[JHN 1:12](https://ref.ly/John1:12)**

The authority of the law over believers during their lifetime
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 7:1](https://ref.ly/Rom7:1)**

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 115:3; 2 Chronicles 20:6; Job 9:12; Job 23:13; Job 42:2; Psalms 135:6; Isaiah 14:27; Isaiah 43:13; Isaiah 45:9; Isaiah 46:10; Daniel 4:35; Romans 9:19-21; Ephesians 1:11; Romans 13:1; Daniel 2:21; Daniel 4:17; Daniel 5:18-21; John 19:11; Matthew 8:9; Luke 7:8; Genesis 41:35; Numbers 27:20; Deuteronomy 1:15; Judges 9:29; Ezra 7:24; Nehemiah 3:7; Esther 9:29; Isaiah 22:21; Jeremiah 38:10; Matthew 25:23; Luke 19:17; Mark 13:34; Luke 20:20; Luke 23:7; John 19:10; John 9:14; Acts 26:10; Acts 26:12; 1 Peter 2:13; John 19:10-11; Matthew 20:25-28; Mark 10:42-45; John 13:4-15; 2 Corinthians 10:8; 2 Corinthians 13:10; 1 Corinthians 9:4-18; 1 Corinthians 6:1-7; 1 Corinthians 8:8-13; 1 Corinthians 10:23-24; Acts 5:4; 1 Corinthians 7:37; John 1:12; Romans 7:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1610: Scripture (ID: `1610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2012: Jesus Christ, authority of (ID: `2012@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2345: Jesus Christ, kingdom of (ID: `2345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3045: Holy Spirit, sovereignty of (ID: `3045@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4110: angels (ID: `4110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4120: Satan (ID: `4120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5452: power (ID: `5452@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6658: freedom (ID: `6658@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7706: apostles (ID: `7706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5217: authority, in the church (id: 5217)

The exercise of freedom under Jesus Christ's authority for the good of the church.

Supreme authority belongs to Jesus Christ as head of the church
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23)** *See also* [EPH 1:22](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22), [EPH 4:15](https://ref.ly/Eph4:15), [EPH 5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:24); [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18)

Spiritual authority under Jesus Christ in the church is possessed by elders
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### As shepherds who teach and care for the flock

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *See also* [JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17); [EPH 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:13); [1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2), [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17); [TIT 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:1-Titus2:10); [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

### As overseers ruling the church

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *See also* [ROM 12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:8); [1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12) *Those serving as overseers must rule their own homes well*; [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)

The response of the church to the authority of elders
-----------------------------------------------------

### Elders are to be obeyed

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)**

### Elders are to be honoured and respected

**[1TH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12-1Thess5:13)** *See also* [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)

Paul limits the authority of women in the church
------------------------------------------------

**[1TI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:12)** *Some believe Paul's restriction to apply to the women in the Ephesian church who were seizing authority rather than having been granted it. Others believe Paul is prohibiting women from being official teachers in the assembled church.* *See also* [1CO 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:5-1Cor11:6), [1CO 11:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:10), [1CO 14:33–14:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:33-1Cor14:37)

### The reason for this prohibition derives from God's order of creation

**[1TI 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:13-1Tim2:14)** *See also* [1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3), [1CO 11:7–11:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:7-1Cor11:10)

Kinds of authority within the church
------------------------------------

### Authority to preach the gospel

[MAT 28:18–28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18-Matt28:19); ; [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15)

### Authority to excommunicate

[MAT 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:18); ; [1CO 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:5) *“hand this man over to Satan” here means “send back into the world ruled by Satan”*; ; [1CO 5:9–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:13)

### Authority to forgive sins

[MAT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Matt18:18); ; [JHN 20:23](https://ref.ly/John20:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 5:23; Ephesians 1:22; Ephesians 4:15; Ephesians 5:24; Colossians 1:18; Acts 20:28; John 21:15-17; Ephesians 4:11-13; 1 Timothy 3:2; 1 Timothy 5:17; Titus 2:1-10; 1 Peter 5:2; Romans 12:8; 1 Thessalonians 5:12; Hebrews 13:17; 1 Thessalonians 5:12-13; 1 Timothy 2:12; 1 Corinthians 11:5-6; 1 Corinthians 11:10; 1 Corinthians 14:33-37; 1 Timothy 2:13-14; 1 Corinthians 11:3; 1 Corinthians 11:7-10; Matthew 28:18-19; Mark 16:15; Matthew 18:15-18; 1 Corinthians 5:1-5; 1 Corinthians 5:9-13; Matthew 18:18; John 20:23
* **Associated Articles:** 2212: Jesus Christ, head of the church (ID: `2212@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7026: church, leadership of (ID: `7026@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7720: elders, in the church (ID: `7720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7784: shepherd (ID: `7784@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7943: ministry, in the church (ID: `7943@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5218: authority, in the home (id: 5218)

The exercise of God\-given authority within the husband\-wife and parent\-child relationships.

A husband is given headship over his wife
-----------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23)**

The basis for this headship
---------------------------

### God's headship over Jesus Christ

**[1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3)**

### Jesus Christ's headship over the church

**[EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23)**

### God's order of creation

**[1CO 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:8-1Cor11:9)** *See also* [GEN 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:22)

This headship has been affected by the fall
-------------------------------------------

**[GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16)**

How this headship is to be exercised
------------------------------------

### Lovingly and sacrificially

**[EPH 5:25](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25)** *See also* [EPH 5:28](https://ref.ly/Eph5:28), [EPH 5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:33); [COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19); [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### As Jesus Christ rules as head of the church

**[EPH 5:25–5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:29)**

### Regarding his wife as part of himself

**[EPH 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:28-Eph5:29)** *See also* [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

Christian wives are to submit to their husbands
-----------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:22](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22)** parallel [COL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Col3:18)

### They are to follow the example of the submission of the church to Jesus Christ

**[EPH 5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:24)**

Fathers are heads of their families
-----------------------------------

**[GEN 18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:19)** *See also* [GEN 35:2](https://ref.ly/Gen35:2); [JOS 24:15](https://ref.ly/Josh24:15); [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4); [1TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:4), [1TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:12); [TIT 1:6](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6)

Fathers' authority is to be exercised reasonably
------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)** *See also* [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

The response of children to parental authority
----------------------------------------------

### Obedience

**[EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1)** parallel [COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20) *See also* [PRO 6:20](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20), [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)

### Respect

**[EPH 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:2-Eph6:3)** *See also* [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16); [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12), [EXO 21:15](https://ref.ly/Exod21:15), [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17); [DEU 27:16](https://ref.ly/Deut27:16); [PRO 19:26](https://ref.ly/Prov19:26), [PRO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Prov20:20); [MAT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Matt19:19) parallels [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19), [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20)

Kinds of authority within the family
------------------------------------

### Authority to discipline

**[PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18)** *See also* [PRO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Prov23:13), [PRO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Prov29:17)

### Authority to teach

**[PRO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8)** *See also* [PRO 3:1](https://ref.ly/Prov3:1), [PRO 13:1](https://ref.ly/Prov13:1)

### Authority to command

**[COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20)** parallel [EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1) *See also* [PRO 6:20–6:23](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 5:23; 1 Corinthians 11:3; 1 Corinthians 11:8-9; Genesis 2:20-22; Genesis 3:16; Ephesians 5:25; Ephesians 5:28; Ephesians 5:33; Colossians 3:19; 1 Peter 3:7; Ephesians 5:25-29; Ephesians 5:28-29; Ephesians 5:22; Colossians 3:18; Ephesians 5:24; Genesis 18:19; Genesis 35:2; Joshua 24:15; Ephesians 6:4; 1 Timothy 3:4; 1 Timothy 3:12; Titus 1:6; Colossians 3:21; Ephesians 6:1; Colossians 3:20; Proverbs 6:20; Proverbs 23:22; Ephesians 6:2-3; Deuteronomy 5:16; Exodus 20:12; Exodus 21:15; Exodus 21:17; Deuteronomy 27:16; Proverbs 19:26; Proverbs 20:20; Matthew 19:19; Mark 10:19; Luke 18:20; Proverbs 19:18; Proverbs 23:13; Proverbs 29:17; Proverbs 1:8; Proverbs 3:1; Proverbs 13:1; Proverbs 6:20-23
* **Associated Articles:** 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5684: fathers (ID: `5684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5700: headship (ID: `5700@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5718: mothers (ID: `5718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5219: authority, of human institutions (id: 5219)

The exercise of God\-established freedom to regulate society for the good of its members.

Without authority sin is unrestrained
-------------------------------------

**[ROM 1:24–1:26](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24-Rom1:26)** *See also* [GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5) *This was before the giving of the law*; [JDG 21:25](https://ref.ly/Judg21:25); [ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)

All human authorities are instituted and established by God
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:2)** *See also* [GEN 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:5-Gen9:6); [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21), [DAN 2:37–2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37-Dan2:38), [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17), [DAN 5:18–5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:18-Dan5:21), [DAN 5:26](https://ref.ly/Dan5:26); [JHN 19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:11); [1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13)

Rulers are God's servants to do good and restrain evil
------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1); [JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9), [JER 27:6](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6), [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10); [ROM 13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6); [1TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:2); [1PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:14)

The proper response to human authorities
----------------------------------------

### They are to be obeyed by everyone

**[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [ECC 8:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:2); [MAT 22:17–22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:17-Matt22:21) parallels [MRK 12:14–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:14-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:22–20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:22-Luke20:25); [ROM 13:5–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:5-Rom13:7); [TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)

### They are to be honoured and respected

**[PRO 24:21](https://ref.ly/Prov24:21)** *See also* [ROM 13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:7); [1PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:17)

### They are not to be obeyed if their demands conflict with the law of God

**[ACT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Acts4:19)** *See also* [EXO 1:17](https://ref.ly/Exod1:17); [1KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:3); [DAN 3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:18), [DAN 6:12](https://ref.ly/Dan6:12); [MAT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:21) parallels [MRK 12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:17), [LUK 20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:25); [HEB 11:23](https://ref.ly/Heb11:23)

### They are to be prayed for

**[1TI 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1-1Tim2:2)** *See also* [JER 29:7](https://ref.ly/Jer29:7)

### Christians are to use rights granted by the authorities

[ACT 16:35–16:39](https://ref.ly/Acts16:35-Acts16:39), [ACT 22:22–22:29](https://ref.ly/Acts22:22-Acts22:29), [ACT 25:1–25:12](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts25:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 1:24-26; Genesis 6:5; Judges 21:25; Romans 13:4; Romans 13:1-2; Genesis 9:5-6; Daniel 2:21; Daniel 2:37-38; Daniel 4:17; Daniel 5:18-21; Daniel 5:26; John 19:11; 1 Peter 2:13; Isaiah 45:1; Jeremiah 25:9; Jeremiah 27:6; Jeremiah 43:10; Romans 13:6; 1 Timothy 2:2; 1 Peter 2:14; Romans 13:1; Ecclesiastes 8:2; Matthew 22:17-21; Mark 12:14-17; Luke 20:22-25; Romans 13:5-7; Titus 3:1; 1 Peter 2:13-14; Proverbs 24:21; Romans 13:7; 1 Peter 2:17; Acts 4:19; Exodus 1:17; 1 Kings 21:3; Daniel 3:18; Daniel 6:12; Matthew 22:21; Mark 12:17; Luke 20:25; Hebrews 11:23; 1 Timothy 2:1-2; Jeremiah 29:7; Acts 16:35-39; Acts 22:22-29; Acts 25:1-12
* **Associated Articles:** 5008: conscience (ID: `5008@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5257: civil authorities (ID: `5257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5404: masters (ID: `5404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5504: rights (ID: `5504@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5509: rulers (ID: `5509@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5520: servants (ID: `5520@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5577: taxation (ID: `5577@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5959: submission (ID: `5959@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8240: ethics (ID: `8240@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8456: obedience, to human authorities (ID: `8456@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5220: authority, abuse of (id: 5220)

The misuse of a person's freedom of action in order to exploit, oppress or treat other people in a way that is not for their good.

Warnings against the abuse of authority
---------------------------------------

### By employers over employees

**[EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)** *See also* [LEV 25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:43); [DEU 24:14](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14)

### By fathers over children

**[EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)** parallel [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

### By husbands over wives

**[COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19)**

### By elders over the church

**[1PE 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2-1Pet5:3)** *See also* [EZK 34:2–34:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:2-Ezek34:7)

### By those mistreating foreigners

**[EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21)** *See also* [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9)

### By leaders over those who are led

**[MAT 20:25–20:27](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25-Matt20:27)** parallel [MRK 10:42–10:44](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:44) *See also* [LUK 22:25–22:26](https://ref.ly/Luke22:25-Luke22:26)

### By those taking advantage of the helpless or poor

**[EXO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22)** *See also* [PRO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22); [EZK 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)

### By those who oppress others

**[JER 22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:17)** *See also* [PSA 73:8](https://ref.ly/Ps73:8), [PSA 119:134](https://ref.ly/Ps119:134); [EZK 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:7)

Examples of the abuse of authority
----------------------------------

**[GEN 16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6)** *See also* [EXO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11); [1KI 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:14); [JAS 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jas2:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 6:9; Leviticus 25:43; Deuteronomy 24:14; Ephesians 6:4; Colossians 3:21; Colossians 3:19; 1 Peter 5:2-3; Ezekiel 34:2-7; Exodus 22:21; Exodus 23:9; Matthew 20:25-27; Mark 10:42-44; Luke 22:25-26; Exodus 22:22; Proverbs 22:22; Ezekiel 22:29; Zechariah 7:10; Jeremiah 22:17; Psalms 73:8; Psalms 119:134; Ezekiel 22:7; Genesis 16:6; Exodus 1:11; 1 Kings 12:14; James 2:6
* **Associated Articles:** 5310: exploitation (ID: `5310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5395: lordship, human and divine (ID: `5395@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5448: poverty, attitudes towards (ID: `5448@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8827: selfishness (ID: `8827@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5222: baking (id: 5222)

Cooking in an oven or over an open fire. Bread baked in this way was the staple diet of the population in biblical times. Bricks were also sometimes produced by baking.

Baking bread
------------

### In ovens

**[LEV 26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:26)** *See also* [EXO 8:3](https://ref.ly/Exod8:3); [LEV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Lev7:9), [LEV 11:35](https://ref.ly/Lev11:35)

### Over open fires

**[1KI 19:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:6)** *See also* [ISA 44:14–44:19](https://ref.ly/Isa44:14-Isa44:19); [EZK 4:9–4:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:9-Ezek4:15); [JHN 21:9](https://ref.ly/John21:9)

### Unleavened bread

**[EXO 12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:39)** *Bread was usually baked with yeast, but where there was insufficient time for kneading and proving, the raising agent was omitted.* *See also* [GEN 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:3); [LEV 6:14–6:17](https://ref.ly/Lev6:14-Lev6:17); [1SA 28:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:24)

Baking of offerings
-------------------

**[LEV 2:4–2:10](https://ref.ly/Lev2:4-Lev2:10)** *See also* [LEV 6:14–6:18](https://ref.ly/Lev6:14-Lev6:18), [LEV 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Lev7:9-Lev7:10), [LEV 23:15–23:18](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:18), [LEV 24:5–24:6](https://ref.ly/Lev24:5-Lev24:6); [2KI 4:42](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:42)

Baking of manna
---------------

**[EXO 16:21–16:23](https://ref.ly/Exod16:21-Exod16:23)**

Baking of clay
--------------

**[DAN 2:32–2:33](https://ref.ly/Dan2:32-Dan2:33)** *See also* [GEN 11:3](https://ref.ly/Gen11:3); [DAN 2:41–2:43](https://ref.ly/Dan2:41-Dan2:43)

Bakers
------

### At home

**[GEN 18:6](https://ref.ly/Gen18:6)** *See also* [LEV 26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:26) *Women baked at home or in communal ovens*; [2SA 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:6-2Sam13:8)

### At royal courts

**[1SA 8:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:13)** *See also* [GEN 40:1–40:22](https://ref.ly/Gen40:1-Gen40:22), [GEN 41:10](https://ref.ly/Gen41:10)

### In the temple

**[1CH 9:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:31)** *See also* [1CH 23:28–23:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:28-1Chr23:29); [EZK 46:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:20)

### As tradesmen in towns

**[JER 37:21](https://ref.ly/Jer37:21)** *See also* [HOS 7:4](https://ref.ly/Hos7:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 26:26; Exodus 8:3; Leviticus 7:9; Leviticus 11:35; 1 Kings 19:6; Isaiah 44:14-19; Ezekiel 4:9-15; John 21:9; Exodus 12:39; Genesis 19:1-3; Leviticus 6:14-17; 1 Samuel 28:24; Leviticus 2:4-10; Leviticus 6:14-18; Leviticus 7:9-10; Leviticus 23:15-18; Leviticus 24:5-6; 2 Kings 4:42; Exodus 16:21-23; Daniel 2:32-33; Genesis 11:3; Daniel 2:41-43; Genesis 18:6; 2 Samuel 13:6-8; 1 Samuel 8:13; Genesis 40:1-22; Genesis 41:10; 1 Chronicles 9:31; 1 Chronicles 23:28-29; Ezekiel 46:20; Jeremiah 37:21; Hosea 7:4
* **Associated Articles:** 4315: clay (ID: `4315@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4318: coal (ID: `4318@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4418: bread (ID: `4418@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4474: manna (ID: `4474@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4554: yeast (ID: `4554@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5239: bricks (ID: `5239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5268: cooking (ID: `5268@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5435: ovens (ID: `5435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5445: potters and pottery (ID: `5445@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5223: banner (id: 5223)

Generally, a standard raised in battle as a rallying point for armies. God is described as Israel's banner in recognition of his powerful defence of his people. The Messiah will raise a banner to rally the scattered exiles of Israel.

Israel's tribes identified by banners
-------------------------------------

**[NUM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Num2:2)** *See also* [NUM 1:52](https://ref.ly/Num1:52), [NUM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Num2:17), [NUM 2:34](https://ref.ly/Num2:34), [NUM 10:14](https://ref.ly/Num10:14)

Banners carried into battle
---------------------------

### Battle standards carried by armies

**[PSA 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ps20:5)** *See also* [PSA 74:4](https://ref.ly/Ps74:4); [SNG 6:4](https://ref.ly/Song6:4); [ISA 30:17](https://ref.ly/Isa30:17) *Banners to rally armies were often set up on the top of a hill*; [JER 4:21](https://ref.ly/Jer4:21)

### The LORD as Israel's banner

**[EXO 17:15–17:16](https://ref.ly/Exod17:15-Exod17:16)** *The LORD is the rallying point for Israel and the source of the nation's confidence in battle.* *See also* [PSA 60:4–60:6](https://ref.ly/Ps60:4-Ps60:6)

### God's banner raised against his people

**[ISA 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Isa5:25-Isa5:26)** *God raises a banner to rally nations to battle against Israel.* *See also* [JER 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jer4:6), [JER 6:1](https://ref.ly/Jer6:1)

### God's banner raised against the nations

**[ISA 13:2–13:5](https://ref.ly/Isa13:2-Isa13:5)** *See also* [ISA 18:3](https://ref.ly/Isa18:3), [ISA 31:9](https://ref.ly/Isa31:9); [JER 50:2](https://ref.ly/Jer50:2), [JER 51:12](https://ref.ly/Jer51:12), [JER 51:27–51:28](https://ref.ly/Jer51:27-Jer51:28)

The Messiah as a banner to the peoples
--------------------------------------

**[ISA 11:10–11:12](https://ref.ly/Isa11:10-Isa11:12)** *See also* [ISA 49:22](https://ref.ly/Isa49:22), [ISA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Isa62:10); [ZEC 10:8](https://ref.ly/Zech10:8); [JHN 12:32](https://ref.ly/John12:32)

Other references to banners
---------------------------

[SNG 2:4](https://ref.ly/Song2:4) *The king's love is like a banner, displayed for all to see*; ; [EZK 27:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:7) *a banner made from embroidered linen*

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 2:2; Numbers 1:52; Numbers 2:17; Numbers 2:34; Numbers 10:14; Psalms 20:5; Psalms 74:4; Song of Songs 6:4; Isaiah 30:17; Jeremiah 4:21; Exodus 17:15-16; Psalms 60:4-6; Isaiah 5:25-26; Jeremiah 4:6; Jeremiah 6:1; Isaiah 13:2-5; Isaiah 18:3; Isaiah 31:9; Jeremiah 50:2; Jeremiah 51:12; Jeremiah 51:27-28; Isaiah 11:10-12; Isaiah 49:22; Isaiah 62:10; Zechariah 10:8; John 12:32; Song of Songs 2:4; Ezekiel 27:7
* **Associated Articles:** 1205: God, titles and names of (ID: `1205@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1448: signs (ID: `1448@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2230: Messiah, coming of (ID: `2230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5208: armies (ID: `5208@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7215: exile, the (ID: `7215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5224: barn (id: 5224)

Barns were associated with industry and prosperity
--------------------------------------------------

### Full barns were a sign of blessing

**[PRO 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Prov3:9-Prov3:10)** *See also* [DEU 28:8](https://ref.ly/Deut28:8); [PSA 144:12–144:15](https://ref.ly/Ps144:12-Ps144:15)

### Empty barns were a sign of poverty

**[HAG 2:15–2:19](https://ref.ly/Hag2:15-Hag2:19)**

### The priority of spiritual over material prosperity

**[LUK 12:15–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15-Luke12:21)** *See also* [MAT 6:25–6:26](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:26); [LUK 12:23–12:24](https://ref.ly/Luke12:23-Luke12:24)

God's barn is a metaphor for the kingdom of heaven
--------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Luke3:16-Luke3:17)** parallel [MAT 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11-Matt3:12) *See also* [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 3:9-10; Deuteronomy 28:8; Psalms 144:12-15; Haggai 2:15-19; Luke 12:15-21; Matthew 6:25-26; Luke 12:23-24; Luke 3:16-17; Matthew 3:11-12; Matthew 13:24-30
* **Associated Articles:** 4035: abundance (ID: `4035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4406: agriculture (ID: `4406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4524: threshing-floor (ID: `4524@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5446: poverty (ID: `5446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5558: storing (ID: `5558@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5225: barrenness (id: 5225)

A lack of fertility, especially the inability of a woman to conceive.

Barren land
-----------

### Barrenness describes waste and unproductive land

**[JER 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jer2:6)** *See also* [DEU 32:10](https://ref.ly/Deut32:10); [JOL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Joel2:20)

### Barren land as a sign of God's disfavour

**[GEN 3:18](https://ref.ly/Gen3:18)** *Thorns and thistles are often used to designate barren or unproductive land.* *See also* [HOS 10:8](https://ref.ly/Hos10:8); [HEB 6:8](https://ref.ly/Heb6:8)

Fertility, as opposed to barrenness, a sign of God's blessing
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Exod23:25-Exod23:26)** *Consequently, the inability to conceive was regarded as a sign of God's displeasure.* *See also* [DEU 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Deut7:13-Deut7:14), [DEU 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:14-Deut11:15); [PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9); [ISA 41:18](https://ref.ly/Isa41:18)ISA [54:1](https://ref.ly/Isa54:1); [GAL 4:27](https://ref.ly/Gal4:27)

The barren woman
----------------

### Her desire for children

**[GEN 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1-Gen30:2)** *See also* [PRO 30:16](https://ref.ly/Prov30:16)

### Examples of barren women

[GEN 16:1](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1) *Sarah*; ; [GEN 29:31](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31) *Rachel*; ; [JDG 13:3](https://ref.ly/Judg13:3) *Manoah's wife*; ; [1SA 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:5) *Hannah*; ; [LUK 1:7](https://ref.ly/Luke1:7) *Elizabeth*; ; [LUK 1:36–1:37](https://ref.ly/Luke1:36-Luke1:37) *The ability to bear a child in these circumstances, especially in old age, was seen as a sign of God's blessing and his miraculous power.*

### Barrenness was sometimes seen as a sign of God's disfavour

[GEN 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:4), [GEN 20:17–20:18](https://ref.ly/Gen20:17-Gen20:18), [GEN 29:31–29:34](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31-Gen29:34); ; [1SA 1:1–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:1-1Sam1:20); ; [ISA 4:1](https://ref.ly/Isa4:1); ; [LUK 1:5–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5-Luke1:25)

### Childlessness was not always the result of barrenness

**[DEU 28:41](https://ref.ly/Deut28:41)**

### Barrenness might be an advantage in some circumstances

**[LUK 23:29](https://ref.ly/Luke23:29)** *Referring to the disasters about to come upon Jerusalem.*

Metaphorically, barrenness may describe an unproductive or desperate condition
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Heb6:7-Heb6:8)** *See also* [JOB 3:7](https://ref.ly/Job3:7); [LUK 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/Luke13:6-Luke13:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 2:6; Deuteronomy 32:10; Joel 2:20; Genesis 3:18; Hosea 10:8; Hebrews 6:8; Exodus 23:25-26; Deuteronomy 7:13-14; Deuteronomy 11:14-15; Psalms 113:9; Isaiah 41:18; Isaiah 54:1; Galatians 4:27; Genesis 30:1-2; Proverbs 30:16; Genesis 16:1; Genesis 29:31; Judges 13:3; 1 Samuel 2:5; Luke 1:7; Luke 1:36-37; Genesis 16:1-4; Genesis 20:17-18; Genesis 29:31-34; 1 Samuel 1:1-20; Isaiah 4:1; Luke 1:5-25; Deuteronomy 28:41; Luke 23:29; Hebrews 6:7-8; Job 3:7; Luke 13:6-9
* **Associated Articles:** 4206: land (ID: `4206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5199: womb (ID: `5199@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5733: pregnancy (ID: `5733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5836: disgrace (ID: `5836@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8845: unfruitfulness (ID: `8845@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5226: basin (id: 5226)

A receptacle for liquids, often used for washing or purification rites.

Basins used as receptacles
--------------------------

**[EXO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22)** *A basin is used to collect the blood of sacrifices.* *See also* [EXO 24:6](https://ref.ly/Exod24:6); [LEV 14:5](https://ref.ly/Lev14:5), [LEV 14:50](https://ref.ly/Lev14:50)

Basins used for ritual washing
------------------------------

### Between the altar and the Tent of Meeting

[EXO 30:18](https://ref.ly/Exod30:18), [EXO 40:11](https://ref.ly/Exod40:11), [EXO 40:30](https://ref.ly/Exod40:30); ; [LEV 8:11](https://ref.ly/Lev8:11)

### In Solomon's temple

**[2CH 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:6)** *See also* [1KI 7:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:30), [1KI 7:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:38) parallel [2CH 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:14); [2KI 16:171](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:171)KI [7:40](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:40); [JER 52:17–52:18](https://ref.ly/Jer52:17-Jer52:18)

A basin used for ritual washing
-------------------------------

**[JHN 13:5](https://ref.ly/John13:5)**

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 12:22; Exodus 24:6; Leviticus 14:5; Leviticus 14:50; Exodus 30:18; Exodus 40:11; Exodus 40:30; Leviticus 8:11; 2 Chronicles 4:6; 1 Kings 7:30; 1 Kings 7:38; 2 Chronicles 4:14; 2 Kings 16:171; 2 Kings 7:40; Jeremiah 52:17-18; John 13:5
* **Associated Articles:** 5153: foot-washing (ID: `5153@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5445: potters and pottery (ID: `5445@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7342: cleanliness (ID: `7342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7416: purification (ID: `7416@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7426: ritual washing (ID: `7426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7467: temple, Solomon's (ID: `7467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7478: washing (ID: `7478@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7766: priests (ID: `7766@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5227: basket (id: 5227)

Flat, open baskets to carry bread
---------------------------------

### In the chief baker's dream

*See also* [GEN 40:16–40:18](https://ref.ly/Gen40:16-Gen40:18)

### For offering unleavened bread

**[EXO 29:23](https://ref.ly/Exod29:23)** parallel [LEV 8:26](https://ref.ly/Lev8:26) *See also* [EXO 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/Exod29:2-Exod29:3) parallel [LEV 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Lev8:1-Lev8:2); [NUM 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/Num6:15-Num6:17)

Baskets used to hold food
-------------------------

### To store household provisions

**[DEU 28:5](https://ref.ly/Deut28:5)** *Signifying the continued provision of food for the household.* *See also* [DEU 28:17](https://ref.ly/Deut28:17)

### To collect food

**[MAT 14:20](https://ref.ly/Matt14:20)** parallels [MRK 6:43](https://ref.ly/Mark6:43), [LUK 9:17](https://ref.ly/Luke9:17), [JHN 6:13](https://ref.ly/John6:13) *See also* [MAT 13:48](https://ref.ly/Matt13:48), [MAT 15:37](https://ref.ly/Matt15:37) parallel [MRK 8:8](https://ref.ly/Mark8:8); [MAT 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Matt16:9-Matt16:10) parallel [MRK 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Mark8:19-Mark8:20)

Baskets used to carry fruit
---------------------------

**[JER 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Jer24:1-Jer24:2)** *See also* [DEU 23:24](https://ref.ly/Deut23:24); [PSA 81:6](https://ref.ly/Ps81:6); [AMO 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Amos8:1-Amos8:2)

Other uses of baskets
---------------------

[EXO 2:3–2:5](https://ref.ly/Exod2:3-Exod2:5) *Moses is put into a papyrus basket*; ; [DEU 26:2–26:4](https://ref.ly/Deut26:2-Deut26:4) *firstfruits presented in a basket*; ; [2KI 10:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:7) *the heads of Ahab's sons sent to Jehu in baskets*; ; [ZEC 5:6–5:11](https://ref.ly/Zech5:6-Zech5:11) *The wickedness of Jerusalem is removed, figuratively, to Babylon in a basket.*; [ACT 9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:25); ; [2CO 11:33](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:33)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 40:16-18; Exodus 29:23; Leviticus 8:26; Exodus 29:2-3; Leviticus 8:1-2; Numbers 6:15-17; Deuteronomy 28:5; Deuteronomy 28:17; Matthew 14:20; Mark 6:43; Luke 9:17; John 6:13; Matthew 13:48; Matthew 15:37; Mark 8:8; Matthew 16:9-10; Mark 8:19-20; Jeremiah 24:1-2; Deuteronomy 23:24; Psalms 81:6; Amos 8:1-2; Exodus 2:3-5; Deuteronomy 26:2-4; 2 Kings 10:7; Zechariah 5:6-11; Acts 9:25; 2 Corinthians 11:33

## 5228: battering-rams (id: 5228)

Battering\-rams used to make breaches in the walls of besieged cities
---------------------------------------------------------------------

### Historic references to the breaking down of walls

[2SA 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:15) *The wall of Abel Beth Maacah. References to breached or broken walls are evidence that the use of battering\-rams was common*; ; [2KI 14:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:13) parallel [2CH 25:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:23); ; [JER 39:2](https://ref.ly/Jer39:2) parallels [2CH 36:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:19), [JER 52:7](https://ref.ly/Jer52:7); ; [NEH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3)

### Figurative references to the breaking down of walls

[PSA 89:40](https://ref.ly/Ps89:40) *referring to God's displeasure with David's dynasty*

### Prophetic references to the breaking down of walls as a picture of the LORD's power

[ISA 22:5](https://ref.ly/Isa22:5), [ISA 24:12](https://ref.ly/Isa24:12); ; [EZK 21:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:22), [EZK 26:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:9); ; [MIC 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mic2:13)

### Peace is symbolised by no breaching of walls

[PSA 144:14](https://ref.ly/Ps144:14)

Model battering\-rams were part of the symbolic representation of the siege of Jerusalem
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[EZK 4:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:2)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 20:15; 2 Kings 14:13; 2 Chronicles 25:23; Jeremiah 39:2; 2 Chronicles 36:19; Jeremiah 52:7; Nehemiah 1:3; Psalms 89:40; Isaiah 22:5; Isaiah 24:12; Ezekiel 21:22; Ezekiel 26:9; Micah 2:13; Psalms 144:14; Ezekiel 4:2
* **Associated Articles:** 5214: attack (ID: `5214@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5529: sieges (ID: `5529@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5604: walls (ID: `5604@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5612: weapons (ID: `5612@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5229: bed (id: 5229)

A household item of furniture set aside for sleep, rest and quietness.

Various kinds of bed
--------------------

**[DEU 3:11](https://ref.ly/Deut3:11)**; **[AMO 6:4](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4)**; **[ACT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Acts9:34)** *See also* [MAT 9:6](https://ref.ly/Matt9:6) parallels [MRK 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:11-Mark2:12), [LUK 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Luke5:18-Luke5:19); [MRK 2:4](https://ref.ly/Mark2:4) parallels [MAT 9:2](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2), [LUK 5:24](https://ref.ly/Luke5:24); [ACT 5:15](https://ref.ly/Acts5:15)

The bed as a place of rest
--------------------------

**[2KI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:10)** *See also* [1SA 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:2); [2SA 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:5), [2SA 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:7), [2SA 11:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:13); [EST 7:8](https://ref.ly/Esth7:8); [JOB 33:15](https://ref.ly/Job33:15); [PSA 139:8](https://ref.ly/Ps139:8); [PRO 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Prov7:16-Prov7:17), [PRO 26:14](https://ref.ly/Prov26:14); [ISA 28:20](https://ref.ly/Isa28:20) *Beds were not always comfortable*; [DAN 4:5](https://ref.ly/Dan4:5), [DAN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Dan7:1); [LUK 11:7](https://ref.ly/Luke11:7)

The bed as a place of recuperation
----------------------------------

**[PSA 41:3](https://ref.ly/Ps41:3)**; **[ACT 28:8](https://ref.ly/Acts28:8)** *See also* [GEN 48:2](https://ref.ly/Gen48:2); [EXO 21:18](https://ref.ly/Exod21:18); [2SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:5); [1KI 17:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:19); [2KI 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:4), [2KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:21); [JOB 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Job7:13-Job7:14), [JOB 33:19](https://ref.ly/Job33:19); [DAN 8:27](https://ref.ly/Dan8:27); [MAT 8:14](https://ref.ly/Matt8:14) parallel [MRK 1:30](https://ref.ly/Mark1:30); [REV 2:22](https://ref.ly/Rev2:22)

The bed as a place in which to be alone
---------------------------------------

**[PSA 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ps4:4)**; **[MIC 2:1](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1)** *See also* [1KI 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:4); [2KI 6:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:12), [2KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:2); [PSA 6:6](https://ref.ly/Ps6:6), [PSA 63:6](https://ref.ly/Ps63:6); [ECC 10:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:20)

The bed representing sexual union
---------------------------------

**[DEU 22:30](https://ref.ly/Deut22:30)**; **[HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4)** *See also* [GEN 39:7](https://ref.ly/Gen39:7); [DEU 27:20](https://ref.ly/Deut27:20); [2SA 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:11); [1CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1); [EZK 22:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:10), [EZK 23:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:17)

Unusual places used as beds
---------------------------

[GEN 28:11](https://ref.ly/Gen28:11); ; [1KI 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5); ; [JON 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:5); ; [MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38)

Bed associated with a description of apostasy
---------------------------------------------

**[ISA 57:7](https://ref.ly/Isa57:7)**

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 3:11; Amos 6:4; Acts 9:34; Matthew 9:6; Mark 2:11-12; Luke 5:18-19; Mark 2:4; Matthew 9:2; Luke 5:24; Acts 5:15; 2 Kings 4:10; 1 Samuel 3:2; 2 Samuel 4:5; 2 Samuel 4:7; 2 Samuel 11:13; Esther 7:8; Job 33:15; Psalms 139:8; Proverbs 7:16-17; Proverbs 26:14; Isaiah 28:20; Daniel 4:5; Daniel 7:1; Luke 11:7; Psalms 41:3; Acts 28:8; Genesis 48:2; Exodus 21:18; 2 Samuel 13:5; 1 Kings 17:19; 2 Kings 1:4; 2 Kings 4:21; Job 7:13-14; Job 33:19; Daniel 8:27; Matthew 8:14; Mark 1:30; Revelation 2:22; Psalms 4:4; Micah 2:1; 1 Kings 21:4; 2 Kings 6:12; 2 Kings 11:2; Psalms 6:6; Psalms 63:6; Ecclesiastes 10:20; Deuteronomy 22:30; Hebrews 13:4; Genesis 39:7; Deuteronomy 27:20; 2 Samuel 13:11; 1 Chronicles 5:1; Ezekiel 22:10; Ezekiel 23:17; Genesis 28:11; 1 Kings 19:5; Jonah 1:5; Mark 4:38; Isaiah 57:7

## 5230: beggars (id: 5230)

People in great need who appeal for help, both from others and from God.

Appeals for material help
-------------------------

**[2KI 6:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:26)** *During a time of famine*; **[LUK 16:3](https://ref.ly/Luke16:3)** *See also* [2KI 8:3–8:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:3-2Kgs8:5); [JOB 19:16](https://ref.ly/Job19:16), [JOB 24:4–24:5](https://ref.ly/Job24:4-Job24:5), [JOB 29:11–29:12](https://ref.ly/Job29:11-Job29:12); [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25) *never having to beg seen as God's blessing*; [PSA 109:10](https://ref.ly/Ps109:10) *begging seen as God's judgment*; [LAM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Lam4:4)

Appeals for mercy from others
-----------------------------

**[EST 8:3](https://ref.ly/Esth8:3)** *See also* [1SA 15:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:25); [2SA 14:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:4); [2KI 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:13); [EST 4:8](https://ref.ly/Esth4:8), [EST 7:7](https://ref.ly/Esth7:7); [JOB 30:28](https://ref.ly/Job30:28); [MAT 18:26–18:32](https://ref.ly/Matt18:26-Matt18:32)

Examples of beggars
-------------------

[MRK 10:46](https://ref.ly/Mark10:46) parallel [LUK 18:35](https://ref.ly/Luke18:35) *blind Bartimaeus*; ; [LUK 16:20–16:21](https://ref.ly/Luke16:20-Luke16:21) *Lazarus*; ; [JHN 9:8](https://ref.ly/John9:8) *the man born blind*; ; [ACT 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2-Acts3:3) *the crippled man at the temple gate*

Care for the destitute commended
--------------------------------

**[ISA 58:6–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:7)**; **[MAT 26:11](https://ref.ly/Matt26:11)** parallels [MRK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Mark14:7), [JHN 12:8](https://ref.ly/John12:8) *Jesus Christ implies that we should care for them.* *See also* [DEU 15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:11); [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17) *kindness to the poor rewarded*; [PRO 21:13](https://ref.ly/Prov21:13), [PRO 22:9](https://ref.ly/Prov22:9), [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27), [PRO 31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:9), [PRO 31:20](https://ref.ly/Prov31:20) *the behaviour of the wife of noble character*; [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10); [MAT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:42) parallel [LUK 6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:30); [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21) parallels [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21), [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22) *Jesus Christ and the rich young man*; [LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Luke12:33), [LUK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Luke14:13)

Pleas for God's help
--------------------

### From the Israelites

**[EXO 2:23](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23)** *In the OT there are many examples of the Israelites begging God for his help in times of dire need.* *See also* [JOS 24:7](https://ref.ly/Josh24:7); [JDG 4:3](https://ref.ly/Judg4:3), [JDG 6:6](https://ref.ly/Judg6:6); [2CH 14:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:11); [AMO 7:5](https://ref.ly/Amos7:5)

### From individuals

**[PSA 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ps18:6)** *See also* [2SA 24:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:10) parallel [1CH 21:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:8); [PSA 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ps28:2); [JON 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:2); [HAB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hab1:2); [LUK 16:27](https://ref.ly/Luke16:27)

People begged Jesus Christ for help
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 14:35–14:36](https://ref.ly/Matt14:35-Matt14:36)** parallel [MRK 6:56](https://ref.ly/Mark6:56) *See also* [MAT 8:31](https://ref.ly/Matt8:31) parallels [MRK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Mark5:12), [LUK 8:32](https://ref.ly/Luke8:32); [MAT 15:22](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22) parallel [MRK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Mark7:26); [MRK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32), [MRK 10:47](https://ref.ly/Mark10:47); [LUK 5:12](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12) parallel [MRK 1:40](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40); [LUK 8:28](https://ref.ly/Luke8:28), [LUK 9:38–9:40](https://ref.ly/Luke9:38-Luke9:40); [JHN 4:47](https://ref.ly/John4:47)

## 5232: book (id: 5232)

Used most frequently to refer to a particular book of the Bible being written, another book of the Bible or a source no longer available. It is used occasionally of books in general or metaphorically of God's record of events.

References to books being written
---------------------------------

[JER 25:13](https://ref.ly/Jer25:13), [JER 30:2](https://ref.ly/Jer30:2); ; [NAM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Nah1:1); ; [JHN 20:30](https://ref.ly/John20:30); ; [REV 22:7](https://ref.ly/Rev22:7), [REV 22:9–22:10](https://ref.ly/Rev22:9-Rev22:10), [REV 22:18–22:19](https://ref.ly/Rev22:18-Rev22:19)

References to other books of Scripture
--------------------------------------

[2CH 35:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:12); ; [EZR 6:18](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:18); ; [NEH 13:1](https://ref.ly/Neh13:1); ; [MRK 12:26](https://ref.ly/Mark12:26); ; [LUK 3:4](https://ref.ly/Luke3:4), [LUK 20:42](https://ref.ly/Luke20:42); ; [ACT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Acts1:1) *Luke's Gospel*; ; [ACT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Acts1:20), [ACT 7:42](https://ref.ly/Acts7:42), [ACT 8:28](https://ref.ly/Acts8:28)

References to books no longer available
---------------------------------------

### The Book of the Wars of the LORD

[NUM 21:14](https://ref.ly/Num21:14) *This was probably an ancient collection of war songs in praise of God.*

### The Book of Jashar

**[JOS 10:13](https://ref.ly/Josh10:13)** *An early account of Israel's wars, possibly in poetic form, but never a part of canonical Scripture.* *See also* [2SA 1:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:18)

### The book of the annals of the kings of Israel and Judah

**[2CH 16:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:11)** *These sources were probably records of the reigns of the kings of Israel and Judah, compiled by the succession of Israel's prophets spanning the kingdom period (see e.g., [1Ch 29:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:29); [2Ch 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:15); [20:34](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:34); [26:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:22); [32:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:32)).* *See also* [1KI 11:41](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:41), [1KI 14:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:19), [1KI 14:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:29); [2KI 1:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:18), [2KI 8:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:23); [1CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:1), [1CH 27:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:24); [2CH 24:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:27), [2CH 27:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:7)

### The book of the annals

[NEH 12:23](https://ref.ly/Neh12:23) *This was probably the official temple chronicle, containing various lists and records (compare with [Ne 7:5](https://ref.ly/Neh7:5)).*; [EST 2:23](https://ref.ly/Esth2:23), [EST 6:1](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1), [EST 10:2](https://ref.ly/Esth10:2)

References to books in general
------------------------------

**[ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12)** *See also* [JHN 21:25](https://ref.ly/John21:25); [ACT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Acts19:19) *as sources of magical knowledge*

Books used metaphorically of God's infallible record of events
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[REV 20:11–20:12](https://ref.ly/Rev20:11-Rev20:12)** *See also* [DAN 7:10](https://ref.ly/Dan7:10), [DAN 10:21](https://ref.ly/Dan10:21), [DAN 12:1](https://ref.ly/Dan12:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 25:13; Jeremiah 30:2; Nahum 1:1; John 20:30; Revelation 22:7; Revelation 22:9-10; Revelation 22:18-19; 2 Chronicles 35:12; Ezra 6:18; Nehemiah 13:1; Mark 12:26; Luke 3:4; Luke 20:42; Acts 1:1; Acts 1:20; Acts 7:42; Acts 8:28; Numbers 21:14; Joshua 10:13; 2 Samuel 1:18; 2 Chronicles 16:11; 1 Chronicles 29:29; 2 Chronicles 12:15; 2 Chronicles 20:34; 2 Chronicles 26:22; 2 Chronicles 32:32; 1 Kings 11:41; 1 Kings 14:19; 1 Kings 14:29; 2 Kings 1:18; 2 Kings 8:23; 1 Chronicles 9:1; 1 Chronicles 27:24; 2 Chronicles 24:27; 2 Chronicles 27:7; Nehemiah 12:23; Nehemiah 7:5; Esther 2:23; Esther 6:1; Esther 10:2; Ecclesiastes 12:12; John 21:25; Acts 19:19; Revelation 20:11-12; Daniel 7:10; Daniel 10:21; Daniel 12:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1610: Scripture (ID: `1610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1630: Book of the Covenant (ID: `1630@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1640: Book of the Law (ID: `1640@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5175: reading (ID: `5175@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5393: literacy (ID: `5393@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5514: scribes (ID: `5514@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5515: scroll (ID: `5515@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5638: writing (ID: `5638@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9420: book of life (ID: `9420@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5233: borrowing (id: 5233)

Asking for and receiving money or goods for temporary use as a neighbourly kindness.

Believers should respond to requests to borrow
----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:42)** *See also* [DEU 15:7–15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:11); [LUK 6:35](https://ref.ly/Luke6:35), [LUK 11:5–11:8](https://ref.ly/Luke11:5-Luke11:8)

God's blessing on Israel made borrowing unnecessary
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 28:12](https://ref.ly/Deut28:12)** *See also* [DEU 15:6](https://ref.ly/Deut15:6)

The responsibilities of those who borrow
----------------------------------------

### Repayment

[PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21); ; [ROM 13:8](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8)

### Restitution

[EXO 22:14–22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:14-Exod22:15); ; [2KI 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:5-2Kgs6:7)

Borrowing can bring debt and servitude
--------------------------------------

**[PRO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Prov22:7)** *See also* [MAT 18:23–18:25](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:25)

Security for borrowing
----------------------

### Warnings against putting up security

**[PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15)** *See also* [PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5), [PRO 17:18](https://ref.ly/Prov17:18), [PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27)

### Security may be required

[PRO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Prov20:16), [PRO 27:13](https://ref.ly/Prov27:13)

### Security must be just

**[DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6)** *See also* [JOB 22:6](https://ref.ly/Job22:6); [NEH 5:3–5:13](https://ref.ly/Neh5:3-Neh5:13)

Jesus Christ was willing to borrow
----------------------------------

### Examples of borrowing

**[LUK 5:3](https://ref.ly/Luke5:3)** *See also* [MAT 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Matt21:1-Matt21:3) parallels [MRK 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/Mark11:1-Mark11:3), [LUK 19:30–19:31](https://ref.ly/Luke19:30-Luke19:31); [MAT 26:17–26:19](https://ref.ly/Matt26:17-Matt26:19) parallels [MRK 14:12–14:16](https://ref.ly/Mark14:12-Mark14:16), [LUK 22:10–22:12](https://ref.ly/Luke22:10-Luke22:12)

### Other examples

[EXO 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/Exod3:21-Exod3:22), [EXO 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/Exod11:2-Exod11:3), [EXO 12:35–12:36](https://ref.ly/Exod12:35-Exod12:36); ; [2KI 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:3); ; [NEH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Neh5:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:42; Deuteronomy 15:7-11; Luke 6:35; Luke 11:5-8; Deuteronomy 28:12; Deuteronomy 15:6; Psalms 37:21; Romans 13:8; Exodus 22:14-15; 2 Kings 6:5-7; Proverbs 22:7; Matthew 18:23-25; Proverbs 11:15; Proverbs 6:1-5; Proverbs 17:18; Proverbs 22:26-27; Proverbs 20:16; Proverbs 27:13; Deuteronomy 24:6; Job 22:6; Nehemiah 5:3-13; Luke 5:3; Matthew 21:1-3; Mark 11:1-3; Luke 19:30-31; Matthew 26:17-19; Mark 14:12-16; Luke 22:10-12; Exodus 3:21-22; Exodus 11:2-3; Exodus 12:35-36; 2 Kings 4:3; Nehemiah 5:3

## 5235: boundary (id: 5235)

The agreed limits to the lands of an invidivudal or people. Designated by God and protected by law, both national and local boundaries could be subject to dispute.

The importance of boundary marks
--------------------------------

**[DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14)**; **[PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10)** *See also* [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17); [PRO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Prov22:28)

Examples of significant boundaries
----------------------------------

### Boundaries of the promised land

## 5236: bow and arrow (id: 5236)

A weapon used for hunting and in warfare. In the Scriptures the tongue is likened to a bow and unfaithful Israel to a faulty bow. The bow is also symbolic of military power.

The bow and arrow used for hunting
----------------------------------

**[GEN 27:3](https://ref.ly/Gen27:3)** *See also* [ISA 7:24](https://ref.ly/Isa7:24)

The bow and arrow used in warfare
---------------------------------

**[GEN 48:22](https://ref.ly/Gen48:22)**; **[1KI 22:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34)** parallel [2CH 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33) *See also* [JOS 24:12](https://ref.ly/Josh24:12); [1SA 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:4), [1SA 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:4); [2SA 1:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:22), [2SA 22:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:35) parallel [PSA 18:34](https://ref.ly/Ps18:34); [2KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:24); [2CH 14:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:8) *Asa's army*; [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14); [NEH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Neh4:13), [NEH 4:16](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16); [ISA 5:28](https://ref.ly/Isa5:28), [ISA 13:18](https://ref.ly/Isa13:18); [JER 6:23](https://ref.ly/Jer6:23), [JER 50:14](https://ref.ly/Jer50:14), [JER 50:29](https://ref.ly/Jer50:29)

The tongue likened to a bow
---------------------------

**[JER 9:3](https://ref.ly/Jer9:3)** *See also* [PSA 64:2–64:4](https://ref.ly/Ps64:2-Ps64:4)

Unfaithful Israel likened to a faulty bow
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 78:57](https://ref.ly/Ps78:57)** *See also* [HOS 7:16](https://ref.ly/Hos7:16)

The bow as a symbol of power and strength
-----------------------------------------

### Of military power

**[PSA 44:6–44:7](https://ref.ly/Ps44:6-Ps44:7), [PSA 60:4](https://ref.ly/Ps60:4)** *See also* [GEN 49:24](https://ref.ly/Gen49:24); [PSA 46:9](https://ref.ly/Ps46:9); [HOS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hos1:5), [HOS 1:7](https://ref.ly/Hos1:7), [HOS 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hos2:18); [ZEC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Zech9:10)

### Of God's power in judgment

**[PSA 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Ps7:11-Ps7:12)** *See also* [PSA 21:11–21:12](https://ref.ly/Ps21:11-Ps21:12); [LAM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Lam2:4), [LAM 3:12](https://ref.ly/Lam3:12); [HAB 3:9](https://ref.ly/Hab3:9); [ZEC 9:13](https://ref.ly/Zech9:13); [REV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Rev6:2)

### Of personal strength and vigour

**[JOB 29:20](https://ref.ly/Job29:20)** *See also* [JOB 30:11](https://ref.ly/Job30:11)

## 5237: breastplate (id: 5237)

A coat of armour comprising one or more pieces of bronze, iron or chain mail covering the chest and back to protect the heart and other vital organs. Used figuratively it refers to righteousness, faith and love.

Breastplates were coats or tunics of armour
-------------------------------------------

### They were part of military dress

**[2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14)** *See also* [1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5), [1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38); [2SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:8)

### They were worn by soldiers

**[2SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:8)** *See also* [NEH 4:16](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16); [JER 46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:4), [JER 51:3](https://ref.ly/Jer51:3)

### Bronze breastplates

**[1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5)**

### Iron breastplates

**[REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Rev9:9)**

Those wearing breastplates were not impregnable
-----------------------------------------------

**[1KI 22:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:34)** parallel [2CH 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:33) *See also* [1SA 31:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:3); [1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11)

The breastplate was often referred to figuratively
--------------------------------------------------

### As a picture of righteousness

**[ISA 59:17](https://ref.ly/Isa59:17)**; **[EPH 6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:14)**

### As a picture of faith and love

**[1TH 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:8)**

### As a picture of invincibility

*See also* [REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Rev9:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 26:14; 1 Samuel 17:5; 1 Samuel 17:38; 2 Samuel 20:8; Nehemiah 4:16; Jeremiah 46:4; Jeremiah 51:3; Revelation 9:9; 1 Kings 22:34; 2 Chronicles 18:33; 1 Samuel 31:3; 1 Kings 20:11; Isaiah 59:17; Ephesians 6:14; 1 Thessalonians 5:8
* **Associated Articles:** 4312: bronze (ID: `4312@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4336: iron (ID: `4336@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5209: armour (ID: `5209@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5480: protection (ID: `5480@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5544: soldiers (ID: `5544@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7320: breastpiece (ID: `7320@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8154: righteousness (ID: `8154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8292: love (ID: `8292@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8486: spiritual warfare, armour (ID: `8486@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5238: bribery (id: 5238)

The practice of offering an incentive, usually money, in order to dishonestly or illegally procure a decision or action in favour of the giver.

Bribery is sin
--------------

### It is forbidden by God on the grounds of truth and justice

**[EXO 23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:8)**; **[2CH 19:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:7)** *See also* [DEU 10:17](https://ref.ly/Deut10:17), [DEU 16:18–16:20](https://ref.ly/Deut16:18-Deut16:20); [JOB 36:18](https://ref.ly/Job36:18)

### The prevalence of the sin

**[PRO 17:8](https://ref.ly/Prov17:8)** *See also* [PRO 6:32–6:35](https://ref.ly/Prov6:32-Prov6:35), [PRO 21:14](https://ref.ly/Prov21:14)

### The seriousness of the sin

**[JOB 15:34](https://ref.ly/Job15:34)** *See also* [DEU 27:25](https://ref.ly/Deut27:25); [ISA 1:23–1:24](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23-Isa1:24), [ISA 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Isa5:22-Isa5:23), [ISA 33:15](https://ref.ly/Isa33:15); [AMO 5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:12); [MIC 3:9–3:12](https://ref.ly/Mic3:9-Mic3:12)

The results of bribery
----------------------

### Personal corruption

**[ECC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:7)** *See also* [PSA 26:9–26:10](https://ref.ly/Ps26:9-Ps26:10); [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)

### The perversion of justice

**[PRO 17:23](https://ref.ly/Prov17:23)** *See also* [EXO 23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:8); [DEU 16:19](https://ref.ly/Deut16:19)

### The decay of society

**[PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4)** *See also* [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12) *Corruption, violence and decay render society subject to God's judgment.*

Examples of bribery
-------------------

Balak and Balaam: [NUM 22:16](https://ref.ly/Num22:16); ; [2PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:15); [JDG 16:5](https://ref.ly/Judg16:5) *Delilah*; [1SA 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:1-1Sam8:3), [1SA 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:2-1Sam12:3); [1KI 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:18-1Kgs15:19) *King Asa*; ; [2KI 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7-2Kgs16:9) *King Ahaz*; ; [EST 3:9](https://ref.ly/Esth3:9) *Haman*; ; [MAT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:16) parallels [MRK 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10-Mark14:11), [LUK 22:4–22:6](https://ref.ly/Luke22:4-Luke22:6) *Judas*; ; [MAT 28:11–28:15](https://ref.ly/Matt28:11-Matt28:15) *the soldiers at the tomb*; ; [ACT 24:26](https://ref.ly/Acts24:26) *Governor Felix*

## 5240: building (id: 5240)

## 5241: burial (id: 5241)

The burying of a dead body, usually in a family tomb. The lack of a proper burial was regarded as a great misfortune and disgrace. Burial was usually accompanied by mourning.

Burial in a family tomb
-----------------------

**[GEN 49:29–49:31](https://ref.ly/Gen49:29-Gen49:31)** *See also* [GEN 23:19–23:20](https://ref.ly/Gen23:19-Gen23:20), [GEN 25:9–25:10](https://ref.ly/Gen25:9-Gen25:10), [GEN 50:13](https://ref.ly/Gen50:13); [JDG 8:32](https://ref.ly/Judg8:32), [JDG 16:31](https://ref.ly/Judg16:31); [1SA 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:1) *“at his home” probably refers to the family tomb, but could mean more literally under the floor of the house or yard*; [2SA 2:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:32), [2SA 17:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:23)

Other places of burial
----------------------

[GEN 35:8](https://ref.ly/Gen35:8), [GEN 35:19–35:20](https://ref.ly/Gen35:19-Gen35:20)

Burial of criminals hung on a tree
----------------------------------

**[DEU 21:22–21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22-Deut21:23)** *See also* [JHN 19:31](https://ref.ly/John19:31) *The request of the Jews was probably not only to avoid desecrating a special Sabbath, but also to identify Jesus Christ as one cursed by God.*

Burial of an executed criminal or enemy under a heap of stones
--------------------------------------------------------------

[JOS 7:26](https://ref.ly/Josh7:26), [JOS 8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:29), [JOS 10:27](https://ref.ly/Josh10:27); ; [2SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:17)

Lack of a proper burial
-----------------------

## 5242: buying and selling (id: 5242)

Buying and selling are an integral part of the everyday life of the world. Scripture makes it clear that God expects moral dealings by his people in this area of life as in every other.

Some biblical transactions
--------------------------

### The first recorded transaction

**[GEN 23:3–23:4](https://ref.ly/Gen23:3-Gen23:4)** *Many other transactions had obviously taken place before this, from the earliest times.*

### Transactions in land

*See also* [GEN 47:20–47:22](https://ref.ly/Gen47:20-Gen47:22); [RUT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:3); [PRO 31:16](https://ref.ly/Prov31:16); [JER 32:9–32:10](https://ref.ly/Jer32:9-Jer32:10); [MAT 27:7](https://ref.ly/Matt27:7); [LUK 14:18](https://ref.ly/Luke14:18); [ACT 4:34](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34)

### Buying animals

[LUK 14:19](https://ref.ly/Luke14:19)

### Buying food and drink

[GEN 41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:57); ; [DEU 2:28](https://ref.ly/Deut2:28); ; [JHN 6:5](https://ref.ly/John6:5), [JHN 13:29](https://ref.ly/John13:29); ; [REV 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rev6:6)

### Transactions in other goods

[REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

### Buying of slaves

[GEN 17:12](https://ref.ly/Gen17:12); ; [EXO 21:2](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2); ; [LEV 25:44–25:45](https://ref.ly/Lev25:44-Lev25:45); ; [HOS 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1-Hos3:2)

### The labour market

[LEV 25:39–25:40](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:40); ; [MAT 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:2)

God's standards in buying and selling
-------------------------------------

### Fairness

[GEN 23:5–23:13](https://ref.ly/Gen23:5-Gen23:13); ; [2SA 24:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:24)

### Honesty

**[LEV 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35-Lev19:36)** *See also* [GEN 23:14–23:16](https://ref.ly/Gen23:14-Gen23:16); [DEU 25:13–25:16](https://ref.ly/Deut25:13-Deut25:16); [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1), [PRO 16:11](https://ref.ly/Prov16:11), [PRO 20:10](https://ref.ly/Prov20:10), [PRO 20:23](https://ref.ly/Prov20:23)

Sins to avoid in buying and selling
-----------------------------------

### Dishonesty

[PRO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Prov20:14); ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5); ; [MIC 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Mic6:10-Mic6:11)

### Greed

[PRO 11:26](https://ref.ly/Prov11:26)

### Exploitation

**[LEV 25:14](https://ref.ly/Lev25:14)** *See also* [LAM 5:4](https://ref.ly/Lam5:4); [AMO 8:6](https://ref.ly/Amos8:6)

### Extravagance

[LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13)

### Desecration of the Sabbath

[NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31), [NEH 13:15–13:22](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15-Neh13:22); ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

### Desecration of the temple

**[MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13)** parallels [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46) *The merchants were operating within the outer court of the temple, itself consecrated as a place of prayer and worship.* *See also* [JHN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:16)

### Worldliness

[EZK 7:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:12); ; [1CO 7:29–7:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:29-1Cor7:31); ; [JAS 4:13](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13)

Oppression through the control of buying and selling
----------------------------------------------------

**[REV 13:16–13:17](https://ref.ly/Rev13:16-Rev13:17)**

Spiritual transactions
----------------------

### Sinners sold to sin

*See also* [1KI 21:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:20); [ROM 7:14](https://ref.ly/Rom7:14)

### Israel sold and redeemed

### Gifts money cannot buy

## 5244: camp (id: 5244)

A dwelling\-place in the desert, often linked with the idea of a wandering people.

-----------------------------------------------

## 5245: captain (id: 5245)

A senior military officer, usually in charge of a ruler's personal guards or bodyguards, the temple guard or a group of soldiers. The word is also used to refer to the commander of a ship's crew.

Captains in charge of a ruler's personal guards or bodyguards
-------------------------------------------------------------

### Potiphar, captain of Pharaoh's guard

[GEN 37:36](https://ref.ly/Gen37:36), [GEN 39:1](https://ref.ly/Gen39:1), [GEN 40:3–40:4](https://ref.ly/Gen40:3-Gen40:4), [GEN 41:10](https://ref.ly/Gen41:10), [GEN 41:12](https://ref.ly/Gen41:12)

### David, captain of Saul's bodyguard

**[1SA 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:14)**

### The captain of the guard arrests Jeremiah

*See also* [JER 37:13](https://ref.ly/Jer37:13)

------------------------------

### King Ahaziah sends a captain with fifty men to Elijah

[2KI 1:9–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:9-2Kgs1:14)

[JDG 5:14](https://ref.ly/Judg5:14)

### Solomon's captains were Israelites

[1KI 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:22) parallel [2CH 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:9)

### God will remove captains in judgment

## 5246: captivity (id: 5246)

The state of individuals, communities or nations held in bondage by a foreign power. Such captivity may be punishment from God who, nevertheless, hears the cry of his captive people. God will punish those who, in their arrogance, take others captive. The term is sometimes used metaphorically of the bondage of sin. Jesus Christ alone can free people from such a state.

Individuals and communities taken into captivity
------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 30:1–30:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:1-1Sam30:3)** *See also* [GEN 14:11–14:14](https://ref.ly/Gen14:11-Gen14:14)

Nations taken into captivity
----------------------------

**[JER 48:46](https://ref.ly/Jer48:46)** *See also* [EXO 6:9](https://ref.ly/Exod6:9); [NUM 31:9–31:12](https://ref.ly/Num31:9-Num31:12); [ISA 20:3–20:4](https://ref.ly/Isa20:3-Isa20:4)

-----------------------

### As a punishment from the LORD

**[PSA 106:39–106:43](https://ref.ly/Ps106:39-Ps106:43)** *See also* [PSA 78:58–78:61](https://ref.ly/Ps78:58-Ps78:61)

### The LORD hears the cry of his people

**[EXO 2:23](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23)** *See also* [EXO 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Exod6:5-Exod6:6); [1KI 8:46–8:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:46-1Kgs8:51); [PSA 69:33](https://ref.ly/Ps69:33), [PSA 106:44–106:46](https://ref.ly/Ps106:44-Ps106:46)

Carpenters among the exiles in Babylon
--------------------------------------

**[2KI 24:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:16)** *Carpenters are literally “craftsmen in wood”.* *See also* [JER 24:1](https://ref.ly/Jer24:1), [JER 29:2](https://ref.ly/Jer29:2)

Joseph as a carpenter
---------------------

## 5248: cart (id: 5248)

General description of carts
----------------------------

*See also* [NUM 7:3](https://ref.ly/Num7:3); [1SA 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:7), [1SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:14) *made of wood*; [ISA 5:18](https://ref.ly/Isa5:18) *pulled by ropes*; [ISA 28:27–28:28](https://ref.ly/Isa28:27-Isa28:28) *equipped with heavy wheels*

Carts as a means of transport
-----------------------------

### Carts used to transport people

**[GEN 46:5](https://ref.ly/Gen46:5)** *See also* [GEN 45:19–45:21](https://ref.ly/Gen45:19-Gen45:21), [GEN 45:27](https://ref.ly/Gen45:27); [ISA 66:20](https://ref.ly/Isa66:20)

### Carts used as military transport

**[EZK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:24)** *See also* [EZK 26:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:10)

### Carts used to transport grain

**[AMO 2:13](https://ref.ly/Amos2:13)** *See also* [PSA 65:11](https://ref.ly/Ps65:11)

### Carts used to transport the tabernacle

[NUM 7:6–7:8](https://ref.ly/Num7:6-Num7:8) *The heavier items of the tabernacle and its furniture were carried on covered carts drawn by oxen.*

### A new cart to transport the ark of the LORD

**[1SA 6:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:8)** *The Philistines send the ark back to Israel.* *See also* [1SA 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:10-1Sam6:11); [2SA 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:2-2Sam6:3) parallel [1CH 13:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:7) *The ark is started on its journey to Jerusalem.*

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 7:3; 1 Samuel 6:7; 1 Samuel 6:14; Isaiah 5:18; Isaiah 28:27-28; Genesis 46:5; Genesis 45:19-21; Genesis 45:27; Isaiah 66:20; Ezekiel 23:24; Ezekiel 26:10; Amos 2:13; Psalms 65:11; Numbers 7:6-8; 1 Samuel 6:8; 1 Samuel 6:10-11; 2 Samuel 6:2-3; 1 Chronicles 13:7
* **Associated Articles:** 4406: agriculture (ID: `4406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5252: chariots (ID: `5252@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5621: wheel (ID: `5621@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7306: ark of the covenant (ID: `7306@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7459: tabernacle, in OT (ID: `7459@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5249: census (id: 5249)

The counting of individuals, usually for the purpose of war or taxation.

Motivations for a census
------------------------

### To count the number of fighting men available

**[NUM 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Num1:1-Num1:4), [NUM 26:1–26:4](https://ref.ly/Num26:1-Num26:4)** *See also* [NUM 1:5–1:49](https://ref.ly/Num1:5-Num1:49), [NUM 26:5–26:65](https://ref.ly/Num26:5-Num26:65)

### For ascertaining those qualified to be included in the Jewish community

[EZR 2:1–2:65](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:1-Ezra2:65) parallel [NEH 7:6–7:67](https://ref.ly/Neh7:6-Neh7:67)

### For purposes of taxation

**[LUK 2:2](https://ref.ly/Luke2:2)**; **[ACT 5:37](https://ref.ly/Acts5:37)** *See also* [EXO 30:12](https://ref.ly/Exod30:12)

God's anger at David's unauthorised census
------------------------------------------

[2SA 24:1–24:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:1-2Sam24:9) parallel [1CH 21:1–21:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:1-1Chr21:6) *David's sin in taking this census was probably that he did so from a motive of self\-sufficiency rather than trusting in God.*

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 1:1-4; Numbers 26:1-4; Numbers 1:5-49; Numbers 26:5-65; Ezra 2:1-65; Nehemiah 7:6-67; Luke 2:2; Acts 5:37; Exodus 30:12; 2 Samuel 24:1-9; 1 Chronicles 21:1-6
* **Associated Articles:** 5577: taxation (ID: `5577@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7230: genealogies (ID: `7230@DictionaryBibleThemes`)
