## 5251: chains (id: 5251)

Used to hold people in captivity, both physically and spiritually, but also an emblem of honour and public office.

Chains as a means of physical restraint
---------------------------------------

**[MRK 5:4](https://ref.ly/Mark5:4)** parallel [LUK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Luke8:29)**[ACT 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/Acts12:6-Acts12:7)** *See also* [2KI 23:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:33); [JER 40:1–40:4](https://ref.ly/Jer40:1-Jer40:4); [ACT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Acts16:26), [ACT 21:33](https://ref.ly/Acts21:33), [ACT 22:29](https://ref.ly/Acts22:29), [ACT 26:29](https://ref.ly/Acts26:29), [ACT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Acts28:20); [EPH 6:20](https://ref.ly/Eph6:20); [PHP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7), [PHP 1:13–1:17](https://ref.ly/Phil1:13-Phil1:17) *Paul in chains for Christ*; [COL 4:3](https://ref.ly/Col4:3), [COL 4:18](https://ref.ly/Col4:18); [2TI 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:16), [2TI 2:9](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:9); [PHM 1:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:10), [PHM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:13); [HEB 11:36](https://ref.ly/Heb11:36)

Chains as a metaphor of captivity
---------------------------------

### Political bondage

**[ISA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Isa45:14)** *See also* [JOB 36:8–36:9](https://ref.ly/Job36:8-Job36:9); [PSA 2:3](https://ref.ly/Ps2:3), [PSA 107:10–107:14](https://ref.ly/Ps107:10-Ps107:14); [ISA 28:22](https://ref.ly/Isa28:22), [ISA 52:2](https://ref.ly/Isa52:2); [EZK 7:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:23); [NAM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Nah3:10)

### Spiritual bondage to sin

**[PSA 116:16](https://ref.ly/Ps116:16)** *See also* [ISA 58:6–58:10](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:10); [LAM 3:7](https://ref.ly/Lam3:7)

### God's binding of sinful angels

**[JUD 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jude1:6)** *See also* [REV 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Rev20:1-Rev20:2)

### The domination of others

**[ECC 7:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:26)**

Chains as a form of ornament
----------------------------

### As emblems of honour or rank

**[DAN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Dan5:29)** *See also* [GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42); [DAN 5:7](https://ref.ly/Dan5:7), [DAN 5:16](https://ref.ly/Dan5:16)

### As a form of jewellery

**[PRO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:9)** *See also* [EXO 28:22–28:25](https://ref.ly/Exod28:22-Exod28:25) *the high priest's breastplate*; [EXO 39:15–39:18](https://ref.ly/Exod39:15-Exod39:18); [JDG 8:26](https://ref.ly/Judg8:26); [ISA 3:20](https://ref.ly/Isa3:20)

### As adornment of idols

**[ISA 40:19](https://ref.ly/Isa40:19)**

### As adornment of places of worship

**[2CH 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:16)** *See also* [EXO 28:13–28:14](https://ref.ly/Exod28:13-Exod28:14); [1KI 6:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:21), [1KI 7:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:17); [2CH 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:5)

## 5252: chariots (id: 5252)

Horse\-drawn vehicles used for carrying important people when travelling or on ceremonial occasions, and in war for carrying warriors. The chariot is used as a symbol of military power, the power of God and as a picture of God's angelic army.

Chariots used for travelling
----------------------------

**[GEN 46:29](https://ref.ly/Gen46:29)** *See also* [GEN 50:9](https://ref.ly/Gen50:9); [JDG 4:15](https://ref.ly/Judg4:15); [2SA 15:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1); [1KI 12:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:18), [1KI 18:44](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:44), [1KI 20:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:33); [2KI 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:21), [2KI 9:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:16), [2KI 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:21), [2KI 10:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:16); [ACT 8:28](https://ref.ly/Acts8:28)

Chariots used on ceremonial occasions
-------------------------------------

**[GEN 41:43](https://ref.ly/Gen41:43)** *See also* [GEN 50:9](https://ref.ly/Gen50:9); [JER 17:25](https://ref.ly/Jer17:25)

Chariots used for carrying warriors
-----------------------------------

**[EXO 14:6–14:7](https://ref.ly/Exod14:6-Exod14:7)** *See also* [DEU 11:4](https://ref.ly/Deut11:4), [DEU 20:1](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1); [JOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Josh11:4), [JOS 17:16](https://ref.ly/Josh17:16); [JDG 1:19](https://ref.ly/Judg1:19), [JDG 4:13](https://ref.ly/Judg4:13); [1SA 8:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11), [1SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:5) *the Philistines prepare for battle*; [2SA 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:6), [2SA 8:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:4) parallel [1CH 18:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:4); [1KI 10:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:26) parallel [2CH 1:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:14); [1KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:1); [2KI 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:14), [2KI 8:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:21), [2KI 10:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:2); [1CH 19:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:6) *the Ammonites hire chariots and charioteers*; [2CH 12:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:3), [2CH 14:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:9); [EZK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:24), [EZK 26:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:7), [EZK 26:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:10); [NAM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Nah2:4)

The chariot as a symbol of military might
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 20:7](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7)** *See also* [2KI 19:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:23) parallel [ISA 37:24](https://ref.ly/Isa37:24); [ISA 2:7](https://ref.ly/Isa2:7), [ISA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Isa22:7), [ISA 22:18](https://ref.ly/Isa22:18), [ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1), [ISA 36:9](https://ref.ly/Isa36:9); [JER 50:37](https://ref.ly/Jer50:37), [JER 51:21](https://ref.ly/Jer51:21); [MIC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Mic5:10); [NAM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Nah2:13); [HAG 2:22](https://ref.ly/Hag2:22)

The chariot as a symbol of God's mighty power
---------------------------------------------

**[PSA 104:3](https://ref.ly/Ps104:3)** *See also* [2KI 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:11); [PSA 68:4](https://ref.ly/Ps68:4), [PSA 68:33](https://ref.ly/Ps68:33); [ISA 19:1](https://ref.ly/Isa19:1); [EZK 1:15–1:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:15-Ezek1:21); [ZEC 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Zech6:1-Zech6:8)

God's mighty angelic army depicted by chariots
----------------------------------------------

**[2KI 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:17)** *See also* [PSA 68:17](https://ref.ly/Ps68:17); [ISA 66:15](https://ref.ly/Isa66:15); [JER 4:13](https://ref.ly/Jer4:13); [HAB 3:8](https://ref.ly/Hab3:8)

## 5253: cheating (id: 5253)

Cheating is condemned
---------------------

**[LEV 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:5)** *See also* [1CO 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:7-1Cor6:8); [1TH 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:6)

Examples of cheating
--------------------

### Not paying fair wages

[GEN 31:6–31:9](https://ref.ly/Gen31:6-Gen31:9); ; [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Using dishonest weights or scales

[DEU 25:13–25:16](https://ref.ly/Deut25:13-Deut25:16); ; [HOS 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7-Hos12:8); ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

[LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

### Offering blemished animals for sacrifice

[MAL 1:12–1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:12-Mal1:14)

### Moving boundary stones

[DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14), [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17)

-------------------------------------------------

## 5254: citadel (id: 5254)

An especially secure city, or part of a city, where soldiers were often based.

--------------------

**[PSA 48:12–48:13](https://ref.ly/Ps48:12-Ps48:13)** *See also* [2SA 12:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:26); [1KI 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:18); [2KI 15:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:25); [NEH 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Neh1:1-Neh1:2), [NEH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Neh2:8), [NEH 7:2](https://ref.ly/Neh7:2); [EST 1:2](https://ref.ly/Esth1:2), [EST 1:5](https://ref.ly/Esth1:5), [EST 3:15](https://ref.ly/Esth3:15), [EST 9:6](https://ref.ly/Esth9:6); [PSA 48:1–48:3](https://ref.ly/Ps48:1-Ps48:3), [PSA 122:7](https://ref.ly/Ps122:7); [ISA 34:13](https://ref.ly/Isa34:13); [DAN 8:2](https://ref.ly/Dan8:2)

## 5255: citizenship (id: 5255)

The privilege of belonging to a city or country. Scripture portrays believers as living in two realms, being members of both human society and of the heavenly city. In both realms there are duties as well as privileges.

Dual citizenship
----------------

**[MAT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:21)** parallels [MRK 12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:17), [LUK 20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:25) *See also* [EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26)

Earthly citizenship
-------------------

### Privileges and duties of citizens

**[ROM 13:1–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:7)** *See also* [ECC 8:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:2); [MAT 17:27](https://ref.ly/Matt17:27); [TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1); [1PE 2:13–2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:17)

### Privileges and duties of Roman citizenship

[ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37), [ACT 22:25–22:29](https://ref.ly/Acts22:25-Acts22:29), [ACT 23:27](https://ref.ly/Acts23:27), [ACT 25:10](https://ref.ly/Acts25:10)

Heavenly citizenship and the Christian hope
-------------------------------------------

**[PHP 3:20](https://ref.ly/Phil3:20)**; **[HEB 13:14](https://ref.ly/Heb13:14)** *See also* [LUK 22:29–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:29-Luke22:30); [JHN 14:2](https://ref.ly/John14:2); [1PE 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:4); [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2), [REV 21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:27), [REV 22:3–22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3-Rev22:5)

### Gentiles are included in the citizenship of heaven

**[EPH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19)** *See also* [EPH 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Eph2:12-Eph2:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 22:21; Mark 12:17; Luke 20:25; Ezra 7:26; Romans 13:1-7; Ecclesiastes 8:2; Matthew 17:27; Titus 3:1; 1 Peter 2:13-17; Acts 16:37; Acts 22:25-29; Acts 23:27; Acts 25:10; Philippians 3:20; Hebrews 13:14; Luke 22:29-30; John 14:2; 1 Peter 1:4; Revelation 21:2; Revelation 21:27; Revelation 22:3-5; Ephesians 2:19; Ephesians 2:12-13

## 5256: city (id: 5256)

A large walled centre of population providing security for its inhabitants. The OT often uses the term as a symbol of the security of heaven.

Types of city
-------------

### Ancient cities

[GEN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Gen4:17), [GEN 10:10–10:12](https://ref.ly/Gen10:10-Gen10:12)

### Fortified cities

[NUM 32:34–32:36](https://ref.ly/Num32:34-Num32:36); ; [DEU 9:1](https://ref.ly/Deut9:1); ; [JOS 10:20](https://ref.ly/Josh10:20), [JOS 14:12](https://ref.ly/Josh14:12)

### Royal cities

[JOS 10:2](https://ref.ly/Josh10:2); ; [1SA 27:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:5); ; [1CH 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:7)

### Cities of refuge

[DEU 4:41–4:43](https://ref.ly/Deut4:41-Deut4:43), [DEU 19:1–19:13](https://ref.ly/Deut19:1-Deut19:13)

Cities were frequently large
----------------------------

**[DEU 1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:28)**; **[JON 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:2-Jonah3:3)** *See also* [DEU 6:10](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10), [DEU 9:1](https://ref.ly/Deut9:1); [NEH 7:4](https://ref.ly/Neh7:4); [JON 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:11)

Cities were surrounded by walls
-------------------------------

**[DEU 3:5](https://ref.ly/Deut3:5)**; **[EZR 4:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:12)**; **[2CO 11:32–11:33](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:32-2Cor11:33)** *See also* [DEU 28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:52); [JOS 2:15](https://ref.ly/Josh2:15); [1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1), [1KI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:13); [2CH 33:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:14)

Cities were places of security
------------------------------

**[NUM 32:17](https://ref.ly/Num32:17)** *See also* [JOS 10:20](https://ref.ly/Josh10:20); [2CH 11:5–11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:5-2Chr11:12), [2CH 14:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:6), [2CH 17:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:2); [JER 4:5](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5)

Cities could fall
-----------------

**[2SA 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:7)**; **[NEH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3)**; **[JER 51:58](https://ref.ly/Jer51:58)** *See also* [DEU 20:12](https://ref.ly/Deut20:12), [DEU 20:19–20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:19-Deut20:20); [1KI 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:17-1Kgs16:18); [2KI 25:1–25:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1-2Kgs25:4); [ISA 13:19](https://ref.ly/Isa13:19); [JER 32:24–32:25](https://ref.ly/Jer32:24-Jer32:25); [AMO 5:9](https://ref.ly/Amos5:9)

The holy city
-------------

**[PSA 48:1–48:2](https://ref.ly/Ps48:1-Ps48:2)** *See also* [1KI 11:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:36), [1KI 14:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:21); [1CH 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:7); [NEH 11:1](https://ref.ly/Neh11:1); [ISA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Isa52:1); [JER 25:29](https://ref.ly/Jer25:29); [DAN 9:16](https://ref.ly/Dan9:16); [MAT 5:35](https://ref.ly/Matt5:35)

City law
--------

[LEV 25:29–25:30](https://ref.ly/Lev25:29-Lev25:30)

The city as a symbol
--------------------

### Of strength

**[JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18)**

### Of heaven

**[HEB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:10)** *See also* [HEB 11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:16), [HEB 12:22](https://ref.ly/Heb12:22), [HEB 13:14](https://ref.ly/Heb13:14); [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2), [REV 21:20–21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:20-Rev21:27), [REV 22:1–22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:5)

## 5257: civil authorities (id: 5257)

Divinely appointed servants and agents of God exercising their God\-given authority for the good of those they govern and punishing those who do wrong, and to whom all must submit.

Civil authorities are divinely appointed
----------------------------------------

**[JHN 19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:11)**; **[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [1SA 9:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:17), [1SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1); [PSA 78:70–78:71](https://ref.ly/Ps78:70-Ps78:71); [PRO 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Prov8:15-Prov8:16); [JER 27:6](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6), [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10); [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21), [DAN 2:37–2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37-Dan2:38), [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17), [DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32), [DAN 5:18–5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:18-Dan5:21), [DAN 5:26–5:28](https://ref.ly/Dan5:26-Dan5:28)

Civil authorities are God's servants and agents
-----------------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1); [JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9), [JER 27:6](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6), [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10); [ROM 13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6)

Civil authority exercised for the good of all and in order to punish wrongdoers
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 21:15](https://ref.ly/Prov21:15)**; **[1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)** *See also* [GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6); [EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26); [PSA 72:12–72:14](https://ref.ly/Ps72:12-Ps72:14), [PSA 78:72](https://ref.ly/Ps78:72); [PRO 19:12](https://ref.ly/Prov19:12), [PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4), [PRO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Prov29:14); [ACT 25:11](https://ref.ly/Acts25:11); [ROM 13:3–13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:3-Rom13:4); [1TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:2)

Everyone must submit to civil authorities
-----------------------------------------

**[PRO 24:21–24:22](https://ref.ly/Prov24:21-Prov24:22)**; **[TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1)** *See also* [ECC 8:2–8:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:2-Eccl8:5); [MAT 17:24–17:27](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:27), [MAT 22:15–22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:21) parallels [MRK 12:13–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:20–20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:25); [ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1), [ROM 13:5–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:5-Rom13:7); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14), [1PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:17)

Civil authorities are only to be obeyed in what is lawful according to Scripture
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29)** *See also* [EXO 1:17](https://ref.ly/Exod1:17); [1KI 21:2–21:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:2-1Kgs21:3); [DAN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Dan1:8), [DAN 3:28](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28), [DAN 6:7–6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7-Dan6:10); [MAT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:21) parallels [MRK 12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:17), [LUK 20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:25); [ACT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Acts4:19); [HEB 11:23](https://ref.ly/Heb11:23)

* **Associated Passages:** John 19:11; Romans 13:1; 1 Samuel 9:17; 1 Samuel 16:1; Psalms 78:70-71; Proverbs 8:15-16; Jeremiah 27:6; Jeremiah 43:10; Daniel 2:21; Daniel 2:37-38; Daniel 4:17; Daniel 4:32; Daniel 5:18-21; Daniel 5:26-28; Romans 13:4; Isaiah 45:1; Jeremiah 25:9; Romans 13:6; Proverbs 21:15; 1 Peter 2:13-14; Genesis 9:6; Ezra 7:26; Psalms 72:12-14; Psalms 78:72; Proverbs 19:12; Proverbs 29:4; Proverbs 29:14; Acts 25:11; Romans 13:3-4; 1 Timothy 2:2; Proverbs 24:21-22; Titus 3:1; Ecclesiastes 8:2-5; Matthew 17:24-27; Matthew 22:15-21; Mark 12:13-17; Luke 20:20-25; Romans 13:5-7; 1 Peter 2:17; Acts 5:29; Exodus 1:17; 1 Kings 21:2-3; Daniel 1:8; Daniel 3:28; Daniel 6:7-10; Matthew 22:21; Mark 12:17; Luke 20:25; Acts 4:19; Hebrews 11:23

## 5258: cloth (id: 5258)

A woven material from which garments or coverings were made. Different kinds of material are associated with different ranks and social positions.

Cloth for daily wear
--------------------

**[PRO 31:22](https://ref.ly/Prov31:22)** *See also* [NUM 15:38](https://ref.ly/Num15:38); [JDG 5:30](https://ref.ly/Judg5:30), [JDG 14:12](https://ref.ly/Judg14:12); [2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24); [1CH 15:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:27); [PSA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ps45:14); [EZK 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:10), [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13); [MAT 9:16](https://ref.ly/Matt9:16); [JHN 19:23](https://ref.ly/John19:23); [ACT 9:39](https://ref.ly/Acts9:39)

Cloth for general coverings
---------------------------

**[1SA 21:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:9)** *See also* [2SA 17:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:19); [PRO 7:16](https://ref.ly/Prov7:16)

Cloth for special uses
----------------------

### For religious garments

**[EXO 28:6](https://ref.ly/Exod28:6)** *See also* [EXO 28:8](https://ref.ly/Exod28:8), [EXO 28:31](https://ref.ly/Exod28:31), [EXO 28:39](https://ref.ly/Exod28:39), [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42), [EXO 35:19](https://ref.ly/Exod35:19), [EXO 39:1](https://ref.ly/Exod39:1), [EXO 39:5](https://ref.ly/Exod39:5), [EXO 39:22](https://ref.ly/Exod39:22), [EXO 39:41](https://ref.ly/Exod39:41)

### For the tabernacle

**[EXO 26:1](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1)** *See also* [EXO 26:4](https://ref.ly/Exod26:4), [EXO 36:8](https://ref.ly/Exod36:8); [2CH 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:14)

### To cover religious objects

[NUM 4:5–4:13](https://ref.ly/Num4:5-Num4:13)

### Sackcloth as a sign of mourning

**[GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34)** *See also* [2SA 3:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:31); [1KI 21:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:27); [2KI 19:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:1); [NEH 9:1](https://ref.ly/Neh9:1); [PSA 35:13](https://ref.ly/Ps35:13); [ISA 3:24](https://ref.ly/Isa3:24); [JER 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jer4:8); [JOL 1:13](https://ref.ly/Joel1:13)

### For burials

**[MAT 27:59](https://ref.ly/Matt27:59)** parallels [MRK 15:46](https://ref.ly/Mark15:46), [LUK 23:53](https://ref.ly/Luke23:53) *See also* [JHN 11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:44), [JHN 20:7](https://ref.ly/John20:7)

### To wrap babies in

**[EZK 16:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:4)** *See also* [LUK 2:7](https://ref.ly/Luke2:7), [LUK 2:12](https://ref.ly/Luke2:12)

-----------------------

## 5259: coat (id: 5259)

Various objects were given an outer coating to fit them for their particular purpose.

### An outer coat of plaster made stone suitable for writing on

**[DEU 27:2–27:4](https://ref.ly/Deut27:2-Deut27:4)**

### An outer coat of glaze rendered earthenware suitable for food use

**[PRO 26:23](https://ref.ly/Prov26:23)**

------------------------------

## 5260: coinage (id: 5260)

Coinage was not used until the late 7th century B.C. Weights of silver and gold were earlier used as “currency”. By the later exile a variety of minted coinage, made from gold, silver, bronze or copper, gradually replaced the method of reckoning currency by weighing out precious metals. Israelite, Persian, Greek and Roman coins are mentioned in Scripture.

The talent
----------

### A unit of weight

**[1KI 10:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14)** parallel [2CH 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13) *See also* [EXO 38:25](https://ref.ly/Exod38:25), [EXO 38:29](https://ref.ly/Exod38:29); [1KI 9:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:28) parallel [2CH 8:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:18); [1KI 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:10) parallel [2CH 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9); [1CH 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14)

### A weight for precious metals used as currency

**[1KI 20:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:39)** *See also* [1KI 9:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:14), [1KI 16:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:24); [2KI 5:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:5), [2KI 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:22-2Kgs5:23), [2KI 15:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:19), [2KI 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:14), [2KI 23:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:33) parallel [2CH 36:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:3); [1CH 19:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:6); [2CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:6), [2CH 25:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:9), [2CH 27:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:5); [EST 3:9](https://ref.ly/Esth3:9)

### Adopted as the name of a coin

**[MAT 18:23–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:35)** *See also* [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30)

The shekel
----------

### Its value

**[EZK 45:10–45:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:10-Ezek45:12)** *See also* [EXO 30:13](https://ref.ly/Exod30:13), [EXO 38:25–38:26](https://ref.ly/Exod38:25-Exod38:26); [LEV 27:25](https://ref.ly/Lev27:25); [NUM 3:46–3:47](https://ref.ly/Num3:46-Num3:47), [NUM 18:16](https://ref.ly/Num18:16)

### Used as currency

**[ISA 7:23](https://ref.ly/Isa7:23)** *See also* [EXO 30:15](https://ref.ly/Exod30:15); [LEV 27:1–27:8](https://ref.ly/Lev27:1-Lev27:8); [DEU 22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:19), [DEU 22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:29); [JDG 9:4](https://ref.ly/Judg9:4), [JDG 16:5](https://ref.ly/Judg16:5), [JDG 17:10](https://ref.ly/Judg17:10); [1SA 13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:21); [2SA 18:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:12); [2KI 6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25), [2KI 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:1), [2KI 7:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:16), [2KI 7:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:18); [1CH 21:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:25) *David's payment for the threshing\-floor as the temple site*; [2CH 1:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:17); [NEH 5:15](https://ref.ly/Neh5:15), [NEH 10:32](https://ref.ly/Neh10:32); [JER 32:9](https://ref.ly/Jer32:9); [HOS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Hos3:2)

The denarius
------------

### Referred to in Jesus Christ's parables

**[MAT 20:1–20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:16)** *One denarius was the average worker's daily wage.* *See also* [MAT 18:21–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:35); [LUK 7:41–7:47](https://ref.ly/Luke7:41-Luke7:47)

### As the unit for the payment of taxes to Rome

**[MAT 22:15–22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:21)** parallels [MRK 12:13–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:20–20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:26)

The penny
---------

**[MAT 10:29](https://ref.ly/Matt10:29)** parallel [LUK 12:6](https://ref.ly/Luke12:6) *The “penny” was not a coin in use in biblical times, but the term is employed in the NIV for two different copper coins, the “assarion” and the “quadrans”, which were of relatively little value.* *See also* [MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26) parallel [LUK 12:58–12:59](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58-Luke12:59); [MRK 12:42](https://ref.ly/Mark12:42)

The gerah
---------

**[LEV 27:25](https://ref.ly/Lev27:25)** *See also* [EXO 30:13](https://ref.ly/Exod30:13); [NUM 3:47](https://ref.ly/Num3:47), [NUM 18:16](https://ref.ly/Num18:16); [EZK 45:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:12)

The drachma
-----------

**[ACT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Acts19:19)** *See also* [EZR 2:69](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:69); [NEH 7:70–7:72](https://ref.ly/Neh7:70-Neh7:72)

The four\-drachma coin
----------------------

**[MAT 17:24–17:27](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:27)**

The daric
---------

**[1CH 29:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:7)** *See also* [EZR 8:27](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:27)

Unnamed coins
-------------

### General references to coins

**[JHN 2:13–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:13-John2:16)** *See also* [MAT 27:6](https://ref.ly/Matt27:6)

### Silver coins

**[MAT 26:14–26:15](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:15)** *See also* [MAT 27:3–27:10](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:10); [ZEC 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/Zech11:12-Zech11:13); [LUK 10:30–10:35](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30-Luke10:35), [LUK 15:8–15:10](https://ref.ly/Luke15:8-Luke15:10) *Although translated simply as “silver coins”, it is the “drachma” which is referred to here.*

### Copper coins

**[MRK 12:41–12:42](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:42)** parallel [LUK 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:2) *The Greek coin “lepton” is translated as “copper coin” in these passages.*

## 5261: commander (id: 5261)

A general term referring to a senior military officer in charge of soldiers.

Commanders of various ranks
---------------------------

### Supreme commander

*See also* [2KI 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:17)

### Field commander

[ISA 36:2](https://ref.ly/Isa36:2)

### Provincial commander

[1KI 20:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:14)

### Commander of captains

[2CH 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:9)

### Commander of the citadel

[NEH 7:2](https://ref.ly/Neh7:2)

Commanders over various numbers of men
--------------------------------------

[DEU 1:15](https://ref.ly/Deut1:15); ; [NUM 31:14](https://ref.ly/Num31:14)

Commanders serving under various rulers
---------------------------------------

[GEN 21:22](https://ref.ly/Gen21:22) *Abimelech*; ; [JDG 4:2](https://ref.ly/Judg4:2) *Jabin*; ; [1SA 14:50](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:50) *Saul*; ; [2KI 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:1) *the king of Aram*; ; [2KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:4) *Jehoiada*; ; [2KI 25:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:8) *Nebuchadnezzar*; [1CH 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:6), [1CH 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:8), [1CH 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:14), [1CH 15:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:25), [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1), [1CH 28:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:1); [2CH 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:2) *Solomon*; ; [MRK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21) *Herod*

The commander of the Roman troops
---------------------------------

[ACT 21:31–21:34](https://ref.ly/Acts21:31-Acts21:34), [ACT 22:26–22:30](https://ref.ly/Acts22:26-Acts22:30), [ACT 23:10](https://ref.ly/Acts23:10), [ACT 23:17–23:23](https://ref.ly/Acts23:17-Acts23:23), [ACT 24:22](https://ref.ly/Acts24:22)

The commander of the army of the LORD
-------------------------------------

[JOS 5:13–5:15](https://ref.ly/Josh5:13-Josh5:15)

Commanders in spiritual warfare
-------------------------------

**[2TI 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:4)**

## 5262: commendation (id: 5262)

The act of speaking favourably of someone. Scripture commends those who are faithful and obedient to God.

Commendation as speaking favourably of others
---------------------------------------------

**[PRO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Prov15:2)** *See also* [PSA 145:4](https://ref.ly/Ps145:4); [ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15)

Examples of those commended by God
----------------------------------

**[MAT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Matt26:10)**; **[LUK 7:9](https://ref.ly/Luke7:9)** *See also* [MAT 15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:28) *a Canaanite woman*; [MAT 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:17-Matt16:18) *Simon Peter*; [LUK 21:3](https://ref.ly/Luke21:3) *a poor widow*; [HEB 11:4](https://ref.ly/Heb11:4) *Abel*; [HEB 11:5](https://ref.ly/Heb11:5) *Enoch*

Commendation for obedience and faith
------------------------------------

**[PSA 40:4](https://ref.ly/Ps40:4)**; **[HEB 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Heb11:1-Heb11:2)** *See also* [PSA 119:1](https://ref.ly/Ps119:1); [PRO 16:20](https://ref.ly/Prov16:20); [ISA 56:2](https://ref.ly/Isa56:2); [JER 17:7](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7); [MAT 25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34); [ROM 14:18](https://ref.ly/Rom14:18); [2CO 8:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:18); [HEB 11:39](https://ref.ly/Heb11:39)

Commendation for perseverance
-----------------------------

**[ISA 30:18](https://ref.ly/Isa30:18)** *See also* [JAS 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jas1:12), [JAS 5:11](https://ref.ly/Jas5:11); [1PE 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:19-1Pet2:20)

Examples of human commendation of others
----------------------------------------

**[2SA 14:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:25)**; **[2CO 10:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:18)** *See also* [GEN 12:15](https://ref.ly/Gen12:15), [GEN 49:8](https://ref.ly/Gen49:8); [NEH 11:2](https://ref.ly/Neh11:2); [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17); [JOB 29:11](https://ref.ly/Job29:11); [PRO 12:8](https://ref.ly/Prov12:8), [PRO 27:2](https://ref.ly/Prov27:2), [PRO 27:21](https://ref.ly/Prov27:21), [PRO 28:4](https://ref.ly/Prov28:4), [PRO 31:28](https://ref.ly/Prov31:28), [PRO 31:30–31:31](https://ref.ly/Prov31:30-Prov31:31); [ECC 8:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:10); [SNG 6:9](https://ref.ly/Song6:9); [DAN 10:11](https://ref.ly/Dan10:11); [LUK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Luke16:8); [JHN 5:41](https://ref.ly/John5:41), [JHN 5:44](https://ref.ly/John5:44), [JHN 12:43](https://ref.ly/John12:43); [ACT 18:27](https://ref.ly/Acts18:27); [ROM 2:28](https://ref.ly/Rom2:28), [ROM 13:3](https://ref.ly/Rom13:3), [ROM 16:1](https://ref.ly/Rom16:1); [1CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:2), [1CO 11:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:17), [1CO 11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:22); [2CO 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:1), [2CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:11); [1TH 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6); [1PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:14)

Self\-commendation is to be avoided
-----------------------------------

**[2CO 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:12)** *See also* [2CO 6:4–6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:4-2Cor6:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 15:2; Psalms 145:4; Ecclesiastes 8:15; Matthew 26:10; Luke 7:9; Matthew 15:28; Matthew 16:17-18; Luke 21:3; Hebrews 11:4; Hebrews 11:5; Psalms 40:4; Hebrews 11:1-2; Psalms 119:1; Proverbs 16:20; Isaiah 56:2; Jeremiah 17:7; Matthew 25:34; Romans 14:18; 2 Corinthians 8:18; Hebrews 11:39; Isaiah 30:18; James 1:12; James 5:11; 1 Peter 2:19-20; 2 Samuel 14:25; 2 Corinthians 10:18; Genesis 12:15; Genesis 49:8; Nehemiah 11:2; Esther 2:17; Job 29:11; Proverbs 12:8; Proverbs 27:2; Proverbs 27:21; Proverbs 28:4; Proverbs 31:28; Proverbs 31:30-31; Ecclesiastes 8:10; Song of Songs 6:9; Daniel 10:11; Luke 16:8; John 5:41; John 5:44; John 12:43; Acts 18:27; Romans 2:28; Romans 13:3; Romans 16:1; 1 Corinthians 11:2; 1 Corinthians 11:17; 1 Corinthians 11:22; 2 Corinthians 3:1; 2 Corinthians 12:11; 1 Thessalonians 2:6; 1 Peter 2:14; 2 Corinthians 5:12; 2 Corinthians 6:4-10

## 5263: communication (id: 5263)

The effective conveyance of a message. God has communicated with humanity, especially through his Son. Christians are encouraged to communicate God's word effectively to others.

God communicates with people
----------------------------

### Through his creation

**[PSA 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:4)** *See also* [ROM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20)

### Through the Scriptures

**[2PE 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:20-2Pet1:21)** *See also* [JER 36:2](https://ref.ly/Jer36:2); [EZK 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:3); [ZEC 7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:12); [2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16)

### Through his Son

**[JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/John1:18)**; **[HEB 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:3)** *See also* [JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14); [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15), [COL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Col2:9)

### Through the human conscience

[JHN 8:9](https://ref.ly/John8:9); ; [ROM 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Rom2:14-Rom2:15)

### Through the providential ordering of life

[ACT 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:16-Acts14:17), [ACT 17:26–17:27](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26-Acts17:27)

### Through angelic appearances

[JDG 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11-Judg6:12), [JDG 13:3](https://ref.ly/Judg13:3); ; [ZEC 1:9](https://ref.ly/Zech1:9); ; [MAT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20); ; [LUK 1:11–1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:11-Luke1:13), [LUK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Luke1:28), [LUK 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Luke2:9-Luke2:11); ; [ACT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26); ; [REV 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rev1:1)

### Through dreams

[1KI 3:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:5); ; [DAN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Dan7:1); ; [MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13)

### Through visions

[ISA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Isa1:1); ; [EZK 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:24-Ezek11:25); ; [DAN 8:1](https://ref.ly/Dan8:1); ; [OBA 1:1–1:21](https://ref.ly/Obad1:1-Obad1:21); ; [ACT 10:3](https://ref.ly/Acts10:3), [ACT 10:9–10:17](https://ref.ly/Acts10:9-Acts10:17)

### Through a donkey

[NUM 22:27–22:31](https://ref.ly/Num22:27-Num22:31)

### Through miracles

[JHN 11:25](https://ref.ly/John11:25), [JHN 11:43–11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:43-John11:44); ; [ACT 2:22](https://ref.ly/Acts2:22) *The miracles of Jesus Christ were not merely wonderful occurrences; they were also signs that conveyed spiritual truth.*

God has communicated directly with individuals
----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 3:2–3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:2-Exod3:6)** *See also* [EXO 33:9](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9), [EXO 33:21](https://ref.ly/Exod33:21); [1KI 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:12-1Kgs19:13)

Jesus Christ and communication
------------------------------

### Jesus Christ communicated his Father's will

**[JHN 12:49](https://ref.ly/John12:49)** *See also* [JHN 14:9–14:10](https://ref.ly/John14:9-John14:10), [JHN 14:24](https://ref.ly/John14:24), [JHN 15:15](https://ref.ly/John15:15), [JHN 17:8](https://ref.ly/John17:8)

### Jesus Christ used parables in order to communicate with some but not with others

[MRK 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:12) parallels [MAT 13:10–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:10-Matt13:15), [LUK 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:9-Luke8:10); ; [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); ; [JHN 9:39](https://ref.ly/John9:39)

The Holy Spirit and communication
---------------------------------

**[JHN 16:15](https://ref.ly/John16:15)** *See also* [JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26), [JHN 15:26](https://ref.ly/John15:26), [JHN 16:8](https://ref.ly/John16:8), [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/John16:13); [ROM 8:16](https://ref.ly/Rom8:16); [GAL 4:6](https://ref.ly/Gal4:6); [1JN 3:24](https://ref.ly/1John3:24)

Christians are to communicate the good news to other people
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 24:46–24:48](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46-Luke24:48)** *See also* [MAT 10:7](https://ref.ly/Matt10:7), [MAT 28:19–28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19-Matt28:20); [MRK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Mark6:12), [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15), [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8), [ACT 4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:20), [ACT 20:27](https://ref.ly/Acts20:27), [ACT 26:17–26:20](https://ref.ly/Acts26:17-Acts26:20); [ROM 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:14-Rom10:15); [1TH 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:8)

The failure of communication
----------------------------

### Inadequate perception of the truth

**[MRK 8:16–8:21](https://ref.ly/Mark8:16-Mark8:21)** *See also* [MRK 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mark4:13); [JHN 1:10](https://ref.ly/John1:10), [JHN 8:27](https://ref.ly/John8:27), [JHN 10:6](https://ref.ly/John10:6), [JHN 13:22](https://ref.ly/John13:22), [JHN 14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:9), [JHN 16:18](https://ref.ly/John16:18)

### Wilful rejection of the truth

**[EPH 4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:18)** *See also* [JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44), [JHN 8:47](https://ref.ly/John8:47); [ROM 1:21–1:25](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21-Rom1:25); [HEB 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12-Heb3:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 19:1-4; Romans 1:20; 2 Peter 1:20-21; Jeremiah 36:2; Ezekiel 1:3; Zechariah 7:12; 2 Timothy 3:16; John 1:18; Hebrews 1:1-3; John 1:14; Colossians 1:15; Colossians 2:9; John 8:9; Romans 2:14-15; Acts 14:16-17; Acts 17:26-27; Judges 6:11-12; Judges 13:3; Zechariah 1:9; Matthew 1:20; Luke 1:11-13; Luke 1:28; Luke 2:9-11; Acts 8:26; Revelation 1:1; 1 Kings 3:5; Daniel 7:1; Matthew 2:13; Isaiah 1:1; Ezekiel 11:24-25; Daniel 8:1; Obadiah 1:1-21; Acts 10:3; Acts 10:9-17; Numbers 22:27-31; John 11:25; John 11:43-44; Acts 2:22; Exodus 3:2-6; Exodus 33:9; Exodus 33:21; 1 Kings 19:12-13; John 12:49; John 14:9-10; John 14:24; John 15:15; John 17:8; Mark 4:10-12; Matthew 13:10-15; Luke 8:9-10; Isaiah 6:9-10; John 9:39; John 16:15; John 14:26; John 15:26; John 16:8; John 16:13; Romans 8:16; Galatians 4:6; 1 John 3:24; Luke 24:46-48; Matthew 10:7; Matthew 28:19-20; Mark 6:12; Mark 16:15; Mark 16:20; Acts 1:8; Acts 4:20; Acts 20:27; Acts 26:17-20; Romans 10:14-15; 1 Thessalonians 1:8; Mark 8:16-21; Mark 4:13; John 1:10; John 8:27; John 10:6; John 13:22; John 14:9; John 16:18; Ephesians 4:18; John 8:44; John 8:47; Romans 1:21-25; Hebrews 3:12-13

## 5264: compensation (id: 5264)

Compensation under the law of Moses
-----------------------------------

### For personal injuries

**[EXO 21:26–21:27](https://ref.ly/Exod21:26-Exod21:27)** *See also* [EXO 21:32](https://ref.ly/Exod21:32)

### For the loss of an animal

**[EXO 21:33–21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:33-Exod21:34)** *See also* [EXO 21:35–21:36](https://ref.ly/Exod21:35-Exod21:36)

### For theft

**[EXO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Exod22:1), [EXO 22:3–22:4](https://ref.ly/Exod22:3-Exod22:4)** *See also* [EXO 22:7–22:9](https://ref.ly/Exod22:7-Exod22:9)

### For damage to property

[EXO 22:5–22:6](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5-Exod22:6)

### For breach of trust

[EXO 22:10–22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:10-Exod22:15); ; [LEV 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:7); ; [NUM 5:5–5:7](https://ref.ly/Num5:5-Num5:7) *Compensation was only paid when someone was at fault. Similar laws apparently existed in patriarchal times: see [Ge 31:39](https://ref.ly/Gen31:39).*

Compensation later in the OT
----------------------------

### The law applied by David

[2SA 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:4-2Sam12:6)

### Sevenfold compensation for theft

[PRO 6:30–6:31](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30-Prov6:31) *presumably “sevenfold” here means “manifold”; he will pay in full*

### Adultery beyond compensation

[PRO 6:32–6:35](https://ref.ly/Prov6:32-Prov6:35)

### Compensation a mark of repentance

[EZK 33:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:15)

Compensation in the NT
----------------------

### For losses inflicted

**[LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)** *In his zeal, after being converted, Zacchaeus goes beyond the requirements of the law.* *See also* [PHM 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:18-Phlm1:19)

### For losses sustained for the gospel

**[MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29)** parallel [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30) *God is far more generous under grace than he required wrongdoers to be under law.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 21:26-27; Exodus 21:32; Exodus 21:33-34; Exodus 21:35-36; Exodus 22:1; Exodus 22:3-4; Exodus 22:7-9; Exodus 22:5-6; Exodus 22:10-15; Leviticus 6:1-7; Numbers 5:5-7; Genesis 31:39; 2 Samuel 12:4-6; Proverbs 6:30-31; Proverbs 6:32-35; Ezekiel 33:15; Luke 19:8; Philemon 1:18-19; Matthew 19:29; Luke 18:29-30
* **Associated Articles:** 5276: crime (ID: `5276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5398: loss (ID: `5398@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5482: punishment (ID: `5482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5492: restitution (ID: `5492@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5555: stealing (ID: `5555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5679: dowry (ID: `5679@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5265: complaints (id: 5265)

The expression of a grievance or displeasure, usually against the actions and attitudes of others including God himself.

Complaints against God
----------------------

### Israel in the wilderness

## 5266: conscription (id: 5266)

The compulsory calling up of people to service, especially military service. The OT refers to men being conscripted to serve in the army, or in major building projects.

Men conscripted to serve in the army
------------------------------------

### Moses drew up lists of potential fighting men for the conquest of Canaan

**[NUM 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:2-Num1:3)** *See also* [NUM 1:17–1:19](https://ref.ly/Num1:17-Num1:19), [NUM 1:45–1:46](https://ref.ly/Num1:45-Num1:46)NUM [26:2](https://ref.ly/Num26:2), [NUM 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Num26:3-Num26:4), [NUM 26:63–26:65](https://ref.ly/Num26:63-Num26:65)

### Deborah called up troops to attack Jabin, a Canaanite king

[JDG 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4-Judg4:7)

### God restricted the number of men conscripted by Gideon

[JDG 7:1–7:8](https://ref.ly/Judg7:1-Judg7:8) *The purpose of this was to make clear that Israel's victory was in God's strength alone.*

### The Benjamites raised a tribal militia to fight the rest of Israel

[JDG 20:15–20:17](https://ref.ly/Judg20:15-Judg20:17)

### King Saul chose men to fight the Philistines

[1SA 13:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:2), [1SA 14:52](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:52)

### King David maintained a regular army

[1CH 27:1–27:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:1-1Chr27:15)

The conscription of labourers
-----------------------------

### Those in charge of conscription

[2KI 25:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:19) parallel [JER 52:25](https://ref.ly/Jer52:25)

### Solomon used conscripts to build the temple and other projects

## 5267: control (id: 5267)

The exercise of authority and restraint over individuals and events. Scripture stresses that God is in sovereign control over his creation. A limited degree of control over creation is delegated to humanity.

God controls the whole world
----------------------------

**[GEN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1), [GEN 1:3](https://ref.ly/Gen1:3)** *See also* [NEH 9:6](https://ref.ly/Neh9:6); [PSA 102:25](https://ref.ly/Ps102:25); [ACT 17:24](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24); [HEB 11:3](https://ref.ly/Heb11:3)

God controls the affairs of heaven and earth
--------------------------------------------

[DEU 4:39](https://ref.ly/Deut4:39); ; [DAN 4:35](https://ref.ly/Dan4:35)

### He gives wealth

**[DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18)** *See also* [1CH 29:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:12)

### He appoints and deposes earthly rulers

[PSA 75:6–75:7](https://ref.ly/Ps75:6-Ps75:7); ; [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21), [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17), [DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32), [DAN 5:18](https://ref.ly/Dan5:18), [DAN 5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:21), [DAN 5:26](https://ref.ly/Dan5:26)

### He controls human hearts

[PRO 21:1](https://ref.ly/Prov21:1)

### He controls times and seasons

**[GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22)** *See also* [PSA 31:15](https://ref.ly/Ps31:15); [DAN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:21); [AMO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Amos4:9); [ACT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Acts1:7)

### He controls the weather

[AMO 4:7](https://ref.ly/Amos4:7); ; [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)

### He saves those whom he wills

[ROM 9:11–9:16](https://ref.ly/Rom9:11-Rom9:16)

### He fixes human destiny

[MAT 25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34), [MAT 25:41](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41)

Human control over creation
---------------------------

**[GEN 1:26–1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26-Gen1:28)** *See also* [GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15), [GEN 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Gen2:19-Gen2:20), [GEN 3:16–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16-Gen3:19) *After the fall it became difficult for human beings to control creation.*

Human control over earthly territories
--------------------------------------

[JOS 18:1](https://ref.ly/Josh18:1); ; [2SA 8:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:1), [2SA 8:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:3); ; [2CH 17:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:5); ; [DAN 11:43](https://ref.ly/Dan11:43)

Both the godly and ungodly should control themselves
----------------------------------------------------

[PRO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Prov16:32), [PRO 29:11](https://ref.ly/Prov29:11); ; [1CO 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:9), [1CO 7:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:37); ; [2CO 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:5); ; [1TH 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:4)

### Prophecy should be under the control of the prophets

[1CO 14:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:32)

The evil one has limited control
--------------------------------

**[1JN 5:19](https://ref.ly/1John5:19)** *See also* [JOB 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Job1:6-Job1:7); [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12); [COL 1:13](https://ref.ly/Col1:13)

Jesus Christ will have final control over all things
----------------------------------------------------

**[PHP 3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:21)** *See also* [1CO 15:24–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24-1Cor15:28) *Jesus Christ himself will be finally made subject to God.*

## 5268: cooking (id: 5268)

Scripture contains much evidence of methods of cooking and types of food cooked in biblical times. Cooking was an essential part of sacrificial worship, as well as being a domestic necessity.

-----

**[1SA 8:10–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:10-1Sam8:13)** *See also* [1SA 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:23-1Sam9:24)

## 5269: cornerstone (id: 5269)

A stone that can be in the foundation, above ground level or at the summit of the roof (the “capstone”). The cornerstone of a large building gives it a reliable and firm foundation, leading to the cohesion and stability of the whole building. In Scripture, such foundation\-stones are taken as symbolic of the basis of faith in Jesus Christ and the church. Jesus Christ is thus represented as both the foundation upon which the church is built, and the capstone which crowns the whole.

The place of the cornerstone in buildings
-----------------------------------------

**[JOB 38:4–38:6](https://ref.ly/Job38:4-Job38:6)** *See also* [JER 51:26](https://ref.ly/Jer51:26); [1PE 2:4–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:4-1Pet2:8)

The cornerstone is prominent, indispensable and should be reliable
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16)** *See also* [PSA 118:22](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22); [ISA 19:13](https://ref.ly/Isa19:13)

Jesus Christ is the cornerstone of the church
---------------------------------------------

**[EPH 2:19–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19-Eph2:22)** *See also* [1PE 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5-1Pet2:6); [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16)

The cornerstone is a sure foundation for believers, but a stumbling\-block for unbelievers
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Rejection of the cornerstone leads to stumbling and rejection

**[MAT 21:42–21:44](https://ref.ly/Matt21:42-Matt21:44)** parallels [MRK 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Mark12:10-Mark12:11), [LUK 20:17–20:18](https://ref.ly/Luke20:17-Luke20:18) *See also* [PSA 118:22–118:23](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22-Ps118:23); [ISA 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/Isa8:13-Isa8:15); [ROM 9:33](https://ref.ly/Rom9:33); [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16); [1PE 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:7-1Pet2:8)

### Reliance on the cornerstone gives security to believers

**[1PE 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:6-1Pet2:7)** *See also* [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16); [ACT 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:10-Acts4:12); [EPH 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:20-Eph2:22)

The capstone as the crowning glory of a building
------------------------------------------------

## 5270: court (id: 5270)

Usually, a legal body dispensing justice, but the word may also refer to a royal court, the original function of which was the dispensation of justice by the monarch.

Courts of law
-------------

### Provision for and procedures of courts

[EXO 18:25–18:26](https://ref.ly/Exod18:25-Exod18:26); ; [DEU 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:15-Deut1:17), [DEU 17:9](https://ref.ly/Deut17:9); ; [RUT 4:2–4:5](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:2-Ruth4:5); ; [1CH 26:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:29); ; [2CH 19:8–19:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:8-2Chr19:11)

### Justice dispensed by the court

**[DEU 25:1](https://ref.ly/Deut25:1)** *See also* [JOB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Job11:10); [AMO 5:15](https://ref.ly/Amos5:15); [ZEC 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Zech8:16-Zech8:17)

### Disputes settled in court

**[JDG 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4-Judg4:5)** *See also* [EXO 21:22](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22); [JOB 5:4](https://ref.ly/Job5:4), [JOB 9:32–9:35](https://ref.ly/Job9:32-Job9:35); [PRO 25:7–25:8](https://ref.ly/Prov25:7-Prov25:8), [PRO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Prov29:9); [MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26) parallel [LUK 12:58–12:59](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58-Luke12:59); [ACT 18:12–18:17](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12-Acts18:17), [ACT 19:38–19:39](https://ref.ly/Acts19:38-Acts19:39), [ACT 25:1–26:27](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts26:27)

### Provision for a court of appeal

**[DEU 17:8–17:13](https://ref.ly/Deut17:8-Deut17:13)**

### Corruption in the court

## 5271: courtyard (id: 5271)

An enclosed, uncovered area within the precincts of large buildings such as temples, palaces and houses. The wilderness tabernacle also incorporated a courtyard.

The courtyard of the tabernacle
-------------------------------

**[EXO 27:9–27:19](https://ref.ly/Exod27:9-Exod27:19)** *See also* [EXO 38:9–38:20](https://ref.ly/Exod38:9-Exod38:20)

---------------------------

**[1KI 7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:12)** *See also* [1KI 8:64](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:64) parallel [2CH 7:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:7); [2CH 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:9), [2CH 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:5), [2CH 24:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:21); [JER 26:2](https://ref.ly/Jer26:2), [JER 36:10](https://ref.ly/Jer36:10); [EZK 40:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:14)

## 5272: craftsmen (id: 5272)

Skilled workers and artisans. God is sometimes compared to a master craftsman in his work of creation. Scripture also identifies God as the giver of talent and ability to certain individuals, in particular those employed in the construction of the tabernacle and the temple.

God the Creator seen as a master craftsman
------------------------------------------

**[PRO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Prov3:19-Prov3:20)** *See also* [GEN 1:1–1:31](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1-Gen1:31); [JOB 38:4–38:38](https://ref.ly/Job38:4-Job38:38) *inanimate creation*; [JOB 38:39–39:30](https://ref.ly/Job38:39-Job39:30) *animate creation*; [PRO 8:22–8:31](https://ref.ly/Prov8:22-Prov8:31)

Craftsmen employed in the construction of the tabernacle and its furnishings
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 31:1–31:11](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:11)** *See also* [EXO 26:1](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1), [EXO 26:31](https://ref.ly/Exod26:31), [EXO 28:6–28:8](https://ref.ly/Exod28:6-Exod28:8), [EXO 28:15](https://ref.ly/Exod28:15), [EXO 35:30–36:3](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod36:3), [EXO 36:8–38:11](https://ref.ly/Exod36:8-Exod38:11), [EXO 39:1–39:3](https://ref.ly/Exod39:1-Exod39:3)

## 5273: creativity (id: 5273)

Imagination and skill used in the expression of visual or performing arts. Human creativity has its source in God, but may be used either to his honour or dishonour.

God's creativity
----------------

**[ISA 64:8](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8)** *See also* [JOB 38:1–38:41](https://ref.ly/Job38:1-Job38:41); [PSA 139:13–139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13-Ps139:16); [ECC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:11); [ISA 45:7–45:12](https://ref.ly/Isa45:7-Isa45:12), [ISA 45:18](https://ref.ly/Isa45:18); [EPH 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eph2:10) *Redemption may be described in terms of re\-creation.*

God is the source of human creativity
-------------------------------------

**[EXO 35:30–35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:35)** *See also* [EXO 31:1–31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:6), [EXO 36:1–36:2](https://ref.ly/Exod36:1-Exod36:2)

Creativity for its own sake
---------------------------

[ECC 2:4–2:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:4-Eccl2:6)

Creativity used in the service of God
-------------------------------------

### Building the tabernacle and temple

**[EXO 36:8](https://ref.ly/Exod36:8)** *See also* [1CH 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:15-1Chr22:16); [2CH 2:5–2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:5-2Chr2:7), [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14)

### Music and dancing

**[PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3)** *See also* [1CH 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:31-1Chr6:32), [1CH 25:1–25:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1-1Chr25:7); [PSA 92:1–92:3](https://ref.ly/Ps92:1-Ps92:3), [PSA 98:5](https://ref.ly/Ps98:5), [PSA 144:9](https://ref.ly/Ps144:9)

The abuse of human creativity
-----------------------------

**[HAB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hab2:18)** *Human creativity, like all of God's gifts, can be abused by sinful human beings, and used to dishonour him rather than serve him.* *See also* [DEU 27:15](https://ref.ly/Deut27:15); [2KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:18); [ISA 40:19–40:20](https://ref.ly/Isa40:19-Isa40:20), [ISA 44:10–44:13](https://ref.ly/Isa44:10-Isa44:13); [ACT 19:23–19:26](https://ref.ly/Acts19:23-Acts19:26)

## 5274: credit (id: 5274)

Money or goods lent under pledge of repayment (with or without interest). In the spiritual sense, Jesus Christ's righteousness is credited to believers.

Credit in Israel: charitable, not commercial
--------------------------------------------

**[DEU 23:19–23:20](https://ref.ly/Deut23:19-Deut23:20)** *See also* [EXO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25); [PSA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:5); [EZK 18:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:7), [EZK 18:16–18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:16-Ezek18:17)

The pledge (security): laws protecting the borrower
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:10–24:13](https://ref.ly/Deut24:10-Deut24:13)** *See also* [EXO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26-Exod22:27); [LEV 25:39–25:40](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:40), [LEV 25:42](https://ref.ly/Lev25:42); [DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6)

Standing security for another may be hazardous
----------------------------------------------

**[PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27)** *See also* [PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5), [PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15), [PRO 17:18](https://ref.ly/Prov17:18) *The binding nature of the commitment cautions against a hasty and unwise engagement.*

Violations of Israel's \`\`credit controls'' roundly condemned
--------------------------------------------------------------

[NEH 5:1–5:12](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:12); ; [JOB 22:6](https://ref.ly/Job22:6), [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3), [JOB 24:9](https://ref.ly/Job24:9); ; [EZK 18:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:12); ; [AMO 2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:8)

The credit system not condemned by Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------

[MAT 25:27](https://ref.ly/Matt25:27) parallel [LUK 19:23](https://ref.ly/Luke19:23)

Christians to view this subject in the light of God's grace
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 6:34–6:35](https://ref.ly/Luke6:34-Luke6:35)** *See also* [MAT 18:23–18:27](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:27) *God is a model creditor.*

Creditors demanding a pledge to assure repayment
------------------------------------------------

### A personal effect

[DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6), [DEU 24:10–24:13](https://ref.ly/Deut24:10-Deut24:13); ; [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3); ; [PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27)

### Mortgage of property

[NEH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Neh5:3)

### The surety of a guarantor

[PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5), [PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15), [PRO 17:18](https://ref.ly/Prov17:18), [PRO 22:26](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26), [PRO 27:13](https://ref.ly/Prov27:13)

Creditors exacting repayments cruelly
-------------------------------------

**[2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1)** *See also* [JOB 22:6](https://ref.ly/Job22:6), [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3), [JOB 24:9](https://ref.ly/Job24:9); [PSA 109:11](https://ref.ly/Ps109:11); [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1); [EZK 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:12-Ezek18:13), [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12); [AMO 2:6](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6), [AMO 2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:8); [MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26), [MAT 18:25–18:26](https://ref.ly/Matt18:25-Matt18:26), [MAT 18:34](https://ref.ly/Matt18:34)

Creditors to be considerate in their demands
--------------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6)** *See also* [EXO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26-Exod22:27); [LEV 25:35–25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:43); [DEU 24:10–24:13](https://ref.ly/Deut24:10-Deut24:13)

Creditors perhaps remitting the debt entirely
---------------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:27](https://ref.ly/Matt18:27)** *See also* [NEH 5:10–5:12](https://ref.ly/Neh5:10-Neh5:12); [MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12); [LUK 7:41–7:43](https://ref.ly/Luke7:41-Luke7:43)

Under Mosaic law creditors to cancel debts eventually
-----------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 15:1–15:3](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1-Deut15:3)** *See also* [DEU 31:10](https://ref.ly/Deut31:10); [NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31)

Spiritual aspects of credit
---------------------------

### Faith credited as righteousness

**[ROM 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rom4:1-Rom4:11)** *See also* [GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6); [PSA 32:2](https://ref.ly/Ps32:2), [PSA 106:31](https://ref.ly/Ps106:31); [EZK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:20); [ROM 4:22–4:24](https://ref.ly/Rom4:22-Rom4:24); [JAS 2:23](https://ref.ly/Jas2:23)

### Jesus Christ's righteousness credited to believers

**[2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21)** *See also* [ROM 3:22](https://ref.ly/Rom3:22); [1CO 1:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:30); [PHP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:9)

Moral aspects of credit
-----------------------

**[PHP 4:17](https://ref.ly/Phil4:17)** *See also* [MAT 10:40–10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40-Matt10:42), [MAT 25:34–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34-Matt25:36); [LUK 6:32–6:35](https://ref.ly/Luke6:32-Luke6:35); [2CO 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:10); [EPH 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:7-Eph6:8) *Good deeds will be remembered and rewarded in heaven.*

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 23:19-20; Exodus 22:25; Psalms 15:5; Ezekiel 18:7; Ezekiel 18:16-17; Deuteronomy 24:10-13; Exodus 22:26-27; Leviticus 25:39-40; Leviticus 25:42; Deuteronomy 24:6; Proverbs 22:26-27; Proverbs 6:1-5; Proverbs 11:15; Proverbs 17:18; Nehemiah 5:1-12; Job 22:6; Job 24:3; Job 24:9; Ezekiel 18:12; Amos 2:8; Matthew 25:27; Luke 19:23; Luke 6:34-35; Matthew 18:23-27; Nehemiah 5:3; Proverbs 22:26; Proverbs 27:13; 2 Kings 4:1; Psalms 109:11; Isaiah 50:1; Ezekiel 18:12-13; Ezekiel 22:12; Amos 2:6; Matthew 5:25-26; Matthew 18:25-26; Matthew 18:34; Leviticus 25:35-43; Matthew 18:27; Nehemiah 5:10-12; Matthew 6:12; Luke 7:41-43; Deuteronomy 15:1-3; Deuteronomy 31:10; Nehemiah 10:31; Romans 4:1-11; Genesis 15:6; Psalms 32:2; Psalms 106:31; Ezekiel 18:20; Romans 4:22-24; James 2:23; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Romans 3:22; 1 Corinthians 1:30; Philippians 3:9; Philippians 4:17; Matthew 10:40-42; Matthew 25:34-36; Luke 6:32-35; 2 Corinthians 5:10; Ephesians 6:7-8

## 5275: cremation (id: 5275)

The burning of a dead body. Cremation was not practised by Israel or the first Christians. In difficult circumstances a corpse might be burnt and the remains buried
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Cremation of corpses
--------------------

**[1SA 31:12–31:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:12-1Sam31:13)** *This was done to prevent any further abuse of the bodies by the Philistines. David later reburied the remains in a family grave (see [2Sa 21:12–14](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:12-2Sam21:14)).*

Burning a body as a punishment
------------------------------

*See also* [LEV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Lev20:14), [LEV 21:9](https://ref.ly/Lev21:9); [JOS 7:15](https://ref.ly/Josh7:15), [JOS 7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25); [2KI 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:20)

Funeral fires for the kings of Judah
------------------------------------

These were not for the purpose of cremating the king's corpse, but public demonstrations in his honour: [2CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:14); ; [JER 34:5](https://ref.ly/Jer34:5)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 31:12-13; 2 Samuel 21:12-14; Leviticus 20:14; Leviticus 21:9; Joshua 7:15; Joshua 7:25; 2 Kings 23:20; 2 Chronicles 16:14; Jeremiah 34:5
* **Associated Articles:** 4826: fire (ID: `4826@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5241: burial (ID: `5241@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5320: funeral (ID: `5320@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5482: punishment (ID: `5482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9030: dead bodies (ID: `9030@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5276: crime (id: 5276)

An offence against the established laws of society including murder, adultery, theft, perjury and treason. Scripture contains both warnings and teaching to help young people avoid a life of crime.

Crime in Israel
---------------

**[DEU 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Deut6:1-Deut6:2)** *Crime in Israel was the breaking of laws given by God.*

### Examples of crimes covered by Israel's laws

*See also* [EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13) *murder*; [EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14) *adultery*; [EXO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Exod20:15) *theft*; [EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16) *perjury*[JOS 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/Josh1:16-Josh1:18); [2SA 15:7–15:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:7-2Sam15:10); [1KI 16:15–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:15-1Kgs16:18); [2KI 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:1-2Kgs11:3) parallel [2CH 22:10–22:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:10-2Chr22:12)

Examples of crime in the NT
---------------------------

### Murder

[MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16); ; [ACT 7:58](https://ref.ly/Acts7:58)

### Adultery

[JHN 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:4); ; [1CO 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1)

### Theft

[JHN 12:6](https://ref.ly/John12:6)

### Perjury

[MAT 26:59–26:60](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:60) parallel [MRK 14:55–14:56](https://ref.ly/Mark14:55-Mark14:56); ; [ACT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Acts6:13), [ACT 25:7](https://ref.ly/Acts25:7)

Scripture warns young people that temptation may lead to crime
--------------------------------------------------------------

### Violence

[PRO 1:10–1:19](https://ref.ly/Prov1:10-Prov1:19)

### Sexual immorality

[PRO 5:1–5:23](https://ref.ly/Prov5:1-Prov5:23), [PRO 6:20–6:29](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:29), [PRO 7:6–7:27](https://ref.ly/Prov7:6-Prov7:27)

Scripture teaches parents to discipline their children
------------------------------------------------------

[PRO 13:24](https://ref.ly/Prov13:24), [PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18), [PRO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Prov22:6), [PRO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Prov22:15), [PRO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Prov23:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 6:1-2; Exodus 20:13; Exodus 20:14; Exodus 20:15; Exodus 20:16; Joshua 1:16-18; 2 Samuel 15:7-10; 1 Kings 16:15-18; 2 Kings 11:1-3; 2 Chronicles 22:10-12; Matthew 2:16; Acts 7:58; John 8:3-4; 1 Corinthians 5:1; John 12:6; Matthew 26:59-60; Mark 14:55-56; Acts 6:13; Acts 25:7; Proverbs 1:10-19; Proverbs 5:1-23; Proverbs 6:20-29; Proverbs 7:6-27; Proverbs 13:24; Proverbs 19:18; Proverbs 22:6; Proverbs 22:15; Proverbs 23:13
* **Associated Articles:** 5040: murder (ID: `5040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5277: criminals (ID: `5277@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5440: perjury (ID: `5440@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5483: punishment, nature of (ID: `5483@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5555: stealing (ID: `5555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6206: offence (ID: `6206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6236: sexual sin (ID: `6236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6632: conviction (ID: `6632@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8232: discipline, in the family (ID: `8232@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8718: disobedience (ID: `8718@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5277: criminals (id: 5277)

Individuals who are guilty of crimes such as murder, adultery, theft, perjury or treason. Scripture refers to and gives examples of young law\-breakers (delinquents).

Criminals guilty of murder
--------------------------

### Cain

**[GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8)**

### Abimelech

*See also* [JDG 9:5](https://ref.ly/Judg9:5)

### Doeg the Edomite

[1SA 22:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:18)

### David

[2SA 11:14–11:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:14-2Sam11:17)

### Absalom

[2SA 13:23–13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:23-2Sam13:29)

### Herod the Great

[MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16)

### Barabbas

[MRK 15:7](https://ref.ly/Mark15:7) parallel [LUK 23:19](https://ref.ly/Luke23:19)

Criminals guilty of adultery
----------------------------

### David

**[2SA 11:2–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:5)**

### Gomer (Hosea's wife)

*See also* [HOS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1)

Criminals guilty of theft
-------------------------

### Rachel

[GEN 31:19](https://ref.ly/Gen31:19), [GEN 31:30](https://ref.ly/Gen31:30)

### Achan

[JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21)

### Judas Iscariot

[JHN 12:6](https://ref.ly/John12:6)

Criminals guilty of perjury
---------------------------

### Joel and Abijah (sons of Samuel)

[1SA 8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:3)

### Two who gave false evidence against Naboth

[1KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:13)

Examples of criminals guilty of treason
---------------------------------------

### Zimri

[1KI 16:15–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:15-1Kgs16:18)

### Athaliah

[2KI 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:1-2Kgs11:3) parallel [2CH 22:10–22:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:10-2Chr22:12)

Examples of delinquents
-----------------------

### Those who break the fifth commandment as minors may have been treated as criminals

[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)

### Manasseh, king of Judah

**[2KI 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:1-2Kgs21:2)**

### Youths who jeered Elisha

**[2KI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23-2Kgs2:24)**

### Those who ignore the wise counsel of Solomon

**[PRO 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:4)** *It seems that sections of Proverbs were written with young people in mind. Those who choose to act upon its wisdom will please God and keep themselves out of the troubles common to youth.*

### Those who succumb to the evil desires of youth

*See also* [2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:22) *Young people are warned that the strength of their desires can easily get them into trouble. Young Christians are, with God's help, able to control such desires.*

The punishment of criminals
---------------------------

### By hanging

**[DEU 21:22–21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22-Deut21:23)** *See also* [2SA 21:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:9); [EZR 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:11); [EST 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:9-Esth7:10)

### By stoning

**[DEU 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:24)** *See also* [LEV 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Lev20:1-Lev20:2); [JOS 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:24-Josh7:25)

### By beating

**[DEU 25:2–25:3](https://ref.ly/Deut25:2-Deut25:3)** *See also* [ACT 16:20–16:23](https://ref.ly/Acts16:20-Acts16:23); [2CO 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:24-2Cor11:25)

### By confiscation of property

**[EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26)** *See also* [EZR 10:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:8)

### By crucifixion

**[LUK 23:32–23:33](https://ref.ly/Luke23:32-Luke23:33)** parallels [MAT 27:38](https://ref.ly/Matt27:38), [MRK 15:27](https://ref.ly/Mark15:27) *See also* [LUK 23:40–23:41](https://ref.ly/Luke23:40-Luke23:41)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 4:8; Judges 9:5; 1 Samuel 22:18; 2 Samuel 11:14-17; 2 Samuel 13:23-29; Matthew 2:16; Mark 15:7; Luke 23:19; 2 Samuel 11:2-5; Hosea 3:1; Genesis 31:19; Genesis 31:30; Joshua 7:21; John 12:6; 1 Samuel 8:3; 1 Kings 21:13; 1 Kings 16:15-18; 2 Kings 11:1-3; 2 Chronicles 22:10-12; Exodus 20:12; 2 Kings 21:1-2; 2 Kings 2:23-24; Proverbs 1:1-4; 2 Timothy 2:22; Deuteronomy 21:22-23; 2 Samuel 21:9; Ezra 6:11; Esther 7:9-10; Deuteronomy 22:23-24; Leviticus 20:1-2; Joshua 7:24-25; Deuteronomy 25:2-3; Acts 16:20-23; 2 Corinthians 11:24-25; Ezra 7:26; Ezra 10:8; Luke 23:32-33; Matthew 27:38; Mark 15:27; Luke 23:40-41

## 5278: cripples (id: 5278)

People whose walk, physical or spiritual, is hindered by deformity, permanent or temporary.

Physically crippled from birth
------------------------------

**[ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2)** *See also* [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8)

Physically crippled in life
---------------------------

### By accident

**[2SA 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:4)** *See also* [2SA 9:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:3), [2SA 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:13)

### By God

**[GEN 32:24–32:25](https://ref.ly/Gen32:24-Gen32:25)** *God had a purpose in the crippling of Jacob: it was a mark of his meeting with God.* *See also* [GEN 32:31–32:32](https://ref.ly/Gen32:31-Gen32:32); [HEB 11:21](https://ref.ly/Heb11:21)

### By Satan

**[LUK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Luke13:11)** *See also* [LUK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Luke13:16)

Cripples healed
---------------

### By Jesus Christ

**[LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22)** parallel [MAT 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:4-Matt11:5) *See also* [MAT 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30-Matt15:31); [JHN 5:1–5:9](https://ref.ly/John5:1-John5:9)

### By others

**[ACT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Acts3:7)** *See also* [ACT 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Acts4:9-Acts4:10), [ACT 8:7](https://ref.ly/Acts8:7), [ACT 14:9–14:10](https://ref.ly/Acts14:9-Acts14:10)

Cripples and OT sacrifice
-------------------------

**[LEV 21:18–21:19](https://ref.ly/Lev21:18-Lev21:19)** *A man must be without defect. Physical disability is a defilement to holiness.* *See also* [LEV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Lev21:6)

### Crippled animals

**[MAL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:8)** *Animals, like people, must be holy, without defect, when brought to the LORD.* *See also* [EXO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Exod12:5); [LEV 3:1](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1), [LEV 4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:3), [LEV 22:18–22:25](https://ref.ly/Lev22:18-Lev22:25); [NUM 6:14](https://ref.ly/Num6:14), [NUM 28:3](https://ref.ly/Num28:3); [DEU 15:21](https://ref.ly/Deut15:21), [DEU 17:1](https://ref.ly/Deut17:1); [MAL 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:13-Mal1:14)

Spiritual aspects of being crippled
-----------------------------------

### Physical crippling preferable to spiritual lameness

**[MAT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Matt18:8)** *See also* [MRK 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Mark5:29-Mark5:30), [MRK 9:43](https://ref.ly/Mark9:43)

### Cripples disadvantaged in life but welcome in the kingdom of God

**[MIC 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Mic4:6-Mic4:7)** *See also* [JER 31:8](https://ref.ly/Jer31:8); [EZK 34:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:16); [LUK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Luke14:13), [LUK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Luke14:21)

### Spiritual cripples healed

**[ISA 35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6)** *See also* [HEB 12:13](https://ref.ly/Heb12:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 3:2; Acts 14:8; 2 Samuel 4:4; 2 Samuel 9:3; 2 Samuel 9:13; Genesis 32:24-25; Genesis 32:31-32; Hebrews 11:21; Luke 13:11; Luke 13:16; Luke 7:22; Matthew 11:4-5; Matthew 15:30-31; John 5:1-9; Acts 3:7; Acts 4:9-10; Acts 8:7; Acts 14:9-10; Leviticus 21:18-19; Leviticus 21:6; Malachi 1:8; Exodus 12:5; Leviticus 3:1; Leviticus 4:3; Leviticus 22:18-25; Numbers 6:14; Numbers 28:3; Deuteronomy 15:21; Deuteronomy 17:1; Malachi 1:13-14; Matthew 18:8; Mark 5:29-30; Mark 9:43; Micah 4:6-7; Jeremiah 31:8; Ezekiel 34:16; Luke 14:13; Luke 14:21; Isaiah 35:6; Hebrews 12:13

## 5279: crowds (id: 5279)

Multitudes of people feature prominently in the narrative parts of Scripture, particularly in the Gospels and Acts. The attitudes of the crowds to Jesus Christ throw light on his ministry, as do his attitudes to them.

Crowds tend towards evil
------------------------

[EXO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:2); ; [PSA 64:2](https://ref.ly/Ps64:2); ; [ISA 53:6](https://ref.ly/Isa53:6); ; [JER 9:2](https://ref.ly/Jer9:2); ; [EZK 23:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:42); ; [MRK 15:8–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:8-Mark15:15)

Crowds and worship
------------------

**[PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4)** *See also* [EZR 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:1)

God's judgment is pronounced on crowds of people
------------------------------------------------

[EZK 7:11–7:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:11-Ezek7:14)**[JOL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Joel3:14)**

Multitudes are seen by John in his vision
-----------------------------------------

**[REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9)** *The redeemed in heaven*; **[REV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Rev17:15)** *Sinful people on earth.*

Crowds played a large part in Jesus Christ's ministry
-----------------------------------------------------

Gathering round him: [MAT 13:2](https://ref.ly/Matt13:2) parallels [MRK 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mark4:1), [LUK 8:4](https://ref.ly/Luke8:4); ; [MRK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20), [MRK 5:21](https://ref.ly/Mark5:21); ; [LUK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Luke12:1); [MAT 8:1](https://ref.ly/Matt8:1); ; [MRK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Mark3:7); ; [LUK 14:25](https://ref.ly/Luke14:25); ; [JHN 6:2](https://ref.ly/John6:2); [MRK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25) *running to the scene of his healings*; ; [LUK 8:42](https://ref.ly/Luke8:42) *almost crushing him*; ; [LUK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Luke3:7) *coming to him to be baptised*; ; [MAT 15:30](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30) *bringing people to him to be healed*; ; [LUK 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29) *eating with him*; ; [JHN 12:34](https://ref.ly/John12:34) *questioning him*; ; [JHN 12:17](https://ref.ly/John12:17) *spreading news of him*; ; [JHN 7:12](https://ref.ly/John7:12) *whispering about him*

Jesus Christ often taught crowds
--------------------------------

**[MRK 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mark2:13)** *See also* [MAT 4:25–5:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:25-Matt5:2) parallel [LUK 6:17–6:20](https://ref.ly/Luke6:17-Luke6:20); [MAT 11:7](https://ref.ly/Matt11:7) parallel [LUK 7:24](https://ref.ly/Luke7:24); [MAT 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Matt13:1-Matt13:3) parallels [MRK 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Mark4:1-Mark4:3), [LUK 8:4–8:5](https://ref.ly/Luke8:4-Luke8:5); [MAT 13:34](https://ref.ly/Matt13:34), [MAT 15:10](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10) parallel [MRK 7:14](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14); [MAT 23:1](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1); [MRK 6:34](https://ref.ly/Mark6:34), [MRK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Mark10:1)

Jesus Christ had compassion on the crowds
-----------------------------------------

**[MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36)** *See also* [MRK 6:34](https://ref.ly/Mark6:34)

### He healed them

**[MAT 14:14](https://ref.ly/Matt14:14)** *See also* [MAT 19:2](https://ref.ly/Matt19:2)

### He fed them

[MAT 14:15–14:21](https://ref.ly/Matt14:15-Matt14:21) parallels [MRK 6:35–6:44](https://ref.ly/Mark6:35-Mark6:44), [LUK 9:12–9:17](https://ref.ly/Luke9:12-Luke9:17), [JHN 6:5–6:13](https://ref.ly/John6:5-John6:13); ; [MAT 15:32–15:38](https://ref.ly/Matt15:32-Matt15:38) parallel [MRK 8:1–8:10](https://ref.ly/Mark8:1-Mark8:10)

Jesus Christ's other contacts with crowds
-----------------------------------------

[MAT 26:55](https://ref.ly/Matt26:55) parallel [MRK 14:48](https://ref.ly/Mark14:48) *questioning them*; [MAT 9:23–9:25](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23-Matt9:25); ; [MRK 2:4](https://ref.ly/Mark2:4) parallel [LUK 5:19](https://ref.ly/Luke5:19); [MAT 14:22](https://ref.ly/Matt14:22) parallel [MRK 6:45](https://ref.ly/Mark6:45) *dismissing them*; ; [LUK 4:30](https://ref.ly/Luke4:30) *passing through them*; ; [MRK 4:36](https://ref.ly/Mark4:36) *leaving them*; ; [JHN 5:13](https://ref.ly/John5:13) *slipping away from them*; ; [MRK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Mark7:33) *leading a man away from the crowd to heal him*

Individuals mentioned in connection with a crowd
------------------------------------------------

[MRK 9:17](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17) parallel [LUK 9:37–9:40](https://ref.ly/Luke9:37-Luke9:40) *the man with a demon\-possessed son*; ; [MRK 5:21–5:34](https://ref.ly/Mark5:21-Mark5:34) *Jairus and the woman with a haemorrhage*; ; [LUK 7:12](https://ref.ly/Luke7:12) *the widow with a dead son*; ; [LUK 19:2–19:3](https://ref.ly/Luke19:2-Luke19:3) *Zaccheus, unable to see Jesus Christ because of the crowd*; ; [MRK 10:46–10:48](https://ref.ly/Mark10:46-Mark10:48) parallel [LUK 18:35–18:39](https://ref.ly/Luke18:35-Luke18:39) *blind Bartimaeus, rebuked by the crowd and healed by Jesus Christ*; ; [LUK 11:27](https://ref.ly/Luke11:27) *the woman who blessed Jesus Christ*; ; [JHN 5:3–5:9](https://ref.ly/John5:3-John5:9) *the man, in the crowd of disabled people by the pool, whom Jesus Christ healed*; [JHN 11:42](https://ref.ly/John11:42), [JHN 12:17](https://ref.ly/John12:17)

The crowds' varied reactions to Jesus Christ's teaching
-------------------------------------------------------

### Awe, amazement, delight and faith

[MAT 9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:8); ; [MRK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:22), [MRK 12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:37); ; [JHN 7:31](https://ref.ly/John7:31)

### Accusation, confusion and questioning

[JHN 7:20](https://ref.ly/John7:20), [JHN 12:29](https://ref.ly/John12:29), [JHN 12:34](https://ref.ly/John12:34)

Crowds played a large part in the final events of Jesus Christ's life
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:46)** parallels [MRK 12:12](https://ref.ly/Mark12:12), [LUK 20:19](https://ref.ly/Luke20:19) *Fear of the crowds restrained the Jewish leaders.* *See also* [MAT 21:8–21:11](https://ref.ly/Matt21:8-Matt21:11) *Crowds demonstrated on Jesus Christ's entry into Jerusalem*; [MRK 15:8–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:8-Mark15:15) *The crowd, stirred up by the chief priests, asked for the release of Barabbas*; [MAT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:24) *Pilate washed his hands in front of the crowd*; [MAT 26:47](https://ref.ly/Matt26:47) parallels [MRK 14:43](https://ref.ly/Mark14:43), [LUK 22:47](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47) *A crowd came with Judas to Gethsemane.*

A crowd gathered in Jerusalem at Pentecost
------------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:6](https://ref.ly/Acts2:6)** *See also* [ACT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14)

Crowds featured prominently in the ministry of the apostles
-----------------------------------------------------------

[ACT 5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:16) *bringing the sick to be healed*; ; [ACT 8:6](https://ref.ly/Acts8:6) *listening to Philip's preaching in Samaria*; ; [ACT 13:44–13:45](https://ref.ly/Acts13:44-Acts13:45) *listening to Paul and Barnabas in Pisidian Antioch*; ; [ACT 14:11–14:19](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:19) *attempting to worship Paul and Barnabas in Lystra*; ; [ACT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22) *attacking Paul and Silas in Philippi*; ; [ACT 17:5–17:13](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5-Acts17:13) *being stirred up against Paul in Thessalonica and Berea*; ; [ACT 19:30–19:35](https://ref.ly/Acts19:30-Acts19:35) *rising up against Paul in Ephesus*; ; [ACT 21:27–21:40](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:40) *rising up against Paul in Jerusalem*; ; [ACT 22:22](https://ref.ly/Acts22:22) *giving Paul a limited hearing in Jerusalem*

Crowds as the source of loud noise
----------------------------------

**[ISA 13:4](https://ref.ly/Isa13:4)** *See also* [DAN 10:6](https://ref.ly/Dan10:6); [REV 19:1](https://ref.ly/Rev19:1), [REV 19:6](https://ref.ly/Rev19:6)
