## 5280: crown (id: 5280)

An encircling ornament for the head, worn by monarchs as symbol of royal authority. Also a wreath, worn on joyful occasions or presented to the winner of a race. Used figuratively, the word indicates honour and blessing, the reward awaiting believers in heaven.

A crown as an ornament worn by monarchs
---------------------------------------

### By kings

**[2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)** parallel [2CH 23:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:11) *Crowns worn by the kings of Israel and Judah signified their consecration to God.* *See also* [2SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:10); [PSA 89:39](https://ref.ly/Ps89:39), [PSA 132:18](https://ref.ly/Ps132:18); [PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24); [ZEC 9:16](https://ref.ly/Zech9:16)

### By queens

[EST 1:11](https://ref.ly/Esth1:11), [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17)

A crown as a symbol of royal authority
--------------------------------------

**[2SA 12:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:30)** parallel [1CH 20:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:2) *See also* [PSA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Ps21:3); [ISA 23:8](https://ref.ly/Isa23:8); [JER 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jer13:18); [EZK 21:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:26); [ZEC 6:11](https://ref.ly/Zech6:11), [ZEC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Zech6:14); [REV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rev12:3), [REV 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rev13:1), [REV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Rev19:12)

A crown as a symbol of honour
-----------------------------

[EST 8:15](https://ref.ly/Esth8:15); ; [JOB 19:9](https://ref.ly/Job19:9), [JOB 31:36](https://ref.ly/Job31:36); ; [EZK 23:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:42)

A crown as a wreath
-------------------

### Worn on joyful occasions, such as a wedding

**[SNG 3:11](https://ref.ly/Song3:11)** *See also* [PRO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Prov4:9); [ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3); [EZK 16:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:12), [EZK 23:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:42)

### A wreath of laurel leaves, worn by the winner of a race

**[1CO 9:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:25)** *See also* [2TI 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:5)

The mock crown (wreath) of thorns put on Jesus Christ's head
------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29)** parallel [MRK 15:17](https://ref.ly/Mark15:17) *See also* [JHN 19:2](https://ref.ly/John19:2), [JHN 19:5](https://ref.ly/John19:5)

Crown is used figuratively to indicate honour and blessing
----------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 62:3](https://ref.ly/Isa62:3)** *See also* [PRO 4:9](https://ref.ly/Prov4:9), [PRO 12:4](https://ref.ly/Prov12:4), [PRO 14:24](https://ref.ly/Prov14:24), [PRO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Prov16:31), [PRO 17:6](https://ref.ly/Prov17:6); [ISA 28:5](https://ref.ly/Isa28:5), [ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3); [LAM 5:16](https://ref.ly/Lam5:16); [REV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Rev6:2), [REV 9:7](https://ref.ly/Rev9:7), [REV 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rev12:1), [REV 14:14](https://ref.ly/Rev14:14)

### Paul's joy and crown

**[1TH 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:19)** *See also* [PHP 4:1](https://ref.ly/Phil4:1)

Crown represents the honour and blessing that await believers in heaven
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5281: crucifixion (id: 5281)

A most painful, cruel and protracted form of execution which involved the victim being roped or nailed to an elevated wooden cross. This form of execution was favoured by the Romans, who regarded it as a deterrent to political rebellion against the authority of Rome at the time of Jesus Christ.

The method of crucifixion
-------------------------

### The victim was scourged before crucifixion

**[JHN 19:1](https://ref.ly/John19:1)** parallels [MAT 27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26), [MRK 15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15), [LUK 23:22](https://ref.ly/Luke23:22) *See also* [LUK 23:16](https://ref.ly/Luke23:16)

### The victim carried his cross

**[JHN 19:17](https://ref.ly/John19:17)** [MAT 27:32–27:33](https://ref.ly/Matt27:32-Matt27:33); ; [MRK 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/Mark15:21-Mark15:22); ; [LUK 23:26](https://ref.ly/Luke23:26)

### The victim was fixed to the cross

**[LUK 23:33](https://ref.ly/Luke23:33)** parallels [MAT 27:35](https://ref.ly/Matt27:35), [MRK 15:24](https://ref.ly/Mark15:24), [JHN 19:23](https://ref.ly/John19:23) *See also* [JHN 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25)

### A charge board was fixed to the cross

**[MAT 27:37](https://ref.ly/Matt27:37)** parallels [MRK 15:26](https://ref.ly/Mark15:26), [LUK 23:38](https://ref.ly/Luke23:38), [JHN 19:19](https://ref.ly/John19:19)

### The victim's sufferings were protracted

**[MAT 27:54](https://ref.ly/Matt27:54)** parallels [MRK 15:39](https://ref.ly/Mark15:39), [LUK 23:47](https://ref.ly/Luke23:47)**[JHN 19:32–19:33](https://ref.ly/John19:32-John19:33), [JHN 19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:34)** *See also* [MRK 15:25](https://ref.ly/Mark15:25), [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33), [MRK 15:37](https://ref.ly/Mark15:37)

OT prophecies concerning Jesus Christ's crucifixion
---------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 22:16–22:18](https://ref.ly/Ps22:16-Ps22:18)** [EXO 12:46](https://ref.ly/Exod12:46); ; [NUM 9:12](https://ref.ly/Num9:12); ; [PSA 34:20](https://ref.ly/Ps34:20); ; [JHN 19:36](https://ref.ly/John19:36); [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1), [PSA 22:7–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7-Ps22:8), [PSA 69:21](https://ref.ly/Ps69:21), [PSA 109:25](https://ref.ly/Ps109:25); ; [ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6), [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5), [ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9); ; [AMO 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:9-Amos8:10); ; [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10), [ZEC 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Zech13:6-Zech13:7)

Jesus Christ's predictions concerning the manner of his death
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 12:32–12:33](https://ref.ly/John12:32-John12:33)** *See also* [JHN 3:14](https://ref.ly/John3:14), [JHN 8:28](https://ref.ly/John8:28), [JHN 18:31–18:32](https://ref.ly/John18:31-John18:32)

Responsibility for the crucifixion ascribed to Jews and Gentiles alike
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:8)** *See also* [JHN 19:15](https://ref.ly/John19:15); [ACT 2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:23)

The crucifixion involved a curse
--------------------------------

**[DEU 21:22–21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22-Deut21:23)** *See also* [GEN 2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:17); [ACT 5:30](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30), [ACT 10:39](https://ref.ly/Acts10:39), [ACT 13:29](https://ref.ly/Acts13:29); [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13); [1PE 2:24](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:24)

The crucifixion involved shame
------------------------------

**[HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)** *See also* [1CO 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:23); [PHP 2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:8); [HEB 13:13](https://ref.ly/Heb13:13)

Believers are united with Jesus Christ in his crucifixion
---------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Gal2:20)** *See also* [ROM 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rom6:6)

Crucifixion illustrates discipleship
------------------------------------

**[MRK 8:34](https://ref.ly/Mark8:34)** parallels [MAT 16:24](https://ref.ly/Matt16:24), [LUK 9:23](https://ref.ly/Luke9:23) *See also* [MAT 10:38](https://ref.ly/Matt10:38); [LUK 14:27](https://ref.ly/Luke14:27); [GAL 5:24](https://ref.ly/Gal5:24), [GAL 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gal6:14)

## 5283: cup (id: 5283)

A drinking vessel, usually made of pottery but sometimes, in wealthy households especially, of gold or silver. Cups might take the form of goblets or of shallow bowls large enough for several people to share, as a sign of fellowship. The word is also used figuratively to refer to the portion of blessing or judgment divinely allotted to individuals or nations.

General references to drinking vessels
--------------------------------------

**[JER 35:5](https://ref.ly/Jer35:5)** *See also* [GEN 40:11](https://ref.ly/Gen40:11), [GEN 44:2–44:5](https://ref.ly/Gen44:2-Gen44:5); [PRO 23:31](https://ref.ly/Prov23:31); [SNG 7:2](https://ref.ly/Song7:2); [DAN 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Dan5:2-Dan5:3); [MAT 10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:42) parallel [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41)

Golden goblets as a sign of wealth
----------------------------------

**[1KI 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:21)** parallel [2CH 9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:20) *See also* [EST 1:7](https://ref.ly/Esth1:7)

Sharing a cup as a sign of fellowship
-------------------------------------

**[2SA 12:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:3)**

### The shared cup at the Lord's Supper

**[MAT 26:27](https://ref.ly/Matt26:27)** parallels [MRK 14:23](https://ref.ly/Mark14:23), [LUK 22:17](https://ref.ly/Luke22:17) *See also* [1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25)**[1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16)** *See also* [1CO 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:21)

Cups symbolising corrupt lives
------------------------------

**[MAT 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25-Matt23:26)** *The Pharisees observed ceremonial washings but neglected the need for inward cleansing.* *See also* [LUK 11:39](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39); [REV 17:4](https://ref.ly/Rev17:4)

The cup of God's wrath
----------------------

### The cup drunk by the nations

**[JER 25:15–25:17](https://ref.ly/Jer25:15-Jer25:17)** *See also* [PSA 75:8](https://ref.ly/Ps75:8); [JER 51:7](https://ref.ly/Jer51:7); [ZEC 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Zech12:2-Zech12:3); [REV 14:10](https://ref.ly/Rev14:10)JER [49:12](https://ref.ly/Rev49:12); [LAM 4:21](https://ref.ly/Lam4:21)HAB [2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:16); [REV 16:19](https://ref.ly/Rev16:19), [REV 18:6](https://ref.ly/Rev18:6)

### The cup drunk by God's people

**[EZK 23:32–23:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:32-Ezek23:34)**; **[ISA 51:17](https://ref.ly/Isa51:17)** *Following judgment, God promises to restore his people.* *See also* [ISA 51:22](https://ref.ly/Isa51:22)

### The cup drunk by Jesus Christ

**[JHN 18:11](https://ref.ly/John18:11)** *See also* [MAT 20:22–20:23](https://ref.ly/Matt20:22-Matt20:23) parallel [MRK 10:38–10:39](https://ref.ly/Mark10:38-Mark10:39)**[MAT 26:42](https://ref.ly/Matt26:42)** *The cup of God's wrath may pass from guilty humanity only if Jesus Christ drinks it.*[MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39) parallels [MRK 14:36](https://ref.ly/Mark14:36), [LUK 22:42](https://ref.ly/Luke22:42)

The cup of God's blessing
-------------------------

**[PSA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Ps16:5)** *See also* [PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5), [PSA 36:8](https://ref.ly/Ps36:8), [PSA 116:12–116:13](https://ref.ly/Ps116:12-Ps116:13)

## 5284: cupbearer (id: 5284)

One who served a king with wine, often having himself first tasted it for quality and as a safety measure. Some cupbearers became favourites of the king and so wielded considerable political influence.

Pharaoh's cupbearer
-------------------

### Imprisoned

**[GEN 40:1](https://ref.ly/Gen40:1), [GEN 40:5](https://ref.ly/Gen40:5)**

### His dream

*See also* [GEN 40:9–40:11](https://ref.ly/Gen40:9-Gen40:11)

### Dream interpreted by Joseph

[GEN 40:12–40:14](https://ref.ly/Gen40:12-Gen40:14)

### Restored to court

[GEN 40:21](https://ref.ly/Gen40:21)

### Forgetful of Joseph

[GEN 40:23](https://ref.ly/Gen40:23)

### Remembers Joseph

[GEN 41:9](https://ref.ly/Gen41:9)

Solomon's cupbearers
--------------------

[1KI 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:4-1Kgs10:5) parallel [2CH 9:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:4)

Artaxerxes' cupbearer, Nehemiah
-------------------------------

**[NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11), [NEH 2:1](https://ref.ly/Neh2:1)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 40:1; Genesis 40:5; Genesis 40:9-11; Genesis 40:12-14; Genesis 40:21; Genesis 40:23; Genesis 41:9; 1 Kings 10:4-5; 2 Chronicles 9:4; Nehemiah 1:11; Nehemiah 2:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1409: dream (ID: `1409@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5118: Solomon (ID: `5118@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5270: court (ID: `5270@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5283: cup (ID: `5283@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5344: imprisonment (ID: `5344@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5345: influence (ID: `5345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5460: prison (ID: `5460@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5523: servants, good (ID: `5523@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8670: remembering (ID: `8670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8763: forgetting (ID: `8763@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9165: restoration (ID: `9165@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5285: cures (id: 5285)

The removing of illness or other disorder, either mental or physical. Scripture describes the curing of various diseases and conditions. It also points to humanity's area of spiritual need, its sinful condition, declaring Jesus Christ to be the only effective cure.

God's curing of illness
-----------------------

**[JOB 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Job5:17-Job5:18)** *See also* [PSA 30:2](https://ref.ly/Ps30:2), [PSA 103:2–103:5](https://ref.ly/Ps103:2-Ps103:5), [PSA 147:3](https://ref.ly/Ps147:3); [JER 33:6](https://ref.ly/Jer33:6)

Illnesses and conditions cured by Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:4-Matt11:5)** parallel [LUK 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:21-Luke7:22) *See also* [ISA 35:5–35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:5-Isa35:6)

### Demon\-possession

[MAT 15:22–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22-Matt15:28); ; [MRK 5:1–5:20](https://ref.ly/Mark5:1-Mark5:20) parallel [LUK 8:26–8:39](https://ref.ly/Luke8:26-Luke8:39)

### Deafness and muteness

[MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22) parallel [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14); ; [MRK 7:31–7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:31-Mark7:37)

### Paralysis and lameness

[MAT 9:2–9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2-Matt9:8) parallels [MRK 2:3–2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:3-Mark2:12), [LUK 5:18–5:26](https://ref.ly/Luke5:18-Luke5:26); ; [JHN 5:1–5:14](https://ref.ly/John5:1-John5:14)

### Bleeding

[MRK 5:25–5:34](https://ref.ly/Mark5:25-Mark5:34)

### Blindness

[MRK 8:22–8:26](https://ref.ly/Mark8:22-Mark8:26); ; [JHN 9:1–9:7](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:7)

### Leprosy

[MAT 8:2–8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:4) parallels [MRK 1:40–1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40-Mark1:44), [LUK 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12-Luke5:14); ; [LUK 17:11–17:19](https://ref.ly/Luke17:11-Luke17:19)

Death itself is cured by Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------

**[JHN 11:1–11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:1-John11:44)** *See also* [MRK 5:35–5:43](https://ref.ly/Mark5:35-Mark5:43); [LUK 7:11–7:16](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11-Luke7:16)

Illnesses and conditions cured by the apostles
----------------------------------------------

**[ACT 5:12–5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:12-Acts5:16)** *See also* [ACT 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Acts3:1-Acts3:10), [ACT 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:11-Acts19:12)

Illnesses cured by the grace of God
-----------------------------------

[PHP 2:26–2:27](https://ref.ly/Phil2:26-Phil2:27) *Epaphroditus, Paul's fellow\-worker*; ; [JAS 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Jas5:15-Jas5:16) *the importance of prayer in curing illness*

Illnesses cured in the name of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------

[MRK 9:39](https://ref.ly/Mark9:39); ; [ACT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6), [ACT 3:16](https://ref.ly/Acts3:16), [ACT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Acts4:10), [ACT 4:30](https://ref.ly/Acts4:30)

Humanity's sinfulness cured by Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------

### Sinfulness affects all humanity

[ROM 3:23](https://ref.ly/Rom3:23)

### Prophecy of healing through Jesus Christ's death

**[ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5)**

### Jesus Christ's mission to cure humanity

**[MAT 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:12-Matt9:13)** parallels [MRK 2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:17), [LUK 5:31](https://ref.ly/Luke5:31) *See also* [LUK 19:10](https://ref.ly/Luke19:10)

## 5286: custom (id: 5286)

An established way or rule (often unwritten) of behaviour, either for an individual or in a society.

Examples of the customs of individuals
--------------------------------------

[1SA 27:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:11); ; [JOB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Job1:5); ; [MRK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Mark10:1); ; [LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16); ; [ACT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2)

Examples of group customs
-------------------------

[1SA 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:13); ; [1KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28); ; [LUK 1:9](https://ref.ly/Luke1:9); ; [ACT 16:20–16:21](https://ref.ly/Acts16:20-Acts16:21)

Examples of social customs
--------------------------

[GEN 29:26](https://ref.ly/Gen29:26) *The older daughter is married before the younger*; ; [JDG 8:24](https://ref.ly/Judg8:24) *The Ishmaelites wore gold ear\-rings*; ; [JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10) *wedding feasts*; ; [RUT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:7) *exchanging sandals as symbol of closing deals*; ; [1SA 20:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:25); [2KI 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:14); ; [EST 1:13](https://ref.ly/Esth1:13); [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17), [EZK 24:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:22); [MAT 27:15](https://ref.ly/Matt27:15) parallels [MRK 15:6](https://ref.ly/Mark15:6), [JHN 18:39](https://ref.ly/John18:39) *release of prisoners at Passover*; [LUK 2:27](https://ref.ly/Luke2:27), [LUK 2:42](https://ref.ly/Luke2:42); [JHN 19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:40) *burial customs*; ; [ACT 25:16](https://ref.ly/Acts25:16) *Roman legal customs*

A custom can commemorate a particular event
-------------------------------------------

[JDG 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Judg11:39-Judg11:40); ; [EST 9:27](https://ref.ly/Esth9:27)

God's people are not to follow improper customs
-----------------------------------------------

**[LEV 18:30](https://ref.ly/Lev18:30)** *See also* [LEV 20:23](https://ref.ly/Lev20:23); [2KI 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:7-2Kgs17:8), [2KI 17:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:19), [2KI 17:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:33), [2KI 17:40–17:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:40-2Kgs17:41); [EST 3:8](https://ref.ly/Esth3:8); [PSA 106:34–106:35](https://ref.ly/Ps106:34-Ps106:35), [PSA 106:40–106:41](https://ref.ly/Ps106:40-Ps106:41); [JER 10:2–10:3](https://ref.ly/Jer10:2-Jer10:3)

The issue of whether or not Gentile Christians should follow Jewish customs
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:14-Gal2:16)** *See also* [ACT 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11-Acts6:14), [ACT 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1-Acts15:2), [ACT 15:28–15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:28-Acts15:29), [ACT 21:21](https://ref.ly/Acts21:21), [ACT 21:24](https://ref.ly/Acts21:24), [ACT 28:17](https://ref.ly/Acts28:17); [GAL 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Gal4:9-Gal4:10); [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)

## 5287: dance (id: 5287)

Movement, usually in response to music, which is often a spontaneous expression of joy and delight in the Lord.

Dance as an expression of joy
-----------------------------

**[EXO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Exod15:20)**; **[PSA 30:11](https://ref.ly/Ps30:11)** *See also* [JDG 11:34](https://ref.ly/Judg11:34), [JDG 21:21–21:23](https://ref.ly/Judg21:21-Judg21:23); [1SA 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:7), [1SA 21:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:11), [1SA 29:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:5); [ECC 3:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:4); [SNG 6:13](https://ref.ly/Song6:13); [JER 31:4](https://ref.ly/Jer31:4), [JER 31:13](https://ref.ly/Jer31:13); [LAM 5:15](https://ref.ly/Lam5:15); [MAT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Matt11:17) parallel [LUK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Luke7:32); [MRK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22) *The daughter of Herodias dances for Herod*; [LUK 15:25](https://ref.ly/Luke15:25)

Dance as an expression of praise
--------------------------------

**[PSA 150:4](https://ref.ly/Ps150:4)** *See also* [PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3); [2SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14), [2SA 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:16); [1CH 15:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:29)

Dance as an aspect of pagan worship
-----------------------------------

**[1KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26)** *See also* [EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 15:20; Psalms 30:11; Judges 11:34; Judges 21:21-23; 1 Samuel 18:6-7; 1 Samuel 21:11; 1 Samuel 29:5; Ecclesiastes 3:4; Song of Songs 6:13; Jeremiah 31:4; Jeremiah 31:13; Lamentations 5:15; Matthew 11:17; Luke 7:32; Mark 6:22; Luke 15:25; Psalms 150:4; Psalms 149:3; 2 Samuel 6:14; 2 Samuel 6:16; 1 Chronicles 15:29; 1 Kings 18:26; Exodus 32:19
* **Associated Articles:** 5273: creativity (ID: `5273@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5420: music (ID: `5420@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8283: joy (ID: `8283@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8627: worship, elements of (ID: `8627@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8666: praise, manner and methods of (ID: `8666@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5288: dead, the (id: 5288)

Human beings who have died. Scripture provides illustrations of the manner in which the dead are to be buried, as well as explaining the doctrines of resurrection and judgment.

Preparation of the dead for burial
----------------------------------

### Washing

**[ACT 9:37](https://ref.ly/Acts9:37)** *A custom common to both Jews and Greeks.*

### Embalming

**[MAT 26:12](https://ref.ly/Matt26:12)** *See also* [GEN 50:2](https://ref.ly/Gen50:2), [GEN 50:26](https://ref.ly/Gen50:26); [2CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:14)

### Grave clothes

**[JHN 11:44](https://ref.ly/John11:44)** *See also* [MRK 15:46](https://ref.ly/Mark15:46) parallels [MAT 27:59](https://ref.ly/Matt27:59), [JHN 19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:40); [JHN 20:7](https://ref.ly/John20:7); [ACT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Acts5:6)

Provision for the dead
----------------------

### Burial

**[GEN 49:29–49:30](https://ref.ly/Gen49:29-Gen49:30)** *It was the normal practice for the people of God to be buried.* *See also* [JOS 24:30](https://ref.ly/Josh24:30); [RUT 1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:17); [1KI 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:15); [ACT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Acts8:2)

### Cremation

**[JOS 7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25)** *Cremation was not a Hebrew practice, except in cases of difficulty.* *See also* [1SA 31:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:12); [AMO 6:10](https://ref.ly/Amos6:10)

The plight of the unburied dead
-------------------------------

**[PSA 79:2](https://ref.ly/Ps79:2)** *To be unburied was a disgrace, a mark of judgment.* *See also* [ECC 6:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:3); [ISA 14:19](https://ref.ly/Isa14:19); [JER 7:33](https://ref.ly/Jer7:33), [JER 16:6](https://ref.ly/Jer16:6)

Personal contact with a dead body conferred uncleanness
-------------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 19:11](https://ref.ly/Num19:11)** *See also* [LEV 21:11](https://ref.ly/Lev21:11); [NUM 6:6](https://ref.ly/Num6:6)

The place of the dead
---------------------

### Sheol and Hades

**[EZK 31:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:16)** *Sheol is the OT term for the place of the dead; a grave, an underworld, a pit. Hades is the NT equivalent.* *See also* [2KI 21:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:26); [PSA 88:3–88:4](https://ref.ly/Ps88:3-Ps88:4), [PSA 89:48](https://ref.ly/Ps89:48), [PSA 116:3](https://ref.ly/Ps116:3); [ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10); [ISA 14:9–14:10](https://ref.ly/Isa14:9-Isa14:10); [ACT 2:31](https://ref.ly/Acts2:31)

### A place of judgment

**[PSA 49:13–49:14](https://ref.ly/Ps49:13-Ps49:14)** *See also* [PSA 31:17](https://ref.ly/Ps31:17), [PSA 88:3–88:5](https://ref.ly/Ps88:3-Ps88:5); [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14); [MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22), [MAT 11:23](https://ref.ly/Matt11:23) parallel [LUK 10:15](https://ref.ly/Luke10:15); [MAT 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18); [REV 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rev1:18), [REV 20:13–20:14](https://ref.ly/Rev20:13-Rev20:14)

### A place of deliverance

**[PSA 139:8](https://ref.ly/Ps139:8)** *See also* [PSA 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ps16:10), [PSA 30:3](https://ref.ly/Ps30:3), [PSA 86:13](https://ref.ly/Ps86:13), [PSA 107:20](https://ref.ly/Ps107:20); [HOS 13:14](https://ref.ly/Hos13:14); [JON 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:2); [ACT 2:27](https://ref.ly/Acts2:27)

The plight of the dead outside of Christ
----------------------------------------

### Physical death is the result of sin

**[ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)** *See also* [GEN 2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:17); [EZK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:20); [ROM 1:32](https://ref.ly/Rom1:32), [ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12), [ROM 8:6](https://ref.ly/Rom8:6); [JAS 1:15](https://ref.ly/Jas1:15)

### Final judgment will follow resurrection

**[REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8)** *The “second death” is the throwing of the lost into the lake of fire.* *See also* [MAT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28), [MAT 25:41](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41); [1JN 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/1John5:16-1John5:17); [JUD 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jude1:12)

Promises to the dead in Christ
------------------------------

### Death is but a sleep

**[1TH 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13-1Thess4:14)** *See also* [JHN 11:11–11:14](https://ref.ly/John11:11-John11:14); [1CO 15:17–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:17-1Cor15:22), [1CO 15:51–15:53](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:51-1Cor15:53)

### Death has no hold on believers

**[JHN 8:51](https://ref.ly/John8:51)** *See also* [JHN 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/John11:25-John11:26); [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39)

### Believers will be delivered from the second death

**[REV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rev2:11)** *See also* [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 9:37; Matthew 26:12; Genesis 50:2; Genesis 50:26; 2 Chronicles 16:14; John 11:44; Mark 15:46; Matthew 27:59; John 19:40; John 20:7; Acts 5:6; Genesis 49:29-30; Joshua 24:30; Ruth 1:17; 1 Kings 11:15; Acts 8:2; Joshua 7:25; 1 Samuel 31:12; Amos 6:10; Psalms 79:2; Ecclesiastes 6:3; Isaiah 14:19; Jeremiah 7:33; Jeremiah 16:6; Numbers 19:11; Leviticus 21:11; Numbers 6:6; Ezekiel 31:16; 2 Kings 21:26; Psalms 88:3-4; Psalms 89:48; Psalms 116:3; Ecclesiastes 9:10; Isaiah 14:9-10; Acts 2:31; Psalms 49:13-14; Psalms 31:17; Psalms 88:3-5; Nahum 1:14; Matthew 5:22; Matthew 11:23; Luke 10:15; Matthew 16:18; Revelation 1:18; Revelation 20:13-14; Psalms 139:8; Psalms 16:10; Psalms 30:3; Psalms 86:13; Psalms 107:20; Hosea 13:14; Jonah 2:2; Acts 2:27; Romans 6:23; Genesis 2:17; Ezekiel 18:20; Romans 1:32; Romans 5:12; Romans 8:6; James 1:15; Revelation 21:8; Matthew 10:28; Matthew 25:41; 1 John 5:16-17; Jude 1:12; 1 Thessalonians 4:13-14; John 11:11-14; 1 Corinthians 15:17-22; 1 Corinthians 15:51-53; John 8:51; John 11:25-26; Romans 8:38-39; Revelation 2:11; Revelation 20:6

## 5289: debt (id: 5289)

God is the Maker and sustainer of all things and therefore all creatures owe everything to him. Scripture provides guidance concerning both debt and debtors.

Everyone is in debt to God as Creator
-------------------------------------

**[1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7)** *See also* [DEU 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:17-Deut8:18); [1CH 29:11–29:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:11-1Chr29:13); [PSA 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Ps24:1-Ps24:2); [ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19); [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17), [ACT 17:25](https://ref.ly/Acts17:25); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

Everyone is in debt to God as law\-giver
----------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rom3:19)**; **[JAS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Jas2:10)** *See also* [DEU 27:26](https://ref.ly/Deut27:26); [PSA 49:7–49:8](https://ref.ly/Ps49:7-Ps49:8); [MAL 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:8-Mal3:10); [LUK 7:41–7:42](https://ref.ly/Luke7:41-Luke7:42); [GAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Gal3:10), [GAL 5:3](https://ref.ly/Gal5:3)

Believers are in debt to God as Redeemer
----------------------------------------

### He has paid their debt

**[GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13)** *See also* [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9); [1PE 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18); [REV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9)

### A sense of debt inspires devotion to God

**[PSA 116:12](https://ref.ly/Ps116:12)** *See also* [LUK 7:41–7:47](https://ref.ly/Luke7:41-Luke7:47); [ROM 8:12](https://ref.ly/Rom8:12); [1CO 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:20)

### Indebtedness to God affects attitude to others

**[MAT 10:8](https://ref.ly/Matt10:8)** *See also* [MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12) parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4); [MAT 18:21–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:35); [1JN 3:16](https://ref.ly/1John3:16), [1JN 4:11](https://ref.ly/1John4:11)

Examples of people in debt
--------------------------

[1SA 22:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:2); ; [2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1); ; [NEH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Neh5:4); ; [MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26), [MAT 18:23–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:35); ; [LUK 16:1–16:8](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1-Luke16:8); ; [ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Rom4:4); ; [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

Debts must be paid
------------------

[LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13); ; [2KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:7); ; [PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21); ; [ROM 13:7–13:8](https://ref.ly/Rom13:7-Rom13:8)

Social ills result from debt
----------------------------

[NEH 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:3-Neh5:5); ; [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3), [JOB 24:9](https://ref.ly/Job24:9); ; [PSA 109:11](https://ref.ly/Ps109:11); ; [EZK 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:12-Ezek18:13), [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12); ; [AMO 2:6](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6)

OT laws sought to minimise debt
-------------------------------

### By commanding generous lending to the poor

[EXO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25); ; [LEV 25:35–25:36](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:36); ; [DEU 15:7–15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:11); ; [PSA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:5); ; [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17)

### By curbing exploitation of debtors

[LEV 25:37–25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:37-Lev25:43); ; [DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6)

### By cancelling debts every seventh year

[DEU 15:1–15:3](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1-Deut15:3), [DEU 31:10](https://ref.ly/Deut31:10); ; [NEH 10:31](https://ref.ly/Neh10:31)

Preventatives against needless debt
-----------------------------------

### Keeping short accounts

[MAT 5:25](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25); ; [ROM 13:8](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8)

### Remembering the evil consequences

[PRO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Prov22:7); ; [MAT 5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:26)

Debts to the state
------------------

### Paying taxes

**[MAT 22:17–22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:17-Matt22:21)** parallels [MRK 12:14–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:14-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:22–20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:22-Luke20:25) *See also* [ROM 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6-Rom13:7)

### Civic honour

------------------

**[ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Rom4:4)** *See also* [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

Debts within the church
-----------------------

### Towards Christian workers

**[PHM 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:18-Phlm1:19)** *See also* [1CO 9:7–9:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7-1Cor9:12); [GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6)

### Towards the unsaved

### The indebtedness of the Gentile church towards the Jerusalem church

**[ROM 15:26–15:27](https://ref.ly/Rom15:26-Rom15:27)**

Debts to be cancelled
---------------------

**[MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12)** parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4) *See also* [1CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:5)

Debts one should never pay
--------------------------

**[ROM 12:17](https://ref.ly/Rom12:17)** *See also* [1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9)

A debt one can never cease paying
---------------------------------

**[ROM 13:8–13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8-Rom13:10)**

## 5290: defeat (id: 5290)

The overcoming of or being overcome by a physical, spiritual or circumstantial enemy. Those humbled before God by such a defeat eventually come to see it as a positive experience, leading to increased spiritual maturity. The way to avoid being defeated is to trust in God and to wear the spiritual armour he provides.

Defeat as the overcoming of enemies
-----------------------------------

### Physical enemies such as armies

**[GEN 14:5–14:6](https://ref.ly/Gen14:5-Gen14:6)** *See also* [GEN 14:15](https://ref.ly/Gen14:15), [GEN 36:35](https://ref.ly/Gen36:35); [DEU 1:4](https://ref.ly/Deut1:4), [DEU 4:46](https://ref.ly/Deut4:46), [DEU 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:2), [DEU 29:7](https://ref.ly/Deut29:7); [JOS 10:33](https://ref.ly/Josh10:33), [JOS 11:1–11:9](https://ref.ly/Josh11:1-Josh11:9); [JDG 7:19–7:22](https://ref.ly/Judg7:19-Judg7:22); [1SA 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:10), [1SA 11:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:11), [1SA 14:48](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:48), [1SA 17:50](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:50); [2SA 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:17), [2SA 5:17–5:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:17-2Sam5:25) parallel [1CH 14:8–14:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:8-1Chr14:17); [PSA 92:11](https://ref.ly/Ps92:11); [JER 37:10](https://ref.ly/Jer37:10); [ZEC 9:15](https://ref.ly/Zech9:15); [HEB 7:1](https://ref.ly/Heb7:1), [HEB 11:34](https://ref.ly/Heb11:34)

### Spiritual enemies such as Satan

**[GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15)** *See also* [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Luke10:19); [JHN 12:31](https://ref.ly/John12:31), [JHN 14:30](https://ref.ly/John14:30); [ROM 16:20](https://ref.ly/Rom16:20); [COL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:15); [2TH 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8); [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14); [1JN 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1John2:13-1John2:14), [1JN 3:8](https://ref.ly/1John3:8); [REV 12:11](https://ref.ly/Rev12:11), [REV 15:2](https://ref.ly/Rev15:2), [REV 17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:14), [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10)

### The world as the realm of sin

**[JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33)** *See also* [ROM 8:37](https://ref.ly/Rom8:37), [ROM 12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:21); [1JN 4:4](https://ref.ly/1John4:4), [1JN 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1John5:4-1John5:5); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7), [REV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rev2:11), [REV 2:17](https://ref.ly/Rev2:17), [REV 2:26](https://ref.ly/Rev2:26), [REV 3:5](https://ref.ly/Rev3:5), [REV 3:12](https://ref.ly/Rev3:12), [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21), [REV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5), [REV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Rev21:7)

Defeat as being overcome by enemies
-----------------------------------

### By physical enemies

**[LEV 26:17](https://ref.ly/Lev26:17)** *The Israelites were often defeated by their enemies as a punishment for disobeying God.* *See also* [NUM 14:41–14:43](https://ref.ly/Num14:41-Num14:43); [DEU 1:42](https://ref.ly/Deut1:42), [DEU 28:25](https://ref.ly/Deut28:25); [JOS 7:4](https://ref.ly/Josh7:4); [JDG 2:15](https://ref.ly/Judg2:15), [JDG 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Judg3:12-Judg3:14); [1SA 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:1-1Sam4:11); [1KI 8:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:33) parallel [2CH 6:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:24); [PSA 13:4](https://ref.ly/Ps13:4), [PSA 44:9–44:12](https://ref.ly/Ps44:9-Ps44:12), [PSA 78:62](https://ref.ly/Ps78:62); [JER 19:7–19:9](https://ref.ly/Jer19:7-Jer19:9), [JER 21:7](https://ref.ly/Jer21:7)

### By spiritual enemies

**[JAS 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jas1:13-Jas1:15)** *See also* [GEN 3:6–3:22](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6-Gen3:22); [2SA 11:2–11:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:17); [1CO 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:7); [1TH 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:18), [1TH 3:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:5); [1TI 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:11); [2PE 2:20](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:20)

### By circumstances

**[PSA 39:10](https://ref.ly/Ps39:10)** *This refers to God's chastisement through affliction*; **[PSA 116:3](https://ref.ly/Ps116:3)** *See also* [PSA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:4), [PSA 38:2](https://ref.ly/Ps38:2); [2SA 22:5–22:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:5-2Sam22:6) parallel [PSA 18:4–18:5](https://ref.ly/Ps18:4-Ps18:5); [1KI 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:4); [JOB 36:8–36:9](https://ref.ly/Job36:8-Job36:9); [JER 23:9](https://ref.ly/Jer23:9); [DAN 10:16](https://ref.ly/Dan10:16); [LUK 8:37](https://ref.ly/Luke8:37); [ROM 12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:21)

Defeat as a positive experience
-------------------------------

**[JAS 4:10](https://ref.ly/Jas4:10)** *See also* [2CH 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14-2Chr7:15); [PSA 119:67](https://ref.ly/Ps119:67), [PSA 119:71](https://ref.ly/Ps119:71); [2CO 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7-2Cor12:10); [HEB 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/Heb12:9-Heb12:11); [1PE 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:6)

Defeat can be avoided
---------------------

### By trusting in God

**[PSA 25:1–25:3](https://ref.ly/Ps25:1-Ps25:3)** *See also* [2CH 13:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:18); [PSA 22:5](https://ref.ly/Ps22:5), [PSA 31:1–31:4](https://ref.ly/Ps31:1-Ps31:4) parallel [PSA 71:1–71:3](https://ref.ly/Ps71:1-Ps71:3); [PSA 112:6–112:8](https://ref.ly/Ps112:6-Ps112:8), [PSA 125:1](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1); [ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3); [ROM 9:33](https://ref.ly/Rom9:33), [ROM 10:11](https://ref.ly/Rom10:11); [1PE 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:6)

### By wearing the spiritual armour God provides

**[EPH 6:10–6:18](https://ref.ly/Eph6:10-Eph6:18)** *See also* [ROM 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12); [1TH 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 14:5-6; Genesis 14:15; Genesis 36:35; Deuteronomy 1:4; Deuteronomy 4:46; Deuteronomy 7:1-2; Deuteronomy 29:7; Joshua 10:33; Joshua 11:1-9; Judges 7:19-22; 1 Samuel 7:10; 1 Samuel 11:11; 1 Samuel 14:48; 1 Samuel 17:50; 2 Samuel 2:17; 2 Samuel 5:17-25; 1 Chronicles 14:8-17; Psalms 92:11; Jeremiah 37:10; Zechariah 9:15; Hebrews 7:1; Hebrews 11:34; Genesis 3:15; Luke 10:19; John 12:31; John 14:30; Romans 16:20; Colossians 2:15; 2 Thessalonians 2:8; Hebrews 2:14; 1 John 2:13-14; 1 John 3:8; Revelation 12:11; Revelation 15:2; Revelation 17:14; Revelation 20:10; John 16:33; Romans 8:37; Romans 12:21; 1 John 4:4; 1 John 5:4-5; Revelation 2:7; Revelation 2:11; Revelation 2:17; Revelation 2:26; Revelation 3:5; Revelation 3:12; Revelation 3:21; Revelation 5:5; Leviticus 26:17; Numbers 14:41-43; Deuteronomy 1:42; Deuteronomy 28:25; Joshua 7:4; Judges 2:15; Judges 3:12-14; 1 Samuel 4:1-11; 1 Kings 8:33; 2 Chronicles 6:24; Psalms 13:4; Psalms 44:9-12; Psalms 78:62; Jeremiah 19:7-9; Jeremiah 21:7; James 1:13-15; Genesis 3:6-22; 2 Samuel 11:2-17; 1 Corinthians 6:7; 1 Thessalonians 2:18; 1 Thessalonians 3:5; 1 Timothy 5:11; 2 Peter 2:20; Psalms 39:10; Psalms 116:3; Psalms 32:4; Psalms 38:2; 2 Samuel 22:5-6; Psalms 18:4-5; 1 Kings 19:1-4; Job 36:8-9; Jeremiah 23:9; Daniel 10:16; Luke 8:37; James 4:10; 2 Chronicles 7:14-15; Psalms 119:67; Psalms 119:71; 2 Corinthians 12:7-10; Hebrews 12:9-11; 1 Peter 5:6; Psalms 25:1-3; 2 Chronicles 13:18; Psalms 22:5; Psalms 31:1-4; Psalms 71:1-3; Psalms 112:6-8; Psalms 125:1; Isaiah 26:3; Romans 9:33; Romans 10:11; 1 Peter 2:6; Ephesians 6:10-18; Romans 13:12; 1 Thessalonians 5:8

## 5291: defence (id: 5291)

A means of protection and security against the attacks of others. Scripture insists that the most reliable form of defence for believers is trust in the Lord.

* **Associated Articles:** 5292: defence, divine (ID: `5292@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5293: defence, human (ID: `5293@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5292: defence, divine (id: 5292)

God defends his people, especially the most vulnerable. Because of God's defence, although Christians sometimes face trials and persecutions their eternal security is never threatened.

God defends his people
----------------------

**[ISA 41:10–41:12](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10-Isa41:12)** *See also* [EXO 23:20](https://ref.ly/Exod23:20), [EXO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Exod23:27); [1SA 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9); [2KI 6:16–6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:16-2Kgs6:17); [PSA 34:7](https://ref.ly/Ps34:7), [PSA 144:10](https://ref.ly/Ps144:10); [ISA 19:20](https://ref.ly/Isa19:20), [ISA 51:16](https://ref.ly/Isa51:16), [ISA 51:22](https://ref.ly/Isa51:22), [ISA 54:16–54:17](https://ref.ly/Isa54:16-Isa54:17); [JER 50:34](https://ref.ly/Jer50:34), [JER 51:36](https://ref.ly/Jer51:36); [ZEC 9:8](https://ref.ly/Zech9:8); [MAT 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18); [JHN 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:29); [2TI 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16-2Tim4:18)

God defends especially the most vulnerable of his people
--------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Ps10:17-Ps10:18)** *See also* [DEU 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:17-Deut10:18); [PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5), [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); [PRO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10-Prov23:11)

God defended the city of Jerusalem
----------------------------------

**[2KI 19:32–19:34](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:32-2Kgs19:34)** parallel [ISA 37:33–37:35](https://ref.ly/Isa37:33-Isa37:35) *See also* [2KI 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:6) parallel [ISA 38:6](https://ref.ly/Isa38:6); [PSA 48:3](https://ref.ly/Ps48:3); [ISA 31:5](https://ref.ly/Isa31:5); [ZEC 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Zech2:4-Zech2:5), [ZEC 12:8](https://ref.ly/Zech12:8)

God's defence of his people is pictured in various ways
-------------------------------------------------------

### As a hiding\-place

**[PSA 32:7](https://ref.ly/Ps32:7)** *See also* [PSA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8), [PSA 27:5](https://ref.ly/Ps27:5), [PSA 31:20](https://ref.ly/Ps31:20)

### As a refuge

**[PSA 57:1](https://ref.ly/Ps57:1)** *See also* [DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27); [PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1); [PRO 14:26](https://ref.ly/Prov14:26), [PRO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Prov18:10); [JOL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Joel3:16); [NAM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Nah1:7)

### As a shield

**[PSA 115:9–115:11](https://ref.ly/Ps115:9-Ps115:11)** *See also* [GEN 15:1](https://ref.ly/Gen15:1); [PSA 84:11](https://ref.ly/Ps84:11), [PSA 119:114](https://ref.ly/Ps119:114); [PRO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Prov30:5)

### As a combination of images

**[2SA 22:1–22:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:1-2Sam22:4)** parallel [PSA 18:2–18:3](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2-Ps18:3) *See also* [PSA 71:3](https://ref.ly/Ps71:3), [PSA 91:1–91:4](https://ref.ly/Ps91:1-Ps91:4), [PSA 144:1–144:2](https://ref.ly/Ps144:1-Ps144:2); [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4)

The most reliable defence for God's people is to trust only in him
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1)** *See also* [2CH 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:7-2Chr16:9); [PSA 20:6–20:8](https://ref.ly/Ps20:6-Ps20:8), [PSA 37:16–37:19](https://ref.ly/Ps37:16-Ps37:19), [PSA 125:1–125:2](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1-Ps125:2); [ISA 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Isa17:7-Isa17:8); [DAN 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:16-Dan3:18); [HOS 14:3](https://ref.ly/Hos14:3)

Examples of prayers for God's defence
-------------------------------------

**[PSA 25:20](https://ref.ly/Ps25:20)** *See also* [2KI 19:14–19:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:14-2Kgs19:19) parallel [ISA 37:14–37:20](https://ref.ly/Isa37:14-Isa37:20); [2CH 20:10–20:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:10-2Chr20:12), [2CH 32:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:17), [2CH 32:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:20); [PSA 35:23](https://ref.ly/Ps35:23), [PSA 74:22](https://ref.ly/Ps74:22), [PSA 86:2](https://ref.ly/Ps86:2)

Because of God's defence the eternal security of believers is never threatened
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:5)** *See also* [MAT 10:28–10:31](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28-Matt10:31) parallel [LUK 12:4–12:7](https://ref.ly/Luke12:4-Luke12:7); [JHN 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:29), [JHN 17:15](https://ref.ly/John17:15); [ROM 8:31–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:31-Rom8:39); [2TI 4:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:18); [HEB 11:32–11:39](https://ref.ly/Heb11:32-Heb11:39); [1PE 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8-1Pet5:10); [JUD 1:24](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10), [REV 7:13–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:13-Rev7:17)

## 5293: defence, human (id: 5293)

God's people are to defend the needy and those in their care. They are also to defend the truth entrusted to them.

Defence of the needy
--------------------

### God commands his people to defend the needy

**[PRO 31:8–31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:8-Prov31:9)** *See also* [ISA 1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:17), [ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2), [ISA 58:6–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:7), [ISA 58:9–58:10](https://ref.ly/Isa58:9-Isa58:10)

### Examples of those who defended the needy

**[PSA 72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:4)** [EXO 2:17](https://ref.ly/Exod2:17); ; [ACT 7:24](https://ref.ly/Acts7:24); [RUT 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:15-Ruth2:16) *Boaz*

### Neglect of the cause of the needy is condemned

**[PSA 82:2–82:4](https://ref.ly/Ps82:2-Ps82:4)** *See also* [JOB 5:4](https://ref.ly/Job5:4); [ISA 1:23–1:24](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23-Isa1:24), [ISA 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:20-Isa29:21); [JER 5:26–5:29](https://ref.ly/Jer5:26-Jer5:29), [JER 22:15–22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:15-Jer22:17)

Church leaders are to defend the flock of God
---------------------------------------------

**[ACT 20:28–20:31](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28-Acts20:31)** *See also* [JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17); [1TI 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:6); [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11); [HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17); [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

Christians are urged to defend the faith
----------------------------------------

### It is the responsibility of all believers

**[JUD 1:3](https://ref.ly/Jude1:3)** *See also* [PHP 1:27–1:28](https://ref.ly/Phil1:27-Phil1:28); [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15)

### It is the responsibility of Christian leaders in particular

**[2TI 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:13-2Tim1:14)** *See also* [1TI 1:3–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:10), [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21); [TIT 1:12–1:14](https://ref.ly/Titus1:12-Titus1:14)

### Examples of leaders who defended the faith

**[PHP 1:16](https://ref.ly/Phil1:16)** *See also* [ACT 6:8–6:10](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8-Acts6:10) *Stephen*; [ACT 18:27–18:28](https://ref.ly/Acts18:27-Acts18:28) *Apollos*[GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9), [GAL 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:14); [PHP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 31:8-9; Isaiah 1:17; Isaiah 10:1-2; Isaiah 58:6-7; Isaiah 58:9-10; Psalms 72:4; Exodus 2:17; Acts 7:24; Ruth 2:15-16; Psalms 82:2-4; Job 5:4; Isaiah 1:23-24; Isaiah 29:20-21; Jeremiah 5:26-29; Jeremiah 22:15-17; Acts 20:28-31; John 21:15-17; 1 Timothy 4:6; Titus 1:10-11; Hebrews 13:17; 1 Peter 5:2; Jude 1:3; Philippians 1:27-28; 2 Thessalonians 2:15; 2 Timothy 1:13-14; 1 Timothy 1:3-10; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; Titus 1:12-14; Philippians 1:16; Acts 6:8-10; Acts 18:27-28; Galatians 1:6-9; Galatians 2:11-14; Philippians 1:7

## 5295: destruction (id: 5295)

The demolition or eradication of buildings, peoples or individuals. Since God is the Creator and sustainer of all things, wanton ruin is contrary to his will as his original purposes cannot be fulfilled. God will bring about the final destruction of all that is evil in the last judgment.

God disapproves of wanton destruction
-------------------------------------

### Of human life

**[EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13)** parallel [DEU 5:17](https://ref.ly/Deut5:17) *See also* [GEN 4:8–4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8-Gen4:12), [GEN 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:5-Gen9:6); [2SA 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:7-2Sam12:10)

### Of the world in general

**[REV 11:18](https://ref.ly/Rev11:18)** [GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28), [GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15); ; [DEU 20:19–20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:19-Deut20:20)

### Of spiritual life

**[ROM 14:15](https://ref.ly/Rom14:15)** *See also* [1CO 8:9–8:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9-1Cor8:11); [2TI 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:17-2Tim2:18); [2PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1)

Agents of destruction
---------------------

### Satan

[JOB 1:12–1:19](https://ref.ly/Job1:12-Job1:19); ; [JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44), [JHN 10:10](https://ref.ly/John10:10)

### The wicked

[PSA 55:11](https://ref.ly/Ps55:11); ; [PRO 1:10–1:12](https://ref.ly/Prov1:10-Prov1:12); ; [LUK 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Luke13:1-Luke13:3)

### Nature

[MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19); ; [LUK 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/Luke13:4-Luke13:5)

God himself brings destruction on the wicked
--------------------------------------------

### God decrees destruction for sinners

**[PSA 37:38](https://ref.ly/Ps37:38)** *See also* [GEN 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5-Gen6:7); [PSA 5:6](https://ref.ly/Ps5:6), [PSA 46:8](https://ref.ly/Ps46:8); [ISA 34:2](https://ref.ly/Isa34:2); [AMO 1:3–1:8](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3-Amos1:8), [AMO 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Amos2:1-Amos2:3); [LUK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:9); [1JN 3:8](https://ref.ly/1John3:8)

### By the forces of nature

**[GEN 7:4](https://ref.ly/Gen7:4)** *See also* [GEN 19:24–19:25](https://ref.ly/Gen19:24-Gen19:25); [DEU 28:15–28:24](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:24); [PSA 48:7](https://ref.ly/Ps48:7); [EZK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:16), [EZK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:13); [LUK 17:29](https://ref.ly/Luke17:29); [REV 6:12–6:14](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:14)

### By means of angels

**[1CO 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:10)** *See also* [EXO 12:23](https://ref.ly/Exod12:23); [2SA 24:15–24:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:15-2Sam24:17) parallel [1CH 21:14–21:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:14-1Chr21:17); [1CH 21:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:12); [2KI 19:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:35) parallels [2CH 32:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:21), [ISA 37:36](https://ref.ly/Isa37:36); [ACT 12:23](https://ref.ly/Acts12:23); [REV 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:2-Rev7:3)

### By means of human beings

**[DEU 7:16](https://ref.ly/Deut7:16)** *See also* [NUM 21:3](https://ref.ly/Num21:3); [DEU 2:34](https://ref.ly/Deut2:34), [DEU 3:6](https://ref.ly/Deut3:6), [DEU 28:47–28:48](https://ref.ly/Deut28:47-Deut28:48); [JOS 6:21](https://ref.ly/Josh6:21), [JOS 8:26](https://ref.ly/Josh8:26), [JOS 11:20](https://ref.ly/Josh11:20); [JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9)

### Destruction in the final judgment

**[2TH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:8-2Thess1:9)** *See also* [MAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mal4:1); [2TH 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8); [REV 17:8](https://ref.ly/Rev17:8), [REV 17:11](https://ref.ly/Rev17:11), [REV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Rev18:21), [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10), [REV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Rev20:14)

### Destruction is sudden

**[1TH 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:3)** *See also* [PRO 6:15](https://ref.ly/Prov6:15), [PRO 24:21–24:22](https://ref.ly/Prov24:21-Prov24:22), [PRO 29:1](https://ref.ly/Prov29:1); [ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12); [ISA 30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:13), [ISA 47:11](https://ref.ly/Isa47:11); [JER 15:8](https://ref.ly/Jer15:8); [2PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10)

### Warnings against the way of destruction

**[MAT 7:13](https://ref.ly/Matt7:13)** *See also* [JOS 6:18](https://ref.ly/Josh6:18); [1TI 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9)

God keeps his people from destruction
-------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:13](https://ref.ly/Exod12:13)** *See also* [PSA 91:9–91:10](https://ref.ly/Ps91:9-Ps91:10); [ROM 8:35–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:39)

God's kingdom is indestructible
-------------------------------

**[HEB 12:28](https://ref.ly/Heb12:28)** *See also* [PSA 46:5](https://ref.ly/Ps46:5), [PSA 125:1](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1); [DAN 2:44](https://ref.ly/Dan2:44)

There will be no destruction in the world to come
-------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:9)** *See also* [DAN 7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:27); [REV 21:3–21:5](https://ref.ly/Rev21:3-Rev21:5), [REV 22:15](https://ref.ly/Rev22:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:13; Deuteronomy 5:17; Genesis 4:8-12; Genesis 9:5-6; 2 Samuel 12:7-10; Revelation 11:18; Genesis 1:28; Genesis 2:15; Deuteronomy 20:19-20; Romans 14:15; 1 Corinthians 8:9-11; 2 Timothy 2:17-18; 2 Peter 2:1; Job 1:12-19; John 8:44; John 10:10; Psalms 55:11; Proverbs 1:10-12; Luke 13:1-3; Matthew 6:19; Luke 13:4-5; Psalms 37:38; Genesis 6:5-7; Psalms 5:6; Psalms 46:8; Isaiah 34:2; Amos 1:3-8; Amos 2:1-3; Luke 3:9; 1 John 3:8; Genesis 7:4; Genesis 19:24-25; Deuteronomy 28:15-24; Psalms 48:7; Ezekiel 5:16; Ezekiel 13:13; Luke 17:29; Revelation 6:12-14; 1 Corinthians 10:10; Exodus 12:23; 2 Samuel 24:15-17; 1 Chronicles 21:14-17; 1 Chronicles 21:12; 2 Kings 19:35; 2 Chronicles 32:21; Isaiah 37:36; Acts 12:23; Revelation 7:2-3; Deuteronomy 7:16; Numbers 21:3; Deuteronomy 2:34; Deuteronomy 3:6; Deuteronomy 28:47-48; Joshua 6:21; Joshua 8:26; Joshua 11:20; Jeremiah 25:9; 2 Thessalonians 1:8-9; Malachi 4:1; 2 Thessalonians 2:8; Revelation 17:8; Revelation 17:11; Revelation 18:21; Revelation 20:10; Revelation 20:14; 1 Thessalonians 5:3; Proverbs 6:15; Proverbs 24:21-22; Proverbs 29:1; Ecclesiastes 9:12; Isaiah 30:13; Isaiah 47:11; Jeremiah 15:8; 2 Peter 3:10; Matthew 7:13; Joshua 6:18; 1 Timothy 6:9; Exodus 12:13; Psalms 91:9-10; Romans 8:35-39; Hebrews 12:28; Psalms 46:5; Psalms 125:1; Daniel 2:44; Isaiah 11:9; Daniel 7:27; Revelation 21:3-5; Revelation 22:15

## 5296: disabilities (id: 5296)

Physical incapacities resulting from physical or spiritual causes.

Examples of disabilities
------------------------

### Barrenness

[GEN 11:30](https://ref.ly/Gen11:30), [GEN 25:21](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21); ; [MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12)

### Blindness

[MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22), [MAT 15:30](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30), [MAT 20:30](https://ref.ly/Matt20:30); ; [MRK 8:22](https://ref.ly/Mark8:22); ; [JHN 9:1](https://ref.ly/John9:1)

### Deafness

[PSA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Ps38:13); ; [MRK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Mark7:32)

### Muteness

[PSA 38:13](https://ref.ly/Ps38:13); ; [MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22); ; [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14)

### Lameness

[LEV 21:18](https://ref.ly/Lev21:18); ; [2SA 5:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6); ; [MAT 15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:31), [MAT 21:14](https://ref.ly/Matt21:14); ; [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8)

Causes of disabilities
----------------------

### God's judgment

[DEU 28:28](https://ref.ly/Deut28:28)

### Accident

[2SA 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:4); ; [2KI 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:2)

### Old age

[GEN 27:1](https://ref.ly/Gen27:1); ; [1SA 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:15); ; [2SA 19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:35); ; [PSA 71:9](https://ref.ly/Ps71:9); ; [ECC 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:3-Eccl12:4); ; [ZEC 8:4](https://ref.ly/Zech8:4); ; [HEB 11:21](https://ref.ly/Heb11:21)

### Congenital defects

[MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12); ; [JHN 9:1](https://ref.ly/John9:1); ; [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8)

### Cruelty

[JDG 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Judg1:6-Judg1:7), [JDG 16:21](https://ref.ly/Judg16:21); ; [1SA 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:2); ; [2KI 25:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:7); ; [EZK 23:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:25)

### Illness

[1KI 15:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:23) parallel [2CH 16:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:12); ; [GAL 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Gal4:13-Gal4:15)

### Acts of God

[GEN 19:11](https://ref.ly/Gen19:11), [GEN 32:25](https://ref.ly/Gen32:25), [GEN 32:31–32:32](https://ref.ly/Gen32:31-Gen32:32); ; [LEV 26:16](https://ref.ly/Lev26:16); ; [1KI 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:4); ; [2KI 6:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:18), [2KI 15:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:5); ; [2CH 21:18–21:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:18-2Chr21:19), [2CH 26:19–26:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19-2Chr26:21); ; [LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20); ; [ACT 9:8](https://ref.ly/Acts9:8) *Saul's blindness*; ; [ACT 13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:11), [ACT 22:11](https://ref.ly/Acts22:11)

### Acts of evil spirits

[MAT 9:32–9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32-Matt9:33), [MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22); ; [MRK 9:17](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17), [MRK 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25-Mark9:26); ; [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14), [LUK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Luke13:11), [LUK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Luke13:16)

Unfavourable aspects of disability
----------------------------------

### Unfair advantage taken of the disabled

[LEV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Lev19:14); ; [2SA 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:26); ; [JHN 5:7](https://ref.ly/John5:7)

### Disabilities restrict service for God

[LEV 21:16–21:23](https://ref.ly/Lev21:16-Lev21:23); ; [2SA 19:31–19:37](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:31-2Sam19:37)

Favourable aspects of disabilities
----------------------------------

### Prayer to the Lord

## 5297: disease (id: 5297)

An illness or other condition which prevents people or animals from achieving their full potential or adversely affects their abilities.

----------------------------------------

## 5298: doctors (id: 5298)

People who are qualified in medicine and treat those who are ill. Doctors do not feature extensively in Scripture; rather God is portrayed as the source of health and healing for his people. Prophets were sometimes intermediaries in healing and priests were responsible for diagnosing certain diseases in accordance with the law. Luke, the author of the Gospel of that name and Acts, was a doctor.

God as the source of physical health and healing
------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 15:26](https://ref.ly/Exod15:26)** *See also* [NUM 12:10–12:15](https://ref.ly/Num12:10-Num12:15), [NUM 21:4–21:9](https://ref.ly/Num21:4-Num21:9); [DEU 32:39](https://ref.ly/Deut32:39); [PSA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2)

God as the source of spiritual healing
--------------------------------------

**[JOB 5:18](https://ref.ly/Job5:18)** *See also* [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5); [JER 33:6](https://ref.ly/Jer33:6); [HOS 6:1](https://ref.ly/Hos6:1)

Jesus Christ as a healer
------------------------

**[MAT 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Matt8:16-Matt8:17)** parallels [MRK 1:32–1:34](https://ref.ly/Mark1:32-Mark1:34), [LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40) *See also* [ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4); [MAT 4:24](https://ref.ly/Matt4:24), [MAT 14:14](https://ref.ly/Matt14:14) parallel [LUK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Luke9:11)

[2KI 5:1–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:1-2Kgs5:14), [2KI 20:1–20:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:1-2Kgs20:7) parallels [2CH 32:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:24), [ISA 38:1–38:5](https://ref.ly/Isa38:1-Isa38:5); ; [ISA 38:21](https://ref.ly/Isa38:21)

## 5299: door (id: 5299)

A point of entry, especially to a house. The door\-frame was given special significance within the law of Moses, and temple doorkeepers had a special status. Used figuratively, it relates to opportunity, and Jesus Christ used it of himself as the means of access to God.

The function of doors: gaining or barring entry
-----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 24:7](https://ref.ly/Ps24:7)** *See also* [GEN 19:6](https://ref.ly/Gen19:6), [GEN 19:9–19:11](https://ref.ly/Gen19:9-Gen19:11); [EXO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22); [2SA 13:17–13:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:17-2Sam13:18); [2KI 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:4-2Kgs4:5) *Elisha and the widow's oil*; [NEH 13:19](https://ref.ly/Neh13:19); [PSA 24:9](https://ref.ly/Ps24:9); [PRO 5:8](https://ref.ly/Prov5:8) *warning against adultery*; [MAT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:6) *Jesus Christ advocates secret prayer*; [MRK 1:33](https://ref.ly/Mark1:33); [LUK 11:7](https://ref.ly/Luke11:7); [JHN 18:15–18:16](https://ref.ly/John18:15-John18:16); [ACT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Acts5:19); [REV 3:8](https://ref.ly/Rev3:8)

-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 84:10](https://ref.ly/Ps84:10)** *See also* [2KI 25:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:18) parallel [JER 52:24](https://ref.ly/Jer52:24); [1CH 15:23–15:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:23-1Chr15:24); [2CH 23:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:19), [2CH 34:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:13)

The door and its surroundings as a symbol of a household
--------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22-Exod12:23)** *See also* [EXO 12:7](https://ref.ly/Exod12:7), [EXO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:6); [DEU 6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:9), [DEU 11:20](https://ref.ly/Deut11:20), [DEU 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Deut15:16-Deut15:17), [DEU 22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:21); [ISA 57:8](https://ref.ly/Isa57:8); [EZK 45:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:19)

Figurative use of opening a door
--------------------------------

### The door as a symbol of opportunity

**[1CO 16:8–16:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:8-1Cor16:9)** *See also* [GEN 4:7](https://ref.ly/Gen4:7); [HOS 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hos2:15); [ACT 14:27](https://ref.ly/Acts14:27); [COL 4:3](https://ref.ly/Col4:3); [REV 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Rev3:7-Rev3:8)

### Opening the door as a symbol of allowing Jesus Christ into one's life

**[REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20)** *See also* [LUK 12:35–12:36](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35-Luke12:36)

### The door opened in answer to prayer

**[MAT 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Matt7:7-Matt7:8)** parallel [LUK 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Luke11:9-Luke11:10)

### Jesus Christ as the means of access to God

**[JHN 10:7](https://ref.ly/John10:7)** *“Door” is sometimes used as a synonym for “gate”.* *See also* [JHN 10:9](https://ref.ly/John10:9)

### The door is indicative of imminent arrival

[MAT 24:33](https://ref.ly/Matt24:33) parallel [MRK 13:29](https://ref.ly/Mark13:29)

## 5302: education (id: 5302)

The imparting of spiritual, intellectual, moral and social instruction, the basis for which is the fear of the LORD. People continue learning throughout the whole of life. The education of children in the OT was centred on the home and synagogue and was largely of a religious and ethical nature. In the NT, children were included in the community of the church, but the home was still the main sphere of their education.

The fear of the LORD is the basis for all education
---------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 111:10](https://ref.ly/Ps111:10)** *See also* [2CH 26:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:5); [PSA 25:12](https://ref.ly/Ps25:12); [PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7), [PRO 2:1–2:8](https://ref.ly/Prov2:1-Prov2:8), [PRO 9:10](https://ref.ly/Prov9:10), [PRO 15:33](https://ref.ly/Prov15:33); [ISA 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1-Isa11:3)

Faith is an essential element of Christian education
----------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 11:3](https://ref.ly/Heb11:3)** *See also* [2TI 3:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:15)

Education should not oppose the truth about God
-----------------------------------------------

**[2CO 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:5)** *See also* [MAT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Matt18:6); [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21)

Education should emphasise that the world has a richness and coherence derived from God
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 24:1](https://ref.ly/Ps24:1)**; **[JHN 1:3](https://ref.ly/John1:3)** *See also* [JOB 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/Job12:7-Job12:10); [PSA 104:5–104:26](https://ref.ly/Ps104:5-Ps104:26); [1CO 10:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:26); [COL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:17); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3)

---------------------

## 5303: embalming (id: 5303)

The Jewish custom of preparing a body for the grave. However, the technically correct use of the term describes the removal of the internal organs of the dead and filling the cavity with spices. This originated with the Egyptians but was not practised by the Jews. Biblical teaching concerning life after death makes embalming unnecessary.

Jacob and Joseph were embalmed in Egypt
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 50:2–50:3](https://ref.ly/Gen50:2-Gen50:3)** *See also* [GEN 50:26](https://ref.ly/Gen50:26)

Jesus Christ was embalmed
-------------------------

**[JHN 19:39–19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:39-John19:40)** parallels [MAT 27:59](https://ref.ly/Matt27:59), [MRK 15:46](https://ref.ly/Mark15:46), [LUK 23:53](https://ref.ly/Luke23:53)

Further embalming of Jesus Christ was planned
---------------------------------------------

**[MRK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Mark16:1)** parallel [LUK 24:1](https://ref.ly/Luke24:1)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 50:2-3; Genesis 50:26; John 19:39-40; Matthew 27:59; Mark 15:46; Luke 23:53; Mark 16:1; Luke 24:1

## 5305: empires (id: 5305)

Spheres of influence of major powers in the ancient world. The most important empires to relate to the history of the people of God are those of Assyria, Babylon and Rome. Scripture stresses that the grandeur and power of such empires are temporary; only God remains.

Empires of biblical times
-------------------------

### The Hittite empire

**[JOS 1:4](https://ref.ly/Josh1:4)** *Canaan was still known as “the land of the Hittites” for centuries after the Hittites withdrew to the north.* *See also* [GEN 23:10](https://ref.ly/Gen23:10), [GEN 49:29–49:30](https://ref.ly/Gen49:29-Gen49:30), [GEN 50:13](https://ref.ly/Gen50:13); [2SA 11:3–11:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:3-2Sam11:24) *Hittites continue to feature in the OT histories, despite the earlier withdrawal of the Hittites to Anatolia. The Hittite most commonly referred to in the OT is Uriah, husband of Bathsheba.*

### The Egyptian empire

[GEN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10); ; [EXO 12:40–12:41](https://ref.ly/Exod12:40-Exod12:41); ; [DEU 6:21](https://ref.ly/Deut6:21); ; [2CH 35:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:20), [2CH 36:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:3)

### The Assyrian empire

[2KI 15:19–15:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:19-2Kgs15:21), [2KI 16:7–16:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7-2Kgs16:18), [2KI 17:3–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:3-2Kgs17:6), [2KI 17:23–17:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:23-2Kgs17:27); ; [2CH 32:1–32:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:1-2Chr32:22); ; [ISA 7:17–7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:17-Isa7:20), [ISA 36:1–36:18](https://ref.ly/Isa36:1-Isa36:18); ; [HOS 11:5](https://ref.ly/Hos11:5), [HOS 14:3](https://ref.ly/Hos14:3)

### The Babylonian empire

[EZR 5:12–5:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:12-Ezra5:17); ; [NEH 7:6](https://ref.ly/Neh7:6); ; [EST 2:6](https://ref.ly/Esth2:6); ; [PSA 137:1](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1); ; [ISA 39:1–39:7](https://ref.ly/Isa39:1-Isa39:7); ; [JER 20:4–20:6](https://ref.ly/Jer20:4-Jer20:6), [JER 21:2–21:7](https://ref.ly/Jer21:2-Jer21:7), [JER 27:6–27:22](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6-Jer27:22), [JER 29:10](https://ref.ly/Jer29:10)

### The Roman empire

[ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37), [ACT 18:2](https://ref.ly/Acts18:2), [ACT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Acts19:21), [ACT 22:25](https://ref.ly/Acts22:25) *Roman citizenship brought considerable privileges to those who possessed it*; ; [ACT 23:11](https://ref.ly/Acts23:11), [ACT 25:25](https://ref.ly/Acts25:25), [ACT 28:16](https://ref.ly/Acts28:16)

The transience of human empires
-------------------------------

**[DAN 2:44](https://ref.ly/Dan2:44)** *See also* [ISA 13:1–13:22](https://ref.ly/Isa13:1-Isa13:22), [ISA 40:6–40:8](https://ref.ly/Isa40:6-Isa40:8); [JER 51:37–51:40](https://ref.ly/Jer51:37-Jer51:40); [DAN 2:31–2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:31-Dan2:45)REV [14:8](https://ref.ly/Dan14:8), [REV 18:1–18:8](https://ref.ly/Rev18:1-Rev18:8)

## 5307: envoy (id: 5307)

An official representative sent on behalf of a king or government to other kings or nations. Believers are God's envoys to his world, bearing the good news of salvation on his behalf.

Envoys sent to other kings or governments
-----------------------------------------

*See also* [PSA 68:31](https://ref.ly/Ps68:31); [PRO 13:17](https://ref.ly/Prov13:17); [ISA 30:4](https://ref.ly/Isa30:4)

### To continue or promote good relations

**[1KI 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:1)** *See also* [2SA 10:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:2) parallel [1CH 19:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:2); [2KI 20:12–20:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:12-2Kgs20:13) parallel [ISA 39:1–39:2](https://ref.ly/Isa39:1-Isa39:2); [ISA 30:6](https://ref.ly/Isa30:6), [ISA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Isa57:9) *in this instance with godless nations*

### To deceive

[JOS 9:4](https://ref.ly/Josh9:4)

### To ask for military help

[2KI 17:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:4); ; [EZK 17:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:15)

### To make enquiries

[2CH 32:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:31); ; [ISA 14:32](https://ref.ly/Isa14:32)

### With a message to prepare for war

[ISA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Isa18:2); ; [JER 49:14](https://ref.ly/Jer49:14) parallel [OBA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Obad1:1)

### To negotiate for peace

[ISA 33:7](https://ref.ly/Isa33:7); ; [LUK 14:32](https://ref.ly/Luke14:32)

[LUK 19:14](https://ref.ly/Luke19:14)

Believers are God's envoys to his world

## 5308: equality (id: 5308)

Scripture declares that all people are equal in the sight of God, and that all are equally in need of the redemption achieved through the saving death of Jesus Christ. All believers have equal status before God, despite differences in their social standing and background.

The equality of Father and Son
------------------------------

### Jesus Christ claims equality with God

**[JHN 5:18](https://ref.ly/John5:18)** *See also* [JHN 10:30](https://ref.ly/John10:30); [MAT 11:27](https://ref.ly/Matt11:27); [JHN 17:10–17:22](https://ref.ly/John17:10-John17:22)

### The apostles treat Jesus Christ as equal with God

**[2TH 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:16-2Thess2:17)** *See also* [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38); [GAL 1:3](https://ref.ly/Gal1:3); [PHP 2:6](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6); [1TH 3:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:11); [1JN 5:20](https://ref.ly/1John5:20)

The equality of all people before God
-------------------------------------

### God is the maker of all people, whatever their status

**[PRO 22:2](https://ref.ly/Prov22:2)** *See also* [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)

### All people have sinned, whatever their origin or status

**[ROM 3:22–3:24](https://ref.ly/Rom3:22-Rom3:24)**

### God shows no partiality in his dealings with people

**[DEU 10:17](https://ref.ly/Deut10:17)** *See also* [JOB 31:13–31:15](https://ref.ly/Job31:13-Job31:15); [ROM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:11); [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9); [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:17)

### The gospel is proclaimed equally to all

**[ACT 10:34](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34)**

### All believers are one, whatever their origin, status or gender

**[GAL 3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:28)**

Equality among believers
------------------------

*See also* [1CO 12:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:25); [2CO 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:13-2Cor8:14), [2CO 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:12)

### Believers must not give preferential treatment to the rich

**[JAS 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2-Jas2:4)** *See also* [LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15)

### Believers must not show favouritism

**[JAS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jas2:9)** *See also* [1TI 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:21); [JAS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1)

* **Associated Passages:** John 5:18; John 10:30; Matthew 11:27; John 17:10-22; 2 Thessalonians 2:16-17; Acts 2:38; Galatians 1:3; Philippians 2:6; 1 Thessalonians 3:11; 1 John 5:20; Proverbs 22:2; Acts 17:26; Romans 3:22-24; Deuteronomy 10:17; Job 31:13-15; Romans 2:11; Ephesians 6:9; 1 Peter 1:17; Acts 10:34; Galatians 3:28; 1 Corinthians 12:25; 2 Corinthians 8:13-14; 2 Corinthians 11:12; James 2:2-4; Leviticus 19:15; James 2:9; 1 Timothy 5:21; James 2:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1512: Trinity, equality of the persons (ID: `1512@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2420: gospel (ID: `2420@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5001: human race, the (ID: `5001@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5034: likeness (ID: `5034@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5554: status (ID: `5554@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5707: male and female (ID: `5707@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5769: behaviour (ID: `5769@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5838: disrespect (ID: `5838@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7105: believers (ID: `7105@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8753: favouritism (ID: `8753@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8800: prejudice (ID: `8800@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5309: eunuchs (id: 5309)

Castrated men. In the Bible eunuchs are usually portrayed as having positions of responsibility in royal courts. Though eunuchs were excluded from certain privileges under the old covenant, they were at no disadvantage under the new.

Eunuchs as royal servants
-------------------------

**[EST 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Esth1:10-Esth1:11)** *See also* [2KI 20:16–20:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:16-2Kgs20:18) parallel [ISA 39:5–39:7](https://ref.ly/Isa39:5-Isa39:7); [EST 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Esth2:1-Esth2:3), [EST 2:15](https://ref.ly/Esth2:15), [EST 4:4–4:11](https://ref.ly/Esth4:4-Esth4:11); [JER 38:7](https://ref.ly/Jer38:7); [ACT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Acts8:27)

Eunuchs among the people of God
-------------------------------

### Exclusions of eunuchs from the assembly and the priesthood

**[DEU 23:1](https://ref.ly/Deut23:1)** *See also* [LEV 21:16–21:23](https://ref.ly/Lev21:16-Lev21:23)

### Eunuchs not excluded from God's blessings if obedient

**[ISA 56:3–56:5](https://ref.ly/Isa56:3-Isa56:5)**

### Eunuchs could become Christians like anyone else

*See also* [ACT 8:26–8:39](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26-Acts8:39) *the Ethiopian eunuch*

Eunuch as an image for celibacy
-------------------------------

**[MAT 19:10–19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:10-Matt19:12)** *Jesus Christ teaches that castration is not the only legitimate reason for celibacy. Some men may be called to voluntary celibacy because of the demands of their role in the kingdom of heaven.*

Paul suggests that a dogmatic demand for circumcision might lead to a demand for emasculation
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [GAL 5:12](https://ref.ly/Gal5:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Esther 1:10-11; 2 Kings 20:16-18; Isaiah 39:5-7; Esther 2:1-3; Esther 2:15; Esther 4:4-11; Jeremiah 38:7; Acts 8:27; Deuteronomy 23:1; Leviticus 21:16-23; Isaiah 56:3-5; Acts 8:26-39; Matthew 19:10-12; Galatians 5:12
* **Associated Articles:** 5736: singleness (ID: `5736@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8468: renunciation (ID: `8468@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5310: exploitation (id: 5310)

Taking advantage of weak and vulnerable groups within society, especially financially; a practice that is prohibited in Scripture. Rather, such groups of people should be treated with special care and concern.

Exploitation is forbidden
-------------------------

**[PRO 22:23](https://ref.ly/Prov22:23)** *See also* [JOB 22:5–22:9](https://ref.ly/Job22:5-Job22:9); [PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10); [ISA 5:8](https://ref.ly/Isa5:8), [ISA 59:12–59:15](https://ref.ly/Isa59:12-Isa59:15); [EZK 45:9–45:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:9-Ezek45:10); [MIC 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1-Mic2:3); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)

God protects those who are liable to exploitation
-------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18)**; **[PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5)** *See also* [DEU 10:17](https://ref.ly/Deut10:17); [PSA 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Ps10:17-Ps10:18), [PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5); [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4)

God provides for those liable to exploitation
---------------------------------------------

[EXO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10); ; [DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29), [DEU 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12-Deut26:13); ; [PSA 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ps22:26), [PSA 68:10](https://ref.ly/Ps68:10), [PSA 132:15](https://ref.ly/Ps132:15)

Examples of groups of people who must not be exploited
------------------------------------------------------

### The poor

[PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5); ; [ISA 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Isa3:14-Isa3:15); ; [AMO 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:7), [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); ; [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)

### Orphans and the fatherless

[PSA 10:17](https://ref.ly/Ps10:17); ; [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23); ; [JER 5:27–5:29](https://ref.ly/Jer5:27-Jer5:29), [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3)

### Widows and aliens

[PSA 94:1–94:6](https://ref.ly/Ps94:1-Ps94:6), [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); ; [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3), [JER 49:11](https://ref.ly/Jer49:11); ; [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)

### The weak

[PSA 41:1](https://ref.ly/Ps41:1), [PSA 72:13](https://ref.ly/Ps72:13), [PSA 82:3](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3)

Believers' responsibilities towards those vulnerable to exploitation
--------------------------------------------------------------------

### To pray for them

**[PSA 82:3](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3)**

### To care and provide for them

**[JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)**

### Particular responsibilities towards the weak

**[ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35)** *See also* [1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)

### Particular responsibilities for widows

[ACT 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:6); ; [1TI 5:3–5:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:3-1Tim5:16); ; [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

### Particular responsibilities towards orphans

[JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

### Particular responsibilities towards the poor and needy

**[PRO 22:9](https://ref.ly/Prov22:9)** *See also* [MAT 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2-Matt6:3), [MAT 19:16–19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16-Matt19:21); [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8); [ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35), [ACT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36), [ACT 10:2](https://ref.ly/Acts10:2); [ROM 15:25–15:27](https://ref.ly/Rom15:25-Rom15:27)

Examples of exploitation
------------------------

[2SA 23:15–23:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:15-2Sam23:17) *David*; ; [MAT 23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:15) *the Pharisees*; ; [ACT 8:18–8:23](https://ref.ly/Acts8:18-Acts8:23) *Simon Magus*

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 22:23; Job 22:5-9; Proverbs 23:10; Isaiah 5:8; Isaiah 59:12-15; Ezekiel 45:9-10; Micah 2:1-3; Zechariah 7:10; Deuteronomy 10:18; Psalms 12:5; Deuteronomy 10:17; Psalms 10:17-18; Psalms 68:5; Isaiah 25:4; Exodus 23:10; Deuteronomy 14:28-29; Deuteronomy 26:12-13; Psalms 22:26; Psalms 68:10; Psalms 132:15; Proverbs 17:5; Isaiah 3:14-15; Amos 2:6-7; Amos 5:11; Psalms 10:17; Isaiah 1:23; Jeremiah 5:27-29; Jeremiah 22:3; Psalms 94:1-6; Psalms 146:9; Jeremiah 49:11; Psalms 41:1; Psalms 72:13; Psalms 82:3; James 1:27; Acts 20:35; 1 Thessalonians 5:14; Acts 6:1-6; 1 Timothy 5:3-16; Proverbs 22:9; Matthew 6:2-3; Matthew 19:16-21; Luke 19:8; Acts 4:34-35; Acts 9:36; Acts 10:2; Romans 15:25-27; 2 Samuel 23:15-17; Matthew 23:15; Acts 8:18-23

## 5311: extortion (id: 5311)

Literally “twisting out”; obtaining money, etc., by violence, threats or unreasonable demands.

Extortion condemned
-------------------

**[PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10)** *See also* [ECC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:7); [ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2); [EZK 22:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:13); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10)

Extortioners can be saved
-------------------------

[1CO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:11)

What God requires from extortioners
-----------------------------------

### Repentance

**[LUK 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:12-Luke3:14)**

### Restitution

*See also* [LEV 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:4-Lev6:5); [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

Curses upon those who are guilty of extortion
---------------------------------------------

**[JER 22:17–22:19](https://ref.ly/Jer22:17-Jer22:19)** *See also* [EZK 18:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:18), [EZK 22:12–22:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12-Ezek22:14); [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); [HAB 2:6](https://ref.ly/Hab2:6)

Blessings upon those who avoid extortion
----------------------------------------

**[ISA 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:15-Isa33:16)**

Examples of extortion
---------------------

*See also* [1SA 2:12–2:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:12-1Sam2:17) *Eli's sons*; [1SA 8:11–8:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11-1Sam8:18) *the wicked king foretold by Samuel*; [1SA 13:19–13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:19-1Sam13:21) *the Philistines*; [NEH 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:5) *the Jews in Nehemiah's day*; [EZK 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29) *Israel under the kings*; [MAT 18:28](https://ref.ly/Matt18:28) *the servant in the parable*; [MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25) *the Pharisees*; [LUK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11) *sinners in the eyes of the Pharisee*; [1CO 5:9–5:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:11) *some Christians before conversion*
