## 5312: feasting (id: 5312)

Taking part in festive meals that were often a feature of Israel's celebrations of religious festivals.

Feasting on special occasions
-----------------------------

### To mark a covenant

**[GEN 31:54](https://ref.ly/Gen31:54)** *The sacrifice and the meal were important elements in the covenant\-making process.* *See also* [GEN 26:28–26:31](https://ref.ly/Gen26:28-Gen26:31); [EXO 24:11](https://ref.ly/Exod24:11); [1CO 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25-1Cor11:26)

### Weddings

**[JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10)** *See also* [GEN 29:22](https://ref.ly/Gen29:22); [EST 2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:18); [MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2); [JHN 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/John2:1-John2:10); [REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9)

### Family celebrations

**[GEN 21:8](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8)** *See also* [LUK 15:23–15:24](https://ref.ly/Luke15:23-Luke15:24)

### National celebrations

**[1KI 1:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:25)** *See also* [EST 9:17](https://ref.ly/Esth9:17)

Israel's religious feasts
-------------------------

### Passover (or the Feast of Unleavened Bread) to celebrate the exodus

[EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15); ; [DEU 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:3); ; [LEV 23:4–23:6](https://ref.ly/Lev23:4-Lev23:6); ; [2CH 35:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:17); ; [MAT 26:17](https://ref.ly/Matt26:17); ; [LUK 2:41](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41)

### Pentecost, to celebrate the early harvest

**[EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22)** *Pentecost was also known in the OT as “the Feast of Weeks” or the “Feast of Harvest”.* *See also* [EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16); [NUM 28:26](https://ref.ly/Num28:26)

### The Feast of Tabernacles, to celebrate the autumn harvest

**[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16)** *See also* [LEV 23:34](https://ref.ly/Lev23:34); [DEU 16:13](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13); [EZR 3:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:4); [NEH 8:14](https://ref.ly/Neh8:14); [ZEC 14:16](https://ref.ly/Zech14:16)

### The Feast of Dedication, to celebrate the rededication of the temple

**[JHN 10:22](https://ref.ly/John10:22)**

### The Feast of Purim, to celebrate deliverance

**[EST 9:17](https://ref.ly/Esth9:17)** *See also* [EST 9:22](https://ref.ly/Esth9:22), [EST 9:26](https://ref.ly/Esth9:26)

Feasting amongst God's people
-----------------------------

**[NEH 8:10](https://ref.ly/Neh8:10)**; **[ACT 2:46](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46)** *See also* [1KI 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:15); [EST 8:17](https://ref.ly/Esth8:17), [EST 9:17](https://ref.ly/Esth9:17); [JOB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Job1:4)

Feasting amongst the heathen
----------------------------

### Examples of pagan feasts

[1SA 30:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:16); ; [DAN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1); ; [MRK 6:21–6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21-Mark6:28)

### Warnings and instructions to Christians regarding pagan feasts

[1CO 10:27–10:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:27-1Cor10:28); ; [1PE 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3-1Pet4:4)

Feasting as an occasion to sin
------------------------------

**[1CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:7)** *See also* [EXO 32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:6); [1SA 25:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:36); [2SA 13:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:28); [HAB 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hab2:15); [1CO 10:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:28)

Feasting as an evangelistic occasion
------------------------------------

**[LUK 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29)** *See also* [MRK 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16-Mark2:17); [LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:2)

Feasting defended by Jesus Christ
---------------------------------

**[MAT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19)** parallel [LUK 7:34–7:35](https://ref.ly/Luke7:34-Luke7:35) *See also* [MAT 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:18–2:20](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:20), [LUK 5:33–5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33-Luke5:35)

Feasting as a symbol of spiritual blessings
-------------------------------------------

**[MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2)** *See also* [PRO 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Prov9:1-Prov9:6); [ISA 25:6](https://ref.ly/Isa25:6), [ISA 55:1–55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1-Isa55:2); [LUK 14:15](https://ref.ly/Luke14:15); [REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20)

Feasting in heaven
------------------

**[REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9)** *See also* [MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11)

## 5313: flogging (id: 5313)

Physical beating inflicted as punishment.

Flogging and beating in the law
-------------------------------

**[DEU 25:2–25:3](https://ref.ly/Deut25:2-Deut25:3)** *See also* [EXO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Exod21:20)

Justified flogging
------------------

**[PRO 19:25](https://ref.ly/Prov19:25)**; **[1PE 2:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:20)** *See also* [NEH 13:25](https://ref.ly/Neh13:25); [PRO 17:10](https://ref.ly/Prov17:10), [PRO 18:6](https://ref.ly/Prov18:6), [PRO 19:29](https://ref.ly/Prov19:29); [LUK 12:47–12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:47-Luke12:48)

Unjustified flogging
--------------------

**[ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6)**; **[HEB 11:36](https://ref.ly/Heb11:36)** *See also* [EXO 5:14](https://ref.ly/Exod5:14); [PRO 17:26](https://ref.ly/Prov17:26); [JER 20:2](https://ref.ly/Jer20:2), [JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15)

Jesus Christ flogged
--------------------

**[MRK 15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15)** parallels [MAT 27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26), [JHN 19:1](https://ref.ly/John19:1) *See also* [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5); [MAT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:19) parallels [MRK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:34), [LUK 18:32](https://ref.ly/Luke18:32)

The apostles flogged
--------------------

**[MAT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17)**; **[ACT 5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:40)** *See also* [MAT 23:34](https://ref.ly/Matt23:34); [MRK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9); [ACT 16:22–16:23](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22-Acts16:23), [ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37), [ACT 18:17](https://ref.ly/Acts18:17), [ACT 22:19](https://ref.ly/Acts22:19), [ACT 22:24–22:25](https://ref.ly/Acts22:24-Acts22:25); [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5), [2CO 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:24-2Cor11:25)

Flogging used figuratively
--------------------------

**[2SA 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:14)** *See also* [JOB 5:21](https://ref.ly/Job5:21); [PSA 89:32](https://ref.ly/Ps89:32); [ISA 10:26](https://ref.ly/Isa10:26), [ISA 54:11](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11)

## 5314: flute (id: 5314)

Early origin of the flute
-------------------------

**[GEN 4:19–4:21](https://ref.ly/Gen4:19-Gen4:21)** *Jubal was descended from Cain and was in the seventh generation after Adam.*

The flute used in worship
-------------------------

### In Israelite worship

**[PSA 150:1–150:6](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1-Ps150:6)** *See also* [1SA 10:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:5); PSA 5:0; [ISA 30:29](https://ref.ly/Isa30:29)

### In pagan worship

**[DAN 3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:7)** *See also* [DAN 3:5](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5), [DAN 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Dan3:10-Dan3:11), [DAN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:15)

The flute played at celebratory events
--------------------------------------

**[1KI 1:38–1:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:38-1Kgs1:40)** *See also* [JOB 21:12](https://ref.ly/Job21:12); [ISA 5:12](https://ref.ly/Isa5:12); [MAT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Matt11:17) parallel [LUK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Luke7:32); [REV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Rev18:22)

The flute used in mourning
--------------------------

**[MAT 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23-Matt9:24)** *See also* [JOB 30:31](https://ref.ly/Job30:31); [JER 48:36](https://ref.ly/Jer48:36)

The flute used metaphorically by Paul
-------------------------------------

**[1CO 14:6–14:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:6-1Cor14:10)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 4:19-21; Psalms 150:1-6; 1 Samuel 10:5; Isaiah 30:29; Daniel 3:7; Daniel 3:5; Daniel 3:10-11; Daniel 3:15; 1 Kings 1:38-40; Job 21:12; Isaiah 5:12; Matthew 11:17; Luke 7:32; Revelation 18:22; Matthew 9:23-24; Job 30:31; Jeremiah 48:36; 1 Corinthians 14:6-10

## 5315: fortifications (id: 5315)

Structures erected as part of a defence policy. Fortifications protected the inhabitants and their possessions, and also guarded the trade routes. Scripture sees God as the secure fortification of his people in times of danger.

City fortifications
-------------------

### Fortified cities

**[NUM 13:28](https://ref.ly/Num13:28)** *See also* [NUM 32:17](https://ref.ly/Num32:17); [DEU 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Deut3:4-Deut3:5), [DEU 28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:52); [JOS 14:12](https://ref.ly/Josh14:12), [JOS 19:35–19:38](https://ref.ly/Josh19:35-Josh19:38); [2KI 18:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:13); [2CH 8:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:5), [2CH 11:5–11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:5-2Chr11:12), [2CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:23); [NEH 9:25](https://ref.ly/Neh9:25); [ISA 36:1](https://ref.ly/Isa36:1); [JER 4:5](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5), [JER 34:7](https://ref.ly/Jer34:7); [EZK 21:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:20); [ZEP 1:16](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:16)

### Walled cities

**[JOS 6:5](https://ref.ly/Josh6:5)** *See also* [DEU 1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:28), [DEU 9:1](https://ref.ly/Deut9:1); [JOS 6:20](https://ref.ly/Josh6:20); [1SA 31:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:10), [1SA 31:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:12); [1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1); [2CH 36:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:19); [NEH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3), [NEH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Neh2:8), [NEH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Neh2:17); [JER 49:27](https://ref.ly/Jer49:27), [JER 51:44](https://ref.ly/Jer51:44); [EZK 26:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:4)

Fortification systems
---------------------

### Garrisons

**[2SA 8:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:6)** *See also* [2SA 8:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:14), [2SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:14); [1CH 11:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:16), [1CH 18:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:6), [1CH 18:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:13); [2CH 1:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:14), [2CH 8:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:6), [2CH 9:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:25), [2CH 17:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:2)

### Towers

**[2CH 26:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:10)** *See also* [JDG 9:46](https://ref.ly/Judg9:46), [JDG 9:50–9:52](https://ref.ly/Judg9:50-Judg9:52); [2KI 9:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:17); [2CH 27:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:4)

### Regional outposts

**[1KI 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:17-1Kgs9:19)** parallel [2CH 8:3–8:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:3-2Chr8:6) *See also* [1SA 22:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:4), [1SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:14); [2SA 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:17), [2SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:14); [2CH 1:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:14), [2CH 9:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:25), [2CH 17:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:2), [2CH 27:3–27:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:3-2Chr27:4)

Peoples at risk without fortifications
--------------------------------------

**[EZK 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:11)** *See also* [JER 49:31](https://ref.ly/Jer49:31)

God and fortresses
------------------

### God as a fortress for his people

**[PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2)** parallel [2SA 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2-2Sam22:3) *See also* [PSA 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ps9:9), [PSA 37:39](https://ref.ly/Ps37:39), [PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1), [PSA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Ps61:3), [PSA 71:3](https://ref.ly/Ps71:3), [PSA 73:28](https://ref.ly/Ps73:28), [PSA 91:2](https://ref.ly/Ps91:2), [PSA 91:9–91:10](https://ref.ly/Ps91:9-Ps91:10); [PRO 14:26](https://ref.ly/Prov14:26)

### God warns against trusting in human fortifications

**[DEU 28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:52)** *See also* [ISA 2:12–2:18](https://ref.ly/Isa2:12-Isa2:18); [JER 5:17](https://ref.ly/Jer5:17); [HOS 8:14](https://ref.ly/Hos8:14); [JOL 2:7–2:9](https://ref.ly/Joel2:7-Joel2:9); [ZEP 1:14–1:16](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14-Zeph1:16)

**[AMO 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Amos5:9-Amos5:10)** *See also* [ISA 17:3](https://ref.ly/Isa17:3), [ISA 25:2](https://ref.ly/Isa25:2), [ISA 25:12](https://ref.ly/Isa25:12); [LAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lam2:2); [HOS 10:14](https://ref.ly/Hos10:14); [AMO 1:3–2:5](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3-Amos2:5); [MIC 5:11](https://ref.ly/Mic5:11)

## 5316: fortress (id: 5316)

A secure dwelling\-place, often a city, protected by walled defences. God is seen as the fortress of his people, their security and divine protector.

Fortresses were places of security
----------------------------------

**[JER 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5-Jer4:6)** *See also* [1SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:14); [2SA 5:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:9) parallel [1CH 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:7-1Chr11:8); [2SA 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:6), [2SA 24:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:7); [1KI 15:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:17)

Examples of fortified cities
----------------------------

**[NUM 32:34–32:36](https://ref.ly/Num32:34-Num32:36)** *See also* [DEU 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Deut3:4-Deut3:5); [JOS 19:35–19:38](https://ref.ly/Josh19:35-Josh19:38); [2SA 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:7) parallel [1CH 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:4-1Chr11:5); [1KI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:13), [1KI 12:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:25)

Fortresses were symbolic of self\-reliance
------------------------------------------

**[DEU 28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:52)** *See also* [PRO 10:15](https://ref.ly/Prov10:15); [EZK 24:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:21); [DAN 11:38–11:39](https://ref.ly/Dan11:38-Dan11:39)

Fortresses cannot withstand the judgment of God
-----------------------------------------------

**[JER 51:53](https://ref.ly/Jer51:53)** *See also* [ISA 2:12–2:18](https://ref.ly/Isa2:12-Isa2:18), [ISA 17:1–17:3](https://ref.ly/Isa17:1-Isa17:3), [ISA 25:2](https://ref.ly/Isa25:2), [ISA 25:12](https://ref.ly/Isa25:12); [JER 48:1](https://ref.ly/Jer48:1); [LAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lam2:2)AMO [1:4](https://ref.ly/Lam1:4), [AMO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Amos1:7), [AMO 1:10](https://ref.ly/Amos1:10), [AMO 1:12](https://ref.ly/Amos1:12), [AMO 1:14](https://ref.ly/Amos1:14), [AMO 2:2](https://ref.ly/Amos2:2), [AMO 2:5](https://ref.ly/Amos2:5)[HOS 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/Hos10:13-Hos10:14) *punishment for Israel*; [NAM 3:12](https://ref.ly/Nah3:12) *judgment on Nineveh*

God is the secure fortress of his people
----------------------------------------

**[PSA 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ps9:9)** *See also* [2SA 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2-2Sam22:3) parallel [PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2); [PSA 31:2–31:3](https://ref.ly/Ps31:2-Ps31:3), [PSA 59:9](https://ref.ly/Ps59:9), [PSA 59:16–59:17](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16-Ps59:17), [PSA 144:2](https://ref.ly/Ps144:2); [PRO 14:26](https://ref.ly/Prov14:26); [ISA 17:10](https://ref.ly/Isa17:10), [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4); [NAM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Nah1:7)

## 5317: foundation (id: 5317)

The solid base on which a secure structure may be built; used chiefly for the base of the temple. It is used figuratively of Jesus Christ and the apostles and prophets as the secure foundation on which the church is built. Obedience to the teaching of Jesus Christ is the true foundation of Christian living.

The place of foundations in building
------------------------------------

**[1KI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:17)** [JOS 6:26](https://ref.ly/Josh6:26); ; [1KI 16:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:34); [1KI 6:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:37), [1KI 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:10); ; [2CH 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:3), [2CH 8:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:16); [EZR 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:3), [EZR 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11-Ezra3:12), [EZR 4:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:12) *restoring the walls of Jerusalem*; ; [EZR 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:16), [EZR 6:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:3); ; [JOB 4:19](https://ref.ly/Job4:19); ; [ISA 44:28](https://ref.ly/Isa44:28); ; [EZK 41:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:8); ; [HAG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hag2:18); ; [ZEC 4:9](https://ref.ly/Zech4:9), [ZEC 8:9](https://ref.ly/Zech8:9); ; [ACT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Acts16:26)

Destruction of foundations is an indication of divine judgment
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[LAM 4:11](https://ref.ly/Lam4:11)** *See also* [DEU 32:22](https://ref.ly/Deut32:22); [2SA 22:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:16); [JER 51:25–51:26](https://ref.ly/Jer51:25-Jer51:26); [EZK 13:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:14), [EZK 30:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:4); [MIC 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mic1:6)

## 5318: fraud (id: 5318)

The obtaining of goods or money by deception. Scripture condemns this practice, requiring believers to be honest in their dealings with one another.

Fraud of all kinds is forbidden
-------------------------------

**[LEV 19:11](https://ref.ly/Lev19:11)** *See also* [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13); [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19); [1CO 6:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:8)

The importance of integrity in public life
------------------------------------------

**[2KI 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:15)** *See also* [NUM 16:15](https://ref.ly/Num16:15); [1SA 12:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:4); [2SA 18:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:12); [1KI 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:8); [2KI 5:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:16)

Kinds of fraud
--------------

### Stealing

**[EXO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Exod20:15)** parallel [DEU 5:19](https://ref.ly/Deut5:19) *See also* [DEU 23:24](https://ref.ly/Deut23:24); [EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28); [TIT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:12); [1PE 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:15)

### Breach of trust

[LEV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Lev6:2); ; [EZK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:17); ; [LUK 16:12](https://ref.ly/Luke16:12)

### Not repaying loans

**[PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21)**

### Not paying fair wages

*See also* [GEN 31:6–31:9](https://ref.ly/Gen31:6-Gen31:9); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Using dishonest weights or scales

**[LEV 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35-Lev19:36)**; **[PRO 20:23](https://ref.ly/Prov20:23)** *See also* [DEU 25:13–25:16](https://ref.ly/Deut25:13-Deut25:16); [HOS 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7-Hos12:8); [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

[LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

### Moving boundary stones

**[PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10)** *See also* [DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14), [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17)

### Extortion

## 5319: fugitives (id: 5319)

People fleeing, especially from persecution or punishment and seeking refuge. The OT makes special provision for the needs of such people.

Fugitives are those in flight
-----------------------------

### From family

**[GEN 35:1](https://ref.ly/Gen35:1)** *See also* [GEN 35:7](https://ref.ly/Gen35:7); [JDG 9:21](https://ref.ly/Judg9:21); [2SA 13:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:34), [2SA 15:13–15:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:13-2Sam15:15)

### From war

**[2SA 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:13)**; **[OBA 1:14](https://ref.ly/Obad1:14)** *See also* [LEV 26:36](https://ref.ly/Lev26:36); [NUM 21:29](https://ref.ly/Num21:29); [JOS 8:20](https://ref.ly/Josh8:20), [JOS 8:22](https://ref.ly/Josh8:22); [2SA 10:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:14); [JER 46:22](https://ref.ly/Jer46:22), [JER 48:19](https://ref.ly/Jer48:19); [EZK 17:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:21), [EZK 24:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:26)

### From other people

**[EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15)**; **[MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13)** *See also* [EXO 14:27](https://ref.ly/Exod14:27); [1SA 22:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:17); [1KI 2:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:39), [1KI 11:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:40); [ISA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Isa15:5), [ISA 15:9](https://ref.ly/Isa15:9), [ISA 45:20](https://ref.ly/Isa45:20); [JER 48:6](https://ref.ly/Jer48:6), [JER 48:45](https://ref.ly/Jer48:45); [MAT 24:15–24:20](https://ref.ly/Matt24:15-Matt24:20) parallels [MRK 13:14–13:17](https://ref.ly/Mark13:14-Mark13:17), [LUK 21:21–21:23](https://ref.ly/Luke21:21-Luke21:23); [ACT 8:1](https://ref.ly/Acts8:1)

### From vengeance

**[JER 50:28](https://ref.ly/Jer50:28)** *See also* [GEN 4:14](https://ref.ly/Gen4:14); [JOB 15:22](https://ref.ly/Job15:22); [PRO 28:17](https://ref.ly/Prov28:17); [JER 44:14](https://ref.ly/Jer44:14), [JER 48:19](https://ref.ly/Jer48:19)

## 5320: funeral (id: 5320)

Funerals were times for expressing grief
----------------------------------------

[GEN 50:10](https://ref.ly/Gen50:10); ; [ACT 9:36–9:39](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36-Acts9:39)

Hired musicians sometimes played a funeral dirge
------------------------------------------------

[MAT 9:23](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23)

The funeral bier
----------------

[2SA 3:31–3:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:31-2Sam3:32); ; [LUK 7:14](https://ref.ly/Luke7:14)

Funeral fires lit in honour of worthy kings of Judah
----------------------------------------------------

[2CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:14), [2CH 21:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:19); ; [JER 34:5](https://ref.ly/Jer34:5)

Funerals often concluded with burial in a family tomb
-----------------------------------------------------

[GEN 49:29–49:31](https://ref.ly/Gen49:29-Gen49:31)

Jesus Christ had no funeral ceremony
------------------------------------

[LUK 23:53](https://ref.ly/Luke23:53) parallels [MAT 27:59](https://ref.ly/Matt27:59), [MRK 15:46](https://ref.ly/Mark15:46), [JHN 19:40–19:42](https://ref.ly/John19:40-John19:42)

## 5321: furnace (id: 5321)

A closed fireplace made of brick or stone for domestic or industrial usage. Used figuratively, it represents God's presence, testing and judgment.

Everyday use of furnaces
------------------------

### As a stove or oven

[NEH 3:11](https://ref.ly/Neh3:11), [NEH 12:38](https://ref.ly/Neh12:38); ; [LAM 5:10](https://ref.ly/Lam5:10); ; [DAN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Dan3:6)

### As a kiln

[EXO 9:8](https://ref.ly/Exod9:8)

### For smelting metals

[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6); ; [PRO 27:21](https://ref.ly/Prov27:21)

Figurative use of furnaces
--------------------------

### As a symbol of God's majestic presence

[GEN 15:17](https://ref.ly/Gen15:17); ; [ISA 31:9](https://ref.ly/Isa31:9)

### As a symbol of affliction and testing

**[ISA 48:10](https://ref.ly/Isa48:10)** *See also* [DEU 4:20](https://ref.ly/Deut4:20); [1KI 8:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:51); [PRO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Prov17:3); [JER 11:4](https://ref.ly/Jer11:4)

### As a symbol of God's wrath

**[MAT 13:42](https://ref.ly/Matt13:42)** *See also* [GEN 19:28](https://ref.ly/Gen19:28); [PSA 21:9](https://ref.ly/Ps21:9); [EZK 22:17–22:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:17-Ezek22:22); [MAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mal4:1); [MAT 13:50](https://ref.ly/Matt13:50); [REV 9:2](https://ref.ly/Rev9:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Nehemiah 3:11; Nehemiah 12:38; Lamentations 5:10; Daniel 3:6; Exodus 9:8; Psalms 12:6; Proverbs 27:21; Genesis 15:17; Isaiah 31:9; Isaiah 48:10; Deuteronomy 4:20; 1 Kings 8:51; Proverbs 17:3; Jeremiah 11:4; Matthew 13:42; Genesis 19:28; Psalms 21:9; Ezekiel 22:17-22; Malachi 4:1; Matthew 13:50; Revelation 9:2
* **Associated Articles:** 4336: iron (ID: `4336@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4345: metalworkers (ID: `4345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4351: refining (ID: `4351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4372: tin (ID: `4372@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4826: fire (ID: `4826@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4829: heat (ID: `4829@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5435: ovens (ID: `5435@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5560: suffering (ID: `5560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8832: testing (ID: `8832@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9510: hell (ID: `9510@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5322: gallows (id: 5322)

A wooden structure, sometimes a tree, used to impale or display a body after death, or for execution by hanging. Those hanged on gallows were deemed to be under God's curse.

A gallows used for impalement and display of bodies
---------------------------------------------------

**[EZR 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:11)** *See also* [JOS 8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:29), [JOS 10:26](https://ref.ly/Josh10:26); [2SA 4:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:12)

A gallows used for execution
----------------------------

**[EST 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:9-Esth7:10)** *See also* [EST 2:23](https://ref.ly/Esth2:23), [EST 9:13](https://ref.ly/Esth9:13)

Those hanged on gallows are under God's curse
---------------------------------------------

**[GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13)** *See also* [DEU 21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 6:11; Joshua 8:29; Joshua 10:26; 2 Samuel 4:12; Esther 7:9-10; Esther 2:23; Esther 9:13; Galatians 3:13; Deuteronomy 21:23
* **Associated Articles:** 4528: trees (ID: `4528@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5281: crucifixion (ID: `5281@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5331: hanging (ID: `5331@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5482: punishment (ID: `5482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5827: curse (ID: `5827@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7346: death penalty (ID: `7346@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5323: gate (id: 5323)

A point of entry to a walled city or walled\-off area, such as the temple. It was a place where business was transacted and where justice and punishment were dispensed. It is used figuratively of the entry to death and also of the starting\-point of the Christian life.

The purpose of gates as defended points of entry
------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 3:5](https://ref.ly/Deut3:5)** [JOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Josh2:5), [JOS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Josh2:7); [JDG 5:8](https://ref.ly/Judg5:8); ; [1SA 23:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:7); ; [2CH 8:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:5), [2CH 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:7); ; [PSA 107:16](https://ref.ly/Ps107:16), [PSA 147:13](https://ref.ly/Ps147:13); ; [ISA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Isa22:7), [ISA 45:1–45:2](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1-Isa45:2); [JER 51:30](https://ref.ly/Jer51:30), [JER 51:58](https://ref.ly/Jer51:58); [EZK 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:11), [EZK 46:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:9); ; [HOS 11:6](https://ref.ly/Hos11:6); ; [LUK 7:12](https://ref.ly/Luke7:12) *Jesus Christ approaches Nain*; ; [ACT 9:24](https://ref.ly/Acts9:24), [ACT 12:10](https://ref.ly/Acts12:10), [ACT 16:13](https://ref.ly/Acts16:13); [REV 21:25](https://ref.ly/Rev21:25), [REV 22:14](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14)

Examples of gates
-----------------

### The gates of Jerusalem

**[NEH 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Neh2:13-Neh2:15)** *See also* [2KI 14:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:13) parallel [2CH 25:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:23); [NEH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3), [NEH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Neh2:3), [NEH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Neh2:8)NEH [3:1](https://ref.ly/Neh3:1), [NEH 3:32](https://ref.ly/Neh3:32); [JHN 5:2](https://ref.ly/John5:2)[NEH 3:3](https://ref.ly/Neh3:3), [NEH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Neh3:6), [NEH 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Neh3:13-Neh3:15) *the Valley Gate, Dung Gate and Fountain Gate*; [NEH 3:26](https://ref.ly/Neh3:26) *the Water Gate*; [NEH 3:28–3:29](https://ref.ly/Neh3:28-Neh3:29), [NEH 3:31](https://ref.ly/Neh3:31), [NEH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Neh6:1), [NEH 7:3](https://ref.ly/Neh7:3), [NEH 11:19](https://ref.ly/Neh11:19), [NEH 12:39](https://ref.ly/Neh12:39); [PSA 122:2](https://ref.ly/Ps122:2); [ISA 60:11](https://ref.ly/Isa60:11); [JER 1:15](https://ref.ly/Jer1:15)

### The gates of the temple

**[ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2)** *See also* [2CH 23:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:19), [2CH 24:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:8), [2CH 31:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:2); [JER 20:2](https://ref.ly/Jer20:2); [EZK 44:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:11); [ACT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Acts3:10)

### Other examples of gates

**[JOS 6:26](https://ref.ly/Josh6:26)** *See also* [GEN 28:17](https://ref.ly/Gen28:17); [1SA 17:52](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:52); [1KI 16:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:34); [MAT 16:18](https://ref.ly/Matt16:18); [JHN 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/John10:1-John10:3); [ACT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Acts10:17)

Uses of the area around the gate
--------------------------------

### A place of (sometimes false) worship

**[2CH 31:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:2)** *See also* [2KI 23:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8); [PSA 9:14](https://ref.ly/Ps9:14), [PSA 118:19–118:20](https://ref.ly/Ps118:19-Ps118:20); [EZK 46:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:2); [ACT 14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:13)

### A place of business

**[GEN 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Gen23:10-Gen23:11)** *See also* [RUT 4:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1), [RUT 4:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:11); [2KI 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:1), [2KI 7:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:18); [NEH 13:19–13:22](https://ref.ly/Neh13:19-Neh13:22)

### A place where decisions are made and justice dispensed

**[GEN 34:19–34:20](https://ref.ly/Gen34:19-Gen34:20)** *See also* [DEU 21:19](https://ref.ly/Deut21:19), [DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19), [DEU 25:7–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:7-Deut25:10); [JOS 20:4](https://ref.ly/Josh20:4); [2SA 15:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:2); [1KI 22:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:10); [EST 2:19](https://ref.ly/Esth2:19), [EST 2:21](https://ref.ly/Esth2:21), [EST 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Esth3:2-Esth3:3)

### A place of punishment, banishment and begging

**[DEU 17:5](https://ref.ly/Deut17:5)** *See also* [DEU 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:24); [JOS 8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:29); [2KI 7:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:3); [JER 20:2](https://ref.ly/Jer20:2); [EZK 21:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:15); [LUK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Luke16:20); [HEB 13:12](https://ref.ly/Heb13:12); [REV 22:14–22:15](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14-Rev22:15)

### A place of counsel

[PRO 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Prov1:20-Prov1:21), [PRO 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Prov8:1-Prov8:3)

Figurative use of the gate
--------------------------

### Coming to worship

[PSA 24:7–24:10](https://ref.ly/Ps24:7-Ps24:10), [PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4)

### The entrance to death

**[ISA 38:10](https://ref.ly/Isa38:10)** *See also* [JOB 17:16](https://ref.ly/Job17:16), [JOB 38:17](https://ref.ly/Job38:17); [PSA 9:13](https://ref.ly/Ps9:13), [PSA 107:18](https://ref.ly/Ps107:18)

### The entrance to the Christian life

**[MAT 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Matt7:13-Matt7:14)** *See also* [JHN 10:7–10:10](https://ref.ly/John10:7-John10:10)

### The entry to the heavenly city of God

**[REV 22:14](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14)** *See also* [EZK 48:30–48:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek48:30-Ezek48:34); [REV 21:12–21:15](https://ref.ly/Rev21:12-Rev21:15), [REV 21:21](https://ref.ly/Rev21:21), [REV 21:25](https://ref.ly/Rev21:25)

## 5324: gatekeepers (id: 5324)

Levite temple servants, responsible for protecting the palace and the temple from those who would desecrate it or steal its treasures. City gatekeepers also existed.

The ancestry of gatekeepers
---------------------------

**[1CH 9:17–9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:17-1Chr9:22)** *See also* [1CH 26:1–26:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:1-1Chr26:13); [EZR 2:42](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:42); [NEH 7:45](https://ref.ly/Neh7:45)

The special status of gatekeepers
---------------------------------

**[EZR 7:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:24)** *See also* [EZR 2:70](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:70); [NEH 7:73](https://ref.ly/Neh7:73), [NEH 12:47](https://ref.ly/Neh12:47), [NEH 13:5](https://ref.ly/Neh13:5)

The functions of gatekeepers
----------------------------

**[1CH 9:23–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:23-1Chr9:27)** *See also* [2CH 8:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:14); [NEH 11:19](https://ref.ly/Neh11:19)

Examples of gatekeepers
-----------------------

**[2CH 35:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:15)** *See also* [1CH 15:16–15:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16-1Chr15:18), [1CH 16:37–16:38](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:37-1Chr16:38), [1CH 23:3–23:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:3-1Chr23:5), [1CH 26:14–26:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:14-1Chr26:19); [NEH 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/Neh10:28-Neh10:29), [NEH 10:39](https://ref.ly/Neh10:39), [NEH 12:25](https://ref.ly/Neh12:25), [NEH 12:45](https://ref.ly/Neh12:45)

City gatekeepers
----------------

**[2KI 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:10-2Kgs7:11)** *See also* [NEH 7:1–7:3](https://ref.ly/Neh7:1-Neh7:3)

## 5325: gifts (id: 5325)

Presents, given freely and without payment. Scripture lays considerable emphasis on salvation as a gift given by God to sinful, undeserving men and women.

Gifts given by God
------------------

### His one and only Son, Jesus Christ

**[JHN 3:16](https://ref.ly/John3:16)** *See also* [2CO 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:14-2Cor9:15)

### Salvation and eternal life

**[ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)** *See also* [JHN 4:10](https://ref.ly/John4:10); [ROM 5:12–5:17](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12-Rom5:17), [ROM 11:25–11:32](https://ref.ly/Rom11:25-Rom11:32); [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7); [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

### Grace

**[EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9)** *See also* [2CO 9:8–9:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:8-2Cor9:11), [2CO 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:14-2Cor9:15); [EPH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7), [EPH 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Eph4:7-Eph4:8)

### Holy Spirit

**[ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38)** *See also* [LUK 11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:13); [ACT 1:4](https://ref.ly/Acts1:4), [ACT 8:18](https://ref.ly/Acts8:18), [ACT 10:44](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44), [ACT 11:15](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15); [HEB 6:4](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4)

### Spiritual gifts

**[1CO 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:1-1Cor12:11)** *See also* [ROM 12:4–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4-Rom12:8); [1CO 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:4-1Cor1:7), [1CO 12:27–12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27-1Cor12:31), [1CO 13:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:2), [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1); [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14); [2TI 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:6); [HEB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Heb2:4); [1PE 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10)

### Other gifts from God

**[JAS 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)** [GEN 30:20](https://ref.ly/Gen30:20); ; [1SA 1:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:27); [JAS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jas1:5) *wisdom*; ; [1CO 7:1–7:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:1-1Cor7:7); [ECC 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:13), [ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19)

Gifts given to God
------------------

**[PSA 68:18](https://ref.ly/Ps68:18)** *See also* [NUM 7:3](https://ref.ly/Num7:3), [NUM 18:9](https://ref.ly/Num18:9), [NUM 31:52](https://ref.ly/Num31:52); [DEU 12:11](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11), [DEU 16:16–16:17](https://ref.ly/Deut16:16-Deut16:17); [2CH 31:12–31:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:12-2Chr31:14); [PSA 68:29](https://ref.ly/Ps68:29), [PSA 76:11](https://ref.ly/Ps76:11); [ISA 18:7](https://ref.ly/Isa18:7); [EZK 20:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:39); [MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24), [MAT 15:3–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:6) parallel [MRK 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9-Mark7:13) *condemnation concerning the Corban oath*; [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4)

### To their subjects

## 5326: government (id: 5326)

God's gracious rule over the nations, particularly Israel. Also, the divinely established ruling of people by God's appointed authorities for the good of society and to prevent anarchy.

God governs the nations
-----------------------

**[PSA 22:28](https://ref.ly/Ps22:28)** *See also* [1CH 29:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:12); [2CH 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:6); [PSA 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Ps9:7-Ps9:8), [PSA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Ps47:2), [PSA 47:7–47:8](https://ref.ly/Ps47:7-Ps47:8), [PSA 66:7](https://ref.ly/Ps66:7), [PSA 67:4](https://ref.ly/Ps67:4), [PSA 103:19](https://ref.ly/Ps103:19); [DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32), [DAN 4:35](https://ref.ly/Dan4:35); [1TI 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15)

Jesus Christ also governs the nations
-------------------------------------

**[ISA 9:6](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6)** *See also* [PSA 72:8–72:11](https://ref.ly/Ps72:8-Ps72:11), [PSA 110:2](https://ref.ly/Ps110:2); [ZEC 6:13](https://ref.ly/Zech6:13), [ZEC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Zech9:10); [REV 2:27](https://ref.ly/Rev2:27); [PSA 2:9](https://ref.ly/Ps2:9); [REV 12:5](https://ref.ly/Rev12:5), [REV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Rev19:15)

The manner of God's government of Israel was unique
---------------------------------------------------

**[JDG 8:23](https://ref.ly/Judg8:23)** *See also* [1SA 8:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:7), [1SA 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12)

God governs individuals
-----------------------

**[PRO 21:1](https://ref.ly/Prov21:1)** *See also* [PRO 16:1](https://ref.ly/Prov16:1), [PRO 16:9](https://ref.ly/Prov16:9), [PRO 19:21](https://ref.ly/Prov19:21), [PRO 20:24](https://ref.ly/Prov20:24); [JER 10:23](https://ref.ly/Jer10:23); [LAM 3:37](https://ref.ly/Lam3:37); [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23); [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26); [JAS 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:15)

God has established human agencies for the government of people
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [GEN 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:5-Gen9:6); [PRO 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Prov8:15-Prov8:16); [DAN 2:37–2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37-Dan2:38), [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17), [DAN 4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:32), [DAN 5:21](https://ref.ly/Dan5:21); [MAT 22:17–22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:17-Matt22:21); [JHN 19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:11); [1TI 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:2-1Tim2:3); [1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13)

### Government is for the good of society

**[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [PSA 72:12–72:14](https://ref.ly/Ps72:12-Ps72:14), [PSA 82:3–82:4](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3-Ps82:4)

### Government is established to prevent anarchy

**[ROM 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Rom13:2-Rom13:3)** *See also* [GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6); [EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26); [PRO 21:15](https://ref.ly/Prov21:15), [PRO 28:2](https://ref.ly/Prov28:2), [PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4); [ACT 25:11](https://ref.ly/Acts25:11); [ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4); [1PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:14)

## 5327: governors (id: 5327)

Those who ruled with an authority delegated to them by a higher ruler to whom they were responsible.

Joseph as governor of Egypt
---------------------------

**[GEN 42:6](https://ref.ly/Gen42:6)** *See also* [GEN 41:41](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41), [GEN 41:43](https://ref.ly/Gen41:43), [GEN 41:55](https://ref.ly/Gen41:55), [GEN 45:8](https://ref.ly/Gen45:8), [GEN 45:26](https://ref.ly/Gen45:26)

Governors of Judah
------------------

### Gedaliah had a short period in office

**[2KI 25:22–25:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:22-2Kgs25:23)** *See also* [JER 40:7](https://ref.ly/Jer40:7), [JER 40:11](https://ref.ly/Jer40:11), [JER 41:2](https://ref.ly/Jer41:2), [JER 41:18](https://ref.ly/Jer41:18)

### Sheshbazzar laid the temple foundations

**[EZR 5:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:14)** *See also* [EZR 1:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:8)

### Zerubbabel

Zerubbabel is possibly the same person as Sheshbazzar: [HAG 1:1](https://ref.ly/Hag1:1), [HAG 1:14](https://ref.ly/Hag1:14), [HAG 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hag2:2), [HAG 2:21](https://ref.ly/Hag2:21)

### Nehemiah rebuilt the walls of Jerusalem

**[NEH 5:14](https://ref.ly/Neh5:14)** *See also* [NEH 8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9), [NEH 10:1](https://ref.ly/Neh10:1), [NEH 12:26](https://ref.ly/Neh12:26)

### Governors before Nehemiah

[NEH 5:15](https://ref.ly/Neh5:15)

Persian governors
-----------------

### Tattenai

**[EZR 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:3)** *“Trans\-Euphrates” (literally “beyond the river”, i.e., the Euphrates River) to the Persians included the areas of Aram, Phoenicia and Palestine.* *See also* [EZR 5:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:6), [EZR 6:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:6), [EZR 6:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:13)

### Other governors of Trans\-Euphrates

**[EZR 8:36](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:36)** *See also* [NEH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Neh2:7), [NEH 2:9](https://ref.ly/Neh2:9), [NEH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Neh3:7)

### Other Persian governors

**[EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12)** *See also* [EST 8:9](https://ref.ly/Esth8:9), [EST 9:3](https://ref.ly/Esth9:3); [DAN 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Dan3:2-Dan3:3), [DAN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:27), [DAN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7)

### Satraps

Satraps were Persian provincial governors: [EZR 8:36](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:36); ; [EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12), [EST 8:9](https://ref.ly/Esth8:9), [EST 9:3](https://ref.ly/Esth9:3); ; [DAN 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Dan3:2-Dan3:3), [DAN 3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:27), [DAN 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Dan6:1-Dan6:4), [DAN 6:6](https://ref.ly/Dan6:6), [DAN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7)

Other OT references to governors
--------------------------------

[JDG 9:30](https://ref.ly/Judg9:30); ; [1KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:7) *Solomon's twelve district governors*; ; [1KI 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:19); ; [2KI 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:5), [2KI 23:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8); ; [2CH 9:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:14); ; [JER 51:23](https://ref.ly/Jer51:23), [JER 51:28](https://ref.ly/Jer51:28), [JER 51:57](https://ref.ly/Jer51:57); ; [EZK 23:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:6), [EZK 23:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:12), [EZK 23:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:23); ; [MAL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:8)

Roman governors
---------------

### Quirinius, governor of Syria

**[LUK 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Luke2:1-Luke2:2)**

### Pilate, governor of Judea

*See also* [MAT 27:2](https://ref.ly/Matt27:2), [MAT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11), [MAT 27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:14), [MAT 27:15](https://ref.ly/Matt27:15), [MAT 27:21](https://ref.ly/Matt27:21), [MAT 27:27](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27), [MAT 28:14](https://ref.ly/Matt28:14); [LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1), [LUK 20:20](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20); [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/John18:28)

### Felix, governor of Judea

[ACT 23:24](https://ref.ly/Acts23:24), [ACT 23:26](https://ref.ly/Acts23:26), [ACT 23:33–23:34](https://ref.ly/Acts23:33-Acts23:34), [ACT 24:1](https://ref.ly/Acts24:1), [ACT 24:10](https://ref.ly/Acts24:10)

### Festus, governor of Judea

[ACT 26:30–26:32](https://ref.ly/Acts26:30-Acts26:32) *Festus did not understand Paul but might well have released him but for his (Paul's) appeal to Rome.*

The governor of Damascus
------------------------

**[2CO 11:32](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:32)**

Other NT references to governors
--------------------------------

*See also* [MAT 10:18](https://ref.ly/Matt10:18); [MRK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9) parallel [LUK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12); [1PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:14)

Standards of performance of governors
-------------------------------------

### Some were good

[GEN 41:39–41:40](https://ref.ly/Gen41:39-Gen41:40); ; [1KI 4:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:27); ; [2KI 10:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:1); ; [EZR 5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:16)

### Some were bad

[NEH 5:14–5:15](https://ref.ly/Neh5:14-Neh5:15); ; [ACT 24:26](https://ref.ly/Acts24:26)

## 5328: greeting (id: 5328)

Spoken greetings
----------------

### Formal greetings

**[2SA 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:9-2Sam8:10)** parallel [1CH 18:9–18:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:9-1Chr18:10) *See also* [GEN 14:17–14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:17-Gen14:20), [GEN 47:7](https://ref.ly/Gen47:7); [1SA 25:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:14); [MAT 10:11–10:15](https://ref.ly/Matt10:11-Matt10:15), [MAT 23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:7) parallels [MRK 12:38](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38), [LUK 20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:46); [LUK 11:43](https://ref.ly/Luke11:43)

### Less formal greetings

**[EXO 18:7](https://ref.ly/Exod18:7)** *See also* [GEN 33:4](https://ref.ly/Gen33:4), [GEN 46:29](https://ref.ly/Gen46:29); [JDG 18:15](https://ref.ly/Judg18:15); [1SA 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:3-1Sam10:4), [1SA 13:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:10), [1SA 17:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:22), [1SA 30:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:21); [2KI 10:12–10:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:12-2Kgs10:15); [ISA 14:9](https://ref.ly/Isa14:9); [MAT 5:47](https://ref.ly/Matt5:47), [MAT 26:49](https://ref.ly/Matt26:49) *Judas betrays Jesus Christ*; [MAT 28:9](https://ref.ly/Matt28:9); [MRK 9:15](https://ref.ly/Mark9:15); [LUK 1:39–1:41](https://ref.ly/Luke1:39-Luke1:41) *Mary visits Elizabeth*; [ACT 18:22](https://ref.ly/Acts18:22), [ACT 21:7](https://ref.ly/Acts21:7), [ACT 21:19](https://ref.ly/Acts21:19)

### Examples of spoken greetings

**[RUT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:4)** *See also* [1SA 25:4–25:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:4-1Sam25:6)

Written greetings
-----------------

### Greetings at the beginning of letters

**[1TH 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:1)** *See also* [EZR 4:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:17), [EZR 5:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:7), [EZR 7:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:12); [ACT 15:23](https://ref.ly/Acts15:23), [ACT 23:26](https://ref.ly/Acts23:26); [ROM 1:1–1:7](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1-Rom1:7); [1CO 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:1-1Cor1:3); [2CO 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:1-2Cor1:2); [GAL 1:1–1:5](https://ref.ly/Gal1:1-Gal1:5); [EPH 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Eph1:1-Eph1:2); [PHP 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1-Phil1:2); [COL 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Col1:1-Col1:2); [2TH 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:1-2Thess1:2)[1TI 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:1-1Tim1:2); [2TI 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:1-2Tim1:2); [TIT 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Titus1:1-Titus1:4); [PHM 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:1-Phlm1:3); [JAS 1:1](https://ref.ly/Jas1:1); [1PE 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:1-1Pet1:2); [2PE 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:1-2Pet1:2); [2JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/2John1:1-2John1:3); [3JN 1:1](https://ref.ly/3John1:1); [JUD 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Jude1:1-Jude1:2)

### Greetings at the close of letters

## 5329: guarantee (id: 5329)

A pledge given that a promise will be fulfilled; a deposit or down payment making fulfilment obligatory. All God's promises are guaranteed in and through Jesus Christ.

Laws relating to guarantees
---------------------------

### Promises must be kept

**[NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2)** *See also* [PSA 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:4-Ps15:5)

### The poor must not be oppressed

**[EXO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26-Exod22:27)** *See also* [DEU 24:6](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6), [DEU 24:10–24:12](https://ref.ly/Deut24:10-Deut24:12), [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17)

### Special laws relating to women

[NUM 30:3–30:16](https://ref.ly/Num30:3-Num30:16)

### Guarantees could be written or spoken

[NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2); ; [EZR 10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:19)

Examples of guarantees
----------------------

### Judah's pledge to bring Benjamin back

**[GEN 43:8–43:9](https://ref.ly/Gen43:8-Gen43:9)** *See also* [GEN 44:32](https://ref.ly/Gen44:32)

### The people's pledge to keep the law

[2KI 23:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:3); ; [2CH 34:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:32)

### Other examples of guarantees

[JOB 17:3](https://ref.ly/Job17:3) *Job's need of a pledge*; ; [JOB 22:6](https://ref.ly/Job22:6) *Eliphaz' accusation against Job*; ; [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3) *Job's complaint against the wicked*; ; [EST 8:8](https://ref.ly/Esth8:8) *the king's seal*; ; [EZK 17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:18) *Zedekiah's broken pledge*; ; [AMO 2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:8) *the priests taking pledges*; ; [MIC 7:20](https://ref.ly/Mic7:20) *God's pledge to his people*; ; [1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:21) *Baptism understood as a pledge*

God's promises are guaranteed in Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ, the guarantee of salvation for believers

**[HEB 7:22](https://ref.ly/Heb7:22)** *See also* [2CO 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:18-2Cor1:20)

### The Holy Spirit, the guarantee of believers' inheritance

**[2CO 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:21-2Cor1:22)**; **[EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14)** *See also* [2CO 5:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:5)

### Salvation guaranteed to all who believe

**[ROM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Rom4:16)** *See also* [REV 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:2-Rev7:3), [REV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Rev9:4), [REV 14:1](https://ref.ly/Rev14:1)

### The oath of God is unbreakable

**[NUM 23:19](https://ref.ly/Num23:19)** *See also* [TIT 1:2](https://ref.ly/Titus1:2); [HEB 6:17–6:20](https://ref.ly/Heb6:17-Heb6:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 30:2; Psalms 15:4-5; Exodus 22:26-27; Deuteronomy 24:6; Deuteronomy 24:10-12; Deuteronomy 24:17; Numbers 30:3-16; Ezra 10:19; Genesis 43:8-9; Genesis 44:32; 2 Kings 23:3; 2 Chronicles 34:32; Job 17:3; Job 22:6; Job 24:3; Esther 8:8; Ezekiel 17:18; Amos 2:8; Micah 7:20; 1 Peter 3:21; Hebrews 7:22; 2 Corinthians 1:18-20; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22; Ephesians 1:13-14; 2 Corinthians 5:5; Romans 4:16; Revelation 7:2-3; Revelation 9:4; Revelation 14:1; Numbers 23:19; Titus 1:2; Hebrews 6:17-20

## 5330: guard (id: 5330)

A watchman, sentinel or bodyguard for a king or ruler. The word is also used metaphorically.

Bodyguards for kings
--------------------

**[1SA 22:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:14)** *See also* [1SA 26:14–26:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:14-1Sam26:15), [1SA 28:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:2); [2SA 16:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:6), [2SA 23:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:23) parallel [1CH 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:25); [1KI 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:8), [1KI 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:10); [2KI 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:7-2Kgs11:8); [2CH 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:11)

Guards at the tomb of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------

**[MAT 27:65–27:66](https://ref.ly/Matt27:65-Matt27:66)** *See also* [MAT 28:4](https://ref.ly/Matt28:4)

Other examples of human guards
------------------------------

**[NEH 4:9](https://ref.ly/Neh4:9), [NEH 4:23](https://ref.ly/Neh4:23)**; **[LUK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Luke22:4)** *See also* [GEN 37:36](https://ref.ly/Gen37:36); [2KI 25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:11) parallel [JER 39:9](https://ref.ly/Jer39:9); [JER 51:12](https://ref.ly/Jer51:12); [MAT 27:54](https://ref.ly/Matt27:54); [LUK 2:8](https://ref.ly/Luke2:8), [LUK 11:21](https://ref.ly/Luke11:21), [LUK 22:52](https://ref.ly/Luke22:52), [LUK 22:63](https://ref.ly/Luke22:63); [ACT 4:1](https://ref.ly/Acts4:1), [ACT 12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4), [ACT 12:6](https://ref.ly/Acts12:6), [ACT 12:10](https://ref.ly/Acts12:10), [ACT 12:19](https://ref.ly/Acts12:19), [ACT 16:23](https://ref.ly/Acts16:23), [ACT 22:20](https://ref.ly/Acts22:20), [ACT 23:35](https://ref.ly/Acts23:35), [ACT 24:23](https://ref.ly/Acts24:23), [ACT 28:16](https://ref.ly/Acts28:16); [PHP 1:13](https://ref.ly/Phil1:13)

Angelic guards
--------------

**[PSA 91:11–91:12](https://ref.ly/Ps91:11-Ps91:12)** *See also* [GEN 3:24](https://ref.ly/Gen3:24); [1CO 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:15)

The LORD guards his people himself
----------------------------------

**[DEU 32:9–32:11](https://ref.ly/Deut32:9-Deut32:11)**; **[PSA 97:10](https://ref.ly/Ps97:10)** *See also* [GEN 28:15](https://ref.ly/Gen28:15); [1SA 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9); [2SA 22:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:31) parallel [PSA 18:30](https://ref.ly/Ps18:30); [PSA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Ps1:6), [PSA 121:3–121:8](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:8); [PRO 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Prov2:7-Prov2:8), [PRO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Prov30:5); [ISA 27:3](https://ref.ly/Isa27:3), [ISA 31:5](https://ref.ly/Isa31:5), [ISA 52:12](https://ref.ly/Isa52:12), [ISA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8)

Figurative references to guarding
---------------------------------

### Guarding one's conduct

**[PRO 4:23](https://ref.ly/Prov4:23)**; **[1TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:16)** *See also* [DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9); [PRO 7:2](https://ref.ly/Prov7:2), [PRO 16:17](https://ref.ly/Prov16:17), [PRO 19:16](https://ref.ly/Prov19:16); [ECC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1); [MAL 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15-Mal2:16)MAT [16:6](https://ref.ly/Mal16:6), [MAT 16:11–16:12](https://ref.ly/Matt16:11-Matt16:12)[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28); [1CO 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:13); [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7); [2PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17); [2JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/2John1:8)

### Guarding one's tongue

**[PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)** *See also* [PRO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Prov13:3); [JAS 3:3–3:8](https://ref.ly/Jas3:3-Jas3:8)

-----------------------------------------------

## 5331: hanging (id: 5331)

Death by suspension from the neck or impaling on a pole, or the suspension of a body after execution by another means.

Suicide by hanging
------------------

[2SA 17:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:23); ; [MAT 27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:5)

Execution by hanging or impaling
--------------------------------

[GEN 40:18–40:22](https://ref.ly/Gen40:18-Gen40:22); [ACT 5:30](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30), [ACT 10:39](https://ref.ly/Acts10:39); [EZR 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:11); ; [EST 2:23](https://ref.ly/Esth2:23), [EST 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:9-Esth7:10), [EST 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/Esth9:12-Esth9:13)

Hanging after execution
-----------------------

[DEU 21:22](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22); ; [JOS 8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:29), [JOS 10:26](https://ref.ly/Josh10:26); ; [2SA 4:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:12), [2SA 21:9–21:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:9-2Sam21:10)

-------------------------------------------

[DEU 21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:23); ; [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13)

## 5332: harp (id: 5332)

Early origins of the harp
-------------------------

**[GEN 4:19–4:21](https://ref.ly/Gen4:19-Gen4:21)** *Jubal was descended from Cain and was in the seventh generation after Adam.*

The harp used in worship
------------------------

### In joyous religious celebrations

**[2SA 6:2–6:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:2-2Sam6:5)** parallel [1CH 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:6-1Chr13:8) *See also* [1CH 15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:28); [2CH 20:27–20:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:27-2Chr20:28); [NEH 12:27](https://ref.ly/Neh12:27); [PSA 49:4](https://ref.ly/Ps49:4), [PSA 137:1–137:4](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1-Ps137:4); [ISA 30:32](https://ref.ly/Isa30:32); [AMO 5:23](https://ref.ly/Amos5:23); [REV 14:2–14:3](https://ref.ly/Rev14:2-Rev14:3)

### In temple worship

**[2CH 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12)** *See also* [1KI 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:12) parallel [2CH 9:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:11); [1CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16), [1CH 15:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:21), [1CH 16:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:5), [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1), [1CH 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:3), [1CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6); [2CH 29:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:25)

### Associated with praising God

**[PSA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:4)** *See also* [PSA 33:2](https://ref.ly/Ps33:2), [PSA 57:7–57:8](https://ref.ly/Ps57:7-Ps57:8), [PSA 71:22](https://ref.ly/Ps71:22), [PSA 81:1–81:2](https://ref.ly/Ps81:1-Ps81:2), [PSA 92:1–92:4](https://ref.ly/Ps92:1-Ps92:4), [PSA 98:4–98:6](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4-Ps98:6), [PSA 108:1–108:2](https://ref.ly/Ps108:1-Ps108:2), [PSA 147:7](https://ref.ly/Ps147:7), [PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3), [PSA 150:3](https://ref.ly/Ps150:3); [REV 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8-Rev5:10), [REV 15:2–15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:2-Rev15:4)

### In pagan worship

**[DAN 3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:7)** *See also* [DAN 3:5](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5), [DAN 3:10](https://ref.ly/Dan3:10), [DAN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:15)

The harp used to elicit divine inspiration
------------------------------------------

**[2KI 3:15–3:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:15-2Kgs3:19)** *See also* [1SA 10:5–10:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:5-1Sam10:6), [1SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10)

The harp used to relieve depression
-----------------------------------

**[1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23)** *See also* [1SA 16:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:16), [1SA 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:10)

-----------------------

### In joyous celebrations

## 5333: healing (id: 5333)

The bringing about of a state of physical or spiritual health. Scripture recognises a close link between physical and spiritual health, with healing often being seen as an image of salvation in Christ.

God as the author of healing
----------------------------

**[EXO 15:26](https://ref.ly/Exod15:26)**; **[PSA 103:2–103:3](https://ref.ly/Ps103:2-Ps103:3)** *See also* [LUK 5:15](https://ref.ly/Luke5:15)

General aspects of healing
--------------------------

### Requests for healing

**[2KI 20:1–20:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:1-2Kgs20:11)** parallels [2CH 32:24–32:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:24-2Chr32:26), [ISA 38:1–38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:1-Isa38:8) *See also* [2KI 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:2); [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)

### Quarantine regulations

[LEV 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:4), [LEV 13:4](https://ref.ly/Lev13:4), [LEV 13:26](https://ref.ly/Lev13:26), [LEV 13:46](https://ref.ly/Lev13:46), [LEV 14:8](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8); ; [NUM 5:2](https://ref.ly/Num5:2), [NUM 31:19](https://ref.ly/Num31:19); ; [2KI 15:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:5); ; [LUK 17:12](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12)

### Healing results in discipleship

**[MRK 10:52](https://ref.ly/Mark10:52)** *See also* [JHN 6:2](https://ref.ly/John6:2)

### Healing results in praise

**[ACT 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Acts3:8-Acts3:9)** *See also* [MRK 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:12); [JHN 9:30–9:33](https://ref.ly/John9:30-John9:33), [JHN 9:38](https://ref.ly/John9:38)

Miraculous healing
------------------

### By Jesus Christ

**[MAT 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:3)**; **[LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18)** *See also* [MAT 9:27–9:30](https://ref.ly/Matt9:27-Matt9:30), [MAT 14:34–14:36](https://ref.ly/Matt14:34-Matt14:36) parallel [MRK 6:53–6:56](https://ref.ly/Mark6:53-Mark6:56); [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30); [MRK 1:32–1:34](https://ref.ly/Mark1:32-Mark1:34) parallel [LUK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Luke4:40); [LUK 6:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/Luke6:17-Luke6:19), [LUK 8:48](https://ref.ly/Luke8:48), [LUK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Luke9:11), [LUK 17:19](https://ref.ly/Luke17:19)

### Through the prophets

[1KI 17:17–17:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:17-1Kgs17:24); ; [2KI 4:29–4:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:29-2Kgs4:37), [2KI 5:10–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:10-2Kgs5:14)

### Through the apostles

[MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1) parallel [LUK 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1-Luke9:2); ; [ACT 3:6–3:8](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6-Acts3:8), [ACT 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:15-Acts5:16), [ACT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Acts9:34), [ACT 14:8–14:10](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8-Acts14:10), [ACT 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:11-Acts19:12), [ACT 28:8–28:9](https://ref.ly/Acts28:8-Acts28:9)

### In the church

**[1CO 12:8–12:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:8-1Cor12:9)** *See also* [MRK 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Mark16:17-Mark16:18); [1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28), [1CO 12:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:30)

Healing of fever
----------------

[MAT 8:15](https://ref.ly/Matt8:15) parallels [MRK 1:31](https://ref.ly/Mark1:31), [LUK 4:39](https://ref.ly/Luke4:39); ; [JHN 4:46–4:54](https://ref.ly/John4:46-John4:54)

Healing of demon\-possession
----------------------------

[MAT 9:32–9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32-Matt9:33), [MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22); ; [MRK 5:1–5:20](https://ref.ly/Mark5:1-Mark5:20), [MRK 9:17–9:27](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17-Mark9:27) parallel [LUK 9:38–9:43](https://ref.ly/Luke9:38-Luke9:43); ; [ACT 16:16–16:19](https://ref.ly/Acts16:16-Acts16:19), [ACT 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Acts19:11-Acts19:12)

Healing withheld
----------------

[2CO 12:8–12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:8-2Cor12:9); ; [2TI 4:20](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:20)

Medical treatments to bring healing
-----------------------------------

### Consulting doctors

**[2CH 16:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:12)**; **[MAT 9:12](https://ref.ly/Matt9:12)** *See also* [JER 8:22](https://ref.ly/Jer8:22); [COL 4:14](https://ref.ly/Col4:14)

### Taking medicine

[1TI 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:23)

### Disinfecting the house

[LEV 14:41–14:42](https://ref.ly/Lev14:41-Lev14:42)

### Using splints

[EZK 30:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:21)

### Using bandages and oil

**[ISA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:6)** *See also* [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34)

The spiritual significance of healing
-------------------------------------

### As an image of salvation

[PSA 41:4](https://ref.ly/Ps41:4), [PSA 103:2–103:5](https://ref.ly/Ps103:2-Ps103:5), [PSA 147:3](https://ref.ly/Ps147:3); ; [JER 3:22](https://ref.ly/Jer3:22), [JER 14:19](https://ref.ly/Jer14:19), [JER 17:14](https://ref.ly/Jer17:14); ; [EZK 47:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:12); ; [HOS 6:1](https://ref.ly/Hos6:1); ; [MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2); ; [MAT 8:17](https://ref.ly/Matt8:17); ; [ISA 53:4–53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4-Isa53:5)

### Heaven is the place of ultimate healing

**[REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)** *See also* [ROM 8:18](https://ref.ly/Rom8:18); [REV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:2)

## 5334: health (id: 5334)

A state of physical and spiritual well\-being, which Scripture declares to be possible only through faith in Jesus Christ.

Salvation as spiritual health
-----------------------------

### The need for salvation

[PSA 38:3](https://ref.ly/Ps38:3), [PSA 38:7](https://ref.ly/Ps38:7); ; [MAT 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:12-Matt9:13); ; [LUK 5:31–5:32](https://ref.ly/Luke5:31-Luke5:32)

### The promise of salvation

[EXO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:2); ; [PSA 32:4–32:5](https://ref.ly/Ps32:4-Ps32:5), [PSA 149:4](https://ref.ly/Ps149:4); ; [1TH 5:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:9); ; [TIT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11); ; [2PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:15); ; [REV 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rev12:10)

### The experience of salvation

[PSA 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Ps13:5-Ps13:6), [PSA 91:16](https://ref.ly/Ps91:16), [PSA 118:14](https://ref.ly/Ps118:14); ; [ISA 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Isa12:1-Isa12:3)

The importance of health
------------------------

### Health is to be sought after

**[3JN 1:2](https://ref.ly/3John1:2)** *See also* [JER 8:15](https://ref.ly/Jer8:15); [JHN 5:6](https://ref.ly/John5:6), [JHN 5:9](https://ref.ly/John5:9); [1TH 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:23)

### Health as a wish in greeting people

[1SA 25:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:6); ; [2SA 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9)

### Health promised by God to his people

**[JER 33:6](https://ref.ly/Jer33:6)**

### A cause for praise

**[MAT 15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:31)** *See also* [ACT 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:9-Acts4:12)

The pursuit of health
---------------------

### Hygiene

[DEU 23:13](https://ref.ly/Deut23:13)

### Care of the body

**[1CO 6:18–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:18-1Cor6:20)** *See also* [PRO 4:20–4:22](https://ref.ly/Prov4:20-Prov4:22); [ACT 27:34](https://ref.ly/Acts27:34); [ROM 6:13](https://ref.ly/Rom6:13); [1CO 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:27); [EPH 5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:29); [1TI 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:7-1Tim4:8)

### The balance of work and rest

**[EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21)** *See also* [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11)

Mental health
-------------

### State of the natural mind

**[ROM 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/Rom8:6-Rom8:7)** *See also* [COL 2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:18)

### Mental disorder

[DEU 28:28](https://ref.ly/Deut28:28); ; [1SA 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14-1Sam16:15); ; [DAN 4:28–4:37](https://ref.ly/Dan4:28-Dan4:37); ; [JHN 10:20](https://ref.ly/John10:20); ; [ACT 26:25](https://ref.ly/Acts26:25)

### An anxious mind

[JOB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Job10:1); ; [JER 15:10](https://ref.ly/Jer15:10); ; [LUK 10:41](https://ref.ly/Luke10:41), [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34), [LUK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Luke24:17)

### Anxiety resulting from judgment

**[DEU 28:65](https://ref.ly/Deut28:65)** *See also* [DEU 28:34](https://ref.ly/Deut28:34); [ZEC 12:4](https://ref.ly/Zech12:4); [ROM 1:28](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28)

### The healthy mind

**[PSA 42:6](https://ref.ly/Ps42:6)**; **[PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25)**; **[ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2)** *See also* [MAT 22:37](https://ref.ly/Matt22:37) parallels [MRK 12:30](https://ref.ly/Mark12:30), [LUK 10:27](https://ref.ly/Luke10:27); [PHP 4:6–4:8](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6-Phil4:8); [1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)

## 5335: herald (id: 5335)

One who proclaims a message or paves the way for a promised event. In Scripture both angels and human beings are employed as heralds, and on occasions even specific events may herald future happenings.

Heralds in human affairs
------------------------

**[2KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:28)** *See also* [EXO 5:10](https://ref.ly/Exod5:10); [1SA 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:7); [2SA 18:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:19); [1KI 21:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:12); [2KI 10:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:20), [2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12); [2CH 30:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:6); [EST 1:19–1:22](https://ref.ly/Esth1:19-Esth1:22), [EST 6:11](https://ref.ly/Esth6:11); [DAN 3:4](https://ref.ly/Dan3:4); [JON 3:7](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:7)

To preach is to act as a herald
-------------------------------

### Pronouncing judgment

**[JON 3:4](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:4)** *See also* [JER 4:5](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5), [JER 46:14](https://ref.ly/Jer46:14)

### Bringing good news

**[ISA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Isa40:9)** *See also* [ISA 48:20](https://ref.ly/Isa48:20), [ISA 52:7–52:8](https://ref.ly/Isa52:7-Isa52:8); [NAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Nah1:15)

### Preaching the gospel

**[MAT 24:14](https://ref.ly/Matt24:14)**; **[ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42)**; **[1TI 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:7)** *See also* [ACT 4:2](https://ref.ly/Acts4:2), [ACT 8:5](https://ref.ly/Acts8:5) *Philip proclaims the gospel*; [ACT 9:20](https://ref.ly/Acts9:20), [ACT 10:42](https://ref.ly/Acts10:42) *Peter at Cornelius' house*; [ACT 15:7](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7), [ACT 17:3](https://ref.ly/Acts17:3), [ACT 28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:31) *Paul preaches at Rome*; [1CO 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:23); [2CO 1:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:19); [GAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:2); [COL 1:23](https://ref.ly/Col1:23); [1TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:16); [2TI 1:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:11)

John the Baptist as a herald
----------------------------

**[MAT 11:10](https://ref.ly/Matt11:10)** parallels [MRK 1:2](https://ref.ly/Mark1:2), [LUK 7:27](https://ref.ly/Luke7:27) *See also* [MAL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Mal3:1); [MAT 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Matt3:1-Matt3:3); [ISA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3); [LUK 1:17](https://ref.ly/Luke1:17), [LUK 1:76](https://ref.ly/Luke1:76); [JHN 1:15](https://ref.ly/John1:15), [JHN 3:28](https://ref.ly/John3:28)

Angels as heralds
-----------------

**[LUK 2:10](https://ref.ly/Luke2:10)** *See also* [JDG 6:12](https://ref.ly/Judg6:12); [MAT 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20-Matt1:21), [MAT 28:5–28:6](https://ref.ly/Matt28:5-Matt28:6) parallels [MRK 16:5–16:6](https://ref.ly/Mark16:5-Mark16:6), [LUK 24:4–24:6](https://ref.ly/Luke24:4-Luke24:6); [LUK 1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:13), [LUK 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Luke1:30-Luke1:31), [LUK 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Luke2:13-Luke2:14); [REV 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rev14:6)

Events heralding what is to happen
----------------------------------

[MAT 24:32–24:33](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32-Matt24:33) parallels [MRK 13:28–13:29](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28-Mark13:29), [LUK 21:29–21:31](https://ref.ly/Luke21:29-Luke21:31); ; [2TH 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:3)

Jesus Christ as a herald
------------------------

**[LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43)** parallel [MRK 1:38](https://ref.ly/Mark1:38) *See also* [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17) parallels [MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15), [LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19); [ISA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:2); [LUK 8:1](https://ref.ly/Luke8:1)

## 5336: highway (id: 5336)

A major road through Edom and Moab
----------------------------------

**[NUM 20:17](https://ref.ly/Num20:17)** *Moses to the king of Edom.* *See also* [NUM 20:19](https://ref.ly/Num20:19), [NUM 21:22](https://ref.ly/Num21:22) parallel [DEU 2:27](https://ref.ly/Deut2:27); [NUM 21:33](https://ref.ly/Num21:33) parallel [DEU 3:1](https://ref.ly/Deut3:1); [DEU 2:8](https://ref.ly/Deut2:8)

Main roads deserted in time of trouble and war
----------------------------------------------

[ISA 33:8](https://ref.ly/Isa33:8)

Highways for the large\-scale movement of people
------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 19:23](https://ref.ly/Isa19:23)** *Isaiah prophesies the future harmony that will exist between former enemies, who will then worship the LORD together.* *See also* [ISA 11:16](https://ref.ly/Isa11:16), [ISA 35:8–35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:8-Isa35:10), [ISA 49:11](https://ref.ly/Isa49:11), [ISA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Isa62:10); [JER 31:21](https://ref.ly/Jer31:21)

The \`\`highway for our God''
-----------------------------

**[ISA 40:3–40:4](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3-Isa40:4)** *Isaiah foresees how God will bring his people home from exile in Babylon.* *See also* [MRK 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Mark1:2-Mark1:4) parallels [MAT 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:3), [LUK 3:2–3:6](https://ref.ly/Luke3:2-Luke3:6) *John the Baptist's call to repentance is seen as the necessary preparation for the coming of the Messiah.*

Used metaphorically
-------------------

**[PRO 15:19](https://ref.ly/Prov15:19)** *See also* [PRO 7:27](https://ref.ly/Prov7:27), [PRO 16:17](https://ref.ly/Prov16:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 20:17; Numbers 20:19; Numbers 21:22; Deuteronomy 2:27; Numbers 21:33; Deuteronomy 3:1; Deuteronomy 2:8; Isaiah 33:8; Isaiah 19:23; Isaiah 11:16; Isaiah 35:8-10; Isaiah 49:11; Isaiah 62:10; Jeremiah 31:21; Isaiah 40:3-4; Mark 1:2-4; Matthew 2:1-3; Luke 3:2-6; Proverbs 15:19; Proverbs 7:27; Proverbs 16:17

## 5337: hiring (id: 5337)

Laws relating to hiring
-----------------------

### Hired workers must be paid on time

**[LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13)** *See also* [DEU 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14-Deut24:15); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Regular rates of pay must be established

[LEV 25:50](https://ref.ly/Lev25:50)

### Hired workers are to be treated with kindness

[LEV 25:6](https://ref.ly/Lev25:6), [LEV 25:39–25:40](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:40), [LEV 25:53](https://ref.ly/Lev25:53)

### Hired foreigners were excluded from the Passover

[EXO 12:43–12:45](https://ref.ly/Exod12:43-Exod12:45)

### Hired workers must be carefully chosen

[PRO 26:10](https://ref.ly/Prov26:10)

### Loss of a hired animal is covered by the price

[EXO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:15)

Various kinds of hiring
-----------------------

### A hired husband

[GEN 30:16](https://ref.ly/Gen30:16)

### A hired prophet

**[DEU 23:4](https://ref.ly/Deut23:4)** *See also* [NEH 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Neh6:12-Neh6:13), [NEH 13:2](https://ref.ly/Neh13:2); [JUD 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jude1:11)

### A hired priest

[JDG 17:10](https://ref.ly/Judg17:10), [JDG 18:4](https://ref.ly/Judg18:4)

### Hired soldiers

[JDG 9:4](https://ref.ly/Judg9:4); ; [2SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:6); ; [2KI 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:6); ; [1CH 19:6–19:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:6-1Chr19:7); ; [2CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:6); ; [JER 46:21](https://ref.ly/Jer46:21) *No hired mercenary army was ever approved by the LORD (see [2Ch 25:7–10](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:7-2Chr25:10)).*

### Other hirings

[1SA 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:5) *hirings for food*; ; [2CH 24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:12) *masons and carpenters*; ; [EZR 4:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:5) *counsellors*; ; [ISA 7:20](https://ref.ly/Isa7:20) *a razor, symbolising the judgment to be brought by the king of Assyria*; ; [ISA 23:17](https://ref.ly/Isa23:17) *prostitutes*; ; [ISA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Isa46:6) *goldsmiths*; ; [MRK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Mark1:20) *fishermen*

Teachings illustrated by hired workers
--------------------------------------

### The brevity and hardness of life

**[JOB 7:1–7:3](https://ref.ly/Job7:1-Job7:3)** *See also* [JOB 14:6](https://ref.ly/Job14:6)

### Christian workers

**[MAT 20:1–20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:15)** *This parable teaches that believers should not serve in a mercenary spirit but recognise that God's reward is based upon grace.*

### The severity of the world and the kindness of God

*See also* [LUK 15:15–15:19](https://ref.ly/Luke15:15-Luke15:19)

The difference between hired and good shepherds
-----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 10:11–10:13](https://ref.ly/John10:11-John10:13)** *See also* [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:13; Deuteronomy 24:14-15; James 5:4; Leviticus 25:50; Leviticus 25:6; Leviticus 25:39-40; Leviticus 25:53; Exodus 12:43-45; Proverbs 26:10; Exodus 22:15; Genesis 30:16; Deuteronomy 23:4; Nehemiah 6:12-13; Nehemiah 13:2; Jude 1:11; Judges 17:10; Judges 18:4; Judges 9:4; 2 Samuel 10:6; 2 Kings 7:6; 1 Chronicles 19:6-7; 2 Chronicles 25:6; Jeremiah 46:21; 2 Chronicles 25:7-10; 1 Samuel 2:5; 2 Chronicles 24:12; Ezra 4:5; Isaiah 7:20; Isaiah 23:17; Isaiah 46:6; Mark 1:20; Job 7:1-3; Job 14:6; Matthew 20:1-15; Luke 15:15-19; John 10:11-13; 1 Peter 5:2

## 5338: holiday (id: 5338)

A day or period of time during which regular work is suspended. In Israel, the Sabbath provided a weekly break from everyday labour, giving opportunity for rest and religious assembly, and was the pattern for set days of rest and annual festivals. The NT records events in the life of Jesus Christ which are marked by Christian holidays.

The Sabbath day
---------------

**[GEN 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Gen2:2-Gen2:3)** *See also* [EXO 31:13–31:17](https://ref.ly/Exod31:13-Exod31:17) *Their observance of the Sabbath marked Israel out as the distinctive people of God*; [JER 17:24](https://ref.ly/Jer17:24); [MRK 2:27](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27); [HEB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Heb4:4)

The purpose of holidays
-----------------------

### For rest and refreshment

**[EXO 23:12](https://ref.ly/Exod23:12)** *The Sabbath provided a rest period each week.* *See also* [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11) parallel [DEU 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:14); [EXO 34:21](https://ref.ly/Exod34:21)

### To restore society

[LEV 25:2–25:5](https://ref.ly/Lev25:2-Lev25:5) *The sabbath year allowed the land to be rested*; ; [LEV 25:10](https://ref.ly/Lev25:10) *The restoration of family property in the Year of Jubilee*; ; [DEU 15:12](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12) *The sabbath year marked the release of Hebrew slaves.*

### To assemble for worship

**[LEV 23:3](https://ref.ly/Lev23:3)** *Release from the ties of everyday labour gives opportunity for the people of God to meet together in worship*; **[NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12)** *See also* [LEV 23:27](https://ref.ly/Lev23:27) parallel [NUM 29:7](https://ref.ly/Num29:7); [DEU 16:8](https://ref.ly/Deut16:8); [ISA 1:13](https://ref.ly/Isa1:13)**[PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4)** *See also* [PSA 68:24–68:27](https://ref.ly/Ps68:24-Ps68:27), [PSA 118:27](https://ref.ly/Ps118:27)

### To recall God's deliverance

**[DEU 5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:15)** *See also* [DEU 16:3](https://ref.ly/Deut16:3), [DEU 16:12](https://ref.ly/Deut16:12); [HEB 4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb4:11)

Israel's seven annual holidays
------------------------------

[EXO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Exod12:16) parallel [LEV 23:7–23:8](https://ref.ly/Lev23:7-Lev23:8) *the first and seventh days of the Feast of Unleavened Bread*; ; [LEV 23:21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:21) parallel [NUM 28:26](https://ref.ly/Num28:26) *the day of firstfruits (Pentecost)*; ; [LEV 23:24–23:25](https://ref.ly/Lev23:24-Lev23:25) parallel [NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1) *the Feast of Trumpets*; ; [LEV 23:28–23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:28-Lev23:32) parallel [LEV 16:29–16:31](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29-Lev16:31) *the Day of Atonement*; ; [LEV 23:34–23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:34-Lev23:36) *the first and eighth days of the Feast of Tabernacles*

Christian holidays linked with the life of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------------

### The Lord's Day

**[ACT 20:7](https://ref.ly/Acts20:7)** *See also* [MRK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Mark16:9) *The church meets together on the first day of the week: the day of Jesus Christ's resurrection*; [1CO 16:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:2); [REV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10)

### Christmas Day

**[LUK 2:7](https://ref.ly/Luke2:7)** *See also* [JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14)

### Epiphany

[MAT 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:2), [MAT 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:9-Matt2:11)

### Lent

[MAT 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:2) parallels [MRK 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Mark1:12-Mark1:13), [LUK 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:2)

### Good Friday

[MAT 27:45–27:50](https://ref.ly/Matt27:45-Matt27:50) parallels [MRK 15:33–15:37](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33-Mark15:37), [LUK 23:44–23:46](https://ref.ly/Luke23:44-Luke23:46); ; [JHN 19:28–19:30](https://ref.ly/John19:28-John19:30)

### Easter Sunday

**[MRK 16:5–16:6](https://ref.ly/Mark16:5-Mark16:6)** parallels [MAT 28:5–28:6](https://ref.ly/Matt28:5-Matt28:6), [LUK 24:4–24:6](https://ref.ly/Luke24:4-Luke24:6)

### Ascension Day

*See also* [LUK 24:50–24:51](https://ref.ly/Luke24:50-Luke24:51); [ACT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Acts1:9)

### Pentecost (Whit Sunday)

[ACT 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:2-3; Exodus 31:13-17; Jeremiah 17:24; Mark 2:27; Hebrews 4:4; Exodus 23:12; Exodus 20:8-11; Deuteronomy 5:12-14; Exodus 34:21; Leviticus 25:2-5; Leviticus 25:10; Deuteronomy 15:12; Leviticus 23:3; Numbers 29:12; Leviticus 23:27; Numbers 29:7; Deuteronomy 16:8; Isaiah 1:13; Psalms 42:4; Psalms 68:24-27; Psalms 118:27; Deuteronomy 5:15; Deuteronomy 16:3; Deuteronomy 16:12; Hebrews 4:11; Exodus 12:16; Leviticus 23:7-8; Leviticus 23:21; Numbers 28:26; Leviticus 23:24-25; Numbers 29:1; Leviticus 23:28-32; Leviticus 16:29-31; Leviticus 23:34-36; Acts 20:7; Mark 16:9; 1 Corinthians 16:2; Revelation 1:10; Luke 2:7; John 1:14; Matthew 2:1-2; Matthew 2:9-11; Matthew 4:1-2; Mark 1:12-13; Luke 4:1-2; Matthew 27:45-50; Mark 15:33-37; Luke 23:44-46; John 19:28-30; Mark 16:5-6; Matthew 28:5-6; Luke 24:4-6; Luke 24:50-51; Acts 1:9; Acts 2:1-4
