## 5339: home (id: 5339)

The place of origin or residence, to which people feel attached and where they belong. The term is used of houses, towns, lands and in various figurative and spiritual senses.

Divine blessing upon homes
--------------------------

### It brings peace and security

**[ISA 32:18](https://ref.ly/Isa32:18)** *See also* [2KI 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:5); [PSA 127:1–127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:1-Ps127:2)

### It is promised to the righteous

**[PRO 3:33](https://ref.ly/Prov3:33)** *See also* [PRO 12:7](https://ref.ly/Prov12:7), [PRO 14:11](https://ref.ly/Prov14:11), [PRO 15:6](https://ref.ly/Prov15:6), [PRO 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Prov24:3-Prov24:4)

### It is granted because of a godly presence

**[2SA 6:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:11)** parallel [1CH 13:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:14) *See also* [GEN 30:27–30:30](https://ref.ly/Gen30:27-Gen30:30), [GEN 39:4–39:5](https://ref.ly/Gen39:4-Gen39:5)

Human affection for homes
-------------------------

**[2SA 19:37](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:37)** *See also* [GEN 31:30](https://ref.ly/Gen31:30); [NUM 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Num10:29-Num10:30); [RUT 1:6–1:18](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:6-Ruth1:18) *Ruth's loyalty to Naomi is stronger than her attachment to her homeland*; [1SA 7:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:17); [JOB 29:18](https://ref.ly/Job29:18); [PSA 102:13–102:14](https://ref.ly/Ps102:13-Ps102:14), [PSA 137:1–137:6](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1-Ps137:6)

Instructions for home life
--------------------------

[DEU 6:6–6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:9), [DEU 20:5–20:8](https://ref.ly/Deut20:5-Deut20:8), [DEU 24:5](https://ref.ly/Deut24:5); ; [EPH 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:4); ; [COL 3:18–4:1](https://ref.ly/Col3:18-Col4:1); ; [1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4), [1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8), [1TI 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:11-1Tim5:14); ; [TIT 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4-Titus2:5)

Homes as places of hospitality
------------------------------

[GEN 29:13–29:14](https://ref.ly/Gen29:13-Gen29:14); ; [JDG 19:18–19:21](https://ref.ly/Judg19:18-Judg19:21); ; [2KI 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:8-2Kgs4:10); ; [JHN 19:25–19:27](https://ref.ly/John19:25-John19:27); ; [PHM 1:22](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:22); ; [HEB 13:2](https://ref.ly/Heb13:2)

Israel's home in Canaan
-----------------------

### It is promised to them

**[2SA 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:10-2Sam7:11)** parallel [1CH 17:9–17:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:9-1Chr17:10) *See also* [EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8); [NUM 14:30](https://ref.ly/Num14:30); [PSA 78:55](https://ref.ly/Ps78:55)

### It was taken from them

**[2KI 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:22-2Kgs17:23), [2KI 25:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:21)** *See also* [2KI 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:5-2Kgs17:6), [2KI 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:10-2Kgs18:11); [2CH 36:20–36:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:20-2Chr36:21); [PSA 79:6–79:7](https://ref.ly/Ps79:6-Ps79:7); [JOL 3:6](https://ref.ly/Joel3:6)

### It was restored to them

**[ZEP 3:20](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:20)** *See also* [EZK 36:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:8)

Homes for animals
-----------------

[JOB 39:5–39:6](https://ref.ly/Job39:5-Job39:6); ; [PSA 84:3](https://ref.ly/Ps84:3), [PSA 104:17–104:18](https://ref.ly/Ps104:17-Ps104:18); ; [PRO 30:26–30:28](https://ref.ly/Prov30:26-Prov30:28)

Homes in Jesus Christ's ministry
--------------------------------

[LUK 9:58](https://ref.ly/Luke9:58) *Jesus Christ had no permanent home.*; [LUK 10:38–10:42](https://ref.ly/Luke10:38-Luke10:42), [LUK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1); [MRK 5:18–5:20](https://ref.ly/Mark5:18-Mark5:20) parallel [LUK 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Luke8:38-Luke8:39) *The healed demoniac is sent back to his home town and family to be a witness.*; [MAT 10:11–10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:11-Matt10:14) parallels [MRK 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:10-Mark6:11), [LUK 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:4-Luke9:5); ; [LUK 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:5-Luke10:7); [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29) parallels [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30), [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30)

Homelessness
------------

[1CO 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:11) *Paul had no permanent home*; ; [JOB 24:1–24:12](https://ref.ly/Job24:1-Job24:12) *the suffering of the homeless*; ; [HEB 11:8–11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:10) *People of faith do not see this world as their final home.*

Home in a spiritual and figurative sense
----------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:2](https://ref.ly/John14:2), [JHN 14:23](https://ref.ly/John14:23)** *See also* [JOB 17:13–17:15](https://ref.ly/Job17:13-Job17:15); [ECC 12:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:5); [LUK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Luke16:9); [2CO 5:1–5:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:1-2Cor5:10); [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13)

## 5340: house (id: 5340)

The place where someone lives. Its extended use includes a household and dynasty and its figurative use includes the house of God and heaven.

The construction of houses
--------------------------

### Foundations

[1KI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:17); ; [EZR 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:3-Ezra6:4); ; [JER 51:26](https://ref.ly/Jer51:26); ; [EZK 41:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:8)

### Building materials

[GEN 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:3-Gen11:4); ; [EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14); ; [LEV 14:40–14:45](https://ref.ly/Lev14:40-Lev14:45); ; [1KI 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:18), [1KI 7:9–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9-1Kgs7:12); ; [SNG 1:17](https://ref.ly/Song1:17); ; [ISA 9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:10)

### Rooms and decoration

[GEN 43:30](https://ref.ly/Gen43:30); ; [JDG 3:20](https://ref.ly/Judg3:20), [JDG 3:23–3:25](https://ref.ly/Judg3:23-Judg3:25); ; [1KI 7:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:6) *Solomon's palace*; ; [1KI 17:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:19) *Elijah stays with the widow at Zarephath*; ; [1KI 22:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:25); ; [2KI 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:2), [2KI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:10) *Elisha's room at the Shunammite's house*; ; [NEH 8:16](https://ref.ly/Neh8:16); ; [JER 22:14](https://ref.ly/Jer22:14); ; [EZK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:10); ; [DAN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5); ; [AMO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Amos3:15); ; [ACT 20:8–20:9](https://ref.ly/Acts20:8-Acts20:9)

### Roofs

[JOS 2:6](https://ref.ly/Josh2:6); ; [1SA 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:25-1Sam9:26); ; [MRK 2:4](https://ref.ly/Mark2:4) parallel [LUK 5:19](https://ref.ly/Luke5:19)

Regulations regarding houses
----------------------------

[LEV 14:33–14:53](https://ref.ly/Lev14:33-Lev14:53), [LEV 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Lev25:29-Lev25:34), [LEV 27:14–27:15](https://ref.ly/Lev27:14-Lev27:15); ; [DEU 22:8](https://ref.ly/Deut22:8)

Various uses for houses
-----------------------

### House arrest

**[JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15)** *See also* [GEN 40:2–40:3](https://ref.ly/Gen40:2-Gen40:3); [2SA 20:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:3); [ACT 28:16](https://ref.ly/Acts28:16), [ACT 28:30–28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:30-Acts28:31)

### Church gatherings

**[COL 4:15](https://ref.ly/Col4:15)** *See also* [ACT 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Acts1:13-Acts1:14), [ACT 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:2), [ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42), [ACT 12:12](https://ref.ly/Acts12:12); [ROM 16:5](https://ref.ly/Rom16:5); [1CO 16:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:19); [PHM 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:1-Phlm1:2)

Parables and proverbs about houses
----------------------------------

**[PRO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Prov21:20)** *See also* [PRO 9:1–9:4](https://ref.ly/Prov9:1-Prov9:4) *the house of wisdom*; [PRO 9:13–9:18](https://ref.ly/Prov9:13-Prov9:18) *the house of folly*; [PRO 14:1](https://ref.ly/Prov14:1), [PRO 17:13](https://ref.ly/Prov17:13), [PRO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Prov21:9), [PRO 24:27](https://ref.ly/Prov24:27), [PRO 25:17](https://ref.ly/Prov25:17); [ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18); [SNG 8:7](https://ref.ly/Song8:7); [MAT 12:29](https://ref.ly/Matt12:29) parallels [MRK 3:27](https://ref.ly/Mark3:27), [LUK 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/Luke11:21-Luke11:22) *plundering the strong man's house*; [MAT 12:43–12:45](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43-Matt12:45) parallel [LUK 11:24–11:26](https://ref.ly/Luke11:24-Luke11:26) *the return of evil spirits to an unoccupied house*; [MAT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Matt13:52), [MAT 24:42–24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:42-Matt24:51) parallel [LUK 12:39–12:46](https://ref.ly/Luke12:39-Luke12:46)

The house of God
----------------

### God's heavenly dwelling

**[ISA 66:1–66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:1-Isa66:2)** *See also* [1KI 8:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:30) parallel [2CH 6:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:21); [1KI 8:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:39) parallel [2CH 6:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:30); [1KI 8:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:43) parallel [2CH 6:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:33); [1KI 8:49–8:50](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:49-1Kgs8:50) parallel [2CH 6:39](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:39); [ISA 57:15](https://ref.ly/Isa57:15); [JHN 14:2](https://ref.ly/John14:2)

### The earthly tabernacle and temple

**[EXO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Exod25:8)** *See also* [EXO 15:17](https://ref.ly/Exod15:17), [EXO 29:44–29:46](https://ref.ly/Exod29:44-Exod29:46) *the Tent of Meeting*; [DEU 12:5](https://ref.ly/Deut12:5), [DEU 12:11](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11); [2SA 7:1–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:1-2Sam7:7) parallel [1CH 17:1–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:1-1Chr17:6) *David's desire to build a permanent house for God to dwell in*; [EZR 5:13–5:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:13-Ezra5:16); [PSA 23:6](https://ref.ly/Ps23:6), [PSA 26:8](https://ref.ly/Ps26:8), [PSA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4), [PSA 84:1–84:4](https://ref.ly/Ps84:1-Ps84:4), [PSA 84:10](https://ref.ly/Ps84:10); [HAG 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Hag1:2-Hag1:3), [HAG 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:8-Hag1:9), [HAG 1:14](https://ref.ly/Hag1:14)

### The church as God's household

## 5341: hunger (id: 5341)

A state of emptiness, reflecting a lack of physical or spiritual food. Scripture recognises the physical aspects of human hunger which can only be met by the living God.

Kinds of hunger
---------------

### Physical

**[DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3)**

### Spiritual

**[MAT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6)** *See also* [PSA 63:1](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1), [PSA 63:5](https://ref.ly/Ps63:5), [PSA 107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:9); [AMO 8:11](https://ref.ly/Amos8:11); [LUK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Luke6:21); [JHN 6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:35); [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2)

Causes of hunger
----------------

### Famine

[GEN 41:55](https://ref.ly/Gen41:55); ; [2KI 6:25–6:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25-2Kgs6:29); ; [NEH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:2)

### Fasting

[MAT 4:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:2) parallel [LUK 4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:2)

### Fatigue

[1SA 21:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:3); ; [2SA 17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:29); ; [MAT 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:4); ; [MRK 6:35–6:36](https://ref.ly/Mark6:35-Mark6:36), [MRK 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Mark8:2-Mark8:3)

### Folly

[PRO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Prov19:15), [PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4); ; [LUK 15:14](https://ref.ly/Luke15:14)

### God's action

[LEV 26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:26); ; [DEU 28:48](https://ref.ly/Deut28:48); ; [ISA 5:13](https://ref.ly/Isa5:13), [ISA 9:20](https://ref.ly/Isa9:20); ; [EZK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:19)

### The hardships of gospel work

[1CO 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:11); ; [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5), [2CO 11:27](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:27); ; [PHP 4:12](https://ref.ly/Phil4:12)

Effects of hunger
-----------------

### Physical deterioration

[JOB 30:3](https://ref.ly/Job30:3); ; [LAM 2:19](https://ref.ly/Lam2:19), [LAM 4:9](https://ref.ly/Lam4:9)

### Mental preoccupation

[ISA 29:8](https://ref.ly/Isa29:8); ; [ACT 10:10–10:13](https://ref.ly/Acts10:10-Acts10:13) *Peter's consciousness was supernaturally heightened to receive a vision from God.*

### Moral degradation

[LEV 26:29](https://ref.ly/Lev26:29); ; [DEU 28:53](https://ref.ly/Deut28:53); ; [2KI 6:28–6:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:28-2Kgs6:29); ; [JER 19:9](https://ref.ly/Jer19:9); ; [LAM 2:20](https://ref.ly/Lam2:20), [LAM 4:10](https://ref.ly/Lam4:10); ; [EZK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:10)

### An occasion for temptation

[GEN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6) *Eve*; ; [GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34) *Esau*; ; [PRO 6:30–6:31](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30-Prov6:31); ; [ISA 8:21](https://ref.ly/Isa8:21); ; [MAT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Matt4:3) parallel [LUK 4:3](https://ref.ly/Luke4:3) *Jesus Christ*

### An opportunity for proving God

[DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3); ; [NEH 9:15](https://ref.ly/Neh9:15); ; [PHP 4:12](https://ref.ly/Phil4:12)

God satisfies hunger
--------------------

### He provides food for all creation

**[PSA 107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:9), [PSA 145:15–145:16](https://ref.ly/Ps145:15-Ps145:16)** *See also* [DEU 32:13–32:14](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13-Deut32:14); [PSA 17:14](https://ref.ly/Ps17:14), [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25), [PSA 104:21](https://ref.ly/Ps104:21), [PSA 104:27–104:28](https://ref.ly/Ps104:27-Ps104:28), [PSA 111:5](https://ref.ly/Ps111:5), [PSA 132:15](https://ref.ly/Ps132:15), [PSA 136:25](https://ref.ly/Ps136:25), [PSA 146:7](https://ref.ly/Ps146:7), [PSA 147:9](https://ref.ly/Ps147:9); [ISA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Isa49:10)

### He provides through supernatural means

**[PSA 78:24–78:25](https://ref.ly/Ps78:24-Ps78:25)** *See also* [EXO 16:12–16:15](https://ref.ly/Exod16:12-Exod16:15); [1KI 17:4–17:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:4-1Kgs17:16); [NEH 9:15](https://ref.ly/Neh9:15); [MAT 14:19–14:21](https://ref.ly/Matt14:19-Matt14:21) parallels [MRK 6:41–6:44](https://ref.ly/Mark6:41-Mark6:44), [LUK 9:16–9:17](https://ref.ly/Luke9:16-Luke9:17), [JHN 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/John6:11-John6:14) *the feeding of the 5,000*; [MRK 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Mark8:7-Mark8:8)

### He provides through natural means

[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19); ; [JOS 5:12](https://ref.ly/Josh5:12); ; [PRO 16:26](https://ref.ly/Prov16:26); ; [ISA 58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:7); ; [MAT 25:35](https://ref.ly/Matt25:35); ; [2TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10)

### He provides completely in heaven

[REV 7:16](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16)

Jesus Christ was hungry
-----------------------

[MAT 4:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:2) parallel [LUK 4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:2); ; [MAT 21:18](https://ref.ly/Matt21:18) parallel [MRK 11:12](https://ref.ly/Mark11:12); ; [JHN 4:31](https://ref.ly/John4:31)

## 5342: hunting (id: 5342)

Hunters who pursue a prey, literally or figuratively
----------------------------------------------------

### Human beings

**[GEN 10:9](https://ref.ly/Gen10:9)**; **[PSA 91:3](https://ref.ly/Ps91:3)** *See also* [GEN 25:27](https://ref.ly/Gen25:27), [GEN 27:3](https://ref.ly/Gen27:3), [GEN 49:9](https://ref.ly/Gen49:9), [GEN 49:27](https://ref.ly/Gen49:27); [LEV 17:13](https://ref.ly/Lev17:13); [1SA 24:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:11); [PSA 124:7](https://ref.ly/Ps124:7)

### Lions

**[JOB 38:39](https://ref.ly/Job38:39)** *See also* [NUM 23:24](https://ref.ly/Num23:24); [JOB 4:11](https://ref.ly/Job4:11); [PSA 17:12](https://ref.ly/Ps17:12), [PSA 22:13](https://ref.ly/Ps22:13), [PSA 104:21](https://ref.ly/Ps104:21); [ISA 5:29](https://ref.ly/Isa5:29); [EZK 19:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:6), [EZK 22:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:25); [AMO 3:4](https://ref.ly/Amos3:4); [NAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Nah2:12)

### Eagles

**[JOB 9:26](https://ref.ly/Job9:26)** *See also* [JOB 39:27–39:30](https://ref.ly/Job39:27-Job39:30)

### God

**[AMO 9:3](https://ref.ly/Amos9:3)** *See also* [JOB 19:22](https://ref.ly/Job19:22); [JER 16:16](https://ref.ly/Jer16:16); [EZK 12:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:13)

People being hunted by their enemies
------------------------------------

**[PSA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ps10:2)**; **[JER 5:26](https://ref.ly/Jer5:26)** *See also* [GEN 31:36](https://ref.ly/Gen31:36); [1SA 23:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:23), [1SA 26:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:20); [JOB 18:8–18:10](https://ref.ly/Job18:8-Job18:10); [PSA 119:110](https://ref.ly/Ps119:110), [PSA 140:5](https://ref.ly/Ps140:5), [PSA 140:11](https://ref.ly/Ps140:11), [PSA 141:9](https://ref.ly/Ps141:9); [PRO 6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:5); [ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12); [ISA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:2), [ISA 13:14](https://ref.ly/Isa13:14), [ISA 59:15](https://ref.ly/Isa59:15); [LAM 3:52](https://ref.ly/Lam3:52); [MIC 7:2](https://ref.ly/Mic7:2)

## 5343: idleness (id: 5343)

Laziness or unproductiveness. As work is hallowed by God's working, idleness is regarded as a sin against oneself and society.

Idleness reproached
-------------------

### In terms of daily labour

**[1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)** *Paul to the Christians at Thessalonica.* *See also* [PRO 19:24](https://ref.ly/Prov19:24), [PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4), [PRO 22:13](https://ref.ly/Prov22:13), [PRO 26:13–26:16](https://ref.ly/Prov26:13-Prov26:16); [2TH 3:6–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:6-2Thess3:15); [1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13); [TIT 1:12](https://ref.ly/Titus1:12)

### In matters of faith

**[HEB 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:11-Heb6:12)** *To Christians who were tempted to give up.*

Idleness discouraged by examples of industriousness
---------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:8)** *See also* [PRO 31:27](https://ref.ly/Prov31:27) *the good wife*; [ECC 11:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:6); [2TH 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:7-2Thess3:9) *Paul referring to his practice of not depending on others for his living*

The results of idleness
-----------------------

**[PRO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:9-Prov6:11)** *See also* [PRO 10:26](https://ref.ly/Prov10:26), [PRO 12:24](https://ref.ly/Prov12:24), [PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4), [PRO 15:19](https://ref.ly/Prov15:19), [PRO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Prov19:15), [PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4); [ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Thessalonians 5:14; Proverbs 19:24; Proverbs 10:4; Proverbs 22:13; Proverbs 26:13-16; 2 Thessalonians 3:6-15; 1 Timothy 5:13; Titus 1:12; Hebrews 6:11-12; Proverbs 6:6-8; Proverbs 31:27; Ecclesiastes 11:6; 2 Thessalonians 3:7-9; Proverbs 6:9-11; Proverbs 10:26; Proverbs 12:24; Proverbs 13:4; Proverbs 15:19; Proverbs 19:15; Proverbs 20:4; Ecclesiastes 10:18
* **Associated Articles:** 5446: poverty (ID: `5446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5524: servants, bad (ID: `5524@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5534: sleep, spiritual (ID: `5534@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5539: sluggard (ID: `5539@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5575: talk, idle (ID: `5575@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5628: work (ID: `5628@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5833: diligence (ID: `5833@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5868: gossip (ID: `5868@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8783: neglect (ID: `8783@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5344: imprisonment (id: 5344)

Being confined in prison is used both literally and figuratively in Scripture. Release from prison is used as a picture of freedom from spiritual bondage.

The anticipation or threat of imprisonment
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26)** parallel [LUK 12:58](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58) *See also* [ACT 20:23](https://ref.ly/Acts20:23); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

Imprisonment for law\-breaking
------------------------------

**[EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26)** *See also* [GEN 40:3](https://ref.ly/Gen40:3); [2KI 17:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:4); [JER 52:11](https://ref.ly/Jer52:11); [MAT 18:30](https://ref.ly/Matt18:30)

Imprisonment while on remand
----------------------------

**[NUM 15:33–15:34](https://ref.ly/Num15:33-Num15:34)** *See also* [LEV 24:12](https://ref.ly/Lev24:12); [MAT 27:16](https://ref.ly/Matt27:16) parallels [MRK 15:7](https://ref.ly/Mark15:7), [LUK 23:19](https://ref.ly/Luke23:19)

Imprisonment of the innocent, often with malicious intent
---------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 38:28](https://ref.ly/Jer38:28)**; **[LUK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12)** *See also* [GEN 39:20–39:22](https://ref.ly/Gen39:20-Gen39:22), [GEN 40:14–40:15](https://ref.ly/Gen40:14-Gen40:15), [GEN 42:16–42:17](https://ref.ly/Gen42:16-Gen42:17) *Joseph planning the welfare of his brothers*; [1KI 22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27) *Ahab orders Micaiah's imprisonment*; [2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10); [JER 32:2–32:3](https://ref.ly/Jer32:2-Jer32:3), [JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15); [MAT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3) *John the Baptist imprisoned by Herod*; [ACT 8:3](https://ref.ly/Acts8:3), [ACT 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/Acts16:23-Acts16:24) *Paul and Silas in prison*; [ACT 22:4](https://ref.ly/Acts22:4), [ACT 22:19](https://ref.ly/Acts22:19), [ACT 23:29](https://ref.ly/Acts23:29), [ACT 24:27](https://ref.ly/Acts24:27), [ACT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10), [ACT 26:31](https://ref.ly/Acts26:31); [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5), [2CO 11:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23); [HEB 11:36](https://ref.ly/Heb11:36)

Release from imprisonment
-------------------------

### By earthly rulers

[GEN 41:14](https://ref.ly/Gen41:14); ; [2KI 25:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:27) parallel [JER 52:31](https://ref.ly/Jer52:31); ; [JER 39:13–39:14](https://ref.ly/Jer39:13-Jer39:14)

### By divine intervention

**[PSA 146:7](https://ref.ly/Ps146:7)** *See also* [PSA 68:6](https://ref.ly/Ps68:6), [PSA 102:20](https://ref.ly/Ps102:20), [PSA 107:10–107:14](https://ref.ly/Ps107:10-Ps107:14), [PSA 142:7](https://ref.ly/Ps142:7); [ISA 42:7](https://ref.ly/Isa42:7), [ISA 49:9](https://ref.ly/Isa49:9), [ISA 51:14](https://ref.ly/Isa51:14), [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [ACT 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Acts12:1-Acts12:11)

Imprisonment used in a figurative or poetic sense
-------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18)** *See also* [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [JOB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Job11:10), [JOB 12:14](https://ref.ly/Job12:14); [PSA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Ps66:11); [ECC 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:14); [ROM 7:23](https://ref.ly/Rom7:23); [GAL 3:22–3:23](https://ref.ly/Gal3:22-Gal3:23); [1PE 3:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:19); [REV 20:7](https://ref.ly/Rev20:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:25-26; Luke 12:58; Acts 20:23; Revelation 2:10; Ezra 7:26; Genesis 40:3; 2 Kings 17:4; Jeremiah 52:11; Matthew 18:30; Numbers 15:33-34; Leviticus 24:12; Matthew 27:16; Mark 15:7; Luke 23:19; Jeremiah 38:28; Luke 21:12; Genesis 39:20-22; Genesis 40:14-15; Genesis 42:16-17; 1 Kings 22:27; 2 Chronicles 16:10; Jeremiah 32:2-3; Jeremiah 37:15; Matthew 14:3; Acts 8:3; Acts 16:23-24; Acts 22:4; Acts 22:19; Acts 23:29; Acts 24:27; Acts 26:10; Acts 26:31; 2 Corinthians 6:5; 2 Corinthians 11:23; Hebrews 11:36; Genesis 41:14; 2 Kings 25:27; Jeremiah 52:31; Jeremiah 39:13-14; Psalms 146:7; Psalms 68:6; Psalms 102:20; Psalms 107:10-14; Psalms 142:7; Isaiah 42:7; Isaiah 49:9; Isaiah 51:14; Isaiah 61:1; Acts 12:1-11; Luke 4:18; Job 11:10; Job 12:14; Psalms 66:11; Ecclesiastes 4:14; Romans 7:23; Galatians 3:22-23; 1 Peter 3:19; Revelation 20:7

## 5345: influence (id: 5345)

The ability to affect others or events. Also, the experience of being affected by someone or something else. Scripture recognises the wide\-ranging power of influence for both good and evil.

Teaching on influence
---------------------

### Exhortations to be influenced by good and to influence others for good

**[MAT 5:13–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:13-Matt5:16)** *See also* [PRO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8-Prov1:9), [PRO 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Prov7:1-Prov7:5), [PRO 25:15](https://ref.ly/Prov25:15); [PHP 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Phil4:8-Phil4:9)

### Exhortations to resist evil influence and not to be a bad influence

[DEU 4:15–4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:15-Deut4:19); ; [PRO 5:1–5:14](https://ref.ly/Prov5:1-Prov5:14), [PRO 6:20–6:26](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:26); ; [MAT 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Matt18:5-Matt18:6); ; [ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2), [ROM 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rom14:13); ; [1CO 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:6-1Cor5:8), [1CO 8:9–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9-1Cor8:13); ; [2CO 6:14–6:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14-2Cor6:18); ; [2TI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:14); ; [JAS 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jas4:7)

Good influence in its various contexts
--------------------------------------

### The influence of the Holy Spirit

[ACT 2:1–2:13](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:13), [ACT 8:26–8:40](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26-Acts8:40), [ACT 16:6–16:10](https://ref.ly/Acts16:6-Acts16:10); ; [ROM 8:5–8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5-Rom8:17), [ROM 8:26–8:27](https://ref.ly/Rom8:26-Rom8:27); ; [1CO 2:10–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:10-1Cor2:16), [1CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:3)

### People of good influence

**[2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:5)** *See also* [GEN 41:41–41:43](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41-Gen41:43); [1KI 10:1–10:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1-1Kgs10:9), [1KI 15:11–15:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:11-1Kgs15:14); [2KI 18:1–18:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:1-2Kgs18:8), [2KI 23:1–23:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:1-2Kgs23:25)

Evil influence in its various contexts
--------------------------------------

### People of evil influence

**[2KI 9:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:22)** *See also* [MAT 15:1–15:14](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:14), [MAT 16:5–16:12](https://ref.ly/Matt16:5-Matt16:12), [MAT 23:13–23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:13-Matt23:15), [MAT 27:20](https://ref.ly/Matt27:20); [2CO 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3-2Cor11:4), [2CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13-2Cor11:15); [2PE 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1-2Pet2:3)

### Corrupting influence of evil cultures

[JOS 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Josh23:6-Josh23:8), [JOS 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12-Josh23:13); ; [JDG 2:10–2:13](https://ref.ly/Judg2:10-Judg2:13); ; [1KI 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:6); ; [EZR 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:1-Ezra9:2); ; [NEH 13:15–13:27](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15-Neh13:27); ; [1CO 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:1-1Cor12:2), [1CO 15:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:33)

### The influence of Satan

**[MAT 16:23](https://ref.ly/Matt16:23)** *See also* [MRK 1:23–1:26](https://ref.ly/Mark1:23-Mark1:26) parallel [LUK 4:33–4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:33-Luke4:35); [MRK 4:15](https://ref.ly/Mark4:15) parallels [MAT 13:19](https://ref.ly/Matt13:19), [LUK 8:12](https://ref.ly/Luke8:12); [LUK 22:3–22:4](https://ref.ly/Luke22:3-Luke22:4); [JHN 13:27–13:30](https://ref.ly/John13:27-John13:30); [2CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7)

Further aspects of influence
----------------------------

### The influence of prayer

[GEN 18:20–18:33](https://ref.ly/Gen18:20-Gen18:33); ; [EXO 32:9–32:14](https://ref.ly/Exod32:9-Exod32:14); ; [2CH 7:13–7:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:13-2Chr7:15); ; [DAN 9:1–9:23](https://ref.ly/Dan9:1-Dan9:23); ; [JON 3:3–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:3-Jonah3:10)

### The influence of words

**[PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18)** *See also* [PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4), [PRO 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/Prov16:23-Prov16:24), [PRO 28:23](https://ref.ly/Prov28:23); [JAS 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Jas3:5-Jas3:6)

### The influence of parents

**[PRO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8-Prov1:9)** *See also* [2CH 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:2-2Chr22:3); [PRO 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1-Prov4:4), [PRO 6:20–6:22](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:22); [2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:5)

## 5346: injury (id: 5346)

Physical or mental hurt, usually resulting from the deliberate actions or words of others.

Physical injury
---------------

**[JDG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Judg1:6)** *See also* [GEN 32:25](https://ref.ly/Gen32:25); [LUK 10:30](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30); [2CO 11:23–11:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:25)

### Reparation must be made for physical injury

[EXO 21:19](https://ref.ly/Exod21:19), [EXO 21:22](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22); ; [LEV 24:17–24:22](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17-Lev24:22)

### Jesus Christ suffered physical injury

[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21); ; [JHN 19:1](https://ref.ly/John19:1)

### God's people may suffer physical injury for his sake

[JER 38:6](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6); ; [GAL 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gal6:17); ; [COL 1:24](https://ref.ly/Col1:24); ; [1PE 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:19)

Mental injury
-------------

### Injury by slander

[LEV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Lev19:16); ; [PRO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Prov30:10); ; [MAT 5:11](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11); ; [TIT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Titus3:2); ; [JAS 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11); ; [1PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1)

### Injury by injustice

[PSA 58:1–58:2](https://ref.ly/Ps58:1-Ps58:2); ; [ECC 3:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:16); ; [ISA 59:4–59:6](https://ref.ly/Isa59:4-Isa59:6); ; [EZK 34:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:4)

### Jesus Christ suffered mental injury

[MAT 26:59–26:60](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:60); ; [1PE 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:20-1Pet2:23)

### God's people may suffer mental injury for his sake

**[MAT 5:11](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11)** *See also* [PSA 38:20](https://ref.ly/Ps38:20); [JER 15:15](https://ref.ly/Jer15:15); [1TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2); [1PE 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:19)

All deliberate injury ruled out by love
---------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:10)**

Spiritual injury suffered by God's people
-----------------------------------------

*See also* [ISA 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:5-Isa1:6); [JER 10:19](https://ref.ly/Jer10:19), [JER 30:12](https://ref.ly/Jer30:12); [HOS 6:1](https://ref.ly/Hos6:1) *Sometimes this is God's judgment.*

## 5347: injustice (id: 5347)

The unnecessary and unjustified ill treatment of others, through false accusation or through the inflicting of pain or punishment. Having its origin in Satan, it works through the sinful human heart and shows itself in every sphere of life. It merits the wrath of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 5348: injustice, nature and source of (ID: `5348@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5349: injustice, examples of (ID: `5349@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5350: injustice, hated by God (ID: `5350@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5348: injustice, nature and source of (id: 5348)

Characterised by deception, dishonesty and heartlessness, injustice is the oppression of the needy and the weak. Its origin is Satan and it works through the sinful hearts of men and women. **[PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6)**

The nature of injustice
-----------------------

### Injustice seen in false accusation

*See also* [GEN 39:19–39:20](https://ref.ly/Gen39:19-Gen39:20); [1KI 21:8–21:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:10)

### Injustice seen in dishonesty

[1SA 8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:3); ; [ISA 59:14](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14); ; [MAT 26:59–26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:61); ; [ACT 24:26–24:27](https://ref.ly/Acts24:26-Acts24:27)

### Injustice seen in pain inficted on the innocent

[MAT 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16-Matt2:18), [MAT 27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26); ; [ACT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Acts4:3), [ACT 23:3](https://ref.ly/Acts23:3)

### Injustice seen in oppression of the poor, the needy and the helpless

[EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14); ; [1SA 8:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11); ; [2CH 10:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:14); ; [JOB 31:13–31:14](https://ref.ly/Job31:13-Job31:14); ; [PSA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ps10:2), [PSA 10:8–10:11](https://ref.ly/Ps10:8-Ps10:11), [PSA 105:17–105:18](https://ref.ly/Ps105:17-Ps105:18); ; [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16); ; [ISA 5:8](https://ref.ly/Isa5:8); ; [JER 22:13](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13); ; [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); ; [MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40); ; [JAS 5:4–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4-Jas5:6)

The sources of injustice
------------------------

### The devil

**[1JN 3:10](https://ref.ly/1John3:10)** *The word “devil” means “slanderer”.* *See also* [JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10); [2TH 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:9-2Thess2:11) *The word “Satan” means “adversary”\-\-the same person as the devil*; [JAS 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:15); [REV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Rev2:13)

### The world

**[1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)** *“the world” here means society without God and hostile to him.* *See also* [EXO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:2); [PSA 69:4](https://ref.ly/Ps69:4); [PRO 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:5); [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12); [MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12); [JHN 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:18-John15:19)

### Sinful hearts

**[MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19)** *See also* [PSA 140:2](https://ref.ly/Ps140:2); [JER 6:13–6:15](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13-Jer6:15); [MIC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:2); [GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 64:6; Genesis 39:19-20; 1 Kings 21:8-10; 1 Samuel 8:3; Isaiah 59:14; Matthew 26:59-61; Acts 24:26-27; Matthew 2:16-18; Matthew 27:26; Acts 4:3; Acts 23:3; Exodus 1:11-14; 1 Samuel 8:11; 2 Chronicles 10:14; Job 31:13-14; Psalms 10:2; Psalms 10:8-11; Psalms 105:17-18; Proverbs 22:16; Isaiah 5:8; Jeremiah 22:13; Amos 5:11; Mark 12:38-40; James 5:4-6; 1 John 3:10; John 8:44; Revelation 2:10; 2 Thessalonians 2:9-11; James 3:14-15; Revelation 2:13; 1 John 2:16; Exodus 23:2; Psalms 69:4; Proverbs 31:4-5; Ezekiel 22:12; Matthew 5:11-12; John 15:18-19; Matthew 15:19; Psalms 140:2; Jeremiah 6:13-15; Micah 2:2; Galatians 5:19-21
* **Associated Articles:** 4025: world, the (ID: `4025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4120: Satan (ID: `4120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5012: heart (ID: `5012@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5446: poverty (ID: `5446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5562: suffering, of the innocent (ID: `5562@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5823: cruelty (ID: `5823@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5831: depression (ID: `5831@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8277: innocence (ID: `8277@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5349: injustice, examples of (id: 5349)

Injustice can be found, in varying degrees of intensity, in every area of human relationships. It produces exploitation and suffering, both mental and physical.

Injustice occurs in family life
-------------------------------

**[MRK 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9-Mark7:13)** parallel [MAT 15:3–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:6) *See also* [GEN 27:35](https://ref.ly/Gen27:35); [LEV 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9-Lev20:10); [MAL 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:13-Mal2:14); [1CO 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:4-1Cor7:5); [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4); [COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19), [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

Injustice occurs in the community
---------------------------------

**[ISA 59:14–59:15](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14-Isa59:15)** *See also* [LEV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Lev19:16); [DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14), [DEU 23:24–23:25](https://ref.ly/Deut23:24-Deut23:25), [DEU 24:6–24:7](https://ref.ly/Deut24:6-Deut24:7); [PSA 101:5](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5); [PRO 3:28–3:30](https://ref.ly/Prov3:28-Prov3:30), [PRO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Prov22:28), [PRO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10-Prov23:11); [JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11)

Injustice occurs in the business world
--------------------------------------

**[HOS 12:7](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7)** *See also* [LEV 19:35](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35); [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1); [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)

Injustice occurs in courts of law
---------------------------------

**[PRO 24:23–24:24](https://ref.ly/Prov24:23-Prov24:24)** *See also* [PSA 26:10](https://ref.ly/Ps26:10); [PRO 14:5](https://ref.ly/Prov14:5), [PRO 28:21](https://ref.ly/Prov28:21); [ISA 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Isa5:22-Isa5:23), [ISA 29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:21); [HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4); [AMO 5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:12); [HAB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:4); [MAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mal2:9); [ACT 16:36–16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:36-Acts16:37)

Injustice can be caused by rulers and governments
-------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2)** *See also* [EXO 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Exod1:10-Exod1:11); [PSA 58:1–58:2](https://ref.ly/Ps58:1-Ps58:2), [PSA 94:20–94:21](https://ref.ly/Ps94:20-Ps94:21); [PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4), [PRO 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:5); [ECC 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:8-Eccl5:9); [ISA 1:21](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21); [MIC 3:8](https://ref.ly/Mic3:8); [HAB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hab2:9)**[LUK 23:4–23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:4-Luke23:25)** parallels [MAT 27:15–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:15-Matt27:26), [MRK 15:6–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:6-Mark15:15), [JHN 18:38–18:40](https://ref.ly/John18:38-John18:40) *See also* [ROM 13:1–13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:4) *the proper role of governing authorities*

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 7:9-13; Matthew 15:3-6; Genesis 27:35; Leviticus 20:9-10; Malachi 2:13-14; 1 Corinthians 7:4-5; Ephesians 6:4; Colossians 3:19; Colossians 3:21; Isaiah 59:14-15; Leviticus 19:16; Deuteronomy 19:14; Deuteronomy 23:24-25; Deuteronomy 24:6-7; Psalms 101:5; Proverbs 3:28-30; Proverbs 22:28; Proverbs 23:10-11; Jeremiah 17:11; Hosea 12:7; Leviticus 19:35; Proverbs 11:1; 1 Timothy 6:10; Proverbs 24:23-24; Psalms 26:10; Proverbs 14:5; Proverbs 28:21; Isaiah 5:22-23; Isaiah 29:21; Hosea 10:4; Amos 5:12; Habakkuk 1:4; Malachi 2:9; Acts 16:36-37; Isaiah 10:1-2; Exodus 1:10-11; Psalms 58:1-2; Psalms 94:20-21; Proverbs 29:4; Proverbs 31:4-5; Ecclesiastes 5:8-9; Isaiah 1:21; Micah 3:8; Habakkuk 2:9; Luke 23:4-25; Matthew 27:15-26; Mark 15:6-15; John 18:38-40; Romans 13:1-4

## 5350: injustice, hated by God (id: 5350)

God's hatred of injustice is seen in his vindication of its victims, his solemn warnings, his penalties against the guilty and his commands to his people to renounce it.

God hates injustice
-------------------

**[PRO 6:16–6:19](https://ref.ly/Prov6:16-Prov6:19)** *See also* [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1), [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22), [PRO 17:15](https://ref.ly/Prov17:15)

God will vindicate the victims of injustice
-------------------------------------------

### They cry for justice

**[PSA 43:1](https://ref.ly/Ps43:1)** *There are some OT passages where vengeance upon persecutors is called for (e.g., [Ps 35:1–8](https://ref.ly/Ps35:1-Ps35:8); [Ps 69:22–28](https://ref.ly/Ps69:22-Ps69:28); [Jer 11:18–20](https://ref.ly/Jer11:18-Jer11:20); [18:19–23](https://ref.ly/Jer18:19-Jer18:23)). These are called “imprecatory”. They arose from zeal for God's honour and from the desire for moral stability in society. They reflect an under\-developed view of God's ultimate judgments and the failure to distinguish between the guilty and their innocent families. In the light of the NT the motives behind these prayers may be applauded but it is recognised that now they are best expressed in the denunciation of injustice accompanied by gospel witness, through which the hearts of oppressors are changed, leaving the matter of judgment as God's prerogative alone.* *See also* [NEH 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Neh4:4-Neh4:5); [PSA 94:1–94:7](https://ref.ly/Ps94:1-Ps94:7); [REV 6:10](https://ref.ly/Rev6:10); [JOB 19:25](https://ref.ly/Job19:25) *In OT times a redeemer was a near relative responsible for securing the just rights of a poor kinsperson.*

### God will answer their cry

**[LUK 18:1–18:8](https://ref.ly/Luke18:1-Luke18:8)** *See also* [PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5), [PSA 94:20–94:23](https://ref.ly/Ps94:20-Ps94:23); [2TH 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:6-2Thess1:7); [2TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:14); [2PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:9); [REV 18:20](https://ref.ly/Rev18:20), [REV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Rev19:2)

### God condemns injustice

**[ISA 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:3)** *See also* [DEU 16:19](https://ref.ly/Deut16:19); [AMO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3-Amos1:4)

### God will punish the unjust

**[MIC 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1-Mic2:3)** *See also* [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17), [DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19); [1KI 21:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:19); [PRO 19:5](https://ref.ly/Prov19:5), [PRO 21:28](https://ref.ly/Prov21:28); [AMO 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11-Amos5:12), [AMO 8:4–8:7](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:7); [NAM 3:1](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); [LUK 12:45–12:461](https://ref.ly/Luke12:45-Luke12:461)CO [6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Luke6:6-Luke6:8); [1TH 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:6)[JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6); [REV 1:7](https://ref.ly/Rev1:7)

### The apparent present success of the unjust

**[PSA 37:1–37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:1-Ps37:2)** *See also* [PSA 73:3–73:11](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:11), [PSA 73:15–73:17](https://ref.ly/Ps73:15-Ps73:17); [ISA 26:10–26:11](https://ref.ly/Isa26:10-Isa26:11); [HAB 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:12-Hab1:13)

God's people must hate injustice
--------------------------------

### Injustice forbidden by God

**[PRO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22)** *See also* [EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16), [EXO 22:21–22:22](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21-Exod22:22), [EXO 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6-Exod23:8); [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13), [LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15), [LEV 19:35](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35); [DEU 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14-Deut24:15), [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17); [EZK 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:9); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10); [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)

### Injustice should be renounced by God's people

**[PSA 119:163](https://ref.ly/Ps119:163)** *See also* [DEU 24:7](https://ref.ly/Deut24:7); [PSA 82:2](https://ref.ly/Ps82:2); [PRO 29:27](https://ref.ly/Prov29:27); [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

### Injustice displaced by love

[MRK 12:31](https://ref.ly/Mark12:31); ; [ROM 13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:10); ; [COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19)

### God's people must bear injustice patiently

**[JAS 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7-Jas5:8)** *See also* [MAT 5:39](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39), [MAT 5:44–5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:44-Matt5:45); [1CO 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 6:16-19; Proverbs 11:1; Proverbs 12:22; Proverbs 17:15; Psalms 43:1; Psalms 35:1-8; Psalms 69:22-28; Jeremiah 11:18-20; Jeremiah 18:19-23; Nehemiah 4:4-5; Psalms 94:1-7; Revelation 6:10; Job 19:25; Luke 18:1-8; Psalms 12:5; Psalms 94:20-23; 2 Thessalonians 1:6-7; 2 Timothy 4:14; 2 Peter 2:9; Revelation 18:20; Revelation 19:2; Isaiah 10:1-3; Deuteronomy 16:19; Amos 1:3-4; Micah 2:1-3; Deuteronomy 27:17; Deuteronomy 27:19; 1 Kings 21:19; Proverbs 19:5; Proverbs 21:28; Amos 5:11-12; Amos 8:4-7; Nahum 3:1; Malachi 3:5; Luke 12:45-461; Luke 6:6-8; 1 Thessalonians 4:6; James 5:1-6; Revelation 1:7; Psalms 37:1-2; Psalms 73:3-11; Psalms 73:15-17; Isaiah 26:10-11; Habakkuk 1:12-13; Proverbs 22:22; Exodus 20:16; Exodus 22:21-22; Exodus 23:6-8; Leviticus 19:13; Leviticus 19:15; Leviticus 19:35; Deuteronomy 24:14-15; Deuteronomy 24:17; Ezekiel 45:9; Zechariah 7:10; Ephesians 6:9; Psalms 119:163; Deuteronomy 24:7; Psalms 82:2; Proverbs 29:27; Colossians 3:21; Mark 12:31; Romans 13:10; Colossians 3:19; James 5:7-8; Matthew 5:39; Matthew 5:44-45; 1 Corinthians 6:7

An inscription can signify ownership or jurisdiction
----------------------------------------------------

The inscription on objects set apart for God
--------------------------------------------

**[ZEC 14:20–14:21](https://ref.ly/Zech14:20-Zech14:21)** *See also* [EXO 39:30](https://ref.ly/Exod39:30)

An inscription can be symbolic of permanence
--------------------------------------------

### Of a person's words

**[ISA 30:8](https://ref.ly/Isa30:8)**; **[2TI 2:19](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:19)** *See also* [JOB 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/Job19:23-Job19:24)

## 5353: interest (id: 5353)

The charge for the lending of money on a commercial or private loan. The practice of charging interest (known as usury) is treated with caution by OT writers on account of its potential for exploitation.

Usury forbidden among the Israelites
------------------------------------

**[EXO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25)** *See also* [LEV 25:35–25:37](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:37); [DEU 23:19](https://ref.ly/Deut23:19)

### Lending without interest: a characteristic of the righteous

[PSA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:5); ; [EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8), [EZK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:17); ; [LUK 6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:30), [LUK 6:31](https://ref.ly/Luke6:31)

### Exacting interest: a characteristic of the wicked

[PRO 28:8](https://ref.ly/Prov28:8); ; [JER 15:10](https://ref.ly/Jer15:10); ; [EZK 18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:13), [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12); ; [1TI 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9), [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)

### Lending: a charitable, not a commercial, activity

[DEU 15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:8); ; [PSA 112:5](https://ref.ly/Ps112:5); ; [LUK 6:35](https://ref.ly/Luke6:35)

### Example of oppression through usury

[NEH 5:6–5:11](https://ref.ly/Neh5:6-Neh5:11)

Usury permissible for commercial transactions involving \`\`outsiders''
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 23:20](https://ref.ly/Deut23:20)** *This legislation permits rather than encourages usury and remains subject to considerations protecting the poor.* *See also* [DEU 15:1–15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1-Deut15:11); [MAT 25:27](https://ref.ly/Matt25:27) parallel [LUK 19:23](https://ref.ly/Luke19:23)

## 5354: invasions (id: 5354)

A hostile incursion into the territory of another tribe or nation, often with the object of its permanent annexation.

The Israelites' invasion of Canaan
----------------------------------

### Their success had been preordained

**[DEU 7:22](https://ref.ly/Deut7:22)**; **[LEV 20:23–20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:23-Lev20:24)** *See also* [EXO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Exod12:25); [LEV 14:34](https://ref.ly/Lev14:34), [LEV 23:10](https://ref.ly/Lev23:10); [DEU 6:10–6:12](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:12), [DEU 7:17–7:24](https://ref.ly/Deut7:17-Deut7:24)

### Moses gave instructions on how to proceed

[DEU 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:5), [DEU 20:10–20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:10-Deut20:20)

### Joshua followed Moses in leading the invasion

**[JOS 11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:23)** *See also* [JOS 6:20](https://ref.ly/Josh6:20), [JOS 8:18–8:19](https://ref.ly/Josh8:18-Josh8:19), [JOS 10:29–10:43](https://ref.ly/Josh10:29-Josh10:43)

### The invasion continued under other leaders

[JDG 1:4](https://ref.ly/Judg1:4), [JDG 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/Judg4:23-Judg4:24), [JDG 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/Judg11:21-Judg11:22), [JDG 11:32–11:33](https://ref.ly/Judg11:32-Judg11:33); ; [2KI 3:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:24); ; [1CH 4:41–4:43](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:41-1Chr4:43)

Invasion of the Israelites' land
--------------------------------

### By the Philistines

[JDG 10:6–10:10](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6-Judg10:10); ; [1CH 14:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:9); ; [2CH 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:16-2Chr21:17)

### By other nations

**[JDG 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/Judg6:3-Judg6:5)** *See also* [JDG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Judg2:14); [2KI 13:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:20); [2CH 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:1), [2CH 24:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:23)

### Israel taken into captivity by Assyrian invaders

**[2KI 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:5-2Kgs17:6)** parallel [2KI 18:9–18:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:9-2Kgs18:11)

### Judah taken into captivity by Babylonian invaders

**[2KI 25:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1)** parallel [JER 39:1](https://ref.ly/Jer39:1)**[2KI 25:10–25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:10-2Kgs25:11)** parallels [JER 39:8–39:9](https://ref.ly/Jer39:8-Jer39:9), [JER 52:4](https://ref.ly/Jer52:4)

Invasion of the promised land was a cause of great sadnesss
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 79:1](https://ref.ly/Ps79:1)** *See also* [NEH 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:1-Neh1:4); [LAM 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Lam1:1-Lam1:3); [DAN 9:4–9:6](https://ref.ly/Dan9:4-Dan9:6)

Examples of non\-human invasion
-------------------------------

### By locusts

**[EXO 10:14](https://ref.ly/Exod10:14)**

### By evil spiritual forces

**[MAT 12:43–12:45](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43-Matt12:45)** parallel [LUK 11:24–11:26](https://ref.ly/Luke11:24-Luke11:26)**[LUK 22:3](https://ref.ly/Luke22:3)** *See also* [MRK 9:17–9:18](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17-Mark9:18) parallels [MAT 17:14–17:16](https://ref.ly/Matt17:14-Matt17:16), [LUK 9:38–9:40](https://ref.ly/Luke9:38-Luke9:40); [ROM 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12-Rom5:14); [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12)

## 5355: invitations (id: 5355)

A request for attendance, often to a celebration or feast. Invitations to the blessings of the gospel are sometimes presented as requests to attend a banquet. In a negative sense one can be invited to sin.

Invitations to receive hospitality
----------------------------------

**[ACT 16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:15)** *See also* [GEN 24:31–24:33](https://ref.ly/Gen24:31-Gen24:33) *Laban shows hospitality to Abraham's servant and his animals*; [GEN 31:54](https://ref.ly/Gen31:54); [EXO 2:20](https://ref.ly/Exod2:20); [NUM 10:29–10:32](https://ref.ly/Num10:29-Num10:32); [1SA 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:22-1Sam9:24) *Saul dines with Samuel*; [1KI 13:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:7); [JOB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Job1:4); [JER 35:2](https://ref.ly/Jer35:2)LUK [7:36](https://ref.ly/Jer7:36), [LUK 11:37](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37)[JHN 21:12](https://ref.ly/John21:12); [ACT 10:23](https://ref.ly/Acts10:23); [1CO 10:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:27)

Invitations to a wedding or a banquet
-------------------------------------

**[LUK 14:8–14:14](https://ref.ly/Luke14:8-Luke14:14)** *See also* [EST 5:4](https://ref.ly/Esth5:4), [EST 5:12](https://ref.ly/Esth5:12); [JHN 2:2](https://ref.ly/John2:2)

Invitations requesting help
---------------------------

**[ACT 16:9](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9)** *See also* [JDG 1:3](https://ref.ly/Judg1:3), [JDG 11:6](https://ref.ly/Judg11:6); [2KI 16:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7); [ACT 8:30–8:31](https://ref.ly/Acts8:30-Acts8:31)

Invitations of the lovers in the Song of Solomon
------------------------------------------------

[SNG 2:13](https://ref.ly/Song2:13), [SNG 4:8](https://ref.ly/Song4:8), [SNG 7:11](https://ref.ly/Song7:11), [SNG 8:14](https://ref.ly/Song8:14)

Invitations to join in worshipping God
--------------------------------------

**[PSA 34:3](https://ref.ly/Ps34:3)** *See also* [PSA 66:5–66:6](https://ref.ly/Ps66:5-Ps66:6), [PSA 66:16](https://ref.ly/Ps66:16), [PSA 95:1–95:2](https://ref.ly/Ps95:1-Ps95:2), [PSA 95:5–95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:5-Ps95:6)

Invitations of the Lord Jesus Christ to follow him
--------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Matt4:19)** parallels [MRK 1:17](https://ref.ly/Mark1:17), [LUK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10) *See also* [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21) parallels [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21), [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22)

Invitations to the blessings of the gospel
------------------------------------------

### Invitations given in varied terms

**[MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30)** *See also* [ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18); [ZEC 3:10](https://ref.ly/Zech3:10) *“to sit under his vine and fig\-tree” refers to the peaceful security of the kingdom of God. See [1Ki 4:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:25); [Mic 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:4)*; [MAT 25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34); [JHN 4:29](https://ref.ly/John4:29); [REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

### Invitations using the imagery of a banquet

**[LUK 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16-Luke14:17)** *See also* [ISA 55:1–55:3](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1-Isa55:3); [MAT 22:1–22:14](https://ref.ly/Matt22:1-Matt22:14); [REV 19:7–19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7-Rev19:9)

Invitations given with deceitful motives
----------------------------------------

**[2SA 15:10–15:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:10-2Sam15:12)** *See also* [2SA 13:23–13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:23-2Sam13:29); [1KI 1:5–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:5-1Kgs1:10); [NEH 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Neh6:1-Neh6:8)

Invitations to do what is sinful
--------------------------------

## 5358: judges (id: 5358)

Individuals responsible for the administration of justice and/or the operation of government. In the OT the term “judge” is also used in a special sense meaning a leader of Israel. God is portrayed in Scripture as the judge of all the earth; in the NT Jesus Christ is presented as the judge appointed by God.

Judges as leaders of Israel
---------------------------

**[1SA 7:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:15)** *See also* [JDG 2:16–2:19](https://ref.ly/Judg2:16-Judg2:19), [JDG 3:9–3:11](https://ref.ly/Judg3:9-Judg3:11), [JDG 4:4–4:10](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4-Judg4:10); [RUT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:1); [2KI 23:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:22)

The role of judges
------------------

### Judicial and punitive

**[DEU 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Deut25:1-Deut25:2)** *See also* [GEN 19:6–19:9](https://ref.ly/Gen19:6-Gen19:9); [EXO 18:13–18:26](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13-Exod18:26), [EXO 22:7–22:9](https://ref.ly/Exod22:7-Exod22:9); [NUM 25:5](https://ref.ly/Num25:5); [DEU 1:16](https://ref.ly/Deut1:16), [DEU 19:16–19:21](https://ref.ly/Deut19:16-Deut19:21), [DEU 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Deut21:1-Deut21:2); [2CH 19:5–19:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:5-2Chr19:7); [EZK 44:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:24); [LUK 12:14](https://ref.ly/Luke12:14); [1CO 6:2–6:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:2-1Cor6:5); [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Rev20:4)

### Administrative

[EXO 21:2–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2-Exod21:6); ; [EZR 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:14); ; [ACT 24:10](https://ref.ly/Acts24:10)

--------------------------------------

## 5359: justice (id: 5359)

A concern to act rightly, and to be seen by others to act rightly. Divine justice embraces every aspect of the right ordering of human society according to the will of God, its creator.

* **Associated Articles:** 5360: justice, of God (ID: `5360@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5361: justice, human (ID: `5361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5362: justice, in believers' lives (ID: `5362@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5360: justice, of God (id: 5360)

The moral righteousness of God is revealed in his laws and expressed in his judicial acts. God's commands and judgments meet perfect standards of justice, and his apportioning of punishments and rewards is also perfectly just. God's justice is impartial. Special praise is his for vindicating the penitent and the needy who have no human champions. Ultimately, all God's ways will be seen as just and equitable.

## 5361: justice, human (id: 5361)

God created the world in justice, and expects that his creatures will deal fairly and justly with one another as a result. Sin brings injustice into the world, by disrupting the justice established by God at creation. As a result, human justice often falls short of God's standards.

God shows his concern for human justice
---------------------------------------

### By commanding it

**[ISA 56:1](https://ref.ly/Isa56:1)**; **[MIC 6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:8)** *See also* [EXO 23:1–23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1-Exod23:9); [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17); [PSA 82:3](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3); [PRO 21:3](https://ref.ly/Prov21:3); [HOS 12:6](https://ref.ly/Hos12:6); [ROM 13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:7)

### By commending its maintenance

**[PSA 106:3](https://ref.ly/Ps106:3)** *See also* [GEN 20:5–20:6](https://ref.ly/Gen20:5-Gen20:6); [1KI 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:11-1Kgs3:12), [1KI 3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:28); [JOB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Job1:8); [PSA 37:37](https://ref.ly/Ps37:37), [PSA 112:5](https://ref.ly/Ps112:5)

### By condemning its neglect

**[MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)** *See also* [DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19); [JOB 31:13–31:14](https://ref.ly/Job31:13-Job31:14); [ISA 3:14](https://ref.ly/Isa3:14), [ISA 3:15](https://ref.ly/Isa3:15), [ISA 10:1](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1); [JER 7:5–7:8](https://ref.ly/Jer7:5-Jer7:8), [JER 7:14](https://ref.ly/Jer7:14) *destruction follows ignored warnings*; [EZK 22:29–22:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29-Ezek22:31)

## 5362: justice, in believers' lives (id: 5362)

God requires justice to be evident in the lives of his people. Through justification, believers are granted the status of being righteous in his sight, and are called upon to live out that righteousness in their lives.

God's law demands justice
-------------------------

### The law is written on the conscience

**[ROM 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Rom2:14-Rom2:15)** *See also* [GEN 20:5–20:6](https://ref.ly/Gen20:5-Gen20:6); [PRO 20:27](https://ref.ly/Prov20:27)**[ISA 51:7](https://ref.ly/Isa51:7)** *See also* [ECC 8:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:5)

### The law in the OT

**[EXO 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Exod20:1-Exod20:3)**; **[DEU 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Deut6:4-Deut6:5)** *See also* [EXO 20:4–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:4-Exod20:17) parallel [DEU 5:6–5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:21); [PSA 119:1](https://ref.ly/Ps119:1), [PSA 119:165](https://ref.ly/Ps119:165) parallel [MAT 22:37–22:40](https://ref.ly/Matt22:37-Matt22:40)

### The law in the NT

**[MAT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17)** *See also* [ROM 7:7](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7), [ROM 7:12](https://ref.ly/Rom7:12), [ROM 7:22](https://ref.ly/Rom7:22), [ROM 13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:10); [1TI 1:8–1:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:8-1Tim1:11)

-------------------------

**[ROM 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rom1:17)** *See also* [HAB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4); [GEN 15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:6); [ROM 3:21–3:24](https://ref.ly/Rom3:21-Rom3:24), [ROM 4:24–4:25](https://ref.ly/Rom4:24-Rom4:25), [ROM 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1), [ROM 9:30](https://ref.ly/Rom9:30); [GAL 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gal3:6), [GAL 3:24](https://ref.ly/Gal3:24); [PHP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:9)

The marks of the just person
----------------------------

**[JAS 2:17](https://ref.ly/Jas2:17)** *Although justification is by faith alone it is always to be accompanied by righteous behaviour.* *See also* [MAT 25:34–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34-Matt25:36); [EPH 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eph2:10); [1JN 3:7](https://ref.ly/1John3:7), [1JN 3:9](https://ref.ly/1John3:9)

### Thinking justly

**[PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2)** *See also* [PSA 24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:4), [PSA 40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:8), [PSA 119:111–119:112](https://ref.ly/Ps119:111-Ps119:112); [MAT 5:8](https://ref.ly/Matt5:8); [PHP 4:8](https://ref.ly/Phil4:8)

### Speaking justly

**[PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)** *See also* [PRO 4:24](https://ref.ly/Prov4:24), [PRO 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/Prov8:6-Prov8:8); [EPH 4:25](https://ref.ly/Eph4:25); [1PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:10)

### Behaving justly

**[MIC 6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:8)** *See also* [DEU 6:25](https://ref.ly/Deut6:25); [PSA 106:3](https://ref.ly/Ps106:3); [PRO 21:3](https://ref.ly/Prov21:3); [ISA 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:15-Isa33:16); [ACT 24:16](https://ref.ly/Acts24:16)

Examples of just people
-----------------------

**[JOB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Job1:8)** *See also* [2SA 8:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:15); [1KI 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:11-1Kgs3:12), [1KI 3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:28); [LUK 23:50–23:51](https://ref.ly/Luke23:50-Luke23:51); [ACT 25:8](https://ref.ly/Acts25:8), [ACT 25:11](https://ref.ly/Acts25:11)

The vindication of the just
---------------------------

**[2TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:8)** *See also* [PSA 86:17](https://ref.ly/Ps86:17); [DAN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Dan12:3); [REV 7:9–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9-Rev7:17)

* **Associated Articles:** 5008: conscience (ID: `5008@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5596: victory (ID: `5596@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8154: righteousness (ID: `8154@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5364: key (id: 5364)

A tool for opening a locked door. Used mainly symbolically in Scripture to speak of Jesus Christ's victory over death and his authority over believers, or of the need of deliverance from the imprisonment brought to human nature by sin and the law.

Keys for opening doors
----------------------

[JDG 3:23–3:25](https://ref.ly/Judg3:23-Judg3:25); ; [1CH 9:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:27); ; [LUK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Luke3:20), [LUK 11:7](https://ref.ly/Luke11:7); ; [JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/John20:19), [JHN 20:26](https://ref.ly/John20:26); ; [ACT 5:23](https://ref.ly/Acts5:23)

The symbolic use of keys
------------------------

### As a symbol of Jesus Christ's complete authority

**[REV 3:7](https://ref.ly/Rev3:7)** *See also* [ISA 22:22](https://ref.ly/Isa22:22)

### As a symbol of Jesus Christ's ultimate victory over death and hell

**[REV 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rev1:18)**

### As a symbol of Satan's ultimate defeat

**[REV 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Rev20:1-Rev20:3)** *See also* [REV 9:1](https://ref.ly/Rev9:1)

### As a symbol of Jesus Christ's authoritative ministry through his church

**[MAT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Matt16:19)** *Usually understood as illustrating how God's forgiveness is made effective through preaching the gospel.*

### As a symbol of living faith

**[ISA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Isa33:6)** *See also* [MAT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Matt13:52)

### Being locked up as a symbol of obstacles to faith

**[GAL 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gal3:23)** *See also* [LUK 11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:52)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 3:23-25; 1 Chronicles 9:27; Luke 3:20; Luke 11:7; John 20:19; John 20:26; Acts 5:23; Revelation 3:7; Isaiah 22:22; Revelation 1:18; Revelation 20:1-3; Revelation 9:1; Matthew 16:19; Isaiah 33:6; Matthew 13:52; Galatians 3:23; Luke 11:52
* **Associated Articles:** 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5299: door (ID: `5299@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5344: imprisonment (ID: `5344@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5365: kidnapping (id: 5365)

The abduction of a person, normally for the purpose of selling into slavery.

Examples of kidnapping
----------------------

### Joseph

[GEN 37:23–37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:23-Gen37:28), [GEN 40:15](https://ref.ly/Gen40:15), [GEN 45:3–45:5](https://ref.ly/Gen45:3-Gen45:5)

### The girls at Shiloh

[JDG 21:16–21:23](https://ref.ly/Judg21:16-Judg21:23)

The penalty for kidnapping
--------------------------

**[EXO 21:16](https://ref.ly/Exod21:16)** *See also* [DEU 24:7](https://ref.ly/Deut24:7); [1TI 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 37:23-28; Genesis 40:15; Genesis 45:3-5; Judges 21:16-23; Exodus 21:16; Deuteronomy 24:7; 1 Timothy 1:9-10
* **Associated Articles:** 7346: death penalty (ID: `7346@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5366: king (id: 5366)

The ruler or monarch of a country or realm. God is king of all the earth and human kings are called to rule in accordance with his will. The failure of kings in Israel and Judah after David led to the expectation of a coming king like David. This was fulfilled in Jesus Christ: the true King of Israel and all the world.

God is king of his people and of all the earth
----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 47:8](https://ref.ly/Ps47:8)** *The Hebrew refers to the rule of a king*; **[1TI 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15-1Tim6:16)** *See also* [EXO 15:18](https://ref.ly/Exod15:18); [1SA 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12); [2CH 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:6); [PSA 24:10](https://ref.ly/Ps24:10), [PSA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Ps47:2), [PSA 47:6–47:7](https://ref.ly/Ps47:6-Ps47:7), [PSA 93:1](https://ref.ly/Ps93:1), [PSA 96:10](https://ref.ly/Ps96:10), [PSA 97:1](https://ref.ly/Ps97:1), [PSA 99:1](https://ref.ly/Ps99:1); [ISA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:5), [ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6); [1TI 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:17); [REV 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3), [REV 19:6](https://ref.ly/Rev19:6)

Significant kings
-----------------

### Melchizedek, king of Salem

[GEN 14:18](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18); ; [HEB 5:6](https://ref.ly/Heb5:6), [HEB 5:10](https://ref.ly/Heb5:10), [HEB 6:20–7:17](https://ref.ly/Heb6:20-Heb7:17); ; [PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4)

### The Pharaohs, the kings of Egypt

[GEN 12:14–12:20](https://ref.ly/Gen12:14-Gen12:20), [GEN 41:39–41:40](https://ref.ly/Gen41:39-Gen41:40); ; [EXO 12:30–12:32](https://ref.ly/Exod12:30-Exod12:32), [EXO 14:4](https://ref.ly/Exod14:4); ; [1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1); ; [2KI 23:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:29), [2KI 23:33–23:34](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:33-2Kgs23:34); ; [EZK 32:1–32:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:1-Ezek32:10); ; [ROM 9:17](https://ref.ly/Rom9:17)

### Saul

Israel's demand for a king is seen as a rejection of God's rule: [1SA 8:4–8:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:4-1Sam8:9), [1SA 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:19-1Sam8:22), [1SA 10:17–10:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:17-1Sam10:19), [1SA 12:12–12:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12-1Sam12:15); [1SA 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:1-1Sam9:2), [1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1), [1SA 10:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:24), [1SA 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:14-1Sam11:15), [1SA 13:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:1), [1SA 13:11–13:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:11-1Sam13:14), [1SA 15:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:17), [1SA 15:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:26); [1SA 31:1–31:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:1-1Sam31:4) parallel [1CH 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:1-1Chr10:4); ; [1CH 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:13-1Chr10:14)

### David

**[1CH 29:26–29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:26-1Chr29:28)** parallel [1KI 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:10-1Kgs2:12) *See also* [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13); [2SA 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:3-2Sam5:5) parallel [1CH 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:3); [2SA 8:1–8:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:1-2Sam8:14) parallel [1CH 18:1–18:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:1-1Chr18:13); [1KI 11:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:38); [PSA 18:50](https://ref.ly/Ps18:50), [PSA 78:70–78:722](https://ref.ly/Ps78:70-Ps78:722)SA [7:8–7:16](https://ref.ly/Ps7:8-Ps7:16) parallel [1CH 17:7–17:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:7-1Chr17:14); [1KI 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:4-1Kgs9:5); [2CH 7:17–7:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:17-2Chr7:18); [PSA 132:11–132:12](https://ref.ly/Ps132:11-Ps132:12)

### Solomon

[2SA 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24-2Sam12:25); ; [1KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:12) parallel [2CH 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:1); ; [1KI 4:29–4:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:29-1Kgs4:34), [1KI 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:1) parallel [2CH 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:1) *Solomon began to build the temple*; ; [1KI 10:23–10:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:23-1Kgs10:25) parallel [2CH 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:22-2Chr9:24); ; [1KI 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1), [1KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4), [1KI 11:42–11:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:42-1Kgs11:43) parallel [2CH 9:30–9:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:30-2Chr9:31) *Solomon's death*

### Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon

[2KI 24:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:1) parallel [2CH 36:6–36:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:6-2Chr36:7); [2KI 25:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1) parallels [2CH 36:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:17), [JER 39:1](https://ref.ly/Jer39:1), [JER 52:4](https://ref.ly/Jer52:4); ; [DAN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Dan1:1); [JER 21:7](https://ref.ly/Jer21:7), [JER 27:6](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6), [JER 27:8](https://ref.ly/Jer27:8); ; [EZK 29:18–29:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:18-Ezek29:19) *Nebuchadnezzar besieged Tyre for 15 years*; ; [DAN 2:1](https://ref.ly/Dan2:1), [DAN 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Dan2:46-Dan2:47), [DAN 3:1](https://ref.ly/Dan3:1) *the image of gold*; ; [DAN 3:28–3:29](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28-Dan3:29), [DAN 4:28–4:37](https://ref.ly/Dan4:28-Dan4:37)

The expectation of a future king like David
-------------------------------------------

**[JER 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Jer23:5-Jer23:6)** *See also* [ISA 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6-Isa9:7), [ISA 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1-Isa11:5), [ISA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Isa16:5); [JER 30:8–30:9](https://ref.ly/Jer30:8-Jer30:9), [JER 33:15](https://ref.ly/Jer33:15); [EZK 34:23–34:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:23-Ezek34:24), [EZK 37:24–37:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:24-Ezek37:25); [AMO 9:11](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11); [MIC 5:2–5:5](https://ref.ly/Mic5:2-Mic5:5); [HAG 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Hag2:20-Hag2:23)

The duties of the king
----------------------

### To fear God

**[2SA 23:3–23:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:3-2Sam23:4)**; **[PRO 20:28](https://ref.ly/Prov20:28)** *See also* [DEU 17:14–17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:14-Deut17:20); [1SA 10:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:25); [PSA 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Ps2:10-Ps2:11)

### To maintain righteousness and administer justice

**[PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4), [PRO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Prov29:14)** *See also* [1KI 3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:28); [PSA 72:1–72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:1-Ps72:4); [PRO 16:12–16:13](https://ref.ly/Prov16:12-Prov16:13), [PRO 20:8](https://ref.ly/Prov20:8), [PRO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Prov20:26), [PRO 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:5), [PRO 31:8–31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:8-Prov31:9); [ISA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Isa11:4); [JER 33:15](https://ref.ly/Jer33:15); [ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)

### To read the law of God and ensure it was obeyed

**[DEU 17:18–17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18-Deut17:20)** *See also* [1KI 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:1-1Kgs2:4), [1KI 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:4-1Kgs9:5); [2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)

The coronation of the king
--------------------------

### His anointing by the prophet of God

[1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1), [1SA 16:1–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:1-1Sam16:13)

### His proclamation as king before the people

**[2KI 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:12)** parallel [2CH 23:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:11) *See also* [1SA 10:17–10:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:17-1Sam10:25), [1SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:15); [2SA 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:4), [2SA 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:1-2Sam5:3) parallel [1CH 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:1-1Chr11:3); [2SA 15:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:10); [1KI 1:28–1:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:28-1Kgs1:40), [1KI 12:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:1) parallel [2CH 10:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:1); [1KI 12:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:20)

### Royal Psalms

These Psalms, although used in honour of the king of the day, possibly at his coronation, share the expectation of a greater king. The NT regards them as fulfilled in Jesus Christ: [PSA 2:6–2:9](https://ref.ly/Ps2:6-Ps2:9), [PSA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Ps45:1), [PSA 72:1](https://ref.ly/Ps72:1), [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1), [PSA 132:10](https://ref.ly/Ps132:10)

Jesus Christ is the true King of Israel and of the world
--------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 27:37](https://ref.ly/Matt27:37)** parallels [MRK 15:26](https://ref.ly/Mark15:26), [LUK 23:38](https://ref.ly/Luke23:38)**[JHN 1:49](https://ref.ly/John1:49)** *See also* [MAT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Matt21:5); [MRK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) parallels [MAT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11), [LUK 23:3](https://ref.ly/Luke23:3); [JHN 18:36–18:37](https://ref.ly/John18:36-John18:37); [ACT 17:7](https://ref.ly/Acts17:7); [1CO 15:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:25); [HEB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Heb1:8); [PSA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Ps45:6); [REV 17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:14), [REV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Rev19:16)

Jesus Christ fulfils the expectation of a son of David who will be a king like David
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5367: kingdoms (id: 5367)

Realms or reigns of kings. The kingdoms of this world have power under the sovereignty of God. They are ultimately judged by God and will be superseded by the kingdom of God. The phrase “the kingdom of the air” is used to describe the spiritual realm in which Satan operates.

Israel and the kingdoms around her
----------------------------------

**[1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21)** *See also* [DEU 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/Deut3:21-Deut3:22), [DEU 28:25](https://ref.ly/Deut28:25); [JOS 11:10](https://ref.ly/Josh11:10); [JDG 2:20–3:4](https://ref.ly/Judg2:20-Judg3:4); [1SA 10:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:18); [2CH 17:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:10), [2CH 20:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:29); [NEH 9:22](https://ref.ly/Neh9:22); [JER 34:1](https://ref.ly/Jer34:1)

The kingdoms of this world
--------------------------

### God is sovereign over the kingdoms of this world

**[2KI 19:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:15)** parallel [ISA 37:16](https://ref.ly/Isa37:16) *See also* [2KI 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:1), [2KI 19:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:19); [2CH 20:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:6), [2CH 36:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:23) parallel [EZR 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:2); [PSA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Ps46:6); [PRO 21:1](https://ref.ly/Prov21:1); [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1)

### The kingdoms of this world will honour and worship God

**[PSA 102:21–102:22](https://ref.ly/Ps102:21-Ps102:22)** *See also* [PSA 68:32](https://ref.ly/Ps68:32); [REV 21:24–21:26](https://ref.ly/Rev21:24-Rev21:26)

### Under the sovereignty of God a succession of kingdoms will rule the earth

[DAN 2:36–2:43](https://ref.ly/Dan2:36-Dan2:43), [DAN 7:17](https://ref.ly/Dan7:17), [DAN 7:23–7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:23-Dan7:25), [DAN 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Dan8:19-Dan8:22)

### The kingdom of God will supersede and be greater than the kingdoms of this world

**[REV 11:15](https://ref.ly/Rev11:15)**; **[JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33)** *See also* [DAN 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Dan2:44-Dan2:45), [DAN 6:26](https://ref.ly/Dan6:26), [DAN 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13-Dan7:14), [DAN 7:18](https://ref.ly/Dan7:18), [DAN 7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:27)

### God's judgment on the kingdoms of this world

**[ZEP 3:8](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:8)** *See also* [PSA 79:6](https://ref.ly/Ps79:6); [ISA 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/Isa10:10-Isa10:11), [ISA 13:19](https://ref.ly/Isa13:19); [JER 28:8](https://ref.ly/Jer28:8); [DAN 7:26](https://ref.ly/Dan7:26); [HAG 2:22](https://ref.ly/Hag2:22); [REV 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/Rev6:15-Rev6:17)

### God's people will be given sovereignty over the kingdoms of this world

**[DAN 7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:27)** *See also* [JER 1:10](https://ref.ly/Jer1:10); [REV 2:26–2:27](https://ref.ly/Rev2:26-Rev2:27)

### In this world God enables his people to overcome \`\`kingdoms'' by faith

[HEB 11:32–11:33](https://ref.ly/Heb11:32-Heb11:33); ; [1JN 5:4](https://ref.ly/1John5:4)

### The devil tempts Jesus Christ by offering him the kingdoms of this world if he will worship him

[MAT 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8-Matt4:10) parallel [LUK 4:5–4:8](https://ref.ly/Luke4:5-Luke4:8)

The kingdom of the air
----------------------

**[EPH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Eph2:1-Eph2:2)**

The kingdom of Satan
--------------------

*See also* [MAT 12:26](https://ref.ly/Matt12:26) parallel [LUK 11:18](https://ref.ly/Luke11:18)

The kingdoms of the sea
-----------------------

[ISA 23:11](https://ref.ly/Isa23:11) *The Phoenicians, who were great sailors, appeared to have dominion over the sea (see [Isa 23:1–4](https://ref.ly/Isa23:1-Isa23:4)), but in reality it belongs to God.*

## 5368: kingship (id: 5368)

The sovereignty and rule of a king. Scripture stresses that all human authority derives ultimately from God, and that human kings are required to acknowledge and respond to this divine authority.

* **Associated Articles:** 5369: kingship, divine (ID: `5369@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5370: kingship, human (ID: `5370@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5369: kingship, divine (id: 5369)

God is sovereign over all the earth. Jesus Christ is the true King of Israel and David's greater son.

The kingship of God
-------------------

### God is sovereign over all the earth

**[1CH 29:11–29:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:11-1Chr29:12)** *See also* [EXO 15:18](https://ref.ly/Exod15:18); [1CH 16:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:31); [PSA 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Ps9:7-Ps9:8), [PSA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Ps45:6), [PSA 103:19](https://ref.ly/Ps103:19); [ISA 37:16](https://ref.ly/Isa37:16)

### God is called king

**[PSA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Ps29:10), [PSA 47:6–47:7](https://ref.ly/Ps47:6-Ps47:7)** *See also* [1SA 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12); [PSA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Ps47:2), [PSA 95:3](https://ref.ly/Ps95:3); [ISA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:5), [ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6); [1TI 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:17), [1TI 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15); [REV 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3)

### God rules as king

**[PSA 96:10](https://ref.ly/Ps96:10)** *See also* [PSA 47:8](https://ref.ly/Ps47:8), [PSA 93:1](https://ref.ly/Ps93:1), [PSA 97:1](https://ref.ly/Ps97:1), [PSA 99:1](https://ref.ly/Ps99:1)

### The kingdom of God

**[MRK 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:14-Mark1:15)** *See also* [PSA 145:11–145:13](https://ref.ly/Ps145:11-Ps145:13); [MAT 4:17](https://ref.ly/Matt4:17), [MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23), [MAT 11:12](https://ref.ly/Matt11:12); [LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43), [LUK 11:20](https://ref.ly/Luke11:20), [LUK 16:16](https://ref.ly/Luke16:16); [1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24); [REV 11:15](https://ref.ly/Rev11:15)

The kingship of Jesus Christ
----------------------------

### Jesus Christ is the true king of Israel and of the world

**[JHN 1:49](https://ref.ly/John1:49)** *See also* [MAT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Matt21:5); [ZEC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9); [MRK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2) parallels [MAT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11), [LUK 23:3](https://ref.ly/Luke23:3); [JHN 18:36–18:37](https://ref.ly/John18:36-John18:37); [ACT 17:7](https://ref.ly/Acts17:7); [HEB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Heb1:8); [PSA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Ps45:6); [REV 17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:14), [REV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Rev19:16)

### Jesus Christ is David's greater son

**[LUK 1:32–1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:32-Luke1:33)**; **[ACT 13:36–13:37](https://ref.ly/Acts13:36-Acts13:37)** *Jesus Christ is not only David's son but he is greater than David.* *See also* [MAT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Matt1:1); [MRK 12:35–12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:35-Mark12:37) parallels [MAT 22:41–22:45](https://ref.ly/Matt22:41-Matt22:45), [LUK 20:41–20:44](https://ref.ly/Luke20:41-Luke20:44); [LUK 1:69](https://ref.ly/Luke1:69); [ACT 2:29–2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:29-Acts2:36), [ACT 13:22–13:23](https://ref.ly/Acts13:22-Acts13:23); [ROM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Rom1:3); [2TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:8); [REV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5), [REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Rev22:16)

The kingship of believers
-------------------------

**[2TI 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12)** *See also* [EPH 2:6](https://ref.ly/Eph2:6); [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9); [REV 2:26–2:27](https://ref.ly/Rev2:26-Rev2:27), [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21), [REV 5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:10)
