* **Associated Articles:** 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2206: Jesus, the Christ (ID: `2206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2215: Jesus Christ, Son of David (ID: `2215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2312: Jesus Christ, as king (ID: `2312@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5370: kingship, human (id: 5370)

Human kings are to be respected. Israel demanded and received a king from God but the failure of kings after David led to the expectation of a coming great king in sucession to David.

Human kingship
--------------

### Human kingship is to be respected as ordained by God

**[ROM 13:1–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:7)** *See also* [1TI 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1-1Tim2:2); [1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13), [1PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:17)

### Allegiance to God is greater than allegiance to human kings

[DAN 2:47](https://ref.ly/Dan2:47); ; [ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29); ; [HEB 11:23](https://ref.ly/Heb11:23), [HEB 11:27](https://ref.ly/Heb11:27)

Kingship in Israel
------------------

### Israel is ruled by God and his appointed leader

[JDG 8:22–8:23](https://ref.ly/Judg8:22-Judg8:23), [JDG 19:1](https://ref.ly/Judg19:1), [JDG 21:25](https://ref.ly/Judg21:25); ; [1SA 7:15–7:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:15-1Sam7:17), [1SA 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12)

### Israel demands and receives a king from God

**[1SA 8:4–8:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:4-1Sam8:9)** *See also* [DEU 17:14–17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:14-Deut17:20); [1SA 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:19-1Sam8:22), [1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1), [1SA 10:17–10:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:17-1Sam10:19), [1SA 10:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:24), [1SA 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:14-1Sam11:15), [1SA 12:12–12:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:12-1Sam12:15) *The demand for a king is seen as a rejection of God's rule but at the same time the king is seen as a gracious gift from God*; [HOS 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Hos13:10-Hos13:11)

### David is Israel's greatest king

**[1CH 29:26–29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:26-1Chr29:28)** parallel [1KI 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:10-1Kgs2:12)**[PSA 78:70–78:72](https://ref.ly/Ps78:70-Ps78:72)** *See also* [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13); [2SA 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:3-2Sam5:5) parallel [1CH 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:3); [2SA 8:1–8:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:1-2Sam8:14) parallel [1CH 18:1–18:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:1-1Chr18:13); [1KI 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:14), [1KI 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:6), [1KI 11:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:38); [2KI 14:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:3); [PSA 18:50](https://ref.ly/Ps18:50)

### God's covenant with David

**[2SA 7:8–7:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:8-2Sam7:16)** parallel [1CH 17:7–17:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:7-1Chr17:14) *See also* [1KI 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:4-1Kgs9:5) parallel [2CH 7:17–7:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:17-2Chr7:18); [PSA 132:11–132:12](https://ref.ly/Ps132:11-Ps132:12)

The failure of the kingship after David leads to the expectation of a future king like David
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 33:15](https://ref.ly/Jer33:15)** *See also* [ISA 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6-Isa9:7), [ISA 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1-Isa11:5), [ISA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Isa16:5); [JER 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Jer23:5-Jer23:6), [JER 30:8–30:9](https://ref.ly/Jer30:8-Jer30:9); [EZK 34:23–34:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:23-Ezek34:24), [EZK 37:24–37:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:24-Ezek37:25); [AMO 9:11](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11); [MIC 5:2–5:5](https://ref.ly/Mic5:2-Mic5:5); [MRK 11:10](https://ref.ly/Mark11:10)

Royal Psalms
------------

These Psalms, although used for the king of the day, share the expectation of a greater future king. The NT regards them as fulfilled in Jesus Christ: [PSA 2:6–2:9](https://ref.ly/Ps2:6-Ps2:9), [PSA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Ps45:1), [PSA 72:1](https://ref.ly/Ps72:1), [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1), [PSA 132:11](https://ref.ly/Ps132:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 13:1-7; 1 Timothy 2:1-2; 1 Peter 2:13; 1 Peter 2:17; Daniel 2:47; Acts 5:29; Hebrews 11:23; Hebrews 11:27; Judges 8:22-23; Judges 19:1; Judges 21:25; 1 Samuel 7:15-17; 1 Samuel 12:12; 1 Samuel 8:4-9; Deuteronomy 17:14-20; 1 Samuel 8:19-22; 1 Samuel 10:1; 1 Samuel 10:17-19; 1 Samuel 10:24; 1 Samuel 11:14-15; 1 Samuel 12:12-15; Hosea 13:10-11; 1 Chronicles 29:26-28; 1 Kings 2:10-12; Psalms 78:70-72; 1 Samuel 16:13; 2 Samuel 5:3-5; 1 Chronicles 11:3; 2 Samuel 8:1-14; 1 Chronicles 18:1-13; 1 Kings 3:14; 1 Kings 11:6; 1 Kings 11:38; 2 Kings 14:3; Psalms 18:50; 2 Samuel 7:8-16; 1 Chronicles 17:7-14; 1 Kings 9:4-5; 2 Chronicles 7:17-18; Psalms 132:11-12; Jeremiah 33:15; Isaiah 9:6-7; Isaiah 11:1-5; Isaiah 16:5; Jeremiah 23:5-6; Jeremiah 30:8-9; Ezekiel 34:23-24; Ezekiel 37:24-25; Amos 9:11; Micah 5:2-5; Mark 11:10; Psalms 2:6-9; Psalms 45:1; Psalms 72:1; Psalms 110:1; Psalms 132:11
* **Associated Articles:** 2230: Messiah, coming of (ID: `2230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5085: David (ID: `5085@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5118: Solomon (ID: `5118@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5219: authority, of human institutions (ID: `5219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5366: king (ID: `5366@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5487: queen (ID: `5487@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5581: throne (ID: `5581@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7233: Israel, as northern kingdom (ID: `7233@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7236: Israel, as united kingdom (ID: `7236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7245: Judah, kingdom of (ID: `7245@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5372: knife (id: 5372)

The knife as a domestic cutting implement
-----------------------------------------

### Used for general purposes

[PRO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Prov23:2), [PRO 30:14](https://ref.ly/Prov30:14); ; [JER 36:23](https://ref.ly/Jer36:23) *cutting a scroll*

Knives used for shaving
-----------------------

[LEV 19:27](https://ref.ly/Lev19:27), [LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5); ; [JDG 16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17); ; [ISA 15:2](https://ref.ly/Isa15:2); ; [EZK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:18); ; [MIC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mic1:16)

Knives used in religious ceremonies
-----------------------------------

### In circumcision

Flint rather than metal knives were used because of their sharpness: [EXO 4:25](https://ref.ly/Exod4:25); ; [JOS 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Josh5:2-Josh5:3)

### In sacrifice

[GEN 15:10](https://ref.ly/Gen15:10), [GEN 22:10](https://ref.ly/Gen22:10); ; [EXO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Exod29:17); ; [LEV 8:20](https://ref.ly/Lev8:20)

Knives used for wounding
------------------------

### For self\-mutilation

This practice was associated with pagan worship, sometimes as an expression of mourning: [LEV 19:28](https://ref.ly/Lev19:28), [LEV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Lev21:5); ; [DEU 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1-Deut14:2); ; [1KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28); ; [JER 16:6](https://ref.ly/Jer16:6), [JER 41:5](https://ref.ly/Jer41:5), [JER 47:5](https://ref.ly/Jer47:5), [JER 48:37](https://ref.ly/Jer48:37)

### In mutilation of others

As a punishment: [EXO 21:23–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:23-Exod21:25); ; [LEV 24:17–24:20](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17-Lev24:20); ; [DEU 25:12](https://ref.ly/Deut25:12); [JDG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Judg1:6), [JDG 16:21](https://ref.ly/Judg16:21); [1SA 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:7)

Knives used in figures of speech
--------------------------------

### For self\-discipline

[PRO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Prov23:2); ; [MAT 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:29-Matt5:30), [MAT 18:8–18:9](https://ref.ly/Matt18:8-Matt18:9); ; [MRK 9:43–9:47](https://ref.ly/Mark9:43-Mark9:47)

### As a symbol of pruning

[ISA 18:5](https://ref.ly/Isa18:5); ; [JHN 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/John15:1-John15:2)

### As a symbol of wickedness

[PRO 30:14](https://ref.ly/Prov30:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 23:2; Proverbs 30:14; Jeremiah 36:23; Leviticus 19:27; Leviticus 21:5; Judges 16:17; Isaiah 15:2; Ezekiel 7:18; Micah 1:16; Exodus 4:25; Joshua 5:2-3; Genesis 15:10; Genesis 22:10; Exodus 29:17; Leviticus 8:20; Leviticus 19:28; Deuteronomy 14:1-2; 1 Kings 18:28; Jeremiah 16:6; Jeremiah 41:5; Jeremiah 47:5; Jeremiah 48:37; Exodus 21:23-25; Leviticus 24:17-20; Deuteronomy 25:12; Judges 1:6; Judges 16:21; 1 Samuel 11:7; Matthew 5:29-30; Matthew 18:8-9; Mark 9:43-47; Isaiah 18:5; John 15:1-2
* **Associated Articles:** 4327: flint (ID: `4327@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5130: beard (ID: `5130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5155: hair (ID: `5155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5346: injury (ID: `5346@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5419: mourning (ID: `5419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5482: punishment (ID: `5482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5571: surgery (ID: `5571@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5572: sword (ID: `5572@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5583: tools (ID: `5583@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5373: lamp and lampstand (id: 5373)

A device to give light, especially in buildings, and the stand on which it was placed. They were both used as symbols of the gospel or of churches which resulted from the preaching of the gospel.

Domestic lamps
--------------

**[PRO 31:18](https://ref.ly/Prov31:18)** *See also* [2KI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:10); [JER 25:10](https://ref.ly/Jer25:10); [DAN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5); [LUK 15:8](https://ref.ly/Luke15:8); [ACT 20:8](https://ref.ly/Acts20:8); [REV 18:23](https://ref.ly/Rev18:23)

Lamps in the tabernacle and temple
----------------------------------

**[2CH 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:7)**; **[HEB 9:2](https://ref.ly/Heb9:2)** *See also* [EXO 25:31–25:40](https://ref.ly/Exod25:31-Exod25:40), [EXO 26:35](https://ref.ly/Exod26:35), [EXO 30:27](https://ref.ly/Exod30:27), [EXO 31:8](https://ref.ly/Exod31:8), [EXO 35:14](https://ref.ly/Exod35:14), [EXO 37:17–37:24](https://ref.ly/Exod37:17-Exod37:24), [EXO 39:37](https://ref.ly/Exod39:37), [EXO 40:4](https://ref.ly/Exod40:4), [EXO 40:24–40:25](https://ref.ly/Exod40:24-Exod40:25); [NUM 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Num4:9-Num4:10), [NUM 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/Num8:1-Num8:4); [1KI 7:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:49); [2CH 4:19–4:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:19-2Chr4:21); [JER 52:19](https://ref.ly/Jer52:19)

Tending the lamps
-----------------

**[EXO 27:20–27:21](https://ref.ly/Exod27:20-Exod27:21)** *See also* [EXO 30:7–30:8](https://ref.ly/Exod30:7-Exod30:8); [LEV 24:2–24:4](https://ref.ly/Lev24:2-Lev24:4); [NUM 3:31](https://ref.ly/Num3:31); [1SA 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:3); [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11), [2CH 29:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:7)

Lamps used metaphorically
-------------------------

**[2SA 21:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:17)** *The lamp of Israel represents the king*; **[PSA 119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105)**; **[MAT 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:15-Matt5:16)** *See also* [MRK 4:21](https://ref.ly/Mark4:21); [LUK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Luke8:16), [LUK 11:33](https://ref.ly/Luke11:33)**[JHN 5:35](https://ref.ly/John5:35)**; **[REV 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Rev1:12-Rev1:13)** *The lampstands represent the seven churches.* *See also* [2SA 22:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:29); [JOB 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Job18:5-Job18:6), [JOB 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/Job29:2-Job29:3); [PSA 18:28](https://ref.ly/Ps18:28); [PRO 13:9](https://ref.ly/Prov13:9); [ZEP 1:12](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:12); [ZEC 4:2](https://ref.ly/Zech4:2); [MAT 6:22](https://ref.ly/Matt6:22) parallel [LUK 11:34–11:36](https://ref.ly/Luke11:34-Luke11:36); [MAT 25:1–25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:13) *the parable of the ten virgins*; [LUK 12:35](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35); [REV 11:4](https://ref.ly/Rev11:4), [REV 22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 31:18; 2 Kings 4:10; Jeremiah 25:10; Daniel 5:5; Luke 15:8; Acts 20:8; Revelation 18:23; 2 Chronicles 4:7; Hebrews 9:2; Exodus 25:31-40; Exodus 26:35; Exodus 30:27; Exodus 31:8; Exodus 35:14; Exodus 37:17-24; Exodus 39:37; Exodus 40:4; Exodus 40:24-25; Numbers 4:9-10; Numbers 8:1-4; 1 Kings 7:49; 2 Chronicles 4:19-21; Jeremiah 52:19; Exodus 27:20-21; Exodus 30:7-8; Leviticus 24:2-4; Numbers 3:31; 1 Samuel 3:3; 2 Chronicles 13:11; 2 Chronicles 29:7; 2 Samuel 21:17; Psalms 119:105; Matthew 5:15-16; Mark 4:21; Luke 8:16; Luke 11:33; John 5:35; Revelation 1:12-13; 2 Samuel 22:29; Job 18:5-6; Job 29:2-3; Psalms 18:28; Proverbs 13:9; Zephaniah 1:12; Zechariah 4:2; Matthew 6:22; Luke 11:34-36; Matthew 25:1-13; Luke 12:35; Revelation 11:4; Revelation 22:5
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4488: oil (ID: `4488@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4833: light (ID: `4833@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7459: tabernacle, in OT (ID: `7459@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7467: temple, Solomon's (ID: `7467@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5374: languages (id: 5374)

The means of communication shared in common by groups of people. Scripture teaches that the diversity of languages in the world is a result of sin, but the outpouring of the Holy Spirit in Acts broke down the barriers of language and nationality.

The origin of different languages
---------------------------------

**[GEN 11:1–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:1-Gen11:9)** *See also* [GEN 10:2–10:5](https://ref.ly/Gen10:2-Gen10:5), [GEN 10:20](https://ref.ly/Gen10:20), [GEN 10:30–10:31](https://ref.ly/Gen10:30-Gen10:31)

The close relationship between language and national identity
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 19:18](https://ref.ly/Isa19:18)** *See also* [NEH 13:23–13:27](https://ref.ly/Neh13:23-Neh13:27); [ZEC 8:23](https://ref.ly/Zech8:23); [REV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9), [REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 10:11](https://ref.ly/Rev10:11), [REV 11:9](https://ref.ly/Rev11:9), [REV 13:7](https://ref.ly/Rev13:7), [REV 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rev14:6), [REV 17:15](https://ref.ly/Rev17:15)

Language barriers
-----------------

**[NEH 13:23–13:24](https://ref.ly/Neh13:23-Neh13:24)** *See also* [DEU 28:49–28:50](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49-Deut28:50); [PSA 81:5](https://ref.ly/Ps81:5); [JER 5:15](https://ref.ly/Jer5:15); [EZK 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:5-Ezek3:6); [DAN 1:4](https://ref.ly/Dan1:4); [1CO 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:10-1Cor14:11)

The diversity of languages in the Persian empire
------------------------------------------------

**[EST 8:9](https://ref.ly/Esth8:9)** *See also* [EZR 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:7); [EST 1:22](https://ref.ly/Esth1:22), [EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12)

God's revelation not limited to any single language
---------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:11)** *See also* [PSA 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:4); [ACT 10:44–10:48](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:48)

Aramaic
-------

### Aramaic in the OT

**[2KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:26)** parallel [ISA 36:11](https://ref.ly/Isa36:11) *Aramaic is a language related to Hebrew, known from Assyrian times. Under the influence of the Persian empire Aramaic was the diplomatic language in later OT times. In Palestine, by the time of the NT, it was spoken widely, having largely displaced Hebrew as the everyday language of the Jews.* *See also* [EZR 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:7) *The text of [Ezr 4:8–6:18](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:8-Ezra6:18) and [7:12–26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:12-Ezra7:26) is in Aramaic*; [JER 10:11](https://ref.ly/Jer10:11) *The text of [Jer 10:11](https://ref.ly/Jer10:11) is in Aramaic*; [DAN 2:4](https://ref.ly/Dan2:4) *The text of [Da 2:4–7:28](https://ref.ly/Dan2:4-Dan7:28) is in Aramaic.*

### By NT times Aramaic had become the common language of Palestinian Jews

**[JHN 20:16](https://ref.ly/John20:16)** *See also* [JHN 5:2](https://ref.ly/John5:2), [JHN 19:13](https://ref.ly/John19:13), [JHN 19:17](https://ref.ly/John19:17), [JHN 19:20](https://ref.ly/John19:20); [ACT 21:40–22:2](https://ref.ly/Acts21:40-Acts22:2), [ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/Acts26:14)

### Aramaic expressions found in the NT

**[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46)** parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34) *See also* [MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22); [MRK 5:41](https://ref.ly/Mark5:41), [MRK 7:34](https://ref.ly/Mark7:34), [MRK 14:36](https://ref.ly/Mark14:36); [JHN 20:16](https://ref.ly/John20:16); [ROM 8:15](https://ref.ly/Rom8:15); [1CO 16:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:22); [GAL 4:6](https://ref.ly/Gal4:6)

Hebrew
------

**[2KI 18:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:26)** parallel [ISA 36:11](https://ref.ly/Isa36:11) *Hebrew was the language of Israel in the period of the OT. By the time of the NT, it had been partially displaced in everyday use by Aramaic.* *See also* [2KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:28) parallels [2CH 32:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:18), [ISA 36:13](https://ref.ly/Isa36:13); [REV 9:11](https://ref.ly/Rev9:11), [REV 16:16](https://ref.ly/Rev16:16)

Greek
-----

**[JHN 19:20](https://ref.ly/John19:20)** *Greek was the major cultural and commercial language of the eastern Mediterranean world. The NT was written in this language, ensuring the rapid spread of the gospel in this region.* *See also* [ACT 21:37–21:39](https://ref.ly/Acts21:37-Acts21:39); [REV 9:11](https://ref.ly/Rev9:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 11:1-9; Genesis 10:2-5; Genesis 10:20; Genesis 10:30-31; Isaiah 19:18; Nehemiah 13:23-27; Zechariah 8:23; Revelation 5:9; Revelation 7:9; Revelation 10:11; Revelation 11:9; Revelation 13:7; Revelation 14:6; Revelation 17:15; Nehemiah 13:23-24; Deuteronomy 28:49-50; Psalms 81:5; Jeremiah 5:15; Ezekiel 3:5-6; Daniel 1:4; 1 Corinthians 14:10-11; Esther 8:9; Ezra 4:7; Esther 1:22; Esther 3:12; Acts 2:1-11; Psalms 19:1-4; Acts 10:44-48; 2 Kings 18:26; Isaiah 36:11; Ezra 4:8-6:18; Ezra 7:12-26; Jeremiah 10:11; Daniel 2:4; Daniel 2:4-7:28; John 20:16; John 5:2; John 19:13; John 19:17; John 19:20; Acts 21:40-22:2; Acts 26:14; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:34; Matthew 5:22; Mark 5:41; Mark 7:34; Mark 14:36; Romans 8:15; 1 Corinthians 16:22; Galatians 4:6; 2 Kings 18:28; 2 Chronicles 32:18; Isaiah 36:13; Revelation 9:11; Revelation 16:16; Acts 21:37-39
* **Associated Articles:** 1440: revelation, through creation (ID: `1440@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3242: Holy Spirit, baptism with (ID: `3242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3251: Holy Spirit, filling with (ID: `3251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3257: Holy Spirit, gift of (ID: `3257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5547: speech, power and significance of (ID: `5547@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5585: towers (ID: `5585@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5627: word (ID: `5627@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7972: tongues, gift of (ID: `7972@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5375: law (id: 5375)

The God\-given regulation of the life of the people of God in relationship with him. As the command of God, it enables and gives shape to the relationship between God and human beings on the one hand, and between fellow human beings on the other.

* **Associated Articles:** 5376: law, purpose of (ID: `5376@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5377: law, Ten Commandments (ID: `5377@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5378: law, OT (ID: `5378@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5379: law, Jesus Christ's attitude to (ID: `5379@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5380: law, and gospel (ID: `5380@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5381: law, letter and spirit of (ID: `5381@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5376: law, purpose of (id: 5376)

The law covers and regulates every area of life of the covenant people of God in accordance with the commands of God. Although the laws may be divided into categories of civil, criminal, social and cultic (or ritual) law, these distinctions are not clear\-cut, and occasionally overlap with one another.

The origins of the law
----------------------

### Law as God's command is found in the story of creation

**[GEN 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:16-Gen2:17)**

### The law expresses the covenant relation between God and his people

**[DEU 4:44–4:45](https://ref.ly/Deut4:44-Deut4:45), [DEU 5:1](https://ref.ly/Deut5:1)** *See also* [EXO 20:1–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:1-Exod20:17) parallel [DEU 5:6–5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:21); [DEU 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12-Deut10:13), [DEU 30:1–30:16](https://ref.ly/Deut30:1-Deut30:16)

The purpose of the law
----------------------

### The law shows the proper response to the holiness of God

**[LEV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Lev19:2)**

### The law ensures that the people continue to receive the blessings of the covenant promises

**[DEU 6:24–6:25](https://ref.ly/Deut6:24-Deut6:25)** *See also* [GEN 22:17–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:17-Gen22:18); [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12); [DEU 6:3–6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:3-Deut6:7)

### Breaking the law leads to forfeiting the covenant blessings

**[JER 11:9–11:11](https://ref.ly/Jer11:9-Jer11:11)** *See also* [EXO 32:1–32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:4)

### The law deepens the believer's knowledge of God through meditation

**[PSA 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1-Ps1:2)** *See also* [DEU 6:2](https://ref.ly/Deut6:2); [PSA 19:7–19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7-Ps19:14), [PSA 119:25–119:32](https://ref.ly/Ps119:25-Ps119:32), [PSA 119:105–119:120](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105-Ps119:120)

### The law will finally be written on believers' hearts

**[JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33)** *See also* [EZK 11:19–11:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:19-Ezek11:20)

The general principles underlying the law
-----------------------------------------

### Justice

**[DEU 16:20](https://ref.ly/Deut16:20)**

### Righteousness

**[DEU 16:18](https://ref.ly/Deut16:18)**

### Holiness

**[LEV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Lev19:2)**

### Love

**[LEV 19:18–19:19](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18-Lev19:19)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:16-17; Deuteronomy 4:44-45; Deuteronomy 5:1; Exodus 20:1-17; Deuteronomy 5:6-21; Deuteronomy 10:12-13; Deuteronomy 30:1-16; Leviticus 19:2; Deuteronomy 6:24-25; Genesis 22:17-18; Exodus 20:12; Deuteronomy 6:3-7; Jeremiah 11:9-11; Exodus 32:1-4; Psalms 1:1-2; Deuteronomy 6:2; Psalms 19:7-14; Psalms 119:25-32; Psalms 119:105-120; Jeremiah 31:33; Ezekiel 11:19-20; Deuteronomy 16:20; Deuteronomy 16:18; Leviticus 19:18-19
* **Associated Articles:** 1075: God, justice of (ID: `1075@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1125: God, righteousness of (ID: `1125@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5276: crime (ID: `5276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5482: punishment (ID: `5482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8154: righteousness (ID: `8154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8266: holiness (ID: `8266@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5377: law, Ten Commandments (id: 5377)

The basic laws given to Israel through Moses following the exodus from Egypt ([Ex 20:1–17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:1-Exod20:17); [Dt 5:6–21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:21)). The first four commandments safeguard Israel's special relation to God; the remaining six protect individuals within the community and promote their well\-being.

The Ten Commandments are to govern the life of Israel as the people of God
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Israel should obey God alone

**[EXO 20:2–20:3](https://ref.ly/Exod20:2-Exod20:3)** parallel [DEU 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:7) *See also* [DEU 6:13–6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13-Deut6:15)

### Idolatry forbidden

**[EXO 20:4–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:4-Exod20:6)** parallel [DEU 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/Deut5:8-Deut5:10) *See also* [EXO 32:1–32:8](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:8); [LEV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Lev19:4); [1CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:7)

### God's name should not be misused

**[EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7)** parallel [DEU 5:11](https://ref.ly/Deut5:11) *See also* [MAT 7:21](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21)

### A day of rest is commanded

**[EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11)** parallel [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15) *See also* [EXO 16:23](https://ref.ly/Exod16:23); [LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3); [ISA 56:2](https://ref.ly/Isa56:2); [JER 17:21–17:22](https://ref.ly/Jer17:21-Jer17:22)

### Parents are to be honoured

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *See also* [MAT 15:4](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4); [EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3)

### Murder is forbidden

**[EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13)** parallel [DEU 5:17](https://ref.ly/Deut5:17) *See also* [GEN 4:8–4:16](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8-Gen4:16); [MAT 5:21](https://ref.ly/Matt5:21)

### Adultery is forbidden

**[EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14)** parallel [DEU 5:18](https://ref.ly/Deut5:18) *See also* [LEV 18:20](https://ref.ly/Lev18:20); [2SA 11:2–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:5); [MAT 5:27](https://ref.ly/Matt5:27); [HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4)

### Stealing is forbidden

**[EXO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Exod20:15)** parallel [DEU 5:19](https://ref.ly/Deut5:19) *See also* [LEV 19:11](https://ref.ly/Lev19:11), [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13)

### False witness is forbidden

**[EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16)** parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20) *See also* [LEV 19:11](https://ref.ly/Lev19:11)

### Coveting is forbidden

**[EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17)** parallel [DEU 5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:21) *See also* [LEV 19:17–19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:17-Lev19:18); [JOB 31:9–31:12](https://ref.ly/Job31:9-Job31:12); [ROM 7:7](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7)

The circumstances surrounding the giving of the Ten Commandments
----------------------------------------------------------------

### The Ten Commandments written on stone tablets

**[EXO 24:12](https://ref.ly/Exod24:12)** *See also* [DEU 4:13](https://ref.ly/Deut4:13), [DEU 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Deut9:9-Deut9:10)

### The stone tablets broken

**[EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19)** *Moses broke the stone tablets when he saw that the Israelites had sinned by making the golden calf.* *See also* [DEU 9:16–9:17](https://ref.ly/Deut9:16-Deut9:17)

### A second set of stone tablets made

**[EXO 34:1](https://ref.ly/Exod34:1), [EXO 34:28](https://ref.ly/Exod34:28)** *See also* [DEU 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Deut10:1-Deut10:2)

### The second set of stone tablets put in the ark of the covenant

**[EXO 40:20](https://ref.ly/Exod40:20)** *See also* [DEU 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Deut10:1-Deut10:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:1-17; Deuteronomy 5:6-21; Exodus 20:2-3; Deuteronomy 5:6-7; Deuteronomy 6:13-15; Exodus 20:4-6; Deuteronomy 5:8-10; Exodus 32:1-8; Leviticus 19:4; 1 Corinthians 10:7; Exodus 20:7; Deuteronomy 5:11; Matthew 7:21; Exodus 20:8-11; Deuteronomy 5:12-15; Exodus 16:23; Leviticus 19:3; Isaiah 56:2; Jeremiah 17:21-22; Exodus 20:12; Deuteronomy 5:16; Matthew 15:4; Ephesians 6:1-3; Exodus 20:13; Deuteronomy 5:17; Genesis 4:8-16; Matthew 5:21; Exodus 20:14; Deuteronomy 5:18; Leviticus 18:20; 2 Samuel 11:2-5; Matthew 5:27; Hebrews 13:4; Exodus 20:15; Deuteronomy 5:19; Leviticus 19:11; Leviticus 19:13; Exodus 20:16; Deuteronomy 5:20; Exodus 20:17; Deuteronomy 5:21; Leviticus 19:17-18; Job 31:9-12; Romans 7:7; Exodus 24:12; Deuteronomy 4:13; Deuteronomy 9:9-10; Exodus 32:19; Deuteronomy 9:16-17; Exodus 34:1; Exodus 34:28; Deuteronomy 10:1-2; Exodus 40:20
* **Associated Articles:** 5040: murder (ID: `5040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5061: sanctity of life (ID: `5061@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5104: Moses, foreshadower of Jesus Christ (ID: `5104@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5555: stealing (ID: `5555@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5800: blasphemy (ID: `5800@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6133: coveting (ID: `6133@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6242: adultery (ID: `6242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7221: exodus, the (ID: `7221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7428: Sabbath (ID: `7428@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8751: false witness (ID: `8751@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5378: law, OT (id: 5378)

OT laws and legal traditions govern every aspect of the life of the covenant people of God.

Kinds of OT law
---------------

### Criminal law

**[EXO 21:12–21:14](https://ref.ly/Exod21:12-Exod21:14)** *Criminal law deals with crimes against persons punishable by a court.*

### Civil law

**[DEU 16:18–16:20](https://ref.ly/Deut16:18-Deut16:20)** *Civil law deals with the upholding in court of legally established rights against possible abuse.* *See also* [DEU 15:12–15:18](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:18)

### Social law

**[EXO 22:21–22:22](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21-Exod22:22)** *Social law deals with behaviour or standards of behaviour which are not strictly enforceable in the courts.* *See also* [DEU 24:19–24:22](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19-Deut24:22)

### Cultic law

Cultic law deals explicitly with the ritual or religious life of the people of God. Leviticus chapters 1–7 are totally devoted to this kind of law: [LEV 1:10–1:13](https://ref.ly/Lev1:10-Lev1:13), [LEV 4:13–4:21](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13-Lev4:21), [LEV 7:11–7:18](https://ref.ly/Lev7:11-Lev7:18)

Examples of OT law
------------------

### Conditions for freeing servants

**[EXO 21:2–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2-Exod21:6)** parallel [DEU 15:12–15:18](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:18) *See also* [EXO 21:3–21:11](https://ref.ly/Exod21:3-Exod21:11); [LEV 25:39–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:55)

### Dealing with injuries

**[EXO 21:23–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:23-Exod21:25)** *See also* [MAT 5:38](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38)

### Property is to be protected

**[EXO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Exod22:1)** *See also* [LEV 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:7); [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

### The rights of aliens must be respected

**[EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21)** *See also* [LEV 19:33](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33); [DEU 10:19](https://ref.ly/Deut10:19)

### Justice must be universally respected

**[EXO 23:2–23:3](https://ref.ly/Exod23:2-Exod23:3)** *See also* [LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15)

### The Sabbath must be observed by all

**[EXO 23:12](https://ref.ly/Exod23:12)** *See also* [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11)

### Three annual festivals are to be celebrated

**[EXO 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15-Exod23:16)** *See also* [EXO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17); [DEU 16:16](https://ref.ly/Deut16:16)

### Worship must be in accordance with God's will and must be kept pure

[DEU 12:1–12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:1-Deut12:7), [DEU 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6-Deut13:8)

### Certain foods are declared to be unclean

[LEV 11:1–11:23](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:23) parallel [DEU 14:3–14:20](https://ref.ly/Deut14:3-Deut14:20)

### A tenth of all produce must be given to God

**[DEU 14:22](https://ref.ly/Deut14:22)** *See also* [LEV 27:30](https://ref.ly/Lev27:30)

### Cultic laws are grounded in the holiness of God

**[LEV 11:44](https://ref.ly/Lev11:44), [LEV 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Lev19:1-Lev19:2)** *See also* [1PE 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:16)LEV [1:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3), [LEV 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Lev2:1-Lev2:2), [LEV 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Lev3:1-Lev3:2), [LEV 4:27–4:28](https://ref.ly/Lev4:27-Lev4:28), [LEV 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Lev5:17-Lev5:18)

### Rules governing infectious or contagious diseases

**[LEV 13:2](https://ref.ly/Lev13:2), [LEV 15:13](https://ref.ly/Lev15:13)**

### There must not be unlawful sexual relations

**[LEV 18:6](https://ref.ly/Lev18:6)**

### A Day of Atonement must be held

*See also* [LEV 23:26–23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:26-Lev23:32) parallels [LEV 16:2–16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:2-Lev16:34), [NUM 29:7–29:11](https://ref.ly/Num29:7-Num29:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 21:12-14; Deuteronomy 16:18-20; Deuteronomy 15:12-18; Exodus 22:21-22; Deuteronomy 24:19-22; Leviticus 1:10-13; Leviticus 4:13-21; Leviticus 7:11-18; Exodus 21:2-6; Exodus 21:3-11; Leviticus 25:39-55; Exodus 21:23-25; Matthew 5:38; Exodus 22:1; Leviticus 6:1-7; Luke 19:8; Exodus 22:21; Leviticus 19:33; Deuteronomy 10:19; Exodus 23:2-3; Leviticus 19:15; Exodus 23:12; Exodus 20:8-11; Exodus 23:15-16; Exodus 12:17; Deuteronomy 16:16; Deuteronomy 12:1-7; Deuteronomy 13:6-8; Leviticus 11:1-23; Deuteronomy 14:3-20; Deuteronomy 14:22; Leviticus 27:30; Leviticus 11:44; Leviticus 19:1-2; 1 Peter 1:16; 1 Peter 1:3; Leviticus 2:1-2; Leviticus 3:1-2; Leviticus 4:27-28; Leviticus 5:17-18; Leviticus 13:2; Leviticus 15:13; Leviticus 18:6; Leviticus 23:26-32; Leviticus 16:2-34; Numbers 29:7-11
* **Associated Articles:** 5475: property (ID: `5475@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6614: atonement (ID: `6614@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7402: offerings (ID: `7402@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7424: ritual law (ID: `7424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7434: sacrifice (ID: `7434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8488: tithing (ID: `8488@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8622: worship (ID: `8622@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5379: law, Jesus Christ's attitude to (id: 5379)

Jesus Christ accepted the authority of the OT law and saw himself as coming to fulfil its purpose.

Jesus Christ's disputes with the Pharisees and teachers of the law
------------------------------------------------------------------

### The Pharisees and teachers of the law accuse Jesus Christ's disciples of not following tradition

**[MRK 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mark7:5)** parallel [MAT 15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:2)

### Jesus Christ accuses the Pharisees and teachers of the law of hypocrisy

**[MRK 7:6–7:8](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6-Mark7:8)** parallel [MAT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:7-Matt15:9) *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)

### Jesus Christ gives examples where human tradition is observed rather than God's law

[MRK 7:10–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10-Mark7:13) parallel [MAT 15:3–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:6); ; [MAT 23:1–23:36](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:36) parallels [MRK 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:39), [LUK 20:45–20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:46)

### The Pharisees accuse Jesus Christ of breaking the Sabbath

**[MRK 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark2:24)** parallels [MAT 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:2), [LUK 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:2)

### Jesus Christ demonstrates his authority over the Sabbath

**[MRK 2:27–3:4](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27-Mark3:4)** parallels [MAT 12:8–12:10](https://ref.ly/Matt12:8-Matt12:10), [LUK 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Luke6:5-Luke6:7)

### Jesus Christ challenges the religious leaders to think about principles not rules

**[MRK 3:4](https://ref.ly/Mark3:4)** parallels [LUK 6:9](https://ref.ly/Luke6:9), [MAT 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/Matt12:11-Matt12:12) *See also* [LUK 13:10–13:17](https://ref.ly/Luke13:10-Luke13:17)

Jesus Christ came to fulfil the law
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17), [MAT 5:21–5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:21-Matt5:22)** *Jesus Christ went beyond the literal rules of the law, to the thought behind the act.* *See also* [EXO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Exod20:13)**[MAT 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:27-Matt5:28)** *See also* [EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14)

Jesus Christ asserts the continuing validity of the law
-------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:18-Matt5:19)**; **[LUK 10:25–10:28](https://ref.ly/Luke10:25-Luke10:28)** parallels [MAT 22:37–22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:37-Matt22:39), [MRK 12:29–12:34](https://ref.ly/Mark12:29-Mark12:34) *See also* [LUK 10:29–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:29-Luke10:37), [LUK 16:16](https://ref.ly/Luke16:16)

Jesus Christ himself was obedient to the law and its commands
-------------------------------------------------------------

### In honouring his parents

[LUK 2:41–2:51](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41-Luke2:51)

### In being baptised

**[MAT 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Matt3:13-Matt3:15)**

### In resisting temptation

*See also* [LUK 4:1–4:13](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:13)

### In observing the Passover

**[LUK 22:7–22:8](https://ref.ly/Luke22:7-Luke22:8)**

### In submitting to the will of God

**[MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39)** parallels [MRK 14:35–14:36](https://ref.ly/Mark14:35-Mark14:36), [LUK 22:41–22:42](https://ref.ly/Luke22:41-Luke22:42)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 7:5; Matthew 15:2; Mark 7:6-8; Matthew 15:7-9; Isaiah 29:13; Mark 7:10-13; Matthew 15:3-6; Matthew 23:1-36; Mark 12:38-39; Luke 20:45-46; Mark 2:23-24; Matthew 12:1-2; Luke 6:1-2; Mark 2:27-3:4; Matthew 12:8-10; Luke 6:5-7; Mark 3:4; Luke 6:9; Matthew 12:11-12; Luke 13:10-17; Matthew 5:17; Matthew 5:21-22; Exodus 20:13; Matthew 5:27-28; Exodus 20:14; Matthew 5:18-19; Luke 10:25-28; Matthew 22:37-39; Mark 12:29-34; Luke 10:29-37; Luke 16:16; Luke 2:41-51; Matthew 3:13-15; Luke 4:1-13; Luke 22:7-8; Matthew 26:39; Mark 14:35-36; Luke 22:41-42
* **Associated Articles:** 2057: Jesus Christ, obedience of (ID: `2057@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2333: Jesus Christ, attitude to OT (ID: `2333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2545: Jesus Christ, opposition to (ID: `2545@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7464: teachers of the law (ID: `7464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8767: hypocrisy (ID: `8767@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5380: law, and gospel (id: 5380)

The law, which bears witness to the grace of God, points ahead to its fulfilment and climax in the gospel of Jesus Christ. The gospel does not abolish the law, but fulfils it, by allowing it to be seen in its proper light.

Human beings cannot fulfil the law by their own efforts
-------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rom3:20)**; **[GAL 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15-Gal2:16)** *See also* [ACT 13:39](https://ref.ly/Acts13:39); [ROM 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Rom4:13-Rom4:15)

The law brings knowledge of human sin and the need for redemption
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rom3:20), [ROM 5:20](https://ref.ly/Rom5:20)** *See also* [1TI 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:10); [1JN 3:4](https://ref.ly/1John3:4)

The law points to the coming of Jesus Christ
--------------------------------------------

**[GAL 3:24](https://ref.ly/Gal3:24)**

Believers are not justified by works of the law, but through faith in the blood of Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:28](https://ref.ly/Rom3:28)**; **[GAL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Gal3:11)** *See also* [HAB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4); [ROM 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/Rom4:1-Rom4:5); [GAL 3:10–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:10-Gal3:14); [EPH 2:15](https://ref.ly/Eph2:15)

The relationship between believers and the law
----------------------------------------------

### Dying to the law through Jesus Christ

**[GAL 2:19](https://ref.ly/Gal2:19)**

### The law remains valid for believers

**[ROM 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Rom7:11-Rom7:12)**; **[1TI 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:8)**; **[1PE 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:15-1Pet1:16)** *See also* [LEV 11:44–11:45](https://ref.ly/Lev11:44-Lev11:45), [LEV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Lev19:2), [LEV 20:7](https://ref.ly/Lev20:7); [ROM 7:7–7:11](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7-Rom7:11), [ROM 13:8–13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8-Rom13:10); [JAS 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Jas2:8-Jas2:11)

### The Holy Spirit enables believers to fulfil the law through Jesus Christ

**[ROM 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3-Rom8:4)** *See also* [JER 31:31–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:31-Jer31:34); [EZK 11:19–11:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:19-Ezek11:20); [GAL 5:13–5:18](https://ref.ly/Gal5:13-Gal5:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 3:20; Galatians 2:15-16; Acts 13:39; Romans 4:13-15; Romans 5:20; 1 Timothy 1:9-10; 1 John 3:4; Galatians 3:24; Romans 3:28; Galatians 3:11; Habakkuk 2:4; Romans 4:1-5; Galatians 3:10-14; Ephesians 2:15; Galatians 2:19; Romans 7:11-12; 1 Timothy 1:8; 1 Peter 1:15-16; Leviticus 11:44-45; Leviticus 19:2; Leviticus 20:7; Romans 7:7-11; Romans 13:8-10; James 2:8-11; Romans 8:3-4; Jeremiah 31:31-34; Ezekiel 11:19-20; Galatians 5:13-18

## 5381: law, letter and spirit of (id: 5381)

A rigid adherence to the letter of the law often masks hypocrisy and neglect of its spirit, namely having God at the centre of one's life and putting others before oneself, or recognising that the law points to Jesus Christ.

The letter of the law
---------------------

### Overemphasis on keeping some parts of the law

**[MRK 7:1–7:8](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:8)** parallel [MAT 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:2) *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13); [MAT 9:10–9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10-Matt9:13) parallels [MRK 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15-Mark2:17), [LUK 5:29–5:32](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29-Luke5:32)

### Hypocrisy with regard to keeping the law

**[MRK 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9-Mark7:13)** parallel [MAT 15:3–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:6) *See also* [MAT 23:1–23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:33); [LUK 11:37–11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37-Luke11:52), [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14); [JHN 9:1–9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:16); [ROM 2:17–2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:17-Rom2:24); [ISA 52:5](https://ref.ly/Isa52:5)

The spirit of the law
---------------------

### Jesus Christ and the law

**[MAT 5:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17-Matt6:19)** *See also* [MAT 19:16–19:30](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16-Matt19:30) parallels [MRK 10:17–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17-Mark10:30), [LUK 18:18–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18-Luke18:30)

### Jesus Christ's attitude to the Sabbath

**[MRK 2:23–3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark3:6)** parallels [MAT 12:1–12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:14), [LUK 6:1–6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:11) *See also* [MRK 1:21–1:28](https://ref.ly/Mark1:21-Mark1:28) parallel [LUK 4:31–4:37](https://ref.ly/Luke4:31-Luke4:37); [LUK 13:10–13:17](https://ref.ly/Luke13:10-Luke13:17), [LUK 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1-Luke14:6); [JHN 5:1–5:16](https://ref.ly/John5:1-John5:16), [JHN 7:21–7:24](https://ref.ly/John7:21-John7:24)

### Jesus Christ's treatment of the woman taken in adultery

**[JHN 8:2–8:11](https://ref.ly/John8:2-John8:11)**

### The greatest commandment

**[MAT 22:34–22:40](https://ref.ly/Matt22:34-Matt22:40)** parallel [MRK 12:28–12:34](https://ref.ly/Mark12:28-Mark12:34) *See also* [MAT 25:31–25:46](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31-Matt25:46)

### The importance of obedience and right attitudes outweighs that of outward actions

**[ROM 2:25–2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:25-Rom2:29)** *See also* [1SA 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22-1Sam15:23); [PSA 51:16–51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:16-Ps51:17); [PRO 21:3](https://ref.ly/Prov21:3); [ISA 1:11–1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:17); [JER 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Jer7:21-Jer7:23); [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24); [MIC 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:6-Mic6:8); [GAL 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gal3:1-Gal3:5)

### The spirit of the law is embodied in the new covenant

**[2CO 3:3–3:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:3-2Cor3:6)** *See also* [JHN 4:19–4:24](https://ref.ly/John4:19-John4:24); [ROM 7:4–7:6](https://ref.ly/Rom7:4-Rom7:6), [ROM 8:1–8:11](https://ref.ly/Rom8:1-Rom8:11); [1CO 15:45–15:46](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:45-1Cor15:46); [2CO 3:13–3:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:13-2Cor3:18); [GAL 5:18](https://ref.ly/Gal5:18); [HEB 7:18–7:22](https://ref.ly/Heb7:18-Heb7:22), [HEB 8:1–8:13](https://ref.ly/Heb8:1-Heb8:13); [JER 31:31–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:31-Jer31:34)

## 5383: lawsuits (id: 5383)

Legal trials in which the court decides between two opposing parties. OT law makes provision for such trials, insisting on justice for all, regardless of social status.

Regulations relating to lawsuits
--------------------------------

### Warnings against discrimination on the basis of social status

**[EXO 23:6](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6)** *See also* [EXO 23:3](https://ref.ly/Exod23:3); [LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15); [DEU 1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17); [PRO 22:22–22:23](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22-Prov22:23)

### Warnings against false testimony

**[EXO 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1-Exod23:2)** *See also* [EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16) parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20); [DEU 19:15–19:21](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15-Deut19:21); [ZEC 8:16](https://ref.ly/Zech8:16)

### Provision for a court of appeal

**[DEU 17:8–17:13](https://ref.ly/Deut17:8-Deut17:13)** *See also* [DEU 1:15–1:18](https://ref.ly/Deut1:15-Deut1:18)

Lawsuits to judge between two parties
-------------------------------------

**[DEU 25:1](https://ref.ly/Deut25:1)** *See also* [EXO 18:16](https://ref.ly/Exod18:16), [EXO 21:22](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22); [JOB 9:32–9:35](https://ref.ly/Job9:32-Job9:35); [PRO 25:7–25:10](https://ref.ly/Prov25:7-Prov25:10), [PRO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Prov29:9); [MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26); [JAS 2:5–2:7](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5-Jas2:7)

Believers should not bring lawsuits against each other
------------------------------------------------------

[1CO 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1-1Cor6:8)

Injustice in lawsuits
---------------------

**[AMO 5:15](https://ref.ly/Amos5:15)** *See also* [ISA 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:20-Isa29:21); [AMO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:7), [AMO 5:7–5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:7-Amos5:12)

Excess of lawsuits a symptom of social disorder
-----------------------------------------------

**[HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4)**

Examples of lawsuits
--------------------

**[2SA 15:1–15:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:4)** *See also* [2SA 14:1–14:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:1-2Sam14:20); [1KI 3:16–3:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:16-1Kgs3:27); [2KI 6:26–6:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:26-2Kgs6:29); [ACT 18:12–18:16](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12-Acts18:16), [ACT 19:35–19:41](https://ref.ly/Acts19:35-Acts19:41)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 23:6; Exodus 23:3; Leviticus 19:15; Deuteronomy 1:17; Proverbs 22:22-23; Exodus 23:1-2; Exodus 20:16; Deuteronomy 5:20; Deuteronomy 19:15-21; Zechariah 8:16; Deuteronomy 17:8-13; Deuteronomy 1:15-18; Deuteronomy 25:1; Exodus 18:16; Exodus 21:22; Job 9:32-35; Proverbs 25:7-10; Proverbs 29:9; Matthew 5:25-26; James 2:5-7; 1 Corinthians 6:1-8; Amos 5:15; Isaiah 29:20-21; Amos 2:7; Amos 5:7-12; Hosea 10:4; 2 Samuel 15:1-4; 2 Samuel 14:1-20; 1 Kings 3:16-27; 2 Kings 6:26-29; Acts 18:12-16; Acts 19:35-41

## 5384: lawyer (id: 5384)

A lawyer as a person skilled in the Mosaic law
----------------------------------------------

[MAT 22:35](https://ref.ly/Matt22:35); ; [LUK 7:30](https://ref.ly/Luke7:30), [LUK 10:25](https://ref.ly/Luke10:25), [LUK 10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:37), [LUK 11:45–11:46](https://ref.ly/Luke11:45-Luke11:46), [LUK 11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:52), [LUK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Luke14:3); ; [TIT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Titus3:13)

A lawyer as a person trained in rhetoric
----------------------------------------

[ACT 24:1](https://ref.ly/Acts24:1) *The word translated “lawyer” means literally “orator”: a person trained in rhetoric who served as an attorney\-at\-law in a court trial.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 22:35; Luke 7:30; Luke 10:25; Luke 10:37; Luke 11:45-46; Luke 11:52; Luke 14:3; Titus 3:13; Acts 24:1
* **Associated Articles:** 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5383: lawsuits (ID: `5383@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7464: teachers of the law (ID: `7464@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5385: leisure (id: 5385)

Time for rest and recreational activities. Economic conditions in biblical times did not allow ordinary people much free time for leisure. However Scripture does give principles which apply to leisure.

* **Associated Articles:** 5386: leisure, nature and purpose of (ID: `5386@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5387: leisure, and pastimes (ID: `5387@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5386: leisure, nature and purpose of (id: 5386)

Scripture affirms that periods of rest and leisure are part of God's creation order. These periods of rest are to be used wisely.

The need for leisure
--------------------

### The Sabbath rest

**[GEN 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gen2:2)**; **[EXO 20:8–20:10](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:10)** parallel [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15) *See also* [EXO 23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:11) *During the sabbath year, the labourers presumably also rested*; [LEV 23:3](https://ref.ly/Lev23:3)

### Rest commanded by Jesus Christ

**[MRK 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31-Mark6:32)**

Leisure a gift of God
---------------------

**[PSA 23:2](https://ref.ly/Ps23:2)** *See also* [MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28); [MRK 2:27](https://ref.ly/Mark2:27); [REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13)

### Loss of leisure a judgment

**[ISA 48:22](https://ref.ly/Isa48:22)** *See also* [ECC 2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:23), [ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12); [ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21); [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:11)

Responsible use of leisure
--------------------------

### Work must come first

[PRO 10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:5); ; [LUK 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Luke17:7-Luke17:8)

### Remember God's presence

**[1CO 10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:31)** *See also* [ECC 9:7–9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:7-Eccl9:10)

### Use time wisely

**[EPH 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Eph5:15-Eph5:16)**

The dangers of leisure
----------------------

### Idleness

**[1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13)** *See also* [PRO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:9-Prov6:11), [PRO 24:30–24:31](https://ref.ly/Prov24:30-Prov24:31), [PRO 26:14](https://ref.ly/Prov26:14); [ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18); [2TH 3:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:11); [HEB 6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:12)

### Preoccupation with pleasure

**[LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14)** *See also* [PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17); [ISA 5:11](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11); [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13); [1TI 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:6); [2TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:4); [JAS 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jas5:5)

### Susceptibility to sin

**[1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13)** *See also* [2SA 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:1-2Sam11:5); [EST 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Esth1:10-Esth1:11); [AMO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1)

### Spiritual complacency

**[AMO 6:1](https://ref.ly/Amos6:1), [AMO 6:4–6:7](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4-Amos6:7)** *See also* [ISA 5:11–5:13](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11-Isa5:13); [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21), [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:2; Exodus 20:8-10; Deuteronomy 5:12-15; Exodus 23:11; Leviticus 23:3; Mark 6:31-32; Psalms 23:2; Matthew 11:28; Mark 2:27; Revelation 14:13; Isaiah 48:22; Ecclesiastes 2:23; Ecclesiastes 5:12; Isaiah 57:20-21; Revelation 14:11; Proverbs 10:5; Luke 17:7-8; 1 Corinthians 10:31; Ecclesiastes 9:7-10; Ephesians 5:15-16; 1 Timothy 5:13; Proverbs 6:9-11; Proverbs 24:30-31; Proverbs 26:14; Ecclesiastes 10:18; 2 Thessalonians 3:11; Hebrews 6:12; Luke 8:14; Proverbs 21:17; Isaiah 5:11; Luke 15:13; 1 Timothy 5:6; 2 Timothy 3:4; James 5:5; 2 Samuel 11:1-5; Esther 1:10-11; Amos 4:1; Amos 6:1; Amos 6:4-7; Isaiah 5:11-13; Luke 12:16-21; Luke 21:34
* **Associated Articles:** 5056: rest (ID: `5056@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5343: idleness (ID: `5343@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5532: sleep (ID: `5532@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5537: sleeplessness (ID: `5537@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5539: sluggard (ID: `5539@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5810: complacency (ID: `5810@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5918: pleasure (ID: `5918@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7428: Sabbath (ID: `7428@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8808: riches (ID: `8808@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5387: leisure, and pastimes (id: 5387)

Scripture gives examples of many different kinds of leisure activity.

Necessary leisure activities
----------------------------

Sleeping
--------

**[PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2)**; **[ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12)** *See also* [PSA 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ps4:8); [MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38); [ACT 12:6](https://ref.ly/Acts12:6)

### Eating and drinking

[GEN 24:54](https://ref.ly/Gen24:54); ; [ECC 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:13)

Pleasurable pastimes
--------------------

### Hospitality

[GEN 18:3–18:5](https://ref.ly/Gen18:3-Gen18:5); ; [1PE 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:9)

### Feasting

[1KI 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:15); ; [NEH 8:10](https://ref.ly/Neh8:10); ; [EST 8:17](https://ref.ly/Esth8:17); ; [JOB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Job1:4)

### Music

**[1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23)** *See also* [1SA 16:16–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:16-1Sam16:18); [2KI 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:14-2Kgs3:15)

### Dancing

**[ECC 3:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:4)** [EXO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Exod15:20); ; [JDG 11:34](https://ref.ly/Judg11:34); ; [1SA 18:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6); ; [JOB 21:11](https://ref.ly/Job21:11); ; [JER 31:4](https://ref.ly/Jer31:4); [JDG 21:19–21:21](https://ref.ly/Judg21:19-Judg21:21); [MRK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22) *dancing in revelry*; [2SA 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14-2Sam6:16), [2SA 6:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:21); ; [PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3), [PSA 150:4](https://ref.ly/Ps150:4)

### Physical recreation

**[1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:8)** *See also* [1CO 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:27) *Although Paul never mentions Christians taking part in the games (which were often associated with pagan festivals), he does show an appreciation of the discipline necessary*; [2TI 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:5)

### Gardening

**[ECC 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:5-Eccl2:6)** *See also* [GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)

### Enjoyment of nature

[PSA 8:3](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3), [PSA 104:10–104:14](https://ref.ly/Ps104:10-Ps104:14)

### Sightseeing

[MRK 13:1](https://ref.ly/Mark13:1); ; [ACT 17:16](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16)

### Education and literature

**[ECC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13)** *See also* [1KI 4:32–4:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:32-1Kgs4:34); [EST 6:1](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1); [PRO 1:5](https://ref.ly/Prov1:5); [ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12); [ACT 17:21](https://ref.ly/Acts17:21); [2TI 4:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:13)

### Children's games

**[ZEC 8:5](https://ref.ly/Zech8:5)** *See also* [MAT 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Matt11:16-Matt11:17) parallel [LUK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Luke7:32)

Harmful pastimes
----------------

### Licentious amusements

**[1PE 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3)** *See also* [GEN 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Gen9:20-Gen9:21); [EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19); [PRO 23:29–23:30](https://ref.ly/Prov23:29-Prov23:30); [ROM 13:13](https://ref.ly/Rom13:13); [EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4), [EPH 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eph5:12)

### Cruel amusements

[JDG 16:25](https://ref.ly/Judg16:25); ; [2SA 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:14)

## 5389: lending (id: 5389)

Freely giving the temporary use of property or possessions at no personal advantage. Scripture lays down conditions for lending and emphasises the responsibilities that it brings.

The motivation for lending
--------------------------

### Generosity

**[PSA 37:25–37:26](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25-Ps37:26)** *See also* [DEU 15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:11); [PRO 3:27–3:28](https://ref.ly/Prov3:27-Prov3:28); [MAT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:42)

### Pity

**[DEU 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:8)** *See also* [LEV 25:35–25:36](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:36); [MRK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Mark14:7)

### Not necessarily expecting repayment

[LUK 6:34–6:36](https://ref.ly/Luke6:34-Luke6:36)

The dangers of lending
----------------------

### Domination

**[PRO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Prov22:7)** *See also* [DEU 15:6](https://ref.ly/Deut15:6), [DEU 28:12–28:13](https://ref.ly/Deut28:12-Deut28:13), [DEU 28:43–28:44](https://ref.ly/Deut28:43-Deut28:44); [NEH 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:4-Neh5:5)

### Loss

[EXO 22:14](https://ref.ly/Exod22:14); ; [2KI 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:5); ; [ISA 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Isa24:1-Isa24:2)

### Exploitation

**[LEV 25:36–25:37](https://ref.ly/Lev25:36-Lev25:37)**

### Usury

**[EXO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25)** *See also* [DEU 23:19](https://ref.ly/Deut23:19); [PSA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:5); [EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8), [EZK 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:12-Ezek18:13)

Safeguards for the lender
-------------------------

### Under the old covenant

**[EXO 22:14](https://ref.ly/Exod22:14)** *This specific case probably represents a general principle of restitution governing all kinds of lending.*

### Under the new covenant

**[LUK 6:30–6:31](https://ref.ly/Luke6:30-Luke6:31)**

### Generosity may be risky but brings its reward

**[PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17)** *See also* [PSA 112:5](https://ref.ly/Ps112:5); [MAT 5:7](https://ref.ly/Matt5:7)

Jesus Christ was lent a donkey
------------------------------

[MAT 21:3](https://ref.ly/Matt21:3) parallels [MRK 11:3](https://ref.ly/Mark11:3), [LUK 19:31](https://ref.ly/Luke19:31); ; [LUK 19:34](https://ref.ly/Luke19:34)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 37:25-26; Deuteronomy 15:11; Proverbs 3:27-28; Matthew 5:42; Deuteronomy 15:7-8; Leviticus 25:35-36; Mark 14:7; Luke 6:34-36; Proverbs 22:7; Deuteronomy 15:6; Deuteronomy 28:12-13; Deuteronomy 28:43-44; Nehemiah 5:4-5; Exodus 22:14; 2 Kings 6:5; Isaiah 24:1-2; Leviticus 25:36-37; Exodus 22:25; Deuteronomy 23:19; Psalms 15:5; Ezekiel 18:8; Ezekiel 18:12-13; Luke 6:30-31; Proverbs 19:17; Psalms 112:5; Matthew 5:7; Matthew 21:3; Mark 11:3; Luke 19:31; Luke 19:34
* **Associated Articles:** 5233: borrowing (ID: `5233@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5274: credit (ID: `5274@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5289: debt (ID: `5289@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5310: exploitation (ID: `5310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5353: interest (ID: `5353@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5446: poverty (ID: `5446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5492: restitution (ID: `5492@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5806: compassion (ID: `5806@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8260: generosity (ID: `8260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8291: kindness (ID: `8291@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8434: giving (ID: `8434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5390: leprosy (id: 5390)

An infectious skin disease. The leprosy mentioned in Scripture, however, covers a wider range of dermatological conditions than that now referred to as the modern Hansen's disease, known colloquially as leprosy.

Regulations for the control of leprosy
--------------------------------------

### Sufferers are banned from society

**[NUM 5:2](https://ref.ly/Num5:2)** *See also* [LEV 13:1–13:46](https://ref.ly/Lev13:1-Lev13:46); [DEU 24:8–24:9](https://ref.ly/Deut24:8-Deut24:9); [2KI 7:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:3)

### Sufferers are banned from Levitical service

**[LEV 22:4](https://ref.ly/Lev22:4)**

### Sufferers are to report to a priest on being cured

**[MAT 8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:4)** *See also* [LUK 17:14](https://ref.ly/Luke17:14); [LEV 14:1–14:9](https://ref.ly/Lev14:1-Lev14:9)

The healing of leprosy
----------------------

[2KI 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:1), [2KI 5:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:14); ; [MAT 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:3) parallel [MRK 1:40–1:41](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40-Mark1:41); ; [LUK 17:12–17:15](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12-Luke17:15)

Spiritual significance of leprosy
---------------------------------

### As a validating sign from God

[EXO 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Exod4:6-Exod4:7)

### As a judgment from God

[NUM 12:10–12:15](https://ref.ly/Num12:10-Num12:15); ; [2KI 5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:27); ; [2CH 26:19–26:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19-2Chr26:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 5:2; Leviticus 13:1-46; Deuteronomy 24:8-9; 2 Kings 7:3; Leviticus 22:4; Matthew 8:4; Luke 17:14; Leviticus 14:1-9; 2 Kings 5:1; 2 Kings 5:14; Matthew 8:2-3; Mark 1:40-41; Luke 17:12-15; Exodus 4:6-7; Numbers 12:10-15; 2 Kings 5:27; 2 Chronicles 26:19-23
* **Associated Articles:** 2351: Jesus Christ, miracles of (ID: `2351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5182: skin (ID: `5182@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5297: disease (ID: `5297@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5333: healing (ID: `5333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8341: separation (ID: `8341@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5391: letters (id: 5391)

A form of written communication often used for the transfer of instruction, information and advice or important news.

Letters of encouragement or exhortation
---------------------------------------

**[EST 9:30](https://ref.ly/Esth9:30)**; **[2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15)**; **[HEB 13:22](https://ref.ly/Heb13:22)** *See also* [2CH 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:11), [2CH 21:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:12); [ISA 39:1](https://ref.ly/Isa39:1); [JER 29:1](https://ref.ly/Jer29:1); [ACT 15:23–15:31](https://ref.ly/Acts15:23-Acts15:31); [1CO 5:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9); [2CO 10:9–10:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:9-2Cor10:11); [COL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Col4:16); [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:27); [2PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:1)

-----------------------------------------

------------------------------------------

**[EZR 7:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:11)**; **[1CO 16:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:3)** *See also* [2KI 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:5-2Kgs5:6); [NEH 2:7–2:9](https://ref.ly/Neh2:7-Neh2:9); [ACT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Acts23:25), [ACT 23:33–23:34](https://ref.ly/Acts23:33-Acts23:34); [2CO 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:1)

## 5392: linen (id: 5392)

Fine cloth woven from flax used in clothing and curtains. It was also used in burials.

People dressed in linen
-----------------------

### Linen clothing

**[JDG 14:12–14:13](https://ref.ly/Judg14:12-Judg14:13)** *See also* [JER 13:1](https://ref.ly/Jer13:1); [HOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hos2:5), [HOS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hos2:9); [MRK 14:51](https://ref.ly/Mark14:51)

### It expresssed prosperity

**[GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42)** *See also* [EST 8:15](https://ref.ly/Esth8:15); [ISA 3:23](https://ref.ly/Isa3:23); [EZK 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:10), [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13); [LUK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19); [REV 18:16](https://ref.ly/Rev18:16), [REV 19:8](https://ref.ly/Rev19:8)

### It was worn by priests

**[LEV 16:23](https://ref.ly/Lev16:23)** *See also* [EXO 28:39](https://ref.ly/Exod28:39), [EXO 28:42](https://ref.ly/Exod28:42); [LEV 6:10](https://ref.ly/Lev6:10), [LEV 16:4](https://ref.ly/Lev16:4), [LEV 16:32](https://ref.ly/Lev16:32); [1SA 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:18), [1SA 22:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:18); [2SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14); [1CH 15:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:27); [2CH 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12); [EZK 44:17–44:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:17-Ezek44:18)

### It was worn by angelic visitors

**[REV 15:6](https://ref.ly/Rev15:6)** *See also* [EZK 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:2-Ezek9:3), [EZK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:11), [EZK 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:2), [EZK 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:6-Ezek10:7), [EZK 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:3); [DAN 10:5](https://ref.ly/Dan10:5), [DAN 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/Dan12:6-Dan12:7); [REV 19:8](https://ref.ly/Rev19:8), [REV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Rev19:14)

## 5393: literacy (id: 5393)

Literacy in the OT
------------------

**[2KI 19:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:14)** *See also* [2KI 5:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:7), [2KI 23:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:2); [NEH 8:2](https://ref.ly/Neh8:2); [JER 29:29](https://ref.ly/Jer29:29), [JER 51:61](https://ref.ly/Jer51:61)

Literacy in the NT
------------------

**[LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16)**; **[ACT 8:28](https://ref.ly/Acts8:28)**; **[REV 1:3](https://ref.ly/Rev1:3)** *See also* [ACT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Acts13:15); [2CO 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:13); [EPH 3:4](https://ref.ly/Eph3:4); [COL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Col4:16); [1TH 5:27](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:27); [1TI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:13)

The use of secretaries for reading and writing
----------------------------------------------

[2KI 22:8–22:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:8-2Kgs22:10); ; [EZR 4:18](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:18), [EZR 4:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:23); ; [EST 6:1](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1); ; [JER 36:4](https://ref.ly/Jer36:4); ; [ROM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Rom16:22)

---------------------------------

**[NEH 9:38](https://ref.ly/Neh9:38)**; **[JHN 8:8](https://ref.ly/John8:8)**; **[PHM 1:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:19)** *See also* [DEU 31:24–31:26](https://ref.ly/Deut31:24-Deut31:26); [JOS 18:9](https://ref.ly/Josh18:9); [1CH 28:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:19); [LUK 1:63](https://ref.ly/Luke1:63); [JHN 19:20](https://ref.ly/John19:20); [ACT 15:27](https://ref.ly/Acts15:27)

## 5394: lordship (id: 5394)

The exercise of power and authority. Lordship belongs to God by nature but may be delegated to human beings. The lordship of Jesus Christ over humanity rests upon his divine nature.

* **Associated Articles:** 5395: lordship, human and divine (ID: `5395@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5396: lordship, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5396@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5395: lordship, human and divine (id: 5395)

Scripture recognises that human beings exercise authority in various capacities and ways. However, it insists that all human authority derives ultimately from God himself.

Human lordship within society
-----------------------------

**[LUK 12:42](https://ref.ly/Luke12:42)**

### The lordship of masters over their servants

*See also* [PSA 123:2](https://ref.ly/Ps123:2); [MAT 18:25](https://ref.ly/Matt18:25), [MAT 25:19](https://ref.ly/Matt25:19); [LUK 12:36](https://ref.ly/Luke12:36), [LUK 16:3](https://ref.ly/Luke16:3); [EPH 6:5](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5), [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9); [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22)

### The lordship of owners over their possessions

[JDG 19:23](https://ref.ly/Judg19:23); ; [MRK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Mark12:9) parallels [MAT 21:40](https://ref.ly/Matt21:40), [LUK 20:15](https://ref.ly/Luke20:15); ; [LUK 19:33](https://ref.ly/Luke19:33); ; [GAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Gal4:1)

### The use of Lord (NIV \`\`Sir'') as a title of respect

[MAT 27:63](https://ref.ly/Matt27:63); ; [LUK 13:8](https://ref.ly/Luke13:8); ; [JHN 12:21](https://ref.ly/John12:21), [JHN 20:15](https://ref.ly/John20:15); ; [ACT 16:30](https://ref.ly/Acts16:30); ; [1PE 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:6)

Human lordship within the church
--------------------------------

**[MRK 10:42–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:45)** parallels [MAT 20:25–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25-Matt20:28), [LUK 22:25–22:27](https://ref.ly/Luke22:25-Luke22:27)

### Christian lordship as a pastoral responsibility

**[1PE 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2-1Pet5:3)**

The lordship of God
-------------------

### \`\`Lord'' as a title for God

**[EXO 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Exod3:14-Exod3:15)** *In the NIV, “Lord” indicates a person with authority (this can be used of God) and “LORD” indicates Yahweh, the special Israelite name for God, revealed here to Moses.* [EXO 6:3](https://ref.ly/Exod6:3), [EXO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6); ; [ISA 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1-Isa6:5), [ISA 45:5–45:8](https://ref.ly/Isa45:5-Isa45:8), [ISA 45:18](https://ref.ly/Isa45:18); ; [HOS 2:20](https://ref.ly/Hos2:20); [LUK 1:32](https://ref.ly/Luke1:32), [LUK 2:9](https://ref.ly/Luke2:9); ; [REV 11:15](https://ref.ly/Rev11:15), [REV 16:7](https://ref.ly/Rev16:7)

### God is Lord of heaven and earth

**[PSA 135:5–135:6](https://ref.ly/Ps135:5-Ps135:6)**; **[MAT 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25)** parallel [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21) *See also* [PSA 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2-Ps2:4), [PSA 57:11](https://ref.ly/Ps57:11), [PSA 97:9](https://ref.ly/Ps97:9); [DAN 2:47](https://ref.ly/Dan2:47); [ACT 17:24](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24); [1TI 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15)

The revelation of the lordship of God
-------------------------------------

### The word of the LORD is the authoritative revelation of God's lordship

**[EZK 3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:11)**; **[1PE 1:25](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:25)** *See also* [ISA 40:8](https://ref.ly/Isa40:8), [ISA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Isa1:2), [ISA 45:19](https://ref.ly/Isa45:19); [JER 1:2](https://ref.ly/Jer1:2), [JER 1:9](https://ref.ly/Jer1:9); [EZK 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:3), [EZK 2:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:4)ROM [12:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:19); [DEU 32:352](https://ref.ly/Deut32:352)CO [6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Deut6:16-Deut6:18); [LEV 26:12](https://ref.ly/Lev26:12); [ISA 52:11](https://ref.ly/Isa52:11); [EZK 20:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:34), [EZK 20:41](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:41); [2SA 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:14)[JAS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Jas5:10)ACT [8:25](https://ref.ly/Jas8:25), [ACT 13:49](https://ref.ly/Acts13:49), [ACT 15:36](https://ref.ly/Acts15:36), [ACT 16:32](https://ref.ly/Acts16:32), [ACT 19:10](https://ref.ly/Acts19:10), [ACT 19:20](https://ref.ly/Acts19:20)

### God's lordship will be revealed to all on the day of the LORD

**[ISA 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11-Isa2:12), [ISA 2:17](https://ref.ly/Isa2:17)**; **[ZEP 1:14–1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14-Zeph1:18)** *See also* [ISA 13:6](https://ref.ly/Isa13:6), [ISA 13:9–13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9-Isa13:11); [JOL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Joel2:1-Joel2:2); [AMO 5:18–5:20](https://ref.ly/Amos5:18-Amos5:20); [1CO 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:5); [1TH 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:2); [2TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:2); [2PE 3:101](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:101)CO [1:8](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:8); [2CO 1:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:14)

## 5396: lordship, of Jesus Christ (id: 5396)

Jesus Christ has authority and dominion over the church and the world on account of his divine status, which was publicly demonstrated through his resurrection from the dead and exaltation to the right hand of God.

Lord as a title for Jesus Christ
--------------------------------

**[ROM 10:9](https://ref.ly/Rom10:9)** *In view of the OT background to the word “LORD” it is clear that this confession was not just of Jesus Christ's authority but also of his divinity*; **[2CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:5)** *See also* [MRK 2:28](https://ref.ly/Mark2:28) parallels [MAT 12:8](https://ref.ly/Matt12:8), [LUK 6:5](https://ref.ly/Luke6:5); [JHN 13:13](https://ref.ly/John13:13), [JHN 20:28](https://ref.ly/John20:28); [ACT 9:17](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17); [1CO 8:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:6), [1CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:3); [EPH 4:5](https://ref.ly/Eph4:5)

Jesus Christ is the Lord of the church
--------------------------------------

**[COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18)** *See also* [ROM 14:8](https://ref.ly/Rom14:8); [1CO 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:4-1Cor5:5); [2CO 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:8), [2CO 13:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:10); [EPH 1:22](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22); [COL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Col2:6)

Jesus Christ is Lord over all
-----------------------------

**[MAT 28:18](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18)**; **[1PE 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:21-1Pet3:22)** *See also* [ACT 10:36](https://ref.ly/Acts10:36); [1CO 15:24–15:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24-1Cor15:25); [EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10); [COL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:10)

The lordship of Jesus Christ is grounded in his resurrection from the dead
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:36)**; **[ROM 14:9](https://ref.ly/Rom14:9)** *See also* [JHN 13:3](https://ref.ly/John13:3); [ROM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Rom1:4); [EPH 1:20–1:22](https://ref.ly/Eph1:20-Eph1:22); [PHP 2:6–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:11); [HEB 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3-Heb1:4)

### The exaltation of Jesus Christ to God's right hand

**[HEB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Heb1:13)** *See also* [PSA 110:1](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1); [MRK 12:36](https://ref.ly/Mark12:36) parallels [MAT 22:44](https://ref.ly/Matt22:44), [LUK 20:42–20:43](https://ref.ly/Luke20:42-Luke20:43); [MRK 14:62](https://ref.ly/Mark14:62) parallels [MAT 26:64](https://ref.ly/Matt26:64), [LUK 22:69](https://ref.ly/Luke22:69); [ACT 2:33–2:35](https://ref.ly/Acts2:33-Acts2:35); [1CO 15:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:25); [EPH 1:20](https://ref.ly/Eph1:20); [COL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Col3:1); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3), [HEB 8:1](https://ref.ly/Heb8:1), [HEB 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Heb10:12-Heb10:13), [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)

The coming of the Lord Jesus Christ will reveal his lordship

## 5398: loss (id: 5398)

In a fallen world, believers are always liable to lose those things that they hold precious. Whilst offering comfort, Scripture urges believers to set their hearts upon eternal treasures, which cannot be lost.

Loss is part of a fallen world
------------------------------

**[MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19)**

Loss of property
----------------

### Through theft

*See also* [JOB 1:15](https://ref.ly/Job1:15), [JOB 1:17](https://ref.ly/Job1:17)

### Through accident

[GEN 31:39](https://ref.ly/Gen31:39); ; [EXO 21:33–21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:33-Exod21:34), [EXO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:15); ; [2KI 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:5)

### Through disaster

[JOB 1:16](https://ref.ly/Job1:16); ; [ACT 27:10](https://ref.ly/Acts27:10), [ACT 27:18–27:21](https://ref.ly/Acts27:18-Acts27:21)

### Through one's own fault

[PRO 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:10-Prov6:11), [PRO 24:33–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:33-Prov24:34), [PRO 13:11](https://ref.ly/Prov13:11), [PRO 13:18](https://ref.ly/Prov13:18); ; [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13)

### Through the judgment of God

[JER 17:4](https://ref.ly/Jer17:4); ; [MAL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mal1:4)

Loss of family and friends
--------------------------

### Through bereavement

[GEN 23:2](https://ref.ly/Gen23:2), [GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34), [GEN 50:3](https://ref.ly/Gen50:3); ; [NUM 20:29](https://ref.ly/Num20:29); ; [DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8); ; [RUT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:21) *Naomi had lost her husband and her two sons*; ; [2SA 12:18–12:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:18-2Sam12:23); ; [2KI 4:18–4:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:18-2Kgs4:21); ; [EZK 24:15–24:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:15-Ezek24:18); ; [LUK 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11-Luke7:13)

### Through violence and disaster

[2SA 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:12); ; [JOB 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Job1:18-Job1:19); ; [ISA 47:8–47:9](https://ref.ly/Isa47:8-Isa47:9)

### Through alienation

[JOB 19:13–19:22](https://ref.ly/Job19:13-Job19:22); ; [PSA 31:11–31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:11-Ps31:12); ; [1CO 7:10–7:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:10-1Cor7:15)

Losses in warfare
-----------------

[GEN 14:11–14:12](https://ref.ly/Gen14:11-Gen14:12); ; [1SA 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:17), [1SA 23:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:5), [1SA 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:1-1Sam30:2); ; [1KI 20:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:21); ; [2CH 13:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:17), [2CH 36:17–36:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:17-2Chr36:20); ; [LUK 21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:24)

-------------------

### Health and strength

Spiritual loss
--------------

### Of the ungodly

### Of the apostate

Voluntary loss
--------------

### By Jesus Christ for the salvation of humanity

[PHP 2:6–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:11)

### For Jesus Christ

**[MAT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Matt16:25)** parallels [MRK 8:35](https://ref.ly/Mark8:35), [LUK 9:24](https://ref.ly/Luke9:24)**[PHP 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:7-Phil3:9)** *See also* [MAT 10:39](https://ref.ly/Matt10:39), [MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12); [1CO 4:9–4:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:9-1Cor4:13); [HEB 10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:34), [HEB 11:24–11:26](https://ref.ly/Heb11:24-Heb11:26)

Losses made good
----------------

### By restitution

[EXO 21:19](https://ref.ly/Exod21:19), [EXO 21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:34)

### By recovery

[LUK 15:4](https://ref.ly/Luke15:4), [LUK 15:8](https://ref.ly/Luke15:8), [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20)

### By God's goodness

[JOB 42:10](https://ref.ly/Job42:10); ; [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29) parallels [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30), [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30); ; [ROM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Rom11:12)

God seeks the lost soul
-----------------------

**[LUK 19:10](https://ref.ly/Luke19:10)** *See also* [LUK 15:4](https://ref.ly/Luke15:4), [LUK 15:8](https://ref.ly/Luke15:8), [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20)

Comfort in loss
---------------

### God's comfort

[PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); ; [2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)

### The comfort of others

[JOB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Job2:11); ; [JHN 11:19](https://ref.ly/John11:19); ; [ACT 11:28–11:29](https://ref.ly/Acts11:28-Acts11:29); ; [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

Treasures that cannot be lost
-----------------------------

**[MAT 6:20](https://ref.ly/Matt6:20)** *See also* [LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Luke12:33); [JHN 16:22](https://ref.ly/John16:22); [1PE 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:5)
