## 5399: luxury (id: 5399)

Opulence or grandeur of lifestyle which may be associated with wealth or status, especially royalty. Scripture warns of the dangers of unnecessary displays of luxury but encourages the right kind of splendour to reflect the glory and honour due to God.

Examples of luxurious lifestyles
--------------------------------

### The opulence of Solomon's court

**[1KI 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:21)** parallel [2CH 9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:20) *See also* [1KI 4:22–4:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:22-1Kgs4:24) *Solomon's daily provisions*; [1KI 7:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:1) *Solomon's palace*[1KI 10:18–10:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:18-1Kgs10:20) parallel [2CH 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:17-2Chr9:19)

### Luxury enjoyed by other kings

**[LUK 7:25](https://ref.ly/Luke7:25)** parallel [MAT 11:8](https://ref.ly/Matt11:8) *See also* [1SA 8:11–8:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:11-1Sam8:17) *Samuel warns Israel about what a king will demand of them*; [2SA 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:2) *David*; [2CH 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:29) *Hezekiah*; [EST 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/Esth1:4-Esth1:7) *Xerxes*; [DAN 4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30) *Nebuchadnezzar*

### Luxury enjoyed by the wealthy

**[LUK 16:19](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19)** *The rich man in Jesus Christ's parable.* *See also* [ISA 3:18–3:23](https://ref.ly/Isa3:18-Isa3:23) *upper\-class women of Zion*; [AMO 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4-Amos6:6) *the wealthy of Israel*

### Luxury accompanying high status

**[GEN 41:41–41:43](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41-Gen41:43)** [EST 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Esth6:7-Esth6:8), [EST 8:15](https://ref.ly/Esth8:15); [DAN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Dan5:29) *Daniel*

### Luxury enjoyed by a bride

**[PSA 45:13–45:15](https://ref.ly/Ps45:13-Ps45:15)** *See also* [ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10); [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32); [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

Luxury and poverty
------------------

### Luxury at the expense of the poor

**[JAS 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4-Jas5:5)** *See also* [JER 22:13–22:14](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13-Jer22:14) *King Jehoiakim*; [EZK 34:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:3) *the leaders of Israel*

### Luxury brings responsibility to the poor

**[1TI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:18)** *See also* [JER 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/Jer22:15-Jer22:16); [LUK 16:20–16:21](https://ref.ly/Luke16:20-Luke16:21), [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8); [ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35)

The perils of luxury
--------------------

### Luxury brings a false sense of security

**[LUK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:19)** *See also* [JOB 31:24–31:25](https://ref.ly/Job31:24-Job31:25); [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

### Luxury distracts from God

**[DEU 32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15)** *See also* [DEU 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:13-Deut8:14); [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [MAT 19:21–19:24](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21-Matt19:24) parallels [MRK 10:21–10:23](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21-Mark10:23), [LUK 18:21–18:24](https://ref.ly/Luke18:21-Luke18:24); [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)

### Earthly luxury does not last

**[MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19)** *See also* [PRO 23:5](https://ref.ly/Prov23:5), [PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24); [ISA 13:22](https://ref.ly/Isa13:22) *The luxurious lifestyle of the wicked will be swept away by God's judgment*; [JAS 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:3); [REV 18:7–18:9](https://ref.ly/Rev18:7-Rev18:9)

The right attitude to luxury
----------------------------

**[PHP 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Phil4:11-Phil4:12)** *See also* [1SA 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:7); [JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21)

The true source of beauty
-------------------------

**[1PE 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:3-1Pet3:4)** *See also* [1TI 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:9-1Tim2:10)

Luxury reflecting God's glory
-----------------------------

[EXO 25:3–25:9](https://ref.ly/Exod25:3-Exod25:9) parallel [EXO 35:5–35:9](https://ref.ly/Exod35:5-Exod35:9) *materials for the tabernacle*; ; [EXO 28:4–28:5](https://ref.ly/Exod28:4-Exod28:5) *the priestly garments*; ; [1KI 6:14–6:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:14-1Kgs6:35) parallel [2CH 3:4–3:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:4-2Chr3:14) *the splendour of Solomon's temple*; ; [MRK 13:1](https://ref.ly/Mark13:1) parallel [LUK 21:5](https://ref.ly/Luke21:5) *the magnificence of Herod's temple*; ; [REV 21:15–21:21](https://ref.ly/Rev21:15-Rev21:21) *the glory of the new Jerusalem*

## 5400: lyre (id: 5400)

The lyre used in Israelite worship
----------------------------------

### In joyful religious celebrations

**[2SA 6:2–6:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:2-2Sam6:5)** parallel [1CH 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:6-1Chr13:8) *See also* [1SA 10:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:5); [1CH 15:25–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:25-1Chr15:28); [NEH 12:27](https://ref.ly/Neh12:27)

### In temple worship

**[2CH 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12)** *See also* [1KI 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:12) parallel [2CH 9:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:11); [1CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16), [1CH 15:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:20), [1CH 16:5–16:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:5-1Chr16:6), [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1), [1CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6); [2CH 29:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:25)

### In praising God

**[PSA 71:22](https://ref.ly/Ps71:22)** *See also* [PSA 33:1–33:3](https://ref.ly/Ps33:1-Ps33:3), [PSA 57:7–57:8](https://ref.ly/Ps57:7-Ps57:8), [PSA 81:1–81:2](https://ref.ly/Ps81:1-Ps81:2), [PSA 92:1–92:4](https://ref.ly/Ps92:1-Ps92:4), [PSA 108:1–108:2](https://ref.ly/Ps108:1-Ps108:2), [PSA 144:9](https://ref.ly/Ps144:9), [PSA 150:3](https://ref.ly/Ps150:3)

The lyre used in pagan worship
------------------------------

**[DAN 3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:7)** *See also* [DAN 3:5](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5), [DAN 3:10](https://ref.ly/Dan3:10), [DAN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:15)

The lyre in secular use
-----------------------

**[ISA 5:12](https://ref.ly/Isa5:12)**

## 5401: madness (id: 5401)

A term used in two different senses. The OT suggests that God may occasionally inflict a state of insanity upon individuals as a punishment. The term may also be used of someone whose words or actions are difficult to understand or easy to reject, as with Jesus Christ's preaching and healing.

Madness as a punishment
-----------------------

**[JER 25:15–25:16](https://ref.ly/Jer25:15-Jer25:16)** *See also* [DEU 28:28](https://ref.ly/Deut28:28), [DEU 28:32–28:34](https://ref.ly/Deut28:32-Deut28:34); [JER 50:35–50:38](https://ref.ly/Jer50:35-Jer50:38), [JER 51:7](https://ref.ly/Jer51:7); [DAN 4:28–4:37](https://ref.ly/Dan4:28-Dan4:37); [ZEC 12:4](https://ref.ly/Zech12:4)

Madness linked with folly
-------------------------

**[ECC 7:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:25)** *See also* [PRO 26:18](https://ref.ly/Prov26:18); [ECC 1:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:17), [ECC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:12), [ECC 9:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:3), [ECC 10:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:13); [2PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16)

Accusations of madness against God's servants
---------------------------------------------

**[JHN 10:19–10:21](https://ref.ly/John10:19-John10:21)** *See also* [2KI 9:1–9:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:1-2Kgs9:11); [JER 29:25–29:28](https://ref.ly/Jer29:25-Jer29:28); [HOS 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Hos9:7-Hos9:8); [MAT 12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24) parallels [MRK 3:20–3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20-Mark3:22), [LUK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15); [ACT 26:24–26:25](https://ref.ly/Acts26:24-Acts26:25); [2CO 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:13)

David feigned madness to save his life
--------------------------------------

**PSA 34:0** *See also* [1SA 21:10–21:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:10-1Sam21:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 25:15-16; Deuteronomy 28:28; Deuteronomy 28:32-34; Jeremiah 50:35-38; Jeremiah 51:7; Daniel 4:28-37; Zechariah 12:4; Ecclesiastes 7:25; Proverbs 26:18; Ecclesiastes 1:17; Ecclesiastes 2:12; Ecclesiastes 9:3; Ecclesiastes 10:13; 2 Peter 2:16; John 10:19-21; 2 Kings 9:1-11; Jeremiah 29:25-28; Hosea 9:7-8; Matthew 12:24; Mark 3:20-22; Luke 11:15; Acts 26:24-25; 2 Corinthians 5:13; 1 Samuel 21:10-15

## 5402: market (id: 5402)

The market was a central feature of life in biblical times. Some of the biblical terms for redemption are drawn from the market\-place.

Ancient market\-places
----------------------

[1KI 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:34) *Ahab's treaty with Ben\-Hadad suggests that by the 9th century B.C. there was a highly developed system of international trade.*; [ISA 23:3](https://ref.ly/Isa23:3); ; [EZK 27:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:24)

The market as a place of commerce
---------------------------------

### Methods of trading

Cash: [GEN 23:10–23:18](https://ref.ly/Gen23:10-Gen23:18); ; [JHN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:16); [GEN 47:17](https://ref.ly/Gen47:17); ; [HOS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Hos3:2)

### Sealing transactions

[RUT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:7) *with a sandal*; ; [JER 32:11–32:12](https://ref.ly/Jer32:11-Jer32:12) *by signature*

Other activities in the market\-place
-------------------------------------

### It was where children played

[MAT 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Matt11:16-Matt11:17) parallel [LUK 7:32](https://ref.ly/Luke7:32)

### It was where labourers waited for hire

[MAT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Matt20:3)

### It was where meat was sold

[1CO 10:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:25) *Some of this meat had come from pagan sacrifices.*

### It was where magistrates were present

[ACT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Acts16:19)

God's work in the market\-place
-------------------------------

### Jesus Christ healed the sick

**[MRK 6:56](https://ref.ly/Mark6:56)**

### Paul preached

**[ACT 17:16–17:17](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16-Acts17:17)**

Sins in the market\-place
-------------------------

### Greed, dishonesty and materialism

*See also* [HOS 12:7](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7); [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5); [MIC 6:11](https://ref.ly/Mic6:11)

### The hypocrisy of the Pharisees

[MAT 23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:7) parallels [MRK 12:38](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38), [LUK 20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:46); ; [MRK 7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:4) *the problem was ritual defilement*; ; [LUK 11:43](https://ref.ly/Luke11:43)

### Rough crowds

[ACT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22), [ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5)

The temple courts become a market
---------------------------------

**[JHN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:16)** parallels [MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13), [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46)

Markets will be destroyed in judgment
-------------------------------------

*See also* [ZEP 1:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:11); [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

Biblical terms for redemption are drawn from market language
------------------------------------------------------------

### In the gospel call

[PRO 8:3–8:5](https://ref.ly/Prov8:3-Prov8:5) *The city gates were often places for business deals*; ; [ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18), [ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1), [ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2); ; [MAT 13:45–13:46](https://ref.ly/Matt13:45-Matt13:46)

### God's people have been bought

**[ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28)** *See also* [EXO 15:16](https://ref.ly/Exod15:16); [PSA 74:2](https://ref.ly/Ps74:2); [1CO 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:20), [1CO 7:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:23); [2PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1); [REV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9), [REV 14:4](https://ref.ly/Rev14:4)

### God's people have been redeemed

**[GAL 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:5)** *The Greek word “exagorazo” means “to buy” and was used, for example, of the buying of slaves.* *See also* [GAL 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13-Gal3:14); [REV 14:3](https://ref.ly/Rev14:3) *The Greek “agorazo”, translated here as “redeemed”, may also be translated as “bought”. Elsewhere (e.g., [Tit 2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:14); [1Pe 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18)), “lutro” meaning “to release” is used, conveying a similar idea.*

### Redeeming the time

The Greek `exagorazo'' here translated as`make the most of'': [EPH 5:16](https://ref.ly/Eph5:16); ; [COL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Col4:5)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 20:34; Isaiah 23:3; Ezekiel 27:24; Genesis 23:10-18; John 2:14-16; Genesis 47:17; Hosea 3:2; Ruth 4:7; Jeremiah 32:11-12; Matthew 11:16-17; Luke 7:32; Matthew 20:3; 1 Corinthians 10:25; Acts 16:19; Mark 6:56; Acts 17:16-17; Hosea 12:7; Amos 8:5; Micah 6:11; Matthew 23:7; Mark 12:38; Luke 20:46; Mark 7:4; Luke 11:43; Acts 16:22; Acts 17:5; Matthew 21:12-13; Mark 11:15-17; Luke 19:45-46; Zephaniah 1:11; Revelation 18:11-13; Proverbs 8:3-5; Isaiah 1:18; Isaiah 55:1; Isaiah 55:2; Matthew 13:45-46; Acts 20:28; Exodus 15:16; Psalms 74:2; 1 Corinthians 6:20; 1 Corinthians 7:23; 2 Peter 2:1; Revelation 5:9; Revelation 14:4; Galatians 4:4-5; Galatians 3:13-14; Revelation 14:3; Titus 2:14; 1 Peter 1:18; Ephesians 5:16; Colossians 4:5
* **Associated Articles:** 1315: God, as redeemer (ID: `1315@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5242: buying and selling (ID: `5242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5407: merchants (ID: `5407@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5587: trade (ID: `5587@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6720: redemption (ID: `6720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7140: people of God (ID: `7140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7754: preaching (ID: `7754@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5403: masons (id: 5403)

Building projects on which masons were employed
-----------------------------------------------

### David's palace

**[2SA 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:11)** parallel [1CH 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:1)

### Solomon's palace

*See also* [1KI 7:9–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9-1Kgs7:12)

### The temple

**[1KI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:7)**; **[1CH 22:14–22:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:14-1Chr22:15)** *See also* [1KI 5:15–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:15-1Kgs5:18) parallel [2CH 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:2); [2CH 2:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:18)

### The repair of the temple

[2KI 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:11-2Kgs12:12) parallel [2CH 24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:12); ; [2KI 22:5–22:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:5-2Kgs22:6) parallel [2CH 34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:11)

### The rebuilding of the temple

**[EZR 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:7)**

### Herod's temple

**[MRK 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Mark13:1-Mark13:2)**

The work of masons
------------------

### Skilled work

**[2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14)**

### Dressed stone was superior

**[ISA 9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:10)** *The arrogant boast of the inhabitants of Samaria.*

### Dangerous work

Masons could make mistakes
--------------------------

**[PSA 118:22](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22)** *See also* [MAT 21:42](https://ref.ly/Matt21:42) parallels [MRK 12:10](https://ref.ly/Mark12:10), [LUK 20:17](https://ref.ly/Luke20:17) *Jesus Christ interprets the saying as referring to his rejection by the Jews*; [ACT 4:11](https://ref.ly/Acts4:11); [1PE 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:7)

God is described as a mason
---------------------------

## 5404: masters (id: 5404)

Those who obtain work from others, whether in the capacity of unpaid service or paid employment. Scripture sets out both the obligations and rights of masters in relation to those who work for them.

Masters have obligations to their servants
------------------------------------------

### To deal fairly with them

**[JOB 31:13–31:14](https://ref.ly/Job31:13-Job31:14)** *See also* [2KI 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:7); [JOB 31:38–31:40](https://ref.ly/Job31:38-Job31:40); [MAT 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/Matt10:10-Matt10:11) parallel [LUK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7); [1TI 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:18)

### To be generous

**[DEU 15:12–15:15](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:15), [DEU 15:18](https://ref.ly/Deut15:18)** *See also* [PHM 1:12–1:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:12-Phlm1:16)

### To make provision for rest and worship

**[DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15)** parallel [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11) *See also* [EXO 12:43–12:44](https://ref.ly/Exod12:43-Exod12:44); [DEU 16:11–16:16](https://ref.ly/Deut16:11-Deut16:16)

### Not to defraud them

**[LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13)** *Day labourers were paid in the evening (see [Mt 20:8](https://ref.ly/Matt20:8)). Delaying payment could cause great hardship to a poor man.* *See also* [DEU 24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:15); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Not to exploit them

**[DEU 24:14](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14)** *See also* [GEN 31:7](https://ref.ly/Gen31:7), [GEN 31:41](https://ref.ly/Gen31:41); [ISA 58:3](https://ref.ly/Isa58:3); [JER 22:13](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13); [ACT 16:16](https://ref.ly/Acts16:16), [ACT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Acts16:19)

### Not to neglect them

**[DEU 12:19](https://ref.ly/Deut12:19)** *See also* [DEU 14:27](https://ref.ly/Deut14:27)

### Not to oppress them

**[EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)** *See also* [GEN 16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6); [EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14), [EXO 21:20–21:21](https://ref.ly/Exod21:20-Exod21:21), [EXO 21:26–21:27](https://ref.ly/Exod21:26-Exod21:27); [LEV 25:39–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:55); [1KI 12:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:4), [1KI 12:10–12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:10-1Kgs12:14); [ISA 30:12–30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:12-Isa30:13)

God is the heavenly Master over earthly masters
-----------------------------------------------

**[COL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Col4:1)** *See also* [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9); [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30) parallel [LUK 19:12–19:26](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:26)

Blessings promised to good masters
----------------------------------

**[PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17)** *See also* [DEU 15:10](https://ref.ly/Deut15:10); [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27)

Examples of good and bad masters
--------------------------------

### Good masters

[GEN 18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:19) *Abraham*; ; [JOB 31:13–31:15](https://ref.ly/Job31:13-Job31:15) *Job*; ; [LUK 7:2](https://ref.ly/Luke7:2) *a Roman centurion*

### Bad masters

[GEN 16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6) *Sarah*; ; [GEN 31:7](https://ref.ly/Gen31:7) *Laban*; ; [GEN 39:7–39:20](https://ref.ly/Gen39:7-Gen39:20) *Potiphar's wife*

* **Associated Passages:** Job 31:13-14; 2 Kings 22:7; Job 31:38-40; Matthew 10:10-11; Luke 10:7; 1 Timothy 5:18; Deuteronomy 15:12-15; Deuteronomy 15:18; Philemon 1:12-16; Deuteronomy 5:12-15; Exodus 20:8-11; Exodus 12:43-44; Deuteronomy 16:11-16; Leviticus 19:13; Matthew 20:8; Deuteronomy 24:15; Malachi 3:5; James 5:4; Deuteronomy 24:14; Genesis 31:7; Genesis 31:41; Isaiah 58:3; Jeremiah 22:13; Acts 16:16; Acts 16:19; Deuteronomy 12:19; Deuteronomy 14:27; Ephesians 6:9; Genesis 16:6; Exodus 1:11-14; Exodus 21:20-21; Exodus 21:26-27; Leviticus 25:39-55; 1 Kings 12:4; 1 Kings 12:10-14; Isaiah 30:12-13; Colossians 4:1; Matthew 25:14-30; Luke 19:12-26; Proverbs 19:17; Deuteronomy 15:10; Proverbs 28:27; Genesis 18:19; Job 31:13-15; Luke 7:2; Genesis 39:7-20
* **Associated Articles:** 5056: rest (ID: `5056@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5522: servants, working conditions of (ID: `5522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5823: cruelty (ID: `5823@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5972: unkindness (ID: `5972@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8260: generosity (ID: `8260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8291: kindness (ID: `8291@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8783: neglect (ID: `8783@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5405: medicine (id: 5405)

The means that God has given for the cure of physical troubles. Medicine often symbolises the application of the gospel of God's grace in Jesus Christ for the healing of moral and spiritual ills.

Preventative medicine
---------------------

### A cheerful disposition

**[PRO 17:22](https://ref.ly/Prov17:22)**

### Disinfection

*See also* [LEV 14:41](https://ref.ly/Lev14:41), [LEV 15:5](https://ref.ly/Lev15:5)

### Salt

[EZK 16:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:4)

### Physical exercise

[1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:8)

Curative medicine
-----------------

### Balm

**[JER 51:8](https://ref.ly/Jer51:8)** *An example of medicine used as a picture of the gospel. The nation had the cure for its ills in the mercy of God if only it would repent and turn to him.* *See also* [JER 8:22](https://ref.ly/Jer8:22), [JER 46:11](https://ref.ly/Jer46:11)

### Leaves

**[EZK 47:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:12)** *See also* [REV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:2)

### Mud

[JHN 9:6](https://ref.ly/John9:6) *The connection between the medium used and the cure is not always clear. For example, the use of mud, spittle, oil and water appears sometimes to be more symbolic than practical.*

### Oil

[MRK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Mark6:13); ; [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34); ; [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)

### A poultice

**[2KI 20:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:7)** *See also* [ISA 38:21](https://ref.ly/Isa38:21)

### Spittle

[MRK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Mark7:33)

### Water

[LEV 15:5](https://ref.ly/Lev15:5); ; [2KI 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:10); ; [JHN 9:7](https://ref.ly/John9:7)

### Wine

**[1TI 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:23)** *See also* [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34)

### Eye salve

**[REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)**

### Music

The music was intended to cure moods of depression in Saul's case, and extreme agitation in Elisha: [1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23), [1SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10); ; [2KI 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:14-2Kgs3:15)

### Spiritual surgery

[MAT 5:27–5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:27-Matt5:30); ; [ROM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Rom8:13)

Soothing medicine
-----------------

### Myrrh

**[MRK 15:23](https://ref.ly/Mark15:23)**

### Oil

*See also* [ISA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:6)

### Wine

[PRO 31:6](https://ref.ly/Prov31:6); ; [MAT 27:48](https://ref.ly/Matt27:48); ; [LUK 23:36](https://ref.ly/Luke23:36)

Medicine cannot cure everything
-------------------------------

[DEU 28:27](https://ref.ly/Deut28:27); ; [JER 17:9](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9), [JER 30:12–30:13](https://ref.ly/Jer30:12-Jer30:13), [JER 46:11](https://ref.ly/Jer46:11); ; [MRK 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Mark5:25-Mark5:26)

Application of the gospel as medicine
-------------------------------------

**[MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28)** *See also* [LUK 5:31–5:32](https://ref.ly/Luke5:31-Luke5:32)

Resurrection as the ultimate medicine for all ills
--------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 15:42–15:44](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:42-1Cor15:44)** *See also* [PHP 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:20-Phil3:21)

## 5406: mercenaries (id: 5406)

Soldiers who offers their services for money, irrespective of the nationality of the army in question.

Mercenaries in the Egyptian army
--------------------------------

**[JER 46:20–46:21](https://ref.ly/Jer46:20-Jer46:21)** *These mercenaries were drawn from Cush to the south of Egypt, Put to the west of Egypt and Lydia in north Africa.*

Mercenaries in the Ammonite army
--------------------------------

*See also* [2SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:6) parallel [1CH 19:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:7)

Mercenaries hired by Jehoiada the priest
----------------------------------------

[2KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:4), [2KI 11:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:19) *The Carites were from Caria in south\-west Asia Minor.*

Mercenaries in the army of Judah
--------------------------------

[2CH 25:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:6)

Fear of mercenary attack
------------------------

[2KI 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:6-2Kgs7:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 46:20-21; 2 Samuel 10:6; 1 Chronicles 19:7; 2 Kings 11:4; 2 Kings 11:19; 2 Chronicles 25:6; 2 Kings 7:6-7

## 5407: merchants (id: 5407)

Traders who bought and sold goods and articles of value, often transporting their wares from one country to another according to the demands in different localities.

Some merchants transported their goods by caravan
-------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 37:25–37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:25-Gen37:28)** *See also* [JOB 6:15–6:21](https://ref.ly/Job6:15-Job6:21)

Some merchants traded by sea
----------------------------

**[PRO 31:14](https://ref.ly/Prov31:14)** *See also* [1KI 9:26–9:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:26-1Kgs9:28) parallel [2CH 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:17-2Chr8:18); [1KI 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:11-1Kgs10:12) parallel [2CH 9:10–9:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:10-2Chr9:11); [1KI 22:48](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:48) parallel [2CH 20:35–20:37](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:35-2Chr20:37); [PSA 107:23](https://ref.ly/Ps107:23); [ISA 23:2–23:3](https://ref.ly/Isa23:2-Isa23:3); [EZK 27:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:25)

Merchants often lived close to one another
------------------------------------------

**[ZEP 1:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:11)** *See also* [NEH 3:31–3:32](https://ref.ly/Neh3:31-Neh3:32); [EZK 17:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:4)

Merchants set the standards for weights and measures
----------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 23:16](https://ref.ly/Gen23:16)** *See also* [HOS 12:7](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7); [AMO 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:6)

Ordinary citizens sold goods to merchants
-----------------------------------------

**[PRO 31:24](https://ref.ly/Prov31:24)** *See also* [GEN 37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:28); [PRO 31:18–31:19](https://ref.ly/Prov31:18-Prov31:19)

Merchants were credited with wisdom and shrewdness
--------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 13:44–13:46](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44-Matt13:46)**

Goods traded by merchants
-------------------------

**[REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)** *See also* [SNG 3:6](https://ref.ly/Song3:6); [EZK 27:12–27:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:12-Ezek27:24)

## 5408: messenger (id: 5408)

An individual whose task is to convey information from one person to another. The word “angel” derives from the Greek word for “messenger”. The word “apostle” comes from the Greek work meaning “sent”.

Messengers conveying information from one person to another
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 13:17](https://ref.ly/Prov13:17), [PRO 25:13](https://ref.ly/Prov25:13)** *There are many references to messengers in this sense.* *See also* [GEN 32:3](https://ref.ly/Gen32:3); [NUM 20:14](https://ref.ly/Num20:14); [JDG 6:35](https://ref.ly/Judg6:35); [1SA 23:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:27); [2SA 11:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:22), [2SA 15:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:13); [1KI 19:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:2), [1KI 22:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:13); [2KI 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:3) *Elijah intercepts the messengers of Ahaziah*; [2KI 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:10) *Elisha sent a messenger with instructions for Naaman's healing*; [2KI 6:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:32); [JOB 1:14](https://ref.ly/Job1:14); [ISA 37:9](https://ref.ly/Isa37:9) *Sennacherib sends messengers to Hezekiah*; [JER 51:31](https://ref.ly/Jer51:31); [DAN 3:4](https://ref.ly/Dan3:4); [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25)

Messengers from God to humanity
-------------------------------

### The OT prophets

**[NUM 16:28](https://ref.ly/Num16:28)**; **[ISA 6:8](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8)** *See also* [DEU 18:18](https://ref.ly/Deut18:18); [JDG 6:8](https://ref.ly/Judg6:8); [2SA 12:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:25); [1KI 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:7); [2KI 17:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:13); [ECC 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:6); [ISA 41:27](https://ref.ly/Isa41:27), [ISA 42:19](https://ref.ly/Isa42:19), [ISA 48:16](https://ref.ly/Isa48:16); [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5) *the call of Jeremiah*[JER 7:25](https://ref.ly/Jer7:25), [JER 25:4](https://ref.ly/Jer25:4), [JER 26:4–26:6](https://ref.ly/Jer26:4-Jer26:6), [JER 44:4](https://ref.ly/Jer44:4)[EZK 38:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:17); [HAB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hab2:2); [HAG 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hag1:13); [ZEC 4:9](https://ref.ly/Zech4:9); [MAL 1:1](https://ref.ly/Mal1:1) *“Malachi” means “my messenger”.*

### Angelic messengers

**[DAN 4:13](https://ref.ly/Dan4:13)**; **[LUK 1:19](https://ref.ly/Luke1:19)** *See also* [JDG 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Judg6:11-Judg6:12); [1KI 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5); [ZEC 1:14](https://ref.ly/Zech1:14), [ZEC 3:6](https://ref.ly/Zech3:6)MAT [1:20](https://ref.ly/Zech1:20), [MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13), [MAT 2:19](https://ref.ly/Matt2:19)[LUK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Luke1:11), [LUK 1:28](https://ref.ly/Luke1:28), [LUK 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Luke2:9-Luke2:10) *The shepherds at Bethlehem heard God's message through an angel*; [ACT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26), [ACT 10:3](https://ref.ly/Acts10:3) *Cornelius receives instructions from an angel*; [GAL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Gal1:8); [HEB 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:2-Heb2:3); [REV 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rev1:1), [REV 22:16](https://ref.ly/Rev22:16)

Jesus Christ as God's ultimate messenger
----------------------------------------

**[JHN 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/John7:28-John7:29)** *See also* [JHN 8:42](https://ref.ly/John8:42), [JHN 9:4](https://ref.ly/John9:4), [JHN 10:36](https://ref.ly/John10:36), [JHN 13:16](https://ref.ly/John13:16), [JHN 17:8](https://ref.ly/John17:8); [HEB 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:2), [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Heb3:1)

A messenger of Satan
--------------------

[2CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7)

## 5409: metaphor (id: 5409)

An image which suggests similarities between two different ideas, without implying that they are identical. Scripture uses metaphors extensively, as a way of illustrating aspects of its teaching.

Examples of metaphors for God
-----------------------------

### Father

[DEU 32:6](https://ref.ly/Deut32:6); ; [ISA 64:8](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8); ; [MAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Mal2:10); ; [EPH 4:6](https://ref.ly/Eph4:6)

### Rock

**[DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4)** *See also* [1SA 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:2); [2SA 22:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2); [PSA 62:2](https://ref.ly/Ps62:2), [PSA 78:35](https://ref.ly/Ps78:35)

### Shepherd

**[PSA 23:1](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1)** *See also* [PSA 28:9](https://ref.ly/Ps28:9), [PSA 80:1](https://ref.ly/Ps80:1); [EZK 34:11–34:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11-Ezek34:16)

Examples of metaphors for Jesus Christ
--------------------------------------

### Bread of life

[JHN 6:35–6:51](https://ref.ly/John6:35-John6:51)

### Good shepherd

[JHN 10:11](https://ref.ly/John10:11), [JHN 10:14](https://ref.ly/John10:14)

### Light of the world

[JHN 8:12](https://ref.ly/John8:12), [JHN 9:5](https://ref.ly/John9:5)

Examples of metaphors for the Holy Spirit
-----------------------------------------

### Counsellor

[JHN 14:16](https://ref.ly/John14:16), [JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26), [JHN 15:26](https://ref.ly/John15:26), [JHN 16:7](https://ref.ly/John16:7)

### Breath

[GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7); ; [JOB 32:8](https://ref.ly/Job32:8), [JOB 33:4](https://ref.ly/Job33:4), [JOB 34:14](https://ref.ly/Job34:14)

### Wind

[JHN 3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:8)

Examples of metaphors for the church
------------------------------------

### Bride

[REV 19:7](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7), [REV 21:2–21:9](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2-Rev21:9), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

### Household of God

[EPH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19); ; [1TI 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:15)

### Body of Christ

[ROM 12:4](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4); ; [1CO 12:12–12:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:12-1Cor12:27); ; [EPH 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22-Eph1:23); ; [COL 1:24](https://ref.ly/Col1:24)

Actual mirrors
--------------

**[JOB 37:18](https://ref.ly/Job37:18)** *Mirrored glass was unknown in ancient times. Metal mirrors gave an adequate though imperfect reflection.* *See also* [EXO 38:8](https://ref.ly/Exod38:8); [ISA 3:23](https://ref.ly/Isa3:23)

------------------------------------

**[JAS 1:23–1:25](https://ref.ly/Jas1:23-Jas1:25)** *See also* [PRO 27:19](https://ref.ly/Prov27:19); [2CO 3:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:18)

---------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:12)**

## 5412: money (id: 5412)

Scripture stresses the positive and negative aspects of money, making it clear that the pursuit or love of money can easily lead to spiritual decay.

* **Associated Articles:** 5413: money, attitudes to (ID: `5413@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5414: money, stewardship of (ID: `5414@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5415: money, uses of (ID: `5415@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5413: money, attitudes to (id: 5413)

Money can be a blessing or a snare according to one's attitude.

Money as a gift from God
------------------------

**[DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18)** *See also* [1SA 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:7); [1KI 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:13); [PRO 8:18–8:21](https://ref.ly/Prov8:18-Prov8:21); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

The dangers of money
--------------------

### Being ruled by money

**[MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24)** parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13) *See also* [EPH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Eph5:5)

### Loving money

**[1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)** *See also* [ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10); [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14), [LUK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Luke16:14); [1TI 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:3), [1TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8); [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2); [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7); [HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5); [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

### Trusting in money

**[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [DEU 8:12–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:12-Deut8:14); [JOB 31:24](https://ref.ly/Job31:24); [PSA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:7), [PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10); [PRO 11:4](https://ref.ly/Prov11:4), [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28), [PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24); [ISA 10:3](https://ref.ly/Isa10:3); [JER 48:7](https://ref.ly/Jer48:7); [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21) *the parable of the rich fool*; [JAS 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11), [JAS 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:3); [REV 18:14](https://ref.ly/Rev18:14)

### Boasting in money

**[PSA 49:5–49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:5-Ps49:6)** *See also* [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8); [JAS 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:16)

Examples of those led astray by the love of money
-------------------------------------------------

Balaam: [DEU 23:4](https://ref.ly/Deut23:4); ; [2PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:15); [JOS 7:20–7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:20-Josh7:21) *Achan*; ; [2KI 5:19–5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:19-2Kgs5:27) *Gehazi*; ; [MAT 19:22–19:23](https://ref.ly/Matt19:22-Matt19:23) parallels [MRK 10:22–10:23](https://ref.ly/Mark10:22-Mark10:23), [LUK 18:23–18:24](https://ref.ly/Luke18:23-Luke18:24) *the rich young ruler*; ; [MAT 26:14–26:15](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:15) parallels [MRK 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10-Mark14:11), [LUK 22:4–22:5](https://ref.ly/Luke22:4-Luke22:5) *Judas Iscariot*

Gifts and qualities that are better than money
----------------------------------------------

### Redemption

**[PSA 49:7–49:9](https://ref.ly/Ps49:7-Ps49:9)** *See also* [ISA 52:3](https://ref.ly/Isa52:3); [MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26) parallels [MRK 8:37](https://ref.ly/Mark8:37), [LUK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Luke9:25); [1PE 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18-1Pet1:19)

### A good reputation

**[PRO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1)**

### A good wife

**[PRO 31:10](https://ref.ly/Prov31:10)**

### Healing

**[ACT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6)**

### Spiritual power

**[ACT 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Acts8:19-Acts8:20)**

### Spiritual riches

**[ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1)** *See also* [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)

## 5414: money, stewardship of (id: 5414)

The righteous handling of money is an important practical test of godliness.

Money must be obtained honestly
-------------------------------

### Not by theft

**[EXO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Exod20:15)** parallel [DEU 5:19](https://ref.ly/Deut5:19) *See also* [PRO 10:2](https://ref.ly/Prov10:2), [PRO 19:26](https://ref.ly/Prov19:26); [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19)

### Not by fraudulent practices

**[LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13)** *See also* [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1), [PRO 13:11](https://ref.ly/Prov13:11), [PRO 20:10](https://ref.ly/Prov20:10); [EZK 28:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:18)

### Not by usury

**[EXO 22:25](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25)** *See also* [PSA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:5); [PRO 28:8](https://ref.ly/Prov28:8)

### Not at the expense of justice

**[EXO 23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:8)** *See also* [PRO 15:27](https://ref.ly/Prov15:27), [PRO 16:8](https://ref.ly/Prov16:8); [JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11)

### Not by extortion

**[LUK 3:13](https://ref.ly/Luke3:13)** *See also* [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12)

### Not by oppression

**[JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)** *See also* [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16); [EZK 18:7–18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:7-Ezek18:8); [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)

### Not at the expense of health

[PRO 23:4](https://ref.ly/Prov23:4)

### Not at the expense of witness

[GEN 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22-Gen14:23); ; [3JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/3John1:7)

### Not at the expense of spiritual well\-being

[MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26) parallels [MRK 8:36](https://ref.ly/Mark8:36), [LUK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Luke9:25); ; [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21); ; [1TI 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9)

### By work, trade, investment or inheritance

**[2TH 3:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:12)** *See also* [GEN 15:2](https://ref.ly/Gen15:2), [GEN 34:21](https://ref.ly/Gen34:21); [PRO 13:11](https://ref.ly/Prov13:11), [PRO 13:22](https://ref.ly/Prov13:22), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23), [PRO 19:14](https://ref.ly/Prov19:14); [MAT 25:27](https://ref.ly/Matt25:27) parallel [LUK 19:23](https://ref.ly/Luke19:23)

Money must be cared for diligently
----------------------------------

### Personal money

[PRO 27:23–27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:23-Prov27:24)

### Money held on trust

[2KI 22:4–22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:4-2Kgs22:7); ; [MAT 25:14–25:27](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:27); ; [LUK 16:10–16:12](https://ref.ly/Luke16:10-Luke16:12); ; [1CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:2)

Money must be used in a God\-honouring way
------------------------------------------

### For the support of the family

**[1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8)** *See also* [1TI 5:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:16)

### For benefiting the poor, especially God's people

**[GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10)** *See also* [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17), [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27); [MAT 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Matt6:3-Matt6:4); [LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Luke12:33); [JHN 13:29](https://ref.ly/John13:29); [ACT 2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:45); [ROM 15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:26); [GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:10); [1TI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:18)

### For the work of God's kingdom

**[GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6)** *See also* [PRO 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Prov3:9-Prov3:10); [MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10); [LUK 8:3](https://ref.ly/Luke8:3), [LUK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Luke16:9); [PHP 4:14–4:19](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14-Phil4:19); [1TI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:18)

Examples of godly people who have used their money well
-------------------------------------------------------

### Those who were wealthy but godly

[GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2), [GEN 26:14](https://ref.ly/Gen26:14), [GEN 30:43](https://ref.ly/Gen30:43); ; [2SA 19:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:32); ; [2CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:22); ; [JOB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Job1:3); ; [MAT 27:57](https://ref.ly/Matt27:57); ; [ACT 4:34–4:36](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:36)

### Those who had little but gave much

[MRK 12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:44) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4); ; [2CO 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:1-2Cor8:4)

## 5415: money, uses of (id: 5415)

Monetary transactions have taken place from the earliest biblical times. Originally precious metals were weighed out; coins were introduced later.

The first mention of money
--------------------------

**[GEN 17:12](https://ref.ly/Gen17:12)**

The use of money
----------------

### For the purchase and sale of land

**[GEN 23:14–23:15](https://ref.ly/Gen23:14-Gen23:15)** *See also* [JER 32:9–32:10](https://ref.ly/Jer32:9-Jer32:10); [MAT 27:7](https://ref.ly/Matt27:7); [ACT 4:34](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34), [ACT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:2), [ACT 7:16](https://ref.ly/Acts7:16)

### For the purchase and sale of food

[GEN 41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:57), [GEN 42:5](https://ref.ly/Gen42:5), [GEN 42:35](https://ref.ly/Gen42:35); ; [2KI 7:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:16); ; [JHN 6:5](https://ref.ly/John6:5), [JHN 6:7](https://ref.ly/John6:7); ; [REV 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rev6:6)

### For other merchandise

## 5418: monotony (id: 5418)

The sameness that arises through dull and wearisome routines, and which cause individuals to feel bored. Scripture often portrays monotony as a cause of sin, in that individuals, in seeking excitement, often fall into sinful practices.

Monotony can give rise to sin
-----------------------------

### Discontent in the desert

**[NUM 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Num11:4-Num11:6)** *See also* [EXO 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:2-Exod16:3), [EXO 16:35](https://ref.ly/Exod16:35); [NUM 14:33](https://ref.ly/Num14:33) *The monotony of 40 years in the desert was a judgment on Israel's unbelief.*

### At Sinai impatience leads to idolatry

**[EXO 32:1](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1)** *See also* [EXO 32:23](https://ref.ly/Exod32:23); [ACT 7:40](https://ref.ly/Acts7:40)

Situations which give rise to monotony
--------------------------------------

### Desert

**[PSA 107:4–107:5](https://ref.ly/Ps107:4-Ps107:5)** *See also* [DEU 1:19](https://ref.ly/Deut1:19); [ISA 40:30](https://ref.ly/Isa40:30); [MRK 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Mark1:12-Mark1:13)

### Prison

**[PSA 107:10](https://ref.ly/Ps107:10)** *See also* [JDG 16:21](https://ref.ly/Judg16:21); [PSA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Ps66:11); [JER 38:6](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6); [MAT 4:12](https://ref.ly/Matt4:12); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

### Illness

**[PSA 107:17–107:18](https://ref.ly/Ps107:17-Ps107:18)** *See also* [LEV 26:15–26:16](https://ref.ly/Lev26:15-Lev26:16); [JOB 30:16–30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:16-Job30:17); [PSA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Ps6:3); [LUK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Luke13:11); [JHN 5:5](https://ref.ly/John5:5)

### Storm at sea

**[PSA 107:25–107:26](https://ref.ly/Ps107:25-Ps107:26)** *See also* [JON 2:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:3); [ACT 27:27](https://ref.ly/Acts27:27)

### Darkness

**[PSA 143:3](https://ref.ly/Ps143:3)** *See also* [EXO 10:22](https://ref.ly/Exod10:22); [PSA 130:6](https://ref.ly/Ps130:6); [MRK 15:33](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33); [LUK 2:8](https://ref.ly/Luke2:8)

### Work

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *See also* [JOB 7:2](https://ref.ly/Job7:2); [ECC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:11); [GAL 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9); [REV 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Rev2:2-Rev2:3)

### Siege

**[2KI 6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25)** *See also* [2KI 25:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:2); [ISA 36:12](https://ref.ly/Isa36:12)

### Slavery

**[EXO 2:23](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23)** *See also* [JOB 7:2](https://ref.ly/Job7:2); [1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:1); [1PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18)

### Sleeplessness

**[JOB 7:4](https://ref.ly/Job7:4)** *See also* [EST 6:1](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1); [JOB 30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:17); [ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12); [DAN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Dan6:18); [HEB 12:3](https://ref.ly/Heb12:3)

Monotony arising from difficult human relations
-----------------------------------------------

### A nagging wife

**[PRO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Prov19:13)** *See also* [PRO 27:15](https://ref.ly/Prov27:15)

### Unhelpful counsellors

## 5419: mourning (id: 5419)

The expression of grief at a time of bereavement or repentance, often accompanied by weeping, tearing of clothes and wearing sackcloth.

---------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:4), [LEV 21:10–21:11](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:11)

------------------------------------------

### David for Saul and Jonathan

### David for Absalom

**[2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33)**

### Job for his children

**[JOB 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:20-Job1:21)**

### Other examples

*See also* [GEN 37:34–37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34-Gen37:35), [GEN 50:11](https://ref.ly/Gen50:11); [2SA 13:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:31), [2SA 14:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:2); [2CH 35:23–35:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:23-2Chr35:25); [MAT 2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:18); [JHN 11:31](https://ref.ly/John11:31), [JHN 11:33](https://ref.ly/John11:33); [ACT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Acts8:2)

## 5420: music (id: 5420)

Music played a major role in the lives of people in biblical times. It was used in both sacred and secular activities and was most often associated with joy and celebration.

Music comes from God
--------------------

### God's presence inspires music

**[PSA 96:13](https://ref.ly/Ps96:13)** *See also* [PSA 98:4](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4)

### It is a gift of God

**[JOB 35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:10)**

Music was closely associated with praise and worship
----------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 33:2](https://ref.ly/Ps33:2)** *See also* [JDG 5:3](https://ref.ly/Judg5:3); [NEH 12:27](https://ref.ly/Neh12:27); [PSA 27:6](https://ref.ly/Ps27:6), [PSA 57:7](https://ref.ly/Ps57:7), [PSA 81:1–81:2](https://ref.ly/Ps81:1-Ps81:2), [PSA 87:7](https://ref.ly/Ps87:7), [PSA 92:3](https://ref.ly/Ps92:3), [PSA 95:2](https://ref.ly/Ps95:2), [PSA 98:4](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4), [PSA 108:1](https://ref.ly/Ps108:1), [PSA 144:9](https://ref.ly/Ps144:9), [PSA 147:7](https://ref.ly/Ps147:7), [PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3), [PSA 150:1–150:6](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1-Ps150:6); [EPH 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19-Eph5:20); [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)

Music was employed extensively in the tabernacle and the temple
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CH 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6-1Chr25:7)** *See also* [1CH 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:31-1Chr6:32), [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1); [2CH 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12-2Chr5:14), [2CH 35:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:15); [EZR 2:65](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:65)

Music and the psalms
--------------------

### Many psalms are dedicated to the \`\`director of music''

**PSA 11:0** *See also* PSA 13:0, PSA 31:0, PSA 42:0, PSA 49:0, PSA 77:0, PSA 139:0; [HAB 3:19](https://ref.ly/Hab3:19)

### The titles of some psalms probably give an indication of melody

**PSA 9:0** *See also* PSA 22:0, PSA 45:0, PSA 56:0, PSA 57:0, PSA 58:0, PSA 59:0, PSA 60:0, PSA 69:0, PSA 75:0, PSA 80:0

### The titles of some psalms indicate specific musical accompaniment

**PSA 4:0** *See also* PSA 5:0, PSA 6:0, PSA 54:0, PSA 55:0, PSA 61:0, PSA 67:0, PSA 76:0; [HAB 3:19](https://ref.ly/Hab3:19)

Uses of music
-------------

### To celebrate victory

**[2CH 20:27–20:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:27-2Chr20:28)** *See also* [EXO 15:1–15:21](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1-Exod15:21); [JDG 5:1–5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:1-Judg5:31); [1SA 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:7); [ISA 30:32](https://ref.ly/Isa30:32)

### At times of celebration

**[GEN 31:27](https://ref.ly/Gen31:27)** *See also* [JOB 21:12](https://ref.ly/Job21:12); [ISA 16:10](https://ref.ly/Isa16:10); [LAM 5:14](https://ref.ly/Lam5:14); [EZK 26:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:13); [LUK 15:25](https://ref.ly/Luke15:25); [REV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Rev18:22)

At banquets
-----------

**[ISA 5:12](https://ref.ly/Isa5:12)** *See also* [AMO 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4-Amos6:6)

### As an expression of joy

**[1SA 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:7)** *See also* [2SA 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:5); [1KI 1:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:40); [JOB 21:12](https://ref.ly/Job21:12); [JAS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jas5:13)

### For pleasure

**[ECC 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:8)** *See also* [2SA 19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:35)

### For divine inspiration

[2KI 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:14-2Kgs3:15); ; [1SA 18:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10)

### To relieve depression

**[1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23)** *See also* [1SA 16:16–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:16-1Sam16:18)

### At times of mourning

**[2SA 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:17-2Sam1:18)**; **[MAT 9:23](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23)** *See also* [JOB 30:31](https://ref.ly/Job30:31), [JOB 35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:10); [JER 48:36](https://ref.ly/Jer48:36)

## 5421: musical instruments (id: 5421)

Scripture names a large number of musical instruments, particularly in the OT, reflecting the importance of music in Israelite worship and culture.

Wind instruments
----------------

### Trumpets

[EXO 19:16](https://ref.ly/Exod19:16), [EXO 20:18](https://ref.ly/Exod20:18); ; [LEV 25:9](https://ref.ly/Lev25:9); ; [JDG 3:27](https://ref.ly/Judg3:27); ; [1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39); ; [PSA 150:3](https://ref.ly/Ps150:3); ; [REV 8:7–8:13](https://ref.ly/Rev8:7-Rev8:13)

### Flutes

[GEN 4:21](https://ref.ly/Gen4:21); ; [JOB 21:12](https://ref.ly/Job21:12); ; [PSA 150:4](https://ref.ly/Ps150:4); ; [DAN 3:5–3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5-Dan3:15); ; [MAT 9:23](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23), [MAT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Matt11:17)

### Horns

**[PSA 98:4–98:6](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4-Ps98:6)** *The NIV translates several different Hebrew words as “ram's horn” or “horn”, including “shophar” ([Ps 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3)) which is translated elsewhere as “trumpet”. The horn was a very similar instrument to the trumpet, being made from the horn of an animal, usually a ram.* *See also* [EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13); [2CH 15:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:14); [PSA 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3)

Stringed instruments
--------------------

### Harps

[GEN 4:21](https://ref.ly/Gen4:21); ; [1SA 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:18); ; [JOB 30:31](https://ref.ly/Job30:31); ; [PSA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:4), [PSA 81:2](https://ref.ly/Ps81:2), [PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3), [PSA 150:3](https://ref.ly/Ps150:3); ; [DAN 3:5–3:11](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5-Dan3:11); ; [REV 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8)

### Lyres

[PSA 33:2](https://ref.ly/Ps33:2), [PSA 71:22](https://ref.ly/Ps71:22), [PSA 81:2](https://ref.ly/Ps81:2), [PSA 108:2](https://ref.ly/Ps108:2), [PSA 144:9](https://ref.ly/Ps144:9); ; [DAN 3:5–3:10](https://ref.ly/Dan3:5-Dan3:10)

### Several of the psalms were intended to be sung to the accompaniment of stringed instruments

**PSA 4:0** *See also* PSA 6:0, PSA 54:0, PSA 55:0, PSA 61:0, PSA 67:0, PSA 76:0

Percussion instruments
----------------------

### Tambourines

## 5422: musicians (id: 5422)

Players of musical instruments. Levitical musicians played an extensive role in Israelite worship. Other musicians are also mentioned in Scripture, in both sacred and secular contexts.

Levitical musicians in the tabernacle
-------------------------------------

### Appointed by David

**[1CH 6:31–6:47](https://ref.ly/1Chr6:31-1Chr6:47)** *See also* [1CH 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16-1Chr15:17), [1CH 25:1–25:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1-1Chr25:5)

### Responsibilities

**[1CH 15:19–15:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:19-1Chr15:21)** *See also* [1CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:16), [1CH 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:6-1Chr25:7)

### Allocation of duties

**[1CH 25:8–25:31](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:8-1Chr25:31)**

Levitical musicians in the temple
---------------------------------

**[2CH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12-2Chr5:13)** *See also* [1CH 9:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:33); [2CH 35:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:15)

Other musicians
---------------

### Jubal was the first musician

**[GEN 4:17–4:21](https://ref.ly/Gen4:17-Gen4:21)**

### King David was a renowned musician

**[1SA 16:15–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:15-1Sam16:18)** *See also* [1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23), [1SA 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10-1Sam18:11), [1SA 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:10); [2SA 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:5), [2SA 23:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:1)

### Unidentified musicians

**[PSA 68:24–68:25](https://ref.ly/Ps68:24-Ps68:25)** *See also* [2KI 3:11–3:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:11-2Kgs3:19); [PSA 137:1–137:3](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1-Ps137:3); [REV 18:21–18:22](https://ref.ly/Rev18:21-Rev18:22)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 6:31-47; 1 Chronicles 15:16-17; 1 Chronicles 25:1-5; 1 Chronicles 15:19-21; 1 Chronicles 15:16; 1 Chronicles 25:6-7; 1 Chronicles 25:8-31; 2 Chronicles 5:12-13; 1 Chronicles 9:33; 2 Chronicles 35:15; Genesis 4:17-21; 1 Samuel 16:15-18; 1 Samuel 16:23; 1 Samuel 18:10-11; 1 Samuel 19:9-10; 2 Samuel 6:5; 2 Samuel 23:1; Psalms 68:24-25; 2 Kings 3:11-19; Psalms 137:1-3; Revelation 18:21-22

## 5423: myths (id: 5423)

Stories that are made up and which are contrary to right doctrine or holy living.

Some prefer myths to truth
--------------------------

**[2TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3-2Tim4:4)**

Myths are harmful to the body of Christ
---------------------------------------

**[1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4)** *See also* [1TI 4:6–4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:6-1Tim4:8)

Sound teaching by God's servants dispels myths
----------------------------------------------

**[TIT 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Titus1:13-Titus1:14)** *See also* [1TI 4:1–4:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:6); [TIT 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:9-Titus1:11)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Timothy 4:3-4; 1 Timothy 1:3-4; 1 Timothy 4:6-8; Titus 1:13-14; 1 Timothy 4:1-6; Titus 1:9-11
* **Associated Articles:** 1462: truth, in NT (ID: `1462@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7230: genealogies (ID: `7230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8750: false teachings (ID: `8750@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8766: heresies (ID: `8766@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8829: superstition (ID: `8829@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5424: nationalism (id: 5424)

A concern to preserve or proclaim the distinctive features of a nation or people.

The recognition of different national groups
--------------------------------------------

**[GEN 10:32](https://ref.ly/Gen10:32)** *[Ge 10:32](https://ref.ly/Gen10:32) is the summary conclusion of the table of nations which begins in [Ge 10:1](https://ref.ly/Gen10:1).* *See also* [GEN 17:4–17:6](https://ref.ly/Gen17:4-Gen17:6), [GEN 19:36–19:38](https://ref.ly/Gen19:36-Gen19:38); [EXO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:8); [1KI 9:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:20) parallel [2CH 8:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:7)

Israel was to be a special nation set apart for God
---------------------------------------------------

### The choosing of the nation of Israel

**[EXO 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5-Exod19:6)** *See also* [GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3); [EXO 33:13](https://ref.ly/Exod33:13); [DEU 7:6–7:8](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6-Deut7:8), [DEU 14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:2); [2SA 7:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:23) parallel [1CH 17:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:21); [PSA 33:12](https://ref.ly/Ps33:12); [ROM 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Rom9:4-Rom9:5)

### The mission of Israel to the nations

**[EXO 34:10](https://ref.ly/Exod34:10)** *See also* [DEU 4:6](https://ref.ly/Deut4:6)

Examples of nationalism
-----------------------

### Positive examples

**[RUT 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:16-Ruth1:17)** *See also* [EXO 8:23](https://ref.ly/Exod8:23), [EXO 34:24](https://ref.ly/Exod34:24); [DEU 26:5](https://ref.ly/Deut26:5); [ISA 10:6](https://ref.ly/Isa10:6)

### Negative examples

**[ISA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Isa1:4)** *See also* [NUM 13:31–13:32](https://ref.ly/Num13:31-Num13:32), [NUM 24:14](https://ref.ly/Num24:14); [1KI 22:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:4); [PRO 14:34](https://ref.ly/Prov14:34); [ISA 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Isa14:1-Isa14:2); [JER 49:31](https://ref.ly/Jer49:31); [EZK 36:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:5); [JOL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Joel1:6), [JOL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Joel3:2)

The people of God transcend all national boundaries
---------------------------------------------------

### The church is God's holy nation

**[1PE 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9-1Pet2:10)** *See also* [EPH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19)

### People of all nations are included

**[MAT 8:11](https://ref.ly/Matt8:11)** *See also* [MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19); [ACT 11:18](https://ref.ly/Acts11:18), [ACT 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/Acts11:20-Acts11:21); [REV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9), [REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rev14:6)

### Christian unity is greater than nationalism

**[GAL 3:26–3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26-Gal3:29)** *See also* [ROM 9:6–9:8](https://ref.ly/Rom9:6-Rom9:8); [EPH 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11-Eph2:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 10:32; Genesis 10:1; Genesis 17:4-6; Genesis 19:36-38; Exodus 3:8; 1 Kings 9:20; 2 Chronicles 8:7; Exodus 19:5-6; Genesis 12:1-3; Exodus 33:13; Deuteronomy 7:6-8; Deuteronomy 14:2; 2 Samuel 7:23; 1 Chronicles 17:21; Psalms 33:12; Romans 9:4-5; Exodus 34:10; Deuteronomy 4:6; Ruth 1:16-17; Exodus 8:23; Exodus 34:24; Deuteronomy 26:5; Isaiah 10:6; Isaiah 1:4; Numbers 13:31-32; Numbers 24:14; 1 Kings 22:4; Proverbs 14:34; Isaiah 14:1-2; Jeremiah 49:31; Ezekiel 36:5; Joel 1:6; Joel 3:2; 1 Peter 2:9-10; Ephesians 2:19; Matthew 8:11; Matthew 28:19; Acts 11:18; Acts 11:20-21; Revelation 5:9; Revelation 7:9; Revelation 14:6; Galatians 3:26-29; Romans 9:6-8; Ephesians 2:11-13

## 5425: net (id: 5425)

Instruments of meshed string, used for fishing or hunting. The term can also be used in a symbolic manner, to refer to the way in which people are ensnared by sin or by their enemies.

Nets used in fishing and hunting
--------------------------------

**[ISA 51:20](https://ref.ly/Isa51:20)**; **[EZK 47:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:10)**; **[MAT 4:18](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18)** *See also* [PRO 1:17](https://ref.ly/Prov1:17); [ISA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:8); [EZK 26:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:5); [MAT 4:21](https://ref.ly/Matt4:21) parallel [MRK 1:19](https://ref.ly/Mark1:19); [MAT 13:47](https://ref.ly/Matt13:47); [MRK 1:18](https://ref.ly/Mark1:18); [LUK 5:2–5:4](https://ref.ly/Luke5:2-Luke5:4); [JHN 21:6](https://ref.ly/John21:6)

Nets used metaphorically
------------------------

### To describe people being caught by others

**[PRO 29:5](https://ref.ly/Prov29:5)**; **[HAB 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Hab1:15-Hab1:17)** *See also* [JOB 18:8](https://ref.ly/Job18:8); [PSA 9:15](https://ref.ly/Ps9:15), [PSA 10:9](https://ref.ly/Ps10:9), [PSA 25:15](https://ref.ly/Ps25:15), [PSA 35:7](https://ref.ly/Ps35:7), [PSA 57:6](https://ref.ly/Ps57:6), [PSA 140:5](https://ref.ly/Ps140:5), [PSA 141:10](https://ref.ly/Ps141:10); [ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12); [EZK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:8); [HOS 5:1](https://ref.ly/Hos5:1); [MIC 7:2](https://ref.ly/Mic7:2)

### To describe being caught by the Lord

**[JOB 19:6](https://ref.ly/Job19:6)**; **[HOS 7:12](https://ref.ly/Hos7:12)**; **[LUK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10)** *See also* [LAM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Lam1:13); [EZK 12:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:13), [EZK 17:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:20), [EZK 32:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:3)

Networks used in design
-----------------------

## 5426: news (id: 5426)

Information on significant or recent events conveyed through a variety of official or unofficial means. The ministry of Jesus Christ centred on the good news of the kingdom. The Greek word for “gospel” means “good news”.

Political news reported by official and self\-appointed messengers
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 31:8–31:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:8-1Sam31:9)** *See also* [2SA 1:1–1:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:1-2Sam1:10), [2SA 15:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:13), [2SA 18:19–18:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:19-2Sam18:32); [2KI 7:8–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:8-2Kgs7:11)

Personal news conveyed through friends, relatives and by letter
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 4:7–4:9](https://ref.ly/Col4:7-Col4:9)** *See also* [GEN 29:11–29:13](https://ref.ly/Gen29:11-Gen29:13); [PHP 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Phil2:19-Phil2:20)

Divine news announced by angels
-------------------------------

**[LUK 1:19](https://ref.ly/Luke1:19)** *See also* [GEN 19:1](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1); [LUK 1:26–1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26-Luke1:33), [LUK 2:8–2:12](https://ref.ly/Luke2:8-Luke2:12)

The effect of good news
-----------------------

**[PRO 25:25](https://ref.ly/Prov25:25)** *See also* [GEN 45:16](https://ref.ly/Gen45:16); [PRO 15:30](https://ref.ly/Prov15:30); [NAM 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:18-Nah3:19); [JHN 4:51–4:53](https://ref.ly/John4:51-John4:53); [1TH 3:6–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:6-1Thess3:10)

Reactions to bad news
---------------------

**[JER 49:23](https://ref.ly/Jer49:23)** *See also* [1SA 4:12–4:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:12-1Sam4:22); [2SA 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:10); [JOB 1:14–1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:14-Job1:21); [JON 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:1-Jonah3:10); [JHN 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/John11:1-John11:6)

The good news of Jesus Christ
-----------------------------

### Foretold

[NUM 24:17](https://ref.ly/Num24:17); ; [ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14), [ISA 9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:7), [ISA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Isa11:2), [ISA 53:3–53:12](https://ref.ly/Isa53:3-Isa53:12), [ISA 61:1–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:3); ; [MIC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Mic5:2); ; [ZEC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9), [ZEC 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/Zech11:12-Zech11:13)

**[MAT 4:23](https://ref.ly/Matt4:23)** *See also* [MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5); [MRK 1:15](https://ref.ly/Mark1:15); [LUK 4:17–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:17-Luke4:19), [LUK 4:43](https://ref.ly/Luke4:43)

### Proclaimed by apostles and preachers

**[ACT 14:21](https://ref.ly/Acts14:21)** *See also* [MRK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Mark16:15); [ROM 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:14-Rom10:15); [2TI 1:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:11)

## 5427: nomads (id: 5427)

People who moved about from place to place, having no settled abode. They lived in tents and frequented desert places
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Examples of nomads
------------------

### The patriarchs

**[PSA 105:12–105:13](https://ref.ly/Ps105:12-Ps105:13)**; **[HEB 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:9)** *See also* [GEN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:8), [GEN 13:3](https://ref.ly/Gen13:3), [GEN 13:15](https://ref.ly/Gen13:15), [GEN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Gen13:18), [GEN 26:25](https://ref.ly/Gen26:25), [GEN 31:25](https://ref.ly/Gen31:25), [GEN 35:21](https://ref.ly/Gen35:21); [DEU 26:5](https://ref.ly/Deut26:5); [1CH 16:18–16:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:18-1Chr16:20)

### Israel during her desert wanderings

**[NUM 32:13](https://ref.ly/Num32:13)** *See also* [1CH 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:5-1Chr17:6) *The Tabernacle was designed as a place of worship during this nomadic existence.*

### Other nomadic peoples

[GEN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Gen4:20) *Jabal*; ; [JDG 4:17](https://ref.ly/Judg4:17) *the Kenites*; [JDG 6:3](https://ref.ly/Judg6:3); ; [HAB 3:7](https://ref.ly/Hab3:7); [PSA 120:5](https://ref.ly/Ps120:5); ; [JER 49:28–49:29](https://ref.ly/Jer49:28-Jer49:29); ; [EZK 25:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:4); [ISA 13:20](https://ref.ly/Isa13:20) *Arabs*; ; [JER 35:1–35:11](https://ref.ly/Jer35:1-Jer35:11) *The Recabites retained their nomadic ways long after Israel had settled in the land of Canaan.*

### Desert tracks followed by nomads

[JDG 8:11](https://ref.ly/Judg8:11); ; [JER 3:2](https://ref.ly/Jer3:2)

The nomadic life as a spiritual symbol
--------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11)** *See also* [HEB 11:13–11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13-Heb11:16); [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:17)

Jesus Christ as a nomad
-----------------------

[MAT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:20)

A woman who breast\-feeds an infant.

---------------------------

------------------

**[EXO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Exod2:7)** *See also* [EXO 2:9](https://ref.ly/Exod2:9)

## 5429: oaths (id: 5429)

Solemn, binding promises, the truth of which are affirmed by appeal to God as witness. God himself makes solemn oaths to individuals. Scripture makes clear that oaths should be made wisely and not be broken.

* **Associated Articles:** 5430: oaths, human (ID: `5430@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5431: oaths, divine (ID: `5431@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5430: oaths, human (id: 5430)

Solemn, binding statements made in God's name. The OT forbids the making of oaths in the name of other gods and the NT suggests that it is best to refrain from making oaths.

Oaths are considered binding
----------------------------

**[NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2)** *See also* [DEU 23:21](https://ref.ly/Deut23:21); [MAT 23:16–23:22](https://ref.ly/Matt23:16-Matt23:22)

Oaths to be taken only in God's name
------------------------------------

**[DEU 6:13](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13)** *See also* [PSA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3-Ps24:4); [JER 12:16](https://ref.ly/Jer12:16)

False oaths forbidden
---------------------

**[LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12)** *See also* [EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7) parallel [DEU 5:11](https://ref.ly/Deut5:11); [LEV 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:3-Lev6:5); [ZEC 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3-Zech5:4)

Examples of oaths
-----------------

### In bearing witness

[EXO 22:11](https://ref.ly/Exod22:11); ; [NUM 5:19](https://ref.ly/Num5:19); ; [1KI 8:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:31)

### In showing allegiance

[2KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:4); ; [ECC 8:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:2); ; [JER 38:16](https://ref.ly/Jer38:16); ; [EZK 17:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:13)

### In covenants

[GEN 21:22–21:31](https://ref.ly/Gen21:22-Gen21:31), [GEN 26:26–26:31](https://ref.ly/Gen26:26-Gen26:31), [GEN 31:44–31:53](https://ref.ly/Gen31:44-Gen31:53); ; [JOS 9:3–9:21](https://ref.ly/Josh9:3-Josh9:21)

### As a curse

[JOS 6:26](https://ref.ly/Josh6:26); ; [MAT 26:71–26:74](https://ref.ly/Matt26:71-Matt26:74) parallel [MRK 14:70–14:71](https://ref.ly/Mark14:70-Mark14:71)

### As assurance that a promise will be kept

**[GEN 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22-Gen14:23)** *See also* [GEN 24:3–24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:3-Gen24:9), [GEN 24:37–24:41](https://ref.ly/Gen24:37-Gen24:41), [GEN 47:28–47:31](https://ref.ly/Gen47:28-Gen47:31), [GEN 50:24–50:25](https://ref.ly/Gen50:24-Gen50:25); [PSA 132:1–132:5](https://ref.ly/Ps132:1-Ps132:5)

Unwise or thoughtless oaths
---------------------------

**[GEN 25:33](https://ref.ly/Gen25:33)** *See also* [LEV 5:4](https://ref.ly/Lev5:4); [MAT 14:6–14:7](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6-Matt14:7) parallel [MRK 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22-Mark6:23)

Advice against taking oaths
---------------------------

**[JAS 5:12](https://ref.ly/Jas5:12)** *See also* [MAT 5:33–5:37](https://ref.ly/Matt5:33-Matt5:37)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 30:2; Deuteronomy 23:21; Matthew 23:16-22; Deuteronomy 6:13; Psalms 24:3-4; Jeremiah 12:16; Leviticus 19:12; Exodus 20:7; Deuteronomy 5:11; Leviticus 6:3-5; Zechariah 5:3-4; Exodus 22:11; Numbers 5:19; 1 Kings 8:31; 2 Kings 11:4; Ecclesiastes 8:2; Jeremiah 38:16; Ezekiel 17:13; Genesis 21:22-31; Genesis 26:26-31; Genesis 31:44-53; Joshua 9:3-21; Joshua 6:26; Matthew 26:71-74; Mark 14:70-71; Genesis 14:22-23; Genesis 24:3-9; Genesis 24:37-41; Genesis 47:28-31; Genesis 50:24-25; Psalms 132:1-5; Genesis 25:33; Leviticus 5:4; Matthew 14:6-7; Mark 6:22-23; James 5:12; Matthew 5:33-37

## 5431: oaths, divine (id: 5431)

Solemn promises made by God to individuals and their offspring. He swears by himself because there is nothing greater to swear by.

---------------------

**[HEB 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Heb6:13-Heb6:14)** *See also* [GEN 22:16](https://ref.ly/Gen22:16); [PSA 89:35](https://ref.ly/Ps89:35); [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23), [ISA 62:8](https://ref.ly/Isa62:8); [HEB 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Heb6:16-Heb6:18)

## 5434: officer (id: 5434)

A person holding a position of authority (serving others), in a government, an army or navy, the church or other organisation.

Examples of officers
--------------------

### Government officers

**[1KI 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:5)** *See also* [1KI 4:7–4:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:7-1Kgs4:19), [1KI 4:27–4:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:27-1Kgs4:28), [1KI 20:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:14); [2KI 15:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:25), [2KI 18:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:24) parallel [ISA 36:9](https://ref.ly/Isa36:9); [EZR 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:8); [NEH 11:9](https://ref.ly/Neh11:9); [ISA 33:18](https://ref.ly/Isa33:18)

### Army officers

**[EXO 14:7](https://ref.ly/Exod14:7)** *See also* [EXO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Exod15:4); [DEU 20:8–20:9](https://ref.ly/Deut20:8-Deut20:9); [2SA 8:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:7); [2KI 6:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:8); [1CH 27:16–27:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:16-1Chr27:22); [ISA 21:5](https://ref.ly/Isa21:5); [JER 38:17–38:18](https://ref.ly/Jer38:17-Jer38:18); [EZK 23:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:15); [ACT 21:32](https://ref.ly/Acts21:32), [ACT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Acts25:23)

### Navy officers

[2CH 8:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:18)

### Church officers

**[PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1)** *See also* [ACT 20:17](https://ref.ly/Acts20:17), [ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28); [1TI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1), [1TI 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8-1Tim3:10), [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17); [TIT 1:5](https://ref.ly/Titus1:5), [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7)

----------------------

### The officer in charge of the king's palace

[1KI 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:6), [1KI 18:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:3); ; [2KI 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:18), [2KI 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:2)

### The officer in charge of the treasuries

[1CH 26:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:24); ; [2CH 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11)

### The priest in charge of punishing troublemakers in the temple court

[JER 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Jer20:1-Jer20:2)

### The commander of the king's guard

[DAN 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Dan2:14-Dan2:15)

### A court attendant

[MAT 5:25](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25) parallel [LUK 12:58](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58)

### A member of the temple guard

[LUK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Luke22:4), [LUK 22:52](https://ref.ly/Luke22:52); ; [ACT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Acts5:22), [ACT 5:26](https://ref.ly/Acts5:26)

### The attendant of a Roman magistrate

[ACT 16:35](https://ref.ly/Acts16:35), [ACT 16:38](https://ref.ly/Acts16:38)

## 5435: ovens (id: 5435)

Ovens were a common feature of domestic life and activity
---------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/Exod8:1-Exod8:4)**

Uses for ovens
--------------

### For baking bread

*See also* [LEV 26:23–26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:23-Lev26:26)

### For baking grain offerings

[LEV 2:4](https://ref.ly/Lev2:4), [LEV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Lev7:9)

Ovens could be rendered unclean
-------------------------------

[LEV 11:35](https://ref.ly/Lev11:35)

Ovens were characterised by great heat
--------------------------------------

[LAM 5:10](https://ref.ly/Lam5:10); ; [HOS 7:4](https://ref.ly/Hos7:4), [HOS 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Hos7:6-Hos7:7)

The Tower of the Ovens was a place in Jerusalem in Nehemiah's day
-----------------------------------------------------------------

## 5436: pain (id: 5436)

A sense of physical suffering, anguish or distress, which has a number of causes. Scripture indicates the sources of pain and its potential spiritual implications and results.

Pain is universal
-----------------

**[ROM 8:22](https://ref.ly/Rom8:22)** *See also* [GEN 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16-Gen3:17); [JOB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Job5:7)

The origins of pain
-------------------

### Satanic activity

**[GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15)** *See also* [JOB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Job1:12), [JOB 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Job2:6-Job2:7); [LUK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Luke13:16); [2CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7)

### Human activity

**[PSA 37:14](https://ref.ly/Ps37:14)** *See also* [GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8); [EXO 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Exod1:10-Exod1:11); [NAM 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1-Nah3:4); [HAB 2:6](https://ref.ly/Hab2:6), [HAB 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hab2:8), [HAB 2:10](https://ref.ly/Hab2:10), [HAB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Hab2:12)

### God's judgment and glory

**[JHN 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:2-John9:3)** *See also* [EXO 9:11](https://ref.ly/Exod9:11); [NUM 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Num12:10-Num12:11); [2CH 26:19–26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19-2Chr26:20); [PSA 38:3–38:5](https://ref.ly/Ps38:3-Ps38:5); [PRO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Prov5:11); [ACT 12:23](https://ref.ly/Acts12:23), [ACT 13:11](https://ref.ly/Acts13:11); [2CO 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9)

Kinds of pain
-------------

### Physical pain

**[PSA 38:7](https://ref.ly/Ps38:7)** *See also* [JOB 30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:17); [MAT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28); [LUK 23:33](https://ref.ly/Luke23:33); [2CO 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:16-2Cor4:18), [2CO 11:23–11:27](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:27)

### Mental pain

**[JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18)** *See also* [PSA 42:5–42:6](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5-Ps42:6), [PSA 42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:11); [JER 12:6](https://ref.ly/Jer12:6); [MAT 26:38](https://ref.ly/Matt26:38); [2CO 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:13); [2PE 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:8)

### Bereavement

**[JHN 11:33–11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:33-John11:35)** *See also* [GEN 35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:18), [GEN 50:1](https://ref.ly/Gen50:1); [2SA 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:22-2Sam12:23); [PHP 2:27](https://ref.ly/Phil2:27); [1TH 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13)

Spiritual pain
--------------

### Conviction of sin

**[JHN 16:8](https://ref.ly/John16:8)** *See also* [PSA 32:3–32:5](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:5), [PSA 51:1–51:2](https://ref.ly/Ps51:1-Ps51:2); [LUK 7:38](https://ref.ly/Luke7:38); [ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/Acts26:14)

### The anguish of hell

**[MRK 9:47–9:48](https://ref.ly/Mark9:47-Mark9:48)** *See also* [MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22), [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12); [LUK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Luke13:28); [REV 6:16](https://ref.ly/Rev6:16)

### Perplexity

**[ISA 50:10](https://ref.ly/Isa50:10)** *See also* [JOB 23:8–23:9](https://ref.ly/Job23:8-Job23:9); [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1), [PSA 88:1–88:3](https://ref.ly/Ps88:1-Ps88:3); [JER 20:7](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7)

### Concern for others

**[GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19)** *See also* [ACT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Acts20:19); [2CO 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:1-2Cor2:5), [2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:28); [PHP 3:18](https://ref.ly/Phil3:18); [COL 1:28–2:1](https://ref.ly/Col1:28-Col2:1); [HEB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7)

### The failure of others

**[MAT 26:56](https://ref.ly/Matt26:56)** *See also* [MAT 26:40](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40); [JHN 13:21](https://ref.ly/John13:21); [GAL 4:16](https://ref.ly/Gal4:16); [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10), [2TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16); [3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/3John1:10)

Fruitful results of pain
------------------------

### It draws believers to God

**[1PE 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:19)** *See also* [JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1), [JHN 14:18](https://ref.ly/John14:18); [ROM 8:26](https://ref.ly/Rom8:26); [2CO 7:5–7:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:5-2Cor7:6); [HEB 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15-Heb4:16), [HEB 5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:8)

### It equips believers to help others

**[2CO 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:4)** *See also* [ROM 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Rom5:3-Rom5:5), [ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15); [HEB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:18), [HEB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Heb12:11)

### It helps believers to anticipate the resurrection

**[1CO 15:54–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:54-1Cor15:57)** *See also* [ROM 8:19](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19); [2CO 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:17); [1TH 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:14)

### It points believers to heaven

**[REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)** *See also* [ROM 8:18](https://ref.ly/Rom8:18); [REV 7:15–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:15-Rev7:17)

## 5437: palaces (id: 5437)

Fortified, luxurious residences for kings. They normally served as the seat and symbol of royal authority.

David's palace on Mount Zion
----------------------------

**[1CH 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:1)** *See also* [2SA 5:6–5:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:6-2Sam5:11) parallel [1CH 11:4–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:4-1Chr11:8); [2SA 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:1-2Sam7:2) parallel [1CH 17:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:1)

Solomon's palace in Jerusalem
-----------------------------

**[1KI 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:4-1Kgs10:5)** parallel [2CH 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:3-2Chr9:4) *See also* [1KI 7:1–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:1-1Kgs7:12), [1KI 10:16–10:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:16-1Kgs10:21) parallel [2CH 9:15–9:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:15-2Chr9:20); [2CH 8:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:11)

Gold and valuable items were stored in the palace in Jerusalem
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:18-1Kgs15:19)** parallel [2CH 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:2-2Chr16:3) *See also* [1KI 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:25-1Kgs14:26) parallel [2CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:9); [2KI 12:17–12:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:17-2Kgs12:18), [2KI 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:13-2Kgs14:14) parallel [2CH 25:23–25:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:23-2Chr25:24); [2KI 16:7–16:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7-2Kgs16:8) parallel [2CH 28:20–28:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:20-2Chr28:21); [2KI 18:14–18:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:14-2Kgs18:15), [2KI 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:13); [2CH 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:16-2Chr21:17); [JER 27:18–27:22](https://ref.ly/Jer27:18-Jer27:22)

The royal palace in Jerusalem was destroyed by the Babylonians
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[2KI 25:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:9)** parallels [2CH 36:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:19), [JER 52:13](https://ref.ly/Jer52:13) *See also* [JER 22:1–22:6](https://ref.ly/Jer22:1-Jer22:6), [JER 39:8](https://ref.ly/Jer39:8)

Other palaces
-------------

### The kings of Israel had palaces in Samaria

**[1KI 21:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1)** *See also* [1KI 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:18), [1KI 20:5–20:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:5-1Kgs20:6), [1KI 20:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:43); [2KI 15:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:25)

### Foreign palaces

Pharaoh's palace in Egypt: [GEN 12:15](https://ref.ly/Gen12:15), [GEN 41:40](https://ref.ly/Gen41:40), [GEN 47:14](https://ref.ly/Gen47:14); ; [EXO 8:3](https://ref.ly/Exod8:3); [DAN 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29-Dan4:30), [DAN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5); [EST 1:9](https://ref.ly/Esth1:9) *King Xerxes' palace in Persia and Media*

### Summer palaces

[JDG 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Judg3:20-Judg3:21)

Palaces in NT times
-------------------

[MAT 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:4); ; [JHN 18:28](https://ref.ly/John18:28); ; [ACT 23:35](https://ref.ly/Acts23:35); ; [PHP 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Phil1:12-Phil1:13)

Features of palaces
-------------------

### Palace gardens

**[2KI 21:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:18)** *See also* [2KI 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:25-2Kgs21:26); [EST 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Esth1:5-Esth1:6)

The luxury of palaces
---------------------

**[1KI 22:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:39)** *See also* [NUM 22:18](https://ref.ly/Num22:18); [PSA 45:8–45:9](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8-Ps45:9); [ISA 13:22](https://ref.ly/Isa13:22); [JER 22:13–22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13-Jer22:17); [MAT 11:8](https://ref.ly/Matt11:8) parallel [LUK 7:25](https://ref.ly/Luke7:25)
