## 5438: parables (id: 5438)

Stories told to convey a general truth or spiritual message, often requiring explanation or interpretation. They were used by the prophets and featured extensively in Jesus Christ's ministry.

Parables in the OT
------------------

### Told by prophets

**[HOS 12:10](https://ref.ly/Hos12:10)** *See also* [2SA 12:1–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:1-2Sam12:10); [1KI 20:35–20:42](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:35-1Kgs20:42); [EZK 17:1–17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:1-Ezek17:18), [EZK 20:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:49), [EZK 24:3–24:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:3-Ezek24:13)

### Told by others

**[PSA 78:1–78:2](https://ref.ly/Ps78:1-Ps78:2)** *See also* [JDG 9:7–9:20](https://ref.ly/Judg9:7-Judg9:20); [2SA 14:4–14:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:4-2Sam14:14); [2KI 14:9–14:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:9-2Kgs14:10) parallel [2CH 25:18–25:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:18-2Chr25:19); [PRO 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Prov1:5-Prov1:6)

Parables in the ministry of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------

### Reasons for Jesus Christ's use of parables

**[MAT 13:10–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:10-Matt13:15)** parallels [MRK 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:12), [LUK 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:9-Luke8:10) *See also* [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10)

### Examples of parables told by Jesus Christ

**[MAT 13:3–13:8](https://ref.ly/Matt13:3-Matt13:8)** parallels [MRK 4:3–4:8](https://ref.ly/Mark4:3-Mark4:8), [LUK 8:5–8:8](https://ref.ly/Luke8:5-Luke8:8) *See also* [MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27) parallel [LUK 6:47–6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:47-Luke6:49) *the wise and foolish builders*; [MAT 13:24–13:30](https://ref.ly/Matt13:24-Matt13:30), [MAT 13:31–13:32](https://ref.ly/Matt13:31-Matt13:32) parallels [MRK 4:30–4:32](https://ref.ly/Mark4:30-Mark4:32), [LUK 13:18–13:19](https://ref.ly/Luke13:18-Luke13:19) *the mustard seed*; [MAT 13:33](https://ref.ly/Matt13:33), [MAT 13:44–13:50](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44-Matt13:50), [MAT 18:12–18:14](https://ref.ly/Matt18:12-Matt18:14) parallel [LUK 15:4–15:7](https://ref.ly/Luke15:4-Luke15:7) *the lost sheep*; [MAT 18:23–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:35), [MAT 20:1–20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:16), [MAT 21:33–21:41](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:41) parallels [MRK 12:1–12:9](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:9), [LUK 20:9–20:16](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:16) *the tenants*; [MAT 22:1–22:14](https://ref.ly/Matt22:1-Matt22:14), [MAT 25:1–25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:13), [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30); [MRK 4:26–4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:26-Mark4:29), [MRK 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14-Mark7:15); [LUK 10:25–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:25-Luke10:37), [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21), [LUK 12:35–12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35-Luke12:48), [LUK 14:16–14:24](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16-Luke14:24), [LUK 15:8–15:32](https://ref.ly/Luke15:8-Luke15:32), [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14), [LUK 19:12–19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:27)

## 5440: perjury (id: 5440)

Swearing falsely, usually in a legal context, either by taking a false oath or by breaking one's oath later. God hates perjury.

God hates perjury
-----------------

**[ZEC 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Zech8:16-Zech8:17)** *See also* [PSA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3-Ps24:4); [ISA 48:1](https://ref.ly/Isa48:1); [JER 5:2](https://ref.ly/Jer5:2), [JER 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Jer7:9-Jer7:10); [HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4); [ZEC 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3-Zech5:4); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); [1TI 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:10)

The Law forbade perjury
-----------------------

[EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16) parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20); ; [EXO 23:1–23:3](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1-Exod23:3); ; [LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12); ; [NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2)

Punishment for perjury
----------------------

[LEV 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:7); ; [DEU 19:16–19:21](https://ref.ly/Deut19:16-Deut19:21)

## 5441: philosophy (id: 5441)

An ideology or system of values which seeks to understand and, through rational argument, to investigate the nature and meaning of reality. Scripture exposes the emptiness of philosophy based purely upon human wisdom, while affirming that, at its best, human wisdom points towards God, and can serve as a preparation for the gospel.

The gospel represents the height of human reason
------------------------------------------------

[ACT 17:18–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18-Acts17:23)

The quest for understanding
---------------------------

**[ECC 7:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:25)**; **[ACT 17:18–17:21](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18-Acts17:21)** *See also* [JOB 34:2–34:4](https://ref.ly/Job34:2-Job34:4); [ECC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13), [ECC 7:27](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:27), [ECC 7:29–8:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:29-Eccl8:1), [ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12)

The benefit of received wisdom
------------------------------

[JOB 15:17–15:18](https://ref.ly/Job15:17-Job15:18); ; [PRO 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Prov1:2-Prov1:4); ; [ECC 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:9-Eccl12:11)

The limits of human enquiry
---------------------------

**[JOB 11:7–11:9](https://ref.ly/Job11:7-Job11:9)**; **[ECC 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:16-Eccl8:17)** *See also* [JOB 38:36–38:37](https://ref.ly/Job38:36-Job38:37); [PSA 145:3](https://ref.ly/Ps145:3); [ECC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:11); [ISA 40:28](https://ref.ly/Isa40:28), [ISA 55:8–55:9](https://ref.ly/Isa55:8-Isa55:9); [ACT 17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:23); [ROM 11:33–11:34](https://ref.ly/Rom11:33-Rom11:34); [ISA 40:13](https://ref.ly/Isa40:13); [1CO 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20-1Cor1:21)

The danger of philosophical speculation
---------------------------------------

**[COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)**; **[1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21)** *See also* [GAL 4:3](https://ref.ly/Gal4:3); [COL 2:20](https://ref.ly/Col2:20); [1TI 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:4), [1TI 6:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:4); [TIT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9)

True insight is given by God
----------------------------

[JOB 12:13](https://ref.ly/Job12:13); ; [ECC 2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:26); ; [MAT 13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:11) parallel [LUK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:10); ; [2TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:7)

True meaning is found in relationship with God
----------------------------------------------

**[ACT 17:24–17:28](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24-Acts17:28)** *See also* [ECC 12:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:1); [REV 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:11)

Epicureanism
------------

### Paul meets some Epicureans in Athens

**[ACT 17:16–17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16-Acts17:18)** *Followers of the Greek philosophy founded by Epicurus (341–270 B.C.). In strong contrast to the Stoics, they taught that pleasure, and the avoidance of all disturbance, pain and fear, was the chief goal of life.*

### Epicureans' assessment of Paul and his message

**[ACT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18), [ACT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18)** *Paul's listeners mistook the Greek word for “resurrection” (“anastasis”) for the name of a strange god. While Epicureans did not deny the existence of gods, they believed they had no interest in the lives of human beings, and that therefore everything in life was the result of mere chance.*

### Epicurean and Stoic philosophers bring Paul to the Areopagus

**[ACT 17:19–17:20](https://ref.ly/Acts17:19-Acts17:20)**

### The response of Epicureans and others to Paul's preaching

**[ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32)**

Docetism
--------

### Docetism questions the reality of the incarnation

**[2JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7)** *Docetism is a denial of the physical reality of Jesus Christ's incarnation that may have been prompted by the typically Greek perception of physical matter as evil.* *See also* [1JN 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1John2:22-1John2:23)JHN [1:14](https://ref.ly/1John1:14); [1TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:16); [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14)ROM [8:3](https://ref.ly/Heb8:3); [PHP 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:8); [1JN 4:10](https://ref.ly/1John4:10)

### Docetic views are identified as heretical

**[1JN 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:1-1John4:3)** *See also* [JHN 6:53–6:56](https://ref.ly/John6:53-John6:56)

Gnosticism
----------

### Contrary to the teaching of Gnosticism, the world is not inherently evil

**[1TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:4)** *Gnosticism was a religious philosophy whose fundamental belief in the inherent evil of the created realm led to a number of heretical teachings about creation, human nature, the person of Jesus Christ, salvation and ethics.* [GEN 1:31](https://ref.ly/Gen1:31); ; [NEH 9:6](https://ref.ly/Neh9:6); ; [PSA 19:1](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1); ; [ACT 17:24](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24); ; [COL 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:15-Col1:17); ; [REV 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:11); [ROM 8:20–8:21](https://ref.ly/Rom8:20-Rom8:21); ; [EPH 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9-Eph1:10); ; [COL 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Col1:19-Col1:20)

### Contrary to the teaching of Gnosticism, human beings are not sparks of divinity trapped in evil, fleshly bodies

**[ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26)** [GEN 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26-Gen1:27), [GEN 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7); ; [PSA 8:3–8:8](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3-Ps8:8); [ROM 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12-Rom6:13), [ROM 8:22–8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:22-Rom8:23), [ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1); ; [1CO 6:12–6:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:12-1Cor6:18), [1CO 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19-1Cor6:20); ; [1TH 5:23](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:23); [ROM 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Rom8:10-Rom8:11); ; [1CO 15:35–15:44](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:35-1Cor15:44); ; [2CO 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:1-2Cor5:4); ; [PHP 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:20-Phil3:21); ; [2TI 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:16-2Tim2:18)

### Contrary to the teaching of Gnosticism, Jesus Christ did not merely appear in human form

**[JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14)** [LUK 24:36–24:43](https://ref.ly/Luke24:36-Luke24:43); ; [COL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Col2:9); ; [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14); ; [1JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:3), [1JN 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:2-1John4:3); ; [2JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7); [JHN 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:33-John19:34); ; [1CO 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:8); ; [PHP 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:8); ; [COL 1:19–1:22](https://ref.ly/Col1:19-Col1:22); ; [1JN 5:6](https://ref.ly/1John5:6)

### Contrary to the teaching of Gnosticism, salvation is not found simply in a divine revelation of special knowledge

Salvation is by faith in the crucified Christ: [1CO 3:18–3:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:18-1Cor3:20); ; [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8), [COL 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/Col2:18-Col2:19); ; [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21); [1CO 1:18–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18-1Cor1:25); ; [COL 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Col1:19-Col1:20), [COL 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Col2:2-Col2:4), [COL 2:8–2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:8-Col2:10); ; [2PE 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:3)

### Contrary to the teaching of Gnosticism, Christian behaviour is not to be marked by licence and ritualistic self\-denial

There is no value in empty ceremonial observance or ritualistic self\-denial: [MAT 15:10–15:11](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10-Matt15:11) parallel [MRK 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14-Mark7:15); ; [ROM 14:5–14:6](https://ref.ly/Rom14:5-Rom14:6); ; [COL 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Col2:16-Col2:17), [COL 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:23); ; [1TI 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:5); [1CO 6:12–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:12-1Cor6:20); ; [GAL 5:13](https://ref.ly/Gal5:13); ; [COL 3:5–3:14](https://ref.ly/Col3:5-Col3:14); ; [TIT 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Titus1:15-Titus1:16); ; [1PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:16); ; [1JN 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/1John1:5-1John1:6), [1JN 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/1John2:3-1John2:6), [1JN 3:3–3:10](https://ref.ly/1John3:3-1John3:10); ; [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

Stoicism
--------

### Paul encounters the Stoics in Athens

**[ACT 17:16–17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16-Acts17:18)** *The Stoics were a leading philosophical group based, like their rivals the Epicureans, in Athens. Stoics held that God was the inner reason of the universe and that salvation lay in accepting one's place in the established order.*

### Stoics ridicule Paul

**[ACT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18)**

### Stoics misunderstand Paul's message

**[ACT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18)**

### Paul presents a view of God very different from that of the Stoics

**[ACT 17:24–17:25](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24-Acts17:25)** *In contrast to Stoic belief God is the Creator*; **[ACT 17:26–17:27](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26-Acts17:27)** *In contrast to Stoic belief God is a personal being.*

### Response of the Stoics to Paul's message

**[ACT 17:32–17:34](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32-Acts17:34)**

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:18-23; Ecclesiastes 7:25; Acts 17:18-21; Job 34:2-4; Ecclesiastes 1:13; Ecclesiastes 7:27; Ecclesiastes 7:29-8:1; Ecclesiastes 12:12; Job 15:17-18; Proverbs 1:2-4; Ecclesiastes 12:9-11; Job 11:7-9; Ecclesiastes 8:16-17; Job 38:36-37; Psalms 145:3; Ecclesiastes 3:11; Isaiah 40:28; Isaiah 55:8-9; Acts 17:23; Romans 11:33-34; Isaiah 40:13; 1 Corinthians 1:20-21; Colossians 2:8; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; Galatians 4:3; Colossians 2:20; 1 Timothy 1:4; 1 Timothy 6:4; Titus 3:9; Job 12:13; Ecclesiastes 2:26; Matthew 13:11; Luke 8:10; 2 Timothy 3:7; Acts 17:24-28; Ecclesiastes 12:1; Revelation 4:11; Acts 17:16-18; Acts 17:18; Acts 17:19-20; Acts 17:32; 2 John 1:7; 1 John 2:22-23; 1 John 1:14; 1 Timothy 3:16; Hebrews 2:14; Hebrews 8:3; Philippians 2:6-8; 1 John 4:10; 1 John 4:1-3; John 6:53-56; 1 Timothy 4:4; Genesis 1:31; Nehemiah 9:6; Psalms 19:1; Acts 17:24; Colossians 1:15-17; Romans 8:20-21; Ephesians 1:9-10; Colossians 1:19-20; Acts 17:26; Genesis 1:26-27; Genesis 2:7; Psalms 8:3-8; Romans 6:12-13; Romans 8:22-23; Romans 12:1; 1 Corinthians 6:12-18; 1 Corinthians 6:19-20; 1 Thessalonians 5:23; Romans 8:10-11; 1 Corinthians 15:35-44; 2 Corinthians 5:1-4; Philippians 3:20-21; 2 Timothy 2:16-18; John 1:14; Luke 24:36-43; Colossians 2:9; 1 John 1:1-3; 1 John 4:2-3; John 19:33-34; 1 Corinthians 2:8; Colossians 1:19-22; 1 John 5:6; 1 Corinthians 3:18-20; Colossians 2:18-19; 1 Corinthians 1:18-25; Colossians 2:2-4; Colossians 2:8-10; 2 Peter 1:3; Matthew 15:10-11; Mark 7:14-15; Romans 14:5-6; Colossians 2:16-17; Colossians 2:20-23; 1 Timothy 4:1-5; 1 Corinthians 6:12-20; Galatians 5:13; Colossians 3:5-14; Titus 1:15-16; 1 Peter 2:16; 1 John 1:5-6; 1 John 2:3-6; 1 John 3:3-10; Jude 1:4; Acts 17:24-25; Acts 17:26-27; Acts 17:32-34

## 5442: pilgrimage (id: 5442)

A journey made to a holy or revered place as an act of devotion or duty. As well as Jerusalem, there were several such holy places in Israel.

The annual pilgrimages
----------------------

**[EXO 34:23–34:24](https://ref.ly/Exod34:23-Exod34:24)**; **[LUK 2:41–2:42](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41-Luke2:42)** *See also* [EXO 23:14](https://ref.ly/Exod23:14), [EXO 23:17](https://ref.ly/Exod23:17); [DEU 16:16](https://ref.ly/Deut16:16), [DEU 31:11](https://ref.ly/Deut31:11); [2CH 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:2-2Chr5:3), [2CH 30:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:1), [2CH 30:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:13); [LAM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Lam1:4); [EZK 46:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:9); [ZEC 14:16–14:19](https://ref.ly/Zech14:16-Zech14:19); [JHN 5:1](https://ref.ly/John5:1), [JHN 7:14](https://ref.ly/John7:14)

Pilgrimages to Jerusalem
------------------------

**[PSA 122:1–122:4](https://ref.ly/Ps122:1-Ps122:4)** *See also* [PSA 84:5](https://ref.ly/Ps84:5); [ISA 2:3](https://ref.ly/Isa2:3), [ISA 30:29](https://ref.ly/Isa30:29); [MIC 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mic4:2); [ACT 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:5-Acts2:11)

Accompanying sacrifices
-----------------------

## 5443: pillars (id: 5443)

Supports or decorations in buildings and free\-standing memorial monuments. The word is also used figuratively, with reference to strength and beauty.

Pillars in the temple
---------------------

**[1KI 7:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:21)**

Pillars erected as memorials
----------------------------

**[GEN 28:18–28:22](https://ref.ly/Gen28:18-Gen28:22)** *See also* [GEN 31:45–31:53](https://ref.ly/Gen31:45-Gen31:53), [GEN 35:14–35:15](https://ref.ly/Gen35:14-Gen35:15), [GEN 35:19–35:20](https://ref.ly/Gen35:19-Gen35:20); [EXO 24:4](https://ref.ly/Exod24:4); [DEU 27:2–27:6](https://ref.ly/Deut27:2-Deut27:6); [JOS 4:1–4:9](https://ref.ly/Josh4:1-Josh4:9); [2SA 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:18)

The pillar of cloud and fire
----------------------------

**[EXO 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/Exod13:21-Exod13:22)** *See also* [EXO 33:9](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9); [NEH 9:12](https://ref.ly/Neh9:12)

The figurative use of pillars
-----------------------------

**[JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18)** *See also* [JOB 9:6](https://ref.ly/Job9:6), [JOB 26:11](https://ref.ly/Job26:11); [PSA 75:3](https://ref.ly/Ps75:3), [PSA 144:12](https://ref.ly/Ps144:12); [SNG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Song5:15); [GAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9); [REV 3:12](https://ref.ly/Rev3:12), [REV 10:1](https://ref.ly/Rev10:1)

## 5444: pledges (id: 5444)

A solemn, binding promise, often involving the deposit of valuable items or property as a guarantee that the promise will be kept. To break a pledge is to sin against God.

Pledges were binding
--------------------

**[NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2)** *See also* [NUM 30:3–30:15](https://ref.ly/Num30:3-Num30:15) *In certain cases pledges made by daughters or wives could be nullified by fathers or husbands respectively.*

Pledges were solemn promises
----------------------------

**[2KI 23:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:3)** parallel [2CH 34:31–34:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:31-2Chr34:32) *See also* [1CH 29:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:24); [EZR 10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:19); [EZK 17:16–17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:16-Ezek17:18); [MIC 7:20](https://ref.ly/Mic7:20); [1PE 3:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:21)

Pledges were guarantees that a promise would be kept
----------------------------------------------------

### Items deposited to secure a pledge

**[GEN 38:17–38:18](https://ref.ly/Gen38:17-Gen38:18)** *See also* [EXO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26-Exod22:27); [DEU 24:10–24:13](https://ref.ly/Deut24:10-Deut24:13); [PRO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Prov20:16), [PRO 27:13](https://ref.ly/Prov27:13)

### Unjust exaction of pledges condemned

**[DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17)** *See also* [JOB 22:4–22:6](https://ref.ly/Job22:4-Job22:6), [JOB 24:2–24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:2-Job24:3); [AMO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:8)

Warning against making pledges on behalf of others
--------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15)** *The foolishness of making a pledge as security for someone else's debt.* *See also* [PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5), [PRO 17:18](https://ref.ly/Prov17:18), [PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27)

Pledges and betrothal
---------------------

**[MAT 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:19)** *Betrothal was a pledge to marry and as such was much more binding than the modern Western practice of engagement. It could only be broken by divorce and the betrothed woman was already described as a wife.* *See also* [EXO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16); [DEU 22:23–22:25](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:25); [LUK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Luke1:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 30:2; Numbers 30:3-15; 2 Kings 23:3; 2 Chronicles 34:31-32; 1 Chronicles 29:24; Ezra 10:19; Ezekiel 17:16-18; Micah 7:20; 1 Peter 3:21; Genesis 38:17-18; Exodus 22:26-27; Deuteronomy 24:10-13; Proverbs 20:16; Proverbs 27:13; Deuteronomy 24:17; Job 22:4-6; Job 24:2-3; Amos 2:6-8; Proverbs 11:15; Proverbs 6:1-5; Proverbs 17:18; Proverbs 22:26-27; Matthew 1:18-19; Exodus 22:16; Deuteronomy 22:23-25; Luke 1:27
* **Associated Articles:** 1346: covenants, nature of (ID: `1346@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1690: word of God (ID: `1690@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3040: Holy Spirit, promise of (ID: `3040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5329: guarantee (ID: `5329@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5429: oaths (ID: `5429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5440: perjury (ID: `5440@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5466: promises (ID: `5466@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5518: seal (ID: `5518@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5654: betrothal (ID: `5654@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5741: vows (ID: `5741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5925: rashness (ID: `5925@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5445: potters and pottery (id: 5445)

Pottery was in common use for both household and ceremonial utensils, though for sacred use precious metals were often preferred. Broken pottery symbolises worthlessness and judgment. The potter formed clay with his hands on a wheel which he turned with his feet. The descriptions of God as a potter and of his people as clay emphasise God's sovereignty and also his concern for what he has made.

Potters in Scripture
--------------------

### Examples of potters

**[JER 18:2](https://ref.ly/Jer18:2)** *See also* [1CH 4:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:23); [JER 19:1](https://ref.ly/Jer19:1); [ZEC 11:13](https://ref.ly/Zech11:13) *Potters may have been associated with the temple, possibly because of the continuing need for new vessels to be used in religious ceremonies*; [MAT 27:7](https://ref.ly/Matt27:7)

### Description of the potter's work

[PRO 26:23](https://ref.ly/Prov26:23); ; [JER 18:3–18:4](https://ref.ly/Jer18:3-Jer18:4)

God depicted as a potter
------------------------

### God's authority over what he has made

**[JER 18:6](https://ref.ly/Jer18:6)** *See also* [ISA 29:16](https://ref.ly/Isa29:16), [ISA 45:9–45:10](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9-Isa45:10); [ROM 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:20-Rom9:21)

### God's concern for what he has made

**[ISA 64:8–64:9](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8-Isa64:9)** *See also* [GEN 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Gen2:7-Gen2:8) *“Formed” commonly describes the action of the potter. God “formed” both individuals and the nation*; [JOB 10:8–10:9](https://ref.ly/Job10:8-Job10:9); [PSA 103:14](https://ref.ly/Ps103:14), [PSA 119:73](https://ref.ly/Ps119:73); [ISA 43:1](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1)

Broken pottery
--------------

### Contaminated pottery to be broken

[LEV 11:33–11:35](https://ref.ly/Lev11:33-Lev11:35), [LEV 15:12](https://ref.ly/Lev15:12)

### Broken pottery as a symbol of judgment

**[JER 19:10–19:11](https://ref.ly/Jer19:10-Jer19:11)** *See also* [PSA 2:9](https://ref.ly/Ps2:9); [REV 2:27](https://ref.ly/Rev2:27); [ISA 30:14](https://ref.ly/Isa30:14); [JER 25:34](https://ref.ly/Jer25:34), [JER 48:12](https://ref.ly/Jer48:12)

### Broken pottery as a symbol of worthlessness

**[PSA 31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:12)** *See also* [JER 22:28](https://ref.ly/Jer22:28), [JER 19:2](https://ref.ly/Jer19:2) *The Potsherd Gate was so called because it overlooked the dump for broken pottery.*

Pottery of little value
-----------------------

**[LAM 4:2](https://ref.ly/Lam4:2)** *See also* [PSA 60:8](https://ref.ly/Ps60:8) parallel [PSA 108:9](https://ref.ly/Ps108:9)**[2CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7)** *Valuables were often concealed in apparently worthless clay jars which did not attract attention.*

Examples of items of pottery
----------------------------

*See also* [2SA 17:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:28); [2TI 2:20](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:20)

### Cooking pots

[JDG 6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:19); ; [1SA 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:14); ; [2KI 4:38–4:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:38-2Kgs4:41); ; [JOB 41:20](https://ref.ly/Job41:20); ; [MIC 3:3](https://ref.ly/Mic3:3)

### Pitchers to carry water

### Water jugs

### Drinking vessels

[JDG 5:25](https://ref.ly/Judg5:25); ; [JER 35:5](https://ref.ly/Jer35:5); ; [MAT 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:27-Matt26:28) parallels [MRK 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:23-Mark14:24), [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20), [1CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:25)

### Other pottery containers

[1KI 14:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:3); ; [2KI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:3-2Kgs4:4), [2KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:13); ; [JER 32:14](https://ref.ly/Jer32:14) *Documents could be kept intact in clay jars for many years*; ; [MAT 26:23](https://ref.ly/Matt26:23); ; [JHN 13:5](https://ref.ly/John13:5)

### Household lamps

[2KI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:10); ; [MAT 5:15](https://ref.ly/Matt5:15)

Pottery used in religious rituals
---------------------------------

### Basins to hold the blood of sacrifices

**[EXO 12:21–12:22](https://ref.ly/Exod12:21-Exod12:22)** *See also* [EXO 24:6](https://ref.ly/Exod24:6); [LEV 14:5](https://ref.ly/Lev14:5), [LEV 14:50](https://ref.ly/Lev14:50)

### Utensils to cook or bake offerings

**[LEV 6:28](https://ref.ly/Lev6:28)** *The sin offering was cooked in a pot which was then broken.* *See also* [LEV 2:5](https://ref.ly/Lev2:5) *The grain offering might be baked on a clay griddle*; [LEV 6:21](https://ref.ly/Lev6:21), [LEV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Lev7:9)

### Clay water jars

[NUM 5:17](https://ref.ly/Num5:17), [NUM 19:17](https://ref.ly/Num19:17)

### Lamps

**[EXO 25:37](https://ref.ly/Exod25:37)** *A clay lamp was held on each of the golden lampstand's seven branches.* *See also* [EXO 27:21](https://ref.ly/Exod27:21), [EXO 30:8](https://ref.ly/Exod30:8); [LEV 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Lev24:3-Lev24:4); [1SA 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:3); [1KI 7:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:49)

## 5446: poverty (id: 5446)

The state of being without material possessions or wealth. Scripture indicates that poverty is contrary to God's intention for his people, and that those who are poor and destitute are to be treated with special consideration and compassion.

* **Associated Articles:** 5447: poverty, causes of (ID: `5447@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5448: poverty, attitudes towards (ID: `5448@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5449: poverty, remedies for (ID: `5449@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5450: poverty, spiritual (ID: `5450@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5447: poverty, causes of (id: 5447)

To want for the necessities of life is ultimately the result of human sin.

Poverty arises from human sin
-----------------------------

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *Compare this with the description of Eden in [Ge 2:9–14](https://ref.ly/Gen2:9-Gen2:14).*

Causes of poverty
-----------------

### Divine retribution brings poverty

**[DEU 28:47–28:48](https://ref.ly/Deut28:47-Deut28:48)** *See also* [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16); [ISA 3:1](https://ref.ly/Isa3:1); [AMO 4:6](https://ref.ly/Amos4:6), [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11)

### Idleness brings poverty

**[PRO 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:10-Prov6:11)** *See also* [PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23), [PRO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Prov20:13), [PRO 24:33–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:33-Prov24:34)

### Dissolute living brings poverty

**[PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17)** *See also* [PRO 6:26](https://ref.ly/Prov6:26), [PRO 28:19](https://ref.ly/Prov28:19); [LUK 15:14](https://ref.ly/Luke15:14)

### Lack of discipline brings poverty

[PRO 13:18](https://ref.ly/Prov13:18), [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5)

### Rash promises bring poverty

[PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27)

### Debt brings poverty

[MAT 18:23–18:25](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:25)

### Neglect of God's law of giving brings poverty

[PRO 11:24](https://ref.ly/Prov11:24), [PRO 28:22](https://ref.ly/Prov28:22); ; [ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35)

### Neglect of God's work brings poverty

[HAG 1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:9)

### Oppression brings poverty

[JDG 6:6](https://ref.ly/Judg6:6); ; [JOB 20:19](https://ref.ly/Job20:19); ; [PRO 13:23](https://ref.ly/Prov13:23); ; [ISA 1:7](https://ref.ly/Isa1:7); ; [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Famine brings poverty

[GEN 45:11](https://ref.ly/Gen45:11), [GEN 47:20–47:21](https://ref.ly/Gen47:20-Gen47:21)

### Misfortune brings poverty

[JOB 1:13–1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:13-Job1:21) *These trials were inflicted by Satan but allowed by God as a test of Job's faith.*

Poverty affects good as well as evil people
-------------------------------------------

### The righteous often escape poverty

[DEU 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:9); ; [PSA 34:9–34:10](https://ref.ly/Ps34:9-Ps34:10), [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25)

### Many of the godly have been poor

[2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1); ; [LUK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Luke16:20); ; [ROM 15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:26)

### Jesus Christ was poor

[MAT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:20)

Results of poverty
------------------

### Poverty results in ruin

**[PRO 10:15](https://ref.ly/Prov10:15)**

### Poverty results in shame

*See also* [PRO 13:18](https://ref.ly/Prov13:18); [LUK 16:3](https://ref.ly/Luke16:3)

### Poverty results in misery

[PRO 31:7](https://ref.ly/Prov31:7)

### Poverty results in crime

[PRO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30), [PRO 30:8–30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:8-Prov30:9)

### Poverty need not lead to ungodliness

[PSA 119:71](https://ref.ly/Ps119:71); ; [MRK 12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:44) parallel [LUK 21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:4); ; [2CO 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:2); ; [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5); ; [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rev2:9)

Voluntary poverty
-----------------

### Jesus Christ's example of voluntary poverty

**[2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9)**

### Voluntary poverty commanded of some

**[MAT 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21-Matt19:22)** *Jesus Christ did not say this to everyone.*

### Voluntary poverty experienced by others

*See also* [HEB 10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:34)

### Love essential in voluntary poverty

[1CO 13:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:3); ; [2CO 6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:10)

## 5448: poverty, attitudes towards (id: 5448)

The plight of those in need calls for compassionate action of which Jesus Christ is the great example and in which the early church was faithful. Such compassion will be rewarded.

The treatment of the poor
-------------------------

### They are not to be neglected

**[DEU 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:8)** *See also* [PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5); [ROM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Rom12:13); [GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10); [JAS 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Jas2:15-Jas2:16); [1JN 3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:17)

### They are not to be unjustly treated

**[EXO 23:6](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6)** *See also* [LEV 25:35–25:37](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:37); [DEU 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14-Deut24:15); [2SA 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:1-2Sam12:4) *Nathan rebukes David in the parable about the rich man and the poor man*; [JOB 24:1–24:4](https://ref.ly/Job24:1-Job24:4); [PRO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22), [PRO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Prov29:7); [JER 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:2-Jer22:3), [JER 22:16](https://ref.ly/Jer22:16); [EZK 18:10–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:10-Ezek18:13); [AMO 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:7); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10); [JAS 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2-Jas2:4) *favouritism forbidden in Christian meetings*

### They are to have special rights and privileges

**[DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29)** *See also* [EXO 23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:11); [LEV 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:9-Lev19:10), [LEV 25:5–25:6](https://ref.ly/Lev25:5-Lev25:6); [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17)

### They are to be cared for

**[PSA 82:3–82:4](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3-Ps82:4)** *See also* [JOB 29:11–29:17](https://ref.ly/Job29:11-Job29:17); [LUK 3:11](https://ref.ly/Luke3:11); [ACT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36)

### They are to be helped generously

**[DEU 15:9–15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:9-Deut15:11)** *See also* [DEU 14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:29); [RUT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:14); [PSA 112:9](https://ref.ly/Ps112:9); [JER 39:10](https://ref.ly/Jer39:10); [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8); [ACT 10:2](https://ref.ly/Acts10:2); [2CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:7)

### They are to be helped without ostentation

**[MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2)**

Jesus Christ's compassion for the poor
--------------------------------------

**[MAT 15:32](https://ref.ly/Matt15:32)** parallel [MRK 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Mark8:1-Mark8:3) *See also* [LUK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18-Luke4:19); [JHN 13:29](https://ref.ly/John13:29); [ACT 10:38](https://ref.ly/Acts10:38)

The attitude of the first Christians towards poverty
----------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44-Acts2:45)** *See also* [ACT 4:32–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32-Acts4:35), [ACT 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29-Acts11:30), [ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35), [ACT 24:17](https://ref.ly/Acts24:17); [ROM 15:25–15:27](https://ref.ly/Rom15:25-Rom15:27); [2CO 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:13-2Cor8:15); [GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:10)

Compassion for the poor will be rewarded
----------------------------------------

**[PSA 41:1](https://ref.ly/Ps41:1)** *See also* [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17), [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27); [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21), [MAT 25:34–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34-Matt25:36); [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41); [HEB 13:16](https://ref.ly/Heb13:16)

Examples of the poor who need help
----------------------------------

### Orphans

[DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18), [DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29); ; [PSA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ps10:14), [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); ; [PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10); ; [ISA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:2)

### Widows

[DEU 14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:29), [DEU 16:11](https://ref.ly/Deut16:11); ; [RUT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:5); ; [2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1); ; [JOB 22:9](https://ref.ly/Job22:9); ; [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); ; [ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1); ; [1TI 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:3), [1TI 5:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:9), [1TI 5:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:16); ; [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

## 5449: poverty, remedies for (id: 5449)

Although poverty can never be totally eliminated from this sinful world, it can be alleviated. Scripture provides guidance concerning how this may be done.

Poverty will always exist in this world
---------------------------------------

**[MAT 26:11](https://ref.ly/Matt26:11)** parallel [JHN 12:8](https://ref.ly/John12:8) *See also* [DEU 15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:11)

God's concern over poverty
--------------------------

### God cares for the poor

**[PSA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:10), [PSA 68:10](https://ref.ly/Ps68:10)** *See also* [DEU 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/Deut15:4-Deut15:5); [JOB 5:16](https://ref.ly/Job5:16); [PSA 14:6](https://ref.ly/Ps14:6), [PSA 113:7](https://ref.ly/Ps113:7), [PSA 140:12](https://ref.ly/Ps140:12); [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4)

### The law made concessions for poverty

[LEV 5:7](https://ref.ly/Lev5:7), [LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11), [LEV 12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:8), [LEV 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Lev14:21-Lev14:22), [LEV 27:8](https://ref.ly/Lev27:8)

### God judges those who oppress the poor

[EZK 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:12-Ezek18:13); ; [AMO 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1-Amos4:2)

The poor should be encouraged to help themselves
------------------------------------------------

### Mosaic law enabled the poor to help themselves

[EXO 23:11](https://ref.ly/Exod23:11); ; [LEV 19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:10), [LEV 23:22](https://ref.ly/Lev23:22), [LEV 25:25](https://ref.ly/Lev25:25), [LEV 25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:28), [LEV 25:39](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39), [LEV 25:41](https://ref.ly/Lev25:41) *The jubilee helped towards release from the poverty trap*; ; [DEU 23:25](https://ref.ly/Deut23:25)

### Paul exhorts the poor to work

[1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11); ; [2TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10)

### Paul sets the poor an example

[ACT 20:34](https://ref.ly/Acts20:34); ; [1TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9); ; [2TH 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:8)

The relatives of the poor should help
-------------------------------------

**[1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4)** *See also* [GEN 45:9–45:11](https://ref.ly/Gen45:9-Gen45:11); [LEV 25:25](https://ref.ly/Lev25:25); [1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8), [1TI 5:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:16)

Those better off should help the poor
-------------------------------------

### The poor can be helped by means of gifts

**[ACT 10:4](https://ref.ly/Acts10:4)** *See also* [DEU 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:8); [PSA 112:9](https://ref.ly/Ps112:9); [PRO 31:20](https://ref.ly/Prov31:20); [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21) parallels [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21), [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22); [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8); [ACT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36); [EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28); [1TI 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17-1Tim6:18)

### The poor can be helped in practical ways

[MAT 25:34–25:40](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34-Matt25:40); ; [LUK 11:41](https://ref.ly/Luke11:41), [LUK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Luke14:13); ; [ACT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36), [ACT 9:39](https://ref.ly/Acts9:39); ; [1TI 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:9-1Tim5:10); ; [JAS 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Jas2:15-Jas2:16); ; [1JN 3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:17)

### The poor can be helped by others upholding justice

**[PSA 82:3](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3)** *See also* [DEU 24:12–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:12-Deut24:15); [PRO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22), [PRO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Prov29:7), [PRO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Prov29:14), [PRO 31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:9); [ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2); [JER 22:16](https://ref.ly/Jer22:16)

The church should help the poor
-------------------------------

**[GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:10)** *See also* [ACT 24:17](https://ref.ly/Acts24:17); [ROM 15:26](https://ref.ly/Rom15:26); [1TI 5:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:16)

God's greatest gift to the poor is the good news of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18)** *See also* [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [MAT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Matt11:5) parallel [LUK 7:22](https://ref.ly/Luke7:22); [LUK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Luke14:21) *Scripture regards spiritual and social welfare as closely connected.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 26:11; John 12:8; Deuteronomy 15:11; Psalms 35:10; Psalms 68:10; Deuteronomy 15:4-5; Job 5:16; Psalms 14:6; Psalms 113:7; Psalms 140:12; Isaiah 25:4; Leviticus 5:7; Leviticus 5:11; Leviticus 12:8; Leviticus 14:21-22; Leviticus 27:8; Ezekiel 18:12-13; Amos 4:1-2; Exodus 23:11; Leviticus 19:10; Leviticus 23:22; Leviticus 25:25; Leviticus 25:28; Leviticus 25:39; Leviticus 25:41; Deuteronomy 23:25; 1 Thessalonians 4:11; 2 Thessalonians 3:10; Acts 20:34; 1 Thessalonians 2:9; 2 Thessalonians 3:8; 1 Timothy 5:4; Genesis 45:9-11; 1 Timothy 5:8; 1 Timothy 5:16; Acts 10:4; Deuteronomy 15:7-8; Psalms 112:9; Proverbs 31:20; Matthew 19:21; Mark 10:21; Luke 18:22; Luke 19:8; Acts 9:36; Ephesians 4:28; 1 Timothy 6:17-18; Matthew 25:34-40; Luke 11:41; Luke 14:13; Acts 9:39; 1 Timothy 5:9-10; James 2:15-16; 1 John 3:17; Psalms 82:3; Deuteronomy 24:12-15; Proverbs 22:22; Proverbs 29:7; Proverbs 29:14; Proverbs 31:9; Isaiah 10:1-2; Jeremiah 22:16; Galatians 2:10; Acts 24:17; Romans 15:26; Luke 4:18; Isaiah 61:1; Matthew 11:5; Luke 7:22; Luke 14:21

## 5450: poverty, spiritual (id: 5450)

The lack of spiritual riches and gifts is seen by Scripture as an especially distressing cause of poverty. Awareness of spiritual poverty leads to a turning towards God in order to receive the riches he graciously offers through the gospel.

The ungodly are spiritually poor
--------------------------------

### They lack faith

[MAT 13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:58) parallel [MRK 6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:6)

### They lack understanding

[PRO 10:21](https://ref.ly/Prov10:21); ; [ISA 5:13](https://ref.ly/Isa5:13), [ISA 56:10–56:11](https://ref.ly/Isa56:10-Isa56:11); ; [JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4); ; [HOS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Hos4:6); ; [1TI 6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:5)

### They lack God

[EPH 2:12](https://ref.ly/Eph2:12)

### They lack eternal life

[MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26) parallel [LUK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Luke9:25); ; [LUK 12:20–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20-Luke12:21), [LUK 16:22–16:23](https://ref.ly/Luke16:22-Luke16:23); ; [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

### They lack knowledge of their spiritual poverty

[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)

Spiritual poverty may be a judgment
-----------------------------------

**[AMO 8:11](https://ref.ly/Amos8:11)** *See also* [PSA 51:11](https://ref.ly/Ps51:11), [PSA 74:9](https://ref.ly/Ps74:9); [LAM 2:9](https://ref.ly/Lam2:9); [EZK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:26); [MAT 13:14–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:14-Matt13:15); [REV 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rev2:5)

The godly are aware of their poverty
------------------------------------

### David recognised that he was poor and needy

**[PSA 40:17](https://ref.ly/Ps40:17)** *See also* [PSA 34:6](https://ref.ly/Ps34:6), [PSA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:10), [PSA 70:5](https://ref.ly/Ps70:5), [PSA 86:1](https://ref.ly/Ps86:1), [PSA 109:22](https://ref.ly/Ps109:22)

### Others recognise their spiritual poverty

**[ROM 7:18](https://ref.ly/Rom7:18)** *See also* [JOB 42:6](https://ref.ly/Job42:6); [PSA 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Ps8:3-Ps8:4); [LUK 5:8](https://ref.ly/Luke5:8), [LUK 7:6](https://ref.ly/Luke7:6); [ROM 7:24](https://ref.ly/Rom7:24)

Those who know that they are poor will be blessed by God
--------------------------------------------------------

### God will satisfy the poor in spirit

**[MAT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3)** parallel [LUK 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Luke6:20-Luke6:21) *See also* [MAT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6)

### God will save the poor in spirit

[PSA 116:6](https://ref.ly/Ps116:6)

### God will accept the poor in spirit

[PSA 51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:17); ; [ISA 66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2)

### God will be close to the poor in spirit

[PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18); ; [ISA 57:15](https://ref.ly/Isa57:15)

### God will hear the prayers of the poor in spirit

[PSA 102:17](https://ref.ly/Ps102:17)

### God will give grace to the poor in spirit

[JAS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jas4:6), [JAS 4:10](https://ref.ly/Jas4:10); ; [1PE 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5-1Pet5:6); ; [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34)

### God will meet the needs of the poor in spirit

[ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); ; [LUK 1:53](https://ref.ly/Luke1:53)

Spiritual riches are better than material riches
------------------------------------------------

### Poverty with righteousness is better

[PSA 37:16](https://ref.ly/Ps37:16); ; [PRO 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Prov15:16-Prov15:17), [PRO 16:8](https://ref.ly/Prov16:8), [PRO 19:1](https://ref.ly/Prov19:1), [PRO 19:22](https://ref.ly/Prov19:22), [PRO 28:6](https://ref.ly/Prov28:6)

### Poverty with wisdom is better

[PRO 16:16](https://ref.ly/Prov16:16), [PRO 19:1](https://ref.ly/Prov19:1), [PRO 19:22](https://ref.ly/Prov19:22), [PRO 28:6](https://ref.ly/Prov28:6)

### Poverty with humility is better

[PRO 16:19](https://ref.ly/Prov16:19)

### Poverty with peace and quiet are better

[PRO 17:1](https://ref.ly/Prov17:1)

### Spiritual riches exalt the poor

[JAS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Jas1:9)

### Spiritual riches endure

[HEB 10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:34), [HEB 11:37–11:40](https://ref.ly/Heb11:37-Heb11:40); ; [1PE 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:4)

### Spiritual riches more than compensate for material poverty

[2CO 6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:10); ; [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5); ; [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rev2:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 13:58; Mark 6:6; Proverbs 10:21; Isaiah 5:13; Isaiah 56:10-11; Jeremiah 5:4; Hosea 4:6; 1 Timothy 6:5; Ephesians 2:12; Matthew 16:26; Luke 9:25; Luke 12:20-21; Luke 16:22-23; James 5:1-6; Revelation 3:17; Amos 8:11; Psalms 51:11; Psalms 74:9; Lamentations 2:9; Ezekiel 7:26; Matthew 13:14-15; Revelation 2:5; Psalms 40:17; Psalms 34:6; Psalms 35:10; Psalms 70:5; Psalms 86:1; Psalms 109:22; Romans 7:18; Job 42:6; Psalms 8:3-4; Luke 5:8; Luke 7:6; Romans 7:24; Matthew 5:3; Luke 6:20-21; Matthew 5:6; Psalms 116:6; Psalms 51:17; Isaiah 66:2; Psalms 34:18; Isaiah 57:15; Psalms 102:17; James 4:6; James 4:10; 1 Peter 5:5-6; Proverbs 3:34; Isaiah 61:1; Luke 1:53; Psalms 37:16; Proverbs 15:16-17; Proverbs 16:8; Proverbs 19:1; Proverbs 19:22; Proverbs 28:6; Proverbs 16:16; Proverbs 16:19; Proverbs 17:1; James 1:9; Hebrews 10:34; Hebrews 11:37-40; 1 Peter 1:4; 2 Corinthians 6:10; James 2:5; Revelation 2:9

## 5452: power (id: 5452)

The quality which enables individuals to achieve their aims. Scripture stresses the power of God, demonstrated in Jesus Christ's resurrection and bestowed through the Holy Spirit. Scripture also stresses the relative powerlessness of humanity in God's sight.

* **Associated Articles:** 5453: power, of God (ID: `5453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5454: power, God's saving (ID: `5454@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5455: power, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5455@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5456: power, of Holy Spirit (ID: `5456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5457: power, human (ID: `5457@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5453: power, of God (id: 5453)

See [1105: God, power of](1105.md)



## 5454: power, God's saving (id: 5454)

God has power to save and protect and through his power he will defeat those who oppose him.

God's power to save
-------------------

**[PSA 20:6](https://ref.ly/Ps20:6)** *See also* [2CH 20:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:12), [2CH 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:15); [ISA 40:9–40:10](https://ref.ly/Isa40:9-Isa40:10); [2PE 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:3-2Pet1:4)

Jesus Christ's power to perform miracles
----------------------------------------

**[LUK 6:19](https://ref.ly/Luke6:19)** *See also* [MAT 13:54](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54), [MAT 14:2](https://ref.ly/Matt14:2) parallel [MRK 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mark6:14); [MRK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Mark5:30) parallel [LUK 8:46](https://ref.ly/Luke8:46); [LUK 4:36](https://ref.ly/Luke4:36), [LUK 5:17](https://ref.ly/Luke5:17); [ACT 10:38](https://ref.ly/Acts10:38)

God's power to protect
----------------------

**[1PE 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:5)** *See also* [DAN 6:26–6:27](https://ref.ly/Dan6:26-Dan6:27); [JHN 17:11](https://ref.ly/John17:11); [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39)

God's people pray for God to act in power
-----------------------------------------

**[2CH 14:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:11)** *See also* [2CH 20:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:12); [PSA 68:1–68:2](https://ref.ly/Ps68:1-Ps68:2), [PSA 68:28](https://ref.ly/Ps68:28); [JER 14:9](https://ref.ly/Jer14:9); [ACT 4:23–4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:23-Acts4:31); [2CO 10:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:4)

The power of the gospel
-----------------------

**[ROM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rom1:16)** *See also* [ROM 4:21](https://ref.ly/Rom4:21) *God has power to fulfil his promises*; [ROM 5:6](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6), [ROM 8:3](https://ref.ly/Rom8:3); [1CO 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18)

The power of God's kingdom
--------------------------

[MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13); ; [MRK 9:1](https://ref.ly/Mark9:1); ; [1CO 4:19–4:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:19-1Cor4:20)

Preaching and power
-------------------

**[1CO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:4-1Cor2:5)** *See also* [ACT 4:33](https://ref.ly/Acts4:33), [ACT 9:22](https://ref.ly/Acts9:22); [ROM 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:18-Rom15:19); [1CO 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17-1Cor1:18); [EPH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7); [1TH 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:5); [2TI 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:7-2Tim1:8)

The powers that oppose God will be defeated
-------------------------------------------

**[1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24)**; **[COL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:15)** *See also* [ISA 24:21](https://ref.ly/Isa24:21), [ISA 59:15–59:20](https://ref.ly/Isa59:15-Isa59:20); [DAN 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Dan7:26-Dan7:27), [DAN 8:25](https://ref.ly/Dan8:25); [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12); [REV 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rev12:10), [REV 18:10](https://ref.ly/Rev18:10)

---------------------------

## 5457: power, human (id: 5457)

All human power and authority comes from God. God also gives special power to his people.

All human power comes from God
------------------------------

**[DEU 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:17-Deut8:18)**; **[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [JDG 3:12](https://ref.ly/Judg3:12); [2KI 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:3), [2KI 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:5); [2CH 25:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:8); [DAN 2:37](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37), [DAN 4:29–4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29-Dan4:32); [JHN 19:10–19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:10-John19:11); [ACT 4:28](https://ref.ly/Acts4:28); [COL 1:16](https://ref.ly/Col1:16); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)

God gives power to his people
-----------------------------

### God's power is at work in believers

**[PSA 68:35](https://ref.ly/Ps68:35)** *See also* [ISA 40:29–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:29-Isa40:31); [2CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7), [2CO 10:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:4), [2CO 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9); [EPH 3:20](https://ref.ly/Eph3:20), [EPH 6:10](https://ref.ly/Eph6:10); [COL 1:11](https://ref.ly/Col1:11); [2TH 1:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:11)

### Examples of God giving power to believers

Moses: [EXO 4:21](https://ref.ly/Exod4:21); ; [DEU 34:12](https://ref.ly/Deut34:12); ; [ACT 7:22](https://ref.ly/Acts7:22); [2SA 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:10) parallel [1CH 11:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:9) *David*; ; [1KI 18:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:46) *Elijah*; ; [2CH 27:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:6) *Jotham*; ; [DAN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Dan2:23) *Daniel*; ; [LUK 1:17](https://ref.ly/Luke1:17) *John the Baptist*; [LUK 9:1](https://ref.ly/Luke9:1) parallels [MAT 10:1](https://ref.ly/Matt10:1), [MRK 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mark6:7); ; [LUK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Luke10:19); [ACT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Acts4:7), [ACT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Acts4:10), [ACT 6:8](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8); ; [1CO 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:10)

### God gives resurrection power to believers

**[EPH 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Eph1:19-Eph1:20)** *See also* [2CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:4); [PHP 3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10); [COL 1:29](https://ref.ly/Col1:29), [COL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Col2:12)

### The Holy Spirit equips individuals for special tasks

[JDG 3:10](https://ref.ly/Judg3:10) *Othniel*; [JDG 14:6](https://ref.ly/Judg14:6), [JDG 14:19](https://ref.ly/Judg14:19), [JDG 15:14](https://ref.ly/Judg15:14); [1SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:6), [1SA 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:10), [1SA 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:6); [1SA 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:13) *David*; ; [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1) *the Messiah, fulfilled in Jesus Christ ([Lk 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18))*; ; [MIC 3:8](https://ref.ly/Mic3:8) *Micah*; ; [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Luke1:35) *Mary*; ; [LUK 4:14](https://ref.ly/Luke4:14) *Jesus Christ*

### The Holy Spirit gives power to believers

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)** *See also* [LUK 24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:49); [ROM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Rom15:13); [EPH 3:16](https://ref.ly/Eph3:16)

Human power derives from wisdom
-------------------------------

**[ECC 7:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:19)** *See also* [PRO 3:27](https://ref.ly/Prov3:27), [PRO 8:14](https://ref.ly/Prov8:14), [PRO 24:5](https://ref.ly/Prov24:5); [ECC 9:13–9:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:13-Eccl9:18)

The abuse of human power
------------------------

**[PRO 28:28](https://ref.ly/Prov28:28)** *See also* [JDG 6:2](https://ref.ly/Judg6:2); [JOB 21:7](https://ref.ly/Job21:7); [PRO 28:12](https://ref.ly/Prov28:12); [ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1); [JER 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:27-Jer5:28), [JER 23:10](https://ref.ly/Jer23:10); [EZK 22:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:6); [MIC 2:1](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1), [MIC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:3)

The absence of human power
--------------------------

[DEU 28:32](https://ref.ly/Deut28:32); ; [2CH 14:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:11); ; [NEH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:5); ; [JOB 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Job5:15-Job5:16), [JOB 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/Job6:11-Job6:13)

The power of the wicked is temporary
------------------------------------

**[PSA 37:16–37:17](https://ref.ly/Ps37:16-Ps37:17)** *See also* [EST 9:1](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1); [PSA 37:32–37:33](https://ref.ly/Ps37:32-Ps37:33); [PRO 11:7](https://ref.ly/Prov11:7); [EZK 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:20-Ezek13:21)

## 5459: prince (id: 5459)

The son of a king, or a very important official, leader or ruler. The title is given to Jesus Christ and also to angelic spirits (in particular Michael and Satan).

A prince as the son of a king
-----------------------------

**[2CH 11:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:22)** *See also* [2SA 13:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:32); [2KI 10:6–10:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:6-2Kgs10:8), [2KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:2); [PSA 45:16](https://ref.ly/Ps45:16)

A prince as someone occupying a very important position
-------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 49:26](https://ref.ly/Gen49:26)** parallel [DEU 33:16](https://ref.ly/Deut33:16) *See also* [GEN 23:6](https://ref.ly/Gen23:6); [1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8); [2SA 3:38](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:38); [PSA 113:8](https://ref.ly/Ps113:8)

A prince as a very important official, leader or ruler
------------------------------------------------------

**[EST 1:3](https://ref.ly/Esth1:3)** *See also* [NUM 21:18](https://ref.ly/Num21:18), [NUM 22:8](https://ref.ly/Num22:8), [NUM 22:13–22:15](https://ref.ly/Num22:13-Num22:15); [JOS 13:21](https://ref.ly/Josh13:21); [JDG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Judg5:15); [2CH 21:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:4), [2CH 22:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:8); [EZR 1:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:8); [EST 6:9](https://ref.ly/Esth6:9); [PSA 68:27](https://ref.ly/Ps68:27); [PRO 8:16](https://ref.ly/Prov8:16); [EZK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:12)EZK [22:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:6), [EZK 45:8–45:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:8-Ezek45:9)[EZK 38:2–38:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:2-Ezek38:3); [DAN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Dan9:6); [HOS 3:4](https://ref.ly/Hos3:4)

The Messiah is described as a prince
------------------------------------

**[ISA 9:6](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6)** *See also* [EZK 34:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:24), [EZK 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:25), [EZK 44:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:3); [DAN 8:11](https://ref.ly/Dan8:11), [DAN 8:25](https://ref.ly/Dan8:25)

Angelic spirits (whether good or evil) are described as princes
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[DAN 10:13](https://ref.ly/Dan10:13)** *The prince of the Persian kingdom was possibly a demon, exercising influence in the interests of Satan.* *See also* [DAN 10:20–10:21](https://ref.ly/Dan10:20-Dan10:21), [DAN 12:1](https://ref.ly/Dan12:1)

Satan is described as a prince
------------------------------

**[MAT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Matt9:34)** *See also* [MAT 12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24) parallels [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22), [LUK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15); [JHN 12:31](https://ref.ly/John12:31), [JHN 14:30](https://ref.ly/John14:30), [JHN 16:11](https://ref.ly/John16:11); [EPH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Eph2:2)

The splendour of the princess
-----------------------------

**[PSA 45:13](https://ref.ly/Ps45:13)** *See also* [PSA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ps45:9)

## 5460: prison (id: 5460)

A place of confinement, such as a jail or dungeon, used to imprison individuals.

Irregular prisons
-----------------

**[GEN 37:22](https://ref.ly/Gen37:22)** *A cistern was usually a plaster\-coated underground rock cavity used for storing water.* *See also* [NUM 12:14–12:15](https://ref.ly/Num12:14-Num12:15) *Miriam's confinement may have been as much isolation as punishment*; [JER 38:6](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6), [JER 38:13](https://ref.ly/Jer38:13)

Houses used as prisons
----------------------

**[JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15)** *See also* [2SA 20:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:3); [LUK 22:54](https://ref.ly/Luke22:54); [ACT 28:16](https://ref.ly/Acts28:16)

Legally established prisons
---------------------------

**[GEN 39:20](https://ref.ly/Gen39:20)**; **[ACT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:18)** *See also* [GEN 42:16–42:19](https://ref.ly/Gen42:16-Gen42:19); [JDG 16:21](https://ref.ly/Judg16:21); [1KI 22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27); [2KI 17:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:4), [2KI 25:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:27); [2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10); [MAT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3); [LUK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12); [ACT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Acts4:3), [ACT 12:4–12:7](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4-Acts12:7), [ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37)

Barracks or guardhouses as prisons
----------------------------------

[JER 32:2](https://ref.ly/Jer32:2); ; [ACT 22:24](https://ref.ly/Acts22:24); ; [PHP 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Phil1:12-Phil1:13)

Figurative and poetic use of prisons
------------------------------------

**[ISA 42:7](https://ref.ly/Isa42:7)** *See also* [JOB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Job11:10); [PSA 88:8](https://ref.ly/Ps88:8), [PSA 142:7](https://ref.ly/Ps142:7); [ISA 24:22](https://ref.ly/Isa24:22); [LUK 12:58](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58); [1PE 3:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:19); [2PE 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:4); [REV 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rev1:18)REV [20:1](https://ref.ly/Rev20:1), [REV 20:7](https://ref.ly/Rev20:7)

## 5461: prisoners (id: 5461)

People who have been deprived of their liberty and confined against their will.

People imprisoned out of anger, envy or jealousy
------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 37:24](https://ref.ly/Gen37:24)**; **[JER 52:11](https://ref.ly/Jer52:11)** *See also* [GEN 39:19](https://ref.ly/Gen39:19), [GEN 40:3](https://ref.ly/Gen40:3), [GEN 41:14](https://ref.ly/Gen41:14), [GEN 42:16](https://ref.ly/Gen42:16)JDG [16:21](https://ref.ly/Gen16:21), [JDG 16:25](https://ref.ly/Judg16:25)[2KI 17:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:4) *Hoshea, king of Israel*; [2KI 25:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:18); [2CH 28:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:5), [2CH 28:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:17), [2CH 33:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:11) *Manasseh*; [ISA 14:17](https://ref.ly/Isa14:17), [ISA 20:4](https://ref.ly/Isa20:4); [HAB 1:9](https://ref.ly/Hab1:9)

## 5463: proclamations (id: 5463)

Edicts or public declarations issued by the civil or religious authorities.

Examples of proclamations in biblical times
-------------------------------------------

[EXO 1:15–1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:15-Exod1:22) *Pharaoh's edict that all Hebrew boys were to be drowned*; ; [EXO 32:5](https://ref.ly/Exod32:5) *Aaron's proclamation of a festival to the LORD*; ; [1KI 21:9–21:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:9-1Kgs21:10) *Jezebel's proclamation to ensnare Naboth*; ; [2KI 10:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:20) *Jehu's proclamation of an assembly in honour of Baal*; ; [2CH 24:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:9) *Joash's proclamation of a tax for the LORD*; ; [2CH 30:1–30:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:1-2Chr30:10) *Hezekiah's invitation to celebrate the Passover*; [EZR 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:1), [EZR 5:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:13), [EZR 6:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:3), [EZR 7:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:13); [NEH 8:14–8:15](https://ref.ly/Neh8:14-Neh8:15) *the Levites' proclamation of festival arrangements*; [EST 1:20](https://ref.ly/Esth1:20), [EST 2:8](https://ref.ly/Esth2:8), [EST 3:14](https://ref.ly/Esth3:14), [EST 4:3](https://ref.ly/Esth4:3), [EST 8:11](https://ref.ly/Esth8:11); [DAN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7), [DAN 6:15](https://ref.ly/Dan6:15); [JON 3:7](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:7) *the king of Nineveh's proclamation of a fast of repentance*; ; [LUK 2:1](https://ref.ly/Luke2:1) *Caesar Augustus' proclamation of a census*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 1:15-22; Exodus 32:5; 1 Kings 21:9-10; 2 Kings 10:20; 2 Chronicles 24:9; 2 Chronicles 30:1-10; Ezra 1:1; Ezra 5:13; Ezra 6:3; Ezra 7:13; Nehemiah 8:14-15; Esther 1:20; Esther 2:8; Esther 3:14; Esther 4:3; Esther 8:11; Daniel 6:7; Daniel 6:15; Jonah 3:7; Luke 2:1
* **Associated Articles:** 2427: gospel, transmission of (ID: `2427@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5219: authority, of human institutions (ID: `5219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5335: herald (ID: `5335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7354: feasts and festivals (ID: `7354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7754: preaching (ID: `7754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8424: evangelism (ID: `8424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8489: urgency (ID: `8489@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

-----------------------------------------------------------

## 5465: profit (id: 5465)

In the financial sense, gain through trading. The word is also used in Scripture of spiritual gain.

Legitimate profit
-----------------

### As the result of hard work

**[PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23), [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5)** *See also* [PRO 31:18](https://ref.ly/Prov31:18)

### As the reward of generosity

[PRO 11:24](https://ref.ly/Prov11:24); ; [LUK 6:38](https://ref.ly/Luke6:38)

### As the gift of God

[DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18)

### It is withheld by God in judgment

[DEU 28:15–28:20](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:20); ; [JER 12:13](https://ref.ly/Jer12:13); ; [HAG 1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:9)

### It is for this life only

[ECC 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:3-Eccl1:4); ; [1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7)

Profiting by oppression
-----------------------

### A common practice of the wicked

**[ECC 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:8-Eccl5:9)**

### It is often cloaked in hypocrisy

*See also* [EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31)

### It is against God's law

**[LEV 25:37](https://ref.ly/Lev25:37)**

### It is of little worth

*See also* [PRO 11:16](https://ref.ly/Prov11:16), [PRO 16:8](https://ref.ly/Prov16:8)

### It is short\-lived

[JOB 20:18–20:19](https://ref.ly/Job20:18-Job20:19); ; [ISA 23:17–23:18](https://ref.ly/Isa23:17-Isa23:18); ; [JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11)

### It is judged by God

[PRO 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Prov1:18-Prov1:19); ; [EZK 22:12–22:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12-Ezek22:14); ; [MIC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:13); ; [HAB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hab2:9)

### Its avoidance is rewarded

[PRO 28:16](https://ref.ly/Prov28:16); ; [ISA 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:15-Isa33:16)

Profiting by dishonesty
-----------------------

### It is common amongst the wicked

[JER 22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:17)

### It is forbidden to God's servants

[EXO 18:21](https://ref.ly/Exod18:21); ; [1SA 8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:3); ; [1TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8); ; [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7)

Profiting selfishly
-------------------

### It is common amongst the wicked

[ISA 56:11](https://ref.ly/Isa56:11)

### It is avoided by the godly

**[PSA 119:36](https://ref.ly/Ps119:36)**

Using religion for profit
-------------------------

### In the OT

*See also* [NUM 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Num22:16-Num22:17); [1SA 2:12–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:12-1Sam2:16); [JER 6:13](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13), [JER 8:10](https://ref.ly/Jer8:10)

### In the NT

**[2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17)** *See also* [ACT 8:18–8:23](https://ref.ly/Acts8:18-Acts8:23); [1TI 6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:5); [TIT 1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:11); [JUD 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jude1:11)

Profiting spiritually
---------------------

### Through the Scriptures

[1TI 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:13-1Tim4:15); ; [2TI 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16-2Tim3:17)

### Through wisdom

**[PRO 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Prov3:13-Prov3:14)**

### Through doctrine

**[TIT 3:8](https://ref.ly/Titus3:8)**

### Through godliness and contentment

**[1TI 6:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:6)** *See also* [1TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:8)

### Through chastening

[HEB 12:10](https://ref.ly/Heb12:10)

### Through spiritual gifts

[1CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:7)

### Through service

[MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30); ; [LUK 19:11–19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:11-Luke19:27)

### Through sacrifice

[MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29) parallels [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30), [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30); ; [HEB 11:35](https://ref.ly/Heb11:35)

### Through death

**[PHP 1:21](https://ref.ly/Phil1:21)** *See also* [PHP 1:23](https://ref.ly/Phil1:23)

### Doubts expressed about profiting spiritually

[JOB 9:29–9:31](https://ref.ly/Job9:29-Job9:31), [JOB 21:15](https://ref.ly/Job21:15), [JOB 34:9](https://ref.ly/Job34:9), [JOB 35:1–35:3](https://ref.ly/Job35:1-Job35:3); ; [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13); ; [ECC 2:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:15), [ECC 6:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:8); ; [MAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mal3:14)

### It is better than worldly wealth

**[MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26)** parallels [MRK 8:36](https://ref.ly/Mark8:36), [LUK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Luke9:25)

Unprofitable things
-------------------

*See also* [ECC 6:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:11) *mere words*; [ECC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:11) *delayed action*; [ISA 44:10](https://ref.ly/Isa44:10) *idolatry*; [PHP 3:7](https://ref.ly/Phil3:7) *legalistic works*; [1CO 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:1-1Cor13:3) *zeal without love*; [1CO 14:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:6) *speech without understanding*; [HEB 4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:2) *hearing without faith*; [TIT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9) *needless controversy*

## 5466: promises (id: 5466)

Binding offers or commitments on the part of one person or people to another. The value of such promises depends upon the reliability and trustworthiness of the person who makes the promises. Scripture stresses the total reliability of God, including his promise to bestow his Spirit and urges believers to demonstrate the same trustworthiness in their dealings with one another.

* **Associated Articles:** 5467: promises, divine (ID: `5467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5468: promises, human (ID: `5468@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5469: promise, of Holy Spirit (ID: `5469@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5467: promises, divine (id: 5467)

The promises of God reveal his particular and eternal purposes to which he is unchangeably committed and upon which believers can totally depend. These promises are, however, conditional upon obedience on the part of believers.

God's promises are irrevocable
------------------------------

### He is absolutely trustworthy

**[NUM 23:19](https://ref.ly/Num23:19)** *See also* [TIT 1:2](https://ref.ly/Titus1:2); [HEB 6:13–6:18](https://ref.ly/Heb6:13-Heb6:18)

### He is unchanging

[PSA 110:4](https://ref.ly/Ps110:4); ; [MAL 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/Mal3:6-Mal3:7); ; [JAS 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17-Jas1:18)

### He has the power and will to fulfil his promises

**[ISA 55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:11)** *See also* [ROM 4:21](https://ref.ly/Rom4:21)

### He is faithful in keeping all his promises

[JOS 21:45](https://ref.ly/Josh21:45), [JOS 23:14–23:15](https://ref.ly/Josh23:14-Josh23:15); ; [1KI 8:56](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:56); ; [PSA 145:13](https://ref.ly/Ps145:13); ; [HEB 10:23](https://ref.ly/Heb10:23)

### His promises stem from his goodness and glory

[2PE 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:3-2Pet1:4)

### God may confirm his promises with an oath

**[GEN 22:15–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:15-Gen22:18)** *See also* [GEN 26:3](https://ref.ly/Gen26:3); [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23); [AMO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Amos6:8), [AMO 8:7](https://ref.ly/Amos8:7)

Examples of God's promises through covenant relationship
--------------------------------------------------------

[GEN 9:8–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:8-Gen9:17) *with Noah*; [GEN 15:9–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:9-Gen15:21), [GEN 17:1–17:22](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1-Gen17:22); ; [HEB 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:9), [HEB 11:17–11:19](https://ref.ly/Heb11:17-Heb11:19); [GEN 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Gen26:3-Gen26:4) *with Isaac*; [GEN 28:13–28:15](https://ref.ly/Gen28:13-Gen28:15), [GEN 46:2–46:4](https://ref.ly/Gen46:2-Gen46:4); [EXO 19:1–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:1-Exod19:6), [EXO 24:1–24:8](https://ref.ly/Exod24:1-Exod24:8); [NUM 25:10–25:12](https://ref.ly/Num25:10-Num25:12) *with Phinehas*; [2SA 7:5–7:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:5-2Sam7:16) parallel [1CH 17:4–17:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:4-1Chr17:14); ; [1KI 8:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:15), [1KI 8:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:24); [JER 31:31–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:31-Jer31:34) *with Jeremiah*

The grounding of God's promises in Christ
-----------------------------------------

### God's promises are fulfilled in Jesus Christ

**[2CO 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:18-2Cor1:20)** *See also* [MAT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17); [LUK 4:16–4:21](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16-Luke4:21); [ACT 2:29–2:31](https://ref.ly/Acts2:29-Acts2:31), [ACT 3:21–3:26](https://ref.ly/Acts3:21-Acts3:26), [ACT 7:37](https://ref.ly/Acts7:37); [DEU 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/Deut18:15-Deut18:18); [ACT 13:23](https://ref.ly/Acts13:23), [ACT 13:32–13:34](https://ref.ly/Acts13:32-Acts13:34), [ACT 26:6–26:7](https://ref.ly/Acts26:6-Acts26:7); [ROM 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Rom1:2-Rom1:3), [ROM 15:8](https://ref.ly/Rom15:8); [HEB 9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:15)

### Jesus Christ brings superior promises through the new covenant

**[HEB 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/Heb8:6-Heb8:8)** *Jesus Christ mediates a covenant of inner transformation, willing obedience, intimate relationship with God and forgiveness of sins forever.* *See also* [EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14); [HEB 7:22](https://ref.ly/Heb7:22), [HEB 11:13](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13), [HEB 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Heb11:39-Heb11:40); [2PE 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:1-2Pet1:4)

### Jesus Christ has the right to make promises on God's behalf

**[JHN 3:34–3:35](https://ref.ly/John3:34-John3:35)** *See also* [JHN 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/John1:1-John1:2), [JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14), [JHN 8:25–8:29](https://ref.ly/John8:25-John8:29); [HEB 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:3)

God's promises must be received by believers
--------------------------------------------

### They are received by faith

**[GAL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Gal3:22)** *See also* [JHN 1:12](https://ref.ly/John1:12); [ROM 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Rom4:13-Rom4:16)

### They are received by perseverance and obedience

**[HEB 10:36](https://ref.ly/Heb10:36)** *See also* [ROM 4:19–4:24](https://ref.ly/Rom4:19-Rom4:24); [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1); [HEB 6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:12)

### God's promises unite believing Jews and Gentiles

**[EPH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:6)** *See also* [ACT 2:38–2:39](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38-Acts2:39); [ROM 9:8](https://ref.ly/Rom9:8); [GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:29), [GAL 4:28](https://ref.ly/Gal4:28); [EPH 2:11–2:18](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11-Eph2:18); [HEB 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Heb11:39-Heb11:40)

### Disaster awaits those who reject God's promises

[JOS 23:12–23:16](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12-Josh23:16); ; [JHN 3:18–3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:18-John3:20), [JHN 3:36](https://ref.ly/John3:36); ; [2TI 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:11-2Tim2:13); ; [HEB 6:4–6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:12); ; [2PE 3:3–3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:3-2Pet3:10)

Particular promises of God in Christ
------------------------------------

### The gift of the Holy Spirit

[LUK 24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:49); ; [ACT 1:4](https://ref.ly/Acts1:4), [ACT 2:33](https://ref.ly/Acts2:33); ; [EPH 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13)

### The fulness of life and eternal life

[2TI 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:1); ; [HEB 12:26–12:28](https://ref.ly/Heb12:26-Heb12:28); ; [JAS 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jas1:12), [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5); ; [1JN 2:25](https://ref.ly/1John2:25)

### Resurrection

[JHN 5:29](https://ref.ly/John5:29), [JHN 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/John11:25-John11:26); ; [1CO 15:48–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:48-1Cor15:57); ; [2CO 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:14); ; [1TH 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:16)

### The forgiveness of sins

[1JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:9)

### The presence of God

[EXO 3:12](https://ref.ly/Exod3:12), [EXO 33:14](https://ref.ly/Exod33:14); ; [JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9); ; [ISA 58:9](https://ref.ly/Isa58:9); ; [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); ; [HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)

### The peace of God

[1CH 22:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:9); ; [PSA 85:8](https://ref.ly/Ps85:8); ; [ISA 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6-Isa9:7); ; [ROM 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1); ; [PHP 4:4–4:9](https://ref.ly/Phil4:4-Phil4:9)

### Joy in God

### The knowledge of God

## 5468: promises, human (id: 5468)

Believers must be faithful in keeping promises made to others and to God, because they have been called to live in integrity and truth. False or broken promises are considered as sin.

Examples of promises between people
-----------------------------------

[GEN 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22-Gen14:24) *Abraham and the king of Sodom*; ; [GEN 21:22–21:24](https://ref.ly/Gen21:22-Gen21:24) *Abraham and Abimelech*; ; [GEN 47:29–47:31](https://ref.ly/Gen47:29-Gen47:31) *Jacob and Joseph*; ; [NUM 30:1–30:16](https://ref.ly/Num30:1-Num30:16) *in family life*; ; [JOS 2:12–2:21](https://ref.ly/Josh2:12-Josh2:21) *Rahab and the spies*

Examples of people making promises to God
-----------------------------------------

[GEN 28:20–28:21](https://ref.ly/Gen28:20-Gen28:21) *Jacob*; ; [JDG 11:29–11:40](https://ref.ly/Judg11:29-Judg11:40) *Jephthah*; ; [1SA 1:11–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11-1Sam1:20) *Hannah*; ; [2SA 15:7–15:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:7-2Sam15:12) *Absalom*; ; [NEH 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/Neh10:28-Neh10:29) *returning exiles*

Accompaniments to human promises
--------------------------------

### Taking an oath

**[DEU 6:13](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13)** *See also* [GEN 14:22](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22), [GEN 21:24](https://ref.ly/Gen21:24), [GEN 47:31](https://ref.ly/Gen47:31); [JOS 2:12–2:14](https://ref.ly/Josh2:12-Josh2:14); [NEH 10:29](https://ref.ly/Neh10:29)

### Declaring a curse

**[2SA 3:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:35)** *See also* [RUT 1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:17); [1SA 14:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:24); [2SA 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:9-2Sam3:10); [1KI 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:23); [MAT 26:73–26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:73-Matt26:75) parallel [MRK 14:70–14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:70-Mark14:72)

Instructions regarding human promises
-------------------------------------

### The importance of keeping promises

**[NUM 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:1-Num30:2)** *See also* [PSA 50:14](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14); [PRO 5:18–5:23](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18-Prov5:23); [MAL 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14-Mal2:16); [MAT 19:4–19:9](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:9)

### The sin and peril of false promises

**[LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12)** *See also* [EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7); [DEU 23:21](https://ref.ly/Deut23:21); [JER 8:11–8:12](https://ref.ly/Jer8:11-Jer8:12); [EZK 13:1–13:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:1-Ezek13:7), [EZK 13:10–13:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:10-Ezek13:12); [ZEC 8:17](https://ref.ly/Zech8:17); [2PE 2:17–2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17-2Pet2:19)

### The danger of making promises rashly, lightly or in ignorance

**[ECC 5:1–5:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1-Eccl5:7)** *See also* [PRO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Prov20:25); [MAT 14:1–14:11](https://ref.ly/Matt14:1-Matt14:11) parallel [MRK 6:21–6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21-Mark6:28); [MAT 23:16–23:22](https://ref.ly/Matt23:16-Matt23:22)

The call of Jesus Christ to integrity and truth in making promises
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:33–5:37](https://ref.ly/Matt5:33-Matt5:37)** *See also* [DEU 23:22–23:23](https://ref.ly/Deut23:22-Deut23:23); [ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2), [ECC 5:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 14:22-24; Genesis 21:22-24; Genesis 47:29-31; Numbers 30:1-16; Joshua 2:12-21; Genesis 28:20-21; Judges 11:29-40; 1 Samuel 1:11-20; 2 Samuel 15:7-12; Nehemiah 10:28-29; Deuteronomy 6:13; Genesis 14:22; Genesis 21:24; Genesis 47:31; Joshua 2:12-14; Nehemiah 10:29; 2 Samuel 3:35; Ruth 1:17; 1 Samuel 14:24; 2 Samuel 3:9-10; 1 Kings 2:23; Matthew 26:73-75; Mark 14:70-72; Numbers 30:1-2; Psalms 50:14; Proverbs 5:18-23; Malachi 2:14-16; Matthew 19:4-9; Leviticus 19:12; Exodus 20:7; Deuteronomy 23:21; Jeremiah 8:11-12; Ezekiel 13:1-7; Ezekiel 13:10-12; Zechariah 8:17; 2 Peter 2:17-19; Ecclesiastes 5:1-7; Proverbs 20:25; Matthew 14:1-11; Mark 6:21-28; Matthew 23:16-22; Matthew 5:33-37; Deuteronomy 23:22-23; Ecclesiastes 5:2; Ecclesiastes 5:5
