## 5469: promise, of Holy Spirit (id: 5469)

See [3040: Holy Spirit, promise of](3040.md)



## 5471: proof (id: 5471)

A confirmation and verification of a belief, such as belief in the existence of God, in the trustworthiness of a witness or in the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 5472: proof, as evidence (ID: `5472@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5473: proof, through testing (ID: `5473@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5472: proof, as evidence (id: 5472)

In the judicial sense, evidence is required to confirm the testimony of a witness. In the lives of believers, practical evidence should be obvious as proof of the reality of their faith.

The Mosaic law required proof to be offered in various legal contexts
---------------------------------------------------------------------

### In giving testimony

**[DEU 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15)** *See also* [EXO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:2); [LEV 5:1](https://ref.ly/Lev5:1); [DEU 17:6](https://ref.ly/Deut17:6)

### In acccusations of immorality

**[DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21)** *See also* [NUM 5:11–5:31](https://ref.ly/Num5:11-Num5:31)

### In accusations of idolatry

**[DEU 13:12–13:15](https://ref.ly/Deut13:12-Deut13:15)**

Evidence offered as proof of the gospel
---------------------------------------

### The evidence of witnesses

**[2PE 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:16-2Pet1:18)** *See also* [ACT 1:3](https://ref.ly/Acts1:3); [1CO 15:5–15:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:5-1Cor15:7); [HEB 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3-Heb2:4); [1JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:3)

### Believers' lives are to prove the reality of their faith

**[JHN 15:8](https://ref.ly/John15:8)** *See also* [MAT 25:31–25:46](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31-Matt25:46); [ACT 26:20](https://ref.ly/Acts26:20); [1CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:2); [2CO 8:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:24)

Evidence and signs given as proof
---------------------------------

### Looking for signs of the will of God

**[JDG 6:36–6:38](https://ref.ly/Judg6:36-Judg6:38)**

### The signs of Jesus Christ as a basis of faith

**[JHN 2:11](https://ref.ly/John2:11)**

### Jesus Christ's criticism of those who demanded other signs

**[MAT 16:4](https://ref.ly/Matt16:4)** *The “sign of Jonah” is a reference to the resurrection of Jesus Christ.*

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 19:15; Exodus 23:2; Leviticus 5:1; Deuteronomy 17:6; Deuteronomy 22:13-21; Numbers 5:11-31; Deuteronomy 13:12-15; 2 Peter 1:16-18; Acts 1:3; 1 Corinthians 15:5-7; Hebrews 2:3-4; 1 John 1:1-3; John 15:8; Matthew 25:31-46; Acts 26:20; 1 Corinthians 4:2; 2 Corinthians 8:24; Judges 6:36-38; John 2:11; Matthew 16:4
* **Associated Articles:** 1416: miracles, nature of (ID: `1416@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1461: truth, nature of (ID: `1461@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2351: Jesus Christ, miracles of (ID: `2351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2366: Jesus Christ, prophecies concerning (ID: `2366@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2422: gospel, confirmation of (ID: `2422@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2560: Jesus Christ, resurrection of (ID: `2560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5440: perjury (ID: `5440@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5624: witnesses, to Jesus Christ (ID: `5624@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5625: witnesses, false (ID: `5625@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7915: confirmation (ID: `7915@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8112: certainty (ID: `8112@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5473: proof, through testing (id: 5473)

God seeks proof of genuine love, faith and growth in the lives of his people. To this end, he tests them in various ways. However, when people test him, it is often a symptom of lack of trust.

Reasons for God's testing of Israel
-----------------------------------

### To refine them

**[ZEC 13:8–13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:8-Zech13:9)** *See also* [PSA 66:10–66:12](https://ref.ly/Ps66:10-Ps66:12); [PRO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Prov17:3); [ISA 48:10](https://ref.ly/Isa48:10); [JER 6:27–6:30](https://ref.ly/Jer6:27-Jer6:30), [JER 9:7](https://ref.ly/Jer9:7)

### To prove their obedience

**[EXO 16:4](https://ref.ly/Exod16:4)** *See also* [EXO 15:25–15:26](https://ref.ly/Exod15:25-Exod15:26); [DEU 8:2–8:5](https://ref.ly/Deut8:2-Deut8:5), [DEU 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Deut8:15-Deut8:16); [JDG 2:22](https://ref.ly/Judg2:22), [JDG 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/Judg3:1-Judg3:4); [1CH 29:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:17)

### To prove their love for him

**[DEU 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/Deut13:1-Deut13:3)**

God tests individuals
---------------------

**[1PE 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6-1Pet1:7)** *See also* [GEN 22:1–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:18) *Abraham*; [JOB 23:10](https://ref.ly/Job23:10) *Job*; [2CH 32:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:31) *Hezekiah*; [JHN 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/John6:5-John6:6) *Philip*; [JAS 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2-Jas1:3), [JAS 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jas1:12)

Those who test God are usually motivated by lack of trust
---------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 78:18–78:22](https://ref.ly/Ps78:18-Ps78:22)** *See also* [EXO 17:1–17:7](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1-Exod17:7); [NUM 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Num14:22-Num14:23); [DEU 6:16](https://ref.ly/Deut6:16); [PSA 78:40–78:44](https://ref.ly/Ps78:40-Ps78:44), [PSA 78:56–78:57](https://ref.ly/Ps78:56-Ps78:57), [PSA 106:13–106:15](https://ref.ly/Ps106:13-Ps106:15); [1CO 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:9); [HEB 3:7–3:11](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7-Heb3:11); [PSA 95:7–95:11](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:11)

God may invite certain people to test him
-----------------------------------------

**[MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10)** *See also* [ISA 7:10–7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:10-Isa7:14)

God's people are to seek proof through testing in certain areas
---------------------------------------------------------------

### Believers are to test themselves

**[2CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:5)** *See also* [GAL 6:4](https://ref.ly/Gal6:4)

### Believers are to test what they hear

**[1TH 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:21)** *See also* [1CO 14:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:29); [1JN 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:1-1John4:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Zechariah 13:8-9; Psalms 66:10-12; Proverbs 17:3; Isaiah 48:10; Jeremiah 6:27-30; Jeremiah 9:7; Exodus 16:4; Exodus 15:25-26; Deuteronomy 8:2-5; Deuteronomy 8:15-16; Judges 2:22; Judges 3:1-4; 1 Chronicles 29:17; Deuteronomy 13:1-3; 1 Peter 1:6-7; Genesis 22:1-18; Job 23:10; 2 Chronicles 32:31; John 6:5-6; James 1:2-3; James 1:12; Psalms 78:18-22; Exodus 17:1-7; Numbers 14:22-23; Deuteronomy 6:16; Psalms 78:40-44; Psalms 78:56-57; Psalms 106:13-15; 1 Corinthians 10:9; Hebrews 3:7-11; Psalms 95:7-11; Malachi 3:10; Isaiah 7:10-14; 2 Corinthians 13:5; Galatians 6:4; 1 Thessalonians 5:21; 1 Corinthians 14:29; 1 John 4:1-3
* **Associated Articles:** 4351: refining (ID: `4351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5560: suffering (ID: `5560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5593: trial (ID: `5593@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7416: purification (ID: `7416@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7774: prophets, false (ID: `7774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8477: self-examination (ID: `8477@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8832: testing (ID: `8832@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5475: property (id: 5475)

The ultimate source of all property is God. While property is entrusted to human beings, they have responsibilities towards God as a result of their stewardship.

* **Associated Articles:** 5476: property, nature of (ID: `5476@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5477: property, land (ID: `5477@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5478: property, houses (ID: `5478@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5476: property, nature of (id: 5476)

Possessions are a gift from God to be used for his glory, for human pleasure and in benefiting others.

Possessions are a cause for thanksgiving
----------------------------------------

**[ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19)** *See also* [GEN 39:2–39:4](https://ref.ly/Gen39:2-Gen39:4); [DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18); [1CH 29:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:12); [2CH 31:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:21); [HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8)

Owning possessions is open to abuse
-----------------------------------

### They can be coveted

**[EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17)** *See also* [1KI 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1-1Kgs21:4); [JER 6:13](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13); [MIC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:2); [LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15); [1CO 7:29–7:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:29-1Cor7:31)

### They can cause deceit

[ACT 5:1–5:9](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:9)

### They can cause pride and forgetfulness

**[PRO 30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:9)** *See also* [2KI 20:13–20:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:13-2Kgs20:17); [DAN 4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30), [DAN 5:20](https://ref.ly/Dan5:20); [HOS 13:6](https://ref.ly/Hos13:6); [MAT 19:24](https://ref.ly/Matt19:24); [LUK 12:13–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:13-Luke12:21)

### Abuse of ownership leads to judgment

[ISA 3:16–3:24](https://ref.ly/Isa3:16-Isa3:24), [ISA 5:8](https://ref.ly/Isa5:8); ; [HAB 2:6–2:9](https://ref.ly/Hab2:6-Hab2:9)

Property is for benefiting others
---------------------------------

### It is to be used to help others

[ACT 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44-Acts2:45), [ACT 4:32–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32-Acts4:35); ; [GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6), [GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10); ; [EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28); ; [1TI 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17-1Tim6:18); ; [JAS 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Jas2:15-Jas2:16); ; [1JN 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/1John3:17-1John3:18)

### It is to be used to assist strangers

[LEV 25:35](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35); ; [NUM 35:15](https://ref.ly/Num35:15); ; [DEU 10:19](https://ref.ly/Deut10:19); ; [MAT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:42)

Property provides no lasting satisfaction
-----------------------------------------

**[ECC 2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:26)** *See also* [JOB 27:16–27:17](https://ref.ly/Job27:16-Job27:17); [PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6); [ECC 2:21](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:21); [EZK 28:4–28:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:4-Ezek28:7); [HAG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hag1:6); [MAT 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19-Matt6:21); [LUK 12:20–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20-Luke12:21); [JAS 5:3](https://ref.ly/Jas5:3)

Laws governing the inheritance of property
------------------------------------------

[NUM 27:3–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:3-Num27:11); ; [DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17); ; [EZK 46:16–46:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:16-Ezek46:18)

Godly men of property
---------------------

[GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2) *Abraham*; ; [GEN 26:14](https://ref.ly/Gen26:14) *Isaac*; [GEN 30:43](https://ref.ly/Gen30:43), [GEN 32:5](https://ref.ly/Gen32:5), [GEN 36:7](https://ref.ly/Gen36:7); [2SA 19:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:32) *Barzillai*; ; [1CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:28) *David*; [2CH 1:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:15), [2CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:22); [2CH 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:29) *Hezekiah*; ; [JOB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Job1:3) *Job*; ; [MAT 27:57](https://ref.ly/Matt27:57) *Joseph of Arimathea*; ; [ACT 4:36–4:37](https://ref.ly/Acts4:36-Acts4:37) *Barnabas*

## 5477: property, land (id: 5477)

All land belongs to God, and he allows people to care for it and use it for their own needs and the needs of others. The land promised to Israel is a special case illustrating the spiritual inheritance of all believers and God's ultimate purpose for the world.

All land belongs to God
-----------------------

### It is in his possession and care

**[PSA 24:1](https://ref.ly/Ps24:1)** *See also* [EXO 9:29](https://ref.ly/Exod9:29); [PSA 33:5](https://ref.ly/Ps33:5); [1CO 10:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:26)

### It is given to human beings to manage

**[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)** *All exploitation of land is therefore contrary to God's purpose.* *See also* [PSA 8:6](https://ref.ly/Ps8:6), [PSA 115:16](https://ref.ly/Ps115:16)

The land promised to Israel
---------------------------

### It was promised on oath

**[GEN 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/Gen12:6-Gen12:7)** *See also* [GEN 13:14–13:17](https://ref.ly/Gen13:14-Gen13:17), [GEN 15:7](https://ref.ly/Gen15:7), [GEN 15:18](https://ref.ly/Gen15:18), [GEN 17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:8), [GEN 50:24](https://ref.ly/Gen50:24); [EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8); [DEU 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:11), [DEU 31:20–31:21](https://ref.ly/Deut31:20-Deut31:21); [JDG 2:1](https://ref.ly/Judg2:1)

### It was a fruitful land

**[EXO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:8)** *See also* [LEV 20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:24); [NUM 14:7–14:8](https://ref.ly/Num14:7-Num14:8); [JOS 5:6](https://ref.ly/Josh5:6)

### The distribution of the land

[LEV 25:34](https://ref.ly/Lev25:34); ; [NUM 33:53–33:54](https://ref.ly/Num33:53-Num33:54), [NUM 35:2–35:5](https://ref.ly/Num35:2-Num35:5)

Individual land rights
----------------------

### Legitimate purchase or sale

[GEN 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Gen23:15-Gen23:16), [GEN 33:19](https://ref.ly/Gen33:19), [GEN 47:20](https://ref.ly/Gen47:20); ; [2SA 24:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:24); ; [PRO 31:16](https://ref.ly/Prov31:16); ; [JER 32:6–32:15](https://ref.ly/Jer32:6-Jer32:15) *Jeremiah's purchase was an act of faith.*; [MAT 27:7–27:10](https://ref.ly/Matt27:7-Matt27:10); ; [ACT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Acts1:18)

### Examples of renting

[SNG 8:11](https://ref.ly/Song8:11); ; [MAT 21:33–21:41](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:41) parallels [MRK 12:1–12:9](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:9), [LUK 20:9–20:16](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:16)

### Laws of inheritance

[NUM 36:6–36:9](https://ref.ly/Num36:6-Num36:9); ; [MIC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:2)

### Encroachment was forbidden

[DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14) *Land boundaries were marked out by stones, the removal of which was a serious offence*; ; [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17); ; [JOB 24:2](https://ref.ly/Job24:2); ; [PRO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Prov22:28), [PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10); ; [HOS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Hos5:10)

### Redemption of land

[LEV 25:23–25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:23-Lev25:28); ; [RUT 4:2–4:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:2-Ruth4:9)

God's ultimate purpose for the world
------------------------------------

### The heavenly land

**[HEB 11:13–11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:13-Heb11:16)** *See also* [HEB 11:8–11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:10)

### A new heaven and earth

**[ISA 65:17](https://ref.ly/Isa65:17)** *See also* [ISA 66:22](https://ref.ly/Isa66:22); [REV 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:2)

### The inheritance of the righteous

**[MAT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Matt5:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11), [PSA 37:29](https://ref.ly/Ps37:29); [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 24:1; Exodus 9:29; Psalms 33:5; 1 Corinthians 10:26; Genesis 2:15; Psalms 8:6; Psalms 115:16; Genesis 12:6-7; Genesis 13:14-17; Genesis 15:7; Genesis 15:18; Genesis 17:8; Genesis 50:24; Exodus 6:8; Deuteronomy 6:10-11; Deuteronomy 31:20-21; Judges 2:1; Exodus 3:8; Leviticus 20:24; Numbers 14:7-8; Joshua 5:6; Leviticus 25:34; Numbers 33:53-54; Numbers 35:2-5; Genesis 23:15-16; Genesis 33:19; Genesis 47:20; 2 Samuel 24:24; Proverbs 31:16; Jeremiah 32:6-15; Matthew 27:7-10; Acts 1:18; Song of Songs 8:11; Matthew 21:33-41; Mark 12:1-9; Luke 20:9-16; Numbers 36:6-9; Micah 2:2; Deuteronomy 19:14; Deuteronomy 27:17; Job 24:2; Proverbs 22:28; Proverbs 23:10; Hosea 5:10; Leviticus 25:23-28; Ruth 4:2-9; Hebrews 11:13-16; Hebrews 11:8-10; Isaiah 65:17; Isaiah 66:22; Revelation 21:1-2; Matthew 5:5; Psalms 37:11; Psalms 37:29; 2 Peter 3:13

## 5478: property, houses (id: 5478)

From at least the time of the flood people have built houses to live in and these have been a major part of their property; the temple was seen as God's house.

Houses are a major part of people's property
--------------------------------------------

[EXO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22); ; [HAG 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hag1:4); ; [MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27); ; [HEB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Heb3:3)

The homeless have no property
-----------------------------

**[1CO 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:11)** *See also* [JOB 24:8](https://ref.ly/Job24:8); [LAM 4:5](https://ref.ly/Lam4:5); [LUK 9:58](https://ref.ly/Luke9:58)

Property has no lasting value
-----------------------------

**[HEB 10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:34)** *See also* [DEU 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Deut6:11-Deut6:12); [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29)

Materials used in house building
--------------------------------

### Clay

[JOB 4:19](https://ref.ly/Job4:19)

### Brick

[EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14); ; [ISA 9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:10)

### Stone and wood

[LEV 14:40](https://ref.ly/Lev14:40), [LEV 14:42](https://ref.ly/Lev14:42); ; [HAB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Hab2:11)

### Luxurious building materials

[1KI 22:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:39); ; [EST 1:6](https://ref.ly/Esth1:6); ; [AMO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Amos3:15)

Houses are protected by God
---------------------------

**[PSA 127:1](https://ref.ly/Ps127:1)** *See also* [EXO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:27)

Houses as dwellings
-------------------

### For sinners

[JOS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Josh2:1); ; [PRO 7:27](https://ref.ly/Prov7:27), [PRO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Prov21:9)

### For friends

[ZEC 13:6](https://ref.ly/Zech13:6); ; [LUK 10:38](https://ref.ly/Luke10:38); ; [JHN 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/John12:1-John12:2)

Other uses for houses
---------------------

### For work

[JER 18:2](https://ref.ly/Jer18:2)

### For hospitality

**[LUK 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:5-Luke10:7)** parallels [MAT 10:11](https://ref.ly/Matt10:11), [MRK 6:10](https://ref.ly/Mark6:10) *See also* [GEN 19:3](https://ref.ly/Gen19:3); [MAT 25:35](https://ref.ly/Matt25:35); [ACT 16:15](https://ref.ly/Acts16:15); [1TI 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:10); [HEB 13:2](https://ref.ly/Heb13:2)

### For church gatherings

**[ACT 20:20](https://ref.ly/Acts20:20)** *See also* [ROM 16:5](https://ref.ly/Rom16:5); [1CO 16:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:19); [COL 4:15](https://ref.ly/Col4:15); [PHM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:2)

### As palaces

[2SA 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:2); ; [1KI 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:1-1Kgs7:2), [1KI 22:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:39); ; [DAN 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29-Dan4:30); ; [MAT 11:8](https://ref.ly/Matt11:8)

-----------------------------

### Respecting the houses of others

[EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17); ; [MIC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:2)

### Redemption of property

[LEV 25:29–25:33](https://ref.ly/Lev25:29-Lev25:33)

----------------

### Solomon's building

## 5480: protection (id: 5480)

Defence from attack, hurt or exploitation. The ultimate protector is God but he also uses others in defending the weak and needy.

Protection from oppression
--------------------------

**[PSA 72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:4)** *See also* [DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18); [PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5), [PSA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:10); [LUK 1:51–1:53](https://ref.ly/Luke1:51-Luke1:53)

Protection from exploitation
----------------------------

**[JOB 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Job24:3-Job24:4)** *See also* [PRO 30:14](https://ref.ly/Prov30:14); [EZK 18:10–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:10-Ezek18:13); [AMO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1), [AMO 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:6); [MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40); [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

Means of protection against physical attack
-------------------------------------------

### Armour

[1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38); ; [1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11); ; [JER 46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:4)

### Shields

[JER 46:3](https://ref.ly/Jer46:3)

### Helmets

[1SA 17:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38); ; [JER 46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:4)

Spiritual protection
--------------------

**[JHN 17:11–17:12](https://ref.ly/John17:11-John17:12)** *See also* [JHN 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:29); [1CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:13); [PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6), [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7); [2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:12); [1PE 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:5); [JUD 1:24](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24)

Believers need protection from spiritual dangers
------------------------------------------------

### From Satan

**[1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)** *See also* [JOB 1:9](https://ref.ly/Job1:9); [MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13); [JHN 17:15](https://ref.ly/John17:15); [EPH 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:11-Eph6:12); [2TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:9), [2TH 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:3)

### From Satan's agents in the world

**[JHN 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:18-John15:19)** *See also* [PSA 27:2](https://ref.ly/Ps27:2), [PSA 31:15](https://ref.ly/Ps31:15), [PSA 109:1–109:3](https://ref.ly/Ps109:1-Ps109:3), [PSA 109:31](https://ref.ly/Ps109:31); [MIC 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:2-Mic7:3); [MAT 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28-Matt10:29), [MAT 10:36](https://ref.ly/Matt10:36); [LUK 1:68–1:74](https://ref.ly/Luke1:68-Luke1:74); [ROM 8:36](https://ref.ly/Rom8:36); [1JN 3:13](https://ref.ly/1John3:13), [1JN 4:4](https://ref.ly/1John4:4)

God protects his people
-----------------------

### By watching over them and helping them

**[PSA 121:5](https://ref.ly/Ps121:5)** *See also* [1SA 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9); [PSA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Ps1:6), [PSA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Ps34:15), [PSA 37:40](https://ref.ly/Ps37:40), [PSA 40:17](https://ref.ly/Ps40:17), [PSA 140:1](https://ref.ly/Ps140:1); [2CO 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:10)

### By his presence with them

**[HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)** *See also* [JOS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Josh1:5); [PSA 119:116](https://ref.ly/Ps119:116), [PSA 119:170](https://ref.ly/Ps119:170); [ISA 43:1–43:3](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1-Isa43:3); [JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33); [ACT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:10)

### By sending angels to protect them

**[PSA 34:7](https://ref.ly/Ps34:7)** *See also* [GEN 21:17](https://ref.ly/Gen21:17); [EXO 23:20](https://ref.ly/Exod23:20); [NUM 22:22](https://ref.ly/Num22:22), [NUM 22:24](https://ref.ly/Num22:24), [NUM 22:26](https://ref.ly/Num22:26), [NUM 22:31](https://ref.ly/Num22:31), [NUM 22:34](https://ref.ly/Num22:34); [2KI 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:17); [PSA 91:11](https://ref.ly/Ps91:11); [MAT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Matt18:10); [HEB 1:14](https://ref.ly/Heb1:14)

### By using people to defend them

**[PRO 31:8–31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:8-Prov31:9)** *See also* [NUM 35:25](https://ref.ly/Num35:25); [ISA 1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:17), [ISA 58:6](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6); [JER 5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:28), [JER 22:16](https://ref.ly/Jer22:16); [LUK 10:33–10:35](https://ref.ly/Luke10:33-Luke10:35); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

Conditions of God's protection
------------------------------

### Prayer

**[PSA 72:12](https://ref.ly/Ps72:12)** *See also* [JOB 6:23](https://ref.ly/Job6:23); [PSA 59:1](https://ref.ly/Ps59:1), [PSA 82:3](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3), [PSA 145:19](https://ref.ly/Ps145:19); [JHN 17:11](https://ref.ly/John17:11)

### Right attitudes

## 5481: proverb (id: 5481)

A popular saying, regarded as conveying wisdom. Scripture records a great number of didactic proverbs and also preserves some popular sayings.

Proverbs identified with wisdom
-------------------------------

**[PRO 26:7](https://ref.ly/Prov26:7)** *See also* [PSA 49:1–49:4](https://ref.ly/Ps49:1-Ps49:4); [PRO 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:6), [PRO 26:9](https://ref.ly/Prov26:9); [ECC 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:9-Eccl12:11)

Solomon's many proverbs
-----------------------

**[1KI 4:29–4:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:29-1Kgs4:34)** *See also* [PRO 1:1](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1), [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1), [PRO 25:1](https://ref.ly/Prov25:1); [ECC 12:9–12:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:9-Eccl12:10)

----------------

## 5482: punishment (id: 5482)

A form of physical pain or deprivation, generally in proportion to the offence which gives rise to it. Scripture stresses the reality of punishment by God for sin, while urging that human punishment should be humane.

* **Associated Articles:** 5483: punishment, nature of (ID: `5483@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5484: punishment, by God (ID: `5484@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5485: punishment, legal aspects of (ID: `5485@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5483: punishment, nature of (id: 5483)

Punishment is the inevitable and equitable consequence of breaking the law of God. In keeping with God's justice and righteousness, punishment is administered by him or by human beings as his agents.

God warned Adam and Eve of the punishment for disobedience
----------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:16-Gen2:17)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:5)

Adam and Eve's original disobedience brought inevitable punishment
------------------------------------------------------------------

### Upon the serpent

**[GEN 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14-Gen3:15)** *See also* [COL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:15); [REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9), [REV 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Rev20:1-Rev20:3), [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10)

### Upon the woman

**[GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16)** *See also* [PSA 48:6](https://ref.ly/Ps48:6); [ISA 13:8](https://ref.ly/Isa13:8), [ISA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Isa21:3); [JER 4:31](https://ref.ly/Jer4:31); [1TI 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:14-1Tim2:15)

### Upon the man

**[GEN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:19)** *See also* [JOB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Job5:7), [JOB 7:1](https://ref.ly/Job7:1); [PSA 90:5](https://ref.ly/Ps90:5), [PSA 90:7](https://ref.ly/Ps90:7), [PSA 90:8](https://ref.ly/Ps90:8); [ECC 1:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:3); [ISA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Isa40:2)

### Upon creation

**[GEN 3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17)** *See also* [GEN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:29); [JER 12:4](https://ref.ly/Jer12:4); [ROM 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Rom8:19-Rom8:22)

The punishment of death
-----------------------

**[ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)** *See also* [EZK 18:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:4), [EZK 33:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:8); [ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12), [ROM 6:21](https://ref.ly/Rom6:21); [1CO 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:22)

Banishment as punishment
------------------------

**[GEN 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gen3:23)** *See also* [GEN 4:10–4:16](https://ref.ly/Gen4:10-Gen4:16); [DEU 29:27](https://ref.ly/Deut29:27), [DEU 29:28](https://ref.ly/Deut29:28); [2KI 25:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:21); [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12)

Punishment must be appropriate to the crime
-------------------------------------------

**[GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6)** *See also* [EXO 21:23–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:23-Exod21:25); [LEV 24:17–24:22](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17-Lev24:22); [JDG 9:24](https://ref.ly/Judg9:24)

Punishment reflects the obligations of the offenders towards their victims
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 22:3](https://ref.ly/Exod22:3)** *See also* [EXO 21:2](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2), [EXO 21:26–21:27](https://ref.ly/Exod21:26-Exod21:27), [EXO 22:4–22:9](https://ref.ly/Exod22:4-Exod22:9)

Divine punishment is always totally just
----------------------------------------

**[GEN 18:25](https://ref.ly/Gen18:25)** *See also* [GEN 20:4](https://ref.ly/Gen20:4); [EXO 34:7](https://ref.ly/Exod34:7); [DEU 32:35](https://ref.ly/Deut32:35); [2CH 6:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:23); [ISA 3:11](https://ref.ly/Isa3:11); [MAT 16:27](https://ref.ly/Matt16:27); [1CO 3:12–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:12-1Cor3:15); [1TH 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:6)

God appoints human agents to administer his punishment
------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1), [ISA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Isa45:14); [JER 51:20](https://ref.ly/Jer51:20); [MIC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:13); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)

## 5484: punishment, by God (id: 5484)

God's chastening of his children and his judgment on impenitent sinners and supernatural evil powers.

God's punishment of his children
--------------------------------

### Through the discipline of suffering and hardship

**[HEB 12:5–12:7](https://ref.ly/Heb12:5-Heb12:7)** *See also* [PRO 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:11-Prov3:12); [DEU 4:36](https://ref.ly/Deut4:36), [DEU 8:5](https://ref.ly/Deut8:5); [2SA 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:14); [JOB 5:17](https://ref.ly/Job5:17)JOB [33:19](https://ref.ly/Job33:19), [JOB 36:10](https://ref.ly/Job36:10)[PSA 32:3–32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:4), [PSA 94:12–94:13](https://ref.ly/Ps94:12-Ps94:13), [PSA 118:18](https://ref.ly/Ps118:18), [PSA 119:75](https://ref.ly/Ps119:75); [ZEP 3:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:7); [JAS 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jas1:12); [REV 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rev3:19)

### Through death

**[1CO 11:30–11:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:30-1Cor11:32)** *See also* [LEV 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Lev10:1-Lev10:3); [NUM 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/Num11:1-Num11:3); [1SA 4:17–4:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:17-1Sam4:21); [2SA 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:6-2Sam6:8) parallel [1CH 13:9–13:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:9-1Chr13:11)

God's punishment of the ungodly
-------------------------------

### Their punishment is certain

**[ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18)** *See also* [PRO 10:24](https://ref.ly/Prov10:24); [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11), [ISA 26:21](https://ref.ly/Isa26:21), [ISA 66:16](https://ref.ly/Isa66:16); [JHN 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/John5:28-John5:29); [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); [COL 3:6](https://ref.ly/Col3:6); [1TH 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:3); [HEB 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:2-Heb2:3)

### Their punishment is just

**[PSA 96:13](https://ref.ly/Ps96:13)** *See also* [GEN 18:25](https://ref.ly/Gen18:25); [EXO 9:27](https://ref.ly/Exod9:27); [PSA 98:9](https://ref.ly/Ps98:9); [JHN 5:28–5:30](https://ref.ly/John5:28-John5:30); [ROM 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/Rom3:5-Rom3:8); [1PE 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:23); [REV 16:5–16:7](https://ref.ly/Rev16:5-Rev16:7)

### Their punishment involves destruction in hell

**[LUK 12:5](https://ref.ly/Luke12:5)** parallel [MAT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28) *See also* [MAT 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:29-Matt5:30)

### Fire as an image of destruction

**[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)** *See also* [ISA 33:14](https://ref.ly/Isa33:14), [ISA 66:15](https://ref.ly/Isa66:15); [NAM 1:6](https://ref.ly/Nah1:6); [MAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mal4:1); [MAT 13:40–13:43](https://ref.ly/Matt13:40-Matt13:43); [MRK 9:43](https://ref.ly/Mark9:43), [MRK 9:48](https://ref.ly/Mark9:48); [LUK 16:22–16:24](https://ref.ly/Luke16:22-Luke16:24); [JUD 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jude1:7), [JUD 1:23](https://ref.ly/Jude1:23); [REV 20:14–20:15](https://ref.ly/Rev20:14-Rev20:15)

### Their punishment will involve exclusion from God's presence

**[MAT 7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:23)** *See also* [MAT 22:13](https://ref.ly/Matt22:13), [MAT 25:41](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41); [LUK 13:24–13:28](https://ref.ly/Luke13:24-Luke13:28); [REV 21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:27)

### Their punishment will be eternal

**[2TH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:8-2Thess1:9)** *Interpreters differ over whether “everlasting destruction” implies an experience which continues for ever, or annihilation that is everlasting in the sense of being irreversible.* *See also* [DAN 12:2](https://ref.ly/Dan12:2); [MAT 25:46](https://ref.ly/Matt25:46); [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:11)

### The punishment of the second death

**[REV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rev2:11)** *The “second death” refers to the final state of the unrepentant.* *See also* [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6), [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8)

### There will be degrees of punishment

**[MRK 12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:40)** parallel [LUK 20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:47) *See also* [MAT 11:22](https://ref.ly/Matt11:22), [MAT 11:24](https://ref.ly/Matt11:24); [LUK 12:47–12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:47-Luke12:48)

The punishment of God's spiritual enemies
-----------------------------------------

### Satan

**[REV 20:2](https://ref.ly/Rev20:2)** *See also* [ISA 14:12–14:15](https://ref.ly/Isa14:12-Isa14:15); [1TI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:6); [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14); [REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9), [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10)

### Fallen angels

**[2PE 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:4)** *See also* [ISA 24:21–24:22](https://ref.ly/Isa24:21-Isa24:22); [MAT 25:41](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41); [JUD 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jude1:6)

## 5485: punishment, legal aspects of (id: 5485)

The law sets out details of penalties which may be imposed upon people as a result of their committing of offences.

Just punishments
----------------

### Banishment

**[GEN 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gen3:23)** *See also* [2SA 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:13-2Sam14:14); [EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26); [JOB 18:18](https://ref.ly/Job18:18); [JER 16:15](https://ref.ly/Jer16:15), [JER 23:8](https://ref.ly/Jer23:8), [JER 29:14](https://ref.ly/Jer29:14); [ZEC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3)

### Beating

**[DEU 25:2–25:3](https://ref.ly/Deut25:2-Deut25:3)**

### Burning

**[GEN 38:24](https://ref.ly/Gen38:24)** *See also* [LEV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Lev20:14), [LEV 21:9](https://ref.ly/Lev21:9); [DAN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Dan3:6)

### Confiscation of property

**[EST 8:1](https://ref.ly/Esth8:1)** *See also* [EZR 10:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:8)

### Hanging

**[JOS 8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:29)** *See also* [DEU 21:22–21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22-Deut21:23); [JOS 10:26](https://ref.ly/Josh10:26); [1SA 31:8–31:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:8-1Sam31:10); [2SA 4:122](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:122)SA [17:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:23); [MAT 27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:5)[2SA 21:9–21:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:9-2Sam21:10); [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13)

### Impaling

**[EZR 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:11)** *See also* [GEN 40:19–40:22](https://ref.ly/Gen40:19-Gen40:22)EST [2:23](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23), [EST 5:14](https://ref.ly/Esth5:14)

### Stoning

**[EXO 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:12-Exod19:13)** *See also* [EXO 21:29](https://ref.ly/Exod21:29); [LEV 20:2](https://ref.ly/Lev20:2), [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27)LEV [24:14](https://ref.ly/Lev24:14), [LEV 24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:16), [LEV 24:23](https://ref.ly/Lev24:23)[NUM 15:36](https://ref.ly/Num15:36); [DEU 13:10](https://ref.ly/Deut13:10), [DEU 17:5](https://ref.ly/Deut17:5), [DEU 21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:21), [DEU 22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:21), [DEU 22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:24); [JOS 7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25); [1KI 21:13–21:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:13-1Kgs21:15)

Unjust punishments and persecutions
-----------------------------------

### Banishment

[ACT 18:2](https://ref.ly/Acts18:2)

### Beating

**[MAT 27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26)** parallels [MRK 15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15), [JHN 19:1](https://ref.ly/John19:1) *See also* [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5); [ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37); [2CO 11:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:24)

### Imprisonment

**[ACT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:18)** *See also* [GEN 39:20–39:22](https://ref.ly/Gen39:20-Gen39:22); [1KI 22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27); [JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15), [JER 38:6](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6); [ACT 12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4), [ACT 16:23](https://ref.ly/Acts16:23), [ACT 23:35](https://ref.ly/Acts23:35), [ACT 26:10](https://ref.ly/Acts26:10); [2CO 11:23](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23); [HEB 11:36](https://ref.ly/Heb11:36)

### Stoning

## 5487: queen (id: 5487)

In OT times the chief wife of a king's harem or a female monarch. A queen mother, as a king's widow and the mother of the reigning king, would have held a position of some authority and influence.

A queen as the wife of a king
-----------------------------

### A chief wife among several

**[EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17)** *See also* [EST 1:9–1:12](https://ref.ly/Esth1:9-Esth1:12), [EST 2:4](https://ref.ly/Esth2:4)

### Enjoying a position of some influence

[EST 1:15–1:18](https://ref.ly/Esth1:15-Esth1:18), [EST 2:22](https://ref.ly/Esth2:22), [EST 5:12](https://ref.ly/Esth5:12), [EST 7:5–7:8](https://ref.ly/Esth7:5-Esth7:8)

### Enjoying the king's special favour

**[EST 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Esth5:2-Esth5:3)** *See also* [EST 7:1–7:3](https://ref.ly/Esth7:1-Esth7:3), [EST 8:1](https://ref.ly/Esth8:1), [EST 8:7](https://ref.ly/Esth8:7), [EST 9:12](https://ref.ly/Esth9:12); [SNG 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Song6:8-Song6:9); [NEH 2:6](https://ref.ly/Neh2:6); [PSA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ps45:9)

### Sometimes granted considerable authority

**[EST 9:29](https://ref.ly/Esth9:29)** *See also* [EST 9:31](https://ref.ly/Esth9:31)

A queen as a female monarch
---------------------------

### The queen of Sheba

**[1KI 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1)** parallel [2CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1) *See also* [1KI 10:2–10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:2-1Kgs10:10) parallel [2CH 9:1–9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1-2Chr9:9); [1KI 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:13) parallel [2CH 9:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:12); [MAT 12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:42) parallel [LUK 11:31](https://ref.ly/Luke11:31)

### Queen Athaliah of Judah

[2KI 11:1–11:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:1-2Kgs11:21) parallel [2CH 22:10–23:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:10-2Chr23:21)

Queen used figuratively
-----------------------

### Of Jerusalem

[LAM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Lam1:1); ; [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13)

### Of Babylon

[ISA 47:5](https://ref.ly/Isa47:5), [ISA 47:7](https://ref.ly/Isa47:7); ; [REV 18:7](https://ref.ly/Rev18:7)

Queen of Heaven: a title for the Babylonian goddess Ishtar
----------------------------------------------------------

[JER 7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:18), [JER 44:17–44:19](https://ref.ly/Jer44:17-Jer44:19), [JER 44:25](https://ref.ly/Jer44:25)

----------------------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:19)** *See also* [1KI 15:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:13) parallel [2CH 15:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:16); [2KI 10:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:13), [2KI 24:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:8), [2KI 24:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:12), [2KI 24:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:15); [JER 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jer13:18), [JER 22:26](https://ref.ly/Jer22:26), [JER 29:2](https://ref.ly/Jer29:2); [DAN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Dan5:10); [ACT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Acts8:27)

## 5488: quivers (id: 5488)

--------------------------------

[JOB 39:23](https://ref.ly/Job39:23); ; [ISA 22:6](https://ref.ly/Isa22:6)

Quivers referred to figuratively
--------------------------------

### As an instrument of the LORD's discipline

## 5489: rank (id: 5489)

A person's seniority and/or status, especially in military, government or priestly positions. Christians should not be concerned about rank or status.

Differences in rank amongst those in charge of both military and civil affairs
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Emperor

[LUK 2:1](https://ref.ly/Luke2:1), [LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1)

### King

[2SA 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:3-2Sam5:5); ; [1KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:1)

### Judge

[JDG 2:16–2:19](https://ref.ly/Judg2:16-Judg2:19) *The book of Judges calls individual judges “deliverers” e.g., [3:9](https://ref.ly/Judg3:9), 15\.*

### Prince

[JDG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Judg5:15); ; [2SA 3:38](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:38)

### Leader

[NUM 2:3–2:7](https://ref.ly/Num2:3-Num2:7), [NUM 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/Num2:10-Num2:12); ; [JDG 4:4](https://ref.ly/Judg4:4); ; [1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1)

### Captain

### Officer

[1KI 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:5), [1KI 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:22); ; [1CH 27:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:1); ; [ACT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Acts25:23)

Civil ranks
-----------

### Noble

[EST 1:3](https://ref.ly/Esth1:3), [EST 2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:18)

### Official

[1CH 28:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:1); ; [JER 52:10](https://ref.ly/Jer52:10)

### Governor

[1KI 4:7–4:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:7-1Kgs4:19); ; [HAG 1:1](https://ref.ly/Hag1:1); ; [ACT 23:24](https://ref.ly/Acts23:24)

### Tetrarch

[LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1)

### Heads of clans/tribes

### Heads of families

[NUM 7:2](https://ref.ly/Num7:2); ; [JOS 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Josh2:12-Josh2:13)

### Division of labour involving different degrees of responsibility

[EXO 18:20–18:23](https://ref.ly/Exod18:20-Exod18:23)

Rank was conferred as a reward
------------------------------

[1SA 18:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:5)

Rank within the priesthood
--------------------------

### The high priest

[LEV 21:10](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10); ; [NUM 35:25](https://ref.ly/Num35:25), [NUM 35:28](https://ref.ly/Num35:28), [NUM 35:32](https://ref.ly/Num35:32); ; [HAG 1:1](https://ref.ly/Hag1:1); ; [ZEC 3:1](https://ref.ly/Zech3:1); ; [LUK 3:2](https://ref.ly/Luke3:2)

### Priests

[EXO 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Exod28:1-Exod28:2); ; [LEV 8:5–8:12](https://ref.ly/Lev8:5-Lev8:12); ; [NUM 4:16](https://ref.ly/Num4:16); ; [JOS 22:31](https://ref.ly/Josh22:31); ; [2KI 11:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:9)

### Levites

[EXO 32:26](https://ref.ly/Exod32:26); ; [NUM 1:50](https://ref.ly/Num1:50); ; [DEU 12:18–12:19](https://ref.ly/Deut12:18-Deut12:19); ; [2CH 11:13–11:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:13-2Chr11:14)

Officers in the NT church
-------------------------

In contrast to the priesthood, offices within the NT Church are according to gifts; individuals with different gifts are listed but do not form a hierarchy: [ROM 12:4–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4-Rom12:8); ; [1CO 12:28–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28-1Cor12:30); ; [EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11); [PHP 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phil1:1); ; [1TI 3:1–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1-1Tim3:13)

### Some elders are worthy of double honour

[1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17) *Comparison between verses 17 and 28 of [Acts 20](https://ref.ly/Acts20:1-Acts20:999) shows that the same individuals could be called “elders”, “overseers” or “shepherds” (pastors).*

### All believers are called priests

[1PE 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:5), [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9); ; [REV 1:6](https://ref.ly/Rev1:6), [REV 5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:10), [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6)

Jesus Christ taught his disciples not to seek rank or status
------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 18:1–18:4](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1-Matt18:4) parallels [MRK 9:33–9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:33-Mark9:37), [LUK 9:46–9:48](https://ref.ly/Luke9:46-Luke9:48)

## 5490: refuge (id: 5490)

A safe retreat; a place of healing and renewal; also a stronghold from which to launch a counter\-attack.

False refuges
-------------

### Flight

**[GEN 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8-Gen3:9)**; **[PSA 139:7–139:12](https://ref.ly/Ps139:7-Ps139:12)** *See also* [ISA 28:15](https://ref.ly/Isa28:15), [ISA 28:17](https://ref.ly/Isa28:17)

### Idolatry

**[ISA 44:9](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9)** *See also* [DEU 32:31](https://ref.ly/Deut32:31), [DEU 32:37–32:38](https://ref.ly/Deut32:37-Deut32:38)

### Human support

**[JER 17:5](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5)** *See also* [PSA 20:7–20:8](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7-Ps20:8), [PSA 142:4](https://ref.ly/Ps142:4); [ISA 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:2); [ZEC 4:6](https://ref.ly/Zech4:6); [PHP 3:3](https://ref.ly/Phil3:3)

### False confidence

**[JER 6:14](https://ref.ly/Jer6:14)** *See also* [EZK 13:10–13:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:10-Ezek13:12); [MAT 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26-Matt7:27); [REV 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/Rev6:15-Rev6:17)

God alone is the true refuge of believers
-----------------------------------------

**[DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27)** *See also* [1SA 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:2); [2SA 22:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:32); [PSA 18:31](https://ref.ly/Ps18:31), [PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1), [PSA 62:6–62:7](https://ref.ly/Ps62:6-Ps62:7); [JHN 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:29); [ROM 8:31–8:32](https://ref.ly/Rom8:31-Rom8:32), [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39); [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7)

Pictures to describe God as a refuge
------------------------------------

### A fortress

**[PSA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Ps46:7)** *See also* [PSA 28:8](https://ref.ly/Ps28:8), [PSA 48:3](https://ref.ly/Ps48:3), [PSA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16), [PSA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16), [PSA 62:2](https://ref.ly/Ps62:2), [PSA 71:3](https://ref.ly/Ps71:3), [PSA 91:2](https://ref.ly/Ps91:2), [PSA 144:1–144:3](https://ref.ly/Ps144:1-Ps144:3)

### A rock

**[2SA 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2-2Sam22:3)** parallel [PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2) *See also* [GEN 49:24](https://ref.ly/Gen49:24); [DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4), [DEU 32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15); [2SA 22:47](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:47); [PSA 31:2](https://ref.ly/Ps31:2), [PSA 94:22](https://ref.ly/Ps94:22); [ISA 26:4](https://ref.ly/Isa26:4); [HAB 1:12](https://ref.ly/Hab1:12)

### A shade

**[PSA 91:1](https://ref.ly/Ps91:1)** *See also* [PSA 121:5](https://ref.ly/Ps121:5); [SNG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Song2:3); [ISA 25:4–25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4-Isa25:5)

### Sheltering wings

**[PSA 57:1](https://ref.ly/Ps57:1)** *See also* [DEU 32:10–32:11](https://ref.ly/Deut32:10-Deut32:11); [RUT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:12); [PSA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8), [PSA 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ps36:7); [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37)

### A shield

**[GEN 15:1](https://ref.ly/Gen15:1)** *See also* [DEU 33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:29); [2SA 22:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:31); [PSA 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ps3:3), [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 84:11](https://ref.ly/Ps84:11); [PRO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Prov30:5); [EPH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Eph6:16)

### A tower

**[PRO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Prov18:10)** *See also* [PSA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Ps61:3)

Cities of refuge
----------------

**[NUM 35:11](https://ref.ly/Num35:11)** *See also* [DEU 4:41–4:43](https://ref.ly/Deut4:41-Deut4:43), [DEU 19:1–19:13](https://ref.ly/Deut19:1-Deut19:13); [JOS 20:1–20:9](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:9)

## 5491: refugees (id: 5491)

Fugitives driven from home because of personal, political or religious persecution, or because of physical danger.

Examples of refugees
--------------------

[GEN 16:6–16:8](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6-Gen16:8) *Hagar*; ; [GEN 46:1](https://ref.ly/Gen46:1); ; [EXO 12:37–12:39](https://ref.ly/Exod12:37-Exod12:39); ; [RUT 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:1); ; [MAT 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13-Matt2:15) *Mary, Joseph and Jesus Christ*; ; [ACT 18:2](https://ref.ly/Acts18:2)

Refugees are to be treated kindly
---------------------------------

[DEU 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Deut23:15-Deut23:16); ; [MAT 25:35](https://ref.ly/Matt25:35); ; [PHM 1:10–1:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:10-Phlm1:16)

Refugees from legal accusation are to be provided for
-----------------------------------------------------

[NUM 35:6](https://ref.ly/Num35:6); ; [JOS 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:6)

The LORD is a place of spiritual refuge
---------------------------------------

[DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27); ; [2SA 22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:3); ; [PSA 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ps9:9), [PSA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16); ; [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4); ; [JER 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jer16:19); ; [NAM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Nah1:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 16:6-8; Genesis 46:1; Exodus 12:37-39; Ruth 1:1; Matthew 2:13-15; Acts 18:2; Deuteronomy 23:15-16; Matthew 25:35; Philemon 1:10-16; Numbers 35:6; Joshua 20:1-6; Deuteronomy 33:27; 2 Samuel 22:3; Psalms 9:9; Psalms 59:16; Isaiah 25:4; Jeremiah 16:19; Nahum 1:7
* **Associated Articles:** 1320: God, as Saviour (ID: `1320@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5319: fugitives (ID: `5319@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5446: poverty (ID: `5446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5480: protection (ID: `5480@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5490: refuge (ID: `5490@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5590: travel (ID: `5590@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5689: friendlessness (ID: `5689@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6687: mercy, nature of God's (ID: `6687@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7338: cities of refuge (ID: `7338@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5492: restitution (id: 5492)

The return of something lost or stolen so that the original situation is restored. This central theme of the OT law is supremely fulfilled through Jesus Christ making restitution for Adam's sin, thus restoring fellowship with God and hope of eternal life.

Reasons for restitution
-----------------------

### For crimes committed

**[EXO 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Exod22:2-Exod22:3)** *See also* [EXO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Exod22:7), [EXO 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16-Exod22:17); [LEV 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:6), [LEV 24:17–24:21](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17-Lev24:21); [NUM 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/Num5:6-Num5:7); [EZK 33:14–33:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:14-Ezek33:15)

### For accidental loss

**[EXO 22:5–22:6](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5-Exod22:6)** *See also* [EXO 21:33–21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:33-Exod21:34), [EXO 22:10–22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:10-Exod22:15)

### For unintentional sin

[LEV 5:14–5:16](https://ref.ly/Lev5:14-Lev5:16)

### As part of confession

[NUM 5:5–5:7](https://ref.ly/Num5:5-Num5:7)

### In cases of misplaced accusation

[PSA 69:4](https://ref.ly/Ps69:4)

The basis of decisions regarding restitution
--------------------------------------------

### Arbitration

[EXO 22:8–22:9](https://ref.ly/Exod22:8-Exod22:9)

### The amount of restitution is to exceed the amount stolen

**[EXO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Exod22:1)** *See also* [EXO 22:4](https://ref.ly/Exod22:4); [LEV 6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:5); [2SA 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:5-2Sam12:6); [PRO 6:31](https://ref.ly/Prov6:31)

### Land should belong to its rightful owners

[2SA 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:7)

Examples of restitution
-----------------------

[1SA 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:17) *The Philistines make restitution for desecrating the ark of God*; ; [1KI 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:34) *Ben\-Hadad makes restitution for the cities his father had taken from Israel*; ; [2KI 8:1–8:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:1-2Kgs8:6) *The king makes restitution to the Shunammite by returning her house and land*; ; [NEH 5:10–5:12](https://ref.ly/Neh5:10-Neh5:12) *Nehemiah orders restitution to be made to the poor*; ; [LUK 19:8–19:9](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8-Luke19:9) *Zacchaeus makes restitution to those he had cheated.*

The act of restitution is to be motivated by love
-------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:8–13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8-Rom13:10)** *See also* [MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24), [MAT 5:44](https://ref.ly/Matt5:44); [COL 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Col3:13-Col3:14)

God promises physical restitution
---------------------------------

[ISA 57:18](https://ref.ly/Isa57:18), [ISA 61:3–61:4](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3-Isa61:4); ; [JER 30:17–30:18](https://ref.ly/Jer30:17-Jer30:18)

Jesus Christ makes restitution for sin
--------------------------------------

### He restores fellowship with God

**[ROM 5:19](https://ref.ly/Rom5:19)** *See also* [2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21); [HEB 9:28](https://ref.ly/Heb9:28)

### He restores the hope of eternal life

**[1CO 15:21–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:21-1Cor15:22)** *See also* [1CO 15:45–15:49](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:45-1Cor15:49)

Restitution is made to Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------

**[PHP 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Phil2:8-Phil2:9)** *Here God's exaltation of Jesus Christ is seen as restitution to his position of glory.* *See also* [ISA 53:10–53:12](https://ref.ly/Isa53:10-Isa53:12)

Restitution is made to those who make sacrifices for the gospel
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21)** parallels [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21), [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22) *See also* [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29) parallels [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30), [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30); [MRK 8:35](https://ref.ly/Mark8:35) parallels [MAT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Matt16:25), [LUK 9:24](https://ref.ly/Luke9:24); [JHN 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/John12:24-John12:25); [PHP 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:8-Phil3:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 22:2-3; Exodus 22:7; Exodus 22:16-17; Leviticus 6:1-6; Leviticus 24:17-21; Numbers 5:6-7; Ezekiel 33:14-15; Exodus 22:5-6; Exodus 21:33-34; Exodus 22:10-15; Leviticus 5:14-16; Numbers 5:5-7; Psalms 69:4; Exodus 22:8-9; Exodus 22:1; Exodus 22:4; Leviticus 6:5; 2 Samuel 12:5-6; Proverbs 6:31; 2 Samuel 9:7; 1 Samuel 6:17; 1 Kings 20:34; 2 Kings 8:1-6; Nehemiah 5:10-12; Luke 19:8-9; Romans 13:8-10; Matthew 5:23-24; Matthew 5:44; Colossians 3:13-14; Isaiah 57:18; Isaiah 61:3-4; Jeremiah 30:17-18; Romans 5:19; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Hebrews 9:28; 1 Corinthians 15:21-22; 1 Corinthians 15:45-49; Philippians 2:8-9; Isaiah 53:10-12; Matthew 19:21; Mark 10:21; Luke 18:22; Matthew 19:29; Mark 10:29-30; Luke 18:29-30; Mark 8:35; Matthew 16:25; Luke 9:24; John 12:24-25; Philippians 3:8-10

## 5493: retribution (id: 5493)

Recompense for evil. In Scripture this is the prerogative of God and of the authorities ordained by God, and never of individuals.

The principle of retribution
----------------------------

**[EXO 20:5–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5-Exod20:6)**; **[ROM 12:19](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19)**; **[HEB 10:30](https://ref.ly/Heb10:30)** *See also* [DEU 32:35](https://ref.ly/Deut32:35); [EXO 32:33–32:34](https://ref.ly/Exod32:33-Exod32:34); [LEV 26:23–26:25](https://ref.ly/Lev26:23-Lev26:25); [DEU 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:9-Deut7:10), [DEU 32:41–32:43](https://ref.ly/Deut32:41-Deut32:43); [JOB 34:11](https://ref.ly/Job34:11); [PRO 24:12](https://ref.ly/Prov24:12); [ISA 1:24](https://ref.ly/Isa1:24); [NAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Nah1:2)

Divine retribution is just
--------------------------

**[GEN 18:25](https://ref.ly/Gen18:25)** *See also* [DEU 32:3–32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:3-Deut32:4); [2CH 12:1–12:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:1-2Chr12:6); [PSA 58:10–58:11](https://ref.ly/Ps58:10-Ps58:11); [JER 5:7–5:9](https://ref.ly/Jer5:7-Jer5:9), [JER 5:26–5:29](https://ref.ly/Jer5:26-Jer5:29); [HOS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Hos4:6)

Retribution is often built into the nature of wrongdoing
--------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 7:15–7:16](https://ref.ly/Ps7:15-Ps7:16)** *See also* [PSA 37:14–37:15](https://ref.ly/Ps37:14-Ps37:15); [PRO 1:18](https://ref.ly/Prov1:18), [PRO 26:27](https://ref.ly/Prov26:27), [PRO 28:10](https://ref.ly/Prov28:10); [ECC 10:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:8); [MAT 26:52](https://ref.ly/Matt26:52); [GAL 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Gal6:7-Gal6:8)

Retribution is placed in the hands of governments
-------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 9:6](https://ref.ly/Gen9:6)**; **[EXO 21:23–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:23-Exod21:25)** *This “lex talionis” (law of exacting like for like) was not for private retribution. It insisted that justice be fair and not out of proportion to the crime*; **[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [LEV 24:17–24:22](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17-Lev24:22); [NUM 35:16–35:25](https://ref.ly/Num35:16-Num35:25); [1PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:14)

Private retribution is forbidden
--------------------------------

**[PRO 24:29](https://ref.ly/Prov24:29)** *See also* [MAT 5:38–5:39](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38-Matt5:39); [ROM 12:19](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19)

Prayer for retribution
----------------------

**[PSA 28:4](https://ref.ly/Ps28:4)** *Christians can agree with these prayers, hating all that opposes the Lord and goodness, and gladly anticipating the ultimate vindication of God's glory. Christians leave judgment in God's hands, but nonetheless grieve over the world's pain and over the death of every unrepentant sinner*; **[2TH 1:6–1:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:6-2Thess1:10)** *See also* [PSA 79:10](https://ref.ly/Ps79:10), [PSA 94:1–94:2](https://ref.ly/Ps94:1-Ps94:2), [PSA 137:8–137:9](https://ref.ly/Ps137:8-Ps137:9); [LAM 3:64–3:66](https://ref.ly/Lam3:64-Lam3:66)

The final retribution
---------------------

**[REV 20:13](https://ref.ly/Rev20:13)** *See also* [PSA 62:11–62:12](https://ref.ly/Ps62:11-Ps62:12); [JHN 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/John5:28-John5:29); [ROM 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5-Rom2:11)

Examples of direct divine retribution on people
-----------------------------------------------

### In the fall

**[GEN 3:14](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14), [GEN 3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17)** *See also* [GEN 3:15–3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15-Gen3:16), [GEN 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:18-Gen3:19)

### In the flood

**[GEN 6:5–6:7](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5-Gen6:7)**

### At the Tower of Babel

*See also* [GEN 11:5–11:7](https://ref.ly/Gen11:5-Gen11:7)

### On Sodom and Gomorrah

[GEN 19:24](https://ref.ly/Gen19:24)

### In the ten plagues on Egypt

[EXO 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Exod7:16-Exod7:17), [EXO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Exod11:1), [EXO 12:29](https://ref.ly/Exod12:29)

### On the Israelites in the wilderness

**[EXO 32:35](https://ref.ly/Exod32:35)** *See also* [1CO 10:6–10:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:6-1Cor10:10)

### On the heathen nations

[LEV 18:25](https://ref.ly/Lev18:25); ; [DEU 9:4](https://ref.ly/Deut9:4)

### On the rebellious nation of Israel

[LEV 18:28](https://ref.ly/Lev18:28); ; [DEU 28:47–28:48](https://ref.ly/Deut28:47-Deut28:48)

### On idolatrous Judah

[ISA 65:6–65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:6-Isa65:7), [ISA 66:6](https://ref.ly/Isa66:6); ; [EZK 5:11–5:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:11-Ezek5:13)

### On Babylon

[JER 51:6](https://ref.ly/Jer51:6), [JER 51:24](https://ref.ly/Jer51:24), [JER 51:56](https://ref.ly/Jer51:56)

### Upon all the nations

**[OBA 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Obad1:15-Obad1:16)**

### On Jerusalem

**[MAT 23:35–23:36](https://ref.ly/Matt23:35-Matt23:36)** *This judgment came on Jerusalem in A.D. 70\.*

### Upon persecutors and unbelievers

**[1TH 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:14-1Thess2:16)**

Examples of divine retribution on individuals
---------------------------------------------

**[ACT 12:23](https://ref.ly/Acts12:23)** *See also* [NUM 16:30](https://ref.ly/Num16:30); [2SA 6:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:7), [2SA 12:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:10); [JER 23:2](https://ref.ly/Jer23:2); [ACT 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Acts5:3-Acts5:5), [ACT 5:9](https://ref.ly/Acts5:9); [1CO 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:29-1Cor11:30)

Examples of divine retribution through human beings
---------------------------------------------------

[JDG 1:7](https://ref.ly/Judg1:7), [JDG 9:56–9:57](https://ref.ly/Judg9:56-Judg9:57); ; [2SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:17); [2KI 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:7), [2KI 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:24), [2KI 9:30–9:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:30-2Kgs9:33)

Natural retribution
-------------------

**[ROM 1:27](https://ref.ly/Rom1:27)**; **[2TI 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:13)**

Examples of retribution by the state
------------------------------------

*See also* [JOS 7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25); [1KI 2:31–2:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:31-1Kgs2:32); [EST 7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:10); [LUK 23:40–23:41](https://ref.ly/Luke23:40-Luke23:41)

National retribution
--------------------

[JOS 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/Josh10:13-Josh10:14); ; [JDG 11:36](https://ref.ly/Judg11:36); ; [EST 9:5](https://ref.ly/Esth9:5) *The special case of God's dealings with the nation of Israel and her enemies is not a basis on which to argue for a concept of a war, just or otherwise. A just war has to be in defence of the nation or of the defenceless, and involve a minimum of suffering.*

Warnings of future retribution
------------------------------

**[ACT 17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:31)** *See also* [MAT 25:31–25:32](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31-Matt25:32)

## 5494: revenge (id: 5494)

The returning of injury or insult. Scripture insists that human revenge is unacceptable and is to be left in the hands of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 5495: revenge, and retaliation (ID: `5495@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5496: revenge, examples of (ID: `5496@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5495: revenge, and retaliation (id: 5495)

Scripture teaches that it is wrong to strike back or to return evil for evil, whether by immediate retaliation or by taking revenge later. Justice must be done, but this must be left in the hands of God, or of the authorities ordained by God.

OT teaching about revenge
-------------------------

### Revenge forbidden in the OT

**[GEN 4:15](https://ref.ly/Gen4:15)**; **[LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18)**; **[DEU 32:35](https://ref.ly/Deut32:35)**; **[PRO 20:22](https://ref.ly/Prov20:22)** *See also* [DEU 19:5–19:7](https://ref.ly/Deut19:5-Deut19:7), [DEU 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:13), [DEU 23:7](https://ref.ly/Deut23:7); [PRO 24:29](https://ref.ly/Prov24:29); [JER 29:7](https://ref.ly/Jer29:7)

### The \`\`lex talionis'' (law of exacting like for like)

**[EXO 21:23–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:23-Exod21:25)** *This was not a rule for personal revenge but to ensure that judicial punishments were neither unjust nor malicious.* *See also* [LEV 24:17–24:21](https://ref.ly/Lev24:17-Lev24:21); [DEU 19:18–19:21](https://ref.ly/Deut19:18-Deut19:21)

Personal revenge forbidden in the NT
------------------------------------

### By Jesus Christ

**[MAT 5:38–5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38-Matt5:45)** *See also* [LUK 6:27–6:36](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27-Luke6:36)

### By the apostles

**[ROM 12:17](https://ref.ly/Rom12:17)**; **[1TH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:15)**; **[1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9)** *See also* [1CO 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4-1Cor13:5)

The evil effects of revenge illustrated in the life of Samson
-------------------------------------------------------------

[JDG 15:1–15:8](https://ref.ly/Judg15:1-Judg15:8), [JDG 15:9–15:10](https://ref.ly/Judg15:9-Judg15:10), [JDG 15:11–15:12](https://ref.ly/Judg15:11-Judg15:12)

Alternatives to revenge
-----------------------

### Avoiding the problem

[ROM 12:18](https://ref.ly/Rom12:18); ; [1PE 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:8)

### Evading the attack

[MAT 10:23](https://ref.ly/Matt10:23); ; [LUK 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Luke4:29-Luke4:30)

### Accepting the injury

[1CO 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:7); ; [HEB 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32-Heb10:34)

### Using the law

[ACT 22:25](https://ref.ly/Acts22:25); ; [1CO 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1-1Cor6:6) *going to law*

### Forgiving and setting an example

[LUK 23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:34); ; [ACT 7:59–7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:59-Acts7:60); [ROM 12:19–12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19-Rom12:21); ; [PRO 25:21–25:22](https://ref.ly/Prov25:21-Prov25:22)

God will avenge his people
--------------------------

**[LUK 18:7–18:8](https://ref.ly/Luke18:7-Luke18:8)** *See also* [DEU 32:43](https://ref.ly/Deut32:43); [1SA 24:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:12); [2KI 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:7); [PSA 18:47](https://ref.ly/Ps18:47); [REV 6:10](https://ref.ly/Rev6:10), [REV 19:2](https://ref.ly/Rev19:2)

## 5496: revenge, examples of (id: 5496)

Ungodly people are often vengeful, and even Christians are not always exemplary in this respect. However, each case should be considered on its merits, and the claims of justice taken into account.

Revenge among the ungodly
-------------------------

**[LUK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:11)** parallels [MAT 12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14), [MRK 3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:6) *See also* [GEN 4:24](https://ref.ly/Gen4:24) *Compare Lamech's words with those of Jesus Christ in [Mt 18:21–22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:22)*; [1KI 19:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:2) *Jezebel*; [1KI 22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27) *Ahab*; [EST 3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:6) *Haman*; [EZK 25:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:15) *the Philistines*[MRK 6:19](https://ref.ly/Mark6:19), [MRK 6:24](https://ref.ly/Mark6:24)[ACT 5:33](https://ref.ly/Acts5:33) *the Sanhedrin*; [ACT 23:12](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12) *unbelieving Jews*

Revenge among God's people
--------------------------

**[JDG 8:4–8:9](https://ref.ly/Judg8:4-Judg8:9)**; **[EST 9:1](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1), [EST 9:5](https://ref.ly/Esth9:5)** *See also* [GEN 16:5–16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:5-Gen16:6); [GAL 4:30](https://ref.ly/Gal4:30); [JDG 16:28–16:30](https://ref.ly/Judg16:28-Judg16:30) *Samson*; [1SA 25:10–25:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:10-1Sam25:13); [2SA 10:4–10:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4-2Sam10:7) *David did not retaliate at once, but only when a military threat appeared*; [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27) *Joab*; [LUK 9:51–9:56](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51-Luke9:56) *James and John*; [JHN 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/John18:10-John18:11) *Peter*; [ACT 23:2–23:5](https://ref.ly/Acts23:2-Acts23:5) *Paul*

Examples of non\-retaliation
----------------------------

### By Jesus Christ

**[1PE 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:23)** *See also* [ISA 50:6–50:7](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6-Isa50:7), [ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7); [LUK 23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:34)

### By David

[1SA 24:1–24:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1-1Sam24:12); ; [PSA 35:11–35:14](https://ref.ly/Ps35:11-Ps35:14), [PSA 35:17](https://ref.ly/Ps35:17), [PSA 38:12–38:15](https://ref.ly/Ps38:12-Ps38:15)

### By others

[GEN 50:15–50:21](https://ref.ly/Gen50:15-Gen50:21) *Joseph*; ; [1SA 12:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:23) *Samuel*; ; [1KI 22:24–22:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:24-1Kgs22:25) *Micaiah*; ; [JOB 31:29](https://ref.ly/Job31:29) *Job*; ; [ACT 7:59–7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:59-Acts7:60) *Stephen*

---------------------------------

## 5498: reward (id: 5498)

Recompense in recognition of services rendered. Scripture stresses that while salvation is a gift of God, believers will be rewarded according to their deeds. Believers should be honest in rewarding others for their work.

* **Associated Articles:** 5499: reward, divine (ID: `5499@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5500: reward, for God's people (ID: `5500@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5501: reward, human (ID: `5501@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5499: reward, divine (id: 5499)

God's dealings with people are always in harmony with his grace and his justice, hating evil and loving good. No gift of God is on the basis of merit, but on the basis of his faithfulness to his promises.

God's reward is in harmony with his character
---------------------------------------------

### His graciousness

**[ROM 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Rom4:4-Rom4:5)** *See also* [PSA 103:10](https://ref.ly/Ps103:10); [MAT 20:14–20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:14-Matt20:15); [LUK 17:7–17:10](https://ref.ly/Luke17:7-Luke17:10); [ROM 11:6–11:7](https://ref.ly/Rom11:6-Rom11:7); [EPH 2:8–2:10](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:10)

### His justice

**[ROM 2:6–2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:6-Rom2:11)** *See also* [DEU 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Deut28:1-Deut28:2) *blessings for obedience*; [DEU 28:15](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15) *curses for disobedience*; [PSA 19:9–19:11](https://ref.ly/Ps19:9-Ps19:11), [PSA 62:12](https://ref.ly/Ps62:12); [PRO 13:21](https://ref.ly/Prov13:21), [PRO 14:14](https://ref.ly/Prov14:14); [JER 17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:10), [JER 32:19](https://ref.ly/Jer32:19); [1CO 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:8); [2CO 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6); [GAL 6:7–6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:7-Gal6:9); [EPH 6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:8); [COL 3:25](https://ref.ly/Col3:25)

The reward of the ungodly
-------------------------

**[ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)** *See also* [EXO 20:5](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5); [DEU 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:9-Deut7:10); [PRO 22:22–22:23](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22-Prov22:23); [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11), [ISA 26:21](https://ref.ly/Isa26:21), [ISA 59:18](https://ref.ly/Isa59:18), [ISA 65:6–65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:6-Isa65:7), [ISA 66:6](https://ref.ly/Isa66:6); [EZK 7:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:4), [EZK 9:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:10), [EZK 11:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:21); [HOS 9:7](https://ref.ly/Hos9:7); [ZEP 1:12](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:12); [2TH 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:6); [HEB 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Heb2:1-Heb2:2)

## 5500: reward, for God's people (id: 5500)

God gives blessings to his people who serve him faithfully and who suffer for him. God himself is his people's greatest reward. The fulness will be theirs at the coming of Jesus Christ.

God promises to reward his people
---------------------------------

### For faithful service

**[1CO 3:12–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:12-1Cor3:15)**; **[COL 3:23–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:23-Col3:24)** *See also* [EXO 32:28–32:29](https://ref.ly/Exod32:28-Exod32:29); [NUM 14:30](https://ref.ly/Num14:30), [NUM 25:10–25:13](https://ref.ly/Num25:10-Num25:13); [ISA 40:10](https://ref.ly/Isa40:10), [ISA 62:11](https://ref.ly/Isa62:11); [DAN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Dan12:3); [MAT 10:41–10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:41-Matt10:42), [MAT 25:28–25:29](https://ref.ly/Matt25:28-Matt25:29); [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41)

### In and through self\-denial

**[MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29)** parallel [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30) *See also* [MAT 16:24–16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:24-Matt16:26), [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21); [LUK 18:28–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:28-Luke18:30); [1CO 9:26–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:26-1Cor9:27); [HEB 10:33–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:33-Heb10:34), [HEB 11:36–11:40](https://ref.ly/Heb11:36-Heb11:40)

### At the coming of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 19:27–19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:27-Matt19:28)** *See also* [MAT 16:27](https://ref.ly/Matt16:27), [MAT 25:31–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31-Matt25:36); [LUK 14:12–14:14](https://ref.ly/Luke14:12-Luke14:14); [2TI 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:7-2Tim4:8); [1PE 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:4); [REV 22:12](https://ref.ly/Rev22:12)

God himself is his people's greatest reward
-------------------------------------------

**[GEN 15:1](https://ref.ly/Gen15:1)** *See also* [DEU 32:9](https://ref.ly/Deut32:9); [PSA 73:26](https://ref.ly/Ps73:26); [REV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Rev21:3)

**[PHP 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Phil3:12-Phil3:14)** *See also* [HEB 10:35](https://ref.ly/Heb10:35), [HEB 11:6](https://ref.ly/Heb11:6), [HEB 11:24–11:26](https://ref.ly/Heb11:24-Heb11:26); [2JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/2John1:8); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

* **Associated Articles:** 1335: blessing (ID: `1335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2565: Jesus Christ, second coming of (ID: `2565@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5280: crown (ID: `5280@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6644: eternal life (ID: `6644@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6744: sanctification (ID: `6744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8110: athletics (ID: `8110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8117: discipleship, benefits of (ID: `8117@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8248: faithfulness (ID: `8248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8266: holiness (ID: `8266@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9310: resurrection (ID: `9310@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5501: reward, human (id: 5501)

Honest reward for services rendered is commended. Even though such reward is often deserved, it is not always given.

Reward for services rendered
----------------------------

### Payment for work done

**[COL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Col4:1)** *See also* [GEN 29:28–29:29](https://ref.ly/Gen29:28-Gen29:29); [2SA 19:32–19:36](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:32-2Sam19:36); [PRO 13:13](https://ref.ly/Prov13:13); [MAT 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:2), [MAT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:23); [ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Rom4:4); [1CO 9:24–9:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:25); [1TI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:18)

### Favour for help received

**[1SA 17:23–17:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:23-1Sam17:25)** *See also* [GEN 41:41–41:45](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41-Gen41:45); [JOS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Josh2:14); [EST 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1-Esth6:3), [EST 6:6–6:10](https://ref.ly/Esth6:6-Esth6:10); [DAN 2:46–2:49](https://ref.ly/Dan2:46-Dan2:49)

Unjust treatment and reward
---------------------------

**[ACT 10:37–10:39](https://ref.ly/Acts10:37-Acts10:39)** [GEN 29:22–29:25](https://ref.ly/Gen29:22-Gen29:25), [GEN 39:19–39:20](https://ref.ly/Gen39:19-Gen39:20), [GEN 40:23](https://ref.ly/Gen40:23); ; [NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4); ; [1SA 25:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:21); ; [2SA 15:1–15:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:4); ; [PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9), [PSA 55:12–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:12-Ps55:14); ; [JER 38:4–38:6](https://ref.ly/Jer38:4-Jer38:6); ; [JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/John13:18)

Dishonest reward forbidden
--------------------------

**[DEU 16:19](https://ref.ly/Deut16:19)** *In a fallen world there are many opportunities to gain by wrongdoing.* *See also* [NUM 22:7](https://ref.ly/Num22:7), [NUM 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Num22:16-Num22:17), [NUM 24:11](https://ref.ly/Num24:11); [1KI 13:7–13:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:7-1Kgs13:8); [2KI 5:20–5:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:20-2Kgs5:24); [JOB 17:5](https://ref.ly/Job17:5); [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23); [MIC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Mic3:11), [MIC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:3); [MAT 26:14–26:15](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:15) parallel [MRK 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10-Mark14:11); [ACT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Acts1:18)

Rewarding the undeserving
-------------------------

**[ROM 12:20](https://ref.ly/Rom12:20)** *See also* [GEN 47:11–47:12](https://ref.ly/Gen47:11-Gen47:12); [1SA 12:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:23), [1SA 24:16–24:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:16-1Sam24:19); [MAT 5:38–5:41](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38-Matt5:41), [MAT 5:44–5:47](https://ref.ly/Matt5:44-Matt5:47)

* **Associated Passages:** Colossians 4:1; Genesis 29:28-29; 2 Samuel 19:32-36; Proverbs 13:13; Matthew 20:1-2; Matthew 25:23; Romans 4:4; 1 Corinthians 9:24-25; 1 Timothy 5:17-18; 1 Samuel 17:23-25; Genesis 41:41-45; Joshua 2:14; Esther 6:1-3; Esther 6:6-10; Daniel 2:46-49; Acts 10:37-39; Genesis 29:22-25; Genesis 39:19-20; Genesis 40:23; Numbers 14:1-4; 1 Samuel 25:21; 2 Samuel 15:1-4; Psalms 41:9; Psalms 55:12-14; Jeremiah 38:4-6; John 13:18; Deuteronomy 16:19; Numbers 22:7; Numbers 22:16-17; Numbers 24:11; 1 Kings 13:7-8; 2 Kings 5:20-24; Job 17:5; Isaiah 1:23; Micah 3:11; Micah 7:3; Matthew 26:14-15; Mark 14:10-11; Acts 1:18; Romans 12:20; Genesis 47:11-12; 1 Samuel 12:23; 1 Samuel 24:16-19; Matthew 5:38-41; Matthew 5:44-47
