## 5503: rich, the (id: 5503)

It is hard but not impossible for rich people to enter God's kingdom. They must put their hope in God, not in their riches. Rich people who live selfishly, abuse their position and fail to repent and live for Jesus Christ, are in peril.

Parables concerning rich people
-------------------------------

[2SA 12:1–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:1-2Sam12:10); ; [MAT 13:44–13:46](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44-Matt13:46), [MAT 18:23](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23), [MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2) parallel [LUK 14:16](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16); ; [MAT 25:14](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14) parallel [LUK 19:12](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12); ; [LUK 12:16](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16), [LUK 15:11–15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:11-Luke15:13), [LUK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1), [LUK 18:10](https://ref.ly/Luke18:10)

Rich people have received blessing
----------------------------------

**[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3), [PSA 112:1–112:3](https://ref.ly/Ps112:1-Ps112:3)

### They have friends

[PRO 14:20](https://ref.ly/Prov14:20), [PRO 19:4](https://ref.ly/Prov19:4)

### They have power

[PRO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Prov22:7)

### They have security

[PRO 10:15](https://ref.ly/Prov10:15), [PRO 18:11](https://ref.ly/Prov18:11)

Warnings to rich people
-----------------------

### They should recognise that God is the giver of riches

**[DEU 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:17-Deut8:18)** *See also* [DEU 32:13–32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:13-Deut32:15); [HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8)

### They should not be arrogant or complacent on account of their riches

**[JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24)** *See also* [PSA 49:5–49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:5-Ps49:6); [JER 49:4](https://ref.ly/Jer49:4); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17); [REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)

### They should not trust in their riches

**[PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28)** *See also* [PSA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:6), [PSA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:7), [PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10); [JER 48:7](https://ref.ly/Jer48:7)

### They face disaster if they lack wisdom

**[PSA 49:20](https://ref.ly/Ps49:20)** *See also* [PRO 28:11](https://ref.ly/Prov28:11); [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24)

### They must live moral lives

**[PRO 28:6](https://ref.ly/Prov28:6)** *See also* [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16); [JER 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:27-Jer5:28), [JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11); [MIC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Mic6:12)

### Wealth can bring anxiety

**[ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12)** *See also* [MAT 6:25–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:34) parallel [LUK 12:22–12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:22-Luke12:31)

### Riches are of fleeting value

**[LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)** *See also* [PSA 49:5–49:12](https://ref.ly/Ps49:5-Ps49:12); [PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24); [ECC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:15); [ISA 10:3](https://ref.ly/Isa10:3); [MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19); [1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7); [JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21); [JAS 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:10-Jas1:11)

### Ultimate security is found in God alone

[EXO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:2); ; [2SA 22:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:33); ; [1CH 16:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:11); ; [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 29:11](https://ref.ly/Ps29:11), [PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1), [PSA 52:6–52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:6-Ps52:7); ; [ISA 12:2](https://ref.ly/Isa12:2); ; [HAB 3:19](https://ref.ly/Hab3:19)

### It is hard for rich people to enter God's kingdom

**[MAT 19:23–19:26](https://ref.ly/Matt19:23-Matt19:26)** parallels [MRK 10:23–10:27](https://ref.ly/Mark10:23-Mark10:27), [LUK 18:24–18:27](https://ref.ly/Luke18:24-Luke18:27) *See also* [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18-Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [MAT 19:16–19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16-Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:17–10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17-Mark10:22), [LUK 18:18–18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18-Luke18:23)

Rich people will be judged for their behaviour
----------------------------------------------

[ZEC 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/Zech11:5-Zech11:6); ; [LUK 1:53](https://ref.ly/Luke1:53)

### If they exploit, oppress or kill the poor

**[JAS 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:4)** *See also* [PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31), [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16), [PRO 28:3](https://ref.ly/Prov28:3); [EZK 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:12-Ezek18:13), [EZK 22:29–22:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29-Ezek22:31); [JAS 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jas2:6), [JAS 5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:6)

### If they hoard wealth and live selfishly

**[JAS 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jas5:5)** *See also* [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21), [LUK 16:19–16:31](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19-Luke16:31)

### If they slander the name of Jesus Christ

[JAS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Jas2:7)

Commands to rich people
-----------------------

### To seek God's kingdom as first priority

[MAT 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19-Matt6:21), [MAT 6:31–6:33](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31-Matt6:33) parallel [LUK 12:29–12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:29-Luke12:31); ; [1TI 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:19)

### To repent from sin

[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17), [REV 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rev3:19)

### To be generous towards the needy

**[1TI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:18)** *See also* [MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21) parallels [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21), [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22); [LUK 3:11](https://ref.ly/Luke3:11), [LUK 11:41](https://ref.ly/Luke11:41), [LUK 12:33–12:34](https://ref.ly/Luke12:33-Luke12:34), [LUK 14:12–14:14](https://ref.ly/Luke14:12-Luke14:14), [LUK 16:9](https://ref.ly/Luke16:9), [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8); [2CO 8:1–8:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:1-2Cor8:5), [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9) *Jesus Christ's example the motivation for generosity*; [2CO 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:13-2Cor8:15), [2CO 9:6–9:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6-2Cor9:15)

Incorrect attitudes to the rich
-------------------------------

[ECC 10:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:20) *cursing them*; ; [PSA 49:16–49:17](https://ref.ly/Ps49:16-Ps49:17) *living in awe of them*; ; [PSA 49:5–49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:5-Ps49:6) *fearing them*; [JOB 34:18–34:19](https://ref.ly/Job34:18-Job34:19); ; [PRO 22:2](https://ref.ly/Prov22:2); ; [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4)

God brings good news for rich people
------------------------------------

[PSA 22:29–22:31](https://ref.ly/Ps22:29-Ps22:31); ; [MAT 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Matt9:9-Matt9:10) parallels [MRK 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Mark2:14-Mark2:15), [LUK 5:27–5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:27-Luke5:29); ; [MAT 27:57](https://ref.ly/Matt27:57) parallels [MRK 15:43](https://ref.ly/Mark15:43), [LUK 23:50–23:51](https://ref.ly/Luke23:50-Luke23:51); ; [LUK 19:1–19:10](https://ref.ly/Luke19:1-Luke19:10)

Examples of rich people
-----------------------

Abraham: [GEN 12:10–12:16](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10-Gen12:16), [GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2); [GEN 26:1–26:14](https://ref.ly/Gen26:1-Gen26:14) *Isaac*; [GEN 27:19](https://ref.ly/Gen27:19), [GEN 27:28](https://ref.ly/Gen27:28), [GEN 30:29–30:43](https://ref.ly/Gen30:29-Gen30:43), [GEN 35:11–35:12](https://ref.ly/Gen35:11-Gen35:12); [GEN 15:14](https://ref.ly/Gen15:14); ; [EXO 3:22](https://ref.ly/Exod3:22), [EXO 12:36](https://ref.ly/Exod12:36); ; [PSA 105:37](https://ref.ly/Ps105:37); [1KI 10:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:23) parallel [2CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:22) *Solomon*; [2KI 20:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:13); ; [2CH 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:29) *Joseph of Arimathea*; ; [MAT 27:57–27:60](https://ref.ly/Matt27:57-Matt27:60) parallels [MRK 15:43–15:46](https://ref.ly/Mark15:43-Mark15:46), [LUK 23:50–23:53](https://ref.ly/Luke23:50-Luke23:53); ; [JHN 19:38–19:42](https://ref.ly/John19:38-John19:42)

Luke 18:18-23; Zechariah 11:5-6; Luke 1:53; James 5:1-4; Proverbs 14:31; Proverbs 28:3; Ezekiel 18:12-13; Ezekiel 22:29-31; James 2:6; James 5:6; James 5:5; Luke 16:19-31; James 2:7; Matthew 6:19-21; 2 Corinthians 8:9; 2 Corinthians 8:13-15; 2 Corinthians 9:6-15; Ecclesiastes 10:20; Psalms 49:16-17; Job 34:18-19; Proverbs 22:2; James 2:1-4; Psalms 22:29-31; Matthew 9:9-10; Mark 2:14-15; Luke 5:27-29; ## 5504: rights (id: 5504)

The basis of a claim to respect, and just and fair treatment. God has rights over his creation as a result of his sovereignty and authority. Through being the height of his creation, human beings also have rights, which are to be respected.

The rights that God gives
-------------------------

### God gives rights to the poor and needy

**[EXO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22)** *See also* [EXO 22:25–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:25-Exod22:27); [LEV 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:9-Lev19:10); [JOB 36:6](https://ref.ly/Job36:6)

### God gives rights to slaves

**[LEV 22:11](https://ref.ly/Lev22:11)** *See also* [EXO 21:2–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:2-Exod21:6) parallel [DEU 15:12–15:18](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:18); [EXO 21:7–21:11](https://ref.ly/Exod21:7-Exod21:11), [EXO 21:26–21:27](https://ref.ly/Exod21:26-Exod21:27)

### God gives rights to strangers

**[LEV 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33-Lev19:34)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21); [LEV 19:10](https://ref.ly/Lev19:10)

### God gives rights to those who commit manslaughter

**[NUM 35:9–35:12](https://ref.ly/Num35:9-Num35:12)** *See also* [DEU 4:41–4:42](https://ref.ly/Deut4:41-Deut4:42); [JOS 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:6)

### God gives rights in marriage

**[1CO 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:3-1Cor7:5)** *See also* [EXO 21:7–21:11](https://ref.ly/Exod21:7-Exod21:11); [DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21); [EPH 5:22–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:33)

### God gives rights to parents

**[COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20)** parallel [EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1) *See also* [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12) parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16); [1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4)

### God gives rights to children

**[HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16)** *See also* [GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34); [DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17); [1CH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1-1Chr5:2); [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)

### God gives rights to Christians

**[JHN 1:12](https://ref.ly/John1:12)** *See also* [GAL 3:26–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26-Gal4:7); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7), [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21), [REV 22:14](https://ref.ly/Rev22:14)

### God gives rights to leaders

**[1CO 9:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:18)** *See also* [1CO 9:1–9:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:1-1Cor9:17); [1TI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:18)

Why God gives rights
--------------------

### God gives rights to keep an orderly society

**[LEV 19:29](https://ref.ly/Lev19:29)** *See also* [DEU 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/Deut15:1-Deut15:6)

### God gives rights to protect people

**[DEU 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15)** *See also* [LEV 25:23–25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:23-Lev25:43), [LEV 25:47–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47-Lev25:55)

The defence of rights
---------------------

### God, the defender of rights

**[MIC 7:8–7:10](https://ref.ly/Mic7:8-Mic7:10)** *The implied speaker here is Israel.* *See also* [DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18); [PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5); [PRO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10-Prov23:11)

### Abuse of rights punished by God

**[ISA 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:4)** *See also* [JER 5:26–5:29](https://ref.ly/Jer5:26-Jer5:29); [AMO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:8)

### Leaders are to protect the rights of others

**[PSA 72:1–72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:1-Ps72:4)** *See also* [PRO 31:4–31:9](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:9); [ECC 5:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:8)

The rights which God has
------------------------

### God has a right to be worshipped

**[2KI 17:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:36)** *See also* [MAT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Matt4:10) parallel [LUK 4:8](https://ref.ly/Luke4:8); [DEU 6:13](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13); [JHN 4:24](https://ref.ly/John4:24)

### God has a right to be obeyed

**[DEU 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12-Deut10:13)** *See also* [DEU 26:16–26:18](https://ref.ly/Deut26:16-Deut26:18), [DEU 30:16](https://ref.ly/Deut30:16)

## 5505: roads (id: 5505)

Recognised routes for travellers, or streets in towns and cities. In biblical times travelling by road was recognised as dangerous.

Kinds of road
-------------

### In the open country

**[EXO 13:17–13:18](https://ref.ly/Exod13:17-Exod13:18)**; **[ACT 19:1](https://ref.ly/Acts19:1)** *See also* [GEN 16:7](https://ref.ly/Gen16:7); [NUM 20:17](https://ref.ly/Num20:17); [DEU 1:2](https://ref.ly/Deut1:2); [JOS 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/Josh10:10-Josh10:11); [JDG 21:19](https://ref.ly/Judg21:19); [1SA 17:52](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:52); [ISA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Isa15:5)

### In towns and cities

**[ACT 9:11](https://ref.ly/Acts9:11)** *See also* [EST 6:9](https://ref.ly/Esth6:9); [JER 37:21](https://ref.ly/Jer37:21)

Activities associated with roads and streets
--------------------------------------------

### Travelling

[JDG 5:10](https://ref.ly/Judg5:10); ; [2KI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23); ; [LUK 9:57](https://ref.ly/Luke9:57), [LUK 10:30–10:33](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30-Luke10:33); ; [ACT 8:36](https://ref.ly/Acts8:36)

### Talking

**[LUK 24:32](https://ref.ly/Luke24:32)** *See also* [DEU 6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:7); [MRK 9:33](https://ref.ly/Mark9:33); [LUK 10:4](https://ref.ly/Luke10:4), [LUK 13:26](https://ref.ly/Luke13:26)

### Waiting

[1SA 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:13); ; [2SA 15:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:2); ; [1KI 20:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:38)

### Helping others

[DEU 22:4](https://ref.ly/Deut22:4), [DEU 27:18](https://ref.ly/Deut27:18); ; [LUK 10:30–10:33](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30-Luke10:33)

### Leisure and play

**[ZEC 8:4–8:5](https://ref.ly/Zech8:4-Zech8:5)**

### Begging

**[LUK 18:35](https://ref.ly/Luke18:35)**

### Living on the streets

**[MAT 22:9–22:10](https://ref.ly/Matt22:9-Matt22:10)** parallel [LUK 14:21–14:23](https://ref.ly/Luke14:21-Luke14:23) *See also* [JOB 31:32](https://ref.ly/Job31:32)

### Prayer

[MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5)

### Idolatry

[2CH 28:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:24); ; [JER 44:17](https://ref.ly/Jer44:17); ; [EZK 16:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:25)

### Injustice and dishonesty

**[ISA 59:14](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14)** *See also* [JER 5:1](https://ref.ly/Jer5:1)

Dangers of the road
-------------------

**[EZR 8:31](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:31)** *See also* [1KI 13:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:24); [EZR 8:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:22); [ECC 12:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:5); [PRO 22:13](https://ref.ly/Prov22:13); [HOS 7:1](https://ref.ly/Hos7:1); [LUK 10:30–10:33](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30-Luke10:33)

Troubled times affecting roads and streets
------------------------------------------

**[PSA 55:11](https://ref.ly/Ps55:11)**; **[NAM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Nah2:4)** *See also* [JOS 2:19](https://ref.ly/Josh2:19); [JER 6:25](https://ref.ly/Jer6:25); [HOS 6:9](https://ref.ly/Hos6:9); [ISA 5:25](https://ref.ly/Isa5:25), [ISA 33:7–33:8](https://ref.ly/Isa33:7-Isa33:8), [ISA 51:20](https://ref.ly/Isa51:20); [AMO 5:16](https://ref.ly/Amos5:16)

### Roads and streets deserted

**[JDG 5:6](https://ref.ly/Judg5:6)** *See also* [JER 9:21](https://ref.ly/Jer9:21), [JER 33:10](https://ref.ly/Jer33:10); [LAM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Lam1:4); [ZEP 3:6](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:6)

### Escape

[JER 48:19](https://ref.ly/Jer48:19)

The road back from exile
------------------------

**[ISA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3)** *See also* [ISA 11:16](https://ref.ly/Isa11:16), [ISA 35:8](https://ref.ly/Isa35:8), [ISA 57:14](https://ref.ly/Isa57:14)

Roads used figuratively
-----------------------

**[MAT 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Matt7:13-Matt7:14)** *See also* [GEN 49:17](https://ref.ly/Gen49:17); [PRO 15:19](https://ref.ly/Prov15:19); [ISA 59:8](https://ref.ly/Isa59:8); [JER 3:2](https://ref.ly/Jer3:2), [JER 18:15](https://ref.ly/Jer18:15), [JER 31:21](https://ref.ly/Jer31:21); [LUK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Luke3:5); [REV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:2)

-------------------------------------

[LUK 24:13–24:32](https://ref.ly/Luke24:13-Luke24:32)

### The road to Damascus

## 5506: roof (id: 5506)

Most buildings in biblical times had flat roofs. With the hot climate, they had a wider range of uses than merely providing shelter for the building.

Uses of roofs
-------------

### Shelter erected on the roof to make an extra room

**[2KI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:10)** *See also* [2SA 16:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:22); [PRO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Prov21:9), [PRO 25:24](https://ref.ly/Prov25:24)

### Guests lodged on the roof

**[JOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Josh2:8)** *See also* [1SA 9:25–9:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:25-1Sam9:26)

### The roof used as a place for leisure and relaxation

**[2SA 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2)** *See also* [DAN 4:29](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29); [MAT 24:17](https://ref.ly/Matt24:17) parallels [MRK 13:15](https://ref.ly/Mark13:15), [LUK 17:31](https://ref.ly/Luke17:31)

### The roof used as a place of religious observance

**[NEH 8:16](https://ref.ly/Neh8:16)** *See also* [2KI 23:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:12); [JER 19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:13), [JER 32:29](https://ref.ly/Jer32:29); [ZEP 1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:5); [ACT 10:9](https://ref.ly/Acts10:9)

In cities, some roofs were open, public places
----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 15:3](https://ref.ly/Isa15:3)** *See also* [JDG 9:50–9:52](https://ref.ly/Judg9:50-Judg9:52); [2SA 18:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:24); [JER 48:38](https://ref.ly/Jer48:38); [MAT 10:26–10:27](https://ref.ly/Matt10:26-Matt10:27) parallel [LUK 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Luke12:2-Luke12:3)

Roofs were usually constructed with a thick layer of clay
---------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 2:4](https://ref.ly/Mark2:4)** parallel [LUK 5:19](https://ref.ly/Luke5:19)

Roof as a synonym for house
---------------------------

**[MAT 8:8](https://ref.ly/Matt8:8)** parallel [LUK 7:6](https://ref.ly/Luke7:6)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 4:10; 2 Samuel 16:22; Proverbs 21:9; Proverbs 25:24; Joshua 2:8; 1 Samuel 9:25-26; 2 Samuel 11:2; Daniel 4:29; Matthew 24:17; Mark 13:15; Luke 17:31; Nehemiah 8:16; 2 Kings 23:12; Jeremiah 19:13; Jeremiah 32:29; Zephaniah 1:5; Acts 10:9; Isaiah 15:3; Judges 9:50-52; 2 Samuel 18:24; Jeremiah 48:38; Matthew 10:26-27; Luke 12:2-3; Mark 2:4; Luke 5:19; Matthew 8:8; Luke 7:6

## 5507: rope and cord (id: 5507)

Items normally used in domestic and military situations, but also used in Scripture in a symbolic sense to refer to bondage or constraint.

Various uses for rope and cord
------------------------------

### In clothes and furnishing

[EXO 28:28](https://ref.ly/Exod28:28), [EXO 28:37](https://ref.ly/Exod28:37); ; [NUM 15:38](https://ref.ly/Num15:38); ; [1KI 20:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:31); ; [EST 1:6](https://ref.ly/Esth1:6); ; [EZK 27:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:24)

### For tents

[EXO 35:18](https://ref.ly/Exod35:18), [EXO 39:40](https://ref.ly/Exod39:40); ; [NUM 3:37](https://ref.ly/Num3:37), [NUM 4:32](https://ref.ly/Num4:32); ; [ISA 54:2](https://ref.ly/Isa54:2)

### In sailing

[ACT 27:17](https://ref.ly/Acts27:17), [ACT 27:32](https://ref.ly/Acts27:32), [ACT 27:40](https://ref.ly/Acts27:40)

### In military use

[2SA 17:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:13); [PSA 7:12](https://ref.ly/Ps7:12), [PSA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Ps11:2); ; [JER 51:3](https://ref.ly/Jer51:3)

### For rescue and escape

[JOS 2:15](https://ref.ly/Josh2:15); ; [1SA 19:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:12); ; [JER 38:6](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6), [JER 38:13](https://ref.ly/Jer38:13); ; [ACT 9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:25); ; [2CO 11:33](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:33)

### To restrain animals

[JOB 39:5](https://ref.ly/Job39:5), [JOB 41:1–41:2](https://ref.ly/Job41:1-Job41:2); ; [PSA 32:9](https://ref.ly/Ps32:9); ; [MAT 21:2](https://ref.ly/Matt21:2) parallels [MRK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Mark11:2), [LUK 19:30](https://ref.ly/Luke19:30)

### To restrain people

[JDG 15:13–15:14](https://ref.ly/Judg15:13-Judg15:14); ; [EZK 3:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:25), [EZK 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:8); ; [MAT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3) parallel [MRK 6:17](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17)

Rope and cord as symbols of bondage
-----------------------------------

### Of evil

**[PRO 5:22](https://ref.ly/Prov5:22)** *See also* [PSA 129:4](https://ref.ly/Ps129:4), [PSA 140:5](https://ref.ly/Ps140:5); [ISA 5:18](https://ref.ly/Isa5:18), [ISA 58:6](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6); [ROM 7:6](https://ref.ly/Rom7:6)

### Of suffering

**[LUK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Luke13:16)** *See also* [JOB 16:8](https://ref.ly/Job16:8), [JOB 36:8](https://ref.ly/Job36:8); [PSA 119:61](https://ref.ly/Ps119:61); [DAN 3:21](https://ref.ly/Dan3:21); [MAT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3) parallel [MRK 6:17](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17); [MRK 15:1](https://ref.ly/Mark15:1); [JHN 18:12](https://ref.ly/John18:12), [JHN 18:24](https://ref.ly/John18:24)

### Of death

**[PSA 18:4–18:5](https://ref.ly/Ps18:4-Ps18:5)** parallel [2SA 22:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:6) *See also* [PSA 116:3](https://ref.ly/Ps116:3)

Rope and cord as symbols of spiritual and emotional ties
--------------------------------------------------------

### Symbols of loving relationships

**[HOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:4)** *See also* [ECC 4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:12); [JER 10:20](https://ref.ly/Jer10:20); [ROM 7:2](https://ref.ly/Rom7:2)

### Symbols of obedience

**[PRO 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Prov7:2-Prov7:3)** *See also* [DEU 6:8](https://ref.ly/Deut6:8), [DEU 11:18](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18); [PRO 3:3](https://ref.ly/Prov3:3), [PRO 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:21)

### Symbols of life

**[ECC 12:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:6)** *See also* [PSA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Ps16:6)

## 5508: ruins (id: 5508)

The remains of a city, town or building after destruction or neglect. In Scripture it mainly describes the aftermath of divine judgment.

Israel's reduction to ruins
---------------------------

### As a covenant curse for disobeying God

**[LEV 26:27–26:33](https://ref.ly/Lev26:27-Lev26:33)** *See also* [DEU 13:16](https://ref.ly/Deut13:16), [DEU 28:49–28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49-Deut28:52)

### The ruin prophesied

**[JER 9:11](https://ref.ly/Jer9:11)**; **[MIC 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Mic1:6-Mic1:7)** *See also* [ISA 5:5–5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:5-Isa5:7), [ISA 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/Isa6:11-Isa6:13); [JER 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jer4:7), [JER 4:20](https://ref.ly/Jer4:20), [JER 4:23–4:26](https://ref.ly/Jer4:23-Jer4:26), [JER 22:5](https://ref.ly/Jer22:5); [EZK 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:6-Ezek6:7), [EZK 6:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:14), [EZK 21:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:27); [AMO 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Amos3:13-Amos3:15), [AMO 7:9](https://ref.ly/Amos7:9)

### The ruin described

[2KI 25:8–25:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:8-2Kgs25:15) parallels [2CH 36:17–36:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:17-2Chr36:19), [JER 39:8–39:10](https://ref.ly/Jer39:8-Jer39:10), [JER 52:12–52:19](https://ref.ly/Jer52:12-Jer52:19)

### The ruin lamented

**[PSA 89:39–89:41](https://ref.ly/Ps89:39-Ps89:41)** *See also* [PSA 74:3](https://ref.ly/Ps74:3), [PSA 80:12–80:16](https://ref.ly/Ps80:12-Ps80:16); [ISA 64:8–64:12](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8-Isa64:12); [LAM 2:5–2:9](https://ref.ly/Lam2:5-Lam2:9)

### The ruin explained

**[JER 44:2–44:6](https://ref.ly/Jer44:2-Jer44:6)** *See also* [EZK 33:23–33:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:23-Ezek33:29)

Restoration of Israel's ruins
-----------------------------

### Restoration of the ruins prophesied

**[ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3), [ISA 61:4](https://ref.ly/Isa61:4)** *See also* [ISA 44:24–44:26](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24-Isa44:26), [ISA 58:12](https://ref.ly/Isa58:12); [JER 30:18–30:19](https://ref.ly/Jer30:18-Jer30:19), [JER 31:27–31:28](https://ref.ly/Jer31:27-Jer31:28); [EZK 36:8–36:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:8-Ezek36:12), [EZK 36:33–36:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:33-Ezek36:36); [AMO 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/Amos9:11-Amos9:12)

### Restoration of the ruins fulfilled at the return from exile

[EZR 3:1–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:1-Ezra3:13); ; [NEH 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3-Neh1:4), [NEH 2:11–2:20](https://ref.ly/Neh2:11-Neh2:20); ; [HAG 1:1–1:15](https://ref.ly/Hag1:1-Hag1:15)

Ruins through God's judgment on Israel's enemies
------------------------------------------------

### Assyria

Nineveh was the capital of Assyria: [NAM 3:7](https://ref.ly/Nah3:7); ; [ZEP 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:13-Zeph2:15)

### Babylon

[EZK 35:1–35:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek35:1-Ezek35:4), [EZK 35:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek35:9); ; [MAL 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Mal1:2-Mal1:4)

Towns and sites ruined
----------------------

### In warfare

[JOS 8:24–8:29](https://ref.ly/Josh8:24-Josh8:29); ; [1CH 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:1)

### In natural disaster

[JOL 1:6–1:12](https://ref.ly/Joel1:6-Joel1:12), [JOL 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Joel1:16-Joel1:17)

Apocalyptic prophecies of ruin
------------------------------

[ISA 24:1–24:13](https://ref.ly/Isa24:1-Isa24:13), [ISA 27:9–27:10](https://ref.ly/Isa27:9-Isa27:10); ; [REV 16:17–16:21](https://ref.ly/Rev16:17-Rev16:21), [REV 18:21–18:23](https://ref.ly/Rev18:21-Rev18:23)

Repairs to the ruined temple, altar and defences
------------------------------------------------

## 5509: rulers (id: 5509)

Officials who, acting as God's servants, govern others and exercise authority over them. Also the originators of the laws of their realms. In the NT, rulers are also spiritual powers.

Officials exercising authority over people
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 20:25](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25)** parallel [MRK 10:42](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42) *See also* [2CH 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:20); [PSA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2); [PRO 28:2](https://ref.ly/Prov28:2); [ISA 16:1](https://ref.ly/Isa16:1); [DAN 5:7](https://ref.ly/Dan5:7); [HOS 13:10](https://ref.ly/Hos13:10); [LUK 1:52](https://ref.ly/Luke1:52), [LUK 12:11](https://ref.ly/Luke12:11); [ACT 7:27](https://ref.ly/Acts7:27); [EXO 2:14](https://ref.ly/Exod2:14); [1CO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6-1Cor2:8)

### Godly rulers in foreign lands

[PSA 105:16–105:22](https://ref.ly/Ps105:16-Ps105:22); ; [DAN 2:48](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48)

### Importance of submitting to civil authorities

[ROM 13:3](https://ref.ly/Rom13:3); ; [TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1)

Examples of rulers
------------------

### Rulers of areas apart from Israel

[GEN 25:16](https://ref.ly/Gen25:16), [GEN 34:2](https://ref.ly/Gen34:2), [GEN 41:41](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41), [GEN 45:8](https://ref.ly/Gen45:8); ; [2CH 32:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:31); ; [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2), [EZK 31:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek31:11); ; [DAN 9:1](https://ref.ly/Dan9:1); ; [ACT 7:10](https://ref.ly/Acts7:10), [ACT 7:18](https://ref.ly/Acts7:18)

### The five Philistine rulers

**[JOS 13:3](https://ref.ly/Josh13:3)** *The Hebrew word translated “rulers” is related to the word “tyrant” and is only used of Philistines rulers.* *See also* [JDG 3:3](https://ref.ly/Judg3:3), [JDG 15:11](https://ref.ly/Judg15:11), [JDG 16:18](https://ref.ly/Judg16:18); [1SA 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:8), [1SA 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:16), [1SA 29:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:2); [1CH 12:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:19)

### The king of Israel is referred to as a ruler

**[2SA 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:1-2Sam5:2)** parallel [1CH 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:1-1Chr11:2) *See also* [1KI 1:34–1:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:34-1Kgs1:35), [1KI 11:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:34); [1CH 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:2); [MIC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Mic5:1)

### Other rulers of Israel (or parts of it)

[1KI 22:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:26) parallel [2CH 18:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:25); ; [NEH 3:9](https://ref.ly/Neh3:9); ; [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23); ; [JER 30:21](https://ref.ly/Jer30:21); ; [MIC 3:1](https://ref.ly/Mic3:1); ; [MAT 2:6](https://ref.ly/Matt2:6); ; [LUK 18:18](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18)

### One of three groups that made up the Sanhedrin

**[ACT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Acts4:5)** *See also* [LUK 23:13](https://ref.ly/Luke23:13), [LUK 24:20](https://ref.ly/Luke24:20); [JHN 7:48](https://ref.ly/John7:48); [ACT 4:8](https://ref.ly/Acts4:8), [ACT 13:27](https://ref.ly/Acts13:27)

### Jesus Christ is described as a ruler

**[REV 1:5](https://ref.ly/Rev1:5)** *See also* [MAT 2:6](https://ref.ly/Matt2:6); [MIC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Mic5:2); [REV 3:14](https://ref.ly/Rev3:14)

### God the Father is also called a ruler

[1TI 6:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15)

A ruler as the originator of the laws of his realm
--------------------------------------------------

[PRO 8:15](https://ref.ly/Prov8:15)

Rulers are appointed by God
---------------------------

**[ROM 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:2)** *See also* [2SA 6:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:21), [2SA 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:7-2Sam7:8) parallel [1CH 17:6–17:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:6-1Chr17:7); [1KI 11:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:34); [PSA 2:7](https://ref.ly/Ps2:7); [ISA 44:28](https://ref.ly/Isa44:28); [JER 27:6](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6); [DAN 2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:38), [DAN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Dan4:17); [JHN 19:11](https://ref.ly/John19:11); [ROM 9:17](https://ref.ly/Rom9:17); [EXO 9:16](https://ref.ly/Exod9:16)

Rulers are God's servants
-------------------------

**[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [ISA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1); [JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9), [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10)

Rulers are God's representatives
--------------------------------

**[PSA 58:1](https://ref.ly/Ps58:1)** *The Hebrew word translated “rulers” literally means “gods” a title given to those whose position called for them to act as earthly representatives of God's heavenly court.* *See also* [PSA 82:1](https://ref.ly/Ps82:1); [JHN 10:34–10:35](https://ref.ly/John10:34-John10:35); [PSA 82:6](https://ref.ly/Ps82:6); [ACT 23:5](https://ref.ly/Acts23:5); [EXO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Exod22:28)

Rulers as spiritual powers
--------------------------

**[EPH 3:10](https://ref.ly/Eph3:10)** *See also* [EPH 1:21](https://ref.ly/Eph1:21) *In the NT, rulers are spiritual powers, some of whom exercise a Satanic authority*; [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12); [COL 1:16](https://ref.ly/Col1:16)

Synagogue rulers
----------------

**[MRK 5:22](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22)** parallels [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18), [LUK 8:41](https://ref.ly/Luke8:41) *The ruler of a synagogue looked after the building and supervised the worship in it.* *See also* [MRK 5:35–5:36](https://ref.ly/Mark5:35-Mark5:36) parallel [LUK 8:49–8:50](https://ref.ly/Luke8:49-Luke8:50); [MRK 5:38](https://ref.ly/Mark5:38); [LUK 13:14](https://ref.ly/Luke13:14); [ACT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Acts13:15), [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8), [ACT 18:17](https://ref.ly/Acts18:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 20:25; Mark 10:42; 2 Chronicles 23:20; Psalms 2:2; Proverbs 28:2; Isaiah 16:1; Daniel 5:7; Hosea 13:10; Luke 1:52; Luke 12:11; Acts 7:27; Exodus 2:14; 1 Corinthians 2:6-8; Psalms 105:16-22; Daniel 2:48; Romans 13:3; Titus 3:1; Genesis 25:16; Genesis 34:2; Genesis 41:41; Genesis 45:8; 2 Chronicles 32:31; Ezekiel 28:2; Ezekiel 31:11; Daniel 9:1; Acts 7:10; Acts 7:18; Joshua 13:3; Judges 3:3; Judges 15:11; Judges 16:18; 1 Samuel 5:8; 1 Samuel 6:16; 1 Samuel 29:2; 1 Chronicles 12:19; 2 Samuel 5:1-2; 1 Chronicles 11:1-2; 1 Kings 1:34-35; 1 Kings 11:34; 1 Chronicles 5:2; Micah 5:1; 1 Kings 22:26; 2 Chronicles 18:25; Nehemiah 3:9; Isaiah 1:23; Jeremiah 30:21; Micah 3:1; Matthew 2:6; Luke 18:18; Acts 4:5; Luke 23:13; Luke 24:20; John 7:48; Acts 4:8; Acts 13:27; Revelation 1:5; Micah 5:2; Revelation 3:14; 1 Timothy 6:15; Proverbs 8:15; Romans 13:1-2; 2 Samuel 6:21; 2 Samuel 7:7-8; 1 Chronicles 17:6-7; Psalms 2:7; Isaiah 44:28; Jeremiah 27:6; Daniel 2:38; Daniel 4:17; John 19:11; Romans 9:17; Exodus 9:16; Romans 13:4; Isaiah 45:1; Jeremiah 25:9; Jeremiah 43:10; Psalms 58:1; Psalms 82:1; John 10:34-35; Psalms 82:6; Acts 23:5; Exodus 22:28; Ephesians 3:10; Ephesians 1:21; Ephesians 6:12; Colossians 1:16; Mark 5:22; Matthew 9:18; Luke 8:41; Mark 5:35-36; Luke 8:49-50; Mark 5:38; Luke 13:14; Acts 13:15; Acts 18:8; Acts 18:17

## 5510: rumours (id: 5510)

Insubstantial information, frequently not factual and leading to groundless fears.

Rumours can give rise to fear
-----------------------------

**[JER 51:46](https://ref.ly/Jer51:46)**; **[MAT 24:6](https://ref.ly/Matt24:6)** parallel [MRK 13:7](https://ref.ly/Mark13:7) *See also* [JOB 28:22](https://ref.ly/Job28:22); [EZK 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:26); [1TI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:7)

Rumours can come from misunderstanding what has been said
---------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 21:23](https://ref.ly/John21:23)**

Rumours are sometimes maliciously spread
----------------------------------------

**[PRO 17:4](https://ref.ly/Prov17:4)**; **[3JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/3John1:10)** *See also* [PRO 16:28](https://ref.ly/Prov16:28), [PRO 26:24](https://ref.ly/Prov26:24); [1TI 3:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:11), [1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13), [1TI 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 51:46; Matthew 24:6; Mark 13:7; Job 28:22; Ezekiel 7:26; 1 Timothy 4:7; John 21:23; Proverbs 17:4; 3 John 1:10; Proverbs 16:28; Proverbs 26:24; 1 Timothy 3:11; 1 Timothy 5:13; 1 Timothy 6:11
* **Associated Articles:** 5193: tongue (ID: `5193@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5426: news (ID: `5426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5575: talk, idle (ID: `5575@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5868: gossip (ID: `5868@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8830: suspicion (ID: `8830@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5511: safety (id: 5511)

Being free from danger or harm. God promises to keep his people from all lasting spiritual and physical danger; he requires, however, that his people act responsibly.

God promises safety
-------------------

### To his people

**[DEU 12:10](https://ref.ly/Deut12:10)** *See also* [DEU 33:12](https://ref.ly/Deut33:12), [DEU 33:26–33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:26-Deut33:29); [PSA 48:8](https://ref.ly/Ps48:8); [JER 32:37–32:38](https://ref.ly/Jer32:37-Jer32:38); [ZEC 14:11](https://ref.ly/Zech14:11); [ROM 8:31](https://ref.ly/Rom8:31)

### To those who trust in him

**[PSA 91:9–91:10](https://ref.ly/Ps91:9-Ps91:10)** *See also* [JOB 11:13–11:19](https://ref.ly/Job11:13-Job11:19); [PSA 37:3](https://ref.ly/Ps37:3); [PRO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Prov18:10), [PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25)

### To those who obey him

**[LEV 25:18–25:19](https://ref.ly/Lev25:18-Lev25:19)** *See also* [LEV 26:5](https://ref.ly/Lev26:5); [PRO 1:33](https://ref.ly/Prov1:33), [PRO 3:21–3:26](https://ref.ly/Prov3:21-Prov3:26), [PRO 28:26](https://ref.ly/Prov28:26)

### To those in need

[JOB 5:11](https://ref.ly/Job5:11), [JOB 5:15](https://ref.ly/Job5:15); ; [ISA 14:30](https://ref.ly/Isa14:30)

### But not to the wicked

[JOB 5:4](https://ref.ly/Job5:4); ; [PRO 28:18](https://ref.ly/Prov28:18); ; [JER 12:12](https://ref.ly/Jer12:12)

Safety from enemies
-------------------

**[HEB 13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:6)** *See also* [JOS 10:21](https://ref.ly/Josh10:21); [PSA 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Ps3:5-Ps3:6), [PSA 12:7](https://ref.ly/Ps12:7), [PSA 31:20](https://ref.ly/Ps31:20), [PSA 78:53](https://ref.ly/Ps78:53); [EZK 28:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:26); [REV 3:10](https://ref.ly/Rev3:10)

Safety from dangers
-------------------

**[ISA 43:2](https://ref.ly/Isa43:2)** *See also* [PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4); [EZK 34:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:25), [EZK 34:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:28)

Spiritual safety
----------------

**[1JN 5:18](https://ref.ly/1John5:18)** *See also* [ROM 8:35–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:39); [2TI 4:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:18); [REV 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rev2:11)

Safety in God alone
-------------------

**[PSA 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ps4:8)** *See also* [ZEC 8:10](https://ref.ly/Zech8:10)

### Safety in Jesus Christ

**[JHN 17:12](https://ref.ly/John17:12)** *See also* [JER 23:5–23:6](https://ref.ly/Jer23:5-Jer23:6) parallel [JER 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Jer33:15-Jer33:16); [JHN 10:11–10:15](https://ref.ly/John10:11-John10:15), [JHN 10:28–10:30](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:30)

Angels providing safety
-----------------------

**[MAT 4:6](https://ref.ly/Matt4:6)** parallel [LUK 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Luke4:10-Luke4:11) *See also* [GEN 19:16](https://ref.ly/Gen19:16), [GEN 48:15–48:16](https://ref.ly/Gen48:15-Gen48:16); [EXO 23:20](https://ref.ly/Exod23:20); [PSA 91:11–91:12](https://ref.ly/Ps91:11-Ps91:12); [DAN 3:28](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28), [DAN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Dan6:22); [ACT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Acts5:19), [ACT 12:11](https://ref.ly/Acts12:11); [REV 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:2-Rev7:3)

Human responsibility for safety
-------------------------------

### Personal

[PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15); ; [JER 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jer4:6), [JER 6:1](https://ref.ly/Jer6:1); ; [MAT 24:15–24:16](https://ref.ly/Matt24:15-Matt24:16) parallels [MRK 13:14](https://ref.ly/Mark13:14), [LUK 21:20–21:21](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20-Luke21:21); ; [ACT 2:40](https://ref.ly/Acts2:40), [ACT 27:44](https://ref.ly/Acts27:44)

### Family

[GEN 4:9](https://ref.ly/Gen4:9), [GEN 43:9](https://ref.ly/Gen43:9), [GEN 44:32](https://ref.ly/Gen44:32); ; [MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13)

### Friends

[1SA 20:12–20:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:12-1Sam20:13), [1SA 22:22–22:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:22-1Sam22:23)

### Neighbours

[EXO 22:5–22:15](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5-Exod22:15)

### Officials

[NEH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Neh2:7); ; [ACT 23:24](https://ref.ly/Acts23:24)

### Kings

[1KI 4:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:25); ; [2CH 15:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:5), [2CH 15:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:15); ; [PRO 20:28](https://ref.ly/Prov20:28)

### Pastors

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)** *See also* [JHN 10:11–10:15](https://ref.ly/John10:11-John10:15); [ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28); [PHP 3:1](https://ref.ly/Phil3:1)

Concern for safety
------------------

### David for Absalom

[2SA 18:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:29), [2SA 18:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:32)

### Mephibosheth for David

[2SA 19:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:24), [2SA 19:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:30)

### Father for son

[LUK 15:27](https://ref.ly/Luke15:27)

Prayers for safety
------------------

**[PSA 16:1](https://ref.ly/Ps16:1)** *See also* [EXO 33:15](https://ref.ly/Exod33:15); [EZR 8:21–8:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:21-Ezra8:23); [JOB 30:15](https://ref.ly/Job30:15), [JOB 30:20](https://ref.ly/Job30:20); [PSA 17:8–17:9](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8-Ps17:9), [PSA 19:13](https://ref.ly/Ps19:13), [PSA 27:4–27:5](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4-Ps27:5), [PSA 119:29](https://ref.ly/Ps119:29), [PSA 140:1–140:4](https://ref.ly/Ps140:1-Ps140:4), [PSA 141:8–141:10](https://ref.ly/Ps141:8-Ps141:10); [MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13)

Possible dangers inherent in safety
-----------------------------------

### False security

**[1TH 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:3)** *See also* [DEU 29:19](https://ref.ly/Deut29:19); [1KI 22:27–22:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:27-1Kgs22:28) parallel [2CH 18:26–18:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:26-2Chr18:27); [JOB 21:9](https://ref.ly/Job21:9), [JOB 21:16–21:18](https://ref.ly/Job21:16-Job21:18); [EZK 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:6); [DAN 8:25](https://ref.ly/Dan8:25); [OBA 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3-Obad1:4); [ZEP 2:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:15)

### Complacency

[PRO 1:32](https://ref.ly/Prov1:32); ; [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)

### Wickedness

[JER 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Jer7:9-Jer7:10); ; [EZK 39:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:26)

Perfect safety in God's kingdom
-------------------------------

## 5512: scales and balances (id: 5512)

Weighing devices designed to ensure fair dealing. The terms are also used metaphorically in Scripture.

Scales and balances were used in business transactions
------------------------------------------------------

[JER 32:10](https://ref.ly/Jer32:10)

Scales and balances should not be used dishonestly
--------------------------------------------------

### Exhortations to honest dealing

**[LEV 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35-Lev19:36)** *See also* [EZK 45:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:10)

### The LORD hates dishonest dealing

**[PRO 20:10](https://ref.ly/Prov20:10)** *See also* [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1), [PRO 16:11](https://ref.ly/Prov16:11), [PRO 20:23](https://ref.ly/Prov20:23)

### Dishonest dealing was one of the reasons for God's judgment on his people

**[JER 5:1](https://ref.ly/Jer5:1)** *See also* [HOS 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/Hos12:6-Hos12:7); [AMO 8:4–8:8](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:8); [MIC 6:10–6:13](https://ref.ly/Mic6:10-Mic6:13)

Scales and balances used metaphorically
---------------------------------------

### To emphasise Job's distress

**[JOB 6:2](https://ref.ly/Job6:2)**

### To highlight God's greatness

**[ISA 40:15](https://ref.ly/Isa40:15)** *See also* [ISA 40:12](https://ref.ly/Isa40:12)

### Of God's judgment

**[DAN 5:25–5:30](https://ref.ly/Dan5:25-Dan5:30)** *See also* [EZK 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:1-Ezek5:2); [REV 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Rev6:5-Rev6:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 32:10; Leviticus 19:35-36; Ezekiel 45:10; Proverbs 20:10; Proverbs 11:1; Proverbs 16:11; Proverbs 20:23; Jeremiah 5:1; Hosea 12:6-7; Amos 8:4-8; Micah 6:10-13; Job 6:2; Isaiah 40:15; Isaiah 40:12; Daniel 5:25-30; Ezekiel 5:1-2; Revelation 6:5-6
* **Associated Articles:** 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5614: weights and measures, laws concerning (ID: `5614@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5513: sceptre (id: 5513)

A staff, generally ornate, held as a symbol of sovereign authority.

God's own authority is symbolised as a sceptre
----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Ps45:6)** *See also* [GEN 49:10](https://ref.ly/Gen49:10); [NUM 24:17](https://ref.ly/Num24:17); [PSA 60:7](https://ref.ly/Ps60:7) parallel [PSA 108:8](https://ref.ly/Ps108:8); [ISA 30:31](https://ref.ly/Isa30:31)

The sceptre is a symbol of Jesus Christ's reign
-----------------------------------------------

**[HEB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Heb1:8)** *See also* [PSA 45:6](https://ref.ly/Ps45:6); [GEN 49:10](https://ref.ly/Gen49:10); [NUM 24:17](https://ref.ly/Num24:17); [PSA 110:2](https://ref.ly/Ps110:2); [REV 2:27](https://ref.ly/Rev2:27); [PSA 2:9](https://ref.ly/Ps2:9); [REV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Rev19:15), [REV 12:5](https://ref.ly/Rev12:5)

Earthly rulers had sceptres as symbols of authority
---------------------------------------------------

### Their description

**[EST 4:11](https://ref.ly/Esth4:11)**; **[EZK 19:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:11)** *See also* [EST 5:2](https://ref.ly/Esth5:2), [EST 8:4](https://ref.ly/Esth8:4); [EZK 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:14)

### OT examples

**[NUM 21:18](https://ref.ly/Num21:18)** *See also* [PSA 125:3](https://ref.ly/Ps125:3); [ISA 14:5](https://ref.ly/Isa14:5); [JER 48:17](https://ref.ly/Jer48:17); [EZK 19:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:11), [EZK 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:14), [EZK 21:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:10), [EZK 21:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:13); [AMO 1:5](https://ref.ly/Amos1:5), [AMO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Amos1:8); [ZEC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Zech10:11)

The extension of a ruler's sceptre to one of his subjects was a sign of favour
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EST 5:2](https://ref.ly/Esth5:2)** *See also* [EST 4:11](https://ref.ly/Esth4:11), [EST 8:4](https://ref.ly/Esth8:4)

Jesus Christ's tormentors gave him a mock sceptre
-------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 27:29](https://ref.ly/Matt27:29)**

## 5514: scribes (id: 5514)

Experts in the understanding and study of the law of Moses. Scribes had political power in NT times, and sat on the Sanhedrin. They were also referred to as lawyers and teachers of the law. They opposed Jesus Christ, who condemned their hypocrisy.

The duties of scribes
---------------------

### Writing records, legal contracts, letters and accounts

*See also* [2SA 8:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:16) parallel [1CH 18:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:15); [1KI 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:3); [2KI 12:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:10) parallel [2CH 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11); [JER 32:12](https://ref.ly/Jer32:12), [JER 36:26](https://ref.ly/Jer36:26)

### Advising the king

[2KI 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:18) parallel [ISA 36:3](https://ref.ly/Isa36:3); ; [2KI 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:2); ; [1CH 27:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:32)

### Conscripting people to the army

[2KI 25:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:19) parallel [JER 52:25](https://ref.ly/Jer52:25)

Examples of scribes who are mentioned by name
---------------------------------------------

### Shaphan

[2KI 22:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:8) parallel [2CH 34:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:18)

### Baruch

[JER 36:4–36:32](https://ref.ly/Jer36:4-Jer36:32)

### Ezra

After the exile, scribes became copyists and interpreters of the law: [EZR 7:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:6); ; [NEH 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/Neh8:1-Neh8:4), [NEH 8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9)

Clans or families of scribes
----------------------------

[1CH 2:55](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:55)

The office of priest and scribe
-------------------------------

Up to the time of the Babylonian exile only priests were allowed to be scribes: [EZR 7:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:6); ; [NEH 8:9](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9), [NEH 12:26](https://ref.ly/Neh12:26)

Scribes had political power in NT times and sat on the Sanhedrin
----------------------------------------------------------------

By this time, scribes were also referred to as `teachers of the law'' and`lawyers'': [MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21) parallels [MRK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31), [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22); ; [MAT 26:3](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3) parallels [MRK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1), [LUK 22:2](https://ref.ly/Luke22:2)

Scribes opposed Jesus Christ
----------------------------

**[MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15)**; **[LUK 19:47](https://ref.ly/Luke19:47)** parallels [MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15), [MRK 11:18](https://ref.ly/Mark11:18)

Jesus Christ condemmed the scribes' hypocrisy
---------------------------------------------

**[MAT 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27-Matt23:28)** *See also* [MAT 23:1–23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:7) parallels [MRK 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:39), [LUK 20:45–20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:46)

## 5515: scroll (id: 5515)

A long strip of leather or papyrus on which people wrote.

Writing on a scroll
-------------------

[EXO 17:14](https://ref.ly/Exod17:14); ; [NUM 5:23](https://ref.ly/Num5:23); ; [DEU 17:18](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18); ; [EZR 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:2); ; [PSA 40:7](https://ref.ly/Ps40:7); ; [ISA 30:8](https://ref.ly/Isa30:8); ; [JER 36:2](https://ref.ly/Jer36:2), [JER 36:4](https://ref.ly/Jer36:4), [JER 36:18](https://ref.ly/Jer36:18); ; [LUK 4:17](https://ref.ly/Luke4:17); ; [HEB 10:7](https://ref.ly/Heb10:7); ; [REV 1:11](https://ref.ly/Rev1:11)

### With a pen

[ISA 8:1](https://ref.ly/Isa8:1)

### In ink

[JER 36:18](https://ref.ly/Jer36:18)

### In columns

[JER 36:23](https://ref.ly/Jer36:23)

### A scroll written on both sides was unusual

These references indicate a saturation of the scroll with words of God's judgment: [EZK 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:9-Ezek2:10); ; [ZEC 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Zech5:2-Zech5:3); ; [REV 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rev5:1)

A scroll was often sealed to protect its contents
-------------------------------------------------

[ISA 29:11](https://ref.ly/Isa29:11); ; [DAN 12:4](https://ref.ly/Dan12:4); ; [REV 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Rev5:1-Rev5:2), [REV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9)

Scrolls were of various sizes
-----------------------------

[ISA 8:1](https://ref.ly/Isa8:1); ; [REV 10:2](https://ref.ly/Rev10:2)

Unrolling and rolling up a scroll
---------------------------------

[EZK 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:9-Ezek2:10); ; [LUK 4:17](https://ref.ly/Luke4:17), [LUK 4:20](https://ref.ly/Luke4:20); [ISA 34:4](https://ref.ly/Isa34:4); ; [REV 6:14](https://ref.ly/Rev6:14)

## 5517: seafaring (id: 5517)

Ideas and images drawn from seafaring are used in Scripture to illustrate important aspects of the Christian life. Although Israel was not a seafaring nation, ships and sailing played a part in her history, as they did in the spreading of the gospel in the NT.

Israel's harbours
-----------------

**[GEN 49:13](https://ref.ly/Gen49:13)** *See also* [JDG 5:17](https://ref.ly/Judg5:17)

The art of shipbuilding
-----------------------

[EZK 27:3–27:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:3-Ezek27:9)

Ships used for various purposes
-------------------------------

### Deliverance from danger: Noah's ark

[GEN 6:1–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen6:1-Gen9:17)

### Trade

**[1KI 9:27–9:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:27-1Kgs9:28)** parallel [2CH 8:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:18)**[EZK 27:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:25)** *See also* [1KI 10:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:11), [1KI 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:22) parallel [2CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:21); [1KI 22:48–22:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:48-1Kgs22:49) parallel [2CH 20:35–20:37](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:35-2Chr20:37)

### Slavery

[DEU 28:68](https://ref.ly/Deut28:68); ; [REV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Rev18:11-Rev18:13)

### Invasion

**[NUM 24:24](https://ref.ly/Num24:24)** *See also* [EZK 30:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:9); [DAN 11:30](https://ref.ly/Dan11:30), [DAN 11:40](https://ref.ly/Dan11:40)

**[ISA 60:9](https://ref.ly/Isa60:9)**

Seafarers
---------

**[PSA 107:23–107:30](https://ref.ly/Ps107:23-Ps107:30)**; **[2CO 11:25](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:25)** *See also* [1KI 22:48](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:48) parallel [2CH 20:37](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:37); [JON 1:3–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:3-Jonah1:15); [MAT 8:23–8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23-Matt8:27) parallels [MRK 4:35–4:41](https://ref.ly/Mark4:35-Mark4:41), [LUK 8:22–8:26](https://ref.ly/Luke8:22-Luke8:26); [MAT 14:22–14:33](https://ref.ly/Matt14:22-Matt14:33) parallels [MRK 6:45–6:53](https://ref.ly/Mark6:45-Mark6:53), [JHN 6:16–6:21](https://ref.ly/John6:16-John6:21); [ACT 27:9–27:44](https://ref.ly/Acts27:9-Acts27:44)

Judgment on seafarers
---------------------

**[ISA 23:1](https://ref.ly/Isa23:1)** *See also* [ISA 2:16](https://ref.ly/Isa2:16), [ISA 23:14](https://ref.ly/Isa23:14), [ISA 33:21–33:23](https://ref.ly/Isa33:21-Isa33:23), [ISA 43:14](https://ref.ly/Isa43:14); [EZK 27:26–27:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:26-Ezek27:36); [REV 18:17–18:19](https://ref.ly/Rev18:17-Rev18:19)

Metaphorical references to ships and seafaring
----------------------------------------------

### Spiritual immaturity as a ship tossed at sea

**[EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)**

### Abandoning sound teaching as shipwreck

**[1TI 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:19)**

### Faith as an anchor

**[HEB 6:19](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19)**

### Other seafaring analogies

**[JAS 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Jas3:4-Jas3:5)** *See also* [PSA 48:7](https://ref.ly/Ps48:7); [PRO 31:14](https://ref.ly/Prov31:14); [JAS 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jas1:6)

### Noah's ark as a symbol of Jesus Christ

[1PE 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:20)

## 5518: seal (id: 5518)

A personalised design used to produce an imprint in clay or wax which was then attached, usually to documents and letters as a mark of ownership, authenticity and authority. A seal might also refer to what is given as a pledge or guarantee or be used to keep something secure.

The nature and use of a seal
----------------------------

### Seals engraved on precious stone

**[EXO 28:11](https://ref.ly/Exod28:11)** parallel [EXO 39:6](https://ref.ly/Exod39:6) *See also* [EXO 28:21](https://ref.ly/Exod28:21) parallel [EXO 39:14](https://ref.ly/Exod39:14); [EXO 28:36](https://ref.ly/Exod28:36) parallel [EXO 39:30](https://ref.ly/Exod39:30)

### Seals used to imprint soft clay

[JOB 38:14](https://ref.ly/Job38:14)

Ways of carrying a personal seal
--------------------------------

### Figuratively used

[SNG 8:6](https://ref.ly/Song8:6)

### Worn round the neck

**[GEN 38:18](https://ref.ly/Gen38:18)** *The seal took the form of an engraved cylinder pierced from end to end and worn on a cord round the neck.* *See also* [GEN 38:25](https://ref.ly/Gen38:25)

### Worn as a signet ring

**[JER 22:24](https://ref.ly/Jer22:24)** *See also* [NUM 31:50](https://ref.ly/Num31:50); [ISA 3:21](https://ref.ly/Isa3:21)

A seal as a mark of authority
-----------------------------

### Given to signify delegation of authority

[GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42); ; [EST 3:10](https://ref.ly/Esth3:10), [EST 8:2](https://ref.ly/Esth8:2); ; [REV 7:2](https://ref.ly/Rev7:2)

### God's seal validates his servants' ministry

[JHN 6:27](https://ref.ly/John6:27); ; [1CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:2)

### A seal authenticates documents

**[EST 8:8–8:10](https://ref.ly/Esth8:8-Esth8:10)** *See also* [1KI 21:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8); [EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12)

A seal used to keep something secure
------------------------------------

**[DAN 6:17](https://ref.ly/Dan6:17)** *See also* [DEU 32:34](https://ref.ly/Deut32:34); [JOB 14:17](https://ref.ly/Job14:17); [PSA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9); [SNG 4:12](https://ref.ly/Song4:12); [MAT 27:65–27:66](https://ref.ly/Matt27:65-Matt27:66); [REV 20:3](https://ref.ly/Rev20:3)

Documents sealed to prevent alteration
--------------------------------------

### Binding agreements

**[NEH 9:38–10:1](https://ref.ly/Neh9:38-Neh10:1)** *See also* [JER 32:10–32:11](https://ref.ly/Jer32:10-Jer32:11), [JER 32:14](https://ref.ly/Jer32:14), [JER 32:44](https://ref.ly/Jer32:44)

### The words of prophecy

**[DAN 12:4](https://ref.ly/Dan12:4)** *See also* [ISA 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Isa8:16-Isa8:17), [ISA 29:10–29:11](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10-Isa29:11); [DAN 8:26](https://ref.ly/Dan8:26), [DAN 9:24](https://ref.ly/Dan9:24), [DAN 12:9](https://ref.ly/Dan12:9); [REV 10:4](https://ref.ly/Rev10:4), [REV 22:10](https://ref.ly/Rev22:10)

### The scroll and its seals

**[REV 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Rev5:1-Rev5:4), [REV 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9)** *See also* [REV 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rev5:5), [REV 6:1–6:12](https://ref.ly/Rev6:1-Rev6:12), [REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rev8:1)

A seal as a pledge or guarantee
-------------------------------

[DEU 29:12](https://ref.ly/Deut29:12); ; [HAG 2:23](https://ref.ly/Hag2:23); ; [ROM 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rom4:11)

A seal as a mark of belonging
-----------------------------

### The mark of the beast

[REV 13:16–13:17](https://ref.ly/Rev13:16-Rev13:17), [REV 14:9–14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:9-Rev14:11)

### God's seal of ownership

**[2TI 2:19](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:19)**; **[REV 7:3–7:8](https://ref.ly/Rev7:3-Rev7:8)** *See also* [EZK 9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:4); [GAL 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gal6:17); [REV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Rev9:4)

### The Spirit as a pledge of final redemption

**[2CO 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:21-2Cor1:22)** *The Holy Spirit both seals believers as belonging to God and guarantees their future inheritance.* *See also* [EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14), [EPH 4:30](https://ref.ly/Eph4:30)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 28:11; Exodus 39:6; Exodus 28:21; Exodus 39:14; Exodus 28:36; Exodus 39:30; Job 38:14; Song of Songs 8:6; Genesis 38:18; Genesis 38:25; Jeremiah 22:24; Numbers 31:50; Isaiah 3:21; Genesis 41:42; Esther 3:10; Esther 8:2; Revelation 7:2; John 6:27; 1 Corinthians 9:2; Esther 8:8-10; 1 Kings 21:8; Esther 3:12; Daniel 6:17; Deuteronomy 32:34; Job 14:17; Psalms 40:9; Song of Songs 4:12; Matthew 27:65-66; Revelation 20:3; Nehemiah 9:38-10:1; Jeremiah 32:10-11; Jeremiah 32:14; Jeremiah 32:44; Daniel 12:4; Isaiah 8:16-17; Isaiah 29:10-11; Daniel 8:26; Daniel 9:24; Daniel 12:9; Revelation 10:4; Revelation 22:10; Revelation 5:1-4; Revelation 5:9; Revelation 5:5; Revelation 6:1-12; Revelation 8:1; Deuteronomy 29:12; Haggai 2:23; Romans 4:11; Revelation 13:16-17; Revelation 14:9-11; 2 Timothy 2:19; Revelation 7:3-8; Ezekiel 9:4; Galatians 6:17; Revelation 9:4; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22; Ephesians 1:13-14; Ephesians 4:30

## 5519: secretary (id: 5519)

A secretary was a royal official often responsible for correspondence
---------------------------------------------------------------------

[2SA 8:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:17), [2SA 20:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:25); ; [1KI 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:3); ; [2KI 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:18), [2KI 18:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:37), [2KI 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:2), [2KI 22:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:12); ; [2CH 34:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:13), [2CH 34:15–34:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:15-2Chr34:18) *Shaphan takes the Book of the Law to King Josiah*; ; [EZR 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:8-Ezra4:9), [EZR 4:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:17), [EZR 4:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:23); ; [EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12), [EST 8:9](https://ref.ly/Esth8:9); ; [JER 36:10](https://ref.ly/Jer36:10)

A secretary collected the temple repair money
---------------------------------------------

[2KI 12:10–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:10-2Kgs12:12); ; [2CH 24:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:11)

A secretary was in charge of conscripting soldiers
--------------------------------------------------

[2KI 25:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:19) parallel [JER 52:25](https://ref.ly/Jer52:25); ; [2CH 26:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:11)

Secretaries to writers of NT letters
------------------------------------

Tertius and Silas acted in the capacity of secretary, writing down what the apostles told them to: [ROM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Rom16:22); ; [1PE 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:12)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 8:17; 2 Samuel 20:25; 1 Kings 4:3; 2 Kings 18:18; 2 Kings 18:37; 2 Kings 19:2; 2 Kings 22:12; 2 Chronicles 34:13; 2 Chronicles 34:15-18; Ezra 4:8-9; Ezra 4:17; Ezra 4:23; Esther 3:12; Esther 8:9; Jeremiah 36:10; 2 Kings 12:10-12; 2 Chronicles 24:11; 2 Kings 25:19; Jeremiah 52:25; 2 Chronicles 26:11; Romans 16:22; 1 Peter 5:12

## 5520: servants (id: 5520)

Someone who serves another. Scripture provides guidance concerning the roles of servants, noting in particular that Jesus Christ chose to be a servant and commands believers to serve one another.

* **Associated Articles:** 5521: servant, Jesus Christ as (ID: `5521@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5522: servants, working conditions of (ID: `5522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5523: servants, good (ID: `5523@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5524: servants, bad (ID: `5524@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5521: servant, Jesus Christ as (id: 5521)

See [2327: Jesus Christ, as servant](2327.md)



## 5522: servants, working conditions of (id: 5522)

Those who serve are to be treated fairly and with respect. Jesus Christ himself chose to be a servant and lays responsibility of service on those who follow.

Servants are to be treated fairly
---------------------------------

### They are not to be exploited

**[EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)** *See also* [LEV 25:39–25:40](https://ref.ly/Lev25:39-Lev25:40), [LEV 25:53](https://ref.ly/Lev25:53)

### They are to be paid fairly and promptly

**[DEU 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14-Deut24:15)** *See also* [GEN 29:15](https://ref.ly/Gen29:15); [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13); [NUM 18:29–18:31](https://ref.ly/Num18:29-Num18:31); [1KI 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:6); [JOB 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Job7:1-Job7:2); [JER 22:13](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13); [MAT 20:4](https://ref.ly/Matt20:4), [MAT 20:8](https://ref.ly/Matt20:8); [ROM 4:4](https://ref.ly/Rom4:4); [COL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Col4:1); [2TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10)

### Examples of servants treated unfairly

**[JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)** *See also* [GEN 31:7](https://ref.ly/Gen31:7), [GEN 31:41](https://ref.ly/Gen31:41); [EXO 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23-Exod2:24); [JOB 24:10–24:11](https://ref.ly/Job24:10-Job24:11); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)

Jesus Christ as a servant
-------------------------

**[MAT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:28)** *See also* [PHP 2:7](https://ref.ly/Phil2:7)

Believers follow Jesus Christ's example as servants
---------------------------------------------------

### They are servants of Jesus Christ and his church

**[ROM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Rom1:1)** *See also* [LUK 1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:38); [1CO 12:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:5), [1CO 16:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15); [EPH 4:12](https://ref.ly/Eph4:12); [COL 1:23](https://ref.ly/Col1:23), [COL 3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:24)

### They work to please God

**[EPH 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5-Eph6:6)** *See also* [COL 3:22–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:22-Col3:24); [TIT 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:9-Titus2:10)

Christian workers should be paid honourably
-------------------------------------------

**[1TI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:18)** *See also* [LUK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7); [1CO 9:7–9:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7-1Cor9:11); [GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6)

God rewards his servants
------------------------

**[EPH 6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:8)** *See also* [JOB 34:10–34:11](https://ref.ly/Job34:10-Job34:11); [PRO 10:16](https://ref.ly/Prov10:16); [MAT 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/Matt6:3-Matt6:4), [MAT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:23); [JHN 4:36](https://ref.ly/John4:36); [2TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:8)

Service of sin has its reward
-----------------------------

**[ROM 6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:23)** *See also* [PRO 11:18](https://ref.ly/Prov11:18); [HOS 9:1](https://ref.ly/Hos9:1); [2PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:15)

## 5523: servants, good (id: 5523)

Scripture identifies certain qualities which are found in good servants and requires that these qualities should be found in believers' service of God.

Qualities found in good servants
--------------------------------

### Diligence

**[ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10)** *See also* [1KI 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:28); [PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4), [PRO 22:29](https://ref.ly/Prov22:29); [MAT 25:14–25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:23) parallel [LUK 19:12–19:19](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:19)

### Faithfulness

**[1CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:2)** *See also* [EXO 21:5–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:5-Exod21:6); [2SA 15:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:21); [2CH 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:11-2Chr34:12); [NEH 13:13](https://ref.ly/Neh13:13); [PRO 25:13](https://ref.ly/Prov25:13); [DAN 6:4](https://ref.ly/Dan6:4)

### Honesty

**[2KI 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:7)** *See also* [GEN 39:6](https://ref.ly/Gen39:6); [2KI 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:15); [TIT 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:9-Titus2:10)

### Obedience

**[EPH 6:5](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5)** *See also* [LUK 17:10](https://ref.ly/Luke17:10); [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22)

### Perseverance

**[GEN 31:40–31:41](https://ref.ly/Gen31:40-Gen31:41)** *See also* [2TH 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:12-2Thess3:13)

### Respect

**[1TI 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:1-1Tim6:2)** *See also* [MAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6); [1PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18)

### Willingness

**[EPH 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Eph6:6-Eph6:7)** *See also* [1CH 28:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:21); [TIT 2:9](https://ref.ly/Titus2:9)

Examples of good servants
-------------------------

Abraham's servant: [GEN 24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:9), [GEN 24:49](https://ref.ly/Gen24:49); [2SA 12:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:18), [2SA 23:13–23:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:13-2Sam23:17); [2KI 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:13) *Naaman's servants*; ; [MAT 8:9](https://ref.ly/Matt8:9) *a centurion's servants*

Good servants are often rewarded by their employers
---------------------------------------------------

### They are advanced by them

[GEN 39:4–39:5](https://ref.ly/Gen39:4-Gen39:5); ; [PRO 27:18](https://ref.ly/Prov27:18); ; [MAT 25:21](https://ref.ly/Matt25:21), [MAT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:23)

### They have their confidence

[GEN 24:2–24:4](https://ref.ly/Gen24:2-Gen24:4), [GEN 24:10](https://ref.ly/Gen24:10)

Good servants often bring blessings on their employers
------------------------------------------------------

[GEN 30:27–30:30](https://ref.ly/Gen30:27-Gen30:30), [GEN 39:2–39:4](https://ref.ly/Gen39:2-Gen39:4)

Good servants have God with them
--------------------------------

[GEN 24:7](https://ref.ly/Gen24:7), [GEN 24:27](https://ref.ly/Gen24:27), [GEN 31:7](https://ref.ly/Gen31:7), [GEN 31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:42), [GEN 39:3](https://ref.ly/Gen39:3), [GEN 39:21](https://ref.ly/Gen39:21); ; [MAT 24:46](https://ref.ly/Matt24:46); ; [ACT 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Acts7:9-Acts7:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Ecclesiastes 9:10; 1 Kings 11:28; Proverbs 10:4; Proverbs 22:29; Matthew 25:14-23; Luke 19:12-19; 1 Corinthians 4:2; Exodus 21:5-6; 2 Samuel 15:21; 2 Chronicles 34:11-12; Nehemiah 13:13; Proverbs 25:13; Daniel 6:4; 2 Kings 22:7; Genesis 39:6; 2 Kings 12:15; Titus 2:9-10; Ephesians 6:5; Luke 17:10; Colossians 3:22; Genesis 31:40-41; 2 Thessalonians 3:12-13; 1 Timothy 6:1-2; Malachi 1:6; 1 Peter 2:18; Ephesians 6:6-7; 1 Chronicles 28:21; Titus 2:9; Genesis 24:9; Genesis 24:49; 2 Samuel 12:18; 2 Samuel 23:13-17; 2 Kings 5:13; Matthew 8:9; Genesis 39:4-5; Proverbs 27:18; Matthew 25:21; Matthew 25:23; Genesis 24:2-4; Genesis 24:10; Genesis 30:27-30; Genesis 39:2-4; Genesis 24:7; Genesis 24:27; Genesis 31:7; Genesis 31:42; Genesis 39:3; Genesis 39:21; Matthew 24:46; Acts 7:9-10

## 5524: servants, bad (id: 5524)

Bad servants are characterised by disobedience, idleness and dishonesty.

Characteristics of bad servants
-------------------------------

### Idleness

**[2TH 3:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:11)** *See also* [PRO 18:9](https://ref.ly/Prov18:9); [MAT 25:24–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:24-Matt25:30) parallel [LUK 19:20–19:26](https://ref.ly/Luke19:20-Luke19:26)

### Dishonesty

**[LUK 16:12](https://ref.ly/Luke16:12)** *See also* [GEN 21:25](https://ref.ly/Gen21:25); [LEV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Lev6:2); [2KI 5:20–5:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:20-2Kgs5:25); [EPH 6:6](https://ref.ly/Eph6:6); [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22)

### Disloyalty

**[LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13)** *See also* [2SA 19:24–19:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:24-2Sam19:27); [JHN 10:13](https://ref.ly/John10:13); [ACT 27:30](https://ref.ly/Acts27:30)

### Quarrelsomeness

**[MAT 24:48–24:49](https://ref.ly/Matt24:48-Matt24:49)** *See also* [GEN 13:7](https://ref.ly/Gen13:7), [GEN 26:20](https://ref.ly/Gen26:20); [MAT 18:23–18:30](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:30)

### Discontentment

**[LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14)** *See also* [MAT 20:9–20:15](https://ref.ly/Matt20:9-Matt20:15)

### Refusing correction

[PRO 29:19](https://ref.ly/Prov29:19)

Examples of bad servants
------------------------

Ziba: [2SA 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:1-2Sam16:4), [2SA 19:26–19:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:26-2Sam19:29); [1KI 2:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:39) *Shimei's slaves*; ; [1KI 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:9-1Kgs16:10) *Zimri*; ; [2KI 5:19–5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:19-2Kgs5:27) *Gehazi*; ; [JOB 19:16](https://ref.ly/Job19:16) *Job's servants*; ; [PHM 1:8–1:16](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:8-Phlm1:16) *Onesimus was a bad servant who became good.*

## 5526: shibboleth (id: 5526)

`Shibboleth'' a Hebrew word meaning`flood'' or \`\`ear of corn''
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 69:15](https://ref.ly/Ps69:15)** *See also* [PSA 69:2](https://ref.ly/Ps69:2); [ISA 27:12](https://ref.ly/Isa27:12) *“Shibboleth” is here translated as flowing.*

Dialectal variation in the pronunciation of \`\`shibboleth''
------------------------------------------------------------

**[JDG 12:6](https://ref.ly/Judg12:6)**

The pronunciation of \`\`shibboleth'' featured in a civil war between Gilead and Ephraim
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The Gileadites had defeated the Ammonites

*See also* [JDG 10:7–10:9](https://ref.ly/Judg10:7-Judg10:9), [JDG 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Judg10:17-Judg10:18), [JDG 11:4–11:11](https://ref.ly/Judg11:4-Judg11:11), [JDG 11:32–11:33](https://ref.ly/Judg11:32-Judg11:33)

### This victory led to civil war between Gilead and Ephraim

[JDG 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/Judg12:1-Judg12:4)

### Ephraimites were identified on the basis of their pronunciation of \`\`shibboleth''

**[JDG 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Judg12:5-Judg12:6)**

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 69:15; Psalms 69:2; Isaiah 27:12; Judges 12:6; Judges 10:7-9; Judges 10:17-18; Judges 11:4-11; Judges 11:32-33; Judges 12:1-4; Judges 12:5-6


## 5527: shield (id: 5527)

A piece of armour used to fend off blows or missiles such as arrows. God is described as the shield of his people; protecting, giving help, strength and favour. Faith is the shield with which believers are to ward off spiritual attack.

Shields are pieces of armour
----------------------------

*See also* [2CH 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:11-2Chr11:12), [2CH 32:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:5); [NEH 4:16](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16); [NAM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Nah2:5) *A number of shields were used to make a wall of defence.*

Kinds of shield
---------------

### Large and small shields

**[JER 46:3](https://ref.ly/Jer46:3)** *See also* [1KI 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:16-1Kgs10:17) parallel [2CH 9:15–9:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:15-2Chr9:16); [2CH 14:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:8)

### Gold shields

**[2SA 8:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:7)** parallel [1CH 18:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:7) *See also* [1KI 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:16-1Kgs10:17), [1KI 14:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:26)

### Bronze shields

**[1KI 14:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:27)**

### Shields made of combustible materials

**[EZK 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:9)**

### Shields covered with leather

**[ISA 21:5](https://ref.ly/Isa21:5)** *Leather shields were rubbed with oil to preserve them and to deflect blows from swords or arrows.*

Shields in warfare
------------------

### As part of an army's equipment

**[2CH 14:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:8)** *See also* [1CH 12:23–12:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:23-1Chr12:24); [2CH 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:12), [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14); [NEH 4:16](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16)

### Their use in battle

**[1CH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:18)** *See also* [1SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:7); [2KI 19:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:32); [JER 46:3](https://ref.ly/Jer46:3), [JER 46:9](https://ref.ly/Jer46:9), [JER 51:11](https://ref.ly/Jer51:11); [EZK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:24)

The figurative use of shields
-----------------------------

### God is called a shield of his people

**[GEN 15:1](https://ref.ly/Gen15:1)** *See also* [2SA 22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:3); [PSA 7:10](https://ref.ly/Ps7:10), [PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2); [PRO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Prov2:7)

### God acts as a shield

**[EXO 14:19–14:20](https://ref.ly/Exod14:19-Exod14:20)** *See also* [ISA 52:12](https://ref.ly/Isa52:12), [ISA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8)

### God protects his people

**[ISA 31:5](https://ref.ly/Isa31:5)** *See also* [PSA 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ps3:3), [PSA 12:7](https://ref.ly/Ps12:7), [PSA 32:10](https://ref.ly/Ps32:10), [PSA 125:2](https://ref.ly/Ps125:2), [PSA 140:7](https://ref.ly/Ps140:7); [ZEC 2:5](https://ref.ly/Zech2:5), [ZEC 12:8](https://ref.ly/Zech12:8)

### God is a refuge for his people

**[PSA 144:2](https://ref.ly/Ps144:2)** *See also* [PSA 18:30](https://ref.ly/Ps18:30), [PSA 119:114](https://ref.ly/Ps119:114); [PRO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Prov30:5)

### God is the help and strength of his people

**[PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7)** *See also* [DEU 33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:29); [PSA 33:20](https://ref.ly/Ps33:20), [PSA 115:9–115:11](https://ref.ly/Ps115:9-Ps115:11)

### God gives victory to his people

**[2SA 22:36](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:36)** parallel [PSA 18:35](https://ref.ly/Ps18:35)

### God's favour is compared to a shield

**[PSA 5:12](https://ref.ly/Ps5:12)** *See also* [PSA 3:3](https://ref.ly/Ps3:3), [PSA 32:10](https://ref.ly/Ps32:10), [PSA 84:9–84:11](https://ref.ly/Ps84:9-Ps84:11)

### God's faithfulness is compared to a shield

**[PSA 91:4](https://ref.ly/Ps91:4)**

### A shield is used as a picture of defiance towards God

**[JOB 15:25–15:26](https://ref.ly/Job15:25-Job15:26)**

### Faith is given to the believer to act as a shield

**[EPH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Eph6:16)** *See also* [1PE 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:5)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 11:11-12; 2 Chronicles 32:5; Nehemiah 4:16; Nahum 2:5; Jeremiah 46:3; 1 Kings 10:16-17; 2 Chronicles 9:15-16; 2 Chronicles 14:8; 2 Samuel 8:7; 1 Chronicles 18:7; 1 Kings 14:26; 1 Kings 14:27; Ezekiel 39:9; Isaiah 21:5; 1 Chronicles 12:23-24; 2 Chronicles 11:12; 2 Chronicles 26:14; 1 Chronicles 5:18; 1 Samuel 17:7; 2 Kings 19:32; Jeremiah 46:9; Jeremiah 51:11; Ezekiel 23:24; Genesis 15:1; 2 Samuel 22:3; Psalms 7:10; Psalms 18:2; Proverbs 2:7; Exodus 14:19-20; Isaiah 52:12; Isaiah 58:8; Isaiah 31:5; Psalms 3:3; Psalms 12:7; Psalms 32:10; Psalms 125:2; Psalms 140:7; Zechariah 2:5; Zechariah 12:8; Psalms 144:2; Psalms 18:30; Psalms 119:114; Proverbs 30:5; Psalms 28:7; Deuteronomy 33:29; Psalms 33:20; Psalms 115:9-11; 2 Samuel 22:36; Psalms 18:35; Psalms 5:12; Psalms 84:9-11; Psalms 91:4; Job 15:25-26; Ephesians 6:16; 1 Peter 1:5

## 5528: shouting (id: 5528)

A loud cry of joy, excitement or distress. Scripture often speaks of praise in terms of shouting, reflecting both the joy of the speaker and the greatness of God.

Shouting to be heard
--------------------

**[1SA 17:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:20)**; **[LUK 23:21](https://ref.ly/Luke23:21)**; **[ACT 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Acts22:23-Acts22:24)** *See also* [JDG 7:20](https://ref.ly/Judg7:20), [JDG 15:14](https://ref.ly/Judg15:14); [2KI 9:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:27); [2CH 13:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:15); [JOB 39:25](https://ref.ly/Job39:25); [PSA 60:8](https://ref.ly/Ps60:8); [ZEP 1:14](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:14); [MAT 20:30–20:31](https://ref.ly/Matt20:30-Matt20:31); [JHN 19:12](https://ref.ly/John19:12); [ACT 16:17](https://ref.ly/Acts16:17), [ACT 17:6](https://ref.ly/Acts17:6), [ACT 21:36](https://ref.ly/Acts21:36)

Shouting in distress
--------------------

**[NUM 16:34](https://ref.ly/Num16:34)**; **[LUK 4:41](https://ref.ly/Luke4:41)** *See also* [1KI 18:26–18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26-1Kgs18:28); [LUK 8:28](https://ref.ly/Luke8:28); [ACT 14:14](https://ref.ly/Acts14:14), [ACT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Acts19:28), [ACT 21:28](https://ref.ly/Acts21:28), [ACT 25:24](https://ref.ly/Acts25:24)

Shouting for joy
----------------

**[EZR 3:11–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11-Ezra3:13)**; **[JHN 12:13](https://ref.ly/John12:13)**; **[REV 19:6](https://ref.ly/Rev19:6)** *See also* [EXO 32:17](https://ref.ly/Exod32:17); [LEV 9:24](https://ref.ly/Lev9:24); [NUM 23:21](https://ref.ly/Num23:21); [1SA 4:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:6); [2SA 6:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:15); [1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39); [1CH 15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:28); [2CH 15:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:14); [JOB 3:7](https://ref.ly/Job3:7), [JOB 8:21](https://ref.ly/Job8:21), [JOB 33:26](https://ref.ly/Job33:26), [JOB 38:7](https://ref.ly/Job38:7); [PSA 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ps20:5), [PSA 33:3](https://ref.ly/Ps33:3), [PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4), [PSA 65:13](https://ref.ly/Ps65:13); [PRO 11:10](https://ref.ly/Prov11:10); [ISA 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Isa16:9-Isa16:10), [ISA 26:19](https://ref.ly/Isa26:19); [MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15); [LUK 17:15](https://ref.ly/Luke17:15); [REV 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rev5:12), [REV 19:1](https://ref.ly/Rev19:1)

Joyful shouting commanded
-------------------------

**[JOS 6:5](https://ref.ly/Josh6:5)**; **[PSA 47:1](https://ref.ly/Ps47:1)**; **[ZEP 3:14](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:14)** *See also* [PSA 66:1](https://ref.ly/Ps66:1), [PSA 95:1](https://ref.ly/Ps95:1), [PSA 100:1](https://ref.ly/Ps100:1); [ISA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Isa40:9), [ISA 44:23](https://ref.ly/Isa44:23), [ISA 58:1](https://ref.ly/Isa58:1); [JER 31:7](https://ref.ly/Jer31:7); [ZEC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9)

## 5529: sieges (id: 5529)

Sustained battle against fortified cities, preventing renewal of supplies; a common tactic in OT times. Samaria and Jerusalem both fell to sieges.

Sieges used by and against Israel
---------------------------------

[DEU 20:12](https://ref.ly/Deut20:12), [DEU 20:19–20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:19-Deut20:20); ; [JDG 9:50](https://ref.ly/Judg9:50); ; [1SA 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:1); ; [2SA 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:1), [2SA 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:15)

Ben\-Hadad II twice besieged Samaria, unsuccessfully
----------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:1)** *See also* [1KI 20:15–20:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:15-1Kgs20:20); [2KI 6:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:24), [2KI 7:3–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:3-2Kgs7:7)

Shalmaneser V besieged Samaria
------------------------------

**[2KI 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:5-2Kgs17:6)** *See also* [2KI 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:9-2Kgs18:12)

Sennacherib besieged Jerusalem, unsuccessfully
----------------------------------------------

[2KI 18:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:17), [2KI 18:35–18:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:35-2Kgs18:36); ; [2CH 32:1–32:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:1-2Chr32:5); ; [ISA 29:5–29:8](https://ref.ly/Isa29:5-Isa29:8), [ISA 37:36–37:37](https://ref.ly/Isa37:36-Isa37:37)

Nebuchadnezzar besieged Jerusalem
---------------------------------

**[2KI 25:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1)** *See also* [2KI 25:2–25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:2-2Kgs25:11) parallel [JER 39:1–39:10](https://ref.ly/Jer39:1-Jer39:10); [2CH 36:17–36:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:17-2Chr36:20) parallel [JER 52:4–52:15](https://ref.ly/Jer52:4-Jer52:15)

Jesus Christ warned that Jerusalem would be besieged and destroyed
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 19:43](https://ref.ly/Luke19:43)** *The Roman army destroyed Jerusalem in A.D. 70 using an embankment to besiege the city.* *See also* [LUK 21:20–21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20-Luke21:24)

Sieges caused great suffering and people adopted desperate measures for survival
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[DEU 28:51–28:57](https://ref.ly/Deut28:51-Deut28:57); ; [2KI 6:24–6:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:24-2Kgs6:29), [2KI 18:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:27)
