* **Associated Articles:** 5208: armies (ID: `5208@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5214: attack (ID: `5214@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5228: battering-rams (ID: `5228@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5244: camp (ID: `5244@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5246: captivity (ID: `5246@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5344: imprisonment (ID: `5344@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5354: invasions (ID: `5354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7215: exile, the (ID: `7215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7239: Jerusalem (ID: `7239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9220: day of the LORD (ID: `9220@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5530: sifting (id: 5530)

The process of separating different substances, especially grain and chaff, normally by passing them through sieves. It is used as a symbol of judgment in which the good are separated from the evil.

Sifting as an act of separation
-------------------------------

**[JDG 7:4](https://ref.ly/Judg7:4)**

Sifting as a symbol of judgment
-------------------------------

**[ISA 30:28](https://ref.ly/Isa30:28)** *See also* [AMO 9:9](https://ref.ly/Amos9:9); [LUK 22:31](https://ref.ly/Luke22:31)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 7:4; Isaiah 30:28; Amos 9:9; Luke 22:31
* **Associated Articles:** 4426: chaff (ID: `4426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4550: winnowing (ID: `4550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5531: skill (id: 5531)

A trade or craft acquired by training or practice, or a special ability or expertise that may be an endowment of the Holy Spirit. Scripture lists many skills and abilities, particularly used in the building of the tabernacle and temple.

Skill offered in God's service
------------------------------

**[EXO 35:10](https://ref.ly/Exod35:10)**; **[1CH 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:15-1Chr22:16)** *See also* [1CH 28:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:21)

Skill in particular crafts
--------------------------

### In working metal

**[2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7)** [JDG 17:4](https://ref.ly/Judg17:4); ; [ACT 19:24](https://ref.ly/Acts19:24); [1KI 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:14-1Kgs7:15), [1KI 7:41–7:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:41-1Kgs7:45) parallel [2CH 4:12–4:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:12-2Chr4:16); [2CH 3:5–3:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:5-2Chr3:7); ; [ISA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Isa46:6); [2TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:14)

### In working stone

**[1CH 22:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:2)** *See also* [1KI 5:15–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:15-1Kgs5:18) parallel [2CH 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:2); [1KI 6:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:36), [1KI 7:9–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9-1Kgs7:12)

### With timber

**[1KI 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:6)** parallel [2CH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:8-2Chr2:9) *See also* [1KI 6:14–6:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:14-1Kgs6:36)

### With fabrics

**[EXO 38:23](https://ref.ly/Exod38:23)** [EXO 26:1](https://ref.ly/Exod26:1), [EXO 26:36](https://ref.ly/Exod26:36), [EXO 28:39](https://ref.ly/Exod28:39); [EXO 29:5](https://ref.ly/Exod29:5), [EXO 39:1–39:5](https://ref.ly/Exod39:1-Exod39:5); [EXO 35:25](https://ref.ly/Exod35:25) *skill in spinning*

### With music

[1CH 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:22); ; [2CH 34:12–34:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:12-2Chr34:13); ; [PSA 33:3](https://ref.ly/Ps33:3)

### Other skills

[GEN 25:27](https://ref.ly/Gen25:27) *hunting*; [1KI 9:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:27); ; [EZK 27:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:8); [JOB 32:22](https://ref.ly/Job32:22) *flattery*; ; [PSA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Ps45:1) *writing*; [PSA 58:5](https://ref.ly/Ps58:5); ; [ISA 3:3](https://ref.ly/Isa3:3); [PSA 78:72](https://ref.ly/Ps78:72) *leadership*; ; [JER 2:33](https://ref.ly/Jer2:33) *love*; ; [JER 9:17](https://ref.ly/Jer9:17) *Mourning: professional mourners were a feature of life in Bible times*; ; [JER 50:9](https://ref.ly/Jer50:9) *fighting*; ; [EZK 28:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:5) *trading*

Undesirable skills
------------------

### In constructing idols

**[ISA 44:13](https://ref.ly/Isa44:13)** *See also* [ISA 40:19–40:20](https://ref.ly/Isa40:19-Isa40:20), [ISA 41:7](https://ref.ly/Isa41:7); [JER 10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:9); [HOS 13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:2); [ACT 17:29](https://ref.ly/Acts17:29)

### Doing evil

**[JER 4:22](https://ref.ly/Jer4:22)** *See also* [ISA 25:11](https://ref.ly/Isa25:11); [EZK 21:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:31); [MIC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:3)

Acquiring skill
---------------

### Through training

**[1CH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:18)**; **[2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14)** [GEN 14:14](https://ref.ly/Gen14:14); ; [2CH 26:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:11); [1CH 25:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:7) *musical skill*; ; [LUK 6:40](https://ref.ly/Luke6:40) *teaching abilities*; ; [1CO 9:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:25) *Athletic prowess requires strict training*; ; [HEB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:14) *distinguishing good from evil through proper training*

### Skills given by God

**[EXO 31:1–31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:6)** parallel [EXO 35:30–35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:35) *These skills may well have been developed through training, but they are recognised as gifts from God.* *See also* [EXO 36:1–36:2](https://ref.ly/Exod36:1-Exod36:2); [DEU 33:11](https://ref.ly/Deut33:11) *Moses prays for Levi's skills to be blessed by God*; [ISA 50:4](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4) *Skill in the right use of words comes from God*; [ROM 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6-Rom12:8) *Special abilities are given by God to believers.*

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 35:10; 1 Chronicles 22:15-16; 1 Chronicles 28:21; 2 Chronicles 2:7; Judges 17:4; Acts 19:24; 1 Kings 7:14-15; 1 Kings 7:41-45; 2 Chronicles 4:12-16; 2 Chronicles 3:5-7; Isaiah 46:6; 2 Timothy 4:14; 1 Chronicles 22:2; 1 Kings 5:15-18; 2 Chronicles 2:2; 1 Kings 6:36; 1 Kings 7:9-12; 1 Kings 5:6; 2 Chronicles 2:8-9; 1 Kings 6:14-36; Exodus 38:23; Exodus 26:1; Exodus 26:36; Exodus 28:39; Exodus 29:5; Exodus 39:1-5; Exodus 35:25; 1 Chronicles 15:22; 2 Chronicles 34:12-13; Psalms 33:3; Genesis 25:27; 1 Kings 9:27; Ezekiel 27:8; Job 32:22; Psalms 45:1; Psalms 58:5; Isaiah 3:3; Psalms 78:72; Jeremiah 2:33; Jeremiah 9:17; Jeremiah 50:9; Ezekiel 28:5; Isaiah 44:13; Isaiah 40:19-20; Isaiah 41:7; Jeremiah 10:9; Hosea 13:2; Acts 17:29; Jeremiah 4:22; Isaiah 25:11; Ezekiel 21:31; Micah 7:3; 1 Chronicles 5:18; 2 Chronicles 2:13-14; Genesis 14:14; 2 Chronicles 26:11; 1 Chronicles 25:7; Luke 6:40; 1 Corinthians 9:25; Hebrews 5:14; Exodus 31:1-6; Exodus 35:30-35; Exodus 36:1-2; Deuteronomy 33:11; Isaiah 50:4; Romans 12:6-8

## 5532: sleep (id: 5532)

A state of slumber or rest, reflecting tiredness and the need for refreshment. Although sleep is a natural physical process, Scripture also uses the term in a number of important spiritual senses.

* **Associated Articles:** 5533: sleep, physical (ID: `5533@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5534: sleep, spiritual (ID: `5534@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5535: sleep, and death (ID: `5535@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5533: sleep, physical (id: 5533)

A gift from God providing necessary rest, usually at night, for the restoration of mind and body. It is sometimes denied, sometimes used by God for his purposes and sometimes abused by people.

Sleep is a gift from God
------------------------

### It is pleasant and refreshing

**[ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12)** *See also* [1KI 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5); [JER 31:26](https://ref.ly/Jer31:26); [JHN 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/John11:12-John11:13)

### It is God's gift to his faithful people

**[PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2)** *See also* [PRO 3:21–3:24](https://ref.ly/Prov3:21-Prov3:24)

### It is often denied to the wicked

[ISA 48:22](https://ref.ly/Isa48:22)

Sleep as a nocturnal activity
-----------------------------

### It is associated with night\-time

**[1TH 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:7)** *See also* [LUK 17:34](https://ref.ly/Luke17:34); [JHN 9:4](https://ref.ly/John9:4); [ROM 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:11-Rom13:12)

### It is associated with dreams

[GEN 28:11–28:12](https://ref.ly/Gen28:11-Gen28:12), [GEN 41:1](https://ref.ly/Gen41:1), [GEN 41:5](https://ref.ly/Gen41:5); ; [ISA 29:7–29:8](https://ref.ly/Isa29:7-Isa29:8); ; [DAN 2:1](https://ref.ly/Dan2:1), [DAN 4:5](https://ref.ly/Dan4:5), [DAN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Dan7:1)

### It is associated with sexual activity

**[GEN 30:15–30:16](https://ref.ly/Gen30:15-Gen30:16)**; **[2SA 11:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:4)** [GEN 16:2](https://ref.ly/Gen16:2), [GEN 16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4); [GEN 26:10](https://ref.ly/Gen26:10), [GEN 30:3–30:5](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3-Gen30:5), [GEN 35:22](https://ref.ly/Gen35:22), [GEN 38:18](https://ref.ly/Gen38:18), [GEN 39:14](https://ref.ly/Gen39:14); ; [EXO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16); ; [DEU 22:22](https://ref.ly/Deut22:22); ; [2SA 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:7); ; [HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4)

Blessings in sleep
------------------

### Peace

**[PSA 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ps4:8)** *See also* [MAT 8:24](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24) parallels [MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38), [LUK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23); [ACT 12:6](https://ref.ly/Acts12:6)

### Protection

**[PSA 121:3–121:4](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:4)** *See also* [PSA 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Ps3:5-Ps3:6), [PSA 68:13](https://ref.ly/Ps68:13); [EZK 34:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:25)

Deep and light sleep
--------------------

### Deep sleep for the creation of Eve

**[GEN 2:21](https://ref.ly/Gen2:21)**

### Deep sleep for revelation

*See also* [GEN 15:12](https://ref.ly/Gen15:12); [JOB 4:12–4:13](https://ref.ly/Job4:12-Job4:13), [JOB 33:14–33:18](https://ref.ly/Job33:14-Job33:18); [DAN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Dan7:2), [DAN 7:13](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13); [ACT 16:9](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9)

### Deep sleep as a judgment

[1SA 26:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:12); ; [ISA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10) *The sleep in this instance is symbolic.*

### Light sleep

[SNG 5:2](https://ref.ly/Song5:2)

Sleeping at the wrong time
--------------------------

### While on duty

[PRO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Prov19:15); ; [MAT 28:13](https://ref.ly/Matt28:13); ; [MRK 13:36](https://ref.ly/Mark13:36)

### During harvest

[PRO 10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:5)

### Instead of praying

[JON 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:5-Jonah1:6); ; [MAT 26:40](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40) parallels [MRK 14:37](https://ref.ly/Mark14:37), [LUK 22:45–22:46](https://ref.ly/Luke22:45-Luke22:46)

### During a sermon

[ACT 20:9](https://ref.ly/Acts20:9)

### At the transfiguration

[LUK 9:32](https://ref.ly/Luke9:32)

### Amidst danger

[JDG 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/Judg16:13-Judg16:14), [JDG 16:19](https://ref.ly/Judg16:19); ; [PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5), [PRO 23:34](https://ref.ly/Prov23:34); ; [MAT 13:25](https://ref.ly/Matt13:25)

Excessive sleep
---------------

**[PRO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:9-Prov6:11)** *See also* [PRO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Prov20:13), [PRO 23:21](https://ref.ly/Prov23:21); [ISA 56:10](https://ref.ly/Isa56:10)

## 5534: sleep, spiritual (id: 5534)

Scripture often uses sleep as an image of spiritual laziness or a lack of alertness towards God.

Sleep as an image of spiritual laziness
---------------------------------------

### Lack of watchfulness

**[ISA 56:10](https://ref.ly/Isa56:10)** *See also* [MAT 24:43–24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:43-Matt24:44) parallel [LUK 12:39–12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:39-Luke12:40); [MRK 13:35–13:36](https://ref.ly/Mark13:35-Mark13:36)

### Spiritual dullness

**[ACT 28:25–28:27](https://ref.ly/Acts28:25-Acts28:27)** *See also* [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); [PRO 23:34–23:35](https://ref.ly/Prov23:34-Prov23:35)ROM [11:8](https://ref.ly/Prov11:8); [ISA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10)

God's call to awake from sleep
------------------------------

### The need to arise

**[ROM 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/Rom13:11-Rom13:12)** *See also* [ISA 51:17](https://ref.ly/Isa51:17), [ISA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Isa52:1), [ISA 60:1](https://ref.ly/Isa60:1); [EPH 5:14](https://ref.ly/Eph5:14); [REV 3:2](https://ref.ly/Rev3:2)

### The command to be alert

**[REV 16:15](https://ref.ly/Rev16:15)** *See also* [MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41); [MRK 13:32–13:34](https://ref.ly/Mark13:32-Mark13:34); [LUK 12:35](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35); [EPH 6:18](https://ref.ly/Eph6:18); [1TH 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:6-1Thess5:8); [1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)

### The plea for revival

[PSA 80:18](https://ref.ly/Ps80:18), [PSA 85:6](https://ref.ly/Ps85:6)

The image of God awaking from sleep
-----------------------------------

### From his past judgments

**[PSA 78:65](https://ref.ly/Ps78:65)**

### Prayers for God to rise up against his enemies

**[PSA 7:6](https://ref.ly/Ps7:6)** *See also* [PSA 35:23](https://ref.ly/Ps35:23), [PSA 44:23–44:24](https://ref.ly/Ps44:23-Ps44:24), [PSA 73:20](https://ref.ly/Ps73:20), [PSA 80:1–80:2](https://ref.ly/Ps80:1-Ps80:2); [ISA 51:9](https://ref.ly/Isa51:9)

The contrast between God who never sleeps and false gods
--------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 121:3–121:4](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:4)** *See also* [1KI 18:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:27); [HAB 2:19](https://ref.ly/Hab2:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 56:10; Matthew 24:43-44; Luke 12:39-40; Mark 13:35-36; Acts 28:25-27; Isaiah 6:9-10; Proverbs 23:34-35; Proverbs 11:8; Isaiah 29:10; Romans 13:11-12; Isaiah 51:17; Isaiah 52:1; Isaiah 60:1; Ephesians 5:14; Revelation 3:2; Revelation 16:15; Matthew 26:41; Mark 13:32-34; Luke 12:35; Ephesians 6:18; 1 Thessalonians 5:6-8; 1 Peter 5:8; Psalms 80:18; Psalms 85:6; Psalms 78:65; Psalms 7:6; Psalms 35:23; Psalms 44:23-24; Psalms 73:20; Psalms 80:1-2; Isaiah 51:9; Psalms 121:3-4; 1 Kings 18:27; Habakkuk 2:19
* **Associated Articles:** 5611: watchman (ID: `5611@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6139: deadness, spiritual (ID: `6139@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8148: revival (ID: `8148@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8490: watchfulness (ID: `8490@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8602: prayer (ID: `8602@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5535: sleep, and death (id: 5535)

Scripture often uses sleep as an image of death, indicating that believers will be awakened to resurrection on the last day.

Death described in the imagery of sleep
---------------------------------------

### The sleep of death for human beings in general

**[PSA 90:3–90:6](https://ref.ly/Ps90:3-Ps90:6)** *See also* [JOB 7:21](https://ref.ly/Job7:21), [JOB 14:10–14:12](https://ref.ly/Job14:10-Job14:12); [PSA 13:3](https://ref.ly/Ps13:3); [DAN 12:2](https://ref.ly/Dan12:2); [MAT 9:24](https://ref.ly/Matt9:24) parallels [MRK 5:39](https://ref.ly/Mark5:39), [LUK 8:52–8:53](https://ref.ly/Luke8:52-Luke8:53)

### Death as resting with the ancestors

**[1KI 2:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:10)** *See also* [DEU 31:16](https://ref.ly/Deut31:16); [2SA 7:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:12); [1KI 11:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:43) *Solomon*; [1KI 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:20), [1KI 14:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:31), [1KI 16:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:6), [1KI 22:50](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:50) *Jehoshaphat*; [2KI 14:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:16), [2KI 15:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:7), [2KI 16:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:20), [2KI 20:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:21) *Hezekiah*; [2KI 21:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:18) *Manasseh*

### Those who fall asleep in Christ

**[REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13)** *See also* [JHN 11:11–11:14](https://ref.ly/John11:11-John11:14); [ACT 7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:60); [1CO 15:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:6), [1CO 15:17–15:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:17-1Cor15:19)

Death is not the end of existence
---------------------------------

**[JHN 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/John5:28-John5:29)** *See also* [JOB 14:13–14:15](https://ref.ly/Job14:13-Job14:15); [PSA 17:15](https://ref.ly/Ps17:15); [ISA 26:19](https://ref.ly/Isa26:19); [DAN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Dan12:3); [LUK 16:22–16:23](https://ref.ly/Luke16:22-Luke16:23); [1CO 15:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:20), [1CO 15:51–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:51-1Cor15:57); [1TH 4:13–4:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13-1Thess4:18)

### Sleep denoting the finality of judgment for the lost

**[JER 51:57](https://ref.ly/Jer51:57)** *See also* [PSA 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/Ps7:3-Ps7:5), [PSA 76:5](https://ref.ly/Ps76:5); [JER 51:39](https://ref.ly/Jer51:39)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 90:3-6; Job 7:21; Job 14:10-12; Psalms 13:3; Daniel 12:2; Matthew 9:24; Mark 5:39; Luke 8:52-53; 1 Kings 2:10; Deuteronomy 31:16; 2 Samuel 7:12; 1 Kings 11:43; 1 Kings 14:20; 1 Kings 14:31; 1 Kings 16:6; 1 Kings 22:50; 2 Kings 14:16; 2 Kings 15:7; 2 Kings 16:20; 2 Kings 20:21; 2 Kings 21:18; Revelation 14:13; John 11:11-14; Acts 7:60; 1 Corinthians 15:6; 1 Corinthians 15:17-19; John 5:28-29; Job 14:13-15; Psalms 17:15; Isaiah 26:19; Daniel 12:3; Luke 16:22-23; 1 Corinthians 15:20; 1 Corinthians 15:51-57; 1 Thessalonians 4:13-18; Jeremiah 51:57; Psalms 7:3-5; Psalms 76:5; Jeremiah 51:39

## 5537: sleeplessness (id: 5537)

The absence of or inability to sleep. Scripture suggests that sleeplessness will sometimes result from divine intervention. Scripture also provides examples of deliberate sleeplessness in order to seek God.

Anxiety and sorrow as causes of sleeplessness
---------------------------------------------

**[ECC 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:22-Eccl2:23)** *See also* [PRO 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:4); [ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12); [JER 45:3](https://ref.ly/Jer45:3)

Other causes of sleeplessness
-----------------------------

### Work

**[PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2)**

### Pain and sickness

**[JOB 30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:17)** *See also* [JOB 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/Job7:4-Job7:5), [JOB 33:19](https://ref.ly/Job33:19); [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:11)

### Evil deeds

**[PRO 4:14–4:16](https://ref.ly/Prov4:14-Prov4:16)** *See also* [ISA 5:27](https://ref.ly/Isa5:27)

### Concern for God's cause

**[PSA 132:4–132:5](https://ref.ly/Ps132:4-Ps132:5)** *See also* [ISA 62:6](https://ref.ly/Isa62:6); [LUK 2:37](https://ref.ly/Luke2:37), [LUK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12); [ACT 12:12](https://ref.ly/Acts12:12)

------------------------------------

### Jacob

**[GEN 31:40](https://ref.ly/Gen31:40)**

### Saul

**[1SA 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14)** *Loss of sleep probably among the troubles of Saul alleviated by David's playing.* *See also* [1SA 16:15–16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:15-1Sam16:23)

### Xerxes

**[EST 6:1](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1)**

### Job

**[JOB 7:4](https://ref.ly/Job7:4)** *See also* [JOB 7:11–7:15](https://ref.ly/Job7:11-Job7:15)

### Darius

[DAN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Dan6:18)

### Paul

**[2CO 11:27](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:27)** *See also* [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5)

God deprives sinners of rest
----------------------------

**[ISA 48:22](https://ref.ly/Isa48:22)** parallels [HEB 3:11](https://ref.ly/Heb3:11), [HEB 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/Heb4:1-Heb4:5)

### Restlessness because of judgment on sin

**[REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:11)** *See also* [DEU 28:67](https://ref.ly/Deut28:67); [1SA 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14); [ISA 21:4](https://ref.ly/Isa21:4), [ISA 23:12](https://ref.ly/Isa23:12), [ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21); [LAM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Lam5:5)

Sleeplessness allows people to receive messages from God
--------------------------------------------------------

**[DAN 2:1](https://ref.ly/Dan2:1)**; **[ZEC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Zech4:1)** *See also* [1SA 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:1-1Sam3:10); [DAN 2:29](https://ref.ly/Dan2:29); [MAT 27:19](https://ref.ly/Matt27:19)

A challenge to abandon sleep
----------------------------

### Abandoning actual sleep

**[LUK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12)** *See also* [MAT 26:36–26:46](https://ref.ly/Matt26:36-Matt26:46) parallels [MRK 14:32–14:42](https://ref.ly/Mark14:32-Mark14:42), [LUK 22:47–22:53](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47-Luke22:53); [LUK 2:37](https://ref.ly/Luke2:37); [REV 16:15](https://ref.ly/Rev16:15)

### Abandoning spiritual lethargy

**[PSA 57:8](https://ref.ly/Ps57:8)** *See also* [PRO 6:10](https://ref.ly/Prov6:10); [ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2); [ISA 51:9](https://ref.ly/Isa51:9)

God's watchfulness
------------------

**[PSA 121:3–121:4](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:4)**; **[ISA 40:28–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:28-Isa40:31)**

The blessing of sleep
---------------------

**[PSA 4:8](https://ref.ly/Ps4:8)** *See also* [LEV 26:6](https://ref.ly/Lev26:6); [PSA 3:5](https://ref.ly/Ps3:5), [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); [PRO 3:24](https://ref.ly/Prov3:24)

## 5538: sling (id: 5538)

Used by shepherds to defend their flocks, it consisted of a strip of leather broad enough to contain a stone. A string was attached to each end of this strip. It was swung around fast and when one string was released the stone could be hurled out with great force and accuracy.

David felled Goliath with a stone from his sling
------------------------------------------------

[1SA 17:40](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:40), [1SA 17:49–17:50](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:49-1Sam17:50)

Slings were also used by soldiers as weapons
--------------------------------------------

[JDG 20:16](https://ref.ly/Judg20:16); ; [2KI 3:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:25); ; [1CH 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:2); ; [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14)

Slings were known for their destructive power
---------------------------------------------

[JOB 41:28](https://ref.ly/Job41:28); ; [ZEC 9:15](https://ref.ly/Zech9:15)

Slings used metaphorically
--------------------------

[1SA 25:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:29); ; [PRO 26:8](https://ref.ly/Prov26:8); ; [JER 10:18](https://ref.ly/Jer10:18)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 17:40; 1 Samuel 17:49-50; Judges 20:16; 2 Kings 3:25; 1 Chronicles 12:2; 2 Chronicles 26:14; Job 41:28; Zechariah 9:15; 1 Samuel 25:29; Proverbs 26:8; Jeremiah 10:18
* **Associated Articles:** 4366: stones (ID: `4366@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5210: arrows (ID: `5210@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5544: soldiers (ID: `5544@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5612: weapons (ID: `5612@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7784: shepherd (ID: `7784@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5539: sluggard (id: 5539)

Someone characterised by a settled attitude of slothfulness and idleness. Such people are consistently condemned throughout Scripture.

Sluggards are characterised by slothfulness and idleness
--------------------------------------------------------

### They are lazy and refuse to work

**[PRO 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:8)** *See also* [PRO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Prov12:27), [PRO 15:19](https://ref.ly/Prov15:19), [PRO 19:24](https://ref.ly/Prov19:24), [PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4), [PRO 21:25](https://ref.ly/Prov21:25), [PRO 24:30–24:31](https://ref.ly/Prov24:30-Prov24:31), [PRO 26:15](https://ref.ly/Prov26:15)

### They love to stay in bed when they should be up and active

**[PRO 6:9](https://ref.ly/Prov6:9)** *See also* [PRO 26:14](https://ref.ly/Prov26:14)

### They make excuses to avoid work

**[PRO 22:13](https://ref.ly/Prov22:13)** *See also* [PRO 26:13](https://ref.ly/Prov26:13)

### Their desires are not met

**[PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4)** *See also* [PRO 20:4](https://ref.ly/Prov20:4), [PRO 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/Prov21:25-Prov21:26)

### Their laziness leads to poverty

**[PRO 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:10-Prov6:11)** *See also* [PRO 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4-Prov10:5), [PRO 12:24](https://ref.ly/Prov12:24), [PRO 18:9](https://ref.ly/Prov18:9), [PRO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Prov19:15), [PRO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Prov20:13), [PRO 24:33–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:33-Prov24:34); [ECC 4:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:5), [ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18)

### Their attitude is irritating to others

[PRO 10:26](https://ref.ly/Prov10:26)

### They are conceited

[PRO 26:16](https://ref.ly/Prov26:16)

Sluggards condemned throughout Scripture
----------------------------------------

### In the book of Proverbs

**[PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4)** *See also* [PRO 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:11), [PRO 12:11](https://ref.ly/Prov12:11), [PRO 12:24](https://ref.ly/Prov12:24), [PRO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Prov12:27), [PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23), [PRO 18:9](https://ref.ly/Prov18:9), [PRO 28:19](https://ref.ly/Prov28:19)

### By Jesus Christ

[MAT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Matt20:3), [MAT 20:6](https://ref.ly/Matt20:6), [MAT 25:24–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:24-Matt25:30)

### In the epistles

**[1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)** *See also* [2TH 3:6](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:6), [2TH 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10-2Thess3:11); [1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13); [TIT 1:12](https://ref.ly/Titus1:12), [TIT 3:14](https://ref.ly/Titus3:14); [HEB 6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 6:6-8; Proverbs 12:27; Proverbs 15:19; Proverbs 19:24; Proverbs 20:4; Proverbs 21:25; Proverbs 24:30-31; Proverbs 26:15; Proverbs 6:9; Proverbs 26:14; Proverbs 22:13; Proverbs 26:13; Proverbs 13:4; Proverbs 21:25-26; Proverbs 6:10-11; Proverbs 10:4-5; Proverbs 12:24; Proverbs 18:9; Proverbs 19:15; Proverbs 20:13; Proverbs 24:33-34; Ecclesiastes 4:5; Ecclesiastes 10:18; Proverbs 10:26; Proverbs 26:16; Proverbs 10:4; Proverbs 6:6-11; Proverbs 12:11; Proverbs 14:23; Proverbs 28:19; Matthew 20:3; Matthew 20:6; Matthew 25:24-30; 1 Thessalonians 5:14; 2 Thessalonians 3:6; 2 Thessalonians 3:10-11; 1 Timothy 5:13; Titus 1:12; Titus 3:14; Hebrews 6:12

## 5540: society (id: 5540)

The network of relationships, institutions and shared values that together make up the corporate aspect of human life. Though often regarded as hostile to God and threatening to faith, society can be viewed positively as capable of redemption and an object of God's saving activity.

* **Associated Articles:** 5541: society, negative aspects of (ID: `5541@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5542: society, positive aspects of (ID: `5542@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5541: society, negative aspects of (id: 5541)

Often pictured as an immoral city or a world of evil, it is regarded as corrupt and godless, hostile to true faith and therefore destined for judgment.

The city as a symbol of corrupt society
---------------------------------------

**[REV 18:2–18:3](https://ref.ly/Rev18:2-Rev18:3)** *Babylon stands for the corrupt Roman state and also for godless society in general.* *See also* [GEN 11:1–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:1-Gen11:9), [GEN 19:1–19:13](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:13); [EZK 22:1–22:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:1-Ezek22:5); [MAT 23:37–23:39](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37-Matt23:39) parallel [LUK 13:34–13:35](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34-Luke13:35); [REV 14:8](https://ref.ly/Rev14:8)

\`\`The world'' as a description of society alienated from, and hostile to, both God and his people
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Gal3:22)** *The Greek word translated “world” often means not the physical world, but the world of human life.* *See also* [JHN 15:18](https://ref.ly/John15:18); [EPH 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12); [1JN 5:19](https://ref.ly/1John5:19); [REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9)

Fallen society becomes pluralistic
----------------------------------

### Israel was continually threatened by the pluralism of the surrounding societies

**[JOS 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Josh23:6-Josh23:8)** *See also* [EXO 23:32–23:33](https://ref.ly/Exod23:32-Exod23:33); [JOS 24:14–24:23](https://ref.ly/Josh24:14-Josh24:23); [1KI 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:6), [1KI 18:16–18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:16-1Kgs18:40) *Baal was a name used for a variety of local Canaanite deities*; [EZR 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:1-Ezra9:2); [ISA 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Isa2:6-Isa2:8); [DAN 3:1–3:12](https://ref.ly/Dan3:1-Dan3:12)

### Pluralism was also a feature of the societies of the NT

**[ACT 17:16–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16-Acts17:23)** *See also* [1CO 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:4-1Cor8:6); [2TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3-2Tim4:4); [1JN 5:21](https://ref.ly/1John5:21)

### Pluralism is to be resisted and faith in one God and Saviour maintained

**[1TI 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:5)** *See also* [ISA 43:10–43:11](https://ref.ly/Isa43:10-Isa43:11); [JHN 14:6](https://ref.ly/John14:6); [ACT 4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:12)

Fallen society becomes secular
------------------------------

### Life in a godless society becomes spiritually futile and morally depraved

**[ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21)** *See also* [ROM 1:18–1:32](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18-Rom1:32); [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5); [REV 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/Rev18:1-Rev18:3)

### Israel ignores warnings about becoming a society which has forgotten God

**[PSA 81:11–81:12](https://ref.ly/Ps81:11-Ps81:12)** *See also* [JER 3:19–3:21](https://ref.ly/Jer3:19-Jer3:21); [HEB 3:7–3:11](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7-Heb3:11)

### Israel became an example of a society which, though outwardly religious, lived without reference to God

**[AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24)** *Amos here summarises, and rejects, current religious practices in Israel in the 8th century B.C.* *See also* [AMO 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:7), [AMO 5:4–5:15](https://ref.ly/Amos5:4-Amos5:15) *Bethel and Gilgal were popular national shrines, where religion had taken the place of righteousness and relationship*; [AMO 8:4–8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 18:2-3; Genesis 11:1-9; Genesis 19:1-13; Ezekiel 22:1-5; Matthew 23:37-39; Luke 13:34-35; Revelation 14:8; Galatians 3:22; John 15:18; Ephesians 6:12; 1 John 5:19; Revelation 12:9; Joshua 23:6-8; Exodus 23:32-33; Joshua 24:14-23; 1 Kings 11:1-6; 1 Kings 18:16-40; Ezra 9:1-2; Isaiah 2:6-8; Daniel 3:1-12; Acts 17:16-23; 1 Corinthians 8:4-6; 2 Timothy 4:3-4; 1 John 5:21; 1 Timothy 2:5; Isaiah 43:10-11; John 14:6; Acts 4:12; Romans 1:21; Romans 1:18-32; 2 Timothy 3:1-5; Revelation 18:1-3; Psalms 81:11-12; Jeremiah 3:19-21; Hebrews 3:7-11; Amos 5:21-24; Amos 2:6-7; Amos 5:4-15; Amos 8:4-10
* **Associated Articles:** 4030: world, human behaviour in (ID: `4030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4215: Babylon (ID: `4215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4263: Rome (ID: `4263@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5001: human race, the (ID: `5001@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5256: city (ID: `5256@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6169: godlessness (ID: `6169@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8710: atheism (ID: `8710@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8848: worldliness (ID: `8848@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5542: society, positive aspects of (id: 5542)

The object of God's love and the sphere of God's blessing. God's people should adopt a positive but critical attitude to, and involvement in society, and should seek to influence it for good, while guarding against its corrupting influence.

Society is the object of God's redeeming love and the sphere of his blessing
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The city as a positive image of society

**[ISA 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Isa1:26-Isa1:27)** *See also* [PSA 122:6–122:9](https://ref.ly/Ps122:6-Ps122:9); [ISA 54:11–54:14](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11-Isa54:14); [HEB 11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:10), [HEB 11:15–11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:15-Heb11:16); [REV 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:4), [REV 21:22–22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev21:22-Rev22:5)

### The world as the object of God's love

**[JHN 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/John3:16-John3:17)** *See also* [JHN 6:33](https://ref.ly/John6:33); [2CO 5:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:19); [1JN 4:14](https://ref.ly/1John4:14); [REV 11:15](https://ref.ly/Rev11:15)

God's people are to be positively involved in society
-----------------------------------------------------

### They are to seek to be a positive influence

**[JHN 17:15–17:18](https://ref.ly/John17:15-John17:18)** *See also* [JER 29:7](https://ref.ly/Jer29:7) *God's people, in exile in Babylon, are to seek the welfare of that society, rather than simply long to return home*; [MAT 5:13–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:13-Matt5:16); [1CO 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:10)

### They are to avoid being corrupted by society's worldliness

**[JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)** *See also* [ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2); [JAS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jas4:4); [1JN 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/1John2:15-1John2:17)

### They do not owe society their ultimate loyalty

**[JHN 17:16](https://ref.ly/John17:16)** *See also* [JHN 15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:19); [HEB 13:14](https://ref.ly/Heb13:14); [1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11)

### They are to adopt a positive attitude to society's properly instituted authorities

**[ROM 13:1–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:7)**; **[TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1)** *See also* [MAT 22:17–22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:17-Matt22:21) parallels [MRK 12:14–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:14-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:22–20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:22-Luke20:25); [1PE 2:13–2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:17)

### While being submissive, they are not to give absolute obedience to any merely human authority in society

**[ACT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:20)** *See also* [ACT 5:27–5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:27-Acts5:29)

Examples of those positively involved in society
------------------------------------------------

**[DAN 2:48–2:49](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48-Dan2:49)** *See also* [GEN 41:41–41:49](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41-Gen41:49) *Joseph*; [EST 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Esth10:1-Esth10:3) *Mordecai*; [DAN 1:1–1:21](https://ref.ly/Dan1:1-Dan1:21) *Daniel and his friends*

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 1:26-27; Psalms 122:6-9; Isaiah 54:11-14; Hebrews 11:10; Hebrews 11:15-16; Revelation 21:1-4; Revelation 21:22-22:5; John 3:16-17; John 6:33; 2 Corinthians 5:19; 1 John 4:14; Revelation 11:15; John 17:15-18; Jeremiah 29:7; Matthew 5:13-16; 1 Corinthians 5:9-10; James 1:27; Romans 12:2; James 4:4; 1 John 2:15-17; John 17:16; John 15:19; Hebrews 13:14; 1 Peter 2:11; Romans 13:1-7; Titus 3:1; Matthew 22:17-21; Mark 12:14-17; Luke 20:22-25; 1 Peter 2:13-17; Acts 4:18-20; Acts 5:27-29; Daniel 2:48-49; Genesis 41:41-49; Esther 10:1-3; Daniel 1:1-21
* **Associated Articles:** 4029: world, human beings in (ID: `4029@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4065: orderliness (ID: `4065@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5219: authority, of human institutions (ID: `5219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5255: citizenship (ID: `5255@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5326: government (ID: `5326@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5577: taxation (ID: `5577@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5587: trade (ID: `5587@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5709: marriage, purpose of (ID: `5709@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8243: ethics, social (ID: `8243@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5544: soldiers (id: 5544)

Active members of an army, whatever their rank. Paul uses the term frequently in his letters to draw attention to the fact that believers are engaged in spiritual warfare.

Armies of soldiers under different commanders
---------------------------------------------

### Moses' soldiers

[NUM 31:27–31:28](https://ref.ly/Num31:27-Num31:28), [NUM 31:32](https://ref.ly/Num31:32)

### Joshua's soldiers

[JOS 8:10–8:19](https://ref.ly/Josh8:10-Josh8:19)

### Abimelech's soldiers

[JDG 9:34–9:35](https://ref.ly/Judg9:34-Judg9:35)

### Benjamin's soldiers

[JDG 20:14–20:16](https://ref.ly/Judg20:14-Judg20:16)

### Philistine soldiers

[1SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:5)

### Saul's soldiers

[1SA 13:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:22), [1SA 15:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:4), [1SA 15:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:15), [1SA 15:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:21), [1SA 26:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:7)

### Hadadezer's soldiers

[2SA 8:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:4)

### Aramean soldiers

[2SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:6)

### Jehoahaz' soldiers

[2KI 13:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:7)

### David's soldiers

[2SA 3:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:23) *David's soldiers under Joab*; ; [2SA 23:8–23:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:8-2Sam23:39) parallel [1CH 11:10–11:47](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:10-1Chr11:47) *David's mighty men*; ; [2SA 24:1–24:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:1-2Sam24:9) parallel [1CH 21:1–21:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:1-1Chr21:16) *David displeased the LORD by counting his fighting men*; ; [1CH 12:23–12:38](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:23-1Chr12:38), [1CH 27:1–27:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:1-1Chr27:22)

### Zedekiah's soldiers

[2KI 25:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:5)

### Babylonian soldiers

[JER 41:3](https://ref.ly/Jer41:3)

Individual soldiers
-------------------

**[2KI 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:1)** *See also* [1SA 14:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:28); [2CH 17:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:17); [DAN 3:20–3:22](https://ref.ly/Dan3:20-Dan3:22)

Soldiers came to John the Baptist
---------------------------------

[LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14)

Soldiers involved in the last events of Jesus Christ's life on earth
--------------------------------------------------------------------

### In his arrest and trial

[LUK 23:11](https://ref.ly/Luke23:11), [LUK 23:36](https://ref.ly/Luke23:36); ; [JHN 18:3](https://ref.ly/John18:3), [JHN 18:12](https://ref.ly/John18:12), [JHN 19:2](https://ref.ly/John19:2)

### In his crucifixion

[MAT 27:27](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27); ; [JHN 19:16](https://ref.ly/John19:16), [JHN 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/John19:23-John19:24), [JHN 19:32–19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:32-John19:34)

### After his crucifixion

[MAT 27:65–27:66](https://ref.ly/Matt27:65-Matt27:66)

### After his resurrection

Soldiers involved in the lives of the apostles
----------------------------------------------

### Peter

[ACT 12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:4), [ACT 12:6](https://ref.ly/Acts12:6), [ACT 12:18](https://ref.ly/Acts12:18)

### Paul

[ACT 21:32](https://ref.ly/Acts21:32), [ACT 21:35](https://ref.ly/Acts21:35), [ACT 21:37](https://ref.ly/Acts21:37)

Soldiers used metaphorically
----------------------------

[EZK 27:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:27), [EZK 39:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:20); ; [JOL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Joel2:7); ; [AMO 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Amos2:15-Amos2:16)

Paul draws parallels between a soldier and a Christian
------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7)** *An apostle has a right to be supported in material things by those to whom he preaches.*

### Christians are called fellow\-soldiers

*See also* [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25); [PHM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:2)

### Christians must put Jesus Christ first

**[2TI 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:3-2Tim2:4)**

God as the commander of an army of soldiers
-------------------------------------------

**[PSA 89:8](https://ref.ly/Ps89:8)** *The term here translated “LORD God Almighty” can be translated “LORD God of hosts” or “LORD God of armies”.* *See also* [1KI 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:10); [JER 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jer5:14); [HOS 12:5](https://ref.ly/Hos12:5); [AMO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Amos4:13)

## 5545: spear (id: 5545)

A weapon of warfare for stabbing or throwing. God promises a time when they will be changed into instruments of peace.

People killed by spears
-----------------------

[NUM 25:6–25:8](https://ref.ly/Num25:6-Num25:8); ; [2SA 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:23), [2SA 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:14), [2SA 23:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:21) parallel [1CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:23)

Jesus Christ pierced by a spear
-------------------------------

**[JHN 19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:34)** *See also* [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5); [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10); [JHN 20:27](https://ref.ly/John20:27); [REV 1:7](https://ref.ly/Rev1:7)

Armies of Judah armed with spears
---------------------------------

[2CH 11:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:12), [2CH 14:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:8), [2CH 25:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5), [2CH 26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:14); ; [NEH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Neh4:13)

Armies of other nations that used spears
----------------------------------------

### The Philistines

[1SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:7)

### The Babylonians

[JER 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Jer6:22-Jer6:23)

### The Egyptians

[2SA 23:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:21) parallel [1CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:23); ; [JER 46:2–46:4](https://ref.ly/Jer46:2-Jer46:4)

### The Romans

[JHN 19:34](https://ref.ly/John19:34)

God's people are not to trust in spears but in God
--------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 17:47](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:47)** *See also* [1SA 17:45–17:46](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45-1Sam17:46)

King Saul and his spear
-----------------------

### Used against David

**[1SA 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10-1Sam18:11)** *See also* [1SA 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:10)

### Used against Jonathan

**[1SA 20:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:33)**

### David takes Saul's spear

*See also* [1SA 26:5–26:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:5-1Sam26:24)

Joshua's spear used as a sign of victory
----------------------------------------

**[JOS 8:18](https://ref.ly/Josh8:18)** *See also* [JOS 8:19](https://ref.ly/Josh8:19), [JOS 8:25–8:26](https://ref.ly/Josh8:25-Josh8:26)

Goliath's spear symbolises his strength
---------------------------------------

[1SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:7), [1SA 17:45–17:47](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45-1Sam17:47); ; [2SA 21:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:19); ; [1CH 20:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:5)

Spears will be turned into pruning hooks
----------------------------------------

**[ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4)** parallel [MIC 4:3](https://ref.ly/Mic4:3) *Some believe that the peace described in [Isa 2:1–4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:1-Isa2:4) has been inaugurated with the coming of Jesus Christ. Others maintain that it refers to conditions that will obtain during a future reign of Jesus Christ on earth. The details are generally taken to be figurative rather than literal.* *See also* [PSA 46:9](https://ref.ly/Ps46:9)

Pruning hooks will be turned into spears
----------------------------------------

**[JOL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Joel3:10)**

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 25:6-8; 2 Samuel 2:23; 2 Samuel 18:14; 2 Samuel 23:21; 1 Chronicles 11:23; John 19:34; Isaiah 53:5; Zechariah 12:10; John 20:27; Revelation 1:7; 2 Chronicles 11:12; 2 Chronicles 14:8; 2 Chronicles 25:5; 2 Chronicles 26:14; Nehemiah 4:13; 1 Samuel 17:7; Jeremiah 6:22-23; Jeremiah 46:2-4; 1 Samuel 17:47; 1 Samuel 17:45-46; 1 Samuel 18:10-11; 1 Samuel 19:9-10; 1 Samuel 20:33; 1 Samuel 26:5-24; Joshua 8:18; Joshua 8:19; Joshua 8:25-26; 1 Samuel 17:45-47; 2 Samuel 21:19; 1 Chronicles 20:5; Isaiah 2:4; Micah 4:3; Isaiah 2:1-4; Psalms 46:9; Joel 3:10

## 5546: speech (id: 5546)

The human capacity to express oneself and communicate with others through the spoken word. God addresses humans through the spoken word, and supremely in and through Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 5547: speech, power and significance of (ID: `5547@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5548: speech, divine (ID: `5548@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5549: speech, positive aspects of (ID: `5549@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5550: speech, negative aspects of (ID: `5550@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5547: speech, power and significance of (id: 5547)

A person's speech has an enormous influence for good or ill and is a sure guide to character.

The impact of speech
--------------------

### It has the power of life and death

**[PRO 18:21](https://ref.ly/Prov18:21)**; **[JAS 3:1–3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1-Jas3:12)** *See also* [PRO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Prov15:2), [PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4)

### It can bring ruin or joy to the speaker

**[PRO 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Prov12:13-Prov12:14)** *See also* [PSA 140:9](https://ref.ly/Ps140:9); [PRO 10:14](https://ref.ly/Prov10:14), [PRO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Prov13:3), [PRO 14:3](https://ref.ly/Prov14:3), [PRO 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:6-Prov18:7), [PRO 18:20](https://ref.ly/Prov18:20), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23); [ECC 10:12–10:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12-Eccl10:14)

### It can destroy others

**[PRO 11:9](https://ref.ly/Prov11:9)** *See also* [JOB 19:2](https://ref.ly/Job19:2); [PSA 55:20–55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:20-Ps55:21), [PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4), [PSA 64:3–64:4](https://ref.ly/Ps64:3-Ps64:4), [PSA 140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:3); [PRO 11:11](https://ref.ly/Prov11:11), [PRO 12:6](https://ref.ly/Prov12:6), [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18), [PRO 16:27](https://ref.ly/Prov16:27)

### It can bring life to others

**[PRO 10:11](https://ref.ly/Prov10:11)** *See also* [PRO 10:20–10:21](https://ref.ly/Prov10:20-Prov10:21), [PRO 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Prov10:31-Prov10:32), [PRO 16:21](https://ref.ly/Prov16:21), [PRO 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/Prov16:23-Prov16:24), [PRO 25:12](https://ref.ly/Prov25:12); [ISA 50:4](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4); [EPH 4:29](https://ref.ly/Eph4:29)

The timing of speech
--------------------

### There is an appropriate time for speaking

**[ECC 3:1–3:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:1-Eccl3:8)** *See also* [PRO 15:23](https://ref.ly/Prov15:23), [PRO 15:28](https://ref.ly/Prov15:28), [PRO 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/Prov25:11-Prov25:12)

### There is an appropriate time for silence

**[PRO 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/Prov11:12-Prov11:13)** *See also* [1SA 10:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:27); [PRO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19), [PRO 17:27–17:28](https://ref.ly/Prov17:27-Prov17:28), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23), [PRO 26:4](https://ref.ly/Prov26:4); [ECC 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2-Eccl5:3); [AMO 5:13](https://ref.ly/Amos5:13); [HAB 2:20](https://ref.ly/Hab2:20); [ZEP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:7); [JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)

Spoken witness produces faith
-----------------------------

**[ROM 10:17](https://ref.ly/Rom10:17)** *See also* [ISA 50:4](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4); [ACT 2:40–2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:40-Acts2:41), [ACT 28:23–28:24](https://ref.ly/Acts28:23-Acts28:24); [1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)

Jesus Christ was silent before his accusers
-------------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22-1Pet2:23)** *See also* [MAT 26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63) parallel [MRK 14:61](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61); [MAT 27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:14) parallel [MRK 15:5](https://ref.ly/Mark15:5); [LUK 23:9](https://ref.ly/Luke23:9); [JHN 19:8–19:9](https://ref.ly/John19:8-John19:9)

Speech is an expression of a person's character
-----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 12:34–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:34-Matt12:37)** *See also* [PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9), [PSA 15:1–15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:1-Ps15:5); [MAT 15:10–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10-Matt15:20) parallel [MRK 7:14–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14-Mark7:23); [LUK 6:45](https://ref.ly/Luke6:45); [JAS 1:26](https://ref.ly/Jas1:26); [REV 14:5](https://ref.ly/Rev14:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 18:21; James 3:1-12; Proverbs 15:2; Proverbs 15:4; Proverbs 12:13-14; Psalms 140:9; Proverbs 10:14; Proverbs 13:3; Proverbs 14:3; Proverbs 18:6-7; Proverbs 18:20; Proverbs 21:23; Ecclesiastes 10:12-14; Proverbs 11:9; Job 19:2; Psalms 55:20-21; Psalms 57:4; Psalms 64:3-4; Psalms 140:3; Proverbs 11:11; Proverbs 12:6; Proverbs 12:18; Proverbs 16:27; Proverbs 10:11; Proverbs 10:20-21; Proverbs 10:31-32; Proverbs 16:21; Proverbs 16:23-24; Proverbs 25:12; Isaiah 50:4; Ephesians 4:29; Ecclesiastes 3:1-8; Proverbs 15:23; Proverbs 15:28; Proverbs 25:11-12; Proverbs 11:12-13; 1 Samuel 10:27; Proverbs 10:19; Proverbs 17:27-28; Proverbs 26:4; Ecclesiastes 5:2-3; Amos 5:13; Habakkuk 2:20; Zephaniah 1:7; James 1:19; Romans 10:17; Acts 2:40-41; Acts 28:23-24; 1 Peter 3:15; 1 Peter 2:22-23; Matthew 26:63; Mark 14:61; Matthew 27:14; Mark 15:5; Luke 23:9; John 19:8-9; Matthew 12:34-37; Psalms 5:9; Psalms 15:1-5; Matthew 15:10-20; Mark 7:14-23; Luke 6:45; James 1:26; Revelation 14:5

## 5548: speech, divine (id: 5548)

Scripture assumes and affirms that God has spoken to many different people throughout the ages in a variety of ways.

Speech as a synonym for revelation
----------------------------------

**[HEB 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:2)** *See also* [DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3); [1SA 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:1), [1SA 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:7); [PSA 147:19](https://ref.ly/Ps147:19); [JHN 17:6–17:8](https://ref.ly/John17:6-John17:8), [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14); [1CO 2:6–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6-1Cor2:10)

Different modes of divine speech
--------------------------------

### The voice of nature

**[ISA 30:30–30:31](https://ref.ly/Isa30:30-Isa30:31)** *See also* [PSA 29:3–29:9](https://ref.ly/Ps29:3-Ps29:9); [JOB 37:1–37:5](https://ref.ly/Job37:1-Job37:5) *God's voice is depicted as thunder, the most awesome sound in nature*; [ISA 33:3](https://ref.ly/Isa33:3)

### A literal, audible voice

**[MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5)** parallels [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7), [LUK 9:34](https://ref.ly/Luke9:34) *See also* [1KI 19:9–19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:9-1Kgs19:13); [DAN 4:31](https://ref.ly/Dan4:31); [JHN 12:28–12:29](https://ref.ly/John12:28-John12:29); [ACT 9:3–9:7](https://ref.ly/Acts9:3-Acts9:7)

### Visions

**[GEN 46:2](https://ref.ly/Gen46:2)** *See also* [ISA 6:8–6:13](https://ref.ly/Isa6:8-Isa6:13); [JER 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Jer1:11-Jer1:14); [EZK 1:28–2:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:28-Ezek2:1); [ACT 18:9–18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:9-Acts18:10), [ACT 22:17–22:18](https://ref.ly/Acts22:17-Acts22:18)

### Dreams

**[GEN 31:24](https://ref.ly/Gen31:24)** *See also* [GEN 20:3–20:7](https://ref.ly/Gen20:3-Gen20:7); [1KI 3:5–3:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:5-1Kgs3:14); [JOB 33:15–33:18](https://ref.ly/Job33:15-Job33:18); [MAT 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20-Matt1:21), [MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13), [MAT 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Matt1:19-Matt1:20)

Divine speech through human messengers
--------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ

**[JHN 14:10](https://ref.ly/John14:10)** *See also* [JHN 7:16](https://ref.ly/John7:16), [JHN 8:28](https://ref.ly/John8:28), [JHN 12:49–12:50](https://ref.ly/John12:49-John12:50), [JHN 14:24](https://ref.ly/John14:24)

### Prophets

**[2PE 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:21)** *See also* [1SA 8:7–8:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:7-1Sam8:9); [1KI 17:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:24); [2CH 36:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15); [ISA 38:4–38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:4-Isa38:8); [EZK 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:10-Ezek3:11); [ZEC 7:4–7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:4-Zech7:12)

### Apostles

**[1TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:13)** *See also* [GAL 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Gal1:11-Gal1:12); [2PE 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:2); [1JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:3)

Divine speech through angels
----------------------------

**[ACT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Acts8:26)** *See also* [DAN 9:20–9:23](https://ref.ly/Dan9:20-Dan9:23); [LUK 1:11–1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:11-Luke1:20), [LUK 1:26–1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26-Luke1:38)

Examples of divine communication
--------------------------------

### Dialogues between individuals and God

**[GEN 4:9–4:15](https://ref.ly/Gen4:9-Gen4:15)** *See also* [GEN 18:16–18:33](https://ref.ly/Gen18:16-Gen18:33); [EXO 3:3–4:17](https://ref.ly/Exod3:3-Exod4:17); [ACT 9:10–9:16](https://ref.ly/Acts9:10-Acts9:16)

### Messages received by people

**[GEN 35:1](https://ref.ly/Gen35:1)** *See also* [GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3); [JOS 1:1–1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:1-Josh1:9); [2SA 7:4–7:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:4-2Sam7:16); [1KI 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:11-1Kgs6:13)

Descriptions of divine speech
-----------------------------

### The sound of God's voice

**[EZK 43:1–43:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:1-Ezek43:2)** *See also* [1KI 19:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:12); [REV 1:15](https://ref.ly/Rev1:15)

### The effect of God's word

**[ISA 55:10–55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10-Isa55:11)** *See also* [PSA 119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105); [JER 23:29](https://ref.ly/Jer23:29); [ZEC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Zech1:13); [HEB 4:12](https://ref.ly/Heb4:12); [REV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Rev19:15)

### The character of divine speech

**[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)** *See also* [PSA 18:30](https://ref.ly/Ps18:30), [PSA 19:7–19:11](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7-Ps19:11), [PSA 119:89](https://ref.ly/Ps119:89), [PSA 119:160](https://ref.ly/Ps119:160); [1PE 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:24-1Pet1:25); [ISA 40:6–40:8](https://ref.ly/Isa40:6-Isa40:8)

God's word is recognised and loved by his people
------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 119:103](https://ref.ly/Ps119:103)** *See also* [EZK 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:1-Ezek3:3); [JHN 10:3–10:5](https://ref.ly/John10:3-John10:5), [JHN 10:27](https://ref.ly/John10:27); [ACT 13:48](https://ref.ly/Acts13:48)

God's word is supremely revealed in Jesus Christ
------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14)** *See also* [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 1:1-2; Deuteronomy 8:3; 1 Samuel 3:1; 1 Samuel 3:7; Psalms 147:19; John 17:6-8; John 17:14; 1 Corinthians 2:6-10; Isaiah 30:30-31; Psalms 29:3-9; Job 37:1-5; Isaiah 33:3; Matthew 17:5; Mark 9:7; Luke 9:34; 1 Kings 19:9-13; Daniel 4:31; John 12:28-29; Acts 9:3-7; Genesis 46:2; Isaiah 6:8-13; Jeremiah 1:11-14; Ezekiel 1:28-2:1; Acts 18:9-10; Acts 22:17-18; Genesis 31:24; Genesis 20:3-7; 1 Kings 3:5-14; Job 33:15-18; Matthew 1:20-21; Matthew 2:13; Matthew 1:19-20; John 14:10; John 7:16; John 8:28; John 12:49-50; John 14:24; 2 Peter 1:21; 1 Samuel 8:7-9; 1 Kings 17:24; 2 Chronicles 36:15; Isaiah 38:4-8; Ezekiel 3:10-11; Zechariah 7:4-12; 1 Thessalonians 2:13; Galatians 1:11-12; 2 Peter 3:2; 1 John 1:1-3; Acts 8:26; Daniel 9:20-23; Luke 1:11-20; Luke 1:26-38; Genesis 4:9-15; Genesis 18:16-33; Exodus 3:3-4:17; Acts 9:10-16; Genesis 35:1; Genesis 12:1-3; Joshua 1:1-9; 2 Samuel 7:4-16; 1 Kings 6:11-13; Ezekiel 43:1-2; 1 Kings 19:12; Revelation 1:15; Isaiah 55:10-11; Psalms 119:105; Jeremiah 23:29; Zechariah 1:13; Hebrews 4:12; Revelation 19:15; Psalms 12:6; Psalms 18:30; Psalms 19:7-11; Psalms 119:89; Psalms 119:160; 1 Peter 1:24-25; Isaiah 40:6-8; Psalms 119:103; Ezekiel 3:1-3; John 10:3-5; John 10:27; Acts 13:48; John 1:14; Hebrews 1:2

## 5549: speech, positive aspects of (id: 5549)

The way in which believers speak should reflect their calling as Christians. Christian speech should be sincere, godly and honouring to God.

The desire for godly speech
---------------------------

### In oneself

**[PSA 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:14)** *See also* [PSA 17:3](https://ref.ly/Ps17:3), [PSA 39:1](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1), [PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)

### In others

**[EPH 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:29-Eph4:31)** *See also* [PRO 4:24](https://ref.ly/Prov4:24); [ECC 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:6); [EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4); [COL 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Col3:8-Col3:10); [1PE 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9-1Pet3:10); [PSA 34:12–34:13](https://ref.ly/Ps34:12-Ps34:13)

Characteristics of godly speech in relation to God
--------------------------------------------------

### It gives glory to God

**[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)** *See also* [PSA 34:1](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1), [PSA 35:28](https://ref.ly/Ps35:28), [PSA 51:15](https://ref.ly/Ps51:15); [1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)

### It gives thanks and praise to God

**[COL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:17)** *See also* [DEU 8:10](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10); [1CH 16:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:4), [1CH 16:7–16:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:7-1Chr16:8), [1CH 16:34–16:36](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:34-1Chr16:36); [PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4), [PSA 118:1](https://ref.ly/Ps118:1), [PSA 118:19](https://ref.ly/Ps118:19), [PSA 118:21](https://ref.ly/Ps118:21), [PSA 118:28–118:29](https://ref.ly/Ps118:28-Ps118:29); [JOL 2:26](https://ref.ly/Joel2:26); [MRK 8:6–8:7](https://ref.ly/Mark8:6-Mark8:7); [COL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:7); [1TH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:18); [JAS 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9-Jas3:10)

### It confesses Jesus Christ as Lord

**[ROM 10:9–10:10](https://ref.ly/Rom10:9-Rom10:10)** *See also* [MAT 10:32–10:33](https://ref.ly/Matt10:32-Matt10:33) parallel [LUK 12:8–12:9](https://ref.ly/Luke12:8-Luke12:9); [MAT 16:16](https://ref.ly/Matt16:16); [1CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:3)

Characteristics of godly speech in relation to others
-----------------------------------------------------

### It is gentle, gracious and tactful

**[COL 4:6](https://ref.ly/Col4:6)** *See also* [JDG 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Judg8:1-Judg8:3); [PRO 10:32](https://ref.ly/Prov10:32), [PRO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Prov15:1), [PRO 16:24](https://ref.ly/Prov16:24); [ECC 10:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12); [DAN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Dan2:14-Dan2:16); [1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)

### It is kind

**[PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25)** *See also* [GEN 50:19–50:21](https://ref.ly/Gen50:19-Gen50:21); [JOB 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Job4:3-Job4:4); [1CO 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:13); [2TI 2:24–2:25](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24-2Tim2:25)

### It is instructive and edifying

**[COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)** *See also* [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18), [PRO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Prov15:2), [PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4), [PRO 15:7](https://ref.ly/Prov15:7), [PRO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Prov20:15), [PRO 25:11–25:12](https://ref.ly/Prov25:11-Prov25:12), [PRO 31:26](https://ref.ly/Prov31:26); [MAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Mal2:7); [EPH 4:15](https://ref.ly/Eph4:15), [EPH 4:29](https://ref.ly/Eph4:29)

### It is prudent and restrained

**[PRO 17:27](https://ref.ly/Prov17:27)** *See also* [PRO 15:28](https://ref.ly/Prov15:28), [PRO 18:13](https://ref.ly/Prov18:13); [ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2), [ECC 9:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:17); [JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)

### It is honest and unadorned

**[MAT 5:37](https://ref.ly/Matt5:37)** *See also* [PSA 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Ps15:2-Ps15:3), [PSA 141:5](https://ref.ly/Ps141:5); [PRO 24:26](https://ref.ly/Prov24:26), [PRO 27:5–27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:5-Prov27:6), [PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9), [PRO 28:23](https://ref.ly/Prov28:23)

### It is truthful and sincere

**[ZEC 8:16](https://ref.ly/Zech8:16)** *See also* [EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16) parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20); [PRO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Prov12:17), [PRO 12:19](https://ref.ly/Prov12:19), [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22); [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6); [2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17), [2CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:2), [2CO 6:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:7); [EPH 4:25](https://ref.ly/Eph4:25)

### It is wise

**[PSA 37:30](https://ref.ly/Ps37:30)** *See also* [PRO 10:13](https://ref.ly/Prov10:13), [PRO 10:31](https://ref.ly/Prov10:31), [PRO 13:14](https://ref.ly/Prov13:14); [ACT 6:10](https://ref.ly/Acts6:10)

### It will speak of God and his works

**[DEU 6:6–6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:9)** *See also* [PSA 40:9–40:10](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9-Ps40:10), [PSA 145:7](https://ref.ly/Ps145:7); [MAL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Mal3:16)

Jesus Christ's speech was perfect and unique
--------------------------------------------

**[JHN 7:46](https://ref.ly/John7:46)** *See also* [MAT 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28-Matt7:29), [MAT 13:54](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54) parallel [MRK 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2); [LUK 4:22](https://ref.ly/Luke4:22); [1PE 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22-1Pet2:23); [ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9)

### Speech as a means of blessing people

**[NUM 6:22–6:27](https://ref.ly/Num6:22-Num6:27)** *See also* [GEN 1:22](https://ref.ly/Gen1:22), [GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28); [DEU 21:5](https://ref.ly/Deut21:5); [1CH 23:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 19:14; Psalms 17:3; Psalms 39:1; Psalms 141:3; Ephesians 4:29-31; Proverbs 4:24; Ecclesiastes 5:6; Ephesians 5:4; Colossians 3:8-10; 1 Peter 3:9-10; Psalms 34:12-13; Hebrews 13:15; Psalms 34:1; Psalms 35:28; Psalms 51:15; 1 Peter 2:9; Colossians 3:17; Deuteronomy 8:10; 1 Chronicles 16:4; 1 Chronicles 16:7-8; 1 Chronicles 16:34-36; Psalms 100:4; Psalms 118:1; Psalms 118:19; Psalms 118:21; Psalms 118:28-29; Joel 2:26; Mark 8:6-7; Colossians 2:7; 1 Thessalonians 5:18; James 3:9-10; Romans 10:9-10; Matthew 10:32-33; Luke 12:8-9; Matthew 16:16; 1 Corinthians 12:3; Colossians 4:6; Judges 8:1-3; Proverbs 10:32; Proverbs 15:1; Proverbs 16:24; Ecclesiastes 10:12; Daniel 2:14-16; 1 Peter 3:15; Proverbs 12:25; Genesis 50:19-21; Job 4:3-4; 1 Corinthians 4:13; 2 Timothy 2:24-25; Colossians 3:16; Proverbs 12:18; Proverbs 15:2; Proverbs 15:4; Proverbs 15:7; Proverbs 20:15; Proverbs 25:11-12; Proverbs 31:26; Malachi 2:7; Ephesians 4:15; Ephesians 4:29; Proverbs 17:27; Proverbs 15:28; Proverbs 18:13; Ecclesiastes 5:2; Ecclesiastes 9:17; James 1:19; Matthew 5:37; Psalms 15:2-3; Psalms 141:5; Proverbs 24:26; Proverbs 27:5-6; Proverbs 27:9; Proverbs 28:23; Zechariah 8:16; Exodus 20:16; Deuteronomy 5:20; Proverbs 12:17; Proverbs 12:19; Proverbs 12:22; Malachi 2:6; 2 Corinthians 2:17; 2 Corinthians 4:2; 2 Corinthians 6:7; Ephesians 4:25; Psalms 37:30; Proverbs 10:13; Proverbs 10:31; Proverbs 13:14; Acts 6:10; Deuteronomy 6:6-9; Psalms 40:9-10; Psalms 145:7; Malachi 3:16; John 7:46; Matthew 7:28-29; Matthew 13:54; Mark 6:2; Luke 4:22; 1 Peter 2:22-23; Isaiah 53:9; Numbers 6:22-27; Genesis 1:22; Genesis 1:28; Deuteronomy 21:5; 1 Chronicles 23:13

## 5550: speech, negative aspects of (id: 5550)

The sinfulness of fallen human nature can express itself in speech, provoking God's displeasure and causing enmity and division amongst people.

Evil speech comes from a corrupt heart
--------------------------------------

**[MAT 12:33–12:35](https://ref.ly/Matt12:33-Matt12:35)** *See also* [PRO 2:12–2:15](https://ref.ly/Prov2:12-Prov2:15), [PRO 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Prov24:1-Prov24:2); [MAT 15:10–15:11](https://ref.ly/Matt15:10-Matt15:11) parallel [MRK 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/Mark7:14-Mark7:15); [LUK 6:43–6:45](https://ref.ly/Luke6:43-Luke6:45)

Evil speech is a feature of a corrupt society
---------------------------------------------

**[MIC 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Mic6:12-Mic6:13)** *See also* [ISA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:5), [ISA 59:1–59:4](https://ref.ly/Isa59:1-Isa59:4), [ISA 59:12–59:15](https://ref.ly/Isa59:12-Isa59:15); [JER 9:1–9:9](https://ref.ly/Jer9:1-Jer9:9); [ROM 1:29–1:32](https://ref.ly/Rom1:29-Rom1:32); [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5)

Evil speech provokes God's displeasure
--------------------------------------

**[PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22)** *See also* [PRO 6:16–6:19](https://ref.ly/Prov6:16-Prov6:19)

Kinds of evil speech
--------------------

### Profaning God's name

**[EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7)** parallel [DEU 5:11](https://ref.ly/Deut5:11) *See also* [EXO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Exod22:28); [LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12); [PSA 139:19–139:20](https://ref.ly/Ps139:19-Ps139:20)

### Lies and deceit

**[PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9)** *See also* [PSA 36:3](https://ref.ly/Ps36:3), [PSA 52:1–52:4](https://ref.ly/Ps52:1-Ps52:4), [PSA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Ps62:4); [PRO 14:5](https://ref.ly/Prov14:5), [PRO 14:25](https://ref.ly/Prov14:25), [PRO 15:4](https://ref.ly/Prov15:4), [PRO 17:20](https://ref.ly/Prov17:20)

### Foolish boasting

**[PSA 94:4](https://ref.ly/Ps94:4)** *See also* [PSA 10:2–10:6](https://ref.ly/Ps10:2-Ps10:6), [PSA 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Ps12:3-Ps12:4); [ISA 10:12–10:14](https://ref.ly/Isa10:12-Isa10:14); [2TH 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:3-2Thess2:4); [JAS 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:16); [2PE 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17-2Pet2:18)

### Ridicule and insults

**[PRO 11:12](https://ref.ly/Prov11:12)** *See also* [1SA 17:41–17:44](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:41-1Sam17:44); [2KI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23-2Kgs2:24); [NEH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Neh2:19); [PRO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Prov9:7); [LAM 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:15-Lam2:16); [MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22), [MAT 27:39–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39-Matt27:44) parallels [MRK 15:29–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29-Mark15:32), [LUK 23:35–23:39](https://ref.ly/Luke23:35-Luke23:39); [JHN 9:28–9:29](https://ref.ly/John9:28-John9:29)

### Criticism and complaint

**[NUM 12:1–12:15](https://ref.ly/Num12:1-Num12:15)** *See also* [NUM 16:1–16:50](https://ref.ly/Num16:1-Num16:50); [1SA 17:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:28); [PRO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Prov19:13), [PRO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Prov21:9), [PRO 21:19](https://ref.ly/Prov21:19), [PRO 25:24](https://ref.ly/Prov25:24); [ACT 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/Acts11:2-Acts11:3); [PHP 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14-Phil2:15)

### Slander

**[LEV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Lev19:16)** *See also* [1KI 21:1–21:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1-1Kgs21:14); [PRO 10:18](https://ref.ly/Prov10:18); [JAS 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11)

### Cursing

**[EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17)** *See also* [2SA 16:5–16:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:5-2Sam16:8); [PRO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Prov20:20); [JAS 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9-Jas3:10)

### Gossip

**[PRO 16:28](https://ref.ly/Prov16:28)** *See also* [PRO 11:13](https://ref.ly/Prov11:13), [PRO 17:9](https://ref.ly/Prov17:9), [PRO 20:19](https://ref.ly/Prov20:19)

### Obscenity

**[EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4)** *See also* [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8)

Evil speech is silenced
-----------------------

### By God's actions

**[PSA 107:41–107:42](https://ref.ly/Ps107:41-Ps107:42)** *See also* [PSA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Ps63:11); [EZK 16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:63)

### By believers' blameless lives

**[1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)** *See also* [TIT 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Titus2:7-Titus2:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 12:33-35; Proverbs 2:12-15; Proverbs 24:1-2; Matthew 15:10-11; Mark 7:14-15; Luke 6:43-45; Micah 6:12-13; Isaiah 6:5; Isaiah 59:1-4; Isaiah 59:12-15; Jeremiah 9:1-9; Romans 1:29-32; 2 Timothy 3:1-5; Proverbs 12:22; Proverbs 6:16-19; Exodus 20:7; Deuteronomy 5:11; Exodus 22:28; Leviticus 19:12; Psalms 139:19-20; Psalms 5:9; Psalms 36:3; Psalms 52:1-4; Psalms 62:4; Proverbs 14:5; Proverbs 14:25; Proverbs 15:4; Proverbs 17:20; Psalms 94:4; Psalms 10:2-6; Psalms 12:3-4; Isaiah 10:12-14; 2 Thessalonians 2:3-4; James 4:13-16; 2 Peter 2:17-18; Proverbs 11:12; 1 Samuel 17:41-44; 2 Kings 2:23-24; Nehemiah 2:19; Proverbs 9:7; Lamentations 2:15-16; Matthew 5:22; Matthew 27:39-44; Mark 15:29-32; Luke 23:35-39; John 9:28-29; Numbers 12:1-15; Numbers 16:1-50; 1 Samuel 17:28; Proverbs 19:13; Proverbs 21:9; Proverbs 21:19; Proverbs 25:24; Acts 11:2-3; Philippians 2:14-15; Leviticus 19:16; 1 Kings 21:1-14; Proverbs 10:18; James 4:11; Exodus 21:17; 2 Samuel 16:5-8; Proverbs 20:20; James 3:9-10; Proverbs 16:28; Proverbs 11:13; Proverbs 17:9; Proverbs 20:19; Ephesians 5:4; Colossians 3:8; Psalms 107:41-42; Psalms 63:11; Ezekiel 16:63; 1 Peter 2:15; Titus 2:7-8

## 5552: spies (id: 5552)

Spies were used to collect information, usually of a military nature
--------------------------------------------------------------------

Joseph's brothers accused of being spies: [GEN 42:9–42:11](https://ref.ly/Gen42:9-Gen42:11), [GEN 42:14–42:16](https://ref.ly/Gen42:14-Gen42:16), [GEN 42:30–42:34](https://ref.ly/Gen42:30-Gen42:34); [NUM 13:1–13:20](https://ref.ly/Num13:1-Num13:20), [NUM 21:32](https://ref.ly/Num21:32); ; [DEU 1:22](https://ref.ly/Deut1:22); ; [JOS 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Josh2:1-Josh2:3); [JOS 6:17](https://ref.ly/Josh6:17), [JOS 6:22](https://ref.ly/Josh6:22), [JOS 6:25](https://ref.ly/Josh6:25); ; [HEB 11:31](https://ref.ly/Heb11:31); ; [JAS 2:25](https://ref.ly/Jas2:25); [JOS 7:2](https://ref.ly/Josh7:2); ; [JDG 1:23–1:24](https://ref.ly/Judg1:23-Judg1:24), [JDG 18:2](https://ref.ly/Judg18:2), [JDG 18:14](https://ref.ly/Judg18:14), [JDG 18:17](https://ref.ly/Judg18:17); ; [2SA 10:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:3) parallel [1CH 19:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:3)

Spies were used to incriminate Jesus Christ and the first Christians
--------------------------------------------------------------------

[LUK 20:20–20:22](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:22) parallels [MAT 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:16), [MRK 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:14); ; [GAL 2:4](https://ref.ly/Gal2:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 42:9-11; Genesis 42:14-16; Genesis 42:30-34; Numbers 13:1-20; Numbers 21:32; Deuteronomy 1:22; Joshua 2:1-3; Joshua 6:17; Joshua 6:22; Joshua 6:25; Hebrews 11:31; James 2:25; Joshua 7:2; Judges 1:23-24; Judges 18:2; Judges 18:14; Judges 18:17; 2 Samuel 10:3; 1 Chronicles 19:3; Luke 20:20-22; Matthew 22:15-16; Mark 12:13-14; Galatians 2:4

## 5553: stairways (id: 5553)

Steps which provide access to upper levels, usually of buildings. The term is also used figuratively as a means of ascent from earth to heaven.

Stairways relating to places of worship
---------------------------------------

### In Solomon's temple

**[1KI 6:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:8)**

### Used by the Levites

**[NEH 9:4](https://ref.ly/Neh9:4)**

### In Ezekiel's vision of the new temple

**[EZK 40:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:49)** *See also* [EZK 40:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:6), [EZK 40:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:22), [EZK 40:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:26), [EZK 40:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:31), [EZK 40:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:34), [EZK 40:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:37), [EZK 40:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:49), [EZK 41:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:7), [EZK 43:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:17)

### Steps forbidden for the altar

**[EXO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Exod20:26)**

Stairways relating to royalty
-----------------------------

**[1KI 10:19–10:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:19-1Kgs10:20)** parallel [2CH 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:18-2Chr9:19) *See also* [2KI 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:13), [2KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:11) parallel [ISA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Isa38:8)

Stairways in city walls and fortresses
--------------------------------------

**[NEH 3:15](https://ref.ly/Neh3:15)** *See also* [NEH 3:19](https://ref.ly/Neh3:19), [NEH 12:37](https://ref.ly/Neh12:37); [ACT 21:35](https://ref.ly/Acts21:35), [ACT 21:40](https://ref.ly/Acts21:40)

Stairways in houses
-------------------

### For access to upper floors and basements

**[ACT 9:39](https://ref.ly/Acts9:39)** *See also* [2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33); [1KI 17:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:19); [ACT 1:13](https://ref.ly/Acts1:13), [ACT 9:37](https://ref.ly/Acts9:37), [ACT 10:20](https://ref.ly/Acts10:20), [ACT 20:8](https://ref.ly/Acts20:8), [ACT 20:11](https://ref.ly/Acts20:11)

Figurative use of stairways
---------------------------

**[GEN 28:12](https://ref.ly/Gen28:12)** *See also* [JHN 1:51](https://ref.ly/John1:51)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 6:8; Nehemiah 9:4; Ezekiel 40:49; Ezekiel 40:6; Ezekiel 40:22; Ezekiel 40:26; Ezekiel 40:31; Ezekiel 40:34; Ezekiel 40:37; Ezekiel 41:7; Ezekiel 43:17; Exodus 20:26; 1 Kings 10:19-20; 2 Chronicles 9:18-19; 2 Kings 9:13; 2 Kings 20:11; Isaiah 38:8; Nehemiah 3:15; Nehemiah 3:19; Nehemiah 12:37; Acts 21:35; Acts 21:40; Acts 9:39; 2 Samuel 18:33; 1 Kings 17:19; Acts 1:13; Acts 9:37; Acts 10:20; Acts 20:8; Acts 20:11; Genesis 28:12; John 1:51
* **Associated Articles:** 1670: symbols (ID: `1670@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5340: house (ID: `5340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5506: roof (ID: `5506@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5604: walls (ID: `5604@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5554: status (id: 5554)

A person's standing in society in the sight of others. The status of individuals in the sight of the world often differs radically from their status in the sight of God, but it is the latter that really matters.

Factors contributing to high social status
------------------------------------------

### High status associated with a position of secular authority

**[DAN 2:37–2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37-Dan2:38)** *See also* [2SA 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:6); [1CH 29:26–29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:26-1Chr29:28)

### High status based upon a position of domestic authority

**[MAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6)** *See also* [PSA 123:2](https://ref.ly/Ps123:2); [LUK 17:7–17:10](https://ref.ly/Luke17:7-Luke17:10); [1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3)

### High status owing to recognition of religious authority

**[ACT 5:34](https://ref.ly/Acts5:34)** *See also* [MAT 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:2), [MAT 23:8–23:10](https://ref.ly/Matt23:8-Matt23:10); [ACT 23:1–23:5](https://ref.ly/Acts23:1-Acts23:5)

### High status owing to pious conduct

**[MAT 23:5–23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5-Matt23:7)** parallels [MRK 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:39), [LUK 20:45–20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:46) *See also* [MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2), [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5), [MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16); [LUK 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/Luke7:4-Luke7:5)

### High status based on wealth

**[PRO 19:4](https://ref.ly/Prov19:4)** *See also* [1KI 10:4–10:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:4-1Kgs10:7) parallel [2CH 9:3–9:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:3-2Chr9:6); [JOB 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Job1:1-Job1:3); [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4)

Factors contributing to low social status
-----------------------------------------

### Low status on account of occupation

**[MAT 9:11](https://ref.ly/Matt9:11)** parallels [MRK 2:16](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16), [LUK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Luke5:30) *See also* [LUK 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:2), [LUK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11)

### Low status through sickness

**[LEV 13:45–13:46](https://ref.ly/Lev13:45-Lev13:46)** *See also* [MAT 20:29–20:31](https://ref.ly/Matt20:29-Matt20:31) parallels [MRK 10:46–10:48](https://ref.ly/Mark10:46-Mark10:48), [LUK 18:35–18:39](https://ref.ly/Luke18:35-Luke18:39); [LUK 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12-Luke17:13)

### Low status through poverty

**[AMO 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11-Amos5:12)** *See also* [JOB 24:1–24:12](https://ref.ly/Job24:1-Job24:12); [LUK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Luke16:20); [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4)

Status in God's kingdom
-----------------------

### The reversal of human status

**[MAT 5:3–5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3-Matt5:6)** parallel [LUK 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Luke6:20-Luke6:21) *See also* [MAT 19:13–19:15](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:15) parallels [MRK 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:16), [LUK 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15-Luke18:17); [MAT 19:16–19:30](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16-Matt19:30) parallels [MRK 10:17–10:31](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17-Mark10:31), [LUK 18:18–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18-Luke18:30); [LUK 1:52–1:53](https://ref.ly/Luke1:52-Luke1:53), [LUK 16:19–16:26](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19-Luke16:26); [1CO 1:26–1:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:26-1Cor1:29)

### Believers must take the lowest status

**[MAT 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1-Matt18:5)** parallels [MRK 9:33–9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:33-Mark9:37), [LUK 9:46–9:48](https://ref.ly/Luke9:46-Luke9:48) *See also* [MAT 20:20–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20-Matt20:28) parallel [MRK 10:35–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35-Mark10:45); [LUK 14:7–14:11](https://ref.ly/Luke14:7-Luke14:11); [JHN 13:12–13:15](https://ref.ly/John13:12-John13:15); [PHP 2:3–2:5](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3-Phil2:5)

### All believers enjoy equal status before God

**[GAL 3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:28)** *See also* [1CO 7:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:22), [1CO 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:11-1Cor11:12); [PHM 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:15-Phlm1:17); [JAS 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jas1:9-Jas1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Daniel 2:37-38; 2 Samuel 15:1-6; 1 Chronicles 29:26-28; Malachi 1:6; Psalms 123:2; Luke 17:7-10; 1 Corinthians 11:3; Acts 5:34; Matthew 23:1-2; Matthew 23:8-10; Acts 23:1-5; Matthew 23:5-7; Mark 12:38-39; Luke 20:45-46; Matthew 6:2; Matthew 6:5; Matthew 6:16; Luke 7:4-5; Proverbs 19:4; 1 Kings 10:4-7; 2 Chronicles 9:3-6; Job 1:1-3; James 2:1-4; Matthew 9:11; Mark 2:16; Luke 5:30; Luke 15:1-2; Luke 18:11; Leviticus 13:45-46; Matthew 20:29-31; Mark 10:46-48; Luke 18:35-39; Luke 17:12-13; Amos 5:11-12; Job 24:1-12; Luke 16:20; Matthew 5:3-6; Luke 6:20-21; Matthew 19:13-15; Mark 10:13-16; Luke 18:15-17; Matthew 19:16-30; Mark 10:17-31; Luke 18:18-30; Luke 1:52-53; Luke 16:19-26; 1 Corinthians 1:26-29; Matthew 18:1-5; Mark 9:33-37; Luke 9:46-48; Matthew 20:20-28; Mark 10:35-45; Luke 14:7-11; John 13:12-15; Philippians 2:3-5; Galatians 3:28; 1 Corinthians 7:22; 1 Corinthians 11:11-12; Philemon 1:15-17; James 1:9-10
