## 5555: stealing (id: 5555)

Theft is always condemned in Scripture, though the possibility of mitigating circumstances is recognised. Converted thieves must show their repentance, if possible, in a practical way.

Stealing is condemned as a sin
------------------------------

### It is prohibited

**[EXO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Exod20:15)** parallel [DEU 5:19](https://ref.ly/Deut5:19) *See also* [LEV 19:11](https://ref.ly/Lev19:11); [PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10); [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19) parallel [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20); [ROM 2:21](https://ref.ly/Rom2:21), [ROM 13:9](https://ref.ly/Rom13:9)

### Punishment for stealing

**[1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10)** *See also* [EXO 21:16](https://ref.ly/Exod21:16), [EXO 22:1–22:4](https://ref.ly/Exod22:1-Exod22:4); [LEV 6:2–6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:2-Lev6:5)

### The disgrace of thieves

**[JER 2:26](https://ref.ly/Jer2:26)**

### The company of thieves is to be avoided

*See also* [PRO 29:24](https://ref.ly/Prov29:24); [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23)

### Stealing is denied

[GEN 44:8](https://ref.ly/Gen44:8); ; [1SA 12:3–12:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:3-1Sam12:5)

Mitigating circumstances for stealing
-------------------------------------

### Poverty

**[PRO 6:30–6:31](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30-Prov6:31)** *See also* [PRO 30:7–30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:7-Prov30:9)

Stealing is commonplace in this world
-------------------------------------

### It is part of life in a sinful world

**[HOS 4:2](https://ref.ly/Hos4:2)**; **[MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19)** *See also* [MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19)

### Roadside robbery

**[LUK 10:30](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30)** *See also* [PRO 1:10–1:15](https://ref.ly/Prov1:10-Prov1:15); [2CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:26)

### Sheep stealing

[JOB 24:2](https://ref.ly/Job24:2); ; [2SA 12:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:4)

### Dishonesty in trade

[JER 22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:17); ; [EZK 28:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:18); ; [HOS 12:7](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7); ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

### Dishonest tax collectors

[LUK 3:13](https://ref.ly/Luke3:13), [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

### Theft in the temple

[MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13) parallels [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46); ; [JER 7:11](https://ref.ly/Jer7:11)

### Theft in the family

[GEN 31:19](https://ref.ly/Gen31:19); ; [JDG 17:1–17:2](https://ref.ly/Judg17:1-Judg17:2)

### Wife stealing

[2SA 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:3)

### Kidnapping

[2KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:2) parallel [2CH 22:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:11)

### Temple robbers

[ACT 19:37](https://ref.ly/Acts19:37); ; [ROM 2:22](https://ref.ly/Rom2:22)

### Body stealing

[MAT 27:64](https://ref.ly/Matt27:64)

Examples of stealing
--------------------

[GEN 31:19](https://ref.ly/Gen31:19) *Rachel*; [JOS 7:11](https://ref.ly/Josh7:11), [JOS 7:20–7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:20-Josh7:21); [JDG 17:1–17:2](https://ref.ly/Judg17:1-Judg17:2) *Micah the Ephraimite*; [MAT 27:38](https://ref.ly/Matt27:38) parallel [MRK 15:27](https://ref.ly/Mark15:27); ; [MAT 27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:44); ; [LUK 23:32](https://ref.ly/Luke23:32); [JHN 12:6](https://ref.ly/John12:6) *Judas*

Stealing as an image
--------------------

### Of forbidden pleasures

[PRO 9:17](https://ref.ly/Prov9:17)

### Of a captivated heart

[2SA 15:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:6); ; [SNG 4:9](https://ref.ly/Song4:9)

### Of unexpected loss

[PRO 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:10-Prov6:11), [PRO 24:33–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:33-Prov24:34)

### Of God's judgment

**[1TH 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:2)** *See also* [JER 49:9–49:10](https://ref.ly/Jer49:9-Jer49:10) parallel [OBA 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Obad1:5-Obad1:6); [MAT 24:42–24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:42-Matt24:44); [2PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:10)

Spiritual stealing
------------------

### Stealing from God

**[MAL 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Mal3:8-Mal3:9)**

### Lying prophets

*See also* [JER 23:30](https://ref.ly/Jer23:30)

### False shepherds

**[JHN 10:1](https://ref.ly/John10:1)** *See also* [JHN 10:8](https://ref.ly/John10:8), [JHN 10:10](https://ref.ly/John10:10)

Converted thieves
-----------------

[LUK 23:39–23:43](https://ref.ly/Luke23:39-Luke23:43) *the thief on the cross*; ; [1CO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:11) *some of the Corinthians*

### Repentance must be borne out in practical actions

**[EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28)** *See also* [EZK 33:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:15); [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8) *Zacchaeus*; [TIT 2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:10)

There is no stealing in heaven
------------------------------

**[MAT 6:20](https://ref.ly/Matt6:20)**

## 5556: stewardship (id: 5556)

The careful use, control and management of the possessions of another that have been entrusted to one. The term is also used to refer to the responsible use of wealth and possessions by Christians.

Individuals acting as stewards
------------------------------

### Adam in the Garden of Eden

**[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)**

### Joseph in Potiphar's household

**[GEN 39:4–39:6](https://ref.ly/Gen39:4-Gen39:6)**

### Daniel as administrator in Babylon

**[DAN 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Dan6:1-Dan6:3)**

Groups acting as stewards
-------------------------

### The priests serving in the tabernacle

*See also* [LEV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Lev22:9); [1SA 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:15)

### The seven chosen by the Jerusalem church

[ACT 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:6)

Household stewards
------------------

[GEN 43:16](https://ref.ly/Gen43:16), [GEN 44:1–44:12](https://ref.ly/Gen44:1-Gen44:12); ; [2SA 16:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:1), [2SA 19:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:17); ; [EST 1:8](https://ref.ly/Esth1:8)

Jesus Christ's teaching on stewardship
--------------------------------------

### Using parables to emphasise accountability

**[LUK 16:1–16:12](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1-Luke16:12)** *See also* [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30); [LUK 19:12–19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:27)

### Emphasising each individual's responsibility

**[LUK 12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:48)** *See also* [MAT 12:36](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36)

The apostles continued this teaching
------------------------------------

**[ROM 14:12](https://ref.ly/Rom14:12)** *See also* [1PE 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:4-1Pet4:5)

To be a good steward is an honourable thing
-------------------------------------------

**[1TI 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:13)** *See also* [PRO 27:18](https://ref.ly/Prov27:18)

Christians are entrusted with the stewardship of the gospel
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:1-1Cor4:2)** *See also* [1CO 9:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:17); [2CO 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:19-2Cor5:21); [GAL 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Gal1:15-Gal1:16), [GAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7); [1TH 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:4); [1TI 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:11), [1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14), [1TI 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20); [2TI 1:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:14); [TIT 1:3](https://ref.ly/Titus1:3), [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7)

Christians are to be wise stewards of their God\-given gifts
------------------------------------------------------------

### They are to use their gifts to benefit others

**[1PE 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10-1Pet4:11)**

### They are to care for their bodies

**[1CO 6:18–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:18-1Cor6:20)**

## 5557: stoning (id: 5557)

To strike an offender with stones until he or she is dead. Stoning normally took place outside the city walls. The prosecution witnesses, of whom there must be at least two, had to throw the first stones, followed if necessary by the spectators.

Stoning was the punishment for certain offences under the Mosaic law
--------------------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Lev20:1-Lev20:2), [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27), [LEV 24:13–24:14](https://ref.ly/Lev24:13-Lev24:14); ; [DEU 13:6–13:10](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6-Deut13:10), [DEU 17:2–17:7](https://ref.ly/Deut17:2-Deut17:7), [DEU 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:24)

Examples of stoning in upholding the Mosaic law
-----------------------------------------------

[LEV 24:23](https://ref.ly/Lev24:23); ; [NUM 15:32–15:36](https://ref.ly/Num15:32-Num15:36); ; [JOS 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:24-Josh7:25); ; [1KI 21:13–21:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:13-1Kgs21:15); ; [JHN 8:1–8:11](https://ref.ly/John8:1-John8:11)

Examples of the threatened or actual stoning of God's servants
--------------------------------------------------------------

## 5558: storing (id: 5558)

A laying up of surpluses for times of scarcity. It includes such facilities as store cities and storehouses and also refers to hoarding treasures. Figuratively the word is used of the accumulation of human sin and God's consequent judgment.

Store cities
------------

**[EXO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11)** *See also* [GEN 41:35](https://ref.ly/Gen41:35), [GEN 41:48](https://ref.ly/Gen41:48); [1KI 9:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:19); [2CH 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:4-2Chr8:6), [2CH 16:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:4), [2CH 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:12-2Chr17:13)

Storehouses
-----------

**[GEN 41:56](https://ref.ly/Gen41:56)** *See also* [1KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:7), [1KI 7:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:51) parallel [2CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:1); [2KI 14:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:14), [2KI 18:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:15), [2KI 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:13); [1CH 26:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:15), [1CH 27:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:25); [2CH 8:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:15), [2CH 16:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:2), [2CH 31:10–31:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:10-2Chr31:11), [2CH 32:27–32:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:28); [EZR 2:69](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:69); [EST 3:9](https://ref.ly/Esth3:9); [PRO 24:4](https://ref.ly/Prov24:4); [MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10); [MRK 12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:44) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4)

The contents of storehouses
---------------------------

### Materials kept in storage

**[GEN 6:21](https://ref.ly/Gen6:21)** *See also* [GEN 41:34–41:35](https://ref.ly/Gen41:34-Gen41:35), [GEN 41:49](https://ref.ly/Gen41:49); [1KI 7:51](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:51) parallel [2CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:1); [2CH 11:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:11), [2CH 31:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:5), [2CH 31:11–31:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:11-2Chr31:12), [2CH 32:27–32:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:28); [EZR 4:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:15), [EZR 5:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:17), [EZR 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:1-Ezra6:2), [EZR 6:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:5); [NEH 10:37–10:39](https://ref.ly/Neh10:37-Neh10:39); [ISA 10:28](https://ref.ly/Isa10:28); [JER 41:8](https://ref.ly/Jer41:8)

### Empty storehouses a disaster

**[JOL 1:17](https://ref.ly/Joel1:17)** *See also* [GEN 41:35–41:36](https://ref.ly/Gen41:35-Gen41:36); [1CH 27:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:25)

--------------------------------------------

## 5559: stress (id: 5559)

A state of personal anxiety, strain or tension resulting from the pressures of human life. Scripture indicates a number of causes and means of dealing with stress.

The experience of stress
------------------------

**[PSA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Ps38:8)**; **[MRK 14:33–14:39](https://ref.ly/Mark14:33-Mark14:39)** parallels [MAT 26:37–26:44](https://ref.ly/Matt26:37-Matt26:44), [LUK 22:39–22:46](https://ref.ly/Luke22:39-Luke22:46)**[2CO 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:8)** *See also* [JOB 6:2](https://ref.ly/Job6:2); [PSA 77:2](https://ref.ly/Ps77:2); [JER 4:19](https://ref.ly/Jer4:19); [JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/John12:27), [JHN 13:21](https://ref.ly/John13:21); [HEB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7)

Possible causes of stress
-------------------------

### Disobedience to God's will

**[DEU 28:65](https://ref.ly/Deut28:65)** *See also* [2SA 24:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:14) parallel [1CH 21:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:13); [PSA 38:4](https://ref.ly/Ps38:4); [MAT 14:9](https://ref.ly/Matt14:9) parallel [MRK 6:26](https://ref.ly/Mark6:26)

### Family ties

**[1CO 7:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:33)** *See also* [GEN 21:11](https://ref.ly/Gen21:11), [GEN 43:6](https://ref.ly/Gen43:6); [1SA 1:6–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:6-1Sam1:16)

### The burdens of leadership

**[2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:28)** *See also* [NUM 11:14](https://ref.ly/Num11:14); [2CO 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:4); [HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)

### Threats of injury or violence

**[LAM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Lam1:20)** *See also* [GEN 32:7](https://ref.ly/Gen32:7); [1SA 28:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:5); [1KI 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:2); [EST 7:4](https://ref.ly/Esth7:4); [PSA 56:1–56:2](https://ref.ly/Ps56:1-Ps56:2); [JER 19:9](https://ref.ly/Jer19:9)

### Unjust leaders

[EXO 5:19](https://ref.ly/Exod5:19); ; [NEH 9:37](https://ref.ly/Neh9:37)

### The wickedness of others

**[1KI 19:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:14)** *See also* [ROM 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Rom9:2-Rom9:3); [2PE 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:7-2Pet2:8)

### Academic study

[ECC 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13)

The effects of stress
---------------------

**[PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25)**; **[MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22)** parallels [MRK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14) *See also* [1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4); [PSA 31:9–31:10](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9-Ps31:10); [LUK 10:40–10:41](https://ref.ly/Luke10:40-Luke10:41)

--------------------------------------

**[PSA 102:2](https://ref.ly/Ps102:2)**; **[JON 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:2)** *See also* [2SA 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:7) parallel [PSA 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ps18:6); [2CH 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:9), [2CH 33:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:12); [PSA 4:1](https://ref.ly/Ps4:1), [PSA 25:17](https://ref.ly/Ps25:17), [PSA 106:44](https://ref.ly/Ps106:44), [PSA 120:1](https://ref.ly/Ps120:1)

God comforts believers under stress
-----------------------------------

## 5560: suffering (id: 5560)

The experience of pain or distress, both physical and emotional. Scripture is thoroughly realistic about the place of suffering in the world and in the lives of believers. To become a Christian is not to escape from suffering, but to be able to bear suffering with dignity and hope.

* **Associated Articles:** 5561: suffering, nature of (ID: `5561@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5562: suffering, of the innocent (ID: `5562@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5563: suffering, of God (ID: `5563@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5564: suffering, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5564@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5565: suffering, of believers (ID: `5565@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5566: suffering, encouragements in (ID: `5566@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5567: suffering, emotional aspects of (ID: `5567@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5568: suffering, causes of (ID: `5568@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5569: suffering, hardship (ID: `5569@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5561: suffering, nature of (id: 5561)

Since the fall, human beings have suffered in various ways. Scripture provides insights into the nature and place of suffering both in the world and in the lives of believers.

Suffering began with the fall
-----------------------------

[GEN 2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:17), [GEN 3:16–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16-Gen3:19); ; [ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12)

Suffering is universal
----------------------

[JOB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Job5:7), [JOB 14:1](https://ref.ly/Job14:1)

Different kinds of suffering
----------------------------

### Physical pain and illness

**[GEN 48:1](https://ref.ly/Gen48:1)** *See also* [2KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:1) parallels [2CH 32:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:24), [ISA 38:1](https://ref.ly/Isa38:1); [JOB 2:7](https://ref.ly/Job2:7); [PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10); [MAT 8:6](https://ref.ly/Matt8:6), [MAT 17:15](https://ref.ly/Matt17:15); [LUK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Luke4:38); [ACT 28:8](https://ref.ly/Acts28:8); [2TI 4:20](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:20); [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14)

### Emotional stress

**[PSA 55:4–55:5](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4-Ps55:5)** *See also* [GEN 35:18](https://ref.ly/Gen35:18) *“Ben\-Oni” means “son of my trouble”*; [PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25); [JHN 11:32–11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:32-John11:35); [PHP 2:27](https://ref.ly/Phil2:27)

### Spiritual suffering

**[PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1)** *See also* [MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46) parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34)

### The prospect of death

**[GEN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:19)** *See also* [ECC 12:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:7)

Major causes of suffering
-------------------------

### The disorder in creation

**[GEN 3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17)** *See also* [GEN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10); [JOL 1:4](https://ref.ly/Joel1:4); [MAT 24:7](https://ref.ly/Matt24:7) parallel [LUK 21:11](https://ref.ly/Luke21:11); [ROM 8:22](https://ref.ly/Rom8:22); [REV 11:13](https://ref.ly/Rev11:13)

### Family troubles

### Blessing

**[PSA 119:71](https://ref.ly/Ps119:71)** *See also* [ISA 38:17](https://ref.ly/Isa38:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:17; Genesis 3:16-19; Romans 5:12; Job 5:7; Job 14:1; Genesis 48:1; 2 Kings 20:1; 2 Chronicles 32:24; Isaiah 38:1; Job 2:7; Psalms 42:10; Matthew 8:6; Matthew 17:15; Luke 4:38; Acts 28:8; 2 Timothy 4:20; James 5:14; Psalms 55:4-5; Genesis 35:18; Proverbs 12:25; John 11:32-35; Philippians 2:27; Psalms 22:1; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:34; Genesis 3:19; Ecclesiastes 12:7; Genesis 3:17; Genesis 12:10; Joel 1:4; Matthew 24:7; Luke 21:11; Romans 8:22; Revelation 11:13; Psalms 54:3; Genesis 4:8; Exodus 1:16; Exodus 1:22; 1 Kings 21:19; Matthew 2:16; Genesis 49:5-7; Exodus 1:11; Amos 2:6-7; Amos 4:1; Malachi 3:5; 2 Kings 6:25; 2 Kings 19:17; 2 Chronicles 10:13-14; Job 1:14-15; Job 1:17; Amos 1:3; Amos 1:13; James 5:4-6; Revelation 6:4; Psalms 27:10; 1 Samuel 1:7; 2 Samuel 16:11; Job 19:14-19; Malachi 2:14; Matthew 10:36; James 1:27; Psalms 71:9; Ecclesiastes 12:1-7; 1 John 5:19; Job 1:12; Job 2:6-7; Luke 13:16; 2 Corinthians 12:7; Revelation 2:10; Revelation 20:7-8; Job 29:2; Job 3:25; Hebrews 2:15; Job 2:9; John 9:3; Job 2:3; Luke 13:2; Romans 1:18; Genesis 6:5-7; Numbers 14:33; Deuteronomy 28:15; Psalms 107:17; Ezekiel 23:49; Acts 5:5; Acts 5:10; Romans 1:27; 1 Corinthians 11:29-30; Jude 1:7; Revelation 2:22; Matthew 25:41; Daniel 12:2; Matthew 8:12; Mark 9:48; Isaiah 66:24; Revelation 20:15; Revelation 16:9; Exodus 7:22; Revelation 9:20-21; 2 Chronicles 33:12; Luke 15:17-18; Psalms 119:71; Isaiah 38:17

## 5562: suffering, of the innocent (id: 5562)

Scripture does not teach that the wicked will suffer, whereas the innocent and blameless will prosper. It offers insights into the place of suffering in the lives of the innocent, and the apparent injustice of this situation.

The apparent injustice of suffering
-----------------------------------

**[HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13)** *See also* [JOB 19:7](https://ref.ly/Job19:7), [JOB 24:1](https://ref.ly/Job24:1), [JOB 24:12](https://ref.ly/Job24:12); [PSA 59:3–59:4](https://ref.ly/Ps59:3-Ps59:4), [PSA 74:1](https://ref.ly/Ps74:1), [PSA 74:11](https://ref.ly/Ps74:11), [PSA 88:5](https://ref.ly/Ps88:5), [PSA 88:14](https://ref.ly/Ps88:14)

The wrong answers to suffering
------------------------------

The concept that those who suffer must be sinners: [JOB 4:7](https://ref.ly/Job4:7); ; [JHN 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:2-John9:3); [EZK 18:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:25) *the idea that God is unjust*

The wrong reactions to suffering
--------------------------------

[JOB 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Job2:9-Job2:10) *resentment against God*; ; [JOB 23:13–23:15](https://ref.ly/Job23:13-Job23:15) *being terrified of God*; ; [JOB 40:2](https://ref.ly/Job40:2) *arguing with God*; ; [PSA 73:3](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3) *envying the wicked*; ; [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13) *disillusionment*

The right reactions to suffering
--------------------------------

### Reverent submission

**[JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21)** *See also* [JOB 28:28](https://ref.ly/Job28:28), [JOB 34:12](https://ref.ly/Job34:12), [JOB 36:26](https://ref.ly/Job36:26), [JOB 37:19](https://ref.ly/Job37:19), [JOB 40:4](https://ref.ly/Job40:4); [LAM 3:40](https://ref.ly/Lam3:40), [LAM 3:49–3:50](https://ref.ly/Lam3:49-Lam3:50); [MAT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Matt10:28)

### Trust in God

**[GEN 18:25](https://ref.ly/Gen18:25)**; **[PSA 55:22–55:23](https://ref.ly/Ps55:22-Ps55:23)** *See also* [EXO 2:23](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23); [PSA 56:3–56:4](https://ref.ly/Ps56:3-Ps56:4), [PSA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16), [PSA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Ps62:5), [PSA 70:2](https://ref.ly/Ps70:2), [PSA 107:6](https://ref.ly/Ps107:6), [PSA 107:13](https://ref.ly/Ps107:13), [PSA 107:19](https://ref.ly/Ps107:19), [PSA 107:28](https://ref.ly/Ps107:28)[PSA 119:50](https://ref.ly/Ps119:50), [PSA 119:153](https://ref.ly/Ps119:153); [DAN 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:17-Dan3:18) *the trust shown by Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego*; [HAB 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Hab3:17-Hab3:18); [1PE 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:23) *the example of Jesus Christ*; [1PE 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:19), [1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)

God's reaction to suffering
---------------------------

### Concern for those who suffer

**[PSA 9:12](https://ref.ly/Ps9:12)** *See also* [EXO 2:25](https://ref.ly/Exod2:25); [2KI 14:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:26); [PSA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Ps1:6), [PSA 33:18–33:19](https://ref.ly/Ps33:18-Ps33:19); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Anger at the wicked

[PSA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Ps11:5), [PSA 59:8](https://ref.ly/Ps59:8); ; [EZK 36:6–36:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:6-Ezek36:7); ; [HAB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Hab2:9), [HAB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Hab2:12)

God's response to suffering
---------------------------

### Deliverance for those who suffer

**[PSA 34:19](https://ref.ly/Ps34:19)** *See also* [JOB 42:10–42:12](https://ref.ly/Job42:10-Job42:12); [REV 7:16](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16), [REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)

### Judgment for the wicked

**[MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)** *See also* [EXO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Exod3:19-Exod3:20); [PSA 73:16–73:18](https://ref.ly/Ps73:16-Ps73:18), [PSA 141:10](https://ref.ly/Ps141:10); [AMO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3); [NAM 3:1](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1), [NAM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:19); [HAB 2:16](https://ref.ly/Hab2:16); [MAT 25:41–25:46](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41-Matt25:46); [JAS 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jas2:13); [1PE 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:18); [REV 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Rev18:6-Rev18:7)

* **Associated Passages:** Habakkuk 1:13; Job 19:7; Job 24:1; Job 24:12; Psalms 59:3-4; Psalms 74:1; Psalms 74:11; Psalms 88:5; Psalms 88:14; Job 4:7; John 9:2-3; Ezekiel 18:25; Job 2:9-10; Job 23:13-15; Job 40:2; Psalms 73:3; Psalms 73:13; Job 1:21; Job 28:28; Job 34:12; Job 36:26; Job 37:19; Job 40:4; Lamentations 3:40; Lamentations 3:49-50; Matthew 10:28; Genesis 18:25; Psalms 55:22-23; Exodus 2:23; Psalms 56:3-4; Psalms 59:16; Psalms 62:5; Psalms 70:2; Psalms 107:6; Psalms 107:13; Psalms 107:19; Psalms 107:28; Psalms 119:50; Psalms 119:153; Daniel 3:17-18; Habakkuk 3:17-18; 1 Peter 2:23; 1 Peter 4:19; 1 Peter 5:7; Psalms 9:12; Exodus 2:25; 2 Kings 14:26; Psalms 1:6; Psalms 33:18-19; James 5:4; Psalms 11:5; Psalms 59:8; Ezekiel 36:6-7; Habakkuk 2:9; Habakkuk 2:12; Psalms 34:19; Job 42:10-12; Revelation 7:16; Revelation 21:4; Malachi 3:5; Exodus 3:19-20; Psalms 73:16-18; Psalms 141:10; Amos 1:3; Nahum 3:1; Nahum 3:19; Habakkuk 2:16; Matthew 25:41-46; James 2:13; 1 Peter 4:18; Revelation 18:6-7

## 5563: suffering, of God (id: 5563)

See [1135: God, suffering of](1135.md)



## 5564: suffering, of Jesus Christ (id: 5564)

Jesus Christ suffered on behalf of his people, showing both the reality of his human nature, and the extent of his love for humanity. Those sufferings reached their climax in the sufferings of Jesus Christ upon the cross, through which God chose to redeem the world.

The OT foretold Jesus Christ's suffering
----------------------------------------

### NT references to the OT Scriptures

**[LUK 24:25–24:26](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25-Luke24:26)** *See also* [LUK 24:46](https://ref.ly/Luke24:46); [ACT 3:18](https://ref.ly/Acts3:18), [ACT 17:2–17:3](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2-Acts17:3), [ACT 26:22–26:23](https://ref.ly/Acts26:22-Acts26:23); [1CO 15:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:3)

### Types and foreshadowings of Jesus Christ's suffering

[GEN 22:2](https://ref.ly/Gen22:2), [GEN 22:8](https://ref.ly/Gen22:8), [GEN 22:13](https://ref.ly/Gen22:13); ; [EXO 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22-Exod12:23); ; [1SA 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:5); ; [2SA 16:11–16:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:11-2Sam16:12); ; [1KI 21:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:15); ; [PSA 55:12–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:12-Ps55:14), [PSA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Ps57:4); ; [HEB 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/Heb13:11-Heb13:12)

### Prophetic statements of Jesus Christ's suffering

[GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15); ; [PSA 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2), [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1), [PSA 22:7–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7-Ps22:8), [PSA 22:12–22:18](https://ref.ly/Ps22:12-Ps22:18), [PSA 69:21](https://ref.ly/Ps69:21); ; [ISA 53:3–53:12](https://ref.ly/Isa53:3-Isa53:12)

Jesus Christ himself foretold his suffering
-------------------------------------------

**[MAT 16:21](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21)** parallel [LUK 9:22](https://ref.ly/Luke9:22) *See also* [MAT 17:12](https://ref.ly/Matt17:12) parallel [MRK 9:12](https://ref.ly/Mark9:12); [MAT 20:18–20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:18-Matt20:19) parallels [MRK 10:33–10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:33-Mark10:34), [LUK 18:31–18:32](https://ref.ly/Luke18:31-Luke18:32); [MRK 9:31](https://ref.ly/Mark9:31); [LUK 17:25](https://ref.ly/Luke17:25), [LUK 20:13–20:15](https://ref.ly/Luke20:13-Luke20:15) *compare with Naboth (1Kings chapter 21\)*; [LUK 22:15](https://ref.ly/Luke22:15); [JHN 12:27](https://ref.ly/John12:27)

Aspects in Jesus Christ's suffering
-----------------------------------

### The enmity of Satan

**[GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15)** [MAT 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:3) parallel [LUK 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:3); ; [MAT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Matt4:5) parallel [LUK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Luke4:9); ; [MAT 4:8](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8) parallels [LUK 4:5](https://ref.ly/Luke4:5), [MRK 1:13](https://ref.ly/Mark1:13); [MAT 16:23](https://ref.ly/Matt16:23); [LUK 22:3](https://ref.ly/Luke22:3); ; [JHN 13:27](https://ref.ly/John13:27); [JHN 14:30](https://ref.ly/John14:30); ; [REV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Rev12:4)

### The hatred of the world

**[JHN 7:7](https://ref.ly/John7:7)** *See also* [MAT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:13); [JHN 1:10](https://ref.ly/John1:10), [JHN 3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:20), [JHN 15:18–15:20](https://ref.ly/John15:18-John15:20); [1CO 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:8)

### Rejection by his own people

**[JHN 1:11](https://ref.ly/John1:11)** *See also* [ISA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1); [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37); [LUK 19:41–19:42](https://ref.ly/Luke19:41-Luke19:42), [LUK 24:20](https://ref.ly/Luke24:20); [JHN 18:40](https://ref.ly/John18:40); [ACT 2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:36), [ACT 3:13](https://ref.ly/Acts3:13), [ACT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Acts4:10); [1TH 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:14-1Thess2:15)

### The failure of his disciples

**[MRK 14:50](https://ref.ly/Mark14:50)** parallel [MAT 26:56](https://ref.ly/Matt26:56) *See also* [MAT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Matt16:22), [MAT 26:40](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40), [MAT 26:74–26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:74-Matt26:75); [LUK 18:34](https://ref.ly/Luke18:34)

### Betrayal by Judas

[MAT 26:23](https://ref.ly/Matt26:23) parallels [MRK 14:18](https://ref.ly/Mark14:18), [LUK 22:21](https://ref.ly/Luke22:21), [JHN 13:21](https://ref.ly/John13:21)

### God's judgment of his Son for the sins of the world

**[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46)** parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34) *See also* [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1); [ISA 53:5–53:6](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5-Isa53:6), [ISA 53:10](https://ref.ly/Isa53:10); [LUK 22:42](https://ref.ly/Luke22:42); [ROM 3:25–3:26](https://ref.ly/Rom3:25-Rom3:26); [2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21); [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13); [HEB 2:10](https://ref.ly/Heb2:10), [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14); [1PE 2:24](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:24)

Jesus Christ's willing submission to suffering
----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 26:42](https://ref.ly/Matt26:42)** parallels [MRK 14:36](https://ref.ly/Mark14:36), [LUK 22:42](https://ref.ly/Luke22:42) *See also* [ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7), [ISA 53:11](https://ref.ly/Isa53:11); [LUK 23:46](https://ref.ly/Luke23:46); [HEB 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7-Heb5:8), [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2); [1PE 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:23)

## 5565: suffering, of believers (id: 5565)

Believers ought to expect to suffer as an inevitable part of their calling. To believe is not to evade suffering; it is to face it with new confidence and hope. Rightly approached, suffering develops the character of believers, equips them for more effective service, draws believers closer to Jesus Christ and prepares them for eternal life.

Believers must expect suffering
-------------------------------

### Jesus Christ foretold it

**[MAT 10:22](https://ref.ly/Matt10:22)** parallel [MRK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Mark13:13) *See also* [MAT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17), [MAT 23:34](https://ref.ly/Matt23:34), [MAT 24:9](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9); [LUK 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16-Luke21:17)

### The apostles foretold it

**[ACT 14:22](https://ref.ly/Acts14:22)**; **[2TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:12)** *See also* [PHP 1:29](https://ref.ly/Phil1:29); [1JN 3:13](https://ref.ly/1John3:13); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

### The experience of the OT shows it

[ROM 8:36](https://ref.ly/Rom8:36); ; [HEB 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/Heb11:25-Heb11:26), [HEB 11:32–11:38](https://ref.ly/Heb11:32-Heb11:38)

### The experience of the NT shows it

[ACT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Acts4:3), [ACT 5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:40), [ACT 9:29](https://ref.ly/Acts9:29), [ACT 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Acts12:1-Acts12:3); ; [2CO 11:23–11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:23-2Cor11:29); ; [GAL 3:4](https://ref.ly/Gal3:4); ; [1TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2), [1TH 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:14); ; [1PE 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:4); ; [HEB 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32-Heb10:34)

Suffering for Jesus Christ is commendable
-----------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:10–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:10-Matt5:12)** parallel [LUK 6:20–6:23](https://ref.ly/Luke6:20-Luke6:23) *See also* [ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:41); [2TI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:8); [1PE 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:19), [1PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:17), [1PE 4:12–4:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:12-1Pet4:16)

Suffering is profitable
-----------------------

### It affirms believers' adoption

**[HEB 12:7](https://ref.ly/Heb12:7)** *See also* [JHN 15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:19), [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14); [ROM 8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:17); [GAL 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gal6:17); [2TH 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:4-2Thess1:5)

### It is the price of godliness

**[HEB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Heb12:11)** *See also* [PSA 119:67](https://ref.ly/Ps119:67), [PSA 119:71](https://ref.ly/Ps119:71); [ISA 38:17](https://ref.ly/Isa38:17); [JHN 15:2](https://ref.ly/John15:2); [1PE 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6-1Pet1:7)

### It is a condition of service

**[2CO 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:10)**; **[COL 1:24](https://ref.ly/Col1:24)** *See also* [PSA 126:5–126:6](https://ref.ly/Ps126:5-Ps126:6); [ACT 9:16](https://ref.ly/Acts9:16), [ACT 20:23–20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:23-Acts20:24); [1CO 4:9–4:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:9-1Cor4:13); [2CO 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:5); [GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19); [PHP 3:18](https://ref.ly/Phil3:18); [COL 2:1](https://ref.ly/Col2:1); [2TI 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:11-2Tim1:12), [2TI 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:3), [2TI 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:10)

### It develops trust

**[2CO 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:9)** *See also* [1PE 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:19)

### It develops character

**[ROM 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Rom5:3-Rom5:4)** *See also* [HEB 5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:8); [JAS 1:3](https://ref.ly/Jas1:3)

### It deepens fellowship

**[1CO 12:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:26)** *See also* [ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15); [2CO 1:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:7), [2CO 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:2); [GAL 4:14–4:15](https://ref.ly/Gal4:14-Gal4:15), [GAL 6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:2)

### It draws believers to the Lord

**[PHP 3:8](https://ref.ly/Phil3:8), [PHP 3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10)** *See also* [JOB 42:5](https://ref.ly/Job42:5); [2CO 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:8-2Cor4:10), [2CO 12:9–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9-2Cor12:10); [1PE 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:13)

### It prepares believers for heaven

[2CO 4:16–5:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:16-2Cor5:4)

## 5566: suffering, encouragements in (id: 5566)

Believers are encouraged by the loving care and faithful promises of God and by the example, support and prayers of fellow believers. The prospect of heaven helps them to endure.

The encouragement of God's love
-------------------------------

### The care of the Father

**[PSA 103:13](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13)** *See also* [PSA 86:17](https://ref.ly/Ps86:17); [ISA 50:10](https://ref.ly/Isa50:10), [ISA 64:8–64:9](https://ref.ly/Isa64:8-Isa64:9); [LUK 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Luke18:6-Luke18:7); [JHN 16:27](https://ref.ly/John16:27), [JHN 17:11](https://ref.ly/John17:11), [JHN 17:15](https://ref.ly/John17:15); [ROM 8:32](https://ref.ly/Rom8:32); [2CO 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3)

### The sympathetic understanding of Jesus Christ

**[HEB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:18)** *See also* [ISA 40:11](https://ref.ly/Isa40:11), [ISA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Isa42:3), [ISA 53:5](https://ref.ly/Isa53:5); [LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18); [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [JHN 14:27](https://ref.ly/John14:27), [JHN 16:22](https://ref.ly/John16:22); [ROM 8:35–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:39); [HEB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15), [HEB 7:25](https://ref.ly/Heb7:25), [HEB 12:3](https://ref.ly/Heb12:3)

### The comfort of the Spirit

**[JHN 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:16-John14:17)** *See also* [LUK 11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:13); [ROM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rom5:5), [ROM 8:26](https://ref.ly/Rom8:26); [GAL 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22-Gal5:23); [EPH 3:16](https://ref.ly/Eph3:16); [1PE 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:14)

### The promises of God

**[PSA 119:50](https://ref.ly/Ps119:50)**; **[ROM 8:28](https://ref.ly/Rom8:28)**; **[2CO 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9)** *See also* [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10), [ISA 43:2–43:3](https://ref.ly/Isa43:2-Isa43:3); [MAT 6:31–6:33](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31-Matt6:33); [ROM 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rom15:4); [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7); [1PE 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10)

The encouragement of the fellowship of believers
------------------------------------------------

### Examples from the past

### Support from other believers

**[1TH 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:11)** *See also* [RUT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:13); [JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11); [ACT 28:15](https://ref.ly/Acts28:15); [ROM 16:4](https://ref.ly/Rom16:4); [2CO 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:6-2Cor7:7); [PHP 4:14](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14); [COL 4:11](https://ref.ly/Col4:11); [1TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:11-1Thess2:12), [1TH 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:18); [2TI 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:9), [2TI 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:11); [PHM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:13); [HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:25)

----------------------------------

### The assurance of victory

**[ROM 8:37](https://ref.ly/Rom8:37)** *See also* [ROM 16:20](https://ref.ly/Rom16:20); [1CO 15:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:20), [1CO 15:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:25), [1CO 15:55–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:55-1Cor15:57); [1TH 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:16-1Thess4:17)

## 5567: suffering, emotional aspects of (id: 5567)

Suffering gives rise to inner distress, on account of a sense of injustice, frustration, loss or anger. Christ himself provides an example of such suffering to believers.

Causes of emotional suffering
-----------------------------

### A sense of injustice

**[PSA 119:53](https://ref.ly/Ps119:53)** *See also* [JOB 19:7](https://ref.ly/Job19:7); [PSA 82:2](https://ref.ly/Ps82:2)

### Frustration

**[JOB 19:8](https://ref.ly/Job19:8)** *See also* [JOB 23:8–23:9](https://ref.ly/Job23:8-Job23:9); [1TH 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:18)

### Loss

**[GEN 27:34](https://ref.ly/Gen27:34)** *See also* [RUT 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:20-Ruth1:21); [PRO 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/Prov5:11-Prov5:14); [LAM 1:12](https://ref.ly/Lam1:12)

### Bereavement

**[2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33)** *See also* [GEN 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Gen23:1-Gen23:2), [GEN 37:34–37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34-Gen37:35), [GEN 50:1–50:3](https://ref.ly/Gen50:1-Gen50:3); [EXO 12:30](https://ref.ly/Exod12:30); [DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8); [2SA 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:4); [MAT 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16-Matt2:18); [JER 31:15](https://ref.ly/Jer31:15); [JHN 11:32–11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:32-John11:35)

### Childlessness

**[GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1)** *See also* [1SA 1:4–1:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:4-1Sam1:8)

### Parting from loved ones

**[ACT 20:38](https://ref.ly/Acts20:38)** *See also* [1SA 20:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41); [2KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:12); [SNG 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/Song5:6-Song5:8)

### Spiritual loss

**[LUK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Luke13:28)** *See also* [JDG 2:4](https://ref.ly/Judg2:4); [MAT 25:11](https://ref.ly/Matt25:11)

### Great fear

**[GEN 45:3](https://ref.ly/Gen45:3)** *See also* [GEN 20:8](https://ref.ly/Gen20:8), [GEN 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Gen32:7-Gen32:8); [EXO 20:18–20:19](https://ref.ly/Exod20:18-Exod20:19); [1SA 17:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:24); [1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4); [ISA 21:2–21:4](https://ref.ly/Isa21:2-Isa21:4); [DAN 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Dan5:8-Dan5:9); [LUK 21:26](https://ref.ly/Luke21:26)

### Guilt

**[MAT 27:3–27:4](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:4)** *See also* [GEN 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8); [JHN 16:8](https://ref.ly/John16:8); [ACT 2:37](https://ref.ly/Acts2:37); [HEB 10:2](https://ref.ly/Heb10:2), [HEB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:22)

Emotional suffering in the Lord's work
--------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's suffering

**[ISA 53:3](https://ref.ly/Isa53:3)** *See also* [MAT 26:38](https://ref.ly/Matt26:38) parallel [MRK 14:34](https://ref.ly/Mark14:34)

### The apostles' suffering

**[2CO 11:28–11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:28-2Cor11:29)** *See also* [ROM 9:1–9:4](https://ref.ly/Rom9:1-Rom9:4); [1CO 15:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:31); [2CO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:8-2Cor1:9); [GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19)

Expressions of emotional suffering
----------------------------------

### Sadness

[NEH 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Neh2:1-Neh2:3); ; [PSA 107:39](https://ref.ly/Ps107:39), [PSA 116:3](https://ref.ly/Ps116:3); ; [MAT 26:22](https://ref.ly/Matt26:22) parallel [MRK 14:19](https://ref.ly/Mark14:19)

### Weeping and groaning

**[JER 9:1](https://ref.ly/Jer9:1)** [GEN 42:24](https://ref.ly/Gen42:24), [GEN 43:30](https://ref.ly/Gen43:30), [GEN 45:1–45:2](https://ref.ly/Gen45:1-Gen45:2); [2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30) *Covering the head and going barefoot were also signs of sorrow*; ; [EZR 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:12-Ezra3:13); ; [NEH 1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:4); ; [PSA 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Ps6:6-Ps6:7), [PSA 32:3–32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:4), [PSA 137:1](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1); ; [ISA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Isa15:5); ; [JER 14:17](https://ref.ly/Jer14:17); ; [MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75) *Peter wept bitterly after disowning Jesus Christ*; ; [JHN 11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:35)

### Anger

**[JON 4:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:1)** *See also* [EXO 32:19–32:20](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19-Exod32:20); [2SA 6:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:8); [REV 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10-Rev16:11)

### Bitterness

[EXO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:3); ; [1SA 30:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:6); ; [JOB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Job3:1), [JOB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Job7:11); ; [JON 4:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:3); ; [ROM 3:14](https://ref.ly/Rom3:14)

God's response to the emotional suffering of believers
------------------------------------------------------

### He comforts them

**[2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)**

### He has delivered them through Jesus Christ

**[ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4)**

### Jesus Christ gives them rest

**[JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1)** *See also* [MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30); [JHN 14:27](https://ref.ly/John14:27)

### There is no suffering in heaven

**[REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)** *See also* [ISA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:10); [REV 7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:17)

The proper response of believers to emotional suffering
-------------------------------------------------------

### Assessing the problem before God

### Controlled emotion before God

[JDG 20:23](https://ref.ly/Judg20:23); ; [PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2), [PSA 62:8](https://ref.ly/Ps62:8), [PSA 74:1](https://ref.ly/Ps74:1)

### Prayer

**[HEB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7)** *See also* [GEN 18:22–18:25](https://ref.ly/Gen18:22-Gen18:25), [GEN 32:9–32:11](https://ref.ly/Gen32:9-Gen32:11); [NUM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Num16:22); [2SA 12:15–12:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:15-2Sam12:17); [PSA 38:9](https://ref.ly/Ps38:9), [PSA 77:1–77:9](https://ref.ly/Ps77:1-Ps77:9); [JON 2:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:1); [MRK 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22-Mark5:23); [JAS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jas5:13)

### Continuing to do what is right

## 5568: suffering, causes of (id: 5568)

The ultimate cause of suffering is sin, which has brought violence, disease and death into the world. All suffering, however, is under the sovereign purpose of God, who is able to use it for his glory.

The ultimate cause of suffering is sin
--------------------------------------

[GEN 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14-Gen3:19); [JOB 1:1](https://ref.ly/Job1:1); ; [JHN 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:3); [JOB 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/Job5:6-Job5:7); ; [ROM 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12-Rom5:14)

Suffering as a result of human wickedness
-----------------------------------------

### Murder

**[GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8)** *See also* [GEN 49:5–49:7](https://ref.ly/Gen49:5-Gen49:7); [1SA 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10-1Sam18:11)

### Injury

[MAT 26:67](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67)

### Cruelty

**[PSA 71:4](https://ref.ly/Ps71:4)** *See also* [JDG 1:7](https://ref.ly/Judg1:7); [PSA 54:3](https://ref.ly/Ps54:3); [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13); [MAT 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16-Matt2:18); [LUK 10:30](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30)

### Rioting

[2KI 7:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:17); ; [ACT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Acts16:22)

### Warfare

**[JER 50:22](https://ref.ly/Jer50:22)** *See also* [GEN 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Gen14:1-Gen14:2), [GEN 14:11–14:12](https://ref.ly/Gen14:11-Gen14:12); [EXO 13:17](https://ref.ly/Exod13:17); [JOS 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Josh6:20-Josh6:21), [JOS 10:22–10:26](https://ref.ly/Josh10:22-Josh10:26); [JDG 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/Judg1:4-Judg1:7); [1KI 22:35](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:35); [2KI 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:5-2Kgs17:6); [ZEC 14:2](https://ref.ly/Zech14:2); [LUK 21:20–21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20-Luke21:24); [REV 6:4](https://ref.ly/Rev6:4)

**[EZK 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:9)** *See also* [GEN 39:19–39:20](https://ref.ly/Gen39:19-Gen39:20); [1KI 21:11–21:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:11-1Kgs21:14); [2KI 21:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:16); [PSA 58:1–58:2](https://ref.ly/Ps58:1-Ps58:2), [PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6)

## 5569: suffering, hardship (id: 5569)

Scripture provides examples of types of hardship which people may expect to encounter.

Different kinds of hardship
---------------------------

### Poverty

**[PRO 10:15](https://ref.ly/Prov10:15)** *See also* [DEU 15:11](https://ref.ly/Deut15:11); [JOB 24:5–24:11](https://ref.ly/Job24:5-Job24:11); [MAT 26:11](https://ref.ly/Matt26:11) parallel [MRK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Mark14:7); [JHN 12:8](https://ref.ly/John12:8)

### Hunger and thirst

**[PSA 107:4–107:5](https://ref.ly/Ps107:4-Ps107:5)** *See also* [GEN 41:53–41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:53-Gen41:57); [EXO 15:22–15:24](https://ref.ly/Exod15:22-Exod15:24), [EXO 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:2-Exod16:3), [EXO 17:1–17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1-Exod17:3); [NUM 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Num11:4-Num11:6); [1KI 17:7–17:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:7-1Kgs17:12); [NEH 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:1-Neh5:5); [MAT 4:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:2) parallel [LUK 4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:2)

### Hard labour

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *See also* [GEN 31:38–31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:38-Gen31:42); [2CH 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:3-2Chr10:4); [ECC 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:21-Eccl2:22); [1TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9)

### Oppression

**[JOB 35:9](https://ref.ly/Job35:9)** *See also* [EXO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11), [EXO 2:23](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23), [EXO 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Exod5:8-Exod5:9); [2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1); [PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5), [PSA 31:9–31:13](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9-Ps31:13); [ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1); [JER 6:6](https://ref.ly/Jer6:6); [AMO 3:9](https://ref.ly/Amos3:9); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

### Danger

**[NEH 4:21–4:23](https://ref.ly/Neh4:21-Neh4:23)** *See also* [EXO 18:8](https://ref.ly/Exod18:8); [1KI 2:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:26); [1CH 11:15–11:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:15-1Chr11:19); [ACT 27:27–27:44](https://ref.ly/Acts27:27-Acts27:44)

---------------------------------------

### Israel

**[PSA 132:1](https://ref.ly/Ps132:1)**

**[2CO 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:4-2Cor6:5)**

---------------------------------

## 5571: surgery (id: 5571)

The physical removal of part of the human body, either in response to severe illness or as a form of punishment.

Medical surgery
---------------

[EZK 30:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:21)

Punitive surgery
----------------

[DEU 25:12](https://ref.ly/Deut25:12); ; [JDG 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Judg1:6-Judg1:7); ; [1SA 17:51](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:51), [1SA 31:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:9); ; [2SA 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:7), [2SA 4:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:12); ; [2KI 25:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:7)

Ritual or cultural surgery
--------------------------

### Castration

The primary meaning of `eunuch'' in the OT is`court officer'' and need not always imply castration; similarly with the NT counterpart: [2KI 20:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:18); ; [EST 2:3](https://ref.ly/Esth2:3); ; [ISA 56:3](https://ref.ly/Isa56:3); ; [MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12); ; [ACT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Acts8:27)

### Circumcision

[GEN 17:10–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10-Gen17:14); ; [EXO 4:25](https://ref.ly/Exod4:25); ; [LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3); ; [JOS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Josh5:8); ; [LUK 2:21](https://ref.ly/Luke2:21)

### Dismemberment

A solemn call to the nation to act: [JDG 19:29](https://ref.ly/Judg19:29); ; [1SA 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:7)

### Ear piercing

[EXO 21:5–21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:5-Exod21:6) *There may be a reference to this in [Ps 40:6](https://ref.ly/Ps40:6).*

### Pagan mutilation

[LEV 19:28](https://ref.ly/Lev19:28); ; [DEU 14:1](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1); ; [1KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28)

### Animal sacrifices

[GEN 15:10](https://ref.ly/Gen15:10); ; [EXO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Exod29:17); ; [LEV 1:6](https://ref.ly/Lev1:6), [LEV 1:12](https://ref.ly/Lev1:12), [LEV 3:9](https://ref.ly/Lev3:9)

------------------

## 5572: sword (id: 5572)

A hand\-wielded blade in common use throughout biblical times as a weapon of war and in OT times as an instrument of execution. The sword is also a symbol of aggression, authority and power.

The sword as the principal weapon of war
----------------------------------------

### It was in common use

**[EXO 17:13](https://ref.ly/Exod17:13)** *See also* [NUM 21:24–21:35](https://ref.ly/Num21:24-Num21:35); [JOS 11:10–11:12](https://ref.ly/Josh11:10-Josh11:12), [JOS 19:47](https://ref.ly/Josh19:47)

### Swordsmen fought and fell in great numbers

**[JDG 3:28–3:30](https://ref.ly/Judg3:28-Judg3:30)** *See also* [JDG 20:17](https://ref.ly/Judg20:17); [2SA 24:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:9)

### Some swordsmen showed great strength and ability

**[2SA 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:10)** *See also* [1SA 21:8–21:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:8-1Sam21:9); [2SA 23:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:12)

The sword used for other purposes
---------------------------------

### As an instrument of execution and decapitation

**[2SA 1:14–1:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:14-2Sam1:16)** *See also* [1SA 15:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:33), [1SA 17:51](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:51), [1SA 22:18–22:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:18-1Sam22:19); [1KI 19:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1); [ACT 12:2](https://ref.ly/Acts12:2); [ROM 8:35](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35)

### For murder

**[2KI 19:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:37)** *See also* [JOS 6:21](https://ref.ly/Josh6:21); [1KI 2:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:34); [ACT 12:2](https://ref.ly/Acts12:2)

### For committing suicide

**[1SA 31:4–31:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:4-1Sam31:6)** parallel [1CH 10:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:4) *See also* [ACT 16:27](https://ref.ly/Acts16:27)

Metaphorical use of the sword
-----------------------------

### To symbolise God's opposition to the sinful

**[GEN 3:24](https://ref.ly/Gen3:24)** *See also* [NUM 22:21–22:23](https://ref.ly/Num22:21-Num22:23), [NUM 22:31](https://ref.ly/Num22:31); [DEU 33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:29)

### To symbolise warfare

[LEV 26:6–26:8](https://ref.ly/Lev26:6-Lev26:8)

### To symbolise temporal power

**[EZK 32:11–32:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:11-Ezek32:12)** *See also* [PSA 45:3](https://ref.ly/Ps45:3); [MIC 5:6](https://ref.ly/Mic5:6)

### To symbolise suffering

**[LUK 2:34–2:35](https://ref.ly/Luke2:34-Luke2:35)**

### To symbolise judgment

**[EXO 5:3](https://ref.ly/Exod5:3)**; **[MAT 10:34](https://ref.ly/Matt10:34)** *See also* [JDG 7:20–7:22](https://ref.ly/Judg7:20-Judg7:22); [2CH 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:9); [PSA 7:12](https://ref.ly/Ps7:12); [ISA 31:8](https://ref.ly/Isa31:8); [JER 50:35–50:37](https://ref.ly/Jer50:35-Jer50:37); [EZK 11:8–11:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:8-Ezek11:10), [EZK 21:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:3), [EZK 33:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:2)

### To symbolise the word of God

**[EPH 6:17](https://ref.ly/Eph6:17)** *See also* [HEB 4:12](https://ref.ly/Heb4:12); [REV 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rev1:16), [REV 2:12](https://ref.ly/Rev2:12)

## 5573: table (id: 5573)

An item of furniture used principally for putting food on. Sharing at the same table was a mark of fellowship and to eat at the table of rulers was deemed a great privilege. In the tabernacle and temple a special table held the bread of the Presence. Tables were also used for business transactions.

Tables used for eating
----------------------

### A table as household furniture

[2KI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:10)

### Reclining at table

**[LUK 7:36](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36)** *See also* [MAT 26:7](https://ref.ly/Matt26:7) parallel [MRK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3); [LUK 11:37](https://ref.ly/Luke11:37); [JHN 12:2](https://ref.ly/John12:2)

### Tables set for meals

**[PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5)**; **[ISA 21:5](https://ref.ly/Isa21:5)** *See also* [JOB 36:16](https://ref.ly/Job36:16); [PSA 69:22](https://ref.ly/Ps69:22), [PSA 78:19](https://ref.ly/Ps78:19) *The people of Israel doubted God's ability to provide*; [PRO 9:2](https://ref.ly/Prov9:2); [EZK 23:41](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:41)

### Giving thanks for food at the table

**[LUK 24:30](https://ref.ly/Luke24:30)** *See also* [MAT 26:26–26:27](https://ref.ly/Matt26:26-Matt26:27) parallels [MRK 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Mark14:22-Mark14:23), [LUK 22:17–22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:17-Luke22:19); [1CO 11:23–11:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:24)

### Dogs eat scraps from the table

**[MAT 15:27](https://ref.ly/Matt15:27)** parallel [MRK 7:28](https://ref.ly/Mark7:28) *See also* [JDG 1:7](https://ref.ly/Judg1:7); [LUK 16:21](https://ref.ly/Luke16:21)

Eating at the table of rulers
-----------------------------

### Rulers were required to entertain

**[NEH 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Neh5:17-Neh5:18)** *See also* [1KI 4:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:27), [1KI 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:4-1Kgs10:5) parallel [2CH 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:3-2Chr9:4)

### A bestowal of authority

**[LUK 22:29–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:29-Luke22:30)** *See also* [MAT 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2-Matt22:3); [LUK 14:15](https://ref.ly/Luke14:15)

### A mark of honour and favour

[GEN 43:34](https://ref.ly/Gen43:34); ; [2SA 9:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:11); ; [1KI 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:7), [1KI 18:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:19); ; [2KI 25:28–25:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:28-2Kgs25:29) parallel [JER 52:32–52:33](https://ref.ly/Jer52:32-Jer52:33); ; [LUK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Luke14:7) *Jesus Christ criticises those who seek the most prestigious places at the table.*

Sharing at the same table was a mark of fellowship
--------------------------------------------------

### A sign of participation

**[1CO 10:16–10:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16-1Cor10:20)**

### Unacceptable table fellowship

**[DAN 1:5–1:8](https://ref.ly/Dan1:5-Dan1:8)**; **[1CO 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:21)** *See also* [1SA 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:34) *Jonathan refuses to eat with Saul*; [1KI 18:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:19); [ISA 65:11](https://ref.ly/Isa65:11); [JER 7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:18)

### Betrayal of table fellowship

**[PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9)** *See also* [1SA 20:29–20:31](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:29-1Sam20:31) *Saul wishes to kill David while inviting him to share his table*; [DAN 11:27](https://ref.ly/Dan11:27); [MAT 26:20–26:21](https://ref.ly/Matt26:20-Matt26:21) parallels [MRK 14:18](https://ref.ly/Mark14:18), [LUK 22:21](https://ref.ly/Luke22:21)

Serving at table
----------------

**[LUK 22:27](https://ref.ly/Luke22:27)** *See also* [LUK 12:37](https://ref.ly/Luke12:37); [ACT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2) *The first Christians recognised the importance of practical service by appointing deacons to “wait on tables”.*

Tables used in business
-----------------------

**[JHN 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:15)** *See also* [MAT 21:12](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12) parallel [MRK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15)

Tables used in worship
----------------------

### The table in the tabernacle

**[EXO 25:23–25:30](https://ref.ly/Exod25:23-Exod25:30)** *The table was for the bread of the Presence: an offering of twelve loaves, one from each of the twelve tribes.* *See also* [EXO 37:10–37:16](https://ref.ly/Exod37:10-Exod37:16), [EXO 26:35](https://ref.ly/Exod26:35), [EXO 30:27](https://ref.ly/Exod30:27) *The table was anointed for use*; [EXO 35:13](https://ref.ly/Exod35:13) parallel [EXO 39:36](https://ref.ly/Exod39:36); [EXO 40:4](https://ref.ly/Exod40:4)NUM [3:31](https://ref.ly/Exod3:31), [NUM 4:7](https://ref.ly/Num4:7)[HEB 9:2](https://ref.ly/Heb9:2)

### The tables in Solomon's temple

**[2CH 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:8)** *See also* [1KI 7:48](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:48) parallel [2CH 4:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:19); [1CH 28:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:16); [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11), [2CH 29:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:18)

### The tables in Ezekiel's temple

[EZK 40:39–40:43](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:39-Ezek40:43), [EZK 41:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:22), [EZK 44:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:16)

### Tables in worship after the exile

[1CH 9:31–9:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:31-1Chr9:32); ; [NEH 10:33](https://ref.ly/Neh10:33); [MAL 1:7](https://ref.ly/Mal1:7), [MAL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Mal1:12)

## 5574: tablet (id: 5574)

A flat, usually rectangular, surface used for writing. Stone tablets, symbolising permanence, were usually engraved with an iron tool. Tablets of moist clay and writing boards of wood or ivory containing a layer of wax were inscribed with a stylus. The term is also used figuratively to refer to the human heart.

Tablets engraved with an iron tool
----------------------------------

**[JOB 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/Job19:23-Job19:24)** *See also* [JER 17:1](https://ref.ly/Jer17:1)

The Ten Commandments were inscribed on stone tablets
----------------------------------------------------

### The tablets were inscribed by God

**[DEU 9:9–9:11](https://ref.ly/Deut9:9-Deut9:11)** *In keeping with ancient Near Eastern practice, the two tablets may have been duplicates of a single covenant document.* *See also* [EXO 24:12](https://ref.ly/Exod24:12), [EXO 31:18](https://ref.ly/Exod31:18); [DEU 4:13](https://ref.ly/Deut4:13), [DEU 5:22](https://ref.ly/Deut5:22)

### The tablets were broken

**[EXO 32:15–32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:15-Exod32:19)** *See also* [DEU 9:15–9:17](https://ref.ly/Deut9:15-Deut9:17)

### New tablets were inscribed by God

**[DEU 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Deut10:1-Deut10:4)** *See also* [EXO 34:4](https://ref.ly/Exod34:4), [EXO 34:28–34:29](https://ref.ly/Exod34:28-Exod34:29)

### The new tablets were put into the ark

**[DEU 10:5](https://ref.ly/Deut10:5)** *See also* [1KI 8:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:9) parallel [2CH 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:10); [HEB 9:4](https://ref.ly/Heb9:4)

### God's commands were copied onto altar stones

**[DEU 27:2–27:8](https://ref.ly/Deut27:2-Deut27:8)** *See also* [JOS 8:30–8:32](https://ref.ly/Josh8:30-Josh8:32)

Writing tablets
---------------

### Tablets of unspecified material

**[HAB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hab2:2)** *See also* [ISA 30:8](https://ref.ly/Isa30:8); [LUK 1:63](https://ref.ly/Luke1:63)

### Other surfaces used for writing

[1KI 7:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:36) *The bases of the bronze lavers in Solomon's temple were engraved*; ; [ISA 8:1](https://ref.ly/Isa8:1) *Isaiah's inscription is with a pen on a scroll*; ; [EZK 4:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:1) *Ezekiel draws a plan of Jerusalem on a clay tablet. This was a common medium for plans in Babylon.*

Tablets of the heart
--------------------

### The commands and teachings of Proverbs

**[PRO 3:3](https://ref.ly/Prov3:3)** *See also* [PRO 6:21](https://ref.ly/Prov6:21), [PRO 7:3](https://ref.ly/Prov7:3)

### The new covenant

**[JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33)** *Under the new covenant God's law will be inward, written on human hearts rather than on tablets of stone.* *See also* [EZK 36:26–36:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:26-Ezek36:27); [2CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:3); [HEB 8:10](https://ref.ly/Heb8:10), [HEB 10:16](https://ref.ly/Heb10:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 19:23-24; Jeremiah 17:1; Deuteronomy 9:9-11; Exodus 24:12; Exodus 31:18; Deuteronomy 4:13; Deuteronomy 5:22; Exodus 32:15-19; Deuteronomy 9:15-17; Deuteronomy 10:1-4; Exodus 34:4; Exodus 34:28-29; Deuteronomy 10:5; 1 Kings 8:9; 2 Chronicles 5:10; Hebrews 9:4; Deuteronomy 27:2-8; Joshua 8:30-32; Habakkuk 2:2; Isaiah 30:8; Luke 1:63; 1 Kings 7:36; Isaiah 8:1; Ezekiel 4:1; Proverbs 3:3; Proverbs 6:21; Proverbs 7:3; Jeremiah 31:33; Ezekiel 36:26-27; 2 Corinthians 3:3; Hebrews 8:10; Hebrews 10:16
* **Associated Articles:** 1260: finger of God (ID: `1260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1349: covenant, God's at Sinai (ID: `1349@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1352: covenant, the new (ID: `1352@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4366: stones (ID: `4366@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5012: heart (ID: `5012@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5306: engraving (ID: `5306@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5352: inscriptions (ID: `5352@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5377: law, Ten Commandments (ID: `5377@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5439: pen (ID: `5439@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5638: writing (ID: `5638@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7306: ark of the covenant (ID: `7306@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7410: phylactery (ID: `7410@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5575: talk, idle (id: 5575)

Loose or careless speech, often motivated by curiosity, which can lead to unacceptable consequences. Scripture emphasises the importance of avoiding careless talk, and considering one's words with care.

Unfounded boasting is idle talk
-------------------------------

**[2PE 2:17–2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17-2Pet2:19)** *See also* [PSA 10:3](https://ref.ly/Ps10:3), [PSA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:6), [PSA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Ps52:1); [PRO 25:14](https://ref.ly/Prov25:14), [PRO 27:1](https://ref.ly/Prov27:1); [ACT 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9-Acts8:10); [JAS 4:16](https://ref.ly/Jas4:16)

Human questioning of God is idle talk
-------------------------------------

**[ROM 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:20-Rom9:21)** *See also* [ISA 29:16](https://ref.ly/Isa29:16), [ISA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9)

Flattery is idle talk
---------------------

**[ROM 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18)** *See also* [PRO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Prov7:21)

Meaningless conversation is idle talk
-------------------------------------

**[JOB 27:11–27:12](https://ref.ly/Job27:11-Job27:12)** *Job referring to the words of his comforters.* [JOB 8:2](https://ref.ly/Job8:2), [JOB 11:2](https://ref.ly/Job11:2), [JOB 35:16](https://ref.ly/Job35:16); [PRO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Prov20:14)

Examples of idle talk
---------------------

**[1KI 20:10–20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:10-1Kgs20:11)** *See also* [NUM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Num12:1-Num12:2); [JDG 9:26–9:41](https://ref.ly/Judg9:26-Judg9:41); [1SA 17:41–17:44](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:41-1Sam17:44)

Idle talk is folly
------------------

### A mark of the fool

**[JOB 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Job15:2-Job15:3)** *See also* [PRO 14:3](https://ref.ly/Prov14:3), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23); [ECC 10:12–10:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12-Eccl10:14); [1TI 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:6-1Tim1:7)

### Talking too much

**[ECC 5:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:7)** *See also* [PRO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19), [PRO 17:28](https://ref.ly/Prov17:28), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23); [ECC 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:3)

### Speaking rashly

**[PRO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Prov20:25)** *See also* [ECC 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:6); [JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)

Idle talk is combated by upright living
---------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)**

Idle talk has no place in the church
------------------------------------

**[1CO 4:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:20)** *See also* [EPH 4:29](https://ref.ly/Eph4:29), [EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4); [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11)

All idle talk will be judged
----------------------------

**[MAT 12:36–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36-Matt12:37)**

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Peter 2:17-19; Psalms 10:3; Psalms 49:6; Psalms 52:1; Proverbs 25:14; Proverbs 27:1; Acts 8:9-10; James 4:16; Romans 9:20-21; Isaiah 29:16; Isaiah 45:9; Romans 16:18; Proverbs 7:21; Job 27:11-12; Job 8:2; Job 11:2; Job 35:16; Proverbs 20:14; 1 Kings 20:10-11; Numbers 12:1-2; Judges 9:26-41; 1 Samuel 17:41-44; Job 15:2-3; Proverbs 14:3; Proverbs 14:23; Ecclesiastes 10:12-14; 1 Timothy 1:6-7; Ecclesiastes 5:7; Proverbs 10:19; Proverbs 17:28; Proverbs 21:23; Ecclesiastes 5:3; Proverbs 20:25; Ecclesiastes 5:6; James 1:19; 1 Peter 2:15; 1 Corinthians 4:20; Ephesians 4:29; Ephesians 5:4; Titus 1:10-11; Matthew 12:36-37

## 5576: tax collectors (id: 5576)

Governments have a right to collect taxes, but those who collect them have often been unpopular, not least because they seem to have demanded more than they were entitled to. Jesus Christ welcomed such people, as he did other sinners, and many repented as a result of his affirming attitude.

Honest tax collectors deserve respect
-------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6-Rom13:7)**

### Temple taxes collected by Levites

*See also* [2CH 24:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:6), [2CH 24:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:9)

### Temple tax paid by Jesus Christ

[MAT 17:24–17:26](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:26)

Tax collectors often unpopular
------------------------------

### In NT times

**[LUK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11)** *Tax collectors were widely suspected of corruption.*

Tax collectors despised as collaborators with Rome
--------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ recognises this popular contempt

*See also* [MAT 5:46](https://ref.ly/Matt5:46), [MAT 18:17](https://ref.ly/Matt18:17); [LUK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11)

Many tax collectors come to John
--------------------------------

**[LUK 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Luke3:12-Luke3:13)** *See also* [MAT 21:31–21:32](https://ref.ly/Matt21:31-Matt21:32); [LUK 7:29](https://ref.ly/Luke7:29)

Many tax collectors flock around Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

**[LUK 15:1](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1)** *See also* [MAT 9:10](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10) parallels [MRK 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15), [LUK 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29)

### Jesus Christ criticised for eating with tax collectors

**[LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:2)** *See also* [MAT 9:11](https://ref.ly/Matt9:11) parallels [MRK 2:16](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16), [LUK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Luke5:30); [MAT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19) parallel [LUK 7:34](https://ref.ly/Luke7:34); [LUK 19:1–19:7](https://ref.ly/Luke19:1-Luke19:7)

### Jesus Christ defends his concern

**[MAT 9:12–9:14](https://ref.ly/Matt9:12-Matt9:14)** parallels [MRK 2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:17), [LUK 5:31](https://ref.ly/Luke5:31) *See also* [LUK 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/Luke15:3-Luke15:4) *The parables in Luke chapter 15 are in part a defence of Jesus Christ's concern for tax collectors and sinners.*

## 5577: taxation (id: 5577)

Any form of payment levied on people by a supervising authority.

Taxation exacted from subject nations
-------------------------------------

### Tribute to Moab

[JDG 3:15](https://ref.ly/Judg3:15), [JDG 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Judg3:17-Judg3:18)

### Tribute to David

[2SA 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:2) parallel [1CH 18:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:2); ; [2SA 8:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam8:6) parallel [1CH 18:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr18:6)

### Tribute to Solomon

[1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21)

### Tribute to Assyria

[2KI 17:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:3); ; [HOS 10:6](https://ref.ly/Hos10:6)

### Tribute to Egypt

[2KI 23:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:33) parallel [2CH 36:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:3)

### Tribute to Judah

[2CH 17:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:11), [2CH 26:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:8); ; [ISA 16:1](https://ref.ly/Isa16:1)

### Tribute to Persia

[EST 10:1](https://ref.ly/Esth10:1)

### Universal tribute

**[PSA 72:10](https://ref.ly/Ps72:10)** *Universal tribute means universal reign. This psalm is widely regarded as Messianic.*

### Refusal to pay tribute was a sign of rebellion

*See also* [2KI 17:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:4); [EZR 4:13](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:13)

Taxation for the support of the state
-------------------------------------

### Land tax

**[2KI 23:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:35)** *See also* [GEN 41:34](https://ref.ly/Gen41:34); [NEH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Neh5:4)

Census tax
----------

[EXO 30:11–30:16](https://ref.ly/Exod30:11-Exod30:16); ; [LUK 2:1](https://ref.ly/Luke2:1)

### Trade tax

**[1KI 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14-1Kgs10:15)** parallel [2CH 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13-2Chr9:14)

### Such levies may be unjustly burdensome

**[1SA 8:10–8:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:10-1Sam8:18)** *See also* [DAN 11:20](https://ref.ly/Dan11:20)

Covenantal taxation: the tithe
------------------------------

### Tithing prescribed

**[LEV 27:30–27:33](https://ref.ly/Lev27:30-Lev27:33)** *As part of their covenantal obligations the Israelites were commanded to offer a tenth of their income to the LORD.* *See also* [GEN 14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:20)

### The tithe supported the priests and the poor

**[NUM 18:21](https://ref.ly/Num18:21)** *See also* [DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29)

### The tithe often seems to have been neglected

**[MAL 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:8-Mal3:10)** *See also* [2CH 31:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:4)

Taxation for the support of the temple
--------------------------------------

**[MAT 17:24–17:26](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:26)** *See also* [2CH 24:4–24:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:4-2Chr24:12) parallel [2KI 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:4-2Kgs12:5)

Jesus Christ and taxation
-------------------------

### Jesus Christ was falsely accused of opposing taxation

[LUK 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Luke23:1-Luke23:2)

### He himself paid tax

[MAT 17:24–17:27](https://ref.ly/Matt17:24-Matt17:27)

### He commanded others to pay taxes

[MAT 22:15–22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:22) parallels [MRK 12:13–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:20–20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:26)

Believers and taxation
----------------------

### Paying taxes demonstrates submission to God

[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1), [ROM 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:5-Rom13:6); ; [1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13)

### Paying taxes should silence unjust criticism

[ROM 13:3](https://ref.ly/Rom13:3); ; [1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)

### Paying taxes is reimbursement for services rendered

[MAT 22:15–22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:22) *“Give” in verse 21 means “give back as in settlement of a debt”*; ; [ROM 13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:7)

## 5578: tents (id: 5578)

A portable structure made of cloth or skins used mainly by nomads or semi\-nomads. God's tent or tabernacle is a meeting\-place with him.

Tents as portable dwellings used especially by semi\-nomadic peoples
--------------------------------------------------------------------

### They were living quarters for God's people

**[GEN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Gen4:20)** *See also* [GEN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:8), [GEN 13:12](https://ref.ly/Gen13:12), [GEN 18:1](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1), [GEN 25:27](https://ref.ly/Gen25:27); [NUM 11:10](https://ref.ly/Num11:10); [1SA 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:10); [2SA 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:1); [1KI 12:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:16)

### Sometimes women of distinction had their own tents

**[GEN 24:67](https://ref.ly/Gen24:67)** *See also* [GEN 31:33](https://ref.ly/Gen31:33); [JDG 4:17–4:18](https://ref.ly/Judg4:17-Judg4:18)

### A tent housed the ark of God

**[2SA 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:17)** parallel [1CH 16:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:1) *See also* [2SA 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:2); [1CH 15:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:1); [2CH 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:4)

### Tents associated with other peoples

**[JDG 6:5](https://ref.ly/Judg6:5)** *See also* [GEN 9:27](https://ref.ly/Gen9:27); [JDG 7:13](https://ref.ly/Judg7:13); [HAB 3:7](https://ref.ly/Hab3:7)

### Kings and armies camped in tents

**[JDG 7:13](https://ref.ly/Judg7:13)** *See also* [NUM 31:10](https://ref.ly/Num31:10); [2KI 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:7-2Kgs7:8)

Tents for storage
-----------------

**[1SA 17:54](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:54)** *See also* [JOS 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21-Josh7:23)

Tents were illustrative of a nomadic life
-----------------------------------------

**[JER 35:7](https://ref.ly/Jer35:7)**; **[HEB 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:9-Heb11:10)** *See also* [GEN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:8), [GEN 13:5](https://ref.ly/Gen13:5), [GEN 13:18](https://ref.ly/Gen13:18), [GEN 18:1–18:6](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1-Gen18:6), [GEN 20:13](https://ref.ly/Gen20:13); [EXO 16:16](https://ref.ly/Exod16:16), [EXO 33:8](https://ref.ly/Exod33:8), [EXO 33:10](https://ref.ly/Exod33:10); [NUM 1:50](https://ref.ly/Num1:50)

-----------------------------------

**[JOB 8:22](https://ref.ly/Job8:22)** *See also* [GEN 9:21](https://ref.ly/Gen9:21) *Noah's drunkenness*; [NUM 16:26](https://ref.ly/Num16:26) *the rebellion of Korah, Dathan and Abiram*; [JOS 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21-Josh7:23) *Achan's sin*; [1KI 20:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:16); [JOB 4:21](https://ref.ly/Job4:21), [JOB 11:14](https://ref.ly/Job11:14), [JOB 12:6](https://ref.ly/Job12:6), [JOB 15:34](https://ref.ly/Job15:34), [JOB 18:6](https://ref.ly/Job18:6), [JOB 21:28](https://ref.ly/Job21:28), [JOB 22:23](https://ref.ly/Job22:23); [PSA 84:10](https://ref.ly/Ps84:10)

God \`\`tenting'' with his people

## 5579: tetrarch (id: 5579)

A ruler over one quarter of a region; a son of Herod the Great who ruled one quarter of the area originally ruled by his father.

Herod Archelaus, governor of Judea, Idumea and Samaria
------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 2:22](https://ref.ly/Matt2:22)** *The tetrarchy system had been started by Philip of Macedon and adopted by the Romans.*

Herod Antipas, tetrarch of Galilee and Perea
--------------------------------------------

*See also* [MAT 14:1](https://ref.ly/Matt14:1), [MAT 14:9](https://ref.ly/Matt14:9) *Sometimes a tetrarch would be given the courtesy title of king*; [MRK 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mark6:14) parallel [LUK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Luke9:7); [LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1), [LUK 3:19](https://ref.ly/Luke3:19), [LUK 23:7](https://ref.ly/Luke23:7); [ACT 13:1](https://ref.ly/Acts13:1)

Herod Philip, tetrarch of Iturea and Traconitis
-----------------------------------------------

[LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1)

Lysanias, tetrarch of Abilene
-----------------------------

[LUK 3:1](https://ref.ly/Luke3:1)

## 5580: thirst (id: 5580)

Water is basic to human life. In Scripture the physical need to drink is often used as a picture of human spiritual need that only God can satisfy.

Physical thirst quenched
------------------------

**[EXO 17:1–17:6](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1-Exod17:6)** *See also* [NUM 20:2–20:11](https://ref.ly/Num20:2-Num20:11); [DEU 8:15](https://ref.ly/Deut8:15); [JDG 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Judg15:18-Judg15:19); [NEH 9:15](https://ref.ly/Neh9:15), [NEH 9:20](https://ref.ly/Neh9:20); [PSA 78:15–78:16](https://ref.ly/Ps78:15-Ps78:16), [PSA 105:41](https://ref.ly/Ps105:41), [PSA 107:4–107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:4-Ps107:9), [PSA 107:33](https://ref.ly/Ps107:33), [PSA 107:35](https://ref.ly/Ps107:35), [PSA 114:8](https://ref.ly/Ps114:8); [ISA 48:21](https://ref.ly/Isa48:21)

The satisfaction of spiritual thirst
------------------------------------

### It is to be found in God

**[ISA 41:17–41:18](https://ref.ly/Isa41:17-Isa41:18)** *See also* [PSA 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1-Ps23:2); [ISA 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Isa12:2-Isa12:3), [ISA 35:6–35:7](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6-Isa35:7), [ISA 58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:11)

### It is experienced through Jesus Christ

**[1CO 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:3-1Cor10:4)** *See also* [JHN 4:7–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:7-John4:14), [JHN 6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:35)

### It is known by the indwelling of the Holy Spirit

**[JHN 7:38–7:39](https://ref.ly/John7:38-John7:39)**

### The blessing is mediated through believers to others

*See also* [ISA 32:2](https://ref.ly/Isa32:2); [JHN 7:38](https://ref.ly/John7:38)

There is no satisfaction of spiritual thirst apart from God
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:13)** *See also* [ISA 65:13](https://ref.ly/Isa65:13); [JER 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Jer2:17-Jer2:18); [2PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17) *False teachers promise blessing and refreshment to others, but prove to be empty and worthless.*

Invitations to seek satisfaction for spiritual thirst in God
------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1)** *See also* [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

Those seeking satisfaction for spiritual thirst in God
------------------------------------------------------

### Examples of seekers

**[PSA 42:1–42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:1-Ps42:2)** *See also* [PSA 38:9](https://ref.ly/Ps38:9), [PSA 63:1](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1), [PSA 143:6](https://ref.ly/Ps143:6); [ISA 26:9](https://ref.ly/Isa26:9)

### They will be rewarded

**[MAT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6)**

### Heaven is their final reward

**[REV 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16-Rev7:17)** *See also* [EZK 47:1–47:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:1-Ezek47:12); [ZEC 14:8–14:9](https://ref.ly/Zech14:8-Zech14:9); [2CO 5:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:2); [2TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:8); [HEB 11:16](https://ref.ly/Heb11:16); [REV 21:6–21:7](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6-Rev21:7), [REV 22:1–22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 17:1-6; Numbers 20:2-11; Deuteronomy 8:15; Judges 15:18-19; Nehemiah 9:15; Nehemiah 9:20; Psalms 78:15-16; Psalms 105:41; Psalms 107:4-9; Psalms 107:33; Psalms 107:35; Psalms 114:8; Isaiah 48:21; Isaiah 41:17-18; Psalms 23:1-2; Isaiah 12:2-3; Isaiah 35:6-7; Isaiah 58:11; 1 Corinthians 10:3-4; John 4:7-14; John 6:35; John 7:38-39; Isaiah 32:2; John 7:38; Jeremiah 2:13; Isaiah 65:13; Jeremiah 2:17-18; 2 Peter 2:17; Isaiah 55:1; John 7:37; Revelation 22:17; Psalms 42:1-2; Psalms 38:9; Psalms 63:1; Psalms 143:6; Isaiah 26:9; Matthew 5:6; Revelation 7:16-17; Ezekiel 47:1-12; Zechariah 14:8-9; 2 Corinthians 5:2; 2 Timothy 4:8; Hebrews 11:16; Revelation 21:6-7; Revelation 22:1-3
