## 5581: throne (id: 5581)

An elevated seat occupied by a person with authority (e.g., a king). It symbolises the dignity, honour, sovereignty and the kingdom of the king whose seat it is. Supremely, these things belong to God himself.

A throne as an elevated seat
----------------------------

**[1KI 10:18–10:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:18-1Kgs10:19)** parallel [2CH 9:17–9:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:17-2Chr9:18)

A throne is occupied by a person with authority (usually a king)
----------------------------------------------------------------

*See also* [PRO 20:8](https://ref.ly/Prov20:8); [ISA 14:9](https://ref.ly/Isa14:9); [LUK 1:52](https://ref.ly/Luke1:52)

### Thrones of the kings of Israel

**[1KI 1:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:46)** *See also* [1KI 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:19), [1KI 7:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:7), [1KI 22:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:10) parallel [2CH 18:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:9); [2KI 11:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:19) parallel [2CH 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:20); [JER 13:18](https://ref.ly/Jer13:18); [ACT 12:21](https://ref.ly/Acts12:21)

### Thrones of the kings of the nations

[EXO 11:5](https://ref.ly/Exod11:5), [EXO 12:29](https://ref.ly/Exod12:29); ; [EST 1:2](https://ref.ly/Esth1:2), [EST 5:1](https://ref.ly/Esth5:1); ; [JER 1:15](https://ref.ly/Jer1:15); ; [EZK 26:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:16); ; [JON 3:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:6)

A throne symbolises dignity and honour
--------------------------------------

**[GEN 41:40](https://ref.ly/Gen41:40)** *See also* [1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8); [2KI 25:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:28) parallel [JER 52:32](https://ref.ly/Jer52:32); [ISA 22:23](https://ref.ly/Isa22:23)

A throne also symbolises sovereignty
------------------------------------

### The royal power of earthly kings

**[1KI 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:13)** *“Sit upon a throne” means “to exercise royal power”.* *See also* [DEU 17:181](https://ref.ly/Deut17:181)KI [1:17–1:35](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17-Deut1:35), [1KI 2:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:4), [1KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:12), [1KI 2:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:24), [1KI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:6), [1KI 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:5), [1KI 8:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:20) parallel [2CH 6:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:10); [1KI 8:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:25) parallel [2CH 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:16); [1KI 9:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:5), [1KI 10:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:9) parallel [2CH 9:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:8); [1CH 28:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:5), [1CH 29:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:23)[1KI 16:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:11); [2KI 10:32](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:32)KI [10:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:30), [2KI 15:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:12)[PSA 122:5](https://ref.ly/Ps122:5), [PSA 132:11–132:12](https://ref.ly/Ps132:11-Ps132:12); [JER 22:2](https://ref.ly/Jer22:2), [JER 22:30](https://ref.ly/Jer22:30), [JER 29:16](https://ref.ly/Jer29:16), [JER 33:17](https://ref.ly/Jer33:17) *This verse is fulfilled ultimately in Jesus Christ ([Lk 1:32](https://ref.ly/Luke1:32))*; [JER 43:10](https://ref.ly/Jer43:10)

### Satan's throne on earth

**[REV 2:13](https://ref.ly/Rev2:13)** *Satan is seen as “ruling” from Pergamum, the official centre of emperor worship in the province of Asia.*

### The beast's throne

**[REV 13:2](https://ref.ly/Rev13:2)** *See also* [REV 16:10](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10)

### The twenty\-four elders are enthroned

**[REV 4:4](https://ref.ly/Rev4:4)** *The elders are thought to be representative of either the whole company of believers in heaven or an exalted angelic order.* *See also* [REV 11:16](https://ref.ly/Rev11:16)

### Jesus Christ reigns from his eternal throne

**[ISA 9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:7)** *See also* [ZEC 6:13](https://ref.ly/Zech6:13); [MAT 25:31](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31); [LUK 1:32–1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:32-Luke1:33)

### Almighty God is seated in majesty on his holy throne

**[EXO 17:16](https://ref.ly/Exod17:16)** *See also* [PSA 47:8](https://ref.ly/Ps47:8); [HEB 8:1](https://ref.ly/Heb8:1), [HEB 12:21](https://ref.ly/Heb12:21)KI [22:19](https://ref.ly/Heb22:19) parallel [2CH 18:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:18); [EZK 1:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:26), [EZK 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:1); [DAN 7:9](https://ref.ly/Dan7:9); [REV 4:2–4:10](https://ref.ly/Rev4:2-Rev4:10), [REV 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rev5:1), [REV 5:7](https://ref.ly/Rev5:7), [REV 5:13](https://ref.ly/Rev5:13), [REV 7:10](https://ref.ly/Rev7:10), [REV 7:15](https://ref.ly/Rev7:15), [REV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Rev19:4), [REV 21:5](https://ref.ly/Rev21:5)PSA [9:4–9:10](https://ref.ly/Rev9:4-Rev9:10), [PSA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Ps11:4); [JER 49:38](https://ref.ly/Jer49:38); [REV 6:16](https://ref.ly/Rev6:16), [REV 20:11–20:12](https://ref.ly/Rev20:11-Rev20:12)REV [7:11](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11), [REV 8:3](https://ref.ly/Rev8:3), [REV 14:3](https://ref.ly/Rev14:3)REV [1:4](https://ref.ly/Rev1:4), [REV 4:5](https://ref.ly/Rev4:5), [REV 7:9](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9), [REV 12:5](https://ref.ly/Rev12:5), [REV 16:17](https://ref.ly/Rev16:17), [REV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Rev21:3), [REV 22:1](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1), [REV 22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3)

A throne symbolises a kingdom
-----------------------------

### The kingdoms of earthly kings

**[2SA 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:10)** *See also* [1KI 1:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:37), [1KI 1:47](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:47); [PRO 20:28](https://ref.ly/Prov20:28), [PRO 25:5](https://ref.ly/Prov25:5), [PRO 29:14](https://ref.ly/Prov29:14); [DAN 4:36](https://ref.ly/Dan4:36), [DAN 5:20](https://ref.ly/Dan5:20); [HAG 2:222](https://ref.ly/Hag2:222)SA [7:13–7:16](https://ref.ly/Hag7:13-Hag7:16) parallel [1CH 17:12–17:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:12-1Chr17:14); [1KI 2:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:33), [1KI 2:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:45), [1KI 9:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:5) parallel [2CH 7:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:18); [1CH 22:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:10); [PSA 89:4](https://ref.ly/Ps89:4), [PSA 89:29](https://ref.ly/Ps89:29), [PSA 89:36](https://ref.ly/Ps89:36)

### The unseen kingdom of God's righteous rule

**[PSA 89:14](https://ref.ly/Ps89:14)** *See also* [PSA 93:2](https://ref.ly/Ps93:2), [PSA 97:2](https://ref.ly/Ps97:2), [PSA 103:19](https://ref.ly/Ps103:19); [LAM 5:19](https://ref.ly/Lam5:19)

The ark of the covenant seen as God's throne
--------------------------------------------

**[1SA 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:4)** *God was understood to be enthroned between the cherubim (i.e., upon the lid, or “mercy seat”) of the ark of the covenant.* *See also* [2SA 6:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:2); [2KI 19:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:15) parallel [ISA 37:16](https://ref.ly/Isa37:16); [PSA 80:1](https://ref.ly/Ps80:1), [PSA 99:1](https://ref.ly/Ps99:1); [JER 17:12](https://ref.ly/Jer17:12)

Jerusalem is also called God's throne
-------------------------------------

**[JER 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jer3:17)**

Heaven described as God's throne
--------------------------------

**[PSA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Ps2:4)** *See also* [PSA 123:1](https://ref.ly/Ps123:1); [ISA 40:22](https://ref.ly/Isa40:22); [MAT 5:34](https://ref.ly/Matt5:34), [MAT 23:22](https://ref.ly/Matt23:22); [ACT 7:49](https://ref.ly/Acts7:49); [ISA 66:1](https://ref.ly/Isa66:1)

God's throne is a throne of grace
---------------------------------

**[HEB 4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:16)**

Believers share in the heavenly reign of Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Matt19:28)** *See also* [LUK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:30); [2TI 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12); [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21), [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Rev20:4), [REV 20:6](https://ref.ly/Rev20:6)

## 5582: tiredness (id: 5582)

A natural outcome of work and stress for which God has provided relief, rest and renewal. Scripture recognises that tiredness may have spiritual, as well as physical, causes.

Causes of tiredness
-------------------

### Work

**[MAT 8:24](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24)** parallels [MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38), [LUK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23) *See also* [ECC 10:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:15), [ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12)

### Afflictions and stress

**[JOB 16:7](https://ref.ly/Job16:7)** *See also* [2SA 17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:29); [1KI 19:3–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:5); [PSA 69:3](https://ref.ly/Ps69:3); [2CO 12:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:15)

### Concern and anxiety

**[PSA 31:9–31:10](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9-Ps31:10)** *See also* [PSA 6:6](https://ref.ly/Ps6:6), [PSA 32:3–32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:4), [PSA 119:28](https://ref.ly/Ps119:28); [JER 45:3](https://ref.ly/Jer45:3); [LUK 22:45](https://ref.ly/Luke22:45)

### Desire for money and possessions

**[PRO 23:4](https://ref.ly/Prov23:4)**

### Travelling

*See also* [DEU 25:18](https://ref.ly/Deut25:18); [JDG 8:4](https://ref.ly/Judg8:4); [2SA 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:14); [JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6)

### Old age

[ECC 12:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:3)

### Zeal for the Lord's work

[PSA 119:139](https://ref.ly/Ps119:139)

### Battles

[JDG 4:21](https://ref.ly/Judg4:21); ; [1SA 14:31](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:31); ; [2SA 21:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:15), [2SA 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:10); ; [JER 51:30](https://ref.ly/Jer51:30)

### Sin and rebellion

[ISA 57:10](https://ref.ly/Isa57:10); ; [JER 9:5](https://ref.ly/Jer9:5); ; [EZK 23:43](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:43)

### Foolishness

[ECC 1:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:8); ; [ISA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Isa46:1), [ISA 47:13](https://ref.ly/Isa47:13); ; [JER 12:13](https://ref.ly/Jer12:13)

Help from God to deal with tiredness
------------------------------------

### God's promises of help and rest

**[ISA 40:28–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:28-Isa40:31)** *See also* [ISA 46:4](https://ref.ly/Isa46:4); [2CO 9:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:8)

### Jesus Christ offers rest and encouragement

**[MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30)**; **[REV 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Rev2:2-Rev2:3)** *See also* [MRK 6:31](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31)

### Trusting God as the antidote to anxiety and sorrow

**[MAT 6:25–6:27](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:27)** parallel [LUK 12:25–12:27](https://ref.ly/Luke12:25-Luke12:27) *See also* [PSA 119:28](https://ref.ly/Ps119:28)

### God's provision of times for rest

[GEN 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gen1:5), [GEN 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gen2:2) parallels [EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11), [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15); ; [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); ; [JER 31:25](https://ref.ly/Jer31:25)

Help from others to deal with tiredness
---------------------------------------

### Encouragements to be strong in the Lord and to persevere

**[PSA 27:14](https://ref.ly/Ps27:14)**; **[1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58)** *See also* [GAL 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9); [2TH 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:13); [HEB 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1-Heb12:3)

### God's help channelled through others

**[ISA 50:4](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4)** *See also* [2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)

### Physical help from others

**[2SA 16:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:2)** *See also* [EXO 17:12](https://ref.ly/Exod17:12); [JDG 8:15](https://ref.ly/Judg8:15); [2SA 17:28–17:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:28-2Sam17:29); [1KI 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5-1Kgs19:6)

### Advice about lifestyle

**[EXO 18:17–18:23](https://ref.ly/Exod18:17-Exod18:23)**

## 5583: tools (id: 5583)

Implements used for agricultural or mechanical purposes. Early tools were of wood, flint or stone. The development of ironworking brought iron\-bladed tools and farm implements into common use. Axes were used to fell trees; hammers, chisels and saws were used by both carpenters and masons. Assyria and Babylon are depicted as tools in God's hand.

Tools made from iron and bronze
-------------------------------

### General references

**[GEN 4:22](https://ref.ly/Gen4:22)** *See also* [2SA 12:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:31) parallel [1CH 20:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:3) *saws, iron picks and axes*; [2SA 23:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:7) *iron tools*

### Iron axe\-heads

**[2KI 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:4-2Kgs6:5)** *Axes comprised an iron head fitted to a wooden shaft.* *See also* [DEU 19:5](https://ref.ly/Deut19:5)

### Sharpening iron tools

**[1SA 13:19–13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:19-1Sam13:21)** *A ploughshare is the cutting blade of a plough. A mattock is a large pick with a flat edge used for loosening soil and cutting.* *See also* [ECC 10:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:10)

Tools used in specific tasks
----------------------------

### Axes used for felling trees

**[JDG 9:48](https://ref.ly/Judg9:48)** *See also* [DEU 20:19–20:20](https://ref.ly/Deut20:19-Deut20:20); [PSA 74:5–74:6](https://ref.ly/Ps74:5-Ps74:6)**[MAT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:10)** parallel [LUK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:9) *Felling trees with an axe as a symbol of judgment.* *See also* [JER 46:22–46:23](https://ref.ly/Jer46:22-Jer46:23)

### Woodworking tools

**[ISA 44:13](https://ref.ly/Isa44:13)** *See also* [EXO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:6) parallel [DEU 15:17](https://ref.ly/Deut15:17) *awl*; [JER 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/Jer10:3-Jer10:4) *chisel, hammer and nails*

### Metalworking tools

**[JOB 19:24](https://ref.ly/Job19:24)** *See also* [EXO 28:36](https://ref.ly/Exod28:36) parallel [EXO 39:30](https://ref.ly/Exod39:30); [JER 17:1](https://ref.ly/Jer17:1)**[ISA 41:7](https://ref.ly/Isa41:7)** *See also* [EXO 32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:4); [ISA 44:12](https://ref.ly/Isa44:12); [JER 10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:9)

### Stoneworking tools

**[1KI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:7), [1KI 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:9)** *Saws were used in both carpentry and stonemasonry.* *See also* [EXO 34:1](https://ref.ly/Exod34:1); [DEU 10:3](https://ref.ly/Deut10:3); [ISA 22:16](https://ref.ly/Isa22:16); [JER 23:29](https://ref.ly/Jer23:29)EXO [20:25](https://ref.ly/Jer20:25); [DEU 27:5](https://ref.ly/Deut27:5); [JOS 8:31](https://ref.ly/Josh8:31)

### Agricultural tools

**[ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4)** parallel [MIC 4:3](https://ref.ly/Mic4:3) *A pruning hook is a curved blade used in pruning.* *See also* [JOL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Joel3:10); [ISA 18:5](https://ref.ly/Isa18:5) *pruning knives*; [ISA 30:24](https://ref.ly/Isa30:24) *fork and shovel*[JER 15:7](https://ref.ly/Jer15:7); [MAT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:12) parallel [LUK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Luke3:17)

----------------------------------

## 5585: towers (id: 5585)

Tall structures, normally constructed of brick or stone. They were chiefly used for observation of the surrounding countryside in order to warn of the approach of enemies or robbers.

Examples of watchtowers
-----------------------

**[ISA 32:14](https://ref.ly/Isa32:14)** *See also* [JDG 8:17](https://ref.ly/Judg8:17), [JDG 9:51–9:52](https://ref.ly/Judg9:51-Judg9:52); [1CH 27:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:25); [2CH 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:7), [2CH 26:9–26:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:9-2Chr26:10), [2CH 26:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:15), [2CH 32:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:5); [NEH 3:25–3:27](https://ref.ly/Neh3:25-Neh3:27)

**[EZK 26:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:4)** *See also* [ISA 30:25](https://ref.ly/Isa30:25), [ISA 32:14](https://ref.ly/Isa32:14); [JER 50:15](https://ref.ly/Jer50:15); [EZK 26:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:9)

### Watchmen stationed in towers

**[2KI 9:17–9:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:17-2Kgs9:20)** *See also* [ISA 21:6–21:9](https://ref.ly/Isa21:6-Isa21:9); [LAM 4:17](https://ref.ly/Lam4:17)

### Towers built as protection against thieves

**[MAT 21:33](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33)** parallel [MRK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1) *See also* [ISA 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Isa5:1-Isa5:2)

## 5586: town (id: 5586)

## 5587: trade (id: 5587)

Fair business transactions are essential to distribute the benefits of God's goodness between individuals and nations. Scripture insists on honesty in trade and warns against abuses.

Trade in ancient Israel
-----------------------

[LEV 25:14](https://ref.ly/Lev25:14) *Land transactions required the approval of the ruler of the state*; ; [2KI 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:7), [2KI 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:1); ; [NEH 3:31–3:32](https://ref.ly/Neh3:31-Neh3:32); [PRO 31:13–31:18](https://ref.ly/Prov31:13-Prov31:18), [PRO 31:24](https://ref.ly/Prov31:24); [ZEP 1:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:11); ; [MAT 21:12](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12) parallels [MRK 11:15–11:16](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:16), [LUK 19:45](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45), [JHN 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:16) *trade in Jerusalem in Jesus Christ's day*

International trade
-------------------

### Under Solomon

**[1KI 9:26–9:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:26-1Kgs9:28)** parallel [2CH 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:17-2Chr8:18) *See also* [1KI 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14-1Kgs10:15) parallel [2CH 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13-2Chr9:14) *Transit taxes were imposed on inter\-state trade. Also some taxes were owed to the state by merchants for some kinds of trade*; [1KI 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:28-1Kgs10:29) parallel [2CH 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:16-2Chr1:17); [2CH 9:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:21); [SNG 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/Song3:6-Song3:7)

### Under Jehoshaphat

[1KI 22:48](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:48) parallel [2CH 20:35–20:37](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:35-2Chr20:37) *Soon afterwards the port of Ezion Geber passed back into the hands of Edom (see [2Ki 8:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:20)).*

## 5588: traditions (id: 5588)

Beliefs or customs handed down from previous generations. Believers are to hold to the traditions of the Christian faith. Human traditions can be contrary to God's word and thereby come into conflict with the Christian faith.

Examples of human traditions
----------------------------

[RUT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:7); ; [2CH 35:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:25); ; [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17), [EZK 24:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:22); ; [MRK 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:3-Mark7:4); ; [LUK 2:42](https://ref.ly/Luke2:42); ; [JHN 19:40](https://ref.ly/John19:40)

Believers are to hold to and hand on the traditions of the Christian faith
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:2)**; **[1TH 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:21)**; **[2TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:2)** *See also* [DEU 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9-Deut4:10), [DEU 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:7), [DEU 11:19](https://ref.ly/Deut11:19); [1CO 11:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23); [EPH 2:20](https://ref.ly/Eph2:20); [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15), [2TH 3:6](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:6)

Human traditions can be contrary to God's word
----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 15:3–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:6)** parallel [MRK 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9-Mark7:13)**[COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)** *See also* [MIC 6:16](https://ref.ly/Mic6:16); [COL 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:22); [TIT 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Titus1:13-Titus1:14)

The Christian faith is in conflict with traditions of purely human origins
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 2:18–2:22](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:22)** parallels [MAT 9:14–9:17](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:17), [LUK 5:33–5:39](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33-Luke5:39)**[1PE 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18-1Pet1:19)** *See also* [MAT 15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:2) parallel [MRK 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mark7:5); [GAL 2:11–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:16), [GAL 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Gal4:9-Gal4:11)

## 5589: trap (id: 5589)

A device used as a snare in hunting or warfare to capture animals or people; a tactic, plot or scheme designed to deceive someone into trouble or sin.

Traps used in hunting and warfare
---------------------------------

[JOB 40:24](https://ref.ly/Job40:24); ; [ISA 42:22](https://ref.ly/Isa42:22); ; [EZK 19:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:4); ; [AMO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Amos3:5)

Kinds of traps
--------------

### Words

[JDG 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Judg12:5-Judg12:6); ; [PRO 6:2](https://ref.ly/Prov6:2), [PRO 12:13](https://ref.ly/Prov12:13); ; [ISA 29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:21); ; [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8); ; [MRK 12:13](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13), [MRK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Mark12:15)

### Actions

[GEN 27:6–27:12](https://ref.ly/Gen27:6-Gen27:12), [GEN 37:23–37:24](https://ref.ly/Gen37:23-Gen37:24); ; [1SA 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:9); ; [2SA 21:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:5); ; [2KI 21:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:24); ; [LAM 1:19](https://ref.ly/Lam1:19); ; [HAB 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Hab2:9-Hab2:11); ; [LUK 22:48](https://ref.ly/Luke22:48)

### Temptation

**[1TI 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9)** *See also* [EXO 23:33](https://ref.ly/Exod23:33), [EXO 34:12](https://ref.ly/Exod34:12); [DEU 7:16](https://ref.ly/Deut7:16), [DEU 7:25](https://ref.ly/Deut7:25); [JOS 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12-Josh23:13); [JDG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Judg2:3), [JDG 8:27](https://ref.ly/Judg8:27), [JDG 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/Judg16:15-Judg16:16); [PRO 11:6](https://ref.ly/Prov11:6), [PRO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Prov20:25), [PRO 22:24–22:25](https://ref.ly/Prov22:24-Prov22:25); [ECC 7:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:26); [EZK 13:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:18); [HOS 5:1](https://ref.ly/Hos5:1)

### Evil times

[ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12)

### Fear of other people

[PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25)

### Wisdom is alert to traps

[1CH 12:16–12:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:16-1Chr12:17); ; [PRO 13:14](https://ref.ly/Prov13:14)

Those who trap others have evil hearts
--------------------------------------

**[ISA 32:7](https://ref.ly/Isa32:7)** *See also* [PSA 36:3–36:4](https://ref.ly/Ps36:3-Ps36:4), [PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6); [PRO 6:14](https://ref.ly/Prov6:14), [PRO 12:20](https://ref.ly/Prov12:20), [PRO 16:27–16:30](https://ref.ly/Prov16:27-Prov16:30)

Traps prepared by sinners
-------------------------

### Against innocent people

**[PSA 38:12](https://ref.ly/Ps38:12)** *See also* [GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8); [2CH 24:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:21); [NEH 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Neh6:1-Neh6:4); [EST 2:22](https://ref.ly/Esth2:22), [EST 9:25](https://ref.ly/Esth9:25); [JOB 30:11–30:12](https://ref.ly/Job30:11-Job30:12); [PSA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ps10:2), [PSA 31:13](https://ref.ly/Ps31:13), [PSA 35:4](https://ref.ly/Ps35:4), [PSA 119:110](https://ref.ly/Ps119:110), [PSA 140:5](https://ref.ly/Ps140:5), [PSA 142:3](https://ref.ly/Ps142:3); [JER 5:26](https://ref.ly/Jer5:26), [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8), [JER 18:22](https://ref.ly/Jer18:22); [LAM 3:60–3:61](https://ref.ly/Lam3:60-Lam3:61), [LAM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Lam4:20); [DAN 6:4–6:7](https://ref.ly/Dan6:4-Dan6:7); [ACT 23:19–23:21](https://ref.ly/Acts23:19-Acts23:21)

### Against God

**[PSA 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1-Ps2:2)** *See also* [ACT 4:25](https://ref.ly/Acts4:25); [PSA 21:11](https://ref.ly/Ps21:11), [PSA 83:5](https://ref.ly/Ps83:5); [HOS 7:15](https://ref.ly/Hos7:15); [NAM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Nah1:11)

### Against Jesus Christ

### Against the truth

**[HOS 9:8](https://ref.ly/Hos9:8)** *See also* [ACT 14:5](https://ref.ly/Acts14:5), [ACT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Acts20:3); [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)

### The wicked caught in their own traps

**[PRO 28:10](https://ref.ly/Prov28:10)** *See also* [EST 7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:10); [JOB 18:7–18:10](https://ref.ly/Job18:7-Job18:10); [PSA 9:16](https://ref.ly/Ps9:16); [PRO 1:18](https://ref.ly/Prov1:18), [PRO 5:22](https://ref.ly/Prov5:22), [PRO 12:13](https://ref.ly/Prov12:13), [PRO 18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:7), [PRO 29:6](https://ref.ly/Prov29:6); [JER 8:9](https://ref.ly/Jer8:9); [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24)

The devil sets traps
--------------------

**[1TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:7)** *See also* [ACT 13:10](https://ref.ly/Acts13:10); [2CO 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:11); [EPH 6:11](https://ref.ly/Eph6:11); [2TI 2:26](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:26)

-----------------------------

### God forbids the trapping of the innocent

[ZEC 8:17](https://ref.ly/Zech8:17)

## 5591: treasure (id: 5591)

Highly valued objects or accumulated riches. Treasure has at best only short\-term benefit but can bring spiritual dangers. Spiritual treasure is of eternal value and comes from knowing and serving God.

The limitations of accumulated treasure
---------------------------------------

### It is easily lost

**[ISA 64:11](https://ref.ly/Isa64:11)** *See also* [2KI 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:13); [JER 15:13](https://ref.ly/Jer15:13), [JER 17:3](https://ref.ly/Jer17:3), [JER 20:5](https://ref.ly/Jer20:5); [LAM 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Lam1:10-Lam1:11); [HOS 13:15](https://ref.ly/Hos13:15); [OBA 1:6](https://ref.ly/Obad1:6); [MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19); [JAS 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Jas5:2-Jas5:3); [REV 18:14](https://ref.ly/Rev18:14)

### It is no security against God's displeasure

**[PRO 11:4](https://ref.ly/Prov11:4)** *See also* [JOB 20:20](https://ref.ly/Job20:20); [JER 51:13](https://ref.ly/Jer51:13); [EZK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:19); [HOS 9:6](https://ref.ly/Hos9:6); [ZEP 1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:18); [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)

### It does not satisfy

**[ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10)** *See also* [PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6); [HAG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hag1:6)

The spiritual dangers of accumulated treasure
---------------------------------------------

### It can lead to pride

**[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [DEU 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:13-Deut8:14); [EZK 7:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:20), [EZK 28:4–28:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:4-Ezek28:5); [DAN 4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30)

### It can lead a person away from God

**[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)** *See also* [JOB 31:24–31:25](https://ref.ly/Job31:24-Job31:25), [JOB 31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:28); [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28)

### It can lead to greed

**[LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15)** *See also* [1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)

### Acquiring it can lead to dishonesty

[JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21); ; [PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10); ; [PRO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Prov21:6)

### It can lead to idolatry

[EXO 32:2–32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:4); ; [ISA 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Isa2:7-Isa2:8); ; [EZK 7:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:20), [EZK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:17)

Spiritual treasure
------------------

### Treasure consists in knowing God and his wisdom

**[COL 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Col2:2-Col2:3)** *See also* [JOB 28:12–28:19](https://ref.ly/Job28:12-Job28:19); [PRO 2:4](https://ref.ly/Prov2:4), [PRO 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Prov3:13-Prov3:15), [PRO 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Prov8:10-Prov8:11), [PRO 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Prov24:3-Prov24:4); [ISA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Isa33:6)

### God's word is supreme treasure

[JOB 23:12](https://ref.ly/Job23:12); ; [PSA 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Ps19:9-Ps19:10), [PSA 119:72](https://ref.ly/Ps119:72), [PSA 119:127](https://ref.ly/Ps119:127)

### The kingdom of God is supreme treasure

**[MAT 13:44](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44)** *See also* [MAT 13:45–13:46](https://ref.ly/Matt13:45-Matt13:46), [MAT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Matt13:52)

### Spiritual treasure is more important than material possessions

**[HEB 11:26](https://ref.ly/Heb11:26)** *See also* [JOB 22:23–22:25](https://ref.ly/Job22:23-Job22:25); [MAT 13:44–13:46](https://ref.ly/Matt13:44-Matt13:46)

### It is of eternal value

**[LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Luke12:33)** *See also* [MAT 6:20](https://ref.ly/Matt6:20); [1PE 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:4)

### It is gained through generosity and wisdom

**[MAT 19:21](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21)** parallels [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21), [LUK 18:22](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22) *See also* [PRO 8:21](https://ref.ly/Prov8:21), [PRO 15:6](https://ref.ly/Prov15:6); [1TI 6:18–6:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:18-1Tim6:19)

Treasure reveals true commitment
--------------------------------

**[MAT 6:21](https://ref.ly/Matt6:21)** parallel [LUK 12:34](https://ref.ly/Luke12:34) *See also* [MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24) parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13)

Believers willingly give God their treasures
--------------------------------------------

**[MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11)** *See also* [EXO 35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:22); [JOS 6:24](https://ref.ly/Josh6:24); [1CH 29:3–29:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:3-1Chr29:5)

God's people are his treasured possession
-----------------------------------------

**[DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6)** *See also* [EXO 19:5](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5); [DEU 14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:2), [DEU 26:18](https://ref.ly/Deut26:18); [PSA 135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:4); [MAL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Mal3:17)

The gospel as treasure in clay jars
-----------------------------------

[2CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7)

## 5592: treaty (id: 5592)

A formal agreement between individuals or nations, promising commitment to each other's interests. Israel was forbidden to enter into such agreements with other nations in the promised land.

Treaties between individuals
----------------------------

**[1SA 18:3–18:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:3-1Sam18:4)** *See also* [GEN 21:22–21:32](https://ref.ly/Gen21:22-Gen21:32), [GEN 26:26–26:31](https://ref.ly/Gen26:26-Gen26:31); [1SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:8), [1SA 20:16–20:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:16-1Sam20:17), [1SA 22:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:8), [1SA 23:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:18); [2SA 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:12-2Sam3:13)

Treaties between individuals acting on behalf of their nation
-------------------------------------------------------------

[1KI 5:12–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:12-1Kgs5:18), [1KI 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:18-1Kgs15:19); ; [ISA 33:8](https://ref.ly/Isa33:8) *Probably the agreement made when Hezekiah paid large sums to Sennacherib ([2Ki 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:14))*; ; [EZK 17:12–17:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:12-Ezek17:14)

Treaties between cities or nations
----------------------------------

[JOS 9:3–9:16](https://ref.ly/Josh9:3-Josh9:16), [JOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Josh10:1), [JOS 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/Josh11:18-Josh11:19); ; [1SA 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:1-1Sam11:2); ; [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1)

Treaties entered into should be honoured
----------------------------------------

**[EZK 17:15–17:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:15-Ezek17:18)** *See also* [JER 34:8–34:20](https://ref.ly/Jer34:8-Jer34:20); [DAN 11:22–11:24](https://ref.ly/Dan11:22-Dan11:24) *“he will act deceitfully” (verse 23\), is a reference to Antiochus Epiphanes (of the Seleucid dynasty) who reneged on the friendship shown to Egypt*; [HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4); [AMO 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Amos1:9-Amos1:10); [GAL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gal3:15)

Israel forbidden to enter into treaties with other nations in the promised land
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:2)** *See also* [EXO 23:31–23:32](https://ref.ly/Exod23:31-Exod23:32), [EXO 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/Exod34:11-Exod34:12), [EXO 34:15–34:16](https://ref.ly/Exod34:15-Exod34:16); [DEU 23:6](https://ref.ly/Deut23:6); [1KI 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:34), [1KI 20:42–20:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:42-1Kgs20:43); [EZR 9:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:12)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 18:3-4; Genesis 21:22-32; Genesis 26:26-31; 1 Samuel 20:8; 1 Samuel 20:16-17; 1 Samuel 22:8; 1 Samuel 23:18; 2 Samuel 3:12-13; 1 Kings 5:12-18; 1 Kings 15:18-19; Isaiah 33:8; 2 Kings 18:14; Ezekiel 17:12-14; Joshua 9:3-16; Joshua 10:1; Joshua 11:18-19; 1 Samuel 11:1-2; Hosea 12:1; Ezekiel 17:15-18; Jeremiah 34:8-20; Daniel 11:22-24; Hosea 10:4; Amos 1:9-10; Galatians 3:15; Deuteronomy 7:1-2; Exodus 23:31-32; Exodus 34:11-12; Exodus 34:15-16; Deuteronomy 23:6; 1 Kings 20:34; 1 Kings 20:42-43; Ezra 9:12
* **Associated Articles:** 1346: covenants, nature of (ID: `1346@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5205: alliance (ID: `5205@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5690: friends (ID: `5690@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5783: agreement (ID: `5783@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5593: trial (id: 5593)

A process of testing or investigation, which may be legal, as in a court case, or spiritual, as in a testing of faith or power. Scripture makes reference to both.

Legal trials
------------

### The need for legal trials

[DEU 16:18–16:20](https://ref.ly/Deut16:18-Deut16:20); ; [JHN 7:50–7:51](https://ref.ly/John7:50-John7:51); ; [ACT 16:37–16:39](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37-Acts16:39)

### Trials concerning accidental killings

**[JOS 20:9](https://ref.ly/Josh20:9)** *See also* [NUM 35:9–35:12](https://ref.ly/Num35:9-Num35:12); [JOS 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/Josh20:1-Josh20:6)

### Unjust trials

**[MRK 13:9–13:13](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9-Mark13:13)** parallels [MAT 24:9–24:11](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9-Matt24:11), [LUK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12) *See also* [1KI 21:1–21:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1-1Kgs21:14); [PSA 37:32–37:33](https://ref.ly/Ps37:32-Ps37:33); [ACT 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/Acts12:1-Acts12:4), [ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37)

### The trial of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:57–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:57-Matt26:68)** parallels [MRK 14:53–14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:53-Mark14:65), [JHN 18:19–18:24](https://ref.ly/John18:19-John18:24) *See also* [MAT 27:11–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:11-Matt27:26) parallels [MRK 15:2–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:2-Mark15:15), [LUK 23:2–23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:2-Luke23:25), [JHN 18:28–19:16](https://ref.ly/John18:28-John19:16)

### The trial of Paul

**[ACT 27:23–27:24](https://ref.ly/Acts27:23-Acts27:24)** *See also* [ACT 22:30–23:11](https://ref.ly/Acts22:30-Acts23:11), [ACT 24:1–24:23](https://ref.ly/Acts24:1-Acts24:23), [ACT 25:1–25:12](https://ref.ly/Acts25:1-Acts25:12), [ACT 25:23–26:32](https://ref.ly/Acts25:23-Acts26:32)

Trials of faith
---------------

### Through testing or temptation

**[JAS 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2-Jas1:3)** *See also* [GEN 22:1](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1); [LUK 22:28](https://ref.ly/Luke22:28); [2CO 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:1-2Cor8:2); [1TH 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:2-1Thess3:4); [2TH 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:4); [JAS 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jas1:12); [1PE 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6), [1PE 4:12–4:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:12-1Pet4:16); [2PE 2:4–2:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:4-2Pet2:9); [REV 3:10](https://ref.ly/Rev3:10)

### Through burdensome responsibilities

**[GAL 4:14](https://ref.ly/Gal4:14)**

Trials of strength or power
---------------------------

**[DEU 7:19](https://ref.ly/Deut7:19)** *See also* [EXO 7:8–7:24](https://ref.ly/Exod7:8-Exod7:24); [DEU 29:2–29:3](https://ref.ly/Deut29:2-Deut29:3); [1KI 18:16–18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:16-1Kgs18:40)

## 5594: tribute (id: 5594)

-----------------------

## 5595: trumpet (id: 5595)

A musical instrument with a loud, piercing sound, used as a signal, both in times of war and of peace. It was also used extensively in Israelite worship and was frequently associated with divine activity.

Trumpets used to summon the Israelites
--------------------------------------

**[NUM 10:1–10:7](https://ref.ly/Num10:1-Num10:7)** *See also* [ISA 27:13](https://ref.ly/Isa27:13)

Trumpets sounded to announce religious festivals
------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:8–25:12](https://ref.ly/Lev25:8-Lev25:12)** *See also* [LEV 23:24](https://ref.ly/Lev23:24); [NUM 10:10](https://ref.ly/Num10:10), [NUM 29:1](https://ref.ly/Num29:1); [PSA 81:3–81:4](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3-Ps81:4) *The NIV usually translates the Hebrew word “shophar” as “trumpet”, but in this instance it is translated “ram's horn”*; [JOL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Joel2:15)

Trumpets sounded to attract public attention
--------------------------------------------

**[2SA 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:1)** *See also* [1SA 13:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:3); [MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2)

Trumpets blown to announce the accession of a king
--------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 1:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:39)** *See also* [2SA 15:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:10); [1KI 1:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:34), [1KI 1:41–1:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:41-1Kgs1:43); [2KI 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:13), [2KI 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:14) parallel [2CH 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:13)

Trumpets used to warn of impending danger
-----------------------------------------

**[JOL 2:1](https://ref.ly/Joel2:1)** *See also* [JER 4:5](https://ref.ly/Jer4:5), [JER 6:1](https://ref.ly/Jer6:1), [JER 6:17](https://ref.ly/Jer6:17); [EZK 33:1–33:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:1-Ezek33:6); [HOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Hos8:1); [AMO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Amos3:6)

Use of the trumpet in war
-------------------------

### As a call to arms

**[JDG 6:33–6:35](https://ref.ly/Judg6:33-Judg6:35)** *See also* [JDG 3:27–3:28](https://ref.ly/Judg3:27-Judg3:28); [NEH 4:16–4:20](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16-Neh4:20); [ISA 18:3](https://ref.ly/Isa18:3); [JER 51:27](https://ref.ly/Jer51:27); [EZK 7:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:14); [1CO 14:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:8)

### As a battle cry

**[2CH 13:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:12)** *See also* [JOS 6:2–6:20](https://ref.ly/Josh6:2-Josh6:20); [JDG 7:8](https://ref.ly/Judg7:8), [JDG 7:16–7:22](https://ref.ly/Judg7:16-Judg7:22); [2CH 13:14–13:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:14-2Chr13:15); [JOB 39:24–39:25](https://ref.ly/Job39:24-Job39:25); [JER 4:19–4:21](https://ref.ly/Jer4:19-Jer4:21), [JER 42:14](https://ref.ly/Jer42:14); [HOS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Hos5:8); [AMO 2:2](https://ref.ly/Amos2:2); [ZEP 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:15-Zeph1:16)

### To signal retreat

**[2SA 2:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:28)** *See also* [2SA 20:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:22)

Use of the trumpet in worship
-----------------------------

### Played by priests

**[2CH 29:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:26)** *See also* [EZR 3:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:10); [NEH 12:31–12:36](https://ref.ly/Neh12:31-Neh12:36), [NEH 12:40–12:42](https://ref.ly/Neh12:40-Neh12:42)

### Played in the tabernacle

**[1CH 16:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:6)** *See also* [1CH 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:24), [1CH 16:42](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:42)

### Played in the temple

**[2CH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12-2Chr5:13)** *See also* [2KI 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:13); [2CH 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:6), [2CH 29:27–29:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:27-2Chr29:28)

### Played to praise God

**[PSA 150:3](https://ref.ly/Ps150:3)** *See also* [1CH 13:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:8); [2CH 15:14–15:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:14-2Chr15:15), [2CH 20:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:28), [2CH 29:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:26); [NEH 12:40–12:41](https://ref.ly/Neh12:40-Neh12:41); [PSA 98:4–98:6](https://ref.ly/Ps98:4-Ps98:6)

### Accompanying the ark of the covenant

**[1CH 15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:28)** parallel [2SA 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14-2Sam6:15) *See also* [JOS 6:4](https://ref.ly/Josh6:4), [JOS 6:8](https://ref.ly/Josh6:8), [JOS 6:13](https://ref.ly/Josh6:13)

The trumpet associated with divine activity
-------------------------------------------

### In visible manifestations of God

**[EXO 19:16–19:19](https://ref.ly/Exod19:16-Exod19:19)** *See also* [EXO 20:18](https://ref.ly/Exod20:18); [ZEC 9:14](https://ref.ly/Zech9:14); [HEB 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Heb12:18-Heb12:21)

### At the second coming of Jesus Christ

**[1TH 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:16)** *See also* [MAT 24:31](https://ref.ly/Matt24:31); [1CO 15:51–15:52](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:51-1Cor15:52)

### In metaphorical use

**[REV 4:1](https://ref.ly/Rev4:1)** *See also* [ISA 58:1](https://ref.ly/Isa58:1); [REV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10)

The trumpet associated with angelic activity
--------------------------------------------

**[REV 8:2](https://ref.ly/Rev8:2)** *See also* [REV 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/Rev8:6-Rev8:8), [REV 8:10](https://ref.ly/Rev8:10), [REV 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/Rev8:12-Rev8:13), [REV 9:1](https://ref.ly/Rev9:1), [REV 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Rev9:13-Rev9:14), [REV 10:7](https://ref.ly/Rev10:7), [REV 11:15](https://ref.ly/Rev11:15)

Trumpets sometimes made from silver
-----------------------------------

**[NUM 10:2](https://ref.ly/Num10:2)**

Trumpets sometimes made from animal horn
----------------------------------------

**[JOS 6:4](https://ref.ly/Josh6:4)** *See also* [EXO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Exod19:13); [PSA 81:3](https://ref.ly/Ps81:3)

The trumpet in non\-Israelite use
---------------------------------

**[REV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Rev18:22)** *See also* [JER 51:27](https://ref.ly/Jer51:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 10:1-7; Isaiah 27:13; Leviticus 25:8-12; Leviticus 23:24; Numbers 10:10; Numbers 29:1; Psalms 81:3-4; Joel 2:15; 2 Samuel 20:1; 1 Samuel 13:3; Matthew 6:2; 1 Kings 1:39; 2 Samuel 15:10; 1 Kings 1:34; 1 Kings 1:41-43; 2 Kings 9:13; 2 Kings 11:14; 2 Chronicles 23:13; Joel 2:1; Jeremiah 4:5; Jeremiah 6:1; Jeremiah 6:17; Ezekiel 33:1-6; Hosea 8:1; Amos 3:6; Judges 6:33-35; Judges 3:27-28; Nehemiah 4:16-20; Isaiah 18:3; Jeremiah 51:27; Ezekiel 7:14; 1 Corinthians 14:8; 2 Chronicles 13:12; Joshua 6:2-20; Judges 7:8; Judges 7:16-22; 2 Chronicles 13:14-15; Job 39:24-25; Jeremiah 4:19-21; Jeremiah 42:14; Hosea 5:8; Amos 2:2; Zephaniah 1:15-16; 2 Samuel 2:28; 2 Samuel 20:22; 2 Chronicles 29:26; Ezra 3:10; Nehemiah 12:31-36; Nehemiah 12:40-42; 1 Chronicles 16:6; 1 Chronicles 15:24; 1 Chronicles 16:42; 2 Chronicles 5:12-13; 2 Kings 12:13; 2 Chronicles 7:6; 2 Chronicles 29:27-28; Psalms 150:3; 1 Chronicles 13:8; 2 Chronicles 15:14-15; 2 Chronicles 20:28; Nehemiah 12:40-41; Psalms 98:4-6; 1 Chronicles 15:28; 2 Samuel 6:14-15; Joshua 6:4; Joshua 6:8; Joshua 6:13; Exodus 19:16-19; Exodus 20:18; Zechariah 9:14; Hebrews 12:18-21; 1 Thessalonians 4:16; Matthew 24:31; 1 Corinthians 15:51-52; Revelation 4:1; Isaiah 58:1; Revelation 1:10; Revelation 8:2; Revelation 8:6-8; Revelation 8:10; Revelation 8:12-13; Revelation 9:1; Revelation 9:13-14; Revelation 10:7; Revelation 11:15; Numbers 10:2; Exodus 19:13; Psalms 81:3; Revelation 18:22

## 5596: victory (id: 5596)

The conquest of, or gaining a decisive advantage over, an enemy. The gospel proclaims the victory of God in Christ over the forces of evil in the world.

* **Associated Articles:** 5597: victory, as an act of God (ID: `5597@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5598: victory, over spiritual forces (ID: `5598@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5599: victory, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5599@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5597: victory, as an act of God (id: 5597)

God is the source of all victory, which he grants to those who obey his commands and put their confidence in him. This principle holds true in all believers' conflicts, whether physical or spiritual.

God is the source of all victory
--------------------------------

**[DEU 20:1–20:4](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1-Deut20:4)** *See also* [2CH 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:15); [PSA 18:35](https://ref.ly/Ps18:35); [1CO 15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:57); [2CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14)

Victory is achieved by God, not people
--------------------------------------

**[PSA 20:7–20:8](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7-Ps20:8)** *See also* [1SA 17:45–17:47](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45-1Sam17:47); [PSA 44:3–44:7](https://ref.ly/Ps44:3-Ps44:7), [PSA 60:11–60:12](https://ref.ly/Ps60:11-Ps60:12), [PSA 146:3](https://ref.ly/Ps146:3); [PRO 21:31](https://ref.ly/Prov21:31)

Victory should be ascribed to God
---------------------------------

**[PSA 118:15](https://ref.ly/Ps118:15)** *See also* [EXO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1); [PSA 21:1](https://ref.ly/Ps21:1); [1CO 15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:57); [REV 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Rev19:1-Rev19:2)

Victory depends on faithfulness
-------------------------------

### God grants victory to those who are faithful to him

**[1CH 22:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:13)** *See also* [EXO 23:20–23:23](https://ref.ly/Exod23:20-Exod23:23); [PSA 112:8](https://ref.ly/Ps112:8); [PRO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Prov2:7)

### Unfaithfulness to God brings defeat

**[NUM 14:41–14:43](https://ref.ly/Num14:41-Num14:43)** *See also* [DEU 28:15](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15), [DEU 28:25](https://ref.ly/Deut28:25); [2CH 24:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:20)

Examples of God giving victory
------------------------------

[EXO 17:8–17:15](https://ref.ly/Exod17:8-Exod17:15) *to Israel over the Amalekites*; [JOS 6:2–6:5](https://ref.ly/Josh6:2-Josh6:5), [JOS 6:20](https://ref.ly/Josh6:20); [JDG 7:15–7:22](https://ref.ly/Judg7:15-Judg7:22) *to Gideon over the Midianites*; ; [JDG 15:12–15:19](https://ref.ly/Judg15:12-Judg15:19) *to Samson over 1,000 Philistines*; ; [1SA 7:7–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:7-1Sam7:12) *to the Israelites at Mizpah*; ; [1SA 17:38–17:50](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:38-1Sam17:50) *to David over Goliath*; ; [1KI 18:36–18:39](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:36-1Kgs18:39) *to Elijah at Mount Carmel*; ; [1CH 11:4–11:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:4-1Chr11:9) *when David conquered Jerusalem*

God is able to work through defeat
----------------------------------

**[GEN 50:20](https://ref.ly/Gen50:20)** *See also* [JDG 16:24](https://ref.ly/Judg16:24), [JDG 16:30](https://ref.ly/Judg16:30); [ROM 8:28](https://ref.ly/Rom8:28); [2CO 4:7–4:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7-2Cor4:12), [2CO 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7-2Cor12:10)

God is able to fulfil his purposes through the victory of unbelievers
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 44:28–45:4](https://ref.ly/Isa44:28-Isa45:4)** *See also* [ISA 41:25](https://ref.ly/Isa41:25), [ISA 45:13](https://ref.ly/Isa45:13); [EZK 33:27–33:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:27-Ezek33:29) *This is how Ezekiel is to explain Babylon's victory over Jerusalem*; [ACT 2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:36), [ACT 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Acts3:17-Acts3:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 20:1-4; 2 Chronicles 20:15; Psalms 18:35; 1 Corinthians 15:57; 2 Corinthians 2:14; Psalms 20:7-8; 1 Samuel 17:45-47; Psalms 44:3-7; Psalms 60:11-12; Psalms 146:3; Proverbs 21:31; Psalms 118:15; Exodus 15:1; Psalms 21:1; Revelation 19:1-2; 1 Chronicles 22:13; Exodus 23:20-23; Psalms 112:8; Proverbs 2:7; Numbers 14:41-43; Deuteronomy 28:15; Deuteronomy 28:25; 2 Chronicles 24:20; Exodus 17:8-15; Joshua 6:2-5; Joshua 6:20; Judges 7:15-22; Judges 15:12-19; 1 Samuel 7:7-12; 1 Samuel 17:38-50; 1 Kings 18:36-39; 1 Chronicles 11:4-9; Genesis 50:20; Judges 16:24; Judges 16:30; Romans 8:28; 2 Corinthians 4:7-12; 2 Corinthians 12:7-10; Isaiah 44:28-45:4; Isaiah 41:25; Isaiah 45:13; Ezekiel 33:27-29; Acts 2:36; Acts 3:17-18
* **Associated Articles:** 1115: God, purpose of (ID: `1115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1230: God, the Lord (ID: `1230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5290: defeat (ID: `5290@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5605: warfare (ID: `5605@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5960: success (ID: `5960@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8727: enemies (ID: `8727@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5598: victory, over spiritual forces (id: 5598)

In spiritual warfare victory is assured since Jesus Christ has already won it on the cross and in the resurrection. Christians will eventually share in Christ's complete victory and through him overcome their spiritual enemies.

The victories won by Jesus Christ
---------------------------------

### Over sin and temptation

**[HEB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15)** *See also* [MAT 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:11); [HEB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:18); [1PE 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22)

### Over the world

**[JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33)** *See also* [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21), [REV 17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:14)

### Over the devil

**[1JN 3:8](https://ref.ly/1John3:8)** *See also* [MAT 12:29](https://ref.ly/Matt12:29) parallels [MRK 3:27](https://ref.ly/Mark3:27), [LUK 11:22](https://ref.ly/Luke11:22); [LUK 10:18](https://ref.ly/Luke10:18); [JHN 12:31](https://ref.ly/John12:31); [COL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:15); [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14)

### Over death

**[ACT 2:24](https://ref.ly/Acts2:24)** *See also* [ROM 5:17](https://ref.ly/Rom5:17), [ROM 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Rom6:9-Rom6:10); [1CO 15:54–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:54-1Cor15:57); [2TI 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:10)

### Over every enemy

**[1CO 15:24–15:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24-1Cor15:25)** *See also* [ISA 42:13](https://ref.ly/Isa42:13); [PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11); [HEB 10:13](https://ref.ly/Heb10:13)

Believers have victory through Christ
-------------------------------------

### Over the power of sin

**[ROM 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Rom7:24-Rom7:25)** *See also* [ROM 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Rom6:11-Rom6:14); [COL 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Col3:9-Col3:10)

### Over temptation

**[1CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:13)** *See also* [MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13) parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4); [HEB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:18), [HEB 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15-Heb4:16)

### Over the world

**[1JN 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1John5:4-1John5:5)** *See also* [JHN 15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:19); [TIT 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:13); [2PE 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:3-2Pet1:4)

### Over the devil

**[REV 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/Rev12:10-Rev12:11)** *See also* [ROM 16:20](https://ref.ly/Rom16:20); [EPH 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Eph6:12-Eph6:13); [1JN 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1John2:13-1John2:14), [1JN 4:4](https://ref.ly/1John4:4)

### Over death

**[1CO 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:22-1Cor15:23)** *See also* [JHN 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/John11:25-John11:26); [1CO 15:54–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:54-1Cor15:57); [1TH 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13-1Thess4:17)

Believers share in Christ's victory
-----------------------------------

**[ROM 8:37](https://ref.ly/Rom8:37)**; **[1CO 15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:57)**; **[1JN 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1John5:4-1John5:5)** *See also* [JHN 11:25](https://ref.ly/John11:25); [ROM 6:5](https://ref.ly/Rom6:5); [1CO 15:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:20); [2CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14); [PHP 4:13](https://ref.ly/Phil4:13); [HEB 11:33–11:34](https://ref.ly/Heb11:33-Heb11:34) *Victory is received by faith.*

The final victory
-----------------

### The vision of final victory in Jesus Christ

[REV 2:26](https://ref.ly/Rev2:26), [REV 3:5](https://ref.ly/Rev3:5)

### It will be complete at the end of the age

**[1CO 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24)** *See also* [REV 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:3), [REV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Rev21:7)

### Although certain it has not yet been fully realised

**[HEB 2:8](https://ref.ly/Heb2:8)** *See also* [ACT 3:21](https://ref.ly/Acts3:21); [ROM 8:24–8:25](https://ref.ly/Rom8:24-Rom8:25); [EPH 1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:10); [PHP 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Phil3:20-Phil3:21); [2TH 2:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 4:15; Matthew 4:1-11; Hebrews 2:18; 1 Peter 2:22; John 16:33; Revelation 3:21; Revelation 17:14; 1 John 3:8; Matthew 12:29; Mark 3:27; Luke 11:22; Luke 10:18; John 12:31; Colossians 2:15; Hebrews 2:14; Acts 2:24; Romans 5:17; Romans 6:9-10; 1 Corinthians 15:54-57; 2 Timothy 1:10; 1 Corinthians 15:24-25; Isaiah 42:13; Philippians 2:9-11; Hebrews 10:13; Romans 7:24-25; Romans 6:11-14; Colossians 3:9-10; 1 Corinthians 10:13; Matthew 6:13; Luke 11:4; Hebrews 4:15-16; 1 John 5:4-5; John 15:19; Titus 2:11-13; 2 Peter 1:3-4; Revelation 12:10-11; Romans 16:20; Ephesians 6:12-13; 1 John 2:13-14; 1 John 4:4; 1 Corinthians 15:22-23; John 11:25-26; 1 Thessalonians 4:13-17; Romans 8:37; 1 Corinthians 15:57; John 11:25; Romans 6:5; 1 Corinthians 15:20; 2 Corinthians 2:14; Philippians 4:13; Hebrews 11:33-34; Revelation 2:26; Revelation 3:5; 1 Corinthians 15:24; Revelation 21:1-3; Revelation 21:7; Hebrews 2:8; Acts 3:21; Romans 8:24-25; Ephesians 1:10; Philippians 3:20-21; 2 Thessalonians 2:8

## 5601: village (id: 5601)

Villages were small rural settlements without walls
---------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:31](https://ref.ly/Lev25:31)** *See also* [DEU 3:5](https://ref.ly/Deut3:5); [1CH 5:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:16); [2CH 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:29); [EST 9:19](https://ref.ly/Esth9:19); [SNG 7:11](https://ref.ly/Song7:11); [MAT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Matt14:15)

Villages associated with neighbouring towns and cities
------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 19:15](https://ref.ly/Jer19:15)** *See also* [JOS 10:37](https://ref.ly/Josh10:37), [JOS 13:23](https://ref.ly/Josh13:23); [1SA 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:18); [NEH 12:28](https://ref.ly/Neh12:28)

Villages were particularly vulnerable to invasion
-------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek38:11)** *See also* [JDG 5:7](https://ref.ly/Judg5:7); [2CH 14:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:14)

Village records and teachers
----------------------------

[1CH 4:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:32), [1CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:22); ; [LUK 5:17](https://ref.ly/Luke5:17)

Villages as the scene of gospel preaching
-----------------------------------------

**[MRK 6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:6)** *See also* [ACT 8:25](https://ref.ly/Acts8:25)

## 5602: vomit (id: 5602)

The literal act of vomiting
---------------------------

**[JON 2:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:10)**

Proverbial references to vomiting
---------------------------------

**[PRO 26:11](https://ref.ly/Prov26:11)** *See also* [PRO 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Prov23:6-Prov23:8), [PRO 25:16–25:17](https://ref.ly/Prov25:16-Prov25:17); [2PE 2:17–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17-2Pet2:22)

Metaphorical references to vomiting
-----------------------------------

### An illustration of exile and destruction

**[LEV 18:24–18:28](https://ref.ly/Lev18:24-Lev18:28)** *See also* [LEV 20:22–20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:22-Lev20:24)

### An illustration of punishment

**[ISA 19:13–19:14](https://ref.ly/Isa19:13-Isa19:14)** *See also* [JER 25:15–25:29](https://ref.ly/Jer25:15-Jer25:29), [JER 48:26](https://ref.ly/Jer48:26)

### An illustration of corruption

**[ISA 28:7–28:8](https://ref.ly/Isa28:7-Isa28:8)**

### An illustration of the fate of the wicked

**[JOB 20:12–20:15](https://ref.ly/Job20:12-Job20:15)**

* **Associated Passages:** Jonah 2:10; Proverbs 26:11; Proverbs 23:6-8; Proverbs 25:16-17; 2 Peter 2:17-22; Leviticus 18:24-28; Leviticus 20:22-24; Isaiah 19:13-14; Jeremiah 25:15-29; Jeremiah 48:26; Isaiah 28:7-8; Job 20:12-15
* **Associated Articles:** 4404: food (ID: `4404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4434: drinking (ID: `4434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4438: eating (ID: `4438@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5185: stomach (ID: `5185@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5297: disease (ID: `5297@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5603: wages (id: 5603)

Payment by an employer for work done; all labourers should be paid fairly and regularly.

Laws relating to wages
----------------------

### Payment should be made regularly

**[DEU 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14-Deut24:15)** *See also* [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13)

### Regular rates of pay

[LEV 25:50](https://ref.ly/Lev25:50); ; [MRK 6:37](https://ref.ly/Mark6:37) parallel [JHN 6:7](https://ref.ly/John6:7); ; [MRK 14:5](https://ref.ly/Mark14:5) parallel [JHN 12:5](https://ref.ly/John12:5)

### Labourers should not be defrauded

**[MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)**; **[JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)** *See also* [JER 22:13](https://ref.ly/Jer22:13)

### A fair wage should be paid

**[MAT 20:2–20:4](https://ref.ly/Matt20:2-Matt20:4)** *A normal day's pay for a labourer in NT times was one denarius.* *See also* [COL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Col4:1)

### A fair day's work should be done

**[2TH 3:10–3:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10-2Thess3:12)** *See also* [EPH 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:7-Eph6:8); [COL 3:23](https://ref.ly/Col3:23)

### Labourers should be content with a fair wage

**[LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14)**

Wage earners suffer in times of trouble
---------------------------------------

### Egypt under judgment

**[ISA 19:10](https://ref.ly/Isa19:10)**

### Jerusalem under judgment

**[HAG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hag1:6)** *See also* [ZEC 8:10](https://ref.ly/Zech8:10)

### The world under judgment

[REV 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rev6:6)

The example of Jacob as a wage earner
-------------------------------------

**[HOS 12:12](https://ref.ly/Hos12:12)** *See also* [GEN 29:15](https://ref.ly/Gen29:15) *He agreed his wages beforehand*; [GEN 29:18](https://ref.ly/Gen29:18) *He was paid in kind*; [GEN 29:25–29:27](https://ref.ly/Gen29:25-Gen29:27) *He was cheated by Laban*; [GEN 30:28–30:34](https://ref.ly/Gen30:28-Gen30:34) *He makes an arrangement he can turn to advantage*; [GEN 31:6–31:7](https://ref.ly/Gen31:6-Gen31:7) *He accuses Laban of dishonesty*; [GEN 31:8–31:9](https://ref.ly/Gen31:8-Gen31:9) *He protests his own honesty*; [GEN 31:38–31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:38-Gen31:42) *He looks back on his experience.*

The wages of God's servants
---------------------------

### The Levites

[NUM 18:30–18:31](https://ref.ly/Num18:30-Num18:31); ; [NEH 12:44](https://ref.ly/Neh12:44), [NEH 13:10–13:13](https://ref.ly/Neh13:10-Neh13:13)

### Temple workers

[1KI 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:6); ; [2KI 12:11–12:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:11-2Kgs12:15), [2KI 22:3–22:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:3-2Kgs22:7) parallel [2CH 34:8–34:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:8-2Chr34:11); ; [2KI 22:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:9) parallel [2CH 34:16–34:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:16-2Chr34:17)

### Zechariah's derisory pay

[ZEC 11:12–11:13](https://ref.ly/Zech11:12-Zech11:13); ; [MAT 27:3–27:10](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:10)

### Labourers in the parable of the workers in the vineyard

[MAT 20:1–20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:16)

### Ministers of the gospel

**[1TI 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17-1Tim5:18)** *See also* [DEU 25:4](https://ref.ly/Deut25:4); [LUK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7); [JHN 4:36–4:38](https://ref.ly/John4:36-John4:38); [1CO 9:7–9:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:7-1Cor9:14); [GAL 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gal6:6)

### The apostle Paul

It was Paul's choice to work freely: [1CO 9:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:15); ; [2CO 11:7–11:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:7-2Cor11:9); [PHP 4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:18) *He expressed gratitude for gifts he received.*

Spiritual lessons from wages
----------------------------

### Life's rewards are hard\-earned

[JOB 7:1–7:3](https://ref.ly/Job7:1-Job7:3); ; [GAL 6:7–6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:7-Gal6:10)

### The wages of sin

**[ROM 6:21–6:23](https://ref.ly/Rom6:21-Rom6:23)** *See also* [JOB 15:31–15:32](https://ref.ly/Job15:31-Job15:32); [ISA 65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:7); [JER 51:6](https://ref.ly/Jer51:6); [2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13)

### The freeness of God's grace

[ROM 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Rom4:4-Rom4:5)

### The generosity of God

[MAT 20:9–20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:9-Matt20:16)

### The rewards of righteousness

[PRO 10:16](https://ref.ly/Prov10:16), [PRO 11:18](https://ref.ly/Prov11:18), [PRO 31:31](https://ref.ly/Prov31:31); ; [MAT 10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:42) parallel [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41); ; [1CO 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:14); ; [HEB 6:10](https://ref.ly/Heb6:10); ; [2JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/2John1:8)

## 5604: walls (id: 5604)

Kinds of wall
-------------

### Walls of houses and palaces

**[LAM 2:7](https://ref.ly/Lam2:7)** *See also* [1SA 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10-1Sam18:11), [1SA 20:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:25); [ISA 38:2](https://ref.ly/Isa38:2); [DAN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5)

### Walls of towns

**[2CH 14:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:7)** *See also* [NUM 35:4](https://ref.ly/Num35:4); [1SA 31:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:10); [2SA 18:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:24)

### Walls of cities

**[DEU 3:5](https://ref.ly/Deut3:5)**; **[NEH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Neh2:17)** *See also* [JOS 2:15](https://ref.ly/Josh2:15), [JOS 6:5](https://ref.ly/Josh6:5), [JOS 6:20](https://ref.ly/Josh6:20); [1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1); [2KI 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:27); [2CH 27:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:3); [PSA 55:10](https://ref.ly/Ps55:10); [ISA 58:12](https://ref.ly/Isa58:12); [ACT 9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:25)

### Walls of the temple

[1KI 6:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:27), [1KI 7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:12)

### Walls of vineyards

**[MAT 21:33](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33)** *See also* [NUM 22:24–22:25](https://ref.ly/Num22:24-Num22:25); [PSA 80:12](https://ref.ly/Ps80:12)

The fragility of walls
----------------------

### Walls broken down by enemies

**[DEU 28:52](https://ref.ly/Deut28:52)**; **[PRO 25:28](https://ref.ly/Prov25:28)**; **[JER 50:15](https://ref.ly/Jer50:15)** *See also* [2SA 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:15); [2KI 14:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:13), [2KI 25:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:4) *the fall of Jerusalem to the Babylonians*; [2CH 26:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:6), [2CH 36:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:19); [NEH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3); [PSA 89:40](https://ref.ly/Ps89:40); [ISA 5:5](https://ref.ly/Isa5:5), [ISA 22:5](https://ref.ly/Isa22:5); [LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8); [EZK 26:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:4); [AMO 4:3](https://ref.ly/Amos4:3)

### Walls collapsing of their own accord

**[ISA 30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:13)** *See also* [1KI 20:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:30); [PSA 62:3](https://ref.ly/Ps62:3); [EZK 13:10–13:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:10-Ezek13:16)

The figurative use of walls
---------------------------

### Denoting protection

**[ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4)** *See also* [1SA 25:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:16); [EZR 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:9); [ISA 26:1](https://ref.ly/Isa26:1); [ZEC 2:5](https://ref.ly/Zech2:5)

### Denoting separation and impregnability

**[EPH 2:14](https://ref.ly/Eph2:14)** *See also* [PRO 18:11](https://ref.ly/Prov18:11); [ISA 59:10](https://ref.ly/Isa59:10); [JER 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jer1:18); [EZK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:30); [ACT 23:3](https://ref.ly/Acts23:3)

The walls in the new Jerusalem
------------------------------

[REV 21:12–21:19](https://ref.ly/Rev21:12-Rev21:19)

## 5605: warfare (id: 5605)

The state of being at war with an opposing nation or people. Scripture neither condemns nor glories in warfare, but recognises it as a continuing aspect of this fallen world. In particular, the NT stresses the importance of spiritual warfare in the life of faith.

* **Associated Articles:** 5606: warfare, nature of (ID: `5606@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5607: warfare, examples of (ID: `5607@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5608: warfare, strategies in (ID: `5608@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5609: warfare, spiritual (ID: `5609@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5606: warfare, nature of (id: 5606)

Warfare is characteristic of a fallen world. Scripture describes how some wars are justified and others are not.

Warfare is characteristic of a fallen world
-------------------------------------------

### It is an aspect of life in a fallen world

[EXO 17:16](https://ref.ly/Exod17:16); ; [2SA 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:1); ; [1KI 14:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:30)

### It is caused by sin

**[JAS 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1-Jas4:2)** *See also* [PSA 140:1–140:2](https://ref.ly/Ps140:1-Ps140:2); [PRO 10:12](https://ref.ly/Prov10:12), [PRO 29:22](https://ref.ly/Prov29:22); [1CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3)

### It is horrific in nature

**[NAM 3:3](https://ref.ly/Nah3:3)** *See also* [2CH 25:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:12); [ZEC 14:2](https://ref.ly/Zech14:2)

Warfare as a sign of the end times
----------------------------------

**[MAT 24:6–24:7](https://ref.ly/Matt24:6-Matt24:7)** parallels [MRK 13:7–13:8](https://ref.ly/Mark13:7-Mark13:8), [LUK 21:9–21:10](https://ref.ly/Luke21:9-Luke21:10)

Sometimes God forbids war
-------------------------

**[DEU 1:41–1:42](https://ref.ly/Deut1:41-Deut1:42)** *See also* [1KI 12:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:24); [1CH 22:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:8); [PSA 68:30](https://ref.ly/Ps68:30), [PSA 120:7](https://ref.ly/Ps120:7)

Sometimes God commands war
--------------------------

**[JOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Josh8:1)** *See also* [NUM 31:7](https://ref.ly/Num31:7); [DEU 20:1–20:4](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1-Deut20:4); [JOS 10:40](https://ref.ly/Josh10:40); [JDG 6:16](https://ref.ly/Judg6:16); [PSA 144:1](https://ref.ly/Ps144:1); [ISA 13:3–13:4](https://ref.ly/Isa13:3-Isa13:4)

Warfare is sometimes a means of judgment
----------------------------------------

**[1SA 15:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:2)** *See also* [2KI 21:10–21:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:10-2Kgs21:15), [2KI 24:2–24:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:2-2Kgs24:4); [ISA 10:12–10:19](https://ref.ly/Isa10:12-Isa10:19); [JER 4:14–4:18](https://ref.ly/Jer4:14-Jer4:18)

God's will that warfare will finally cease
------------------------------------------

[PSA 46:9](https://ref.ly/Ps46:9); ; [ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4); ; [MIC 4:3](https://ref.ly/Mic4:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 17:16; 2 Samuel 3:1; 1 Kings 14:30; James 4:1-2; Psalms 140:1-2; Proverbs 10:12; Proverbs 29:22; 1 Corinthians 3:3; Nahum 3:3; 2 Chronicles 25:12; Zechariah 14:2; Matthew 24:6-7; Mark 13:7-8; Luke 21:9-10; Deuteronomy 1:41-42; 1 Kings 12:24; 1 Chronicles 22:8; Psalms 68:30; Psalms 120:7; Joshua 8:1; Numbers 31:7; Deuteronomy 20:1-4; Joshua 10:40; Judges 6:16; Psalms 144:1; Isaiah 13:3-4; 1 Samuel 15:2; 2 Kings 21:10-15; 2 Kings 24:2-4; Isaiah 10:12-19; Jeremiah 4:14-18; Psalms 46:9; Isaiah 2:4; Micah 4:3

## 5607: warfare, examples of (id: 5607)

From the time of entering the promised land, Israel was obliged to engage in warfare against other nations. Success is attributed to Israel's trust in the LORD and failure to lack of faith and disobedience.

Israel had to engage in warfare to enter Canaan
-----------------------------------------------

### It was commanded by God

**[DEU 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:2)** *See also* [EXO 23:27–23:31](https://ref.ly/Exod23:27-Exod23:31); [DEU 9:1–9:5](https://ref.ly/Deut9:1-Deut9:5), [DEU 20:16–20:18](https://ref.ly/Deut20:16-Deut20:18)

### Joshua conquers the land

**[JOS 11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:23)** *See also* [JOS 6:2](https://ref.ly/Josh6:2), [JOS 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Josh8:1-Josh8:2), [JOS 10:40–10:42](https://ref.ly/Josh10:40-Josh10:42); [NEH 9:24](https://ref.ly/Neh9:24); [PSA 44:1–44:3](https://ref.ly/Ps44:1-Ps44:3)

### Israel failed to drive out the remaining Canaanites

**[JDG 1:19](https://ref.ly/Judg1:19)** *Judah distrusted God's power to destroy their enemy's iron chariots.* *See also* [JDG 1:28](https://ref.ly/Judg1:28), [JDG 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Judg2:1-Judg2:3), [JDG 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Judg2:14-Judg2:15)

Israel was obliged to engage in warfare to defend the land
----------------------------------------------------------

### When unfaithful they were defeated

**[JDG 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Judg3:12-Judg3:14)** *See also* [LEV 26:14–26:17](https://ref.ly/Lev26:14-Lev26:17), [LEV 26:33](https://ref.ly/Lev26:33); [DEU 28:15](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15), [DEU 28:25](https://ref.ly/Deut28:25); [JDG 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Judg3:7-Judg3:8), [JDG 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Judg6:1-Judg6:3); [2CH 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:1-2Chr12:4), [2CH 24:23–24:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:23-2Chr24:24)

### When faithful they were victorious

**[2CH 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20)** *See also* [DEU 28:1](https://ref.ly/Deut28:1), [DEU 28:7](https://ref.ly/Deut28:7); [1SA 7:7–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:7-1Sam7:14), [1SA 11:1–11:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:1-1Sam11:11); [2CH 20:15–20:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:15-2Chr20:24)

Israel's internal struggles
---------------------------

**[2SA 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:1)**; **[1KI 12:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:21)** *See also* [2SA 2:8–2:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:8-2Sam2:17), [2SA 15:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:14); [1KI 15:6–15:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:6-1Kgs15:7), [1KI 15:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:16); [2KI 13:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:12), [2KI 14:9–14:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:9-2Kgs14:15)

Israel's constant unfaithfulness led to the exile
-------------------------------------------------

**[1CH 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:25-1Chr5:26)** *See also* [2KI 17:5–17:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:5-2Kgs17:8), [2KI 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:22-2Kgs17:23)

Judah's constant unfaithfulness led to the Babylonian captivity
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[2CH 36:16–36:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:16-2Chr36:17)** *See also* [2KI 25:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:1) parallel [JER 52:4–52:5](https://ref.ly/Jer52:4-Jer52:5); [2KI 25:8–25:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:8-2Kgs25:11) parallel [JER 52:12–52:15](https://ref.ly/Jer52:12-Jer52:15); [2KI 25:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:21) parallel [JER 52:27](https://ref.ly/Jer52:27); [JER 1:13–1:16](https://ref.ly/Jer1:13-Jer1:16)

## 5608: warfare, strategies in (id: 5608)

Scripture describes the strategies employed by Israel in warfare. Although recognising the importance of military preparation, Scripture stresses that Israel's trust is to be in God, rather than military might or prowess.

Preparations for warfare
------------------------

### Holding a council of war

**[PRO 20:18](https://ref.ly/Prov20:18)** *See also* [PRO 11:14](https://ref.ly/Prov11:14), [PRO 24:6](https://ref.ly/Prov24:6); [LUK 14:31–14:32](https://ref.ly/Luke14:31-Luke14:32)

### Assembling an army

**[2SA 18:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:1)** *See also* [NUM 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:2-Num1:3); [1SA 11:6–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:6-1Sam11:8); [2CH 25:5–25:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5-2Chr25:6), [2CH 26:11–26:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:11-2Chr26:14); [DAN 11:10–11:13](https://ref.ly/Dan11:10-Dan11:13)

### Reconnaissance

**[NUM 21:32](https://ref.ly/Num21:32)** *See also* [DEU 1:22](https://ref.ly/Deut1:22); [JOS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Josh2:1), [JOS 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Josh7:2-Josh7:3); [JDG 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Judg7:9-Judg7:11), [JDG 18:2](https://ref.ly/Judg18:2), [JDG 18:5–18:10](https://ref.ly/Judg18:5-Judg18:10)

Avoiding warfare
----------------

### Making a treaty

**[JOS 9:15](https://ref.ly/Josh9:15)** *See also* [GEN 26:28–26:29](https://ref.ly/Gen26:28-Gen26:29); [JOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Josh10:1); [1SA 11:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:1); [1KI 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:12)

### Representative combat

**[1SA 17:8–17:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:8-1Sam17:10)** *See also* [2SA 2:12–2:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:12-2Sam2:17)

Strategies employed in warfare
------------------------------

### Dividing into groups

**[JDG 9:43–9:44](https://ref.ly/Judg9:43-Judg9:44)** *See also* [GEN 14:14–14:15](https://ref.ly/Gen14:14-Gen14:15), [GEN 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/Gen32:7-Gen32:8); [JDG 7:16–7:21](https://ref.ly/Judg7:16-Judg7:21); [1SA 11:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:11); [2SA 18:1–18:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:1-2Sam18:2)

### Attacking by night

**[JDG 7:19](https://ref.ly/Judg7:19)** *The Hebrews divided the night into three watches.* *See also* [GEN 14:14–14:15](https://ref.ly/Gen14:14-Gen14:15); [1SA 14:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:36); [JER 6:5](https://ref.ly/Jer6:5)

### Setting an ambush

**[2CH 13:13–13:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:13-2Chr13:14)** *See also* [JOS 8:4–8:7](https://ref.ly/Josh8:4-Josh8:7); [JDG 9:34](https://ref.ly/Judg9:34), [JDG 9:42–9:44](https://ref.ly/Judg9:42-Judg9:44), [JDG 20:29–20:44](https://ref.ly/Judg20:29-Judg20:44)

### Swiftness of action

**[JOS 8:19](https://ref.ly/Josh8:19)** *See also* [JOS 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Josh10:6-Josh10:9), [JOS 11:7](https://ref.ly/Josh11:7); [2SA 5:22–5:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:22-2Sam5:25)

Trusting in God in warfare
--------------------------

### Of greater importance than military might or prowess

**[DEU 20:1](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1)**; **[PSA 20:7](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7)** *See also* [1SA 17:45–17:47](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45-1Sam17:47); [PSA 33:16–33:17](https://ref.ly/Ps33:16-Ps33:17), [PSA 147:10–147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10-Ps147:11); [ZEC 4:6](https://ref.ly/Zech4:6)

### Demonstrated by seeking God before a battle

**[JDG 1:1](https://ref.ly/Judg1:1)** *See also* [JDG 20:27–20:28](https://ref.ly/Judg20:27-Judg20:28); [2SA 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:18-2Sam5:19), [2SA 5:22–5:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:22-2Sam5:25)

### Strengthened by seeing God fighting for Israel

[EXO 14:29–14:31](https://ref.ly/Exod14:29-Exod14:31); ; [JOS 10:10–10:14](https://ref.ly/Josh10:10-Josh10:14); ; [2KI 19:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:35)

## 5609: warfare, spiritual (id: 5609)

See [8482: spiritual warfare](8482.md)



## 5611: watchman (id: 5611)

A person stationed on the highest part of a city wall to warn inhabitants of an approaching enemy or messenger; also a person who kept watch over pastures, vineyards and sheep. The OT prophets were spiritual watchmen, warning the people of Israel of God's judgment.

Watchmen stationed on the highest part of a city wall
-----------------------------------------------------

### To warn inhabitants of an approaching enemy

**[2KI 9:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:17)** *See also* [1SA 14:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:16); [2SA 13:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:34); [PSA 127:1](https://ref.ly/Ps127:1), [PSA 130:6](https://ref.ly/Ps130:6); [SNG 3:3](https://ref.ly/Song3:3), [SNG 5:7](https://ref.ly/Song5:7); [JER 51:12](https://ref.ly/Jer51:12)

### To give notice of approaching messengers

**[2SA 18:24–18:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:24-2Sam18:27)** *See also* [2KI 9:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:18), [2KI 9:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:20); [ISA 21:6](https://ref.ly/Isa21:6), [ISA 21:8](https://ref.ly/Isa21:8), [ISA 52:8](https://ref.ly/Isa52:8)

Watchmen stationed on towers in pastures and vineyards to guard against thieves
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[2CH 26:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:10); ; [ISA 27:3](https://ref.ly/Isa27:3); ; [MIC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Mic4:8)

Watchmen stationed on hills
---------------------------

[JER 31:6](https://ref.ly/Jer31:6) *This was to give notice of the various phases of the moon in order to fix the times for the most important festivals.*

Watchmen who guarded sheep
--------------------------

[JHN 10:3](https://ref.ly/John10:3)

The OT prophets were spiritual watchmen warning the people of Israel of God's judgment
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 6:17](https://ref.ly/Jer6:17)** *See also* [ISA 21:11–21:12](https://ref.ly/Isa21:11-Isa21:12), [ISA 56:10](https://ref.ly/Isa56:10), [ISA 62:6](https://ref.ly/Isa62:6); [EZK 3:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:17), [EZK 33:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:2), [EZK 33:6–33:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:6-Ezek33:7); [HOS 9:8](https://ref.ly/Hos9:8); [HAB 2:1](https://ref.ly/Hab2:1)

## 5612: weapons (id: 5612)

Instruments of warfare. God's people are to trust in him rather than in weapons. Scripture speaks of a day when weapons will become instruments of peace. The term is also used metaphorically.

Weapons of warfare
------------------

**[1CH 12:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:23), [1CH 12:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:33)**; **[NEH 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/Neh4:16-Neh4:18)** *See also* [GEN 49:5](https://ref.ly/Gen49:5); [1SA 8:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:12), [1SA 17:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45), [1SA 17:54](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:54), [1SA 21:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:8); [2SA 1:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:27); [2KI 11:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:8) parallel [2CH 23:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:7); [2KI 11:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:11) parallel [2CH 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:10); [1CH 12:34–12:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:34-1Chr12:37); [2CH 32:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:5); [NEH 4:23](https://ref.ly/Neh4:23); [EZK 26:7–26:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:7-Ezek26:11), [EZK 32:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:27); [JHN 18:3](https://ref.ly/John18:3)

Trust in God rather than in weapons
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 20:7–20:8](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7-Ps20:8)**; **[ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1)** *See also* [DEU 1:41–1:44](https://ref.ly/Deut1:41-Deut1:44), [DEU 20:1](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1); [1SA 17:45–17:47](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45-1Sam17:47); [2CH 20:20–20:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20-2Chr20:24), [2CH 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:7-2Chr32:8); [PSA 33:16–33:19](https://ref.ly/Ps33:16-Ps33:19), [PSA 76:3](https://ref.ly/Ps76:3), [PSA 147:10–147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10-Ps147:11); [PRO 21:31](https://ref.ly/Prov21:31); [ECC 9:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:18); [ISA 22:8–22:11](https://ref.ly/Isa22:8-Isa22:11), [ISA 54:16–54:17](https://ref.ly/Isa54:16-Isa54:17); [ZEC 4:6](https://ref.ly/Zech4:6)

A day when weapons of war will become instruments of peace
----------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4)** parallel [MIC 4:3](https://ref.ly/Mic4:3) *See also* [PSA 46:9](https://ref.ly/Ps46:9); [ISA 9:5](https://ref.ly/Isa9:5); [EZK 39:7–39:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:7-Ezek39:10); [HOS 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hos2:18); [ZEC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Zech9:10)

Weapons used metaphorically by God
----------------------------------

**[ISA 59:15–59:18](https://ref.ly/Isa59:15-Isa59:18)**; **[JER 50:25](https://ref.ly/Jer50:25)** *See also* [PSA 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Ps7:11-Ps7:13); [ISA 13:5](https://ref.ly/Isa13:5); [JER 51:20](https://ref.ly/Jer51:20); [EZK 9:1–9:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:1-Ezek9:8)
