## 5613: weights and measures (id: 5613)

The peoples of the ancient Near East used a variety of standards of weights and measures, so that modern equivalents can only be approximate. The focus of Scripture is on the importance of keeping just weights and measures, and the prophets denounced those who used false balances and deceitful measures.

* **Associated Articles:** 5614: weights and measures, laws concerning (ID: `5614@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5615: weights and measures, weights (ID: `5615@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5616: weights and measures, dry (ID: `5616@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5617: weights and measures, liquid (ID: `5617@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5618: weights and measures, linear (ID: `5618@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5619: weights and measures, distance and area (ID: `5619@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5614: weights and measures, laws concerning (id: 5614)

God expects his people to use accurate weights and measures, and to use just balances. This is underlined in the Law, the Prophets and the wisdom literature.

The law demanded honesty in using weights and measures
------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35-Lev19:36)** *Cheating by falsifying weights and measures was common in a culture where there was no standardisation or regulatory authority.* *See also* [DEU 25:13–25:16](https://ref.ly/Deut25:13-Deut25:16)

The prophets called for the right use of weights and measures
-------------------------------------------------------------

### A call for honesty

**[EZK 45:9–45:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:9-Ezek45:10)**

### Condemnation of dishonesty

**[AMO 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:6)** *See also* [HOS 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7-Hos12:8); [MIC 6:10–6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:10-Mic6:14)

Instruction from Proverbs on weights and measures
-------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 20:10](https://ref.ly/Prov20:10)** *See also* [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1), [PRO 16:11](https://ref.ly/Prov16:11) *Merchants carried stones of different sizes in a pouch for weighing silver for payment*; [PRO 20:23](https://ref.ly/Prov20:23), [PRO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Prov22:28) *To move a boundary stone was in effect to steal land.*

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:35-36; Deuteronomy 25:13-16; Ezekiel 45:9-10; Amos 8:4-6; Hosea 12:7-8; Micah 6:10-14; Proverbs 20:10; Proverbs 11:1; Proverbs 16:11; Proverbs 20:23; Proverbs 22:28
* **Associated Articles:** 5242: buying and selling (ID: `5242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5253: cheating (ID: `5253@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5318: fraud (ID: `5318@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5407: merchants (ID: `5407@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5512: scales and balances (ID: `5512@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8275: honesty (ID: `8275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5615: weights and measures, weights (id: 5615)

Ancient weights were usually made of stone or metal, often inscribed with their weight and standard, the shekel being the basic weight of all Semitic nations. Weights of gold and silver served as currency, since coinage was not invented until the seventh century B.C. Weights are given in ascending order, with their approximate imperial and metric equivalents.

Gerah (1/20 shekel): about 1/50 ounce (about 0\.6 gram)
-------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 27:25](https://ref.ly/Lev27:25)** *See also* [EXO 30:13](https://ref.ly/Exod30:13); [NUM 3:47](https://ref.ly/Num3:47), [NUM 18:16](https://ref.ly/Num18:16); [EZK 45:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:12)

Beka (10 gerahs): about 1/5 ounce (about 5\.8 grams)
----------------------------------------------------

**[2CH 9:15–9:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:15-2Chr9:16)** parallel [1KI 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:16-1Kgs10:17) *See also* [GEN 24:22](https://ref.ly/Gen24:22); [EXO 38:25–38:26](https://ref.ly/Exod38:25-Exod38:26)

Pim (2/3 shekel): about 1/4 ounce (about 7\.7 grams)
----------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 13:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:21)** *The Hebrew word translated “two thirds of a shekel” is “pim”, its only mention in Scripture.*

Shekel (2 bekas): about 2/5 ounce (about 11\.5 grams)
-----------------------------------------------------

### The shekel in relation to other weights

**[EZK 45:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:12)**

### The shekel as a weight of metal objects

**[1SA 17:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:5)** *See also* [1SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:7); [2SA 21:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:16)

### The shekel as a weight of food

**[EZK 4:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:10)**

### The shekel as a weight of gold or silver objects

**[2CH 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:9)** *See also* [GEN 24:22](https://ref.ly/Gen24:22); [NUM 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Num7:13-Num7:14); [JDG 8:26](https://ref.ly/Judg8:26)

### The shekel as a weight of gold or silver

**[GEN 23:14–23:16](https://ref.ly/Gen23:14-Gen23:16)** *The phrase “the weight current among the merchants” reflects the local variation in standards.* *See also* [GEN 37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:28); [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29); [JOS 7:20–7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:20-Josh7:21); [JDG 17:1–17:4](https://ref.ly/Judg17:1-Judg17:4); [2SA 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:11-2Sam18:12); [1KI 10:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:29) parallel [2CH 1:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:17); [2KI 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:4-2Kgs5:5); [NEH 5:15](https://ref.ly/Neh5:15); [JER 32:9](https://ref.ly/Jer32:9); [DAN 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Dan5:25-Dan5:27) *“Tekel” can mean “weighed” or “shekel”.*

### The royal shekel

**[2SA 14:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:26)** *The royal shekel, also known in Babylonia, was slightly heavier (at about 13 grams) than the standard shekel and reflects David's attempts to bring some standardisation to this weight.*

### The sanctuary shekel

**[NUM 18:15–18:16](https://ref.ly/Num18:15-Num18:16)** *The sanctuary shekel was more precisely regulated than the common shekel, weighing exactly 20 gerahs. The common shekel may have weighed slightly less as a result of greater handling and wider circulation.* *See also* [EXO 30:13–30:15](https://ref.ly/Exod30:13-Exod30:15), [EXO 30:22–30:25](https://ref.ly/Exod30:22-Exod30:25), [EXO 38:24–38:26](https://ref.ly/Exod38:24-Exod38:26); [LEV 5:15](https://ref.ly/Lev5:15), [LEV 27:1–27:7](https://ref.ly/Lev27:1-Lev27:7); [NUM 3:46–3:50](https://ref.ly/Num3:46-Num3:50)

Mina (50 shekels): about 1 1/4 pounds (about 0\.6 kilogram)
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 45:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:12)** *Although the mina is valued here at 60 shekels, there is some evidence that in the pre\-exilic period it was valued at 50 shekels.* *See also* [1KI 10:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:17); [EZR 2:69](https://ref.ly/Ezra2:69); [NEH 7:71–7:72](https://ref.ly/Neh7:71-Neh7:72); [DAN 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Dan5:25-Dan5:26) *“Mene” can mean both “numbered” and “mina”*; [LUK 19:11–19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:11-Luke19:27) *By NT times the mina had become coinage rather than simply a weight of silver, and was worth about three months' wages.*

Talent (3,000 shekels, 60 minas): about 75 pounds (about 34 kilograms)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CH 29:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:7)** *See also* [EXO 25:39](https://ref.ly/Exod25:39); [2SA 12:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:30) parallel [1CH 20:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:2); [1KI 10:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:14) parallel [2CH 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:13); [1KI 16:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:24); [2KI 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:14); [EZR 8:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:26); [EST 3:9](https://ref.ly/Esth3:9); [MAT 18:24–18:25](https://ref.ly/Matt18:24-Matt18:25) *By NT times the talent had become coinage, and ten thousand talents would have been the equivalent of millions of pounds sterling*; [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30)

Other minor weights
-------------------

### Kesitah

[GEN 33:19](https://ref.ly/Gen33:19) *The price in the original Hebrew is “one hundred kesitahs”, a unit of unknown weight and value.*

### Litra

The word translated `pint'' in [Jn 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3) and`pound'' in 19:39 is `litra'', a loanword from the Latin`libra'', meaning \`\`pound'' and weighing 12 ounces (327 grams): [JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3), [JHN 19:39](https://ref.ly/John19:39)

### Peres

[DAN 5:28](https://ref.ly/Dan5:28) *“Peres” means “division” and denotes a half\-shekel.*

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 27:25; Exodus 30:13; Numbers 3:47; Numbers 18:16; Ezekiel 45:12; 2 Chronicles 9:15-16; 1 Kings 10:16-17; Genesis 24:22; Exodus 38:25-26; 1 Samuel 13:21; 1 Samuel 17:5; 1 Samuel 17:7; 2 Samuel 21:16; Ezekiel 4:10; 2 Chronicles 3:9; Numbers 7:13-14; Judges 8:26; Genesis 23:14-16; Genesis 37:28; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; Joshua 7:20-21; Judges 17:1-4; 2 Samuel 18:11-12; 1 Kings 10:29; 2 Chronicles 1:17; 2 Kings 5:4-5; Nehemiah 5:15; Jeremiah 32:9; Daniel 5:25-27; 2 Samuel 14:26; Numbers 18:15-16; Exodus 30:13-15; Exodus 30:22-25; Exodus 38:24-26; Leviticus 5:15; Leviticus 27:1-7; Numbers 3:46-50; 1 Kings 10:17; Ezra 2:69; Nehemiah 7:71-72; Daniel 5:25-26; Luke 19:11-27; 1 Chronicles 29:7; Exodus 25:39; 2 Samuel 12:30; 1 Chronicles 20:2; 1 Kings 10:14; 2 Chronicles 9:13; 1 Kings 16:24; 2 Kings 18:14; Ezra 8:26; Esther 3:9; Matthew 18:24-25; Matthew 25:14-30; Genesis 33:19; John 12:3; John 19:39; Daniel 5:28
* **Associated Articles:** 4303: metals (ID: `4303@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5260: coinage (ID: `5260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5412: money (ID: `5412@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5616: weights and measures, dry (id: 5616)

These terms derive originally from the containers used, which held a fixed amount. Measures are given in ascending order, with their approximate imperial and metric equivalents.

Cab (1/18 ephah): about 2 pints (about 1 litre)
-----------------------------------------------

**[2KI 6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25)** *This is the only mention in Scripture of this measure.*

Omer (1/10 ephah): about 4 pints (about 2 litres)
-------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 16:16–16:22](https://ref.ly/Exod16:16-Exod16:22)** *This incident records the only mention in Scripture of this measure.* *See also* [EXO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Exod16:32), [EXO 16:36](https://ref.ly/Exod16:36)

Seah (1/3 ephah): about 13 pints (about 7\.3 litres)
----------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 18:6](https://ref.ly/Gen18:6)** *The seah was a measure used for flour and cereals.* *See also* [1SA 25:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:18); [1KI 18:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:32); [2KI 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:1)

Ephah: about 3/5 bushel (about 22 litres)
-----------------------------------------

**[RUT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:17)** *The ephah was used only for flour and cereals, and had subdivisions of 1/6 and 1/10\.* *See also* [LEV 5:11](https://ref.ly/Lev5:11), [LEV 24:5](https://ref.ly/Lev24:5); [NUM 15:4–15:9](https://ref.ly/Num15:4-Num15:9); [JDG 6:19](https://ref.ly/Judg6:19); [1SA 17:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17); [EZK 45:10–45:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:10-Ezek45:11); [ZEC 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/Zech5:6-Zech5:8) *The word for “measuring basket” in the Hebrew is “ephah”.*

Lethek (5 ephahs or 1/2 homer): about 3 bushels (about 110 litres)
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[HOS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Hos3:2)** *Possibly a Phoenician measure, this is the only mention in Scripture of this measure.*

Cor (10 ephahs): about 6 bushels (about 220 litres)
---------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 4:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:22)** *The cor was used for measuring flour and cereals, and also appears to have been used for measuring oil.* *See also* [1KI 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:11); [2CH 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:10), [2CH 27:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:5); [EZR 7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:22); [EZK 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:14)

Homer (10 ephahs): about 6 bushels (about 220 litres)
-----------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 27:16](https://ref.ly/Lev27:16)** *The homer is an older word than the cor, but is equivalent to it. It originally meant “a donkey load” and was widely used throughout the ancient Near East from the second millennium B.C. as a measure for cereals.* *See also* [NUM 11:32](https://ref.ly/Num11:32); [ISA 5:10](https://ref.ly/Isa5:10); [EZK 45:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:11), [EZK 45:13–45:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:13-Ezek45:14); [HOS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Hos3:2)

Other dry measures found in the NT
----------------------------------

### Quart (choinix): about 1 1/2–2 pints (about 1 litre)

[REV 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rev6:6) *The word translated “quart” is “choinix”, a Greek measure.*

### Saton (equivalent to the OT seah)

[MAT 13:33](https://ref.ly/Matt13:33) parallel [LUK 13:21](https://ref.ly/Luke13:21) *The “large amount” referred to is “three sata” in the Greek.*

### Bowl (modius): about 15 1/2 pints (about 8\.75 litres)

[MAT 5:15](https://ref.ly/Matt5:15) parallels [MRK 4:21](https://ref.ly/Mark4:21), [LUK 11:33](https://ref.ly/Luke11:33) *The word translated “bowl” is the Roman measure “modius”.*

### Koros (equivalent to the OT cor)

[LUK 16:7](https://ref.ly/Luke16:7) *The measure translated as “a thousand bushels” is “one hundred korous” in the Greek.*

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 6:25; Exodus 16:16-22; Exodus 16:32; Exodus 16:36; Genesis 18:6; 1 Samuel 25:18; 1 Kings 18:32; 2 Kings 7:1; Ruth 2:17; Leviticus 5:11; Leviticus 24:5; Numbers 15:4-9; Judges 6:19; 1 Samuel 17:17; Ezekiel 45:10-11; Zechariah 5:6-8; Hosea 3:2; 1 Kings 4:22; 1 Kings 5:11; 2 Chronicles 2:10; 2 Chronicles 27:5; Ezra 7:22; Ezekiel 45:14; Leviticus 27:16; Numbers 11:32; Isaiah 5:10; Ezekiel 45:11; Ezekiel 45:13-14; Revelation 6:6; Matthew 13:33; Luke 13:21; Matthew 5:15; Mark 4:21; Luke 11:33; Luke 16:7
* **Associated Articles:** 4456: grain (ID: `4456@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4542: wheat (ID: `4542@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5558: storing (ID: `5558@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7435: sacrifice, in OT (ID: `7435@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5617: weights and measures, liquid (id: 5617)

These terms derive originally from the containers used, which held a fixed amount. There is some uncertainty about the capacity of the bath, and therefore of other liquid measures dependent upon it. Measures are given in ascending order, with their approximate imperial and metric equivalents.

Log (1/72 bath): about 1/2 pint (about 0\.3 litre)
--------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 14:10](https://ref.ly/Lev14:10)** *This is the only mention in Scripture of the log, as a measure of oil used in the ritual for cleansing infectious skin diseases.* *See also* [LEV 14:12–14:18](https://ref.ly/Lev14:12-Lev14:18), [LEV 14:21–14:22](https://ref.ly/Lev14:21-Lev14:22)

Hin (1/6 bath): about 7 pints (about 4 litres)
----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 29:38–29:41](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:41)** *The hin was used as a measure of oil, wine and water.* *See also* [LEV 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Lev23:12-Lev23:13); [NUM 15:4–15:10](https://ref.ly/Num15:4-Num15:10); [EZK 4:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:11), [EZK 46:5–46:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:5-Ezek46:7)

Bath (1 ephah): about 5 gallons (about 22 litres)
-------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 45:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:11)** *The bath was the equivalent liquid measure of the ephah, and was used to measure oil, wine and water.* *See also* [1KI 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:11), [1KI 7:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:38); [2CH 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:10); [EZR 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:21-Ezra7:22); [ISA 5:10](https://ref.ly/Isa5:10); [EZK 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:14); [LUK 16:6](https://ref.ly/Luke16:6) *The measure translated as “eight hundred gallons” is “one hundred batous” in the original (“batos” being the Greek form of the Hebrew “bath”)*; [JHN 2:6](https://ref.ly/John2:6) *The measure translated as “twenty to thirty gallons” is “two to three metretas” in the original, a Greek measure roughly equivalent to the bath.*

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 14:10; Leviticus 14:12-18; Leviticus 14:21-22; Exodus 29:38-41; Leviticus 23:12-13; Numbers 15:4-10; Ezekiel 4:11; Ezekiel 46:5-7; Ezekiel 45:11; 1 Kings 5:11; 1 Kings 7:38; 2 Chronicles 2:10; Ezra 7:21-22; Isaiah 5:10; Ezekiel 45:14; Luke 16:6; John 2:6
* **Associated Articles:** 4293: water (ID: `4293@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4488: oil (ID: `4488@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4544: wine (ID: `4544@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5618: weights and measures, linear (id: 5618)

These terms were based on natural units of measurement that could be easily applied. Measures are given in ascending order, with their approximate imperial and metric equivalents.

Finger (1/4 handbreadth): about 3/4 inch (about 1\.85 centimetres)
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 52:21](https://ref.ly/Jer52:21)** *The finger was 1/4 handbreadth and was the smallest subdivision of the cubit. This is its only mention in Scripture.*

Handbreadth (4 fingers): about 3 inches (about 8 centimetres)
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 25:25](https://ref.ly/Exod25:25)** parallel [EXO 37:12](https://ref.ly/Exod37:12) *The handbreadth was the width of the hand at the base of the four fingers.* *See also* [1KI 7:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:26) parallel [2CH 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:5); [PSA 39:5](https://ref.ly/Ps39:5) *The handbreadth is here used figuratively of the shortness of human life*; [EZK 40:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:5)

Span (half a cubit): about 9 inches (about 23 centimetres)
----------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 28:16](https://ref.ly/Exod28:16)** parallel [EXO 39:9](https://ref.ly/Exod39:9) *The span was the width of the outstretched hand from thumb to little finger.* *See also* [1SA 17:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:4) *Goliath's height is given in the Hebrew as “six cubits and a span”*; [PSA 90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:10) *The span is here used figuratively of the shortness of human life*; [EZK 43:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:13)

Cubit (2 spans): about 18 inches (about 0\.5 metre)
---------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 25:10](https://ref.ly/Exod25:10)** parallel [EXO 37:1](https://ref.ly/Exod37:1) *The cubit was the distance from the fingertip to the elbow, and was used to measure height, size, depth and distance.* *See also* [GEN 7:20](https://ref.ly/Gen7:20) *The Hebrew describes the depth of the water as “fifteen cubits”*; [EXO 27:9–27:18](https://ref.ly/Exod27:9-Exod27:18) parallel [EXO 38:9–38:15](https://ref.ly/Exod38:9-Exod38:15); [1KI 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:2-1Kgs6:3) parallel [2CH 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:3-2Chr3:4); [1CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:23) *The Hebrew describes the man as “five cubits tall”*; [EZK 40:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:5), [EZK 45:1–45:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:1-Ezek45:6), [EZK 47:3–47:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:3-Ezek47:5); [JHN 21:8](https://ref.ly/John21:8) *The Greek text describes the distance as “about two hundred cubits”*; [REV 21:17](https://ref.ly/Rev21:17)

Reed (6 cubits): about 10 feet (about 3 metres)
-----------------------------------------------

**[EZK 41:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:8)** *Originally a measuring instrument, the reed became a recognised measure of six cubits (the rod).* *See also* [EZK 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:3), [EZK 40:5–40:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:5-Ezek40:7); [REV 11:1](https://ref.ly/Rev11:1), [REV 21:15–21:16](https://ref.ly/Rev21:15-Rev21:16)

Mile
----

**[MAT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Matt5:41)** *The Greek word for “mile” here is “milion”, a transliteration of the Roman measurement “mille passuum”, “a thousand paces”.* *See also* [LUK 24:13](https://ref.ly/Luke24:13) *The measurement in the Greek is “sixty stadia”. Eight stadia were about one mile (about 1480 metres)*; [JHN 6:19](https://ref.ly/John6:19) *The measurement in the Greek is “twenty\-five or thirty stadia”*; [JHN 11:18](https://ref.ly/John11:18) *The measurement in the Greek is “fifteen stadia”.*

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 52:21; Exodus 25:25; Exodus 37:12; 1 Kings 7:26; 2 Chronicles 4:5; Psalms 39:5; Ezekiel 40:5; Exodus 28:16; Exodus 39:9; 1 Samuel 17:4; Psalms 90:10; Ezekiel 43:13; Exodus 25:10; Exodus 37:1; Genesis 7:20; Exodus 27:9-18; Exodus 38:9-15; 1 Kings 6:2-3; 2 Chronicles 3:3-4; 1 Chronicles 11:23; Ezekiel 45:1-6; Ezekiel 47:3-5; John 21:8; Revelation 21:17; Ezekiel 41:8; Ezekiel 40:3; Ezekiel 40:5-7; Revelation 11:1; Revelation 21:15-16; Matthew 5:41; Luke 24:13; John 6:19; John 11:18
* **Associated Articles:** 4803: breadth (ID: `4803@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4813: depth (ID: `4813@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4830: height (ID: `4830@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4832: length (ID: `4832@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4841: narrowness (ID: `4841@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4847: smallness (ID: `4847@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4903: time (ID: `4903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5152: fingers (ID: `5152@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7254: plumb-line (ID: `7254@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5619: weights and measures, distance and area (id: 5619)

Diverse expressions were used for these measurements, their being determined in the most practical way possible, reckoned by known averages.

Expressions of distance
-----------------------

### A bow\-shot

**[GEN 21:15–21:16](https://ref.ly/Gen21:15-Gen21:16)**

### Some distance

**[EXO 33:7](https://ref.ly/Exod33:7)** *A variety of distance often lies behind this expression, as can be seen in the examples that follow.* *See also* [GEN 35:16](https://ref.ly/Gen35:16), [GEN 36:6](https://ref.ly/Gen36:6); [NUM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Num2:2); [1SA 26:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:13); [2SA 15:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:17); [MAT 8:30](https://ref.ly/Matt8:30)

### A Sabbath day's journey

**[ACT 1:12](https://ref.ly/Acts1:12)** *While this is the only mention of this phrase in Scripture, it was in fact a popular expression, indicating the distance a devout Jew could walk on the Sabbath. It was based on the rabbinical interpretation of [Ex 16:29](https://ref.ly/Exod16:29) in the light of [Nu 35:5](https://ref.ly/Num35:5), and was limited to 2,000 cubits.*

### A day's journey

**[1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4)** *See also* [NUM 11:31](https://ref.ly/Num11:31)

### A three\-day journey

**[GEN 30:36](https://ref.ly/Gen30:36)** *See also* [EXO 3:18](https://ref.ly/Exod3:18); [NUM 10:33](https://ref.ly/Num10:33); [EZR 10:7–10:9](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:7-Ezra10:9); [JON 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:3)

### Far away

**[DEU 28:49](https://ref.ly/Deut28:49)** *See also* [1KI 8:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:46) parallel [2CH 6:36](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:36); [ISA 6:12](https://ref.ly/Isa6:12); [EZK 11:15–11:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:15-Ezek11:16); [JOL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Joel3:8); [ZEC 6:15](https://ref.ly/Zech6:15); [ACT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Acts22:21)

Expressions of area
-------------------

### Area expressed by the yoke

**[1SA 14:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:14)** *The area translated “about half an acre” in the Hebrew means “half a yoke”. The yoke was the area of land ploughed by a yoke of oxen in one day, approximately one acre.* *See also* [ISA 5:10](https://ref.ly/Isa5:10) *The Hebrew describes the area as “ten\-yoke”, that is, the land ploughed by ten yoke of oxen in one day.*

### Area expressed by the amount of seed required to sow the land

**[LEV 27:16](https://ref.ly/Lev27:16)**

### Area expressed by its constituent measurements

**[EZK 40:47](https://ref.ly/Ezek40:47)** *See also* [EXO 28:16](https://ref.ly/Exod28:16) parallel [EXO 39:9](https://ref.ly/Exod39:9); [1KI 7:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:23); [EZK 45:1–45:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:1-Ezek45:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 21:15-16; Exodus 33:7; Genesis 35:16; Genesis 36:6; Numbers 2:2; 1 Samuel 26:13; 2 Samuel 15:17; Matthew 8:30; Acts 1:12; Exodus 16:29; Numbers 35:5; 1 Kings 19:3-4; Numbers 11:31; Genesis 30:36; Exodus 3:18; Numbers 10:33; Ezra 10:7-9; Jonah 3:3; Deuteronomy 28:49; 1 Kings 8:46; 2 Chronicles 6:36; Isaiah 6:12; Ezekiel 11:15-16; Joel 3:8; Zechariah 6:15; Acts 22:21; 1 Samuel 14:14; Isaiah 5:10; Leviticus 27:16; Ezekiel 40:47; Exodus 28:16; Exodus 39:9; 1 Kings 7:23; Ezekiel 45:1-6
* **Associated Articles:** 4206: land (ID: `4206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4406: agriculture (ID: `4406@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5235: boundary (ID: `5235@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5357: journey (ID: `5357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5590: travel (ID: `5590@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5621: wheel (id: 5621)

Early wheels were solid wooden discs. These were replaced around 1500 B.C. by lighter, spoked wheels. The most common use of wheels is on chariots and carts; they were also used by potters and in machinery for drawing water from a well. The chariot throne of God has wheels which are vividly described.

Chariot wheels
--------------

**[EXO 14:25](https://ref.ly/Exod14:25)**; **[NAM 3:2](https://ref.ly/Nah3:2)** *The sound of chariot wheels announces the approach of the enemy.* *See also* [2KI 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:6); [ISA 5:28](https://ref.ly/Isa5:28); [JER 47:3](https://ref.ly/Jer47:3); [JOL 2:5](https://ref.ly/Joel2:5); [REV 9:9](https://ref.ly/Rev9:9)

Wagon wheels
------------

The Hebrew word for `wheel'' is here used for`wagon'': [EZK 23:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:24), [EZK 26:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:10)

Threshing wheels
----------------

**[PRO 20:26](https://ref.ly/Prov20:26)** *Threshing was carried out by driving a sledge or a cart with heavy wheels over the grain.* *See also* [ISA 28:27–28:28](https://ref.ly/Isa28:27-Isa28:28)

Other examples of wheels
------------------------

**[1KI 7:32–7:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:32-1Kgs7:33)** *The stands for basins in Solomon's temple were mounted on wheels.* *See also* [1KI 7:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:30); [ECC 12:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:6) *A wheel formed part of the machinery for drawing water from a well*; [JER 18:3](https://ref.ly/Jer18:3) *Potters turned their clay on a wheel.*

The wheels of God's chariot throne
----------------------------------

**[EZK 11:22–11:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:22-Ezek11:23)**; **[DAN 7:9](https://ref.ly/Dan7:9)** *See also* [2KI 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:11); [ISA 66:15](https://ref.ly/Isa66:15); [EZK 1:15–1:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek1:15-Ezek1:21), [EZK 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:12-Ezek3:13), [EZK 10:1–10:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek10:1-Ezek10:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 14:25; Nahum 3:2; 2 Kings 7:6; Isaiah 5:28; Jeremiah 47:3; Joel 2:5; Revelation 9:9; Ezekiel 23:24; Ezekiel 26:10; Proverbs 20:26; Isaiah 28:27-28; 1 Kings 7:32-33; 1 Kings 7:30; Ecclesiastes 12:6; Jeremiah 18:3; Ezekiel 11:22-23; Daniel 7:9; 2 Kings 2:11; Isaiah 66:15; Ezekiel 1:15-21; Ezekiel 3:12-13; Ezekiel 10:1-19
* **Associated Articles:** 4150: cherubim (ID: `4150@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4296: wells (ID: `4296@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4522: threshing (ID: `4522@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5248: cart (ID: `5248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5252: chariots (ID: `5252@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5445: potters and pottery (ID: `5445@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5581: throne (ID: `5581@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5622: witnesses (id: 5622)

Individuals who, having observed something take place, are able to give an accurate and full account of what has happened. Witnesses were of central importance to OT law. The theme of giving account is also of major importance to evangelism, which rests upon believers explaining the impact of Jesus Christ upon their lives.

* **Associated Articles:** 5623: witnesses, legal (ID: `5623@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5624: witnesses, to Jesus Christ (ID: `5624@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5625: witnesses, false (ID: `5625@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5623: witnesses, legal (id: 5623)

God gave detailed instructions about the conduct of witnesses in the legal processes of Israel and also in the affairs of the church.

God himself as a witness
------------------------

[GEN 31:50](https://ref.ly/Gen31:50); ; [MAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14)

General legal requirements about witnesses
------------------------------------------

### A witness to a crime was required to give evidence

**[LEV 5:1](https://ref.ly/Lev5:1)** *See also* [PRO 29:24](https://ref.ly/Prov29:24)

### Two or more witnesses were required to prove someone guilty

**[DEU 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15)** *See also* [DEU 17:6](https://ref.ly/Deut17:6); [MAT 18:15–18:16](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:16); [2CO 13:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:1)

The role of witnesses in capital cases
--------------------------------------

### At least two witnesses were required

**[NUM 35:30](https://ref.ly/Num35:30)** *See also* [DEU 17:2–17:6](https://ref.ly/Deut17:2-Deut17:6); [HEB 10:28](https://ref.ly/Heb10:28)

### Witnesses were required to participate in the execution

**[DEU 17:7](https://ref.ly/Deut17:7)** *See also* [LEV 24:13–24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:13-Lev24:16); [DEU 13:6–13:11](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6-Deut13:11); [ACT 7:57–7:58](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57-Acts7:58)

The role of witnesses in other legal matters
--------------------------------------------

### Land transactions

**[JER 32:9–32:12](https://ref.ly/Jer32:9-Jer32:12)** *See also* [GEN 23:10–23:18](https://ref.ly/Gen23:10-Gen23:18); [RUT 4:1–4:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:9); [JER 32:25](https://ref.ly/Jer32:25), [JER 32:44](https://ref.ly/Jer32:44)

### Levirate marriage

**[RUT 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:9-Ruth4:11)**

The role of witnesses in disputes concerning believers
------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:17)** *See also* [2CO 13:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:1); [1TI 5:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 31:50; Malachi 2:14; Leviticus 5:1; Proverbs 29:24; Deuteronomy 19:15; Deuteronomy 17:6; Matthew 18:15-16; 2 Corinthians 13:1; Numbers 35:30; Deuteronomy 17:2-6; Hebrews 10:28; Deuteronomy 17:7; Leviticus 24:13-16; Deuteronomy 13:6-11; Acts 7:57-58; Jeremiah 32:9-12; Genesis 23:10-18; Ruth 4:1-9; Jeremiah 32:25; Jeremiah 32:44; Ruth 4:9-11; Matthew 18:15-17; 1 Timothy 5:19
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1461: truth, nature of (ID: `1461@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5270: court (ID: `5270@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5277: criminals (ID: `5277@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5471: proof (ID: `5471@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5834: disagreement (ID: `5834@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7346: death penalty (ID: `7346@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8331: reliability (ID: `8331@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5624: witnesses, to Jesus Christ (id: 5624)

The NT identifies a variety of witnesses to Jesus Christ, who confirm his identity and importance. It also stresses the importance of believers bearing witness to Christ in the world.

The witness of the OT
---------------------

**[ACT 26:22–26:23](https://ref.ly/Acts26:22-Acts26:23)** *See also* [LUK 24:27](https://ref.ly/Luke24:27); [JHN 5:39–5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:39-John5:40); [HEB 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1-Heb12:2)

The witness of John the Baptist
-------------------------------

**[JHN 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/John1:6-John1:8)** *See also* [MAT 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Matt3:1-Matt3:3); [ISA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Isa40:3); [MAT 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Matt3:11-Matt3:12) parallels [MRK 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Mark1:7-Mark1:8), [LUK 3:15–3:18](https://ref.ly/Luke3:15-Luke3:18); [JHN 1:15](https://ref.ly/John1:15), [JHN 1:19–1:31](https://ref.ly/John1:19-John1:31), [JHN 3:26–3:30](https://ref.ly/John3:26-John3:30), [JHN 5:33–5:35](https://ref.ly/John5:33-John5:35)

The witness of the Holy Spirit
------------------------------

**[JHN 15:26](https://ref.ly/John15:26)** *See also* [ACT 5:32](https://ref.ly/Acts5:32); [ROM 8:16](https://ref.ly/Rom8:16); [1JN 5:6–5:9](https://ref.ly/1John5:6-1John5:9)

The witness of the Father
-------------------------

**[JHN 8:18](https://ref.ly/John8:18)** *See also* [MAT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17) parallels [MRK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mark1:11), [LUK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Luke3:22); [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) parallels [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7), [LUK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Luke9:35); [JHN 5:37–5:38](https://ref.ly/John5:37-John5:38)

The witness of Jesus Christ to himself
--------------------------------------

**[JHN 5:36](https://ref.ly/John5:36), [JHN 8:14](https://ref.ly/John8:14)** *See also* [JHN 13:13](https://ref.ly/John13:13), [JHN 14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:11)

The witness of Jesus Christ's miracles
--------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:11)** *See also* [JHN 10:25](https://ref.ly/John10:25), [JHN 15:24](https://ref.ly/John15:24)

The witness of the first Christians
-----------------------------------

### Commissioned by Jesus Christ

**[ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)** [MAT 10:17–10:20](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17-Matt10:20); ; [LUK 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12-Luke21:13); ; [ACT 4:18–4:21](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:21); [LUK 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Luke1:1-Luke1:2), [LUK 24:48](https://ref.ly/Luke24:48); ; [JHN 15:27](https://ref.ly/John15:27); ; [ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22), [ACT 2:32](https://ref.ly/Acts2:32), [ACT 4:33](https://ref.ly/Acts4:33), [ACT 13:31](https://ref.ly/Acts13:31); [ACT 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Acts9:5-Acts9:6), [ACT 22:14–22:15](https://ref.ly/Acts22:14-Acts22:15), [ACT 23:11](https://ref.ly/Acts23:11), [ACT 26:16](https://ref.ly/Acts26:16); [HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3); ; [1PE 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1); ; [2PE 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:16); ; [1JN 1:1](https://ref.ly/1John1:1)

The witness of converts
-----------------------

**[1PE 3:15–3:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15-1Pet3:16)** *See also* [2TI 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:8)

## 5625: witnesses, false (id: 5625)

Scripture prohibits individuals from bearing false witness against their neighbours.

False testimony forbidden
-------------------------

**[EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16)** parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20) *See also* [EXO 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1-Exod23:2); [LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12); [PRO 6:16–6:19](https://ref.ly/Prov6:16-Prov6:19), [PRO 24:28](https://ref.ly/Prov24:28); [LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14); [MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19), [MAT 19:18](https://ref.ly/Matt19:18) parallels [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19), [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20)

The effect of false witnesses on other people and society
---------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 25:18](https://ref.ly/Prov25:18)** *See also* [PSA 27:12](https://ref.ly/Ps27:12), [PSA 35:11](https://ref.ly/Ps35:11); [PRO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Prov12:17), [PRO 14:5](https://ref.ly/Prov14:5), [PRO 14:25](https://ref.ly/Prov14:25), [PRO 18:5](https://ref.ly/Prov18:5), [PRO 19:28](https://ref.ly/Prov19:28)

False witnesses will be punished
--------------------------------

**[DEU 19:16–19:21](https://ref.ly/Deut19:16-Deut19:21)** *See also* [LEV 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Lev6:1-Lev6:7); [PRO 19:5](https://ref.ly/Prov19:5), [PRO 19:9](https://ref.ly/Prov19:9), [PRO 21:28](https://ref.ly/Prov21:28); [ISA 29:20–29:21](https://ref.ly/Isa29:20-Isa29:21); [ZEC 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Zech5:3-Zech5:4)

Examples of false witnesses
---------------------------

[1KI 21:8–21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:13); ; [MAT 26:59–26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:61) parallel [MRK 14:55–14:59](https://ref.ly/Mark14:55-Mark14:59); ; [MAT 28:12–28:15](https://ref.ly/Matt28:12-Matt28:15); ; [ACT 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11-Acts6:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:16; Deuteronomy 5:20; Exodus 23:1-2; Leviticus 19:12; Proverbs 6:16-19; Proverbs 24:28; Luke 3:14; Matthew 15:19; Matthew 19:18; Mark 10:19; Luke 18:20; Proverbs 25:18; Psalms 27:12; Psalms 35:11; Proverbs 12:17; Proverbs 14:5; Proverbs 14:25; Proverbs 18:5; Proverbs 19:28; Deuteronomy 19:16-21; Leviticus 6:1-7; Proverbs 19:5; Proverbs 19:9; Proverbs 21:28; Isaiah 29:20-21; Zechariah 5:3-4; 1 Kings 21:8-13; Matthew 26:59-61; Mark 14:55-59; Matthew 28:12-15; Acts 6:11-14

## 5627: word (id: 5627)

Jesus Christ is described as God's word. The word encompassed the promise of God and the gospel and is a help in trial and temptation. Human beings should use words wisely.

Jesus Christ and God's word
---------------------------

### Jesus Christ described as the Word

**[JHN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/John1:1-John1:3)** *See also* [JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14); [HEB 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:2); [1JN 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:2); [REV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Rev19:13)

### Jesus Christ preaches the word

**[MRK 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mark2:2)** *See also* [MAT 7:24–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:29) parallel [LUK 6:47–6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:47-Luke6:49); [MAT 13:20–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:20-Matt13:23) parallels [MRK 4:13–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:13-Mark4:20), [LUK 8:11–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:15) *the parable of the sower*; [LUK 11:28](https://ref.ly/Luke11:28); [JHN 3:34](https://ref.ly/John3:34), [JHN 5:24](https://ref.ly/John5:24), [JHN 6:63–6:68](https://ref.ly/John6:63-John6:68), [JHN 8:31–8:32](https://ref.ly/John8:31-John8:32), [JHN 8:47](https://ref.ly/John8:47), [JHN 8:52](https://ref.ly/John8:52), [JHN 12:47–12:50](https://ref.ly/John12:47-John12:50), [JHN 14:10–14:24](https://ref.ly/John14:10-John14:24), [JHN 15:3](https://ref.ly/John15:3), [JHN 17:6–17:8](https://ref.ly/John17:6-John17:8)

The word and the gospel
-----------------------

**[ACT 4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31)** *See also* [ACT 4:29](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29), [ACT 13:44–13:46](https://ref.ly/Acts13:44-Acts13:46), [ACT 19:10](https://ref.ly/Acts19:10), [ACT 20:24](https://ref.ly/Acts20:24); [DEU 30:14](https://ref.ly/Deut30:14); [ROM 10:8–10:10](https://ref.ly/Rom10:8-Rom10:10); [COL 1:5](https://ref.ly/Col1:5), [COL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Col2:2); [1TH 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:5); [HEB 4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:2)

God's word is a promise and help in trial and temptation
--------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 119:9–119:11](https://ref.ly/Ps119:9-Ps119:11)** *See also* [PSA 33:4–33:5](https://ref.ly/Ps33:4-Ps33:5), [PSA 119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:105), [PSA 119:133](https://ref.ly/Ps119:133); [DEU 8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:3); [MAT 4:4](https://ref.ly/Matt4:4); [JHN 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/John14:23-John14:24), [JHN 17:17](https://ref.ly/John17:17); [EPH 6:17](https://ref.ly/Eph6:17); [HEB 4:12](https://ref.ly/Heb4:12); [1JN 5:3](https://ref.ly/1John5:3)

Words should be used wisely
---------------------------

**[MAT 12:36–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36-Matt12:37)** *See also* [JDG 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Judg8:1-Judg8:3); [2CH 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:13-2Chr10:14); [PRO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19), [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18), [PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25), [PRO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Prov15:1), [PRO 16:24](https://ref.ly/Prov16:24), [PRO 25:15](https://ref.ly/Prov25:15); [ECC 12:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:11); [JAS 3:1–3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1-Jas3:12)

## 5628: work (id: 5628)

Work was ordained by God as a means of fulfilment, service and praise. It is to be supplemented by rest, following the pattern of God's creation of the world. Despite the effects of sin, work can still be honouring to God.

* **Associated Articles:** 5629: work, as ordained by God (ID: `5629@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5630: work, divine and human (ID: `5630@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5631: work, of God (ID: `5631@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5632: work, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5632@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5633: work, of Holy Spirit (ID: `5633@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5634: work, and the fall (ID: `5634@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5635: work, and redemption (ID: `5635@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5636: work, and rest (ID: `5636@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5629: work, as ordained by God (id: 5629)

God ordained work as the normal routine of living. Every legitimate human task, therefore, is of intrinsic worth, however menial it may seem, and is potentially a means of glorifying God.

Work is ordained by God
-----------------------

**[GEN 1:27–1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27-Gen1:28)** *See also* [EXO 20:9](https://ref.ly/Exod20:9) parallel [DEU 5:13](https://ref.ly/Deut5:13); [PSA 104:23](https://ref.ly/Ps104:23) *Work is part of the rhythm of life.*

God's purposes in ordaining work
--------------------------------

### That people should be self\-supporting

**[GEN 3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:19)** *See also* [PSA 128:2](https://ref.ly/Ps128:2); [1TH 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:12)

### That people should find self\-fulfilment

**[ECC 2:24](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24)** *See also* [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23); [ECC 3:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:22), [ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19)

### That people should serve others

**[EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28)** *See also* [PRO 31:15](https://ref.ly/Prov31:15); [1TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9); [1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8)

### That people should glorify God

**[COL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:17)** *See also* [1CO 10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:31); [EPH 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5-Eph6:8) parallel [COL 3:22–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:22-Col3:24)

Consequences of viewing work as God's ordinance
-----------------------------------------------

### Work is seen as a moral duty

**[TIT 3:14](https://ref.ly/Titus3:14)** *See also* [PRO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6); [ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10); [1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11); [2TH 3:7–3:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:7-2Thess3:12)

### Any legitimate work may be seen as God's calling

**[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)** *See also* [EXO 31:1–31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:1-Exod31:6), [EXO 35:30–35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:35); [PSA 78:70–78:71](https://ref.ly/Ps78:70-Ps78:71); [MAT 13:55](https://ref.ly/Matt13:55) parallel [MRK 6:3](https://ref.ly/Mark6:3); [ROM 13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6); [1CO 7:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:17), [1CO 7:20–7:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:20-1Cor7:24)

### Work is seen as a stewardship from God himself

**[COL 3:23–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:23-Col3:24)** *See also* [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30) parallel [LUK 19:12–19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:27); [EPH 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5-Eph6:8)

### Criticism of those who will not work

**[2TH 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10-2Thess3:11)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:27-28; Exodus 20:9; Deuteronomy 5:13; Psalms 104:23; Genesis 3:19; Psalms 128:2; 1 Thessalonians 4:12; Ecclesiastes 2:24; Proverbs 14:23; Ecclesiastes 3:22; Ecclesiastes 5:19; Ephesians 4:28; Proverbs 31:15; 1 Thessalonians 2:9; 1 Timothy 5:8; Colossians 3:17; 1 Corinthians 10:31; Ephesians 6:5-8; Colossians 3:22-24; Titus 3:14; Proverbs 6:6; Ecclesiastes 9:10; 1 Thessalonians 4:11; 2 Thessalonians 3:7-12; Genesis 2:15; Exodus 31:1-6; Exodus 35:30-35; Psalms 78:70-71; Matthew 13:55; Mark 6:3; Romans 13:6; 1 Corinthians 7:17; 1 Corinthians 7:20-24; Colossians 3:23-24; Matthew 25:14-30; Luke 19:12-27; 2 Thessalonians 3:10-11

## 5630: work, divine and human (id: 5630)

God has ordained that some of his purposes be fulfilled through the co\-operation (conscious or unconscious) of human beings.

God works in co\-operation with people's work
---------------------------------------------

### Human labour is futile apart from God

**[PSA 127:1](https://ref.ly/Ps127:1)** *See also* [GEN 11:8](https://ref.ly/Gen11:8); [JHN 15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:5)

### God often works through human means

**[PSA 77:20](https://ref.ly/Ps77:20)** *See also* [ACT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Acts9:15); [ROM 13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:6); [2CO 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:6)

### God gives the gifts needed for work

[EXO 31:3](https://ref.ly/Exod31:3), [EXO 31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:6); ; [LUK 12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:48); ; [1PE 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:11)

### Human workers should acknowledge their dependence upon God

**[DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18)** *See also* [PSA 90:17](https://ref.ly/Ps90:17); [NEH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:9), [NEH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Neh6:16); [2CO 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:5)

### The apostles as God's fellow\-workers

**[1CO 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:9)**; **[2CO 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:1)** *See also* [MRK 16:20](https://ref.ly/Mark16:20)

Examples of divine and human co\-operation in work
--------------------------------------------------

[PHP 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Phil2:12-Phil2:13)

### God creates and people cultivate

[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15); ; [ISA 28:23–28:29](https://ref.ly/Isa28:23-Isa28:29); ; [1CO 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:7)

### God gives and people are generous

[1KI 17:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:9); ; [2CO 8:1–8:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:1-2Cor8:5)

### God speaks through prophets' words

[DEU 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/Deut18:15-Deut18:18); ; [2SA 12:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:25); ; [JER 37:2](https://ref.ly/Jer37:2); ; [HOS 12:13](https://ref.ly/Hos12:13); ; [MAT 1:22](https://ref.ly/Matt1:22); ; [2PE 1:21](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:21)

## 5631: work, of God (id: 5631)

See [1300: God, work of](1300.md)

## 5634: work, and the fall (id: 5634)

Human work, which was once a pleasure, became a burden only on account of human disobedience.

Work is under God's judgment
----------------------------

**[GEN 3:17–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17-Gen3:19)** *See also* [GEN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:29)

Work is often frustrating
-------------------------

**[ECC 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:22-Eccl2:23)** *See also* [ECC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:11), [ECC 2:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:18), [ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8), [ECC 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:16-Eccl5:17)

Work may exploit rather than enhance society
--------------------------------------------

### Through dishonesty

[LEV 19:35](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35); ; [DEU 25:13](https://ref.ly/Deut25:13); ; [HOS 12:7](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7)

### Through oppression

[EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14); ; [1KI 12:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:4), [1KI 12:10–12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:10-1Kgs12:14)

Work may be undervalued or overvalued
-------------------------------------

### Some people avoid work to their cost

[PRO 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:11), [PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4), [PRO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Prov19:15), [PRO 24:30–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:30-Prov24:34); ; [ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18); ; [2TH 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10-2Thess3:11)

### Some people overwork to their cost

[EXO 18:17–18:18](https://ref.ly/Exod18:17-Exod18:18); ; [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); ; [LUK 10:41–10:42](https://ref.ly/Luke10:41-Luke10:42), [LUK 12:15–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15-Luke12:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 3:17-19; Genesis 5:29; Ecclesiastes 2:22-23; Ecclesiastes 2:11; Ecclesiastes 2:18; Ecclesiastes 4:8; Ecclesiastes 5:16-17; Leviticus 19:35; Deuteronomy 25:13; Hosea 12:7; Exodus 1:11-14; 1 Kings 12:4; 1 Kings 12:10-14; Proverbs 6:6-11; Proverbs 13:4; Proverbs 19:15; Proverbs 24:30-34; Ecclesiastes 10:18; 2 Thessalonians 3:10-11; Exodus 18:17-18; Psalms 127:2; Luke 10:41-42; Luke 12:15-21
* **Associated Articles:** 5343: idleness (ID: `5343@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5418: monotony (ID: `5418@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5864: futility (ID: `5864@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6154: fall, the (ID: `6154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8713: discouragement (ID: `8713@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8718: disobedience (ID: `8718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8778: materialism (ID: `8778@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5635: work, and redemption (id: 5635)

Although human activity cannot merit eternal redemption, work done in this life may, with God's enabling, substantially reflect the divine ideal, in anticipation of the perfect service of heaven.

Human work cannot merit redemption
----------------------------------

[EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9); ; [2TI 1:9](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:9); ; [TIT 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:4-Titus3:5)

Jesus Christ's finished work secures redemption
-----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 17:4](https://ref.ly/John17:4)** *See also* [JHN 4:34](https://ref.ly/John4:34), [JHN 19:30](https://ref.ly/John19:30); [HEB 9:12](https://ref.ly/Heb9:12)

Faith leads believers to perform good works
-------------------------------------------

**[JAS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Jas2:14)** *See also* [MAT 7:16–7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:16-Matt7:20), [MAT 12:33](https://ref.ly/Matt12:33); [LUK 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:8-Luke3:9); [ROM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Rom2:13); [HEB 6:10–6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:10-Heb6:12); [JAS 2:17–2:26](https://ref.ly/Jas2:17-Jas2:26)

Redeemed workers fulfil God's purpose in work
---------------------------------------------

### By applying biblical virtues in employment

[PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23); ; [GAL 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9); ; [COL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Col4:1)

### By the pursuit of excellence

[ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10); ; [TIT 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Titus2:7-Titus2:8), [TIT 3:8](https://ref.ly/Titus3:8); ; [1PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:12), [1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)

### By doing their work as those responsible to God

[ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1); ; [1CO 7:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:24), [1CO 10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:31); ; [EPH 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eph6:7); ; [COL 3:23](https://ref.ly/Col3:23)

Work done, as for the Lord, will be rewarded by the Lord
--------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 3:23–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:23-Col3:24)** *See also* [2CH 31:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:21); [MAT 25:21](https://ref.ly/Matt25:21); [1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58)

Work will continue in the life to come
--------------------------------------

**[REV 22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3)** *See also* [MAT 25:21](https://ref.ly/Matt25:21), [MAT 25:23](https://ref.ly/Matt25:23); [LUK 19:17](https://ref.ly/Luke19:17), [LUK 19:19](https://ref.ly/Luke19:19)

## 5636: work, and rest (id: 5636)

In creation, God has established a pattern of work and rest that is to be a model for believers.

Work and rest built into creation
---------------------------------

**[EXO 20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:11)** *See also* [GEN 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Gen2:1-Gen2:3); [PSA 104:19–104:23](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19-Ps104:23)

The pattern of work and rest confirmed in the OT
------------------------------------------------

### By Sabbath observance

[EXO 20:10](https://ref.ly/Exod20:10) parallel [DEU 5:14](https://ref.ly/Deut5:14); ; [LEV 23:3](https://ref.ly/Lev23:3)

### By observing holy festivals

[EXO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Exod12:16); ; [LEV 16:29](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29), [LEV 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Lev23:6-Lev23:8) parallel [NUM 28:17–28:18](https://ref.ly/Num28:17-Num28:18); ; [LEV 23:28–23:31](https://ref.ly/Lev23:28-Lev23:31) parallel [NUM 29:7](https://ref.ly/Num29:7); ; [LEV 23:35–23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:35-Lev23:36), [LEV 23:39](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39) parallel [NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12); ; [EST 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/Esth9:17-Esth9:19)

Work and rest in the ministry of Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

**[MRK 6:30–6:32](https://ref.ly/Mark6:30-Mark6:32)** *See also* [JHN 4:6](https://ref.ly/John4:6)

Wisdom needed to balance work and rest
--------------------------------------

### To avoid idleness

[PRO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:9-Prov6:11), [PRO 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4-Prov10:5), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23), [PRO 20:13](https://ref.ly/Prov20:13), [PRO 24:30–24:34](https://ref.ly/Prov24:30-Prov24:34)

### To avoid overwork

[EXO 18:13–18:24](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13-Exod18:24); ; [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); ; [LUK 10:38–10:42](https://ref.ly/Luke10:38-Luke10:42)

Work and rest are both potential means of glorifying God
--------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:31)** *See also* [ECC 2:24](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24); [COL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:17)

Work and rest will be perfectly fulfilled in heaven
---------------------------------------------------

**[REV 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rev14:13), [REV 22:3](https://ref.ly/Rev22:3)**

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:11; Genesis 2:1-3; Psalms 104:19-23; Exodus 20:10; Deuteronomy 5:14; Leviticus 23:3; Exodus 12:16; Leviticus 16:29; Leviticus 23:6-8; Numbers 28:17-18; Leviticus 23:28-31; Numbers 29:7; Leviticus 23:35-36; Leviticus 23:39; Numbers 29:12; Esther 9:17-19; Mark 6:30-32; John 4:6; Proverbs 6:9-11; Proverbs 10:4-5; Proverbs 14:23; Proverbs 20:13; Proverbs 24:30-34; Exodus 18:13-24; Psalms 127:2; Luke 10:38-42; 1 Corinthians 10:31; Ecclesiastes 2:24; Colossians 3:17; Revelation 14:13; Revelation 22:3

## 5638: writing (id: 5638)

A form of communication, including the writing of books, letters and scrolls. Many kinds of materials have been written on. Figuratively speaking, the heart can be written on.

Uses of writing
---------------

### Books

**[JER 30:2](https://ref.ly/Jer30:2)** *See also* [LUK 1:3](https://ref.ly/Luke1:3)

### The law

**[DEU 31:24](https://ref.ly/Deut31:24)** *See also* [JOS 24:26](https://ref.ly/Josh24:26); [2KI 23:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:21)

### Annals and records

**[1KI 11:41](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:41)** *See also* [2KI 12:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:19); [1CH 29:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:29); [2CH 35:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:25)

### Agreements

[NEH 9:38](https://ref.ly/Neh9:38)

### Legal documents

[DEU 24:1](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1); ; [MRK 10:4](https://ref.ly/Mark10:4)

### Letters

**[2SA 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:14)**; **[ROM 16:22](https://ref.ly/Rom16:22)**; **[COL 4:18](https://ref.ly/Col4:18)** *In those days the sender would add a few words to a letter dictated to and written by a secretary.* *See also* [1KI 21:8–21:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:11); [EST 3:12](https://ref.ly/Esth3:12); [GAL 6:11](https://ref.ly/Gal6:11); [2PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:1)

Writing materials
-----------------

### Stone

**[EXO 32:15–32:16](https://ref.ly/Exod32:15-Exod32:16)** *See also* [DEU 27:2–27:3](https://ref.ly/Deut27:2-Deut27:3); [JOB 19:24](https://ref.ly/Job19:24)

### Wood

[NUM 17:2–17:3](https://ref.ly/Num17:2-Num17:3); ; [DEU 6:9](https://ref.ly/Deut6:9)

### Metal

[ZEC 14:20](https://ref.ly/Zech14:20)

### Walls

[DAN 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5-Dan5:8)

### Writing tablets

[HAB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hab2:2); ; [LUK 1:63](https://ref.ly/Luke1:63)

### Scrolls

**[DEU 17:18](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18)**; **[REV 1:11](https://ref.ly/Rev1:11)** *See also* [EXO 17:14](https://ref.ly/Exod17:14); [NUM 5:23](https://ref.ly/Num5:23); [JOB 19:23](https://ref.ly/Job19:23); [ISA 8:1](https://ref.ly/Isa8:1); [JER 36:18](https://ref.ly/Jer36:18), [JER 51:60](https://ref.ly/Jer51:60)

Writing kits
------------

**[EZK 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:2-Ezek9:3)** *See also* [EZK 9:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:11); [2JN 1:12](https://ref.ly/2John1:12); [3JN 1:13](https://ref.ly/3John1:13)

God writes
----------

### The law

[EXO 31:18](https://ref.ly/Exod31:18), [EXO 32:15–32:16](https://ref.ly/Exod32:15-Exod32:16), [EXO 34:1](https://ref.ly/Exod34:1)

### Figurative writing of God

**[PSA 139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:16)** *See also* [PSA 87:6](https://ref.ly/Ps87:6); [LUK 10:20](https://ref.ly/Luke10:20)

Jesus Christ writes
-------------------

**[JHN 8:6](https://ref.ly/John8:6)**

Writing on the heart
--------------------

**[JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33)** *See also* [HEB 8:10](https://ref.ly/Heb8:10), [HEB 10:16](https://ref.ly/Heb10:16)**[ROM 2:15](https://ref.ly/Rom2:15)** *See also* [PRO 3:3](https://ref.ly/Prov3:3), [PRO 7:3](https://ref.ly/Prov7:3); [JER 17:1](https://ref.ly/Jer17:1); [2CO 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:2)

## 5651: ancestors (id: 5651)

The ancestors of Israel
-----------------------

### Abraham, Isaac and Jacob

**[LEV 26:45](https://ref.ly/Lev26:45)** *See also* [JAS 2:21](https://ref.ly/Jas2:21)

### More immediate forebears

**[AMO 2:4](https://ref.ly/Amos2:4)** *See also* [1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4); [ACT 28:17](https://ref.ly/Acts28:17)

Ancestors of specific groups of people
--------------------------------------

**[JOS 17:1](https://ref.ly/Josh17:1)** *See also* [GEN 10:21](https://ref.ly/Gen10:21)

Ancestors of individuals
------------------------

**[ROM 9:5](https://ref.ly/Rom9:5)** *See also* [HEB 7:10](https://ref.ly/Heb7:10)

The figurative use of ancestors
-------------------------------

**[EZK 16:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:3)**

## 5652: babies (id: 5652)

The gift of young children from God is usually seen as a blessing. The term is also used figuratively of those who are young or immature in the faith.

Babies as a blessing from God
-----------------------------

**[PSA 127:3](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3)** *See also* [GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28), [GEN 17:16](https://ref.ly/Gen17:16), [GEN 24:59–24:60](https://ref.ly/Gen24:59-Gen24:60); [PRO 17:6](https://ref.ly/Prov17:6); [LUK 1:41–1:42](https://ref.ly/Luke1:41-Luke1:42)

The promise of a baby
---------------------

**[GEN 17:15–17:19](https://ref.ly/Gen17:15-Gen17:19)** *See also* [GEN 18:10–18:14](https://ref.ly/Gen18:10-Gen18:14); [2KI 4:14–4:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:14-2Kgs4:17); [LUK 1:11–1:24](https://ref.ly/Luke1:11-Luke1:24)

Examples of biblical characters as babies
-----------------------------------------

[GEN 21:1–21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:1-Gen21:7) *Isaac*; ; [GEN 25:21–25:26](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21-Gen25:26) *Esau and Jacob*; ; [EXO 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Exod2:1-Exod2:10) *Moses*; ; [JDG 13:2–13:25](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2-Judg13:25) *Samson*; ; [RUT 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13-Ruth4:17) *Obed, the father of Jesse*; ; [2SA 12:24–12:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24-2Sam12:25) *Solomon*; ; [LUK 1:57–1:66](https://ref.ly/Luke1:57-Luke1:66) *John the Baptist*

Babies as prophetic signs
-------------------------

**[ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14)** *See also* [ISA 8:3–8:4](https://ref.ly/Isa8:3-Isa8:4), [ISA 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6-Isa9:7); [HOS 1:2–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2-Hos1:11); [LUK 2:8–2:12](https://ref.ly/Luke2:8-Luke2:12)

OT laws concerned with the birth of babies
------------------------------------------

**[LUK 2:21–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:21-Luke2:24)** *See also* [GEN 17:9–17:12](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:12); [EXO 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Exod13:1-Exod13:2); [LEV 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:8); [LUK 1:59](https://ref.ly/Luke1:59)

A mother's love for her baby
----------------------------

**[ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)** *See also* [1KI 3:16–3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:16-1Kgs3:28); [ISA 66:12–66:13](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12-Isa66:13)

Babies are sinful from birth
----------------------------

**[PSA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Ps51:5)** *See also* [PSA 58:3](https://ref.ly/Ps58:3); [JHN 9:34](https://ref.ly/John9:34)

The death of babies
-------------------

**[EXO 1:15–1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:15-Exod1:22)** *See also* [EXO 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/Exod11:4-Exod11:5); [2SA 12:13–12:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:13-2Sam12:23); [LAM 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11-Lam2:12); [MAT 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16-Matt2:18); [ACT 7:17–7:19](https://ref.ly/Acts7:17-Acts7:19)

Jesus Christ and babies
-----------------------

### Jesus Christ's own birth and babyhood

**[MAT 1:18–1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:23)** *See also* [ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14); [MAT 2:1–2:23](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:23); [LUK 1:26–1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26-Luke1:38), [LUK 2:1–2:40](https://ref.ly/Luke2:1-Luke2:40)

### Jesus Christ's response to babies

**[LUK 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15-Luke18:17)** parallels [MAT 19:13–19:15](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:15), [MRK 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:16)

Babyhood used as a spiritual image
----------------------------------

**[1CO 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:1-1Cor3:2)** *See also* [PSA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ps8:2); [MAT 21:16](https://ref.ly/Matt21:16), [MAT 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25); [ROM 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rom2:20); [1CO 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:20); [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14); [HEB 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:11-Heb5:14); [1PE 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2-1Pet2:3)

## 5654: betrothal (id: 5654)

The period of engagement preceding marriage; betrothal was a binding contract established between two families and sealed by the exchange of gifts. During this period the couple did not live together; sexual relations with each other at this stage was regarded as equivalent to adultery. Betrothal describes the relationship between God and his people and between Jesus Christ and the church.

Betrothal and the choice of a spouse
------------------------------------

### Wives were often chosen by parents for their sons

**[GEN 21:21](https://ref.ly/Gen21:21)** *It was usual practice for the groom's parents to choose his wife and arrange the wedding.* *See also* [GEN 24:4](https://ref.ly/Gen24:4), [GEN 38:6](https://ref.ly/Gen38:6)

### Suitable husbands were sought by parents for their daughters

**[RUT 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:1-Ruth3:4)** *Naomi, in the role of parent, assumes responsibility to find a husband for Ruth.* *See also* [JDG 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Judg1:12-Judg1:13); [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17), [1SA 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:21)

### Betrothal following the couple's wishes

**[JDG 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Judg14:1-Judg14:4)** *Sometimes the man chose a prospective bride and his parents negotiated the marriage.* *See also* [GEN 34:1–34:4](https://ref.ly/Gen34:1-Gen34:4), [GEN 24:57–24:58](https://ref.ly/Gen24:57-Gen24:58), [GEN 26:34–26:35](https://ref.ly/Gen26:34-Gen26:35) *Esau chose a bride against his parents' wishes.*

Betrothal preceded marriage
---------------------------

[GEN 29:21](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21); ; [DEU 20:7](https://ref.ly/Deut20:7) *A betrothed soldier was exempted from military service until after his marriage*; ; [MAT 1:24](https://ref.ly/Matt1:24); ; [1CO 7:36–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36-1Cor7:38)

An exchange of gifts accompanyed betrothal
------------------------------------------

### The bride\-price

**[GEN 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/Gen34:11-Gen34:12)** *This gift, which was given by the groom to the bride's family as compensation, sealed the marriage contract.* *See also* [GEN 24:53](https://ref.ly/Gen24:53), [GEN 29:18](https://ref.ly/Gen29:18), [GEN 29:27](https://ref.ly/Gen29:27); [EXO 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16-Exod22:17); [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29); [2SA 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:14)

### The dowry

**[1KI 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:16)** *The dowry was a gift from the bride's father to the bride and/or groom.* [GEN 24:59](https://ref.ly/Gen24:59), [GEN 29:24](https://ref.ly/Gen29:24), [GEN 29:29](https://ref.ly/Gen29:29); [JDG 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:14-Judg1:15)

Betrothal was treated as marriage
---------------------------------

**[DEU 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:24)** *Though there were no sexual relations within the betrothal period, the commitment was regarded almost as seriously as marriage and infidelity of a betrothed partner was treated as adultery.* *See also* [GEN 19:14](https://ref.ly/Gen19:14) *The men betrothed to Lot's daughters are described as his “sons\-in\-law”*; [MAT 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:20) *Mary's pregnancy during betrothal left her open to the charge and consequences of unfaithfulness.*

Betrothal portrays the relationship between God and his people
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[HOS 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Hos2:19-Hos2:20)**; **[2CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2)** *See also* [JHN 3:29](https://ref.ly/John3:29); [EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27); [REV 19:7–19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7-Rev19:9), [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 21:21; Genesis 24:4; Genesis 38:6; Ruth 3:1-4; Judges 1:12-13; 1 Samuel 18:17; 1 Samuel 18:21; Judges 14:1-4; Genesis 34:1-4; Genesis 24:57-58; Genesis 26:34-35; Genesis 29:21; Deuteronomy 20:7; Matthew 1:24; 1 Corinthians 7:36-38; Genesis 34:11-12; Genesis 24:53; Genesis 29:18; Genesis 29:27; Exodus 22:16-17; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; 2 Samuel 3:14; 1 Kings 9:16; Genesis 24:59; Genesis 29:24; Genesis 29:29; Judges 1:14-15; Deuteronomy 22:23-24; Genesis 19:14; Matthew 1:18-20; Hosea 2:19-20; 2 Corinthians 11:2; John 3:29; Ephesians 5:25-27; Revelation 19:7-9; Revelation 21:2
* **Associated Articles:** 5444: pledges (ID: `5444@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5659: bride (ID: `5659@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5660: bridegroom (ID: `5660@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5679: dowry (ID: `5679@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5710: marriage, customs concerning (ID: `5710@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5740: virgin (ID: `5740@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5742: wedding (ID: `5742@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6236: sexual sin (ID: `6236@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5655: birth (id: 5655)

The beginning or origin of life, in which a living creature comes into the world. Scripture draws a distinction between physical and spiritual birth, insisting that a person must be not only physically but also spiritually born in order to have life to the full.

Physical birth
--------------

### God creates all life

[JOB 10:8–10:12](https://ref.ly/Job10:8-Job10:12); ; [PSA 71:6](https://ref.ly/Ps71:6), [PSA 139:13](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13)

### Human beings are sinful from the moment of birth

**[PSA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Ps51:5)** *See also* [JOB 15:14](https://ref.ly/Job15:14), [JOB 25:4](https://ref.ly/Job25:4); [PSA 58:3](https://ref.ly/Ps58:3); [JHN 3:6](https://ref.ly/John3:6), [JHN 9:34](https://ref.ly/John9:34); [ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12), [ROM 5:17–5:19](https://ref.ly/Rom5:17-Rom5:19)

The necessity of spiritual birth
--------------------------------

### Being born again

**[JHN 3:3](https://ref.ly/John3:3)**

### Being born of the Spirit

**[JHN 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:5-John3:8)** *See also* [PSA 87:4–87:6](https://ref.ly/Ps87:4-Ps87:6); [MAT 18:3](https://ref.ly/Matt18:3); [JHN 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:12-John1:13); [1PE 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3), [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2)

The agents of the new birth: word and Spirit
--------------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:23)** *See also* [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21); [JHN 3:5](https://ref.ly/John3:5), [JHN 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/John3:7-John3:8); [TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5); [JAS 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jas1:18)

The fruit of the new birth: holiness and love
---------------------------------------------

**[1JN 3:9](https://ref.ly/1John3:9), [1JN 4:7](https://ref.ly/1John4:7)** *See also* [MAT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Matt18:4); [1JN 2:29](https://ref.ly/1John2:29), [1JN 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/1John5:3-1John5:4), [1JN 5:18](https://ref.ly/1John5:18)

Spiritual birth and the nation of Israel
----------------------------------------

**[DEU 32:18](https://ref.ly/Deut32:18)** *See also* [NUM 11:12](https://ref.ly/Num11:12) *Moses implies that God himself gave birth to the people.*

Birth seen as entry into a condition or status
----------------------------------------------

### Ethnic identity from birth

[EXO 12:48](https://ref.ly/Exod12:48); ; [EZK 16:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:3); ; [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26), [ACT 22:27–22:28](https://ref.ly/Acts22:27-Acts22:28); ; [GAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15), [GAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4); ; [EPH 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eph2:11); ; [PHP 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:5)

### Physical or mental disability from birth

[JHN 9:1](https://ref.ly/John9:1); ; [ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2), [ACT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Acts14:8)
