## 5657: birthright (id: 5657)

The right of the firstborn son in ancient Israel to inherit his father's estate. Where the property was divided, the firstborn received a double share.

Birthright was recognised amongst the patriarchs
------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 25:5–25:6](https://ref.ly/Gen25:5-Gen25:6), [GEN 25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:34)**

Birthright and the law
----------------------

### It was guaranteed under the law

**[DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17)** *See also* [1CH 26:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:10) *some ignored this law*

### Daughters and others could inherit if there were no sons

[NUM 27:1–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:11)

### Only sons of a lawful marriage qualified

[GEN 21:8–21:13](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8-Gen21:13); ; [JDG 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Judg11:1-Judg11:2)

Birthright and kingship
-----------------------

### Succession was normally to the firstborn

**[2CH 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:1-2Chr21:3)** *See also* [2CH 11:18–11:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:18-2Chr11:22) *favouritism could intrude here also*

Transfer of the birthright
--------------------------

### Through misconduct

**[1CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1)** *See also* [GEN 49:3–49:4](https://ref.ly/Gen49:3-Gen49:4)

### By transaction

**[GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34)** *See also* [HEB 12:16–12:17](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16-Heb12:17)

The Christian birthright
------------------------

**[ROM 8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:17)** *Jesus Christ is the firstborn over all creation; by the grace of God Christians share in his inheritance.* *See also* [GAL 4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:7); [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15); [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 25:5-6; Genesis 25:34; Deuteronomy 21:15-17; 1 Chronicles 26:10; Numbers 27:1-11; Genesis 21:8-13; Judges 11:1-2; 2 Chronicles 21:1-3; 2 Chronicles 11:18-22; 1 Chronicles 5:1; Genesis 49:3-4; Genesis 25:29-34; Hebrews 12:16-17; Romans 8:17; Galatians 4:7; Colossians 1:15; Hebrews 1:2
* **Associated Articles:** 5368: kingship (ID: `5368@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5672: concubines (ID: `5672@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5674: daughters (ID: `5674@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5681: family, nature of (ID: `5681@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5684: fathers (ID: `5684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5688: firstborn (ID: `5688@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5701: heir (ID: `5701@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5703: inheritance (ID: `5703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5738: sons (ID: `5738@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6608: adoption (ID: `6608@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9413: heaven, inheritance of (ID: `9413@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5658: boys (id: 5658)

As those who would continue the family line boys were seen as a great blessing from God. Circumcised on the eighth day, as they grew towards maturity they were to be taught by their fathers. In the story of God's people, Israelite boys are seen serving God, being ministered to by men of God and suffering at the hands of ungodly kings.

Boys in general are seen as a blessing from God
-----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 127:3–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:5)** *See also* [RUT 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13-Ruth4:17); [PSA 128:3–128:4](https://ref.ly/Ps128:3-Ps128:4)

Particular boys who are seen as a special gift from God
-------------------------------------------------------

### The boy Isaac, a gift to Abraham and Sarah

**[GEN 17:15–17:22](https://ref.ly/Gen17:15-Gen17:22)** *See also* [GEN 21:1–21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:1-Gen21:7)

### The boy Samson, a gift to Manoah and his wife

**[JDG 13:2–13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2-Judg13:5)** *See also* [JDG 13:24–13:25](https://ref.ly/Judg13:24-Judg13:25)

### The boy Samuel, a gift to Elkanah and Hannah

**[1SA 1:1–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:1-1Sam1:20)**

### The boy John, a gift to Zechariah and Elizabeth

**[LUK 1:5–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5-Luke1:25)** *See also* [LUK 1:57–1:66](https://ref.ly/Luke1:57-Luke1:66)

### The boy Jesus, a gift to Mary and Joseph

**[LUK 1:26–1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26-Luke1:38)** *See also* [MAT 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20-Matt1:21)

Boys were circumcised on the eighth day
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 17:9–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:14)** *See also* [LEV 12:3](https://ref.ly/Lev12:3); [LUK 1:59](https://ref.ly/Luke1:59), [LUK 2:21](https://ref.ly/Luke2:21)

Boys were taught by their fathers
---------------------------------

**[PRO 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1-Prov4:4)** *While examples of fatherly instruction in Scripture are confined to wisdom literature and liturgical material, other sources indicate that the education provided was more wide\-ranging, and would include the teaching of a trade.* *See also* [EXO 13:8](https://ref.ly/Exod13:8); [DEU 6:20–6:25](https://ref.ly/Deut6:20-Deut6:25); [PRO 4:10–4:13](https://ref.ly/Prov4:10-Prov4:13)

Israelite boys who served God
-----------------------------

### The boy Samuel served God in the temple

**[1SA 3:1–3:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:1-1Sam3:21)**

### The boy David served God by killing Goliath

**[1SA 17:20–17:51](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:20-1Sam17:51)**

### The boy King Joash served God

**[2CH 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:1-2Chr24:2)** parallel [2KI 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:1-2Kgs12:2)

### The boy King Josiah served God

**[2CH 34:1–34:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:1-2Chr34:2)** parallel [2KI 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:1-2Kgs22:2)

### The boy Jesus had a conscious relationship with God

**[LUK 2:41–2:50](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41-Luke2:50)**

Boys who are ministered to by men of God
----------------------------------------

### Through Elijah, God restored the life of a widow's son

**[1KI 17:17–17:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:17-1Kgs17:23)**

### Through Elisha, God restored the life of a Shunammite's son

**[2KI 4:32–4:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:32-2Kgs4:37)**

### Jesus Christ healed a demon\-possessed boy

**[MAT 17:14–17:18](https://ref.ly/Matt17:14-Matt17:18)** parallels [MRK 9:14–9:27](https://ref.ly/Mark9:14-Mark9:27), [LUK 9:37–9:43](https://ref.ly/Luke9:37-Luke9:43)

Both Herod and Pharaoh ordered that Israelite boys be killed
------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16)** *See also* [EXO 1:15–1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:15-Exod1:22)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 127:3-5; Ruth 4:13-17; Psalms 128:3-4; Genesis 17:15-22; Genesis 21:1-7; Judges 13:2-5; Judges 13:24-25; 1 Samuel 1:1-20; Luke 1:5-25; Luke 1:57-66; Luke 1:26-38; Matthew 1:20-21; Genesis 17:9-14; Leviticus 12:3; Luke 1:59; Luke 2:21; Proverbs 4:1-4; Exodus 13:8; Deuteronomy 6:20-25; Proverbs 4:10-13; 1 Samuel 3:1-21; 1 Samuel 17:20-51; 2 Chronicles 24:1-2; 2 Kings 12:1-2; 2 Chronicles 34:1-2; 2 Kings 22:1-2; Luke 2:41-50; 1 Kings 17:17-23; 2 Kings 4:32-37; Matthew 17:14-18; Mark 9:14-27; Luke 9:37-43; Matthew 2:16; Exodus 1:15-22
* **Associated Articles:** 2520: Jesus Christ, childhood of (ID: `2520@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5098: John the Baptist (ID: `5098@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5225: barrenness (ID: `5225@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5302: education (ID: `5302@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5695: girls (ID: `5695@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5738: sons (ID: `5738@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7334: circumcision (ID: `7334@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5659: bride (id: 5659)

A woman who is about to be married or who has just got married. In the NT the church is described as the bride of Christ.

Jewels and ornaments worn by a bride at her wedding
---------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10)** *See also* [PSA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ps45:9), [PSA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ps45:14); [SNG 4:9](https://ref.ly/Song4:9); [ISA 49:18](https://ref.ly/Isa49:18); [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32)

Joy and happiness associated with a bride
-----------------------------------------

**[JER 33:10–33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:10-Jer33:11)** *See also* [JER 7:34](https://ref.ly/Jer7:34), [JER 16:9](https://ref.ly/Jer16:9), [JER 25:10](https://ref.ly/Jer25:10); [REV 18:23](https://ref.ly/Rev18:23)

The bride as the object of the bridegroom's love and affection
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5)** *See also* [SNG 4:8–4:15](https://ref.ly/Song4:8-Song4:15), [SNG 5:1](https://ref.ly/Song5:1)

The bridegroom as the object of the bride's love and affection
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2-Jer2:3)**

Exemptions for a newly married bride
------------------------------------

**[JOL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Joel2:16)** *A newly married bride would normally be excused from certain civic and religious duties.* *See also* [DEU 24:5](https://ref.ly/Deut24:5)

The bride price
---------------

**[GEN 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/Gen34:11-Gen34:12)** *A bridegroom was required to pay a bride\-price to the father of his bride.* *See also* [GEN 29:18–29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:18-Gen29:30); [1SA 18:20–18:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:20-1Sam18:27)

Proof of a bride's virginity was required in certain circumstances
------------------------------------------------------------------

[DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21)

The church depicted as the bride of Christ
------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27)** *See also* [REV 19:7–19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7-Rev19:9), [REV 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:2), [REV 21:9–21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:9-Rev21:27), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 61:10; Psalms 45:9; Psalms 45:14; Song of Songs 4:9; Isaiah 49:18; Jeremiah 2:32; Jeremiah 33:10-11; Jeremiah 7:34; Jeremiah 16:9; Jeremiah 25:10; Revelation 18:23; Isaiah 62:5; Song of Songs 4:8-15; Song of Songs 5:1; Jeremiah 2:2-3; Joel 2:16; Deuteronomy 24:5; Genesis 34:11-12; Genesis 29:18-30; 1 Samuel 18:20-27; Deuteronomy 22:13-21; Ephesians 5:25-27; Revelation 19:7-9; Revelation 21:1-2; Revelation 21:9-27; Revelation 22:17
* **Associated Articles:** 4342: jewels (ID: `4342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5660: bridegroom (ID: `5660@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5679: dowry (ID: `5679@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5740: virgin (ID: `5740@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5742: wedding (ID: `5742@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7024: church, nature and foundations of (ID: `7024@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7241: Jerusalem, significance of (ID: `7241@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8283: joy (ID: `8283@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8292: love (ID: `8292@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5660: bridegroom (id: 5660)

A man who is to be married or who has just got married. In the NT Jesus Christ is portrayed as a bridegroom and the church as his bride.

The bridegroom and the wedding ceremony
---------------------------------------

### The bridegroom wore special clothing

**[ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10)**

### The bridegroom took part in the procession to the bride's home

*See also* [MAT 25:1–25:12](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:12)

### The bridegroom had a group of companions

**[JDG 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10-Judg14:11)**

### The bridegroom had a special friend as an attendant

**[JHN 3:29](https://ref.ly/John3:29)** *See also* [JDG 14:20–15:2](https://ref.ly/Judg14:20-Judg15:2); [JHN 2:7–2:10](https://ref.ly/John2:7-John2:10) *The “master of the banquet” was probably the friend who attended the bridegroom.*

### The bridegroom was sometimes responsible for giving the wedding banquet

**[JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10)** *See also* [JHN 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/John2:9-John2:10)

### The bridegroom led his bride to a specially prepared bridal chamber

**[JOL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Joel2:16)** *See also* [PSA 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/Ps19:4-Ps19:5)

Bridegrooms were associated with happiness and rejoicing
--------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 7:34](https://ref.ly/Jer7:34)** *See also* [ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5); [JER 16:9](https://ref.ly/Jer16:9), [JER 25:10](https://ref.ly/Jer25:10), [JER 33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:11); [REV 18:23](https://ref.ly/Rev18:23)

Bridegrooms were excused from military service and certain other duties
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:5](https://ref.ly/Deut24:5)** *See also* [DEU 20:1–20:7](https://ref.ly/Deut20:1-Deut20:7)

Jesus Christ is portrayed as a bridegroom
-----------------------------------------

**[MAT 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:15)** parallels [MRK 2:18–2:20](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:20), [LUK 5:33–5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33-Luke5:35) *Jesus Christ refers to himself as the bridegroom.* *See also* [EPH 5:22–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:33)

The future coming of Jesus Christ compared to the sudden arrival of the bridegroom
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 25:1–25:10](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 61:10; Matthew 25:1-12; Judges 14:10-11; John 3:29; Judges 14:20-15:2; John 2:7-10; Judges 14:10; John 2:9-10; Joel 2:16; Psalms 19:4-5; Jeremiah 7:34; Isaiah 62:5; Jeremiah 16:9; Jeremiah 25:10; Jeremiah 33:11; Revelation 18:23; Deuteronomy 24:5; Deuteronomy 20:1-7; Matthew 9:14-15; Mark 2:18-20; Luke 5:33-35; Ephesians 5:22-33; Matthew 25:1-10
* **Associated Articles:** 2212: Jesus Christ, head of the church (ID: `2212@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5659: bride (ID: `5659@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5742: wedding (ID: `5742@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8283: joy (ID: `8283@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9150: Messianic banquet (ID: `9150@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5661: brothers (id: 5661)

The son of one's parents, or of either mother or father. God's people may be referred to collectively as brothers, all being children of one heavenly Father.

Close relationship between brothers
-----------------------------------

**[LEV 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:3)** *See also* [NUM 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Num6:6-Num6:7); [DEU 28:54](https://ref.ly/Deut28:54); [PRO 18:24](https://ref.ly/Prov18:24); [EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25)

Duties of brothers
------------------

### Levirate marriage

**[DEU 25:5–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:10)** *See also* [GEN 38:6–38:11](https://ref.ly/Gen38:6-Gen38:11); [MAT 22:23–22:30](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23-Matt22:30) parallels [MRK 12:18–12:25](https://ref.ly/Mark12:18-Mark12:25), [LUK 20:27–20:36](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27-Luke20:36)

### Participation in arrangement of marriages for sisters

**[GEN 34:11–34:17](https://ref.ly/Gen34:11-Gen34:17)** *See also* [GEN 24:50–24:60](https://ref.ly/Gen24:50-Gen24:60)

### Protection of sisters

**[2SA 13:1–13:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:33)** *See also* [GEN 34:24–34:31](https://ref.ly/Gen34:24-Gen34:31); [LEV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Lev21:3)

### Younger brothers taking news of older brothers to their father

**[1SA 17:17–17:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17-1Sam17:18)** *See also* [GEN 37:12–37:17](https://ref.ly/Gen37:12-Gen37:17)

The priority of the eldest brother
----------------------------------

**[DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17)** *See also* [GEN 43:33](https://ref.ly/Gen43:33), [GEN 44:12](https://ref.ly/Gen44:12); [1SA 20:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:29); [1CH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1-1Chr5:2); [2CH 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:1-2Chr21:3); [PSA 89:27](https://ref.ly/Ps89:27); [HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16)

Examples of younger brothers superseding their elder brothers
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 25:21–25:23](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21-Gen25:23)** *See also* [GEN 48:8–48:20](https://ref.ly/Gen48:8-Gen48:20); [DEU 33:13–33:17](https://ref.ly/Deut33:13-Deut33:17); [1SA 16:6–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:6-1Sam16:13); [1KI 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:22)

Brothers as heirs
-----------------

**[NUM 27:8–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:8-Num27:11)** *See also* [ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8)

Twin brothers
-------------

**[GEN 25:24–25:26](https://ref.ly/Gen25:24-Gen25:26)** *See also* [GEN 38:27–38:30](https://ref.ly/Gen38:27-Gen38:30); [MAL 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Mal1:2-Mal1:3)

Half\-brothers
--------------

**[JDG 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Judg11:1-Judg11:2)** *See also* [GEN 21:8–21:9](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8-Gen21:9); [JDG 8:30–8:31](https://ref.ly/Judg8:30-Judg8:31), [JDG 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Judg9:1-Judg9:6)

Brothers\-in\-law
-----------------

**[NUM 10:29–10:32](https://ref.ly/Num10:29-Num10:32)** *See also* [GEN 38:8](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8); [DEU 25:5](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5), [DEU 25:7](https://ref.ly/Deut25:7); [JDG 4:11](https://ref.ly/Judg4:11)

Examples of brothers failing to live up to expectations of them
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 4:8–4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8-Gen4:12)** *See also* [GEN 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3-Gen37:4), [GEN 37:12–37:33](https://ref.ly/Gen37:12-Gen37:33), [GEN 38:6–38:10](https://ref.ly/Gen38:6-Gen38:10); [JDG 9:1–9:5](https://ref.ly/Judg9:1-Judg9:5); [2CH 21:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:4)

Application of the term \`\`brothers''
--------------------------------------

### To siblings

**[GEN 42:32](https://ref.ly/Gen42:32)** *See also* [GEN 16:9–16:12](https://ref.ly/Gen16:9-Gen16:12), [GEN 25:18](https://ref.ly/Gen25:18); [JDG 16:31](https://ref.ly/Judg16:31); [1SA 17:17–17:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17-1Sam17:22), [1SA 22:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:1); [2CH 21:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:13); [NEH 1:2](https://ref.ly/Neh1:2), [NEH 5:14](https://ref.ly/Neh5:14); [JOB 19:17](https://ref.ly/Job19:17), [JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11); [PRO 17:17](https://ref.ly/Prov17:17); [MAT 10:21](https://ref.ly/Matt10:21) parallels [MRK 13:12](https://ref.ly/Mark13:12), [LUK 21:16](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16); [MAT 13:55](https://ref.ly/Matt13:55) parallel [MRK 6:3](https://ref.ly/Mark6:3); [JHN 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/John7:3-John7:5)

### To relatives

**[RUT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:3)** *See also* [NUM 36:2](https://ref.ly/Num36:2); [PRO 27:10](https://ref.ly/Prov27:10)

### To members of the same tribe

**[2SA 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:11-2Sam19:12)** *See also* [NUM 8:26](https://ref.ly/Num8:26)

### To Israelites

**[DEU 3:18](https://ref.ly/Deut3:18)** *See also* [DEU 10:9](https://ref.ly/Deut10:9), [DEU 17:15](https://ref.ly/Deut17:15); [JDG 20:23](https://ref.ly/Judg20:23); [2SA 19:41](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:41); [1CH 13:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:2); [JER 7:15](https://ref.ly/Jer7:15)

### To descendants of a common ancestor

**[NUM 20:14](https://ref.ly/Num20:14)** *See also* [DEU 2:4](https://ref.ly/Deut2:4), [DEU 23:7](https://ref.ly/Deut23:7)

### To equals and fellow\-citizens

**[LEV 19:17](https://ref.ly/Lev19:17)** *See also* [DEU 1:16](https://ref.ly/Deut1:16), [DEU 15:3](https://ref.ly/Deut15:3), [DEU 22:1](https://ref.ly/Deut22:1), [DEU 23:19](https://ref.ly/Deut23:19); [2SA 2:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:26); [MAT 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22-Matt5:24), [MAT 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:3-Matt7:5) parallel [LUK 6:41–6:42](https://ref.ly/Luke6:41-Luke6:42)

Love for God must exceed love for brothers
------------------------------------------

**[LUK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Luke14:26)** *See also* [DEU 33:9](https://ref.ly/Deut33:9); [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29) parallels [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30), [LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30)

Jesus Christ calls his disciples his \`\`brothers''
---------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 12:49–12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:49-Matt12:50)** parallels [MRK 3:34–3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:34-Mark3:35), [LUK 8:21](https://ref.ly/Luke8:21) *See also* [MAT 25:34–25:40](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34-Matt25:40), [MAT 28:10](https://ref.ly/Matt28:10); [JHN 20:17](https://ref.ly/John20:17); [HEB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Heb2:11)

The fraternal relationship among God's people
---------------------------------------------

### Fellow Christians called brothers

**[MAT 23:8](https://ref.ly/Matt23:8)** *See also* [ACT 15:23](https://ref.ly/Acts15:23), [ACT 28:14](https://ref.ly/Acts28:14); [1CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:11); [2CO 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:1), [2CO 9:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:3), [2CO 11:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:9), [2CO 12:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:18); [2TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:13); [PHM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:1)

### Believers urged to love each other

**[1TH 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:10)** *See also* [ROM 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rom12:10); [JAS 2:14–2:17](https://ref.ly/Jas2:14-Jas2:17); [1PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:17); [2PE 1:5–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:5-2Pet1:9)

### The church referred to as \`\`the brothers''

**[ACT 14:2](https://ref.ly/Acts14:2)** *See also* [ACT 9:30](https://ref.ly/Acts9:30), [ACT 11:1](https://ref.ly/Acts11:1), [ACT 11:29](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29), [ACT 15:3](https://ref.ly/Acts15:3), [ACT 16:2](https://ref.ly/Acts16:2), [ACT 17:14](https://ref.ly/Acts17:14), [ACT 21:17](https://ref.ly/Acts21:17)

Brother as an honorific term
----------------------------

### Equals called \`\`brothers''

**[GEN 29:4](https://ref.ly/Gen29:4)** *See also* [1SA 30:22–30:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:22-1Sam30:23); [2SA 1:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:26), [2SA 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9); [1KI 13:29–13:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:29-1Kgs13:30); [ACT 1:16](https://ref.ly/Acts1:16), [ACT 2:37](https://ref.ly/Acts2:37), [ACT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Acts3:17), [ACT 7:2](https://ref.ly/Acts7:2), [ACT 22:1](https://ref.ly/Acts22:1), [ACT 28:17](https://ref.ly/Acts28:17)

### Fellow Christians called \`\`brothers''

**[1CO 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:10)** *See also* [ACT 9:17](https://ref.ly/Acts9:17), [ACT 22:13](https://ref.ly/Acts22:13); [ROM 8:12](https://ref.ly/Rom8:12), [ROM 11:25](https://ref.ly/Rom11:25); [1CO 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:1), [1CO 7:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:24); [2CO 8:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:1); [GAL 4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:31); [1TH 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:14); [HEB 3:1](https://ref.ly/Heb3:1); [JAS 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jas1:16), [JAS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Jas5:10)

## 5662: certificate of divorce (id: 5662)

A certificate of divorce was required under Mosaic law
------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 10:4](https://ref.ly/Mark10:4)** parallel [MAT 19:7](https://ref.ly/Matt19:7) *The certificate provided a divorced wife with some legal protection and permitted her to remarry.* *See also* [DEU 24:1–24:4](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1-Deut24:4); [HOS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2) *These words may have been adapted from a divorce bill*; [MAT 5:31](https://ref.ly/Matt5:31)

God gave his unfaithful people a certificate of divorce
-------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 3:8](https://ref.ly/Jer3:8)** *See also* [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1) *Unlike Israel, Judah had been given no certificate of divorce. The exile is thus portrayed as only a temporary separation.*

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 10:4; Matthew 19:7; Deuteronomy 24:1-4; Hosea 2:2; Matthew 5:31; Jeremiah 3:8; Isaiah 50:1
* **Associated Articles:** 5675: divorce (ID: `5675@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5711: marriage, restrictions concerning (ID: `5711@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6242: adultery (ID: `6242@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5663: childbirth (id: 5663)

Seen by Scripture as a token of God's favour, and used figuratively to picture a number of central biblical themes.

Childbirth in the OT
--------------------

### Childbirth as a blessing

**[GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28)** *See also* [DEU 7:14](https://ref.ly/Deut7:14); [PSA 127:3–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:5)

### Childlessness a cause of shame in Israel

**[GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1)** *See also* [1SA 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:6), [1SA 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:10-1Sam1:11); [LUK 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:24-Luke1:25)

The nature of human childbirth
------------------------------

### The pain of childbirth a consequence of the fall

**[GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16)**

### The need for ceremonial purification after childbirth

*See also* [LEV 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:8) *Under the old covenant, unsanctioned contact with blood was ceremonially defiling*; [LUK 2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:24)

### The role of midwives in childbirth

[EXO 1:15–1:21](https://ref.ly/Exod1:15-Exod1:21)

### The joy of childbirth

[GEN 21:6](https://ref.ly/Gen21:6); ; [JHN 16:21](https://ref.ly/John16:21)

Childbirth and salvation
------------------------

### The birth of a Saviour

**[GEN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15)** *Although childbirth is a focus of judgment, through it the human race continues, and the Messiah comes into the world.* *See also* [GEN 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:2-Gen12:3), [GEN 15:3–15:6](https://ref.ly/Gen15:3-Gen15:6); [ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14), [ISA 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6-Isa9:7); [MAT 1:18–1:25](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:25); [LUK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Luke1:35), [LUK 2:4–2:7](https://ref.ly/Luke2:4-Luke2:7); [GAL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gal3:16), [GAL 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4); [REV 12:5](https://ref.ly/Rev12:5)

### Salvation in childbearing

**[1TI 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:15)** *A Christian mother fulfils her calling in childbearing; some take it to mean she will be kept safe in childbirth. Some, however, take this verse as a reference to [Ge 3:15](https://ref.ly/Gen3:15), and the coming of Jesus Christ, as above.*

Childbirth used figuratively
----------------------------

### To describe weakness and vulnerability

*See also* [EZK 16:4–16:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:4-Ezek16:6)

### To describe weakness under judgment

**[2KI 19:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:3)** parallel [ISA 37:3](https://ref.ly/Isa37:3) *See also* [PSA 48:6](https://ref.ly/Ps48:6); [ISA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Isa23:4), [ISA 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Isa26:17-Isa26:18); [JER 4:31](https://ref.ly/Jer4:31); [HOS 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hos2:3), [HOS 13:13](https://ref.ly/Hos13:13); [MIC 4:10](https://ref.ly/Mic4:10)

### To describe troubles prior to God's final triumph

**[MAT 24:7–24:8](https://ref.ly/Matt24:7-Matt24:8)** parallel [MRK 13:8](https://ref.ly/Mark13:8) *See also* [ROM 8:22](https://ref.ly/Rom8:22)

### To describe love and compassion

[ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)

### To describe regeneration

**[JHN 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/John3:6-John3:7)** *See also* [JHN 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:12-John1:13); [JAS 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jas1:18); [1PE 1:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:23)

### To describe pastoral concern

[GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19)

### To describe the disciples' joy

[JHN 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/John16:21-John16:22)

### To describe triumph and salvation

**[ISA 54:1](https://ref.ly/Isa54:1)** *See also* [GAL 4:27](https://ref.ly/Gal4:27); [ISA 26:19](https://ref.ly/Isa26:19), [ISA 66:7–66:11](https://ref.ly/Isa66:7-Isa66:11)

God's sovereignty in childbirth
-------------------------------

### All children are God's gift

[PSA 127:3](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3)

### God controls the events of childbirth

[GEN 25:22–25:23](https://ref.ly/Gen25:22-Gen25:23); ; [PSA 139:13](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13), [PSA 139:16](https://ref.ly/Ps139:16)

### Childbirth in old age

[GEN 17:16–17:19](https://ref.ly/Gen17:16-Gen17:19); ; [LUK 1:5–1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5-Luke1:13), [LUK 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18-Luke1:20), [LUK 1:23–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:23-Luke1:25)

### Childbirth as an answer to prayer

[GEN 25:21](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21); ; [1SA 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:20); ; [LUK 1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:13)

### Childbirth for the barren woman

**[PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9)** *See also* [GEN 25:21](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21); [JDG 13:2–13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2-Judg13:5); [1SA 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:5); [ISA 54:1](https://ref.ly/Isa54:1)

### Jesus Christ born of a virgin

**[ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14)** *See also* [MAT 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:22-Matt1:23), [MAT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18); [LUK 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Luke1:30-Luke1:31), [LUK 1:34–1:37](https://ref.ly/Luke1:34-Luke1:37) *“impossible” births demonstrate God's power and grace and show that salvation comes from the Lord.*

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:28; Deuteronomy 7:14; Psalms 127:3-5; Genesis 30:1; 1 Samuel 1:6; 1 Samuel 1:10-11; Luke 1:24-25; Genesis 3:16; Leviticus 12:1-8; Luke 2:24; Exodus 1:15-21; Genesis 21:6; John 16:21; Genesis 3:15; Genesis 12:2-3; Genesis 15:3-6; Isaiah 7:14; Isaiah 9:6-7; Matthew 1:18-25; Luke 1:35; Luke 2:4-7; Galatians 3:16; Galatians 4:4; Revelation 12:5; 1 Timothy 2:15; Ezekiel 16:4-6; 2 Kings 19:3; Isaiah 37:3; Psalms 48:6; Isaiah 23:4; Isaiah 26:17-18; Jeremiah 4:31; Hosea 2:3; Hosea 13:13; Micah 4:10; Matthew 24:7-8; Mark 13:8; Romans 8:22; Isaiah 49:15; John 3:6-7; John 1:12-13; James 1:18; 1 Peter 1:23; Galatians 4:19; John 16:21-22; Isaiah 54:1; Galatians 4:27; Isaiah 26:19; Isaiah 66:7-11; Psalms 127:3; Genesis 25:22-23; Psalms 139:13; Psalms 139:16; Genesis 17:16-19; Luke 1:5-13; Luke 1:18-20; Luke 1:23-25; Genesis 25:21; 1 Samuel 1:20; Luke 1:13; Psalms 113:9; Judges 13:2-5; 1 Samuel 2:5; Matthew 1:22-23; Matthew 1:18; Luke 1:30-31; Luke 1:34-37

## 5664: children (id: 5664)

Scripture indicates that children are a gift from God and are to be loved, disciplined and cared for. The term “children of God” is used to describe Christian believers, who must grow up in the faith which they have accepted.

* **Associated Articles:** 5665: children, attitudes towards (ID: `5665@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5666: children, needs of (ID: `5666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5667: children, responsibilities to God (ID: `5667@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5668: children, responsibilities to parents (ID: `5668@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5669: children, examples of (ID: `5669@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5665: children, attitudes towards (id: 5665)

Christians must welcome children as Jesus Christ did. They are used by Christ as an example of how believers should receive the kingdom, and as an example of immaturity by Paul.

Children as a gift from God
---------------------------

**[PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9), [PSA 127:3](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3)** *See also* [GEN 33:5](https://ref.ly/Gen33:5), [GEN 48:9](https://ref.ly/Gen48:9); [JOS 24:3](https://ref.ly/Josh24:3)

Jesus Christ welcomes children
------------------------------

**[MAT 19:13–19:14](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:14)** parallels [MRK 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:16), [LUK 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15-Luke18:17)

Those who enter the kingdom must be like children
-------------------------------------------------

### God's truth is revealed to the childlike

**[MAT 11:25](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25)** parallel [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21) *See also* [1CO 1:26–1:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:26-1Cor1:29)

### Children as people welcomed by Jesus Christ

**[MAT 18:2–18:5](https://ref.ly/Matt18:2-Matt18:5)** parallels [MRK 9:36–9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:36-Mark9:37), [LUK 9:47–9:48](https://ref.ly/Luke9:47-Luke9:48) *See also* [MAT 10:40–10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40-Matt10:42) parallel [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41); [MAT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Matt18:10), [MAT 18:14](https://ref.ly/Matt18:14)

Children as a picture of immature Christians
--------------------------------------------

**[1CO 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:20)**; **[1PE 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2-1Pet2:3)** *See also* [1CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:11); [HEB 5:13](https://ref.ly/Heb5:13)

Promises to children
--------------------

**[DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16)**; **[ACT 2:39](https://ref.ly/Acts2:39)** *See also* [PRO 8:32](https://ref.ly/Prov8:32); [EPH 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:2-Eph6:3)

Warnings relating to children
-----------------------------

### Warnings to those who cause children to stumble

**[MAT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Matt18:6)** parallel [MRK 9:42](https://ref.ly/Mark9:42)

### Warnings to Israel, the disobedient child

**[ISA 30:1](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1)** *See also* [ISA 30:9](https://ref.ly/Isa30:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 113:9; Psalms 127:3; Genesis 33:5; Genesis 48:9; Joshua 24:3; Matthew 19:13-14; Mark 10:13-16; Luke 18:15-17; Matthew 11:25; Luke 10:21; 1 Corinthians 1:26-29; Matthew 18:2-5; Mark 9:36-37; Luke 9:47-48; Matthew 10:40-42; Mark 9:41; Matthew 18:10; Matthew 18:14; 1 Corinthians 14:20; 1 Peter 2:2-3; 1 Corinthians 13:11; Hebrews 5:13; Deuteronomy 5:16; Acts 2:39; Proverbs 8:32; Ephesians 6:2-3; Matthew 18:6; Mark 9:42; Isaiah 30:1; Isaiah 30:9

## 5666: children, needs of (id: 5666)

Children need love, affection, discipline and guidance from their parents. This is shown through teaching, training, discipline, the meeting of material needs and the provision of a personal example of faith.

Teaching children
-----------------

**[DEU 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:7)** *See also* [EXO 10:2](https://ref.ly/Exod10:2), [EXO 12:26–12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:26-Exod12:27), [EXO 13:14–13:15](https://ref.ly/Exod13:14-Exod13:15); [DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9), [DEU 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Deut6:20-Deut6:21), [DEU 11:19](https://ref.ly/Deut11:19); [PRO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8); [ISA 38:19](https://ref.ly/Isa38:19); [JOL 1:3](https://ref.ly/Joel1:3)

Training children
-----------------

**[PRO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Prov22:6)**; **[EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)** *See also* [DEU 31:12–31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12-Deut31:13); [JOS 8:35](https://ref.ly/Josh8:35); [2KI 12:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:2); [PSA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Ps34:11), [PSA 78:5](https://ref.ly/Ps78:5); [PRO 3:1](https://ref.ly/Prov3:1)

Disciplining children
---------------------

**[PRO 13:24](https://ref.ly/Prov13:24)**; **[HEB 12:7–12:11](https://ref.ly/Heb12:7-Heb12:11)** *See also* [PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18), [PRO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Prov22:15), [PRO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Prov23:13), [PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15), [PRO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Prov29:17); [1TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:4), [1TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:12)

### Lack of discipline

[1SA 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:13); ; [1KI 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:6)

Good examples set for children by parents
-----------------------------------------

David: [1KI 9:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:4); ; [2CH 17:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:3); [2CH 26:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:4) *Amaziah*; [2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:5), [2TI 3:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:15)

Bad examples set for children by parents
----------------------------------------

Ahab and Jezebel: [1KI 22:52](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:52); ; [2CH 22:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:3); ; [JER 9:14](https://ref.ly/Jer9:14); [MAT 14:8](https://ref.ly/Matt14:8) *Herodias*

Provision and care for children
-------------------------------

**[MAT 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:11)** parallel [LUK 11:11–11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11-Luke11:13) *See also* [1SA 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:19); [PRO 31:15](https://ref.ly/Prov31:15), [PRO 31:21](https://ref.ly/Prov31:21); [2CO 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:14)

Love for children
-----------------

**[TIT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4)** *See also* [GEN 37:3](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3) *Jacob's special love for Joseph*[GEN 44:20](https://ref.ly/Gen44:20), [GEN 44:29–44:31](https://ref.ly/Gen44:29-Gen44:31)[2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33) *David's love for Absalom*; [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20) *the father's love for the prodigal son*

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 6:6-7; Exodus 10:2; Exodus 12:26-27; Exodus 13:14-15; Deuteronomy 4:9; Deuteronomy 6:20-21; Deuteronomy 11:19; Proverbs 1:8; Isaiah 38:19; Joel 1:3; Proverbs 22:6; Ephesians 6:4; Deuteronomy 31:12-13; Joshua 8:35; 2 Kings 12:2; Psalms 34:11; Psalms 78:5; Proverbs 3:1; Proverbs 13:24; Hebrews 12:7-11; Proverbs 19:18; Proverbs 22:15; Proverbs 23:13; Proverbs 29:15; Proverbs 29:17; 1 Timothy 3:4; 1 Timothy 3:12; 1 Samuel 3:13; 1 Kings 1:6; 1 Kings 9:4; 2 Chronicles 17:3; 2 Chronicles 26:4; 2 Timothy 1:5; 2 Timothy 3:15; 1 Kings 22:52; 2 Chronicles 22:3; Jeremiah 9:14; Matthew 14:8; Matthew 7:9-11; Luke 11:11-13; 1 Samuel 2:19; Proverbs 31:15; Proverbs 31:21; 2 Corinthians 12:14; Titus 2:4; Genesis 37:3; Genesis 44:20; Genesis 44:29-31; 2 Samuel 18:33; Luke 15:20

## 5667: children, responsibilities to God (id: 5667)

The responsibilities of children to God as their heavenly Father include honouring and obeying him.

The responsibilities of children to their heavenly Father
---------------------------------------------------------

### To honour and obey him in reverent fear

**[PSA 34:11](https://ref.ly/Ps34:11)** *See also* [PRO 2:5](https://ref.ly/Prov2:5), [PRO 9:10](https://ref.ly/Prov9:10)

### To seek wisdom

**[PRO 4:5–4:7](https://ref.ly/Prov4:5-Prov4:7)** *See also* [PRO 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:4), [PRO 8:32–8:33](https://ref.ly/Prov8:32-Prov8:33), [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1)

### To listen and learn

**[PRO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8-Prov1:9)** *See also* [PRO 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1-Prov4:4), [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)

### To praise God

**[PSA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ps8:2)** *See also* [MAT 21:15–21:16](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15-Matt21:16)

### To join in worship

**[NEH 12:43](https://ref.ly/Neh12:43)** *See also* [JOS 8:35](https://ref.ly/Josh8:35)

Relationship to Jesus Christ transcends the child\-parent relationship
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:29-Luke18:30)** parallels [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29), [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 34:11; Proverbs 2:5; Proverbs 9:10; Proverbs 4:5-7; Proverbs 1:1-4; Proverbs 8:32-33; Proverbs 10:1; Proverbs 1:8-9; Proverbs 4:1-4; Proverbs 23:22; Psalms 8:2; Matthew 21:15-16; Nehemiah 12:43; Joshua 8:35; Luke 18:29-30; Matthew 19:29; Mark 10:29-30
* **Associated Articles:** 1040: God, fatherhood of (ID: `1040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5052: responsibility, to God (ID: `5052@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8120: following Jesus Christ (ID: `8120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8444: honouring God (ID: `8444@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8460: pleasing God (ID: `8460@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8664: praise (ID: `8664@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5668: children, responsibilities to parents (id: 5668)

Children owe their parents certain important responsibilities, including the duty to honour and obey them.

The duties of children towards their parents
--------------------------------------------

### They are to honour their parents

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *See also* [PRO 17:6](https://ref.ly/Prov17:6); [MRK 7:10](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10); [EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3)

### They are to obey their parents

**[COL 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:20-Col3:21)** *See also* [EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1)

### They are to behave well

**[PRO 20:11](https://ref.ly/Prov20:11)**

Children as a blessing and source of joy to their parents
---------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 127:4–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:4-Ps127:5)**; **[PRO 23:24](https://ref.ly/Prov23:24)** *See also* [PSA 128:3](https://ref.ly/Ps128:3); [PRO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Prov15:20), [PRO 27:11](https://ref.ly/Prov27:11), [PRO 29:3](https://ref.ly/Prov29:3); [LUK 15:23–15:24](https://ref.ly/Luke15:23-Luke15:24)

Children born to barren mothers bring special joy
-------------------------------------------------

Sarah: [GEN 18:11](https://ref.ly/Gen18:11), [GEN 21:2](https://ref.ly/Gen21:2); [GEN 29:31](https://ref.ly/Gen29:31), [GEN 30:22](https://ref.ly/Gen30:22); [JDG 13:2](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2), [JDG 13:24](https://ref.ly/Judg13:24); [1SA 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:5), [1SA 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:20); [LUK 1:7](https://ref.ly/Luke1:7), [LUK 1:24](https://ref.ly/Luke1:24), [LUK 1:57](https://ref.ly/Luke1:57)

Children as a source of grief and disappointment to their parents
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 17:25](https://ref.ly/Prov17:25)** *See also* [1SA 8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:3); [PRO 17:21](https://ref.ly/Prov17:21), [PRO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Prov19:13), [PRO 19:26](https://ref.ly/Prov19:26), [PRO 28:7](https://ref.ly/Prov28:7), [PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15)

The punishment of children
--------------------------

### The punishment of wicked children

[EXO 21:15](https://ref.ly/Exod21:15); ; [LEV 20:9](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9); ; [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); ; [PRO 30:17](https://ref.ly/Prov30:17); ; [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2)

The punishment of children for the sins of their fathers
--------------------------------------------------------

[EXO 20:5–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5-Exod20:6); ; [NUM 14:18](https://ref.ly/Num14:18); ; [JER 31:29–31:30](https://ref.ly/Jer31:29-Jer31:30) *Jeremiah here stresses the importance of individual responsibility in the present crisis without denying the notion of corporate responsibility*; ; [JER 32:18](https://ref.ly/Jer32:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:12; Deuteronomy 5:16; Proverbs 17:6; Mark 7:10; Ephesians 6:1-3; Colossians 3:20-21; Ephesians 6:1; Proverbs 20:11; Psalms 127:4-5; Proverbs 23:24; Psalms 128:3; Proverbs 15:20; Proverbs 27:11; Proverbs 29:3; Luke 15:23-24; Genesis 18:11; Genesis 21:2; Genesis 29:31; Genesis 30:22; Judges 13:2; Judges 13:24; 1 Samuel 1:5; 1 Samuel 1:20; Luke 1:7; Luke 1:24; Luke 1:57; Proverbs 17:25; 1 Samuel 8:3; Proverbs 17:21; Proverbs 19:13; Proverbs 19:26; Proverbs 28:7; Proverbs 29:15; Exodus 21:15; Leviticus 20:9; Deuteronomy 21:18-21; Proverbs 30:17; 2 Timothy 3:2; Exodus 20:5-6; Numbers 14:18; Jeremiah 31:29-30; Jeremiah 32:18
* **Associated Articles:** 5225: barrenness (ID: `5225@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5663: childbirth (ID: `5663@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5685: fathers, responsibilities of (ID: `5685@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5719: mothers, responsibilities of (ID: `5719@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6160: fathers, sin of the (ID: `6160@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5669: children, examples of (id: 5669)

In Scripture specific children are marked out as belonging to the Lord. Mention of others is made in association with miracles or notable situations.

The child Jesus Christ
----------------------

**[MAT 1:23](https://ref.ly/Matt1:23)** *See also* [MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11); [LUK 2:34](https://ref.ly/Luke2:34), [LUK 2:49–2:51](https://ref.ly/Luke2:49-Luke2:51)

Other notable children
----------------------

[EXO 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Exod2:7-Exod2:8) *Moses*; ; [JDG 11:36](https://ref.ly/Judg11:36) *Jephthah's daughter*; [1SA 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:18), [1SA 2:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:26); [1SA 16:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:11), [1SA 17:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:33), [1SA 17:41–17:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:41-1Sam17:42); [2KI 5:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:2) *the young girl who helped Naaman*; ; [LUK 1:80](https://ref.ly/Luke1:80) *John the Baptist*; ; [2TI 3:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:15) *Timothy*

Children in the miracles of Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------

Jairus' daughter: [MAT 9:18](https://ref.ly/Matt9:18) parallels [MRK 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22-Mark5:23), [LUK 8:41–8:42](https://ref.ly/Luke8:41-Luke8:42); ; [MAT 9:23–9:25](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23-Matt9:25) parallels [MRK 5:38–5:43](https://ref.ly/Mark5:38-Mark5:43), [LUK 8:49–8:55](https://ref.ly/Luke8:49-Luke8:55); ; [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30) *Canaanite woman's daughter*; ; [MAT 17:14–17:18](https://ref.ly/Matt17:14-Matt17:18) parallels [MRK 9:17–9:27](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17-Mark9:27), [LUK 9:38–9:43](https://ref.ly/Luke9:38-Luke9:43) *boy with epilepsy*; ; [LUK 7:11–7:15](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11-Luke7:15) *widow's son at Nain*; ; [JHN 4:46–4:52](https://ref.ly/John4:46-John4:52) *official's son at Capernaum*; [JHN 6:9](https://ref.ly/John6:9) *the boy who gave his lunch to help feed the crowd*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 1:23; Matthew 2:11; Luke 2:34; Luke 2:49-51; Exodus 2:7-8; Judges 11:36; 1 Samuel 2:18; 1 Samuel 2:26; 1 Samuel 16:11; 1 Samuel 17:33; 1 Samuel 17:41-42; 2 Kings 5:2; Luke 1:80; 2 Timothy 3:15; Matthew 9:18; Mark 5:22-23; Luke 8:41-42; Matthew 9:23-25; Mark 5:38-43; Luke 8:49-55; Matthew 15:21-28; Mark 7:24-30; Matthew 17:14-18; Mark 9:17-27; Luke 9:38-43; Luke 7:11-15; John 4:46-52; John 6:9

## 5671: clan (id: 5671)

A social unit, larger than a family but smaller than a tribe.

Membership of a clan
--------------------

**[JDG 6:15](https://ref.ly/Judg6:15)** *See also* [LEV 25:10](https://ref.ly/Lev25:10); [JOS 7:14–7:18](https://ref.ly/Josh7:14-Josh7:18); [RUT 2:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:1); [1SA 9:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:21), [1SA 10:17–10:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:17-1Sam10:21), [1SA 18:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:18)

Marriage and the clan
---------------------

**[JDG 12:9](https://ref.ly/Judg12:9)** *See also* [GEN 24:34–24:41](https://ref.ly/Gen24:34-Gen24:41); [NUM 36:1–36:12](https://ref.ly/Num36:1-Num36:12)

Inheritance within the clan
---------------------------

**[NUM 27:8–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:8-Num27:11)**

The responsibility of the clan for protection and revenge
---------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 25:47–25:49](https://ref.ly/Lev25:47-Lev25:49)** *See also* [RUT 3:7–3:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:7-Ruth3:12), [RUT 4:1–4:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:12); [2SA 14:5–14:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:5-2Sam14:7)

The clans of Israel listed
--------------------------

[NUM 26:1–26:61](https://ref.ly/Num26:1-Num26:61)

The land allocated to the clans
-------------------------------

[JOS 13:15–13:33](https://ref.ly/Josh13:15-Josh13:33), [JOS 15:1–19:48](https://ref.ly/Josh15:1-Josh19:48)

Non\-Israelite clans
--------------------

**[GEN 10:5](https://ref.ly/Gen10:5)** *See also* [GEN 10:15–10:20](https://ref.ly/Gen10:15-Gen10:20), [GEN 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Gen10:31-Gen10:32), [GEN 36:40–36:43](https://ref.ly/Gen36:40-Gen36:43)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 6:15; Leviticus 25:10; Joshua 7:14-18; Ruth 2:1; 1 Samuel 9:21; 1 Samuel 10:17-21; 1 Samuel 18:18; Judges 12:9; Genesis 24:34-41; Numbers 36:1-12; Numbers 27:8-11; Leviticus 25:47-49; Ruth 3:7-12; Ruth 4:1-12; 2 Samuel 14:5-7; Numbers 26:1-61; Joshua 13:15-33; Joshua 15:1-19:48; Genesis 10:5; Genesis 10:15-20; Genesis 10:31-32; Genesis 36:40-43
* **Associated Articles:** 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5704: inheritance, material (ID: `5704@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7135: Israel, as the people of God (ID: `7135@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7266: tribes of Israel (ID: `7266@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7388: kinsman-redeemer (ID: `7388@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5672: concubines (id: 5672)

A woman, often a servant or slave, with whom a man had regular sexual relations, but to whom he was not married. A concubine did not have the rights of a wife and her children were not rightful heirs, though a wife might offer a servant to her husband as a concubine to have children on her behalf.

Concubines and wives
--------------------

### Concubines were distinguished from wives

**[1KI 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:3)** *Having concubines alongside wives was accepted in a polygamous society.* *See also* [JDG 8:30–8:31](https://ref.ly/Judg8:30-Judg8:31); [2SA 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:13), [2SA 19:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:5); [2CH 11:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:21); [DAN 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Dan5:2-Dan5:3)

### Concubines were sometimes called wives

**[GEN 16:3](https://ref.ly/Gen16:3)** *Hagar was Abraham's “wife” only in a secondary sense.* *See also* [GEN 25:1](https://ref.ly/Gen25:1) *Keturah was one of Abraham's concubines*; [JDG 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/Judg19:1-Judg19:4)

### Concubines did not have the rights of a wife

[GEN 21:8–21:14](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8-Gen21:14) *They were more easily dismissed*; ; [EXO 21:7–21:11](https://ref.ly/Exod21:7-Exod21:11) *They did have some legal protection.*

### Maidservants were given as concubines

**[GEN 30:3–30:6](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3-Gen30:6)** *Bilhah's sons would be regarded by Rachel as her own and would thus share the inheritance with Jacob's other sons.* *See also* [GEN 30:9](https://ref.ly/Gen30:9), [GEN 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:2); [GAL 4:22–4:23](https://ref.ly/Gal4:22-Gal4:23)

### The tension between concubines and wives

**[GEN 16:4–16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4-Gen16:6)** *Though Abraham's concubine, Hagar's status was still that of servant.* *See also* [GEN 21:9](https://ref.ly/Gen21:9); [PRO 30:23](https://ref.ly/Prov30:23); [GAL 4:29](https://ref.ly/Gal4:29)

### Children born to concubines were not rightful heirs

**[GEN 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/Gen21:10-Gen21:12)** *See also* [GEN 25:5–25:6](https://ref.ly/Gen25:5-Gen25:6); [JDG 9:18](https://ref.ly/Judg9:18); [ROM 9:7](https://ref.ly/Rom9:7); [GAL 4:30](https://ref.ly/Gal4:30); [HEB 11:18](https://ref.ly/Heb11:18)

Concubines were a symbol of possession
--------------------------------------

### By taking Jacob's concubine, Reuben laid premature claim to his father's estate

**[GEN 35:22](https://ref.ly/Gen35:22)** *See also* [GEN 49:3–49:4](https://ref.ly/Gen49:3-Gen49:4); [1CH 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1)

### Taking the concubine(s) of a king was tantamount to laying claim to the kingdom

**[2SA 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:7)** *Abner is accused here of trying to usurp Ish\-Bosheth's inheritance.* *See also* [2SA 12:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:8), [2SA 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21-2Sam16:22); [1KI 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:21-1Kgs2:22)

Further examples of concubines
------------------------------

[GEN 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Gen22:23-Gen22:24) *Nahor's concubine, Reumah*; ; [GEN 36:12](https://ref.ly/Gen36:12) *Eliphaz' concubine, Timna*; ; [JDG 19:1](https://ref.ly/Judg19:1) *a Levite's concubine*; ; [2SA 15:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:16) *David's concubines*; ; [1CH 1:32](https://ref.ly/1Chr1:32) *Abraham's concubine, Keturah*; ; [1CH 2:46–2:48](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:46-1Chr2:48) *Caleb's concubines, Ephah and Maacah*; ; [1CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:14) *Manasseh's concubine*; ; [EST 2:14](https://ref.ly/Esth2:14) *Xerxes' concubines*; ; [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23) *Belshazzar's concubines*

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 11:3; Judges 8:30-31; 2 Samuel 5:13; 2 Samuel 19:5; 2 Chronicles 11:21; Daniel 5:2-3; Genesis 16:3; Genesis 25:1; Judges 19:1-4; Genesis 21:8-14; Exodus 21:7-11; Genesis 30:3-6; Genesis 30:9; Genesis 16:1-2; Galatians 4:22-23; Genesis 16:4-6; Genesis 21:9; Proverbs 30:23; Galatians 4:29; Genesis 21:10-12; Genesis 25:5-6; Judges 9:18; Romans 9:7; Galatians 4:30; Hebrews 11:18; Genesis 35:22; Genesis 49:3-4; 1 Chronicles 5:1; 2 Samuel 3:7; 2 Samuel 12:8; 2 Samuel 16:21-22; 1 Kings 2:21-22; Genesis 22:23-24; Genesis 36:12; Judges 19:1; 2 Samuel 15:16; 1 Chronicles 1:32; 1 Chronicles 2:46-48; 1 Chronicles 7:14; Esther 2:14; Daniel 5:23
* **Associated Articles:** 5703: inheritance (ID: `5703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5717: monogamy (ID: `5717@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5732: polygamy (ID: `5732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

-------------------

------------------------------
