## 5674: daughters (id: 5674)

The female offspring of parents. Daughters lived in their father's house, under his protection, until marriage. Usually they became his heirs only if he had no sons to inherit his estate. Normally they shared in the inheritance of their husbands.

Protection of daughters
-----------------------

### Fathers were responsible for protecting their daughters

**[GEN 31:50](https://ref.ly/Gen31:50)** *See also* [GEN 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/Gen19:12-Gen19:13), [GEN 19:15–19:17](https://ref.ly/Gen19:15-Gen19:17); [NUM 30:3–30:5](https://ref.ly/Num30:3-Num30:5)

### Examples of fathers who failed to protect their daughters

**[GEN 19:8](https://ref.ly/Gen19:8)** *See also* [JDG 19:24](https://ref.ly/Judg19:24); [2SA 13:6–13:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:6-2Sam13:14)

Marriage of daughters
---------------------

### Fathers were responsible for arranging marriages for their daughters

**[GEN 34:8–34:9](https://ref.ly/Gen34:8-Gen34:9)** *See also* [GEN 29:26](https://ref.ly/Gen29:26); [JOS 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Josh15:16-Josh15:17) parallel [JDG 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Judg1:12-Judg1:13); [JDG 12:9](https://ref.ly/Judg12:9), [JDG 21:1](https://ref.ly/Judg21:1); [1SA 18:17–18:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17-1Sam18:21), [1SA 25:44](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:44)

### Fathers received a bride\-price from their daughters' husbands

**[1SA 18:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:25)** *See also* [GEN 29:16–29:27](https://ref.ly/Gen29:16-Gen29:27)

### Fathers gave a dowry to their daughters

**[1KI 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:16)** *See also* [JOS 15:17–15:19](https://ref.ly/Josh15:17-Josh15:19) parallel [JDG 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:13-Judg1:15)

### Sometimes the marriage of a daughter was the basis of a political alliance

**[1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1)** *See also* [DAN 11:6](https://ref.ly/Dan11:6), [DAN 11:17](https://ref.ly/Dan11:17)

### In cases of dispute, parents were expected to prove their daughter's virginity

**[DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21)**

Inheritance of daughters
------------------------

### If a man had no sons, the inheritance went to his daughters

**[NUM 27:1–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:11)** *See also* [JOS 17:3–17:6](https://ref.ly/Josh17:3-Josh17:6)

### Daughters who inherited their father's property were obliged to marry within his clan

**[NUM 36:1–36:12](https://ref.ly/Num36:1-Num36:12)**

### Job allocated his daughters an inheritance

**[JOB 42:13–42:15](https://ref.ly/Job42:13-Job42:15)**

Priests' daughters
------------------

**[LEV 10:14](https://ref.ly/Lev10:14)** *See also* [LEV 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:2), [LEV 21:9](https://ref.ly/Lev21:9), [LEV 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Lev22:12-Lev22:13); [NUM 18:11](https://ref.ly/Num18:11), [NUM 18:19](https://ref.ly/Num18:19); [EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25)

Slavery of daughters
--------------------

### In dire circumstances fathers sometimes sold their daughters into slavery

**[NEH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Neh5:5)** *See also* [EXO 21:7](https://ref.ly/Exod21:7)

### A slave girl married to a son was to be accorded the rights of a daughter

**[EXO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Exod21:9)**

Immorality of daughters
-----------------------

### Prostitution of daughters forbidden

**[LEV 19:29](https://ref.ly/Lev19:29)** *See also* [LEV 21:9](https://ref.ly/Lev21:9)

### Incest of Lot's daughters

**[GEN 19:30–19:38](https://ref.ly/Gen19:30-Gen19:38)**

Daughters\-in\-law
------------------

### Sexual relations with daughters\-in\-law forbidden

**[LEV 18:15](https://ref.ly/Lev18:15)** *See also* [LEV 20:12](https://ref.ly/Lev20:12); [EZK 22:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:11)

### Examples of daughters\-in\-law

**[RUT 4:14–4:15](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:14-Ruth4:15)** *See also* [GEN 38:6–38:26](https://ref.ly/Gen38:6-Gen38:26); [RUT 1:3–1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:3-Ruth1:17)

Daughters in a spiritual role
-----------------------------

**[ACT 21:9](https://ref.ly/Acts21:9)** *See also* [JOL 2:28](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28); [ACT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17)

An adopted daughter
-------------------

**[EST 2:7](https://ref.ly/Esth2:7)**

Daughter in a non\-literal sense
--------------------------------

### Of an area, town or country or its population

**[MAT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Matt21:5)** parallel [JHN 12:15](https://ref.ly/John12:15) *See also* [2KI 19:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:21); [PSA 137:8](https://ref.ly/Ps137:8); [ISA 10:30](https://ref.ly/Isa10:30), [ISA 23:12](https://ref.ly/Isa23:12), [ISA 52:2](https://ref.ly/Isa52:2); [JER 6:23](https://ref.ly/Jer6:23), [JER 48:18](https://ref.ly/Jer48:18), [JER 51:33](https://ref.ly/Jer51:33); [LAM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Lam1:15), [LAM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Lam2:10); [MIC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Mic4:8); [ZEP 3:14](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:14); [ZEC 2:7](https://ref.ly/Zech2:7), [ZEC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Zech9:9)

### Of the female inhabitants of an area, town or country

**[LUK 23:27–23:29](https://ref.ly/Luke23:27-Luke23:29)** *See also* [GEN 24:3](https://ref.ly/Gen24:3), [GEN 24:13](https://ref.ly/Gen24:13); [2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24); [SNG 3:5](https://ref.ly/Song3:5), [SNG 5:8](https://ref.ly/Song5:8); [ISA 32:9–32:11](https://ref.ly/Isa32:9-Isa32:11); [EZK 13:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:17), [EZK 32:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:18)

### Of the female descendants

**[LUK 13:16](https://ref.ly/Luke13:16)** *See also* [1PE 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:5-1Pet3:6)

### As an honorific term

**[MAT 9:22](https://ref.ly/Matt9:22)** parallels [MRK 5:34](https://ref.ly/Mark5:34), [LUK 8:48](https://ref.ly/Luke8:48)

Daughters in a theological sense
--------------------------------

### God's people are his sons and daughters

**[2CO 6:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:18)** *See also* [JER 14:17](https://ref.ly/Jer14:17), [JER 31:21–31:22](https://ref.ly/Jer31:21-Jer31:22)

### Family relationships must be subservient to one's relationship with God

**[MAT 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37)** *See also* [MAT 10:35](https://ref.ly/Matt10:35) parallel [LUK 12:53](https://ref.ly/Luke12:53)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 31:50; Genesis 19:12-13; Genesis 19:15-17; Numbers 30:3-5; Genesis 19:8; Judges 19:24; 2 Samuel 13:6-14; Genesis 34:8-9; Genesis 29:26; Joshua 15:16-17; Judges 1:12-13; Judges 12:9; Judges 21:1; 1 Samuel 18:17-21; 1 Samuel 25:44; 1 Samuel 18:25; Genesis 29:16-27; 1 Kings 9:16; Joshua 15:17-19; Judges 1:13-15; 1 Kings 3:1; Daniel 11:6; Daniel 11:17; Deuteronomy 22:13-21; Numbers 27:1-11; Joshua 17:3-6; Numbers 36:1-12; Job 42:13-15; Leviticus 10:14; Leviticus 21:1-2; Leviticus 21:9; Leviticus 22:12-13; Numbers 18:11; Numbers 18:19; Ezekiel 44:25; Nehemiah 5:5; Exodus 21:7; Exodus 21:9; Leviticus 19:29; Genesis 19:30-38; Leviticus 18:15; Leviticus 20:12; Ezekiel 22:11; Ruth 4:14-15; Genesis 38:6-26; Ruth 1:3-17; Acts 21:9; Joel 2:28; Acts 2:17; Esther 2:7; Matthew 21:5; John 12:15; 2 Kings 19:21; Psalms 137:8; Isaiah 10:30; Isaiah 23:12; Isaiah 52:2; Jeremiah 6:23; Jeremiah 48:18; Jeremiah 51:33; Lamentations 1:15; Lamentations 2:10; Micah 4:8; Zephaniah 3:14; Zechariah 2:7; Zechariah 9:9; Luke 23:27-29; Genesis 24:3; Genesis 24:13; 2 Samuel 1:24; Song of Songs 3:5; Song of Songs 5:8; Isaiah 32:9-11; Ezekiel 13:17; Ezekiel 32:18; Luke 13:16; 1 Peter 3:5-6; Matthew 9:22; Mark 5:34; Luke 8:48; 2 Corinthians 6:18; Jeremiah 14:17; Jeremiah 31:21-22; Matthew 10:37; Matthew 10:35; Luke 12:53
* **Associated Articles:** 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5679: dowry (ID: `5679@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5684: fathers (ID: `5684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5695: girls (ID: `5695@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5701: heir (ID: `5701@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5724: offspring (ID: `5724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5737: sisters (ID: `5737@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5738: sons (ID: `5738@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6239: prostitution (ID: `6239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7115: children of God (ID: `7115@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5675: divorce (id: 5675)

The legal dissolution of a marriage. Though not part of God's original intention for marriage, divorce is permitted under certain circumstances, especially marital unfaithfulness. In general, properly divorced parties are free to remarry.

* **Associated Articles:** 5676: divorce, in OT (ID: `5676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5677: divorce, amongst believers (ID: `5677@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5676: divorce, in OT (id: 5676)

Usually initiated by the husband who issued his wife with a certificate of divorce and sent her from his home, divorce broke the marriage bond and allowed parties to remarry. It was not part of God's original purpose for marriage, and is permitted only because of human sinfulness. God is depicted as taking divorce proceedings against adulterous Israel.

The nature of divorce
---------------------

### Divorce dissolves a marriage

**[MRK 10:4](https://ref.ly/Mark10:4)** parallel [MAT 19:7](https://ref.ly/Matt19:7) *The certificate of divorce was a legal document ending the marriage.* *See also* [LEV 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Lev22:12-Lev22:13) *Divorce ends the restrictions on a priest's daughter which were the result of her marriage*; [NUM 30:9](https://ref.ly/Num30:9) *Following divorce a husband is no longer responsible for his wife*; [DEU 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1-Deut24:2) *Divorce properly entered into allows the parties to remarry*; [HOS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2); [MAT 5:31](https://ref.ly/Matt5:31)

### Divorce was not God's original intention

**[MAL 2:10–2:16](https://ref.ly/Mal2:10-Mal2:16)** *God's displeasure at those of his people who divorced their Jewish wives to marry foreigners, thus breaking faith with their partners and with God*; **[MAT 19:4–19:8](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:8)** parallel [MRK 10:5–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:5-Mark10:9) *The Law of Moses did not command divorce, but regulated an existing practice, particularly to protect the wife.* *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27), [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24)

Circumstances permitting divorce
--------------------------------

### Displeasure

**[DEU 24:1](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1)** *See also* [DEU 21:13–21:14](https://ref.ly/Deut21:13-Deut21:14); [MAT 19:3](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3)

### Returning exiles were required to divorce their foreign wives

**[EZR 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:10-Ezra10:11)** *See also* [EZR 10:2–10:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:2-Ezra10:3)

Circumstances where divorce was not allowed
-------------------------------------------

[DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19) *where a husband makes false accusations about his wife's virginity*; ; [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29) *where the marriage is contracted following the rape of a virgin*

Restrictions on remarriage after divorce
----------------------------------------

### Former partners who marry others

**[DEU 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Deut24:3-Deut24:4)** *See also* [JER 3:1](https://ref.ly/Jer3:1)

### Priests could not marry divorcees

[LEV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Lev21:7), [LEV 21:14](https://ref.ly/Lev21:14); ; [EZK 44:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:22)

The divorce between God and Israel
----------------------------------

### It was due to Israel's unfaithfulness

**[JER 3:6–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jer3:6-Jer3:10)** *See also* [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1); [JER 31:32](https://ref.ly/Jer31:32)

### God's desire for reconciliation

**[ISA 54:4–54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:4-Isa54:8)** *See also* [ISA 62:4–62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:4-Isa62:5); [EZK 16:60–16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:60-Ezek16:63); [HOS 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14-Hos2:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 10:4; Matthew 19:7; Leviticus 22:12-13; Numbers 30:9; Deuteronomy 24:1-2; Hosea 2:2; Matthew 5:31; Malachi 2:10-16; Matthew 19:4-8; Mark 10:5-9; Genesis 1:27; Genesis 2:24; Deuteronomy 24:1; Deuteronomy 21:13-14; Matthew 19:3; Ezra 10:10-11; Ezra 10:2-3; Deuteronomy 22:13-19; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; Deuteronomy 24:3-4; Jeremiah 3:1; Leviticus 21:7; Leviticus 21:14; Ezekiel 44:22; Jeremiah 3:6-10; Isaiah 50:1; Jeremiah 31:32; Isaiah 54:4-8; Isaiah 62:4-5; Ezekiel 16:60-63; Hosea 2:14-16
* **Associated Articles:** 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5662: certificate of divorce (ID: `5662@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5740: virgin (ID: `5740@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6243: adultery, spiritual (ID: `6243@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8248: faithfulness (ID: `8248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8839: unfaithfulness (ID: `8839@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5677: divorce, amongst believers (id: 5677)

Jesus Christ challenged the liberal approach to divorce taken by some rabbis of his day, and upheld the idea of marriage as a lifelong commitment. Remarriage after divorce is generally classed as adultery and, where possible, divorced parties should seek reconciliation. There are circumstances, however, where remarriage is permissible.

The liberal attitude to divorce
-------------------------------

**[MAT 19:3](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3)** *See also* [DEU 21:14](https://ref.ly/Deut21:14), [DEU 24:1](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1)

The undesirability of divorce
-----------------------------

### God's plan for marriage

**[MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6)** parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9) *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27), [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24); [MAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15); [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

### Divorce is permitted because of human sinfulness

**[MAT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Matt19:8)** parallel [MRK 10:5](https://ref.ly/Mark10:5)

### Christians should not seek divorce

**[1CO 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:10)** *This command, echoing Jesus Christ's words, applies where both parties are believers.* *See also* [1CO 7:12–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:12-1Cor7:14)

Remarriage after divorce
------------------------

### Believers should seek reconciliation

**[1CO 7:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:11)** *See also* [DEU 24:2–24:4](https://ref.ly/Deut24:2-Deut24:4) *Marriage to another after divorce prevents reconciliation.*

### Remarriage after divorce is classed as adultery

**[LUK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Luke16:18)** parallel [MRK 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Mark10:11-Mark10:12) *This is a general rule, though there may be exceptions (see below).* *See also* [ROM 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Rom7:2-Rom7:3); [1CO 7:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:39)

### Remarriage may be permissible

[1CO 7:27–7:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:27-1Cor7:28) *“unmarried” means literally “freed from a wife” and thus may be applied to those widowed or properly divorced. It is no sin for them to (re)marry.*

### Possible grounds for divorce and remarriage

**[MAT 19:9](https://ref.ly/Matt19:9)**; **[1CO 7:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:15)** *See also* [JER 3:8](https://ref.ly/Jer3:8) *God divorced Israel because of her adultery*; [MAT 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:19), [MAT 5:32](https://ref.ly/Matt5:32)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 19:3; Deuteronomy 21:14; Deuteronomy 24:1; Matthew 19:4-6; Mark 10:6-9; Genesis 1:27; Genesis 2:24; Malachi 2:15; Ephesians 5:31; Matthew 19:8; Mark 10:5; 1 Corinthians 7:10; 1 Corinthians 7:12-14; 1 Corinthians 7:11; Deuteronomy 24:2-4; Luke 16:18; Mark 10:11-12; Romans 7:2-3; 1 Corinthians 7:39; 1 Corinthians 7:27-28; Matthew 19:9; 1 Corinthians 7:15; Jeremiah 3:8; Matthew 1:18-19; Matthew 5:32
* **Associated Articles:** 6242: adultery (ID: `6242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6658: freedom (ID: `6658@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6718: reconciliation, between believers (ID: `6718@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5679: dowry (id: 5679)

A present given by a woman's father on her marriage. At betrothal the bridegroom gave a compensatory gift, known as the bride\-price, to the woman's family.

The bride\-price
----------------

**[EXO 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16-Exod22:17)** *See also* [GEN 34:12](https://ref.ly/Gen34:12)

### Usually it consisted of payment in kind

**[GEN 24:53](https://ref.ly/Gen24:53)** *See also* [GEN 29:16–29:28](https://ref.ly/Gen29:16-Gen29:28), [GEN 31:41](https://ref.ly/Gen31:41); [1SA 18:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:25); [2SA 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:14)

The dowry given by the bride's father
-------------------------------------

**[1KI 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:16)** *See also* [GEN 24:59–24:61](https://ref.ly/Gen24:59-Gen24:61), [GEN 29:24](https://ref.ly/Gen29:24), [GEN 29:29](https://ref.ly/Gen29:29); [JDG 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:14-Judg1:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 22:16-17; Genesis 34:12; Genesis 24:53; Genesis 29:16-28; Genesis 31:41; 1 Samuel 18:25; 2 Samuel 3:14; 1 Kings 9:16; Genesis 24:59-61; Genesis 29:24; Genesis 29:29; Judges 1:14-15
* **Associated Articles:** 5325: gifts (ID: `5325@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5654: betrothal (ID: `5654@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5659: bride (ID: `5659@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5660: bridegroom (ID: `5660@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5742: wedding (ID: `5742@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5680: family (id: 5680)

People who are linked by marriage or physical descent. The western idea of a “nuclear” family represents a narrow definition of the scope of the family. The ancient Near East had a broader and more extended understanding of family, which is often reflected in the Scriptural material.

* **Associated Articles:** 5681: family, nature of (ID: `5681@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5682: family, significance of (ID: `5682@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5681: family, nature of (id: 5681)

A unit ordained by God for the comfort and protection of its members. It is usually a group of relatives, but the people of God are also seen as a family.

The scope of the family in Scripture
------------------------------------

### The family unit

**[GEN 43:7](https://ref.ly/Gen43:7)** *See also* [EXO 1:1](https://ref.ly/Exod1:1), [EXO 12:3](https://ref.ly/Exod12:3); [JDG 11:2](https://ref.ly/Judg11:2); [1SA 1:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:21), [1SA 27:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:3); [2SA 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:3); [1KI 17:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:15); [1CH 13:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:14) *The family of Obed\-Edom is blessed by the presence of the ark*; [PRO 27:27](https://ref.ly/Prov27:27), [PRO 31:15](https://ref.ly/Prov31:15) *The wife of noble character provides for her family*; [MRK 3:21](https://ref.ly/Mark3:21); [LUK 12:52](https://ref.ly/Luke12:52); [ACT 16:33–16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:33-Acts16:34) *The Philippian jailer and his family are baptised*; [1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8)

### The extended family or clan

**[GEN 24:37–24:38](https://ref.ly/Gen24:37-Gen24:38)** *See also* [GEN 24:40](https://ref.ly/Gen24:40), [GEN 46:27](https://ref.ly/Gen46:27), [GEN 50:22](https://ref.ly/Gen50:22); [NUM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Num2:2), [NUM 18:1](https://ref.ly/Num18:1) *the priesthood of Aaron and his family*; [DEU 25:5](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5) *Levirate marriage provides for the continuity of the family*; [JOS 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Josh2:17-Josh2:18), [JOS 6:23](https://ref.ly/Josh6:23), [JOS 6:25](https://ref.ly/Josh6:25); [1SA 22:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:22); [1KI 14:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:14); [2KI 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:1) parallel [2CH 22:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:10) *Athaliah destroys the royal family of Judah*; [EZR 10:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:16); [JER 35:3](https://ref.ly/Jer35:3), [JER 38:17](https://ref.ly/Jer38:17); [DAN 1:3](https://ref.ly/Dan1:3)

### The descendants of any individual

**[JHN 7:42](https://ref.ly/John7:42)** *See also* [DEU 25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:10); [JOS 13:29](https://ref.ly/Josh13:29); [1SA 2:31–2:32](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:31-1Sam2:32), [1SA 2:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:36), [1SA 3:11–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:11-1Sam3:13); [DAN 11:7](https://ref.ly/Dan11:7)

### The people of God

**[HEB 2:10–2:15](https://ref.ly/Heb2:10-Heb2:15)** *See also* [PSA 22:22](https://ref.ly/Ps22:22); [AMO 3:1](https://ref.ly/Amos3:1); [GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10); [1PE 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:17)

Marriage obligations and restrictions within the family
-------------------------------------------------------

### Marriage within the wider family circle was preferable in early times

**[JDG 14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:3)** *See also* [GEN 24:1–24:9](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1-Gen24:9), [GEN 24:37](https://ref.ly/Gen24:37), [GEN 24:40](https://ref.ly/Gen24:40), [GEN 27:46–28:2](https://ref.ly/Gen27:46-Gen28:2), [GEN 28:6–28:9](https://ref.ly/Gen28:6-Gen28:9); [EXO 2:1](https://ref.ly/Exod2:1)

### Incestuous marriage was forbidden

**[LEV 18:6–18:18](https://ref.ly/Lev18:6-Lev18:18)** *See also* [LEV 20:17](https://ref.ly/Lev20:17), [LEV 20:19–20:21](https://ref.ly/Lev20:19-Lev20:21); [DEU 27:20–27:23](https://ref.ly/Deut27:20-Deut27:23); [MAT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3) parallel [MRK 6:17](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17); [1CO 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:5)

### The obligation to marry the widow of a dead brother

**[DEU 25:5–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:10)** *See also* [GEN 38:8–38:10](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8-Gen38:10); [RUT 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:19-Ruth2:20), [RUT 4:1–4:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:10); [MAT 22:23–22:32](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23-Matt22:32) parallels [MRK 12:18–12:27](https://ref.ly/Mark12:18-Mark12:27), [LUK 20:27–20:38](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27-Luke20:38)

Laws of inheritance kept property within the family
---------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 27:1–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:11)** *See also* [LEV 25:25–25:28](https://ref.ly/Lev25:25-Lev25:28); [JOB 42:13–42:15](https://ref.ly/Job42:13-Job42:15)

Examples of family solidarity
-----------------------------

[GEN 14:8–14:16](https://ref.ly/Gen14:8-Gen14:16) *Abraham and Lot*; ; [2SA 18:31–18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:31-2Sam18:33) *David and Absalom*; ; [RUT 1:11–1:18](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:11-Ruth1:18) *Naomi and Ruth*; ; [NEH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Neh4:13); ; [ACT 23:12–23:24](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12-Acts23:24); ; [1TI 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:3-1Tim5:4) *Family solidarity is encouraged.*

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 43:7; Exodus 1:1; Exodus 12:3; Judges 11:2; 1 Samuel 1:21; 1 Samuel 27:3; 2 Samuel 2:3; 1 Kings 17:15; 1 Chronicles 13:14; Proverbs 27:27; Proverbs 31:15; Mark 3:21; Luke 12:52; Acts 16:33-34; 1 Timothy 5:8; Genesis 24:37-38; Genesis 24:40; Genesis 46:27; Genesis 50:22; Numbers 2:2; Numbers 18:1; Deuteronomy 25:5; Joshua 2:17-18; Joshua 6:23; Joshua 6:25; 1 Samuel 22:22; 1 Kings 14:14; 2 Kings 11:1; 2 Chronicles 22:10; Ezra 10:16; Jeremiah 35:3; Jeremiah 38:17; Daniel 1:3; John 7:42; Deuteronomy 25:10; Joshua 13:29; 1 Samuel 2:31-32; 1 Samuel 2:36; 1 Samuel 3:11-13; Daniel 11:7; Hebrews 2:10-15; Psalms 22:22; Amos 3:1; Galatians 6:10; 1 Peter 4:17; Judges 14:3; Genesis 24:1-9; Genesis 24:37; Genesis 27:46-28:2; Genesis 28:6-9; Exodus 2:1; Leviticus 18:6-18; Leviticus 20:17; Leviticus 20:19-21; Deuteronomy 27:20-23; Matthew 14:3; Mark 6:17; 1 Corinthians 5:1-5; Deuteronomy 25:5-10; Genesis 38:8-10; Ruth 2:19-20; Ruth 4:1-10; Matthew 22:23-32; Mark 12:18-27; Luke 20:27-38; Numbers 27:1-11; Leviticus 25:25-28; Job 42:13-15; Genesis 14:8-16; 2 Samuel 18:31-33; Ruth 1:11-18; Nehemiah 4:13; Acts 23:12-24; 1 Timothy 5:3-4
* **Associated Articles:** 2535: Jesus Christ, family of (ID: `2535@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5671: clan (ID: `5671@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5688: firstborn (ID: `5688@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5701: heir (ID: `5701@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5703: inheritance (ID: `5703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5717: monogamy (ID: `5717@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5732: polygamy (ID: `5732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5736: singleness (ID: `5736@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6608: adoption (ID: `6608@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7140: people of God (ID: `7140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7388: kinsman-redeemer (ID: `7388@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5682: family, significance of (id: 5682)

God created the family and is deeply concerned for its welfare. Scripture uses family metaphors to describe the relationship between God and his people.

God created the family
----------------------

**[PSA 68:6](https://ref.ly/Ps68:6)** *See also* [GEN 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26-Gen1:27), [GEN 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23-Gen2:24)

God cares for the families of those who honour him
--------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Gen7:1)** *See also* [GEN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Gen6:18) parallel [DEU 5:8–5:10](https://ref.ly/Deut5:8-Deut5:10); [2SA 6:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:11); [JER 35:18–35:19](https://ref.ly/Jer35:18-Jer35:19), [JER 38:17–38:18](https://ref.ly/Jer38:17-Jer38:18)

God judges families who disobey him
-----------------------------------

**[EXO 20:4–20:5](https://ref.ly/Exod20:4-Exod20:5)** *See also* [NUM 16:23–16:32](https://ref.ly/Num16:23-Num16:32); [DEU 11:6](https://ref.ly/Deut11:6); [JOS 7:24–7:26](https://ref.ly/Josh7:24-Josh7:26); [1SA 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:12-1Sam3:14); [2SA 24:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:17)

Families in Israel
------------------

### The extended family was the basic social unit

**[NEH 7:5](https://ref.ly/Neh7:5)** *See also* [LEV 25:10](https://ref.ly/Lev25:10), [LEV 27:16](https://ref.ly/Lev27:16); [NUM 2:32](https://ref.ly/Num2:32), [NUM 4:40](https://ref.ly/Num4:40)

### Family heads were community leaders

**[NUM 36:1](https://ref.ly/Num36:1)** *See also* [NUM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Num1:4), [NUM 31:26](https://ref.ly/Num31:26); [1KI 8:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:1); [2CH 1:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:2)

### Families had religious obligations

**[DEU 12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:7)** *See also* [EXO 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/Exod12:22-Exod12:23); [DEU 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11-Deut12:12); [1SA 1:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:21)

### Families were to teach their children God's law

**[PSA 78:5–78:7](https://ref.ly/Ps78:5-Ps78:7)** *See also* [GEN 18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:19); [EXO 12:26–12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:26-Exod12:27); [DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9), [DEU 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:7); [PRO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8-Prov1:9)

The relationship between God and his people described in family metaphors
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

### They are his family

**[GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10)** *See also* [AMO 3:1](https://ref.ly/Amos3:1); [EPH 3:15](https://ref.ly/Eph3:15); [1PE 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:17)

### They are his children

**[JHN 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:12-John1:13)** *See also* [DEU 14:1](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1); [ISA 63:16](https://ref.ly/Isa63:16); [HOS 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1-Hos11:4); [ROM 8:15–8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:15-Rom8:17); [GAL 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:7); [EPH 1:5](https://ref.ly/Eph1:5)

### They are brothers and sisters in God's family

**[HEB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Heb2:11)** *See also* [PSA 22:22](https://ref.ly/Ps22:22); [ISA 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Isa8:17-Isa8:18); [MAT 12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:50) parallel [LUK 8:21](https://ref.ly/Luke8:21); [MRK 3:31–3:34](https://ref.ly/Mark3:31-Mark3:34); [HEB 2:12–2:17](https://ref.ly/Heb2:12-Heb2:17)

The salvation of families
-------------------------

**[ACT 16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:34)** *See also* [ACT 11:14](https://ref.ly/Acts11:14), [ACT 18:8](https://ref.ly/Acts18:8); [1CO 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:16)

Good family management as a qualification for church office
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[1TI 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:4-1Tim3:5)** *See also* [1TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:12)

Christians are to support their families
----------------------------------------

**[1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8)** *See also* [1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4)

The urgent demands of the kingdom of God should take priority over family responsibilities
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5684: fathers (id: 5684)

The male parent of children. Scripture indicates the role and responsibilities of fathers, and shows how God can be thought of as a loving father, who directs and guides his children.

* **Associated Articles:** 5685: fathers, responsibilities of (ID: `5685@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5686: fathers, examples of (ID: `5686@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5685: fathers, responsibilities of (id: 5685)

Fathers have a responsibility to love, instruct and discipline their children in the ways of the Lord. In return they are to be honoured and obeyed by their children.

The role of fathers
-------------------

### To love and care for their children

**[PSA 103:13](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13)** *See also* [DEU 1:31](https://ref.ly/Deut1:31); [JOB 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Job1:4-Job1:5); [MAT 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:11) parallel [LUK 11:11–11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11-Luke11:13); [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

### To discipline their children

**[PRO 13:24](https://ref.ly/Prov13:24)** *See also* [DEU 8:5](https://ref.ly/Deut8:5), [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); [PRO 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:11-Prov3:12), [PRO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Prov15:5), [PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18), [PRO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Prov22:15), [PRO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Prov23:13), [PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15); [1TI 3:2–3:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:5), [1TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:12); [HEB 12:7–12:11](https://ref.ly/Heb12:7-Heb12:11)

### To instruct their children

**[PSA 78:2–78:8](https://ref.ly/Ps78:2-Ps78:8)** *See also* [DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9), [DEU 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Deut6:6-Deut6:7), [DEU 6:20–6:24](https://ref.ly/Deut6:20-Deut6:24), [DEU 11:18–11:21](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18-Deut11:21), [DEU 31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:13); [PRO 13:1](https://ref.ly/Prov13:1), [PRO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Prov22:6); [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)

The rights of fathers
---------------------

### To be honoured by their children

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *See also* [LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3); [MAT 15:4](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4) parallel [MRK 7:10](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10); [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17); [MAT 19:17–19:19](https://ref.ly/Matt19:17-Matt19:19) parallels [MRK 10:18–10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:18-Mark10:19), [LUK 18:19–18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:19-Luke18:20); [EPH 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:2-Eph6:3)

### To be obeyed by their children

**[COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20)** *See also* [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); [PRO 6:20](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20), [PRO 8:32](https://ref.ly/Prov8:32); [LUK 2:51](https://ref.ly/Luke2:51); [EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1)

### To be heeded by their children

**[PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)** *See also* [PRO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8), [PRO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1), [PRO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Prov4:10), [PRO 13:1](https://ref.ly/Prov13:1)

The joy of fathers
------------------

**[PRO 23:24](https://ref.ly/Prov23:24)** *See also* [PSA 127:3–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:5), [PSA 128:3–128:4](https://ref.ly/Ps128:3-Ps128:4); [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1), [PRO 28:7](https://ref.ly/Prov28:7), [PRO 29:3](https://ref.ly/Prov29:3)

The sorrow of fathers
---------------------

**[PRO 17:21](https://ref.ly/Prov17:21)** *See also* [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1), [PRO 17:25](https://ref.ly/Prov17:25), [PRO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Prov19:13)

The roles of fathers\-in\-law
-----------------------------

[GEN 29:21–29:23](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21-Gen29:23), [GEN 31:39–31:42](https://ref.ly/Gen31:39-Gen31:42); ; [EXO 3:1](https://ref.ly/Exod3:1), [EXO 18:14–18:27](https://ref.ly/Exod18:14-Exod18:27); ; [JDG 19:3–19:9](https://ref.ly/Judg19:3-Judg19:9); ; [1SA 4:19–4:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:19-1Sam4:21); ; [JHN 18:13](https://ref.ly/John18:13)

Children's responsibility to honour their parents
-------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *“Honour” implies respect, obedience and care for the parents' needs.* *See also* [LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3); [MAT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Matt19:19) parallels [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19), [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20); [EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3)

Penalties for disobeying parents
--------------------------------

[EXO 21:15](https://ref.ly/Exod21:15), [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17); ; [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); ; [PRO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Prov20:20); ; [MAT 15:4–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4-Matt15:6); ; [MRK 7:10–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10-Mark7:13)

The ministry of Jesus Christ may produce conflict within families
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 12:53](https://ref.ly/Luke12:53)** parallel [MAT 10:35–10:36](https://ref.ly/Matt10:35-Matt10:36)

Following Jesus Christ involves a commitment to him even deeper than love of one's parents
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37)** *See also* [LUK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Luke14:26)

## 5686: fathers, examples of (id: 5686)

The ideal father, from a biblical perspective, is one who loves God, is obedient to him and reflects this in his daily living and in the care and upbringing of his children.

Fathers who followed divine principles within their families
------------------------------------------------------------

### Abraham

**[GEN 18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:19)** *See also* [GEN 17:1–17:7](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1-Gen17:7), [GEN 17:18–17:20](https://ref.ly/Gen17:18-Gen17:20), [GEN 21:1–21:5](https://ref.ly/Gen21:1-Gen21:5), [GEN 21:8–21:13](https://ref.ly/Gen21:8-Gen21:13), [GEN 22:1–22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:18)

**[LUK 1:5–1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:5-Luke1:20)** *See also* [LUK 1:62–1:80](https://ref.ly/Luke1:62-Luke1:80)

### The father of the prodigal

[LUK 15:11–15:31](https://ref.ly/Luke15:11-Luke15:31)

-------------------------

### Through favouritism

**[GEN 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3-Gen37:4)** *See also* [GEN 25:28](https://ref.ly/Gen25:28)

### Through lack of discipline

**[1SA 2:22–2:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:22-1Sam2:25)** *See also* [1SA 2:27–2:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:27-1Sam2:36), [1SA 3:11–3:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:11-1Sam3:18)

## 5688: firstborn (id: 5688)

The firstborn male in a family; as in many cultures, in an Israelite family the eldest son had unique privileges, including the right of inheritance. The title “firstborn” was therefore a title of honour.

The privileges of the firstborn
-------------------------------

### The place of honour in the family

**[GEN 10:15](https://ref.ly/Gen10:15)** *In this and other lists, the firstborn is specially noted*; **[GEN 43:33](https://ref.ly/Gen43:33)** *See also* [GEN 22:21](https://ref.ly/Gen22:21), [GEN 25:13](https://ref.ly/Gen25:13), [GEN 35:23](https://ref.ly/Gen35:23), [GEN 36:15](https://ref.ly/Gen36:15), [GEN 46:8](https://ref.ly/Gen46:8); [DEU 33:17](https://ref.ly/Deut33:17)

### The right of inheritance

[DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17); ; [2CH 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:3)

### The right to a blessing

[GEN 27:19](https://ref.ly/Gen27:19), [GEN 27:30–27:39](https://ref.ly/Gen27:30-Gen27:39)

### The sin of despising these privileges

**[GEN 25:31–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:31-Gen25:34)** *See also* [HEB 12:16–12:17](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16-Heb12:17)

### These privileges could be transferred

**[GEN 48:14](https://ref.ly/Gen48:14)** *See also* [GEN 48:18–48:19](https://ref.ly/Gen48:18-Gen48:19), [GEN 49:3–49:4](https://ref.ly/Gen49:3-Gen49:4); [1CH 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:3), [1CH 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:1-1Chr5:2), [1CH 26:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr26:10)

The death of the firstborn a great tragedy
------------------------------------------

**[EXO 4:23](https://ref.ly/Exod4:23)** *See also* [EXO 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Exod11:4-Exod11:6), [EXO 12:12](https://ref.ly/Exod12:12), [EXO 12:29](https://ref.ly/Exod12:29); [NUM 33:4](https://ref.ly/Num33:4); [JOS 6:26](https://ref.ly/Josh6:26); [1KI 16:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:34); [PSA 78:51](https://ref.ly/Ps78:51), [PSA 105:36](https://ref.ly/Ps105:36), [PSA 135:8](https://ref.ly/Ps135:8), [PSA 136:10](https://ref.ly/Ps136:10); [HEB 11:28](https://ref.ly/Heb11:28)

### Repentance likened to grief over the death of a firstborn

[ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10)

God's claim upon the firstborn
------------------------------

### In ancient times

**[GEN 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4)** *See also* [GEN 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:2), [GEN 22:12](https://ref.ly/Gen22:12)

### After the exodus

**[EXO 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Exod13:1-Exod13:2)** *See also* [EXO 13:11–13:15](https://ref.ly/Exod13:11-Exod13:15), [EXO 22:29](https://ref.ly/Exod22:29), [EXO 34:19–34:20](https://ref.ly/Exod34:19-Exod34:20); [NUM 18:15](https://ref.ly/Num18:15), [NUM 18:17](https://ref.ly/Num18:17); [DEU 14:23](https://ref.ly/Deut14:23), [DEU 15:19](https://ref.ly/Deut15:19); [NEH 10:36](https://ref.ly/Neh10:36)

### The offering of a firstborn insufficient to redeem from the consequences of sin

[MIC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Mic6:7)

----------------------------------

[2CH 28:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:3); ; [EZK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:26)

Jesus Christ the \`\`firstborn''
--------------------------------

### The firstborn of Mary

[LUK 2:7](https://ref.ly/Luke2:7), [LUK 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22-Luke2:23)

### The firstborn of God

**[ROM 8:29](https://ref.ly/Rom8:29)** *“firstborn” here means that Jesus Christ is above creation, rather than part of it. The title “firstborn” has strong Messianic associations*; **[COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15)**; **[HEB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Heb1:6)**

## 5689: friendlessness (id: 5689)

The bitter experience of the absence of friendship, leading to a sense of isolation and despair. It is often blamed on the faithlessness of others or even on God himself.

The experience of friendlessness
--------------------------------

### The darkness of being alone

**[PSA 88:18](https://ref.ly/Ps88:18)** *See also* [PSA 88:8](https://ref.ly/Ps88:8), [PSA 102:7](https://ref.ly/Ps102:7), [PSA 142:4](https://ref.ly/Ps142:4)

### Contempt and rejection

**[PSA 31:11–31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:11-Ps31:12)** *See also* [PSA 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ps38:11)

### Hatred and loathing

-----------------------------------------------------------

## 5690: friends (id: 5690)

Those to whom one is close. Scripture stresses that friendship is often but not always a positive thing: where good friends can be invaluable in the life of faith, bad friends can be obstacles to the faith of believers, or even lead them astray totally.

* **Associated Articles:** 5691: friends, good (ID: `5691@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5692: friends, bad (ID: `5692@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5691: friends, good (id: 5691)

Those whose commitment from the heart is shown in practical, often sacrificial, care, love and service. They are a source of encouragement, sympathy, comfort and support in time of need. Such relationships are to be cultivated, especially among believers.

Examples of good friends
------------------------

### David and Jonathan

**[1SA 18:1–18:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:1-1Sam18:3)** *See also* [1SA 20:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:17), [1SA 20:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:42); [2SA 1:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:26)

### Job's friends

**[JOB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Job2:11)**

### Ruth and Naomi

**[RUT 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:16-Ruth1:17)**

### Paul's friends in ministry

**[ROM 16:3–16:5](https://ref.ly/Rom16:3-Rom16:5)** *See also* [2CO 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:12-2Cor2:13) *Titus*; [PHP 2:25](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25) *Epaphroditus*; [COL 4:7](https://ref.ly/Col4:7) *Tychicus*; [COL 4:14](https://ref.ly/Col4:14) *Luke*; [2TI 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:2-2Tim1:4) *Timothy*; [PHM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:1) *Philemon*

### Other examples

[1SA 22:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:23) *David and Abiathar*; ; [2SA 10:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:2) *David and Nahash*; ; [2SA 15:19–15:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:19-2Sam15:21) *David and Ittai*; ; [2SA 15:32–15:37](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:32-2Sam15:37) *David and Hushai*; ; [1KI 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:1) *David and Hiram*; ; [2KI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:2) *Elijah and Elisha*; ; [2KI 10:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:15) *Jehonadab and Jehu*; ; [MRK 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/Mark2:3-Mark2:4) *the friends who brought the paralysed man to Jesus Christ*

The benefits brought by good friends
------------------------------------

### Comfort and sympathy

**[JOB 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Job2:11-Job2:13)** *See also* [PRO 17:17](https://ref.ly/Prov17:17)

### Support and encouragement

**[1SA 23:16–23:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:16-1Sam23:17)**; **[PRO 18:24](https://ref.ly/Prov18:24)**; **[ECC 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:9-Eccl4:10)** *See also* [PRO 27:10](https://ref.ly/Prov27:10); [ECC 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:11-Eccl4:12); [AMO 3:3](https://ref.ly/Amos3:3); [MAT 27:55–27:56](https://ref.ly/Matt27:55-Matt27:56), [MAT 28:1](https://ref.ly/Matt28:1); [ROM 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Rom1:11-Rom1:12)

### Sacrificial service

**[JHN 15:13](https://ref.ly/John15:13)** *See also* [ROM 5:7](https://ref.ly/Rom5:7)

### Shared joy and sorrow

[LUK 15:5](https://ref.ly/Luke15:5); ; [ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15)

### Loving rebuke

[PRO 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6), [PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9); ; [EPH 4:15](https://ref.ly/Eph4:15)

### Encouragement in good works

[ROM 14:13](https://ref.ly/Rom14:13); ; [HEB 10:24](https://ref.ly/Heb10:24)

### Challenge

[PRO 27:17](https://ref.ly/Prov27:17)

-------------------------------

### Unity in the love and fear of the LORD

**[PSA 119:63](https://ref.ly/Ps119:63)** *See also* [MAL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Mal3:16); [PHP 4:1](https://ref.ly/Phil4:1)

### Worshipping together

### Sharing possessions

[ACT 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44-Acts2:45), [ACT 4:32–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32-Acts4:35), [ACT 11:29](https://ref.ly/Acts11:29)

### Serving together

### Living peaceably

### Covering sins

### Restoring from sin

[GAL 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1-Gal6:2); ; [JAS 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Jas5:19-Jas5:20)

## 5692: friends, bad (id: 5692)

Those who are close or who exercise influence, who prove to be unreliable or deceptive or who lead astray. Scripture provides numerous instructive examples of the negative effects of bad friends upon believers.

Friends who lead astray
-----------------------

### From worship

**[DEU 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6-Deut13:8)** *See also* [DEU 23:6](https://ref.ly/Deut23:6); [1KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:2)

### From good character

**[1CO 15:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:33)** *See also* [PRO 12:26](https://ref.ly/Prov12:26), [PRO 22:24–22:25](https://ref.ly/Prov22:24-Prov22:25)

### From friendship with God

**[JAS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jas4:4)** *See also* [ISA 52:11](https://ref.ly/Isa52:11); [JER 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Jer9:4-Jer9:5); [MIC 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mic7:5); [2CO 6:14–6:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14-2Cor6:18), [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1)

Friends who are unreliable
--------------------------

### In their commitment

**[JOB 6:14–6:17](https://ref.ly/Job6:14-Job6:17)**

### In their trustworthiness

**[PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9)** *See also* [PSA 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ps38:11), [PSA 55:20–55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:20-Ps55:21); [OBA 1:7](https://ref.ly/Obad1:7); [MAT 26:14–26:15](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:15); [JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/John13:18) *Jesus Christ is speaking about his betrayal by Judas.*

### In their support

**[2TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16)** *See also* [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10)

Examples of bad friends
-----------------------

### Those who lead astray

**[GEN 3:12](https://ref.ly/Gen3:12)** *See also* [1KI 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:8-1Kgs12:11); [EST 5:14](https://ref.ly/Esth5:14)

### Those who are inconstant

**[JOB 19:19](https://ref.ly/Job19:19)** *Presumably these friends are not merely the ones who attempted to comfort him.* *See also* [MAT 26:31](https://ref.ly/Matt26:31), [MAT 26:33–26:34](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33-Matt26:34); [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10), [2TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16)

## 5694: generation (id: 5694)

The population as a whole at any one time, or the interval of time separating parents and children.

All the contemporaries of an individual
---------------------------------------

**[GEN 7:1](https://ref.ly/Gen7:1)** *See also* [EXO 1:6](https://ref.ly/Exod1:6); [JDG 2:10](https://ref.ly/Judg2:10); [PSA 24:6](https://ref.ly/Ps24:6), [PSA 78:8](https://ref.ly/Ps78:8), [PSA 112:2](https://ref.ly/Ps112:2); [JER 2:31](https://ref.ly/Jer2:31), [JER 7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:29); [MAT 11:16](https://ref.ly/Matt11:16) parallel [LUK 7:31](https://ref.ly/Luke7:31); [MAT 24:32–24:34](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32-Matt24:34) parallels [MRK 13:28–13:30](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28-Mark13:30), [LUK 21:29–21:32](https://ref.ly/Luke21:29-Luke21:32); [ACT 2:40](https://ref.ly/Acts2:40), [ACT 13:36](https://ref.ly/Acts13:36); [PHP 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14-Phil2:16)

The everlasting kingdom of God
------------------------------

**[LAM 5:19](https://ref.ly/Lam5:19)** *See also* [PSA 72:5](https://ref.ly/Ps72:5), [PSA 145:13](https://ref.ly/Ps145:13); [DAN 4:3](https://ref.ly/Dan4:3); [EPH 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Eph3:20-Eph3:21)

-----------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Exod3:15)** *See also* [GEN 9:12](https://ref.ly/Gen9:12), [GEN 17:12](https://ref.ly/Gen17:12); [EXO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Exod12:17), [EXO 20:5](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5) parallel [DEU 5:9](https://ref.ly/Deut5:9) *the enduring consequences of rebellion*; [EXO 31:16](https://ref.ly/Exod31:16); [LEV 6:18](https://ref.ly/Lev6:18), [LEV 23:41](https://ref.ly/Lev23:41); [NUM 15:38](https://ref.ly/Num15:38), [NUM 35:29](https://ref.ly/Num35:29); [DEU 29:22](https://ref.ly/Deut29:22); [JOS 22:27](https://ref.ly/Josh22:27); [EST 9:28](https://ref.ly/Esth9:28); [PSA 72:5](https://ref.ly/Ps72:5) *traditionally taken as referring to the Messiah*; [PSA 105:8](https://ref.ly/Ps105:8); [ISA 34:10](https://ref.ly/Isa34:10); [JER 50:39](https://ref.ly/Jer50:39); [LUK 1:46–1:50](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:50)

## 5695: girls (id: 5695)

Scripture provides insights into, and examples of, the way in which girls have a place in the purposes of God. Although the OT tends to portray girls mainly as potential wives or sexual partners, Jesus Christ's raising of Jairus' daughter can be seen as a symbol of the positive value placed on them.

The portrayal of girls in the OT
--------------------------------

### As potential wives

**[JDG 21:21–21:25](https://ref.ly/Judg21:21-Judg21:25)** *See also* [GEN 24:12–24:51](https://ref.ly/Gen24:12-Gen24:51); [JDG 11:29–11:40](https://ref.ly/Judg11:29-Judg11:40); [1SA 18:17–18:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17-1Sam18:27)

### As sexual partners

**[JDG 19:24](https://ref.ly/Judg19:24)** *This way of treating girls as mere sexual objects illustrates the moral corruption of the men of Gibeah. Though not condemned in this passage, such behaviour was totally contrary to OT law.* *See also* [GEN 19:1–19:8](https://ref.ly/Gen19:1-Gen19:8), [GEN 34:1–34:4](https://ref.ly/Gen34:1-Gen34:4)

### Chaste girls were to be protected but promiscuous girls were to be punished

[DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21)

Girls undertaking various kinds of work
---------------------------------------

### As slaves or servants

**[EXO 21:7–21:9](https://ref.ly/Exod21:7-Exod21:9)** *See also* [RUT 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:8-Ruth2:9); [1KI 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:1-1Kgs1:4); [ACT 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Acts12:13-Acts12:14)

### Drawing water and tending animals

**[EXO 2:16](https://ref.ly/Exod2:16)** *See also* [GEN 24:15–24:20](https://ref.ly/Gen24:15-Gen24:20); [1SA 9:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:11)

### Repairing the walls of Jerusalem

**[NEH 3:12](https://ref.ly/Neh3:12)**

### In domestic service

**[1SA 8:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:13)**

The place of girls in serving the purposes of God
-------------------------------------------------

### Girls receive ministry from Jesus Christ and the apostles

**[MRK 5:35–5:43](https://ref.ly/Mark5:35-Mark5:43)** parallels [MAT 9:23–9:25](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23-Matt9:25), [LUK 8:49–8:56](https://ref.ly/Luke8:49-Luke8:56) *See also* [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30); [ACT 16:16–16:18](https://ref.ly/Acts16:16-Acts16:18)

### Girls receive and use spiritual gifts

**[ACT 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Acts2:16-Acts2:17)** *See also* [JOL 2:28](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28); [ACT 21:8–21:9](https://ref.ly/Acts21:8-Acts21:9)

### Examples of girls serving the purposes of God

[2KI 5:2–5:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:2-2Kgs5:6) *The example of this servant girl, instrumental in Naaman's healing, shows that girls have a value and a status before God not generally acknowledged in OT culture*; ; [EST 2:1–2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:1-Esth2:18) *Having become queen under the providence of God, Esther, chosen while a young virgin, is later able to use her influence to save the Jews from fierce persecution*; ; [LUK 1:26–1:33](https://ref.ly/Luke1:26-Luke1:33) *Mary should be regarded as a girl, as she is a virgin betrothed and not a woman married.*

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 21:21-25; Genesis 24:12-51; Judges 11:29-40; 1 Samuel 18:17-27; Judges 19:24; Genesis 19:1-8; Genesis 34:1-4; Deuteronomy 22:13-21; Exodus 21:7-9; Ruth 2:8-9; 1 Kings 1:1-4; Acts 12:13-14; Exodus 2:16; Genesis 24:15-20; 1 Samuel 9:11; Nehemiah 3:12; 1 Samuel 8:13; Mark 5:35-43; Matthew 9:23-25; Luke 8:49-56; Matthew 15:21-28; Mark 7:24-30; Acts 16:16-18; Acts 2:16-17; Joel 2:28; Acts 21:8-9; 2 Kings 5:2-6; Esther 2:1-18; Luke 1:26-33

## 5699: guests (id: 5699)

People who receive hospitality, especially at others' homes. Guests are often invited to special celebrations such as weddings or birthdays. God invites all people as guests at his feast but many refuse.

Occasions for inviting guests
-----------------------------

### Weddings

**[GEN 29:21–29:22](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21-Gen29:22)** *See also* [EST 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17-Esth2:18); [JHN 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/John2:1-John2:10)

### Birthday celebrations

[GEN 40:20](https://ref.ly/Gen40:20); ; [MAT 14:6](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6) parallel [MRK 6:21–6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21-Mark6:22); ; [MAT 14:9](https://ref.ly/Matt14:9) parallel [MRK 6:26](https://ref.ly/Mark6:26)

### Other special occasions

[1SA 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:22-1Sam9:24); ; [1KI 1:41](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:41), [1KI 1:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:49); ; [EST 1:8](https://ref.ly/Esth1:8)

Receiving guests
----------------

### Receiving Jesus Christ as a guest

**[LUK 19:7](https://ref.ly/Luke19:7)** *See also* [MAT 9:10](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10) parallels [MRK 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15), [LUK 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29); [MAT 26:6–26:7](https://ref.ly/Matt26:6-Matt26:7) parallels [MRK 14:3](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3), [JHN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:1-John12:3); [LUK 7:36](https://ref.ly/Luke7:36), [LUK 7:49](https://ref.ly/Luke7:49), [LUK 10:38](https://ref.ly/Luke10:38), [LUK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Luke14:1), [LUK 24:29](https://ref.ly/Luke24:29); [JHN 2:2](https://ref.ly/John2:2); [REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20)

### God's people give hospitality

**[ACT 10:32](https://ref.ly/Acts10:32)** *See also* [2KI 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:8-2Kgs4:10); [MAT 10:11](https://ref.ly/Matt10:11) parallels [MRK 6:10](https://ref.ly/Mark6:10), [LUK 9:4](https://ref.ly/Luke9:4); [LUK 10:33–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:33-Luke10:37); [ACT 16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:34), [ACT 21:8](https://ref.ly/Acts21:8), [ACT 28:7](https://ref.ly/Acts28:7)

Customs associated with receiving guests
----------------------------------------

### Anointing them with oil

**[PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5)** *Anointing with oil was a customary treatment for honoured guests.* *See also* [PSA 92:10](https://ref.ly/Ps92:10); [LUK 7:46](https://ref.ly/Luke7:46); [JHN 12:3](https://ref.ly/John12:3)

### Washing their feet

**[GEN 18:4–18:5](https://ref.ly/Gen18:4-Gen18:5)** *See also* [GEN 19:2](https://ref.ly/Gen19:2), [GEN 24:32](https://ref.ly/Gen24:32), [GEN 43:24](https://ref.ly/Gen43:24); [1SA 25:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:41); [LUK 7:44](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44); [JHN 13:1–13:17](https://ref.ly/John13:1-John13:17) *It was usually the slave's duty to wash away dirt accumulated in travelling.*

### Guests treated badly

[JDG 19:20–19:23](https://ref.ly/Judg19:20-Judg19:23); ; [2SA 15:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:11); ; [JER 41:1–41:2](https://ref.ly/Jer41:1-Jer41:2); ; [LUK 7:44–7:46](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44-Luke7:46)

Guests rejecting their hosts
----------------------------

[1KI 1:49](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:49); ; [JOB 19:15](https://ref.ly/Job19:15)

Advice given to guests
----------------------

### Do not promote yourself

**[PRO 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/Prov25:6-Prov25:7)** *See also* [LUK 14:7–14:11](https://ref.ly/Luke14:7-Luke14:11)

### Do not criticise the food

**[1CO 10:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:27)** *See also* [LUK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7)

### Do not outstay your welcome

[PRO 25:17](https://ref.ly/Prov25:17)

God invites people to be his guests
-----------------------------------

**[REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9)** *See also* [ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1); [MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28); [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

### Some refuse God's invitation

**[LUK 14:24](https://ref.ly/Luke14:24)** *See also* [MAT 22:1–22:7](https://ref.ly/Matt22:1-Matt22:7); [LUK 14:16–14:20](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16-Luke14:20)

### Some accept God's invitation

**[MAT 22:8–22:10](https://ref.ly/Matt22:8-Matt22:10)** *See also* [MAT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:19](https://ref.ly/Mark2:19), [LUK 5:34](https://ref.ly/Luke5:34); [LUK 14:21–14:23](https://ref.ly/Luke14:21-Luke14:23)

Guest rooms
-----------

[MRK 14:14](https://ref.ly/Mark14:14) parallels [MAT 26:18](https://ref.ly/Matt26:18), [LUK 22:11](https://ref.ly/Luke22:11); ; [PHM 1:22](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:22)

## 5700: headship (id: 5700)

The quality of being in a position of leadership or guidance. Headship has to do with God's relation to this created world, and to his ordering of relationships within it. Headship among human beings does not necessarily signify superior status, but rather a role of leadership and care. Scripture sees it as involving servanthood, and views Jesus Christ as the supreme model of this.

God as head over all things
---------------------------

**[1CH 29:11](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:11)** *See also* [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23); [DAN 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Dan4:34-Dan4:35); [ROM 9:14–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:14-Rom9:21); [1CO 15:24–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24-1Cor15:28)

Headship within the Godhead
---------------------------

### The Father's eternal headship

**[1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3)** *See also* [1CO 15:24–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:24-1Cor15:28); [PHP 2:6](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6)

### The Father's headship in the Son's earthly life and ministry

**[JHN 6:38](https://ref.ly/John6:38)** *See also* [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39) parallels [MRK 14:36](https://ref.ly/Mark14:36), [LUK 22:42](https://ref.ly/Luke22:42); [PHP 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:8); [HEB 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7-Heb5:8)

Jesus Christ as head of all creation
------------------------------------

### Head of the created order

**[COL 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Col1:15-Col1:17)** *The description of Jesus Christ as the firstborn does not imply he is part of creation, but rather stresses his rights of inheritance over it.* *See also* [HEB 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2-Heb1:4)

### Head of all earthly and spiritual powers

**[EPH 1:20–1:22](https://ref.ly/Eph1:20-Eph1:22)** *See also* [COL 1:15–1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:15-Col1:18), [COL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:10); [HEB 2:8](https://ref.ly/Heb2:8)

### Head of the church

**[EPH 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Eph4:15-Eph4:16)** *See also* [EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23); [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18), [COL 2:19](https://ref.ly/Col2:19)

### Head over all things at the end

**[EPH 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9-Eph1:10)** *See also* [1CO 15:25–15:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:25-1Cor15:27); [REV 19:11–19:16](https://ref.ly/Rev19:11-Rev19:16)

Delegated headship in God's world
---------------------------------

### Headship of humanity over creation

**[GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28)** *See also* [PSA 8:1–8:9](https://ref.ly/Ps8:1-Ps8:9); [HEB 2:5–2:8](https://ref.ly/Heb2:5-Heb2:8)

### Headship of rulers over their nations

**[PSA 18:43](https://ref.ly/Ps18:43)** *See also* [1SA 15:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:17); [ISA 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Isa7:8-Isa7:9); [DAN 2:31–2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:31-Dan2:38); [ROM 13:1–13:6](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:6); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)

### Headship of chiefs over their tribes and families

**[JOS 22:13–22:14](https://ref.ly/Josh22:13-Josh22:14)** *See also* [NUM 1:1–1:16](https://ref.ly/Num1:1-Num1:16); [JDG 10:17–11:11](https://ref.ly/Judg10:17-Judg11:11); [1CH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:15)

### Headship of the husband over his wife

**[EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24)** *See also* [1CO 11:3–11:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3-1Cor11:16) *Women, including wives, were told to cover their heads when praying or prophesying in public as an acknowledgment, apparently, that headship attaches to males*; [COL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Col3:18); [1PE 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:6)

### Headship of leaders over the people of God

**[1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12)** *See also* [1SA 24:1–24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1-1Sam24:7); [ISA 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/Isa3:1-Isa3:7) *The removal of headship and leadership is seen as a mark of God's judgment*; [HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)

### Humility, love and respect are to be the framework for delegated headship

**[1PE 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:3)** *See also* [EPH 5:21](https://ref.ly/Eph5:21), [EPH 5:25–5:30](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:30)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 29:11; Isaiah 45:23; Daniel 4:34-35; Romans 9:14-21; 1 Corinthians 15:24-28; 1 Corinthians 11:3; Philippians 2:6; John 6:38; Matthew 26:39; Mark 14:36; Luke 22:42; Philippians 2:6-8; Hebrews 5:7-8; Colossians 1:15-17; Hebrews 1:2-4; Ephesians 1:20-22; Colossians 1:15-18; Colossians 2:10; Hebrews 2:8; Ephesians 4:15-16; Ephesians 5:23; Colossians 1:18; Colossians 2:19; Ephesians 1:9-10; 1 Corinthians 15:25-27; Revelation 19:11-16; Genesis 1:28; Psalms 8:1-9; Hebrews 2:5-8; Psalms 18:43; 1 Samuel 15:17; Isaiah 7:8-9; Daniel 2:31-38; Romans 13:1-6; 1 Peter 2:13-14; Joshua 22:13-14; Numbers 1:1-16; Judges 10:17-11:11; 1 Chronicles 5:15; Ephesians 5:22-24; 1 Corinthians 11:3-16; Colossians 3:18; 1 Peter 3:1-6; 1 Thessalonians 5:12; 1 Samuel 24:1-7; Isaiah 3:1-7; Hebrews 13:17; 1 Peter 5:1-3; Ephesians 5:21; Ephesians 5:25-30

## 5701: heir (id: 5701)

Property and power in biblical times normally descended from father to son. Jesus Christ is therefore called God's heir, and believers are said to inherit in him.

The heir in ancient Israel
--------------------------

### The heir of property

**[GEN 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/Gen15:3-Gen15:4)** *See also* [PRO 13:22](https://ref.ly/Prov13:22), [PRO 19:14](https://ref.ly/Prov19:14)

### The heir of power

**[1KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:12)** parallel [1CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:28) *See also* [1KI 11:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:43), [1KI 14:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:31), [1KI 15:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:3)

### Lack of an heir regarded as a very serious matter

[DEU 25:5–25:6](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:6); ; [JDG 21:17](https://ref.ly/Judg21:17); ; [2SA 14:4–14:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:4-2Sam14:7) *The woman of Tekoa was interceding indirectly for Absalom, David's heir, who had been banished.*

### Daughters and other relatives could be heirs if there were no sons

[NUM 27:1–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:11)

### Property could be inherited before death

[LUK 15:11–15:12](https://ref.ly/Luke15:11-Luke15:12) *The prodigal son's request of inheritance before death could perhaps be regarded as an insult, but graciously the father complied out of love.*

### An heir must not give up his inheritance

**[HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16)** *See also* [GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34); [NUM 36:6–36:7](https://ref.ly/Num36:6-Num36:7); [1KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:3); [JER 49:1](https://ref.ly/Jer49:1)

### The firstborn son was heir to a double share

[DEU 21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:17)

Jesus Christ as the heir of God
-------------------------------

**[HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2)** *See also* [MAT 28:18](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18); [LUK 19:12](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12); [JHN 3:35](https://ref.ly/John3:35)

### Jesus Christ is heir as the Father's firstborn

[HEB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Heb1:6)

### Envy of Jesus Christ as heir

[MAT 21:37–21:39](https://ref.ly/Matt21:37-Matt21:39) parallels [MRK 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Mark12:6-Mark12:8), [LUK 20:13–20:15](https://ref.ly/Luke20:13-Luke20:15)

God's people as heirs
---------------------

### God himself as his people's inheritance

[JOS 18:7](https://ref.ly/Josh18:7); ; [PSA 73:26](https://ref.ly/Ps73:26), [PSA 119:57](https://ref.ly/Ps119:57), [PSA 142:5](https://ref.ly/Ps142:5); ; [JER 51:19](https://ref.ly/Jer51:19)

### Israel as heirs to land

**[EXO 32:13](https://ref.ly/Exod32:13)** *See also* [PSA 105:44](https://ref.ly/Ps105:44)

### Noah, heir of righteousness

[HEB 11:7](https://ref.ly/Heb11:7)

### Abraham, heir of promise

[GEN 17:4–17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:4-Gen17:8); ; [LUK 1:54–1:55](https://ref.ly/Luke1:54-Luke1:55); ; [HEB 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:9)

### David, heir of a kingdom

[2SA 7:8–7:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:8-2Sam7:16)

### Israel, heirs of the prophets

[ACT 3:25](https://ref.ly/Acts3:25); ; [ROM 3:2](https://ref.ly/Rom3:2)

### All believers are heirs by faith

**[GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:29)** *See also* [ROM 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Rom4:13-Rom4:17)

### Heirs under the covenant

**[HEB 6:17](https://ref.ly/Heb6:17)** *See also* [HEB 9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:15)

### Heirs through God's grace

**[GAL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Gal3:18)** *See also* [COL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Col1:12)

### Co\-heirs with Jesus Christ

**[ROM 8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:17)**

The privileges of God's heirs
-----------------------------

### Believers are heirs to the full rights of sons

*See also* [GAL 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:1-Gal4:7)

### Believers are heirs as the firstborn

[HEB 12:23](https://ref.ly/Heb12:23)

### Believers are heirs of salvation

[HEB 1:14](https://ref.ly/Heb1:14)

### Believers are heirs of eternal life

[TIT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Titus3:7); ; [1PE 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:4)

### Believers are heirs of the earth

[MAT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Matt5:5); ; [PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11)

### Believers are heirs of the kingdom

[MAT 25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34)

### Believers are heirs of all things

[1CO 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:21-1Cor3:22); ; [2CO 6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:10); ; [REV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Rev21:7)

### Believers are heirs of life

[1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### Believers as heirs share Jesus Christ's throne

[LUK 22:29–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:29-Luke22:30); ; [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21)

God's inheritance is his people
-------------------------------

[DEU 32:9](https://ref.ly/Deut32:9); ; [ZEC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Zech2:12)

## 5702: husband (id: 5702)

The male partner in a marriage relationship. The origin of this God\-ordained institution is traced back in the Bible to the Garden of Eden. Scripture stresses that marriage is a God\-ordained institution, within which the husband is pledged to love and care for his wife. Scripture contains many examples, both good and bad, of how husbands behaved. The ultimate examples of a good husband in Scripture are God as the husband of Israel and Jesus Christ as the bridegroom of the church.

God's pattern for marriage
--------------------------

### Husband and wife are one flesh

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23-Gen2:24); [MAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15); [MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9)

### The husband is head of the wife

**[EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23)** *See also* [1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3)

Duties of a husband
-------------------

### To leave his parents and be united to his wife

**[MAT 19:5](https://ref.ly/Matt19:5)** parallel [MRK 10:7–10:8](https://ref.ly/Mark10:7-Mark10:8) *See also* [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24)

### To love his wife

**[EPH 5:25](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25)** *See also* [DEU 24:5](https://ref.ly/Deut24:5); [PRO 5:18](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18); [ECC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:9); [EPH 5:28–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:28-Eph5:33); [COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19); [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### To fulfil his marital duty

**[1CO 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:3-1Cor7:5)** *See also* [EXO 21:10–21:11](https://ref.ly/Exod21:10-Exod21:11)

### To be faithful to his wife

**[EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14)** parallel [DEU 5:18](https://ref.ly/Deut5:18) *See also* [LEV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Lev20:10); [DEU 22:22](https://ref.ly/Deut22:22); [MAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14); [MAT 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:27-Matt5:28); [1CO 7:14–7:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:14-1Cor7:16)

Adam, the first husband
-----------------------

**[GEN 2:20–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:24)**

Behaviour which is commended in husbands
----------------------------------------

**[1SA 1:3–1:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:3-1Sam1:8)** *See also* [GEN 24:67](https://ref.ly/Gen24:67), [GEN 29:20](https://ref.ly/Gen29:20); [RUT 3:7–4:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:7-Ruth4:11); [EST 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Esth5:1-Esth5:3); [HOS 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1-Hos3:3); [MAT 1:18–1:25](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:25)

Behaviour which is condemned in husbands
----------------------------------------

**[2SA 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:7-2Sam12:10)** *See also* [GEN 29:30–29:31](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30-Gen29:31), [GEN 38:8–38:10](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8-Gen38:10); [JDG 19:16–19:29](https://ref.ly/Judg19:16-Judg19:29); [MAL 2:13–2:16](https://ref.ly/Mal2:13-Mal2:16)

God as a husband
----------------

**[ISA 54:5](https://ref.ly/Isa54:5)** *See also* [JER 3:14](https://ref.ly/Jer3:14), [JER 31:32](https://ref.ly/Jer31:32); [EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8)

Jesus Christ as a bridegroom
----------------------------

**[EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27)** *See also* [MAT 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:18–2:20](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:20), [LUK 5:33–5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33-Luke5:35); [2CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2); [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:27; Genesis 2:23-24; Malachi 2:15; Matthew 19:4-6; Mark 10:6-9; Ephesians 5:23; 1 Corinthians 11:3; Matthew 19:5; Mark 10:7-8; Genesis 2:24; Ephesians 5:25; Deuteronomy 24:5; Proverbs 5:18; Ecclesiastes 9:9; Ephesians 5:28-33; Colossians 3:19; 1 Peter 3:7; 1 Corinthians 7:3-5; Exodus 21:10-11; Exodus 20:14; Deuteronomy 5:18; Leviticus 20:10; Deuteronomy 22:22; Malachi 2:14; Matthew 5:27-28; 1 Corinthians 7:14-16; Genesis 2:20-24; 1 Samuel 1:3-8; Genesis 24:67; Genesis 29:20; Ruth 3:7-4:11; Esther 5:1-3; Hosea 3:1-3; Matthew 1:18-25; 2 Samuel 12:7-10; Genesis 29:30-31; Genesis 38:8-10; Judges 19:16-29; Malachi 2:13-16; Isaiah 54:5; Jeremiah 3:14; Jeremiah 31:32; Ezekiel 16:8; Ephesians 5:25-27; Matthew 9:14-15; Mark 2:18-20; Luke 5:33-35; 2 Corinthians 11:2; Revelation 21:2

## 5703: inheritance (id: 5703)

A birthright or endowment, often in the form of goods or land, passed down from one generation to another. Scripture treats eternal life and other privileges of faith as an inheritance from God.

* **Associated Articles:** 5704: inheritance, material (ID: `5704@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5705: inheritance, spiritual (ID: `5705@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5704: inheritance, material (id: 5704)

Family property was normally handed down in biblical times through sons, with a special place for the firstborn. The land of Israel was regarded as the nation's inheritance.

Inheritance of family property
------------------------------

### It was normally handed down through sons

**[GEN 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/Gen15:3-Gen15:4)** *See also* [GEN 25:5](https://ref.ly/Gen25:5); [JER 49:1](https://ref.ly/Jer49:1); [EZK 46:16–46:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:16-Ezek46:17); [MAT 21:38](https://ref.ly/Matt21:38) parallels [MRK 12:7](https://ref.ly/Mark12:7), [LUK 20:14](https://ref.ly/Luke20:14); [GAL 4:30](https://ref.ly/Gal4:30); [GEN 21:10](https://ref.ly/Gen21:10)

### The absence of a son as a male heir was seen as a serious disadvantage

[GEN 25:21](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21); ; [DEU 25:5–25:6](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:6); ; [JDG 21:17](https://ref.ly/Judg21:17); ; [1SA 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:10-1Sam1:11); ; [2SA 14:4–14:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:4-2Sam14:7)

### Other relatives could inherit

**[NUM 27:8–27:11](https://ref.ly/Num27:8-Num27:11)** *See also* [NUM 27:1–27:7](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:7), [NUM 36:2–36:4](https://ref.ly/Num36:2-Num36:4); [RUT 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:1-Ruth4:4); [JOB 42:15](https://ref.ly/Job42:15)

### Servants could inherit

[GEN 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Gen15:2-Gen15:3)

### Inheritance before death

[LUK 15:11–15:12](https://ref.ly/Luke15:11-Luke15:12) *The son's asking for his inheritance is perhaps to be seen as an insult.*

### The rights of the firstborn

**[DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17)** *See also* [GEN 27:19](https://ref.ly/Gen27:19), [GEN 27:32](https://ref.ly/Gen27:32); [2CH 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:3)

Stewardship of one's own inheritance
------------------------------------

### It must be preserved

**[1KI 21:2–21:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:2-1Kgs21:3)** *See also* [PRO 13:22](https://ref.ly/Prov13:22)

### It must not be squandered

**[HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16)** *See also* [GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34); [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13)

### It must not be fought over

[LUK 12:13–12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:13-Luke12:15)

Respect for the inheritance of others
-------------------------------------

**[MIC 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:2-Mic2:3)** *See also* [EZK 46:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:18)

The land as Israel's inheritance
--------------------------------

### It was promised to Abraham

**[GEN 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/Gen12:6-Gen12:7)** *See also* [GEN 13:14–13:15](https://ref.ly/Gen13:14-Gen13:15), [GEN 15:7](https://ref.ly/Gen15:7), [GEN 15:18–15:21](https://ref.ly/Gen15:18-Gen15:21), [GEN 17:8](https://ref.ly/Gen17:8); [ACT 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/Acts7:3-Acts7:5); [HEB 11:8](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8)

### It was confirmed to Moses and the patriarchs

[EXO 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Exod6:2-Exod6:4), [EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8)

### Entry was denied because of sin

[NUM 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Num14:22-Num14:23); ; [DEU 4:21](https://ref.ly/Deut4:21)

### It was entered by faith

[HEB 11:8–11:9](https://ref.ly/Heb11:8-Heb11:9), [HEB 11:30](https://ref.ly/Heb11:30)

### It was conquered by Joshua

[JOS 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Josh1:1-Josh1:4), [JOS 11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:23)

### It was allotted to the tribes

[JOS 14:1–14:5](https://ref.ly/Josh14:1-Josh14:5)

### It was a land for the godly

[PSA 37:9](https://ref.ly/Ps37:9), [PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11), [PSA 37:22](https://ref.ly/Ps37:22), [PSA 37:29](https://ref.ly/Ps37:29), [PSA 37:34](https://ref.ly/Ps37:34)

### It was a good inheritance

**[EXO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:8)** *See also* [LEV 20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:24); [NUM 13:21–13:27](https://ref.ly/Num13:21-Num13:27); [DEU 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/Deut8:7-Deut8:8), [DEU 11:10–11:12](https://ref.ly/Deut11:10-Deut11:12); [PSA 65:9–65:10](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9-Ps65:10); [JER 2:7](https://ref.ly/Jer2:7)

### The LORD's inheritance in the land

[EXO 15:17](https://ref.ly/Exod15:17); ; [JOS 22:19](https://ref.ly/Josh22:19); ; [ZEC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Zech2:12)

### The inheritance was lost through sin

**[JER 17:4](https://ref.ly/Jer17:4)** *See also* [PSA 79:1](https://ref.ly/Ps79:1); [LAM 5:2](https://ref.ly/Lam5:2)

### God's judgment on the nations

[JER 12:14–12:17](https://ref.ly/Jer12:14-Jer12:17)

### The land was regained through grace

[EZR 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra1:1-Ezra1:2); ; [JER 29:10–29:11](https://ref.ly/Jer29:10-Jer29:11)

### God renewed his promises

**[PSA 69:34–69:36](https://ref.ly/Ps69:34-Ps69:36)** *The Jews did return to the land; some look, however, for a further fulfilment of these promises in the future; others see them as fulfilled in Jesus Christ.* *See also* [ISA 57:13](https://ref.ly/Isa57:13), [ISA 58:13–58:14](https://ref.ly/Isa58:13-Isa58:14), [ISA 61:7](https://ref.ly/Isa61:7), [ISA 65:9](https://ref.ly/Isa65:9); [ZEC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Zech8:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 15:3-4; Genesis 25:5; Jeremiah 49:1; Ezekiel 46:16-17; Matthew 21:38; Mark 12:7; Luke 20:14; Galatians 4:30; Genesis 21:10; Genesis 25:21; Deuteronomy 25:5-6; Judges 21:17; 1 Samuel 1:10-11; 2 Samuel 14:4-7; Numbers 27:8-11; Numbers 27:1-7; Numbers 36:2-4; Ruth 4:1-4; Job 42:15; Genesis 15:2-3; Luke 15:11-12; Deuteronomy 21:15-17; Genesis 27:19; Genesis 27:32; 2 Chronicles 21:3; 1 Kings 21:2-3; Proverbs 13:22; Hebrews 12:16; Genesis 25:29-34; Luke 15:13; Luke 12:13-15; Micah 2:2-3; Ezekiel 46:18; Genesis 12:6-7; Genesis 13:14-15; Genesis 15:7; Genesis 15:18-21; Genesis 17:8; Acts 7:3-5; Hebrews 11:8; Exodus 6:2-4; Exodus 6:8; Numbers 14:22-23; Deuteronomy 4:21; Hebrews 11:8-9; Hebrews 11:30; Joshua 1:1-4; Joshua 11:23; Joshua 14:1-5; Psalms 37:9; Psalms 37:11; Psalms 37:22; Psalms 37:29; Psalms 37:34; Exodus 3:8; Leviticus 20:24; Numbers 13:21-27; Deuteronomy 8:7-8; Deuteronomy 11:10-12; Psalms 65:9-10; Jeremiah 2:7; Exodus 15:17; Joshua 22:19; Zechariah 2:12; Jeremiah 17:4; Psalms 79:1; Lamentations 5:2; Jeremiah 12:14-17; Ezra 1:1-2; Jeremiah 29:10-11; Psalms 69:34-36; Isaiah 57:13; Isaiah 58:13-14; Isaiah 61:7; Isaiah 65:9; Zechariah 8:12
