## 5705: inheritance, spiritual (id: 5705)

Believers are privileged to receive a spiritual inheritance from their heavenly Father as a result of their adoption into the family of God through faith.

Believers have an inheritance in Christ
---------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ as God's heir

**[HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2)** *See also* [MAT 21:37–21:39](https://ref.ly/Matt21:37-Matt21:39) parallels [MRK 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Mark12:6-Mark12:8), [LUK 20:13–20:15](https://ref.ly/Luke20:13-Luke20:15); [MAT 28:18](https://ref.ly/Matt28:18); [LUK 19:12](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12); [JHN 3:35](https://ref.ly/John3:35); [HEB 1:4–1:6](https://ref.ly/Heb1:4-Heb1:6); [PSA 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Ps2:7-Ps2:8)

### Believers are co\-heirs with Christ

**[ROM 8:17](https://ref.ly/Rom8:17)** *See also* [EPH 3:6](https://ref.ly/Eph3:6)

### Believers are heirs under the covenant

[HEB 6:17](https://ref.ly/Heb6:17), [HEB 9:15](https://ref.ly/Heb9:15)

### Believers are heirs by grace

**[GAL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Gal3:18)** *See also* [ACT 20:32](https://ref.ly/Acts20:32); [COL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Col1:12)

### Believers are heirs through faith

**[GAL 3:29](https://ref.ly/Gal3:29)** *See also* [ROM 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Rom4:13-Rom4:17); [HEB 11:7–11:10](https://ref.ly/Heb11:7-Heb11:10)

The nature of the spiritual inheritance of believers
----------------------------------------------------

### They inherit salvation

**[HEB 1:14](https://ref.ly/Heb1:14)**

### They inherit eternal life

*See also* [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29); [LUK 10:25](https://ref.ly/Luke10:25); [TIT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Titus3:7); [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### They have the full rights of sons

[GAL 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:4-Gal4:7)

### They have the rights of the firstborn

**[HEB 12:22–12:23](https://ref.ly/Heb12:22-Heb12:23)** *See also* [EXO 4:22](https://ref.ly/Exod4:22); [2KI 2:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:9)

### They inherit the earth

**[MAT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Matt5:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11)

### They inherit the kingdom

**[MAT 25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34)** *See also* [LUK 22:28–22:30](https://ref.ly/Luke22:28-Luke22:30); [REV 3:21](https://ref.ly/Rev3:21)

### They inherit all things

[LUK 15:31](https://ref.ly/Luke15:31); ; [1CO 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:21-1Cor3:22); ; [REV 21:7](https://ref.ly/Rev21:7)

### Their inheritance is the word of God

[PSA 119:111](https://ref.ly/Ps119:111)

### They inherit the Lord himself

**[PSA 73:26](https://ref.ly/Ps73:26)** *See also* [NUM 18:20](https://ref.ly/Num18:20); [DEU 10:9](https://ref.ly/Deut10:9); [PSA 142:5](https://ref.ly/Ps142:5); [JER 10:16](https://ref.ly/Jer10:16), [JER 51:19](https://ref.ly/Jer51:19)

### It is a good inheritance

[PSA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Ps16:6)

The permanence of believers' spiritual inheritance
--------------------------------------------------

### It has been prepared from the creation

[MAT 25:34](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34)

### It is guaranteed for eternity

**[1PE 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:18](https://ref.ly/Ps37:18); [DAN 12:13](https://ref.ly/Dan12:13)

### It is sealed with God's promise

**[EPH 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13-Eph1:14)** *See also* [REV 7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:3), [REV 9:4](https://ref.ly/Rev9:4)

The conditions of spiritual inheritance
---------------------------------------

### It is conditional upon continuing obedience and faith

**[1PE 3:9–3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9-1Pet3:12)** *See also* [PSA 34:12–34:16](https://ref.ly/Ps34:12-Ps34:16), [PSA 25:12–25:13](https://ref.ly/Ps25:12-Ps25:13), [PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11), [PSA 37:29](https://ref.ly/Ps37:29), [PSA 37:34](https://ref.ly/Ps37:34), [PSA 61:5](https://ref.ly/Ps61:5); [PRO 28:10](https://ref.ly/Prov28:10); [MAT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Matt5:5); [COL 3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:24); [HEB 6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:12); [JAS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5)

### It is denied to the ungodly

**[EPH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Eph5:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:9](https://ref.ly/Ps37:9), [PSA 37:22](https://ref.ly/Ps37:22); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10), [1CO 10:1–10:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:1-1Cor10:5); [GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21); [HEB 3:12–3:19](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12-Heb3:19); [REV 21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 1:2; Matthew 21:37-39; Mark 12:6-8; Luke 20:13-15; Matthew 28:18; Luke 19:12; John 3:35; Hebrews 1:4-6; Psalms 2:7-8; Romans 8:17; Ephesians 3:6; Hebrews 6:17; Hebrews 9:15; Galatians 3:18; Acts 20:32; Colossians 1:12; Galatians 3:29; Romans 4:13-17; Hebrews 11:7-10; Hebrews 1:14; Matthew 19:29; Luke 10:25; Titus 3:7; 1 Peter 3:7; Galatians 4:4-7; Hebrews 12:22-23; Exodus 4:22; 2 Kings 2:9; Matthew 5:5; Psalms 37:11; Matthew 25:34; Luke 22:28-30; Revelation 3:21; Luke 15:31; 1 Corinthians 3:21-22; Revelation 21:7; Psalms 119:111; Psalms 73:26; Numbers 18:20; Deuteronomy 10:9; Psalms 142:5; Jeremiah 10:16; Jeremiah 51:19; Psalms 16:6; 1 Peter 1:3-5; Psalms 37:18; Daniel 12:13; Ephesians 1:13-14; Revelation 7:3; Revelation 9:4; 1 Peter 3:9-12; Psalms 34:12-16; Psalms 25:12-13; Psalms 37:29; Psalms 37:34; Psalms 61:5; Proverbs 28:10; Colossians 3:24; Hebrews 6:12; James 2:5; Ephesians 5:5; Psalms 37:9; Psalms 37:22; 1 Corinthians 6:9-10; 1 Corinthians 10:1-5; Galatians 5:19-21; Hebrews 3:12-19; Revelation 21:27

## 5707: male and female (id: 5707)

God created humanity as male and female. Although gender differences are evident in behaviour and role, Scripture teaches the equality and complementarity of the sexes.

The creation of male and female
-------------------------------

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 2:20–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:24), [GEN 5:2](https://ref.ly/Gen5:2)

The fall disrupted relationships between male and female
--------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14-Gen3:19)** *See also* [2SA 13:1–13:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:19); [PRO 5:3–5:14](https://ref.ly/Prov5:3-Prov5:14); [MAT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28); [ROM 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Rom1:26-Rom1:27)

The equality of male and female
-------------------------------

### Equality under many old covenant regulations

**[NUM 5:5–5:7](https://ref.ly/Num5:5-Num5:7)** *See also* [LEV 20:10–20:12](https://ref.ly/Lev20:10-Lev20:12); [NUM 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Num5:1-Num5:4); [DEU 15:12–15:15](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:15), [DEU 23:17–23:18](https://ref.ly/Deut23:17-Deut23:18)

### Equality in religious observance

**[EXO 35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:22)** *See also* [NUM 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:4); [DEU 31:12](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12); [2SA 6:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:19)

### Equality of accountability

**[LAM 2:21](https://ref.ly/Lam2:21)** *See also* [LEV 20:27](https://ref.ly/Lev20:27); [2CH 36:15–36:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:17); [ACT 5:1–5:11](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:11)

### Equality in Christ

**[GAL 3:26–3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26-Gal3:28)** *See also* [ACT 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17-Acts2:18); [JOL 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28-Joel2:29); [1CO 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:11-1Cor11:12)

Variations in the treatment of male and female
----------------------------------------------

### In OT patriarchal society

**[LEV 27:1–27:7](https://ref.ly/Lev27:1-Lev27:7)** *See also* [GEN 17:9–17:14](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:14); [EXO 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Exod13:1-Exod13:2), [EXO 13:14–13:15](https://ref.ly/Exod13:14-Exod13:15); [NUM 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Num3:14-Num3:15), [NUM 5:11–5:31](https://ref.ly/Num5:11-Num5:31), [NUM 18:8–18:10](https://ref.ly/Num18:8-Num18:10)

### In legislation protecting women under the old covenant

**[NUM 27:1–27:7](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:7)** *See also* [NUM 36:1–36:12](https://ref.ly/Num36:1-Num36:12); [DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19), [DEU 22:25–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:25-Deut22:29)

### In roles within the NT church community

**[1CO 14:33–14:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:33-1Cor14:35)** *See also* [1CO 11:3–11:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3-1Cor11:10); [EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24) *The idea of “headship” is complex, but is generally regarded as including the ideas of responsibility and authority within a relationship*; [1TI 2:11–2:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:11-1Tim2:15); [TIT 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:1-Titus2:5)

### In behaviour within the NT church community

**[1TI 2:8–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8-1Tim2:10)** *See also* [1CO 11:3–11:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3-1Cor11:16); [1TI 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1-1Tim5:2)

Male and female roles within marriage
-------------------------------------

**[1CO 7:1–7:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:1-1Cor7:7)** *See also* [PRO 31:10–31:31](https://ref.ly/Prov31:10-Prov31:31); [EPH 5:22–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:33); [COL 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:18-Col3:19); [1PE 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:7)

Male and female imagery used of God
-----------------------------------

### Female imagery

**[MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37)** parallel [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34) *See also* [PRO 8:1–8:11](https://ref.ly/Prov8:1-Prov8:11); [ISA 66:12–66:13](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12-Isa66:13)

### Male imagery

**[PSA 47:2](https://ref.ly/Ps47:2)** *See also* [EXO 15:3](https://ref.ly/Exod15:3); [PSA 23:1](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1); [MAT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:6); [LUK 11:11–11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11-Luke11:13) parallel [MAT 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:27; Genesis 2:20-24; Genesis 5:2; Genesis 3:14-19; 2 Samuel 13:1-19; Proverbs 5:3-14; Matthew 5:28; Romans 1:26-27; Numbers 5:5-7; Leviticus 20:10-12; Numbers 5:1-4; Deuteronomy 15:12-15; Deuteronomy 23:17-18; Exodus 35:22; Numbers 6:1-4; Deuteronomy 31:12; 2 Samuel 6:19; Lamentations 2:21; Leviticus 20:27; 2 Chronicles 36:15-17; Acts 5:1-11; Galatians 3:26-28; Acts 2:17-18; Joel 2:28-29; 1 Corinthians 11:11-12; Leviticus 27:1-7; Genesis 17:9-14; Exodus 13:1-2; Exodus 13:14-15; Numbers 3:14-15; Numbers 5:11-31; Numbers 18:8-10; Numbers 27:1-7; Numbers 36:1-12; Deuteronomy 22:13-19; Deuteronomy 22:25-29; 1 Corinthians 14:33-35; 1 Corinthians 11:3-10; Ephesians 5:22-24; 1 Timothy 2:11-15; Titus 2:1-5; 1 Timothy 2:8-10; 1 Corinthians 11:3-16; 1 Timothy 5:1-2; 1 Corinthians 7:1-7; Proverbs 31:10-31; Ephesians 5:22-33; Colossians 3:18-19; 1 Peter 3:1-7; Matthew 23:37; Luke 13:34; Proverbs 8:1-11; Isaiah 66:12-13; Psalms 47:2; Exodus 15:3; Psalms 23:1; Matthew 6:6; Luke 11:11-13; Matthew 7:9-11

## 5708: marriage (id: 5708)

The union of a man and a woman in a permanent relationship. Though a lifelong monogamous commitment is presented as the ideal, polygamous marriages were occasionally known in OT times and carried the same legal and moral rights and responsibilities. God's relationship with his people is described in terms of the marriage bond.

* **Associated Articles:** 5709: marriage, purpose of (ID: `5709@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5710: marriage, customs concerning (ID: `5710@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5711: marriage, restrictions concerning (ID: `5711@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5712: marriage, between God and his people (ID: `5712@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5709: marriage, purpose of (id: 5709)

Marriage is part of God's intention for humanity from creation and forms the basis for the family which is the primary unit of society. Where marriage flourishes it blesses both the couple and the wider community.

Marriage is part of God's plan for the human race
-------------------------------------------------

### From creation

**[MAT 19:4](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4)** parallel [MRK 10:6](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6) *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27); [1CO 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:11-1Cor11:12) *Man and woman are not independent of each other.*

### It is to provide companionship

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [GEN 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:22), [GEN 3:12](https://ref.ly/Gen3:12); [PRO 31:10–31:12](https://ref.ly/Prov31:10-Prov31:12)

### It is to be a committed, exclusive relationship

**[GEN 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23-Gen2:24)** *See also* [MAT 19:5](https://ref.ly/Matt19:5) parallel [MRK 10:7–10:8](https://ref.ly/Mark10:7-Mark10:8); [1CO 7:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:2); [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

### It is a lifelong partnership

**[MAT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:6)** parallel [MRK 10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:9) *See also* [ROM 7:2](https://ref.ly/Rom7:2); [1CO 7:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:39)

### It is the intended context for raising children

**[MAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15)** *See also* [1CO 7:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:14)

### It will not exist in the life to come

[MAT 22:30](https://ref.ly/Matt22:30) parallels [MRK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Mark12:25), [LUK 20:34–20:35](https://ref.ly/Luke20:34-Luke20:35); ; [1CO 7:29–7:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:29-1Cor7:31)

Marriage as a covenant relationship
-----------------------------------

**[MAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14)** *See also* [PRO 2:17](https://ref.ly/Prov2:17); [EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8)

Sex and marriage
----------------

### Sex belongs within marriage

**[1CO 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:9)** *See also* [GEN 29:21](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21)EXO [22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Gen22:16-Gen22:17); [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29)[DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21) *A woman was expected to be a virgin when she married.*

### The sexual relationship is exclusive

**[SNG 2:16](https://ref.ly/Song2:16)** *See also* [DEU 22:22–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:22-Deut22:24) *Marital unfaithfulness is condemned*; [PRO 5:15–5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:15-Prov5:19); [1CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16), [1CO 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:3-1Cor7:5)

Love and submission in marriage
-------------------------------

### Falling in love prior to marriage

**[GEN 29:20](https://ref.ly/Gen29:20)** *See also* [GEN 34:3–34:4](https://ref.ly/Gen34:3-Gen34:4); [1SA 18:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:20)

### Husbands are to love their wives

**[EPH 5:25](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25)** *See also* [GEN 24:67](https://ref.ly/Gen24:67); [1SA 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:5); [ECC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:9); [HOS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1); [EPH 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:28-Eph5:29), [EPH 5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:33); [COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19)

### Wives are to submit to their husbands

**[GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16)**; **[EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24)** *See also* [COL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Col3:18); [TIT 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4-Titus2:5); [1PE 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:6)

Celibacy as a calling from God
------------------------------

**[1CO 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:7-1Cor7:8)** *See also* [JER 16:2](https://ref.ly/Jer16:2); [MAT 19:10–19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:10-Matt19:12); [1CO 7:36–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36-1Cor7:38) *Whilst Paul commends the unmarried state, for those not so called it is no sin to marry*; [1TI 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:11-1Tim5:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 19:4; Mark 10:6; Genesis 1:27; 1 Corinthians 11:11-12; Genesis 2:18; Genesis 2:20-22; Genesis 3:12; Proverbs 31:10-12; Genesis 2:23-24; Matthew 19:5; Mark 10:7-8; 1 Corinthians 7:2; Ephesians 5:31; Matthew 19:6; Mark 10:9; Romans 7:2; 1 Corinthians 7:39; Malachi 2:15; 1 Corinthians 7:14; Matthew 22:30; Mark 12:25; Luke 20:34-35; 1 Corinthians 7:29-31; Malachi 2:14; Proverbs 2:17; Ezekiel 16:8; 1 Corinthians 7:9; Genesis 29:21; Genesis 22:16-17; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; Deuteronomy 22:13-21; Song of Songs 2:16; Deuteronomy 22:22-24; Proverbs 5:15-19; 1 Corinthians 6:16; 1 Corinthians 7:3-5; Genesis 29:20; Genesis 34:3-4; 1 Samuel 18:20; Ephesians 5:25; Genesis 24:67; 1 Samuel 1:5; Ecclesiastes 9:9; Hosea 3:1; Ephesians 5:28-29; Ephesians 5:33; Colossians 3:19; Genesis 3:16; Ephesians 5:22-24; Colossians 3:18; Titus 2:4-5; 1 Peter 3:1-6; 1 Corinthians 7:7-8; Jeremiah 16:2; Matthew 19:10-12; 1 Corinthians 7:36-38; 1 Timothy 5:11-14
* **Associated Articles:** 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5707: male and female (ID: `5707@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5717: monogamy (ID: `5717@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5735: sexuality (ID: `5735@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5736: singleness (ID: `5736@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5740: virgin (ID: `5740@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5959: submission (ID: `5959@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8248: faithfulness (ID: `8248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8299: love, in relationships (ID: `8299@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5710: marriage, customs concerning (id: 5710)

Marriage between two individuals united their families and involved the whole community. Parents, particularly fathers, took an active part in selecting partners for their children. There was often a long period of betrothal; wedding celebrations were usually elaborate and might last a week or more.

The choice of a marriage partner
--------------------------------

### Parents usually chose a suitable partner

**[GEN 38:6](https://ref.ly/Gen38:6)** *See also* [GEN 24:2–24:4](https://ref.ly/Gen24:2-Gen24:4), [GEN 21:21](https://ref.ly/Gen21:21) *Occasionally mothers made the wedding arrangements.*

### The wishes of the couple

**[GEN 24:57–24:58](https://ref.ly/Gen24:57-Gen24:58)** *Rebekah is consulted about her proposed marriage to Isaac.* *See also* [GEN 34:4](https://ref.ly/Gen34:4), [GEN 34:8–34:9](https://ref.ly/Gen34:8-Gen34:9); [JDG 14:2](https://ref.ly/Judg14:2); [1SA 18:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:20)

The bride\-price
----------------

### Daughters were \`\`given'' by their fathers

[GEN 29:28](https://ref.ly/Gen29:28); ; [EXO 2:21](https://ref.ly/Exod2:21); ; [1SA 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:21)

### Fathers often received payment

**[GEN 29:18](https://ref.ly/Gen29:18), [GEN 34:12](https://ref.ly/Gen34:12)** *See also* [GEN 29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30); [EXO 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16-Exod22:17); [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29); [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17), [1SA 18:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:27)

### Daughters were given as a reward

**[JDG 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Judg1:12-Judg1:13)** parallel [JOS 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Josh15:16-Josh15:17) *See also* [1SA 18:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:25)

The exchange of wedding gifts
-----------------------------

### Gifts from the bridegroom's family

[GEN 24:53](https://ref.ly/Gen24:53)

### Gifts from the bride's father

Servants given to the bride as a dowry: [GEN 24:59](https://ref.ly/Gen24:59), [GEN 29:24](https://ref.ly/Gen29:24), [GEN 29:29](https://ref.ly/Gen29:29); [JDG 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:14-Judg1:15) parallel [JOS 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Josh15:18-Josh15:19); ; [1KI 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:16)

The period of betrothal
-----------------------

### Betrothal followed payment of the bride\-price

**[2SA 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:14)** *See also* [GEN 29:19–29:20](https://ref.ly/Gen29:19-Gen29:20) *Jacob worked for Laban during his period of betrothal to Rachel.*

### A betrothed couple were regarded as husband and wife

[GEN 19:14](https://ref.ly/Gen19:14); ; [DEU 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:24) *Unfaithfulness during the period of betrothal is treated as adultery*; ; [MAT 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:20) *Divorce is required to break a betrothal.*

### Sexual relationships took place only after the wedding

**[GEN 29:21](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21)** [JOL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Joel1:8); ; [LUK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Luke1:27); [MAT 1:25](https://ref.ly/Matt1:25); ; [1CO 7:36–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36-1Cor7:38)

Soldiers and marriage
---------------------

[DEU 20:7](https://ref.ly/Deut20:7) *Engaged soldiers were to marry before going into battle*; ; [DEU 24:5](https://ref.ly/Deut24:5) *Recently married soldiers were exempted from military service for a year.*

The wedding celebrations
------------------------

### The bride wore an elaborate dress and jewellery

**[EZK 16:9–16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:9-Ezek16:13)** *An allegorical description of God dressing Jerusalem as a bride.* *See also* [PSA 45:13](https://ref.ly/Ps45:13); [ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10); [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32); [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

### Attendants on the bride and groom

**[PSA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ps45:14)**; **[JHN 3:29](https://ref.ly/John3:29)** *See also* [JDG 14:11](https://ref.ly/Judg14:11), [JDG 14:20](https://ref.ly/Judg14:20); [MAT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:19](https://ref.ly/Mark2:19), [LUK 5:34](https://ref.ly/Luke5:34); [MAT 25:1–25:10](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:10)

### The wedding banquet

[GEN 29:22](https://ref.ly/Gen29:22); ; [JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10); ; [MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2); ; [REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9)

### Wedding festivities

[PSA 78:63](https://ref.ly/Ps78:63); ; [ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5); ; [JER 7:34](https://ref.ly/Jer7:34), [JER 33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:11)

### The invitation to a wedding

[MAT 22:3–22:4](https://ref.ly/Matt22:3-Matt22:4) *Invited guests were called when the feast was ready.*; [MAT 22:5–22:7](https://ref.ly/Matt22:5-Matt22:7); ; [LUK 14:18–14:21](https://ref.ly/Luke14:18-Luke14:21); [MAT 22:11–22:12](https://ref.ly/Matt22:11-Matt22:12) *Guests were expected to dress appropriately.*

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 38:6; Genesis 24:2-4; Genesis 21:21; Genesis 24:57-58; Genesis 34:4; Genesis 34:8-9; Judges 14:2; 1 Samuel 18:20; Genesis 29:28; Exodus 2:21; 1 Samuel 18:21; Genesis 29:18; Genesis 34:12; Genesis 29:30; Exodus 22:16-17; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; 1 Samuel 18:17; 1 Samuel 18:27; Judges 1:12-13; Joshua 15:16-17; 1 Samuel 18:25; Genesis 24:53; Genesis 24:59; Genesis 29:24; Genesis 29:29; Judges 1:14-15; Joshua 15:18-19; 1 Kings 9:16; 2 Samuel 3:14; Genesis 29:19-20; Genesis 19:14; Deuteronomy 22:23-24; Matthew 1:18-20; Genesis 29:21; Joel 1:8; Luke 1:27; Matthew 1:25; 1 Corinthians 7:36-38; Deuteronomy 20:7; Deuteronomy 24:5; Ezekiel 16:9-13; Psalms 45:13; Isaiah 61:10; Jeremiah 2:32; Revelation 21:2; Psalms 45:14; John 3:29; Judges 14:11; Judges 14:20; Matthew 9:15; Mark 2:19; Luke 5:34; Matthew 25:1-10; Genesis 29:22; Judges 14:10; Matthew 22:2; Revelation 19:9; Psalms 78:63; Isaiah 62:5; Jeremiah 7:34; Jeremiah 33:11; Matthew 22:3-4; Matthew 22:5-7; Luke 14:18-21; Matthew 22:11-12
* **Associated Articles:** 4410: banquets (ID: `4410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5654: betrothal (ID: `5654@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5659: bride (ID: `5659@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5660: bridegroom (ID: `5660@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5679: dowry (ID: `5679@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5684: fathers (ID: `5684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5742: wedding (ID: `5742@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5711: marriage, restrictions concerning (id: 5711)

The OT law forbade intermarriage with people who worshipped idols because it threatened the covenant relationship with God and his people. Marriage with close relations was also forbidden. Remarriage is permissible following the death of a spouse and, in certain circumstances, following divorce.

Intermarriage with foreigners
-----------------------------

### Examples of marriages to foreigners

[GEN 38:2](https://ref.ly/Gen38:2), [GEN 41:45](https://ref.ly/Gen41:45); ; [EXO 2:21](https://ref.ly/Exod2:21); ; [LEV 24:10](https://ref.ly/Lev24:10); ; [RUT 1:4](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:4); ; [1CH 2:34–2:35](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:34-1Chr2:35); ; [EZR 10:18–10:44](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:18-Ezra10:44)

### Warnings against marrying foreigners

**[EXO 34:16](https://ref.ly/Exod34:16)** *See also* [DEU 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Deut7:3-Deut7:4); [JOS 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12-Josh23:13)

### Intermarriage with foreigners led to idolatry

**[JDG 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Judg3:5-Judg3:6)**; **[1KI 11:1–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:8)** *Royal marriages to foreigners brought disastrous consequences.* *See also* [1KI 16:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:31); [MAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Mal2:11)EZR [9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Mal9:1-Mal9:2), [EZR 9:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:14), [EZR 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:1-Ezra10:2); [NEH 13:23–13:27](https://ref.ly/Neh13:23-Neh13:27)

### Parents sought to avoid their children marrying foreigners

**[NEH 10:30](https://ref.ly/Neh10:30)** *See also* [GEN 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Gen24:3-Gen24:4) *Abraham seeks a bride for Isaac from among his own people.*[GEN 26:34–26:35](https://ref.ly/Gen26:34-Gen26:35), [GEN 27:46](https://ref.ly/Gen27:46)[GEN 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Gen28:1-Gen28:2) *Isaac forbids Jacob to marry a Canaanite woman*; [JDG 14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:3) *Samson's parents encourage him to marry an Israelite.*

### Marriage to foreigners may be permissible

[RUT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13) *Boaz' marriage to Ruth the Moabitess was acceptable because Ruth embraced the Israelite faith. Restrictions were on the grounds of religion not of race.*

### Foreign wives were put aside after the exile

**[EZR 10:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:3)** *See also* [EZR 10:10–10:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:10-Ezra10:17)

Marriages between Christians and unbelievers
--------------------------------------------

### Christians should not marry unbelievers

[1CO 7:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:39); ; [2CO 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14)

### Christians should not leave an unbelieving spouse

[1CO 7:12–7:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:12-1Cor7:16)

Restrictions on marriage to close relatives
-------------------------------------------

### Sexual relations with close relatives is forbidden

**[LEV 18:6–18:18](https://ref.ly/Lev18:6-Lev18:18)** *See also* [LEV 20:11–20:12](https://ref.ly/Lev20:11-Lev20:12), [LEV 20:14](https://ref.ly/Lev20:14), [LEV 20:19–20:21](https://ref.ly/Lev20:19-Lev20:21); [DEU 22:30](https://ref.ly/Deut22:30); [EZK 22:10–22:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:10-Ezek22:11); [MAT 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3-Matt14:4) parallel [MRK 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:18)

### Examples of marriage to close relatives

[GEN 20:12](https://ref.ly/Gen20:12) *Abraham was married to his half\-sister*; ; [2SA 13:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:13) *Tamar suggests marriage with her half\-brother, Amnon.*

Levirate marriage
-----------------

### The levirate law was instituted to preserve the dead father's name

**[DEU 25:5–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:10)**; **[RUT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:10)** *Levirate marriage refers to the legal obligation of a brother\-in\-law to produce heirs for his dead brother by marrying his widow. When there was no brother\-in\-law responsibility fell to a near relative, also described as a “kinsman\-redeemer”.* *See also* [GEN 38:8](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8), [GEN 38:11](https://ref.ly/Gen38:11); [RUT 1:11–1:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:11-Ruth1:13), [RUT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:9), [RUT 4:5](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:5); [MAT 22:24–22:26](https://ref.ly/Matt22:24-Matt22:26) parallels [MRK 12:19–12:22](https://ref.ly/Mark12:19-Mark12:22), [LUK 20:28–20:31](https://ref.ly/Luke20:28-Luke20:31)

### Unwillingness to fulfil the levirate law

[GEN 38:9](https://ref.ly/Gen38:9), [GEN 38:14](https://ref.ly/Gen38:14), [GEN 38:26](https://ref.ly/Gen38:26); ; [RUT 4:6](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:6)

Regulations governing seduction and rape
----------------------------------------

[EXO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16); ; [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29)

Remarriage
----------

### Widows are free to remarry

**[ROM 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Rom7:2-Rom7:3)** *See also* [RUT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:9); [1CO 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8-1Cor7:9); [1TI 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:14)

### Remarriage after divorce may be adultery

**[LUK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Luke16:18)** parallels [MAT 19:9](https://ref.ly/Matt19:9), [MRK 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Mark10:11-Mark10:12) *See also* [1CO 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:10-1Cor7:11)

### Remarriage after divorce permissible in certain circumstances

[DEU 24:1–24:4](https://ref.ly/Deut24:1-Deut24:4); ; [MAT 5:32](https://ref.ly/Matt5:32); ; [1CO 7:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:15), [1CO 7:27–7:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:27-1Cor7:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 38:2; Genesis 41:45; Exodus 2:21; Leviticus 24:10; Ruth 1:4; 1 Chronicles 2:34-35; Ezra 10:18-44; Exodus 34:16; Deuteronomy 7:3-4; Joshua 23:12-13; Judges 3:5-6; 1 Kings 11:1-8; 1 Kings 16:31; Malachi 2:11; Malachi 9:1-2; Ezra 9:14; Ezra 10:1-2; Nehemiah 13:23-27; Nehemiah 10:30; Genesis 24:3-4; Genesis 26:34-35; Genesis 27:46; Genesis 28:1-2; Judges 14:3; Ruth 4:13; Ezra 10:3; Ezra 10:10-17; 1 Corinthians 7:39; 2 Corinthians 6:14; 1 Corinthians 7:12-16; Leviticus 18:6-18; Leviticus 20:11-12; Leviticus 20:14; Leviticus 20:19-21; Deuteronomy 22:30; Ezekiel 22:10-11; Matthew 14:3-4; Mark 6:17-18; Genesis 20:12; 2 Samuel 13:13; Deuteronomy 25:5-10; Ruth 4:10; Genesis 38:8; Genesis 38:11; Ruth 1:11-13; Ruth 3:9; Ruth 4:5; Matthew 22:24-26; Mark 12:19-22; Luke 20:28-31; Genesis 38:9; Genesis 38:14; Genesis 38:26; Ruth 4:6; Exodus 22:16; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; Romans 7:2-3; Ruth 1:9; 1 Corinthians 7:8-9; 1 Timothy 5:14; Luke 16:18; Matthew 19:9; Mark 10:11-12; 1 Corinthians 7:10-11; Deuteronomy 24:1-4; Matthew 5:32; 1 Corinthians 7:15; 1 Corinthians 7:27-28
* **Associated Articles:** 5661: brothers (ID: `5661@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5672: concubines (ID: `5672@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5675: divorce (ID: `5675@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5703: inheritance (ID: `5703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5732: polygamy (ID: `5732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5743: widows (ID: `5743@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6240: rape (ID: `6240@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6241: seduction (ID: `6241@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7388: kinsman-redeemer (ID: `7388@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7530: foreigners (ID: `7530@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5712: marriage, between God and his people (id: 5712)

Marriage is used to describe the relationship between God and Israel in the OT and between Jesus Christ and the church in the NT. Contemplating marriage deepens understanding of God's love for his people; examining God's covenant love for his people similarly enriches an understanding of marriage.

God's marriage relationship with Israel
---------------------------------------

### God's marriage covenant with Israel

**[EZK 16:8–16:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8-Ezek16:14)** *The covenant at Sinai was seen as a form of marriage.* *See also* [JER 31:32](https://ref.ly/Jer31:32); [EZK 16:59–16:60](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:59-Ezek16:60)

### God as Israel's husband

**[ISA 54:5](https://ref.ly/Isa54:5)** *See also* [HOS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Hos2:7); [JOL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Joel1:8)

### Israel's early devotion

**[JER 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2)** *See also* [EZK 16:43](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:43); [HOS 2:15](https://ref.ly/Hos2:15)

The breakdown of God's marriage to Israel
-----------------------------------------

### Israel's adultery

**[JER 3:20](https://ref.ly/Jer3:20)** *See also* [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32); [EZK 16:32–16:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:32-Ezek16:34); [HOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2), [HOS 9:1](https://ref.ly/Hos9:1)

### Israel's alienation from God is likened to a divorce

**[HOS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2)** *Israel's unfaithfulness led to a form of divorce between God and his people. This was sometimes identified with the exile.* *See also* [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1); [JER 3:6–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jer3:6-Jer3:10)

The renewal of God's marriage to Israel
---------------------------------------

### God calls his bride to return

**[JER 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Jer3:12-Jer3:14)**; **[HOS 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1-Hos3:3)** *See also* [ISA 54:6–54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:6-Isa54:8); [HOS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14)

### The renewed relationship

**[ISA 62:4–62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:4-Isa62:5)** *See also* [JER 31:31–31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:31-Jer31:33); [EZK 16:62](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:62); [HOS 2:16](https://ref.ly/Hos2:16), [HOS 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Hos2:19-Hos2:20)

Jesus Christ's marriage relationship with the church
----------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's love as a model for marriage

**[EPH 5:25–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:33)**

### Jesus Christ is described as a bridegroom

**[JHN 3:29](https://ref.ly/John3:29)** *John the Baptist describes Jesus Christ as the bridegroom and himself as the best man.* *See also* [MAT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Mark2:19-Mark2:20), [LUK 5:34–5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:34-Luke5:35); [MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2), [MAT 25:1–25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:13)

### The church as Christ's bride

**[2CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2)**; **[REV 19:7–19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7-Rev19:9)** *See also* [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2), [REV 21:9–21:10](https://ref.ly/Rev21:9-Rev21:10), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 16:8-14; Jeremiah 31:32; Ezekiel 16:59-60; Isaiah 54:5; Hosea 2:7; Joel 1:8; Jeremiah 2:2; Ezekiel 16:43; Hosea 2:15; Jeremiah 3:20; Jeremiah 2:32; Ezekiel 16:32-34; Hosea 1:2; Hosea 9:1; Hosea 2:2; Isaiah 50:1; Jeremiah 3:6-10; Jeremiah 3:12-14; Hosea 3:1-3; Isaiah 54:6-8; Hosea 2:14; Isaiah 62:4-5; Jeremiah 31:31-33; Ezekiel 16:62; Hosea 2:16; Hosea 2:19-20; Ephesians 5:25-33; John 3:29; Matthew 9:15; Mark 2:19-20; Luke 5:34-35; Matthew 22:2; Matthew 25:1-13; 2 Corinthians 11:2; Revelation 19:7-9; Revelation 21:2; Revelation 21:9-10; Revelation 22:17
* **Associated Articles:** 1030: God, compassion of (ID: `1030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1035: God, faithfulness of (ID: `1035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1085: God, love of (ID: `1085@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1349: covenant, God's at Sinai (ID: `1349@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1680: types (ID: `1680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6243: adultery, spiritual (ID: `6243@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6740: returning to God (ID: `6740@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7020: church, the (ID: `7020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8251: faithfulness, to God (ID: `8251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8840: unfaithfulness, to God (ID: `8840@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5714: men (id: 5714)

God created both men and women in his image and likeness. Scripture portrays men in both their strengths and weaknesses, their successes and failures. While noting that they are incomplete without women, and share equally in the privileges and tasks entrusted to them by God, men have nevertheless been given specific responsibilities by God.

The place of men
----------------

### Men are equal with women in creation

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 5:2](https://ref.ly/Gen5:2)

### Men are incomplete without women

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [GEN 2:21–2:25](https://ref.ly/Gen2:21-Gen2:25); [1CO 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:11-1Cor11:12)

### Absence of men in society seen as a curse

**[ISA 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Isa3:1-Isa3:3)** *See also* [LAM 5:14](https://ref.ly/Lam5:14); [EZK 17:11–17:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:11-Ezek17:14)

### Men are equal with women in redemption

**[GAL 3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:28)** *See also* [ACT 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17-Acts2:18); [JOL 2:28–2:29](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28-Joel2:29)

The role of men
---------------

### As leaders in marriage and family life

**[EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24)** *See also* [1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3); [COL 3:18–3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:18-Col3:21)

### As leaders in Israelite society and worship

**[NUM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Num1:16)** *See also* [EXO 34:23–34:24](https://ref.ly/Exod34:23-Exod34:24); [NUM 3:10–3:15](https://ref.ly/Num3:10-Num3:15); [DEU 1:9–1:15](https://ref.ly/Deut1:9-Deut1:15); [1SA 8:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:1); [EZR 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:16-Ezra10:17)

### As leaders in the early church

**[1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2)** *See also* [ACT 6:2–6:6](https://ref.ly/Acts6:2-Acts6:6); [1TI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:12) *The role of teaching and exercising authority is here restricted to men. Interpretations vary as to whether Paul is reflecting cultural circumstance or establishing theological principle*; [1TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8)

The character requirements of men
---------------------------------

### To be prayerful and godly

**[1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8)** *See also* [DAN 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10-Dan6:11); [1TI 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:7-1Tim4:8), [1TI 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11-1Tim6:12)

### To be courageous

**[1CO 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:13)** *While courage can be exercised by all, it is identified here as a specifically male characteristic.* *See also* [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13), [ACT 27:21–27:25](https://ref.ly/Acts27:21-Acts27:25)

### To be determined and decisive

**[MAT 11:12](https://ref.ly/Matt11:12)** *See also* [1CH 12:38](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:38)

### To be humble

**[1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)** *See also* [NUM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Num12:3); [DAN 10:12](https://ref.ly/Dan10:12); [MAT 11:29](https://ref.ly/Matt11:29)

### To love their wives

**[COL 3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:19)** *See also* [PRO 5:15–5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:15-Prov5:19); [EPH 5:25–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:33)

### To be good fathers to their children

**[EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)** *See also* [GEN 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3-Gen37:4) *Jacob's favouritism was the cause of resentment developing in the family*; [1SA 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:12-1Sam3:13) *Eli is rebuked for having failed to father his sons properly*; [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

### To exercise proper sexual restraint

**[1TH 4:3–4:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:7)** *That Paul is particularly thinking of men here seems apparent from the NIV footnote at verse 4\.* *See also* [LEV 18:1–18:22](https://ref.ly/Lev18:1-Lev18:22); [PRO 6:23–6:29](https://ref.ly/Prov6:23-Prov6:29); [MAT 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:27-Matt5:28); [1CO 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:2), [1CO 7:2–7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:2-1Cor7:5)

Examples of godly men
---------------------

[GEN 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gen6:9) *Noah*; ; [GEN 22:1–22:12](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:12) *Abraham*; ; [EXO 32:30–32:32](https://ref.ly/Exod32:30-Exod32:32) *Moses*; ; [1SA 24:1–24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1-1Sam24:7) *David*; ; [2KI 18:1–18:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:1-2Kgs18:7) *Hezekiah*; ; [JOB 1:1–1:8](https://ref.ly/Job1:1-Job1:8) *Job*; ; [DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10) *Daniel*; ; [ACT 4:13–4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13-Acts4:20) *Peter and John*; ; [ACT 7:54–7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54-Acts7:60) *Stephen*; ; [HEB 11:4–11:28](https://ref.ly/Heb11:4-Heb11:28) *OT heroes of faith*; ; [JAS 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Jas5:17-Jas5:18) *Elijah*

Examples of godly men who had moments of failure
------------------------------------------------

[GEN 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Gen20:1-Gen20:3) *Abraham was deceitful*; ; [NUM 20:2–20:12](https://ref.ly/Num20:2-Num20:12) *Moses and Aaron showed unbelief*; ; [2SA 11:1–11:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:1-2Sam11:27) *David committed adultery with Bathsheba and plotted Uriah's death*; ; [1KI 19:1–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:5) *Elijah became fearful and wanted to die*; ; [JON 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:3) *Jonah ran from God's call on his life*; ; [MAT 26:69–26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:69-Matt26:75) parallels [MRK 14:66–14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:66-Mark14:72), [LUK 22:55–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:55-Luke22:62), [JHN 18:16–18:18](https://ref.ly/John18:16-John18:18), [JHN 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:25-John18:27) *Peter denied Jesus Christ*; ; [ACT 16:36–16:40](https://ref.ly/Acts16:36-Acts16:40) *Paul and Barnabas fell into sharp disagreement.*

Examples of ungodly men
-----------------------

[GEN 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/Gen19:4-Gen19:5) *The ungodliness of the men of Sodom lay in sexual immorality*; ; [GEN 38:8–38:10](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8-Gen38:10) *Onan's ungodliness lay in failing to fulfil his obligations to his brother's wife*; ; [NUM 16:1–16:35](https://ref.ly/Num16:1-Num16:35) *Korah's ungodliness lay in rebellion against God's leaders*; ; [1SA 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:12) *Eli's sons' ungodliness lay in their abuse of the sacrifices*; ; [1KI 16:29–16:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:29-1Kgs16:33) *Ahab's ungodliness lay in intermarriage and idolatry*; ; [2KI 21:1–21:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:1-2Kgs21:9) parallel [2CH 33:1–33:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:1-2Chr33:9) *Manasseh's ungodliness lay in idolatry, divination and human sacrifice*; ; [MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16) *Herod's ungodliness lay in the wholesale slaughter of infants*; ; [MAT 14:3–14:11](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3-Matt14:11) parallel [MRK 6:17–6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:28) *Herod the tetrarch's ungodliness lay in immorality and murder*; ; [ACT 8:9–8:23](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9-Acts8:23) *Simon's ungodliness lay in the practice of magic*; ; [JUD 1:4–1:19](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4-Jude1:19) *The ungodliness of men in the church lay in their rejection of apostolic doctrine and lifestyle.*

Examples of fearful men
-----------------------

[GEN 26:7–26:9](https://ref.ly/Gen26:7-Gen26:9) *Isaac's fear of the Philistines led him to lie*; ; [NUM 13:26–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:26-Num13:33) *The spies' fear led them to give a bad report*; ; [1SA 28:5–28:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:5-1Sam28:7) *Saul's fear of the Philistines led him to enquire of a medium when the LORD did not answer him*; ; [2SA 3:7–3:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:7-2Sam3:11) *Ish\-Bosheth's fear of his brother led him to stay silent concerning sin*; ; [JHN 19:4–19:16](https://ref.ly/John19:4-John19:16) parallels [MAT 27:19–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:19-Matt27:26), [MRK 15:9–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:9-Mark15:15), [LUK 23:13–23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:13-Luke23:25) *Pilate's fear of the crowd led him to condemn Jesus Christ, even though he knew he was innocent*; ; [ACT 24:24–24:25](https://ref.ly/Acts24:24-Acts24:25) *Felix' fear arose from the message of the gospel.*

Examples of weak men
--------------------

[LEV 26:36–26:39](https://ref.ly/Lev26:36-Lev26:39) *Men who are disobedient to God will be weak*; ; [1SA 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:12-1Sam3:13) *Eli's weakness lay in failing to restrain his sons*; ; [1KI 21:1–21:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:1-1Kgs21:16) *Ahab's weakness lay in his allowing his wife to rule his life and his kingdom.*

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 1:27; Genesis 5:2; Genesis 2:18; Genesis 2:21-25; 1 Corinthians 11:11-12; Isaiah 3:1-3; Lamentations 5:14; Ezekiel 17:11-14; Galatians 3:28; Acts 2:17-18; Joel 2:28-29; Ephesians 5:22-24; 1 Corinthians 11:3; Colossians 3:18-21; Numbers 1:16; Exodus 34:23-24; Numbers 3:10-15; Deuteronomy 1:9-15; 1 Samuel 8:1; Ezra 10:16-17; 1 Timothy 3:2; Acts 6:2-6; 1 Timothy 2:12; 1 Timothy 3:8; 1 Timothy 2:8; Daniel 6:10-11; 1 Timothy 4:7-8; 1 Timothy 6:11-12; 1 Corinthians 16:13; Acts 4:13; Acts 27:21-25; Matthew 11:12; 1 Chronicles 12:38; 1 Peter 5:5; Numbers 12:3; Daniel 10:12; Matthew 11:29; Colossians 3:19; Proverbs 5:15-19; Ephesians 5:25-33; Ephesians 6:4; Genesis 37:3-4; 1 Samuel 3:12-13; Colossians 3:21; 1 Thessalonians 4:3-7; Leviticus 18:1-22; Proverbs 6:23-29; Matthew 5:27-28; 1 Corinthians 5:1-2; 1 Corinthians 7:2-5; Genesis 6:9; Genesis 22:1-12; Exodus 32:30-32; 1 Samuel 24:1-7; 2 Kings 18:1-7; Job 1:1-8; Daniel 6:10; Acts 4:13-20; Acts 7:54-60; Hebrews 11:4-28; James 5:17-18; Genesis 20:1-3; Numbers 20:2-12; 2 Samuel 11:1-27; 1 Kings 19:1-5; Jonah 1:1-3; Matthew 26:69-75; Mark 14:66-72; Luke 22:55-62; John 18:16-18; John 18:25-27; Acts 16:36-40; Genesis 19:4-5; Genesis 38:8-10; Numbers 16:1-35; 1 Samuel 2:12; 1 Kings 16:29-33; 2 Kings 21:1-9; 2 Chronicles 33:1-9; Matthew 2:16; Matthew 14:3-11; Mark 6:17-28; Acts 8:9-23; Jude 1:4-19; Genesis 26:7-9; Numbers 13:26-33; 1 Samuel 28:5-7; 2 Samuel 3:7-11; John 19:4-16; Matthew 27:19-26; Mark 15:9-15; Luke 23:13-25; Acts 24:24-25; Leviticus 26:36-39; 1 Kings 21:1-16

## 5715: menstruation (id: 5715)

A woman was considered ceremonially unclean during menstruation
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 15:19–15:23](https://ref.ly/Lev15:19-Lev15:23)** *See also* [LEV 12:1–12:5](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:5), [LEV 15:25–15:30](https://ref.ly/Lev15:25-Lev15:30); [ISA 30:22](https://ref.ly/Isa30:22)

Sexual intercourse during menstruation forbidden
------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 18:19](https://ref.ly/Lev18:19)** *See also* [LEV 20:18](https://ref.ly/Lev20:18); [EZK 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:5-Ezek18:6), [EZK 22:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:10)

The discomfort of menstruation
------------------------------

[GEN 31:35](https://ref.ly/Gen31:35)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 15:19-23; Leviticus 12:1-5; Leviticus 15:25-30; Isaiah 30:22; Leviticus 18:19; Leviticus 20:18; Ezekiel 18:5-6; Ezekiel 22:10; Genesis 31:35
* **Associated Articles:** 6236: sexual sin (ID: `6236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7424: ritual law (ID: `7424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7426: ritual washing (ID: `7426@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5716: middle age (id: 5716)

The period between youth and old age. Middle age is marked by physical strength and experience and should bring maturity and stability. Many of God's servants began ministry in middle age, often after having been prepared in their youth. Middle age may also bring complacency and the temptation to leave the devotion and commitments of youth.

Middle age defined
------------------

### Adulthood reckoned from age 20

**[NUM 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:2-Num1:3)** *See also* [EXO 30:14](https://ref.ly/Exod30:14), [EXO 38:26](https://ref.ly/Exod38:26); [NUM 26:2–26:4](https://ref.ly/Num26:2-Num26:4)**[1CH 23:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:24)** *See also* [2CH 31:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:17); [EZR 3:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8)

### Full maturity reckoned from age 30

**[LUK 3:23](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23)** *See also* [NUM 4:30](https://ref.ly/Num4:30), [NUM 4:43](https://ref.ly/Num4:43)

Characteristics of middle age
-----------------------------

### Physical strength

**[LEV 27:3–27:7](https://ref.ly/Lev27:3-Lev27:7)** *Strength is greatest between 20 and 60\.* *See also* [NUM 1:45](https://ref.ly/Num1:45) *Men served in the army from age 20*; [JOS 14:10](https://ref.ly/Josh14:10) *Caleb, in his prime at 40, is still strong at 85\.*

### Growth in wisdom and experience

**[ECC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:16)** *See also* [1SA 17:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:33); [2SA 17:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:8); [2CH 17:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:13)**[1CH 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:5)** *See also* [1CH 29:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1); [PRO 7:7](https://ref.ly/Prov7:7)

### Maturity and stability

**[EPH 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:13-Eph4:14)** *See also* [1CO 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6); [HEB 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:11-Heb5:14); [JAS 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:4)

The responsibilities of middle age
----------------------------------

### The need to be prepared for these responsibilities

**[PRO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Prov22:6)** *See also* [PRO 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1-Prov4:4) *Solomon passes on wisdom learned from his father.***[LUK 1:80](https://ref.ly/Luke1:80)**[1SA 2:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:26); [LUK 2:40](https://ref.ly/Luke2:40)

### The need to train for service

**[NUM 8:24](https://ref.ly/Num8:24)** *The period from 25 to 30 (see [Nu 4:3](https://ref.ly/Num4:3)) may have been a period of apprenticeship.* *See also* [1CH 23:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:27) *In later years the apprenticeship may have started at 20*; [NUM 11:28](https://ref.ly/Num11:28)

### The start of service in middle age

**[NUM 4:3](https://ref.ly/Num4:3)** *See also* [ACT 7:23](https://ref.ly/Acts7:23) *Moses showed concern for his fellow Israelites at age 40*; [JOS 14:7](https://ref.ly/Josh14:7) *Caleb was 40 when he explored Canaan*; [1SA 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:3-1Sam7:4) *Samuel became judge over Israel in his thirties*; [LUK 3:3](https://ref.ly/Luke3:3) *John the Baptist began his public ministry at about 30*; [JHN 8:57](https://ref.ly/John8:57) *Jesus Christ had not achieved the status of wisdom and authority associated with old age in Israel*; [1TI 3:1–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1-1Tim3:13) *The mention of families in the instructions to elders and deacons suggests that these leaders were of middle age.*

### Rulers coming to office in middle age

[GEN 41:46](https://ref.ly/Gen41:46) *Joseph*; ; [1SA 13:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:1) *Saul*; ; [2SA 5:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:4) *David*; ; [2SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:10) *Ish\-Bosheth*; ; [2CH 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:13) parallel [1KI 14:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:21) *Rehoboam*; ; [2CH 20:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:31) *Jehoshaphat*

Temptations of middle age
-------------------------

### Complacency

[2SA 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:1); ; [ISA 32:9–32:11](https://ref.ly/Isa32:9-Isa32:11); ; [AMO 6:1](https://ref.ly/Amos6:1)

### Worldly distractions

[2SA 11:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:4); ; [1KI 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:3-1Kgs11:4); ; [ECC 2:1–2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1-Eccl2:8); ; [LUK 12:18–12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:18-Luke12:19)

### Turning from the commitment of youth

[PRO 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Prov2:16-Prov2:17); ; [MAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 1:2-3; Exodus 30:14; Exodus 38:26; Numbers 26:2-4; 1 Chronicles 23:24; 2 Chronicles 31:17; Ezra 3:8; Luke 3:23; Numbers 4:30; Numbers 4:43; Leviticus 27:3-7; Numbers 1:45; Joshua 14:10; Ecclesiastes 1:16; 1 Samuel 17:33; 2 Samuel 17:8; 2 Chronicles 17:13; 1 Chronicles 22:5; 1 Chronicles 29:1; Proverbs 7:7; Ephesians 4:13-14; 1 Corinthians 2:6; Hebrews 5:11-14; James 1:4; Proverbs 22:6; Proverbs 4:1-4; Luke 1:80; 1 Samuel 2:26; Luke 2:40; Numbers 8:24; Numbers 4:3; 1 Chronicles 23:27; Numbers 11:28; Acts 7:23; Joshua 14:7; 1 Samuel 7:3-4; Luke 3:3; John 8:57; 1 Timothy 3:1-13; Genesis 41:46; 1 Samuel 13:1; 2 Samuel 5:4; 2 Samuel 2:10; 2 Chronicles 12:13; 1 Kings 14:21; 2 Chronicles 20:31; 2 Samuel 11:1; Isaiah 32:9-11; Amos 6:1; 2 Samuel 11:4; 1 Kings 11:3-4; Ecclesiastes 2:1-8; Luke 12:18-19; Proverbs 2:16-17; Malachi 2:14
* **Associated Articles:** 4971: seasons, of life (ID: `4971@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5204: age (ID: `5204@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5810: complacency (ID: `5810@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5853: experience, of life (ID: `5853@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5902: maturity (ID: `5902@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5956: strength, human (ID: `5956@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6248: temptation (ID: `6248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8203: character (ID: `8203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8207: commitment (ID: `8207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8344: servanthood, in life of believers (ID: `8344@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8370: zeal (ID: `8370@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5717: monogamy (id: 5717)

The practice of having and remaining faithful to only one marriage partner at one time. It is set out as God's ideal for human relationships; an ideal demonstrated in the use of the marriage bond to portray the relationship between God and his people.

Monogamy as God's original intention for marriage
-------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:22-Gen2:24)** *See also* [MAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15); [MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9); [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

Monogamy is shown in God's relationship with his people
-------------------------------------------------------

### God's marriage with Israel

**[EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8)** *See also* [ISA 54:5–54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:5-Isa54:8); [JER 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2-Jer2:3); [HOS 2:16–2:20](https://ref.ly/Hos2:16-Hos2:20), [HOS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Hos3:1); [EPH 5:32–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:32-Eph5:33)

### God's exclusive commitment to Israel

**[DEU 14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:2)**; **[AMO 3:2](https://ref.ly/Amos3:2)** *See also* [EXO 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5-Exod19:6); [DEU 26:18](https://ref.ly/Deut26:18), [DEU 32:8](https://ref.ly/Deut32:8); [PSA 135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:4)

Monogamy in marriage
--------------------

### The commitment to a lifelong, loving companionship

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)**; **[PRO 5:15–5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:15-Prov5:19)** *The satisfaction that may be found in marriage makes marital unfaithfulness and polygamy unnecessary.* *See also* [PRO 2:17](https://ref.ly/Prov2:17); [ECC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:9); [MAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14)

### Polygamy undermines full commitment

**[DEU 21:15](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15)** *Favouritism within polygamous relationships undermines the close companionship which should characterise marriage.* *See also* [GEN 29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30); [1SA 1:1–1:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:1-1Sam1:8); [2CH 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:21-2Chr11:22)

Monogamy is implicit in teaching about divorce
----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 19:9](https://ref.ly/Matt19:9)** parallel [MRK 10:11](https://ref.ly/Mark10:11) *See also* [LUK 16:18](https://ref.ly/Luke16:18)

Monogamy is commanded
---------------------

**[1CO 7:2–7:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:2-1Cor7:4)** *This is an implicit, but clear prohibition of polygamy*; **[1TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:12)** *Monogamy is obligatory in leaders.* *See also* [1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2); [TIT 1:6](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:22-24; Malachi 2:15; Matthew 19:4-6; Mark 10:6-9; Ephesians 5:31; Ezekiel 16:8; Isaiah 54:5-8; Jeremiah 2:2-3; Hosea 2:16-20; Hosea 3:1; Ephesians 5:32-33; Deuteronomy 14:2; Amos 3:2; Exodus 19:5-6; Deuteronomy 26:18; Deuteronomy 32:8; Psalms 135:4; Genesis 2:18; Proverbs 5:15-19; Proverbs 2:17; Ecclesiastes 9:9; Malachi 2:14; Deuteronomy 21:15; Genesis 29:30; 1 Samuel 1:1-8; 2 Chronicles 11:21-22; Matthew 19:9; Mark 10:11; Luke 16:18; 1 Corinthians 7:2-4; 1 Timothy 3:12; 1 Timothy 3:2; Titus 1:6
* **Associated Articles:** 5675: divorce (ID: `5675@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5732: polygamy (ID: `5732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6242: adultery (ID: `6242@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5718: mothers (id: 5718)

The female parent of children. The role and influence of mothers are frequently stressed in Scripture, and their care for children is used as a picture of God's care for his people.

* **Associated Articles:** 5719: mothers, responsibilities of (ID: `5719@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5720: mothers, examples of (ID: `5720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5721: mothers, as a symbol (ID: `5721@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5719: mothers, responsibilities of (id: 5719)

The fundamental importance of motherhood is recognised throughout Scripture.

Motherhood and childbirth
-------------------------

### God plans happiness for women in motherhood

**[PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9)**

### The need for a mother's cleansing after childbirth

*See also* [LEV 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:8); [LUK 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22-Luke2:24)

A mother's relationship with her children
-----------------------------------------

### A mother loves and cares for her children

**[1TH 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:7)** *Paul's statement assumes that a mother's normal attitude towards her child is one of love and care.* *See also* [LUK 2:48](https://ref.ly/Luke2:48)

### A mother's role as teacher

**[PRO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8)** *See also* [PRO 6:20](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20); [2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:5)

### The responsibility to discipline children

**[PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15)**

The joy or sorrow of parents at their children's conduct
--------------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1)** *See also* [PRO 23:25](https://ref.ly/Prov23:25)

The negative influence of some mothers upon their children
----------------------------------------------------------

**[2CH 22:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:3)** *See also* [GEN 27:5–27:17](https://ref.ly/Gen27:5-Gen27:17); [1KI 22:52](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:52); [MAT 14:6–14:8](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6-Matt14:8), [MAT 20:20–20:22](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20-Matt20:22)

Children's reponsibility to honour their parents
------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *“Honour” implies respect, obedience and care for the parents' needs.* *See also* [LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3); [MAT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Matt19:19) parallels [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19), [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20); [EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3)

### This honour is due especially to mothers in old age

[PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)

Penalties for disobeying parents
--------------------------------

[EXO 21:15](https://ref.ly/Exod21:15), [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17); ; [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); ; [PRO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Prov20:20); ; [MAT 15:4–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4-Matt15:6) parallel [MRK 7:10–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10-Mark7:13)

Sexual relations with one's mother or mother\-in\-law are forbidden
-------------------------------------------------------------------

Upon marriage the mother\-child relationship becomes secondary
--------------------------------------------------------------

## 5721: mothers, as a symbol (id: 5721)

Motherhood is used in Scripture to illustrate the relationship between God and his people, to describe certain aspects of Israel and also to explain the difference between the two covenants.

God's tender care for his children is like a mother's
-----------------------------------------------------

### God's care like that of a mother bird

[DEU 32:11](https://ref.ly/Deut32:11); ; [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37) parallel [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34)

### God never forgets his children

**[ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)**

### Becoming a Christian is like birth

**[JHN 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/John3:5-John3:6)**

### The believer trusts God as a weaned child trusts his or her mother

*See also* [PSA 131:1–131:2](https://ref.ly/Ps131:1-Ps131:2); [ISA 66:13](https://ref.ly/Isa66:13)

Israel pictured as a mother
---------------------------

**[EZK 16:44–16:45](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:44-Ezek16:45)** *See also* [ISA 50:1](https://ref.ly/Isa50:1), [ISA 54:1](https://ref.ly/Isa54:1), [ISA 66:7–66:13](https://ref.ly/Isa66:7-Isa66:13); [EZK 16:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:3), [EZK 19:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:2), [EZK 19:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10); [HOS 2:2–2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2-Hos2:13)

-----------------------------------------------

## 5725: old age (id: 5725)

Living for a relatively long time was regarded as a blessing from God and the aged in society are to be respected. Advancing years can bring greater wisdom and in both the OT and NT elders are given authority and leadership. Old age brings physical weakness and Scripture recognises this but states God's ability to strengthen and sustain the aged.

* **Associated Articles:** 5726: old age, attainment of (ID: `5726@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5727: old age, attitudes to (ID: `5727@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5726: old age, attainment of (id: 5726)

Attaining old age is regarded as a blessing from God, often as a reward for obedience. The physical weakness brought by old age is recognised; so too is God's ability to strengthen and sustain the aged. Old age is often accompanied by a growth in wisdom and discernment.

Life expectancy in the OT
-------------------------

### Before the flood

[GEN 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gen5:5), [GEN 5:8](https://ref.ly/Gen5:8), [GEN 5:11](https://ref.ly/Gen5:11), [GEN 5:27](https://ref.ly/Gen5:27) *Methuselah died in the year of the flood*; ; [GEN 9:29](https://ref.ly/Gen9:29)

### After the flood

**[GEN 6:3](https://ref.ly/Gen6:3)** *See also* [GEN 11:10–11:25](https://ref.ly/Gen11:10-Gen11:25) *Following the flood, the span of life gradually reduces*; [GEN 25:7–25:8](https://ref.ly/Gen25:7-Gen25:8) *Abraham's death at 175 is considered “a good old age”.*

### In later generations

**[PSA 90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:10)** *See also* [2SA 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:4-2Sam5:5), [2SA 19:34–19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:34-2Sam19:35)

Attaining old age is a blessing from God
----------------------------------------

### Long life as a reward for obedience

**[DEU 5:33](https://ref.ly/Deut5:33)** *See also* [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12) parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16); [DEU 4:40](https://ref.ly/Deut4:40); [PRO 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Prov3:1-Prov3:2), [PRO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Prov16:31); [ECC 8:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:13)

### Reaching old age is a mark of God's favour

**[GEN 15:15](https://ref.ly/Gen15:15)** *See also* [GEN 24:1](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1), [GEN 35:29](https://ref.ly/Gen35:29) *Isaac*; [JDG 8:32](https://ref.ly/Judg8:32) *Gideon*; [1CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:28) *David, like Abraham and Gideon is described as dying at “a good old age”*; [2CH 24:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:15) *Jehoiada*; [JOB 42:17](https://ref.ly/Job42:17) *Job*

### People reaching old age indicates God's blessing on society

**[ZEC 8:4](https://ref.ly/Zech8:4)** *See also* [ISA 65:20](https://ref.ly/Isa65:20)

### Seeing grandchildren is a sign of blessing

**[PRO 17:6](https://ref.ly/Prov17:6)** *See also* [GEN 48:11](https://ref.ly/Gen48:11), [GEN 50:22–50:23](https://ref.ly/Gen50:22-Gen50:23); [RUT 4:15](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:15); [JOB 42:16](https://ref.ly/Job42:16); [PSA 128:5–128:6](https://ref.ly/Ps128:5-Ps128:6); [ISA 53:10](https://ref.ly/Isa53:10)

### Failure to reach old age is a result of God's judgment

[1SA 2:31–2:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:31-1Sam2:33)

The disabilities of old age
---------------------------

### Its frailties

**[PSA 71:9](https://ref.ly/Ps71:9)** *See also* [GEN 27:1](https://ref.ly/Gen27:1), [GEN 48:10](https://ref.ly/Gen48:10); [1KI 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:1), [1KI 15:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:23); [ECC 12:2–12:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:2-Eccl12:7) *an allegory portraying physical decline*

### The progressive inability to have children

**[GEN 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Gen18:11-Gen18:13)** *See also* [GEN 17:17](https://ref.ly/Gen17:17), [GEN 37:3](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3) *Children born in old age were rare and therefore special*; [2KI 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:14); [LUK 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18)

### The approach of death

[GEN 27:2](https://ref.ly/Gen27:2); ; [1KI 1:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:15); ; [1CH 23:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:1)

God's provision for the aged
----------------------------

### God strengthens and sustains them

**[ISA 46:4](https://ref.ly/Isa46:4)** *See also* [DEU 34:7](https://ref.ly/Deut34:7); [JOS 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Josh14:10-Josh14:11); [JOB 5:26](https://ref.ly/Job5:26); [PSA 92:14](https://ref.ly/Ps92:14)

### God gives them children

[GEN 21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:7) *Abraham and Sarah*; ; [2KI 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:16-2Kgs4:17) *the Shunammite woman*; ; [LUK 1:36](https://ref.ly/Luke1:36) *Elizabeth*

Wisdom and self\-control should accompany old age
-------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 12:12](https://ref.ly/Job12:12)** *See also* [JOB 15:10](https://ref.ly/Job15:10); [PRO 20:29](https://ref.ly/Prov20:29); [JHN 8:9](https://ref.ly/John8:9); [TIT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Titus2:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 5:5; Genesis 5:8; Genesis 5:11; Genesis 5:27; Genesis 9:29; Genesis 6:3; Genesis 11:10-25; Genesis 25:7-8; Psalms 90:10; 2 Samuel 5:4-5; 2 Samuel 19:34-35; Deuteronomy 5:33; Exodus 20:12; Deuteronomy 5:16; Deuteronomy 4:40; Proverbs 3:1-2; Proverbs 16:31; Ecclesiastes 8:13; Genesis 15:15; Genesis 24:1; Genesis 35:29; Judges 8:32; 1 Chronicles 29:28; 2 Chronicles 24:15; Job 42:17; Zechariah 8:4; Isaiah 65:20; Proverbs 17:6; Genesis 48:11; Genesis 50:22-23; Ruth 4:15; Job 42:16; Psalms 128:5-6; Isaiah 53:10; 1 Samuel 2:31-33; Psalms 71:9; Genesis 27:1; Genesis 48:10; 1 Kings 1:1; 1 Kings 15:23; Ecclesiastes 12:2-7; Genesis 18:11-13; Genesis 17:17; Genesis 37:3; 2 Kings 4:14; Luke 1:18; Genesis 27:2; 1 Kings 1:15; 1 Chronicles 23:1; Isaiah 46:4; Deuteronomy 34:7; Joshua 14:10-11; Job 5:26; Psalms 92:14; Genesis 21:7; 2 Kings 4:16-17; Luke 1:36; Job 12:12; Job 15:10; Proverbs 20:29; John 8:9; Titus 2:2
* **Associated Articles:** 1335: blessing (ID: `1335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4015: life (ID: `4015@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5296: disabilities (ID: `5296@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5446: poverty (ID: `5446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5498: reward (ID: `5498@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5696: grandchildren (ID: `5696@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5724: offspring (ID: `5724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5795: bereavement (ID: `5795@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8357: weakness (ID: `8357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5727: old age, attitudes to (id: 5727)

Old age is generally held in honour and the aged in society are to be respected. Because of the wisdom associated with their advancing years elders had an important role of authority and guidance in Israelite society and the NT speaks of the leadership of elders within the church.

Old age is to be held in honour
-------------------------------

### Respect for the aged

**[LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32)**; **[1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)** *See also* [JOB 29:8](https://ref.ly/Job29:8); [PRO 16:31](https://ref.ly/Prov16:31), [PRO 20:29](https://ref.ly/Prov20:29); [1TI 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1)

### Respect for parents

**[LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3)**; **[PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)** *See also* [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12), [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17); [LEV 20:9](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9); [DEU 27:16](https://ref.ly/Deut27:16); [PRO 30:17](https://ref.ly/Prov30:17); [MAT 15:4](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4) parallel [MRK 7:10](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10); [EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3); [COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20)

Old age is associated with wisdom
---------------------------------

### Deferring to the wisdom of the aged

**[JOB 32:6–32:7](https://ref.ly/Job32:6-Job32:7)** *See also* [1KI 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:6-1Kgs12:8) parallel [2CH 10:6–10:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:6-2Chr10:8) *Rehoboam rejected the advice of elders and rebellion ensued*; [JOB 12:12](https://ref.ly/Job12:12), [JOB 15:10](https://ref.ly/Job15:10); [PHM 1:9](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:9) *Paul refers to his age to add authority to his words.*

### Learning from the previous generation

**[PSA 71:18](https://ref.ly/Ps71:18)** *See also* [JOB 8:8](https://ref.ly/Job8:8); [PSA 37:25–37:26](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25-Ps37:26)

### Old age does not necessarily bring superior wisdom

**[ECC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:13)** *See also* [JOB 32:9](https://ref.ly/Job32:9); [PSA 105:22](https://ref.ly/Ps105:22)

Showing disrespect for the elderly is a sign of evil days
---------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 3:5](https://ref.ly/Isa3:5)** *See also* [DEU 28:50](https://ref.ly/Deut28:50); [JOB 30:1](https://ref.ly/Job30:1); [ISA 47:6](https://ref.ly/Isa47:6); [LAM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Lam5:12)

The role and status of elders
-----------------------------

### Elders of non\-Israelite nations

[GEN 50:7](https://ref.ly/Gen50:7); ; [NUM 22:7](https://ref.ly/Num22:7)

### Elders in Israel

Israel's elders were probably the heads of families, qualified to lead by virtue of age and experience: [EXO 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Exod3:16-Exod3:18), [EXO 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Exod4:29-Exod4:31), [EXO 19:7](https://ref.ly/Exod19:7); [NUM 11:25](https://ref.ly/Num11:25) *The number of elders is reduced to 70 who are anointed by the Spirit for leadership alongside Moses.*; [DEU 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:12), [DEU 22:15–22:18](https://ref.ly/Deut22:15-Deut22:18), [DEU 25:7](https://ref.ly/Deut25:7); [2SA 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:3) *Israel's elders accept David as king*; ; [MAT 26:57](https://ref.ly/Matt26:57) parallel [MRK 14:53](https://ref.ly/Mark14:53) *Jewish elders were among those who tried Jesus Christ. By NT times they had become involved with religious as well as civil affairs and their title related more to their role than to age.*

### Elders in the church

**[1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)** *See also* [1TI 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1-1Tim3:7); [TIT 1:5–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:5-Titus1:9); [JAS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Jas5:14); [1PE 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:2)

God as the \`\`Ancient of Days''
--------------------------------

### God as one \`\`advanced in years''

**[DAN 7:9](https://ref.ly/Dan7:9)** *This description of God as the “Ancient of Days” implies that he possesses the respectability, authority and wisdom to judge that accompanies age. It also points to his eternity.* *See also* [DAN 7:13](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13), [DAN 7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:22); [REV 1:14](https://ref.ly/Rev1:14)

### The eternity of God

**[PSA 9:7](https://ref.ly/Ps9:7)** *See also* [PSA 29:10](https://ref.ly/Ps29:10), [PSA 90:2](https://ref.ly/Ps90:2); [ISA 41:4](https://ref.ly/Isa41:4), [ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:32; 1 Peter 5:5; Job 29:8; Proverbs 16:31; Proverbs 20:29; 1 Timothy 5:1; Leviticus 19:3; Proverbs 23:22; Exodus 20:12; Exodus 21:17; Leviticus 20:9; Deuteronomy 27:16; Proverbs 30:17; Matthew 15:4; Mark 7:10; Ephesians 6:1-3; Colossians 3:20; Job 32:6-7; 1 Kings 12:6-8; 2 Chronicles 10:6-8; Job 12:12; Job 15:10; Philemon 1:9; Psalms 71:18; Job 8:8; Psalms 37:25-26; Ecclesiastes 4:13; Job 32:9; Psalms 105:22; Isaiah 3:5; Deuteronomy 28:50; Job 30:1; Isaiah 47:6; Lamentations 5:12; Genesis 50:7; Numbers 22:7; Exodus 3:16-18; Exodus 4:29-31; Exodus 19:7; Numbers 11:25; Deuteronomy 19:11-12; Deuteronomy 22:15-18; Deuteronomy 25:7; 2 Samuel 5:3; Matthew 26:57; Mark 14:53; 1 Timothy 5:17; 1 Timothy 3:1-7; Titus 1:5-9; James 5:14; 1 Peter 5:1-2; Daniel 7:9; Daniel 7:13; Daniel 7:22; Revelation 1:14; Psalms 9:7; Psalms 29:10; Psalms 90:2; Isaiah 41:4; Isaiah 44:6
* **Associated Articles:** 1140: God, the eternal (ID: `1140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1205: God, titles and names of (ID: `1205@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5358: judges (ID: `5358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5746: youth (ID: `5746@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5838: disrespect (ID: `5838@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5853: experience, of life (ID: `5853@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7718: elders (ID: `7718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8469: respect (ID: `8469@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5729: one flesh (id: 5729)

A sign and expression of the inseparable union between husband and wife. This is not only physical but emotional, mental and spiritual. It is also used to describe the union between Jesus Christ and the church.

One flesh describes the inseparable union between husband and wife
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:22–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:22-Gen2:24)** *See also* [1CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16); [EPH 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:28-Eph5:29), [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

One flesh refers to sexual union which Scripture describes in various ways
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

### To lie with

[GEN 4:1](https://ref.ly/Gen4:1), [GEN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Gen4:17), [GEN 4:25](https://ref.ly/Gen4:25), [GEN 19:31–19:35](https://ref.ly/Gen19:31-Gen19:35), [GEN 29:21](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21), [GEN 29:23](https://ref.ly/Gen29:23), [GEN 29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30); ; [1SA 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:19); ; [2SA 11:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:11), [2SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24), [2SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21)

### To come together

[MAT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18); ; [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5)

### To have union or unite with

**[1CO 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:15-1Cor6:16)** *See also* [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24); [MAT 1:25](https://ref.ly/Matt1:25), [MAT 19:5](https://ref.ly/Matt19:5) parallel [MRK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Mark10:7); [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

### Natural relations

[ROM 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Rom1:26-Rom1:27)

One flesh expresses an intimacy encouraged by God
-------------------------------------------------

### Only within heterosexual marriage

**[MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6)** parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9) *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27), [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24); [EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14) parallel [DEU 5:18](https://ref.ly/Deut5:18); [LEV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Lev20:10); [MAT 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:27-Matt5:28); [1CO 5:9–5:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:11), [1CO 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9), [1CO 7:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:4)

### Intimate relations between members of the same sex are unnatural and wrong

**[LEV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Lev18:22)** *See also* [ROM 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Rom1:26-Rom1:27); [1CO 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9)

One flesh describes the union between Jesus Christ and the church
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:31–5:32](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31-Eph5:32)** *See also* [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24); [1CO 6:16–6:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16-1Cor6:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:22-24; 1 Corinthians 6:16; Ephesians 5:28-29; Ephesians 5:31; Genesis 4:1; Genesis 4:17; Genesis 4:25; Genesis 19:31-35; Genesis 29:21; Genesis 29:23; Genesis 29:30; 1 Samuel 1:19; 2 Samuel 11:11; 2 Samuel 12:24; 2 Samuel 16:21; Matthew 1:18; 1 Corinthians 7:5; 1 Corinthians 6:15-16; Genesis 2:24; Matthew 1:25; Matthew 19:5; Mark 10:7; Romans 1:26-27; Matthew 19:4-6; Mark 10:6-9; Genesis 1:27; Exodus 20:14; Deuteronomy 5:18; Leviticus 20:10; Matthew 5:27-28; 1 Corinthians 5:9-11; 1 Corinthians 6:9; 1 Corinthians 7:4; Leviticus 18:22; Ephesians 5:31-32; 1 Corinthians 6:16-17
