## 5730: orphans (id: 5730)

Children whose parents are dead; a vulnerable group of people to whom God shows special kindness. He expects his people to do the same. Those who exploit them incur God's judgment.

Orphans as symbols
------------------

### Of weakness

**[PSA 82:3](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3)** *See also* [JOB 29:12](https://ref.ly/Job29:12); [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17)

### Of disgrace

**[LAM 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Lam5:1-Lam5:3)** *See also* [PSA 109:9](https://ref.ly/Ps109:9), [PSA 109:12](https://ref.ly/Ps109:12)

God cares for orphans
---------------------

**[DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18)** *See also* [PSA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ps10:14), [PSA 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Ps10:17-Ps10:18), [PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5), [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); [JER 49:11](https://ref.ly/Jer49:11); [HOS 14:3](https://ref.ly/Hos14:3)

God expects his people to care for orphans
------------------------------------------

### They are not to be oppressed

**[ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)** *See also* [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17), [DEU 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12-Deut26:13); [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3)

### Caring for them brings blessing

**[DEU 24:19](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19)** *See also* [DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29); [ISA 1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:17); [JER 7:5–7:7](https://ref.ly/Jer7:5-Jer7:7); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

Exploitation of orphans is a mark of wickedness
-----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 94:5–94:6](https://ref.ly/Ps94:5-Ps94:6)** *See also* [JOB 6:27](https://ref.ly/Job6:27), [JOB 22:9](https://ref.ly/Job22:9), [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3), [JOB 24:9](https://ref.ly/Job24:9), [JOB 31:17](https://ref.ly/Job31:17), [JOB 31:21](https://ref.ly/Job31:21); [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23); [JER 5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:28); [EZK 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:7)

God punishes those who oppress orphans
--------------------------------------

**[DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19)** *See also* [EXO 22:22–22:24](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22-Exod22:24); [PRO 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10-Prov23:11); [ISA 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:4); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)

Orphans are included among God's people
---------------------------------------

[DEU 16:11](https://ref.ly/Deut16:11), [DEU 16:14](https://ref.ly/Deut16:14)

Jesus Christ includes those who are spiritually fatherless
----------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:18](https://ref.ly/John14:18)** *See also* [GAL 4:5](https://ref.ly/Gal4:5), [GAL 4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:7)

Examples of orphans
-------------------

[GEN 11:27–11:28](https://ref.ly/Gen11:27-Gen11:28) *Lot*; ; [NUM 27:1–27:5](https://ref.ly/Num27:1-Num27:5) *the daughters of Zelophedad*; ; [EST 2:7](https://ref.ly/Esth2:7) *Esther*

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 82:3; Job 29:12; Isaiah 9:17; Lamentations 5:1-3; Psalms 109:9; Psalms 109:12; Deuteronomy 10:18; Psalms 10:14; Psalms 10:17-18; Psalms 68:5; Psalms 146:9; Jeremiah 49:11; Hosea 14:3; Zechariah 7:10; Deuteronomy 24:17; Deuteronomy 26:12-13; Jeremiah 22:3; Deuteronomy 24:19; Deuteronomy 14:28-29; Isaiah 1:17; Jeremiah 7:5-7; James 1:27; Psalms 94:5-6; Job 6:27; Job 22:9; Job 24:3; Job 24:9; Job 31:17; Job 31:21; Isaiah 1:23; Jeremiah 5:28; Ezekiel 22:7; Deuteronomy 27:19; Exodus 22:22-24; Proverbs 23:10-11; Isaiah 10:1-4; Malachi 3:5; Deuteronomy 16:11; Deuteronomy 16:14; John 14:18; Galatians 4:5; Galatians 4:7; Genesis 11:27-28; Numbers 27:1-5; Esther 2:7
* **Associated Articles:** 1030: God, compassion of (ID: `1030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1040: God, fatherhood of (ID: `1040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1310: God, as judge (ID: `1310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5310: exploitation (ID: `5310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5666: children, needs of (ID: `5666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5797: bereavement, God's comfort in (ID: `5797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6608: adoption (ID: `6608@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7115: children of God (ID: `7115@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7140: people of God (ID: `7140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8291: kindness (ID: `8291@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5731: parents (id: 5731)

Scripture stipulates that children should show respect and concern for their parents.

Honouring parents
-----------------

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *See also* [LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3); [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22); [MAT 19:16–19:19](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16-Matt19:19) parallels [MRK 10:17–10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17-Mark10:19), [LUK 18:18–18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18-Luke18:20); [EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3); [COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20); [1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4)

Dishonouring parents
--------------------

**[2TI 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:2)** *See also* [EXO 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:15-Exod21:17); [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); [EZK 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:7); [MAT 15:1–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:9) parallel [MRK 7:1–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:1-Mark7:13); [ROM 1:28–1:32](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28-Rom1:32)

Love between parents and children can be suppressed
---------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16-Luke21:17)** parallels [MAT 10:21](https://ref.ly/Matt10:21), [MRK 13:12](https://ref.ly/Mark13:12) *See also* [ZEC 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Zech13:2-Zech13:3)

Love for God must surpass love for parents
------------------------------------------

**[LUK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Luke14:26)** *See also* [DEU 33:8–33:11](https://ref.ly/Deut33:8-Deut33:11); [LUK 18:28–18:30](https://ref.ly/Luke18:28-Luke18:30) parallels [MAT 19:27–19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:27-Matt19:29), [MRK 10:28–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:28-Mark10:30)

Divine love surpasses parental love
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 27:10](https://ref.ly/Ps27:10)**

The belief that children were punished for their parents' sins
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 9:1–9:3](https://ref.ly/John9:1-John9:3)** *See also* [EXO 20:5–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5-Exod20:6) parallel [DEU 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Deut5:9-Deut5:10); [JOB 21:19–21:21](https://ref.ly/Job21:19-Job21:21); [EZK 18:1–18:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:1-Ezek18:4)

Parents caring for their children
---------------------------------

**[2CO 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:14)** *See also* [PRO 19:14](https://ref.ly/Prov19:14), [PRO 23:24–23:25](https://ref.ly/Prov23:24-Prov23:25); [HEB 11:23](https://ref.ly/Heb11:23)

Children caring for their parents
---------------------------------

**[1SA 22:3–22:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:3-1Sam22:4)** *See also* [JOS 2:8–2:20](https://ref.ly/Josh2:8-Josh2:20), [JOS 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Josh6:22-Josh6:23); [PRO 17:6](https://ref.ly/Prov17:6)

Mourning for parents
--------------------

**[LEV 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:2)** *See also* [LEV 21:10–21:11](https://ref.ly/Lev21:10-Lev21:11); [NUM 6:2–6:7](https://ref.ly/Num6:2-Num6:7); [DEU 21:10–21:13](https://ref.ly/Deut21:10-Deut21:13); [EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25)

Parents and the marriage of their children
------------------------------------------

### A man leaves his parents to marry his wife

**[GEN 2:21–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:21-Gen2:24)** *See also* [MAT 19:3–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3-Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:2–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:2-Mark10:9); [EPH 5:25–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:33)

### Parents were responsible for vindicating their daughter's reputation

[DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19)

### Sometimes parents jointly arranged their children's marriages

**[JDG 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:1-Judg14:3)**

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:12; Deuteronomy 5:16; Leviticus 19:3; Proverbs 23:22; Matthew 19:16-19; Mark 10:17-19; Luke 18:18-20; Ephesians 6:1-3; Colossians 3:20; 1 Timothy 5:4; 2 Timothy 3:1-2; Exodus 21:15-17; Deuteronomy 21:18-21; Ezekiel 22:7; Matthew 15:1-9; Mark 7:1-13; Romans 1:28-32; Luke 21:16-17; Matthew 10:21; Mark 13:12; Zechariah 13:2-3; Luke 14:26; Deuteronomy 33:8-11; Luke 18:28-30; Matthew 19:27-29; Mark 10:28-30; Psalms 27:10; John 9:1-3; Exodus 20:5-6; Deuteronomy 5:9-10; Job 21:19-21; Ezekiel 18:1-4; 2 Corinthians 12:14; Proverbs 19:14; Proverbs 23:24-25; Hebrews 11:23; 1 Samuel 22:3-4; Joshua 2:8-20; Joshua 6:22-23; Proverbs 17:6; Leviticus 21:1-2; Leviticus 21:10-11; Numbers 6:2-7; Deuteronomy 21:10-13; Ezekiel 44:25; Genesis 2:21-24; Matthew 19:3-6; Mark 10:2-9; Ephesians 5:25-33; Deuteronomy 22:13-19; Judges 14:1-3
* **Associated Articles:** 2078: Jesus Christ, sonship of (ID: `2078@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5218: authority, in the home (ID: `5218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5674: daughters (ID: `5674@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5684: fathers (ID: `5684@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5696: grandchildren (ID: `5696@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5697: grandparents (ID: `5697@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5718: mothers (ID: `5718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5738: sons (ID: `5738@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6160: fathers, sin of the (ID: `6160@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5732: polygamy (id: 5732)

The practice of having more than one wife at one time. Although not explicitly forbidden in Scripture as a general practice, it is presented as problematic and monogamy is the clearly preferred option.

Monogamy is God's intention for marriage
----------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24)** *See also* [JER 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2); [HOS 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Hos2:19-Hos2:20); [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31)

Early examples of polygamy
--------------------------

**[GEN 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gen4:19)** *See also* [GEN 26:34](https://ref.ly/Gen26:34), [GEN 28:9](https://ref.ly/Gen28:9), [GEN 29:28](https://ref.ly/Gen29:28)

Reasons for the practice of polygamy
------------------------------------

### Sealing foreign treaties

**[1KI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:1)** *See also* [1KI 16:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:31)

### Producing offspring

**[GEN 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:3)**; **[2CH 13:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:21)** *A large family, and especially numerous progeny, was regarded as a sign of divine blessing.* *See also* [GEN 30:3–30:5](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3-Gen30:5), [GEN 30:9](https://ref.ly/Gen30:9); [JDG 8:30](https://ref.ly/Judg8:30); [2SA 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:1-2Sam3:5); [1CH 7:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:4); [2CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:23), [2CH 24:2–24:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:2-2Chr24:3)

Problems associated with polygamy
---------------------------------

### Being led away from God

**[DEU 17:17](https://ref.ly/Deut17:17)** *It was common for kings to cement foreign alliances by marriage. Israel's king is warned against the practice*; **[1KI 11:1–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:8)** *See also* [NEH 13:25–13:26](https://ref.ly/Neh13:25-Neh13:26); [MAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Mal2:11)

### Conflicts within families

**[GEN 29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30), [GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1)**; **[DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17)** *See also* [GEN 16:4–16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4-Gen16:6) *Sarah and Hagar*; [1SA 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:4-1Sam1:7) *Hannah and Peninnah*; [2CH 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:21-2Chr11:22); [1KI 1:11–1:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:11-1Kgs1:13) *the conflict over the succession to David's throne*

Further examples of polygamy
----------------------------

**[2SA 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:13)** parallel [1CH 14:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:3) *See also* [1SA 25:40–25:43](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:40-1Sam25:43); [2SA 12:8–12:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:8-2Sam12:9); [1KI 20:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:5) *Ahab*; [2KI 24:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:15) *Jehoiachin*; [1CH 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:5) *Ashhur*; [DAN 5:2](https://ref.ly/Dan5:2) *Belshazzar*

Restrictions on polygamous relationships
----------------------------------------

[GEN 31:50](https://ref.ly/Gen31:50); ; [EXO 21:10](https://ref.ly/Exod21:10); ; [LEV 18:18](https://ref.ly/Lev18:18)

Polygamy forbidden
------------------

[1CO 7:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:2); ; [1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2) *Polygamy is expressly forbidden for church leaders.*

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:24; Jeremiah 2:2; Hosea 2:19-20; Ephesians 5:31; Genesis 4:19; Genesis 26:34; Genesis 28:9; Genesis 29:28; 1 Kings 3:1; 1 Kings 16:31; Genesis 16:1-3; 2 Chronicles 13:21; Genesis 30:3-5; Genesis 30:9; Judges 8:30; 2 Samuel 3:1-5; 1 Chronicles 7:4; 2 Chronicles 11:23; 2 Chronicles 24:2-3; Deuteronomy 17:17; 1 Kings 11:1-8; Nehemiah 13:25-26; Malachi 2:11; Genesis 29:30; Genesis 30:1; Deuteronomy 21:15-17; Genesis 16:4-6; 1 Samuel 1:4-7; 2 Chronicles 11:21-22; 1 Kings 1:11-13; 2 Samuel 5:13; 1 Chronicles 14:3; 1 Samuel 25:40-43; 2 Samuel 12:8-9; 1 Kings 20:5; 2 Kings 24:15; 1 Chronicles 4:5; Daniel 5:2; Genesis 31:50; Exodus 21:10; Leviticus 18:18; 1 Corinthians 7:2; 1 Timothy 3:2
* **Associated Articles:** 5672: concubines (ID: `5672@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5702: husband (ID: `5702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5717: monogamy (ID: `5717@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5744: wife (ID: `5744@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8252: faithfulness, in human relationships (ID: `8252@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5733: pregnancy (id: 5733)

Pregnancy regarded as a blessing from God
-----------------------------------------

**[GEN 4:1](https://ref.ly/Gen4:1)** *See also* [GEN 4:17](https://ref.ly/Gen4:17), [GEN 16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4), [GEN 20:17–20:18](https://ref.ly/Gen20:17-Gen20:18), [GEN 29:32](https://ref.ly/Gen29:32), [GEN 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1-Gen30:2), [GEN 30:4–30:6](https://ref.ly/Gen30:4-Gen30:6), [GEN 30:17](https://ref.ly/Gen30:17), [GEN 30:23](https://ref.ly/Gen30:23)

Pregnancy after barrenness
--------------------------

**[GEN 25:21](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21)** *See also* [GEN 30:22–30:23](https://ref.ly/Gen30:22-Gen30:23); [1SA 1:1–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:1-1Sam1:20); [2KI 4:11–4:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:11-2Kgs4:17); [LUK 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:24-Luke1:25)

Pregnancy in old age
--------------------

**[LUK 1:36–1:37](https://ref.ly/Luke1:36-Luke1:37)** *See also* [GEN 18:9–18:15](https://ref.ly/Gen18:9-Gen18:15), [GEN 21:1–21:7](https://ref.ly/Gen21:1-Gen21:7)

Ritual uncleanness after pregnancy
----------------------------------

**[LEV 12:1–12:7](https://ref.ly/Lev12:1-Lev12:7)**

Pregnancy outside marriage
--------------------------

### As a result of incest

**[GEN 19:30–19:38](https://ref.ly/Gen19:30-Gen19:38)**

### As a result of prostitution

**[GEN 38:24–38:25](https://ref.ly/Gen38:24-Gen38:25)** *See also* [GEN 38:13–38:19](https://ref.ly/Gen38:13-Gen38:19)

### As a result of adultery

**[2SA 11:2–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:5)**

Pregnancy in troubled times sometimes a misfortune
--------------------------------------------------

### Because of atrocities committed in war

**[2KI 15:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:16)** *See also* [2KI 8:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:12); [HOS 13:16](https://ref.ly/Hos13:16); [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13)

### Because of additional hardship in harsh circumstances

**[MAT 24:15–24:21](https://ref.ly/Matt24:15-Matt24:21)** parallels [MRK 13:14–13:19](https://ref.ly/Mark13:14-Mark13:19), [LUK 21:20–21:24](https://ref.ly/Luke21:20-Luke21:24)

Premature birth
---------------

**[EXO 21:22–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22-Exod21:25)** *See also* [1SA 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:19)

Labour pains
------------

**[1TH 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:3)** *See also* [GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16); [1SA 4:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:19); [REV 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rev12:2)

Miscarriage
-----------

**[EXO 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Exod23:25-Exod23:26)** *See also* [GEN 31:38](https://ref.ly/Gen31:38); [JOB 21:10](https://ref.ly/Job21:10); [HOS 9:14](https://ref.ly/Hos9:14)

Figurative use of pregnancy
---------------------------

**[PSA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Ps7:14)** *See also* [HOS 9:11](https://ref.ly/Hos9:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 4:1; Genesis 4:17; Genesis 16:4; Genesis 20:17-18; Genesis 29:32; Genesis 30:1-2; Genesis 30:4-6; Genesis 30:17; Genesis 30:23; Genesis 25:21; Genesis 30:22-23; 1 Samuel 1:1-20; 2 Kings 4:11-17; Luke 1:24-25; Luke 1:36-37; Genesis 18:9-15; Genesis 21:1-7; Leviticus 12:1-7; Genesis 19:30-38; Genesis 38:24-25; Genesis 38:13-19; 2 Samuel 11:2-5; 2 Kings 15:16; 2 Kings 8:12; Hosea 13:16; Amos 1:13; Matthew 24:15-21; Mark 13:14-19; Luke 21:20-24; Exodus 21:22-25; 1 Samuel 4:19; 1 Thessalonians 5:3; Genesis 3:16; Revelation 12:2; Exodus 23:25-26; Genesis 31:38; Job 21:10; Hosea 9:14; Psalms 7:14; Hosea 9:11
* **Associated Articles:** 5061: sanctity of life (ID: `5061@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5199: womb (ID: `5199@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5225: barrenness (ID: `5225@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5410: midwife (ID: `5410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5428: nurse (ID: `5428@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5652: babies (ID: `5652@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5655: birth (ID: `5655@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5663: childbirth (ID: `5663@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5724: offspring (ID: `5724@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5739: twins (ID: `5739@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5734: relationships (id: 5734)

God created people to be in relationship with himself and with one another in friendship, marriage, family, society and the church.

Human beings are created to be in relationship
----------------------------------------------

### With God

**[REV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Rev21:3)** *See also* [ISA 43:6–43:7](https://ref.ly/Isa43:6-Isa43:7); [HOS 11:1](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1); [2CO 6:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:18)

### With one another

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [PSA 127:4](https://ref.ly/Ps127:4); [PRO 17:6](https://ref.ly/Prov17:6), [PRO 17:17](https://ref.ly/Prov17:17), [PRO 18:24](https://ref.ly/Prov18:24), [PRO 27:10](https://ref.ly/Prov27:10); [ECC 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:9-Eccl4:12)

Relationship with God
---------------------

### Is entered by faith

**[JHN 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:12-John1:13)** *See also* [MAT 12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:50) parallels [MRK 3:35](https://ref.ly/Mark3:35), [LUK 8:21](https://ref.ly/Luke8:21); [JHN 17:3](https://ref.ly/John17:3); [GAL 3:26](https://ref.ly/Gal3:26)

### Is expressed through covenant

**[EXO 6:7](https://ref.ly/Exod6:7)** *This is often known as the covenant formula.* *See also* [GEN 17:7](https://ref.ly/Gen17:7); [2SA 7:14–7:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:14-2Sam7:15) parallel [1CH 17:13–17:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:13-1Chr17:14); [LUK 22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:20) parallels [MAT 26:28](https://ref.ly/Matt26:28), [MRK 14:24](https://ref.ly/Mark14:24); [2CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:16); [LEV 26:12](https://ref.ly/Lev26:12); [HEB 10:15–10:18](https://ref.ly/Heb10:15-Heb10:18); [JER 31:33–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33-Jer31:34)

Relationships between believers
-------------------------------

### Believers are to be in relationship with one another in the church

**[GAL 3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:28)** *See also* [EPH 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Eph2:14-Eph2:16), [EPH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eph2:19)

### Believers are to maintain good relationships

**[EPH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:3)** *See also* [ROM 12:18](https://ref.ly/Rom12:18), [ROM 14:19](https://ref.ly/Rom14:19); [HEB 12:14](https://ref.ly/Heb12:14)

### Believers are to restore broken relationships

**[MAT 18:15](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15)** *See also* [MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24); [EPH 4:26](https://ref.ly/Eph4:26)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 21:3; Isaiah 43:6-7; Hosea 11:1; 2 Corinthians 6:18; Genesis 2:18; Psalms 127:4; Proverbs 17:6; Proverbs 17:17; Proverbs 18:24; Proverbs 27:10; Ecclesiastes 4:9-12; John 1:12-13; Matthew 12:50; Mark 3:35; Luke 8:21; John 17:3; Galatians 3:26; Exodus 6:7; Genesis 17:7; 2 Samuel 7:14-15; 1 Chronicles 17:13-14; Luke 22:20; Matthew 26:28; Mark 14:24; 2 Corinthians 6:16; Leviticus 26:12; Hebrews 10:15-18; Jeremiah 31:33-34; Galatians 3:28; Ephesians 2:14-16; Ephesians 2:19; Ephesians 4:3; Romans 12:18; Romans 14:19; Hebrews 12:14; Matthew 18:15; Matthew 5:23-24; Ephesians 4:26
* **Associated Articles:** 1040: God, fatherhood of (ID: `1040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1511: Trinity, relationships between the persons (ID: `1511@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5361: justice, human (ID: `5361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5690: friends (ID: `5690@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6608: adoption (ID: `6608@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6672: grace, in human relationships (ID: `6672@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7025: church, unity and fellowship of (ID: `7025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8252: faithfulness, in human relationships (ID: `8252@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8299: love, in relationships (ID: `8299@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8603: prayer, as a relationship with God (ID: `8603@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5735: sexuality (id: 5735)

Men and women are intended to complement one another and to take pleasure in one another. God has provided for the full and glad expression of this in marriage.

Sexuality comes from God
------------------------

### It was created by God

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)**

### God intended men and women to complement one another

**[1CO 11:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:11)** *See also* [GEN 2:18–2:22](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18-Gen2:22)

Sexual differences
------------------

### In strength

[1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### In role

[1CO 11:3–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3-1Cor11:5), [1CO 14:34–14:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:34-1Cor14:35); ; [EPH 5:22–5:25](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:25); ; [COL 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Col3:18-Col3:19); ; [1TI 2:12–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:12-1Tim2:14); ; [1PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1)

### In dress

**[DEU 22:5](https://ref.ly/Deut22:5)** *See also* [1CO 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:14-1Cor11:15)

Sexual activity
---------------

### It was ordained by God

**[GEN 1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:28)** *This command was made before the fall, and no guilt attaches to it.*

### Marriage in its proper context

**[GEN 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23-Gen2:24)** *“one flesh” implies both bodily union and an inseparable intimate relationship.* *See also* [MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9); [1CO 7:2–7:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:2-1Cor7:4); [EPH 5:31–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31-Eph5:33)

### Sexual desire

**[SNG 4:3–4:15](https://ref.ly/Song4:3-Song4:15)** *“Song of Songs” celebrates the beauty of sex in a loving context. It contains much delicate and intimate imagery that applies directly to human lovers and can also be applied to Jesus Christ and the church.* *See also* [GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16); [SNG 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Song1:2-Song1:4), [SNG 4:16](https://ref.ly/Song4:16), [SNG 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Song7:11-Song7:13), [SNG 8:10](https://ref.ly/Song8:10)

### Abstinence is possible

[MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12); ; [1CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:1), [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5)

Perversions of sexuality
------------------------

### Adultery

**[EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14)** parallel [DEU 5:18](https://ref.ly/Deut5:18) *See also* [HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4)

### Lust

**[MAT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28)** *Lust may also lead to other sexual sins.* *See also* [EPH 4:19](https://ref.ly/Eph4:19), [EPH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3); [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5); [1PE 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3)

### Promiscuity

[DEU 22:13–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:21); ; [ROM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24)

### Prostitution

[LEV 19:29](https://ref.ly/Lev19:29)

### Homosexuality

**[ROM 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Rom1:26-Rom1:27)** *See also* [GEN 19:4–19:7](https://ref.ly/Gen19:4-Gen19:7); [LEV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Lev18:22)

### Bestiality

[EXO 22:19](https://ref.ly/Exod22:19)

### Sexual perversion will be punished

[1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10); ; [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8), [REV 22:15](https://ref.ly/Rev22:15)

### Sexual perversion can be cleansed

[1CO 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:11)

Sexuality is transcended in heaven
----------------------------------

**[MAT 22:30](https://ref.ly/Matt22:30)**

## 5736: singleness (id: 5736)

Not being married; the state prior to betrothal or following separation or the death of a spouse. In Scripture, an unmarried woman generally lives with, and is the responsibility of, her family. Whilst the unmarried are freer of worldly concerns and may devote themselves more fully to God, those already married should not separate, and those without the gift of celibacy should not remain single.

Responsibility for an unmarried woman
-------------------------------------

### An unmarried woman is responsible to her father

**[DEU 22:16](https://ref.ly/Deut22:16)** *See also* [EXO 2:21](https://ref.ly/Exod2:21); [NUM 30:3–30:5](https://ref.ly/Num30:3-Num30:5); [JDG 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Judg1:12-Judg1:13); [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17), [1SA 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:21)

### An unmarried woman is dependent on her brothers

**[LEV 21:3](https://ref.ly/Lev21:3)** *See also* [GEN 24:51](https://ref.ly/Gen24:51), [GEN 34:14–34:15](https://ref.ly/Gen34:14-Gen34:15); [EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25)

### Widowed or divorced women could return to their parents' home

**[RUT 1:8–1:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:8-Ruth1:13)** *See also* [GEN 38:11](https://ref.ly/Gen38:11); [LEV 22:13](https://ref.ly/Lev22:13); [JDG 19:2](https://ref.ly/Judg19:2)

Regulations relating to the unmarried
-------------------------------------

[DEU 20:7](https://ref.ly/Deut20:7), [DEU 22:20–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:20-Deut22:21), [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29)

The unwelcome prospect of remaining unmarried
---------------------------------------------

**[JDG 11:37–11:39](https://ref.ly/Judg11:37-Judg11:39)**; **[ISA 54:6](https://ref.ly/Isa54:6)** *See also* [GEN 19:31–19:32](https://ref.ly/Gen19:31-Gen19:32); [ISA 49:20–49:21](https://ref.ly/Isa49:20-Isa49:21), [ISA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Isa62:4); [HOS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2); [GAL 4:27](https://ref.ly/Gal4:27); [ISA 54:1](https://ref.ly/Isa54:1)

Celibacy
--------

### Celibacy is a calling from God

**[1CO 7:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:7)** *See also* [JER 16:2](https://ref.ly/Jer16:2); [MAT 19:10–19:121](https://ref.ly/Matt19:10-Matt19:121)CO [7:9](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9), [1CO 7:36](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36); [1TI 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:11-1Tim5:14)

### Believers should remain as they are when called

**[1CO 7:25–7:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:25-1Cor7:28)** *See also* [1CO 7:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:10), [1CO 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:12-1Cor7:13), [1CO 7:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:17), [1CO 7:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:24)

### Sexual abstinence within marriage is strictly limited

**[1CO 7:2–7:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:2-1Cor7:6)** *See also* [EXO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:15); [1SA 21:4–21:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:4-1Sam21:5)

### The desirability of being unmarried

**[1CO 7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8)** *See also* [1CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:1), [1CO 7:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:11), [1CO 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:37-1Cor7:38)

### The unmarried may devote themselves to God

**[1CO 7:32–7:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:32-1Cor7:35)** *See also* [LUK 14:20](https://ref.ly/Luke14:20), [LUK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Luke14:26); [ACT 21:9](https://ref.ly/Acts21:9); [1TI 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:5)

### Marriage belongs to a passing order

**[MAT 22:30](https://ref.ly/Matt22:30)** parallels [MRK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Mark12:25), [LUK 20:35](https://ref.ly/Luke20:35) *See also* [1CO 7:29–7:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:29-1Cor7:31)

## 5737: sisters (id: 5737)

The daughter of one's parents, or of either mother or father. The relationship with a sister is a close one, especially with an unmarried sister.

The relationship with a sister was a close one
----------------------------------------------

**[EZK 44:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:25)** *See also* [LEV 21:1–21:3](https://ref.ly/Lev21:1-Lev21:3); [NUM 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Num6:6-Num6:7); [JOB 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Job1:1-Job1:4)

A brother had some measure of responsibility for his sister
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[SNG 8:8–8:9](https://ref.ly/Song8:8-Song8:9)** *See also* [GEN 24:28–24:60](https://ref.ly/Gen24:28-Gen24:60); [2SA 13:19–13:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:19-2Sam13:20)

Sexual relationships with sisters
---------------------------------

### Marrying a sister was permissible in pre\-Mosaic times

**[GEN 20:12](https://ref.ly/Gen20:12)**

### Sexual relationships with a sister were later forbidden in Mosaic law

**[LEV 18:9](https://ref.ly/Lev18:9)** *See also* [LEV 18:11–18:13](https://ref.ly/Lev18:11-Lev18:13), [LEV 18:18](https://ref.ly/Lev18:18), [LEV 20:17](https://ref.ly/Lev20:17); [DEU 27:22](https://ref.ly/Deut27:22); [EZK 22:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:11)

### Rape

[GEN 34:1–34:31](https://ref.ly/Gen34:1-Gen34:31) *Dinah's rape was avenged by her brothers*; ; [2SA 13:1–13:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:19) *Tamar was raped by her half\-brother*; ; [2SA 13:23–13:36](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:23-2Sam13:36) *Absalom avenged Tamar's rape.*

Sometimes the wives of the patriarchs posed as their sisters
------------------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 20:13](https://ref.ly/Gen20:13)** *See also* [GEN 12:10–12:20](https://ref.ly/Gen12:10-Gen12:20), [GEN 20:1–20:7](https://ref.ly/Gen20:1-Gen20:7), [GEN 26:1–26:10](https://ref.ly/Gen26:1-Gen26:10)

Examples of the behaviour of sisters
------------------------------------

### Jealousy

**[GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1)** *See also* [GEN 30:7–30:8](https://ref.ly/Gen30:7-Gen30:8)

### Criticism

**[LUK 10:38–10:42](https://ref.ly/Luke10:38-Luke10:42)** *See also* [NUM 12:1–12:15](https://ref.ly/Num12:1-Num12:15)

### Kindness and concern

**[2CH 22:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:11)** parallel [2KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:2) *See also* [EXO 2:1–2:8](https://ref.ly/Exod2:1-Exod2:8); [JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11); [JHN 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/John11:1-John11:3), [JHN 11:17–11:32](https://ref.ly/John11:17-John11:32)

Sisters\-in\-law
----------------

### Marriage to a sister\-in\-law was allowed in specific circumstances

**[DEU 25:5–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:10)** *See also* [GEN 38:6–38:11](https://ref.ly/Gen38:6-Gen38:11)

### Sexual relationships with sisters\-in\-law were forbidden under normal circumstances

**[LEV 18:16](https://ref.ly/Lev18:16)** *See also* [LEV 20:21](https://ref.ly/Lev20:21); [MRK 6:17–6:20](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:20); [LUK 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Luke3:19-Luke3:20)

Sister used as a term of affection
----------------------------------

### By a husband

**[SNG 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Song4:9-Song4:10)** *See also* [SNG 4:12](https://ref.ly/Song4:12), [SNG 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Song5:1-Song5:2)

### In the church

**[ROM 16:1](https://ref.ly/Rom16:1)** *See also* [1TI 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1-1Tim5:2); [PHM 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:1-Phlm1:2); [JAS 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/Jas2:15-Jas2:17); [2JN 1:13](https://ref.ly/2John1:13)

The metaphorical use of sister
------------------------------

### Of death

**[JOB 17:13–17:16](https://ref.ly/Job17:13-Job17:16)**

### Of wisdom

**[PRO 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Prov7:1-Prov7:5)**

### Of nations

**[JER 3:6–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jer3:6-Jer3:10)** *See also* [EZK 16:44–16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:44-Ezek16:63), [EZK 23:1–23:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:1-Ezek23:49)

Sister as a theological term
----------------------------

### Describing the relationship of believers to Jesus Christ

### Jesus Christ requires his followers to put him before family ties

**[LUK 14:26](https://ref.ly/Luke14:26)** *See also* [MAT 19:29](https://ref.ly/Matt19:29) parallel [MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30)

## 5738: sons (id: 5738)

The male offspring of parents. While sharing in the general privileges and responsibilities of children, sons had special claims to inheritance from their parents. To refer to Christians as “sons of God” is to identify both their relationship to God and also their rights as heirs of God. A significant number of titles for Jesus Christ make use of the term “Son”, stressing the close relationship between the Father and Son.

Privileges of sonship
---------------------

### Inheritance

[PRO 13:22](https://ref.ly/Prov13:22); ; [GAL 4:7](https://ref.ly/Gal4:7); ; [HEB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2)

### Being disciplined

Discipline is here seen as a loving means of enabling a child to grow to maturity: [DEU 8:5](https://ref.ly/Deut8:5); ; [PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18), [PRO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Prov29:17); ; [HEB 12:5–12:11](https://ref.ly/Heb12:5-Heb12:11); ; [REV 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rev3:19)

### Being loved and provided for

**[LUK 11:11–11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11-Luke11:13)** parallel [MAT 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:11)

### Being prayed for

*See also* [GEN 17:18–17:20](https://ref.ly/Gen17:18-Gen17:20); [2SA 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:16); [JOB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Job1:5)

Examples of good sons
---------------------

[GEN 9:23](https://ref.ly/Gen9:23) *Shem and Japheth*; ; [GEN 22:6–22:12](https://ref.ly/Gen22:6-Gen22:12) *Isaac*; [GEN 45:8–45:11](https://ref.ly/Gen45:8-Gen45:11), [GEN 47:11–47:12](https://ref.ly/Gen47:11-Gen47:12); [LUK 2:51](https://ref.ly/Luke2:51) *The voluntary obedience of the child Jesus towards Mary and Joseph is widely regarded as setting an example for believers.*; [2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:5), [2TI 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:14-2Tim3:15)

Examples of bad sons
--------------------

[GEN 37:1–37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:1-Gen37:35) *Jacob's eleven sons*; ; [LUK 15:11–15:32](https://ref.ly/Luke15:11-Luke15:32)

Christians as sons of God
-------------------------

**[HEB 12:5–12:6](https://ref.ly/Heb12:5-Heb12:6)** *See also* [ROM 8:14](https://ref.ly/Rom8:14), [ROM 9:26](https://ref.ly/Rom9:26); [2CO 6:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:18); [HEB 12:7](https://ref.ly/Heb12:7)

Adopted sons
------------

**[EXO 2:10](https://ref.ly/Exod2:10)** *See also* [GEN 48:5](https://ref.ly/Gen48:5)

Sons\-in\-law
-------------

[GEN 19:12–19:14](https://ref.ly/Gen19:12-Gen19:14); ; [JDG 19:5](https://ref.ly/Judg19:5); ; [1SA 18:18–18:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:18-1Sam18:27)

Family relationships must be subservient to one's relationship with God
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 10:35](https://ref.ly/Matt10:35) parallel [LUK 12:53](https://ref.ly/Luke12:53); ; [MAT 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37)

Son as an honorific term
------------------------

[MAT 9:2](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2) parallel [MAT 9:5](https://ref.ly/Matt9:5); ; [LUK 15:31](https://ref.ly/Luke15:31)

Sons in a spiritual role
------------------------

**[1TI 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:18)** *See also* [JOL 2:28](https://ref.ly/Joel2:28); [ACT 2:17](https://ref.ly/Acts2:17); [2TI 1:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:1)

---------------------------

### Son

**[ISA 9:6](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6)** *See also* [PSA 2:7](https://ref.ly/Ps2:7), [PSA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ps2:12); [MAT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17); [JHN 3:35](https://ref.ly/John3:35)

Jesus Christ is described in this way by those who have yet to understand the circumstances of his conception and birth: [LUK 3:23](https://ref.ly/Luke3:23); ; [JHN 1:45](https://ref.ly/John1:45), [JHN 6:42](https://ref.ly/John6:42)

## 5739: twins (id: 5739)

Two children born of the same parents at the same time. Figuratively used to describe a matching pair.

Examples of twins
-----------------

**[GEN 25:21–25:26](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21-Gen25:26)** *Although Jacob and Esau were twins, their very different characters indicate that God deals with and relates to people individually.* *See also* [GEN 38:27–38:30](https://ref.ly/Gen38:27-Gen38:30); [JHN 20:24](https://ref.ly/John20:24) *Didymus means “twin”. It is not known who his twin was*; [JHN 21:2](https://ref.ly/John21:2); [ACT 28:11](https://ref.ly/Acts28:11) *the mythical twin sons of Zeus regarded as special protectors of sailors*

Twins used to illustrate God's election
---------------------------------------

[ROM 9:10–9:16](https://ref.ly/Rom9:10-Rom9:16)

Twins used figuratively for matching pairs
------------------------------------------

[SNG 4:2](https://ref.ly/Song4:2), [SNG 4:5](https://ref.ly/Song4:5), [SNG 6:6](https://ref.ly/Song6:6), [SNG 7:3](https://ref.ly/Song7:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 25:21-26; Genesis 38:27-30; John 20:24; John 21:2; Acts 28:11; Romans 9:10-16; Song of Songs 4:2; Song of Songs 4:5; Song of Songs 6:6; Song of Songs 7:3
* **Associated Articles:** 5094: Jacob (ID: `5094@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5661: brothers (ID: `5661@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5737: sisters (ID: `5737@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5740: virgin (id: 5740)

Generally a young person, especially a female, who has not had sexual intercourse. Virginity is an important quality in a bride, and its surrender or violation prior to marriage is sternly condemned. In the miracle of the incarnation, Mary was still a virgin when Jesus Christ was conceived. Virginity is also used metaphorically to denote purity and innocence.

Virginity is important in a bride
---------------------------------

### Virgins were sought as brides

**[GEN 24:14–24:16](https://ref.ly/Gen24:14-Gen24:16)**; **[SNG 4:12](https://ref.ly/Song4:12)** *See also* [GEN 24:42–24:45](https://ref.ly/Gen24:42-Gen24:45) *The word translated “maiden” might also refer to a virgin*; [LEV 21:13–21:14](https://ref.ly/Lev21:13-Lev21:14); [DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19); [EST 2:2](https://ref.ly/Esth2:2), [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17); [ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5); [EZK 44:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:22)

### The church presented as a virgin bride

**[2CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2)** *See also* [EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27); [REV 19:7–19:8](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7-Rev19:8)

The loss of virginity before marriage
-------------------------------------

### Of a virgin not pledged in marriage

**[EXO 22:16–22:17](https://ref.ly/Exod22:16-Exod22:17)** *See also* [DEU 22:28–22:29](https://ref.ly/Deut22:28-Deut22:29); [2SA 13:12–13:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:12-2Sam13:16)

### Of a virgin pledged in marriage

**[DEU 22:20–22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:20-Deut22:21)** *See also* [DEU 22:23–22:24](https://ref.ly/Deut22:23-Deut22:24) *Betrothal was as serious a commitment as marriage itself; consorting with another person during this period was tantamount to adultery*; [DEU 22:25–22:27](https://ref.ly/Deut22:25-Deut22:27); [MAT 1:18–1:19](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:19)

### Some fathers surrendered their daughters' virginity

**[GEN 19:8](https://ref.ly/Gen19:8)** *See also* [JDG 19:24](https://ref.ly/Judg19:24)

Particular roles involving virgins
----------------------------------

### Maidens in victory processions

**[PSA 68:25](https://ref.ly/Ps68:25)**; **[JER 31:4](https://ref.ly/Jer31:4)** *See also* [EXO 15:20–15:21](https://ref.ly/Exod15:20-Exod15:21); [JDG 11:34](https://ref.ly/Judg11:34); [1SA 18:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6)

### Virgins as bridesmaids

**[PSA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ps45:14)** *See also* [MAT 25:1](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1)

### A virgin looked after King David

[1KI 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:1-1Kgs1:4)

Virginity as a sign of purity and innocence
-------------------------------------------

### Virgins were spared after military victories

**[NUM 31:17–31:18](https://ref.ly/Num31:17-Num31:18)** *See also* [JDG 21:11–21:12](https://ref.ly/Judg21:11-Judg21:12)

### Israel's virgin purity lost

**[JER 18:13](https://ref.ly/Jer18:13)** *See also* [JER 31:21–31:23](https://ref.ly/Jer31:21-Jer31:23); [EZK 23:3–23:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:3-Ezek23:8)

Virginity as a metaphor for (apparent) inviolability
----------------------------------------------------

[2KI 19:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:21) parallel [ISA 37:22](https://ref.ly/Isa37:22); ; [ISA 47:1](https://ref.ly/Isa47:1)

Lost opportunities of virgin youthfulness
-----------------------------------------

**[AMO 5:2](https://ref.ly/Amos5:2)** *See also* [JDG 11:36–11:40](https://ref.ly/Judg11:36-Judg11:40); [ISA 23:12](https://ref.ly/Isa23:12); [JER 46:11](https://ref.ly/Jer46:11); [LAM 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Lam1:15-Lam1:16), [LAM 2:13](https://ref.ly/Lam2:13); [JOL 1:8](https://ref.ly/Joel1:8)

Paul's advice about virgins
---------------------------

### A virgin is freer to serve God

**[1CO 7:32–7:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:32-1Cor7:35)** *The unmarried have fewer responsibilities and worldly cares and so may offer undivided devotion to God.* *See also* [1CO 7:25–7:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:25-1Cor7:28)

### Remaining unmarried is a preferred option

[1CO 7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8), [1CO 7:37–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:37-1Cor7:38)

### Marriage is the proper outlet for sexual passion

[1CO 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:9) *Though Paul urges abstinence, sexual passion is not wrong; however, sexual relationships must be confined to marriage*; ; [1CO 7:36](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36)

Mary's virginity at Jesus Christ's conception
---------------------------------------------

**[MAT 1:20–1:25](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20-Matt1:25)** *See also* [ISA 7:14](https://ref.ly/Isa7:14); [LUK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Luke1:27), [LUK 1:34–1:35](https://ref.ly/Luke1:34-Luke1:35)

Parable of the ten virgins
--------------------------

[MAT 25:1–25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 24:14-16; Song of Songs 4:12; Genesis 24:42-45; Leviticus 21:13-14; Deuteronomy 22:13-19; Esther 2:2; Esther 2:17; Isaiah 62:5; Ezekiel 44:22; 2 Corinthians 11:2; Ephesians 5:25-27; Revelation 19:7-8; Exodus 22:16-17; Deuteronomy 22:28-29; 2 Samuel 13:12-16; Deuteronomy 22:20-21; Deuteronomy 22:23-24; Deuteronomy 22:25-27; Matthew 1:18-19; Genesis 19:8; Judges 19:24; Psalms 68:25; Jeremiah 31:4; Exodus 15:20-21; Judges 11:34; 1 Samuel 18:6; Psalms 45:14; Matthew 25:1; 1 Kings 1:1-4; Numbers 31:17-18; Judges 21:11-12; Jeremiah 18:13; Jeremiah 31:21-23; Ezekiel 23:3-8; 2 Kings 19:21; Isaiah 37:22; Isaiah 47:1; Amos 5:2; Judges 11:36-40; Isaiah 23:12; Jeremiah 46:11; Lamentations 1:15-16; Lamentations 2:13; Joel 1:8; 1 Corinthians 7:32-35; 1 Corinthians 7:25-28; 1 Corinthians 7:8; 1 Corinthians 7:37-38; 1 Corinthians 7:9; 1 Corinthians 7:36; Matthew 1:20-25; Isaiah 7:14; Luke 1:27; Luke 1:34-35; Matthew 25:1-13

## 5741: vows (id: 5741)

Promises made to God, usually in the context of worship or religious practice. There was no requirement on any Israelite to make vows, but once made, they were binding and had to be kept.

Vows were binding
-----------------

**[DEU 23:21–23:23](https://ref.ly/Deut23:21-Deut23:23)** *See also* [NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2), [NUM 30:9](https://ref.ly/Num30:9); [PRO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Prov20:25); [ECC 5:4–5:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:4-Eccl5:6); [MAL 1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:14)

### Exceptions to keeping vows

**[NUM 30:3–30:8](https://ref.ly/Num30:3-Num30:8)** *In certain cases vows made by daughters or wives could be nullified by fathers or husbands respectively.* *See also* [NUM 30:10–30:15](https://ref.ly/Num30:10-Num30:15)

Vows made to God in the context of worship
------------------------------------------

### To offer sacrifices

**[LEV 22:17–22:19](https://ref.ly/Lev22:17-Lev22:19)** *See also* [LEV 7:16](https://ref.ly/Lev7:16), [LEV 22:21](https://ref.ly/Lev22:21), [LEV 27:9](https://ref.ly/Lev27:9); [NUM 15:2–15:4](https://ref.ly/Num15:2-Num15:4), [NUM 15:8–15:10](https://ref.ly/Num15:8-Num15:10); [DEU 12:6](https://ref.ly/Deut12:6), [DEU 12:11](https://ref.ly/Deut12:11); [PSA 50:14](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14), [PSA 66:13](https://ref.ly/Ps66:13); [PRO 7:14](https://ref.ly/Prov7:14); [JON 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:9)

### To dedicate people, animals or property to God

**[LEV 27:1–27:25](https://ref.ly/Lev27:1-Lev27:25)**

### To give money

**[2KI 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:4-2Kgs12:5)** *See also* [DEU 23:18](https://ref.ly/Deut23:18)

Motives for making vows
-----------------------

### To elicit God's help

**[1SA 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11)** *See also* [GEN 28:20–28:22](https://ref.ly/Gen28:20-Gen28:22); [NUM 21:2](https://ref.ly/Num21:2); [JDG 11:30–11:40](https://ref.ly/Judg11:30-Judg11:40); [2SA 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:7-2Sam15:8); [PSA 66:13–66:14](https://ref.ly/Ps66:13-Ps66:14)

### To express thanksgiving

**[PSA 116:12–116:14](https://ref.ly/Ps116:12-Ps116:14)** *See also* [PSA 50:14](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14), [PSA 56:12](https://ref.ly/Ps56:12), [PSA 116:17–116:18](https://ref.ly/Ps116:17-Ps116:18); [JON 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:16), [JON 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:9)

### To praise God

**[PSA 22:25](https://ref.ly/Ps22:25)** *See also* [PSA 61:8](https://ref.ly/Ps61:8), [PSA 65:1](https://ref.ly/Ps65:1)

Vows of Nazirites
-----------------

[NUM 6:1–6:21](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:21); ; [JDG 13:2–13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:2-Judg13:5) *Samson was set apart as a Nazirite from birth*; ; [1SA 1:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:11) *Samuel may have been a Nazirite.*

Examples of vows made to God
----------------------------

### By David

**[PSA 132:2–132:5](https://ref.ly/Ps132:2-Ps132:5)**

### By Paul

**[ACT 18:18](https://ref.ly/Acts18:18)** *See also* [ACT 21:24–21:26](https://ref.ly/Acts21:24-Acts21:26)

Abuse of vows
-------------

[MAT 15:5–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:5-Matt15:6) parallel [MRK 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:11-Mark7:13)

Vows made to idols
------------------

**[JER 44:25–44:28](https://ref.ly/Jer44:25-Jer44:28)**

## 5742: wedding (id: 5742)

The public acknowledgment and celebration of a couple's marriage. The wedding followed betrothal and was a preliminary to sexual union. The occasion was marked by feasting, singing and rejoicing. The bride wore a wedding dress, jewellery and ornaments and both she and the groom were waited on by attendants.

Wedding garments
----------------

### The bride's wedding dress

**[PSA 45:13](https://ref.ly/Ps45:13)** *See also* [GEN 24:65](https://ref.ly/Gen24:65); [EZK 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:10); [REV 19:7–19:8](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7-Rev19:8), [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

### The bride's jewellery and ornaments

**[ISA 49:18](https://ref.ly/Isa49:18)** *See also* [ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10); [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32); [EZK 16:11–16:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:11-Ezek16:12)

Wedding festivities
-------------------

### Weddings were occasions for rejoicing

**[JER 33:11](https://ref.ly/Jer33:11)** [GEN 29:27–29:28](https://ref.ly/Gen29:27-Gen29:28); ; [JDG 14:12](https://ref.ly/Judg14:12); [SNG 3:11](https://ref.ly/Song3:11); ; [ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5); ; [JER 7:34](https://ref.ly/Jer7:34)

### Wedding songs

PSA 45:0, [PSA 78:63](https://ref.ly/Ps78:63)

### The wedding banquet

**[JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10)** *See also* [GEN 29:21–29:22](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21-Gen29:22) *A feast celebrates Jacob's wedding*; [MAT 22:2](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2) *a wedding banquet given by a king for his son*; [JHN 2:3–2:10](https://ref.ly/John2:3-John2:10) *Jesus Christ changes water into wine at a wedding feast*; [REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9) *the marriage supper of the Lamb*

Guests and attendants at weddings
---------------------------------

### Wedding guests

**[JHN 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/John2:1-John2:2)** *See also* [MAT 22:3–22:10](https://ref.ly/Matt22:3-Matt22:10), [MAT 22:11–22:12](https://ref.ly/Matt22:11-Matt22:12) *Wedding guests were expected to wear appropriate clothes*; [LUK 14:8–14:10](https://ref.ly/Luke14:8-Luke14:10)

### Attendants on the groom

**[JHN 3:29](https://ref.ly/John3:29)** *See also* [JDG 14:11](https://ref.ly/Judg14:11), [JDG 14:20](https://ref.ly/Judg14:20); [MAT 9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:19](https://ref.ly/Mark2:19), [LUK 5:34](https://ref.ly/Luke5:34)

### Bridesmaids

**[PSA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Ps45:14)** *See also* [MAT 25:1–25:10](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:10)

Wedding gifts
-------------

**[1KI 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:16)** *See also* [GEN 24:59](https://ref.ly/Gen24:59), [GEN 29:24](https://ref.ly/Gen29:24), [GEN 29:29](https://ref.ly/Gen29:29); [JDG 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Judg1:14-Judg1:15)

Elements in the wedding ceremony
--------------------------------

### Covering the bride

Sometimes the groom would cover the bride with his cloak as a symbol of protection: [RUT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:9); ; [EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8)

### A covenanted commitment

[PRO 2:17](https://ref.ly/Prov2:17); ; [MAL 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14)

### Blessing the couple

[GEN 24:60](https://ref.ly/Gen24:60); ; [RUT 4:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:11)

### Consummating the marriage

**[JOL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Joel2:16)** *See also* [GEN 29:23](https://ref.ly/Gen29:23); [PSA 19:5](https://ref.ly/Ps19:5)

## 5743: widows (id: 5743)

God's tender concern for bereaved wives is declared throughout the whole of Scripture. He defends their rights and expects his people to do the same.

God is the protector of widows
------------------------------

**[PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5)** *See also* [DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18); [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); [PRO 15:25](https://ref.ly/Prov15:25); [JER 49:11](https://ref.ly/Jer49:11)

Jesus Christ's concern for widows
---------------------------------

[MRK 12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:44) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4); ; [LUK 7:11–7:15](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11-Luke7:15), [LUK 18:1–18:5](https://ref.ly/Luke18:1-Luke18:5)

Widows are to be treated justly
-------------------------------

**[DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19)** *See also* [EXO 22:22–22:24](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22-Exod22:24); [JOB 24:21–24:22](https://ref.ly/Job24:21-Job24:22); [PSA 94:6](https://ref.ly/Ps94:6); [ISA 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:4); [JER 22:3–22:5](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3-Jer22:5); [EZK 22:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:25)

Widows are not to be exploited
------------------------------

**[EXO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22)** *See also* [ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2); [EZK 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:7); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); [MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40) parallel [LUK 20:45–20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:47)

The law requires God's people to provide for widows
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:19](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19)** *See also* [DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29), [DEU 16:11](https://ref.ly/Deut16:11), [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17), [DEU 24:20–24:21](https://ref.ly/Deut24:20-Deut24:21), [DEU 26:12](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12); [ISA 1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:17) *The prophet encourages God's people to return to the law.*

The Christian community are to care for widows
----------------------------------------------

**[1TI 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:3)** *See also* [1TI 5:4–5:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4-1Tim5:16); [1CO 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8-1Cor7:9); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

Examples of widows and their treatment
--------------------------------------

[DEU 16:14](https://ref.ly/Deut16:14); ; [RUT 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:16-Ruth1:17); ; [1KI 17:13–17:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:13-1Kgs17:14); ; [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3), [JOB 29:13](https://ref.ly/Job29:13); ; [LUK 2:36–2:38](https://ref.ly/Luke2:36-Luke2:38) *Anna*; ; [LUK 4:25–4:26](https://ref.ly/Luke4:25-Luke4:26), [LUK 7:11–7:17](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11-Luke7:17) *the widow of Nain*; ; [ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1), [ACT 9:36–9:41](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36-Acts9:41) *Dorcas*

Women widowed because of God's judgment
---------------------------------------

**[EXO 22:22–22:24](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22-Exod22:24)** *See also* [PSA 109:9](https://ref.ly/Ps109:9); [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17); [JER 15:8](https://ref.ly/Jer15:8); [LAM 5:3](https://ref.ly/Lam5:3)

Laws governing the remarriage of widows
---------------------------------------

### The people generally

**[DEU 25:5–25:10](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5-Deut25:10)** *The OT emphasises continuity of the family line.* *See also* [GEN 38:8](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8); [RUT 3:12](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:12) *Boaz refers to the law which gave the nearest male relative the duty of marrying the widow*; [RUT 4:10](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:10); [MRK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Mark12:19) parallel [LUK 20:28](https://ref.ly/Luke20:28)

### The priests

**[LEV 21:13–21:14](https://ref.ly/Lev21:13-Lev21:14)** *See also* [EZK 44:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:22)

Widowhood as the personification of a city
------------------------------------------

## 5744: wife (id: 5744)

The female partner in a marriage relationship. The origin of this God\-ordained institution is traced back in the Bible to the Garden of Eden. Scripture stresses that marriage is a God\-ordained institution, within which the husband should love and care for his wife, just as the wife should obey, honour and care for her husband. A good wife is portrayed as a great blessing, but a bad wife influences her husband for evil. In the OT, Israel is sometimes portrayed as God's wife; in the NT, the church is described as Christ's bride.

God's pattern for marriage
--------------------------

### Husband and wife are one flesh

**[GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:23-Gen2:24); [MAL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:15); [MAT 19:4–19:6](https://ref.ly/Matt19:4-Matt19:6) parallel [MRK 10:6–10:9](https://ref.ly/Mark10:6-Mark10:9)

### The wife as helper and companion

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [GEN 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:22)

### The husband is head of the wife

**[EPH 5:23](https://ref.ly/Eph5:23)** *See also* [1CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:3)

### The wife is the weaker partner

**[1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)** *See also* [GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16)

The Israelites were forbidden to take foreign wives
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/Deut7:3-Deut7:4)** *See also* [EXO 34:16](https://ref.ly/Exod34:16); [JOS 23:12–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12-Josh23:13)

Duties of a wife
----------------

### To submit to and respect her husband

**[COL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Col3:18)** *See also* [EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24), [EPH 5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:33); [1PE 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:6)

### To care for her husband and household

**[PRO 31:10–31:31](https://ref.ly/Prov31:10-Prov31:31)** *See also* [TIT 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4-Titus2:5)

### To be faithful to her husband

**[EXO 20:14](https://ref.ly/Exod20:14)** parallel [DEU 5:18](https://ref.ly/Deut5:18) *See also* [NUM 5:11–5:31](https://ref.ly/Num5:11-Num5:31); [PRO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Prov23:27); [MAT 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3-Matt14:4) parallel [MRK 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:18); [MRK 10:12](https://ref.ly/Mark10:12); [LUK 3:19](https://ref.ly/Luke3:19)

## 5745: women (id: 5745)

God created both women and men in his image and likeness. Scripture shows women in a variety of roles, playing an important part in God's salvation plan. In Christ, there is no fundamental distinction between believers on account of gender, race or social status. Women played a significant part in the life of Jesus Christ and in the early church. The NT both records their role and faces the questions thereby raised.

The creation of women
---------------------

**[GEN 2:20–2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:20-Gen2:24)** *See also* [GEN 1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27), [GEN 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Gen5:1-Gen5:2); [1TI 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:13)

Women and the fall
------------------

**[1TI 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:14)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:16); [2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3)

## 5746: youth (id: 5746)

Scripture characterises youth as a time of vigour and strength. It speaks, too, of the pressures and expectations of youth, warns about the temptations facing young people, and instructs the young about what is expected of them.

The vigour of youth
-------------------

**[PRO 20:29](https://ref.ly/Prov20:29)** *See also* [JOB 33:25](https://ref.ly/Job33:25); [PSA 103:5](https://ref.ly/Ps103:5), [PSA 144:12](https://ref.ly/Ps144:12); [SNG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Song2:3); [ECC 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:9-Eccl11:10)**[ISA 40:30](https://ref.ly/Isa40:30)** *Even youthful strength and vigour may fail.* *See also* [JOB 20:11](https://ref.ly/Job20:11); [PSA 78:31](https://ref.ly/Ps78:31); [AMO 8:13](https://ref.ly/Amos8:13)

The duties of youth
-------------------

### To remember God

**[ECC 12:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:1)** *See also* [1KI 18:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:12); [PSA 71:5](https://ref.ly/Ps71:5); [LAM 3:25–3:27](https://ref.ly/Lam3:25-Lam3:27)**[PSA 119:9](https://ref.ly/Ps119:9)** *See also* [PSA 71:17](https://ref.ly/Ps71:17); [EZK 4:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:14)

### To respect the elderly

**[LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32)** *See also* [JOB 29:8](https://ref.ly/Job29:8), [JOB 32:6](https://ref.ly/Job32:6); [1TI 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1); [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)

### To honour parents

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)** parallel [DEU 5:16](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16) *See also* [EPH 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:2-Eph6:3); [LEV 19:3](https://ref.ly/Lev19:3); [1TI 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:4)

### To obey parents

**[PRO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8)** *See also* [PRO 6:20](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20), [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22); [EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1); [COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20)

### To remain faithful to one's partner

**[PRO 5:18](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18)** *See also* [PRO 2:17](https://ref.ly/Prov2:17); [MAL 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14-Mal2:15)

Examples of godly young people
------------------------------

### Godly young men

[1SA 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:18) *Samuel*; ; [1SA 17:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:33) *David, fighting Goliath*; ; [2CH 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:1-2Chr24:2) parallel [2KI 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:1-2Kgs12:2) *Joash became king at the age of seven*; ; [2CH 34:1–34:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:1-2Chr34:2) parallel [2KI 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:1-2Kgs22:2) *Josiah became king at the age of eight*; ; [LUK 2:42–2:52](https://ref.ly/Luke2:42-Luke2:52) *Jesus Christ at the age of twelve*; ; [2TI 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:5) *Timothy*

### Godly young women

[JDG 11:36](https://ref.ly/Judg11:36) *Jephthah's daughter*; ; [RUT 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:16-Ruth1:17) *Ruth*; ; [EST 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Esth4:15-Esth4:16) *Esther*; ; [LUK 1:38](https://ref.ly/Luke1:38) *the virgin Mary*; ; [ACT 21:9](https://ref.ly/Acts21:9) *Philip's daughters*

The promise of youth
--------------------

### Israel's youthful devotion

**[JER 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jer2:2)** *See also* [JER 3:4](https://ref.ly/Jer3:4); [EZK 16:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:22), [EZK 16:43](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:43)**[HOS 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14-Hos2:15)** *See also* [ISA 54:4](https://ref.ly/Isa54:4), [ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5); [EZK 16:60](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:60)

### Dying young as a sign of judgment

**[PSA 89:45](https://ref.ly/Ps89:45)** *See also* [JOB 36:14](https://ref.ly/Job36:14); [PSA 78:63](https://ref.ly/Ps78:63); [ISA 13:12](https://ref.ly/Isa13:12); [JER 9:21](https://ref.ly/Jer9:21) *The death of children and young people symbolises loss of hope for the future*; [JER 11:22](https://ref.ly/Jer11:22)

Particular pressures of youth
-----------------------------

### The inexperience of youth

[1KI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:7); ; [1CH 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:5), [1CH 29:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1); ; [2CH 13:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:7); ; [JER 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6); ; [1TI 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:12)

### Temptations facing the young

**[2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:22)** *See also* [PRO 7:7](https://ref.ly/Prov7:7); [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13), [LUK 15:30](https://ref.ly/Luke15:30); [MAT 19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:22), [LUK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:23); [TIT 2:6](https://ref.ly/Titus2:6)

### Troubles faced from the time of one's youth

[2SA 19:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:7); ; [PSA 88:15](https://ref.ly/Ps88:15), [PSA 129:1–129:2](https://ref.ly/Ps129:1-Ps129:2)

The sins of youth
-----------------

**[PSA 25:7](https://ref.ly/Ps25:7)** *See also* [JOB 13:26](https://ref.ly/Job13:26)

### Disrespect for older people

[2KI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23-2Kgs2:24); ; [JOB 30:1](https://ref.ly/Job30:1); ; [ISA 3:5](https://ref.ly/Isa3:5); ; [LAM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Lam5:12)

### Dishonouring parents

[DEU 21:18–21:20](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:20); ; [PRO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Prov15:20), [PRO 30:11](https://ref.ly/Prov30:11); ; [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2)

### Persistent sinfulness

[JER 3:24–3:25](https://ref.ly/Jer3:24-Jer3:25), [JER 22:21](https://ref.ly/Jer22:21), [JER 32:30](https://ref.ly/Jer32:30)

### The sinfulness of Israel's youth

[EZK 23:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:3), [EZK 23:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:8), [EZK 23:19–23:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:19-Ezek23:21); ; [HOS 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1-Hos11:2)

Examples of sinful young men
----------------------------

[1SA 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:17) *the sons of Eli*; ; [1SA 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:2-1Sam8:3) *the sons of Samuel*; ; [2SA 15:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:6) *Absalom*; ; [1KI 12:8–12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:8-1Kgs12:14) parallel [2CH 10:8–10:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:8-2Chr10:11) *Rehoboam*; ; [2KI 21:1–21:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:1-2Kgs21:2) parallel [2CH 33:1–33:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:1-2Chr33:2) *Manasseh*

## 5761: attitudes (id: 5761)

The ways in which people think or feel about something or someone and which affect their behaviour.

* **Associated Articles:** 5762: attitudes, God's to people (ID: `5762@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5763: attitudes, positive to God (ID: `5763@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5764: attitudes, negative to God (ID: `5764@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5765: attitudes, to other people (ID: `5765@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5766: attitudes, to life (ID: `5766@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5767: attitudes, in prayer (ID: `5767@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5762: attitudes, God's to people (id: 5762)

Scripture indicates the ways in which God relates to people, often suggesting that these illustrate the ways in which believers should behave.

God's love
----------

**[JHN 3:16](https://ref.ly/John3:16)** *See also* [PSA 36:5–36:7](https://ref.ly/Ps36:5-Ps36:7), [PSA 107:1](https://ref.ly/Ps107:1), [PSA 136:1](https://ref.ly/Ps136:1); [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/John13:1); [ROM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rom5:5), [ROM 8:35–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:39); [EPH 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eph5:2); [1JN 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/1John4:8-1John4:10), [1JN 4:16](https://ref.ly/1John4:16)

God's compassion
----------------

**[PSA 116:5](https://ref.ly/Ps116:5)** *See also* [ISA 54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:8); [JER 9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:24); [HOS 11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:4); [MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36); [ROM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rom2:4), [ROM 11:22](https://ref.ly/Rom11:22); [2CO 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3); [EPH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Eph2:7); [TIT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Titus3:4); [JAS 5:11](https://ref.ly/Jas5:11)

God's grace and mercy
---------------------

**[JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14)** *See also* [DEU 4:31](https://ref.ly/Deut4:31); [DAN 9:9](https://ref.ly/Dan9:9); [ROM 5:15](https://ref.ly/Rom5:15); [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9); [EPH 2:4](https://ref.ly/Eph2:4), [EPH 2:7](https://ref.ly/Eph2:7); [1TI 1:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:14); [1PE 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10)

God's jealousy
--------------

**[EXO 20:5](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5)** parallel [DEU 5:9](https://ref.ly/Deut5:9) *See also* [DEU 4:24](https://ref.ly/Deut4:24); [JOS 24:19](https://ref.ly/Josh24:19); [NAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Nah1:2)

God's patience
--------------

**[2PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:9)** *See also* [NEH 9:30](https://ref.ly/Neh9:30); [ROM 9:22](https://ref.ly/Rom9:22); [1TI 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:16); [1PE 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:20)

God's faithfulness
------------------

**[PSA 117:2](https://ref.ly/Ps117:2)** *See also* [EXO 34:6](https://ref.ly/Exod34:6); [DEU 7:9](https://ref.ly/Deut7:9); [PSA 25:10](https://ref.ly/Ps25:10), [PSA 36:5](https://ref.ly/Ps36:5), [PSA 119:90](https://ref.ly/Ps119:90); [LAM 3:22–3:23](https://ref.ly/Lam3:22-Lam3:23); [1CO 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:13); [1JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:9)

* **Associated Passages:** John 3:16; Psalms 36:5-7; Psalms 107:1; Psalms 136:1; John 13:1; Romans 5:5; Romans 8:35-39; Ephesians 5:2; 1 John 4:8-10; 1 John 4:16; Psalms 116:5; Isaiah 54:8; Jeremiah 9:24; Hosea 11:4; Matthew 9:36; Romans 2:4; Romans 11:22; 2 Corinthians 1:3; Ephesians 2:7; Titus 3:4; James 5:11; John 1:14; Deuteronomy 4:31; Daniel 9:9; Romans 5:15; 2 Corinthians 8:9; Ephesians 2:4; 1 Timothy 1:14; 1 Peter 5:10; Exodus 20:5; Deuteronomy 5:9; Deuteronomy 4:24; Joshua 24:19; Nahum 1:2; 2 Peter 3:9; Nehemiah 9:30; Romans 9:22; 1 Timothy 1:16; 1 Peter 3:20; Psalms 117:2; Exodus 34:6; Deuteronomy 7:9; Psalms 25:10; Psalms 36:5; Psalms 119:90; Lamentations 3:22-23; 1 Corinthians 10:13; 1 John 1:9
* **Associated Articles:** 1030: God, compassion of (ID: `1030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1035: God, faithfulness of (ID: `1035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1055: God, grace and mercy of (ID: `1055@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1085: God, love of (ID: `1085@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1095: God, patience of (ID: `1095@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5379: law, Jesus Christ's attitude to (ID: `5379@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5825: cruelty, God's attitude to (ID: `5825@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6025: sin, and God's character (ID: `6025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8773: jealousy (ID: `8773@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8792: oppression, God's attitude to (ID: `8792@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5763: attitudes, positive to God (id: 5763)

Scripture identifies a range of positive attitudes towards God, which believers are encouraged to adopt.

Trust
-----

**[PRO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Prov3:5)** *See also* [PSA 20:7](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7), [PSA 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Ps25:1-Ps25:2), [PSA 40:4](https://ref.ly/Ps40:4), [PSA 56:11](https://ref.ly/Ps56:11); [JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1); [ROM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Rom15:13)

Faith
-----

**[MAT 8:10](https://ref.ly/Matt8:10)** parallel [LUK 7:9](https://ref.ly/Luke7:9) *See also* [MAT 9:2](https://ref.ly/Matt9:2) parallels [MRK 2:5](https://ref.ly/Mark2:5), [LUK 5:20](https://ref.ly/Luke5:20); [HEB 11:1–11:39](https://ref.ly/Heb11:1-Heb11:39)

Hope
----

**[JOB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Job13:15)** *See also* [PSA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Ps25:5); [ISA 40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:31); [MIC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Mic7:7); [ROM 4:18](https://ref.ly/Rom4:18)

Love
----

**[DEU 6:5](https://ref.ly/Deut6:5)** *See also* [DEU 30:6](https://ref.ly/Deut30:6); [PSA 18:1](https://ref.ly/Ps18:1), [PSA 116:1](https://ref.ly/Ps116:1); [JHN 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17)

Holiness
--------

**[HEB 12:14](https://ref.ly/Heb12:14)**; **[1PE 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:15-1Pet1:16)** *See also* [DEU 7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:6), [DEU 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1-Deut14:2); [ROM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1-Rom12:2); [1CO 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7-1Cor5:8); [COL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Col3:2)

Awesome respect
---------------

**[ECC 12:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:13)** *See also* [DEU 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12-Deut10:13); [PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7), [PRO 9:10](https://ref.ly/Prov9:10); [JER 5:22](https://ref.ly/Jer5:22); [ACT 9:31](https://ref.ly/Acts9:31); [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7)

Repentance
----------

**[2CO 7:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:10)** *See also* [JOB 42:6](https://ref.ly/Job42:6); [EZK 14:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:6); [ACT 2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:38), [ACT 17:30](https://ref.ly/Acts17:30)

Humility
--------

**[ISA 66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2)** *See also* [EZR 8:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:21); [JAS 4:10](https://ref.ly/Jas4:10); [1PE 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:6)

Self\-denial
------------

**[MAT 16:24](https://ref.ly/Matt16:24)** parallels [MRK 8:34](https://ref.ly/Mark8:34), [LUK 9:23](https://ref.ly/Luke9:23)

Submission
----------

**[JAS 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jas4:7)** *See also* [JOB 22:21](https://ref.ly/Job22:21); [ROM 8:7](https://ref.ly/Rom8:7), [ROM 10:3](https://ref.ly/Rom10:3)

Thankfulness
------------

**[1TH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:18)** *See also* [1CH 16:34](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:34); [PSA 35:18](https://ref.ly/Ps35:18), [PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4); [MAT 14:19](https://ref.ly/Matt14:19); [EPH 5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:20); [PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6); [COL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:7)

Praise
------

**[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)** *See also* [EZR 3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11); [PSA 148:1–148:5](https://ref.ly/Ps148:1-Ps148:5), [PSA 150:1–150:6](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1-Ps150:6); [ISA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Isa42:10), [ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3); [ACT 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46-Acts2:47); [JAS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jas5:13)

Prayerfulness
-------------

**[JAS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Jas5:13)** *See also* [MAT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Matt14:23), [MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41); [ROM 8:26](https://ref.ly/Rom8:26); [1TH 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:17)

Reverence
---------

**[PSA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Ps5:7)** *See also* [NEH 5:15](https://ref.ly/Neh5:15); [MAL 2:5](https://ref.ly/Mal2:5), [MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2); [EPH 5:21](https://ref.ly/Eph5:21); [HEB 12:28](https://ref.ly/Heb12:28); [1PE 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:2)

Watchfulness
------------

## 5764: attitudes, negative to God (id: 5764)

Scripture identifies a range of negative attitudes towards God, which believers are encouraged to avoid.

Rejection
---------

**[ISA 53:3](https://ref.ly/Isa53:3)** *See also* [LEV 26:15–26:16](https://ref.ly/Lev26:15-Lev26:16); [1SA 8:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:7); [EZK 5:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:6), [EZK 20:15–20:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:15-Ezek20:16); [JHN 1:11](https://ref.ly/John1:11)

Lack of faith
-------------

**[MRK 4:40](https://ref.ly/Mark4:40)** parallels [MAT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Matt8:26), [LUK 8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:25) *See also* [MAT 13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:58) parallel [MRK 6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:6)

Pride
-----

**[DEU 8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:14)** *See also* [LEV 26:19](https://ref.ly/Lev26:19); [LUK 1:51](https://ref.ly/Luke1:51); [JAS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jas4:6); [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5); [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34)

Stubbornness
------------

**[JER 5:23](https://ref.ly/Jer5:23)** *See also* [PSA 78:8](https://ref.ly/Ps78:8); [ISA 48:4](https://ref.ly/Isa48:4); [MRK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mark3:5), [MRK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:14); [ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5)

Rebellion
---------

**[PSA 78:40](https://ref.ly/Ps78:40)** *See also* [DEU 1:26](https://ref.ly/Deut1:26); [ISA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Isa1:2); [JER 2:29](https://ref.ly/Jer2:29); [EZK 2:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:3); [HOS 7:13](https://ref.ly/Hos7:13); [TIT 1:10](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10); [HEB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Heb3:16)

Hostility
---------

**[ROM 8:7](https://ref.ly/Rom8:7)** *See also* [LEV 26:21](https://ref.ly/Lev26:21), [LEV 26:23–26:24](https://ref.ly/Lev26:23-Lev26:24), [LEV 26:27–26:28](https://ref.ly/Lev26:27-Lev26:28); [HOS 9:7](https://ref.ly/Hos9:7)

## 5765: attitudes, to other people (id: 5765)

Scripture illustrates a range of attitudes towards other people, some of which are commended, while others are condemned.

Love
----

**[JHN 13:34–13:35](https://ref.ly/John13:34-John13:35)** *See also* [MAT 22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:39) parallels [MRK 12:31](https://ref.ly/Mark12:31), [LUK 10:27](https://ref.ly/Luke10:27); [1CO 13:1–13:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:1-1Cor13:13); [EPH 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eph5:2); [1TH 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:9); [1JN 3:11](https://ref.ly/1John3:11), [1JN 4:7](https://ref.ly/1John4:7)

Forgiveness
-----------

**[COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:13)** *See also* [MAT 6:14](https://ref.ly/Matt6:14); [MRK 11:25](https://ref.ly/Mark11:25); [LUK 6:37](https://ref.ly/Luke6:37), [LUK 17:3](https://ref.ly/Luke17:3); [EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32)

Helpfulness
-----------

**[ACT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36)** *See also* [EZK 16:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:49); [MAT 25:44](https://ref.ly/Matt25:44); [ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35); [1CO 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28); [2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:2); [1TI 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:9-1Tim5:10); [HEB 6:10](https://ref.ly/Heb6:10)

Hospitality
-----------

**[ROM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Rom12:13)** *See also* [GEN 18:1–18:8](https://ref.ly/Gen18:1-Gen18:8); [LUK 10:38](https://ref.ly/Luke10:38); [HEB 13:2](https://ref.ly/Heb13:2)

Generosity
----------

**[2CO 8:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:2)** *See also* [PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21), [PSA 37:26](https://ref.ly/Ps37:26); [MAT 10:8](https://ref.ly/Matt10:8); [ACT 10:2](https://ref.ly/Acts10:2); [2CO 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6-2Cor9:7); [1TI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:18)

Kindness
--------

**[EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32)** *See also* [PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31); [1CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4); [COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12); [2TI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24); [TIT 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4-Titus2:5)

Humility
--------

**[1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)** *See also* [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34); [MAT 20:26–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:26-Matt20:28) parallel [MRK 10:43–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:43-Mark10:45); [PHP 2:3–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3-Phil2:8); [TIT 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1-Titus3:2); [JAS 3:13](https://ref.ly/Jas3:13)

Patience
--------

**[1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14)** *See also* [PRO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Prov16:32); [1CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4); [GAL 5:22](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22); [EPH 4:2](https://ref.ly/Eph4:2); [COL 1:11](https://ref.ly/Col1:11); [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2); [JAS 5:7–5:11](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7-Jas5:11)

Submission
----------

**[EPH 5:21](https://ref.ly/Eph5:21)** [ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1), [ROM 13:5](https://ref.ly/Rom13:5); ; [1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13); [EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24); ; [COL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Col3:18); ; [1TI 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:11); ; [1PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1), [1PE 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:5-1Pet3:6); [JAS 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jas3:17); ; [1PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18) *submission of slaves to masters*; ; [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)

Gentleness
----------

**[PHP 4:5](https://ref.ly/Phil4:5)** *See also* [GAL 5:23](https://ref.ly/Gal5:23); [EPH 4:2](https://ref.ly/Eph4:2); [COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12)

Unselfishness
-------------

Pride
-----

**[ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16)** *See also* [PRO 8:13](https://ref.ly/Prov8:13); [2CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7); [GAL 5:26](https://ref.ly/Gal5:26)

Jealousy
--------

**[PRO 27:4](https://ref.ly/Prov27:4)** *See also* [GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1), [GEN 37:11](https://ref.ly/Gen37:11); [ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17), [ACT 7:9](https://ref.ly/Acts7:9); [1CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)

Judgment
--------

**[LUK 6:37](https://ref.ly/Luke6:37)** parallel [MAT 7:1](https://ref.ly/Matt7:1) *See also* [JHN 8:3–8:7](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:7)

Anger
-----

**[EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31)** *See also* [GEN 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gen4:3-Gen4:5); [2KI 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:11); [MRK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mark3:5); [EPH 4:26](https://ref.ly/Eph4:26); [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8)

Hatred
------

**[1JN 3:15](https://ref.ly/1John3:15)** *See also* [1JN 2:9](https://ref.ly/1John2:9), [1JN 2:11](https://ref.ly/1John2:11), [1JN 4:20](https://ref.ly/1John4:20)

Partiality
----------

## 5766: attitudes, to life (id: 5766)

The way in which people think or feel about human life is of major importance to morality. Scripture declares the sanctity of life, and the attitudes towards life which should follow.

The sanctity of life
--------------------

[PSA 49:7–49:8](https://ref.ly/Ps49:7-Ps49:8); ; [MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26) parallel [MRK 8:37](https://ref.ly/Mark8:37)

Fear
----

**[1JN 4:18](https://ref.ly/1John4:18)** *See also* [PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4), [PSA 91:5](https://ref.ly/Ps91:5), [PSA 112:7–112:8](https://ref.ly/Ps112:7-Ps112:8); [ISA 35:4](https://ref.ly/Isa35:4), [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10); [1PE 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:6)

Courage
-------

**[1CO 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:13)** *See also* [JOS 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Josh1:6-Josh1:7); [1SA 17:32](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:32), [1SA 17:37](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:37); [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13)

Rejoicing
---------

**[PSA 118:24](https://ref.ly/Ps118:24)** *See also* [ROM 5:3](https://ref.ly/Rom5:3), [ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15); [1PE 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:13)

Anxiety
-------

**[PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6)** *See also* [PSA 94:19](https://ref.ly/Ps94:19); [PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25); [ECC 11:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:10); [MAT 6:25–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:34) parallel [LUK 12:22–12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:22-Luke12:31); [LUK 10:41](https://ref.ly/Luke10:41); [1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)

Being forward\-looking and positive
-----------------------------------

## 5767: attitudes, in prayer (id: 5767)

Attitudes in prayer include both the postures people adopt while praying and the hopes that they have concerning its outcome. Scripture points to the need for humility and expectation on the part of those who pray.

Positions adopted for prayer
----------------------------

### Bowing down and kneeling

**[PSA 95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6)** *See also* [GEN 24:26](https://ref.ly/Gen24:26); [EXO 4:31](https://ref.ly/Exod4:31); [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23); [DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10); [LUK 22:41](https://ref.ly/Luke22:41); [ACT 7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:60); [EPH 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eph3:14); [PHP 2:10](https://ref.ly/Phil2:10)

### Falling on one's face before God

**[MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39)** *See also* [NUM 20:6](https://ref.ly/Num20:6); [JOS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Josh5:14); [2CH 20:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:18)

### Standing

**[MRK 11:25](https://ref.ly/Mark11:25)** *See also* [1KI 8:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:22); [LUK 18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11)

-------------------------------

### Standing for warfare

**[EPH 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Eph6:13-Eph6:14)** *See also* [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15); [JAS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Jas5:8)

### Walking with God
