## 5769: behaviour (id: 5769)

A person's actions or way of life. Good behaviour will not earn salvation, but believers must practise good behaviour in accordance with Scripture as evidence of their conversion. Scripture stresses that bad behaviour is inconsistent with Christian faith and urges believers to mend their ways.

Examples of good behaviour
--------------------------

**[GEN 6:22](https://ref.ly/Gen6:22)**; **[LUK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Luke1:6)** *See also* [GEN 5:24](https://ref.ly/Gen5:24); [2KI 18:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:3); [JOB 1:8](https://ref.ly/Job1:8); [JHN 1:47](https://ref.ly/John1:47); [3JN 1:3](https://ref.ly/3John1:3), [3JN 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/3John1:5-3John1:6), [3JN 1:12](https://ref.ly/3John1:12)

Examples of bad behaviour
-------------------------

**[1KI 16:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:30)** *See also* [GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8), [GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5); [1SA 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:3); [2TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:14); [3JN 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/3John1:9-3John1:10)

Good behaviour towards God is commanded
---------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:2–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:2-Exod20:11)** parallel [DEU 5:6–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6-Deut5:15)

### Obedience to God's word

**[PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2)** *See also* [PSA 19:7–19:11](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7-Ps19:11), [PSA 119:1–119:4](https://ref.ly/Ps119:1-Ps119:4)

Good behaviour towards other people is commanded
------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:12)** *See also* [EXO 20:12–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12-Exod20:17) parallel [DEU 5:16–5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16-Deut5:21); [PSA 15:1–15:5](https://ref.ly/Ps15:1-Ps15:5); [PRO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Prov12:2) *[Pr 10:1–31:31](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1-Prov31:31) contains much wisdom concerning good and bad behaviour*; [MAT 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:21-Matt5:24)

Good behaviour does not earn salvation
--------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rom3:20)**; **[TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5)** *See also* [GAL 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15-Gal2:16); [EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9); [PHP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:9)

Good behaviour confirms a believer's profession of faith
--------------------------------------------------------

**[JAS 2:14–2:19](https://ref.ly/Jas2:14-Jas2:19)**; **[2PE 1:5–1:11](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:5-2Pet1:11)** *See also* [MAT 7:16–7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:16-Matt7:20) parallel [LUK 6:43–6:45](https://ref.ly/Luke6:43-Luke6:45)

### Obedience to Jesus Christ and his teachings

**[MAT 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:25)** parallel [LUK 6:47–6:48](https://ref.ly/Luke6:47-Luke6:48) *See also* [JHN 14:15](https://ref.ly/John14:15), [JHN 14:23](https://ref.ly/John14:23), [JHN 15:10](https://ref.ly/John15:10); [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)

### A believer has died to sin and is alive to God

**[ROM 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Rom6:11-Rom6:14)** *See also* [ROM 6:2](https://ref.ly/Rom6:2), [ROM 6:19](https://ref.ly/Rom6:19); [GAL 5:16](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16), [GAL 5:24–5:25](https://ref.ly/Gal5:24-Gal5:25); [EPH 4:22–4:24](https://ref.ly/Eph4:22-Eph4:24); [COL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Col3:1), [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5), [COL 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Col3:9-Col3:10); [1TH 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:5-1Thess5:8)

## 5770: abandonment (id: 5770)

The state and experience of being forsaken, shared by God as well as by human beings.

God is abandoned
----------------

### God's people deliberately abandon him

**[JER 2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:13)** *See also* [DEU 32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15); [JDG 2:12](https://ref.ly/Judg2:12); [1KI 9:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:9) parallel [2CH 7:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:22); [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11); [ISA 1:4](https://ref.ly/Isa1:4); [JER 17:13](https://ref.ly/Jer17:13)

### God's people abandon his law

**[JER 9:12–9:14](https://ref.ly/Jer9:12-Jer9:14)** *See also* [1KI 18:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:18); [2CH 12:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:1); [PSA 89:30](https://ref.ly/Ps89:30), [PSA 119:53](https://ref.ly/Ps119:53); [PRO 4:2](https://ref.ly/Prov4:2), [PRO 4:6](https://ref.ly/Prov4:6); [HEB 10:28](https://ref.ly/Heb10:28)

### God's people abandon his covenant

**[DEU 29:25](https://ref.ly/Deut29:25)** *See also* [JER 22:9](https://ref.ly/Jer22:9), [JER 31:32](https://ref.ly/Jer31:32); [DAN 11:30](https://ref.ly/Dan11:30)

### God's people abandon his temple

[2CH 24:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:18), [2CH 29:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:6)

### Those who abandon the gospel

**[1TI 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1)** *See also* [HEB 10:26](https://ref.ly/Heb10:26), [HEB 10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:29); [REV 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rev2:4)

God abandons those who forsake him
----------------------------------

**[2CH 24:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:20)** *See also* [NUM 32:15](https://ref.ly/Num32:15) *Moses warns the Transjordan tribes*; [DEU 31:17](https://ref.ly/Deut31:17) *Israel's rebellion predicted*; [DEU 32:301](https://ref.ly/Deut32:301)SA [18:12](https://ref.ly/Deut18:12), [1SA 28:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:15)[1KI 9:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:7) parallel [2CH 7:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:20); [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); [NEH 9:28](https://ref.ly/Neh9:28); [ISA 2:6](https://ref.ly/Isa2:6); [JER 12:7](https://ref.ly/Jer12:7); [LAM 2:7](https://ref.ly/Lam2:7)

God shows mercy to those who have been justly abandoned
-------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 54:7](https://ref.ly/Isa54:7)** *See also* [NEH 9:19](https://ref.ly/Neh9:19), [NEH 9:28](https://ref.ly/Neh9:28), [NEH 9:31](https://ref.ly/Neh9:31); [JER 51:5](https://ref.ly/Jer51:5)

Those who feel abandoned by God
-------------------------------

### God's people

**[LAM 5:20](https://ref.ly/Lam5:20)** *See also* [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1), [PSA 43:2](https://ref.ly/Ps43:2), [PSA 71:11](https://ref.ly/Ps71:11), [PSA 74:1](https://ref.ly/Ps74:1), [PSA 89:38](https://ref.ly/Ps89:38)

### Their prayers

**[PSA 38:21–38:22](https://ref.ly/Ps38:21-Ps38:22)** *See also* [1KI 8:57](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:57); [PSA 27:9](https://ref.ly/Ps27:9), [PSA 71:9](https://ref.ly/Ps71:9), [PSA 71:18](https://ref.ly/Ps71:18), [PSA 119:8](https://ref.ly/Ps119:8); [JER 14:9](https://ref.ly/Jer14:9)

### Jesus Christ's feelings of abandonment

**[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46)** parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34) *See also* [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1); [ACT 2:27](https://ref.ly/Acts2:27); [PSA 16:10](https://ref.ly/Ps16:10)

God promises never to abandon his people
----------------------------------------

**[DEU 31:8](https://ref.ly/Deut31:8)** *See also* [LEV 26:44](https://ref.ly/Lev26:44); [DEU 4:31](https://ref.ly/Deut4:31), [DEU 31:6](https://ref.ly/Deut31:6); [1SA 12:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:22); [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25), [PSA 37:28](https://ref.ly/Ps37:28), [PSA 94:14](https://ref.ly/Ps94:14); [ISA 49:14–49:16](https://ref.ly/Isa49:14-Isa49:16); [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); [2CO 4:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:9); [HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)

## 5771: abstinence (id: 5771)

The deliberate decision to do without something, especially as a form of spiritual discipline expressing commitment or repentance.

## 5773: abstinence, as a discipline (id: 5773)

Israel's abstinence from certain sorts of meat
----------------------------------------------

[LEV 7:22–7:27](https://ref.ly/Lev7:22-Lev7:27), [LEV 11:1–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:1-Lev11:47); ; [DEU 14:1–14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:1-Deut14:21); ; [ACT 10:9–10:14](https://ref.ly/Acts10:9-Acts10:14) *God used the food laws to teach Peter a spiritual lesson.*

Israel's abstinence from other things
-------------------------------------

**[EXO 19:14–19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:14-Exod19:15)**; **[LEV 16:29–16:31](https://ref.ly/Lev16:29-Lev16:31)**; **[NUM 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:4)** *See also* [EXO 31:16–31:17](https://ref.ly/Exod31:16-Exod31:17); [NUM 29:7](https://ref.ly/Num29:7); [JDG 13:4](https://ref.ly/Judg13:4); [1SA 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:4)

Abstinence from food
--------------------

### As an expression of repentance

**[1SA 7:5–7:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:5-1Sam7:6)**; **[EZR 10:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:6)** *See also* [2SA 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:11-2Sam1:12); [NEH 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3-Neh1:4); [JOL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12); [JON 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:5)

### Expressing a seeking after God

**[ACT 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Acts13:1-Acts13:2)** *See also* [MAT 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:2) parallel [LUK 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:2); [MAT 9:14–9:15](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:15) parallels [MRK 2:18–2:20](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:20), [LUK 5:33–5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33-Luke5:35); [LUK 2:36–2:37](https://ref.ly/Luke2:36-Luke2:37); [ACT 9:9](https://ref.ly/Acts9:9), [ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23); [ROM 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rom14:6)

Temporary sexual abstinence as a form of discipline
---------------------------------------------------

[EXO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:15); ; [1SA 21:4–21:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:4-1Sam21:5); ; [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5)

Denial of selfish desires
-------------------------

**[MAT 16:24](https://ref.ly/Matt16:24)** parallels [MRK 8:34](https://ref.ly/Mark8:34), [LUK 9:23](https://ref.ly/Luke9:23)**[1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11)** *See also* [ROM 13:13](https://ref.ly/Rom13:13)

Voluntary abstinence for the sake of others
-------------------------------------------

**[1CO 8:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:13)** *See also* [ACT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:20), [ACT 15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:29), [ACT 21:25](https://ref.ly/Acts21:25); [ROM 14:21](https://ref.ly/Rom14:21); [1CO 9:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:12), [1CO 9:19–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:19-1Cor9:27), [1CO 10:15–10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:15-1Cor10:31)

Abstinence may be hypocritical
------------------------------

**[MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16)** *See also* [ISA 58:3–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:3-Isa58:7); [JER 14:10–14:12](https://ref.ly/Jer14:10-Jer14:12); [ZEC 7:2–7:6](https://ref.ly/Zech7:2-Zech7:6); [LUK 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11-Luke18:12); [1TI 4:2–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:2-1Tim4:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 7:22-27; Leviticus 11:1-47; Deuteronomy 14:1-21; Acts 10:9-14; Exodus 19:14-15; Leviticus 16:29-31; Numbers 6:1-4; Exodus 31:16-17; Numbers 29:7; Judges 13:4; 1 Samuel 21:4; 1 Samuel 7:5-6; Ezra 10:6; 2 Samuel 1:11-12; Nehemiah 1:3-4; Joel 2:12; Jonah 3:5; Acts 13:1-2; Matthew 4:1-2; Luke 4:1-2; Matthew 9:14-15; Mark 2:18-20; Luke 5:33-35; Luke 2:36-37; Acts 9:9; Acts 14:23; Romans 14:6; Exodus 19:15; 1 Samuel 21:4-5; 1 Corinthians 7:5; Matthew 16:24; Mark 8:34; Luke 9:23; 1 Peter 2:11; Romans 13:13; 1 Corinthians 8:13; Acts 15:19-20; Acts 15:29; Acts 21:25; Romans 14:21; 1 Corinthians 9:12; 1 Corinthians 9:19-27; 1 Corinthians 10:15-31; Matthew 6:16; Isaiah 58:3-7; Jeremiah 14:10-12; Zechariah 7:2-6; Luke 18:11-12; 1 Timothy 4:2-5
* **Associated Articles:** 4436: drinking, abstention from (ID: `4436@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4544: wine (ID: `4544@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5794: asceticism (ID: `5794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5850: excess (ID: `5850@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7340: clean and unclean (ID: `7340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8204: chastity (ID: `8204@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8339: self-control (ID: `8339@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8429: fasting (ID: `8429@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8451: mortification (ID: `8451@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8475: self-denial (ID: `8475@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8737: evil, believers' responses to (ID: `8737@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5775: abuse (id: 5775)

The deliberate misuse of gifts or privileges, and the insulting of individuals. Scripture stresses that neither God's gifts nor his people are to be abused.

The abuse of God and his people
-------------------------------

### Of God himself

**[2KI 19:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:16)**

### Of God's people

**[NEH 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Neh4:1-Neh4:4)**; **[1PE 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:4)** *See also* [MAT 5:11](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11); [ACT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Acts2:13), [ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32), [ACT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Acts18:6); [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rev2:9)

### Of God's servants

**[ACT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Acts2:13)** *See also* [2CH 36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:16); [PSA 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6-Ps22:8), [PSA 69:7–69:12](https://ref.ly/Ps69:7-Ps69:12), [PSA 123:3](https://ref.ly/Ps123:3); [JER 20:7](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7)

### Of Jesus Christ

**[LUK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Luke16:14)** *See also* [LUK 23:11](https://ref.ly/Luke23:11)

The abuse of Christian freedom is condemned
-------------------------------------------

### Believers are required to resist sin

**[ROM 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12)** *See also* [ROM 6:14](https://ref.ly/Rom6:14); [HEB 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1-Heb12:2); [1JN 5:16–5:18](https://ref.ly/1John5:16-1John5:18)

### Grace does not give believers the freedom to sin

**[ROM 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Rom6:1-Rom6:2)** *See also* [ROM 6:15](https://ref.ly/Rom6:15), [ROM 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/Rom3:5-Rom3:8); [GAL 2:17–2:21](https://ref.ly/Gal2:17-Gal2:21)

The dangers of abusing Christian freedom
----------------------------------------

[1CO 8:9–8:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9-1Cor8:12) *becoming a stumbling\-block to others*; ; [GAL 5:13](https://ref.ly/Gal5:13) *indulging oneself*; ; [1PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:16) *as means of covering up evil*

Examples of the abuse of Christian freedom
------------------------------------------

### Sexual excesses

**[1CO 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:2)** *See also* [1CO 6:12–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:12-1Cor6:20); [REV 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20-Rev2:22)

### Abuse of giving

[ACT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:2)

### Abuse of the Lord's Supper

**[1CO 11:20–11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:22)**

### Falling back into sin

*See also* [ROM 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Rom6:1-Rom6:2); [HEB 12:1](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1); [1JN 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:8-1John1:10)

### Disobedience towards God

[EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); ; [1PE 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:8); ; [1JN 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/1John2:3-1John2:4), [1JN 3:4](https://ref.ly/1John3:4)

### Abuse of spiritual gifts

[1CO 14:1–14:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1-1Cor14:20)

### Eating food sacrificed to idols

[1CO 8:1–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:1-1Cor8:13); ; [REV 2:14](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14), [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 19:16; Nehemiah 4:1-4; 1 Peter 4:4; Matthew 5:11; Acts 2:13; Acts 17:32; Acts 18:6; Revelation 2:9; 2 Chronicles 36:16; Psalms 22:6-8; Psalms 69:7-12; Psalms 123:3; Jeremiah 20:7; Luke 16:14; Luke 23:11; Romans 6:12; Romans 6:14; Hebrews 12:1-2; 1 John 5:16-18; Romans 6:1-2; Romans 6:15; Romans 3:5-8; Galatians 2:17-21; 1 Corinthians 8:9-12; Galatians 5:13; 1 Peter 2:16; 1 Corinthians 5:1-2; 1 Corinthians 6:12-20; Revelation 2:20-22; Acts 5:1-2; 1 Corinthians 11:20-22; Hebrews 12:1; 1 John 1:8-10; Ephesians 5:6; 1 Peter 2:8; 1 John 2:3-4; 1 John 3:4; 1 Corinthians 14:1-20; 1 Corinthians 8:1-13; Revelation 2:14; Revelation 2:20
* **Associated Articles:** 4434: drinking (ID: `4434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5220: authority, abuse of (ID: `5220@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5457: power, human (ID: `5457@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5769: behaviour (ID: `5769@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6106: addiction (ID: `6106@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6187: immorality (ID: `6187@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6236: sexual sin (ID: `6236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6662: freedom, abuse of Christian (ID: `6662@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8302: love, abuse of (ID: `8302@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8703: antinomianism (ID: `8703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8775: libertinism (ID: `8775@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8815: ridicule (ID: `8815@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5776: achievement (id: 5776)

Human beings can achieve many things, though any achievement which disregards God is futile. God always achieves what he intends. Anything of eternal value needs God\-given resources.

Kinds of human achievement
--------------------------

### Accumulation of wealth

**[EST 1:4](https://ref.ly/Esth1:4)** *See also* [1KI 10:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:23) parallel [2CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:22); [1KI 10:26–10:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:26-1Kgs10:27) parallel [2CH 9:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:27); [JOB 42:12](https://ref.ly/Job42:12); [ECC 2:4–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:4-Eccl2:9)

### Political power

**[GEN 41:41–41:43](https://ref.ly/Gen41:41-Gen41:43)** *See also* [1KI 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:21) parallel [2CH 9:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:26); [EST 1:1](https://ref.ly/Esth1:1), [EST 10:2–10:3](https://ref.ly/Esth10:2-Esth10:3); [DAN 2:37–2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:37-Dan2:38), [DAN 2:48](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48)

### Buildings

[1KI 7:1–7:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:1-1Kgs7:12); ; [DAN 4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30); ; [MRK 13:1](https://ref.ly/Mark13:1)

### Great learning

[1KI 4:32–4:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:32-1Kgs4:34); ; [ECC 1:13–1:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13-Eccl1:16); ; [DAN 1:17](https://ref.ly/Dan1:17)

### Military victory

[GEN 14:14–14:16](https://ref.ly/Gen14:14-Gen14:16); ; [EXO 17:8–17:13](https://ref.ly/Exod17:8-Exod17:13); ; [JOS 8:24–8:26](https://ref.ly/Josh8:24-Josh8:26); ; [JDG 4:14–4:16](https://ref.ly/Judg4:14-Judg4:16); ; [1SA 18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:7); ; [2SA 22:35–22:43](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:35-2Sam22:43) parallel [PSA 18:34–18:42](https://ref.ly/Ps18:34-Ps18:42)

### Acts of courage

[1SA 14:6–14:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:6-1Sam14:14), [1SA 17:50](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:50); ; [2SA 23:8–23:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:8-2Sam23:21) parallel [1CH 11:11–11:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:11-1Chr11:23)

### Spiritual achievements

**[ROM 15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:19)** *See also* [LUK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Luke10:17); [JHN 14:12](https://ref.ly/John14:12); [ACT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:3), [ACT 20:26–20:27](https://ref.ly/Acts20:26-Acts20:27); [2CO 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:1-2Cor3:3); [1JN 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1John2:13-1John2:14); [REV 2:19](https://ref.ly/Rev2:19)

Limitations of human achievements
---------------------------------

### Human achievements can lead to pride

**[ISA 10:12–10:15](https://ref.ly/Isa10:12-Isa10:15)** *See also* [DEU 8:10–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10-Deut8:18); [2CH 26:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16); [DAN 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29-Dan4:30)

### Human achievements can give false security

**[LUK 12:16–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:20)** *See also* [2KI 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:11-2Kgs19:13) parallel [ISA 37:11–37:13](https://ref.ly/Isa37:11-Isa37:13); [2KI 19:21–19:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:21-2Kgs19:28) parallel [ISA 37:21–37:29](https://ref.ly/Isa37:21-Isa37:29); [JER 48:7](https://ref.ly/Jer48:7); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17); [REV 18:7–18:8](https://ref.ly/Rev18:7-Rev18:8)

### Human achievements are ultimately futile

**[ECC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:11)** *See also* [PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6); [ECC 2:17–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17-Eccl2:23); [ISA 40:6](https://ref.ly/Isa40:6); [HAB 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hab2:13)

### Human achievements do not lead to justification

[ACT 13:39](https://ref.ly/Acts13:39); ; [ROM 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1); ; [1CO 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:11); ; [GAL 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:15-Gal2:16), [GAL 2:21](https://ref.ly/Gal2:21)

God can thwart human achievements
---------------------------------

**[GEN 11:1–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:1-Gen11:9)** *God will overthrow all human achievement that is in opposition to him.* *See also* [JOB 5:12](https://ref.ly/Job5:12); [PSA 33:10](https://ref.ly/Ps33:10); [ISA 13:19](https://ref.ly/Isa13:19); [REV 18:10](https://ref.ly/Rev18:10), [REV 18:16–18:19](https://ref.ly/Rev18:16-Rev18:19)

God's achievements
------------------

### God's purposes are always accomplished

**[ISA 46:10](https://ref.ly/Isa46:10)** *See also* [JOB 42:2](https://ref.ly/Job42:2); [ISA 14:24](https://ref.ly/Isa14:24), [ISA 55:10–55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10-Isa55:11); [JER 23:20](https://ref.ly/Jer23:20), [JER 30:24](https://ref.ly/Jer30:24)

### Creation as God's achievement

**[PSA 104:24](https://ref.ly/Ps104:24)** *See also* [GEN 1:1](https://ref.ly/Gen1:1), [GEN 1:31](https://ref.ly/Gen1:31); [PSA 104:2–104:23](https://ref.ly/Ps104:2-Ps104:23); [ISA 40:25–40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:25-Isa40:26)

### Salvation as God's achievement

**[HEB 7:25](https://ref.ly/Heb7:25)** *See also* [JHN 17:4](https://ref.ly/John17:4), [JHN 19:28](https://ref.ly/John19:28), [JHN 19:30](https://ref.ly/John19:30); [ACT 4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:12); [PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6); [HEB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:14), [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)

Spiritual resources for human achievements
------------------------------------------

### Achieving things with God's help

**[PSA 127:1](https://ref.ly/Ps127:1)** *See also* [2SA 22:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:30) parallel [PSA 18:29](https://ref.ly/Ps18:29); [2CH 26:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:5); [ZEC 4:6–4:9](https://ref.ly/Zech4:6-Zech4:9); [JHN 15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:5); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8); [ROM 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/Rom15:18-Rom15:19); [1CO 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:6); [PHP 2:13](https://ref.ly/Phil2:13), [PHP 4:13](https://ref.ly/Phil4:13)

### Achieving things by faith

**[MRK 9:23](https://ref.ly/Mark9:23)** *See also* [MAT 17:20](https://ref.ly/Matt17:20), [MAT 21:21](https://ref.ly/Matt21:21) parallel [MRK 11:22–11:23](https://ref.ly/Mark11:22-Mark11:23); [LUK 17:6](https://ref.ly/Luke17:6)

### Achieving things through prayer

**[JHN 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/John14:13-John14:14)** *See also* [1SA 1:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:27); [JAS 5:16–5:18](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16-Jas5:18)

### Achievements out of trials and sorrows

**[2CO 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:17)** *See also* [ROM 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Rom5:3-Rom5:4); [2CO 7:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:10), [2CO 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7-2Cor12:10); [JAS 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2-Jas1:4)

### Achieving things with the help of fellow believers

**[GAL 6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:2)** *See also* [ACT 2:42–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:42-Acts2:45), [ACT 4:32–4:37](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32-Acts4:37), [ACT 5:12–5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:12-Acts5:16); [PHP 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Phil4:13-Phil4:14), [PHP 4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:18); [HEB 10:24–10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:24-Heb10:25)

* **Associated Passages:** Esther 1:4; 1 Kings 10:23; 2 Chronicles 9:22; 1 Kings 10:26-27; 2 Chronicles 9:27; Job 42:12; Ecclesiastes 2:4-9; Genesis 41:41-43; 1 Kings 4:21; 2 Chronicles 9:26; Esther 1:1; Esther 10:2-3; Daniel 2:37-38; Daniel 2:48; 1 Kings 7:1-12; Daniel 4:30; Mark 13:1; 1 Kings 4:32-34; Ecclesiastes 1:13-16; Daniel 1:17; Genesis 14:14-16; Exodus 17:8-13; Joshua 8:24-26; Judges 4:14-16; 1 Samuel 18:7; 2 Samuel 22:35-43; Psalms 18:34-42; 1 Samuel 14:6-14; 1 Samuel 17:50; 2 Samuel 23:8-21; 1 Chronicles 11:11-23; Romans 15:19; Luke 10:17; John 14:12; Acts 14:3; Acts 20:26-27; 2 Corinthians 3:1-3; 1 John 2:13-14; Revelation 2:19; Isaiah 10:12-15; Deuteronomy 8:10-18; 2 Chronicles 26:16; Daniel 4:29-30; Luke 12:16-20; 2 Kings 19:11-13; Isaiah 37:11-13; 2 Kings 19:21-28; Isaiah 37:21-29; Jeremiah 48:7; 1 Timothy 6:17; Revelation 18:7-8; Ecclesiastes 2:11; Psalms 39:6; Ecclesiastes 2:17-23; Isaiah 40:6; Habakkuk 2:13; Acts 13:39; Romans 5:1; 1 Corinthians 6:11; Galatians 2:15-16; Galatians 2:21; Genesis 11:1-9; Job 5:12; Psalms 33:10; Isaiah 13:19; Revelation 18:10; Revelation 18:16-19; Isaiah 46:10; Job 42:2; Isaiah 14:24; Isaiah 55:10-11; Jeremiah 23:20; Jeremiah 30:24; Psalms 104:24; Genesis 1:1; Genesis 1:31; Psalms 104:2-23; Isaiah 40:25-26; Hebrews 7:25; John 17:4; John 19:28; John 19:30; Acts 4:12; Philippians 1:6; Hebrews 10:14; Hebrews 12:2; Psalms 127:1; 2 Samuel 22:30; Psalms 18:29; 2 Chronicles 26:5; Zechariah 4:6-9; John 15:5; Acts 1:8; Romans 15:18-19; 1 Corinthians 3:6; Philippians 2:13; Philippians 4:13; Mark 9:23; Matthew 17:20; Matthew 21:21; Mark 11:22-23; Luke 17:6; John 14:13-14; 1 Samuel 1:27; James 5:16-18; 2 Corinthians 4:17; Romans 5:3-4; 2 Corinthians 7:10; 2 Corinthians 12:7-10; James 1:2-4; Galatians 6:2; Acts 2:42-45; Acts 4:32-37; Acts 5:12-16; Philippians 4:13-14; Philippians 4:18; Hebrews 10:24-25

## 5777: admonition (id: 5777)

A loving attempt to correct another's attitude or behaviour. Christians are to admonish fellow believers.

The prophets admonished Israel
------------------------------

**[2KI 17:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:13)**; **[NEH 9:29–9:30](https://ref.ly/Neh9:29-Neh9:30)** *See also* [JDG 6:7–6:10](https://ref.ly/Judg6:7-Judg6:10); [MAL 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Mal2:1-Mal2:4)

Parents are to admonish their children
--------------------------------------

**[PRO 22:6](https://ref.ly/Prov22:6)**; **[EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)** *See also* [GEN 18:19](https://ref.ly/Gen18:19); [DEU 11:19](https://ref.ly/Deut11:19); [PRO 13:24](https://ref.ly/Prov13:24), [PRO 19:18](https://ref.ly/Prov19:18), [PRO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Prov22:15), [PRO 23:13](https://ref.ly/Prov23:13)

Leaders are to admonish those in their care
-------------------------------------------

**[2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)** *See also* [1CO 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:14); [COL 1:28](https://ref.ly/Col1:28); [1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12); [TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Titus2:15), [TIT 3:10](https://ref.ly/Titus3:10)

Christians are to admonish fellow believers
-------------------------------------------

**[COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)** *See also* [PSA 141:5](https://ref.ly/Ps141:5); [LUK 17:3](https://ref.ly/Luke17:3); [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14); [1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14); [2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16)

It is wise to accept legitimate admonition
------------------------------------------

**[PRO 13:18](https://ref.ly/Prov13:18), [PRO 25:12](https://ref.ly/Prov25:12)** *See also* [PRO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Prov15:5); [ECC 7:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:5); [JER 2:30](https://ref.ly/Jer2:30), [JER 5:3](https://ref.ly/Jer5:3), [JER 7:28](https://ref.ly/Jer7:28); [ZEP 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:1-Zeph3:2), [ZEP 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:7-Zeph3:8)

## 5778: adorning (id: 5778)

A form of ornamentation, using especially jewels and precious metals, to enhance the beauty of buildings or people.

Buildings were adorned with various materials
---------------------------------------------

**[2CH 3:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr3:6)** *See also* [PSA 45:8](https://ref.ly/Ps45:8); [ISA 60:13](https://ref.ly/Isa60:13); [AMO 3:15](https://ref.ly/Amos3:15)

Objects of worship were adorned
-------------------------------

**[HOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Hos10:1)** *See also* [JER 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/Jer10:3-Jer10:4)

Women adorned themselves with jewellery and fine clothes
--------------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 16:11–16:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:11-Ezek16:12)** *See also* [2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24); [JER 4:30](https://ref.ly/Jer4:30); [EZK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:13)

Personal adornment should not be allowed to detract from worship
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[1TI 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:9)** *See also* [1PE 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:3-1Pet3:4)

Adorning used in a metaphorical sense
-------------------------------------

**[ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10)** *See also* [JOB 40:10](https://ref.ly/Job40:10); [PSA 93:5](https://ref.ly/Ps93:5), [PSA 144:12](https://ref.ly/Ps144:12); [PRO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8-Prov1:9); [ISA 60:7](https://ref.ly/Isa60:7); [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 3:6; Psalms 45:8; Isaiah 60:13; Amos 3:15; Hosea 10:1; Jeremiah 10:3-4; Ezekiel 16:11-12; 2 Samuel 1:24; Jeremiah 4:30; Ezekiel 16:13; 1 Timothy 2:9; 1 Peter 3:3-4; Isaiah 61:10; Job 40:10; Psalms 93:5; Psalms 144:12; Proverbs 1:8-9; Isaiah 60:7; Revelation 21:2
* **Associated Articles:** 4040: beauty (ID: `4040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4342: jewels (ID: `4342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5145: clothing (ID: `5145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5207: architecture (ID: `5207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5211: art (ID: `5211@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5915: ornaments (ID: `5915@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8308: modesty (ID: `8308@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8808: riches (ID: `8808@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5779: advice (id: 5779)

Counsel is essential for gaining wisdom, though human advice is not always reliable. God's advice is necessary and trustworthy for guidance in life.

Human advice
------------

### A wise person seeks advice from others

**[PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15)** *See also* [PRO 11:14](https://ref.ly/Prov11:14), [PRO 13:10](https://ref.ly/Prov13:10), [PRO 15:12](https://ref.ly/Prov15:12), [PRO 15:22](https://ref.ly/Prov15:22), [PRO 19:20](https://ref.ly/Prov19:20), [PRO 24:6](https://ref.ly/Prov24:6), [PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9); [ECC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:13)

### Rejecting good advice can lead to disaster

**[PRO 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Prov1:30-Prov1:31)** *See also* [1KI 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:8) parallel [2CH 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:8); [1KI 12:13–12:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:13-1Kgs12:15) parallel [2CH 10:13–10:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:13-2Chr10:15); [PRO 1:25–1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:25-Prov1:26); [JER 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jer18:18); [ACT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Acts27:11)

### Human advice can have unseen dangers

**[PRO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Prov12:5)** *See also* [JOB 21:16](https://ref.ly/Job21:16); [PSA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1); [ISA 19:11](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11); [EZK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:2)

Examples of good human advice
-----------------------------

[EXO 18:17–18:24](https://ref.ly/Exod18:17-Exod18:24) *Jethro*; ; [2SA 17:5–17:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:5-2Sam17:16) *Hushai*; [EST 1:13–1:22](https://ref.ly/Esth1:13-Esth1:22), [EST 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Esth2:1-Esth2:4); [DAN 4:27](https://ref.ly/Dan4:27) *Daniel*; ; [ACT 5:38](https://ref.ly/Acts5:38) *Gamaliel*; ; [2CO 8:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:10) *Paul*

Examples of bad human advice
----------------------------

[NUM 31:15–31:16](https://ref.ly/Num31:15-Num31:16) *Balaam*; [2SA 15:31–15:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:31-2Sam15:34), [2SA 16:20–16:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:20-2Sam16:23), [2SA 17:1–17:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:1-2Sam17:4), [2SA 17:21–17:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:21-2Sam17:23); [1KI 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28) *Jeroboam*; ; [ISA 19:11](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11) *Egyptian advisers*

Seeking advice from false gods is futile
----------------------------------------

**[LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31)** *See also* [DEU 18:14](https://ref.ly/Deut18:14); [2CH 25:15–25:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:15-2Chr25:16); [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3), [ISA 41:28–41:29](https://ref.ly/Isa41:28-Isa41:29), [ISA 47:12–47:13](https://ref.ly/Isa47:12-Isa47:13)

God needs no\-one to advise him
-------------------------------

**[ISA 40:13–40:14](https://ref.ly/Isa40:13-Isa40:14)** *See also* [JOB 12:13](https://ref.ly/Job12:13); [PRO 8:14](https://ref.ly/Prov8:14); [ISA 28:29](https://ref.ly/Isa28:29)

God gives advice
----------------

### To his people

**[PSA 32:8](https://ref.ly/Ps32:8)** *See also* [PSA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ps16:7), [PSA 73:24](https://ref.ly/Ps73:24); [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)

### Through Jesus Christ and his Spirit

**[ISA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Isa11:2)** *See also* [ISA 9:6](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6); [JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26), [JHN 15:26](https://ref.ly/John15:26), [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/John16:13)

### Through his word

**[2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16)**

God frustrates bad advice
-------------------------

**[1CO 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:19)** *See also* [2SA 17:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:14); [PSA 33:10](https://ref.ly/Ps33:10)

Examples of receiving God's advice
----------------------------------

David: [1SA 23:1–23:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:1-1Sam23:4); ; [2SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:1); [1KI 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:5) parallel [2CH 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:4)

Examples of rejecting God's advice
----------------------------------

Job: [JOB 38:2](https://ref.ly/Job38:2), [JOB 42:2–42:3](https://ref.ly/Job42:2-Job42:3); [PSA 106:13](https://ref.ly/Ps106:13), [PSA 107:11](https://ref.ly/Ps107:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 12:15; Proverbs 11:14; Proverbs 13:10; Proverbs 15:12; Proverbs 15:22; Proverbs 19:20; Proverbs 24:6; Proverbs 27:9; Ecclesiastes 4:13; Proverbs 1:30-31; 1 Kings 12:8; 2 Chronicles 10:8; 1 Kings 12:13-15; 2 Chronicles 10:13-15; Proverbs 1:25-26; Jeremiah 18:18; Acts 27:11; Proverbs 12:5; Job 21:16; Psalms 1:1; Isaiah 19:11; Ezekiel 11:2; Exodus 18:17-24; 2 Samuel 17:5-16; Esther 1:13-22; Esther 2:1-4; Daniel 4:27; Acts 5:38; 2 Corinthians 8:10; Numbers 31:15-16; 2 Samuel 15:31-34; 2 Samuel 16:20-23; 2 Samuel 17:1-4; 2 Samuel 17:21-23; 1 Kings 12:28; Leviticus 19:31; Deuteronomy 18:14; 2 Chronicles 25:15-16; Isaiah 19:3; Isaiah 41:28-29; Isaiah 47:12-13; Isaiah 40:13-14; Job 12:13; Proverbs 8:14; Isaiah 28:29; Psalms 32:8; Psalms 16:7; Psalms 73:24; Revelation 3:18; Isaiah 11:2; Isaiah 9:6; John 14:26; John 15:26; John 16:13; 2 Timothy 3:16; 1 Corinthians 1:19; 2 Samuel 17:14; Psalms 33:10; 1 Samuel 23:1-4; 2 Samuel 2:1; 1 Kings 22:5; 2 Chronicles 18:4; Job 38:2; Job 42:2-3; Psalms 106:13; Psalms 107:11
* **Associated Articles:** 1180: God, wisdom of (ID: `1180@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3130: Holy Spirit, as Counsellor (ID: `3130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3263: Holy Spirit, guidance of (ID: `3263@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5780: advisers (ID: `5780@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8124: guidance (ID: `8124@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8408: decision-making (ID: `8408@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8412: decisions (ID: `8412@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5780: advisers (id: 5780)

Those who give advice and counsel. Human advice is desirable, but not always reliable. Only God's counsel is wholly dependable.

Human advisers
--------------

### It is wise to consult advisers

**[PRO 15:22](https://ref.ly/Prov15:22)** *See also* [PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15), [PRO 13:10](https://ref.ly/Prov13:10), [PRO 15:12](https://ref.ly/Prov15:12), [PRO 19:20](https://ref.ly/Prov19:20), [PRO 20:18](https://ref.ly/Prov20:18), [PRO 24:6](https://ref.ly/Prov24:6); [ECC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:13)

### It is folly to reject wise advisers

**[PRO 11:14](https://ref.ly/Prov11:14)** *See also* [1KI 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:8) parallel [2CH 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:8); [1KI 12:13–12:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:13-1Kgs12:15) parallel [2CH 10:13–10:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:13-2Chr10:15); [PRO 1:25–1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:25-Prov1:26), [PRO 1:30–1:31](https://ref.ly/Prov1:30-Prov1:31); [JER 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jer18:18); [ACT 27:11](https://ref.ly/Acts27:11), [ACT 27:21](https://ref.ly/Acts27:21)

### Unwise advisers are dangerous

**[PSA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1)** *See also* [JOB 21:16](https://ref.ly/Job21:16), [JOB 42:7](https://ref.ly/Job42:7); [PRO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Prov12:5); [ISA 19:11](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11); [EZK 11:2–11:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:2-Ezek11:4)

### Advisers who trust in false gods must be rejected

**[ISA 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19-Isa8:20)** *See also* [2CH 25:15–25:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:15-2Chr25:16); [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3), [ISA 47:13–47:15](https://ref.ly/Isa47:13-Isa47:15)

Examples of wise advisers
-------------------------

[EXO 18:17–18:24](https://ref.ly/Exod18:17-Exod18:24) *Jethro*; [DAN 2:23](https://ref.ly/Dan2:23), [DAN 2:48](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48), [DAN 4:18](https://ref.ly/Dan4:18), [DAN 4:27](https://ref.ly/Dan4:27), [DAN 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:11-Dan5:12); [ACT 5:38–5:39](https://ref.ly/Acts5:38-Acts5:39) *Gamaliel*; ; [2CO 8:10–8:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:10-2Cor8:12) *Paul*

Examples of bad advisers
------------------------

[NUM 31:16](https://ref.ly/Num31:16) *Balaam*; [2SA 15:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:31), [2SA 17:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:1), [2SA 17:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:7); [1KI 12:28–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28-1Kgs12:30) *Jeroboam*; ; [ISA 19:11](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11) *Egyptian advisers*; ; [JHN 11:49–11:53](https://ref.ly/John11:49-John11:53) *Caiaphas*

Examples of official advisers
-----------------------------

[GEN 26:26](https://ref.ly/Gen26:26); ; [2SA 15:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:12), [2SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:23); ; [1KI 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:5); ; [1CH 27:32–27:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr27:32-1Chr27:33)

God as adviser
--------------

### God is an adviser

**[PSA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ps16:7)** *See also* [1KI 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:5) parallel [2CH 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:4); [PSA 32:8](https://ref.ly/Ps32:8), [PSA 73:24](https://ref.ly/Ps73:24); [REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)

### The promised Messiah is an adviser

**[ISA 9:6](https://ref.ly/Isa9:6)** *See also* [ISA 11:2](https://ref.ly/Isa11:2)

### The Holy Spirit is an adviser

**[JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26)** *See also* [JHN 14:16](https://ref.ly/John14:16), [JHN 15:26](https://ref.ly/John15:26), [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/John16:13) *The Holy Spirit's counsel defends believers from every accusation and provides all necessary teaching and guidance.*

## 5781: affection (id: 5781)

Positive feelings towards another, often expressed in terms of attachment and warmth.

People and things arouse affection
----------------------------------

### Whole churches

**[PHP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7)** *See also* [2CO 7:13–7:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:13-2Cor7:15)

### Friends

**[1SA 18:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:1)** *See also* [1SA 18:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:3), [1SA 20:41–20:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41-1Sam20:42); [JHN 11:36](https://ref.ly/John11:36); [ROM 16:5](https://ref.ly/Rom16:5), [ROM 16:9](https://ref.ly/Rom16:9), [ROM 16:12](https://ref.ly/Rom16:12); [COL 4:14](https://ref.ly/Col4:14); [3JN 1:1](https://ref.ly/3John1:1)

### Strangers

**[LUK 7:13](https://ref.ly/Luke7:13)**

### The temple

**[EZK 24:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:21)**

### Jerusalem

**[NEH 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3-Neh1:4)** *See also* [PSA 137:6](https://ref.ly/Ps137:6)

Affection is sometimes expressed in familial terms
--------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 3:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:16)**; **[1CO 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:17)** *See also* [1SA 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:16), [1SA 24:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:11); [2SA 18:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:22); [2KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:12); [JHN 13:33](https://ref.ly/John13:33); [2CO 6:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:13); [2TI 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:1); [PHM 1:10](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:10); [1PE 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:13); [1JN 2:28](https://ref.ly/1John2:28)

Emotions expressing affection
-----------------------------

### Tenderness

**[GEN 34:3](https://ref.ly/Gen34:3)** *See also* [LUK 7:38](https://ref.ly/Luke7:38); [PHP 2:1](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1)

### Longing

**[PHP 1:8](https://ref.ly/Phil1:8)** *See also* [2SA 14:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:1); [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37) parallel [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34); [ROM 15:23](https://ref.ly/Rom15:23); [2CO 7:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:11); [GAL 4:20](https://ref.ly/Gal4:20); [PHP 2:26](https://ref.ly/Phil2:26), [PHP 4:1](https://ref.ly/Phil4:1); [2TI 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:4)

### Openness

**[2CO 6:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:11)** *See also* [2CO 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:12-2Cor6:13)

### Compassion

**[MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36)** parallel [MRK 6:34](https://ref.ly/Mark6:34) *See also* [1KI 3:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:26); [ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15); [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20); [EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32); [COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12); [1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)

### Sorrow

**[2CO 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:4)** *See also* [PSA 35:14](https://ref.ly/Ps35:14); [LAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11); [ROM 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Rom9:2-Rom9:3)

### Joy

**[PHM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:7)** *See also* [PRO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Prov23:15), [PRO 27:11](https://ref.ly/Prov27:11); [GAL 4:14–4:15](https://ref.ly/Gal4:14-Gal4:15); [2JN 1:12](https://ref.ly/2John1:12)

### Concern

**[1CO 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:14)** *See also* [PRO 1:8](https://ref.ly/Prov1:8), [PRO 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6), [PRO 31:2–31:3](https://ref.ly/Prov31:2-Prov31:3); [ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12); [2CO 7:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:7), [2CO 8:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:16), [2CO 12:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:19); [GAL 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Gal4:11-Gal4:12), [GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19); [PHP 4:10](https://ref.ly/Phil4:10); [1TI 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:18)

## 5782: agony (id: 5782)

Intense suffering of body, mind or spirit, brought about by illness, death or the judgment of God.

Examples of agony
-----------------

### Agony of body

**[PSA 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2)** *See also* [PSA 31:10](https://ref.ly/Ps31:10), [PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10); [ISA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Isa21:3); [JHN 16:21](https://ref.ly/John16:21); [REV 9:5](https://ref.ly/Rev9:5), [REV 16:10](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10)

### Agony of heart

**[JER 4:19](https://ref.ly/Jer4:19)** *See also* [DEU 2:25](https://ref.ly/Deut2:25); [1SA 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:16) *Hannah prays for a son*; [JOB 6:2](https://ref.ly/Job6:2), [JOB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Job7:11); [PSA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Ps6:3), [PSA 25:17](https://ref.ly/Ps25:17), [PSA 31:7](https://ref.ly/Ps31:7), [PSA 38:8](https://ref.ly/Ps38:8), [PSA 55:4](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4); [ISA 65:14](https://ref.ly/Isa65:14); [MAT 26:38](https://ref.ly/Matt26:38); [MRK 14:33](https://ref.ly/Mark14:33); [LUK 22:44](https://ref.ly/Luke22:44) *Jesus Christ prays on the Mount of Olives*; [ROM 9:2](https://ref.ly/Rom9:2); [2CO 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:4)

Causes of agony
---------------

### Illness

**[JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18)** *See also* [JOB 30:16–30:17](https://ref.ly/Job30:16-Job30:17); [PSA 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2-Ps6:3), [PSA 38:7](https://ref.ly/Ps38:7), [PSA 69:29](https://ref.ly/Ps69:29)

### Death

**[ACT 2:24](https://ref.ly/Acts2:24)** *See also* [JOB 26:5](https://ref.ly/Job26:5); [PSA 18:4–18:6](https://ref.ly/Ps18:4-Ps18:6), [PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10), [PSA 55:4](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4), [PSA 116:3](https://ref.ly/Ps116:3)

### The judgment of God

**[LUK 16:24–16:25](https://ref.ly/Luke16:24-Luke16:25)** *See also* [JER 4:19](https://ref.ly/Jer4:19); [EZK 30:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:16); [MIC 4:10](https://ref.ly/Mic4:10); [ZEC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Zech9:5); [MAT 22:13](https://ref.ly/Matt22:13), [MAT 24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:51), [MAT 25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:30)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 6:2; Psalms 31:10; Psalms 42:10; Isaiah 21:3; John 16:21; Revelation 9:5; Revelation 16:10; Jeremiah 4:19; Deuteronomy 2:25; 1 Samuel 1:16; Job 6:2; Job 7:11; Psalms 6:3; Psalms 25:17; Psalms 31:7; Psalms 38:8; Psalms 55:4; Isaiah 65:14; Matthew 26:38; Mark 14:33; Luke 22:44; Romans 9:2; 2 Corinthians 2:4; Jeremiah 15:18; Job 30:16-17; Psalms 6:2-3; Psalms 38:7; Psalms 69:29; Acts 2:24; Job 26:5; Psalms 18:4-6; Psalms 116:3; Luke 16:24-25; Ezekiel 30:16; Micah 4:10; Zechariah 9:5; Matthew 22:13; Matthew 24:51; Matthew 25:30

## 5783: agreement (id: 5783)

Being of one mind with others, whether for good or evil purposes. Agreement among believers is commended as a means of enhancing unity and peace, and building up the people of God.

Agreements made by deliberate choice
------------------------------------

### Agreement to do good

**[JOB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Job2:11)** *See also* [NEH 5:9–5:13](https://ref.ly/Neh5:9-Neh5:13)

### Agreement to do wrong

**[DAN 6:7](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7)** *See also* [JER 7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:18); [LUK 22:5–22:6](https://ref.ly/Luke22:5-Luke22:6) parallel [MRK 14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:11); [ACT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:2), [ACT 23:20–23:21](https://ref.ly/Acts23:20-Acts23:21)

Legal agreements
----------------

### Between witnesses

**[DEU 17:6](https://ref.ly/Deut17:6)** *See also* [NUM 35:30](https://ref.ly/Num35:30); [DEU 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15); [RUT 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:9-Ruth4:11); [MAT 18:16](https://ref.ly/Matt18:16)

### Treaties

**[1KI 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:19-1Kgs15:20)** parallel [2CH 16:3–16:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:3-2Chr16:4) *See also* [GEN 21:27](https://ref.ly/Gen21:27), [GEN 26:28–26:31](https://ref.ly/Gen26:28-Gen26:31); [JOS 9:15](https://ref.ly/Josh9:15), [JOS 11:19](https://ref.ly/Josh11:19); [1KI 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:12), [1KI 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:34)

Ways of making agreements
-------------------------

### By accepting terms offered

**[GEN 23:16](https://ref.ly/Gen23:16)** *See also* [GEN 30:31–30:34](https://ref.ly/Gen30:31-Gen30:34), [GEN 34:24](https://ref.ly/Gen34:24); [JOS 2:21](https://ref.ly/Josh2:21); [JDG 15:12–15:13](https://ref.ly/Judg15:12-Judg15:13), [JDG 17:10–17:11](https://ref.ly/Judg17:10-Judg17:11); [1SA 20:16–20:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:16-1Sam20:17)

### Through mutually acceptable terms

**[GEN 37:26–37:27](https://ref.ly/Gen37:26-Gen37:27)** *See also* [GEN 13:8–13:9](https://ref.ly/Gen13:8-Gen13:9), [GEN 26:19–26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:19-Gen26:22); [ACT 15:28–15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:28-Acts15:29)

### Agreement reached by persuasion

**[DAN 1:12–1:14](https://ref.ly/Dan1:12-Dan1:14)**

Agreement commended to believers
--------------------------------

### Agreement enhances unity

**[EPH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:3)** *See also* [2CH 30:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:12); [PSA 133:1](https://ref.ly/Ps133:1); [JHN 17:11](https://ref.ly/John17:11), [JHN 17:21–17:23](https://ref.ly/John17:21-John17:23); [ROM 15:5](https://ref.ly/Rom15:5)

### Agreement avoids division

**[1CO 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:10)** *See also* [ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16); [PHP 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1-Phil2:3), [PHP 4:2](https://ref.ly/Phil4:2)

Agreements made before God
--------------------------

### Agreement in prayer

**[MAT 18:19](https://ref.ly/Matt18:19)** *See also* [1KI 1:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:36); [ACT 1:14](https://ref.ly/Acts1:14), [ACT 4:24](https://ref.ly/Acts4:24), [ACT 12:12](https://ref.ly/Acts12:12); [ROM 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Rom15:30-Rom15:31)

### Agreement in praise

**[NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6)** *See also* [1CH 16:36](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:36) parallel [PSA 106:48](https://ref.ly/Ps106:48); [PSA 41:13](https://ref.ly/Ps41:13), [PSA 72:19](https://ref.ly/Ps72:19); [1CO 14:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:16); [REV 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:13-Rev5:14)

### Agreement with God's terms

**[EXO 24:3](https://ref.ly/Exod24:3)** *See also* [NUM 5:22](https://ref.ly/Num5:22); [DEU 27:15–27:26](https://ref.ly/Deut27:15-Deut27:26); [2KI 23:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:3) parallel [2CH 34:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:32); [JER 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Jer11:1-Jer11:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 2:11; Nehemiah 5:9-13; Daniel 6:7; Jeremiah 7:18; Luke 22:5-6; Mark 14:11; Acts 5:1-2; Acts 23:20-21; Deuteronomy 17:6; Numbers 35:30; Deuteronomy 19:15; Ruth 4:9-11; Matthew 18:16; 1 Kings 15:19-20; 2 Chronicles 16:3-4; Genesis 21:27; Genesis 26:28-31; Joshua 9:15; Joshua 11:19; 1 Kings 5:12; 1 Kings 20:34; Genesis 23:16; Genesis 30:31-34; Genesis 34:24; Joshua 2:21; Judges 15:12-13; Judges 17:10-11; 1 Samuel 20:16-17; Genesis 37:26-27; Genesis 13:8-9; Genesis 26:19-22; Acts 15:28-29; Daniel 1:12-14; Ephesians 4:3; 2 Chronicles 30:12; Psalms 133:1; John 17:11; John 17:21-23; Romans 15:5; 1 Corinthians 1:10; Romans 12:16; Philippians 2:1-3; Philippians 4:2; Matthew 18:19; 1 Kings 1:36; Acts 1:14; Acts 4:24; Acts 12:12; Romans 15:30-31; Nehemiah 8:6; 1 Chronicles 16:36; Psalms 106:48; Psalms 41:13; Psalms 72:19; 1 Corinthians 14:16; Revelation 5:13-14; Exodus 24:3; Numbers 5:22; Deuteronomy 27:15-26; 2 Kings 23:3; 2 Chronicles 34:32; Jeremiah 11:1-5

## 5784: amazement (id: 5784)

Astonishment and surprise are frequent responses to the actions and words of God and Jesus Christ.

Amazement at God's actions
--------------------------

### His marvellous deeds

**[PSA 98:1](https://ref.ly/Ps98:1)** *See also* [JOS 3:5](https://ref.ly/Josh3:5) *The LORD promises to do amazing things when his people cross the Jordan*; [JDG 13:19](https://ref.ly/Judg13:19); [1CH 16:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:24); [PSA 71:17](https://ref.ly/Ps71:17), [PSA 72:18](https://ref.ly/Ps72:18), [PSA 86:10](https://ref.ly/Ps86:10), [PSA 96:3](https://ref.ly/Ps96:3), [PSA 118:23](https://ref.ly/Ps118:23); [ISA 25:1](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1); [DAN 3:24](https://ref.ly/Dan3:24) *Nebuchadnezzar is amazed at seeing four men in the furnace*; [REV 15:1](https://ref.ly/Rev15:1), [REV 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3)

### His creation

[JOB 37:5](https://ref.ly/Job37:5)

### His judgment

[ISA 29:9](https://ref.ly/Isa29:9); ; [HAB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hab1:5); ; [JHN 5:28](https://ref.ly/John5:28)

Throughout his life Jesus Christ inspired amazement
---------------------------------------------------

[MAT 27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:14) parallel [MRK 15:5](https://ref.ly/Mark15:5); ; [LUK 2:33](https://ref.ly/Luke2:33), [LUK 2:47](https://ref.ly/Luke2:47), [LUK 4:22](https://ref.ly/Luke4:22)

Amazement at Jesus Christ's teaching
------------------------------------

### At his inherent authority

**[MAT 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28-Matt7:29)** *See also* [MAT 13:54](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54) parallel [MRK 6:2](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2); [MRK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:22) parallel [LUK 4:32](https://ref.ly/Luke4:32); [MRK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Mark1:27) parallel [LUK 4:36](https://ref.ly/Luke4:36); [JHN 7:15](https://ref.ly/John7:15)

### In debate with the Pharisees

[MAT 22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:22) parallels [MRK 12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:17), [LUK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:26)

### In theological debate with the Sadducees

[MAT 22:33](https://ref.ly/Matt22:33)

### After talking with the rich young man

[MAT 19:25](https://ref.ly/Matt19:25) parallel [MRK 10:26](https://ref.ly/Mark10:26); ; [MRK 10:24](https://ref.ly/Mark10:24)

### After the cleansing of the temple

**[MRK 11:18](https://ref.ly/Mark11:18)** parallel [LUK 19:47](https://ref.ly/Luke19:47) *Here the crowds' amazement causes the chief priests and teachers of the law to fear Jesus Christ.*

Amazement at Jesus Christ's revelation of himself
-------------------------------------------------

*See also* [LUK 24:41](https://ref.ly/Luke24:41); [JHN 5:20](https://ref.ly/John5:20); [2TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:10)

Amazement at Jesus Christ's miracles
------------------------------------

### Miracles of healing

**[MAT 15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:31)** *See also* [MAT 9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:8) parallels [MRK 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:12), [LUK 5:26](https://ref.ly/Luke5:26) *at the healing of the paralytic*; [MRK 5:42](https://ref.ly/Mark5:42) parallel [LUK 8:56](https://ref.ly/Luke8:56) *at the healing of Jairus' daughter*; [ACT 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Acts3:10-Acts3:11) *at the healing of the crippled beggar*

### The casting out of demons

**[MRK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Mark1:27)** [MAT 9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:33); ; [LUK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14); [MRK 5:20](https://ref.ly/Mark5:20) *Legion*; ; [LUK 9:43](https://ref.ly/Luke9:43) *a boy with an evil spirit*

### Miracles of nature

**[MAT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:27)** parallel [LUK 8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:25) *See also* [MAT 21:20](https://ref.ly/Matt21:20) *the withered fig\-tree*; [MRK 6:51](https://ref.ly/Mark6:51) *Jesus Christ walking on the water*; [LUK 5:9](https://ref.ly/Luke5:9) *the miraculous haul of fish*

Amazement at the coming of the Holy Spirit and subsequent events
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Acts2:12)** *See also* [ACT 2:7](https://ref.ly/Acts2:7), [ACT 8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:13) *Simon's amazement at the miracles performed by Philip*; [ACT 9:21](https://ref.ly/Acts9:21) *amazement at the change in Saul*; [ACT 10:45](https://ref.ly/Acts10:45) *astonishment at the Holy Spirit being poured out on the Gentiles*; [ACT 12:16](https://ref.ly/Acts12:16) *amazement at Peter's release from prison*; [ACT 13:12](https://ref.ly/Acts13:12)

Jesus Christ himself experienced amazement
------------------------------------------

[MRK 6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:6); ; [LUK 7:9](https://ref.ly/Luke7:9)

## 5785: ambition (id: 5785)

A powerful personal drive towards achieving goals which are regarded as being of importance to the individual. Ambition is viewed negatively when these goals are selflish or evil, and positively when they are directed towards building up the church or furthering the kingdom of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 5786: ambition, negative aspects of (ID: `5786@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5787: ambition, positive aspects of (ID: `5787@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5786: ambition, negative aspects of (id: 5786)

In its negative sense, ambition is the pursuit of personal status and fortune, which is contrary to the purposes of God as expressed in Scripture.

Selfish ambition: a desire for personal aggrandisement
------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 10:36–10:37](https://ref.ly/Mark10:36-Mark10:37)** *See also* [GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4) *the men of Babel*; [2SA 15:1–15:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:4) *Absalom*; [1KI 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:5) *Adonijah*; [ISA 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/Isa14:13-Isa14:14) *the king of Babylon*; [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2) *the ruler of Tyre*; [DAN 11:36–11:38](https://ref.ly/Dan11:36-Dan11:38); [MIC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:3); [HAB 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4-Hab2:5); [MAT 20:21](https://ref.ly/Matt20:21); [LUK 22:24](https://ref.ly/Luke22:24); [PHP 1:17](https://ref.ly/Phil1:17); [2TH 2:4–2:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:4-2Thess2:7) *the antichrist*

Selfish ambition arises from fallen human nature
------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21)** *See also* [PRO 6:18](https://ref.ly/Prov6:18); [EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:3); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

The results of selfish ambition
-------------------------------

### Humiliation by God

**[MAT 23:12](https://ref.ly/Matt23:12)**

### The believer becomes spiritually unproductive

*See also* [MRK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18-Mark4:19); [JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16)

Warnings against selfish ambition
---------------------------------

**[PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3)** *See also* [JER 45:5](https://ref.ly/Jer45:5) *God's word to Baruch*

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 10:36-37; Genesis 11:4; 2 Samuel 15:1-4; 1 Kings 1:5; Isaiah 14:13-14; Ezekiel 28:2; Daniel 11:36-38; Micah 7:3; Habakkuk 2:4-5; Matthew 20:21; Luke 22:24; Philippians 1:17; 2 Thessalonians 2:4-7; Galatians 5:19-21; Proverbs 6:18; Ephesians 2:3; 1 John 2:16; Matthew 23:12; Mark 4:18-19; James 3:14-16; Philippians 2:3; Jeremiah 45:5
* **Associated Articles:** 5541: society, negative aspects of (ID: `5541@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5869: greed (ID: `5869@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6133: coveting (ID: `6133@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8777: lust (ID: `8777@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8804: pride, examples of (ID: `8804@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8827: selfishness (ID: `8827@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8848: worldliness (ID: `8848@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5787: ambition, positive aspects of (id: 5787)

In its positive sense, ambition is a personal desire for spiritual progress or achievement.

A longing for God and for what he can do in the believer
--------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6)**; **[PHP 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13-Phil3:14)** *See also* [PHP 3:10](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10); [PSA 40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:8); [LUK 13:24](https://ref.ly/Luke13:24); [COL 1:29](https://ref.ly/Col1:29); [1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11); [2TI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15); [HEB 12:1](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1); [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2); [2PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:14)

The desire for spiritual gifts
------------------------------

**[1CO 14:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:12)** *See also* [1KI 3:10–3:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:10-1Kgs3:14) parallel [2CH 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:11-2Chr1:12); [1CO 12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:31), [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1), [1CO 14:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:39)

The desire for a leadership role in the church
----------------------------------------------

**[1TI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1)**

The longing for communion with God
----------------------------------

**[PSA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4)** *See also* [PSA 73:25](https://ref.ly/Ps73:25), [PSA 84:2](https://ref.ly/Ps84:2)

The compelling desire to preach the gospel
------------------------------------------

**[ROM 15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:20)** *See also* [1CO 9:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:16); [PHP 1:15–1:18](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15-Phil1:18) *Jesus Christ can be proclaimed, despite selfish personal ambition.*

Focus on future rewards
-----------------------

**[1CO 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:27)** *See also* [2TI 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:8); [HEB 10:35–10:36](https://ref.ly/Heb10:35-Heb10:36), [HEB 11:26](https://ref.ly/Heb11:26)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 5:6; Philippians 3:13-14; Philippians 3:10; Psalms 40:8; Luke 13:24; Colossians 1:29; 1 Thessalonians 4:11; 2 Timothy 2:15; Hebrews 12:1; 1 Peter 2:2; 2 Peter 3:14; 1 Corinthians 14:12; 1 Kings 3:10-14; 2 Chronicles 1:11-12; 1 Corinthians 12:31; 1 Corinthians 14:1; 1 Corinthians 14:39; 1 Timothy 3:1; Psalms 27:4; Psalms 73:25; Psalms 84:2; Romans 15:20; 1 Corinthians 9:16; Philippians 1:15-18; 1 Corinthians 9:24-27; 2 Timothy 4:8; Hebrews 10:35-36; Hebrews 11:26
* **Associated Articles:** 5840: eagerness (ID: `5840@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5904: maturity, spiritual (ID: `5904@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7754: preaching (ID: `7754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7966: spiritual gifts (ID: `7966@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8114: discipleship (ID: `8114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8134: knowing God (ID: `8134@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8206: Christlikeness (ID: `8206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8266: holiness (ID: `8266@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8424: evangelism (ID: `8424@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8441: goals (ID: `8441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8461: priorities (ID: `8461@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8465: progress (ID: `8465@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5789: anger (id: 5789)

A state of indignation and outrage, often resulting from distress caused by injustice or insult. Scripture affirms God's righteous anger against sin and urges moderation in regard to human anger.

* **Associated Articles:** 5790: anger, divine (ID: `5790@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5791: anger, human (ID: `5791@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5790: anger, divine (id: 5790)

The anger of God is directed against all human sin, disobedience, rebellion and wickedness, which threaten to thwart his purposes for his creation and people.

Causes of divine anger
----------------------

### Disobedience

**[ISA 5:24–5:25](https://ref.ly/Isa5:24-Isa5:25)** *See also* [NUM 22:22](https://ref.ly/Num22:22); [JOS 7:1](https://ref.ly/Josh7:1); [2KI 22:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:13); [PSA 78:21–78:22](https://ref.ly/Ps78:21-Ps78:22); [MIC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Mic5:15); [ZEC 7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:12); [MRK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mark3:5); [ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5); [EPH 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:5-Eph5:6); [HEB 3:7–3:12](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7-Heb3:12)

### Sinfulness

**[ISA 57:17](https://ref.ly/Isa57:17)** *See also* [1KI 8:46](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:46), [1KI 11:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:9); [2CH 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:2), [2CH 32:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:25); [EPH 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:1-Eph2:3); [COL 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Col3:5-Col3:6)

### Apostasy and idolatry

**[NUM 25:3](https://ref.ly/Num25:3)** *See also* [DEU 4:25](https://ref.ly/Deut4:25), [DEU 9:8](https://ref.ly/Deut9:8); [JOS 22:18](https://ref.ly/Josh22:18), [JOS 23:16](https://ref.ly/Josh23:16); [JDG 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Judg2:19-Judg2:20), [JDG 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6-Judg10:7); [1KI 14:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:15); [2KI 22:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:17); [EZR 9:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:14); [JER 7:17–7:18](https://ref.ly/Jer7:17-Jer7:18)

The characteristics of God that provoke his anger
-------------------------------------------------

### He is a holy God

**[PSA 7:11](https://ref.ly/Ps7:11)** *See also* [JOS 24:19–24:20](https://ref.ly/Josh24:19-Josh24:20); [JER 10:10](https://ref.ly/Jer10:10)

### He is a jealous God

**[DEU 6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:15)** *See also* [1KI 14:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:22); [PSA 79:5](https://ref.ly/Ps79:5); [EZK 16:38](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:38); [NAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Nah1:2)

### He is the universal judge

**[PSA 79:6](https://ref.ly/Ps79:6)** *See also* [EZR 8:22](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:22); [ISA 34:2](https://ref.ly/Isa34:2); [ZEP 3:8](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:8)

### He is also compassionate and slow to anger

**[PSA 103:8](https://ref.ly/Ps103:8)** *See also* [EXO 34:6](https://ref.ly/Exod34:6); [NEH 9:17](https://ref.ly/Neh9:17); [PSA 78:38](https://ref.ly/Ps78:38), [PSA 86:15](https://ref.ly/Ps86:15); [JON 4:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:2); [NAM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Nah1:3)

Consequences of divine anger
----------------------------

### Death and destruction

**[DEU 9:8](https://ref.ly/Deut9:8)** *See also* [EXO 15:7](https://ref.ly/Exod15:7), [EXO 32:10–32:11](https://ref.ly/Exod32:10-Exod32:11); [NUM 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/Num11:1-Num11:2); [JOB 4:9](https://ref.ly/Job4:9); [ISA 13:5](https://ref.ly/Isa13:5); [JER 32:29](https://ref.ly/Jer32:29)

### Present judgment

**[LAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lam2:2)** *See also* [NUM 32:13](https://ref.ly/Num32:13); [2KI 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs13:3); [ISA 51:20](https://ref.ly/Isa51:20); [EZK 7:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:8); [HAB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Hab3:12); [MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13)

### Future judgment

**[ISA 13:9](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9)** *See also* [DAN 8:19](https://ref.ly/Dan8:19); [MAT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Matt3:7); [ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5)

### God's rejection of his people

**[JER 7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:29)** *See also* [DEU 31:17](https://ref.ly/Deut31:17); [2KI 17:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:18), [2KI 23:25–23:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:25-2Kgs23:27); [PSA 78:59](https://ref.ly/Ps78:59), [PSA 89:38](https://ref.ly/Ps89:38); [LAM 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Lam2:6-Lam2:7)

Being saved from the anger of God
---------------------------------

### God's anger may be temporary

**[PSA 30:5](https://ref.ly/Ps30:5)** *See also* [PSA 103:9](https://ref.ly/Ps103:9); [ISA 54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:8), [ISA 57:16](https://ref.ly/Isa57:16), [ISA 60:10](https://ref.ly/Isa60:10)

### God's anger cannot be averted by human means

**[ZEP 1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:18)** *See also* [PRO 11:4](https://ref.ly/Prov11:4)

### Humility and repentance are required

**[ZEP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:3)** *See also* [GEN 18:30–18:32](https://ref.ly/Gen18:30-Gen18:32); [DEU 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/Deut9:18-Deut9:19); [2CH 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:7), [2CH 12:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:12), [2CH 30:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:8); [ISA 64:9](https://ref.ly/Isa64:9); [JOL 2:13](https://ref.ly/Joel2:13)

### God's anger is averted on account of his love for believers

**[LAM 3:22](https://ref.ly/Lam3:22)** *See also* [HOS 11:9](https://ref.ly/Hos11:9)

### God's anger is averted on account of the work of Jesus Christ

**[ROM 5:9](https://ref.ly/Rom5:9)** *See also* [JHN 3:36](https://ref.ly/John3:36); [1TH 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:10), [1TH 5:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 5:24-25; Numbers 22:22; Joshua 7:1; 2 Kings 22:13; Psalms 78:21-22; Micah 5:15; Zechariah 7:12; Mark 3:5; Romans 2:5; Ephesians 5:5-6; Hebrews 3:7-12; Isaiah 57:17; 1 Kings 8:46; 1 Kings 11:9; 2 Chronicles 19:2; 2 Chronicles 32:25; Ephesians 2:1-3; Colossians 3:5-6; Numbers 25:3; Deuteronomy 4:25; Deuteronomy 9:8; Joshua 22:18; Joshua 23:16; Judges 2:19-20; Judges 10:6-7; 1 Kings 14:15; 2 Kings 22:17; Ezra 9:14; Jeremiah 7:17-18; Psalms 7:11; Joshua 24:19-20; Jeremiah 10:10; Deuteronomy 6:15; 1 Kings 14:22; Psalms 79:5; Ezekiel 16:38; Nahum 1:2; Psalms 79:6; Ezra 8:22; Isaiah 34:2; Zephaniah 3:8; Psalms 103:8; Exodus 34:6; Nehemiah 9:17; Psalms 78:38; Psalms 86:15; Jonah 4:2; Nahum 1:3; Exodus 15:7; Exodus 32:10-11; Numbers 11:1-2; Job 4:9; Isaiah 13:5; Jeremiah 32:29; Lamentations 2:2; Numbers 32:13; 2 Kings 13:3; Isaiah 51:20; Ezekiel 7:8; Habakkuk 3:12; Matthew 21:12-13; Isaiah 13:9; Daniel 8:19; Matthew 3:7; Jeremiah 7:29; Deuteronomy 31:17; 2 Kings 17:18; 2 Kings 23:25-27; Psalms 78:59; Psalms 89:38; Lamentations 2:6-7; Psalms 30:5; Psalms 103:9; Isaiah 54:8; Isaiah 57:16; Isaiah 60:10; Zephaniah 1:18; Proverbs 11:4; Zephaniah 2:3; Genesis 18:30-32; Deuteronomy 9:18-19; 2 Chronicles 12:7; 2 Chronicles 12:12; 2 Chronicles 30:8; Isaiah 64:9; Joel 2:13; Lamentations 3:22; Hosea 11:9; Romans 5:9; John 3:36; 1 Thessalonians 1:10; 1 Thessalonians 5:9
* **Associated Articles:** 1025: God, anger of (ID: `1025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1030: God, compassion of (ID: `1030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1055: God, grace and mercy of (ID: `1055@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1075: God, justice of (ID: `1075@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1125: God, righteousness of (ID: `1125@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1310: God, as judge (ID: `1310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2009: Jesus Christ, anger of (ID: `2009@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5283: cup (ID: `5283@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6125: condemnation, divine (ID: `6125@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6218: provoking God (ID: `6218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6712: propitiation (ID: `6712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5791: anger, human (id: 5791)

Human anger arises for a number of reasons, some of which are acceptable and others which are not. Scripture stresses the potentially destructive aspects of human anger and urges moderation.

Reasons for human anger
-----------------------

### Jealousy

**[GEN 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4-Gen4:5)** *See also* [1SA 17:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:28), [1SA 18:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:8); [1KI 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:4); [LUK 15:28](https://ref.ly/Luke15:28)

### Pride

**[JON 4:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:1)** *See also* [2SA 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:8), [2SA 6:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:8); [2KI 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:11); [2CH 26:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19)

### The truth

**[2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10)** *Hanani had prophesied against the king.* *See also* [NUM 24:10](https://ref.ly/Num24:10); [JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15)

### Human anger is sometimes justified

**[EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19)** *See also* [EXO 11:8](https://ref.ly/Exod11:8), [EXO 16:20](https://ref.ly/Exod16:20); [NUM 16:15](https://ref.ly/Num16:15); [LEV 10:16](https://ref.ly/Lev10:16); [1SA 11:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:6), [1SA 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:34); [2SA 12:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:5); [NEH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Neh5:6); [MRK 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mark3:5); [LUK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Luke14:21)

Results of human anger
----------------------

### Strife

**[GEN 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:8)** *See also* [1SA 20:30](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:30); [PSA 124:2–124:3](https://ref.ly/Ps124:2-Ps124:3); [PRO 29:22](https://ref.ly/Prov29:22), [PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33)

### God's judgment

**[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)** *See also* [GEN 49:5–49:7](https://ref.ly/Gen49:5-Gen49:7)

### Sin

**[JAS 1:20](https://ref.ly/Jas1:20)** *See also* [PSA 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ps4:4); [PRO 14:17](https://ref.ly/Prov14:17); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20); [EPH 4:26](https://ref.ly/Eph4:26)

Dealing with anger
------------------

### Becoming slow to anger

**[1CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:5)** *See also* [ECC 7:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:9); [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7); [JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)

### Not taking revenge

**[ROM 12:19](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19)**

### Renouncing anger

**[EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31)** *See also* [PSA 37:8](https://ref.ly/Ps37:8); [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8); [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 4:4-5; 1 Samuel 17:28; 1 Samuel 18:8; 1 Kings 21:4; Luke 15:28; Jonah 4:1; 2 Samuel 3:8; 2 Samuel 6:8; 2 Kings 5:11; 2 Chronicles 26:19; 2 Chronicles 16:10; Numbers 24:10; Jeremiah 37:15; Exodus 32:19; Exodus 11:8; Exodus 16:20; Numbers 16:15; Leviticus 10:16; 1 Samuel 11:6; 1 Samuel 20:34; 2 Samuel 12:5; Nehemiah 5:6; Mark 3:5; Luke 14:21; Genesis 4:8; 1 Samuel 20:30; Psalms 124:2-3; Proverbs 29:22; Proverbs 30:33; Matthew 5:22; Genesis 49:5-7; James 1:20; Psalms 4:4; Proverbs 14:17; 2 Corinthians 12:20; Ephesians 4:26; 1 Corinthians 13:5; Ecclesiastes 7:9; Titus 1:7; James 1:19; Romans 12:19; Ephesians 4:31; Psalms 37:8; Colossians 3:8; 1 Timothy 2:8
* **Associated Articles:** 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5494: revenge (ID: `5494@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5927: resentment (ID: `5927@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6666: grace (ID: `6666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6686: mercy (ID: `6686@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8773: jealousy (ID: `8773@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5792: appetite (id: 5792)

A hunger or desire for something. Although the primary reference of the term is to a hunger for food, it can also refer to other human desires. Scripture distinguishes between healthy and unhealthy appetites, indicating what it is appropriate to seek after.

Appetite as physical hunger
---------------------------

**[NUM 11:6](https://ref.ly/Num11:6)**; **[PRO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30)** *See also* [PSA 107:5–107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:5-Ps107:9); [PRO 16:26](https://ref.ly/Prov16:26); [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7); [ISA 29:8](https://ref.ly/Isa29:8); [JER 31:25](https://ref.ly/Jer31:25), [JER 50:19](https://ref.ly/Jer50:19); [LAM 2:12](https://ref.ly/Lam2:12)

Appetite as a spiritual hunger
------------------------------

**[PSA 143:6](https://ref.ly/Ps143:6)**; **[PRO 25:25](https://ref.ly/Prov25:25)**; **[MAT 5:6](https://ref.ly/Matt5:6)** *See also* [PSA 119:20](https://ref.ly/Ps119:20), [PSA 119:131](https://ref.ly/Ps119:131)

The gospel satisfies human hunger and thirst
--------------------------------------------

**[JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14), [JHN 6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:35)** *See also* [PSA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:2); [ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1); [JHN 6:27](https://ref.ly/John6:27), [JHN 6:50–6:51](https://ref.ly/John6:50-John6:51), [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [REV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

Lust as sexual appetite
-----------------------

[PRO 6:25](https://ref.ly/Prov6:25); ; [EZK 23:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:11); ; [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5); ; [1TH 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 11:6; Proverbs 6:30; Psalms 107:5-9; Proverbs 16:26; Ecclesiastes 6:7; Isaiah 29:8; Jeremiah 31:25; Jeremiah 50:19; Lamentations 2:12; Psalms 143:6; Proverbs 25:25; Matthew 5:6; Psalms 119:20; Psalms 119:131; John 4:13-14; John 6:35; Psalms 42:2; Isaiah 55:1; John 6:27; John 6:50-51; John 7:37; Revelation 21:6; Revelation 22:17; Proverbs 6:25; Ezekiel 23:11; Colossians 3:5; 1 Thessalonians 4:3-5
* **Associated Articles:** 4404: food (ID: `4404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4438: eating (ID: `4438@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4822: famine (ID: `4822@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5341: hunger (ID: `5341@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5580: thirst (ID: `5580@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5832: desire (ID: `5832@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5839: dissatisfaction (ID: `5839@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5866: gluttony (ID: `5866@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5869: greed (ID: `5869@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8656: longing for God (ID: `8656@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8777: lust (ID: `8777@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8821: self-indulgence (ID: `8821@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5793: arrogance (id: 5793)

Proud and unpleasant behaviour towards other people, based on a belief in one's own superiority or greater importance.

Arrogance characterises the wicked person
-----------------------------------------

### As an attitude of the heart

**[MRK 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:22)** *See also* [JOB 35:12](https://ref.ly/Job35:12); [PSA 10:2–10:11](https://ref.ly/Ps10:2-Ps10:11), [PSA 73:3–73:12](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:12), [PSA 86:14](https://ref.ly/Ps86:14), [PSA 94:3–94:7](https://ref.ly/Ps94:3-Ps94:7); [MAL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mal3:15); [ROM 1:28–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28-Rom1:31)

### Arising from self\-confidence

**[ISA 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:9-Isa9:10)**; **[DAN 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29-Dan4:30)**; **[REV 18:7](https://ref.ly/Rev18:7)** *See also* [EXO 15:9](https://ref.ly/Exod15:9); [1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11); [2KI 14:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:10) parallel [2CH 25:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:19); [ISA 28:15](https://ref.ly/Isa28:15); [EZK 16:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:49); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8); [HAB 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Hab2:4-Hab2:5); [LUK 18:9](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9); [ACT 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9-Acts8:10); [2PE 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10-2Pet2:12)

### Issuing in words

**[PSA 17:10](https://ref.ly/Ps17:10), [PSA 119:51](https://ref.ly/Ps119:51)**; **[JAS 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jas3:5)** *See also* [1SA 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:3); [PSA 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:18), [PSA 119:69](https://ref.ly/Ps119:69), [PSA 123:4](https://ref.ly/Ps123:4); [PRO 17:7](https://ref.ly/Prov17:7), [PRO 21:24](https://ref.ly/Prov21:24); [JER 43:1–43:2](https://ref.ly/Jer43:1-Jer43:2)

Arrogance is essentially rebellion against God
----------------------------------------------

**[DEU 1:43](https://ref.ly/Deut1:43)**; **[1SA 15:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:23)** *See also* [NEH 9:16–9:17](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16-Neh9:17), [NEH 9:29](https://ref.ly/Neh9:29); [JOB 36:8–36:9](https://ref.ly/Job36:8-Job36:9); [PSA 5:5](https://ref.ly/Ps5:5), [PSA 119:85](https://ref.ly/Ps119:85); [HOS 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Hos5:4-Hos5:5), [HOS 7:10](https://ref.ly/Hos7:10); [ZEP 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:1-Zeph3:4)

Arrogance may even be found in the church
-----------------------------------------

**[2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)** *See also* [1CO 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:18); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

The godly should reject arrogance
---------------------------------

**[PRO 8:13](https://ref.ly/Prov8:13)**; **[JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24)** *See also* [JER 13:15](https://ref.ly/Jer13:15); [ROM 11:20](https://ref.ly/Rom11:20); [1CO 1:28–1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:28-1Cor1:31), [1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7), [1CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4); [EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9)

God punishes the arrogant
-------------------------

**[ISA 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Isa2:17-Isa2:18)** *See also* [EXO 18:11](https://ref.ly/Exod18:11); [1SA 15:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:23) *The LORD rejects Saul as king*; [2CH 36:15–36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:16); [NEH 9:10](https://ref.ly/Neh9:10) *the arrogance of Egypt punished*; [PSA 75:2–75:4](https://ref.ly/Ps75:2-Ps75:4), [PSA 119:21](https://ref.ly/Ps119:21), [PSA 119:78](https://ref.ly/Ps119:78); [ISA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11), [ISA 5:15](https://ref.ly/Isa5:15), [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11); [JER 48:29–48:30](https://ref.ly/Jer48:29-Jer48:30), [JER 50:31–50:32](https://ref.ly/Jer50:31-Jer50:32); [EZK 7:8–7:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:8-Ezek7:10); [DAN 5:20](https://ref.ly/Dan5:20) *Nebuchadnezzar deposed*; [ZEC 10:11](https://ref.ly/Zech10:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 7:21-22; Job 35:12; Psalms 10:2-11; Psalms 73:3-12; Psalms 86:14; Psalms 94:3-7; Malachi 3:15; Romans 1:28-31; Isaiah 9:9-10; Daniel 4:29-30; Revelation 18:7; Exodus 15:9; 1 Kings 20:11; 2 Kings 14:10; 2 Chronicles 25:19; Isaiah 28:15; Ezekiel 16:49; Hosea 12:8; Habakkuk 2:4-5; Luke 18:9; Acts 8:9-10; 2 Peter 2:10-12; Psalms 17:10; Psalms 119:51; James 3:5; 1 Samuel 2:3; Psalms 31:18; Psalms 119:69; Psalms 123:4; Proverbs 17:7; Proverbs 21:24; Jeremiah 43:1-2; Deuteronomy 1:43; 1 Samuel 15:23; Nehemiah 9:16-17; Nehemiah 9:29; Job 36:8-9; Psalms 5:5; Psalms 119:85; Hosea 5:4-5; Hosea 7:10; Zephaniah 3:1-4; 2 Corinthians 12:20; 1 Corinthians 4:18; 1 Timothy 6:17; Proverbs 8:13; Jeremiah 9:23-24; Jeremiah 13:15; Romans 11:20; 1 Corinthians 1:28-31; 1 Corinthians 4:7; 1 Corinthians 13:4; Ephesians 2:8-9; Isaiah 2:17-18; Exodus 18:11; 2 Chronicles 36:15-16; Nehemiah 9:10; Psalms 75:2-4; Psalms 119:21; Psalms 119:78; Isaiah 2:11; Isaiah 5:15; Isaiah 13:11; Jeremiah 48:29-30; Jeremiah 50:31-32; Ezekiel 7:8-10; Daniel 5:20; Zechariah 10:11
