## 5794: asceticism (id: 5794)

Rigorous self\-denial of bodily pleasures and needs. This can be motivated either by a praiseworthy desire to dedicate oneself completely to God, or by an erroneous belief that the physical body is evil.

Types of ascetic practice
-------------------------

### The ascetic practice of fasting

**[EST 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Esth4:15-Esth4:16)** *See also* [JDG 20:26](https://ref.ly/Judg20:26); [2SA 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:11-2Sam1:12); [JER 36:5–36:6](https://ref.ly/Jer36:5-Jer36:6); [LUK 2:37](https://ref.ly/Luke2:37)

### The ascetic practice of wearing sackcloth and ashes

**[DAN 9:3](https://ref.ly/Dan9:3)** *See also* [1KI 21:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:27); [NEH 9:1](https://ref.ly/Neh9:1); [EST 4:3](https://ref.ly/Esth4:3)

### The ascetic practice of celibacy

**[1CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:1)** *See also* [EXO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:15); [1SA 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:4); [1CO 7:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:8), [1CO 7:36–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36-1Cor7:38); [REV 14:4](https://ref.ly/Rev14:4)

People who practised asceticism
-------------------------------

### The Nazirites and asceticism

[NUM 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Num6:1-Num6:8); ; [JDG 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/Judg13:4-Judg13:5), [JDG 16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:17)

### John the Baptist and asceticism

[MAT 3:4](https://ref.ly/Matt3:4) parallel [MRK 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mark1:6); ; [MAT 11:18](https://ref.ly/Matt11:18) parallel [LUK 7:33](https://ref.ly/Luke7:33)

### John's disciples, the Pharisees and asceticism

[MAT 9:14](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14) parallels [MRK 2:18](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18), [LUK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Luke5:33); ; [LUK 18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:12)

### The Jews and the ascetic practice of regular fasting

[LEV 23:26–23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:26-Lev23:32); ; [ZEC 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Zech7:1-Zech7:5)

Jesus Christ refuses to enforce ascetic practices
-------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/Mark2:18-Mark2:19)** parallels [MAT 9:14–9:17](https://ref.ly/Matt9:14-Matt9:17), [LUK 5:34–5:35](https://ref.ly/Luke5:34-Luke5:35)

Ascetic practice symbolises separation from the world and dedication to God
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Asceticism as a sign of repentance

**[JON 3:6–3:9](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:6-Jonah3:9)** *See also* [1SA 7:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:6); [EZR 10:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:6); [JOL 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Joel1:13-Joel1:14)

### Asceticism accompanying prayer

**[EZR 8:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:23)** *See also* [2SA 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:16); [NEH 1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:4); [ACT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:23); [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5)

### Ascetic dedication to God's service

**[1CO 9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:27)** *See also* [DAN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Dan1:8); [MAT 19:12](https://ref.ly/Matt19:12); [1CO 7:32–7:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:32-1Cor7:35)

False asceticism opposed
------------------------

### Rejection of fasting without true repentance

[ISA 58:3–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:3-Isa58:7); ; [JOL 2:12–2:14](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12-Joel2:14)

### Condemnation of ostentatious asceticism

[MAT 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16-Matt6:18)

### Asceticism not necessary for right worship and conduct

[COL 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:23); ; [1TI 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:5) *Asceticism that is based on a negative view of the material world is rejected.*

* **Associated Passages:** Esther 4:15-16; Judges 20:26; 2 Samuel 1:11-12; Jeremiah 36:5-6; Luke 2:37; Daniel 9:3; 1 Kings 21:27; Nehemiah 9:1; Esther 4:3; 1 Corinthians 7:1; Exodus 19:15; 1 Samuel 21:4; 1 Corinthians 7:8; 1 Corinthians 7:36-38; Revelation 14:4; Numbers 6:1-8; Judges 13:4-5; Judges 16:17; Matthew 3:4; Mark 1:6; Matthew 11:18; Luke 7:33; Matthew 9:14; Mark 2:18; Luke 5:33; Luke 18:12; Leviticus 23:26-32; Zechariah 7:1-5; Mark 2:18-19; Matthew 9:14-17; Luke 5:34-35; Jonah 3:6-9; 1 Samuel 7:6; Ezra 10:6; Joel 1:13-14; Ezra 8:23; 2 Samuel 12:16; Nehemiah 1:4; Acts 14:23; 1 Corinthians 7:5; 1 Corinthians 9:27; Daniel 1:8; Matthew 19:12; 1 Corinthians 7:32-35; Isaiah 58:3-7; Joel 2:12-14; Matthew 6:16-18; Colossians 2:20-23; 1 Timothy 4:1-5

## 5795: bereavement (id: 5795)

The experience or status of suffering the loss through death of a close relative or friend.

* **Associated Articles:** 5796: bereavement, experience of (ID: `5796@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5797: bereavement, God's comfort in (ID: `5797@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5796: bereavement, experience of (id: 5796)

Scripture gives many examples of how people respond to the death of those they love.

The experience of bereavement
-----------------------------

### Abraham

**[GEN 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Gen23:1-Gen23:2)**

### Jacob

**[GEN 37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:35)** *See also* [GEN 42:38](https://ref.ly/Gen42:38), [GEN 48:7](https://ref.ly/Gen48:7)

### Joseph

**[GEN 50:10](https://ref.ly/Gen50:10)**

### David

**[2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33)** *See also* [2SA 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:11-2Sam1:12), [2SA 3:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:32), [2SA 12:15–12:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:15-2Sam12:21)

### Job

**[JOB 1:20](https://ref.ly/Job1:20)** *At the news of the deaths of his sons and daughters.* *See also* [JOB 17:7](https://ref.ly/Job17:7)

### Jesus Christ

**[JHN 11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:35)** *Because of the death of his friend, Lazarus.*

### The early Christians

**[ACT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Acts8:2)** *See also* [ACT 9:39](https://ref.ly/Acts9:39)

The expression of grief
-----------------------

**[GEN 37:34](https://ref.ly/Gen37:34)**; **[PSA 31:9](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9)** *See also* [DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8); [1SA 2:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:33); [2SA 1:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:17), [2SA 3:31–3:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:31-2Sam3:34), [2SA 14:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:2); [2CH 35:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:25); [PSA 35:14](https://ref.ly/Ps35:14); [ISA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Isa16:7); [LAM 3:48](https://ref.ly/Lam3:48); [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17); [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10) *prediction of mourning for the Messiah*; [PHP 2:27](https://ref.ly/Phil2:27)

Grief passes with time
----------------------

[GEN 24:67](https://ref.ly/Gen24:67), [GEN 38:12](https://ref.ly/Gen38:12); ; [2SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24)

## 5797: bereavement, God's comfort in (id: 5797)

God cares for the bereaved, and can turn their mourning into joy and hope.

God comforts the bereaved and the sorrowful
-------------------------------------------

**[LAM 3:32–3:33](https://ref.ly/Lam3:32-Lam3:33)**; **[2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)** *See also* [PSA 116:8](https://ref.ly/Ps116:8); [MAT 5:4](https://ref.ly/Matt5:4); [REV 7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:17)

### Mourning turned into joy

**[PSA 30:11](https://ref.ly/Ps30:11)** *See also* [ISA 61:1–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:3); [JER 31:13](https://ref.ly/Jer31:13); [JHN 16:20](https://ref.ly/John16:20)

### Despair turned into hope

**[1TH 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13-1Thess4:14)** *“Falling asleep” was a euphemistic way of talking about dying.*

### Fear turned into assurance

**[PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4)**; **[ISA 25:8–25:9](https://ref.ly/Isa25:8-Isa25:9)** *In the OT, death was generally feared because it meant the loss of meaningful existence. However, like several references, this one looks beyond the grave and anticipates NT hope for all who trust in God*; **[ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39)** *Paul's conclusion after working through the implications of the gospel.* *See also* [JHN 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/John11:25-John11:26); [1CO 15:55–15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:55-1Cor15:57); [2CO 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:6-2Cor5:8); [PHP 1:21–1:24](https://ref.ly/Phil1:21-Phil1:24); [REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)

God's care for widows and orphans
---------------------------------

**[DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18)**; **[PSA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ps10:14)** *See also* [PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5), [PSA 146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:9); [JER 49:11](https://ref.ly/Jer49:11); [HOS 14:3](https://ref.ly/Hos14:3)

Jesus Christ cared for the bereaved
-----------------------------------

**[LUK 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Luke7:11-Luke7:13)**; **[JHN 11:33](https://ref.ly/John11:33)** *At the grave of his friend, Lazarus.* *See also* [LUK 8:49–8:56](https://ref.ly/Luke8:49-Luke8:56)

Caring for the bereaved
-----------------------

**[DEU 24:19](https://ref.ly/Deut24:19)** *Special provisions in the law for the bereaved*; **[ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15)** *See also* [DEU 26:12](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12); [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3); [ACT 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:3) *The early church provided for widows in the fellowship with a special fund*; [1TI 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:3); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

* **Associated Passages:** Lamentations 3:32-33; 2 Corinthians 1:3-4; Psalms 116:8; Matthew 5:4; Revelation 7:17; Psalms 30:11; Isaiah 61:1-3; Jeremiah 31:13; John 16:20; 1 Thessalonians 4:13-14; Psalms 23:4; Isaiah 25:8-9; Romans 8:38-39; John 11:25-26; 1 Corinthians 15:55-57; 2 Corinthians 5:6-8; Philippians 1:21-24; Revelation 21:4; Deuteronomy 10:18; Psalms 10:14; Psalms 68:5; Psalms 146:9; Jeremiah 49:11; Hosea 14:3; Luke 7:11-13; John 11:33; Luke 8:49-56; Deuteronomy 24:19; Romans 12:15; Deuteronomy 26:12; Jeremiah 22:3; Acts 6:1-3; 1 Timothy 5:3; James 1:27

## 5798: betrayal (id: 5798)

The treacherous exposing or deceiving of people by those they formerly trusted. It is usually associated with an enemy masquerading as a friend, or with a broken or abused relationship. Betrayal was suffered by Jesus Christ and can be expected by his followers.

Betrayal is part of human experience
------------------------------------

### Examples of those experiencing betrayal

**[PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9)** *See also* [JDG 16:18](https://ref.ly/Judg16:18); [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17); [2SA 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:26-2Sam19:27); [PSA 55:12–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:12-Ps55:14) *Betrayal carries acute feelings for its victims*; [JER 12:6](https://ref.ly/Jer12:6); [LAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Lam1:2), [LAM 1:19](https://ref.ly/Lam1:19)

### Examples of those murdered through betrayal

The power struggles of Israel's early kingship period gave rise to many bloody betrayals: [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27), [2SA 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:6), [2SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:15), [2SA 13:28–13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:28-2Sam13:29), [2SA 20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:10); ; [1KI 21:8–21:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:10)

Betrayal is seen as a particularly wicked act
---------------------------------------------

**[ISA 33:1](https://ref.ly/Isa33:1)** *See also* [1CH 12:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:17); [PRO 25:9–25:10](https://ref.ly/Prov25:9-Prov25:10); [ISA 24:16–24:17](https://ref.ly/Isa24:16-Isa24:17); [MAT 26:24](https://ref.ly/Matt26:24) parallels [MRK 14:21](https://ref.ly/Mark14:21), [LUK 22:22](https://ref.ly/Luke22:22)

The betrayal of Jesus Christ
----------------------------

### Judas determines to betray Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:16)** parallels [MRK 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10-Mark14:11), [LUK 22:3–22:6](https://ref.ly/Luke22:3-Luke22:6)

### Jesus Christ's knowledge of events

**[MAT 26:21–26:23](https://ref.ly/Matt26:21-Matt26:23)** parallel [MRK 14:18–14:20](https://ref.ly/Mark14:18-Mark14:20) *See also* [MAT 20:17–20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:17-Matt20:19) parallel [MRK 10:33–10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:33-Mark10:34); [LUK 22:21](https://ref.ly/Luke22:21); [JHN 13:11](https://ref.ly/John13:11), [JHN 13:18](https://ref.ly/John13:18); [PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9); [JHN 13:21–13:27](https://ref.ly/John13:21-John13:27)

### Jesus Christ is betrayed by a kiss

**[MAT 26:47–26:49](https://ref.ly/Matt26:47-Matt26:49)** parallels [MRK 14:43–14:45](https://ref.ly/Mark14:43-Mark14:45), [LUK 22:47–22:48](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47-Luke22:48) *The kiss featured in several betrayals as part of feigned friendship (see e.g., [2Sa 20:9–10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9-2Sam20:10) and [Pr 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6)).*

### The betrayer's remorse

**[MAT 27:3–27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:5)**

Betrayal may be part of Christian experience
--------------------------------------------

**[MAT 10:21](https://ref.ly/Matt10:21)** parallels [MRK 13:12](https://ref.ly/Mark13:12), [LUK 21:16](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16) *See also* [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25); [MAT 10:35–10:36](https://ref.ly/Matt10:35-Matt10:36); [MIC 7:6](https://ref.ly/Mic7:6)

Betrayal as a sign of the end times
-----------------------------------

[MAT 24:10](https://ref.ly/Matt24:10)

God never betrays those who trust in him
----------------------------------------

**[PSA 89:33](https://ref.ly/Ps89:33)** *See also* [PSA 118:8](https://ref.ly/Ps118:8), [PSA 125:1](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1); [PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25); [ISA 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3-Isa26:4); [2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 41:9; Judges 16:18; 1 Samuel 18:17; 2 Samuel 19:26-27; Psalms 55:12-14; Jeremiah 12:6; Lamentations 1:2; Lamentations 1:19; 2 Samuel 3:27; 2 Samuel 4:6; 2 Samuel 11:15; 2 Samuel 13:28-29; 2 Samuel 20:10; 1 Kings 21:8-10; Isaiah 33:1; 1 Chronicles 12:17; Proverbs 25:9-10; Isaiah 24:16-17; Matthew 26:24; Mark 14:21; Luke 22:22; Matthew 26:14-16; Mark 14:10-11; Luke 22:3-6; Matthew 26:21-23; Mark 14:18-20; Matthew 20:17-19; Mark 10:33-34; Luke 22:21; John 13:11; John 13:18; John 13:21-27; Matthew 26:47-49; Mark 14:43-45; Luke 22:47-48; 2 Samuel 20:9-10; Proverbs 27:6; Matthew 27:3-5; Matthew 10:21; Mark 13:12; Luke 21:16; Daniel 7:25; Matthew 10:35-36; Micah 7:6; Matthew 24:10; Psalms 89:33; Psalms 118:8; Psalms 125:1; Proverbs 29:25; Isaiah 26:3-4; 2 Timothy 1:12

## 5799: bitterness (id: 5799)

A feeling of anger and resentment, caused particularly by perceived unfairness in suffering or by adverse circumstances.

The causes of bitterness
------------------------

### Adverse personal circumstances

**[JOB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Job10:1)** *See also* [GEN 27:34](https://ref.ly/Gen27:34); [RUT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:20) *“Mara” means “bitter”*; [1SA 30:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:6); [2SA 13:36](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:36); [2KI 4:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:27); [PSA 55:4–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4-Ps55:14); [PRO 17:25](https://ref.ly/Prov17:25); [JER 6:26](https://ref.ly/Jer6:26)

### External circumstances

**[EZK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:14)** *Because of the exiles' hardness of heart.* *See also* [JOB 21:25](https://ref.ly/Job21:25), [JOB 27:2](https://ref.ly/Job27:2); [LAM 3:1–3:20](https://ref.ly/Lam3:1-Lam3:20); [EZK 21:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:6)

### Human failure

**[MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75)** parallel [LUK 22:61–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:61-Luke22:62) *See also* [EZR 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:1); [JER 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19), [JER 4:18](https://ref.ly/Jer4:18); [LAM 1:4](https://ref.ly/Lam1:4)

Bitterness is evidence of a sinful heart
----------------------------------------

**[ACT 8:23](https://ref.ly/Acts8:23)** *See also* [ROM 3:10–3:18](https://ref.ly/Rom3:10-Rom3:18)

Bitterness is to be avoided
---------------------------

**[HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Heb12:15)** *See also* [EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31); [JAS 3:14](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14)

The remedy for bitterness
-------------------------

### Forgiveness

[GEN 33:1–33:11](https://ref.ly/Gen33:1-Gen33:11); ; [LUK 15:25–15:32](https://ref.ly/Luke15:25-Luke15:32); ; [EPH 4:31–4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31-Eph4:32)

### The causes of bitterness will pass away

**[PSA 37:1–37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:1-Ps37:2)**

Examples of bitterness in people's lives
----------------------------------------

*See also* [RUT 1:19–1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:19-Ruth1:21); [2SA 13:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:22); [EST 5:9](https://ref.ly/Esth5:9); [LUK 15:30](https://ref.ly/Luke15:30)

## 5800: blasphemy (id: 5800)

The profaning, desecration and taking in vain of the name of God, or the reviling of any of his works or deeds. It is strongly forbidden by God as dishonouring to his name.

Blasphemy strongly forbidden
----------------------------

**[EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7)** parallel [DEU 5:11](https://ref.ly/Deut5:11) *See also* [EXO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Exod22:28); [LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21), [LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12), [LEV 22:32](https://ref.ly/Lev22:32); [NUM 15:30–15:31](https://ref.ly/Num15:30-Num15:31)

Blasphemy against God punished
------------------------------

[LEV 24:10–24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:10-Lev24:16), [LEV 24:23](https://ref.ly/Lev24:23); ; [2KI 19:20–19:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:20-2Kgs19:37) parallel [ISA 37:21–37:38](https://ref.ly/Isa37:21-Isa37:38)

Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit is unpardonable
-------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 3:28–3:30](https://ref.ly/Mark3:28-Mark3:30)** parallels [MAT 12:31–12:32](https://ref.ly/Matt12:31-Matt12:32), [LUK 12:10](https://ref.ly/Luke12:10) *See also* [HEB 10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:29)

God blasphemed indirectly
-------------------------

### Rejecting his word and his servants blasphemes God

**[NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26)** *See also* [2CH 36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:16); [PSA 107:11](https://ref.ly/Ps107:11); [ISA 5:24](https://ref.ly/Isa5:24)

### Defiling sacred things blasphemes God

**[LEV 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Lev22:1-Lev22:2)** *See also* [EZK 20:27–20:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:27-Ezek20:28), [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26); [MAL 1:6–1:13](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:13)

### Despising the poor blasphemes God

**[PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31)** *See also* [AMO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:7); [JAS 2:5–2:7](https://ref.ly/Jas2:5-Jas2:7)

### Speaking against his people blasphemes God

[ZEP 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:8-Zeph2:11); ; [ACT 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Acts9:4-Acts9:5) *To persecute the church is to persecute Jesus Christ*; ; [ACT 26:9](https://ref.ly/Acts26:9); ; [1TI 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:13); ; [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rev2:9)

### Slandering celestial beings blasphemes God

[2PE 2:10–2:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10-2Pet2:12); ; [JUD 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jude1:8-Jude1:10)

The sins of God's people may cause others to blaspheme
------------------------------------------------------

**[1TI 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:1)** *See also* [2SA 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:13-2Sam12:14) *The phrase “you have made the enemies of the LORD show utter contempt” reflects a scribal tradition which avoided any reference to blasphemy. The underlying idea of blasphemy would have been obvious to the original readers*; [EZK 36:20–36:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:20-Ezek36:23); [ROM 2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:24); [ISA 52:5](https://ref.ly/Isa52:5)

### God's enemies blaspheme him

**[PSA 139:20](https://ref.ly/Ps139:20)** *See also* [PSA 74:10–74:11](https://ref.ly/Ps74:10-Ps74:11), [PSA 74:18](https://ref.ly/Ps74:18); [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25), [DAN 11:36](https://ref.ly/Dan11:36); [1CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:3); [2TH 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:4); [REV 13:5](https://ref.ly/Rev13:5), [REV 16:9](https://ref.ly/Rev16:9), [REV 16:11](https://ref.ly/Rev16:11), [REV 16:21](https://ref.ly/Rev16:21), [REV 17:3](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3)

False accusations of blasphemy
------------------------------

### Jesus Christ falsely accused of blasphemy

**[MRK 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Mark2:6-Mark2:7)** parallels [MAT 9:3](https://ref.ly/Matt9:3), [LUK 5:21](https://ref.ly/Luke5:21) *See also* [MAT 26:65–26:66](https://ref.ly/Matt26:65-Matt26:66) parallel [MRK 14:63–14:64](https://ref.ly/Mark14:63-Mark14:64); [JHN 10:33–10:36](https://ref.ly/John10:33-John10:36)

### God's servants falsely accused of blasphemy

[1KI 21:9–21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:9-1Kgs21:13); ; [ACT 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11-Acts6:14)

## 5801: brokenness (id: 5801)

God breaks the spirit of the proud and arrogant so that in their humility and brokenness they may come to repentance and restoration. A crushed heart or spirit may be caused by tragic circumstances or by God's judgment.

Brokenness before God
---------------------

**[PSA 51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:17)** *See also* [PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18), [PSA 51:8](https://ref.ly/Ps51:8); [ISA 57:15](https://ref.ly/Isa57:15), [ISA 66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2); [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10)

Brokenness of heart
-------------------

**[PSA 147:3](https://ref.ly/Ps147:3)** *See also* [PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18), [PSA 69:20](https://ref.ly/Ps69:20), [PSA 109:16](https://ref.ly/Ps109:16); [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [JER 23:9](https://ref.ly/Jer23:9); [EZK 21:6–21:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:6-Ezek21:7)

Brokenness of spirit
--------------------

**[JOB 17:1](https://ref.ly/Job17:1)** *See also* [JOB 30:24](https://ref.ly/Job30:24), [JOB 31:38–31:40](https://ref.ly/Job31:38-Job31:40)

Brokenness as a result of God's judgment
----------------------------------------

### On individuals

**[ISA 1:28](https://ref.ly/Isa1:28)** *See also* [JOB 24:20](https://ref.ly/Job24:20); [ISA 8:12–8:15](https://ref.ly/Isa8:12-Isa8:15), [ISA 65:14–65:15](https://ref.ly/Isa65:14-Isa65:15); [JER 22:24–22:30](https://ref.ly/Jer22:24-Jer22:30)

### On nations

**[EZK 32:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:28)** *See also* [JER 48:4–48:5](https://ref.ly/Jer48:4-Jer48:5), [JER 48:16–48:17](https://ref.ly/Jer48:16-Jer48:17), [JER 48:38–48:39](https://ref.ly/Jer48:38-Jer48:39), [JER 50:23–50:24](https://ref.ly/Jer50:23-Jer50:24), [JER 51:6–51:10](https://ref.ly/Jer51:6-Jer51:10)

Brokenness as contrition
------------------------

**[ISA 66:1–66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:1-Isa66:2)** *See also* [PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18), [PSA 51:16–51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:16-Ps51:17); [ISA 57:14–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:14-Isa57:21)

## 5802: care (id: 5802)

A practical outworking of loving vigilance, efforts and tenderness. Care is perfectly shown by God. People should imitate God's care, but human care is often limited, faulty or misplaced in self\-centred ways.

Caring as part of God's character
---------------------------------

### God's care for his children

**[PSA 121:3](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3)** *See also* [PSA 8:4](https://ref.ly/Ps8:4), [PSA 23:1](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1), [PSA 65:9](https://ref.ly/Ps65:9), [PSA 115:12](https://ref.ly/Ps115:12); [MAT 6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:32) parallel [LUK 12:30](https://ref.ly/Luke12:30); [MAT 10:30–10:31](https://ref.ly/Matt10:30-Matt10:31) parallel [LUK 12:7](https://ref.ly/Luke12:7); [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20) *God is the father in this parable.*

### Human care should reflect God's nature

**[JHN 15:12](https://ref.ly/John15:12)** *See also* [GEN 37:14](https://ref.ly/Gen37:14); [1SA 10:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:2); [2SA 18:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:29); [EST 2:11](https://ref.ly/Esth2:11); [LUK 10:34–10:35](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34-Luke10:35); [ACT 20:31](https://ref.ly/Acts20:31); [PHP 4:10](https://ref.ly/Phil4:10), [PHP 4:14](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14); [1TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:10)

### God's care contrasted with human care

**[ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)** *See also* [PSA 118:9](https://ref.ly/Ps118:9); [EZK 34:2–34:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:2-Ezek34:3), [EZK 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11-Ezek34:12); [ZEC 10:2–10:3](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2-Zech10:3); [JHN 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/John10:13-John10:14); [1TI 3:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:5)

Care involves responsibility
----------------------------

**[GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15)** *See also* [GEN 39:4](https://ref.ly/Gen39:4); [NUM 1:50](https://ref.ly/Num1:50); [PRO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Prov29:7); [MAT 21:33–21:34](https://ref.ly/Matt21:33-Matt21:34), [MAT 21:41](https://ref.ly/Matt21:41) parallels [MRK 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Mark12:1-Mark12:2), [MRK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Mark12:9), [LUK 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/Luke20:9-Luke20:10), [LUK 20:16](https://ref.ly/Luke20:16); [1TI 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20)

God's care reflected in human relationships
-------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:25](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25)** *See also* [PSA 103:13–103:14](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13-Ps103:14); [ECC 11:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:5); [ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15); [EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1), [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4); [1TI 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:8)

To be careless or uncaring is wrong
-----------------------------------

**[JER 48:10](https://ref.ly/Jer48:10)** *See also* [DEU 8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:14); [2CH 24:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:5); [ISA 47:8](https://ref.ly/Isa47:8); [ZEC 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:9-Zech7:10); [MAT 12:36](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36)

Care for worldly things is wrong
--------------------------------

**[PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6)** *See also* [ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12); [MAT 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31-Matt6:32) parallel [LUK 12:29–12:30](https://ref.ly/Luke12:29-Luke12:30); [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18-Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [LUK 10:40–10:42](https://ref.ly/Luke10:40-Luke10:42), [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34); [1CO 7:32–7:34](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:32-1Cor7:34)

God relieves his people of cares
--------------------------------

**[1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)** *There is a vital distinction between “care” in the sense of “loving care” which Christians are to practise, and “worldly care” in the sense of “anxiety” which Christians should transfer to God, since they and their needs are his responsibility.* *See also* [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25), [PSA 55:22](https://ref.ly/Ps55:22); [PRO 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Prov3:5-Prov3:6); [ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4); [MAT 11:28–11:29](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:29); [LUK 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/Luke12:11-Luke12:12); [JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1)

## 5803: carelessness (id: 5803)

A lack of attention to one's thoughts and deeds, or a failure to give adequate thought to the consequences of one's actions, often leading to distressing results.

Carelessness in speech
----------------------

**[LEV 5:4](https://ref.ly/Lev5:4)** *See also* [PRO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Prov20:25); [ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2), [ECC 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:6); [MAT 12:36](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36)

Warnings against carelessness in actions
----------------------------------------

**[EXO 21:33–21:34](https://ref.ly/Exod21:33-Exod21:34)** *See also* [EXO 21:22–21:25](https://ref.ly/Exod21:22-Exod21:25), [EXO 21:28–21:29](https://ref.ly/Exod21:28-Exod21:29), [EXO 21:35–21:36](https://ref.ly/Exod21:35-Exod21:36), [EXO 22:5](https://ref.ly/Exod22:5); [LEV 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Lev5:2-Lev5:3); [NUM 35:22–35:24](https://ref.ly/Num35:22-Num35:24)

Unintentional sins resulting from carelessness
----------------------------------------------

### By the nation

**[LEV 4:13](https://ref.ly/Lev4:13)** *See also* [NUM 15:22–15:26](https://ref.ly/Num15:22-Num15:26)

### By an individual

**[LEV 4:27](https://ref.ly/Lev4:27)** *See also* [LEV 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Lev4:2-Lev4:3), [LEV 4:22–4:23](https://ref.ly/Lev4:22-Lev4:23); [NUM 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/Num15:27-Num15:28), [NUM 35:22–35:24](https://ref.ly/Num35:22-Num35:24)

Examples of carelessness
------------------------

### Careless speech

**[JDG 11:30–11:35](https://ref.ly/Judg11:30-Judg11:35)** *See also* [GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34); [MAT 14:6–14:11](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6-Matt14:11) parallel [MRK 6:21–6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21-Mark6:28)

### Careless actions and omissions

**[MAT 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26-Matt7:27)** parallel [LUK 6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:49) *See also* [MAT 25:1–25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:13)

## 5804: charm (id: 5804)

The gracious effect of attracting and captivating others or the subtle ability to deceive and mislead them.

Charm to attract and captivate others
-------------------------------------

### King Xerxes by Esther

[EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17)

### A husband by his wife

**[PRO 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18-Prov5:19)**

### The lovers in the Song of Solomon

**[SNG 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Song1:15-Song1:16)** *See also* [SNG 2:14](https://ref.ly/Song2:14), [SNG 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Song4:9-Song4:10), [SNG 5:16](https://ref.ly/Song5:16), [SNG 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Song6:4-Song6:5), [SNG 7:6](https://ref.ly/Song7:6)

Charm to deceive people
-----------------------

**[PRO 26:24–26:25](https://ref.ly/Prov26:24-Prov26:25)** *See also* [PRO 6:25–6:26](https://ref.ly/Prov6:25-Prov6:26), [PRO 31:30](https://ref.ly/Prov31:30)

### The devil subtly seeks to deceive people

**[2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3), [2CO 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:14)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:5), [GEN 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gen3:13); [MAT 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:11) parallels [MRK 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Mark1:12-Mark1:13), [LUK 4:1–4:13](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:13)

### Absalom deceived others

[2SA 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:6)

False teachers use charm to deceive others
------------------------------------------

### For their own gain

**[ROM 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18)** *See also* [JUD 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16)

### To draw people away from Jesus Christ

**[2PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18)** *See also* [2CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13-2Cor11:15)

### Christians must not be deceived by such charm

[EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14), [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); ; [COL 2:4](https://ref.ly/Col2:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Esther 2:17; Proverbs 5:18-19; Song of Songs 1:15-16; Song of Songs 2:14; Song of Songs 4:9-10; Song of Songs 5:16; Song of Songs 6:4-5; Song of Songs 7:6; Proverbs 26:24-25; Proverbs 6:25-26; Proverbs 31:30; 2 Corinthians 11:3; 2 Corinthians 11:14; Genesis 3:1-5; Genesis 3:13; Matthew 4:1-11; Mark 1:12-13; Luke 4:1-13; 2 Samuel 15:1-6; Romans 16:18; Jude 1:16; 2 Peter 2:18; 2 Corinthians 11:13-15; Ephesians 4:14; Ephesians 5:6; Colossians 2:4

## 5805: comfort (id: 5805)

The consolation and reassurance of those who are in distress, anxiety or need. Such comfort is an essential aspect of human relationships. Scripture declares that God comforts his people in times of distress.

The comfort of friends and relations
------------------------------------

**[JOB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Job2:11)**; **[JHN 11:19](https://ref.ly/John11:19)** *See also* [GEN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:29), [GEN 24:67](https://ref.ly/Gen24:67), [GEN 37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:35); [RUT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:13); [2SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24); [1CH 7:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:22); [JOB 16:5](https://ref.ly/Job16:5), [JOB 29:25](https://ref.ly/Job29:25), [JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11); [JHN 11:31](https://ref.ly/John11:31); [ACT 20:12](https://ref.ly/Acts20:12)

Parental comfort
----------------

**[ISA 66:13](https://ref.ly/Isa66:13)**; **[1TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:11-1Thess2:12)** *See also* [ISA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Isa66:11); [JER 31:15](https://ref.ly/Jer31:15); [MAT 2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:18)

God's comfort
-------------

**[ISA 49:13](https://ref.ly/Isa49:13)**; **[2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)** *See also* [PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4), [PSA 71:20–71:21](https://ref.ly/Ps71:20-Ps71:21), [PSA 119:50](https://ref.ly/Ps119:50), [PSA 119:76](https://ref.ly/Ps119:76); [ISA 12:1](https://ref.ly/Isa12:1), [ISA 40:1](https://ref.ly/Isa40:1), [ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3), [ISA 51:12](https://ref.ly/Isa51:12), [ISA 52:9](https://ref.ly/Isa52:9), [ISA 57:18](https://ref.ly/Isa57:18), [ISA 61:2](https://ref.ly/Isa61:2); [JER 8:18](https://ref.ly/Jer8:18), [JER 31:13](https://ref.ly/Jer31:13); [ZEC 1:3](https://ref.ly/Zech1:3), [ZEC 1:17](https://ref.ly/Zech1:17); [MAT 5:4](https://ref.ly/Matt5:4); [LUK 16:25](https://ref.ly/Luke16:25); [PHP 2:1](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1)

Comfort as a specific work of the Holy Spirit
---------------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:16-John14:17)** *The title “Counsellor” may be translated “Comforter”.* *See also* [ISA 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/Isa11:2-Isa11:3); [JHN 15:26](https://ref.ly/John15:26), [JHN 16:7](https://ref.ly/John16:7)

Comfort within the fellowship of believers
------------------------------------------

**[2CO 1:5–1:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:5-2Cor1:7)** *See also* [1CO 14:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:3); [2CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:7), [2CO 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:6-2Cor7:7); [COL 4:11](https://ref.ly/Col4:11); [HEB 12:12](https://ref.ly/Heb12:12)

Promises of comfort
-------------------

### When worried

[ISA 43:1–43:13](https://ref.ly/Isa43:1-Isa43:13); ; [MAT 6:25–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:34); ; [PHP 4:4–4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:4-Phil4:7); ; [1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)

### When lonely

[PSA 73:23–73:24](https://ref.ly/Ps73:23-Ps73:24); ; [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10), [ISA 49:14–49:16](https://ref.ly/Isa49:14-Isa49:16); ; [JHN 14:15–14:21](https://ref.ly/John14:15-John14:21)

### When tired or weary

[ISA 40:28–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:28-Isa40:31); ; [MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30); ; [2CO 4:16–4:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:16-2Cor4:18); ; [PHP 4:12–4:13](https://ref.ly/Phil4:12-Phil4:13)

### When discouraged

[PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18), [PSA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5); ; [LAM 3:20–3:23](https://ref.ly/Lam3:20-Lam3:23); ; [ROM 8:28–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:28-Rom8:39)

False comfort
-------------

**[LUK 6:24](https://ref.ly/Luke6:24)** *See also* [JOB 16:2](https://ref.ly/Job16:2); [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2)

Lack of comfort
---------------

**[JER 16:7](https://ref.ly/Jer16:7)** *See also* [PSA 69:20](https://ref.ly/Ps69:20); [ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1); [ISA 51:19](https://ref.ly/Isa51:19), [ISA 54:11](https://ref.ly/Isa54:11); [LAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Lam1:2), [LAM 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Lam1:16-Lam1:17), [LAM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Lam1:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 2:11; John 11:19; Genesis 5:29; Genesis 24:67; Genesis 37:35; Ruth 2:13; 2 Samuel 12:24; 1 Chronicles 7:22; Job 16:5; Job 29:25; Job 42:11; John 11:31; Acts 20:12; Isaiah 66:13; 1 Thessalonians 2:11-12; Isaiah 66:11; Jeremiah 31:15; Matthew 2:18; Isaiah 49:13; 2 Corinthians 1:3-4; Psalms 23:4; Psalms 71:20-21; Psalms 119:50; Psalms 119:76; Isaiah 12:1; Isaiah 40:1; Isaiah 51:3; Isaiah 51:12; Isaiah 52:9; Isaiah 57:18; Isaiah 61:2; Jeremiah 8:18; Jeremiah 31:13; Zechariah 1:3; Zechariah 1:17; Matthew 5:4; Luke 16:25; Philippians 2:1; John 14:16-17; Isaiah 11:2-3; John 15:26; John 16:7; 2 Corinthians 1:5-7; 1 Corinthians 14:3; 2 Corinthians 2:7; 2 Corinthians 7:6-7; Colossians 4:11; Hebrews 12:12; Isaiah 43:1-13; Matthew 6:25-34; Philippians 4:4-7; 1 Peter 5:7; Psalms 73:23-24; Isaiah 41:10; Isaiah 49:14-16; John 14:15-21; Isaiah 40:28-31; Matthew 11:28-30; 2 Corinthians 4:16-18; Philippians 4:12-13; Psalms 34:18; Psalms 42:5; Lamentations 3:20-23; Romans 8:28-39; Luke 6:24; Job 16:2; Zechariah 10:2; Jeremiah 16:7; Psalms 69:20; Ecclesiastes 4:1; Isaiah 51:19; Isaiah 54:11; Lamentations 1:2; Lamentations 1:16-17; Lamentations 1:21

## 5806: compassion (id: 5806)

A quality of care and sympathy, characteristic of God and Jesus Christ, usually shown in acts of kindness and consideration towards those in any kind of difficulty or crisis.

* **Associated Articles:** 5807: compassion, of God (ID: `5807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5808: compassion, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5808@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5809: compassion, human (ID: `5809@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5807: compassion, of God (id: 5807)

See [1030: God, compassion of](1030.md)



## 5808: compassion, of Jesus Christ (id: 5808)

See [2015: Jesus Christ, compassion of](2015.md)



## 5809: compassion, human (id: 5809)

An attitude of care and concern, grounded in pity and sympathy towards others. Christian compassion towards others should reflect the compassion of God for his people.

Compassion is a vital requirement in Christian character
--------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32)**; **[COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12)** *See also* [2CO 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:5); [1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)

Compassion needs to be shown in actions
---------------------------------------

**[1JN 3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:17)** *See also* [ISA 58:6–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:7); [MAT 10:42](https://ref.ly/Matt10:42) parallel [MRK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Mark9:41); [MAT 25:35–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:35-Matt25:36); [ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35)

Examples of compassionate behaviour
-----------------------------------

### To the oppressed and needy

[JOB 29:12–29:17](https://ref.ly/Job29:12-Job29:17), [JOB 30:25](https://ref.ly/Job30:25); ; [PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31); ; [LUK 10:33–10:35](https://ref.ly/Luke10:33-Luke10:35); ; [ACT 28:2](https://ref.ly/Acts28:2)

### To the bereaved

[GEN 37:35](https://ref.ly/Gen37:35); ; [RUT 2:1–2:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:1-Ruth2:20); ; [2SA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:1-2Sam10:2); ; [1CH 7:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:22); ; [JHN 11:19](https://ref.ly/John11:19)

### To children

[EXO 2:6](https://ref.ly/Exod2:6); ; [1KI 3:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:26); ; [ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)

### To prisoners

[2CH 28:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:15), [2CH 30:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:9); ; [ACT 16:33–16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:33-Acts16:34); ; [HEB 13:3](https://ref.ly/Heb13:3)

### To forgiven sinners

[2CO 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:7-2Cor2:8); ; [GAL 6:1](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1)

### To the sick

[JOB 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Job2:11-Job2:13); ; [PSA 35:13](https://ref.ly/Ps35:13)

### To the solitary

[ECC 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:10-Eccl4:11)

### To widows and orphans

[ZEC 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:9-Zech7:10); ; [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

### To Jerusalem

[PSA 102:14](https://ref.ly/Ps102:14)

God inspires human compassion
-----------------------------

**[DAN 1:9](https://ref.ly/Dan1:9)** *See also* [EXO 3:21](https://ref.ly/Exod3:21); [PSA 106:46](https://ref.ly/Ps106:46); [JER 42:12](https://ref.ly/Jer42:12)

God rewards compassion
----------------------

**[PRO 14:21](https://ref.ly/Prov14:21)** *See also* [PSA 112:4–112:5](https://ref.ly/Ps112:4-Ps112:5); [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17)

Lack of compassion
------------------

[2SA 12:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:6); ; [PSA 69:20](https://ref.ly/Ps69:20), [PSA 109:16](https://ref.ly/Ps109:16); ; [EZK 16:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:5); ; [AMO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Amos1:11)

Limits of human compassion
--------------------------

[DEU 28:53–28:57](https://ref.ly/Deut28:53-Deut28:57); ; [ISA 13:18](https://ref.ly/Isa13:18); ; [JER 21:7](https://ref.ly/Jer21:7); ; [LAM 4:10](https://ref.ly/Lam4:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 4:32; Colossians 3:12; 2 Corinthians 1:3-5; 1 Peter 3:8; 1 John 3:17; Isaiah 58:6-7; Matthew 10:42; Mark 9:41; Matthew 25:35-36; Acts 20:35; Job 29:12-17; Job 30:25; Proverbs 14:31; Luke 10:33-35; Acts 28:2; Genesis 37:35; Ruth 2:1-20; 2 Samuel 10:1-2; 1 Chronicles 7:22; John 11:19; Exodus 2:6; 1 Kings 3:26; Isaiah 49:15; 2 Chronicles 28:15; 2 Chronicles 30:9; Acts 16:33-34; Hebrews 13:3; 2 Corinthians 2:7-8; Galatians 6:1; Job 2:11-13; Psalms 35:13; Ecclesiastes 4:10-11; Zechariah 7:9-10; James 1:27; Psalms 102:14; Daniel 1:9; Exodus 3:21; Psalms 106:46; Jeremiah 42:12; Proverbs 14:21; Psalms 112:4-5; Proverbs 19:17; 2 Samuel 12:6; Psalms 69:20; Psalms 109:16; Ezekiel 16:5; Amos 1:11; Deuteronomy 28:53-57; Isaiah 13:18; Jeremiah 21:7; Lamentations 4:10

## 5810: complacency (id: 5810)

A self\-satisfied state of negligence or carelessness, especially in relation to one's personal situation.

Spiritual complacency
---------------------

**[ZEP 1:12](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:12)**; **[LUK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:19)** *See also* [DEU 8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:14); [2CH 20:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:33); [PSA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4); [ISA 43:22](https://ref.ly/Isa43:22), [ISA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Isa64:7); [JER 10:21](https://ref.ly/Jer10:21); [EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31); [DAN 9:13](https://ref.ly/Dan9:13); [HOS 7:7](https://ref.ly/Hos7:7); [ZEP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:6); [HEB 6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:12), [HEB 12:25](https://ref.ly/Heb12:25)

Examples of complacency
-----------------------

**[JER 6:14](https://ref.ly/Jer6:14)**; **[OBA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3)**; **[1CO 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:2)** *See also* [PRO 30:20](https://ref.ly/Prov30:20); [ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6); [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2); [HAG 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hag1:2); [MAL 1:6–1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:14); [MAT 7:26](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26), [MAT 22:5](https://ref.ly/Matt22:5), [MAT 26:33](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33); [LUK 12:47–12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:47-Luke12:48), [LUK 14:18](https://ref.ly/Luke14:18); [1CO 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:8); [REV 3:15–3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:15-Rev3:17)

Warnings against complacency
----------------------------

**[AMO 6:1](https://ref.ly/Amos6:1)**; **[2CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:5)** *See also* [PRO 1:32](https://ref.ly/Prov1:32), [PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4), [PRO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Prov12:27); [ECC 10:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:18); [ISA 32:9](https://ref.ly/Isa32:9), [ISA 32:11](https://ref.ly/Isa32:11), [ISA 47:8–47:9](https://ref.ly/Isa47:8-Isa47:9); [EZK 30:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:9); [1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12); [HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Zephaniah 1:12; Luke 12:19; Deuteronomy 8:14; 2 Chronicles 20:33; Psalms 10:4; Isaiah 43:22; Isaiah 64:7; Jeremiah 10:21; Ezekiel 33:31; Daniel 9:13; Hosea 7:7; Zephaniah 1:6; Hebrews 6:12; Hebrews 12:25; Jeremiah 6:14; Obadiah 1:3; 1 Corinthians 5:1-2; Proverbs 30:20; Isaiah 16:6; Ezekiel 28:2; Haggai 1:2; Malachi 1:6-14; Matthew 7:26; Matthew 22:5; Matthew 26:33; Luke 12:47-48; Luke 14:18; 1 Corinthians 4:8; Revelation 3:15-17; Amos 6:1; 2 Corinthians 13:5; Proverbs 1:32; Proverbs 10:4; Proverbs 12:27; Ecclesiastes 10:18; Isaiah 32:9; Isaiah 32:11; Isaiah 47:8-9; Ezekiel 30:9; 1 Corinthians 10:12; Hebrews 2:3

## 5811: compromise (id: 5811)

Usually a combination of contrasting values, practices or persons incurring God's disapproval, but it can also be a positive way to achieve a greater good.

God forbids compromising associations
-------------------------------------

### Israel was not to associate with paganism

**[EXO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Exod20:3)** parallel [DEU 5:7](https://ref.ly/Deut5:7) *See also* [EXO 23:32–23:33](https://ref.ly/Exod23:32-Exod23:33), [EXO 34:12–34:16](https://ref.ly/Exod34:12-Exod34:16); [DEU 7:2–7:4](https://ref.ly/Deut7:2-Deut7:4); [1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21)

### Christians must not compromise their allegiance

**[JAS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jas4:4)** *See also* [MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24) parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13); [ROM 13:14](https://ref.ly/Rom13:14); [2CO 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14-2Cor6:16); [GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9); [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8)

Israel's compromises with paganism
----------------------------------

### In worship

**[1KI 12:28–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28-1Kgs12:30)** *The LORD's action in bringing Israel up out of Egypt is here attributed to the golden calves representing Baal.* *See also* [EXO 32:2–32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:6); [JDG 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Judg2:11-Judg2:12), [JDG 8:27](https://ref.ly/Judg8:27); [2KI 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:2-2Kgs1:3), [2KI 16:10–16:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10-2Kgs16:16), [2KI 17:32–17:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:32-2Kgs17:33), [2KI 17:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:41); [2CH 28:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:22); [REV 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20-Rev2:23)

### In foreign alliances

**[ISA 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:2)** *See also* [2CH 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:2), [2CH 20:35–20:37](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:35-2Chr20:37), [2CH 28:16–28:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:16-2Chr28:21); [ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1); [HOS 5:13](https://ref.ly/Hos5:13), [HOS 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Hos7:8-Hos7:9), [HOS 7:11](https://ref.ly/Hos7:11)

### In marriage

**[1KI 11:1–11:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:11)** *See also* [1KI 16:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:31); [2CH 18:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:1); [EZR 9:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:2); [NEH 13:23](https://ref.ly/Neh13:23), [NEH 13:25–13:26](https://ref.ly/Neh13:25-Neh13:26)

Motives for compromise
----------------------

### Fear

**[GEN 26:7–26:9](https://ref.ly/Gen26:7-Gen26:9)** *See also* [GEN 12:11–12:13](https://ref.ly/Gen12:11-Gen12:13), [GEN 12:18–12:19](https://ref.ly/Gen12:18-Gen12:19), [GEN 19:18–19:20](https://ref.ly/Gen19:18-Gen19:20), [GEN 20:2](https://ref.ly/Gen20:2), [GEN 20:11](https://ref.ly/Gen20:11); [GAL 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Gal2:12-Gal2:13)

### Lack of faith

**[GEN 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:2)** *See also* [GEN 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Gen15:2-Gen15:3); [1KI 12:26–12:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26-1Kgs12:33)

### Desire for material things

**[JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21)** *See also* [HAG 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Hag1:2-Hag1:4); [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18-Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [ACT 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:4); [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10)

Examples of those who refused to compromise
-------------------------------------------

**[EST 3:4](https://ref.ly/Esth3:4)** *See also* [GEN 39:7–39:12](https://ref.ly/Gen39:7-Gen39:12); [1KI 22:13–22:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:13-1Kgs22:14); [DAN 3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:18), [DAN 6:7–6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:7-Dan6:10); [2CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:2)

Compromise as a mutually acceptable solution
--------------------------------------------

### For the sake of the gospel

**[1CO 9:19–9:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:19-1Cor9:23)** *See also* [ACT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:20), [ACT 15:28–15:29](https://ref.ly/Acts15:28-Acts15:29), [ACT 21:20–21:26](https://ref.ly/Acts21:20-Acts21:26); [ROM 14:19–14:22](https://ref.ly/Rom14:19-Rom14:22), [ROM 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Rom15:1-Rom15:2)

### As a means to peace

## 5812: concealment (id: 5812)

The deliberate hiding from view of a person, object or action. Those who sin against God often attempt to hide from him. The Bible teaches the need to confess sin and receive God's forgiveness. Nothing is concealed from him and all secrets will ultimately be revealed.

Attempted concealment of sin from God
-------------------------------------

**[GEN 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:10)** *See also* [NUM 32:23](https://ref.ly/Num32:23); [JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21); [2KI 17:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:9); [JOB 24:13–24:17](https://ref.ly/Job24:13-Job24:17); [EZK 8:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:12)

### Such attempts are foolish

**[PRO 28:13](https://ref.ly/Prov28:13)** *See also* [ISA 29:15](https://ref.ly/Isa29:15)

### No sin can be concealed from God

**[HEB 4:13](https://ref.ly/Heb4:13)** *See also* [JOB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Job13:9), [JOB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Job10:14); [PSA 44:20–44:21](https://ref.ly/Ps44:20-Ps44:21), [PSA 69:5](https://ref.ly/Ps69:5); [JER 16:17](https://ref.ly/Jer16:17); [EZK 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:5-Ezek11:6)

### Concealed sins will be exposed

**[ECC 12:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:14)** *See also* [JOB 20:27](https://ref.ly/Job20:27); [MAT 10:26](https://ref.ly/Matt10:26) parallel [LUK 12:2](https://ref.ly/Luke12:2); [ROM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Rom2:16); [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5)

### Sins should be confessed to God and not concealed

**[1JN 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:8-1John1:9)** *See also* [PSA 32:5–32:6](https://ref.ly/Ps32:5-Ps32:6)

Believers should not conceal their light
----------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:14–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:14-Matt5:16)**

God may conceal matters
-----------------------

**[COL 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Col2:2-Col2:3), [COL 3:3](https://ref.ly/Col3:3)** *Christ is the mystery of God, which has now been made known. Through faith, believers share in this mystery.* *See also* [PRO 25:2](https://ref.ly/Prov25:2); [DEU 29:29](https://ref.ly/Deut29:29); [MAT 13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:11), [MAT 13:35](https://ref.ly/Matt13:35); [MRK 13:32](https://ref.ly/Mark13:32); [ACT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Acts1:7); [ROM 16:25](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25); [COL 1:26](https://ref.ly/Col1:26)

-------------------------

**[HEB 11:23](https://ref.ly/Heb11:23)** *See also* [EXO 2:2](https://ref.ly/Exod2:2)

## 5813: conceit (id: 5813)

A state of pride arising out of an overestimation of one's own ability, possessions or importance.

The definition of conceit
-------------------------

**[GAL 6:3](https://ref.ly/Gal6:3)**

Examples of conceit
-------------------

### In military strength

**[2KI 18:33–18:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:33-2Kgs18:35)** *See also* [EXO 15:9](https://ref.ly/Exod15:9); [1SA 17:42–17:44](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:42-1Sam17:44)

### In the power of idols

**[DAN 3:15](https://ref.ly/Dan3:15)** *See also* [ACT 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9-Acts8:10)

### In personal abilities

**[MAT 26:33](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33)** parallel [MRK 14:29](https://ref.ly/Mark14:29) *See also* [GEN 16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4); [1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11); [EST 5:10–5:12](https://ref.ly/Esth5:10-Esth5:12)

### In false teaching

**[1TI 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:4)**

### In possessions

**[HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8)**

### In intellectual ability

**[PSA 73:7–73:9](https://ref.ly/Ps73:7-Ps73:9)**

### In national status

**[ISA 47:6–47:8](https://ref.ly/Isa47:6-Isa47:8)** *See also* [ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6); [JER 48:29](https://ref.ly/Jer48:29)

-------------------------

**[2SA 22:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:28)**; **[1CO 1:27–1:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:27-1Cor1:29)** *See also* [ISA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Isa2:12), [ISA 23:9](https://ref.ly/Isa23:9), [ISA 47:8–47:11](https://ref.ly/Isa47:8-Isa47:11); [OBA 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3-Obad1:4)

Warnings against conceit
------------------------

**[PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3)** *See also* [PRO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Prov3:7); [ISA 5:21](https://ref.ly/Isa5:21); [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24); [ROM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rom12:3), [ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16); [1CO 3:18–3:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:18-1Cor3:21), [1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7), [1CO 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:2); [GAL 5:26](https://ref.ly/Gal5:26); [2TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:4)

--------------------

**[PRO 26:12](https://ref.ly/Prov26:12)** *See also* [PRO 28:26](https://ref.ly/Prov28:26); [ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6); [HOS 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/Hos10:13-Hos10:14); [JAS 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jas3:5), [JAS 4:16](https://ref.ly/Jas4:16)

The danger of conceit for Christians
------------------------------------

**[1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12)** *See also* [ROM 11:25](https://ref.ly/Rom11:25); [2CO 12:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7); [1TI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:6); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

--------------------

### Being aware of one's sin

**[ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16)**

### Considering others as being superior

## 5815: confusion (id: 5815)

A state of perplexity, bewilderment or disorder. Confusion may be brought about by God to cause the schemes of the wicked to fail and opposition to his people to prove ineffective. Confusion over spiritual truth is the natural state of humanity; believers, too, may be perplexed by God's purposes or thrown into confusion by false teaching.

Examples of people in confusion
-------------------------------

[2SA 18:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:29); ; [EST 3:15](https://ref.ly/Esth3:15); ; [LUK 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/Luke21:25-Luke21:26); ; [ACT 17:8](https://ref.ly/Acts17:8), [ACT 19:32](https://ref.ly/Acts19:32)

God sends confusion as judgment
-------------------------------

### The righteous ask God to confuse their enemies

**[PSA 35:26](https://ref.ly/Ps35:26)** *See also* [PSA 40:14](https://ref.ly/Ps40:14), [PSA 55:9](https://ref.ly/Ps55:9), [PSA 70:2](https://ref.ly/Ps70:2)

### God sends confusion on Israel's enemies

**[EXO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Exod23:27)** *See also* [DEU 7:23](https://ref.ly/Deut7:23); [PSA 71:24](https://ref.ly/Ps71:24)

### Israel, too, may experience confusion

**[DEU 28:20](https://ref.ly/Deut28:20)** *See also* [DEU 28:28](https://ref.ly/Deut28:28); [2CH 15:5–15:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:5-2Chr15:6); [JER 51:34](https://ref.ly/Jer51:34)

### Examples of God sending confusion

[GEN 11:7–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:7-Gen11:9) *at the tower of Babel*; ; [JOS 10:10](https://ref.ly/Josh10:10) *on the Amorites*; ; [1SA 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:20) *on the Philistines*; ; [2CH 20:22–20:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:22-2Chr20:23) *on Jehoshaphat's enemies*

Spiritual confusion is humanity's natural state
-----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21)** *See also* [ISA 41:29](https://ref.ly/Isa41:29); [1CO 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18), [1CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:14); [2CO 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:3-2Cor4:4)

Confusion relating to Jesus Christ
----------------------------------

### Confusion among the Jews

**[JHN 6:52](https://ref.ly/John6:52)**; **[ACT 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Acts2:6-Acts2:7)** *See also* [MAT 12:23–12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:23-Matt12:24) parallels [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22), [LUK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15); [LUK 9:7–9:9](https://ref.ly/Luke9:7-Luke9:9) parallels [MAT 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Matt14:1-Matt14:2), [MRK 6:14–6:16](https://ref.ly/Mark6:14-Mark6:16); [JHN 3:4](https://ref.ly/John3:4); [ACT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Acts2:12); [2CO 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:14)

### Confusion among Jesus Christ's disciples

**[MAT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:27)** parallels [MRK 4:41](https://ref.ly/Mark4:41), [LUK 8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:25) *See also* [MRK 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Mark9:5-Mark9:6) parallels [MAT 17:4](https://ref.ly/Matt17:4), [LUK 9:33–9:34](https://ref.ly/Luke9:33-Luke9:34); [MRK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Mark16:8); [LUK 24:22–24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:22-Luke24:25); [JHN 6:60](https://ref.ly/John6:60), [JHN 16:17–16:19](https://ref.ly/John16:17-John16:19)

Further examples of believers in confusion
------------------------------------------

### Confusion over God's purposes

**[2CO 4:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:8)** *See also* [PSA 73:12–73:14](https://ref.ly/Ps73:12-Ps73:14); [ECC 7:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:15); [ISA 21:3–21:4](https://ref.ly/Isa21:3-Isa21:4); [DAN 4:19](https://ref.ly/Dan4:19); [HAB 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:2-Hab1:4); [2CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:5); [GAL 4:19–4:20](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19-Gal4:20)

### Confusion over doctrine

**[GAL 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:7)** *See also* [GAL 4:9](https://ref.ly/Gal4:9), [GAL 4:17](https://ref.ly/Gal4:17), [GAL 5:10](https://ref.ly/Gal5:10)

Remedies for confusion
----------------------

### Confusion dispelled by asking God for understanding

**[JER 33:3](https://ref.ly/Jer33:3)** *See also* [JAS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jas1:5)

### Confusion dispelled by the enlightenment of the Holy Spirit

**[JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26)** *See also* [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/John16:13); [1CO 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:9-1Cor2:10), [1CO 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:13)

### Confusion combated by the teaching of wise leaders

[ACT 20:28–20:30](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28-Acts20:30); ; [2TI 2:24–2:25](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24-2Tim2:25), [2TI 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2-2Tim4:3); ; [HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)

### Confusion dispelled by the instruction of Scripture

[PSA 119:103–119:105](https://ref.ly/Ps119:103-Ps119:105); ; [2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16)

### Confusion goes as believers grow to spiritual maturity

**[EPH 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:13-Eph4:14)** *See also* [PHP 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Phil1:9-Phil1:10); [HEB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:14)

## 5816: consciousness (id: 5816)

The state of being aware, especially of one's actions and motivations, or of the existence and concerns of others. All people have a general awareness or consciousness of God and of sin, but it is often suppressed or destroyed.

Consciousness of sin
--------------------

### Through the law

**[ROM 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Rom3:19-Rom3:20)** *See also* [ROM 7:7–7:13](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7-Rom7:13); [JAS 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Jas2:9-Jas2:10)

### Through the convicting power of the Holy Spirit

**[JHN 16:8](https://ref.ly/John16:8)** *See also* [1CO 14:24–14:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:24-1Cor14:25); [JUD 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jude1:14-Jude1:15)

### Through comparison with God's righteousness

**[PSA 143:2](https://ref.ly/Ps143:2)** *See also* [JOB 25:2–25:6](https://ref.ly/Job25:2-Job25:6); [PSA 130:3–130:4](https://ref.ly/Ps130:3-Ps130:4)

Consciousness of God
--------------------

### Through an innate knowledge of the creator

**[ROM 1:19–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:19-Rom1:23)** *See also* [ACT 17:22–17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:22-Acts17:31); [ROM 1:28](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28); [EPH 4:17–4:19](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17-Eph4:19)

### Through the revelation of God in nature

**[ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)** *See also* [PSA 19:1–19:6](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:6), [PSA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Ps50:6)

### Through observation of divine judgment

**[EZK 6:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:13)** *See also* [PSA 83:16–83:18](https://ref.ly/Ps83:16-Ps83:18); [EZK 29:6–29:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:6-Ezek29:9), [EZK 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:6)

### Through observation of divine blessing

**[JOS 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/Josh4:23-Josh4:24)** *See also* [EXO 7:5](https://ref.ly/Exod7:5); [DEU 4:35](https://ref.ly/Deut4:35); [ISA 52:9–52:10](https://ref.ly/Isa52:9-Isa52:10)

Lack of consciousness
---------------------

**[JOB 14:10–14:12](https://ref.ly/Job14:10-Job14:12)** *See also* [PSA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Ps6:5), [PSA 30:9](https://ref.ly/Ps30:9), [PSA 31:17](https://ref.ly/Ps31:17), [PSA 88:10–88:12](https://ref.ly/Ps88:10-Ps88:12), [PSA 115:17–115:18](https://ref.ly/Ps115:17-Ps115:18); [ECC 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:5-Eccl9:6), [ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10); [ISA 38:18–38:19](https://ref.ly/Isa38:18-Isa38:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 3:19-20; Romans 7:7-13; James 2:9-10; John 16:8; 1 Corinthians 14:24-25; Jude 1:14-15; Psalms 143:2; Job 25:2-6; Psalms 130:3-4; Romans 1:19-23; Acts 17:22-31; Romans 1:28; Ephesians 4:17-19; Acts 14:17; Psalms 19:1-6; Psalms 50:6; Ezekiel 6:13; Psalms 83:16-18; Ezekiel 29:6-9; Ezekiel 39:6; Joshua 4:23-24; Exodus 7:5; Deuteronomy 4:35; Isaiah 52:9-10; Job 14:10-12; Psalms 6:5; Psalms 30:9; Psalms 31:17; Psalms 88:10-12; Psalms 115:17-18; Ecclesiastes 9:5-6; Ecclesiastes 9:10; Isaiah 38:18-19
* **Associated Articles:** 1440: revelation, through creation (ID: `1440@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3248: Holy Spirit, conviction of (ID: `3248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5008: conscience (ID: `5008@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5026: knowledge (ID: `5026@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5029: knowledge, of God (ID: `5029@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5031: knowledge, of sin (ID: `5031@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5035: mind (ID: `5035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8134: knowing God (ID: `8134@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9020: death (ID: `9020@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5817: conspiracies (id: 5817)

Malicious plots or plans to harm or to kill, which may be directed against other people, or even against God. Conspiracies are sometimes motivated by political aspirations, but may be simply an outlet for wrongdoing.

Conspiracies to sin
-------------------

**[MIC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Mic7:3)** *See also* [PSA 52:2](https://ref.ly/Ps52:2), [PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6); [PRO 16:27](https://ref.ly/Prov16:27), [PRO 16:30](https://ref.ly/Prov16:30); [JER 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Jer11:9-Jer11:10); [EZK 22:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:25)

Conspiracies to murder
----------------------

**[JER 18:23](https://ref.ly/Jer18:23)** *See also* [GEN 37:17–37:23](https://ref.ly/Gen37:17-Gen37:23); [2SA 11:2–11:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:17); [2CH 24:20–24:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:20-2Chr24:21)

Conspiracies to commit genocide
-------------------------------

**[EST 9:24–9:25](https://ref.ly/Esth9:24-Esth9:25)** *See also* [2SA 21:1–21:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1-2Sam21:6); [EST 3:5–3:9](https://ref.ly/Esth3:5-Esth3:9)

Conspiracies to hinder the work of God
--------------------------------------

**[NEH 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Neh4:7-Neh4:8)** *See also* [NEH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Neh4:11), [NEH 4:15](https://ref.ly/Neh4:15)

Conspiracies against kings
--------------------------

### Conspiracies to overthrow the king

**[2KI 15:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:10)** *See also* [1KI 15:25–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:25-1Kgs15:28), [1KI 16:8–16:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:8-1Kgs16:10), [1KI 16:15–16:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:15-1Kgs16:18); [2KI 9:14–9:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:14-2Kgs9:24), [2KI 10:9–10:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:9-2Kgs10:11), [2KI 12:19–12:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:19-2Kgs12:21) parallel [2CH 24:25–24:26](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:25-2Chr24:26) *the assassination of Joash*; [2KI 14:17–14:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:17-2Kgs14:19) parallel [2CH 25:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:27) *the assassination of Amaziah*; [2KI 15:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:15), [2KI 15:23–15:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:23-2Kgs15:25), [2KI 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:30), [2KI 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:23-2Kgs21:24) parallel [2CH 33:24–33:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:24-2Chr33:25) *the assassination of Amon*; [EST 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/Esth2:21-Esth2:22), [EST 6:2](https://ref.ly/Esth6:2)

### Conspiracies against King David

**[2SA 15:10–15:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:10-2Sam15:12)** *See also* [1SA 23:7–23:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:7-1Sam23:13), [1SA 23:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:15), [1SA 23:19–23:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:19-1Sam23:23); [2SA 15:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:31); [1KI 1:5–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:5-1Kgs1:10), [1KI 2:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:28)

### False accusations of conspiracy against kings

**[AMO 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/Amos7:10-Amos7:11)** *See also* [1SA 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:6-1Sam22:8), [1SA 22:13–22:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:13-1Sam22:16); [NEH 6:5–6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:5-Neh6:9); [DAN 2:9](https://ref.ly/Dan2:9)

Conspiracies against cities and nations
---------------------------------------

**[JER 48:2](https://ref.ly/Jer48:2)** *See also* [ISA 7:5–7:6](https://ref.ly/Isa7:5-Isa7:6); [JER 49:30–49:33](https://ref.ly/Jer49:30-Jer49:33); [EZK 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:2-Ezek11:3); [DAN 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/Dan11:24-Dan11:25); [HAB 2:10](https://ref.ly/Hab2:10)

Conspiracies against God
------------------------

**[PSA 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1-Ps2:3)** *See also* [PSA 21:11](https://ref.ly/Ps21:11), [PSA 83:5–83:8](https://ref.ly/Ps83:5-Ps83:8); [HOS 7:11–7:16](https://ref.ly/Hos7:11-Hos7:16); [NAM 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/Nah1:9-Nah1:11)

Conspiracies against God's servants
-----------------------------------

**[2CH 24:20–24:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:20-2Chr24:21)** *See also* [PSA 31:13](https://ref.ly/Ps31:13), [PSA 35:4](https://ref.ly/Ps35:4), [PSA 36:4](https://ref.ly/Ps36:4), [PSA 37:12](https://ref.ly/Ps37:12), [PSA 38:12](https://ref.ly/Ps38:12), [PSA 52:2](https://ref.ly/Ps52:2), [PSA 56:5](https://ref.ly/Ps56:5), [PSA 59:3](https://ref.ly/Ps59:3), [PSA 64:2](https://ref.ly/Ps64:2), [PSA 71:10](https://ref.ly/Ps71:10), [PSA 83:3](https://ref.ly/Ps83:3), [PSA 105:24–105:25](https://ref.ly/Ps105:24-Ps105:25); [JER 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/Jer11:18-Jer11:19) *a plot against Jeremiah*; [JER 18:23](https://ref.ly/Jer18:23); [LAM 3:60–3:61](https://ref.ly/Lam3:60-Lam3:61)

Conspiracies against Jesus Christ
---------------------------------

**[MAT 12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14)** parallels [MRK 3:6](https://ref.ly/Mark3:6), [LUK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Luke6:11) *See also* [MAT 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:4) parallels [MRK 14:1](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1), [LUK 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Luke22:1-Luke22:2); [JHN 11:53](https://ref.ly/John11:53); [ACT 4:27](https://ref.ly/Acts4:27)

Conspiracies against Paul
-------------------------

**[ACT 23:12–23:16](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12-Acts23:16)** *See also* [ACT 9:20–9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:20-Acts9:25), [ACT 14:1–14:7](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1-Acts14:7), [ACT 18:12](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12), [ACT 20:3](https://ref.ly/Acts20:3), [ACT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Acts20:19), [ACT 23:30](https://ref.ly/Acts23:30)

----------------------

**[ISA 33:15–33:16](https://ref.ly/Isa33:15-Isa33:16)** *See also* [2SA 12:1–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:1-2Sam12:12); [PRO 3:29](https://ref.ly/Prov3:29), [PRO 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/Prov6:12-Prov6:15), [PRO 12:20](https://ref.ly/Prov12:20), [PRO 14:22](https://ref.ly/Prov14:22), [PRO 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Prov24:1-Prov24:2), [PRO 24:8](https://ref.ly/Prov24:8); [ISA 8:12](https://ref.ly/Isa8:12); [MIC 2:1](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1); [ZEC 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Zech8:16-Zech8:17)

## 5818: contempt (id: 5818)

An attitude of scorn and derision. Examples are given of the way in which a rebellious and fallen world treats its creator with contempt.

Contempt of some people for others
----------------------------------

### Eliab for David

[1SA 17:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:28)

### Nabal for David

[1SA 25:10–25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:10-1Sam25:11), [1SA 25:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:14)

### King Hanun for David's men

[2SA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4-2Sam10:5)

### Sanballat, Geshem and Tobiah for Nehemiah's men

[NEH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Neh2:19), [NEH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Neh4:3)

Contempt for parents
--------------------

[PRO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Prov15:5), [PRO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Prov15:20), [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22)

Contempt from the godless
-------------------------

**[JOB 16:10](https://ref.ly/Job16:10)**; **[PSA 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:18)** *See also* [JOB 19:18](https://ref.ly/Job19:18), [JOB 30:1](https://ref.ly/Job30:1); [PSA 35:21](https://ref.ly/Ps35:21), [PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10), [PSA 69:19–69:20](https://ref.ly/Ps69:19-Ps69:20), [PSA 119:22](https://ref.ly/Ps119:22); [ISA 28:9–28:10](https://ref.ly/Isa28:9-Isa28:10), [ISA 28:14](https://ref.ly/Isa28:14); [JER 20:7](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7)

God's contempt for the godless
------------------------------

**[PSA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Ps2:4)** *See also* [JOB 12:21](https://ref.ly/Job12:21); [PRO 1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:26); [NAM 3:6](https://ref.ly/Nah3:6)

God will bring contempt on his people
-------------------------------------

**[JER 18:16](https://ref.ly/Jer18:16)** *See also* [JER 25:9](https://ref.ly/Jer25:9), [JER 25:18](https://ref.ly/Jer25:18); [EZK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:4); [DAN 9:16](https://ref.ly/Dan9:16); [MIC 6:16](https://ref.ly/Mic6:16)

Human contempt for God
----------------------

### For God himself

**[NUM 14:11](https://ref.ly/Num14:11)** *See also* [NUM 14:23](https://ref.ly/Num14:23), [NUM 16:30](https://ref.ly/Num16:30); [PSA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1), [PSA 73:11](https://ref.ly/Ps73:11)

### For his law

**[PSA 78:10](https://ref.ly/Ps78:10)** *See also* [LEV 22:9](https://ref.ly/Lev22:9); [ISA 30:9](https://ref.ly/Isa30:9); [HOS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Hos4:6); [MRK 7:9](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9) parallel [MAT 15:3](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3); [HEB 10:28](https://ref.ly/Heb10:28)

### For his prophets

**[2CH 36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:16)** *See also* [AMO 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Amos7:12-Amos7:13); [MAT 5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:12)

### For his works

**[ZEC 4:10](https://ref.ly/Zech4:10)** *See also* [HAB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hab1:5); [ACT 13:41](https://ref.ly/Acts13:41)

### For his day

[NEH 13:15–13:21](https://ref.ly/Neh13:15-Neh13:21); ; [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

### For his worship and service

[MAL 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:8), [MAL 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Mal1:12-Mal1:13), [MAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Mal2:11), [MAL 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Mal3:8-Mal3:9), [MAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mal3:14)

### For his gospel

[MAT 7:6](https://ref.ly/Matt7:6)

### For his kindness

[ROM 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rom2:4)

### For his authority

[2PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10)

### For his patience

[2PE 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:3-2Pet3:4), [2PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:9)

Jesus Christ as the object of contempt
--------------------------------------

### Anticipated in prophecy

**[PSA 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6-Ps22:8)** *See also* [MAT 27:43](https://ref.ly/Matt27:43)**[PSA 69:19–69:21](https://ref.ly/Ps69:19-Ps69:21)** *See also* [ISA 53:3](https://ref.ly/Isa53:3)

### During his earthly ministry

**[MAT 13:54–13:57](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54-Matt13:57)** parallel [MRK 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Mark6:1-Mark6:4) *See also* [MAT 9:24](https://ref.ly/Matt9:24); [LUK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Luke16:14); [JHN 9:34](https://ref.ly/John9:34)

### While on the cross

**[MAT 27:39](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39)** parallel [MRK 15:29](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29) *See also* [MRK 15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:32); [LUK 23:39](https://ref.ly/Luke23:39)

Contempt will be punished
-------------------------

**[PRO 1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:26)** *See also* [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34), [PRO 19:29](https://ref.ly/Prov19:29), [PRO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Prov22:10); [EZK 25:3–25:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:3-Ezek25:7), [EZK 36:2–36:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:2-Ezek36:5)

Final punishment is contempt
----------------------------

[DAN 12:2](https://ref.ly/Dan12:2)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 17:28; 1 Samuel 25:10-11; 1 Samuel 25:14; 2 Samuel 10:4-5; Nehemiah 2:19; Nehemiah 4:3; Proverbs 15:5; Proverbs 15:20; Proverbs 23:22; Job 16:10; Psalms 31:18; Job 19:18; Job 30:1; Psalms 35:21; Psalms 42:10; Psalms 69:19-20; Psalms 119:22; Isaiah 28:9-10; Isaiah 28:14; Jeremiah 20:7; Psalms 2:4; Job 12:21; Proverbs 1:26; Nahum 3:6; Jeremiah 18:16; Jeremiah 25:9; Jeremiah 25:18; Ezekiel 22:4; Daniel 9:16; Micah 6:16; Numbers 14:11; Numbers 14:23; Numbers 16:30; Psalms 14:1; Psalms 73:11; Psalms 78:10; Leviticus 22:9; Isaiah 30:9; Hosea 4:6; Mark 7:9; Matthew 15:3; Hebrews 10:28; 2 Chronicles 36:16; Amos 7:12-13; Matthew 5:12; Zechariah 4:10; Habakkuk 1:5; Acts 13:41; Nehemiah 13:15-21; Amos 8:5; Malachi 1:6-8; Malachi 1:12-13; Malachi 2:11; Malachi 3:8-9; Malachi 3:14; Matthew 7:6; Romans 2:4; 2 Peter 2:10; 2 Peter 3:3-4; 2 Peter 3:9; Psalms 22:6-8; Matthew 27:43; Psalms 69:19-21; Isaiah 53:3; Matthew 13:54-57; Mark 6:1-4; Matthew 9:24; Luke 16:14; John 9:34; Matthew 27:39; Mark 15:29; Mark 15:32; Luke 23:39; Proverbs 3:34; Proverbs 19:29; Proverbs 22:10; Ezekiel 25:3-7; Ezekiel 36:2-5; Daniel 12:2
