## 5819: cowardice (id: 5819)

An unwillingness to act in a situation of danger or opposition, because of fear. Avoiding the source of one's anxiety or fear, rather than dealing with it directly.

Cowardice prevents people from acknowledging Jesus Christ publicly
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 12:42–12:43](https://ref.ly/John12:42-John12:43)** *See also* [JHN 7:13](https://ref.ly/John7:13), [JHN 9:22–9:23](https://ref.ly/John9:22-John9:23) *parents of the man born blind*; [JHN 19:38](https://ref.ly/John19:38), [JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/John20:19)

### Cowardice inhibits bad as well as good behaviour

[MAT 14:5](https://ref.ly/Matt14:5), [MAT 21:26](https://ref.ly/Matt21:26), [MAT 21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:46)

### Cowardice leads to avoidance of the difficulty

**[MAT 26:56](https://ref.ly/Matt26:56)** *See also* [1SA 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:6-1Sam13:7) *the Philistine threat*; [1SA 17:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:24) *Goliath*; [1KI 19:2–19:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:2-1Kgs19:3); [JON 1:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:10); [MAT 26:69–26:74](https://ref.ly/Matt26:69-Matt26:74) parallels [MRK 14:66–14:71](https://ref.ly/Mark14:66-Mark14:71), [LUK 22:57–22:60](https://ref.ly/Luke22:57-Luke22:60), [JHN 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/John18:15-John18:18), [JHN 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:25-John18:27); [LUK 8:37](https://ref.ly/Luke8:37) *after the destruction of the pigs*; [GAL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Gal2:12) *Peter*

### Cowardice can spread within a group

**[DEU 20:8](https://ref.ly/Deut20:8)** [NUM 13:30–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:30-Num13:33); ; [JOS 14:8](https://ref.ly/Josh14:8); [JDG 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/Judg7:2-Judg7:3); ; [JER 38:4](https://ref.ly/Jer38:4)

God can work through cowardice
------------------------------

### In humbling disobedient people

[LEV 26:36–26:39](https://ref.ly/Lev26:36-Lev26:39); ; [ISA 30:15–30:17](https://ref.ly/Isa30:15-Isa30:17)

### In routing the enemy

Rahab and the Israelite spies: [JOS 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Josh2:8-Josh2:11), [JOS 2:24](https://ref.ly/Josh2:24); [1SA 14:15–14:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:15-1Sam14:16) *the Philistines*

God gives hope to the cowardly or fearful
-----------------------------------------

**[PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25)** *See also* [DEU 1:27–1:31](https://ref.ly/Deut1:27-Deut1:31); [JDG 7:10–7:11](https://ref.ly/Judg7:10-Judg7:11); [PSA 56:3–56:4](https://ref.ly/Ps56:3-Ps56:4); [ISA 51:12–51:15](https://ref.ly/Isa51:12-Isa51:15); [LUK 21:12–21:19](https://ref.ly/Luke21:12-Luke21:19)ACT [4:5–4:10](https://ref.ly/Luke4:5-Luke4:10), [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13), [ACT 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29-Acts4:31)

Judgment on cowards
-------------------

**[REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8)** *Where cowardice meant rejecting the gospel and denying Jesus Christ.*

* **Associated Passages:** John 12:42-43; John 7:13; John 9:22-23; John 19:38; John 20:19; Matthew 14:5; Matthew 21:26; Matthew 21:46; Matthew 26:56; 1 Samuel 13:6-7; 1 Samuel 17:24; 1 Kings 19:2-3; Jonah 1:10; Matthew 26:69-74; Mark 14:66-71; Luke 22:57-60; John 18:15-18; John 18:25-27; Luke 8:37; Galatians 2:12; Deuteronomy 20:8; Numbers 13:30-33; Joshua 14:8; Judges 7:2-3; Jeremiah 38:4; Leviticus 26:36-39; Isaiah 30:15-17; Joshua 2:8-11; Joshua 2:24; 1 Samuel 14:15-16; Proverbs 29:25; Deuteronomy 1:27-31; Judges 7:10-11; Psalms 56:3-4; Isaiah 51:12-15; Luke 21:12-19; Luke 4:5-10; Acts 4:13; Acts 4:29-31; Revelation 21:8
* **Associated Articles:** 5798: betrayal (ID: `5798@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8202: boldness (ID: `8202@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8219: courage (ID: `8219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (ID: `8712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8839: unfaithfulness (ID: `8839@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5820: criticism (id: 5820)

The judgment or evaluation of others. The word often has negative associations, implying a harsh or disapproving estimate of people or their actions.

* **Associated Articles:** 5821: criticism, amongst believers (ID: `5821@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5822: criticism, against believers (ID: `5822@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5821: criticism, amongst believers (id: 5821)

Harsh criticism is out of place among believers, who are urged to see each other in the best possible light. Criticism of God arises from a failure to understand his actions and purposes.

Criticism forbidden among believers
-----------------------------------

**[JAS 5:9](https://ref.ly/Jas5:9)** *See also* [MAT 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:1-Matt7:5) parallel [LUK 6:37–6:42](https://ref.ly/Luke6:37-Luke6:42); [ROM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Rom14:1-Rom14:4), [ROM 14:10–14:13](https://ref.ly/Rom14:10-Rom14:13); [1CO 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:10); [GAL 5:15](https://ref.ly/Gal5:15); [PHP 2:14–2:16](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14-Phil2:16); [JAS 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11-Jas4:12)

Reacting to criticism
---------------------

### Some criticism can be disregarded

**[ECC 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:21-Eccl7:22)** *See also* [1CO 4:2–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:2-1Cor4:5); [COL 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:16-Col2:18)

### Criticism can be dealt with by settling the dispute

**[MAT 18:15](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15)** *See also* [PRO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Prov15:1)

Integrity is a defence against criticism
----------------------------------------

**[DAN 6:4](https://ref.ly/Dan6:4)** *See also* [2CO 8:20–8:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:20-2Cor8:21); [TIT 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Titus2:7-Titus2:8)

Criticism can be constructive
-----------------------------

**[PSA 141:5](https://ref.ly/Ps141:5)** *See also* [PRO 10:17](https://ref.ly/Prov10:17), [PRO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Prov15:5), [PRO 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6), [PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9), [PRO 28:23](https://ref.ly/Prov28:23)

Criticism and complaint directed against God
--------------------------------------------

### Criticism of God for his apparent injustice

**[DEU 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Deut1:26-Deut1:27)** *See also* [JER 20:7–20:9](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7-Jer20:9); [JON 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:1-Jonah4:4); [HAB 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:1-Hab1:4), [HAB 1:12–1:17](https://ref.ly/Hab1:12-Hab1:17); [MAL 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Mal3:13-Mal3:15); [ROM 9:19–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rom9:19-Rom9:21); [ISA 29:16](https://ref.ly/Isa29:16), [ISA 45:9](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9)

### Criticism of God for his apparent disregard of his people's plight

**[ISA 40:27](https://ref.ly/Isa40:27)** *See also* [PSA 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ps10:1), [PSA 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Ps13:1-Ps13:2), [PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2), [PSA 42:9–42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:9-Ps42:10), [PSA 44:23–44:24](https://ref.ly/Ps44:23-Ps44:24), [PSA 74:10–74:11](https://ref.ly/Ps74:10-Ps74:11), [PSA 80:4–80:6](https://ref.ly/Ps80:4-Ps80:6), [PSA 108:11–108:12](https://ref.ly/Ps108:11-Ps108:12)

* **Associated Passages:** James 5:9; Matthew 7:1-5; Luke 6:37-42; Romans 14:1-4; Romans 14:10-13; 1 Corinthians 10:10; Galatians 5:15; Philippians 2:14-16; James 4:11-12; Ecclesiastes 7:21-22; 1 Corinthians 4:2-5; Colossians 2:16-18; Matthew 18:15; Proverbs 15:1; Daniel 6:4; 2 Corinthians 8:20-21; Titus 2:7-8; Psalms 141:5; Proverbs 10:17; Proverbs 15:5; Proverbs 27:6; Proverbs 27:9; Proverbs 28:23; Deuteronomy 1:26-27; Jeremiah 20:7-9; Jonah 4:1-4; Habakkuk 1:1-4; Habakkuk 1:12-17; Malachi 3:13-15; Romans 9:19-21; Isaiah 29:16; Isaiah 45:9; Isaiah 40:27; Psalms 10:1; Psalms 13:1-2; Psalms 22:1-2; Psalms 42:9-10; Psalms 44:23-24; Psalms 74:10-11; Psalms 80:4-6; Psalms 108:11-12
* **Associated Articles:** 5265: complaints (ID: `5265@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5897: judging others (ID: `5897@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6115: blame (ID: `6115@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5822: criticism, against believers (id: 5822)

Many notable servants of God were subject to criticism, which was sometimes vicious and severe. This was particularly true of Jesus Christ, who was constantly subject to complaint and criticism.

Examples of criticism directed against believers
------------------------------------------------

### The patriarchs criticised

**[GEN 31:25–31:30](https://ref.ly/Gen31:25-Gen31:30)** *See also* [GEN 12:17–12:20](https://ref.ly/Gen12:17-Gen12:20), [GEN 16:5](https://ref.ly/Gen16:5), [GEN 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/Gen20:9-Gen20:10), [GEN 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1-Gen30:2)

### Moses criticised

**[EXO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:3)** *See also* [EXO 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Exod2:11-Exod2:14), [EXO 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Exod5:19-Exod5:21), [EXO 14:11–14:12](https://ref.ly/Exod14:11-Exod14:12), [EXO 15:24](https://ref.ly/Exod15:24), [EXO 16:2](https://ref.ly/Exod16:2); [NUM 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Num12:1-Num12:2), [NUM 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Num16:1-Num16:3), [NUM 16:12–16:14](https://ref.ly/Num16:12-Num16:14), [NUM 16:41](https://ref.ly/Num16:41)

### David criticised

**[1SA 17:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:28)** *See also* [2SA 6:16–6:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:16-2Sam6:23), [2SA 15:1–15:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:4)

### Job criticised

**[JOB 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Job8:1-Job8:2)** *See also* [JOB 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/Job11:1-Job11:6), [JOB 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/Job15:1-Job15:6)

### The prophets criticised

**[JER 20:8](https://ref.ly/Jer20:8)** *See also* [1KI 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:17), [1KI 22:24–22:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:24-1Kgs22:27); [AMO 7:10–7:12](https://ref.ly/Amos7:10-Amos7:12)

Criticism directed against Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ criticised by his family

**[MRK 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Mark3:20-Mark3:21)** *See also* [LUK 2:48](https://ref.ly/Luke2:48); [JHN 7:2–7:5](https://ref.ly/John7:2-John7:5)

### Jesus Christ criticised by his disciples

**[MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38)** *See also* [MAT 16:21–16:23](https://ref.ly/Matt16:21-Matt16:23) parallel [MRK 8:31–8:33](https://ref.ly/Mark8:31-Mark8:33); [LUK 10:40](https://ref.ly/Luke10:40); [JHN 6:60–6:61](https://ref.ly/John6:60-John6:61), [JHN 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/John13:6-John13:9)

### Jesus Christ criticised by the Pharisees

**[MAT 9:10–9:11](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10-Matt9:11)** parallels [MRK 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15-Mark2:16), [LUK 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29-Luke5:30) *See also* [MAT 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:1-Matt12:2) parallels [MRK 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Mark2:23-Mark2:24), [LUK 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Luke6:1-Luke6:2); [LUK 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:2)

### Jesus Christ criticised for his teaching

**[JHN 6:41–6:42](https://ref.ly/John6:41-John6:42)** *See also* [MAT 9:3](https://ref.ly/Matt9:3) parallels [MRK 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Mark2:6-Mark2:7), [LUK 5:21](https://ref.ly/Luke5:21); [JHN 8:52–8:53](https://ref.ly/John8:52-John8:53)

Paul criticised
---------------

**[ACT 21:27–21:28](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:28)** *See also* [ACT 18:12–18:13](https://ref.ly/Acts18:12-Acts18:13); [2CO 10:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 31:25-30; Genesis 12:17-20; Genesis 16:5; Genesis 20:9-10; Genesis 30:1-2; Exodus 17:3; Exodus 2:11-14; Exodus 5:19-21; Exodus 14:11-12; Exodus 15:24; Exodus 16:2; Numbers 12:1-2; Numbers 16:1-3; Numbers 16:12-14; Numbers 16:41; 1 Samuel 17:28; 2 Samuel 6:16-23; 2 Samuel 15:1-4; Job 8:1-2; Job 11:1-6; Job 15:1-6; Jeremiah 20:8; 1 Kings 18:17; 1 Kings 22:24-27; Amos 7:10-12; Mark 3:20-21; Luke 2:48; John 7:2-5; Mark 4:38; Matthew 16:21-23; Mark 8:31-33; Luke 10:40; John 6:60-61; John 13:6-9; Matthew 9:10-11; Mark 2:15-16; Luke 5:29-30; Matthew 12:1-2; Mark 2:23-24; Luke 6:1-2; Luke 15:1-2; John 6:41-42; Matthew 9:3; Mark 2:6-7; Luke 5:21; John 8:52-53; Acts 21:27-28; Acts 18:12-13; 2 Corinthians 10:10
* **Associated Articles:** 2545: Jesus Christ, opposition to (ID: `2545@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5823: cruelty (id: 5823)

Behaviour in which pain or suffering is deliberately inflicted on others, especially the weak and defenceless.

* **Associated Articles:** 5824: cruelty, examples of (ID: `5824@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5825: cruelty, God's attitude to (ID: `5825@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5824: cruelty, examples of (id: 5824)

Scripture condemns cruelty, and gives examples of situations in which it was practised.

Cruelty is an expression of wickedness
--------------------------------------

**[PSA 71:4](https://ref.ly/Ps71:4)**; **[ROM 1:28–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28-Rom1:31)** *See also* [PSA 35:16](https://ref.ly/Ps35:16), [PSA 86:14](https://ref.ly/Ps86:14); [PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5); [ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1); [AMO 5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:12)

Forms of cruelty
----------------

### The cruel nature of war

**[2KI 8:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:12)** *See also* [2KI 15:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:16); [ISA 13:15](https://ref.ly/Isa13:15), [ISA 13:18](https://ref.ly/Isa13:18); [HOS 10:14](https://ref.ly/Hos10:14), [HOS 13:16](https://ref.ly/Hos13:16); [NAM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Nah3:10)

### The cruel nature of rape

**[GEN 34:2](https://ref.ly/Gen34:2)** *See also* [JDG 19:25](https://ref.ly/Judg19:25); [2SA 13:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:14); [ZEC 14:2](https://ref.ly/Zech14:2)

### The cruel nature of words

**[JOB 17:1–17:2](https://ref.ly/Job17:1-Job17:2)**; **[PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10)**; **[MRK 15:31](https://ref.ly/Mark15:31)** *See also* [1SA 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:6-1Sam1:7); [JOB 11:3](https://ref.ly/Job11:3); [PSA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7), [PSA 35:11](https://ref.ly/Ps35:11), [PSA 89:50–89:51](https://ref.ly/Ps89:50-Ps89:51), [PSA 102:8](https://ref.ly/Ps102:8); [JER 20:7](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7); [LAM 3:14](https://ref.ly/Lam3:14)

Particular examples of cruelty
------------------------------

[GEN 37:23–37:24](https://ref.ly/Gen37:23-Gen37:24) *Joseph's brothers*; ; [GEN 49:5–49:7](https://ref.ly/Gen49:5-Gen49:7) *Simeon and Levi*; ; [EXO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:22) *Pharaoh*; ; [JDG 1:6](https://ref.ly/Judg1:6) *Judah's cruelty to a local king*; ; [JDG 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Judg4:2-Judg4:3) *Sisera*; ; [JDG 10:6–10:8](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6-Judg10:8) *the Philistines and Ammonites*; ; [2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10) *Asa*; ; [PRO 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Prov5:8-Prov5:9) *the adulteress*; [JER 21:7](https://ref.ly/Jer21:7); ; [DAN 3:13–3:20](https://ref.ly/Dan3:13-Dan3:20); [JER 37:15](https://ref.ly/Jer37:15), [JER 38:6–38:7](https://ref.ly/Jer38:6-Jer38:7); [EZK 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29) *Israel*; ; [NAM 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:18-Nah3:19) *the king of Assyria*; ; [MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16) *King Herod*; [MAT 27:26–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26-Matt27:31) parallels [MRK 15:15–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15-Mark15:20), [JHN 19:15](https://ref.ly/John19:15); [LUK 23:33–23:39](https://ref.ly/Luke23:33-Luke23:39) parallels [MAT 27:35–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:35-Matt27:44), [MRK 15:24–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:24-Mark15:32), [JHN 19:18](https://ref.ly/John19:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 71:4; Romans 1:28-31; Psalms 35:16; Psalms 86:14; Proverbs 17:5; Ecclesiastes 4:1; Amos 5:12; 2 Kings 8:12; 2 Kings 15:16; Isaiah 13:15; Isaiah 13:18; Hosea 10:14; Hosea 13:16; Nahum 3:10; Genesis 34:2; Judges 19:25; 2 Samuel 13:14; Zechariah 14:2; Job 17:1-2; Psalms 42:10; Mark 15:31; 1 Samuel 1:6-7; Job 11:3; Psalms 22:7; Psalms 35:11; Psalms 89:50-51; Psalms 102:8; Jeremiah 20:7; Lamentations 3:14; Genesis 37:23-24; Genesis 49:5-7; Exodus 1:22; Judges 1:6; Judges 4:2-3; Judges 10:6-8; 2 Chronicles 16:10; Proverbs 5:8-9; Jeremiah 21:7; Daniel 3:13-20; Jeremiah 37:15; Jeremiah 38:6-7; Ezekiel 22:29; Nahum 3:18-19; Matthew 2:16; Matthew 27:26-31; Mark 15:15-20; John 19:15; Luke 23:33-39; Matthew 27:35-44; Mark 15:24-32; John 19:18

## 5825: cruelty, God's attitude to (id: 5825)

God condemns cruelty. As part of the work of redemption, God suffers cruelty from the world in order to redeem believers.

God reproves cruelty
--------------------

**[DEU 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Deut23:15-Deut23:16)**; **[PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31)**; **[ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2)**; **[EZK 45:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek45:9)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21), [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9); [LEV 25:42–25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:42-Lev25:43), [LEV 25:46](https://ref.ly/Lev25:46), [LEV 25:53](https://ref.ly/Lev25:53); [PRO 12:10](https://ref.ly/Prov12:10); [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11); [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)

God allows cruelty as part of his judgment
------------------------------------------

**[ISA 13:9](https://ref.ly/Isa13:9)**; **[JER 30:14](https://ref.ly/Jer30:14)**; **[LAM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Lam2:17)** *See also* [DEU 28:15–28:42](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:42) *The LORD promises cruel judgment on those who disobey his laws*; [ISA 19:4](https://ref.ly/Isa19:4); [JER 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Jer6:22-Jer6:23), [JER 13:14](https://ref.ly/Jer13:14), [JER 20:16](https://ref.ly/Jer20:16); [LAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lam2:2), [LAM 2:21](https://ref.ly/Lam2:21); [HAB 1:5–2:1](https://ref.ly/Hab1:5-Hab2:1)

God delivers from cruelty
-------------------------

**[EXO 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:9)**; **[NEH 9:27](https://ref.ly/Neh9:27)**; **[PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5)** *See also* [JDG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Judg2:18), [JDG 6:9](https://ref.ly/Judg6:9); [1SA 10:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:18); [2SA 7:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:10); [PSA 72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:4), [PSA 72:14](https://ref.ly/Ps72:14); [ISA 9:4](https://ref.ly/Isa9:4), [ISA 25:4–25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4-Isa25:5), [ISA 29:20](https://ref.ly/Isa29:20)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 23:15-16; Proverbs 14:31; Isaiah 10:1-2; Ezekiel 45:9; Exodus 22:21; Exodus 23:9; Leviticus 25:42-43; Leviticus 25:46; Leviticus 25:53; Proverbs 12:10; Isaiah 13:11; Jeremiah 22:3; Zechariah 7:10; Isaiah 13:9; Jeremiah 30:14; Lamentations 2:17; Deuteronomy 28:15-42; Isaiah 19:4; Jeremiah 6:22-23; Jeremiah 13:14; Jeremiah 20:16; Lamentations 2:2; Lamentations 2:21; Habakkuk 1:5-2:1; Exodus 3:7-9; Nehemiah 9:27; Psalms 12:5; Judges 2:18; Judges 6:9; 1 Samuel 10:18; 2 Samuel 7:10; Psalms 72:4; Psalms 72:14; Isaiah 9:4; Isaiah 25:4-5; Isaiah 29:20

## 5827: curse (id: 5827)

An expression of contempt or malediction for someone. To be cursed is to suffer various kinds of misfortune, sometimes to the extent of being cut off from one's family or community, or of suffering death itself.

Divine curses
-------------

### Divine curses came into effect after the fall

**[GEN 3:14–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:14-Gen3:19)** *See also* [GEN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Gen5:29), [GEN 8:21](https://ref.ly/Gen8:21) *This curse refers to the flood; the curse of [Ge 3:17](https://ref.ly/Gen3:17) is not revoked here.*

### Divine curses come from disobeying the law

**[DEU 28:15–28:19](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:19)** *See also* [LEV 26:14–26:39](https://ref.ly/Lev26:14-Lev26:39); [DEU 11:26–11:28](https://ref.ly/Deut11:26-Deut11:28), [DEU 27:9–27:26](https://ref.ly/Deut27:9-Deut27:26), [DEU 28:20–28:68](https://ref.ly/Deut28:20-Deut28:68), [DEU 29:18–29:21](https://ref.ly/Deut29:18-Deut29:21); [JER 11:2–11:4](https://ref.ly/Jer11:2-Jer11:4), [JER 11:8](https://ref.ly/Jer11:8); [DAN 9:11–9:14](https://ref.ly/Dan9:11-Dan9:14); [GAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Gal3:10)

Human curses
------------

### Human curses upon other human beings

**[2KI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23-2Kgs2:24)** *See also* [GEN 9:24–9:25](https://ref.ly/Gen9:24-Gen9:25); [JOS 9:22–9:23](https://ref.ly/Josh9:22-Josh9:23); [1SA 14:24–14:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:24-1Sam14:28); [NEH 13:25](https://ref.ly/Neh13:25)

### Human curses upon God

**[JOB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Job2:9)**; **[REV 16:9](https://ref.ly/Rev16:9)** *See also* [ISA 52:5](https://ref.ly/Isa52:5); [EZK 20:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:27)

### Human beings are forbidden to curse God and others

**[LEV 24:15–24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:15-Lev24:16)** [EXO 22:28](https://ref.ly/Exod22:28); ; [1KI 21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:13); [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17); ; [MAT 15:4](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4) parallel [MRK 7:10](https://ref.ly/Mark7:10); ; [LEV 20:9](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9); [LEV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Lev19:14) *cursing the deaf*

People can curse the day they were born
---------------------------------------

**[JER 20:14](https://ref.ly/Jer20:14)** *See also* [JOB 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Job3:1-Job3:10)

Paul wishes himself cursed for the sake of Israel
-------------------------------------------------

[ROM 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/Rom9:3-Rom9:4)

Jesus Christ took the curse of the law upon himself
---------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 3:13–3:14](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13-Gal3:14)** *See also* [DEU 21:22–21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:22-Deut21:23)

Christians are exhorted to bless those who curse them
-----------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 6:27–6:28](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27-Luke6:28)** *See also* [MAT 5:39–5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39-Matt5:42) parallel [LUK 6:29–6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:29-Luke6:30); [ROM 12:14](https://ref.ly/Rom12:14); [1CO 4:12–4:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:12-1Cor4:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 3:14-19; Genesis 5:29; Genesis 8:21; Genesis 3:17; Deuteronomy 28:15-19; Leviticus 26:14-39; Deuteronomy 11:26-28; Deuteronomy 27:9-26; Deuteronomy 28:20-68; Deuteronomy 29:18-21; Jeremiah 11:2-4; Jeremiah 11:8; Daniel 9:11-14; Galatians 3:10; 2 Kings 2:23-24; Genesis 9:24-25; Joshua 9:22-23; 1 Samuel 14:24-28; Nehemiah 13:25; Job 2:9; Revelation 16:9; Isaiah 52:5; Ezekiel 20:27; Leviticus 24:15-16; Exodus 22:28; 1 Kings 21:13; Exodus 21:17; Matthew 15:4; Mark 7:10; Leviticus 20:9; Leviticus 19:14; Jeremiah 20:14; Job 3:1-10; Romans 9:3-4; Galatians 3:13-14; Deuteronomy 21:22-23; Luke 6:27-28; Matthew 5:39-42; Luke 6:29-30; Romans 12:14; 1 Corinthians 4:12-13

## 5828: danger (id: 5828)

A condition of threat, physical or spiritual, in which evil, malice, accident or destruction looms.

Physical danger
---------------

### In nature

**[LUK 8:23](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23)** parallels [MAT 8:24](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24), [MRK 4:37](https://ref.ly/Mark4:37) *See also* [1KI 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:11-1Kgs19:12); [PSA 83:14–83:15](https://ref.ly/Ps83:14-Ps83:15), [PSA 107:23–107:28](https://ref.ly/Ps107:23-Ps107:28); [ACT 16:26–16:30](https://ref.ly/Acts16:26-Acts16:30); [2CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:26); [REV 11:13](https://ref.ly/Rev11:13)

### From human violence

**[PSA 140:1](https://ref.ly/Ps140:1)** *See also* [GEN 27:41–27:43](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41-Gen27:43); [EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15); [1SA 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:1-1Sam19:2); [2SA 22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:3); [1KI 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:3); [NEH 4:7–4:9](https://ref.ly/Neh4:7-Neh4:9); [PSA 7:9](https://ref.ly/Ps7:9), [PSA 64:1](https://ref.ly/Ps64:1), [PSA 74:20](https://ref.ly/Ps74:20); [ECC 12:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:5); [LUK 4:28–4:29](https://ref.ly/Luke4:28-Luke4:29); [ACT 21:35](https://ref.ly/Acts21:35)

### Through foolishness

**[PRO 22:3](https://ref.ly/Prov22:3)** *See also* [PRO 27:12](https://ref.ly/Prov27:12); [ECC 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:13-Eccl2:14)

Spiritual danger
----------------

### From the devil

**[1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)** *See also* [GEN 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1-Gen3:5); [JOB 1:6–1:12](https://ref.ly/Job1:6-Job1:12), [JOB 2:1–2:6](https://ref.ly/Job2:1-Job2:6); [MAT 4:1–4:11](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:11) parallel [LUK 4:2–4:13](https://ref.ly/Luke4:2-Luke4:13); [JHN 13:2](https://ref.ly/John13:2); [HEB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14); [1JN 3:8](https://ref.ly/1John3:8); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10), [REV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:10)

### Danger of hell through disobedience

**[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)** *See also* [LUK 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/Luke12:4-Luke12:5); [1CO 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:4-1Cor5:5); [HEB 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:6); [2PE 2:4–2:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:4-2Pet2:10)

### Danger of loss through unfaithfulness

**[1CO 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:13-1Cor3:15)** *See also* [ROM 6:21](https://ref.ly/Rom6:21), [ROM 6:21](https://ref.ly/Rom6:21); [1CO 9:26–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:26-1Cor9:27); [1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)

### Danger does not overcome the faithful

**[ROM 8:35](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35)** *See also* [2TI 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:10-2Tim3:11)

Examples of those endangered
----------------------------

### Israel by her enemies

[EXO 13:17–13:22](https://ref.ly/Exod13:17-Exod13:22); ; [EST 3:1–3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:1-Esth3:6)

### Joseph, through betrayal

[GEN 37:18–37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:18-Gen37:28)

### Prophets, because of persecution

[JER 38:1–38:11](https://ref.ly/Jer38:1-Jer38:11)

### Jonah, through disobedience

[JON 1:4–1:17](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:4-Jonah1:17)

### Jesus Christ, through betrayal

[MAT 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:4), [MAT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:16)

### Paul, through shipwreck

[ACT 27:13–27:20](https://ref.ly/Acts27:13-Acts27:20), [ACT 27:42–27:44](https://ref.ly/Acts27:42-Acts27:44)

## 5829: defiance (id: 5829)

Open refusal to obey any authority. Defiance is seen in the Bible as evil when authority is legitimate but good when authority is ungodly.

Defiance of God is condemned
----------------------------

**[JER 50:29](https://ref.ly/Jer50:29)** *See also* [1KI 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:21-1Kgs13:22), [1KI 13:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:26); [PSA 139:19–139:22](https://ref.ly/Ps139:19-Ps139:22); [ISA 3:8](https://ref.ly/Isa3:8), [ISA 65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:7); [JER 48:26](https://ref.ly/Jer48:26), [JER 48:42](https://ref.ly/Jer48:42)

### To defy God's people is to defy God

[1SA 17:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:10), [1SA 17:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:23), [1SA 17:25–17:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:25-1Sam17:26), [1SA 17:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:36), [1SA 17:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45)

Defiance of legitimate authority
--------------------------------

**[ROM 13:2](https://ref.ly/Rom13:2)** *See also* [NUM 15:30](https://ref.ly/Num15:30); [1PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13)

Defiance of ungodly authority
-----------------------------

**[DAN 3:28](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28)** *See also* [DAN 6:13](https://ref.ly/Dan6:13); [ACT 4:19](https://ref.ly/Acts4:19), [ACT 5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:29), [ACT 17:7](https://ref.ly/Acts17:7); [REV 20:4](https://ref.ly/Rev20:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 50:29; 1 Kings 13:21-22; 1 Kings 13:26; Psalms 139:19-22; Isaiah 3:8; Isaiah 65:7; Jeremiah 48:26; Jeremiah 48:42; 1 Samuel 17:10; 1 Samuel 17:23; 1 Samuel 17:25-26; 1 Samuel 17:36; 1 Samuel 17:45; Romans 13:2; Numbers 15:30; 1 Peter 2:13; Daniel 3:28; Daniel 6:13; Acts 4:19; Acts 5:29; Acts 17:7; Revelation 20:4
* **Associated Articles:** 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5257: civil authorities (ID: `5257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5326: government (ID: `5326@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5457: power, human (ID: `5457@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6193: impenitence (ID: `6193@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6221: rebellion (ID: `6221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8718: disobedience (ID: `8718@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5830: delight (id: 5830)

A sense of joy and pleasure, experienced especially through achievements or relationships.

God as the source of delight
----------------------------

### Delight in the person of God

**[ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10)** *See also* [NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11); [JOB 22:26](https://ref.ly/Job22:26); [PSA 22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:8), [PSA 37:4](https://ref.ly/Ps37:4), [PSA 43:3–43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:3-Ps43:4); [ISA 58:13–58:14](https://ref.ly/Isa58:13-Isa58:14)

### Delight in the word of God

**[PSA 119:16](https://ref.ly/Ps119:16)**; **[ROM 7:22](https://ref.ly/Rom7:22)** *See also* [PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2), [PSA 19:7–19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7-Ps19:14), [PSA 119:24](https://ref.ly/Ps119:24), [PSA 119:47](https://ref.ly/Ps119:47); [JER 15:16](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16)

### Delight in the works of God

[1SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1); ; [PSA 35:4–35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:4-Ps35:10); ; [ACT 11:23](https://ref.ly/Acts11:23)

God expresses delight
---------------------

### Over his world

**[PSA 104:31](https://ref.ly/Ps104:31)** *See also* [GEN 1:31](https://ref.ly/Gen1:31); [PRO 8:30–8:31](https://ref.ly/Prov8:30-Prov8:31)

### Over his people

**[DEU 30:9–30:10](https://ref.ly/Deut30:9-Deut30:10)** *See also* [2SA 22:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:20); [PSA 147:10–147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10-Ps147:11), [PSA 149:4](https://ref.ly/Ps149:4); [ISA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Isa62:4), [ISA 65:18–65:19](https://ref.ly/Isa65:18-Isa65:19); [JER 31:20](https://ref.ly/Jer31:20), [JER 32:41](https://ref.ly/Jer32:41); [MIC 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mic7:18)

### Over justice

**[PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1)** *See also* [PRO 11:20](https://ref.ly/Prov11:20), [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22); [JER 9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:24)

### Over his Messiah

**[MAT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17)** parallels [MRK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mark1:11), [LUK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Luke3:22) *See also* [ISA 42:1](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1); [MAT 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Matt12:18-Matt12:21), [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5) parallels [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7), [LUK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Luke9:35); [2PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:17)

### God's delight is absent when something is viewed unfavourably

**[1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22)** *See also* [LEV 26:31](https://ref.ly/Lev26:31); [PSA 51:16](https://ref.ly/Ps51:16), [PSA 147:10](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10)

Natural delight in parenthood
-----------------------------

**[PRO 3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:12)** *See also* [PRO 23:24](https://ref.ly/Prov23:24), [PRO 29:17](https://ref.ly/Prov29:17); [MIC 1:16](https://ref.ly/Mic1:16)

Sexual delight
--------------

**[SNG 1:4](https://ref.ly/Song1:4)** *See also* [GEN 34:19](https://ref.ly/Gen34:19); [PRO 5:18](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18); [SNG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Song2:3), [SNG 4:1](https://ref.ly/Song4:1), [SNG 7:10](https://ref.ly/Song7:10)

Delight in the created order
----------------------------

### In God's gifts

**[DEU 26:11](https://ref.ly/Deut26:11)** *See also* [ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2); [JER 31:12](https://ref.ly/Jer31:12); [JOL 2:23](https://ref.ly/Joel2:23)

### In one's work

**[ECC 2:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:10)** *See also* [DEU 12:7](https://ref.ly/Deut12:7); [ECC 3:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:22), [ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19), [ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15)

### In the achievements of others

**[COL 2:5](https://ref.ly/Col2:5)** *See also* [EXO 18:9](https://ref.ly/Exod18:9); [EST 6:9](https://ref.ly/Esth6:9); [PRO 14:35](https://ref.ly/Prov14:35); [1TH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:13-1Thess2:14)

The delight of believers in their relationship with God
-------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 9:2](https://ref.ly/Ps9:2)** *See also* [1SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1); [NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11); [JOB 22:26](https://ref.ly/Job22:26); [PSA 9:14](https://ref.ly/Ps9:14), [PSA 13:5](https://ref.ly/Ps13:5), [PSA 31:7](https://ref.ly/Ps31:7), [PSA 35:9](https://ref.ly/Ps35:9), [PSA 37:4](https://ref.ly/Ps37:4), [PSA 89:16](https://ref.ly/Ps89:16), [PSA 149:2](https://ref.ly/Ps149:2); [LUK 1:46–1:47](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:47)

Delight in wrongdoing
---------------------

**[PSA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Ps62:4)**; **[1CO 13:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:6)** *See also* [PSA 68:30](https://ref.ly/Ps68:30); [PRO 2:12–2:15](https://ref.ly/Prov2:12-Prov2:15), [PRO 18:2](https://ref.ly/Prov18:2); [ISA 66:3](https://ref.ly/Isa66:3); [COL 2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:18); [2TH 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:12)

## 5831: depression (id: 5831)

A deep sense of despondency, discouragement and sadness, often linked with a sense of personal powerlessness and a loss of meaning in and enthusiasm for life. Many biblical characters show evidence of such behaviour which originates in a number of different ways.

Some causes of depression
-------------------------

### External circumstances

**[2CO 1:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:8)** *See also* [1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4); [JOB 9:23](https://ref.ly/Job9:23); [PSA 42:1–42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:1-Ps42:11), [PSA 88:1–88:18](https://ref.ly/Ps88:1-Ps88:18); [JOL 1:11](https://ref.ly/Joel1:11)

### Physical illness or exhaustion

**[1KI 19:5–19:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:5-1Kgs19:8)** *See also* [GEN 21:15–21:16](https://ref.ly/Gen21:15-Gen21:16); [PSA 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Ps6:1-Ps6:7), [PSA 22:14–22:17](https://ref.ly/Ps22:14-Ps22:17), [PSA 31:9–31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9-Ps31:12); [ISA 38:1–38:2](https://ref.ly/Isa38:1-Isa38:2); [JON 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:5)

### Fear of others

**[1KI 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:3)**

### Fear of failure

**[1KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:4)** *See also* [EXO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Exod4:1); [NUM 11:11–11:15](https://ref.ly/Num11:11-Num11:15)

### Serious sin

**[MAT 27:3–27:4](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:4)** *See also* [PSA 25:16–25:18](https://ref.ly/Ps25:16-Ps25:18); [JER 14:2–14:3](https://ref.ly/Jer14:2-Jer14:3), [JER 14:7](https://ref.ly/Jer14:7), [JER 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Jer16:9-Jer16:10); [EZK 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:16-Ezek4:17), [EZK 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:26-Ezek7:27), [EZK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:19); [REV 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/Rev16:10-Rev16:11)

### A sense of the futility of life

**[ECC 2:17–2:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17-Eccl2:20)**

### Loss of sense of God's presence

**[PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2)** *See also* [MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46); [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34)

Symptoms of depression
----------------------

### A loathing of life and a desire to end it

**[NUM 11:15](https://ref.ly/Num11:15)** *See also* [1KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:4); [JOB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Job10:1); [ECC 2:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17); [JER 20:15–20:18](https://ref.ly/Jer20:15-Jer20:18); [JON 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:8)

### Deep sorrow

**[GEN 21:16](https://ref.ly/Gen21:16)** *See also* [JDG 21:2](https://ref.ly/Judg21:2); [1SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:10), [1SA 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:16); [PSA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Ps42:3); [NEH 1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:4), [NEH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Neh2:2); [EST 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Esth4:1-Esth4:3); [JOB 16:16](https://ref.ly/Job16:16); [LAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Lam2:11)

### Loneliness

**[1KI 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:10)** *See also* [NUM 11:14](https://ref.ly/Num11:14); [1KI 19:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:14)

### Perplexity

**[PSA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5)** *See also* [HAB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hab1:2); [LUK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Luke24:17); [JHN 16:6](https://ref.ly/John16:6)

### Despair

**[JOB 17:1](https://ref.ly/Job17:1)** *See also* [PSA 88:3–88:9](https://ref.ly/Ps88:3-Ps88:9); [2CO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:8-2Cor1:9)

### Escapism

**[PSA 55:6–55:8](https://ref.ly/Ps55:6-Ps55:8)** *See also* [1KI 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:3); [JER 8:22](https://ref.ly/Jer8:22)

### Feeling forsaken by God

**[PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1)** *See also* [PSA 27:9](https://ref.ly/Ps27:9), [PSA 38:21](https://ref.ly/Ps38:21); [ISA 49:14](https://ref.ly/Isa49:14); [JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18); [LAM 3:1–3:20](https://ref.ly/Lam3:1-Lam3:20)

Examples of spiritual depression
--------------------------------

[JDG 6:13](https://ref.ly/Judg6:13); ; [1KI 19:9–19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:9-1Kgs19:18); ; [PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2), [PSA 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6-Ps22:8), [PSA 42:1–42:7](https://ref.ly/Ps42:1-Ps42:7), [PSA 42:9–42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:9-Ps42:11), [PSA 43:1–43:5](https://ref.ly/Ps43:1-Ps43:5)

Remedies for depression
-----------------------

### Renewed vision of God and being centred upon him

**[1KI 19:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:11)**; **[ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3)** *See also* [JER 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7-Jer17:8); [ROM 8:6](https://ref.ly/Rom8:6)

### Hope and trust in God

**[PSA 42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:11)** *See also* [PSA 22:9–22:11](https://ref.ly/Ps22:9-Ps22:11), [PSA 22:22–22:31](https://ref.ly/Ps22:22-Ps22:31); [HAB 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Hab3:16-Hab3:18)

### Praise

**[PSA 30:1](https://ref.ly/Ps30:1)** *See also* [PSA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5), [PSA 107:1–107:43](https://ref.ly/Ps107:1-Ps107:43); [ISA 61:1–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:3); [2CO 4:8–4:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:8-2Cor4:12)

### Reviewing what God has done

**[PSA 77:11–77:12](https://ref.ly/Ps77:11-Ps77:12)** *See also* [PSA 42:6](https://ref.ly/Ps42:6), [PSA 143:5](https://ref.ly/Ps143:5); [LAM 3:19–3:26](https://ref.ly/Lam3:19-Lam3:26); [2CO 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:6)

### Prayer

**[PHP 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6-Phil4:7)** *See also* [PSA 139:23](https://ref.ly/Ps139:23)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 1:8; 1 Kings 19:3-4; Job 9:23; Psalms 42:1-11; Psalms 88:1-18; Joel 1:11; 1 Kings 19:5-8; Genesis 21:15-16; Psalms 6:1-7; Psalms 22:14-17; Psalms 31:9-12; Isaiah 38:1-2; Jonah 1:5; 1 Kings 19:1-3; 1 Kings 19:4; Exodus 4:1; Numbers 11:11-15; Matthew 27:3-4; Psalms 25:16-18; Jeremiah 14:2-3; Jeremiah 14:7; Jeremiah 16:9-10; Ezekiel 4:16-17; Ezekiel 7:26-27; Ezekiel 12:19; Revelation 16:10-11; Ecclesiastes 2:17-20; Psalms 22:1-2; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:34; Numbers 11:15; Job 10:1; Ecclesiastes 2:17; Jeremiah 20:15-18; Jonah 4:8; Genesis 21:16; Judges 21:2; 1 Samuel 1:10; 1 Samuel 1:16; Psalms 42:3; Nehemiah 1:4; Nehemiah 2:2; Esther 4:1-3; Job 16:16; Lamentations 2:11; 1 Kings 19:10; Numbers 11:14; 1 Kings 19:14; Psalms 42:5; Habakkuk 1:2; Luke 24:17; John 16:6; Job 17:1; Psalms 88:3-9; 2 Corinthians 1:8-9; Psalms 55:6-8; Jeremiah 8:22; Psalms 22:1; Psalms 27:9; Psalms 38:21; Isaiah 49:14; Jeremiah 15:18; Lamentations 3:1-20; Judges 6:13; 1 Kings 19:9-18; Psalms 22:6-8; Psalms 42:1-7; Psalms 42:9-11; Psalms 43:1-5; 1 Kings 19:11; Isaiah 26:3; Jeremiah 17:7-8; Romans 8:6; Psalms 42:11; Psalms 22:9-11; Psalms 22:22-31; Habakkuk 3:16-18; Psalms 30:1; Psalms 107:1-43; Isaiah 61:1-3; 2 Corinthians 4:8-12; Psalms 77:11-12; Psalms 42:6; Psalms 143:5; Lamentations 3:19-26; 2 Corinthians 7:6; Philippians 4:6-7; Psalms 139:23

## 5832: desire (id: 5832)

A sense of longing for or wanting something. It may be a positive or negative force in the human character, but sinful desire is characteristic of human nature and must be overcome by the believer. God also expresses desires in Scripture.

Righteous desire
----------------

### Desire for God

**[PSA 73:25](https://ref.ly/Ps73:25)** *See also* [PSA 42:1–42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:1-Ps42:2), [PSA 63:1–63:8](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1-Ps63:8), [PSA 143:6](https://ref.ly/Ps143:6); [ISA 26:8–26:9](https://ref.ly/Isa26:8-Isa26:9)

### Desire for right living

**[PSA 40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:8)** *See also* [PSA 119:174](https://ref.ly/Ps119:174); [PRO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1); [HEB 13:18](https://ref.ly/Heb13:18)

### Desire for wisdom

**[2CH 1:7–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:7-2Chr1:12)** parallel [1KI 3:5–3:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:5-1Kgs3:14) *See also* [JAS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jas1:5)

### Desire for spiritual gifts

**[1CO 14:1–14:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1-1Cor14:5)** *See also* [1CO 12:27–12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27-1Cor12:31)

### Desire for church office

**[1TI 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:1)**

Sinful desire
-------------

**[DEU 5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:21)** parallel [EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17) *See also* [PRO 11:6](https://ref.ly/Prov11:6); [ROM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24), [ROM 7:8](https://ref.ly/Rom7:8); [1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10); [JAS 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jas1:13-Jas1:15); [2PE 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:3); [JUD 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16-Jude1:18)

### Sinful desire is inherent in human nature

**[EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:3)** *See also* [2PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10), [2PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18)

### Following Jesus Christ means putting to death sinful desires

**[GAL 5:24](https://ref.ly/Gal5:24)** *See also* [ROM 8:5–8:14](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5-Rom8:14), [ROM 13:11–13:14](https://ref.ly/Rom13:11-Rom13:14); [EPH 4:22–4:24](https://ref.ly/Eph4:22-Eph4:24); [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5); [2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:22); [1PE 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:14-1Pet1:15), [1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11), [1PE 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:1-1Pet4:5)

The tension in believers between the desires of the old and new natures
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16-Gal5:17)** *See also* [ROM 7:18](https://ref.ly/Rom7:18); [JAS 4:1](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1)

Sexual desire
-------------

**[GEN 3:16](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16)** *See also* [SNG 7:10](https://ref.ly/Song7:10); [1TI 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:11)

### Sinful lust condemned

**[MAT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28)** *See also* [ROM 1:26](https://ref.ly/Rom1:26); [1TH 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:4); [1PE 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3); [2PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18)

God's desires
-------------

### God desires true worship

**[MAT 12:7](https://ref.ly/Matt12:7)** *See also* [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [MAT 9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:13); [AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24); [JHN 4:21–4:24](https://ref.ly/John4:21-John4:24); [HEB 10:5–10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:5-Heb10:14); [PSA 40:6–40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:6-Ps40:8)

### God desires right living

**[PSA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Ps51:6)** *See also* [JAS 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19-Jas1:20)

### God's desires for his people

**[2CH 9:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:8)** *See also* [PSA 132:13](https://ref.ly/Ps132:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 73:25; Psalms 42:1-2; Psalms 63:1-8; Psalms 143:6; Isaiah 26:8-9; Psalms 40:8; Psalms 119:174; Proverbs 22:1; Hebrews 13:18; 2 Chronicles 1:7-12; 1 Kings 3:5-14; James 1:5; 1 Corinthians 14:1-5; 1 Corinthians 12:27-31; 1 Timothy 3:1; Deuteronomy 5:21; Exodus 20:17; Proverbs 11:6; Romans 1:24; Romans 7:8; 1 Timothy 6:9-10; James 1:13-15; 2 Peter 3:3; Jude 1:16-18; Ephesians 2:3; 2 Peter 2:10; 2 Peter 2:18; Galatians 5:24; Romans 8:5-14; Romans 13:11-14; Ephesians 4:22-24; Colossians 3:5; 2 Timothy 2:22; 1 Peter 1:14-15; 1 Peter 2:11; 1 Peter 4:1-5; Galatians 5:16-17; Romans 7:18; James 4:1; Genesis 3:16; Song of Songs 7:10; 1 Timothy 5:11; Matthew 5:28; Romans 1:26; 1 Thessalonians 4:3-4; 1 Peter 4:3; Matthew 12:7; Hosea 6:6; Matthew 9:13; Amos 5:21-24; John 4:21-24; Hebrews 10:5-14; Psalms 40:6-8; Psalms 51:6; James 1:19-20; 2 Chronicles 9:8; Psalms 132:13

## 5833: diligence (id: 5833)

Conscientious hard work and perseverance, particularly with regard to a relationship with God. In Scripture diligence is highly commended. It may, however, be misguided.

Diligence and its results are commended
---------------------------------------

**[PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5)** *See also* [1KI 11:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:28); [2CH 24:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:13); [EZR 5:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:8), [EZR 6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:12), [EZR 7:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:23); [PRO 12:14](https://ref.ly/Prov12:14), [PRO 14:23](https://ref.ly/Prov14:23), [PRO 31:17](https://ref.ly/Prov31:17) *characteristic of the wife of noble character*; [ECC 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:13); [JER 31:16](https://ref.ly/Jer31:16); [2CO 8:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:11) *regarding the collection for the poor Christians in Jerusalem*; [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17); [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

Diligence contrasted with laziness
----------------------------------

**[PRO 10:4](https://ref.ly/Prov10:4)** *See also* [PRO 12:24](https://ref.ly/Prov12:24), [PRO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Prov12:27), [PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4); [2TH 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:10)

Diligence is important in the outworking of one's relationship with God
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58)**; **[1TI 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:15-1Tim4:16)**

### Examples of diligence

*See also* [2CH 15:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:15), [2CH 29:34](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:34) *the Levites*; [ISA 62:6–62:7](https://ref.ly/Isa62:6-Isa62:7); [ZEC 6:15](https://ref.ly/Zech6:15); [LUK 8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:15); [ACT 17:11](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11) *the Bereans*[ROM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:15); [1CO 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:12), [1CO 9:24–9:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:26); [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5), [2CO 11:27](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:27); [1TH 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:9); [2TH 3:8](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:8); [2TI 4:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:7)[ROM 12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:8), [ROM 16:12](https://ref.ly/Rom16:12); [1CO 12:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:31), [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1); [2CO 8:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:5) *the Macedonian churches*; [PHP 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Phil2:12-Phil2:13); [1TH 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12); [HEB 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Heb6:11-Heb6:12); [2PE 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:10); [REV 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Rev2:2-Rev2:3), [REV 2:19](https://ref.ly/Rev2:19)

Diligence was a characteristic of Jesus Christ's mission
--------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 9:51](https://ref.ly/Luke9:51)** *See also* [HEB 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1-Heb12:2)

Misguided diligence
-------------------

**[JHN 5:39–5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:39-John5:40)** *See also* [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); [PRO 28:20](https://ref.ly/Prov28:20); [ECC 1:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:3), [ECC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:11), [ECC 2:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:19), [ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8); [MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15), [MAT 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/Matt21:23-Matt21:24); [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 21:5; 1 Kings 11:28; 2 Chronicles 24:13; Ezra 5:8; Ezra 6:12; Ezra 7:23; Proverbs 12:14; Proverbs 14:23; Proverbs 31:17; Ecclesiastes 3:13; Jeremiah 31:16; 2 Corinthians 8:11; 1 Timothy 5:17; 1 Peter 5:2; Proverbs 10:4; Proverbs 12:24; Proverbs 12:27; Proverbs 13:4; 2 Thessalonians 3:10; 1 Corinthians 15:58; 1 Timothy 4:15-16; 2 Chronicles 15:15; 2 Chronicles 29:34; Isaiah 62:6-7; Zechariah 6:15; Luke 8:15; Acts 17:11; Romans 1:15; 1 Corinthians 4:12; 1 Corinthians 9:24-26; 2 Corinthians 6:5; 2 Corinthians 11:27; 1 Thessalonians 2:9; 2 Thessalonians 3:8; 2 Timothy 4:7; Romans 12:8; Romans 16:12; 1 Corinthians 12:31; 1 Corinthians 14:1; 2 Corinthians 8:5; Philippians 2:12-13; 1 Thessalonians 5:12; Hebrews 6:11-12; 2 Peter 1:10; Revelation 2:2-3; Revelation 2:19; Luke 9:51; Hebrews 12:1-2; John 5:39-40; Psalms 127:2; Proverbs 28:20; Ecclesiastes 1:3; Ecclesiastes 2:11; Ecclesiastes 2:19; Ecclesiastes 4:8; Matthew 21:15; Matthew 21:23-24; 1 Timothy 6:10
* **Associated Articles:** 5343: idleness (ID: `5343@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5414: money, stewardship of (ID: `5414@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5628: work (ID: `5628@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5840: eagerness (ID: `5840@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7939: ministry (ID: `7939@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8206: Christlikeness (ID: `8206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8207: commitment (ID: `8207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8230: discipline (ID: `8230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8239: earnestness (ID: `8239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8248: faithfulness (ID: `8248@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8370: zeal (ID: `8370@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8459: perseverance (ID: `8459@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5834: disagreement (id: 5834)

A failure to be of one mind, which should be avoided whenever possible within the church, the family and other areas of everyday life. Christians are urged to maintain the unity of the church and avoid damaging division.

Disagreement within the church
------------------------------

### Between individuals

**[PHP 4:2](https://ref.ly/Phil4:2)** *See also* [MRK 9:33–9:34](https://ref.ly/Mark9:33-Mark9:34) parallel [LUK 9:46](https://ref.ly/Luke9:46); [ACT 15:36–15:40](https://ref.ly/Acts15:36-Acts15:40) *these differences later reconciled; see [1Co 9:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:6); [2Ti 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:11)*; [1CO 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:11-1Cor1:12), [1CO 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3-1Cor3:4); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)

### About doctrine

**[ACT 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1-Acts15:2)** *See also* [ACT 15:11–15:14](https://ref.ly/Acts15:11-Acts15:14), [ACT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19-Acts15:20); [GAL 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:14)

Disagreement in the family
--------------------------

### May cause estrangement

**[MIC 7:6](https://ref.ly/Mic7:6)** *See also* [GEN 27:41–27:45](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41-Gen27:45), [GEN 37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:4)

### Is unpleasant to live with

[PRO 19:13](https://ref.ly/Prov19:13), [PRO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Prov21:9), [PRO 21:19](https://ref.ly/Prov21:19), [PRO 25:24](https://ref.ly/Prov25:24), [PRO 27:15](https://ref.ly/Prov27:15)

Disagreement among witnesses
----------------------------

**[MRK 14:56](https://ref.ly/Mark14:56)** *The background to this statement is [Dt 19:15](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15), which demands that conviction can only take place on the testimony of two or three agreed witnesses.*

The wisdom of avoiding disagreement
-----------------------------------

**[2TI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:14)** *See also* [PRO 17:14](https://ref.ly/Prov17:14), [PRO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Prov20:3), [PRO 26:17](https://ref.ly/Prov26:17), [PRO 26:21](https://ref.ly/Prov26:21); [ROM 16:17](https://ref.ly/Rom16:17); [2TI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:23)

### To prevent division

**[MAT 12:25](https://ref.ly/Matt12:25)** parallels [MRK 3:24–3:25](https://ref.ly/Mark3:24-Mark3:25), [LUK 11:17](https://ref.ly/Luke11:17) *See also* [1KI 12:16–12:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:16-1Kgs12:17) parallel [2CH 10:16–10:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:16-2Chr10:17)

Sin provokes disagreement
-------------------------

**[GAL 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:20)** *See also* [PRO 6:12–6:14](https://ref.ly/Prov6:12-Prov6:14), [PRO 17:19](https://ref.ly/Prov17:19); [TIT 3:9–3:11](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9-Titus3:11); [JAS 3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:16)

Examples where disagreement is inevitable
-----------------------------------------

[1SA 11:1–11:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:1-1Sam11:2) *Nahash's suggestion to the men of Jabesh Gilead*; ; [1KI 20:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:8) *Ben\-Hadad's demands on Ahab*

Examples of resolved disagreement
---------------------------------

[GEN 13:5–13:9](https://ref.ly/Gen13:5-Gen13:9) *Abraham and Lot resolve their disagreement over land and livestock*; ; [GEN 26:19–26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:19-Gen26:22) *Isaac settles a disagreement about the water supply*; ; [1KI 3:16–3:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:16-1Kgs3:27) *Solomon resolves a disagreement between two prostitutes.*

Ways to resolve disagreement
----------------------------

### Settlement out of court

**[MAT 5:25](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25)** parallel [LUK 12:58](https://ref.ly/Luke12:58) *See also* [1CO 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1)

### Accepting weaknesses

[ROM 14:1](https://ref.ly/Rom14:1)

### Striving for harmony

**[1CO 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:10)** *See also* [ROM 14:19](https://ref.ly/Rom14:19), [ROM 15:5](https://ref.ly/Rom15:5); [2CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:11); [EPH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:3); [PHP 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:2-Phil2:3); [COL 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Col3:13-Col3:15); [1TH 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:13); [2TI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:23-2Tim2:24); [1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)

### Appointing wise leaders

**[DEU 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Deut1:12-Deut1:13)** *Moses realised that he was unable to deal with all the people's disputes alone.* *See also* [MAT 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15-Matt18:17); [1CO 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:5-1Cor6:6)

### Being of sound doctrine

**[1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4)** *See also* [TIT 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9-Titus3:10)

Those who seek to resolve disagreement are blessed
--------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:9](https://ref.ly/Matt5:9)** *See also* [JAS 3:18](https://ref.ly/Jas3:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Philippians 4:2; Mark 9:33-34; Luke 9:46; Acts 15:36-40; 1 Corinthians 9:6; 2 Timothy 4:11; 1 Corinthians 1:11-12; 1 Corinthians 3:3-4; 2 Corinthians 12:20; Acts 15:1-2; Acts 15:11-14; Acts 15:19-20; Galatians 2:11-14; Micah 7:6; Genesis 27:41-45; Genesis 37:4; Proverbs 19:13; Proverbs 21:9; Proverbs 21:19; Proverbs 25:24; Proverbs 27:15; Mark 14:56; Deuteronomy 19:15; 2 Timothy 2:14; Proverbs 17:14; Proverbs 20:3; Proverbs 26:17; Proverbs 26:21; Romans 16:17; 2 Timothy 2:23; Matthew 12:25; Mark 3:24-25; Luke 11:17; 1 Kings 12:16-17; 2 Chronicles 10:16-17; Galatians 5:19-20; Proverbs 6:12-14; Proverbs 17:19; Titus 3:9-11; James 3:16; 1 Samuel 11:1-2; 1 Kings 20:8; Genesis 13:5-9; Genesis 26:19-22; 1 Kings 3:16-27; Matthew 5:25; Luke 12:58; 1 Corinthians 6:1; Romans 14:1; 1 Corinthians 1:10; Romans 14:19; Romans 15:5; 2 Corinthians 13:11; Ephesians 4:3; Philippians 2:2-3; Colossians 3:13-15; 1 Thessalonians 5:13; 2 Timothy 2:23-24; 1 Peter 3:8; Deuteronomy 1:12-13; Matthew 18:15-17; 1 Corinthians 6:5-6; 1 Timothy 1:3-4; Titus 3:9-10; Matthew 5:9; James 3:18
* **Associated Articles:** 5270: court (ID: `5270@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5383: lawsuits (ID: `5383@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5675: divorce (ID: `5675@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5681: family, nature of (ID: `5681@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5783: agreement (ID: `5783@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5811: compromise (ID: `5811@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5814: confrontation (ID: `5814@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7025: church, unity and fellowship of (ID: `7025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7032: unity, of God's people (ID: `7032@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8458: peacemakers (ID: `8458@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5835: disappointment (id: 5835)

The sadness experienced when people or circumstances do not fulfil expectations.

Disappointment caused by people or circumstances
------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Job6:19-Job6:21)**; **[JER 8:15](https://ref.ly/Jer8:15)** *See also* [PSA 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ps10:1); [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1), [PRO 13:12](https://ref.ly/Prov13:12), [PRO 17:21](https://ref.ly/Prov17:21), [PRO 29:21](https://ref.ly/Prov29:21); [ECC 6:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:2); [ISA 49:4](https://ref.ly/Isa49:4); [JER 2:36](https://ref.ly/Jer2:36), [JER 14:19](https://ref.ly/Jer14:19); [LAM 4:17](https://ref.ly/Lam4:17); [EZK 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:11)

Disappointment as a result of not understanding God's purposes
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 24:21](https://ref.ly/Luke24:21)** *See also* [JOB 30:26](https://ref.ly/Job30:26); [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13); [JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18)

Disappointment as the result of sin
-----------------------------------

**[PRO 11:7](https://ref.ly/Prov11:7)**; **[HAG 1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:9)** *See also* [JOB 11:20](https://ref.ly/Job11:20); [PRO 10:28](https://ref.ly/Prov10:28); [LAM 3:18](https://ref.ly/Lam3:18); [HOS 13:15](https://ref.ly/Hos13:15); [JOL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Joel1:10); [ZEC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Zech9:5)

Examples of disappointment
--------------------------

[RUT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:20) *Naomi at her losses*; ; [1SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:10) *Hannah with her childlessness*; ; [1SA 15:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:11) *Samuel with Saul*; ; [1SA 30:3–30:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:3-1Sam30:6) *David and his men at Ziklag*; ; [1KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:4) *Elijah with his own performance*; ; [MAT 19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:22), [LUK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:23) *the rich young man*; ; [2CO 12:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:21) *Paul's possible disappointment with the Corinthians*; ; [GAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6) *Paul with the Galatians*

God's disappointment with people
--------------------------------

**[GEN 6:6](https://ref.ly/Gen6:6)**; **[1SA 15:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:35)** *See also* [PSA 78:40](https://ref.ly/Ps78:40); [ISA 63:10](https://ref.ly/Isa63:10); [EZK 6:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:9)

God does not disappoint his faithful people
-------------------------------------------

**[JOS 21:45](https://ref.ly/Josh21:45)**; **[ROM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rom5:5)** *See also* [JOS 23:14](https://ref.ly/Josh23:14); [1KI 8:56](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:56); [PSA 22:5](https://ref.ly/Ps22:5), [PSA 25:3](https://ref.ly/Ps25:3), [PSA 119:116](https://ref.ly/Ps119:116)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 6:19-21; Jeremiah 8:15; Psalms 10:1; Proverbs 10:1; Proverbs 13:12; Proverbs 17:21; Proverbs 29:21; Ecclesiastes 6:2; Isaiah 49:4; Jeremiah 2:36; Jeremiah 14:19; Lamentations 4:17; Ezekiel 37:11; Luke 24:21; Job 30:26; Psalms 73:13; Jeremiah 15:18; Proverbs 11:7; Haggai 1:9; Job 11:20; Proverbs 10:28; Lamentations 3:18; Hosea 13:15; Joel 1:10; Zechariah 9:5; Ruth 1:20; 1 Samuel 1:10; 1 Samuel 15:11; 1 Samuel 30:3-6; 1 Kings 19:4; Matthew 19:22; Mark 10:22; Luke 18:23; 2 Corinthians 12:21; Galatians 1:6; Genesis 6:6; 1 Samuel 15:35; Psalms 78:40; Isaiah 63:10; Ezekiel 6:9; Joshua 21:45; Romans 5:5; Joshua 23:14; 1 Kings 8:56; Psalms 22:5; Psalms 25:3; Psalms 119:116
* **Associated Articles:** 5799: bitterness (ID: `5799@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5831: depression (ID: `5831@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5839: dissatisfaction (ID: `5839@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5938: sadness (ID: `5938@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5952: sorrow (ID: `5952@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5970: unhappiness (ID: `5970@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8713: discouragement (ID: `8713@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8741: failure (ID: `8741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9610: hope (ID: `9610@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5836: disgrace (id: 5836)

Shame and dishonour that can arise from sin and idolatry. The exile of Israel brought disgrace upon God's name. God's people may suffer disgrace for the sake of Jesus Christ.

Examples of disgrace
--------------------

### Brought on Israel

As the result of Achan stealing what was dedicated to God: [JOS 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Josh7:1-Josh7:5), [JOS 7:11–7:15](https://ref.ly/Josh7:11-Josh7:15); [1SA 17:23–17:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:23-1Sam17:26) *as the result of Goliath's boasting*; ; [PSA 44:9–44:19](https://ref.ly/Ps44:9-Ps44:19) *when God no longer acted on Israel's behalf*

### Brought on Israel's enemies

[PSA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Ps6:10), [PSA 35:4](https://ref.ly/Ps35:4), [PSA 35:26](https://ref.ly/Ps35:26), [PSA 40:14](https://ref.ly/Ps40:14), [PSA 44:15](https://ref.ly/Ps44:15), [PSA 70:2](https://ref.ly/Ps70:2), [PSA 71:13](https://ref.ly/Ps71:13), [PSA 83:17–83:18](https://ref.ly/Ps83:17-Ps83:18)

Sin brings disgrace
-------------------

**[GEN 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8-Gen3:10)** *See also* [PSA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Ps52:1); [PRO 13:5](https://ref.ly/Prov13:5), [PRO 14:35](https://ref.ly/Prov14:35), [PRO 18:3](https://ref.ly/Prov18:3); [JER 3:25](https://ref.ly/Jer3:25); [DAN 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Dan9:7-Dan9:8); [HOS 4:7](https://ref.ly/Hos4:7); [ROM 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:23-Rom2:24)

### False prophets disgraced

[JER 23:38–23:40](https://ref.ly/Jer23:38-Jer23:40)

### Idolaters disgraced

**[ISA 45:16](https://ref.ly/Isa45:16)** *See also* [EXO 32:25](https://ref.ly/Exod32:25); [ISA 1:29](https://ref.ly/Isa1:29); [JER 2:26–2:28](https://ref.ly/Jer2:26-Jer2:28); [HOS 10:6](https://ref.ly/Hos10:6)

### The sexually immoral disgraced

**[PRO 6:32–6:33](https://ref.ly/Prov6:32-Prov6:33)** *See also* [GEN 34:1–34:7](https://ref.ly/Gen34:1-Gen34:7); [LEV 20:17](https://ref.ly/Lev20:17); [DEU 22:21](https://ref.ly/Deut22:21); [JDG 19:22–19:24](https://ref.ly/Judg19:22-Judg19:24), [JDG 20:4–20:7](https://ref.ly/Judg20:4-Judg20:7); [2SA 11:2–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:5), [2SA 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:14), [2SA 13:12–13:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:12-2Sam13:14), [2SA 13:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:22)

Barrenness could bring disgrace
-------------------------------

[GEN 30:22–30:23](https://ref.ly/Gen30:22-Gen30:23); ; [1SA 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:6); ; [LUK 1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:25)

The exile brought disgrace upon God's name
------------------------------------------

**[EZK 36:20–36:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:20-Ezek36:21)** *See also* [NEH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Neh1:3), [NEH 2:17](https://ref.ly/Neh2:17); [JER 22:22](https://ref.ly/Jer22:22), [JER 51:51](https://ref.ly/Jer51:51); [LAM 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Lam5:1-Lam5:2)

God's people may suffer disgrace for the sake of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 11:26](https://ref.ly/Heb11:26)** *See also* [MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12) parallel [LUK 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Luke6:22-Luke6:23); [ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:41); [HEB 13:13](https://ref.ly/Heb13:13); [1PE 2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21), [1PE 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:1), [1PE 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:13), [1PE 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Joshua 7:1-5; Joshua 7:11-15; 1 Samuel 17:23-26; Psalms 44:9-19; Psalms 6:10; Psalms 35:4; Psalms 35:26; Psalms 40:14; Psalms 44:15; Psalms 70:2; Psalms 71:13; Psalms 83:17-18; Genesis 3:8-10; Psalms 52:1; Proverbs 13:5; Proverbs 14:35; Proverbs 18:3; Jeremiah 3:25; Daniel 9:7-8; Hosea 4:7; Romans 2:23-24; Jeremiah 23:38-40; Isaiah 45:16; Exodus 32:25; Isaiah 1:29; Jeremiah 2:26-28; Hosea 10:6; Proverbs 6:32-33; Genesis 34:1-7; Leviticus 20:17; Deuteronomy 22:21; Judges 19:22-24; Judges 20:4-7; 2 Samuel 11:2-5; 2 Samuel 12:14; 2 Samuel 13:12-14; 2 Samuel 13:22; Genesis 30:22-23; 1 Samuel 1:6; Luke 1:25; Ezekiel 36:20-21; Nehemiah 1:3; Nehemiah 2:17; Jeremiah 22:22; Jeremiah 51:51; Lamentations 5:1-2; Hebrews 11:26; Matthew 5:11-12; Luke 6:22-23; Acts 5:41; Hebrews 13:13; 1 Peter 2:21; 1 Peter 4:1; 1 Peter 4:13; 1 Peter 5:10
* **Associated Articles:** 5225: barrenness (ID: `5225@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5838: disrespect (ID: `5838@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5843: embarrassment (ID: `5843@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5878: honour (ID: `5878@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5879: humiliation (ID: `5879@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6236: sexual sin (ID: `6236@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7215: exile, the (ID: `7215@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5837: disguise (id: 5837)

The hiding of one's personal identity for a variety of reasons
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[2CH 35:20–35:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:20-2Chr35:24)** *See also* [GEN 38:13–38:26](https://ref.ly/Gen38:13-Gen38:26); [1SA 28:4–28:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:4-1Sam28:12); [1KI 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:1-1Kgs14:6), [1KI 20:35–20:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:35-1Kgs20:43), [1KI 22:29–22:37](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:29-1Kgs22:37) parallel [2CH 18:28–18:34](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:28-2Chr18:34)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 35:20-24; Genesis 38:13-26; 1 Samuel 28:4-12; 1 Kings 14:1-6; 1 Kings 20:35-43; 1 Kings 22:29-37; 2 Chronicles 18:28-34
* **Associated Articles:** 4123: Satan, as deceiver (ID: `4123@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5920: pretence (ID: `5920@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5838: disrespect (id: 5838)

The attitude of heart that leads people to treat God and others in ways that demonstrate a lack of esteem.

Causes of disrespect for God and Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------

### Disrespect caused by a wrong view of God and Jesus Christ

**[ROM 1:21–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21-Rom1:23)** *See also* [LUK 23:8–23:11](https://ref.ly/Luke23:8-Luke23:11)

### Disrespect caused by disobedience to God

**[NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26)** *See also* [2CH 24:17–24:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:17-2Chr24:20); [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5)

### Disrespect caused by Satan

**[ACT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Acts5:3)** *See also* [MAT 8:28–8:29](https://ref.ly/Matt8:28-Matt8:29) parallels [MRK 5:1–5:7](https://ref.ly/Mark5:1-Mark5:7), [LUK 8:26–8:28](https://ref.ly/Luke8:26-Luke8:28)

Causes of disrespect for others
-------------------------------

### Disrespect for others caused by a wrong view of people

**[MAT 19:13–19:14](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:14)** parallels [MRK 10:13–10:14](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:14), [LUK 18:15–18:16](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15-Luke18:16) *The lack of respect for the children arose from the erroneous belief that they were not important enough for Jesus Christ to bother with.* *See also* [LUK 10:25–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:25-Luke10:37) *Those who passed by failed to heed God's command to love their neighbours as themselves*; [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4)

### Disrespect for others caused by seeking power and wealth

**[ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1)** *See also* [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

### Disrespect for others caused by envy

**[MIC 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1-Mic2:2)** *See also* [GEN 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3-Gen37:4)

### Disrespect for others caused by pride and contempt

**[PSA 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:18)** *See also* [MAT 27:27–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:31) parallel [MRK 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:16-Mark15:20)

Disrespect shown in speech
--------------------------

**[JAS 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9)** *See also* [MAT 27:39–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39-Matt27:44) parallels [MRK 15:29–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29-Mark15:32), [LUK 23:35–23:39](https://ref.ly/Luke23:35-Luke23:39); [1TI 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:5)

The development of disrespect
-----------------------------

### Disrespect can grow insidiously

**[1KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4)** *See also* [JER 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Jer2:1-Jer2:11)MRK [11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Jer11:15-Jer11:17) parallels [MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46), [JHN 2:13–2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:13-John2:17)

### Disrespect can spread infectiously

**[DEU 1:27–1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:27-Deut1:28)** *See also* [EST 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/Esth1:16-Esth1:18); [MRK 15:9–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:9-Mark15:15) parallels [MAT 27:17–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:17-Matt27:26), [LUK 23:13–23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:13-Luke23:25); [ROM 2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:24)

### Disrespect can end fatally

**[LEV 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Lev10:1-Lev10:2)** *See also* [EXO 32:25–32:28](https://ref.ly/Exod32:25-Exod32:28); [DEU 21:18–21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18-Deut21:21); [JER 25:4–25:11](https://ref.ly/Jer25:4-Jer25:11)

## 5839: dissatisfaction (id: 5839)

The human feeling of emptiness and unfulfilment, at either a physical or spiritual level.

Hunger and thirst are never permanently satisfied
-------------------------------------------------

**[ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7)** *See also* [PSA 59:15](https://ref.ly/Ps59:15); [ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2); [JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14)

Dissatisfaction as a result of divine judgment
----------------------------------------------

**[LEV 26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:26)** *See also* [ISA 9:19–9:21](https://ref.ly/Isa9:19-Isa9:21); [EZK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:19); [MIC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:14)

Death is never satisfied
------------------------

**[PRO 27:20](https://ref.ly/Prov27:20)** *See also* [PRO 30:15–30:16](https://ref.ly/Prov30:15-Prov30:16); [HAB 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hab2:5)

Dissatisfaction is characteristic of the unrighteous
----------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 13:4](https://ref.ly/Prov13:4)** *See also* [EST 5:9–5:13](https://ref.ly/Esth5:9-Esth5:13); [ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10); [EZK 16:28–16:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:28-Ezek16:29); [3JN 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/3John1:9-3John1:10)

## 5840: eagerness (id: 5840)

A willingness, zeal or longing to accomplish a task or fulfil an ambition.

Eagerness to perform a task
---------------------------

**[1CH 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:8)**; **[NEH 3:20](https://ref.ly/Neh3:20)** *See also* [EZR 5:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:8); [PRO 31:13](https://ref.ly/Prov31:13); [ACT 20:16](https://ref.ly/Acts20:16); [1TH 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:17)

Eagerness to fulfil personal ambitions
--------------------------------------

**[ZEP 3:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:7)**; **[1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)** *See also* [PSA 56:6](https://ref.ly/Ps56:6); [PRO 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Prov1:11-Prov1:12), [PRO 28:20](https://ref.ly/Prov28:20); [MRK 6:25](https://ref.ly/Mark6:25); [GAL 4:17–4:18](https://ref.ly/Gal4:17-Gal4:18); [PHP 3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:6)

Eagerness for personal godliness
--------------------------------

**[2CH 15:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:15)**; **[2PE 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:10)** *See also* [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); [PSA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4), [PSA 42:1–42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:1-Ps42:2), [PSA 63:1](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1); [PRO 23:17](https://ref.ly/Prov23:17); [ACT 26:7](https://ref.ly/Acts26:7); [1CO 14:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:1); [2CO 7:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:11); [HEB 4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb4:11), [HEB 6:11](https://ref.ly/Heb6:11); [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2); [2PE 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:5)

Spiritual eagerness may be superficial and unreal
-------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 58:2](https://ref.ly/Isa58:2)** *See also* [PSA 78:34–78:37](https://ref.ly/Ps78:34-Ps78:37); [ISA 48:1](https://ref.ly/Isa48:1)

Believers should be eager to follow and serve their Lord
--------------------------------------------------------

### Eager to do good works

**[TIT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:14)** *See also* [2CO 8:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:7), [2CO 8:22](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:22); [TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1), [TIT 3:8](https://ref.ly/Titus3:8), [TIT 3:14](https://ref.ly/Titus3:14); [1PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:13)

### Eager to serve

**[ROM 12:11](https://ref.ly/Rom12:11)**; **[1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)** *See also* [PSA 110:3](https://ref.ly/Ps110:3); [ROM 12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:8); [1CO 14:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:12); [EPH 4:3](https://ref.ly/Eph4:3); [TIT 3:8](https://ref.ly/Titus3:8)

### Eager to share the truth

**[ROM 1:15](https://ref.ly/Rom1:15)**; **[2PE 1:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:15)** *See also* [ACT 18:25](https://ref.ly/Acts18:25), [ACT 21:13](https://ref.ly/Acts21:13); [ROM 15:20](https://ref.ly/Rom15:20); [PHP 1:20](https://ref.ly/Phil1:20); [JUD 1:3](https://ref.ly/Jude1:3)

### Eager to receive or understand the truth

**[MRK 12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:37)**; **[ACT 10:33](https://ref.ly/Acts10:33)** *See also* [MRK 10:17](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17) parallels [MAT 19:16](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16), [LUK 18:18](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18); [LUK 2:15](https://ref.ly/Luke2:15), [LUK 21:38](https://ref.ly/Luke21:38); [JHN 4:40](https://ref.ly/John4:40); [ACT 13:42](https://ref.ly/Acts13:42), [ACT 17:11](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11); [1TI 4:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:15)

### Eager to give

**[2CO 8:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:7)** *See also* [EXO 35:21–35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:21-Exod35:22); [1CH 29:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:9); [2CO 8:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:11), [2CO 8:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:19); [GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:10); [1TI 6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:18)

### Eager to pray

**[COL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Col4:2)** *See also* [ROM 15:30](https://ref.ly/Rom15:30); [PHP 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Phil1:3-Phil1:4); [COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12)

### Eager for God's honour

## 5841: ecstasy (id: 5841)

An exalted condition of spiritual joy or rapture; usually reflecting the experience of the Holy Spirit.

Ecstasy in response to prophetic and visionary experiences
----------------------------------------------------------

**[NUM 24:4](https://ref.ly/Num24:4)**; **[2CO 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:3-2Cor12:4)**; **[REV 1:10](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10)** *See also* [NUM 24:16](https://ref.ly/Num24:16); [1SA 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:10-1Sam10:11); [ISA 6:1](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1) *Isaiah's vision of the Lord*; [EZK 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:24-Ezek11:25); [DAN 2:19–2:21](https://ref.ly/Dan2:19-Dan2:21), [DAN 10:7–10:8](https://ref.ly/Dan10:7-Dan10:8), [DAN 10:16](https://ref.ly/Dan10:16); [ACT 9:10](https://ref.ly/Acts9:10) *Ananias' vision*; [ACT 10:3](https://ref.ly/Acts10:3) *Cornelius' vision of the angel of God*; [ACT 10:10–10:16](https://ref.ly/Acts10:10-Acts10:16), [ACT 11:5](https://ref.ly/Acts11:5); [REV 4:2](https://ref.ly/Rev4:2), [REV 17:3](https://ref.ly/Rev17:3), [REV 21:10](https://ref.ly/Rev21:10)

Ecstasy in response to the Holy Spirit
--------------------------------------

**[ACT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Acts2:4)** *See also* [ACT 10:44–10:46](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:46), [ACT 13:52](https://ref.ly/Acts13:52), [ACT 19:6](https://ref.ly/Acts19:6)

Ecstasy in response to Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------

**[LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21)**; **[1PE 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:8)** *See also* [PSA 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ps4:7), [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7); [MAT 28:8](https://ref.ly/Matt28:8)

Ecstasy as a corporate experience
---------------------------------

**[EZR 3:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:12)** *See also* [1KI 8:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:11) parallel [2CH 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:2); [EZR 6:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:16)

Transfiguration of appearance through ecstasy
---------------------------------------------

**[EXO 34:29](https://ref.ly/Exod34:29)** *See also* [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2); [LUK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Luke1:22)

Future ecstasy in response to God's coming kingdom
--------------------------------------------------

## 5842: eloquence (id: 5842)

The ability to speak with fluency and persuasive power, using words appropriate to the circumstances.

Examples of lack of eloquence
-----------------------------

**[EXO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10)** *See also* [EXO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Exod6:12), [EXO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Exod6:30); [2CO 10:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:10), [2CO 11:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:6)

Preaching the gospel does not rely on human eloquence
-----------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:17)** *See also* [1CO 1:19–1:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:19-1Cor1:21), [1CO 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:1), [1CO 13:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:1)

Eloquence that comes from God
-----------------------------

**[ACT 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Acts6:9-Acts6:10)** *See also* [EXO 4:11](https://ref.ly/Exod4:11), [EXO 4:15–4:16](https://ref.ly/Exod4:15-Exod4:16); [ISA 32:4](https://ref.ly/Isa32:4); [MRK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Mark13:11); [LUK 21:15](https://ref.ly/Luke21:15); [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4), [ACT 19:8–19:10](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8-Acts19:10)

Examples of eloquence in speeches
---------------------------------

[ACT 5:35–5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:35-Acts5:40), [ACT 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:22-Acts17:23), [ACT 18:24–18:28](https://ref.ly/Acts18:24-Acts18:28)

Eloquent words may lead to sin
------------------------------

**[PRO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Prov7:21)** *See also* [PSA 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Ps12:3-Ps12:4), [PSA 55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:21); [PRO 2:16](https://ref.ly/Prov2:16), [PRO 5:3](https://ref.ly/Prov5:3), [PRO 6:24](https://ref.ly/Prov6:24), [PRO 29:5](https://ref.ly/Prov29:5)

Sayings concerning eloquence
----------------------------

**[PSA 45:1](https://ref.ly/Ps45:1)** *See also* [PRO 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Prov10:31-Prov10:32), [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18), [PRO 22:11](https://ref.ly/Prov22:11); [ECC 10:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12)
