## 5843: embarrassment (id: 5843)

A state of discomfort and shame, caused by a sense of guilt over something that has been said or done.

Embarrassment may be a sign of misunderstanding or mistrust
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 13:6–13:8](https://ref.ly/John13:6-John13:8)** *See also* [MRK 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16-Mark2:17) *Jesus Christ's enemies approached the disciples knowing they would be embarrassed by the company he was keeping*; [MRK 3:21](https://ref.ly/Mark3:21) *The family of Jesus Christ were embarrassed by what they were hearing about him.*

Love includes not causing unnecessary embarrassment
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:10–24:11](https://ref.ly/Deut24:10-Deut24:11)**

Embarrassment caused by another person
--------------------------------------

*See also* [2KI 8:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:11) *Elisha's fixed gaze embarrassed Hazael who, as the following verses suggest, may already have had a guilty conscience.*

Embarrassment may motivate actions
----------------------------------

[JDG 3:23–3:26](https://ref.ly/Judg3:23-Judg3:26); ; [2KI 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:17)

### Doing right may be embarrassing

**[ACT 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/Acts10:28-Acts10:29)**

### Embarrassment may motivate wrong behaviour

**[MRK 6:26–6:27](https://ref.ly/Mark6:26-Mark6:27)**

* **Associated Passages:** John 13:6-8; Mark 2:16-17; Mark 3:21; Deuteronomy 24:10-11; 2 Kings 8:11; Judges 3:23-26; 2 Kings 2:17; Acts 10:28-29; Mark 6:26-27
* **Associated Articles:** 5836: disgrace (ID: `5836@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5879: humiliation (ID: `5879@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6172: guilt (ID: `6172@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5844: emotions (id: 5844)

Scripture portrays both human beings and God as having emotions. Human emotions and sentiments are of importance to the life of faith. Human feelings can be positive or negative and are subject to change and misinterpretation.

Emotions shown by God
---------------------

### Love

**[DEU 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Deut7:7-Deut7:8)** *See also* [ISA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Isa43:4); [JER 31:3](https://ref.ly/Jer31:3); [HOS 11:1](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1); [MRK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21); [JHN 13:1](https://ref.ly/John13:1), [JHN 17:23](https://ref.ly/John17:23); [1JN 4:8](https://ref.ly/1John4:8), [1JN 4:10](https://ref.ly/1John4:10), [1JN 4:19](https://ref.ly/1John4:19)

### Compassion

**[PSA 103:13](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13)** *See also* [EXO 33:19](https://ref.ly/Exod33:19); [DEU 13:17](https://ref.ly/Deut13:17); [JDG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Judg2:18); [PSA 116:5](https://ref.ly/Ps116:5); [MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36), [MAT 14:14](https://ref.ly/Matt14:14), [MAT 15:32](https://ref.ly/Matt15:32) parallel [MRK 8:2](https://ref.ly/Mark8:2); [MAT 20:34](https://ref.ly/Matt20:34)

### Joy and delight

**[JER 32:41](https://ref.ly/Jer32:41)** *See also* [DEU 30:9](https://ref.ly/Deut30:9); [ISA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Isa62:4); [LUK 15:32](https://ref.ly/Luke15:32)

### Anger

**[EXO 22:24](https://ref.ly/Exod22:24)** *See also* [DEU 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:14-Deut6:15); [JOS 7:1](https://ref.ly/Josh7:1); [JER 7:20](https://ref.ly/Jer7:20); [EZK 5:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:13); [MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13) parallels [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46), [JHN 2:14–2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:17)

Human emotions experienced by God's people
------------------------------------------

### Joy

**[ISA 51:11](https://ref.ly/Isa51:11)** *See also* [1SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1); [PSA 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ps4:7), [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 30:11–30:12](https://ref.ly/Ps30:11-Ps30:12); [LUK 1:47](https://ref.ly/Luke1:47); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25)

### Love for God and one another

### Sorrow and pain

### Fear

### God's people are to feel for one another

**[PHP 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7-Phil1:8)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21), [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9); [1CO 12:25–12:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:25-1Cor12:26); [2CO 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:11-2Cor6:13), [2CO 7:2–7:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:2-2Cor7:3), [2CO 11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:29)

---------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22-Gal5:23)** *See also* [PRO 25:28](https://ref.ly/Prov25:28); [EPH 4:26](https://ref.ly/Eph4:26), [EPH 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eph5:18); [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8); [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8); [JAS 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19-Jas1:20)

Emotions are changeable
-----------------------

**[PRO 14:13](https://ref.ly/Prov14:13)**

### The wicked may feel an unwarranted sense of security

## 5845: emptiness (id: 5845)

Scripture stresses the emptiness of human life without God, and notes that Jesus Christ chose to empty himself in order to redeem humanity.

Emptiness and creation
----------------------

### The world created to be filled

**[ISA 45:18](https://ref.ly/Isa45:18)**; **[HAB 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hab2:14)** *See also* [GEN 1:2](https://ref.ly/Gen1:2)

### Emptiness as a symbol of judgment

**[JER 4:23](https://ref.ly/Jer4:23)**

Human life can be empty
-----------------------

**[RUT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:21)** *See also* [ECC 1:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:2), [ECC 3:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:19), [ECC 12:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:8)

Human words can be empty
------------------------

**[JOB 35:13](https://ref.ly/Job35:13)** *See also* [JOB 35:16](https://ref.ly/Job35:16); [ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6), [ISA 36:5](https://ref.ly/Isa36:5) parallel [2KI 18:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:20); [ISA 59:4](https://ref.ly/Isa59:4); [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); [2PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18)

Human attempts at fulfilment are empty
--------------------------------------

### Work does not satisfy

**[ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2)** *See also* [PSA 127:1–127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:1-Ps127:2); [ECC 2:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17), [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7); [HAG 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Hag1:5-Hag1:6)

### Pleasure does not last

**[JOB 20:5](https://ref.ly/Job20:5)** *See also* [PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17); [ECC 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1-Eccl2:3), [ECC 2:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:25)

### Wealth does not satisfy

**[ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10)** *See also* [ECC 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:7-Eccl4:8); [LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15); [1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)

### Material possessions are unreliable

**[LUK 12:18–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:18-Luke12:20)** *See also* [JOB 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/Job8:13-Job8:15), [JOB 15:31](https://ref.ly/Job15:31); [PSA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Ps49:10), [PSA 49:12](https://ref.ly/Ps49:12), [PSA 49:16–49:17](https://ref.ly/Ps49:16-Ps49:17); [PRO 11:4](https://ref.ly/Prov11:4); [ECC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:15); [1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7)

### False religion cannot save

**[ISA 57:13](https://ref.ly/Isa57:13)** *See also* [JDG 10:14](https://ref.ly/Judg10:14); [ISA 44:17–44:18](https://ref.ly/Isa44:17-Isa44:18), [ISA 45:20](https://ref.ly/Isa45:20), [ISA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Isa46:7); [JER 11:12](https://ref.ly/Jer11:12); [ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15)

Emptiness as the result of sin
------------------------------

**[MIC 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:13-Mic6:14)** *See also* [LEV 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Lev26:14-Lev26:16); [PSA 78:32–78:33](https://ref.ly/Ps78:32-Ps78:33); [HOS 4:10](https://ref.ly/Hos4:10); [HAG 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:7-Hag1:9)

God wants to remove the emptiness of people's lives
---------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18)** *See also* [PSA 81:10](https://ref.ly/Ps81:10); [JHN 1:16](https://ref.ly/John1:16), [JHN 10:10](https://ref.ly/John10:10); [EPH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:13); [COL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Col2:10)

Jesus Christ accepts emptiness in order to redeem humanity
----------------------------------------------------------

**[PHP 2:7](https://ref.ly/Phil2:7)** *See also* [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 45:18; Habakkuk 2:14; Genesis 1:2; Jeremiah 4:23; Ruth 1:21; Ecclesiastes 1:2; Ecclesiastes 3:19; Ecclesiastes 12:8; Job 35:13; Job 35:16; Isaiah 16:6; Isaiah 36:5; 2 Kings 18:20; Isaiah 59:4; Ephesians 5:6; 2 Peter 2:18; Isaiah 55:2; Psalms 127:1-2; Ecclesiastes 2:17; Ecclesiastes 6:7; Haggai 1:5-6; Job 20:5; Proverbs 21:17; Ecclesiastes 2:1-3; Ecclesiastes 2:25; Ecclesiastes 5:10; Ecclesiastes 4:7-8; Luke 12:15; 1 Timothy 6:9-10; Luke 12:18-20; Job 8:13-15; Job 15:31; Psalms 49:10; Psalms 49:12; Psalms 49:16-17; Proverbs 11:4; Ecclesiastes 5:15; 1 Timothy 6:7; Isaiah 57:13; Judges 10:14; Isaiah 44:17-18; Isaiah 45:20; Isaiah 46:7; Jeremiah 11:12; Acts 14:15; Micah 6:13-14; Leviticus 26:14-16; Psalms 78:32-33; Hosea 4:10; Haggai 1:7-9; 1 Peter 1:18; Psalms 81:10; John 1:16; John 10:10; Ephesians 4:13; Colossians 2:10; Philippians 2:7; 2 Corinthians 8:9

## 5846: enjoyment (id: 5846)

The ability to find pleasure and joy in life. Scripture sees the capacity for enjoyment as a gift from God, whose people are to find pleasure and happiness in him and the many blessings he has given to them.

Human capacity and opportunity for enjoyment is bestowed by God
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[ECC 5:18–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:18-Eccl5:20)**; **[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [NEH 9:35](https://ref.ly/Neh9:35); [ECC 2:24–2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24-Eccl2:26)

People find enjoyment in a great diversity of things
----------------------------------------------------

### Enjoyment of the land and its produce

**[NUM 14:31](https://ref.ly/Num14:31)** *See also* [GEN 45:17–45:18](https://ref.ly/Gen45:17-Gen45:18); [DEU 20:6](https://ref.ly/Deut20:6), [DEU 28:30](https://ref.ly/Deut28:30); [ISA 65:22](https://ref.ly/Isa65:22); [JER 31:5](https://ref.ly/Jer31:5)

### Enjoyment of long life and health

**[ECC 11:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:8)** *See also* [DEU 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Deut6:1-Deut6:2); [1CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:28); [PRO 28:16](https://ref.ly/Prov28:16); [3JN 1:2](https://ref.ly/3John1:2)

### Enjoyment of peace and security

**[PSA 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:11)** *See also* [JDG 8:28](https://ref.ly/Judg8:28); [PSA 37:3](https://ref.ly/Ps37:3); [JER 33:6–33:9](https://ref.ly/Jer33:6-Jer33:9); [ACT 9:31](https://ref.ly/Acts9:31), [ACT 24:2](https://ref.ly/Acts24:2)

### Enjoyment of prosperity

**[PSA 106:4–106:5](https://ref.ly/Ps106:4-Ps106:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:18–37:19](https://ref.ly/Ps37:18-Ps37:19)

### Enjoyment of food and drink

**[ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15)** *See also* [NEH 8:9–8:12](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9-Neh8:12); [HAB 1:16](https://ref.ly/Hab1:16)

### Enjoyment of friendship and fellowship

**[ROM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24)** *See also* [JDG 19:6](https://ref.ly/Judg19:6), [JDG 19:9](https://ref.ly/Judg19:9), [JDG 19:22](https://ref.ly/Judg19:22); [PSA 55:12–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:12-Ps55:14)

### Enjoyment of love

**[PRO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Prov7:18)** *See also* [ECC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:9)

### Enjoyment of the fruits of righteous living

**[ISA 3:10](https://ref.ly/Isa3:10)** *See also* [PRO 13:2](https://ref.ly/Prov13:2)

### Enjoyment of good favour

**[ACT 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46-Acts2:47)** *See also* [ACT 7:45–7:46](https://ref.ly/Acts7:45-Acts7:46)

### Enjoyment of work

**[ECC 3:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:22)** *See also* [ECC 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:13)

### Enjoyment of light of the world

**[JHN 5:35](https://ref.ly/John5:35)** *See also* [JOB 33:28](https://ref.ly/Job33:28)

### Enjoyment of hospitality

**[ROM 16:23](https://ref.ly/Rom16:23)**

### Enjoyment of rest

**[JOB 3:18](https://ref.ly/Job3:18)**

### Enjoyment of fragrance

**[EXO 30:38](https://ref.ly/Exod30:38)**

People also find unwholesome enjoyment in sin
---------------------------------------------

**[HEB 11:24–11:25](https://ref.ly/Heb11:24-Heb11:25)** *See also* [JOB 20:12–20:14](https://ref.ly/Job20:12-Job20:14)

The land enjoying rest
----------------------

**[2CH 36:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:21)** *See also* [LEV 26:34](https://ref.ly/Lev26:34), [LEV 26:43](https://ref.ly/Lev26:43)

Lack of enjoyment
-----------------

**[ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8)** *See also* [DEU 28:30](https://ref.ly/Deut28:30); [JOB 20:17–20:19](https://ref.ly/Job20:17-Job20:19), [JOB 21:23–21:25](https://ref.ly/Job21:23-Job21:25); [ECC 2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:26), [ECC 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:1-Eccl6:6)

## 5847: enthusiasm (id: 5847)

A state of eagerness, leading to the positive and committed performance of actions.

Enthusiasm for doing good
-------------------------

**[1PE 3:10–3:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:10-1Pet3:13)** *See also* [TIT 2:11–2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:14)

Enthusiasm for doing evil
-------------------------

**[JER 8:6](https://ref.ly/Jer8:6)** *See also* [MIC 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1-Mic2:2); [ZEP 3:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:7)

Enthusiasm for work
-------------------

**[PRO 31:10–31:19](https://ref.ly/Prov31:10-Prov31:19)** *See also* [PRO 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:11), [PRO 31:24](https://ref.ly/Prov31:24), [PRO 31:27](https://ref.ly/Prov31:27)

Enthusiasm for giving
---------------------

**[2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:2)** *See also* [2CO 8:10–8:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:10-2Cor8:12); [GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:10)

Enthusiasm for spiritual gifts
------------------------------

**[1CO 14:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:12)** *See also* [1CO 14:39](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:39)

Enthusiasm for serving God
--------------------------

**[2CO 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:16-2Cor8:17)** *See also* [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Peter 3:10-13; Titus 2:11-14; Jeremiah 8:6; Micah 2:1-2; Zephaniah 3:7; Proverbs 31:10-19; Proverbs 6:6-11; Proverbs 31:24; Proverbs 31:27; 2 Corinthians 9:2; 2 Corinthians 8:10-12; Galatians 2:10; 1 Corinthians 14:12; 1 Corinthians 14:39; 2 Corinthians 8:16-17; 1 Peter 5:2

## 5848: exaggeration (id: 5848)

An overstatement of something, usually relating to its importance or value. People exaggerate because of fear, envy, pride or self\-pity. Exaggeration can also be used positively to good effect, for example, in the teaching of Jesus Christ.

Exaggeration of difficulties through fear
-----------------------------------------

**[NUM 13:32–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:32-Num13:33)** *See also* [DEU 1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:28), [DEU 9:1](https://ref.ly/Deut9:1)

Exaggeration of the lifestyle of others through envy
----------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 21:7–21:10](https://ref.ly/Job21:7-Job21:10)** *Job's complaint that the wicked often appear to prosper is stated with exaggeration in order to make his point clear.* *See also* [PSA 73:3–73:12](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:12)

Exaggerating one's status or abilities through pride
----------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 27:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:3)** *See also* [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8); [MAT 8:19](https://ref.ly/Matt8:19) parallel [LUK 9:57](https://ref.ly/Luke9:57); [LUK 22:33](https://ref.ly/Luke22:33); [ACT 12:21–12:23](https://ref.ly/Acts12:21-Acts12:23); [1CO 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:8); [REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)

Exaggeration of the achievements of others
------------------------------------------

**[1SA 21:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:11)** *The reference to David as king by the Philistines may be an exaggeration reflecting the great success and popularity of David among the Israelite people.* *See also* [1SA 18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:7), [1SA 29:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:5)

Exaggeration of troubles through self\-pity
-------------------------------------------

**[1KI 18:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:22)** *See also* [1KI 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:10), [1KI 19:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:14), [1KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:18); [PSA 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Ps12:1-Ps12:2)

Exaggeration of the appearance of others through admiration
-----------------------------------------------------------

These verses represent exaggeration typical in poetic descriptive passages: [SNG 5:10–5:16](https://ref.ly/Song5:10-Song5:16), [SNG 7:1–7:9](https://ref.ly/Song7:1-Song7:9)

Exaggeration used by Jesus Christ as a teaching tool
----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:3-Matt7:5)** parallel [LUK 6:41–6:42](https://ref.ly/Luke6:41-Luke6:42) *See also* [MAT 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:29-Matt5:30), [MAT 7:9–7:10](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:10) parallel [LUK 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/Luke11:11-Luke11:12); [MAT 13:33](https://ref.ly/Matt13:33) parallel [LUK 13:20](https://ref.ly/Luke13:20); [MAT 19:24](https://ref.ly/Matt19:24) parallels [MRK 10:25](https://ref.ly/Mark10:25), [LUK 18:25](https://ref.ly/Luke18:25)

* **Associated Passages:** Numbers 13:32-33; Deuteronomy 1:28; Deuteronomy 9:1; Job 21:7-10; Psalms 73:3-12; Ezekiel 27:3; Ezekiel 28:2; Hosea 12:8; Matthew 8:19; Luke 9:57; Luke 22:33; Acts 12:21-23; 1 Corinthians 4:8; Revelation 3:17; 1 Samuel 21:11; 1 Samuel 18:7; 1 Samuel 29:5; 1 Kings 18:22; 1 Kings 19:10; 1 Kings 19:14; 1 Kings 19:18; Psalms 12:1-2; Song of Songs 5:10-16; Song of Songs 7:1-9; Matthew 7:3-5; Luke 6:41-42; Matthew 5:29-30; Matthew 7:9-10; Luke 11:11-12; Matthew 13:33; Luke 13:20; Matthew 19:24; Mark 10:25; Luke 18:25
* **Associated Articles:** 2357: Jesus Christ, parables of (ID: `2357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of (ID: `2363@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8302: love, abuse of (ID: `8302@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8733: envy (ID: `8733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8776: lies (ID: `8776@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5849: exaltation (id: 5849)

The lifting up or raising on high of a person, in words or actions. The exaltation of Jesus Christ, first on the cross and then in his resurrection, provides a model of how God exalts the humble and obedient. God also humbles those who exalt themselves. Believers are called upon to exalt God in their praise of him.

True exaltation comes from God
------------------------------

**[PSA 75:6–75:7](https://ref.ly/Ps75:6-Ps75:7)** *See also* [GEN 39:1–39:6](https://ref.ly/Gen39:1-Gen39:6); [1SA 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1-1Sam2:10); [2SA 7:18–7:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:18-2Sam7:29)

### God exalts those who are humble

**[JAS 4:10](https://ref.ly/Jas4:10)** *See also* [MAT 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3-Matt5:5); [LUK 1:46–1:55](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:55); [1PE 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5-1Pet5:6); [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34)

Examples of God's exaltation of his people
------------------------------------------

### God's exaltation of Joshua

**[JOS 3:7](https://ref.ly/Josh3:7)**

### God's exaltation of David

**[2SA 5:9–5:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:9-2Sam5:12)** *See also* [2SA 22:47–22:51](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:47-2Sam22:51) parallel [PSA 18:46–18:50](https://ref.ly/Ps18:46-Ps18:50); [PSA 89:19–89:29](https://ref.ly/Ps89:19-Ps89:29)

### God's exaltation of Solomon

**[1CH 29:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:25)** *See also* [1KI 3:5–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:5-1Kgs3:15) parallel [2CH 1:7–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:7-2Chr1:12); [1KI 10:1–10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1-1Kgs10:10) parallel [2CH 9:1–9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1-2Chr9:9)

God's exaltation of Jesus Christ
--------------------------------

### Prophecies of Jesus Christ's exaltation

**[ISA 52:13](https://ref.ly/Isa52:13)** *See also* [PSA 110:1–110:7](https://ref.ly/Ps110:1-Ps110:7)

### The exaltation of Jesus Christ upon the cross

**[JHN 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/John3:14-John3:15), [JHN 12:32](https://ref.ly/John12:32)** *See also* [JHN 8:28](https://ref.ly/John8:28)

### God's exaltation of Jesus Christ in the resurrection

**[ACT 2:32–2:36](https://ref.ly/Acts2:32-Acts2:36)** *See also* [ACT 5:30–5:31](https://ref.ly/Acts5:30-Acts5:31); [PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11); [HEB 7:26](https://ref.ly/Heb7:26)

Self\-exaltation is wrong
-------------------------

### Warnings against self\-exaltation

**[PRO 25:6–25:7](https://ref.ly/Prov25:6-Prov25:7)** *See also* [DAN 4:19–4:27](https://ref.ly/Dan4:19-Dan4:27); [MAT 23:5–23:12](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5-Matt23:12) parallels [MRK 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:39), [LUK 20:45–20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:47); [LUK 14:7–14:11](https://ref.ly/Luke14:7-Luke14:11)

### Examples of self\-exaltation

**[ISA 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/Isa14:13-Isa14:14)** *See also* [GEN 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:3-Gen11:4); [EZK 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:1-Ezek28:2); [DAN 3:1–3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:1-Dan3:7); [2TH 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:3-2Thess2:4)

### Consequences of self\-exaltation

**[MAT 23:12](https://ref.ly/Matt23:12)** parallel [LUK 14:11](https://ref.ly/Luke14:11) *See also* [GEN 11:5–11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:5-Gen11:9); [ISA 2:11–2:17](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11-Isa2:17), [ISA 14:12–14:20](https://ref.ly/Isa14:12-Isa14:20) *taunt against the king of Babylon*; [DAN 4:28–4:33](https://ref.ly/Dan4:28-Dan4:33); [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)

The exaltation of God in his people's praise
--------------------------------------------

**[EXO 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1-Exod15:2)** *Rejoicing after the exodus from Egypt.* *See also* [PSA 34:1–34:3](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1-Ps34:3), [PSA 57:9–57:11](https://ref.ly/Ps57:9-Ps57:11), [PSA 99:5](https://ref.ly/Ps99:5), [PSA 145:1–145:13](https://ref.ly/Ps145:1-Ps145:13); [ISA 25:1](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1); [DAN 4:36–4:37](https://ref.ly/Dan4:36-Dan4:37)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 75:6-7; Genesis 39:1-6; 1 Samuel 2:1-10; 2 Samuel 7:18-29; James 4:10; Matthew 5:3-5; Luke 1:46-55; 1 Peter 5:5-6; Proverbs 3:34; Joshua 3:7; 2 Samuel 5:9-12; 2 Samuel 22:47-51; Psalms 18:46-50; Psalms 89:19-29; 1 Chronicles 29:25; 1 Kings 3:5-15; 2 Chronicles 1:7-12; 1 Kings 10:1-10; 2 Chronicles 9:1-9; Isaiah 52:13; Psalms 110:1-7; John 3:14-15; John 12:32; John 8:28; Acts 2:32-36; Acts 5:30-31; Philippians 2:9-11; Hebrews 7:26; Proverbs 25:6-7; Daniel 4:19-27; Matthew 23:5-12; Mark 12:38-39; Luke 20:45-47; Luke 14:7-11; Isaiah 14:13-14; Genesis 11:3-4; Ezekiel 28:1-2; Daniel 3:1-7; 2 Thessalonians 2:3-4; Matthew 23:12; Luke 14:11; Genesis 11:5-9; Isaiah 2:11-17; Isaiah 14:12-20; Daniel 4:28-33; Luke 18:9-14; Exodus 15:1-2; Psalms 34:1-3; Psalms 57:9-11; Psalms 99:5; Psalms 145:1-13; Isaiah 25:1; Daniel 4:36-37

## 5850: excess (id: 5850)

A surplus of material or wealth, especially food or drink. Scripture criticises excessive consumption and extravagance, which are seen as exploiting the less fortunate and leading to a lack of concern for God.

Examples of excessive eating and drinking
-----------------------------------------

### The Israelites in idolatry

**[EXO 32:2–32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:6)**

### The Israelites' greed, after God's miraculous provision

**[NUM 11:32](https://ref.ly/Num11:32)**

### The Israelites in luxury

**[AMO 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4-Amos6:6)**

### Other examples of excess

*See also* [1SA 25:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:36) *Nabal*; [1SA 30:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:16) *the Amalekites in victory*; [ISA 56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:12) *Israel's wicked watchmen*; [DAN 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1-Dan5:4) *King Belshazzar in pride*; [PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19) *the enemies of the cross of Christ*; [REV 18:3](https://ref.ly/Rev18:3) *the godless nations*

Mentions of excess in Jesus Christ's parables
---------------------------------------------

### As a warning to the rich

**[LUK 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:18-Luke12:21)**

### As a warning against ingratitude and spiritual indifference

*See also* [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13), [LUK 16:19–16:31](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19-Luke16:31)

Excessive eating and drinking is never encouraged
-------------------------------------------------

### Church leaders are not to overindulge

**[1TI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:3), [1TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8)** *See also* [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7)

### It causes sickness

**[PRO 25:16](https://ref.ly/Prov25:16)** *See also* [PRO 25:27](https://ref.ly/Prov25:27)

### It brings poverty

**[PRO 23:20–23:21](https://ref.ly/Prov23:20-Prov23:21)** *See also* [PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17)

### It brings disgrace

**[PRO 28:7](https://ref.ly/Prov28:7)**

### It is associated with laziness

**[TIT 1:12](https://ref.ly/Titus1:12)**

### It shows a disregard for God

**[ISA 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11-Isa5:12)** *See also* [HOS 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Hos4:10-Hos4:11); [PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19)

### It leads the unwise man astray

**[PRO 20:1](https://ref.ly/Prov20:1)**

Commands for believers to abstain from such excesses
----------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21)**; **[EPH 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eph5:18)** *See also* [1CO 6:10–6:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:10-1Cor6:13); [1PE 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3); [2PE 2:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13)

The charging of excessive interest on loans is forbidden
--------------------------------------------------------

[EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8), [EZK 18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:13), [EZK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:17), [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 32:2-6; Numbers 11:32; Amos 6:4-6; 1 Samuel 25:36; 1 Samuel 30:16; Isaiah 56:12; Daniel 5:1-4; Philippians 3:19; Revelation 18:3; Luke 12:18-21; Luke 15:13; Luke 16:19-31; 1 Timothy 3:2-3; 1 Timothy 3:8; Titus 1:7; Proverbs 25:16; Proverbs 25:27; Proverbs 23:20-21; Proverbs 21:17; Proverbs 28:7; Titus 1:12; Isaiah 5:11-12; Hosea 4:10-11; Proverbs 20:1; Galatians 5:19-21; Ephesians 5:18; 1 Corinthians 6:10-13; 1 Peter 4:3; 2 Peter 2:13; Ezekiel 18:8; Ezekiel 18:13; Ezekiel 18:17; Ezekiel 22:12

## 5851: excuse (id: 5851)

The attempt to deny responsibility for an action or for a lack of action. There is no excuse for sin.

Examples of excuses given for wrong actions
-------------------------------------------

[GEN 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Gen3:12-Gen3:13) *by Adam and Eve for eating the forbidden fruit*; ; [EXO 32:22–32:24](https://ref.ly/Exod32:22-Exod32:24) *by Aaron for making the golden calf*; ; [1SA 13:11–13:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:11-1Sam13:12) *by Saul for acting as priest*; [1SA 15:20–15:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:20-1Sam15:21), [1SA 15:24–15:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:24-1Sam15:25)

Examples of excuses given for inaction
--------------------------------------

By Moses: [EXO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Exod3:11), [EXO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10); [JDG 6:15](https://ref.ly/Judg6:15) *by Gideon*; [PRO 22:13](https://ref.ly/Prov22:13), [PRO 26:13](https://ref.ly/Prov26:13); [JER 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6) *by Jeremiah*; [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30); ; [LUK 19:12–19:27](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:27); [MAT 25:41–25:45](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41-Matt25:45) *by the ignorant*

Excuses given by those unwilling to follow Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 14:16–14:24](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16-Luke14:24)** *See also* [MAT 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2-Matt22:3); [LUK 9:59–9:62](https://ref.ly/Luke9:59-Luke9:62)

There is no excuse for sin
--------------------------

**[ROM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20)** *See also* [JHN 9:41](https://ref.ly/John9:41), [JHN 15:22](https://ref.ly/John15:22); [ROM 2:1](https://ref.ly/Rom2:1), [ROM 3:19–3:23](https://ref.ly/Rom3:19-Rom3:23), [ROM 5:12](https://ref.ly/Rom5:12); [1JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/1John1:8), [1JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 3:12-13; Exodus 32:22-24; 1 Samuel 13:11-12; 1 Samuel 15:20-21; 1 Samuel 15:24-25; Exodus 3:11; Exodus 4:10; Judges 6:15; Proverbs 22:13; Proverbs 26:13; Jeremiah 1:6; Matthew 25:14-30; Luke 19:12-27; Matthew 25:41-45; Luke 14:16-24; Matthew 22:2-3; Luke 9:59-62; Romans 1:20; John 9:41; John 15:22; Romans 2:1; Romans 3:19-23; Romans 5:12; 1 John 1:8; 1 John 1:10
* **Associated Articles:** 4924: delay (ID: `4924@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5877: hesitation (ID: `5877@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6154: fall, the (ID: `6154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6183: ignorance, of God (ID: `6183@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (ID: `8712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5852: experience (id: 5852)

The direct personal knowledge of people and situations which comes from a firsthand encounter with life. To experience God is to encounter and know him directly, rather than merely to know facts about him.

* **Associated Articles:** 5853: experience, of life (ID: `5853@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5854: experience, of God (ID: `5854@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5853: experience, of life (id: 5853)

Experience of life comes with increasing age and through observation, reflection and the application of lessons learned. Experience in a craft or profession comes through training and practice.

Experience associated with advancing years
------------------------------------------

**[JOB 12:12](https://ref.ly/Job12:12)** *See also* [JOB 32:6–32:7](https://ref.ly/Job32:6-Job32:7); [1KI 12:6–12:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:6-1Kgs12:7) parallel [2CH 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:6-2Chr10:7); [PSA 37:25](https://ref.ly/Ps37:25); [PRO 4:1–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:1-Prov4:4)

Dissatisfaction with life's experiences
---------------------------------------

**[ECC 2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:11)** *See also* [ECC 3:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:11) *People are made for eternity and cannot be fully satisfied with the things of this world*; [ECC 1:13–1:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13-Eccl1:17) *application to study*; [ECC 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1-Eccl2:3) *devotion to pleasure*; [ECC 2:4–2:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:4-Eccl2:6) *pursuit of great projects*; [ECC 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:7-Eccl2:8) *a life of materialism***[ECC 2:24–2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24-Eccl2:26)** *Human experiences find fulfilment only in relation to God.*[ECC 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:12-Eccl3:14), [ECC 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:13-Eccl12:14)

Learning from life's experiences
--------------------------------

**[PRO 24:32](https://ref.ly/Prov24:32)** *See also* [JOB 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Job4:8-Job4:9), [JOB 5:3](https://ref.ly/Job5:3); [PSA 107:43](https://ref.ly/Ps107:43)

### Sympathy resulting from personal experience

**[EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21); [DEU 15:12–15:15](https://ref.ly/Deut15:12-Deut15:15); [2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4); [HEB 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32-Heb10:34)

### Growing through experiences

**[ROM 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Rom5:3-Rom5:4)** *See also* [HEB 5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:8); [JAS 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2-Jas1:4)

### Learning from everyday experiences

**[MAT 24:32](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32)** parallel [MRK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28) *See also* [MAT 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/Matt16:2-Matt16:3); [LUK 12:54–12:55](https://ref.ly/Luke12:54-Luke12:55); [MAT 6:28–6:31](https://ref.ly/Matt6:28-Matt6:31) parallel [LUK 12:27–12:29](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27-Luke12:29)

Jesus Christ shared human experience
------------------------------------

**[HEB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15)** *See also* [HEB 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:17-Heb2:18); [PHP 2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:8); [HEB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Heb2:9)

Experience in a craft or profession
-----------------------------------

### Skilled craftsmen

**[2CH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:7)** *See also* [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14); [1KI 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:13-1Kgs7:14); [EXO 36:1–36:2](https://ref.ly/Exod36:1-Exod36:2); [1CH 22:15–22:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:15-1Chr22:16)

### Experienced soldiers

**[2CH 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:12-2Chr17:13)** *See also* [1SA 17:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:33); [2SA 17:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:8); [1CH 12:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:33); [SNG 3:7–3:8](https://ref.ly/Song3:7-Song3:8)

### Other competent people

[1SA 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:2) *Samuel's experience in leadership*; ; [EST 1:13](https://ref.ly/Esth1:13) *Xerxes consults experts in law and justice*; ; [MAT 2:4](https://ref.ly/Matt2:4) *Herod consults teachers of the law*; ; [ACT 18:2–18:3](https://ref.ly/Acts18:2-Acts18:3) *Paul and Aquila were experienced tent makers.*

## 5854: experience, of God (id: 5854)

Scripture stresses the importance and also the possibility of a true experience of the living God. This is contrasted with the more academic knowledge about him. Experiential knowledge of God comes through personal encounter, through the witness of the Holy Spirit, through reflection on his past goodness and through the shared experiences of others.

Personal experience of God
--------------------------

### Contrasted with academic knowledge

**[JOB 42:5](https://ref.ly/Job42:5)** *See also* [JER 31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:34); [JHN 4:42](https://ref.ly/John4:42), [JHN 5:39–5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:39-John5:40); [JAS 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jas2:19)

### Experience of God's blessing

**[PSA 34:8](https://ref.ly/Ps34:8)** *See also* [1PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:3); [HEB 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:5)

### Experiencing God through Scripture

[MAT 22:29](https://ref.ly/Matt22:29); ; [JHN 5:39–5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:39-John5:40); ; [ACT 17:11–17:12](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11-Acts17:12); ; [2TI 3:15–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:15-2Tim3:17)

### Knowing God through the Spirit

**[ROM 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/Rom8:15-Rom8:16)** *See also* [JHN 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/John14:16-John14:17), [JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26), [JHN 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/John16:14-John16:15); [1CO 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:9-1Cor2:10); [ISA 64:4](https://ref.ly/Isa64:4)

### Examples of personal encounter with God

**[DEU 11:2–11:7](https://ref.ly/Deut11:2-Deut11:7)** *See also* [DEU 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Deut5:3-Deut5:4); [GEN 32:30](https://ref.ly/Gen32:30) *Jacob*; [EXO 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/Exod3:3-Exod3:4) *Moses*; [ISA 6:1](https://ref.ly/Isa6:1) *Isaiah*; [ACT 9:3–9:6](https://ref.ly/Acts9:3-Acts9:6) *Saul of Tarsus*; [2CO 12:2–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:2-2Cor12:4) *Paul*; [REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17) *John*

Sharing experience of God
-------------------------

### Experience of God through his people

**[ZEC 8:23](https://ref.ly/Zech8:23)** *See also* [ISA 45:14](https://ref.ly/Isa45:14); [1CO 14:24–14:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:24-1Cor14:25)

### Leaders with experience of God

**[JOS 24:31](https://ref.ly/Josh24:31)** *See also* [NUM 12:8](https://ref.ly/Num12:8); [JDG 6:22](https://ref.ly/Judg6:22); [ACT 1:21–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:21-Acts1:22); [1CO 15:3–15:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:3-1Cor15:7); [2PE 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:16-2Pet1:18)

### Speaking to future generations

**[DEU 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9-Deut4:10)** *See also* [EXO 10:2](https://ref.ly/Exod10:2); [DEU 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Deut6:20-Deut6:21); [JOB 8:8–8:9](https://ref.ly/Job8:8-Job8:9); [PSA 71:18](https://ref.ly/Ps71:18)

### Speaking from personal experience: OT

**[DEU 7:18–7:19](https://ref.ly/Deut7:18-Deut7:19)** *See also* [1SA 17:34–17:37](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:34-1Sam17:37) *David's experience teaches him to trust God.*[ISA 43:14–43:17](https://ref.ly/Isa43:14-Isa43:17), [ISA 51:9–51:11](https://ref.ly/Isa51:9-Isa51:11)[JER 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Jer16:14-Jer16:15)

### Speaking from personal experience: NT

**[JHN 3:11](https://ref.ly/John3:11)** *See also* [JHN 20:18](https://ref.ly/John20:18); [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13); [1JN 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/1John1:1-1John1:3)**[JHN 9:25](https://ref.ly/John9:25)** *See also* [MRK 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Mark5:19-Mark5:20) parallel [LUK 8:39](https://ref.ly/Luke8:39); [JHN 4:28–4:29](https://ref.ly/John4:28-John4:29)

-----------------------------------

## 5856: extravagance (id: 5856)

Exaggerated, excessive or immoderate behaviour often linked with wastefulness which may result in personal harm. God's people are called to be wise stewards of what has been entrusted to them. However, Scripture also points to a right kind of open\-handedness and generosity of spirit which reflects love and worship towards God and concern for others.

Examples of sinful extravagance
-------------------------------

### Wastefulness

**[LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13)** *See also* [PRO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Prov21:20), [PRO 29:3](https://ref.ly/Prov29:3)

### Drunkenness and gluttony

**[PRO 23:20](https://ref.ly/Prov23:20)** *See also* [DEU 21:20](https://ref.ly/Deut21:20); [EPH 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eph5:18); [PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19)

The results of sinful extravagance
----------------------------------

### Wastefulness leads to poverty

**[LUK 15:14–15:17](https://ref.ly/Luke15:14-Luke15:17)** *See also* [PRO 13:18](https://ref.ly/Prov13:18), [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16), [PRO 23:21](https://ref.ly/Prov23:21)

### Extravagant living leads to spiritual complacency

**[LUK 12:19–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:19-Luke12:20)** *See also* [ISA 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Isa5:11-Isa5:12), [ISA 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12-Isa22:13), [ISA 56:11–56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:11-Isa56:12); [AMO 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Amos6:1-Amos6:6); [ZEP 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:12-Zeph1:13)

The alternative to sinful extravagance
--------------------------------------

### Thankful enjoyment of God's gifts

**[1TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:3-1Tim4:4)** *See also* [ACT 10:13–10:15](https://ref.ly/Acts10:13-Acts10:15); [1CO 10:30–10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:30-1Cor10:31)

### Stewardship of God's blessings

**[EZK 34:18–34:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:18-Ezek34:19)** *See also* [GEN 2:15](https://ref.ly/Gen2:15); [EXO 23:10–23:12](https://ref.ly/Exod23:10-Exod23:12); [DEU 15:14–15:15](https://ref.ly/Deut15:14-Deut15:15); [PRO 22:9](https://ref.ly/Prov22:9)

### Discipline and self\-control

**[1CO 9:25–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:25-1Cor9:27)** *See also* [GEN 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:16-Gen2:17); [MAT 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16-Matt6:18) *fasting*; [ROM 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rom14:6) *abstinence from certain foods*; [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5) *temporary abstinence from sexual relationships*

Examples of commendable extravagance in worship
-----------------------------------------------

**[MRK 14:3–14:5](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3-Mark14:5)** parallels [MAT 26:6–26:9](https://ref.ly/Matt26:6-Matt26:9), [JHN 12:3–12:5](https://ref.ly/John12:3-John12:5) *See also* [EXO 36:3–36:7](https://ref.ly/Exod36:3-Exod36:7) *gifts for the tabernacle*; [2SA 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14-2Sam6:15) *David dancing before the ark*[1CH 29:3–29:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:3-1Chr29:4); [2CH 24:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:10)PSA [119:171](https://ref.ly/2Chr119:171); [2CO 4:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:15); [COL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:7)MRK [12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Col12:41-Col12:44) parallel [LUK 21:1–21:4](https://ref.ly/Luke21:1-Luke21:4)

Examples of extravagance not commended in worship
-------------------------------------------------

**[MIC 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:7-Mic6:8)** *Extravagant offerings are no substitute for doing what God requires.* *See also* [1SA 15:20–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:20-1Sam15:22)

Other examples of commendable extravagance
------------------------------------------

### Extravagance in caring for others

**[2CO 8:2–8:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:2-2Cor8:4)** *See also* [MAT 5:39–5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39-Matt5:42) parallel [LUK 6:29–6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:29-Luke6:30); [2CO 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:12-2Cor9:13); [1TH 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:12)

### Extravagance in forgiveness

**[MAT 18:21–18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:22)** *See also* [LUK 17:3–17:4](https://ref.ly/Luke17:3-Luke17:4); [MAT 18:26](https://ref.ly/Matt18:26) *the king's generosity in forgiving his servant's debt*; [LUK 15:22–15:23](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22-Luke15:23) *celebrations at the return of the prodigal son*

Warnings against failing to be extravagant towards God and others
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Stinginess cheats God: [ISA 43:24](https://ref.ly/Isa43:24); ; [MAL 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Mal3:8-Mal3:9); ; [ACT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:2); [DEU 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:8); ; [PRO 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Prov23:6-Prov23:8); [PRO 11:24](https://ref.ly/Prov11:24), [PRO 28:22](https://ref.ly/Prov28:22), [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27); ; [MAT 18:32–18:34](https://ref.ly/Matt18:32-Matt18:34)

The extravagance of God's grace
-------------------------------

### The gift of his Son

[JHN 3:16](https://ref.ly/John3:16); ; [2CO 9:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:15)

### The blessings of God's salvation

**[ROM 8:32](https://ref.ly/Rom8:32)** *See also* [ROM 5:5](https://ref.ly/Rom5:5), [ROM 5:15](https://ref.ly/Rom5:15); [EPH 1:8](https://ref.ly/Eph1:8); [TIT 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5-Titus3:6); [1JN 3:1](https://ref.ly/1John3:1)

### The abundance of God's provision

[PSA 23:5](https://ref.ly/Ps23:5), [PSA 65:11](https://ref.ly/Ps65:11); ; [ISA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Isa66:11); ; [JOL 2:24](https://ref.ly/Joel2:24); ; [ZEC 1:17](https://ref.ly/Zech1:17)

### God's blessings promised to the generous

**[MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10)** *See also* [PRO 19:17](https://ref.ly/Prov19:17), [PRO 22:9](https://ref.ly/Prov22:9); [LUK 6:38](https://ref.ly/Luke6:38); [2CO 9:6–9:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6-2Cor9:11)

## 5857: fame (id: 5857)

The reputation gained by individuals or groups of people as a result of their achievements. Scripture shows how God's fame among the nations rested upon his saving actions and, in the NT, how the miracles of Jesus Christ brought him widespread fame. God's people are to rejoice in his fame, without lapsing into arrogance.

God's acts of salvation have brought him renown
-----------------------------------------------

**[JOS 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Josh9:9-Josh9:10)** *See also* [EXO 8:9–8:11](https://ref.ly/Exod8:9-Exod8:11); [NUM 14:13–14:16](https://ref.ly/Num14:13-Num14:16); [PSA 72:1–72:19](https://ref.ly/Ps72:1-Ps72:19), [PSA 135:13–135:14](https://ref.ly/Ps135:13-Ps135:14); [ISA 55:13](https://ref.ly/Isa55:13), [ISA 63:11–63:14](https://ref.ly/Isa63:11-Isa63:14); [JER 32:20](https://ref.ly/Jer32:20); [HAB 3:2](https://ref.ly/Hab3:2)

Jesus Christ's teaching and miracles brought him fame
-----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 4:24](https://ref.ly/Matt4:24)** *See also* [MAT 8:32–8:34](https://ref.ly/Matt8:32-Matt8:34) parallels [MRK 5:14–5:17](https://ref.ly/Mark5:14-Mark5:17), [LUK 8:34–8:37](https://ref.ly/Luke8:34-Luke8:37); [MAT 9:27–9:31](https://ref.ly/Matt9:27-Matt9:31); [MRK 1:27–1:28](https://ref.ly/Mark1:27-Mark1:28) parallel [LUK 4:36–4:37](https://ref.ly/Luke4:36-Luke4:37)

### Jesus Christ taught servanthood rather than the pursuit of status and fame

**[MRK 9:33–9:35](https://ref.ly/Mark9:33-Mark9:35)** parallels [MAT 18:1–18:4](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1-Matt18:4), [LUK 9:46–9:48](https://ref.ly/Luke9:46-Luke9:48)**[MRK 10:42–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:42-Mark10:45)** parallel [MAT 20:25–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:25-Matt20:28)

Israel's fame dependent on her obedience to God
-----------------------------------------------

**[DEU 26:18–26:19](https://ref.ly/Deut26:18-Deut26:19)** *See also* [DEU 2:24–2:25](https://ref.ly/Deut2:24-Deut2:25)

### Israel made famous in order to glorify God

**[JER 13:11](https://ref.ly/Jer13:11)** *See also* [JER 33:9](https://ref.ly/Jer33:9)

### Israel's abuse of her fame led to judgment

**[EZK 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:14-Ezek16:15)** *See also* [EZK 16:35–16:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:35-Ezek16:42)

### Glorious restoration promised by God

**[HOS 14:7](https://ref.ly/Hos14:7)** *See also* [ISA 61:1–61:4](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:4)

Local, national or international fame
-------------------------------------

### Local

**[1CH 12:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:30)**

### National

**[JOS 6:27](https://ref.ly/Josh6:27)** *See also* [EST 9:4](https://ref.ly/Esth9:4)

### International

**[1CH 14:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:17)** *See also* [1KI 4:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:31), [1KI 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:1) parallel [2CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1); [2CH 26:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:8); [ISA 45:1–45:7](https://ref.ly/Isa45:1-Isa45:7)

God's judgment on the arrogance that may come with fame
-------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 23:8–23:9](https://ref.ly/Isa23:8-Isa23:9)** *See also* [JER 48:16–48:18](https://ref.ly/Jer48:16-Jer48:18), [JER 48:29–48:30](https://ref.ly/Jer48:29-Jer48:30); [EZK 26:15–26:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:15-Ezek26:18)

Famous for doing wrong
----------------------

[NUM 16:40](https://ref.ly/Num16:40); ; [1KI 15:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:34); ; [JUD 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jude1:11)

## 5858: fat (id: 5858)

Used to describe a successful person, fruitful land or healthy animals. It is often associated with the idea of prosperity and contentment. It also designates the part of an animal used in OT sacrifices.

Fat as a symbol of success
--------------------------

**[ISA 10:27](https://ref.ly/Isa10:27)**; **[JER 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:27-Jer5:28)** *See also* [DEU 32:15](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15); [JOB 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/Job15:27-Job15:28); [EZK 34:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:16)

Fat representing fruitful land
------------------------------

**[GEN 45:18](https://ref.ly/Gen45:18)**

Fat animals
-----------

**[GEN 41:2](https://ref.ly/Gen41:2)** *See also* [GEN 41:17–41:21](https://ref.ly/Gen41:17-Gen41:21); [1SA 15:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:9); [PSA 66:15](https://ref.ly/Ps66:15); [EZK 34:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:20)

Fat people
----------

**[JDG 3:17](https://ref.ly/Judg3:17)** *See also* [JDG 3:18–3:22](https://ref.ly/Judg3:18-Judg3:22)

Fat used in the context of judgment
-----------------------------------

**[ISA 17:4](https://ref.ly/Isa17:4)**

The fat of animals
------------------

### Not to be eaten

**[LEV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Lev3:17)** *See also* [LEV 7:22–7:25](https://ref.ly/Lev7:22-Lev7:25)

### To be offered to God in sacrifice

**[LEV 3:16](https://ref.ly/Lev3:16)** *See also* [GEN 4:4](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4); [LEV 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Lev3:3-Lev3:5); [NUM 18:17](https://ref.ly/Num18:17); [DEU 32:37–32:38](https://ref.ly/Deut32:37-Deut32:38); [1SA 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:15-1Sam2:16), [1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22); [1KI 8:64](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:64); [ISA 34:6–34:7](https://ref.ly/Isa34:6-Isa34:7), [ISA 43:24](https://ref.ly/Isa43:24); [EZK 39:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 10:27; Jeremiah 5:27-28; Deuteronomy 32:15; Job 15:27-28; Ezekiel 34:16; Genesis 45:18; Genesis 41:2; Genesis 41:17-21; 1 Samuel 15:9; Psalms 66:15; Ezekiel 34:20; Judges 3:17; Judges 3:18-22; Isaiah 17:4; Leviticus 3:17; Leviticus 7:22-25; Leviticus 3:16; Genesis 4:4; Leviticus 3:3-5; Numbers 18:17; Deuteronomy 32:37-38; 1 Samuel 2:15-16; 1 Samuel 15:22; 1 Kings 8:64; Isaiah 34:6-7; Isaiah 43:24; Ezekiel 39:19

## 5859: favour (id: 5859)

Looking kindly upon someone or treating someone with special regard. Scripture stresses the kindness and graciousness of God and Jesus Christ, which is not the result of human merit or achievement.

* **Associated Articles:** 5860: favour, divine (ID: `5860@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5861: favour, human (ID: `5861@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5860: favour, divine (id: 5860)

See [1055: God, grace and mercy of](1055.md), [2027: Jesus Christ, grace and mercy of](2027.md)



## 5861: favour, human (id: 5861)

Looking kindly upon someone or treating someone with special regard. Favour can be gained by means of good character, gifts, service or through the intervention of God. Scripture warns against undue favouritism.

Examples of those who were regarded with favour by others
---------------------------------------------------------

### OT characters

[1SA 2:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:26) *Samuel*; [1SA 16:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:18), [1SA 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:21-1Sam16:22), [1SA 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:7), [1SA 20:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:3); [EST 2:15](https://ref.ly/Esth2:15), [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17); [DAN 1:9](https://ref.ly/Dan1:9) *Daniel*

### Jesus Christ

**[LUK 2:52](https://ref.ly/Luke2:52)**

### The first Christians

*See also* [ACT 2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:47); [ROM 14:18](https://ref.ly/Rom14:18)

Finding favour can lead to being treated with special regard
------------------------------------------------------------

**[RUT 2:13–2:16](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:13-Ruth2:16)** *See also* [PRO 16:15](https://ref.ly/Prov16:15), [PRO 19:12](https://ref.ly/Prov19:12)

Being held in favour can create an opportunity for a request to be made
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[NEH 2:5](https://ref.ly/Neh2:5)** *See also* [GEN 30:27](https://ref.ly/Gen30:27), [GEN 47:29–47:30](https://ref.ly/Gen47:29-Gen47:30), [GEN 50:4–50:6](https://ref.ly/Gen50:4-Gen50:6); [NUM 32:5](https://ref.ly/Num32:5); [RUT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:2); [1SA 20:28–20:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:28-1Sam20:29), [1SA 27:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:5); [EST 7:3](https://ref.ly/Esth7:3), [EST 8:5](https://ref.ly/Esth8:5)

Finding favour with others
--------------------------

### Through good character

**[PRO 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/Prov3:3-Prov3:4)** *See also* [PRO 13:15](https://ref.ly/Prov13:15), [PRO 28:23](https://ref.ly/Prov28:23); [ECC 9:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:11); [PHM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:14)

### Through the giving of gifts

[GEN 32:5](https://ref.ly/Gen32:5), [GEN 33:8–33:11](https://ref.ly/Gen33:8-Gen33:11), [GEN 33:15](https://ref.ly/Gen33:15), [GEN 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/Gen34:11-Gen34:12); ; [PSA 45:12](https://ref.ly/Ps45:12); ; [PRO 19:6](https://ref.ly/Prov19:6); ; [EZK 16:33–16:34](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:33-Ezek16:34)

### Through service rendered

**[GEN 39:3–39:4](https://ref.ly/Gen39:3-Gen39:4)** *See also* [2SA 14:19–14:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:19-2Sam14:22); [EPH 6:6](https://ref.ly/Eph6:6) parallel [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22)

### Through the intervention of God

[GEN 39:21](https://ref.ly/Gen39:21); ; [EXO 3:21](https://ref.ly/Exod3:21), [EXO 11:3](https://ref.ly/Exod11:3), [EXO 12:36](https://ref.ly/Exod12:36); ; [NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11)

Warnings against favouritism
----------------------------

**[LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15)**; **[1TI 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:21)** *See also* [EXO 23:3](https://ref.ly/Exod23:3); [DEU 1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17); [JOB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Job13:10); [PRO 24:23–24:25](https://ref.ly/Prov24:23-Prov24:25); [MAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mal2:9); [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4), [JAS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jas2:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 2:26; 1 Samuel 16:18; 1 Samuel 16:21-22; 1 Samuel 18:6-7; 1 Samuel 20:3; Esther 2:15; Esther 2:17; Daniel 1:9; Luke 2:52; Acts 2:47; Romans 14:18; Ruth 2:13-16; Proverbs 16:15; Proverbs 19:12; Nehemiah 2:5; Genesis 30:27; Genesis 47:29-30; Genesis 50:4-6; Numbers 32:5; Ruth 2:2; 1 Samuel 20:28-29; 1 Samuel 27:5; Esther 7:3; Esther 8:5; Proverbs 3:3-4; Proverbs 13:15; Proverbs 28:23; Ecclesiastes 9:11; Philemon 1:14; Genesis 32:5; Genesis 33:8-11; Genesis 33:15; Genesis 34:11-12; Psalms 45:12; Proverbs 19:6; Ezekiel 16:33-34; Genesis 39:3-4; 2 Samuel 14:19-22; Ephesians 6:6; Colossians 3:22; Genesis 39:21; Exodus 3:21; Exodus 11:3; Exodus 12:36; Nehemiah 1:11; Leviticus 19:15; 1 Timothy 5:21; Exodus 23:3; Deuteronomy 1:17; Job 13:10; Proverbs 24:23-25; Malachi 2:9; James 2:1-4; James 2:9

## 5863: flattery (id: 5863)

Insincere and undeserved praise or words of encouragement, used with the intention of gaining favour with others.

Flattery is deceitful
---------------------

**[PSA 12:2–12:3](https://ref.ly/Ps12:2-Ps12:3)** *See also* [PSA 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ps5:9), [PSA 55:20–55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:20-Ps55:21), [PSA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Ps62:4); [PRO 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6); [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8)

The results of flattery are ruinous
-----------------------------------

**[PRO 26:28](https://ref.ly/Prov26:28)** *See also* [PRO 29:5](https://ref.ly/Prov29:5)

Flattery is rejected by godly people
------------------------------------

**[JOB 32:21–32:22](https://ref.ly/Job32:21-Job32:22)** *See also* [GAL 1:10](https://ref.ly/Gal1:10); [1TH 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:5-1Thess2:6)

Honesty is preferable to flattery
---------------------------------

**[PRO 28:23](https://ref.ly/Prov28:23)** *See also* [PRO 16:13](https://ref.ly/Prov16:13), [PRO 27:9](https://ref.ly/Prov27:9)

False teachers use flattery
---------------------------

**[ROM 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:17-Rom16:18)** *See also* [2TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3-2Tim4:4); [JUD 1:16–1:19](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16-Jude1:19)

Examples of flattery
--------------------

[2SA 14:17–14:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:17-2Sam14:20) *The woman from Tekoa flatters David*; ; [2SA 15:2–15:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:2-2Sam15:6) *Absalom to Israelites*; ; [DAN 6:21](https://ref.ly/Dan6:21) *Daniel flatters the king*; ; [MAT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Matt22:16) parallels [MRK 12:14](https://ref.ly/Mark12:14), [LUK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Luke20:21) *the Pharisees and Herodians to Jesus Christ*; ; [LUK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Luke20:21) *The spies from the teachers of the law flatter Jesus Christ*; ; [ACT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Acts12:22) *The crowds flatter Herod.*

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 12:2-3; Psalms 5:9; Psalms 55:20-21; Psalms 62:4; Proverbs 27:6; Jeremiah 9:8; Proverbs 26:28; Proverbs 29:5; Job 32:21-22; Galatians 1:10; 1 Thessalonians 2:5-6; Proverbs 28:23; Proverbs 16:13; Proverbs 27:9; Romans 16:17-18; 2 Timothy 4:3-4; Jude 1:16-19; 2 Samuel 14:17-20; 2 Samuel 15:2-6; Daniel 6:21; Matthew 22:16; Mark 12:14; Luke 20:21; Acts 12:22
* **Associated Articles:** 5164: lips (ID: `5164@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5546: speech (ID: `5546@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5575: talk, idle (ID: `5575@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5690: friends (ID: `5690@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5820: criticism (ID: `5820@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8237: doctrine, false (ID: `8237@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5864: futility (id: 5864)

All human life and human systems are ultimately useless without God, because death comes to all. God wants to deliver people from their futile ways.

Human life is futile
--------------------

### The brevity of life

**[PSA 89:47](https://ref.ly/Ps89:47)** *See also* [JOB 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Job7:6-Job7:7), [JOB 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Job14:1-Job14:2); [PSA 39:4–39:5](https://ref.ly/Ps39:4-Ps39:5); [ECC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:12); [ISA 40:6–40:7](https://ref.ly/Isa40:6-Isa40:7); [JAS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Jas4:14)

### Death is the destiny of all

[JOB 9:22](https://ref.ly/Job9:22); ; [ECC 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:19-Eccl3:20), [ECC 9:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:2)

### The dead are forgotten

[PSA 103:15–103:16](https://ref.ly/Ps103:15-Ps103:16); ; [ECC 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:11), [ECC 2:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:16), [ECC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:5)

Human endeavour is futile
-------------------------

**[ECC 1:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:14)** *The writer speaks of human life without reference to God.* *See also* [PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6), [PSA 127:2](https://ref.ly/Ps127:2); [ECC 2:10–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:10-Eccl2:23); [HAB 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hab2:13); [JAS 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11)

Human thoughts are futile
-------------------------

**[PSA 94:11](https://ref.ly/Ps94:11)** *See also* [1CO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:19-1Cor3:20); [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21); [EPH 4:17–4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17-Eph4:18)

Human attitudes are futile
--------------------------

### Self\-confidence is futile

**[ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6)** *See also* [JER 48:29–48:30](https://ref.ly/Jer48:29-Jer48:30); [LUK 1:51–1:52](https://ref.ly/Luke1:51-Luke1:52)

### Relying solely on human help is futile

**[JER 17:5](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5)** *See also* [PSA 60:11](https://ref.ly/Ps60:11) parallel [PSA 108:12](https://ref.ly/Ps108:12); [PSA 146:3–146:4](https://ref.ly/Ps146:3-Ps146:4); [ISA 2:22](https://ref.ly/Isa2:22), [ISA 30:1–30:5](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:5), [ISA 31:1–31:3](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1-Isa31:3); [LAM 4:17](https://ref.ly/Lam4:17)

False religion is futile
------------------------

### Idolatry is futile

**[2KI 17:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:15)** *See also* [1SA 12:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:21); [PSA 31:6](https://ref.ly/Ps31:6); [JER 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5), [JER 10:8](https://ref.ly/Jer10:8), [JER 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Jer10:14-Jer10:15), [JER 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jer16:19); [JON 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:8)

### Divination is futile

[ISA 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Isa8:19-Isa8:22); ; [EZK 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:6-Ezek13:9); ; [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2)

### Human religious systems are futile

**[1KI 18:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:29)** *See also* [ISA 16:12](https://ref.ly/Isa16:12); [JER 10:5](https://ref.ly/Jer10:5); [ACT 5:36–5:38](https://ref.ly/Acts5:36-Acts5:38); [COL 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:23)

Nominal religion is futile
--------------------------

### Religious observance without true faith is futile

**[ISA 1:13](https://ref.ly/Isa1:13)** *See also* [MAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mal3:14); [MAT 15:8–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:8-Matt15:9) parallel [MRK 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6-Mark7:7); [1CO 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:1-1Cor3:3); [JAS 1:26](https://ref.ly/Jas1:26)

### Mere religious language is futile

**[TIT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9)** *See also* [JER 7:8](https://ref.ly/Jer7:8); [LAM 2:14](https://ref.ly/Lam2:14); [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); [COL 2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:18); [1TI 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:6); [2TI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:14); [2PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18)

### Christian endeavour using only human strength is futile

[JHN 15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:5); ; [1CO 3:12–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:12-1Cor3:15)

### Hearing God's word without responding to it is futile

**[HEB 4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:2)** *See also* [MAT 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26-Matt7:27), [MAT 13:19–13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:19-Matt13:22); [JAS 1:22–1:24](https://ref.ly/Jas1:22-Jas1:24), [JAS 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jas2:20)

Sin is futile because it achieves nothing
-----------------------------------------

**[JER 13:10](https://ref.ly/Jer13:10)** *See also* [PSA 2:1](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1), [PSA 112:10](https://ref.ly/Ps112:10), [PSA 119:118](https://ref.ly/Ps119:118); [PRO 10:28](https://ref.ly/Prov10:28), [PRO 11:7](https://ref.ly/Prov11:7); [JER 51:58](https://ref.ly/Jer51:58); [EPH 5:11](https://ref.ly/Eph5:11)

Faithfulness to God may seem to be futile
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13)** *See also* [JOB 34:9](https://ref.ly/Job34:9); [ECC 7:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:15), [ECC 8:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:14); [ISA 49:4](https://ref.ly/Isa49:4)

God wants to deliver people from futile ways
--------------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:18)** *See also* [2TI 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 89:47; Job 7:6-7; Job 14:1-2; Psalms 39:4-5; Ecclesiastes 6:12; Isaiah 40:6-7; James 4:14; Job 9:22; Ecclesiastes 3:19-20; Ecclesiastes 9:2; Psalms 103:15-16; Ecclesiastes 1:11; Ecclesiastes 2:16; Ecclesiastes 9:5; Ecclesiastes 1:14; Psalms 39:6; Psalms 127:2; Ecclesiastes 2:10-23; Habakkuk 2:13; James 1:11; Psalms 94:11; 1 Corinthians 3:19-20; Romans 1:21; Ephesians 4:17-18; Isaiah 16:6; Jeremiah 48:29-30; Luke 1:51-52; Jeremiah 17:5; Psalms 60:11; Psalms 108:12; Psalms 146:3-4; Isaiah 2:22; Isaiah 30:1-5; Isaiah 31:1-3; Lamentations 4:17; 2 Kings 17:15; 1 Samuel 12:21; Psalms 31:6; Jeremiah 2:5; Jeremiah 10:8; Jeremiah 10:14-15; Jeremiah 16:19; Jonah 2:8; Isaiah 8:19-22; Ezekiel 13:6-9; Zechariah 10:2; 1 Kings 18:29; Isaiah 16:12; Jeremiah 10:5; Acts 5:36-38; Colossians 2:20-23; Isaiah 1:13; Malachi 3:14; Matthew 15:8-9; Mark 7:6-7; 1 Corinthians 3:1-3; James 1:26; Titus 3:9; Jeremiah 7:8; Lamentations 2:14; Ephesians 5:6; Colossians 2:18; 1 Timothy 1:6; 2 Timothy 2:14; 2 Peter 2:18; John 15:5; 1 Corinthians 3:12-15; Hebrews 4:2; Matthew 7:26-27; Matthew 13:19-22; James 1:22-24; James 2:20; Jeremiah 13:10; Psalms 2:1; Psalms 112:10; Psalms 119:118; Proverbs 10:28; Proverbs 11:7; Jeremiah 51:58; Ephesians 5:11; Psalms 73:13; Job 34:9; Ecclesiastes 7:15; Ecclesiastes 8:14; Isaiah 49:4; 1 Peter 1:18; 2 Timothy 2:21
* **Associated Articles:** 4015: life (ID: `4015@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4030: world, human behaviour in (ID: `4030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4190: spiritism (ID: `4190@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5067: suicide (ID: `5067@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5839: dissatisfaction (ID: `5839@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5845: emptiness (ID: `5845@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6203: mortality (ID: `6203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8032: trust, lack of (ID: `8032@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8784: nominal religion (ID: `8784@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8820: self-confidence (ID: `8820@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5865: gestures (id: 5865)

Significant acts, bodily movements or signals which carry meaning, evoke responses or convey positive or negative feelings. They may or may not be accompanied by words or other communications.

Gestures are part of human communication
----------------------------------------

### They give weight to a contract

**[RUT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:7)** *See also* [2SA 24:22–24:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:22-2Sam24:24) parallel [1CH 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:23-1Chr21:24); [JER 32:10–32:12](https://ref.ly/Jer32:10-Jer32:12)

### They add force to words

**[1SA 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:7)** *See also* [JOS 7:25–7:26](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25-Josh7:26); [1SA 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:27-1Sam15:28); [2SA 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:16); [MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13) parallels [MRK 11:15–11:17](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:17), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46), [JHN 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/John2:15-John2:16) *Use of gestures illustrates the demonstrative nature of much communication in the cultures of biblical times.*

Gestures often convey more than words
-------------------------------------

### Gestures of joy

**[2SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14)** [GEN 21:6](https://ref.ly/Gen21:6); ; [LEV 9:24](https://ref.ly/Lev9:24); ; [ACT 3:8](https://ref.ly/Acts3:8)

### Gestures of love

**[1SA 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:5)** *See also* [GEN 37:3](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3); [1SA 2:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:19); [LUK 7:46–7:47](https://ref.ly/Luke7:46-Luke7:47), [LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34), [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20); [JHN 15:13](https://ref.ly/John15:13); [ROM 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rom5:8)

### Gestures of mercy

**[ISA 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Isa6:6-Isa6:7)** *See also* [1SA 24:10–24:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:10-1Sam24:11); [2SA 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:1), [2SA 9:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:7); [MAT 18:27](https://ref.ly/Matt18:27) *Mercy itself is a gesture of love.*

### Gestures of worship and reverence

**[EXO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Exod3:5)** *See also* [EXO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:6); [JOS 5:14](https://ref.ly/Josh5:14); [NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6); [PSA 63:4](https://ref.ly/Ps63:4), [PSA 134:2](https://ref.ly/Ps134:2); [MAT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Matt2:11); [LUK 5:8](https://ref.ly/Luke5:8); [JHN 11:32](https://ref.ly/John11:32); [REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17)

### Gestures of repentance

**[JOB 42:6](https://ref.ly/Job42:6)** *See also* [1KI 21:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:27); [EZR 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:1); [ISA 22:12](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12); [JOL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Joel2:12); [JON 3:6–3:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:6-Jonah3:8); [MAT 11:21](https://ref.ly/Matt11:21) parallel [LUK 10:13](https://ref.ly/Luke10:13); [MAT 27:3–27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:3-Matt27:5)

### Gestures of sorrow and mourning

**[2SA 3:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:31)** *Gestures of sorrow and mourning are very similar to those of repentance and remorse.* *See also* [2SA 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:4); [EST 4:1](https://ref.ly/Esth4:1); [PSA 137:1](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1); [JOL 1:13](https://ref.ly/Joel1:13); [AMO 5:16](https://ref.ly/Amos5:16)

### Gestures of dedication

**[ACT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Acts22:16)** *See also* [GEN 17:10](https://ref.ly/Gen17:10); [EXO 21:6](https://ref.ly/Exod21:6); [1SA 20:41–20:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41-1Sam20:42); [NEH 10:35–10:37](https://ref.ly/Neh10:35-Neh10:37)

### Gestures of anger

**[LUK 15:28](https://ref.ly/Luke15:28)** *See also* [EXO 32:19](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19); [NUM 22:27](https://ref.ly/Num22:27); [1SA 20:33–20:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:33-1Sam20:34); [MAT 26:65](https://ref.ly/Matt26:65) parallel [MRK 14:63](https://ref.ly/Mark14:63); [ACT 7:54](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54), [ACT 7:57](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57); [REV 3:16](https://ref.ly/Rev3:16)

### Gestures of treachery

**[LUK 22:47–22:48](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47-Luke22:48)** parallels [MAT 26:47–26:49](https://ref.ly/Matt26:47-Matt26:49), [MRK 14:43–14:45](https://ref.ly/Mark14:43-Mark14:45) *See also* [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27), [2SA 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9-2Sam20:10)

### Gestures of contempt

Shaking of the head: [PSA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7), [PSA 64:8](https://ref.ly/Ps64:8); ; [MAT 27:39](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39) parallel [MRK 15:29](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29); [MAT 26:67](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67) parallel [MRK 14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:65); ; [REV 3:16](https://ref.ly/Rev3:16)

Other examples of gestures
--------------------------

### The gesture of washing hands

**[MAT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:24)** *See also* [DEU 21:6–21:7](https://ref.ly/Deut21:6-Deut21:7); [PSA 26:6](https://ref.ly/Ps26:6), [PSA 73:13](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13)

### The gesture of shaking dust from the feet

**[MAT 10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:14)** parallels [MRK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:11), [LUK 9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:5) *See also* [LUK 10:11](https://ref.ly/Luke10:11); [ACT 13:51](https://ref.ly/Acts13:51)

### Jesus Christ's gesture of washing the disciples' feet

[JHN 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/John13:4-John13:5), [JHN 13:12–13:15](https://ref.ly/John13:12-John13:15) *This was a gesture of service and a lesson in both serving and being willing to accept service.*

## 5866: gluttony (id: 5866)

Scripture condemns an excessive greed for food. Its unrestrained self\-indulgence often leads to deeper iniquity and represents a rejection of godly moderation.

Gluttony is a form of idolatry and therefore condemned as offensive to God
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Eph5:5)**; **[PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19)** *See also* [PSA 10:3](https://ref.ly/Ps10:3); [ISA 22:12–22:14](https://ref.ly/Isa22:12-Isa22:14) *Gluttony as escapism, trying to avoid God's requirements*; [ZEC 7:4–7:6](https://ref.ly/Zech7:4-Zech7:6) *Even their religious feasts were nothing but self\-indulgence*; [MRK 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:23); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10), [1CO 10:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:7); [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5); [JAS 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jas5:5)

Gluttony is closely linked with drunkenness
-------------------------------------------

[EXO 32:6](https://ref.ly/Exod32:6); ; [JDG 9:27](https://ref.ly/Judg9:27); ; [1SA 25:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:36)

The destructive consequences of gluttony
----------------------------------------

**[PRO 23:20–23:21](https://ref.ly/Prov23:20-Prov23:21), [PRO 28:7](https://ref.ly/Prov28:7)** *See also* [PRO 15:27](https://ref.ly/Prov15:27), [PRO 28:25](https://ref.ly/Prov28:25); [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7)

Warnings against gluttony
-------------------------

**[PRO 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Prov23:1-Prov23:2)**; **[LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15)**; **[EPH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3)**; **[COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)** *See also* [LUK 12:19–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:19-Luke12:20), [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34); [1CO 5:9–5:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:11); [GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21); [TIT 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Titus1:12-Titus1:13); [1PE 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3-1Pet4:4)

Examples of gluttony
--------------------

[GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34) *Esau putting food before his birthright*; ; [NUM 11:32](https://ref.ly/Num11:32) *the children of Israel gorging on quail*; ; [1SA 30:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:16) *an Amalekite raiding party*; ; [1KI 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:24-1Kgs1:25) *Adonijah feasting with his supporters*; ; [DAN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1) *King Belshazzar*; ; [MAT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19) parallel [LUK 7:34](https://ref.ly/Luke7:34) *Jesus Christ falsely accused of gluttony*; ; [MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25) parallel [LUK 11:39](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39) *the Pharisees*; ; [MAT 24:49](https://ref.ly/Matt24:49) parallel [LUK 12:45](https://ref.ly/Luke12:45) *the irresponsible servant*; ; [LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13) *the prodigal son*; ; [2PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13-2Pet2:14) *false teachers*

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 5:5; Philippians 3:19; Psalms 10:3; Isaiah 22:12-14; Zechariah 7:4-6; Mark 7:21-23; 1 Corinthians 6:9-10; 1 Corinthians 10:7; Colossians 3:5; James 5:5; Exodus 32:6; Judges 9:27; 1 Samuel 25:36; Proverbs 23:20-21; Proverbs 28:7; Proverbs 15:27; Proverbs 28:25; Ecclesiastes 6:7; Proverbs 23:1-2; Luke 12:15; Ephesians 5:3; Luke 12:19-20; Luke 21:34; 1 Corinthians 5:9-11; Galatians 5:19-21; Titus 1:12-13; 1 Peter 4:3-4; Genesis 25:29-34; Numbers 11:32; 1 Samuel 30:16; 1 Kings 1:24-25; Daniel 5:1; Matthew 11:19; Luke 7:34; Matthew 23:25; Luke 11:39; Matthew 24:49; Luke 12:45; Luke 15:13; 2 Peter 2:13-14
* **Associated Articles:** 4404: food (ID: `4404@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4434: drinking (ID: `4434@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5850: excess (ID: `5850@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5869: greed (ID: `5869@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8307: moderation (ID: `8307@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8821: self-indulgence (ID: `8821@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

The command of Jesus Christ to love others as ourselves
-------------------------------------------------------

## 5868: gossip (id: 5868)

Idle talk which foolishly or maliciously spreads rumours or facts. The effects of gossiping are divisive and destructive.

Examples of gossiping
---------------------

[NEH 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Neh6:5-Neh6:8) *False rumours were used to intimidate Nehemiah*; ; [PSA 41:5–41:8](https://ref.ly/Ps41:5-Ps41:8); ; [JHN 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/John7:12-John7:13); ; [3JN 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/3John1:9-3John1:10)

Dangers of talking too much
---------------------------

**[PRO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19)** *See also* [PRO 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:6-Prov18:7), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23), [PRO 29:11](https://ref.ly/Prov29:11); [ECC 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2-Eccl5:3); [MAT 12:36–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:36-Matt12:37)

Gossip can result from idleness
-------------------------------

**[1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13)**

Gossip has disastrous effects
-----------------------------

**[PRO 16:28](https://ref.ly/Prov16:28)** *See also* [PRO 11:13](https://ref.ly/Prov11:13), [PRO 17:9](https://ref.ly/Prov17:9), [PRO 25:9–25:10](https://ref.ly/Prov25:9-Prov25:10), [PRO 26:20](https://ref.ly/Prov26:20)

Gossiping condemned and forbidden
---------------------------------

**[LEV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Lev19:16)** *See also* [EXO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1); [PSA 101:5](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5); [PRO 10:18](https://ref.ly/Prov10:18); [ROM 1:29–1:30](https://ref.ly/Rom1:29-Rom1:30); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)

Advice on avoiding gossip
-------------------------

**[PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)** *See also* [PRO 20:19](https://ref.ly/Prov20:19)

## 5869: greed (id: 5869)

An excessive appetite for further goods or food, often linked with selfishness and gluttony.

* **Associated Articles:** 5870: greed, condemnation of (ID: `5870@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5871: greed, believers' response to (ID: `5871@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5870: greed, condemnation of (id: 5870)

Greed is condemned by Scripture as contrary to the purposes of God.

Greed is a feature of the fallen world
--------------------------------------

**[PSA 10:3](https://ref.ly/Ps10:3)**; **[ROM 1:29](https://ref.ly/Rom1:29)** *See also* [1CO 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:10)

Greed is an expression of sinful human nature
---------------------------------------------

**[MRK 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:22)** parallel [MAT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19-Matt15:20) *See also* [EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31); [ROM 7:8](https://ref.ly/Rom7:8)

Examples of greed
-----------------

### Of individuals

[JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21); ; [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12); ; [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23)

### Of false teachers

**[MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25)** *See also* [JER 6:13](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13), [JER 8:10](https://ref.ly/Jer8:10); [LUK 11:39](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39); [2PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:3), [2PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:14)

Greed invites God's judgment both now and later
-----------------------------------------------

**[EPH 5:5](https://ref.ly/Eph5:5)** *See also* [PRO 15:27](https://ref.ly/Prov15:27), [PRO 28:8](https://ref.ly/Prov28:8), [PRO 28:25](https://ref.ly/Prov28:25), [PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4); [EZK 18:10–18:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:10-Ezek18:13), [EZK 28:5–28:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:5-Ezek28:7); [AMO 8:4–8:7](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4-Amos8:7); [MIC 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1-Mic2:3); [HAB 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Hab2:5-Hab2:6); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10)

## 5871: greed, believers' response to (id: 5871)

Greed should have no place in the lives of God's people.

The righteous are not to be greedy
----------------------------------

**[PRO 28:6](https://ref.ly/Prov28:6)** *See also* [EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17) parallel [DEU 5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:21); [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28), [PRO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1), [PRO 23:4](https://ref.ly/Prov23:4); [EZK 18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:8), [EZK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:17); [ROM 13:9](https://ref.ly/Rom13:9)

The Christian leader is not to be greedy
----------------------------------------

**[1TI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:3)** *See also* [TIT 1:7](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7); [1PE 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:2)

### The example of Paul

**[ACT 20:33](https://ref.ly/Acts20:33)** *See also* [1TH 2:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:5)

The antidotes to greed
----------------------

### Greed must be repented of

**[LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14)** *John the Baptist counselling soldiers on how they must express their repentance.*

### The need to be on guard against greed

**[PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10)**; **[LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15)** *See also* [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)

### The need to be separate from greed and from the greedy

**[1CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:11)**; **[COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)** *See also* [JOB 36:18](https://ref.ly/Job36:18); [EPH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3)

### The need to be content

**[HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)** *See also* [PHP 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Phil4:11-Phil4:13); [1TI 6:3–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:10)

### The need to seek God, not gain

**[1TI 6:17–6:18](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17-1Tim6:18)** *See also* [1KI 3:11–3:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:11-1Kgs3:14); [PSA 119:14](https://ref.ly/Ps119:14); [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24)

* **Associated Articles:** 5353: interest (ID: `5353@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5874: happiness (ID: `5874@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5934: restraint (ID: `5934@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8808: riches (ID: `8808@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5873: habits (id: 5873)

Set patterns of behaviour. These patterns can be helpful when they lead to spiritual discipline and a regular pattern of prayer and worship; others can be detrimental to faith.

Examples of good habits
-----------------------

### Reading and obeying God's word

**[PSA 119:56](https://ref.ly/Ps119:56)**; **[HEB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:14)** *See also* [PSA 119:44](https://ref.ly/Ps119:44), [PSA 119:97](https://ref.ly/Ps119:97)

### Private prayer

**[LUK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Luke5:16)** *See also* [PSA 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ps5:3); [MAT 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5-Matt6:6), [MAT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Matt14:23) parallel [MRK 6:46](https://ref.ly/Mark6:46); [MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35) parallel [LUK 4:42](https://ref.ly/Luke4:42); [LUK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12), [LUK 9:18](https://ref.ly/Luke9:18), [LUK 11:1](https://ref.ly/Luke11:1)

### Attendance at public worship

[LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16); ; [ACT 2:46](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46), [ACT 14:1](https://ref.ly/Acts14:1), [ACT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Acts17:2)

Examples of bad habits
----------------------

**[JER 22:21](https://ref.ly/Jer22:21)** *See also* [EXO 21:29](https://ref.ly/Exod21:29), [EXO 21:36](https://ref.ly/Exod21:36); [JDG 2:19](https://ref.ly/Judg2:19); [EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31)

Warnings to avoid certain habits
--------------------------------

**[HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:25)** *See also* [1TI 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:13)

The difficulty of breaking bad habits
-------------------------------------

**[JER 13:23](https://ref.ly/Jer13:23)**
