## 5874: happiness (id: 5874)

A state of pleasure or joy experienced both by people and by God, but subject to change according to circumstances. True happiness derives from a secure and settled knowledge of God and a rejoicing in his works and covenant faithfulness. God rejoices over his faithful people.

Happiness found in human relationships
--------------------------------------

**[DEU 24:5](https://ref.ly/Deut24:5)** *See also* [GEN 30:13](https://ref.ly/Gen30:13), [GEN 34:19](https://ref.ly/Gen34:19); [1KI 10:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:8); [PSA 113:9](https://ref.ly/Ps113:9); [PRO 23:24](https://ref.ly/Prov23:24); [ECC 9:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:9); [SNG 4:10](https://ref.ly/Song4:10); [ROM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24); [2CO 7:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:13)

Happiness found in people and things
------------------------------------

**[1KI 4:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs4:20)**; **[LUK 1:14](https://ref.ly/Luke1:14)**; **[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [DEU 20:61](https://ref.ly/Deut20:61)SA [18:3](https://ref.ly/Deut18:3), [1SA 20:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:17)[PSA 127:3–127:5](https://ref.ly/Ps127:3-Ps127:5); [ECC 3:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:12), [ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19), [ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15), [ECC 11:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:9); [ISA 65:22](https://ref.ly/Isa65:22); [JON 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:6); [LUK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:19); [JHN 16:21](https://ref.ly/John16:21); [PHP 2:25–2:30](https://ref.ly/Phil2:25-Phil2:30)

Happiness found in God
----------------------

### In God's presence

**[PSA 16:11](https://ref.ly/Ps16:11)** *See also* [PSA 21:6](https://ref.ly/Ps21:6), [PSA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:4); [1TH 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:9)

### In God's word

**[PSA 119:35](https://ref.ly/Ps119:35)** *See also* [ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2); [JER 15:16](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16); [MRK 12:37](https://ref.ly/Mark12:37); [ROM 7:22](https://ref.ly/Rom7:22)

### In God's actions

**[GEN 21:6](https://ref.ly/Gen21:6)**; **[ACT 11:23](https://ref.ly/Acts11:23)** *See also* [EXO 18:9](https://ref.ly/Exod18:9); [1SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1), [1SA 19:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:5); [1KI 8:66](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:66); [EST 8:16](https://ref.ly/Esth8:16); [PSA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Ps16:6), [PSA 92:4](https://ref.ly/Ps92:4), [PSA 111:2](https://ref.ly/Ps111:2); [ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10); [ACT 13:48](https://ref.ly/Acts13:48)

God's happiness
---------------

### In obedient people

**[DEU 30:9–30:10](https://ref.ly/Deut30:9-Deut30:10)** *See also* [1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22); [PSA 147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:11); [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17); [MAT 25:21](https://ref.ly/Matt25:21)

### In the righteous

**[PRO 11:20](https://ref.ly/Prov11:20)** *See also* [PSA 147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:11); [PRO 11:1](https://ref.ly/Prov11:1); [ECC 2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:26); [JER 9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:24)

-----------------------------------

[MAT 5:3–5:10](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3-Matt5:10) parallel [LUK 6:20–6:22](https://ref.ly/Luke6:20-Luke6:22)

Happiness may not be permanent
------------------------------

## 5875: hatred (id: 5875)

Hatred for others or God is associated with human sin. Scripture also emphasises God's hatred of sin and evil. Believers are commanded to love those who hate them.

Hatred as evidence of corrupt human nature
------------------------------------------

**[2SA 13:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:15)**; **[TIT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3)** *See also* [GEN 37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:4); [DEU 7:15](https://ref.ly/Deut7:15), [DEU 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Deut19:11-Deut19:12); [JDG 11:7](https://ref.ly/Judg11:7); [2SA 13:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:22); [PSA 25:19](https://ref.ly/Ps25:19), [PSA 38:19](https://ref.ly/Ps38:19); [PRO 10:12](https://ref.ly/Prov10:12); [MAT 24:10](https://ref.ly/Matt24:10); [GAL 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:20); [1JN 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/1John2:9-1John2:11), [1JN 3:15](https://ref.ly/1John3:15), [1JN 4:20](https://ref.ly/1John4:20)

Hatred of God himself
---------------------

**[PSA 81:15](https://ref.ly/Ps81:15)**; **[ROM 1:29–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:29-Rom1:31)** *See also* [EXO 20:5](https://ref.ly/Exod20:5); [DEU 7:10](https://ref.ly/Deut7:10), [DEU 32:41](https://ref.ly/Deut32:41); [2CH 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:2); [PSA 50:17](https://ref.ly/Ps50:17); [PRO 1:29](https://ref.ly/Prov1:29); [JHN 3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:20), [JHN 15:23–15:25](https://ref.ly/John15:23-John15:25); [JAS 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jas4:4)

Hatred of Jesus Christ
----------------------

**[JHN 7:7](https://ref.ly/John7:7)** *See also* [ISA 53:3](https://ref.ly/Isa53:3); [MAT 12:14](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14), [MAT 21:46](https://ref.ly/Matt21:46), [MAT 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:4), [MAT 27:22](https://ref.ly/Matt27:22); [LUK 19:14](https://ref.ly/Luke19:14); [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14)

Hatred of the godly and upright
-------------------------------

**[1KI 22:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:8)**; **[MAT 10:22](https://ref.ly/Matt10:22)** *See also* [EST 9:1](https://ref.ly/Esth9:1); [PSA 35:19](https://ref.ly/Ps35:19), [PSA 69:4](https://ref.ly/Ps69:4), [PSA 109:3–109:5](https://ref.ly/Ps109:3-Ps109:5); [PRO 29:10](https://ref.ly/Prov29:10); [ISA 66:5](https://ref.ly/Isa66:5); [AMO 5:10](https://ref.ly/Amos5:10); [MAT 24:9–24:10](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9-Matt24:10) parallels [MRK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Mark13:13), [LUK 21:16–21:19](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16-Luke21:19); [LUK 1:69–1:71](https://ref.ly/Luke1:69-Luke1:71), [LUK 6:22](https://ref.ly/Luke6:22); [JHN 15:18](https://ref.ly/John15:18); [ACT 9:29](https://ref.ly/Acts9:29), [ACT 23:12–23:15](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12-Acts23:15); [1JN 3:13](https://ref.ly/1John3:13)

Believers are to love those who hate them
-----------------------------------------

**[LUK 6:27–6:28](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27-Luke6:28)** *See also* [MAT 5:43–5:44](https://ref.ly/Matt5:43-Matt5:44)

God's hatred of evil
--------------------

**[PSA 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/Ps5:4-Ps5:5)**; **[PRO 6:16–6:19](https://ref.ly/Prov6:16-Prov6:19)**; **[ISA 61:8](https://ref.ly/Isa61:8)** *See also* [DEU 12:31](https://ref.ly/Deut12:31), [DEU 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/Deut16:21-Deut16:22); [PSA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Ps11:5); [ISA 1:14](https://ref.ly/Isa1:14); [JER 12:8](https://ref.ly/Jer12:8); [HOS 9:15](https://ref.ly/Hos9:15); [AMO 5:21](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21); [ZEC 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Zech8:16-Zech8:17); [MAL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Mal2:16)

-------------------------------

## 5876: helpfulness (id: 5876)

God's help
----------

### God promises his help

**[ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10)** *See also* [PSA 121:3–121:4](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:4); [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); [JHN 14:16](https://ref.ly/John14:16)

### People value God's help

**[PSA 118:6–118:7](https://ref.ly/Ps118:6-Ps118:7)** *See also* [DEU 33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:29); [2CH 25:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:8); [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 54:4](https://ref.ly/Ps54:4); [ISA 50:9](https://ref.ly/Isa50:9); [ACT 26:22](https://ref.ly/Acts26:22)

### People need God's help

**[PSA 94:17](https://ref.ly/Ps94:17)** *See also* [JOS 14:12](https://ref.ly/Josh14:12); [PSA 22:11](https://ref.ly/Ps22:11), [PSA 22:19](https://ref.ly/Ps22:19), [PSA 40:17](https://ref.ly/Ps40:17), [PSA 116:6](https://ref.ly/Ps116:6); [ISA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Isa59:16), [ISA 63:5](https://ref.ly/Isa63:5); [MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36); [HEB 13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:6); [PSA 118:6–118:7](https://ref.ly/Ps118:6-Ps118:7)

### God's help can be withheld

**[2SA 22:42](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:42)** *See also* [JOB 19:7](https://ref.ly/Job19:7); [PSA 107:11–107:13](https://ref.ly/Ps107:11-Ps107:13); [HOS 13:9](https://ref.ly/Hos13:9); [ZEC 7:13](https://ref.ly/Zech7:13)

### God's help is not imposed

**[JER 29:12](https://ref.ly/Jer29:12)** *See also* [2KI 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:11-2Kgs5:12); [2CH 16:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:12); [1PE 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:6-1Pet5:7)

### People should imitate God's help by being helpful

[2SA 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam9:1); ; [LUK 10:36–10:37](https://ref.ly/Luke10:36-Luke10:37); ; [GAL 6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:2); ; [EPH 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eph5:1); ; [JAS 2:14–2:17](https://ref.ly/Jas2:14-Jas2:17)

Examples of human helpfulness
-----------------------------

### Shown to those in need

**[LUK 10:34](https://ref.ly/Luke10:34)** *See also* [2CH 28:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:15); [JOB 29:15–29:16](https://ref.ly/Job29:15-Job29:16); [ACT 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44-Acts2:45), [ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35)

### Shown by young people

[1SA 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:18), [1SA 17:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:15), [1SA 17:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:17), [1SA 20:36](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:36); ; [2KI 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:2-2Kgs5:3); ; [JHN 6:9](https://ref.ly/John6:9)

### Shown within marriage

[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18), [GEN 2:22](https://ref.ly/Gen2:22); ; [PRO 18:22](https://ref.ly/Prov18:22), [PRO 31:12](https://ref.ly/Prov31:12), [PRO 31:20](https://ref.ly/Prov31:20); ; [1PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:7)

### Shown to God's servants

**[ROM 16:2](https://ref.ly/Rom16:2)** *See also* [JOS 2:4](https://ref.ly/Josh2:4), [JOS 2:15](https://ref.ly/Josh2:15); [1KI 17:10–17:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:10-1Kgs17:11); [LUK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Luke10:7); [1CO 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15-1Cor16:16); [2CO 1:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:11); [PHP 4:3](https://ref.ly/Phil4:3); [PHM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:11)

Helpfulness is remembered and rewarded
--------------------------------------

**[MAT 25:34–25:36](https://ref.ly/Matt25:34-Matt25:36)** *See also* [JOS 6:17](https://ref.ly/Josh6:17); [1KI 17:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:14); [ROM 16:2](https://ref.ly/Rom16:2)

## 5877: hesitation (id: 5877)

A delay in reaching a decision, or a reluctance to reach a decision, on account of uncertainty, caution or rebellion. Scripture provides examples of individuals who hesitate in the face of divine commands or promises, and indicates its dangers.

Examples of people who hesitated
--------------------------------

### Lot

**[GEN 19:16](https://ref.ly/Gen19:16)**

### Lot's wife

**[GEN 19:26](https://ref.ly/Gen19:26)** *See also* [GEN 19:17](https://ref.ly/Gen19:17); [LUK 17:32](https://ref.ly/Luke17:32)

### Moses

[EXO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Exod3:11), [EXO 3:13](https://ref.ly/Exod3:13), [EXO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Exod4:1), [EXO 4:10](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10), [EXO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Exod4:13), [EXO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Exod6:12)

### Peter in obeying the Lord

**[ACT 11:12](https://ref.ly/Acts11:12)** *See also* [ACT 10:14–10:20](https://ref.ly/Acts10:14-Acts10:20)

### Other examples

Israel on approaching the promised land: [NUM 13:31–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:31-Num13:33), [NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4); [JDG 6:36–6:40](https://ref.ly/Judg6:36-Judg6:40) *Gideon*; ; [1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21) *Israel in the time of Elijah*; ; [JER 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6) *Jeremiah*

Examples of people who did not hesitate
---------------------------------------

### Caleb

[NUM 13:30](https://ref.ly/Num13:30)

### Simon and Andrew

**[MAT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18-Matt4:20)** parallels [MRK 1:16–1:18](https://ref.ly/Mark1:16-Mark1:18), [LUK 5:10–5:11](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10-Luke5:11), [JHN 1:40–1:42](https://ref.ly/John1:40-John1:42)

### Paul

**[ACT 20:27](https://ref.ly/Acts20:27)** *See also* [ACT 20:20](https://ref.ly/Acts20:20)

Believers should not hesitate to act when they know what is right
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### They should not hesitate to speak

[ISA 58:1](https://ref.ly/Isa58:1)

### Jesus Christ commands immediate obedience

**[LUK 9:62](https://ref.ly/Luke9:62)** *See also* [LUK 9:57–9:60](https://ref.ly/Luke9:57-Luke9:60) parallel [MAT 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Matt8:19-Matt8:22)

God does not hesitate
---------------------

### To bring judgment

**[EZK 24:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:14)** *See also* [EZK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:25); [DAN 9:14](https://ref.ly/Dan9:14)

### The delay in Jesus Christ's return is not due to hesitation

**[2PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:9)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 19:16; Genesis 19:26; Genesis 19:17; Luke 17:32; Exodus 3:11; Exodus 3:13; Exodus 4:1; Exodus 4:10; Exodus 4:13; Exodus 6:12; Acts 11:12; Acts 10:14-20; Numbers 13:31-33; Numbers 14:1-4; Judges 6:36-40; 1 Kings 18:21; Jeremiah 1:6; Numbers 13:30; Matthew 4:18-20; Mark 1:16-18; Luke 5:10-11; John 1:40-42; Acts 20:27; Acts 20:20; Isaiah 58:1; Luke 9:62; Luke 9:57-60; Matthew 8:19-22; Ezekiel 24:14; Ezekiel 12:25; Daniel 9:14; 2 Peter 3:9
* **Associated Articles:** 1310: God, as judge (ID: `1310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4924: delay (ID: `4924@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5186: stumbling (ID: `5186@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5884: indecision (ID: `5884@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6221: rebellion (ID: `6221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8120: following Jesus Christ (ID: `8120@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8721: doubt (ID: `8721@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5878: honour (id: 5878)

Great respect; the creation is called upon to respect and revere God as its Creator. Since human beings are made in the image of God, they should respect one another.

The creation honours its Creator
--------------------------------

### All creatures honour God

**[ISA 43:20](https://ref.ly/Isa43:20)** *See also* [REV 5:13](https://ref.ly/Rev5:13)

### The people of God honour God

**[EXO 12:40–12:42](https://ref.ly/Exod12:40-Exod12:42)**; **[PSA 22:23](https://ref.ly/Ps22:23)** *See also* [PSA 50:14–50:15](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14-Ps50:15); [ISA 25:1–25:3](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1-Isa25:3); [JER 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jer3:17), [JER 33:7–33:9](https://ref.ly/Jer33:7-Jer33:9); [2TH 3:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:1)

How God wishes to be honoured
-----------------------------

### Honouring God by worshipping him

**[REV 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Rev5:11-Rev5:12)** *See also* [PSA 50:23](https://ref.ly/Ps50:23); [REV 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:9-Rev4:11)

### Honouring God by obeying him

**[EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31)** *These people honoured God with their words only, but this was not true honour for they failed to obey his commands.* *See also* [NUM 25:10–25:13](https://ref.ly/Num25:10-Num25:13); [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)

### Honouring God with one's body

**[1CO 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19-1Cor6:20)** *See also* [ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1); [2CO 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:10-2Cor4:11)

### Honouring God with one's possessions

**[PRO 3:9](https://ref.ly/Prov3:9)** *See also* [ISA 60:9](https://ref.ly/Isa60:9); [2CO 8:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:19)

### Honouring God by caring for others

**[PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31)** *See also* [MAT 25:31–25:40](https://ref.ly/Matt25:31-Matt25:40); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

The consequences of not honouring God
-------------------------------------

**[MAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:2)** *See also* [NUM 20:12](https://ref.ly/Num20:12); [DAN 5:22–5:31](https://ref.ly/Dan5:22-Dan5:31)

Jesus Christ and honour
-----------------------

### Honour given to Jesus Christ

**[HEB 2:9](https://ref.ly/Heb2:9)** *See also* [ACT 19:17](https://ref.ly/Acts19:17); [2PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:17); [MAT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Matt17:5); [MRK 9:7](https://ref.ly/Mark9:7); [LUK 9:35](https://ref.ly/Luke9:35)

### The relationship of honour between the Father and the Son

**[JHN 5:23](https://ref.ly/John5:23)** *See also* [JHN 8:49–8:50](https://ref.ly/John8:49-John8:50)

God brings honour to people
---------------------------

**[1SA 2:30](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:30)** *See also* [PSA 62:7](https://ref.ly/Ps62:7); [ZEP 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:19-Zeph3:20); [JHN 12:26](https://ref.ly/John12:26); [ROM 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:9-Rom2:11)

Honouring others as a duty to God
---------------------------------

### Honouring one's parents

**[EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12)**; **[EPH 6:2–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:2-Eph6:3)** *See also* [MAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6); [MAT 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/Matt15:1-Matt15:6); [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20)

### Honouring fellow believers

**[ROM 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rom12:10)** *See also* [PHP 2:29](https://ref.ly/Phil2:29); [1TI 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:17)

## 5879: humiliation (id: 5879)

The act of being put to shame, often in a public manner. Jesus Christ was humiliated through his suffering, degradation and execution. Believers may expect to suffer humiliation on account of their faith.

Examples of humiliation
-----------------------

### David's men at the hands of Hanun

[2SA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4-2Sam10:5) parallel [1CH 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:4-1Chr19:5)

### Michal, by David's dancing

[2SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14), [2SA 6:20–6:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:20-2Sam6:22)

### Joab and his men, by David's seemingly inappropriate mourning

[2SA 19:5–19:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:5-2Sam19:6)

### Haman, by his death on the gallows prepared for Mordecai

[EST 6:11–6:12](https://ref.ly/Esth6:11-Esth6:12), [EST 7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:10)

### Job

[JOB 19:5](https://ref.ly/Job19:5)

### Peter, by his denial of Jesus Christ

[MAT 26:69–26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:69-Matt26:75) parallels [MRK 14:66–14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:66-Mark14:72), [LUK 22:55–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:55-Luke22:62), [JHN 18:16–18:18](https://ref.ly/John18:16-John18:18), [JHN 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:25-John18:27)

### Warnings against presumption

[PRO 25:7](https://ref.ly/Prov25:7); ; [LUK 14:7–14:11](https://ref.ly/Luke14:7-Luke14:11); ; [1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12)

People are often humiliated as a punishment for sin
---------------------------------------------------

**[OBA 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/Obad1:8-Obad1:10)** *See also* [2KI 19:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:28); [EZR 9:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:7); [PSA 35:26](https://ref.ly/Ps35:26); [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11), [ISA 22:19](https://ref.ly/Isa22:19), [ISA 25:11](https://ref.ly/Isa25:11), [ISA 26:5](https://ref.ly/Isa26:5); [JER 15:9](https://ref.ly/Jer15:9), [JER 23:40](https://ref.ly/Jer23:40), [JER 31:19](https://ref.ly/Jer31:19), [JER 49:16](https://ref.ly/Jer49:16); [EZK 28:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:8); [MAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mal2:9)

Jesus Christ suffered humiliation
---------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's humiliation prophesied in the OT

**[ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9), [ISA 53:12](https://ref.ly/Isa53:12)** *See also* [ISA 50:6](https://ref.ly/Isa50:6), [ISA 52:14](https://ref.ly/Isa52:14), [ISA 53:2–53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:2-Isa53:4); [ACT 8:32–8:33](https://ref.ly/Acts8:32-Acts8:33); [ISA 53:7–53:8](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7-Isa53:8)

### Jesus Christ's humiliation in his incarnation

**[PHP 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6-Phil2:8)** *See also* [2CO 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:9)

### Jesus Christ's humilation in his humble birth

[LUK 2:7](https://ref.ly/Luke2:7)

### Jesus Christ's humiliation through the words of others

[MAT 9:34](https://ref.ly/Matt9:34), [MAT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19) parallel [LUK 7:34](https://ref.ly/Luke7:34); ; [MRK 3:21](https://ref.ly/Mark3:21); ; [JHN 7:20](https://ref.ly/John7:20), [JHN 9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:16)

### Jesus Christ's humiliation through the actions of others

[LUK 4:28–4:29](https://ref.ly/Luke4:28-Luke4:29), [LUK 7:44–7:46](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44-Luke7:46)

### Jesus Christ's humiliation at his trials

**[MRK 14:55–14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:55-Mark14:65)**; **[MAT 27:27–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:31)** *See also* [MAT 26:59–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:68) parallel [JHN 18:19–18:24](https://ref.ly/John18:19-John18:24); [MAT 27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:26) parallels [MRK 15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15), [LUK 23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:25), [JHN 19:16](https://ref.ly/John19:16), [MRK 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:16-Mark15:20), [JHN 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/John19:1-John19:3); [LUK 23:9–23:11](https://ref.ly/Luke23:9-Luke23:11)

### Jesus Christ's humiliation at his crucifixion

**[LUK 23:33–23:43](https://ref.ly/Luke23:33-Luke23:43)** *See also* [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13); [DEU 21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:23); [LUK 22:37](https://ref.ly/Luke22:37); [ISA 53:12](https://ref.ly/Isa53:12); [MAT 27:33–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:33-Matt27:44) parallels [MRK 15:22–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:22-Mark15:32), [JHN 19:17–19:24](https://ref.ly/John19:17-John19:24)

Believers should be prepared to suffer humiliation from unbelievers
-------------------------------------------------------------------

**[2TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:12)** *See also* [MAT 5:11](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11), [MAT 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Matt10:17-Matt10:18); [JHN 15:20](https://ref.ly/John15:20), [JHN 16:2](https://ref.ly/John16:2); [PHP 1:28–1:30](https://ref.ly/Phil1:28-Phil1:30); [1TH 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:6); [HEB 10:33–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:33-Heb10:34); [1PE 2:19–2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:19-1Pet2:21); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

Believers are not to humiliate others
-------------------------------------

**[JAS 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2-Jas2:4)** *See also* [MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22), [MAT 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:4-Matt7:5); [1CO 11:20–11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:22)

## 5881: immaturity (id: 5881)

The result of insufficient growth, or a failure to develop to one's full potential.

Marks of physical immaturity
----------------------------

### Pride

[2SA 15:1–15:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:4)

### Rejection of wise counsel

[DEU 21:18](https://ref.ly/Deut21:18); ; [1KI 12:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:8)

### Tactlessness

[1KI 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:13-1Kgs12:14)

### Lack of respect

[2KI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23); ; [JOB 19:18](https://ref.ly/Job19:18)

### Weakness of character

[2CH 13:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:7)

### Foolishness

[PRO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Prov22:15)

### Lack of understanding

[DEU 1:39](https://ref.ly/Deut1:39); ; [PRO 1:4](https://ref.ly/Prov1:4); ; [JON 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:11); ; [1CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:11)

### Lack of self\-control

[TIT 2:6](https://ref.ly/Titus2:6)

### Disgrace

[PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15)

### Waste

[LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13)

Spiritual immaturity is to be avoided
-------------------------------------

**[1CO 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:20)**; **[HEB 6:1](https://ref.ly/Heb6:1)** *See also* [2CO 13:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:9), [2CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:11) *The word “perfection” may be understood in terms of maturity*; [EPH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:13), [EPH 4:15](https://ref.ly/Eph4:15); [COL 1:28](https://ref.ly/Col1:28), [COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12); [JAS 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:4); [2PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:18)

Marks of spiritual immaturity
-----------------------------

### Backsliding

**[MAT 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Matt13:20-Matt13:21)** parallels [MRK 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/Mark4:16-Mark4:17), [LUK 8:13](https://ref.ly/Luke8:13) *Jesus Christ to his disciples.* *See also* [GAL 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Gal4:8-Gal4:9)

### An over\-sensitive conscience

**[ROM 14:2](https://ref.ly/Rom14:2)**

### Lack of spiritual understanding

**[1CO 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:1-1Cor3:2)** *See also* [MAT 11:16–11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:16-Matt11:19) parallel [LUK 7:31–7:35](https://ref.ly/Luke7:31-Luke7:35); [HEB 5:11–5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:11-Heb5:14)

### Divisiveness

**[1CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3)**

### Instability

**[EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)** *See also* [JAS 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Jas1:6-Jas1:7)

### Pride

**[1TI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:6)** *The church overseer must be a spiritually mature person.* *See also* [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)

Remedies for immaturity
-----------------------

### Confession of sin

**[PRO 28:13](https://ref.ly/Prov28:13)** *See also* [JER 3:13–3:22](https://ref.ly/Jer3:13-Jer3:22); [JAS 5:16](https://ref.ly/Jas5:16); [1JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:9)

### Discipline

**[PRO 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:11-Prov3:12)** *See also* [PSA 119:67–119:71](https://ref.ly/Ps119:67-Ps119:71); [1PE 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:1-1Pet4:2); [REV 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rev3:19)

### Prayer and meditation

**[PSA 119:97–119:99](https://ref.ly/Ps119:97-Ps119:99)** *See also* [PSA 119:27](https://ref.ly/Ps119:27); [JHN 17:17–17:19](https://ref.ly/John17:17-John17:19); [ROM 8:26](https://ref.ly/Rom8:26); [HEB 4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:16); [JAS 1:25](https://ref.ly/Jas1:25)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 15:1-4; Deuteronomy 21:18; 1 Kings 12:8; 1 Kings 12:13-14; 2 Kings 2:23; Job 19:18; 2 Chronicles 13:7; Proverbs 22:15; Deuteronomy 1:39; Proverbs 1:4; Jonah 4:11; 1 Corinthians 13:11; Titus 2:6; Proverbs 29:15; Luke 15:13; 1 Corinthians 14:20; Hebrews 6:1; 2 Corinthians 13:9; 2 Corinthians 13:11; Ephesians 4:13; Ephesians 4:15; Colossians 1:28; Colossians 4:12; James 1:4; 2 Peter 3:18; Matthew 13:20-21; Mark 4:16-17; Luke 8:13; Galatians 4:8-9; Romans 14:2; 1 Corinthians 3:1-2; Matthew 11:16-19; Luke 7:31-35; Hebrews 5:11-14; 1 Corinthians 3:3; Ephesians 4:14; James 1:6-7; 1 Timothy 3:6; 1 Peter 5:5; Proverbs 28:13; Jeremiah 3:13-22; James 5:16; 1 John 1:9; Proverbs 3:11-12; Psalms 119:67-71; 1 Peter 4:1-2; Revelation 3:19; Psalms 119:97-99; Psalms 119:27; John 17:17-19; Romans 8:26; Hebrews 4:16; James 1:25
* **Associated Articles:** 5652: babies (ID: `5652@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5902: maturity (ID: `5902@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5925: rashness (ID: `5925@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6199: imperfection (ID: `6199@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8205: childlikeness (ID: `8205@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8230: discipline (ID: `8230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8320: perfection (ID: `8320@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8339: self-control (ID: `8339@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8347: spiritual growth (ID: `8347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8755: folly (ID: `8755@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5882: impartiality (id: 5882)

The ability to be unbiased towards any one race, class or person. This attitude is demonstrated by God and is to be emulated by the human race.

God is impartial
----------------

**[DEU 10:17](https://ref.ly/Deut10:17)** *See also* [2CH 19:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:7)

### God treats people impartially

**[MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)** *See also* [JOB 25:3](https://ref.ly/Job25:3), [JOB 34:19](https://ref.ly/Job34:19); [PRO 22:2](https://ref.ly/Prov22:2); [ROM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:11); [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)

### God judges impartially

**[COL 3:25](https://ref.ly/Col3:25)** *See also* [JOB 34:11–34:12](https://ref.ly/Job34:11-Job34:12); [PSA 62:12](https://ref.ly/Ps62:12); [JER 17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:10); [ROM 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:9-Rom2:11); [2CO 5:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:10); [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:17); [REV 22:12](https://ref.ly/Rev22:12)

### God does not distinguish between people on the basis of external appearance

**[1SA 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:7)** *See also* [PSA 147:10–147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10-Ps147:11); [JER 9:25](https://ref.ly/Jer9:25); [LUK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:15); [GAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Gal2:6); [REV 2:23](https://ref.ly/Rev2:23)

### God does not discriminate against different races or classes

**[ACT 10:34–10:35](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34-Acts10:35)**; **[GAL 3:28](https://ref.ly/Gal3:28)** *See also* [DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18); [JOB 34:19](https://ref.ly/Job34:19); [ACT 10:28](https://ref.ly/Acts10:28), [ACT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7-Acts15:9)

The impartiality of Jesus Christ
--------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's teaching was impartial

**[LUK 20:21](https://ref.ly/Luke20:21)** parallels [MAT 22:16](https://ref.ly/Matt22:16), [MRK 12:14](https://ref.ly/Mark12:14) *See also* [MAT 20:1–20:16](https://ref.ly/Matt20:1-Matt20:16) *a parable of Jesus Christ about divine impartiality*

### Jesus Christ showed impartiality in his dealings with people

**[JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9)** *Jesus Christ contravened accepted boundaries of partiality in this encounter.* *See also* [MAT 9:10–9:12](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10-Matt9:12) parallels [MRK 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/Mark2:15-Mark2:17), [LUK 5:29–5:31](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29-Luke5:31); [MAT 15:22–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:26–7:29](https://ref.ly/Mark7:26-Mark7:29); [MAT 20:20–20:23](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20-Matt20:23) parallel [MRK 10:35–10:40](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35-Mark10:40)

Impartiality commended
----------------------

**[JAS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1)** *See also* [PRO 28:21](https://ref.ly/Prov28:21); [1CO 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3-1Cor3:4), [1CO 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:6-1Cor4:7); [1TI 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:21); [JAS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jas2:9)

### Humanity should judge impartially

**[PRO 24:23–24:25](https://ref.ly/Prov24:23-Prov24:25)** *See also* [EXO 18:16](https://ref.ly/Exod18:16); [DEU 1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17), [DEU 16:19](https://ref.ly/Deut16:19), [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17); [PRO 17:15](https://ref.ly/Prov17:15), [PRO 18:5](https://ref.ly/Prov18:5); [MAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mal2:9)

Showing impartiality to all people is commanded
-----------------------------------------------

### Impartiality to foreigners

**[EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21)** *See also* [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9); [LEV 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33-Lev19:34); [NUM 15:15–15:16](https://ref.ly/Num15:15-Num15:16); [ROM 10:12](https://ref.ly/Rom10:12)

### Impartiality to children

[DEU 21:15–21:17](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:17)

### Impartiality to the poor

**[EXO 23:6](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6)** *See also* [EXO 23:3](https://ref.ly/Exod23:3); [LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15); [PRO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Prov29:7); [JAS 2:2–2:7](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2-Jas2:7)

Ways to avoid partiality
------------------------

### By not accepting bribes

**[EXO 23:8](https://ref.ly/Exod23:8)** *See also* [JOB 36:18](https://ref.ly/Job36:18); [PRO 15:27](https://ref.ly/Prov15:27); [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23)

### By not following the crowd

[EXO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:2); ; [MRK 15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:15)

Christians should be partial towards spiritual principles
---------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24)** parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13) *See also* [MAT 12:30](https://ref.ly/Matt12:30) parallel [LUK 11:23](https://ref.ly/Luke11:23); [MAT 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/Matt7:13-Matt7:14); [MRK 9:39–9:40](https://ref.ly/Mark9:39-Mark9:40) parallel [LUK 9:49–9:50](https://ref.ly/Luke9:49-Luke9:50)

Examples where impartiality is absent
-------------------------------------

[GEN 25:27–25:28](https://ref.ly/Gen25:27-Gen25:28) *Isaac and Rebekah to Jacob and Esau*; ; [GEN 29:30–29:32](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30-Gen29:32) *Jacob to Leah and Rachel*; ; [GEN 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3-Gen37:4) *Jacob to Joseph and his brothers*; ; [1SA 1:4–1:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:4-1Sam1:6) *Elkanah to Hannah and Peninnah*

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 10:17; 2 Chronicles 19:7; Matthew 5:45; Job 25:3; Job 34:19; Proverbs 22:2; Romans 2:11; Ephesians 6:9; Colossians 3:25; Job 34:11-12; Psalms 62:12; Jeremiah 17:10; Romans 2:9-11; 2 Corinthians 5:10; 1 Peter 1:17; Revelation 22:12; 1 Samuel 16:7; Psalms 147:10-11; Jeremiah 9:25; Luke 16:15; Galatians 2:6; Revelation 2:23; Acts 10:34-35; Galatians 3:28; Deuteronomy 10:18; Acts 10:28; Acts 15:7-9; Luke 20:21; Matthew 22:16; Mark 12:14; Matthew 20:1-16; John 4:9; Matthew 9:10-12; Mark 2:15-17; Luke 5:29-31; Matthew 15:22-28; Mark 7:26-29; Matthew 20:20-23; Mark 10:35-40; James 2:1; Proverbs 28:21; 1 Corinthians 3:3-4; 1 Corinthians 4:6-7; 1 Timothy 5:21; James 2:9; Proverbs 24:23-25; Exodus 18:16; Deuteronomy 1:17; Deuteronomy 16:19; Deuteronomy 24:17; Proverbs 17:15; Proverbs 18:5; Malachi 2:9; Exodus 22:21; Exodus 23:9; Leviticus 19:33-34; Numbers 15:15-16; Romans 10:12; Deuteronomy 21:15-17; Exodus 23:6; Exodus 23:3; Leviticus 19:15; Proverbs 29:7; James 2:2-7; Exodus 23:8; Job 36:18; Proverbs 15:27; Isaiah 1:23; Exodus 23:2; Mark 15:15; Matthew 6:24; Luke 16:13; Matthew 12:30; Luke 11:23; Matthew 7:13-14; Mark 9:39-40; Luke 9:49-50; Genesis 25:27-28; Genesis 29:30-32; Genesis 37:3-4; 1 Samuel 1:4-6
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1075: God, justice of (ID: `1075@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2042: Jesus Christ, justice of (ID: `2042@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5448: poverty, attitudes towards (ID: `5448@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8207: commitment (ID: `8207@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8277: innocence (ID: `8277@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8324: purity (ID: `8324@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8753: favouritism (ID: `8753@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8800: prejudice (ID: `8800@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5883: impatience (id: 5883)

A refusal to wait for people or developments, frequently displaying a lack of faith.

Examples of impatience
----------------------

[GEN 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/Gen16:1-Gen16:2) *Abraham and Sarah who did not wait for God to give them the son he had promised*; ; [GEN 25:30](https://ref.ly/Gen25:30) *Esau's impatience cost him his birthright*; ; [NUM 20:9–20:11](https://ref.ly/Num20:9-Num20:11) *Moses*; [NUM 21:4](https://ref.ly/Num21:4); ; [PSA 106:13](https://ref.ly/Ps106:13); ; [ISA 59:7](https://ref.ly/Isa59:7); [1SA 13:6–13:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:6-1Sam13:12) *Saul made the offering without waiting for Samuel*; ; [2SA 13:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:2) *Amnon with his half\-sister, Tamar*; ; [2SA 14:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:29) *Absalom with Joab*; ; [2SA 18:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:14) *Joab with Absalom*; ; [2KI 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:11-2Kgs5:12) *Naaman*; ; [2KI 6:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:33) *King Joram*; [JOB 6:11](https://ref.ly/Job6:11), [JOB 21:4](https://ref.ly/Job21:4); [JOB 32:16](https://ref.ly/Job32:16) *Elihu*; ; [MAT 18:29–18:30](https://ref.ly/Matt18:29-Matt18:30) *the unforgiving debtor*; ; [LUK 15:2](https://ref.ly/Luke15:2) *The younger son could not wait for his inheritance*; ; [JHN 21:3](https://ref.ly/John21:3) *Peter*; ; [ACT 20:16](https://ref.ly/Acts20:16) *Paul*; ; [1CO 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:21) *the Corinthian Christians at their communal meal*; ; [REV 6:10–6:11](https://ref.ly/Rev6:10-Rev6:11) *the martyrs waiting for the Lord to avenge their deaths*

Warnings against impatience
---------------------------

**[PSA 37:7](https://ref.ly/Ps37:7)**; **[PRO 19:2](https://ref.ly/Prov19:2)**; **[GAL 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9)**; **[JAS 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19-Jas1:20)** *See also* [PRO 6:18](https://ref.ly/Prov6:18), [PRO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Prov12:16), [PRO 14:29](https://ref.ly/Prov14:29), [PRO 15:18](https://ref.ly/Prov15:18), [PRO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Prov16:32), [PRO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Prov20:3)PRO [21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5), [PRO 28:22](https://ref.ly/Prov28:22)[PRO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Prov25:8) *the need to exercise caution in disputes*[PRO 29:20](https://ref.ly/Prov29:20); [ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2)[ECC 7:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:9), [ECC 8:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:3); [HAB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 16:1-2; Genesis 25:30; Numbers 20:9-11; Numbers 21:4; Psalms 106:13; Isaiah 59:7; 1 Samuel 13:6-12; 2 Samuel 13:2; 2 Samuel 14:29; 2 Samuel 18:14; 2 Kings 5:11-12; 2 Kings 6:33; Job 6:11; Job 21:4; Job 32:16; Matthew 18:29-30; Luke 15:2; John 21:3; Acts 20:16; 1 Corinthians 11:20-21; Revelation 6:10-11; Psalms 37:7; Proverbs 19:2; Galatians 6:9; James 1:19-20; Proverbs 6:18; Proverbs 12:16; Proverbs 14:29; Proverbs 15:18; Proverbs 16:32; Proverbs 20:3; Proverbs 21:5; Proverbs 28:22; Proverbs 25:8; Proverbs 29:20; Ecclesiastes 5:2; Ecclesiastes 7:9; Ecclesiastes 8:3; Habakkuk 2:3
* **Associated Articles:** 1095: God, patience of (ID: `1095@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2060: Jesus Christ, patience of (ID: `2060@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5418: monotony (ID: `5418@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5925: rashness (ID: `5925@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5977: waiting (ID: `5977@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8318: patience (ID: `8318@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8459: perseverance (ID: `8459@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8613: prayer, persistence in (ID: `8613@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8718: disobedience (ID: `8718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8834: unbelief (ID: `8834@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5884: indecision (id: 5884)

Uncertainty, hesitation and doubt reflect a failure of the basic human ability to choose. They reveal an internal debate and lack of conviction.

Indecision arising from doubt and hesitation
--------------------------------------------

**[JAS 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Jas1:6-Jas1:8)** *See also* [1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21); [MAT 14:31](https://ref.ly/Matt14:31), [MAT 21:21](https://ref.ly/Matt21:21); [MRK 11:23](https://ref.ly/Mark11:23); [ROM 14:23](https://ref.ly/Rom14:23)

Examples of indecisive behaviour
--------------------------------

**[EXO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Exod3:11)** *See also* [EXO 4:13](https://ref.ly/Exod4:13), [EXO 14:10–14:15](https://ref.ly/Exod14:10-Exod14:15); [JOS 7:10](https://ref.ly/Josh7:10); [EST 5:8](https://ref.ly/Esth5:8); [MAT 27:21–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:21-Matt27:26) parallels [MRK 15:9–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:9-Mark15:15), [LUK 23:13–23:25](https://ref.ly/Luke23:13-Luke23:25), [JHN 18:38–18:40](https://ref.ly/John18:38-John18:40); [MRK 16:11–16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:11-Mark16:14); [JHN 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25); [ACT 24:25](https://ref.ly/Acts24:25)

Indecision may be indicative of divided loyalty
-----------------------------------------------

**[MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24)** parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13) *See also* [MAT 19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:22), [LUK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:23)

Indecision or uncertainty may be prompted by Satan
--------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 3:1](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1)** *See also* [GEN 3:4](https://ref.ly/Gen3:4); [JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44); [2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3); [REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9)

* **Associated Passages:** James 1:6-8; 1 Kings 18:21; Matthew 14:31; Matthew 21:21; Mark 11:23; Romans 14:23; Exodus 3:11; Exodus 4:13; Exodus 14:10-15; Joshua 7:10; Esther 5:8; Matthew 27:21-26; Mark 15:9-15; Luke 23:13-25; John 18:38-40; Mark 16:11-14; John 20:25; Acts 24:25; Matthew 6:24; Luke 16:13; Matthew 19:22; Mark 10:22; Luke 18:23; Genesis 3:1; Genesis 3:4; John 8:44; 2 Corinthians 11:3; Revelation 12:9
* **Associated Articles:** 4926: delay, human (ID: `4926@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5186: stumbling (ID: `5186@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5877: hesitation (ID: `5877@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6622: choice (ID: `6622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8124: guidance (ID: `8124@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8329: readiness (ID: `8329@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8408: decision-making (ID: `8408@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8615: prayer, doubts about (ID: `8615@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8702: agnosticism (ID: `8702@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8720: double-mindedness (ID: `8720@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8721: doubt (ID: `8721@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8837: unbelief, and life of faith (ID: `8837@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5885: indifference (id: 5885)

A neutral attitude to God that is as dangerous as hostility. It is condemned as a rejection of God's love and of the needs of others.

Indifference towards God
------------------------

### No desire to seek God

**[ROM 3:11](https://ref.ly/Rom3:11)** *See also* [ISA 42:20](https://ref.ly/Isa42:20); [JER 5:21](https://ref.ly/Jer5:21); [ZEP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:6); [MAT 13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:15), [MAT 24:12](https://ref.ly/Matt24:12); [ACT 28:27](https://ref.ly/Acts28:27); [REV 3:15–3:22](https://ref.ly/Rev3:15-Rev3:22)

### Refusal to heed God's word

**[JER 37:2](https://ref.ly/Jer37:2)** *See also* [PSA 81:11](https://ref.ly/Ps81:11); [JER 6:10](https://ref.ly/Jer6:10), [JER 7:13](https://ref.ly/Jer7:13), [JER 25:3–25:7](https://ref.ly/Jer25:3-Jer25:7), [JER 35:15](https://ref.ly/Jer35:15); [EZK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:7); [ZEC 1:4](https://ref.ly/Zech1:4), [ZEC 7:11–7:13](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11-Zech7:13)

### Refusal to respond to God's discipline

**[JER 5:3](https://ref.ly/Jer5:3)** *See also* [JER 2:30](https://ref.ly/Jer2:30), [JER 7:27–7:28](https://ref.ly/Jer7:27-Jer7:28), [JER 17:23](https://ref.ly/Jer17:23), [JER 32:33](https://ref.ly/Jer32:33)

### Ignoring God is rebellion against him

**[EZK 12:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:2)** *See also* [JER 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19), [JER 7:26](https://ref.ly/Jer7:26), [JER 36:24](https://ref.ly/Jer36:24); [EZK 20:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:8)

### People ignore God and his warnings at their peril

**[JER 12:11](https://ref.ly/Jer12:11)** *See also* [EXO 9:21](https://ref.ly/Exod9:21), [EXO 9:25](https://ref.ly/Exod9:25); [2CH 33:10–33:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:10-2Chr33:11); [NEH 9:30](https://ref.ly/Neh9:30); [PRO 1:24–1:27](https://ref.ly/Prov1:24-Prov1:27), [PRO 13:18](https://ref.ly/Prov13:18); [JER 11:8](https://ref.ly/Jer11:8), [JER 36:31](https://ref.ly/Jer36:31), [JER 44:5–44:6](https://ref.ly/Jer44:5-Jer44:6); [MAT 22:5–22:7](https://ref.ly/Matt22:5-Matt22:7)

### For those who ignore God's salvation, there is no hope

**[HEB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Heb2:3)** *See also* [JER 7:24](https://ref.ly/Jer7:24); [MAT 12:30](https://ref.ly/Matt12:30) parallel [LUK 11:23](https://ref.ly/Luke11:23)

Indifference to God's declared intentions
-----------------------------------------

**[EZK 22:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:30)** *See also* [NUM 32:6–32:7](https://ref.ly/Num32:6-Num32:7); [JDG 5:15–5:17](https://ref.ly/Judg5:15-Judg5:17), [JDG 5:23](https://ref.ly/Judg5:23); [2CH 24:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:5); [PSA 22:11](https://ref.ly/Ps22:11); [ISA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Isa64:7); [JER 48:10](https://ref.ly/Jer48:10)

Indifference of religious leaders condemned
-------------------------------------------

[EZK 34:2–34:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:2-Ezek34:4); ; [ZEC 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Zech11:16-Zech11:17); ; [JHN 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/John10:12-John10:13)

Indifference towards the needs of others
----------------------------------------

### Indifference arises from sin

**[PRO 29:7](https://ref.ly/Prov29:7)** *See also* [JOB 21:21](https://ref.ly/Job21:21); [ISA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Isa32:6); [JAS 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Jas2:15-Jas2:16), [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

### Examples of indifference towards others

[GEN 40:23](https://ref.ly/Gen40:23) *towards Joseph*; ; [1SA 25:10–25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:10-1Sam25:11) *towards David*; [MAT 26:40](https://ref.ly/Matt26:40) parallels [MRK 14:37](https://ref.ly/Mark14:37), [LUK 22:45](https://ref.ly/Luke22:45); [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10), [2TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16)

Jesus Christ condemns indifference
----------------------------------

**[MAT 25:41–25:45](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41-Matt25:45)** *See also* [LUK 10:30–10:32](https://ref.ly/Luke10:30-Luke10:32), [LUK 16:19–16:21](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19-Luke16:21)

Jesus Christ's apparent indifference
------------------------------------

**[MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38)** parallels [MAT 8:24–8:25](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24-Matt8:25), [LUK 8:23–8:24](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23-Luke8:24) *Jesus Christ sometimes delayed responding to cries for help in order to draw out faith.* *See also* [MAT 15:26](https://ref.ly/Matt15:26) parallel [MRK 7:27](https://ref.ly/Mark7:27); [LUK 10:40](https://ref.ly/Luke10:40); [JHN 11:6](https://ref.ly/John11:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 3:11; Isaiah 42:20; Jeremiah 5:21; Zephaniah 1:6; Matthew 13:15; Matthew 24:12; Acts 28:27; Revelation 3:15-22; Jeremiah 37:2; Psalms 81:11; Jeremiah 6:10; Jeremiah 7:13; Jeremiah 25:3-7; Jeremiah 35:15; Ezekiel 3:7; Zechariah 1:4; Zechariah 7:11-13; Jeremiah 5:3; Jeremiah 2:30; Jeremiah 7:27-28; Jeremiah 17:23; Jeremiah 32:33; Ezekiel 12:2; Jeremiah 2:19; Jeremiah 7:26; Jeremiah 36:24; Ezekiel 20:8; Jeremiah 12:11; Exodus 9:21; Exodus 9:25; 2 Chronicles 33:10-11; Nehemiah 9:30; Proverbs 1:24-27; Proverbs 13:18; Jeremiah 11:8; Jeremiah 36:31; Jeremiah 44:5-6; Matthew 22:5-7; Hebrews 2:3; Jeremiah 7:24; Matthew 12:30; Luke 11:23; Ezekiel 22:30; Numbers 32:6-7; Judges 5:15-17; Judges 5:23; 2 Chronicles 24:5; Psalms 22:11; Isaiah 64:7; Jeremiah 48:10; Ezekiel 34:2-4; Zechariah 11:16-17; John 10:12-13; Proverbs 29:7; Job 21:21; Isaiah 32:6; James 2:15-16; James 5:1-6; Genesis 40:23; 1 Samuel 25:10-11; Matthew 26:40; Mark 14:37; Luke 22:45; 2 Timothy 4:10; 2 Timothy 4:16; Matthew 25:41-45; Luke 10:30-32; Luke 16:19-21; Mark 4:38; Matthew 8:24-25; Luke 8:23-24; Matthew 15:26; Mark 7:27; Luke 10:40; John 11:6

## 5886: individualism (id: 5886)

The recognition of individuality, of the differences between particular people, and of their special gifts, needs and character. In a negative sense, the term can mean placing the needs of individuals above those of the church as a whole, which is contrary to the biblical emphasis on all believers sharing in the common ministry of the body of Christ.

Individualism stresses the differences between people
-----------------------------------------------------

### Recognising people's special gifts

**[1CO 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:4-1Cor12:6)** *See also* [ROM 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Rom12:6-Rom12:8); [1CO 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:8-1Cor12:11), [1CO 12:28–12:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:28-1Cor12:30); [EPH 4:11](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11); [HEB 2:4](https://ref.ly/Heb2:4); [1PE 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10)

### Recognising people's abilities and opportunities

**[MAT 25:14–25:15](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:15)** *See also* [LUK 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:13); [1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7)

Individualism and the church
----------------------------

### Recognising the parts people play in the church, the body of Christ

**[1CO 12:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:12)** *See also* [ROM 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/Rom12:4-Rom12:5); [1CO 12:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:14), [1CO 12:17–12:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:17-1Cor12:20), [1CO 12:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:27)

### Individualism must not emphasise the individual at the expense of the church as a whole

**[1CO 12:21–12:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:21-1Cor12:26)** *See also* [ROM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rom12:3), [ROM 12:10](https://ref.ly/Rom12:10), [ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16); [PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3)

### Contrary to self\-seeking individualism, believers should share in the common ministry of the body of Christ

**[ACT 2:42](https://ref.ly/Acts2:42)** *See also* [ACT 2:44](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44), [ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35); [2CO 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:13-2Cor8:14), [2CO 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:13); [HEB 13:16](https://ref.ly/Heb13:16)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Corinthians 12:4-6; Romans 12:6-8; 1 Corinthians 12:8-11; 1 Corinthians 12:28-30; Ephesians 4:11; Hebrews 2:4; 1 Peter 4:10; Matthew 25:14-15; Luke 19:12-13; 1 Corinthians 4:7; 1 Corinthians 12:12; Romans 12:4-5; 1 Corinthians 12:14; 1 Corinthians 12:17-20; 1 Corinthians 12:27; 1 Corinthians 12:21-26; Romans 12:3; Romans 12:10; Romans 12:16; Philippians 2:3; Acts 2:42; Acts 2:44; Acts 4:34-35; 2 Corinthians 8:13-14; 2 Corinthians 9:13; Hebrews 13:16
* **Associated Articles:** 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church (ID: `3275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5308: equality (ID: `5308@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5325: gifts (ID: `5325@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5937: rivalry (ID: `5937@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7025: church, unity and fellowship of (ID: `7025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7032: unity, of God's people (ID: `7032@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7110: body of Christ (ID: `7110@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7206: community (ID: `7206@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7921: fellowship (ID: `7921@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7943: ministry, in the church (ID: `7943@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8437: giving, of talents (ID: `8437@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8827: selfishness (ID: `8827@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5887: inexperience (id: 5887)

Lack of experience of life or a particular aspect of life. Inexperience is often associated with the young who are thus vulnerable and who should be taught by those older and wiser. God sometimes calls the inexperienced to leadership but usually also provides encouragement and support from more experienced leaders.

The inexperience of youth
-------------------------

**[1SA 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:4-1Sam3:7)**; **[JER 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6)** *See also* [DEU 1:39](https://ref.ly/Deut1:39); [1KI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:7); [1CH 29:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1); [2CH 10:8–10:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:8-2Chr10:11) parallel [1KI 12:8–12:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:8-1Kgs12:11); [2CH 13:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:7); [ISA 7:15–7:16](https://ref.ly/Isa7:15-Isa7:16)

The inexperienced may be led astray
-----------------------------------

**[2KI 17:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:41)** *Children often follow in the ways of their parents*; **[MAT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Matt18:6)** *See also* [1KI 22:53](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:53); [2KI 24:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:9); [MRK 9:42](https://ref.ly/Mark9:42); [LUK 17:2](https://ref.ly/Luke17:2)

God's call to the inexperienced
-------------------------------

**[JER 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Jer1:7-Jer1:8)** *See also* [1SA 16:11–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:11-1Sam16:13) *David, Jesse's youngest son, is chosen by God*; [1SA 17:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:33) *David's inexperience in the face of Goliath*; [1TI 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:12) *Timothy was called to pastor a church despite his youth. In general, however, church leaders should be people of experience (see [1Ti 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:6)).*

Support for the inexperienced
-----------------------------

### Teaching the inexperienced

**[DEU 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9-Deut4:10)**; **[JOB 8:8–8:9](https://ref.ly/Job8:8-Job8:9)** *Each generation learns from the wisdom and experience of those going before.* *See also* [DEU 11:2–11:7](https://ref.ly/Deut11:2-Deut11:7), [DEU 31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:13); [JOB 32:6–32:7](https://ref.ly/Job32:6-Job32:7); [PSA 71:18](https://ref.ly/Ps71:18), [PSA 78:3–78:5](https://ref.ly/Ps78:3-Ps78:5); [PRO 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Prov5:1-Prov5:2); [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4)

### Examples of help given to the inexperienced

**[1CH 22:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:5)** *See also* [DEU 1:38](https://ref.ly/Deut1:38) *Moses is to encourage his young successor, Joshua*; [JDG 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Judg3:1-Judg3:2) *teaching warfare to those who have no experience of it*; [1SA 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:8-1Sam3:10) *Eli instructs the inexperienced Samuel*; [1KI 19:19–19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:19-1Kgs19:21) *Elisha becomes Elijah's attendant*; [1CH 28:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:20) *David encourages Solomon to build the temple*; [PRO 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Prov4:3-Prov4:4) *Solomon recalls the instruction given by his father*; [ACT 15:39](https://ref.ly/Acts15:39) *Barnabas takes John Mark with him*; [2TI 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:14-2Tim3:15) *Paul reminds Timothy of the example he has been given.*

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 3:4-7; Jeremiah 1:6; Deuteronomy 1:39; 1 Kings 3:7; 1 Chronicles 29:1; 2 Chronicles 10:8-11; 1 Kings 12:8-11; 2 Chronicles 13:7; Isaiah 7:15-16; 2 Kings 17:41; Matthew 18:6; 1 Kings 22:53; 2 Kings 24:9; Mark 9:42; Luke 17:2; Jeremiah 1:7-8; 1 Samuel 16:11-13; 1 Samuel 17:33; 1 Timothy 4:12; 1 Timothy 3:6; Deuteronomy 4:9-10; Job 8:8-9; Deuteronomy 11:2-7; Deuteronomy 31:13; Job 32:6-7; Psalms 71:18; Psalms 78:3-5; Proverbs 5:1-2; Ephesians 6:4; 1 Chronicles 22:5; Deuteronomy 1:38; Judges 3:1-2; 1 Samuel 3:8-10; 1 Kings 19:19-21; 1 Chronicles 28:20; Proverbs 4:3-4; Acts 15:39; 2 Timothy 3:14-15
* **Associated Articles:** 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5664: children (ID: `5664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5725: old age (ID: `5725@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5731: parents (ID: `5731@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5746: youth (ID: `5746@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5853: experience, of life (ID: `5853@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5949: shyness (ID: `5949@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6181: ignorance (ID: `6181@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8277: innocence (ID: `8277@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8414: encouragement (ID: `8414@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5888: inferiority (id: 5888)

A discouraging sense of being of less importance or value in comparison with others.

Inferiority as a depressing lack of self\-worth
-----------------------------------------------

**[JDG 6:15](https://ref.ly/Judg6:15)**; **[PSA 22:6](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6)**; **[1CO 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:3)** *See also* [1SA 18:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:23); [NEH 4:4](https://ref.ly/Neh4:4), [NEH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:9); [PSA 31:9–31:10](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9-Ps31:10), [PSA 70:5](https://ref.ly/Ps70:5), [PSA 74:21](https://ref.ly/Ps74:21), [PSA 86:1](https://ref.ly/Ps86:1), [PSA 109:22](https://ref.ly/Ps109:22), [PSA 119:25](https://ref.ly/Ps119:25), [PSA 119:141](https://ref.ly/Ps119:141); [ECC 9:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:16); [ISA 33:8](https://ref.ly/Isa33:8); [JER 22:28](https://ref.ly/Jer22:28); [LAM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Lam1:11); [MAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mal2:9)

Inferiority as a healthy sense of unworthiness
----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 6:5](https://ref.ly/Isa6:5)**; **[MAT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Matt5:3)**; **[LUK 5:8](https://ref.ly/Luke5:8)** *See also* [2SA 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:13); [JOB 42:6](https://ref.ly/Job42:6); [PSA 38:1–38:22](https://ref.ly/Ps38:1-Ps38:22), [PSA 51:3–51:5](https://ref.ly/Ps51:3-Ps51:5); [REV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rev1:17), [REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)

God's judgment will bring inferiority on on proud nations
---------------------------------------------------------

**[OBA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Obad1:2)** *See also* [ISA 16:14](https://ref.ly/Isa16:14); [JER 49:15](https://ref.ly/Jer49:15); [EZK 29:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:15)

Refusing to be intimidated by a sense of inferiority
----------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 12:3](https://ref.ly/Job12:3)**; **[1TI 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:12)** *See also* [JOB 13:2](https://ref.ly/Job13:2); [2CO 11:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:5), [2CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:11); [TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Titus2:15)

God values those regarded as inferior and uses them
---------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Deut7:7-Deut7:8)**; **[1CO 1:27–1:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:27-1Cor1:28)** *See also* [1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8); [PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5), [PSA 34:6](https://ref.ly/Ps34:6), [PSA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:10), [PSA 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ps36:7), [PSA 40:17](https://ref.ly/Ps40:17), [PSA 72:13](https://ref.ly/Ps72:13), [PSA 136:23](https://ref.ly/Ps136:23), [PSA 140:12](https://ref.ly/Ps140:12); [ISA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Isa11:4), [ISA 25:4](https://ref.ly/Isa25:4), [ISA 29:19](https://ref.ly/Isa29:19), [ISA 41:14](https://ref.ly/Isa41:14), [ISA 49:7](https://ref.ly/Isa49:7), [ISA 61:1–61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1-Isa61:3); [HOS 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1-Hos11:4)

Jesus Christ valued those traditionally regarded as inferior
------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 8:2–8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:4) *the diseased and ritually unclean*; ; [MAT 9:10–9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:10-Matt9:13) *the morally unacceptable*; ; [MAT 15:21–15:29](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:29) *non\-Jews*; ; [MAT 19:13–19:14](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:14) *children*; ; [LUK 18:13](https://ref.ly/Luke18:13) *those who are ashamed of their sins*; ; [JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9) *women*

Believers are to help and encourage those who feel inferior
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Luke14:13)**; **[ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16)** *See also* [PSA 82:3–82:4](https://ref.ly/Ps82:3-Ps82:4); [1TH 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:14); [HEB 12:12–12:13](https://ref.ly/Heb12:12-Heb12:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 6:15; Psalms 22:6; 1 Corinthians 2:3; 1 Samuel 18:23; Nehemiah 4:4; Nehemiah 6:9; Psalms 31:9-10; Psalms 70:5; Psalms 74:21; Psalms 86:1; Psalms 109:22; Psalms 119:25; Psalms 119:141; Ecclesiastes 9:16; Isaiah 33:8; Jeremiah 22:28; Lamentations 1:11; Malachi 2:9; Isaiah 6:5; Matthew 5:3; Luke 5:8; 2 Samuel 12:13; Job 42:6; Psalms 38:1-22; Psalms 51:3-5; Revelation 1:17; Revelation 3:17; Obadiah 1:2; Isaiah 16:14; Jeremiah 49:15; Ezekiel 29:15; Job 12:3; 1 Timothy 4:12; Job 13:2; 2 Corinthians 11:5; 2 Corinthians 12:11; Titus 2:15; Deuteronomy 7:7-8; 1 Corinthians 1:27-28; 1 Samuel 2:8; Psalms 12:5; Psalms 34:6; Psalms 35:10; Psalms 36:7; Psalms 40:17; Psalms 72:13; Psalms 136:23; Psalms 140:12; Isaiah 11:4; Isaiah 25:4; Isaiah 29:19; Isaiah 41:14; Isaiah 49:7; Isaiah 61:1-3; Hosea 11:1-4; Matthew 8:2-4; Matthew 9:10-13; Matthew 15:21-29; Matthew 19:13-14; Luke 18:13; John 4:9; Luke 14:13; Romans 12:16; Psalms 82:3-4; 1 Thessalonians 5:14; Hebrews 12:12-13
* **Associated Articles:** 4050: dust (ID: `4050@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5916: pessimism (ID: `5916@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5961: superiority (ID: `5961@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6151: dirt (ID: `6151@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8276: humility (ID: `8276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8305: meekness (ID: `8305@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8340: self-respect (ID: `8340@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8357: weakness (ID: `8357@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8713: discouragement (ID: `8713@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8741: failure (ID: `8741@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5889: ingratitude (id: 5889)

The absence of due thankfulness. Scripture gives examples of human ingratitude to God for all the blessings and gifts which he provides for them. It is especially important for Christians to give thanks to God.

The ingratitude of human beings to one another
----------------------------------------------

### Examples of ingratitude

[GEN 40:23](https://ref.ly/Gen40:23) *the chief butler towards Joseph*; ; [NUM 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/Num16:13-Num16:14) *Dathan and Abiram to Moses*; [JDG 8:35](https://ref.ly/Judg8:35), [JDG 9:18](https://ref.ly/Judg9:18); [1SA 25:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:21) *Nabal to David*; ; [2CH 24:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:22) *King Joash to Jehoiada*; ; [ECC 9:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:15) *the citizens to the wise man who delivered a city*

### Returning evil for good

**[PSA 35:12](https://ref.ly/Ps35:12)** *See also* [GEN 44:4](https://ref.ly/Gen44:4); [PSA 109:5](https://ref.ly/Ps109:5); [PRO 17:13](https://ref.ly/Prov17:13); [JER 18:20](https://ref.ly/Jer18:20)

Ingratitude to God
------------------

### It was in evidence at the fall

[GEN 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/Gen3:2-Gen3:3) *Eve showed ingratitude for God's provision by not believing him.*

### It is part of fallen human nature

**[ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21)**

### It is a sign of the last days

*See also* [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2)

### Israel's repeated ingratitude to the LORD

For deliverance from Egypt: [EXO 16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:3), [EXO 16:8](https://ref.ly/Exod16:8), [EXO 17:7](https://ref.ly/Exod17:7); ; [HEB 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7-Heb3:9), [HEB 3:15](https://ref.ly/Heb3:15); ; [PSA 95:7–95:9](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:9); [DEU 32:6](https://ref.ly/Deut32:6) *They were rebuked by Moses for the way they repaid God*; ; [1SA 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:7-1Sam8:8) *They rejected the LORD when they rejected Samuel*; ; [NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26) *They rejected God's law and killed his prophets.*

Ingratitude compared with other sins
------------------------------------

### Becoming like animals

[ISA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Isa1:3)

### Becoming like prostitutes

[EZK 16:17–16:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:17-Ezek16:19)

### Becoming like an adulterous wife

[HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8), [HOS 4:12](https://ref.ly/Hos4:12), [HOS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Hos5:4), [HOS 9:1](https://ref.ly/Hos9:1)

Jesus Christ exposed ingratitude
--------------------------------

[LUK 17:15–17:18](https://ref.ly/Luke17:15-Luke17:18)

God is kind even to the ungrateful
----------------------------------

[LUK 6:35](https://ref.ly/Luke6:35)

It is important for believers to show gratitude to God
------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4)** *See also* [PSA 107:22](https://ref.ly/Ps107:22); [COL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Col1:12), [COL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Col3:15); [1TH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:18)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 40:23; Numbers 16:13-14; Judges 8:35; Judges 9:18; 1 Samuel 25:21; 2 Chronicles 24:22; Ecclesiastes 9:15; Psalms 35:12; Genesis 44:4; Psalms 109:5; Proverbs 17:13; Jeremiah 18:20; Genesis 3:2-3; Romans 1:21; 2 Timothy 3:2; Exodus 16:3; Exodus 16:8; Exodus 17:7; Hebrews 3:7-9; Hebrews 3:15; Psalms 95:7-9; Deuteronomy 32:6; 1 Samuel 8:7-8; Nehemiah 9:26; Isaiah 1:3; Ezekiel 16:17-19; Hosea 2:8; Hosea 4:12; Hosea 5:4; Hosea 9:1; Luke 17:15-18; Luke 6:35; Psalms 100:4; Psalms 107:22; Colossians 1:12; Colossians 3:15; 1 Thessalonians 5:18
* **Associated Articles:** 1055: God, grace and mercy of (ID: `1055@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8352: thankfulness (ID: `8352@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8676: thanksgiving (ID: `8676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8764: forgetting God (ID: `8764@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5890: insecurity (id: 5890)

Lack of security, often in relation to one's future or to one's present position. All human life is insecure, but the future of sinners is particularly dangerous and real security is found only in God.

The insecurity of human life
----------------------------

**[ISA 40:6–40:8](https://ref.ly/Isa40:6-Isa40:8)** *See also* [JOB 1:13–1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:13-Job1:21); [PSA 90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:10), [PSA 146:3–146:6](https://ref.ly/Ps146:3-Ps146:6); [ECC 9:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:1), [ECC 9:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:12), [ECC 10:8–10:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:8-Eccl10:9); [JER 51:58](https://ref.ly/Jer51:58); [1TH 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:1-1Thess5:3)

The insecurity of the sinner's life
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 37:1–37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:1-Ps37:2)** *See also* [PSA 37:9–37:11](https://ref.ly/Ps37:9-Ps37:11); [PRO 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/Prov6:12-Prov6:15), [PRO 11:5](https://ref.ly/Prov11:5), [PRO 24:19–24:22](https://ref.ly/Prov24:19-Prov24:22), [PRO 29:1](https://ref.ly/Prov29:1); [ISA 30:12–30:14](https://ref.ly/Isa30:12-Isa30:14), [ISA 47:8–47:11](https://ref.ly/Isa47:8-Isa47:11); [JER 6:13–6:15](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13-Jer6:15), [JER 15:5–15:9](https://ref.ly/Jer15:5-Jer15:9), [JER 49:16–49:18](https://ref.ly/Jer49:16-Jer49:18); [AMO 8:14](https://ref.ly/Amos8:14)

The insecurity of the rich
--------------------------

**[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24); [LUK 12:15–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15-Luke12:21); [1TI 6:7–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7-1Tim6:10)

Examples of behaviour motivated by insecurity
---------------------------------------------

### Saul's vendetta against David

**[1SA 18:6–18:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:12)** *See also* [1SA 18:17–18:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17-1Sam18:29), [1SA 19:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:1), [1SA 19:9–19:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:9-1Sam19:17), [1SA 20:30–20:31](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:30-1Sam20:31)

### Assassination of political rivals

**[2KI 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:1)** parallel [2CH 22:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr22:10) *See also* [JDG 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Judg9:1-Judg9:6)

True security is to be found in God alone
-----------------------------------------

**[PRO 14:26](https://ref.ly/Prov14:26)** *See also* [DEU 33:12](https://ref.ly/Deut33:12); [1KI 2:45](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:45); [PSA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Ps16:5), [PSA 48:8](https://ref.ly/Ps48:8); [PRO 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Prov3:5-Prov3:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 40:6-8; Job 1:13-21; Psalms 90:10; Psalms 146:3-6; Ecclesiastes 9:1; Ecclesiastes 9:12; Ecclesiastes 10:8-9; Jeremiah 51:58; 1 Thessalonians 5:1-3; Psalms 37:1-2; Psalms 37:9-11; Proverbs 6:12-15; Proverbs 11:5; Proverbs 24:19-22; Proverbs 29:1; Isaiah 30:12-14; Isaiah 47:8-11; Jeremiah 6:13-15; Jeremiah 15:5-9; Jeremiah 49:16-18; Amos 8:14; 1 Timothy 6:17; Proverbs 27:24; Luke 12:15-21; 1 Timothy 6:7-10; 1 Samuel 18:6-12; 1 Samuel 18:17-29; 1 Samuel 19:1; 1 Samuel 19:9-17; 1 Samuel 20:30-31; 2 Kings 11:1; 2 Chronicles 22:10; Judges 9:1-6; Proverbs 14:26; Deuteronomy 33:12; 1 Kings 2:45; Psalms 16:5; Psalms 48:8; Proverbs 3:5-6

## 5891: instability (id: 5891)

-----------------------------------------------

**[EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)**; **[JAS 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Jas1:6-Jas1:8)** *See also* [MAT 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26-Matt7:27); [2PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:14), [2PE 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:16)

Firmness of faith as an antidote to instability
-----------------------------------------------

**[1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58)** *See also* [MAT 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:25); [COL 1:23](https://ref.ly/Col1:23); [1PE 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10)

Political instability
---------------------

**[PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4)**

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 4:14; James 1:6-8; Matthew 7:26-27; 2 Peter 2:14; 2 Peter 3:16; 1 Corinthians 15:58; Matthew 7:24-25; Colossians 1:23; 1 Peter 5:10; Proverbs 29:4

## 5892: instinct (id: 5892)

An innate urge, rooted in natural conditioning rather than reason. Scripture likens sinners to creatures of instinct.

Animals are creatures of instinct
---------------------------------

**[2PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:12)** *See also* [JUD 1:10](https://ref.ly/Jude1:10)

Sinners are likened to creatures of instinct
--------------------------------------------

**[JUD 1:10](https://ref.ly/Jude1:10)**

People without the Holy Spirit are likened to creatures of instinct
-------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JUD 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jude1:19)**

Believers ought instinctively to obey God
-----------------------------------------

**[ISA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Isa1:3)** *Just as the animals instinctively know their masters, so Israel should know and obey God.* *See also* [JER 8:7](https://ref.ly/Jer8:7)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Peter 2:12; Jude 1:10; Jude 1:19; Isaiah 1:3; Jeremiah 8:7
* **Associated Articles:** 4604: animals, nature of (ID: `4604@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5050: reason (ID: `5050@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5769: behaviour (ID: `5769@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5800: blasphemy (ID: `5800@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5832: desire (ID: `5832@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6040: sinners (ID: `6040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6257: unbelievers (ID: `6257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7130: flock, God's (ID: `7130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8454: obedience, to God (ID: `8454@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5893: insults (id: 5893)

Expressions of an individual's hostility or contempt for God and his servants or fellow human beings. Believers are to follow Jesus Christ's example in not returning insults.

Insults to God
--------------

### Insults to God coming from open unbelief

[2KI 19:14–19:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:14-2Kgs19:16), [2KI 19:20–19:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:20-2Kgs19:23) parallels [2CH 32:16–32:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:16-2Chr32:19), [ISA 37:14–37:17](https://ref.ly/Isa37:14-Isa37:17), [ISA 37:21–37:24](https://ref.ly/Isa37:21-Isa37:24); ; [HEB 10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:29)

### Insults to God coming through the suffering of believers

**[PSA 69:7–69:9](https://ref.ly/Ps69:7-Ps69:9)** *See also* [JER 20:7–20:8](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7-Jer20:8); [ROM 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rom15:3); [HEB 13:12–13:13](https://ref.ly/Heb13:12-Heb13:13)

### Insults to God lamented by the psalmist

**[PSA 74:18](https://ref.ly/Ps74:18), [PSA 74:22–74:23](https://ref.ly/Ps74:22-Ps74:23)** *See also* [PSA 10:3](https://ref.ly/Ps10:3)

Jesus Christ insulted
---------------------

**[LUK 18:31–18:33](https://ref.ly/Luke18:31-Luke18:33)** parallels [MAT 20:17–20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:17-Matt20:19), [MRK 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:32-Mark10:34) *See also* [MAT 26:67–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67-Matt26:68) parallels [MRK 14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:65), [LUK 22:63–22:65](https://ref.ly/Luke22:63-Luke22:65); [MAT 27:39–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39-Matt27:44) parallels [MRK 15:29–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29-Mark15:32), [LUK 23:35–23:39](https://ref.ly/Luke23:35-Luke23:39); [JHN 8:48–8:49](https://ref.ly/John8:48-John8:49), [JHN 18:22–18:23](https://ref.ly/John18:22-John18:23); [1PE 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:23)

Israel insulted
---------------

**[JER 51:51](https://ref.ly/Jer51:51)** *See also* [1SA 17:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:26); [2SA 21:20–21:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:20-2Sam21:21) parallel [1CH 20:6–20:7](https://ref.ly/1Chr20:6-1Chr20:7); [EZK 21:28–21:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:28-Ezek21:32); [ZEP 2:8–2:10](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:8-Zeph2:10)

Kings, prophets and others insulted
-----------------------------------

[1SA 10:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:27), [1SA 25:9–25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:9-1Sam25:11), [1SA 25:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:14); ; [2SA 10:1–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:1-2Sam10:5) parallel [1CH 19:1–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:1-1Chr19:5); ; [2KI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:23-2Kgs2:24); ; [NEH 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/Neh4:1-Neh4:5); ; [ECC 10:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:20)

The poor insulted
-----------------

[1CO 11:20–11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:22); ; [JAS 2:1–2:7](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:7)

Christians insulted
-------------------

**[ACT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Acts18:6)**; **[1PE 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:14)** *See also* [ACT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Acts2:13), [ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32), [ACT 19:8–19:9](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8-Acts19:9); [1PE 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:4)

The pain of insult
------------------

**[PSA 55:12–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:12-Ps55:14)** *See also* [PSA 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6-Ps22:8); [JOB 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/Job19:1-Job19:3), [JOB 20:2–20:3](https://ref.ly/Job20:2-Job20:3); [PSA 44:16](https://ref.ly/Ps44:16), [PSA 55:3](https://ref.ly/Ps55:3), [PSA 89:50–89:51](https://ref.ly/Ps89:50-Ps89:51), [PSA 109:21–109:25](https://ref.ly/Ps109:21-Ps109:25); [LAM 3:55–3:63](https://ref.ly/Lam3:55-Lam3:63); [1TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2); [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rev2:9)

Dealing with insults
--------------------

### Avoiding insults

[PRO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Prov9:7), [PRO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Prov22:10)

### Not repaying insults

**[PRO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Prov12:16)** *See also* [MAT 5:39](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39) parallel [LUK 6:27–6:29](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27-Luke6:29); [1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9)

### Rejoicing in reproach for Jesus Christ's sake

**[MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12)** parallel [LUK 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Luke6:22-Luke6:23) *See also* [ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:41); [2CO 12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:10); [HEB 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32-Heb10:34); [1PE 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:13-1Pet4:14)

### Insult supposed but not intended

[LUK 11:45](https://ref.ly/Luke11:45); ; [ACT 23:1–23:5](https://ref.ly/Acts23:1-Acts23:5)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Kings 19:14-16; 2 Kings 19:20-23; 2 Chronicles 32:16-19; Isaiah 37:14-17; Isaiah 37:21-24; Hebrews 10:29; Psalms 69:7-9; Jeremiah 20:7-8; Romans 15:3; Hebrews 13:12-13; Psalms 74:18; Psalms 74:22-23; Psalms 10:3; Luke 18:31-33; Matthew 20:17-19; Mark 10:32-34; Matthew 26:67-68; Mark 14:65; Luke 22:63-65; Matthew 27:39-44; Mark 15:29-32; Luke 23:35-39; John 8:48-49; John 18:22-23; 1 Peter 2:23; Jeremiah 51:51; 1 Samuel 17:26; 2 Samuel 21:20-21; 1 Chronicles 20:6-7; Ezekiel 21:28-32; Zephaniah 2:8-10; 1 Samuel 10:27; 1 Samuel 25:9-11; 1 Samuel 25:14; 2 Samuel 10:1-5; 1 Chronicles 19:1-5; 2 Kings 2:23-24; Nehemiah 4:1-5; Ecclesiastes 10:20; 1 Corinthians 11:20-22; James 2:1-7; Acts 18:6; 1 Peter 4:14; Acts 2:13; Acts 17:32; Acts 19:8-9; 1 Peter 4:4; Psalms 55:12-14; Psalms 22:6-8; Job 19:1-3; Job 20:2-3; Psalms 44:16; Psalms 55:3; Psalms 89:50-51; Psalms 109:21-25; Lamentations 3:55-63; 1 Thessalonians 2:2; Revelation 2:9; Proverbs 9:7; Proverbs 22:10; Proverbs 12:16; Matthew 5:39; Luke 6:27-29; 1 Peter 3:9; Matthew 5:11-12; Luke 6:22-23; Acts 5:41; 2 Corinthians 12:10; Hebrews 10:32-34; 1 Peter 4:13-14; Luke 11:45; Acts 23:1-5

## 5894: intelligence (id: 5894)

The natural ability to acquire knowledge and to learn skills. It is not emphasised in Scripture because wisdom is viewed primarily in spiritual and moral terms. Human cleverness proves ultimately futile; nevertheless, believers are called to develop and use their minds to the full in God's service and to dedicate gifts and abilities to him.

Wisdom is primarily spiritual and moral
---------------------------------------

**[PRO 9:10](https://ref.ly/Prov9:10)** *See also* [JOB 28:28](https://ref.ly/Job28:28); [PSA 111:10](https://ref.ly/Ps111:10); [JAS 3:13](https://ref.ly/Jas3:13)

The spiritual and moral inadequacy of unaided human intelligence
----------------------------------------------------------------

### Unaided human intelligence proves futile

**[PSA 94:11](https://ref.ly/Ps94:11)** *See also* [ECC 9:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:11), [ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12); [DAN 12:4](https://ref.ly/Dan12:4); [ACT 17:21](https://ref.ly/Acts17:21); [1CO 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:20)

### Human intelligence does not bring knowledge of God

**[1CO 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20-1Cor1:21)** *See also* [JHN 5:39–5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:39-John5:40); [ROM 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:22-Rom1:23); [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)

### Human intelligence cannot promote godliness

**[COL 2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:23)** *See also* [1CO 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:1-1Cor8:2); [JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16)

### Reliance on human intelligence brings judgment

**[ISA 29:14](https://ref.ly/Isa29:14)** *See also* [JOB 5:13](https://ref.ly/Job5:13); [ISA 44:25](https://ref.ly/Isa44:25); [1CO 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:19)

Many of the first Christians were not intelligent
-------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:26–1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:26-1Cor1:31)** *See also* [ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13)

Believers are to use their minds
--------------------------------

### The mind to be used in serving God

**[MRK 12:30](https://ref.ly/Mark12:30)** parallels [MAT 22:37](https://ref.ly/Matt22:37), [LUK 10:27](https://ref.ly/Luke10:27) *See also* [1CO 7:37](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:37) *The mind is to be used in seeking to discern God's will*; [1CO 14:15–14:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:15-1Cor14:18) *The mind is to be used in worship.*

### God wants to renew the mind

**[ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2)** *See also* [EPH 4:23](https://ref.ly/Eph4:23); [HEB 10:16](https://ref.ly/Heb10:16); [JER 31:33](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33)

### The mind must be correctly focused

[ROM 8:5–8:6](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5-Rom8:6); ; [PHP 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6-Phil4:7); ; [COL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Col3:2)

Examples of intelligent people
------------------------------

[1SA 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:3) *Abigail*; ; [2CH 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:12) *Solomon*; ; [DAN 1:4](https://ref.ly/Dan1:4) *young men from Israel exiled in Babylon*; ; [DAN 1:17](https://ref.ly/Dan1:17) *Daniel, Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego*; ; [ACT 5:34](https://ref.ly/Acts5:34) *Gamaliel*; ; [ACT 7:22](https://ref.ly/Acts7:22) *Moses*; ; [ACT 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/Acts13:6-Acts13:7) *Sergius Paulus*; ; [ACT 26:24](https://ref.ly/Acts26:24) *Paul*

Examples of believers using their minds
---------------------------------------

**[ECC 8:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:9)** *See also* [EZR 7:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:10) *Ezra*; [ECC 7:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:25) *the Teacher*; [DAN 10:12](https://ref.ly/Dan10:12) *Daniel*; [ACT 17:11](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11) *the Bereans*; [2TI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15) *Paul's expectation for Timothy*

God uses intelligence dedicated to him
--------------------------------------

[GEN 41:46–41:49](https://ref.ly/Gen41:46-Gen41:49) *God uses Joseph's administrative skill*; ; [EXO 31:2–31:6](https://ref.ly/Exod31:2-Exod31:6) parallel [EXO 35:30–35:35](https://ref.ly/Exod35:30-Exod35:35) *Craftsmen use artistic expertise*; ; [1KI 7:13–7:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs7:13-1Kgs7:14) parallel [2CH 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr2:13-2Chr2:14) *Huram does work in bronze for Solomon's temple*; ; [2CH 26:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:15) *Skilful men design machines for the defence of Jerusalem*; ; [DAN 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/Dan5:13-Dan5:14) *Daniel's intelligence aids his witness in the Babylonian court*; ; [LUK 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Luke1:3-Luke1:4) *Luke's thorough research owes something to his medical training.*

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 9:10; Job 28:28; Psalms 111:10; James 3:13; Psalms 94:11; Ecclesiastes 9:11; Ecclesiastes 12:12; Daniel 12:4; Acts 17:21; 1 Corinthians 3:20; 1 Corinthians 1:20-21; John 5:39-40; Romans 1:22-23; Colossians 2:8; Colossians 2:23; 1 Corinthians 8:1-2; James 3:14-16; Isaiah 29:14; Job 5:13; Isaiah 44:25; 1 Corinthians 1:19; 1 Corinthians 1:26-31; Acts 4:13; Mark 12:30; Matthew 22:37; Luke 10:27; 1 Corinthians 7:37; 1 Corinthians 14:15-18; Romans 12:2; Ephesians 4:23; Hebrews 10:16; Jeremiah 31:33; Romans 8:5-6; Philippians 4:6-7; Colossians 3:2; 1 Samuel 25:3; 2 Chronicles 2:12; Daniel 1:4; Daniel 1:17; Acts 5:34; Acts 7:22; Acts 13:6-7; Acts 26:24; Ecclesiastes 8:9; Ezra 7:10; Ecclesiastes 7:25; Daniel 10:12; Acts 17:11; 2 Timothy 2:15; Genesis 41:46-49; Exodus 31:2-6; Exodus 35:30-35; 1 Kings 7:13-14; 2 Chronicles 2:13-14; 2 Chronicles 26:15; Daniel 5:13-14; Luke 1:3-4
