## 5895: intimacy (id: 5895)

Close relationships are part of God's will for human life. Scripture speaks of intimate relationships between Jesus Christ and the Father, between God and his people, and between various individuals.

Jesus Christ's intimate relationship with his Father
----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 11:27](https://ref.ly/Matt11:27)** *See also* [MAT 3:17](https://ref.ly/Matt3:17) parallels [MRK 1:11](https://ref.ly/Mark1:11), [LUK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Luke3:22); [JHN 5:20](https://ref.ly/John5:20), [JHN 10:30](https://ref.ly/John10:30), [JHN 10:38](https://ref.ly/John10:38), [JHN 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:10-John14:11), [JHN 17:21](https://ref.ly/John17:21)

God's intimate relationship with his people
-------------------------------------------

**[JHN 17:3](https://ref.ly/John17:3)** *“know” speaks of close personal relationship rather than theoretical or academic knowledge.* *See also* [2CH 7:15–7:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:15-2Chr7:16); [PSA 148:14](https://ref.ly/Ps148:14); [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [JHN 14:21](https://ref.ly/John14:21), [JHN 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4), [JHN 15:15](https://ref.ly/John15:15)

Images of God's desire for intimacy with his people
---------------------------------------------------

### God as a father

**[JER 31:9](https://ref.ly/Jer31:9)** *See also* [PSA 103:13](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13); [JER 31:20](https://ref.ly/Jer31:20); [HOS 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1-Hos11:4); [ROM 8:15](https://ref.ly/Rom8:15); [1JN 3:1](https://ref.ly/1John3:1)

### God as a husband

**[ISA 62:5](https://ref.ly/Isa62:5)** *See also* [ISA 54:4–54:8](https://ref.ly/Isa54:4-Isa54:8); [JER 3:14](https://ref.ly/Jer3:14); [HOS 2:19](https://ref.ly/Hos2:19); [EPH 5:25–5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:27); [REV 19:7](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7), [REV 21:2](https://ref.ly/Rev21:2)

Intimate relationships between men and women
--------------------------------------------

### As God's good purpose

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [GEN 1:27–1:28](https://ref.ly/Gen1:27-Gen1:28), [GEN 2:24](https://ref.ly/Gen2:24); [MAT 19:5](https://ref.ly/Matt19:5) parallel [MRK 10:7](https://ref.ly/Mark10:7); [1CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:16); [EPH 5:28](https://ref.ly/Eph5:28), [EPH 5:31](https://ref.ly/Eph5:31); [1TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:3-1Tim4:4)

### Examples of such close relationships

Jacob and Rachel: [GEN 29:20](https://ref.ly/Gen29:20), [GEN 29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30); [RUT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13) *Boaz and Ruth*; [1SA 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:5), [1SA 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:8); [EST 2:17](https://ref.ly/Esth2:17) *Xerxes and Esther*; ; [SNG 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Song1:15-Song1:16) *Solomon and his beloved*

Intimacy in other relationships
-------------------------------

### Close friendships commended

**[PRO 18:24](https://ref.ly/Prov18:24)** *See also* [PRO 17:17](https://ref.ly/Prov17:17), [PRO 27:10](https://ref.ly/Prov27:10); [ECC 4:9–4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:9-Eccl4:12); [JHN 15:13](https://ref.ly/John15:13)

### Examples of close friendships

David and Jonathan: [1SA 18:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:1), [1SA 18:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:3); [2KI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:2), [2KI 2:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:4); [2TI 1:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:16) *Paul and Onesiphorus*

Examples of intimate family relationships
-----------------------------------------

[GEN 37:3](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3) *Jacob and his son Joseph*; ; [RUT 1:16](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:16) *Ruth and her mother\-in\-law Naomi*; ; [2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33) *David and his son Absalom*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 11:27; Matthew 3:17; Mark 1:11; Luke 3:22; John 5:20; John 10:30; John 10:38; John 14:10-11; John 17:21; John 17:3; 2 Chronicles 7:15-16; Psalms 148:14; Hosea 6:6; John 14:21; John 15:4; John 15:15; Jeremiah 31:9; Psalms 103:13; Jeremiah 31:20; Hosea 11:1-4; Romans 8:15; 1 John 3:1; Isaiah 62:5; Isaiah 54:4-8; Jeremiah 3:14; Hosea 2:19; Ephesians 5:25-27; Revelation 19:7; Revelation 21:2; Genesis 2:18; Genesis 1:27-28; Genesis 2:24; Matthew 19:5; Mark 10:7; 1 Corinthians 6:16; Ephesians 5:28; Ephesians 5:31; 1 Timothy 4:3-4; Genesis 29:20; Genesis 29:30; Ruth 4:13; 1 Samuel 1:5; 1 Samuel 1:8; Esther 2:17; Song of Songs 1:15-16; Proverbs 18:24; Proverbs 17:17; Proverbs 27:10; Ecclesiastes 4:9-12; John 15:13; 1 Samuel 18:1; 1 Samuel 18:3; 2 Kings 2:2; 2 Kings 2:4; 2 Timothy 1:16; Genesis 37:3; Ruth 1:16; 2 Samuel 18:33
* **Associated Articles:** 1040: God, fatherhood of (ID: `1040@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1250: Abba (ID: `1250@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5690: friends (ID: `5690@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5708: marriage (ID: `5708@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5729: one flesh (ID: `5729@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5735: sexuality (ID: `5735@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5966: tenderness (ID: `5966@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6754: union with Christ (ID: `6754@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8102: abiding in Christ (ID: `8102@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8135: knowing God, nature of (ID: `8135@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8622: worship (ID: `8622@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5896: irreverence (id: 5896)

A complete absence of the proper sense of awe, respect and wonder that is inspired and demanded by the character and activity of the living God. Irreverence involves disrespect for the character, deeds, words, law and ministers of God. As such, it brings judgment upon those who behave in this way.

Irreverence as the root of sin
------------------------------

**[ROM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Rom3:10), [ROM 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rom3:18)** *See also* [PSA 36:1](https://ref.ly/Ps36:1), [PSA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4-Ps10:5), [PSA 55:19](https://ref.ly/Ps55:19); [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21)

### Rebellion leads to irreverence

**[JER 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:23-Jer5:24)** *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13); [MAT 15:3–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:9)

Causes of irreverence
---------------------

### Arrogance

**[PSA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4-Ps10:5)** *See also* [DEU 17:19–17:20](https://ref.ly/Deut17:19-Deut17:20)

### Self\-deception

**[PSA 36:1–36:2](https://ref.ly/Ps36:1-Ps36:2)**

### Prosperity

**[PRO 30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:9)** *See also* [PSA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4-Ps10:5)

Kinds of irreverence
--------------------

### Insulting God

**[PSA 69:9](https://ref.ly/Ps69:9)** *See also* [PSA 74:22–74:23](https://ref.ly/Ps74:22-Ps74:23); [HEB 10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:29)

### Misusing God's name

**[EXO 20:7](https://ref.ly/Exod20:7)** *See also* [LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12), [LEV 24:10–24:16](https://ref.ly/Lev24:10-Lev24:16)

### Insulting God's representatives

**[1KI 21:10–21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:10-1Kgs21:13)** *See also* [DEU 17:12](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12); [ACT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11)

### Ignoring God's law

**[NUM 15:30](https://ref.ly/Num15:30)** *See also* [ISA 63:17](https://ref.ly/Isa63:17); [JER 44:10](https://ref.ly/Jer44:10); [AMO 2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:7); [1TH 5:20](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:20)

### Worshipping idols

**[EZK 20:39](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:39)** *See also* [LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21), [LEV 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Lev20:1-Lev20:3)

### Sacrificial worship

**[LEV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Lev22:2)** *See also* [LEV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Lev21:6); [1SA 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:17); [MAL 1:6–1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:8)

### Towards holy objects and places

**[2SA 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:6-2Sam6:7)** *See also* [PSA 74:7–74:8](https://ref.ly/Ps74:7-Ps74:8), [PSA 79:1](https://ref.ly/Ps79:1); [JER 7:30](https://ref.ly/Jer7:30)

### Attacking God's people

**[PSA 22:7–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7-Ps22:8)** *See also* [2KI 19:9–19:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:9-2Kgs19:12), [2KI 19:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:16); [PSA 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:18), [PSA 35:15–35:16](https://ref.ly/Ps35:15-Ps35:16), [PSA 74:10](https://ref.ly/Ps74:10), [PSA 74:18](https://ref.ly/Ps74:18), [PSA 89:50–89:51](https://ref.ly/Ps89:50-Ps89:51); [EZK 35:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek35:12); [DAN 7:8](https://ref.ly/Dan7:8), [DAN 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Dan7:21-Dan7:22); [ZEP 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:10-Zeph2:11)EZK [36:20–36:23](https://ref.ly/Zeph36:20-Zeph36:23); [ROM 2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:24)

### Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit

**[MAT 12:31–12:32](https://ref.ly/Matt12:31-Matt12:32)** parallels [MRK 3:29](https://ref.ly/Mark3:29), [LUK 12:10](https://ref.ly/Luke12:10) *Jesus Christ's opponents were beyond hope, so long as they continued to say that the work of the Holy Spirit was the work of Satan ([Mt 12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24))* *See also* [1CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:3)JHN [10:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:33); [MAT 26:63–26:65](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63-Matt26:65) parallel [MRK 14:63–14:64](https://ref.ly/Mark14:63-Mark14:64)

Social consequences of irreverence
----------------------------------

### In family relationships

**[MAT 15:4](https://ref.ly/Matt15:4)** *See also* [EXO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Exod21:17) parallel [LEV 20:9](https://ref.ly/Lev20:9); [PRO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Prov20:20), [PRO 30:11](https://ref.ly/Prov30:11)

### Oppression and cruelty

**[PSA 10:9–10:11](https://ref.ly/Ps10:9-Ps10:11)** *See also* [PSA 73:2–73:11](https://ref.ly/Ps73:2-Ps73:11), [PSA 94:4–94:7](https://ref.ly/Ps94:4-Ps94:7); [PRO 8:13](https://ref.ly/Prov8:13)

### Social irresponsibility

**[PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5)** *See also* [PRO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Prov14:31); [JER 34:16](https://ref.ly/Jer34:16); [EZK 22:11–22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:11-Ezek22:12); [LUK 18:2](https://ref.ly/Luke18:2); [ROM 1:28–1:32](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28-Rom1:32); [1CO 11:20–11:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:22)

Irreverence brings condemnation and judgment
--------------------------------------------

**[DEU 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12-Deut17:13)**; **[PSA 55:19](https://ref.ly/Ps55:19)** *See also* [NUM 14:23](https://ref.ly/Num14:23); [2CH 26:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:5), [2CH 26:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16), [2CH 36:15–36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:16); [PRO 1:29–1:31](https://ref.ly/Prov1:29-Prov1:31), [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34), [PRO 28:14](https://ref.ly/Prov28:14); [HOS 10:3](https://ref.ly/Hos10:3), [HOS 12:14](https://ref.ly/Hos12:14); [HEB 10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:29); [JUD 1:15](https://ref.ly/Jude1:15)

### Particular warnings about the consequences of irreverence

**[1SA 12:14–12:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:14-1Sam12:15)** *See also* [DEU 28:58–28:59](https://ref.ly/Deut28:58-Deut28:59); [MAL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:1-Mal2:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 3:10; Romans 3:18; Psalms 36:1; Psalms 10:4-5; Psalms 55:19; Romans 1:21; Jeremiah 5:23-24; Isaiah 29:13; Matthew 15:3-9; Deuteronomy 17:19-20; Psalms 36:1-2; Proverbs 30:9; Psalms 69:9; Psalms 74:22-23; Hebrews 10:29; Exodus 20:7; Leviticus 19:12; Leviticus 24:10-16; 1 Kings 21:10-13; Deuteronomy 17:12; Acts 6:11; Numbers 15:30; Isaiah 63:17; Jeremiah 44:10; Amos 2:7; 1 Thessalonians 5:20; Ezekiel 20:39; Leviticus 18:21; Leviticus 20:1-3; Leviticus 22:2; Leviticus 21:6; 1 Samuel 2:17; Malachi 1:6-8; 2 Samuel 6:6-7; Psalms 74:7-8; Psalms 79:1; Jeremiah 7:30; Psalms 22:7-8; 2 Kings 19:9-12; 2 Kings 19:16; Psalms 31:18; Psalms 35:15-16; Psalms 74:10; Psalms 74:18; Psalms 89:50-51; Ezekiel 35:12; Daniel 7:8; Daniel 7:21-22; Zephaniah 2:10-11; Zephaniah 36:20-23; Romans 2:24; Matthew 12:31-32; Mark 3:29; Luke 12:10; Matthew 12:24; 1 Corinthians 12:3; 1 Corinthians 10:33; Matthew 26:63-65; Mark 14:63-64; Matthew 15:4; Exodus 21:17; Leviticus 20:9; Proverbs 20:20; Proverbs 30:11; Psalms 10:9-11; Psalms 73:2-11; Psalms 94:4-7; Proverbs 8:13; Proverbs 17:5; Proverbs 14:31; Jeremiah 34:16; Ezekiel 22:11-12; Luke 18:2; Romans 1:28-32; 1 Corinthians 11:20-22; Deuteronomy 17:12-13; Numbers 14:23; 2 Chronicles 26:5; 2 Chronicles 26:16; 2 Chronicles 36:15-16; Proverbs 1:29-31; Proverbs 3:34; Proverbs 28:14; Hosea 10:3; Hosea 12:14; Jude 1:15; 1 Samuel 12:14-15; Deuteronomy 28:58-59; Malachi 2:1-2
* **Associated Articles:** 5800: blasphemy (ID: `5800@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5818: contempt (ID: `5818@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5829: defiance (ID: `5829@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5838: disrespect (ID: `5838@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5893: insults (ID: `5893@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8333: reverence (ID: `8333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8771: idolatry, objections to (ID: `8771@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8782: mockery (ID: `8782@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8807: profanity (ID: `8807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5897: judging others (id: 5897)

God's people are to exercise fairness and impartiality when called upon to exercise judgment in legal or church matters. However, the temptation to pass judgment upon the life or testimony of other individuals is clearly warned against.

Judging others in a legal situation
-----------------------------------

### Fairness and impartiality are required by God

**[DEU 16:18–16:20](https://ref.ly/Deut16:18-Deut16:20)** *See also* [EXO 23:6–23:7](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6-Exod23:7); [LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15); [DEU 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:16-Deut1:17); [PRO 24:23](https://ref.ly/Prov24:23); [EZK 18:5–18:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:5-Ezek18:8)

### Judges are appointed by God

**[1KI 3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:28)** *See also* [EXO 18:13–18:16](https://ref.ly/Exod18:13-Exod18:16); [1SA 7:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:15), [1SA 12:1–12:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:1-1Sam12:4)

Jesus Christ refuses to judge others
------------------------------------

**[JHN 8:3–8:11](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:11)** *See also* [LUK 12:13–12:14](https://ref.ly/Luke12:13-Luke12:14); [JHN 7:24](https://ref.ly/John7:24), [JHN 8:15–8:16](https://ref.ly/John8:15-John8:16)

Judging others in the church
----------------------------

### Judgmental attitudes condemned

**[MAT 7:1–7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:1-Matt7:5)** parallel [LUK 6:37–6:42](https://ref.ly/Luke6:37-Luke6:42) *See also* [ROM 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Rom2:1-Rom2:3), [ROM 14:1–14:13](https://ref.ly/Rom14:1-Rom14:13); [COL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Col2:16); [JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4), [JAS 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11-Jas4:12)

### A discerning attitude commended

**[1CO 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:12-1Cor5:13)** *See also* [1CO 5:1–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:5), [1CO 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1-1Cor6:6)

### Evaluation of ministries commended

**[MAT 7:15–7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15-Matt7:20)** *See also* [2CO 11:10–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:10-2Cor11:15); [PHP 3:2](https://ref.ly/Phil3:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 16:18-20; Exodus 23:6-7; Leviticus 19:15; Deuteronomy 1:16-17; Proverbs 24:23; Ezekiel 18:5-8; 1 Kings 3:28; Exodus 18:13-16; 1 Samuel 7:15; 1 Samuel 12:1-4; John 8:3-11; Luke 12:13-14; John 7:24; John 8:15-16; Matthew 7:1-5; Luke 6:37-42; Romans 2:1-3; Romans 14:1-13; Colossians 2:16; James 2:1-4; James 4:11-12; 1 Corinthians 5:12-13; 1 Corinthians 5:1-5; 1 Corinthians 6:1-6; Matthew 7:15-20; 2 Corinthians 11:10-15; Philippians 3:2
* **Associated Articles:** 2309: Jesus Christ, as judge (ID: `2309@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5202: accusation, false (ID: `5202@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5358: judges (ID: `5358@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5820: criticism (ID: `5820@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5951: slander (ID: `5951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8226: discernment (ID: `8226@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8230: discipline (ID: `8230@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5898: kissing (id: 5898)

Kissing within the family
-------------------------

**[GEN 29:13](https://ref.ly/Gen29:13)** *See also* [GEN 27:26–27:27](https://ref.ly/Gen27:26-Gen27:27), [GEN 33:4](https://ref.ly/Gen33:4), [GEN 48:10](https://ref.ly/Gen48:10)

Kissing as an expression of love
--------------------------------

**[SNG 1:2](https://ref.ly/Song1:2)** *See also* [GEN 29:11](https://ref.ly/Gen29:11); [SNG 8:1](https://ref.ly/Song8:1); [1PE 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:14)

Kissing as a sign of reconciliation
-----------------------------------

**[LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20)** *See also* [GEN 45:15](https://ref.ly/Gen45:15); [2SA 14:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:33)

Kissing as a sign of homage
---------------------------

**[1SA 10:1](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:1)** *See also* [1KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:18); [JOB 31:27](https://ref.ly/Job31:27); [PSA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ps2:12); [HOS 13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:2); [LUK 7:38](https://ref.ly/Luke7:38), [LUK 7:45](https://ref.ly/Luke7:45)

Greeting with a kiss
--------------------

**[EXO 18:7](https://ref.ly/Exod18:7)** *See also* [EXO 4:27](https://ref.ly/Exod4:27)ROM [16:16](https://ref.ly/Exod16:16); [1CO 16:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:20); [2CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:12); [1TH 5:26](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:26); [1PE 5:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:14)

Kissing good\-bye
-----------------

**[RUT 1:14](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:14)** *See also* [GEN 31:28](https://ref.ly/Gen31:28), [GEN 31:55](https://ref.ly/Gen31:55), [GEN 50:1](https://ref.ly/Gen50:1); [RUT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:9); [1SA 20:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41); [1KI 19:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:20); [ACT 20:37](https://ref.ly/Acts20:37)

Blessing with a kiss
--------------------

[2SA 19:39](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:39)

Kissing linked with treachery
-----------------------------

**[2SA 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9-2Sam20:10)** *See also* [PRO 7:13](https://ref.ly/Prov7:13), [PRO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Prov7:21), [PRO 27:6](https://ref.ly/Prov27:6); [MAT 26:48–26:49](https://ref.ly/Matt26:48-Matt26:49) parallels [MRK 14:44–14:45](https://ref.ly/Mark14:44-Mark14:45), [LUK 22:47–22:48](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47-Luke22:48)

## 5899: lament (id: 5899)

A song of mourning or sorrow. Laments may be occasioned by bereavement, personal trouble, national disaster or the judgment of God.

Lamentation occasioned by bereavement
-------------------------------------

**[2SA 3:33–3:34](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:33-2Sam3:34)** *See also* [GEN 50:10](https://ref.ly/Gen50:10); [JER 31:15](https://ref.ly/Jer31:15); [MAT 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16-Matt2:18), [MAT 9:23](https://ref.ly/Matt9:23) parallels [MRK 5:38](https://ref.ly/Mark5:38), [LUK 8:52](https://ref.ly/Luke8:52); [LUK 23:27](https://ref.ly/Luke23:27); [JHN 11:31–11:33](https://ref.ly/John11:31-John11:33)

Lamentation occasioned by personal trouble
------------------------------------------

[PSA 56:2](https://ref.ly/Ps56:2), [PSA 56:8](https://ref.ly/Ps56:8), [PSA 102:1–102:2](https://ref.ly/Ps102:1-Ps102:2)

Lamentation occasioned by national disaster
-------------------------------------------

**[2CH 35:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:25)** *See also* [2SA 1:17–1:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:17-2Sam1:27); [EST 4:1](https://ref.ly/Esth4:1), [EST 9:31](https://ref.ly/Esth9:31); [JER 8:21–9:1](https://ref.ly/Jer8:21-Jer9:1)

Lamentation occasioned by the judgment of God on Israel
-------------------------------------------------------

**[AMO 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Amos5:1-Amos5:2)** *The call to lament is sometimes meant as a warning of impending judgment*; **[LAM 1:1](https://ref.ly/Lam1:1)** *The book of Lamentations is a series of laments after the fall of Jerusalem.* *See also* [ISA 3:24–3:26](https://ref.ly/Isa3:24-Isa3:26), [ISA 29:1–29:2](https://ref.ly/Isa29:1-Isa29:2); [JER 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Jer4:7-Jer4:8), [JER 7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:29), [JER 9:10](https://ref.ly/Jer9:10), [JER 9:17–9:21](https://ref.ly/Jer9:17-Jer9:21); [LAM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Lam2:5); [EZK 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek2:9-Ezek2:10), [EZK 19:1–19:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:1-Ezek19:14)

Lamentation occasioned by the judgment of God on other nations
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 16:9–16:11](https://ref.ly/Isa16:9-Isa16:11)** *See also* [ISA 15:5](https://ref.ly/Isa15:5), [ISA 15:8](https://ref.ly/Isa15:8), [ISA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Isa16:7), [ISA 19:8](https://ref.ly/Isa19:8); [JER 48:36](https://ref.ly/Jer48:36); [EZK 26:17–26:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:17-Ezek26:18), [EZK 27:2–27:36](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:2-Ezek27:36), [EZK 28:12–28:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:12-Ezek28:19), [EZK 30:2–30:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:2-Ezek30:4), [EZK 32:2–32:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek32:2-Ezek32:16)

Lamentation as a sign of repentance
-----------------------------------

[LAM 1:5](https://ref.ly/Lam1:5), [LAM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Lam1:8), [LAM 3:39–3:40](https://ref.ly/Lam3:39-Lam3:40); ; [EZK 9:3–9:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:3-Ezek9:6)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Samuel 3:33-34; Genesis 50:10; Jeremiah 31:15; Matthew 2:16-18; Matthew 9:23; Mark 5:38; Luke 8:52; Luke 23:27; John 11:31-33; Psalms 56:2; Psalms 56:8; Psalms 102:1-2; 2 Chronicles 35:25; 2 Samuel 1:17-27; Esther 4:1; Esther 9:31; Jeremiah 8:21-9:1; Amos 5:1-2; Lamentations 1:1; Isaiah 3:24-26; Isaiah 29:1-2; Jeremiah 4:7-8; Jeremiah 7:29; Jeremiah 9:10; Jeremiah 9:17-21; Lamentations 2:5; Ezekiel 2:9-10; Ezekiel 19:1-14; Isaiah 16:9-11; Isaiah 15:5; Isaiah 15:8; Isaiah 16:7; Isaiah 19:8; Jeremiah 48:36; Ezekiel 26:17-18; Ezekiel 27:2-36; Ezekiel 28:12-19; Ezekiel 30:2-4; Ezekiel 32:2-16; Lamentations 1:5; Lamentations 1:8; Lamentations 3:39-40; Ezekiel 9:3-6
* **Associated Articles:** 5198: weeping (ID: `5198@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5265: complaints (ID: `5265@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5419: mourning (ID: `5419@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5795: bereavement (ID: `5795@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5952: sorrow (ID: `5952@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7963: song (ID: `7963@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9250: woe (ID: `9250@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5900: laughter (id: 5900)

The act or sound of laughing, often associated with joy and delight, yet occasionally linked with ridicule and scorn.

People laughing
---------------

### Out of pleasure

**[GEN 21:6](https://ref.ly/Gen21:6)** *See also* [PRO 31:25](https://ref.ly/Prov31:25); [ECC 3:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:4), [ECC 10:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:19)

### In scorn and derision

**[MAT 9:24](https://ref.ly/Matt9:24)** parallels [MRK 5:40](https://ref.ly/Mark5:40), [LUK 8:53](https://ref.ly/Luke8:53) *See also* [JOB 39:18](https://ref.ly/Job39:18), [JOB 41:29](https://ref.ly/Job41:29); [PSA 52:6](https://ref.ly/Ps52:6); [LAM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Lam1:7); [HAB 1:10](https://ref.ly/Hab1:10)

God laughing
------------

**[PSA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Ps2:4)** *See also* [PSA 37:13](https://ref.ly/Ps37:13), [PSA 59:8](https://ref.ly/Ps59:8); [PRO 1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:26)

God helping people to laugh
---------------------------

**[PSA 126:2](https://ref.ly/Ps126:2)**; **[LUK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Luke6:21)** *See also* [JOB 5:22](https://ref.ly/Job5:22), [JOB 8:21](https://ref.ly/Job8:21); [JER 51:39](https://ref.ly/Jer51:39) *as a prelude to judgment*

Laughter qualified
------------------

**[PRO 14:13](https://ref.ly/Prov14:13)** *See also* [ECC 2:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:2), [ECC 7:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:3)

Laughter rebuked
----------------

**[LUK 6:25](https://ref.ly/Luke6:25)** *See also* [ECC 7:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:6); [JAS 4:9](https://ref.ly/Jas4:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 21:6; Proverbs 31:25; Ecclesiastes 3:4; Ecclesiastes 10:19; Matthew 9:24; Mark 5:40; Luke 8:53; Job 39:18; Job 41:29; Psalms 52:6; Lamentations 1:7; Habakkuk 1:10; Psalms 2:4; Psalms 37:13; Psalms 59:8; Proverbs 1:26; Psalms 126:2; Luke 6:21; Job 5:22; Job 8:21; Jeremiah 51:39; Proverbs 14:13; Ecclesiastes 2:2; Ecclesiastes 7:3; Luke 6:25; Ecclesiastes 7:6; James 4:9
* **Associated Articles:** 5818: contempt (ID: `5818@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5830: delight (ID: `5830@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5874: happiness (ID: `5874@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5880: humour (ID: `5880@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8283: joy (ID: `8283@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8782: mockery (ID: `8782@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8815: ridicule (ID: `8815@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5901: loneliness (id: 5901)

God intends human beings to live together in harmony and to value one another, but Scripture recognises many reasons for individuals to feel unwanted and isolated.

Loneliness is not part of God's plan for human beings
-----------------------------------------------------

**[GEN 2:18](https://ref.ly/Gen2:18)** *See also* [PSA 68:5–68:6](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5-Ps68:6); [ISA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15)

God is with his people at all times and in all circumstances
------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10)** *See also* [DEU 31:8](https://ref.ly/Deut31:8); [JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9); [PSA 27:10](https://ref.ly/Ps27:10); [ISA 43:2](https://ref.ly/Isa43:2); [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); [JHN 14:18](https://ref.ly/John14:18); [HEB 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5-Heb13:6)

Reasons why people may experience loneliness
--------------------------------------------

### Sin

**[ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2)** *See also* [GEN 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Gen4:10-Gen4:12); [1SA 28:5–28:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:5-1Sam28:6); [PSA 81:11–81:12](https://ref.ly/Ps81:11-Ps81:12); [ISA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Isa64:7); [HOS 5:6](https://ref.ly/Hos5:6); [EPH 4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:18)

### Remorse

**[MAT 27:4–27:5](https://ref.ly/Matt27:4-Matt27:5)** *See also* [1CH 21:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:17); [MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75)

### Lack of friends

**[PSA 142:4](https://ref.ly/Ps142:4)** *See also* [JHN 5:7](https://ref.ly/John5:7), [JHN 14:18](https://ref.ly/John14:18)

### Social ostracism

**[JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9)** *A moral outcast (see [Jn 4:18](https://ref.ly/John4:18)), she was collecting water at the hottest part of the day, when she could do so alone.* *See also* [LUK 19:7](https://ref.ly/Luke19:7) *A Jew who worked for the Roman occupying power was considered a traitor.*

### Public shame

**[JHN 8:3](https://ref.ly/John8:3)** *See also* [MRK 15:29–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29-Mark15:32)

### Desertion

**[2TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16)** *See also* [PSA 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ps38:11), [PSA 88:18](https://ref.ly/Ps88:18)

### Loss of family

**[LUK 7:12](https://ref.ly/Luke7:12)** *See also* [RUT 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:3-Ruth1:5); [2SA 18:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:33); [2KI 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:1); [JOB 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:20-Job1:21); [PSA 27:10](https://ref.ly/Ps27:10); [MAT 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/Matt2:17-Matt2:18); [JER 31:15](https://ref.ly/Jer31:15); [1TI 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:5)

### Loss of identity

**[LUK 8:27](https://ref.ly/Luke8:27)** parallel [MRK 5:2–5:3](https://ref.ly/Mark5:2-Mark5:3) *See also* [LUK 8:30](https://ref.ly/Luke8:30) parallel [MRK 5:9](https://ref.ly/Mark5:9)

### Discontent

**[ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8)** *See also* [ECC 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:10-Eccl2:11), [ECC 2:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:18); [HEB 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5-Heb13:6)

### Disability

**[ACT 3:2](https://ref.ly/Acts3:2)** *See also* [LEV 21:17–21:18](https://ref.ly/Lev21:17-Lev21:18); [MAT 9:32](https://ref.ly/Matt9:32), [MAT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22)

### Disease

**[LUK 17:12](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12)** *Lepers were forbidden to mix with those not affected.* *See also* [LEV 13:4](https://ref.ly/Lev13:4); [NUM 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Num5:1-Num5:3); [2KI 7:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:3); [2CH 26:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:21)

### Insomnia

**[PSA 102:7](https://ref.ly/Ps102:7)** *See also* [JOB 7:4](https://ref.ly/Job7:4); [DAN 6:18](https://ref.ly/Dan6:18)

### Depression

**[1KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:4)** *See also* [DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27); [JOB 7:16](https://ref.ly/Job7:16); [PSA 43:5](https://ref.ly/Ps43:5), [PSA 73:16–73:17](https://ref.ly/Ps73:16-Ps73:17); [JER 15:10](https://ref.ly/Jer15:10); [MIC 7:1](https://ref.ly/Mic7:1)

### Poverty

**[PRO 19:7](https://ref.ly/Prov19:7)** *See also* [1KI 17:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs17:12); [PRO 19:4](https://ref.ly/Prov19:4); [LUK 15:14–15:16](https://ref.ly/Luke15:14-Luke15:16)

### Old age

**[PSA 71:9](https://ref.ly/Ps71:9)** *See also* [2SA 19:34–19:35](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:34-2Sam19:35)

### Dying and the approach of death

**[PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4)** *See also* [PSA 18:4–18:6](https://ref.ly/Ps18:4-Ps18:6); [ROM 8:38–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:38-Rom8:39)

Loneliness experienced as a consequence of God's calling
--------------------------------------------------------

### God's setting apart for service

**[1KI 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:10)** *See also* [JDG 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Judg6:14-Judg6:15); [JER 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jer1:5), [JER 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Jer4:18-Jer4:19), [JER 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16-Jer15:17)

### Leadership

**[MRK 10:32](https://ref.ly/Mark10:32)** *See also* [EXO 24:1–24:2](https://ref.ly/Exod24:1-Exod24:2); [JOS 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Josh1:6-Josh1:7); [MAT 20:17–20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:17-Matt20:19)

### Responsibility

**[NUM 11:14](https://ref.ly/Num11:14)** *See also* [EXO 18:17–18:18](https://ref.ly/Exod18:17-Exod18:18); [2CO 11:28](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:28)

Jesus Christ experienced loneliness
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46)** parallel [MRK 15:33–15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:33-Mark15:34) *See also* [PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2); [MAT 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Matt4:1-Matt4:2) parallel [LUK 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Luke4:1-Luke4:2); [MAT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Matt14:23); [LUK 4:28–4:30](https://ref.ly/Luke4:28-Luke4:30); [JHN 6:67](https://ref.ly/John6:67), [JHN 16:32](https://ref.ly/John16:32)

Positive aspects of loneliness
------------------------------

### Solitude provides an opportunity for communion with God

**[MAT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:6)** *See also* [EXO 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Exod3:1-Exod3:2), [EXO 33:9](https://ref.ly/Exod33:9); [2KI 4:32–4:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:32-2Kgs4:33); [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39); [MRK 1:35](https://ref.ly/Mark1:35), [MRK 6:45–6:46](https://ref.ly/Mark6:45-Mark6:46); [REV 1:9](https://ref.ly/Rev1:9)

### Engaging in spiritual warfare

**[GEN 32:24](https://ref.ly/Gen32:24)**

### The opportunity for spiritual experiences away from the crowd

**[MRK 9:2](https://ref.ly/Mark9:2)** *See also* [DAN 10:8](https://ref.ly/Dan10:8); [2CO 12:2–12:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:2-2Cor12:4)

### Solitude as rest from the turmoil of everyday life

**[MRK 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31-Mark6:32)** *See also* [MAT 11:28](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 2:18; Psalms 68:5-6; Isaiah 49:15; Isaiah 41:10; Deuteronomy 31:8; Joshua 1:9; Psalms 27:10; Isaiah 43:2; Matthew 28:20; John 14:18; Hebrews 13:5-6; Isaiah 59:2; Genesis 4:10-12; 1 Samuel 28:5-6; Psalms 81:11-12; Isaiah 64:7; Hosea 5:6; Ephesians 4:18; Matthew 27:4-5; 1 Chronicles 21:17; Matthew 26:75; Psalms 142:4; John 5:7; John 4:9; John 4:18; Luke 19:7; John 8:3; Mark 15:29-32; 2 Timothy 4:16; Psalms 38:11; Psalms 88:18; Luke 7:12; Ruth 1:3-5; 2 Samuel 18:33; 2 Kings 4:1; Job 1:20-21; Matthew 2:17-18; Jeremiah 31:15; 1 Timothy 5:5; Luke 8:27; Mark 5:2-3; Luke 8:30; Mark 5:9; Ecclesiastes 4:8; Ecclesiastes 2:10-11; Ecclesiastes 2:18; Acts 3:2; Leviticus 21:17-18; Matthew 9:32; Matthew 12:22; Luke 17:12; Leviticus 13:4; Numbers 5:1-3; 2 Kings 7:3; 2 Chronicles 26:21; Psalms 102:7; Job 7:4; Daniel 6:18; 1 Kings 19:4; Deuteronomy 33:27; Job 7:16; Psalms 43:5; Psalms 73:16-17; Jeremiah 15:10; Micah 7:1; Proverbs 19:7; 1 Kings 17:12; Proverbs 19:4; Luke 15:14-16; Psalms 71:9; 2 Samuel 19:34-35; Psalms 23:4; Psalms 18:4-6; Romans 8:38-39; 1 Kings 19:10; Judges 6:14-15; Jeremiah 1:5; Jeremiah 4:18-19; Jeremiah 15:16-17; Mark 10:32; Exodus 24:1-2; Joshua 1:6-7; Matthew 20:17-19; Numbers 11:14; Exodus 18:17-18; 2 Corinthians 11:28; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:33-34; Psalms 22:1-2; Matthew 4:1-2; Luke 4:1-2; Matthew 14:23; Luke 4:28-30; John 6:67; John 16:32; Matthew 6:6; Exodus 3:1-2; Exodus 33:9; 2 Kings 4:32-33; Matthew 26:39; Mark 1:35; Mark 6:45-46; Revelation 1:9; Genesis 32:24; Mark 9:2; Daniel 10:8; 2 Corinthians 12:2-4; Mark 6:31-32; Matthew 11:28

## 5902: maturity (id: 5902)

The full development at the end of a process of growth, by means of which a human being reaches adulthood.

* **Associated Articles:** 5903: maturity, physical (ID: `5903@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5904: maturity, spiritual (ID: `5904@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5903: maturity, physical (id: 5903)

In the OT, reaching adulthood brought with it certain privileges and responsibilities.

Growth to physical and psychological maturity
---------------------------------------------

**[LUK 2:52](https://ref.ly/Luke2:52)** *Jesus Christ's normal childhood is evidence of his full humanity.* *See also* [1SA 2:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:26); [LUK 1:80](https://ref.ly/Luke1:80), [LUK 2:40](https://ref.ly/Luke2:40); [1CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:11)

The responsibilities of maturity in the Israelite community
-----------------------------------------------------------

### The payment of atonement money

[EXO 30:11–30:16](https://ref.ly/Exod30:11-Exod30:16), [EXO 38:25–38:26](https://ref.ly/Exod38:25-Exod38:26)

### Eligibility for military service

[NUM 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Num1:2-Num1:3), [NUM 1:45](https://ref.ly/Num1:45), [NUM 26:2](https://ref.ly/Num26:2); ; [2CH 25:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5)

### Accountability for actions

[NUM 14:29](https://ref.ly/Num14:29), [NUM 32:11](https://ref.ly/Num32:11)

### Inheritance of the land

[NUM 26:52–26:54](https://ref.ly/Num26:52-Num26:54)

### Serving Levites

[NUM 8:24–8:25](https://ref.ly/Num8:24-Num8:25); ; [1CH 23:24](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:24), [1CH 23:27](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:27); ; [EZR 3:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:8)

Maturity should produce wisdom
------------------------------

**[JOB 12:12](https://ref.ly/Job12:12)** *See also* [JOB 15:10](https://ref.ly/Job15:10), [JOB 32:6–32:9](https://ref.ly/Job32:6-Job32:9); [PRO 7:7](https://ref.ly/Prov7:7); [ECC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:13)

Respect should be shown to the mature
-------------------------------------

**[LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32)** *See also* [JOB 32:4](https://ref.ly/Job32:4); [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22); [1TI 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1-1Tim5:2); [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)

Maturity of years seen as God's blessing
----------------------------------------

[GEN 24:1](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1); ; [RUT 4:15](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:15); ; [1CH 29:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:28)

The maturity of crops for harvest used as a metaphor
----------------------------------------------------

**[JOL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Joel3:13)**; **[JHN 4:35–4:36](https://ref.ly/John4:35-John4:36)** *See also* [AMO 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Amos8:1-Amos8:2); [MAT 9:37–9:38](https://ref.ly/Matt9:37-Matt9:38) parallel [LUK 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Luke10:1-Luke10:2); [MRK 4:26–4:29](https://ref.ly/Mark4:26-Mark4:29); [REV 14:15](https://ref.ly/Rev14:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 2:52; 1 Samuel 2:26; Luke 1:80; Luke 2:40; 1 Corinthians 13:11; Exodus 30:11-16; Exodus 38:25-26; Numbers 1:2-3; Numbers 1:45; Numbers 26:2; 2 Chronicles 25:5; Numbers 14:29; Numbers 32:11; Numbers 26:52-54; Numbers 8:24-25; 1 Chronicles 23:24; 1 Chronicles 23:27; Ezra 3:8; Job 12:12; Job 15:10; Job 32:6-9; Proverbs 7:7; Ecclesiastes 4:13; Leviticus 19:32; Job 32:4; Proverbs 23:22; 1 Timothy 5:1-2; 1 Peter 5:5; Genesis 24:1; Ruth 4:15; 1 Chronicles 29:28; Joel 3:13; John 4:35-36; Amos 8:1-2; Matthew 9:37-38; Luke 10:1-2; Mark 4:26-29; Revelation 14:15
* **Associated Articles:** 2520: Jesus Christ, childhood of (ID: `2520@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4464: harvest (ID: `4464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5051: responsibility (ID: `5051@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5716: middle age (ID: `5716@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5725: old age (ID: `5725@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8443: growth (ID: `8443@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8469: respect (ID: `8469@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5904: maturity, spiritual (id: 5904)

The development of Christlike character and behaviour in the Christian through a renewed mind and tested faith.

Christlikeness as the goal and model for spiritual maturity
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[EPH 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Eph4:13-Eph4:15)** *See also* [COL 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:6-Col2:7); [HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)

Marks of spiritual maturity
---------------------------

### Spiritual understanding

**[COL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Col2:2)** *See also* [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14); [1CO 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6), [1CO 14:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:20); [EPH 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/Eph1:17-Eph1:18); [HEB 5:12–6:1](https://ref.ly/Heb5:12-Heb6:1)

### Discernment of God's will and changed behaviour

**[COL 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Col1:9-Col1:10)** *See also* [ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2); [1CO 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:1-1Cor3:3); [GAL 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22-Gal5:23); [EPH 4:22–4:23](https://ref.ly/Eph4:22-Eph4:23); [PHP 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/Phil1:9-Phil1:11); [2TH 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:3)

### Stability

**[COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12)** *See also* [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14); [2PE 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17-2Pet3:18)

### Care for the weaker brother

**[ROM 15:1](https://ref.ly/Rom15:1)** *See also* [GAL 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1-Gal6:2)

Maturity is to be the aim of the Christian
------------------------------------------

**[PHP 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13-Phil3:15)** *See also* [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14) *a reason for spiritual immaturity*; [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1), [2CO 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:11); [1TI 6:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:11)

The process of maturity
-----------------------

### God causes spiritual growth

**[PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6)** *See also* [GAL 3:3](https://ref.ly/Gal3:3); [HEB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:14)

### Possessing gifts of ministry

**[EPH 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:13)** *See also* [ROM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Rom1:11)

### Being equipped by the word of God

**[2TI 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16-2Tim3:17)** *See also* [ROM 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rom15:4); [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2); [1JN 2:5](https://ref.ly/1John2:5)

### Persevering through trials

**[ROM 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Rom5:3-Rom5:5)** *See also* [HEB 2:10](https://ref.ly/Heb2:10); [JAS 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:3-Jas1:4); [1PE 5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10)

The concern of the pastor is to help others to maturity
-------------------------------------------------------

**[COL 1:28–1:29](https://ref.ly/Col1:28-Col1:29)** *See also* [2CO 13:9–13:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:9-2Cor13:10); [GAL 4:19](https://ref.ly/Gal4:19)

## 5907: miserliness (id: 5907)

A reluctance to part with money, often associated with the hoarding of money or possessions. While Scripture censures the squandering of resources, it also regards the hoarding of money or possessions as potentially idolatrous.

Miserliness as a form of idolatry
---------------------------------

**[PRO 18:11](https://ref.ly/Prov18:11)**; **[MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24)** *See also* [DEU 15:9](https://ref.ly/Deut15:9); [PRO 11:26](https://ref.ly/Prov11:26); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8); [MAT 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19-Matt6:21); [EPH 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3-Eph5:5); [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

The foolishness of miserliness
------------------------------

**[ECC 2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:26)**; **[1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7)** *See also* [PSA 39:6](https://ref.ly/Ps39:6), [PSA 39:11](https://ref.ly/Ps39:11), [PSA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Ps49:10); [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16), [PRO 23:6](https://ref.ly/Prov23:6), [PRO 28:8](https://ref.ly/Prov28:8), [PRO 28:22](https://ref.ly/Prov28:22); [ECC 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:7-Eccl4:8), [ECC 5:10–5:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10-Eccl5:11), [ECC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:15); [LUK 12:15–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15-Luke12:21)

Miserliness leads to misery
---------------------------

**[PRO 11:24](https://ref.ly/Prov11:24)**; **[1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)** *See also* [PRO 11:16](https://ref.ly/Prov11:16); [ECC 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12-Eccl5:14); [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

Some examples of miserliness
----------------------------

[1SA 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:3); ; [AMO 3:10](https://ref.ly/Amos3:10); ; [LUK 16:19–16:31](https://ref.ly/Luke16:19-Luke16:31); ; [ACT 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:2)

## 5908: motives (id: 5908)

The underlying reasons for a course of action. Scripture stresses the importance of doing things with the intention of honouring and glorifying God and building up his people.

* **Associated Articles:** 5909: motives, importance of (ID: `5909@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5910: motives, examples of (ID: `5910@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5909: motives, importance of (id: 5909)

God acts from the highest, purest motives and expects his people to do the same.

God's supreme motive is the honour of his name
----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 106:8](https://ref.ly/Ps106:8)** *See also* [EXO 9:16](https://ref.ly/Exod9:16); [PSA 23:3](https://ref.ly/Ps23:3); [ISA 43:7](https://ref.ly/Isa43:7); [EZK 36:20–36:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:20-Ezek36:23); [EPH 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eph1:11-Eph1:14)

God's glory is to be the highest motive of his people
-----------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:31)** *See also* [NUM 25:7–25:13](https://ref.ly/Num25:7-Num25:13); [ISA 26:8](https://ref.ly/Isa26:8); [COL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:17); [1PE 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:11)

Purity of motive is essential for acceptable worship
----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 6:1–6:18](https://ref.ly/Matt6:1-Matt6:18)** *See also* [PSA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Ps51:6), [PSA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Ps51:10), [PSA 86:11](https://ref.ly/Ps86:11); [MAT 5:8](https://ref.ly/Matt5:8)

Wrong motives hinder effective prayer
-------------------------------------

**[JAS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Jas4:3)** *See also* [PSA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Ps66:18); [1JN 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/1John3:21-1John3:22)

Motives for good moral behaviour
--------------------------------

### Wanting to please God

**[COL 3:22–3:25](https://ref.ly/Col3:22-Col3:25)** *See also* [EPH 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5-Eph6:8)

### Compassion for others

**[DEU 24:17–24:22](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17-Deut24:22)** *The experience of slavery and the exodus were to promote compassion for the poor and the alien.* *See also* [EXO 22:21–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21-Exod22:27); [LEV 25:35–25:42](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:42), [LEV 25:54–25:55](https://ref.ly/Lev25:54-Lev25:55)

### Fear of punishment for sin

**[HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4)** *See also* [COL 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Col3:5-Col3:6); [1TH 4:3–4:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:6)

God judges the motives of the heart
-----------------------------------

**[PRO 16:2](https://ref.ly/Prov16:2)** *See also* [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); [PRO 20:27](https://ref.ly/Prov20:27)

## 5910: motives, examples of (id: 5910)

Scripture reflects the wide range of motives which influence human behaviour.

Right actions can result from wrong motives
-------------------------------------------

**[PHP 1:15–1:18](https://ref.ly/Phil1:15-Phil1:18)** *See also* [1SA 18:20–18:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:20-1Sam18:23)

Right motives do not always produce right actions
-------------------------------------------------

**[1KI 8:17–8:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:17-1Kgs8:19)** parallel [2CH 6:7–6:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:7-2Chr6:9) *See also* [MAT 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/Matt1:18-Matt1:20)

Examples of wrong motives
-------------------------

### Malice and envy

**[GEN 37:11](https://ref.ly/Gen37:11)** *See also* [GEN 37:18–37:28](https://ref.ly/Gen37:18-Gen37:28); [EST 3:1–3:9](https://ref.ly/Esth3:1-Esth3:9); [EZK 25:15–25:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:15-Ezek25:16), [EZK 36:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:5); [MAT 2:7–2:13](https://ref.ly/Matt2:7-Matt2:13)

### Greed

**[JHN 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/John12:4-John12:6)** *See also* [2KI 5:19–5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:19-2Kgs5:27); [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11)

Motives are sometimes misjudged
-------------------------------

**[JOS 22:9–22:34](https://ref.ly/Josh22:9-Josh22:34)** *See also* [NUM 32:1–32:33](https://ref.ly/Num32:1-Num32:33); [2SA 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:1-2Sam10:4) parallel [1CH 19:1–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:1-1Chr19:4); [2KI 5:5–5:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:5-2Kgs5:7)

Jesus Christ's actions motivated by a desire to do God's will
-------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 8:29](https://ref.ly/John8:29)** *See also* [JHN 4:34](https://ref.ly/John4:34), [JHN 6:38](https://ref.ly/John6:38); [HEB 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Heb10:5-Heb10:7); [PSA 40:6–40:8](https://ref.ly/Ps40:6-Ps40:8)

Paul's motives
--------------

### Paul rejected all base motives in ministry

**[1TH 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:3-1Thess2:6)** *See also* [2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17)

### Paul was motivated by his knowledge of God

**[2CO 5:9–5:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:9-2Cor5:12)**

### Paul was concerned for the spiritual growth of others

**[PHP 4:14–4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14-Phil4:18)** *Paul's motive in rejoicing in the gift from the Philippians was the spiritual benefit which they would gain as a result of it.* *See also* [PHP 1:22–1:26](https://ref.ly/Phil1:22-Phil1:26); [PHM 1:8–1:14](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:8-Phlm1:14)

## 5914: optimism (id: 5914)

The ability to face the future with confidence. For believers this is possible because of their knowledge of God.

Optimism resulting from faith
-----------------------------

### Confidence in God

**[PSA 52:8](https://ref.ly/Ps52:8), [PSA 125:1](https://ref.ly/Ps125:1)** *See also* [PSA 25:4–25:5](https://ref.ly/Ps25:4-Ps25:5), [PSA 56:4](https://ref.ly/Ps56:4), [PSA 71:5](https://ref.ly/Ps71:5), [PSA 91:2](https://ref.ly/Ps91:2); [NAM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Nah1:7)

### Confidence in what God can do

**[2CO 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:10-2Cor1:11)** *See also* [PSA 27:1–27:3](https://ref.ly/Ps27:1-Ps27:3); [JER 17:7](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7); [MIC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Mic7:7); [HEB 13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:6); [1JN 5:14](https://ref.ly/1John5:14)

### Confidence in what God will do in the future

**[2CO 5:5–5:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:5-2Cor5:8)** *See also* [ACT 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Acts24:14-Acts24:15); [GAL 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gal5:5); [PHP 1:3–1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:3-Phil1:6)

### Confidence founded on God's grace

**[ISA 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Isa12:1-Isa12:2)** *See also* [EZR 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:2); [2CO 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor3:4-2Cor3:5); [EPH 3:12](https://ref.ly/Eph3:12); [2TH 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:16-2Thess2:17); [1JN 3:21–3:22](https://ref.ly/1John3:21-1John3:22)

Encouragements to be optimistic
-------------------------------

**[JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1)**; **[ROM 15:13](https://ref.ly/Rom15:13)** *See also* [PSA 9:10](https://ref.ly/Ps9:10), [PSA 37:3–37:5](https://ref.ly/Ps37:3-Ps37:5), [PSA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5), [PSA 115:9–115:11](https://ref.ly/Ps115:9-Ps115:11); [JHN 14:27](https://ref.ly/John14:27), [JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33); [ROM 12:12](https://ref.ly/Rom12:12); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17); [HEB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15), [HEB 10:19–10:23](https://ref.ly/Heb10:19-Heb10:23); [1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13); [1JN 2:28](https://ref.ly/1John2:28)

### In the OT

------------------

## 5915: ornaments (id: 5915)

The use of jewellery and other adornments is not forbidden in Scripture, but excessive use can indicate worldliness. Inner spiritual adornment is of greater value.

Possession and use of various ornaments show wealth
---------------------------------------------------

### Ornaments used to beautify

**[SNG 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Song1:10-Song1:11)** *See also* [GEN 37:3](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3), [GEN 37:23](https://ref.ly/Gen37:23), [GEN 37:32](https://ref.ly/Gen37:32); [2SA 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:24); [JER 2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:32)

### Rings, ear\-rings and nose rings

[GEN 35:4](https://ref.ly/Gen35:4); ; [EXO 32:2–32:3](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:3); ; [JDG 8:24](https://ref.ly/Judg8:24); ; [EST 8:8](https://ref.ly/Esth8:8); ; [PRO 25:12](https://ref.ly/Prov25:12); ; [ISA 3:19](https://ref.ly/Isa3:19), [ISA 3:21](https://ref.ly/Isa3:21); ; [EZK 16:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:12); ; [LUK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22); ; [JAS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2)

### Bracelets and gold chains

[GEN 24:22](https://ref.ly/Gen24:22), [GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42); ; [PRO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Prov1:9); ; [EZK 16:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:11); ; [DAN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Dan5:29)

### Bells

[EXO 28:33–28:35](https://ref.ly/Exod28:33-Exod28:35)

Ornaments plundered from Egypt
------------------------------

**[EXO 3:22](https://ref.ly/Exod3:22)**

The misuse of ornaments for pride and seduction
-----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 3:16](https://ref.ly/Isa3:16)** *See also* [EZK 23:40](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:40), [EZK 23:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:42); [HOS 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:13)

Ornaments as gifts
------------------

**[GEN 24:53](https://ref.ly/Gen24:53)** *See also* [GEN 24:22](https://ref.ly/Gen24:22); [JOB 42:11](https://ref.ly/Job42:11)

Adornment as a picture of God's love
------------------------------------

**[EZK 16:11–16:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:11-Ezek16:13)** *See also* [ISA 61:10](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10); [LUK 15:22](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22)

Ornaments as symbols of authority
---------------------------------

**[GEN 41:42](https://ref.ly/Gen41:42)** *See also* [2SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:10); [EST 3:10](https://ref.ly/Esth3:10); [DAN 5:29](https://ref.ly/Dan5:29)

Ornaments discarded
-------------------

### In mourning

**[EXO 33:4–33:6](https://ref.ly/Exod33:4-Exod33:6)** *See also* [EZK 26:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:16)

### To make idols

**[EXO 32:2–32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:2-Exod32:4)** *See also* [JDG 8:24–8:27](https://ref.ly/Judg8:24-Judg8:27)

### As offerings to God

**[EXO 35:22](https://ref.ly/Exod35:22)** *See also* [NUM 31:50](https://ref.ly/Num31:50)

Inner, not outer, adornment encouraged
--------------------------------------

## 5916: pessimism (id: 5916)

A negative and hopeless attitude, resulting either from viewing the world as being without God or from loss of faith in God.

The world may appear to be meaningless
--------------------------------------

**[ECC 1:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:14)** *See also* [ECC 1:1–1:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:1-Eccl1:11)

Wisdom may be pointless
-----------------------

**[ECC 1:17–1:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:17-Eccl1:18)** *See also* [ECC 2:12–2:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:12-Eccl2:16)

Pleasures may be empty
----------------------

**[ECC 2:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1)**

Work may be in vain
-------------------

**[ECC 2:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17)** *See also* [ECC 2:18–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:18-Eccl2:23), [ECC 4:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:4)

The world may seem corrupt and oppressive
-----------------------------------------

**[ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1)** *See also* [ECC 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:2-Eccl4:3), [ECC 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:8-Eccl5:9)

Loneliness can be depressing
----------------------------

**[ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8)**

Wealth is no answer
-------------------

**[ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10)** *See also* [ECC 5:11–5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:11-Eccl5:15), [ECC 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:1-Eccl6:6); [LUK 12:18–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:18-Luke12:20)

Life is short and often miserable
---------------------------------

**[ECC 6:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:12)** *In the OT, before the resurrection hope was fully developed, this life seemed to be all that there was.* *See also* [1CH 29:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:15); [JOB 7:6](https://ref.ly/Job7:6), [JOB 14:7–14:12](https://ref.ly/Job14:7-Job14:12), [JOB 14:18–14:22](https://ref.ly/Job14:18-Job14:22), [JOB 17:13–17:16](https://ref.ly/Job17:13-Job17:16)

Suffering may lead to pessimism
-------------------------------

**[JOB 6:11](https://ref.ly/Job6:11)** *See also* [JOB 30:26](https://ref.ly/Job30:26); [LAM 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Lam3:16-Lam3:18); [EZK 37:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek37:11)

Suffering may cause a sense of God's rejection
----------------------------------------------

**[JOB 14:18–14:19](https://ref.ly/Job14:18-Job14:19)** *See also* [JOB 19:5–19:13](https://ref.ly/Job19:5-Job19:13)

The godless have no grounds for optimism
----------------------------------------

**[EPH 2:12](https://ref.ly/Eph2:12)** *See also* [JOB 8:13–8:15](https://ref.ly/Job8:13-Job8:15); [PRO 10:24](https://ref.ly/Prov10:24), [PRO 11:7](https://ref.ly/Prov11:7), [PRO 24:19–24:20](https://ref.ly/Prov24:19-Prov24:20); [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3); [JER 4:9](https://ref.ly/Jer4:9); [MAT 7:26–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26-Matt7:27); [1TH 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13)

Examples of pessimism
---------------------

### The Israelites on hearing about the strength of the Canaanites

[NUM 13:31–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:31-Num13:33); ; [DEU 1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:28)

### Israel's enemies

[DEU 2:25](https://ref.ly/Deut2:25); ; [JOS 2:9](https://ref.ly/Josh2:9)

### Naomi on her return to Israel

[RUT 1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:21)

### The Israelites threatened by the Philistines

[1SA 17:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:11), [1SA 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:6-1Sam13:7)

### Elijah at Mount Horeb

[1KI 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:9-1Kgs19:10)

### The men with leprosy at the gate of Samaria

[2KI 7:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:4)

### Judah facing the threat from Aram and Israel

[ISA 7:2](https://ref.ly/Isa7:2)

### The Philistine cities on seeing Tyre's destruction

[ZEC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Zech9:5)

### Thomas after the resurrection

[JHN 20:24–20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:24-John20:25)

### Paul's shipboard companions during the storm

[ACT 27:20](https://ref.ly/Acts27:20)

The antidote to pessimism is faith in God
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 42:5](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5)**; **[2CO 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:13-2Cor4:16)** *See also* [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10); [LAM 3:21–3:24](https://ref.ly/Lam3:21-Lam3:24); [2CO 4:7–4:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:7-2Cor4:9)

## 5917: plans (id: 5917)

Designs or schemes, prepared in advance of doing something. Scripture indicates the sovereign plans of God and the varied plans of men and women.

God's plans
-----------

### For judgment

**[JER 18:11](https://ref.ly/Jer18:11)** *See also* [NUM 33:55–33:56](https://ref.ly/Num33:55-Num33:56); [2KI 19:25–19:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:25-2Kgs19:26); [JOB 23:14–23:16](https://ref.ly/Job23:14-Job23:16); [ISA 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1-Isa25:2), [ISA 37:26–37:27](https://ref.ly/Isa37:26-Isa37:27); [JER 26:3](https://ref.ly/Jer26:3); [LAM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Lam2:17); [MIC 2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:3)ISA [14:24–14:27](https://ref.ly/Mic14:24-Mic14:27), [ISA 19:12](https://ref.ly/Isa19:12), [ISA 19:17](https://ref.ly/Isa19:17), [ISA 23:8–23:9](https://ref.ly/Isa23:8-Isa23:9); [JER 49:20](https://ref.ly/Jer49:20), [JER 50:45](https://ref.ly/Jer50:45); [MIC 4:11–4:13](https://ref.ly/Mic4:11-Mic4:13)

### For salvation

**[EPH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eph1:11)** *See also* [PSA 40:5](https://ref.ly/Ps40:5); [HEB 11:40](https://ref.ly/Heb11:40)

### For the tabernacle and the temple

**[EXO 26:30–26:37](https://ref.ly/Exod26:30-Exod26:37)** *See also* [1CH 28:11–28:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:11-1Chr28:12), [1CH 28:18–28:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:18-1Chr28:19); [EZK 43:10–43:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:10-Ezek43:11)

God's plans are revealed to his people
--------------------------------------

**[AMO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Amos3:7)** *See also* [EXO 26:30](https://ref.ly/Exod26:30); [JER 49:20–49:21](https://ref.ly/Jer49:20-Jer49:21)

God's plans are always fulfilled
--------------------------------

**[JOB 42:2](https://ref.ly/Job42:2)** *See also* [PSA 33:11](https://ref.ly/Ps33:11); [ISA 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Isa5:18-Isa5:19), [ISA 14:24–14:27](https://ref.ly/Isa14:24-Isa14:27), [ISA 46:11](https://ref.ly/Isa46:11)

God may sometimes reverse his plans
-----------------------------------

**[JER 36:3](https://ref.ly/Jer36:3)** *See also* [JER 18:5–18:10](https://ref.ly/Jer18:5-Jer18:10), [JER 26:3](https://ref.ly/Jer26:3); [JON 3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:10)

Plans of the wicked
-------------------

**[PSA 64:5–64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:5-Ps64:6)** *See also* [2SA 17:1–17:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:1-2Sam17:4); [2KI 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:10-2Kgs16:11); [PSA 140:2](https://ref.ly/Ps140:2); [PRO 14:22](https://ref.ly/Prov14:22), [PRO 30:32](https://ref.ly/Prov30:32); [ISA 30:1](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1); [JER 18:12](https://ref.ly/Jer18:12); [MIC 2:1](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1); [MAT 28:12–28:13](https://ref.ly/Matt28:12-Matt28:13)

Plans of the righteous
----------------------

**[PRO 12:5](https://ref.ly/Prov12:5)** *See also* [1CH 28:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:2); [PSA 14:6](https://ref.ly/Ps14:6); [PRO 14:22](https://ref.ly/Prov14:22), [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5); [ISA 32:8](https://ref.ly/Isa32:8); [ROM 1:13](https://ref.ly/Rom1:13), [ROM 15:24](https://ref.ly/Rom15:24); [2CO 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:15-2Cor1:17)

Plans and the role of counsellors
---------------------------------

**[PRO 15:22](https://ref.ly/Prov15:22)** *See also* [EZR 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:4-Ezra4:5); [PRO 20:18](https://ref.ly/Prov20:18)

Malicious plans
---------------

### Against other people

**[1SA 18:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:25)** *See also* [1SA 23:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:10); [EST 8:3](https://ref.ly/Esth8:3); [PSA 140:4](https://ref.ly/Ps140:4); [JER 49:30](https://ref.ly/Jer49:30)

### Against God's servants

**[JER 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jer18:18)** *See also* [MAT 22:15](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15); [JHN 12:10–12:11](https://ref.ly/John12:10-John12:11); [ACT 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Acts9:23-Acts9:24), [ACT 27:42–27:43](https://ref.ly/Acts27:42-Acts27:43)

Human plans are subject to God's will
-------------------------------------

**[JAS 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:15)**; **[PSA 33:10](https://ref.ly/Ps33:10)** *See also* [PSA 20:4](https://ref.ly/Ps20:4), [PSA 140:8](https://ref.ly/Ps140:8), [PSA 146:4](https://ref.ly/Ps146:4); [PRO 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Prov16:1-Prov16:3), [PRO 16:9](https://ref.ly/Prov16:9), [PRO 19:21](https://ref.ly/Prov19:21), [PRO 21:30](https://ref.ly/Prov21:30); [ECC 9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:10); [ISA 8:10](https://ref.ly/Isa8:10), [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3); [JER 19:7](https://ref.ly/Jer19:7)

## 5918: pleasure (id: 5918)

A gratifying emotion, used in Scripture to refer to both human and divine emotions. Believers are invited to take pleasure in every aspect of God's creation, while not allowing pleasure in the creation to overshadow pleasure in the Creator himself.

Divine pleasure
---------------

### That which gives God pleasure

**[PSA 147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:11)**; **[JER 9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:24)** *See also* [PSA 37:23](https://ref.ly/Ps37:23), [PSA 149:4](https://ref.ly/Ps149:4); [PRO 11:20](https://ref.ly/Prov11:20), [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22); [MAT 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/Matt11:25-Matt11:26) parallel [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21); [MAT 12:18](https://ref.ly/Matt12:18); [ISA 42:1](https://ref.ly/Isa42:1); [EPH 1:5](https://ref.ly/Eph1:5), [EPH 1:9](https://ref.ly/Eph1:9)

### That which does not give God pleasure

**[PSA 5:4](https://ref.ly/Ps5:4)**; **[EZK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:23)** *See also* [PSA 51:16](https://ref.ly/Ps51:16), [PSA 147:10](https://ref.ly/Ps147:10); [ISA 1:11](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11); [EZK 18:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:32), [EZK 33:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:11)

Human pleasure
--------------

### God provides everything for the enjoyment of life

**[ECC 8:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:15)**; **[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)**

### Pleasure in human physical relationships

**[SNG 4:10](https://ref.ly/Song4:10)** *See also* [SNG 1:2](https://ref.ly/Song1:2), [SNG 2:3](https://ref.ly/Song2:3), [SNG 7:6](https://ref.ly/Song7:6)

### Such pleasures are not satisfying in themselves

**[ECC 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:10-Eccl2:11)** *See also* [ECC 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1-Eccl2:2), [ECC 2:24–2:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24-Eccl2:25)

### True pleasure is found in the things of God

**[PSA 16:11](https://ref.ly/Ps16:11), [PSA 37:4](https://ref.ly/Ps37:4)**; **[ROM 7:22](https://ref.ly/Rom7:22)** *See also* [PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2), [PSA 35:9](https://ref.ly/Ps35:9), [PSA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:4), [PSA 111:2](https://ref.ly/Ps111:2), [PSA 112:1](https://ref.ly/Ps112:1), [PSA 119:16](https://ref.ly/Ps119:16); [PRO 10:23](https://ref.ly/Prov10:23), [PRO 16:13](https://ref.ly/Prov16:13), [PRO 18:2](https://ref.ly/Prov18:2); [ISA 55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:2); [JER 6:10](https://ref.ly/Jer6:10), [JER 15:16](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16)

### Pleasures may also be misleading or positively sinful

**[LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14)**; **[TIT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3)** *See also* [PRO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Prov1:22), [PRO 2:14](https://ref.ly/Prov2:14); [ECC 7:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:4); [ISA 66:3](https://ref.ly/Isa66:3); [2TH 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:12); [1TI 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:6); [2TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:4); [HEB 11:25](https://ref.ly/Heb11:25); [JAS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Jas4:3); [2PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13-2Pet2:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 147:11; Jeremiah 9:24; Psalms 37:23; Psalms 149:4; Proverbs 11:20; Proverbs 12:22; Matthew 11:25-26; Luke 10:21; Matthew 12:18; Isaiah 42:1; Ephesians 1:5; Ephesians 1:9; Psalms 5:4; Ezekiel 18:23; Psalms 51:16; Psalms 147:10; Isaiah 1:11; Ezekiel 18:32; Ezekiel 33:11; Ecclesiastes 8:15; 1 Timothy 6:17; Song of Songs 4:10; Song of Songs 1:2; Song of Songs 2:3; Song of Songs 7:6; Ecclesiastes 2:10-11; Ecclesiastes 2:1-2; Ecclesiastes 2:24-25; Psalms 16:11; Psalms 37:4; Romans 7:22; Psalms 1:2; Psalms 35:9; Psalms 43:4; Psalms 111:2; Psalms 112:1; Psalms 119:16; Proverbs 10:23; Proverbs 16:13; Proverbs 18:2; Isaiah 55:2; Jeremiah 6:10; Jeremiah 15:16; Luke 8:14; Titus 3:3; Proverbs 1:22; Proverbs 2:14; Ecclesiastes 7:4; Isaiah 66:3; 2 Thessalonians 2:12; 1 Timothy 5:6; 2 Timothy 3:4; Hebrews 11:25; James 4:3; 2 Peter 2:13-14

## 5919: popularity (id: 5919)

The state of being liked and admired. Scripture notes how the faithful preaching of the gospel will lead to hostility and unpopularity, not least on account of its demands on people and its proclamation of judgment.

The popularity of Jesus Christ during his early ministry
--------------------------------------------------------

### Crowds gathered round him

**[MRK 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mark2:2)** *See also* [MRK 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mark3:10), [MRK 5:24](https://ref.ly/Mark5:24); [LUK 5:1](https://ref.ly/Luke5:1), [LUK 8:19](https://ref.ly/Luke8:19), [LUK 8:45](https://ref.ly/Luke8:45)

### Many people followed him

[MAT 4:25](https://ref.ly/Matt4:25), [MAT 13:2](https://ref.ly/Matt13:2), [MAT 15:30](https://ref.ly/Matt15:30); ; [MRK 1:33](https://ref.ly/Mark1:33), [MRK 2:13](https://ref.ly/Mark2:13); ; [LUK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Luke12:1)

The popularity of David
-----------------------

[1SA 18:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:7), [1SA 21:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:11), [1SA 29:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:5); ; [2SA 3:36](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:36)

Popularity can result from seeking approval from human beings, rather than from God
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 12:43](https://ref.ly/John12:43)** *See also* [LUK 6:26](https://ref.ly/Luke6:26); [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22)

Actions carried out to gain popularity
--------------------------------------

[2SA 15:1–15:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:6) *Absalom's attempt to curry favour*; ; [ACT 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Acts12:1-Acts12:3) *Herod's arrest of Christians*; ; [ACT 24:27](https://ref.ly/Acts24:27) *Felix' imprisonment of Paul*; ; [ACT 25:9](https://ref.ly/Acts25:9) *Festus' prosecution of Paul*

Causes of unpopularity for the gospel
-------------------------------------

### Its appeal to those whom the world regards as weak and foolish

[1CO 1:26–1:29](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:26-1Cor1:29), [1CO 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6-1Cor2:7)

### Its insistence that people are sinful

[ROM 7:7](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7); ; [1TI 5:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:20); ; [1JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/1John1:8)

### Its challenge to vested interests

[JHN 11:45–11:48](https://ref.ly/John11:45-John11:48) *the Pharisees' privileged position*; ; [ACT 19:23–19:40](https://ref.ly/Acts19:23-Acts19:40) *the silversmith's trade*; ; [ACT 5:17–5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17-Acts5:41), [ACT 21:27–21:36](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:36)

### The high demands that the gospel makes of people

[MRK 10:17–10:23](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17-Mark10:23)

### The difficulty of some of Jesus Christ's sayings

[JHN 6:60–6:66](https://ref.ly/John6:60-John6:66)

* **Associated Passages:** Mark 2:2; Mark 3:10; Mark 5:24; Luke 5:1; Luke 8:19; Luke 8:45; Matthew 4:25; Matthew 13:2; Matthew 15:30; Mark 1:33; Mark 2:13; Luke 12:1; 1 Samuel 18:7; 1 Samuel 21:11; 1 Samuel 29:5; 2 Samuel 3:36; John 12:43; Luke 6:26; Colossians 3:22; 2 Samuel 15:1-6; Acts 12:1-3; Acts 24:27; Acts 25:9; 1 Corinthians 1:26-29; 1 Corinthians 2:6-7; Romans 7:7; 1 Timothy 5:20; 1 John 1:8; John 11:45-48; Acts 19:23-40; Acts 5:17-41; Acts 21:27-36; Mark 10:17-23; John 6:60-66

## 5920: pretence (id: 5920)

The deception of others by individuals, usually for the sake of their own interest, safety or pride. Scripture condemns such pretence and demands honesty from believers at every point.

Pretence practised by people for their own interests
----------------------------------------------------

### The wearing of a disguise

**[1KI 14:1–14:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:1-1Kgs14:6)** *See also* [GEN 27:5–27:19](https://ref.ly/Gen27:5-Gen27:19), [GEN 27:35–27:36](https://ref.ly/Gen27:35-Gen27:36), [GEN 29:16–29:25](https://ref.ly/Gen29:16-Gen29:25), [GEN 38:13–38:16](https://ref.ly/Gen38:13-Gen38:16); [2SA 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:1-2Sam14:3)

### Other pretences

**[GEN 42:7](https://ref.ly/Gen42:7)** *See also* [GEN 34:13–34:15](https://ref.ly/Gen34:13-Gen34:15), [GEN 34:25](https://ref.ly/Gen34:25); [2SA 13:1–13:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:11); [2KI 10:18–10:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:18-2Kgs10:19); [PRO 13:7](https://ref.ly/Prov13:7); [JER 9:6–9:9](https://ref.ly/Jer9:6-Jer9:9); [MAT 22:15–22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:22) parallels [MRK 12:13–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:20–20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:26)

Pretence practised by people for their own safety
-------------------------------------------------

### The wearing of a disguise

**[JOS 9:3–9:22](https://ref.ly/Josh9:3-Josh9:22)** *See also* [1SA 28:7–28:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:7-1Sam28:12); [1KI 22:29–22:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:29-1Kgs22:32) parallel [2CH 18:28–18:32](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:28-2Chr18:32)

### Other pretences

**[1SA 21:10–21:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:10-1Sam21:13)** *See also* [1SA 19:11–19:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:11-1Sam19:17); PSA 34:0; [ACT 27:29–27:30](https://ref.ly/Acts27:29-Acts27:30)

Examples of pretence
--------------------

### That practised by people because of their pride

**[PRO 12:9](https://ref.ly/Prov12:9)** *See also* [2CH 35:20–35:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:20-2Chr35:22); [PRO 13:7](https://ref.ly/Prov13:7); [MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2), [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5), [MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16), [MAT 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27-Matt23:28), [MAT 24:4–24:5](https://ref.ly/Matt24:4-Matt24:5) parallels [MRK 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Mark13:5-Mark13:6), [LUK 21:8](https://ref.ly/Luke21:8)

### That practised by people in their approach to God

**[JER 3:10](https://ref.ly/Jer3:10)** *See also* [MAT 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Matt15:7-Matt15:8) parallel [MRK 7:6](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6); [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)

### That commanded by the LORD for his own purpose

[1KI 20:37–20:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:37-1Kgs20:43)

God's people must rid themselves of pretence
--------------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1)** *See also* [PSA 119:29](https://ref.ly/Ps119:29); [LUK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Luke12:1); [1PE 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21-1Pet2:22); [ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9)

The gospel demolishes pretence
------------------------------

**[2CO 10:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:5)**

## 5921: privacy (id: 5921)

Privacy to conduct conversations
--------------------------------

**[JER 37:17](https://ref.ly/Jer37:17)** *See also* [1SA 18:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:22); [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27); [JER 40:15](https://ref.ly/Jer40:15)

Privacy to express emotions
---------------------------

**[GEN 43:30](https://ref.ly/Gen43:30)** *See also* [MAT 14:9–14:13](https://ref.ly/Matt14:9-Matt14:13)

Privacy to teach and to learn

## 5922: prudence (id: 5922)

Careful, wise discernment; the avoidance of rash behaviour or speech; the good management of talents and resources and the showing of tact and wisdom in relationships with other people.

The link between prudence and wisdom
------------------------------------

**[PRO 8:12](https://ref.ly/Prov8:12)** *See also* [PRO 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:6)

Characteristics of prudence
---------------------------

### Discernment

**[PRO 14:15](https://ref.ly/Prov14:15)** *See also* [PRO 12:16](https://ref.ly/Prov12:16), [PRO 14:8](https://ref.ly/Prov14:8); [LUK 14:28–14:32](https://ref.ly/Luke14:28-Luke14:32)

### Foresight and caution

**[PRO 22:3](https://ref.ly/Prov22:3)** *See also* [PRO 23:1–23:3](https://ref.ly/Prov23:1-Prov23:3); [AMO 5:13](https://ref.ly/Amos5:13)

### Wise use of knowledge

**[PRO 12:23](https://ref.ly/Prov12:23)** *See also* [PRO 14:18](https://ref.ly/Prov14:18); [MAT 7:6](https://ref.ly/Matt7:6)

### Wise speech

**[PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23)** *See also* [PSA 39:1](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1); [JAS 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/Jas3:5-Jas3:8)

### A teachable spirit

**[PRO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Prov15:5)**

Prudence involves good management of talents and resources
----------------------------------------------------------

### Good management commended

**[PRO 6:6–6:11](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:11)** *See also* [GEN 41:33–41:40](https://ref.ly/Gen41:33-Gen41:40); [PRO 24:27](https://ref.ly/Prov24:27); [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30)

### Warnings against unwise financial dealings

**[PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27)** *See also* [PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5); [MAT 5:25–5:26](https://ref.ly/Matt5:25-Matt5:26)

Examples of prudent behaviour
-----------------------------

### In averting disaster

**[1SA 25:14–25:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:14-1Sam25:35)** *See also* [GEN 32:3–32:21](https://ref.ly/Gen32:3-Gen32:21)

### In avoiding persecution

**[ACT 14:5–14:7](https://ref.ly/Acts14:5-Acts14:7)** *See also* [MAT 12:14–12:16](https://ref.ly/Matt12:14-Matt12:16) parallel [MRK 3:6–3:7](https://ref.ly/Mark3:6-Mark3:7) *Jesus Christ*[ACT 9:23–9:25](https://ref.ly/Acts9:23-Acts9:25), [ACT 9:29–9:30](https://ref.ly/Acts9:29-Acts9:30)[ACT 12:17](https://ref.ly/Acts12:17) *Peter*

### Prudence in dealing with other people

[GEN 26:26–26:31](https://ref.ly/Gen26:26-Gen26:31) *Abimelech makes a treaty with Isaac*; ; [GEN 32:3–32:21](https://ref.ly/Gen32:3-Gen32:21) *Jacob prepares to meet Esau*; ; [NEH 2:1–2:6](https://ref.ly/Neh2:1-Neh2:6) *Nehemiah petitions Artaxerxes*; ; [DAN 1:8–1:14](https://ref.ly/Dan1:8-Dan1:14) *Daniel's diplomacy in avoiding Nebuchanezzar's food*; ; [DAN 2:10–2:16](https://ref.ly/Dan2:10-Dan2:16) *Daniel's wisdom and tact in the face of death*; ; [MAT 22:15–22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:22) *Jesus Christ's wisdom on the question of paying taxes to Caesar*; ; [ACT 5:33–5:40](https://ref.ly/Acts5:33-Acts5:40) *Gamaliel's speech to the Sanhedrin*

## 5923: public opinion (id: 5923)

The concepts and views held by the majority of the population. Scripture urges personal, independent thinking, based on God's word, rather than adherence to popular beliefs.

God's people should not be concerned about public opinion
---------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 23:2](https://ref.ly/Exod23:2)** *See also* [JOB 31:33–31:34](https://ref.ly/Job31:33-Job31:34); [ECC 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:21-Eccl7:22)

Examples of the impact of public opinion
----------------------------------------

[1SA 14:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:45) *Public opinion saved Jonathan's life*; ; [1SA 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:24) *Public opinion led to Saul losing his kingdom*; ; [MAT 27:15–27:26](https://ref.ly/Matt27:15-Matt27:26) *Public opinion led to Jesus Christ being crucified*; ; [ACT 12:3](https://ref.ly/Acts12:3) *Public opinion led to Peter being imprisoned.*

Public opinion is often contrary to divine standards
----------------------------------------------------

**[HAG 1:2–1:11](https://ref.ly/Hag1:2-Hag1:11)** *See also* [NUM 13:21–14:10](https://ref.ly/Num13:21-Num14:10); [DEU 1:19–1:40](https://ref.ly/Deut1:19-Deut1:40); [1SA 8:4–8:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:4-1Sam8:9); [1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21); [JER 2:31–2:35](https://ref.ly/Jer2:31-Jer2:35), [JER 26:7–26:16](https://ref.ly/Jer26:7-Jer26:16)

Public opinion is sometimes in accord with divine standards
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/Deut5:28-Deut5:29)** *See also* [JOS 24:19–24:24](https://ref.ly/Josh24:19-Josh24:24)

Public opinion of Jesus Christ was divided
------------------------------------------

### Concerning his identity

**[MAT 16:13–16:17](https://ref.ly/Matt16:13-Matt16:17)** parallels [MRK 8:27–8:29](https://ref.ly/Mark8:27-Mark8:29), [LUK 9:18–9:20](https://ref.ly/Luke9:18-Luke9:20) *See also* [JHN 7:40–7:43](https://ref.ly/John7:40-John7:43)

### Concerning his teaching and ministry

[LUK 12:51](https://ref.ly/Luke12:51); ; [JHN 7:12](https://ref.ly/John7:12), [JHN 7:43](https://ref.ly/John7:43), [JHN 9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:16), [JHN 10:19](https://ref.ly/John10:19)
