## 5924: quarrelsomeness (id: 5924)

The evil of quarrelling
-----------------------

**[JAS 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1-Jas4:2)** *See also* [EXO 21:18–21:19](https://ref.ly/Exod21:18-Exod21:19); [PRO 10:12](https://ref.ly/Prov10:12), [PRO 13:10](https://ref.ly/Prov13:10), [PRO 15:18](https://ref.ly/Prov15:18), [PRO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Prov20:3), [PRO 21:9](https://ref.ly/Prov21:9), [PRO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Prov22:10), [PRO 26:20–26:21](https://ref.ly/Prov26:20-Prov26:21), [PRO 27:15–27:16](https://ref.ly/Prov27:15-Prov27:16), [PRO 30:33](https://ref.ly/Prov30:33); [2TI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:23)

Quarrelsomeness is unfitting for God's people
---------------------------------------------

**[1CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3)**; **[1TI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:3)** *See also* [PRO 17:19](https://ref.ly/Prov17:19); [ISA 58:4](https://ref.ly/Isa58:4); [ROM 13:12–13:14](https://ref.ly/Rom13:12-Rom13:14); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20); [1TI 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:5); [2TI 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:14), [2TI 2:23–2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:23-2Tim2:24)

Examples of quarrelsomeness
---------------------------

**[1CO 1:10–1:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:10-1Cor1:12)** *See also* [GEN 13:7–13:8](https://ref.ly/Gen13:7-Gen13:8), [GEN 26:20–26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:20-Gen26:22); [EXO 17:1–17:7](https://ref.ly/Exod17:1-Exod17:7) *NIV footnote at verse 7*; [NUM 20:1–20:13](https://ref.ly/Num20:1-Num20:13) *NIV footnote at verse 13*; [DEU 33:8](https://ref.ly/Deut33:8); [PSA 95:8](https://ref.ly/Ps95:8), [PSA 106:32](https://ref.ly/Ps106:32); [MRK 9:33–9:34](https://ref.ly/Mark9:33-Mark9:34) parallel [LUK 9:46](https://ref.ly/Luke9:46); [LUK 22:24](https://ref.ly/Luke22:24); [PHP 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Phil4:2-Phil4:3)

* **Associated Passages:** James 4:1-2; Exodus 21:18-19; Proverbs 10:12; Proverbs 13:10; Proverbs 15:18; Proverbs 20:3; Proverbs 21:9; Proverbs 22:10; Proverbs 26:20-21; Proverbs 27:15-16; Proverbs 30:33; 2 Timothy 2:23; 1 Corinthians 3:3; 1 Timothy 3:2-3; Proverbs 17:19; Isaiah 58:4; Romans 13:12-14; 2 Corinthians 12:20; 1 Timothy 6:3-5; 2 Timothy 2:14; 2 Timothy 2:23-24; 1 Corinthians 1:10-12; Genesis 13:7-8; Genesis 26:20-22; Exodus 17:1-7; Numbers 20:1-13; Deuteronomy 33:8; Psalms 95:8; Psalms 106:32; Mark 9:33-34; Luke 9:46; Luke 22:24; Philippians 4:2-3
* **Associated Articles:** 5789: anger (ID: `5789@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5834: disagreement (ID: `5834@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5964: temper (ID: `5964@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6221: rebellion (ID: `6221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8828: spite (ID: `8828@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5925: rashness (id: 5925)

Acting without careful consideration of the possible consequences or risks.

Examples of rashness
--------------------

[GEN 34:25–34:31](https://ref.ly/Gen34:25-Gen34:31) *Jacob's sons*; [EXO 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Exod2:11-Exod2:12); ; [ACT 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Acts7:24-Acts7:25); ; [NUM 20:8–20:12](https://ref.ly/Num20:8-Num20:12); ; [PSA 106:32](https://ref.ly/Ps106:32); [JOS 8:14–8:17](https://ref.ly/Josh8:14-Josh8:17) *the king of Ai*; ; [JDG 11:30–11:31](https://ref.ly/Judg11:30-Judg11:31) *Jephthah*; ; [1SA 14:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:24) *King Saul*; ; [2SA 6:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:6) *Uzzah*; ; [2SA 12:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:18) *fear for King David*; ; [1KI 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:5) *King Adonijah*; ; [2KI 5:20–5:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:20-2Kgs5:21) *Gehazi*; ; [2KI 10:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:16) *Jehu*; ; [2CH 26:16–26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16-2Chr26:20) *King Uzziah*; ; [2CH 35:21–35:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:21-2Chr35:22) *King Josiah*; ; [HAB 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hab1:6) *the Babylonians*; ; [MAT 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:19-Matt8:20) *a would\-be disciple*; ; [MAT 14:6–14:10](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6-Matt14:10) parallel [MRK 6:26](https://ref.ly/Mark6:26) *King Herod*; [MAT 26:33–26:34](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33-Matt26:34); ; [JHN 18:10](https://ref.ly/John18:10); [ACT 19:36](https://ref.ly/Acts19:36) *the Ephesian crowd*; ; [GAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6) *the Galatian Christians*; ; [2TI 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2-2Tim3:4) *a characteristic of the last days*

Warnings against rashness
-------------------------

**[PRO 14:16](https://ref.ly/Prov14:16)**; **[ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2)** *See also* [PRO 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Prov6:16-Prov6:18), [PRO 12:18](https://ref.ly/Prov12:18), [PRO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Prov13:3), [PRO 19:2](https://ref.ly/Prov19:2), [PRO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Prov20:3), [PRO 20:25](https://ref.ly/Prov20:25), [PRO 21:5](https://ref.ly/Prov21:5), [PRO 25:8](https://ref.ly/Prov25:8), [PRO 29:20](https://ref.ly/Prov29:20); [ECC 7:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:9), [ECC 8:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:3); [ISA 52:12](https://ref.ly/Isa52:12); [ZEP 3:7](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:7); [ACT 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Acts19:35-Acts19:36); [1TI 5:22](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:22); [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5)

Antidotes to rashness
---------------------

### Trust in the guiding presence of the Lord

**[ISA 52:12](https://ref.ly/Isa52:12)**

### Appreciating the importance of considered actions

**[JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)** *See also* [LUK 14:28–14:33](https://ref.ly/Luke14:28-Luke14:33)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 34:25-31; Exodus 2:11-12; Acts 7:24-25; Numbers 20:8-12; Psalms 106:32; Joshua 8:14-17; Judges 11:30-31; 1 Samuel 14:24; 2 Samuel 6:6; 2 Samuel 12:18; 1 Kings 1:5; 2 Kings 5:20-21; 2 Kings 10:16; 2 Chronicles 26:16-20; 2 Chronicles 35:21-22; Habakkuk 1:6; Matthew 8:19-20; Matthew 14:6-10; Mark 6:26; Matthew 26:33-34; John 18:10; Acts 19:36; Galatians 1:6; 2 Timothy 3:2-4; Proverbs 14:16; Ecclesiastes 5:2; Proverbs 6:16-18; Proverbs 12:18; Proverbs 13:3; Proverbs 19:2; Proverbs 20:3; Proverbs 20:25; Proverbs 21:5; Proverbs 25:8; Proverbs 29:20; Ecclesiastes 7:9; Ecclesiastes 8:3; Isaiah 52:12; Zephaniah 3:7; Acts 19:35-36; 1 Timothy 5:22; 2 Timothy 3:1-5; James 1:19; Luke 14:28-33
* **Associated Articles:** 5575: talk, idle (ID: `5575@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5802: care (ID: `5802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5803: carelessness (ID: `5803@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5810: complacency (ID: `5810@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5883: impatience (ID: `5883@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5922: prudence (ID: `5922@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8755: folly (ID: `8755@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5926: rebuke (id: 5926)

A verbal expression of blame. Scripture gives examples of both divine and human rebuke. Sometimes God issues rebukes directly but more commonly he did so through the OT prophets. In the NT Jesus Christ and the apostles issue rebukes and teach the church how to address those who sin.

God has an absolute right to rebuke those who depart from his ways
------------------------------------------------------------------

### He sometimes exercises this right directly

**[LEV 26:23–26:24](https://ref.ly/Lev26:23-Lev26:24)** *See also* [DEU 28:20](https://ref.ly/Deut28:20); [NEH 9:30](https://ref.ly/Neh9:30); [JOB 5:17](https://ref.ly/Job5:17); [PSA 39:11](https://ref.ly/Ps39:11), [PSA 50:21](https://ref.ly/Ps50:21), [PSA 94:12](https://ref.ly/Ps94:12), [PSA 105:14](https://ref.ly/Ps105:14), [PSA 119:21](https://ref.ly/Ps119:21); [JER 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19), [JER 2:30](https://ref.ly/Jer2:30); [HEB 8:8](https://ref.ly/Heb8:8)

### His rebuke is powerful

[JOB 26:11](https://ref.ly/Job26:11); ; [PSA 80:16](https://ref.ly/Ps80:16), [PSA 104:7](https://ref.ly/Ps104:7); ; [ISA 50:2](https://ref.ly/Isa50:2)

In the OT much rebuke came indirectly
-------------------------------------

Balaam's ass rebukes Balaam: [NUM 22:28](https://ref.ly/Num22:28); ; [2PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16); [1SA 2:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:25) *Eli rebukes his sons*; ; [1SA 15:12–15:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:12-1Sam15:26) *Samuel rebukes Saul*; ; [1SA 24:5–24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:5-1Sam24:7) *David rebukes his men*; ; [2SA 12:1–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:1-2Sam12:12) *Nathan rebukes David*; ; [1KI 18:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:18) *Elijah rebukes Ahab*; ; [EZR 10:9–10:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:9-Ezra10:11) *Ezra rebukes the men of Judah and Benjamin*; ; [ISA 1:10–1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:10-Isa1:17) *Isaiah rebukes Judah and Jerusalem*; ; [HOS 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:1-Hos4:3) *Hosea rebukes the Israelites.*

Examples of Jesus Christ issuing rebukes
----------------------------------------

### To evil spirits

**[MAT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Matt17:18)** parallels [MRK 9:25](https://ref.ly/Mark9:25), [LUK 9:42](https://ref.ly/Luke9:42) *See also* [LUK 4:41](https://ref.ly/Luke4:41)

### To his disciples

[MRK 8:33](https://ref.ly/Mark8:33) parallel [MAT 16:23](https://ref.ly/Matt16:23); ; [MRK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:14)

Other examples of rebuke being administered in the NT
-----------------------------------------------------

Inappropriate rebukes by Jesus Christ's disciples: [MAT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Matt16:22) parallel [MRK 8:32](https://ref.ly/Mark8:32); ; [MAT 19:13](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13) parallels [MRK 10:13](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13), [LUK 18:15](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15); [LUK 3:19](https://ref.ly/Luke3:19) *John the Baptist rebukes Herod*; ; [LUK 23:40–23:41](https://ref.ly/Luke23:40-Luke23:41) *The repentant thief on the cross rebukes the other crucified criminal.*

Proverbs on the subject of rebuke
---------------------------------

### The importance of responding correctly to a rebuke

**[PRO 1:23–1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:23-Prov1:26)** *See also* [PRO 6:23](https://ref.ly/Prov6:23), [PRO 10:17](https://ref.ly/Prov10:17), [PRO 12:1](https://ref.ly/Prov12:1), [PRO 15:10](https://ref.ly/Prov15:10), [PRO 15:31–15:32](https://ref.ly/Prov15:31-Prov15:32), [PRO 17:10](https://ref.ly/Prov17:10), [PRO 19:25](https://ref.ly/Prov19:25); [ECC 7:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:5)

### Different responses to a rebuke

**[PRO 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Prov9:7-Prov9:8)**

Instructions to believers on giving and receiving rebuke
--------------------------------------------------------

### On giving a rebuke

**[LEV 19:17](https://ref.ly/Lev19:17)** *See also* [MAT 18:15](https://ref.ly/Matt18:15); [LUK 17:3](https://ref.ly/Luke17:3); [GAL 6:1](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1); [COL 1:28](https://ref.ly/Col1:28), [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16); [1TI 5:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:1) *advice on rebuking those who are older*; [1TI 5:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:20) *disciplining elders*; [2TI 3:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16) *the usefulness of Scripture for rebuking*; [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2); [TIT 1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:9), [TIT 1:13](https://ref.ly/Titus1:13), [TIT 2:15](https://ref.ly/Titus2:15); [JAS 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Jas5:19-Jas5:20)

### On receiving a rebuke

**[HEB 12:5](https://ref.ly/Heb12:5)** *See also* [1CO 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:14)

### Believers thus follow the example of the Lord himself

**[HEB 12:6](https://ref.ly/Heb12:6)** *See also* [PRO 3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:12); [REV 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rev3:19)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 26:23-24; Deuteronomy 28:20; Nehemiah 9:30; Job 5:17; Psalms 39:11; Psalms 50:21; Psalms 94:12; Psalms 105:14; Psalms 119:21; Jeremiah 2:19; Jeremiah 2:30; Hebrews 8:8; Job 26:11; Psalms 80:16; Psalms 104:7; Isaiah 50:2; Numbers 22:28; 2 Peter 2:16; 1 Samuel 2:25; 1 Samuel 15:12-26; 1 Samuel 24:5-7; 2 Samuel 12:1-12; 1 Kings 18:18; Ezra 10:9-11; Isaiah 1:10-17; Hosea 4:1-3; Matthew 17:18; Mark 9:25; Luke 9:42; Luke 4:41; Mark 8:33; Matthew 16:23; Mark 16:14; Matthew 16:22; Mark 8:32; Matthew 19:13; Mark 10:13; Luke 18:15; Luke 3:19; Luke 23:40-41; Proverbs 1:23-26; Proverbs 6:23; Proverbs 10:17; Proverbs 12:1; Proverbs 15:10; Proverbs 15:31-32; Proverbs 17:10; Proverbs 19:25; Ecclesiastes 7:5; Proverbs 9:7-8; Leviticus 19:17; Matthew 18:15; Luke 17:3; Galatians 6:1; Colossians 1:28; Colossians 3:16; 1 Timothy 5:1; 1 Timothy 5:20; 2 Timothy 3:16; 2 Timothy 4:2; Titus 1:9; Titus 1:13; Titus 2:15; James 5:19-20; Hebrews 12:5; 1 Corinthians 16:14; Hebrews 12:6; Proverbs 3:12; Revelation 3:19

## 5927: resentment (id: 5927)

A feeling of bitterness towards someone, usually as a result of their actions or words. Resentment is possible against God or against other people.

* **Associated Articles:** 5928: resentment, against God (ID: `5928@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5929: resentment, against people (ID: `5929@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5928: resentment, against God (id: 5928)

Resentment against God or Jesus Christ is usually the consequence of a lack of faith or unbelief or as a response to adverse circumstances.

Israel's resentment against God expressed in constant grumbling
---------------------------------------------------------------

### Resentment at hardships and lack of provisions

[EXO 15:24](https://ref.ly/Exod15:24), [EXO 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:2-Exod16:3), [EXO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:3); ; [NUM 20:2–20:5](https://ref.ly/Num20:2-Num20:5), [NUM 21:4–21:5](https://ref.ly/Num21:4-Num21:5)

### Resentment due to faithless fear

[EXO 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Exod14:10-Exod14:11); ; [NUM 14:2–14:3](https://ref.ly/Num14:2-Num14:3), [NUM 14:36](https://ref.ly/Num14:36); ; [DEU 1:27](https://ref.ly/Deut1:27); ; [PSA 106:25](https://ref.ly/Ps106:25)

God's response to Israel's resentment
-------------------------------------

### Punishment

[NUM 11:1](https://ref.ly/Num11:1), [NUM 14:27–14:30](https://ref.ly/Num14:27-Num14:30), [NUM 14:37](https://ref.ly/Num14:37); ; [PSA 106:26–106:27](https://ref.ly/Ps106:26-Ps106:27); ; [1CO 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:10)

### Merciful provision

[EXO 16:4](https://ref.ly/Exod16:4), [EXO 16:8–16:9](https://ref.ly/Exod16:8-Exod16:9), [EXO 16:11–16:12](https://ref.ly/Exod16:11-Exod16:12); ; [NUM 17:5](https://ref.ly/Num17:5), [NUM 17:10](https://ref.ly/Num17:10)

Expressions of resentment
-------------------------

### At suffering

**[JOB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Job10:1)** *See also* [JOB 3:20–3:26](https://ref.ly/Job3:20-Job3:26), [JOB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Job7:11); [1SA 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:10); [PSA 77:1–77:3](https://ref.ly/Ps77:1-Ps77:3), [PSA 142:2](https://ref.ly/Ps142:2); [JER 15:18](https://ref.ly/Jer15:18)

### At apparently unjust treatment by God

**[RUT 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:20-Ruth1:21)** *See also* [JOB 23:2](https://ref.ly/Job23:2); [ISA 40:27](https://ref.ly/Isa40:27)

### At God's word

**[MRK 6:18–6:19](https://ref.ly/Mark6:18-Mark6:19)** *See also* [1KI 20:42–20:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:42-1Kgs20:43), [1KI 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:4); [2CH 30:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:10), [2CH 36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:16)

The folly of resentment
-----------------------

**[JOB 5:2](https://ref.ly/Job5:2)** *See also* [PSA 73:21–73:22](https://ref.ly/Ps73:21-Ps73:22); [PRO 15:12](https://ref.ly/Prov15:12), [PRO 19:3](https://ref.ly/Prov19:3); [LAM 3:39](https://ref.ly/Lam3:39)

The evil of resentment
----------------------

**[JOB 36:13](https://ref.ly/Job36:13)** *See also* [JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16); [JUD 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16)

Resentment towards Jesus Christ
-------------------------------

### At his miracles

[MAT 13:54–13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54-Matt13:58) parallel [MRK 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:1-Mark6:6); ; [MAT 21:15](https://ref.ly/Matt21:15)

### At his teaching

### At his claims

[JHN 6:41–6:43](https://ref.ly/John6:41-John6:43)

## 5929: resentment, against people (id: 5929)

A sinful, emotional response to others or to circumstances, frequently caused by envy or a sense of injustice.

Causes of resentment
--------------------

### Unwelcome instruction

**[PRO 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Prov3:11-Prov3:12)** *See also* [PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7), [PRO 5:12](https://ref.ly/Prov5:12)

### Feelings of rejection

**[GEN 4:2–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gen4:2-Gen4:5)** *See also* [JDG 8:1](https://ref.ly/Judg8:1); [MAT 20:24](https://ref.ly/Matt20:24)

### Feelings of injustice

**[ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1)** *See also* [GEN 27:34](https://ref.ly/Gen27:34), [GEN 50:15](https://ref.ly/Gen50:15); [MAT 20:10–20:11](https://ref.ly/Matt20:10-Matt20:11); [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

### Hard circumstances

**[1SA 30:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:6)** *See also* [1SA 22:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:2)

### Disappointment with leaders

[JOS 9:18](https://ref.ly/Josh9:18)

Resentment has no place in godly living
---------------------------------------

**[EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31)**; **[PHP 2:14](https://ref.ly/Phil2:14)** *See also* [LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18); [DEU 15:10](https://ref.ly/Deut15:10); [2CO 9:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:5); [2TI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24); [HEB 12:15](https://ref.ly/Heb12:15); [JAS 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:15), [JAS 5:9](https://ref.ly/Jas5:9); [1PE 4:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:9)

Dealing with resentment
-----------------------

### Not an easy task

**[PRO 18:19](https://ref.ly/Prov18:19)**

### Through forgiveness

**[PRO 17:9](https://ref.ly/Prov17:9)** *See also* [PRO 19:11](https://ref.ly/Prov19:11)

### Through God\-given contentment

**[PHP 4:11](https://ref.ly/Phil4:11)** *See also* [1TI 6:6–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:6-1Tim6:10); [HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)

### Through conciliation

[JDG 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Judg8:1-Judg8:3)

* **Associated Articles:** 5265: complaints (ID: `5265@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5560: suffering (ID: `5560@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5835: disappointment (ID: `5835@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5839: dissatisfaction (ID: `5839@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6230: rejection (ID: `6230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8733: envy (ID: `8733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8765: grudge (ID: `8765@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8773: jealousy (ID: `8773@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5931: resistance (id: 5931)

Opposition to the demands of the state or other legitimate authorities. Individuals must not resist such demands unless they are contrary to God's requirements. God will himself judge unjust rulers. Christians should not resist the evil person but, following Jesus Christ's teaching and example, entrust themselves to God.

God should not be resisted
--------------------------

[2CH 35:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:21); ; [ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51), [ACT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Acts11:17); ; [ROM 9:19](https://ref.ly/Rom9:19)

Individuals should not resist legitimate authorities
----------------------------------------------------

### Civil authorities

**[ROM 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1-Rom13:2)** *See also* [DEU 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12-Deut17:13); [1SA 24:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:6); [TIT 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1-Titus3:2); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14), [1PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:17)

### Religious authorities

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)** *See also* [ACT 23:5](https://ref.ly/Acts23:5)

### Parental authority

[EPH 6:1](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1); ; [COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20); ; [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2)

Resistance of unjust authorities
--------------------------------

### Resistance to human authorities is justified when they oppose God's requirements

**[EXO 1:15–1:17](https://ref.ly/Exod1:15-Exod1:17)** *See also* [EXO 1:22–2:3](https://ref.ly/Exod1:22-Exod2:3); [DAN 3:13–3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:13-Dan3:18), [DAN 6:6–6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:6-Dan6:10); [ACT 4:18–4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:20), [ACT 5:27–5:29](https://ref.ly/Acts5:27-Acts5:29); [HEB 11:23](https://ref.ly/Heb11:23)

### God will enable Christians, unjustly brought before human authorities, to defend themselves

**[MRK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Mark13:11)** parallels [MAT 10:19–10:20](https://ref.ly/Matt10:19-Matt10:20), [LUK 12:11–12:12](https://ref.ly/Luke12:11-Luke12:12) *See also* [LUK 21:14–21:15](https://ref.ly/Luke21:14-Luke21:15)

### God will vindicate those of his people who cannot resist unjust authorities

**[DAN 3:28–3:29](https://ref.ly/Dan3:28-Dan3:29)** *See also* [GEN 39:20–39:21](https://ref.ly/Gen39:20-Gen39:21); [ECC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:12); [DAN 6:19–6:28](https://ref.ly/Dan6:19-Dan6:28)

### God will himself judge unjust rulers

**[HOS 5:10](https://ref.ly/Hos5:10)** *See also* [1KI 21:15–21:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:15-1Kgs21:19); [ISA 1:23–1:26](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23-Isa1:26), [ISA 3:11–3:15](https://ref.ly/Isa3:11-Isa3:15), [ISA 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:3), [ISA 59:14–59:20](https://ref.ly/Isa59:14-Isa59:20); [EZK 22:27–22:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:27-Ezek22:31); [MIC 7:2–7:4](https://ref.ly/Mic7:2-Mic7:4)

Resisting evil
--------------

### Christians should not resist the evil person

**[MAT 5:38–5:41](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38-Matt5:41)** *See also* [MAT 5:43–5:48](https://ref.ly/Matt5:43-Matt5:48), [MAT 26:52](https://ref.ly/Matt26:52); [LUK 6:27–6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27-Luke6:30); [ROM 12:19–12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19-Rom12:21)

### Christians should follow the teaching and example of Jesus Christ and entrust themselves to God

**[1PE 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:20-1Pet2:23)** *See also* [ISA 53:7–53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7-Isa53:9); [2TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:14)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 35:21; Acts 7:51; Acts 11:17; Romans 9:19; Romans 13:1-2; Deuteronomy 17:12-13; 1 Samuel 24:6; Titus 3:1-2; 1 Peter 2:13-14; 1 Peter 2:17; Hebrews 13:17; Acts 23:5; Ephesians 6:1; Colossians 3:20; 2 Timothy 3:2; Exodus 1:15-17; Exodus 1:22-2:3; Daniel 3:13-18; Daniel 6:6-10; Acts 4:18-20; Acts 5:27-29; Hebrews 11:23; Mark 13:11; Matthew 10:19-20; Luke 12:11-12; Luke 21:14-15; Daniel 3:28-29; Genesis 39:20-21; Ecclesiastes 8:12; Daniel 6:19-28; Hosea 5:10; 1 Kings 21:15-19; Isaiah 1:23-26; Isaiah 3:11-15; Isaiah 10:1-3; Isaiah 59:14-20; Ezekiel 22:27-31; Micah 7:2-4; Matthew 5:38-41; Matthew 5:43-48; Matthew 26:52; Luke 6:27-30; Romans 12:19-21; 1 Peter 2:20-23; Isaiah 53:7-9; 2 Timothy 4:14
* **Associated Articles:** 3284: Holy Spirit, resisting (ID: `3284@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4126: Satan, resistance to (ID: `4126@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5215: authority (ID: `5215@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5257: civil authorities (ID: `5257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5829: defiance (ID: `5829@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6030: sin, avoidance of (ID: `6030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6221: rebellion (ID: `6221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6251: temptation, resisting (ID: `6251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8482: spiritual warfare (ID: `8482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8737: evil, believers' responses to (ID: `8737@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8785: opposition (ID: `8785@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5932: response (id: 5932)

People are to respond to God in obedience, worship and repentance and in love for others. Lack of response from others can be a sign of death, disobedience and defiance.

The response of people to God
-----------------------------

### Obedience and love for others

**[EXO 24:3](https://ref.ly/Exod24:3)**; **[1JN 4:11](https://ref.ly/1John4:11)** *See also* [EXO 19:8](https://ref.ly/Exod19:8), [EXO 24:7](https://ref.ly/Exod24:7); [1CH 29:6–29:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:6-1Chr29:9); [ROM 15:7](https://ref.ly/Rom15:7); [GAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Gal2:2); [PHP 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1-Phil2:4); [1JN 2:3–2:6](https://ref.ly/1John2:3-1John2:6), [1JN 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:16-1John3:17)

### Worship

**[NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6)**; **[ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1)** *See also* [NEH 12:24](https://ref.ly/Neh12:24); [DAN 4:34–4:37](https://ref.ly/Dan4:34-Dan4:37)

### Repentance

[2KI 22:18–22:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:18-2Kgs22:20) parallel [2CH 34:26–34:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:26-2Chr34:28); ; [ACT 2:37–2:38](https://ref.ly/Acts2:37-Acts2:38)

### Response to the preaching of the gospel

**[ACT 16:14](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14)** *See also* [ACT 16:31–16:34](https://ref.ly/Acts16:31-Acts16:34); [1CO 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:1-1Cor15:2); [EPH 1:13](https://ref.ly/Eph1:13); [COL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Col1:6); [1TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:13)

### Inappropriate responses

**[2CH 32:24–32:25](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:24-2Chr32:25)** *See also* [DAN 4:24–4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:24-Dan4:32); [ROM 1:21–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21-Rom1:23)

The response of God to people
-----------------------------

### God responds to those who seek him in prayer

**[PSA 102:17](https://ref.ly/Ps102:17)** *See also* [PSA 3:4](https://ref.ly/Ps3:4), [PSA 40:1–40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:1-Ps40:3); [ISA 19:22](https://ref.ly/Isa19:22); [DAN 10:12](https://ref.ly/Dan10:12); [MAT 7:7–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:7-Matt7:11) parallel [LUK 11:9–11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:9-Luke11:13); [LUK 11:5–11:8](https://ref.ly/Luke11:5-Luke11:8); [JHN 14:13–14:14](https://ref.ly/John14:13-John14:14), [JHN 15:7](https://ref.ly/John15:7), [JHN 16:24](https://ref.ly/John16:24)

### Sometimes God appears not to respond to prayer

[JOB 19:7](https://ref.ly/Job19:7); ; [PSA 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ps10:1), [PSA 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Ps13:1-Ps13:2), [PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2), [PSA 42:9–42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:9-Ps42:10); ; [ISA 1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:15), [ISA 59:1–59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:1-Isa59:2)

False gods do not respond to people
-----------------------------------

**[1KI 18:26–18:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:26-1Kgs18:29)** *See also* [DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28); [ISA 44:18](https://ref.ly/Isa44:18); [JER 10:5](https://ref.ly/Jer10:5)

The response of people to others
--------------------------------

### Believers should respond to others in kindness and forgiveness

**[EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32)** *See also* [GEN 50:15](https://ref.ly/Gen50:15), [GEN 50:21](https://ref.ly/Gen50:21); [MAT 5:38–5:42](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38-Matt5:42) parallel [LUK 6:29–6:30](https://ref.ly/Luke6:29-Luke6:30)

### Harsh or aggressive responses

[2SA 19:43](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:43); ; [ROM 12:19–12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19-Rom12:21)

Lack of response
----------------

### As an indication of death

[1SA 4:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:20); ; [2KI 4:31](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:31)

### As a sign of disobedience

**[JER 7:27–7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:27-Jer7:29)** *See also* [PRO 29:19](https://ref.ly/Prov29:19); [JER 2:30](https://ref.ly/Jer2:30), [JER 17:23](https://ref.ly/Jer17:23), [JER 32:33](https://ref.ly/Jer32:33)

### As a sign of defiance

[2KI 18:36](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:36) parallel [ISA 36:21](https://ref.ly/Isa36:21)

### From not knowing what to say

[JOB 32:15–32:16](https://ref.ly/Job32:15-Job32:16), [JOB 40:3–40:5](https://ref.ly/Job40:3-Job40:5); ; [MAT 22:46](https://ref.ly/Matt22:46); ; [LUK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:26)

Jesus Christ made no response at his trial
------------------------------------------

[MAT 27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:14) parallel [MRK 15:5](https://ref.ly/Mark15:5); ; [MRK 14:61](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61); ; [1PE 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22-1Pet2:23); ; [ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 24:3; 1 John 4:11; Exodus 19:8; Exodus 24:7; 1 Chronicles 29:6-9; Romans 15:7; Galatians 2:2; Philippians 2:1-4; 1 John 2:3-6; 1 John 3:16-17; Nehemiah 8:6; Romans 12:1; Nehemiah 12:24; Daniel 4:34-37; 2 Kings 22:18-20; 2 Chronicles 34:26-28; Acts 2:37-38; Acts 16:14; Acts 16:31-34; 1 Corinthians 15:1-2; Ephesians 1:13; Colossians 1:6; 1 Thessalonians 2:13; 2 Chronicles 32:24-25; Daniel 4:24-32; Romans 1:21-23; Psalms 102:17; Psalms 3:4; Psalms 40:1-3; Isaiah 19:22; Daniel 10:12; Matthew 7:7-11; Luke 11:9-13; Luke 11:5-8; John 14:13-14; John 15:7; John 16:24; Job 19:7; Psalms 10:1; Psalms 13:1-2; Psalms 22:1-2; Psalms 42:9-10; Isaiah 1:15; Isaiah 59:1-2; 1 Kings 18:26-29; Deuteronomy 4:28; Isaiah 44:18; Jeremiah 10:5; Ephesians 4:32; Genesis 50:15; Genesis 50:21; Matthew 5:38-42; Luke 6:29-30; 2 Samuel 19:43; Romans 12:19-21; 1 Samuel 4:20; 2 Kings 4:31; Jeremiah 7:27-29; Proverbs 29:19; Jeremiah 2:30; Jeremiah 17:23; Jeremiah 32:33; 2 Kings 18:36; Isaiah 36:21; Job 32:15-16; Job 40:3-5; Matthew 22:46; Luke 20:26; Matthew 27:14; Mark 15:5; Mark 14:61; 1 Peter 2:22-23; Isaiah 53:9

## 5933: restlessness (id: 5933)

A lack of peace, experienced physically, spiritually or mentally.

Causes of restlessness
----------------------

### Worry

**[ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12)**; **[JER 49:23](https://ref.ly/Jer49:23)** *See also* [GEN 41:8](https://ref.ly/Gen41:8); [EST 6:1](https://ref.ly/Esth6:1); [PRO 15:16](https://ref.ly/Prov15:16); [DAN 2:1](https://ref.ly/Dan2:1), [DAN 6:18–6:19](https://ref.ly/Dan6:18-Dan6:19)

### Illness

**[JOB 7:4–7:5](https://ref.ly/Job7:4-Job7:5)** *See also* [PSA 6:2–6:6](https://ref.ly/Ps6:2-Ps6:6), [PSA 32:3–32:4](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3-Ps32:4), [PSA 38:3–38:10](https://ref.ly/Ps38:3-Ps38:10); [ISA 38:13–38:14](https://ref.ly/Isa38:13-Isa38:14)

### Lack of purpose

**[ECC 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:22-Eccl2:23)** *See also* [PSA 107:4–107:5](https://ref.ly/Ps107:4-Ps107:5)

### Uncompleted business

[RUT 3:18](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:18)

### Rebelliousness

[GEN 27:40](https://ref.ly/Gen27:40)

### Demon\-possession

[MRK 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Mark5:3-Mark5:5) parallel [LUK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Luke8:29); ; [MAT 12:43](https://ref.ly/Matt12:43) parallel [LUK 11:24](https://ref.ly/Luke11:24)

### Sinfulness

**[ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21)**; **[JUD 1:13](https://ref.ly/Jude1:13)** *See also* [JOB 3:17](https://ref.ly/Job3:17); [PSA 59:15](https://ref.ly/Ps59:15); [ISA 48:22](https://ref.ly/Isa48:22); [JER 14:10](https://ref.ly/Jer14:10), [JER 50:6](https://ref.ly/Jer50:6); [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21); [JAS 3:8](https://ref.ly/Jas3:8); [REV 14:11](https://ref.ly/Rev14:11)

Restlessness as part of God's judgment against sin
--------------------------------------------------

**[DEU 28:65–28:67](https://ref.ly/Deut28:65-Deut28:67)** *See also* [GEN 4:11–4:14](https://ref.ly/Gen4:11-Gen4:14); [PSA 107:40](https://ref.ly/Ps107:40), [PSA 109:10](https://ref.ly/Ps109:10); [LAM 4:15](https://ref.ly/Lam4:15); [AMO 8:12](https://ref.ly/Amos8:12); [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2)

Antidotes to restlessness
-------------------------

### Trusting in God

**[PSA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Ps62:1)**; **[ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3), [ISA 30:15](https://ref.ly/Isa30:15)**; **[PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6)** *See also* [EXO 33:14](https://ref.ly/Exod33:14); [PSA 46:10](https://ref.ly/Ps46:10); [ISA 32:17](https://ref.ly/Isa32:17); [JER 6:16](https://ref.ly/Jer6:16); [MAT 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31-Matt6:32)

### Trusting in Jesus Christ

**[MAT 11:28–11:30](https://ref.ly/Matt11:28-Matt11:30)**

* **Associated Passages:** Ecclesiastes 5:12; Jeremiah 49:23; Genesis 41:8; Esther 6:1; Proverbs 15:16; Daniel 2:1; Daniel 6:18-19; Job 7:4-5; Psalms 6:2-6; Psalms 32:3-4; Psalms 38:3-10; Isaiah 38:13-14; Ecclesiastes 2:22-23; Psalms 107:4-5; Ruth 3:18; Genesis 27:40; Mark 5:3-5; Luke 8:29; Matthew 12:43; Luke 11:24; Isaiah 57:20-21; Jude 1:13; Job 3:17; Psalms 59:15; Isaiah 48:22; Jeremiah 14:10; Jeremiah 50:6; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; James 3:8; Revelation 14:11; Deuteronomy 28:65-67; Genesis 4:11-14; Psalms 107:40; Psalms 109:10; Lamentations 4:15; Amos 8:12; Zechariah 10:2; Psalms 62:1; Isaiah 26:3; Isaiah 30:15; Philippians 4:6; Exodus 33:14; Psalms 46:10; Isaiah 32:17; Jeremiah 6:16; Matthew 6:31-32; Matthew 11:28-30

## 5934: restraint (id: 5934)

The holding back of actions, emotions or forces, whether one's own or another's. God holds back the power and extent of evil in the world and restrains his anger. Believers are to show similar self\-control in life, especially in speech.

Restraint as the holding back of actions
----------------------------------------

### The example of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:52–26:53](https://ref.ly/Matt26:52-Matt26:53)**

### Other examples

*See also* [EXO 36:6](https://ref.ly/Exod36:6) *God restrains the Israelites*; [1SA 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:13) *Eli's failure to restrain his sons*[1SA 24:1–24:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1-1Sam24:22), [1SA 26:1–26:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:1-1Sam26:25)[1KI 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:6) *David's failure to restrain Adonijah*; [EST 5:10](https://ref.ly/Esth5:10) *Haman's self\-restraint*; [JER 14:10](https://ref.ly/Jer14:10) *Judah's lack of restraint*; [2PE 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:16) *Balaam's donkey*

Restraint as the holding back of emotions
-----------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ's silence under suffering

**[1PE 2:21–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21-1Pet2:23)** *See also* [MAT 26:62–26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:62-Matt26:63) parallel [MRK 14:60–14:61](https://ref.ly/Mark14:60-Mark14:61); [MAT 27:12–27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:12-Matt27:14) parallel [MRK 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/Mark15:4-Mark15:5)

Restraint as the holding back of forces
---------------------------------------

### God and the Red Sea

**[EXO 14:21](https://ref.ly/Exod14:21)** *See also* [EXO 15:8](https://ref.ly/Exod15:8); [PSA 78:13](https://ref.ly/Ps78:13)

### Jesus Christ and the wind and the waves

[MAT 8:23–8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:23-Matt8:27) parallels [MRK 4:36–4:41](https://ref.ly/Mark4:36-Mark4:41), [LUK 8:22–8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:22-Luke8:25)

God restrains the power of evil
-------------------------------

### In the world

**[MRK 13:20](https://ref.ly/Mark13:20)** parallel [MAT 24:22](https://ref.ly/Matt24:22) *See also* [2KI 19:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:28); [PSA 76:10](https://ref.ly/Ps76:10); [2TH 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:6-2Thess2:7); [REV 20:2](https://ref.ly/Rev20:2)

### From the righteous

**[JHN 17:15](https://ref.ly/John17:15)** *See also* [1SA 25:39](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:39); [1CH 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:10); [PSA 19:13](https://ref.ly/Ps19:13), [PSA 34:7](https://ref.ly/Ps34:7), [PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3); [MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13) parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4); [REV 7:3](https://ref.ly/Rev7:3)

God restrains his anger
-----------------------

**[PSA 78:38](https://ref.ly/Ps78:38)** *See also* [EXO 34:6](https://ref.ly/Exod34:6); [NUM 14:18](https://ref.ly/Num14:18); [NEH 9:17](https://ref.ly/Neh9:17); [PSA 74:11](https://ref.ly/Ps74:11), [PSA 86:15](https://ref.ly/Ps86:15), [PSA 103:8](https://ref.ly/Ps103:8), [PSA 145:8](https://ref.ly/Ps145:8); [ISA 48:9](https://ref.ly/Isa48:9); [JOL 2:13](https://ref.ly/Joel2:13); [JON 4:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:2); [2PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:9)

Believers are to show restraint
-------------------------------

### In their speech

**[PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)**; **[JAS 1:26](https://ref.ly/Jas1:26)** *See also* [PSA 39:1](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1); [PRO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Prov13:3), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23); [JAS 3:2–3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:2-Jas3:12); [1PE 3:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:10); [PSA 34:13](https://ref.ly/Ps34:13)

### In their actions

## 5935: riddles (id: 5935)

Enigmatic or allegorical statements whose meaning must be searched for or revealed by God. They may be compared with other forms of obscure communication and contrasted with clear messages. Riddles are associated with proverbs and parables as means of teaching wisdom.

Samson's riddle
---------------

### The riddle posed

**[JDG 14:5–14:14](https://ref.ly/Judg14:5-Judg14:14)** *In the ancient world, riddles were popular at feasts and special occasions.*

### The riddle solved

**[JDG 14:15–14:20](https://ref.ly/Judg14:15-Judg14:20)**

Further examples of riddles
---------------------------

### Riddles and dreams

*See also* [GEN 37:5–37:9](https://ref.ly/Gen37:5-Gen37:9) *Joseph's dreams*; [GEN 40:5–40:8](https://ref.ly/Gen40:5-Gen40:8) *dreams of Pharaoh's cupbearer and baker*; [GEN 41:1–41:8](https://ref.ly/Gen41:1-Gen41:8) *Pharaoh's dreams*; [DAN 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Dan2:1-Dan2:3) *Nebuchadnezzar's dream*

### Four mysteries

[PRO 30:18–30:19](https://ref.ly/Prov30:18-Prov30:19)

### The eagles and the vine

[EZK 17:2–17:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:2-Ezek17:8), [EZK 17:11–17:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:11-Ezek17:15)

### The writing on the wall

[DAN 5:5–5:9](https://ref.ly/Dan5:5-Dan5:9), [DAN 5:25–5:28](https://ref.ly/Dan5:25-Dan5:28)

### Riddles in the ministry of Jesus Christ

**[JHN 16:25](https://ref.ly/John16:25)** *See also* [JHN 2:19](https://ref.ly/John2:19) *the destruction of the temple*; [JHN 16:16–16:19](https://ref.ly/John16:16-John16:19) *Jesus Christ predicts his death and resurrection in enigmatic form.*

### The riddle of the number of the beast

[REV 13:18](https://ref.ly/Rev13:18), [REV 17:7–17:9](https://ref.ly/Rev17:7-Rev17:9)

Riddles and prophetic communication
-----------------------------------

### Riddles contrasted with clear messages

**[NUM 12:6–12:8](https://ref.ly/Num12:6-Num12:8)** *See also* [DEU 34:10](https://ref.ly/Deut34:10); [1CO 13:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:12); [1PE 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:10-1Pet1:11)

### Messianic prophecies cast as riddles

[GEN 49:8–49:11](https://ref.ly/Gen49:8-Gen49:11); ; [ISA 11:1](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1), [ISA 53:2](https://ref.ly/Isa53:2)

Riddles as means of teaching wisdom
-----------------------------------

### Riddles explained by the wise

**[PSA 49:3–49:4](https://ref.ly/Ps49:3-Ps49:4)** *See also* [1KI 10:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:3) parallel [2CH 9:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:2); [DAN 5:12](https://ref.ly/Dan5:12)

### Riddles and proverbs

**[PRO 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:6)** *See also* [PRO 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:8), [PRO 26:1–26:2](https://ref.ly/Prov26:1-Prov26:2), [PRO 26:7–26:10](https://ref.ly/Prov26:7-Prov26:10)

### Riddles and parables

## 5936: riots (id: 5936)

The hostile reactions of crowds who take the law into their own hands, frequently in opposition to a messenger of God.

Riotous responses to unpopular actions or teaching
--------------------------------------------------

[GEN 19:4–19:11](https://ref.ly/Gen19:4-Gen19:11) *the hostility of the men of Sodom against Lot*; ; [JDG 6:30–6:31](https://ref.ly/Judg6:30-Judg6:31) *The men of Ophrah call for the death of Gideon*; ; [JER 26:8–26:11](https://ref.ly/Jer26:8-Jer26:11) *The people react to Jeremiah's message*; ; [LUK 4:28–4:30](https://ref.ly/Luke4:28-Luke4:30) *The people of Nazareth expel Jesus Christ*; ; [ACT 19:23–19:34](https://ref.ly/Acts19:23-Acts19:34) *the anger of the Ephesians over Paul*; ; [2CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:5)

Riots stirred up intentionally
------------------------------

[MAT 27:20–27:24](https://ref.ly/Matt27:20-Matt27:24) parallel [MRK 15:6–15:15](https://ref.ly/Mark15:6-Mark15:15); ; [ACT 6:8–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:8-Acts6:14), [ACT 17:5–17:9](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5-Acts17:9), [ACT 21:27–21:35](https://ref.ly/Acts21:27-Acts21:35)

Riotous behaviour avoided or quelled
------------------------------------

[MAT 26:3–26:5](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:5) parallel [MRK 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1-Mark14:2); ; [ACT 19:35–19:41](https://ref.ly/Acts19:35-Acts19:41), [ACT 24:5–24:12](https://ref.ly/Acts24:5-Acts24:12)

## 5937: rivalry (id: 5937)

Viewing other people as a threat to oneself in the accomplishment of some aim, or being in active competition with others for recognition or achievement.

Examples of rivalry at a national level
---------------------------------------

### Between Israel and Judah

[1KI 15:25–15:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:25-1Kgs15:31) *This is typical of the conflict that existed for 80 years after the division of the kingdom.*

### Within Israel

**[1KI 16:21–16:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:21-1Kgs16:22)**

### Between Assyria and Judah

*See also* [2CH 32:13–32:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:13-2Chr32:22); [ISA 37:10–37:13](https://ref.ly/Isa37:10-Isa37:13) *Assyria claimed that behind the rivalry of the nations lay the rivalry of their gods. The LORD vindicated himself in answer to Hezekiah's prayer (see [Isa 37:14–38](https://ref.ly/Isa37:14-Isa37:38)).*

Examples of rivalry at an individual level
------------------------------------------

### Between Esau and Jacob

[GEN 25:29–25:34](https://ref.ly/Gen25:29-Gen25:34), [GEN 27:41](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41)

### Between Peninnah and Hannah

**[1SA 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:6-1Sam1:7)**

### Between Saul and David

**[1SA 18:7–18:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:7-1Sam18:9)** *See also* [1SA 20:31](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:31)

### Between Absalom and David

[2SA 15:1–15:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:1-2Sam15:13)

### Between Rehoboam and Jeroboam

[1KI 12:26–12:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:26-1Kgs12:27)

Condemnation of rivalry at an individual level
----------------------------------------------

### By the Mosaic law

[LEV 18:18](https://ref.ly/Lev18:18)

### By Jesus Christ

**[LUK 9:46–9:48](https://ref.ly/Luke9:46-Luke9:48)** parallels [MAT 18:1–18:4](https://ref.ly/Matt18:1-Matt18:4), [MRK 9:34–9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:34-Mark9:37) *See also* [MAT 20:20–20:24](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20-Matt20:24) parallel [MRK 10:35–10:41](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35-Mark10:41); [MAT 23:8–23:11](https://ref.ly/Matt23:8-Matt23:11)

### By Paul

**[1CO 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3-1Cor3:4)** *See also* [1CO 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:12-1Cor1:13)

Rivalry between individuals is overcome in Christ
-------------------------------------------------

## 5938: sadness (id: 5938)

Heaviness of heart brought about through disappointment, disillusionment, failure or sin. It can lead to depression of spirit, but this can be relieved.

Sadness caused by disappointment or disillusionment
---------------------------------------------------

**[NEH 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Neh2:1-Neh2:3)** *See also* [GEN 40:6–40:7](https://ref.ly/Gen40:6-Gen40:7); [1KI 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:4); [ECC 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:1-Eccl6:2); [ISA 19:1–19:10](https://ref.ly/Isa19:1-Isa19:10); [MAT 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21-Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:21–10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21-Mark10:22), [LUK 18:22–18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22-Luke18:23); [MAT 26:21–26:22](https://ref.ly/Matt26:21-Matt26:22); [LUK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Luke24:17)

Sadness leading to depression of spirit
---------------------------------------

**[NUM 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/Num11:14-Num11:15)** *See also* [JOS 7:7](https://ref.ly/Josh7:7); [1KI 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3-1Kgs19:4); [PSA 42:3–42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:3-Ps42:4); [JER 15:10](https://ref.ly/Jer15:10)

---------------------

## 5939: satisfaction (id: 5939)

A state of contentment or fulfilment. Only God brings true satisfaction and Scripture advocates that believers should cultivate satisfaction in every situation. Jesus Christ's atoning death was the unique means of satisfying the demands of the law.

Satisfaction of hunger and thirst
---------------------------------

### Literal

**[PRO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30)** *See also* [LEV 26:26](https://ref.ly/Lev26:26); [DEU 14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:29), [DEU 26:12](https://ref.ly/Deut26:12); [JOB 38:39–38:41](https://ref.ly/Job38:39-Job38:41); [PSA 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ps22:26), [PSA 59:15](https://ref.ly/Ps59:15), [PSA 105:40](https://ref.ly/Ps105:40), [PSA 132:15](https://ref.ly/Ps132:15), [PSA 147:14](https://ref.ly/Ps147:14); [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7); [JER 31:25](https://ref.ly/Jer31:25); [HOS 13:6](https://ref.ly/Hos13:6); [JOL 2:19](https://ref.ly/Joel2:19); [MIC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:14)

### Metaphorical

**[PSA 104:13](https://ref.ly/Ps104:13)** *See also* [JOB 38:25–38:27](https://ref.ly/Job38:25-Job38:27); [ISA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Isa66:11); [JER 46:10](https://ref.ly/Jer46:10), [JER 50:19](https://ref.ly/Jer50:19)

Satisfaction in one's work
--------------------------

**[ECC 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:13)** *See also* [ECC 2:24](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:24), [ECC 5:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:18)

Satisfaction with one's situation
---------------------------------

**[HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)** *See also* [LUK 3:14](https://ref.ly/Luke3:14); [PHP 4:10–4:13](https://ref.ly/Phil4:10-Phil4:13); [1TI 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:6-1Tim6:8)

Spiritual satisfaction
----------------------

### Found in God

**[PSA 17:15](https://ref.ly/Ps17:15)** *See also* [PSA 63:1–63:5](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1-Ps63:5), [PSA 81:13–81:16](https://ref.ly/Ps81:13-Ps81:16), [PSA 90:14](https://ref.ly/Ps90:14); [PRO 19:23](https://ref.ly/Prov19:23); [ISA 55:1–55:2](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1-Isa55:2); [LUK 6:21](https://ref.ly/Luke6:21); [JHN 4:7–4:15](https://ref.ly/John4:7-John4:15)

### As a result of God's good provision

**[PSA 145:16](https://ref.ly/Ps145:16)** *See also* [DEU 8:10](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10), [DEU 11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:15); [PSA 91:16](https://ref.ly/Ps91:16), [PSA 107:8–107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:8-Ps107:9); [ISA 58:9–58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:9-Isa58:11)

### Jesus Christ satisfies the hungry

**[MAT 14:15–14:20](https://ref.ly/Matt14:15-Matt14:20)** parallels [MRK 6:35–6:44](https://ref.ly/Mark6:35-Mark6:44), [LUK 9:12–9:17](https://ref.ly/Luke9:12-Luke9:17) *See also* [MAT 15:32–15:37](https://ref.ly/Matt15:32-Matt15:37) parallel [MRK 8:2–8:8](https://ref.ly/Mark8:2-Mark8:8)

Dissatisfaction
---------------

### Riches do not bring satisfaction

**[ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10)** *See also* [EST 5:10–5:13](https://ref.ly/Esth5:10-Esth5:13); [ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8); [EZK 7:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:19); [LUK 12:13–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:13-Luke12:21); [1TI 6:7–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7-1Tim6:10); [HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5)

### Death is never satisfied

**[PRO 27:20](https://ref.ly/Prov27:20)** *See also* [PRO 30:15–30:16](https://ref.ly/Prov30:15-Prov30:16); [HAB 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hab2:5)

Jesus Christ's satisfaction of the demands of the law
-----------------------------------------------------

## 5940: searching (id: 5940)

Scripture points to several aspects of searching: the human search for satisfaction in the things of this world, the human search for God and God's search for lost humanity.

The human search for security in the world
------------------------------------------

### The search for material well\-being

**[LUK 12:16–12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:19)** *See also* [GEN 42:1–42:3](https://ref.ly/Gen42:1-Gen42:3); [RUT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:1); [JOB 28:1–28:11](https://ref.ly/Job28:1-Job28:11); [LAM 1:11](https://ref.ly/Lam1:11)

### The search for comfort

**[PSA 119:82](https://ref.ly/Ps119:82)** *See also* [EXO 14:10–14:12](https://ref.ly/Exod14:10-Exod14:12); [NUM 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Num11:4-Num11:6); [1KI 1:1–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:1-1Kgs1:4)

### The search for success

**[ECC 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1-Eccl2:11)** *See also* [2CH 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:9-2Chr18:12); [JER 45:5](https://ref.ly/Jer45:5)

### The search for security

**[JOS 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/Josh9:22-Josh9:24)** *The Gibeonites resorted to deception in their eagerness to be secure.* *See also* [EXO 16:2–16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:2-Exod16:3); [JDG 18:7](https://ref.ly/Judg18:7); [2KI 20:19](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:19) parallel [ISA 39:8](https://ref.ly/Isa39:8)

### The search for love

**[PRO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Prov7:18)** *See also* [JDG 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/Judg14:1-Judg14:3); [2SA 13:1–13:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:14); [1KI 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:3)

### The search for wisdom and understanding

**[ECC 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:16-Eccl8:17)** *See also* [ECC 7:24–7:29](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:24-Eccl7:29)

Instructions to search for God
------------------------------

**[ISA 55:6–55:7](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6-Isa55:7)** *See also* [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); [2CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14); [JER 29:13](https://ref.ly/Jer29:13); [MAT 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Matt7:7-Matt7:8)

God's search for lost humanity
------------------------------

**[EZK 34:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:11)** *See also* [LUK 15:1–15:10](https://ref.ly/Luke15:1-Luke15:10), [LUK 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/Luke19:9-Luke19:10)

God searches a person's innermost being
---------------------------------------

**[PRO 20:27](https://ref.ly/Prov20:27)** *See also* [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); [PSA 139:1–139:4](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:4); [JER 17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:10); [ROM 8:27](https://ref.ly/Rom8:27); [REV 2:23](https://ref.ly/Rev2:23)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 12:16-19; Genesis 42:1-3; Ruth 3:1; Job 28:1-11; Lamentations 1:11; Psalms 119:82; Exodus 14:10-12; Numbers 11:4-6; 1 Kings 1:1-4; Ecclesiastes 2:1-11; 2 Chronicles 18:9-12; Jeremiah 45:5; Joshua 9:22-24; Exodus 16:2-3; Judges 18:7; 2 Kings 20:19; Isaiah 39:8; Proverbs 7:18; Judges 14:1-3; 2 Samuel 13:1-14; 1 Kings 11:1-3; Ecclesiastes 8:16-17; Ecclesiastes 7:24-29; Isaiah 55:6-7; 1 Chronicles 28:9; 2 Chronicles 7:14; Jeremiah 29:13; Matthew 7:7-8; Ezekiel 34:11; Luke 15:1-10; Luke 19:9-10; Proverbs 20:27; Psalms 139:1-4; Jeremiah 17:10; Romans 8:27; Revelation 2:23
* **Associated Articles:** 1020: God, all-knowing (ID: `1020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1220: God, as shepherd (ID: `1220@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5935: riddles (ID: `5935@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5960: success (ID: `5960@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6650: finding (ID: `6650@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6701: peace, human search for (ID: `6701@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8160: seeking God (ID: `8160@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8648: enquiring of God (ID: `8648@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8778: materialism (ID: `8778@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5941: secrecy (id: 5941)

Behaving in a way designed to keep intentions or feelings hidden from others. In Scripture acting or speaking in secret is often linked with wrongdoing, although not in all cases. But nothing can be kept secret from God.

Secrecy as a cover for wrongdoing
---------------------------------

**[EPH 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Eph5:11-Eph5:12)** *See also* [DEU 13:6](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6), [DEU 27:15](https://ref.ly/Deut27:15), [DEU 27:24](https://ref.ly/Deut27:24); [2SA 15:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:10) *Absalom's camp*; [2KI 17:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:9) *high places, centres of pagan Canaanite worship*; [JOB 31:24–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:24-Job31:28); [PSA 10:8](https://ref.ly/Ps10:8); [PRO 9:17](https://ref.ly/Prov9:17), [PRO 17:23](https://ref.ly/Prov17:23); [ISA 65:4](https://ref.ly/Isa65:4); [ACT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11); [2CO 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:2); [2TH 2:72](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:72)PE [2:1](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:1); [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

Secrecy associated with Satan and the black arts
------------------------------------------------

**[REV 2:24](https://ref.ly/Rev2:24)** *See also* [EXO 7:11](https://ref.ly/Exod7:11), [EXO 7:22](https://ref.ly/Exod7:22), [EXO 8:7](https://ref.ly/Exod8:7), [EXO 8:18](https://ref.ly/Exod8:18); [2TH 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:7)

Secrecy sometimes ensures safety
--------------------------------

**[JOS 2:1](https://ref.ly/Josh2:1)** *See also* [JDG 3:15–3:26](https://ref.ly/Judg3:15-Judg3:26); [2SA 21:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:12)**[EST 2:20](https://ref.ly/Esth2:20)** *See also* [JER 13:17](https://ref.ly/Jer13:17), [JER 38:16](https://ref.ly/Jer38:16); [MRK 7:24](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24); [JHN 7:2–7:10](https://ref.ly/John7:2-John7:10), [JHN 19:38](https://ref.ly/John19:38)

Nothing can be kept secret from God
-----------------------------------

**[JER 23:24](https://ref.ly/Jer23:24)** *See also* [2SA 12:7–12:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:7-2Sam12:12); [JOB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Job13:10); [PSA 44:20–44:21](https://ref.ly/Ps44:20-Ps44:21), [PSA 90:8](https://ref.ly/Ps90:8), [PSA 101:5](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5), [PSA 139:1–139:15](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:15)

God's truths are not made known in secret
-----------------------------------------

**[ISA 45:19](https://ref.ly/Isa45:19)** *See also* [ISA 48:16](https://ref.ly/Isa48:16); [JHN 18:20](https://ref.ly/John18:20); [ACT 26:26](https://ref.ly/Acts26:26)

Secrecy in worship
------------------

**[MAT 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16-Matt6:18)** *See also* [MAT 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:1-Matt6:6)

Secrets
-------

### Divine secrets

**[DEU 29:29](https://ref.ly/Deut29:29)** *See also* [JOB 11:5–11:6](https://ref.ly/Job11:5-Job11:6); [EZK 28:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:3); [MAT 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:10-Matt13:11) parallel [MRK 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:11); [LUK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:10); [ISA 6:9](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9); [1CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:7), [1CO 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:1)

### Human secrets

**[PRO 11:13](https://ref.ly/Prov11:13)** *See also* [JDG 16:5–16:17](https://ref.ly/Judg16:5-Judg16:17); [ROM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Rom2:16); [1CO 14:24–14:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:24-1Cor14:25); [PHP 4:12](https://ref.ly/Phil4:12)

## 5942: security (id: 5942)

Guaranteed safety, whether natural or spiritual, from danger or loss. Scripture stresses that ultimate security is to be found only in God himself.

The security of Israel
----------------------

### Their security was found in God himself

**[DEU 33:27](https://ref.ly/Deut33:27)** *See also* [DEU 33:26](https://ref.ly/Deut33:26), [DEU 33:28–33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:28-Deut33:29); [EZR 8:21–8:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:21-Ezra8:23); [JER 31:10–31:11](https://ref.ly/Jer31:10-Jer31:11)

### They were secure from danger and loss

**[ISA 54:17](https://ref.ly/Isa54:17)** *See also* [EZK 34:27–34:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:27-Ezek34:28)

### Their security was conditional upon obedience

**[EXO 19:5](https://ref.ly/Exod19:5)** *See also* [DEU 28:1–28:6](https://ref.ly/Deut28:1-Deut28:6), [DEU 28:15–28:19](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:19)

The security of believers is guaranteed
---------------------------------------

### By the everlasting covenant

**[2SA 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:5)** *See also* [ROM 8:29–8:30](https://ref.ly/Rom8:29-Rom8:30)

### In spiritual security in Jesus Christ

**[JHN 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:29)** *See also* [JHN 17:12](https://ref.ly/John17:12), [JHN 17:15](https://ref.ly/John17:15); [ROM 8:35–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:39); [PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6); [HEB 7:25](https://ref.ly/Heb7:25)

### In the provision of needs

**[MAT 6:33](https://ref.ly/Matt6:33)** *See also* [PSA 23:1](https://ref.ly/Ps23:1), [PSA 23:4](https://ref.ly/Ps23:4); [PHP 4:19](https://ref.ly/Phil4:19); [HEB 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5-Heb13:6); [PSA 118:6–118:7](https://ref.ly/Ps118:6-Ps118:7)

### In safety from danger

**[PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1)** *See also* [PSA 91:1–91:15](https://ref.ly/Ps91:1-Ps91:15), [PSA 121:3–121:5](https://ref.ly/Ps121:3-Ps121:5); [JUD 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24-Jude1:25)

### In a sure hope

**[2TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:12)** *See also* [JOB 19:25–19:27](https://ref.ly/Job19:25-Job19:27); [HEB 6:19](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19); [1PE 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:5)

### In prophecies of security

[ISA 2:4](https://ref.ly/Isa2:4), [ISA 11:6–11:9](https://ref.ly/Isa11:6-Isa11:9), [ISA 65:21–65:25](https://ref.ly/Isa65:21-Isa65:25); ; [MIC 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:3-Mic4:4); ; [REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)

Provision for the security of human beings
------------------------------------------

### Protection for the weak

[RUT 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth2:8-Ruth2:9); ; [1SA 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:1-1Sam11:6), [1SA 22:22–22:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:22-1Sam22:23); ; [EZR 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:6-Ezra6:7); ; [PRO 3:29](https://ref.ly/Prov3:29); ; [ACT 23:10](https://ref.ly/Acts23:10), [ACT 23:23–23:24](https://ref.ly/Acts23:23-Acts23:24); ; [ROM 13:3](https://ref.ly/Rom13:3)

### Financial pledges

[PRO 6:1–6:5](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:5), [PRO 11:15](https://ref.ly/Prov11:15), [PRO 17:18](https://ref.ly/Prov17:18), [PRO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Prov22:26-Prov22:27); ; [LUK 10:35](https://ref.ly/Luke10:35); ; [PHM 1:17–1:19](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:17-Phlm1:19)

### Pastoral protection

[JHN 21:16](https://ref.ly/John21:16); ; [ACT 20:28](https://ref.ly/Acts20:28); ; [PHP 2:19–2:20](https://ref.ly/Phil2:19-Phil2:20); ; [1PE 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:2)

False security
--------------

### In Israel

**[JER 22:21](https://ref.ly/Jer22:21)** *See also* [ISA 28:17–28:18](https://ref.ly/Isa28:17-Isa28:18), [ISA 29:13–29:15](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13-Isa29:15); [DAN 11:21–11:24](https://ref.ly/Dan11:21-Dan11:24); [AMO 6:1](https://ref.ly/Amos6:1); [LUK 3:8](https://ref.ly/Luke3:8); [JHN 8:33](https://ref.ly/John8:33)

### In the church

**[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)** *See also* [REV 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rev2:4), [REV 2:14](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14), [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20), [REV 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Rev3:1-Rev3:2)

### In professing Christians

**[1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12)** *See also* [MAT 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21-Matt7:23), [MAT 22:11–22:12](https://ref.ly/Matt22:11-Matt22:12), [MAT 25:41–25:46](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41-Matt25:46); [LUK 18:9](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9); [HEB 10:26–10:27](https://ref.ly/Heb10:26-Heb10:27); [2PE 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:21-2Pet2:22); [1JN 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:8-1John1:10); [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

### In the world

**[1TH 5:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:3)** *See also* [JDG 8:11](https://ref.ly/Judg8:11), [JDG 18:7](https://ref.ly/Judg18:7); [1SA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:3-1Sam24:4); [JOB 12:6](https://ref.ly/Job12:6), [JOB 18:14](https://ref.ly/Job18:14), [JOB 31:24](https://ref.ly/Job31:24); [PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24); [ZEC 1:15](https://ref.ly/Zech1:15); [LUK 17:26–17:28](https://ref.ly/Luke17:26-Luke17:28); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 33:27; Deuteronomy 33:26; Deuteronomy 33:28-29; Ezra 8:21-23; Jeremiah 31:10-11; Isaiah 54:17; Ezekiel 34:27-28; Exodus 19:5; Deuteronomy 28:1-6; Deuteronomy 28:15-19; 2 Samuel 23:5; Romans 8:29-30; John 10:28-29; John 17:12; John 17:15; Romans 8:35-39; Philippians 1:6; Hebrews 7:25; Matthew 6:33; Psalms 23:1; Psalms 23:4; Philippians 4:19; Hebrews 13:5-6; Psalms 118:6-7; Psalms 46:1; Psalms 91:1-15; Psalms 121:3-5; Jude 1:24-25; 2 Timothy 1:12; Job 19:25-27; Hebrews 6:19; 1 Peter 1:3-5; Isaiah 2:4; Isaiah 11:6-9; Isaiah 65:21-25; Micah 4:3-4; Revelation 21:4; Ruth 2:8-9; 1 Samuel 11:1-6; 1 Samuel 22:22-23; Ezra 6:6-7; Proverbs 3:29; Acts 23:10; Acts 23:23-24; Romans 13:3; Proverbs 6:1-5; Proverbs 11:15; Proverbs 17:18; Proverbs 22:26-27; Luke 10:35; Philemon 1:17-19; John 21:16; Acts 20:28; Philippians 2:19-20; 1 Peter 5:1-2; Jeremiah 22:21; Isaiah 28:17-18; Isaiah 29:13-15; Daniel 11:21-24; Amos 6:1; Luke 3:8; John 8:33; Revelation 3:17; Revelation 2:4; Revelation 2:20; Revelation 3:1-2; 1 Corinthians 10:12; Matthew 7:21-23; Matthew 22:11-12; Matthew 25:41-46; Luke 18:9; Hebrews 10:26-27; 2 Peter 2:21-22; 1 John 1:8-10; Jude 1:4; 1 Thessalonians 5:3; Judges 8:11; Judges 18:7; 1 Samuel 24:3-4; Job 12:6; Job 18:14; Job 31:24; Proverbs 27:24; Zechariah 1:15; Luke 17:26-28; 1 Timothy 6:17

## 5943: self-deception (id: 5943)

The state of individuals or nations who do not face up to their sin, their spiritual state or their future with any realism. It also describes those who presume upon their relationship with God or their own ability, or who have a misplaced trust in idols.

Those who are deceived over their sin
-------------------------------------

**[1JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/1John1:8)** *See also* [PSA 36:1–36:4](https://ref.ly/Ps36:1-Ps36:4); [JER 2:34–2:35](https://ref.ly/Jer2:34-Jer2:35), [JER 17:9–17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9-Jer17:10); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10); [1JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:10)

Those who are deceived over their spiritual state
-------------------------------------------------

**[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)** *See also* [MAT 23:13–23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:13-Matt23:15), [MAT 23:23–23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23-Matt23:33) parallel [LUK 11:42–11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:42-Luke11:52); [JHN 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/John8:33-John8:34), [JHN 8:39–8:47](https://ref.ly/John8:39-John8:47); [JAS 1:22–1:26](https://ref.ly/Jas1:22-Jas1:26)

Those who are deceived concerning the future
--------------------------------------------

**[JAS 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:16)** *See also* [JER 5:31](https://ref.ly/Jer5:31), [JER 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13-Jer6:14) parallel [JER 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/Jer8:10-Jer8:11); [JER 14:14–14:16](https://ref.ly/Jer14:14-Jer14:16), [JER 23:16–23:18](https://ref.ly/Jer23:16-Jer23:18); [EZK 13:7–13:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:7-Ezek13:9); [MIC 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mic3:5); [LUK 12:16–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:20)

Those who presume on their relationship with God
------------------------------------------------

**[JER 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Jer7:9-Jer7:11)** *See also* [DEU 29:14–29:21](https://ref.ly/Deut29:14-Deut29:21); [JER 37:9–37:10](https://ref.ly/Jer37:9-Jer37:10); [MAT 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Matt3:8-Matt3:9) parallel [LUK 3:8](https://ref.ly/Luke3:8); [MAT 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21-Matt7:23) parallel [LUK 13:25–13:27](https://ref.ly/Luke13:25-Luke13:27); [MAT 25:41–25:46](https://ref.ly/Matt25:41-Matt25:46); [JHN 8:39–8:47](https://ref.ly/John8:39-John8:47)

### The value of circumcision

[ROM 2:17–2:29](https://ref.ly/Rom2:17-Rom2:29); ; [1CO 7:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:19); ; [PHP 3:2–3:7](https://ref.ly/Phil3:2-Phil3:7)

Those who presume on their own ability
--------------------------------------

**[1CO 3:18–3:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:18-1Cor3:20)** *See also* [PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15); [JER 49:16](https://ref.ly/Jer49:16) parallel [OBA 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3-Obad1:4); [MAT 26:33–26:35](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33-Matt26:35) parallels [MRK 14:27–14:31](https://ref.ly/Mark14:27-Mark14:31), [LUK 22:33–22:34](https://ref.ly/Luke22:33-Luke22:34), [JHN 13:37–13:38](https://ref.ly/John13:37-John13:38); [2CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:12); [GAL 6:3](https://ref.ly/Gal6:3)

Those who deceive themselves about the acceptability of their worship
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 1:11–1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:17)** *See also* [ISA 58:2–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:2-Isa58:7); [JER 7:2–7:8](https://ref.ly/Jer7:2-Jer7:8), [JER 7:21–7:29](https://ref.ly/Jer7:21-Jer7:29); [AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24); [MAL 1:6–1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:14); [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5), [MAT 6:7](https://ref.ly/Matt6:7)

Those who have a misplaced trust in their own righteousness
-----------------------------------------------------------

## 5944: self-defence (id: 5944)

The people of God in the OT defended themselves, but their ultimate defence was to be God himself. Paul, using the channels open to him, answered false charges against him in the church and before civil authorities. Christians are not to take personal revenge but, following Jesus Christ's example, are to entrust themselves to God.

Defence of one's property
-------------------------

**[EXO 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Exod22:2-Exod22:3)**

Defence of one's person
-----------------------

### The use of a stronghold

*See also* [JDG 6:2](https://ref.ly/Judg6:2); [1SA 23:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:14); [2SA 5:17](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:17)

### The strength of numbers

**[ECC 4:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:12)**

### Trust in the Lord

**[DAN 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:16-Dan3:18)**

## 5945: self-pity (id: 5945)

Sorrow or despondency aroused by one's own suffering or misfortune. It can result from personal loss or suffering, hardship in God's service or the judgment of God. Its cure is to remember the goodness of God.

Examples of self\-pity as a result of personal loss or suffering
----------------------------------------------------------------

### The Israelites in the wilderness

**[EXO 16:3](https://ref.ly/Exod16:3)** *See also* [EXO 14:10–14:12](https://ref.ly/Exod14:10-Exod14:12), [EXO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Exod17:3); [NUM 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Num11:4-Num11:6), [NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4); [DEU 1:26–1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:26-Deut1:28); [1CO 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:10-1Cor10:11)

### Job

**[JOB 6:1–6:13](https://ref.ly/Job6:1-Job6:13), [JOB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Job10:1)** *See also* [JOB 3:1–3:26](https://ref.ly/Job3:1-Job3:26), [JOB 12:1–12:6](https://ref.ly/Job12:1-Job12:6), [JOB 16:6–17:2](https://ref.ly/Job16:6-Job17:2), [JOB 30:9–30:31](https://ref.ly/Job30:9-Job30:31)

### Other examples

[GEN 27:34](https://ref.ly/Gen27:34), [GEN 27:38](https://ref.ly/Gen27:38); ; [2SA 15:30](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:30); ; [2KI 20:2–20:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:2-2Kgs20:3) parallel [ISA 38:2–38:3](https://ref.ly/Isa38:2-Isa38:3); ; [PSA 10:1](https://ref.ly/Ps10:1), [PSA 73:2–73:3](https://ref.ly/Ps73:2-Ps73:3), [PSA 73:13–73:14](https://ref.ly/Ps73:13-Ps73:14), [PSA 74:1](https://ref.ly/Ps74:1)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

### Moses

**[NUM 11:11–11:15](https://ref.ly/Num11:11-Num11:15)** *See also* [EXO 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Exod5:22-Exod5:23), [EXO 17:4](https://ref.ly/Exod17:4)

Examples of self\-pity as a result of God's judgment
----------------------------------------------------

[PSA 137:1–137:9](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1-Ps137:9); ; [LAM 3:1–3:18](https://ref.ly/Lam3:1-Lam3:18); ; [REV 18:9–18:19](https://ref.ly/Rev18:9-Rev18:19) *The kings, merchants and sailors will be full of self\-pity when they see the destruction of Babylon, because their opportunities for making wealth will be gone.*

---------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------

## 5946: sensitivity (id: 5946)

Deep feelings for others and sympathy for their needs, leading to appropriate action on their behalf. Spiritual sensitivity is the ability to perceive and respond to the call of God or the spiritual demands of the moment. Oversensitivity and lack of sensitivity are extremes to be avoided.

God's sensitivity to his people
-------------------------------

**[EXO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7)** *See also* [JDG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Judg2:18); [ISA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Isa40:2); [LAM 3:22](https://ref.ly/Lam3:22); [HOS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14); [LUK 1:78](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78)

Sensitivity shown by Jesus Christ
---------------------------------

**[MAT 12:20](https://ref.ly/Matt12:20)** *See also* [ISA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Isa42:3)**[MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37)** parallel [LUK 13:34](https://ref.ly/Luke13:34) *See also* [LUK 7:12–7:13](https://ref.ly/Luke7:12-Luke7:13), [LUK 19:41](https://ref.ly/Luke19:41); [JHN 9:34–9:35](https://ref.ly/John9:34-John9:35), [JHN 11:33–11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:33-John11:35)

### Jesus Christ's own experience of temptation

**[HEB 4:14–4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:14-Heb4:15)** *See also* [HEB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:18)

Sensitivity to others
---------------------

### Arises from an experience of God's compassion

**[2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)** *See also* [PHP 2:1](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1)

### Believers urged to show sensitivity

**[ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15)** *See also* [ISA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Isa40:2); [EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32); [COL 3:12–3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:12-Col3:13); [1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8); [HEB 13:3](https://ref.ly/Heb13:3)

### Sensitivity to foreigners in Israel

**[EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9)** parallel [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21) *See also* [LEV 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33-Lev19:34)

### Sensitivity to those in distress

**[JOB 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Job2:11-Job2:13)** *See also* [MAT 26:38–26:40](https://ref.ly/Matt26:38-Matt26:40) parallel [MRK 14:34–14:37](https://ref.ly/Mark14:34-Mark14:37); [2CO 7:5–7:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:5-2Cor7:7); [HEB 10:33–10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:33-Heb10:34); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)

### Sensitivity to those weak in faith

**[ROM 15:1](https://ref.ly/Rom15:1)** *See also* [ROM 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/Rom14:1-Rom14:3), [ROM 14:15](https://ref.ly/Rom14:15); [1CO 8:9–8:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:9-1Cor8:12); [1TH 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:7)

### Sensitivity in speech

[PRO 15:23](https://ref.ly/Prov15:23), [PRO 16:24](https://ref.ly/Prov16:24), [PRO 25:11](https://ref.ly/Prov25:11), [PRO 25:15](https://ref.ly/Prov25:15); ; [1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)

Insensitivity to others
-----------------------

### Examples of insensitivity to others

[DEU 28:53–28:57](https://ref.ly/Deut28:53-Deut28:57) *As a result of God's judgment, the most sensitive person will become insensitive even to the needs of family*; ; [1SA 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:13-1Sam1:14) *Eli's insensitivity to Hannah*; ; [JOB 16:2](https://ref.ly/Job16:2) *the insensitivity of Job's “comforters”*; ; [JON 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:9-Jonah4:11) *Jonah's insensitivity to the people of Nineveh*; ; [MAT 19:13](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13) parallels [MRK 10:13](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13), [LUK 18:15](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15) *the disciples' insensitivity towards children brought to Jesus Christ*; ; [1CO 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:20-1Cor11:21) *insensitivity to the needs of others*; ; [JAS 2:2–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2-Jas2:4) *insensitivity towards poorer visitors to church*

### The results of insensitivity

[PSA 69:20](https://ref.ly/Ps69:20); ; [PRO 25:17](https://ref.ly/Prov25:17), [PRO 25:20](https://ref.ly/Prov25:20)

Oversensitivity
---------------

### Oversensitivity in the face of rejection

[1SA 25:11–25:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:11-1Sam25:13) *David's hasty reaction towards Nabal*; ; [LUK 9:53–9:54](https://ref.ly/Luke9:53-Luke9:54) *James' and John's hasty reaction*

[ROM 14:2](https://ref.ly/Rom14:2); ; [1CO 8:6–8:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:6-1Cor8:8)

---------------------

### Spiritual awareness

**[1SA 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:8)** *See also* [MAT 24:32–24:34](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32-Matt24:34) parallels [MRK 13:28–13:29](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28-Mark13:29), [LUK 21:29–21:30](https://ref.ly/Luke21:29-Luke21:30); [ACT 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Acts16:9-Acts16:10)

### Lack of spiritual sensitivity
