## 5947: shame (id: 5947)

An uncomfortable feeling of guilt and humiliation, usually arising from sin or failure.

Shame is to be avoided
----------------------

**[PSA 25:2](https://ref.ly/Ps25:2)** *See also* [PSA 25:20](https://ref.ly/Ps25:20), [PSA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Ps31:1), [PSA 31:17](https://ref.ly/Ps31:17), [PSA 44:15](https://ref.ly/Ps44:15), [PSA 119:31](https://ref.ly/Ps119:31), [PSA 119:80](https://ref.ly/Ps119:80)

Causes of shame
---------------

### Military defeat

**[2KI 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:26)**

### Natural afflictions

**[LUK 1:25](https://ref.ly/Luke1:25)** *See also* [GEN 30:23](https://ref.ly/Gen30:23); [ISA 4:1](https://ref.ly/Isa4:1), [ISA 54:4](https://ref.ly/Isa54:4) *not only childlessness but widowhood*

### Humiliation by an enemy

**[PSA 69:19](https://ref.ly/Ps69:19)** *See also* [PSA 69:6–69:7](https://ref.ly/Ps69:6-Ps69:7); [1SA 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam11:2); [2SA 10:4–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:4-2Sam10:5); [ISA 3:17](https://ref.ly/Isa3:17); [MRK 12:4](https://ref.ly/Mark12:4)

### Nakedness

**[REV 3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:18)** *See also* [GEN 2:25](https://ref.ly/Gen2:25), [GEN 3:10](https://ref.ly/Gen3:10), [GEN 9:22–9:23](https://ref.ly/Gen9:22-Gen9:23); [ISA 47:3](https://ref.ly/Isa47:3); [EZK 16:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:37); [REV 16:15](https://ref.ly/Rev16:15)

### The sin of relations or associates

**[PRO 14:34](https://ref.ly/Prov14:34)**

Heathen gods as a source of shame
---------------------------------

**[2SA 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:10)** *“Bosheth” means “shame”. According to [1Ch 8:33](https://ref.ly/1Chr8:33) he was originally Ish\-Baal, but the name “Baal”, subsequently associated with a heathen god, was changed by later writers to “Bosheth”.* *See also* [JER 3:24](https://ref.ly/Jer3:24), [JER 11:13](https://ref.ly/Jer11:13); [HOS 9:10](https://ref.ly/Hos9:10)

Shame as a divine judgment
--------------------------

**[JER 23:40](https://ref.ly/Jer23:40)** *See also* [PSA 44:9](https://ref.ly/Ps44:9); [JER 46:24](https://ref.ly/Jer46:24)

### Shame brought upon God's enemies

[PSA 6:10](https://ref.ly/Ps6:10), [PSA 31:17](https://ref.ly/Ps31:17), [PSA 35:26](https://ref.ly/Ps35:26), [PSA 70:2](https://ref.ly/Ps70:2), [PSA 71:13](https://ref.ly/Ps71:13), [PSA 109:29](https://ref.ly/Ps109:29), [PSA 129:5](https://ref.ly/Ps129:5)

Shame as guilt for sin
----------------------

**[EZR 9:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:6)** *See also* [PRO 6:32](https://ref.ly/Prov6:32); [DAN 9:5–9:7](https://ref.ly/Dan9:5-Dan9:7); [ROM 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/Rom6:20-Rom6:21)

### Shame may lead to repentance

[1CO 6:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:5); ; [2CO 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:9-2Cor7:11); ; [2TH 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:14)

### Some people are without shame

[JER 3:3](https://ref.ly/Jer3:3), [JER 6:15](https://ref.ly/Jer6:15); ; [ZEP 3:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:5)

Jesus Christ bore shame
-----------------------

**[HEB 12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:2)** *See also* [DEU 21:23](https://ref.ly/Deut21:23); [MAT 27:39–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39-Matt27:44); [HEB 6:6](https://ref.ly/Heb6:6)

### The temptation of being ashamed of Jesus Christ and the cross

**[MRK 8:38](https://ref.ly/Mark8:38)** parallel [LUK 9:26](https://ref.ly/Luke9:26) *See also* [ROM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Rom1:16); [GAL 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gal6:14)

Those who trust in God will not be put to shame
-----------------------------------------------

**[PSA 25:3](https://ref.ly/Ps25:3)** *See also* [PSA 119:5–119:6](https://ref.ly/Ps119:5-Ps119:6); [ISA 28:16](https://ref.ly/Isa28:16); [ROM 9:33](https://ref.ly/Rom9:33), [ROM 10:11](https://ref.ly/Rom10:11); [1PE 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:6), [1PE 4:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 25:2; Psalms 25:20; Psalms 31:1; Psalms 31:17; Psalms 44:15; Psalms 119:31; Psalms 119:80; 2 Kings 19:26; Luke 1:25; Genesis 30:23; Isaiah 4:1; Isaiah 54:4; Psalms 69:19; Psalms 69:6-7; 1 Samuel 11:2; 2 Samuel 10:4-5; Isaiah 3:17; Mark 12:4; Revelation 3:18; Genesis 2:25; Genesis 3:10; Genesis 9:22-23; Isaiah 47:3; Ezekiel 16:37; Revelation 16:15; Proverbs 14:34; 2 Samuel 2:10; 1 Chronicles 8:33; Jeremiah 3:24; Jeremiah 11:13; Hosea 9:10; Jeremiah 23:40; Psalms 44:9; Jeremiah 46:24; Psalms 6:10; Psalms 35:26; Psalms 70:2; Psalms 71:13; Psalms 109:29; Psalms 129:5; Ezra 9:6; Proverbs 6:32; Daniel 9:5-7; Romans 6:20-21; 1 Corinthians 6:5; 2 Corinthians 7:9-11; 2 Thessalonians 3:14; Jeremiah 3:3; Jeremiah 6:15; Zephaniah 3:5; Hebrews 12:2; Deuteronomy 21:23; Matthew 27:39-44; Hebrews 6:6; Mark 8:38; Luke 9:26; Romans 1:16; Galatians 6:14; Psalms 25:3; Psalms 119:5-6; Isaiah 28:16; Romans 9:33; Romans 10:11; 1 Peter 2:6; 1 Peter 4:16
* **Associated Articles:** 5128: baldness (ID: `5128@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5169: nakedness (ID: `5169@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5801: brokenness (ID: `5801@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5836: disgrace (ID: `5836@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5843: embarrassment (ID: `5843@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5879: humiliation (ID: `5879@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6172: guilt (ID: `6172@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6227: regret (ID: `6227@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8451: mortification (ID: `8451@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5948: shrewdness (id: 5948)

Astuteness or craftiness in dealings with others, especially in using one's understanding and judgment to one's own advantage. Scripture commends it when it is seen in wise words and actions directed towards a worthy goal, but condemns it when it takes the form of cunning and deceitful scheming for sinful and selfish ends.

Shrewdness as astuteness
------------------------

### It is encouraged in believers

**[MAT 10:16](https://ref.ly/Matt10:16)**; **[LUK 16:8–16:9](https://ref.ly/Luke16:8-Luke16:9)** *Jesus Christ explains the parable of the dishonest manager ([Lk 16:1–7](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1-Luke16:7)) and urges his disciples to be shrewd in their use of worldly wealth.*

### Practical demonstrations of astuteness

**[MAT 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:25)** parallel [LUK 6:47–6:48](https://ref.ly/Luke6:47-Luke6:48) *See also* [PRO 12:23](https://ref.ly/Prov12:23), [PRO 14:15](https://ref.ly/Prov14:15), [PRO 15:5](https://ref.ly/Prov15:5), [PRO 18:15](https://ref.ly/Prov18:15); [MAT 24:45–24:47](https://ref.ly/Matt24:45-Matt24:47) parallel [LUK 12:42–12:44](https://ref.ly/Luke12:42-Luke12:44)

### Jesus Christ's wisdom in answering his enemies

[MAT 22:18–22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:18-Matt22:22) parallels [MRK 12:15–12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:15-Mark12:17), [LUK 20:23–20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:23-Luke20:26) *on the matter of paying taxes to Caesar*; ; [MAT 22:31–22:32](https://ref.ly/Matt22:31-Matt22:32) parallels [MRK 12:26–12:27](https://ref.ly/Mark12:26-Mark12:27), [LUK 20:37–20:38](https://ref.ly/Luke20:37-Luke20:38) *on the question of the resurrection*; ; [JHN 8:7](https://ref.ly/John8:7) *regarding the woman caught in adultery*

### Further examples of astuteness

[1SA 23:22–23:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:22-1Sam23:23) *David's shrewdness in fleeing from Saul*; ; [2SA 14:1–14:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:1-2Sam14:3) *Joab's ruse to persuade David to call Absalom back from exile*; ; [1KI 3:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:28) *Solomon's wise judgment*; ; [2CH 11:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:23) *Rehoboam acts wisely to secure his kingdom*; ; [DAN 1:11–1:13](https://ref.ly/Dan1:11-Dan1:13) *Daniel avoids eating royal food and wine*; ; [MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16) *Herod is outwitted by the Magi*; ; [MRK 7:27–7:29](https://ref.ly/Mark7:27-Mark7:29) parallel [MAT 15:26–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:26-Matt15:28) *the Syro\-Phoenician woman's wise response*

Shrewdness as crafty and deceitful scheming
-------------------------------------------

### It demonstrates human sinfulness

**[PSA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Ps64:6)** *See also* [JOB 15:5](https://ref.ly/Job15:5); [PSA 83:3](https://ref.ly/Ps83:3), [PSA 140:1–140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:1-Ps140:3); [PRO 7:10](https://ref.ly/Prov7:10); [ACT 13:10](https://ref.ly/Acts13:10); [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)

### It is condemned by God

**[2SA 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:26-2Sam22:27)** parallel [PSA 18:25–18:26](https://ref.ly/Ps18:25-Ps18:26) *God is shrewd in dealing with those who cleverly deceive others*; **[JOB 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/Job5:12-Job5:13)** *See also* [PSA 94:11](https://ref.ly/Ps94:11); [PRO 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/Prov6:12-Prov6:15), [PRO 12:2](https://ref.ly/Prov12:2); [ISA 5:21](https://ref.ly/Isa5:21), [ISA 44:24–44:25](https://ref.ly/Isa44:24-Isa44:25); [1CO 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:19); [ISA 29:14](https://ref.ly/Isa29:14); [1CO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:19-1Cor3:20)

### Others dislike it

[PRO 14:17](https://ref.ly/Prov14:17), [PRO 25:23](https://ref.ly/Prov25:23)

### It is a characteristic of Satan

**[2CO 11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3)** *See also* [GEN 3:1](https://ref.ly/Gen3:1); [2CO 2:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:11), [2CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13-2Cor11:15)

### The craftiness of Jesus Christ's enemies

[MAT 22:15–22:17](https://ref.ly/Matt22:15-Matt22:17) parallels [MRK 12:13–12:15](https://ref.ly/Mark12:13-Mark12:15), [LUK 20:20–20:22](https://ref.ly/Luke20:20-Luke20:22) *The Pharisees try to trap Jesus Christ over paying taxes*; ; [MAT 26:3–26:4](https://ref.ly/Matt26:3-Matt26:4) parallels [MRK 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Mark14:1-Mark14:2), [LUK 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Luke22:1-Luke22:2) *The religious leaders plot Jesus Christ's death.*

### Further examples of crafty scheming

[GEN 25:31–25:33](https://ref.ly/Gen25:31-Gen25:33) *Jacob buys Esau's birthright*; ; [GEN 27:15–27:16](https://ref.ly/Gen27:15-Gen27:16) *Isaac is tricked into blessing Jacob*; ; [EXO 1:10](https://ref.ly/Exod1:10) *Pharaoh plans to reduce the number of Israelites*; ; [JOS 9:3–9:6](https://ref.ly/Josh9:3-Josh9:6) *The Gibeonites deceive Joshua*; ; [2SA 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:3) *Jonadab plots for Amnon against Tamar*; ; [NEH 6:2–6:13](https://ref.ly/Neh6:2-Neh6:13) *schemes against Nehemiah*; ; [2CO 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:16) *Paul sarcastically echoes the slander against him that he tricked people into giving him money.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 10:16; Luke 16:8-9; Luke 16:1-7; Matthew 7:24-25; Luke 6:47-48; Proverbs 12:23; Proverbs 14:15; Proverbs 15:5; Proverbs 18:15; Matthew 24:45-47; Luke 12:42-44; Matthew 22:18-22; Mark 12:15-17; Luke 20:23-26; Matthew 22:31-32; Mark 12:26-27; Luke 20:37-38; John 8:7; 1 Samuel 23:22-23; 2 Samuel 14:1-3; 1 Kings 3:28; 2 Chronicles 11:23; Daniel 1:11-13; Matthew 2:16; Mark 7:27-29; Matthew 15:26-28; Psalms 64:6; Job 15:5; Psalms 83:3; Psalms 140:1-3; Proverbs 7:10; Acts 13:10; Ephesians 4:14; 2 Samuel 22:26-27; Psalms 18:25-26; Job 5:12-13; Psalms 94:11; Proverbs 6:12-15; Proverbs 12:2; Isaiah 5:21; Isaiah 44:24-25; 1 Corinthians 1:19; Isaiah 29:14; 1 Corinthians 3:19-20; Proverbs 14:17; Proverbs 25:23; 2 Corinthians 11:3; Genesis 3:1; 2 Corinthians 2:11; 2 Corinthians 11:13-15; Matthew 22:15-17; Mark 12:13-15; Luke 20:20-22; Matthew 26:3-4; Mark 14:1-2; Luke 22:1-2; Genesis 25:31-33; Genesis 27:15-16; Exodus 1:10; Joshua 9:3-6; 2 Samuel 13:3; Nehemiah 6:2-13; 2 Corinthians 12:16
* **Associated Articles:** 2081: Jesus Christ, wisdom of (ID: `2081@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5407: merchants (ID: `5407@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5922: prudence (ID: `5922@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6186: imagination, evil scheming (ID: `6186@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8365: wisdom, nature of human (ID: `8365@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5949: shyness (id: 5949)

Moses was shy of public speaking
--------------------------------

[EXO 4:10–4:15](https://ref.ly/Exod4:10-Exod4:15), [EXO 6:12](https://ref.ly/Exod6:12), [EXO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Exod6:30)

Shyness caused by youth and inexperience
----------------------------------------

### Jeremiah

[JER 1:6](https://ref.ly/Jer1:6)

### Timothy

[1CO 16:10–16:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:10-1Cor16:11); ; [1TI 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:12); ; [2TI 1:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:7)

The woman subject to bleeding was shy of making herself known
-------------------------------------------------------------

[MRK 5:25–5:28](https://ref.ly/Mark5:25-Mark5:28), [MRK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Mark5:33)

Jesus Christ taught his disciples not to be shy of bearing witness to him
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[MAT 5:14–5:16](https://ref.ly/Matt5:14-Matt5:16)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 4:10-15; Exodus 6:12; Exodus 6:30; Jeremiah 1:6; 1 Corinthians 16:10-11; 1 Timothy 4:12; 2 Timothy 1:7; Mark 5:25-28; Mark 5:33; Matthew 5:14-16
* **Associated Articles:** 5546: speech (ID: `5546@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5746: youth (ID: `5746@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5843: embarrassment (ID: `5843@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5887: inexperience (ID: `5887@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5968: timidity (ID: `5968@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8202: boldness (ID: `8202@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8219: courage (ID: `8219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8754: fear (ID: `8754@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5950: silence (id: 5950)

A stillness or absence of noise, often associated with the absence of life or people, but also associated with a sense of expectancy or reverence, especially in the presence of God. Scripture stresses that God is not silent, but has spoken his word to his creation. The silence of Jesus Christ before his accusers is seen as the fulfilment of OT prophecy.

The silence of God
------------------

### His apparent silence in the face of sin

**[PSA 50:21](https://ref.ly/Ps50:21)** *See also* [JOB 34:29](https://ref.ly/Job34:29); [ISA 42:14](https://ref.ly/Isa42:14), [ISA 57:11](https://ref.ly/Isa57:11)

### God will not remain silent in the face of sin

**[PSA 50:3](https://ref.ly/Ps50:3)** *See also* [ISA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Isa62:1), [ISA 65:6–65:7](https://ref.ly/Isa65:6-Isa65:7)

### Prayers that God will not be silent

**[PSA 28:1](https://ref.ly/Ps28:1)** *See also* [PSA 35:22](https://ref.ly/Ps35:22), [PSA 39:12](https://ref.ly/Ps39:12), [PSA 83:1](https://ref.ly/Ps83:1), [PSA 109:1](https://ref.ly/Ps109:1); [ISA 64:12](https://ref.ly/Isa64:12); [HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13)

God silences those who oppose him
---------------------------------

**[1SA 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:9)** *See also* [PSA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ps8:2), [PSA 63:11](https://ref.ly/Ps63:11), [PSA 107:42](https://ref.ly/Ps107:42); [ISA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:5), [ISA 47:5](https://ref.ly/Isa47:5); [JER 47:5](https://ref.ly/Jer47:5), [JER 48:2](https://ref.ly/Jer48:2), [JER 51:55](https://ref.ly/Jer51:55)MAT [22:34](https://ref.ly/Jer22:34); [MRK 3:4](https://ref.ly/Mark3:4); [LUK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:26)[ACT 4:14](https://ref.ly/Acts4:14); [ROM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Rom3:19)

The silence of Jesus Christ
---------------------------

### It was foretold in the OT

**[ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7)** *See also* [ACT 8:32](https://ref.ly/Acts8:32)

### Its fulfilment in the NT

[MAT 26:63](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63) parallel [MRK 14:61](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61) *before the Sanhedrin*; ; [MAT 27:14](https://ref.ly/Matt27:14) parallels [MRK 15:4–15:5](https://ref.ly/Mark15:4-Mark15:5), [JHN 19:9](https://ref.ly/John19:9) *before Pilate*; ; [LUK 23:9](https://ref.ly/Luke23:9) *before Herod*

Jesus Christ commands silence as a sign of his authority
--------------------------------------------------------

[MRK 1:25–1:26](https://ref.ly/Mark1:25-Mark1:26) parallel [LUK 4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:35) *Jesus Christ silenced evil spirits*; ; [MRK 4:39](https://ref.ly/Mark4:39) *Jesus Christ silenced the storm.*

There are times when it is appropriate for God's people to be silent
--------------------------------------------------------------------

### The silence of expecting God to act

**[JOS 6:10](https://ref.ly/Josh6:10)** *See also* [GEN 24:21](https://ref.ly/Gen24:21); [EXO 14:14–14:15](https://ref.ly/Exod14:14-Exod14:15); [2KI 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:3), [2KI 2:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:5); [REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rev8:1)

### The silence anticipating the wisdom of another person

[JOB 29:7–29:10](https://ref.ly/Job29:7-Job29:10), [JOB 29:21](https://ref.ly/Job29:21)

### The silence of prudence

**[PRO 10:19](https://ref.ly/Prov10:19)**; **[ECC 3:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:7)** *See also* [1SA 10:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:27); [JOB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Job13:5), [JOB 33:31–33:33](https://ref.ly/Job33:31-Job33:33); [PRO 11:12](https://ref.ly/Prov11:12), [PRO 17:28](https://ref.ly/Prov17:28)

### The silence of suffering

**[LAM 3:28](https://ref.ly/Lam3:28)** *See also* [PSA 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ps39:9)

### The silence of shame

[EZK 16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:63)

### The silence of anger

**[PSA 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ps4:4)** *The psalmist is warning against hasty action when angry.*

### The silence of amazement at God's actions

*See also* [LUK 9:36](https://ref.ly/Luke9:36); [ACT 15:12](https://ref.ly/Acts15:12)

### A tongues\-speaker should be silent if no interpreter is present

[1CO 14:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:28) *silence in the church*; ; [1CO 14:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:30) *A prophet should be silent if another receives a revelation.*

### Christian women are sometimes exorted to be silent

**[1TI 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:11-1Tim2:12)** *See also* [1CO 14:34–14:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:34-1Cor14:35); [1PE 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:4)

There are times when it is inappropriate for God's people to be silent
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### When there is good news to tell

[2KI 7:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:9); ; [PSA 40:9](https://ref.ly/Ps40:9)

### When much depends upon a person's words

**[EST 4:14](https://ref.ly/Esth4:14)** *See also* [JER 4:19](https://ref.ly/Jer4:19), [JER 20:9](https://ref.ly/Jer20:9); [ACT 18:9–18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:9-Acts18:10)

### When God should be praised

**[PSA 30:11–30:12](https://ref.ly/Ps30:11-Ps30:12)** *See also* [LUK 19:37–19:40](https://ref.ly/Luke19:37-Luke19:40)

### When sin is unconfessed

**[PSA 32:3](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3)**

### When God's people feel they have a genuine complaint

**[PSA 39:1–39:2](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1-Ps39:2)** *See also* [JOB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Job7:11), [JOB 23:16–23:17](https://ref.ly/Job23:16-Job23:17)

### When prayer is urgently required

[ISA 62:6–62:7](https://ref.ly/Isa62:6-Isa62:7)

God's people are sometimes brought to silence
---------------------------------------------

[JOB 31:34](https://ref.ly/Job31:34) *Job fearing the crowd*; ; [EZK 3:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:26) *Ezekiel as a judgment on Israel*; ; [MRK 9:34](https://ref.ly/Mark9:34) *the disciples when their pride was exposed*; ; [LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20) *Zechariah because of his unbelief*

Silencing the wicked
--------------------

### Believers pray that the wicked will be silenced

**[PSA 31:17–31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:17-Ps31:18)**; **[1PE 2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:23)** *See also* [PSA 143:12](https://ref.ly/Ps143:12)

### Believers so live that the wicked may be silenced

[TIT 2:8](https://ref.ly/Titus2:8); ; [1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)

False teachers must be silenced
-------------------------------

**[TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11)** *“the circumcision group” believed that in order to be saved it was necessary to be circumcised and keep the Jewish ceremonial law.*

Death is compared to silence
----------------------------

*See also* [PSA 94:17](https://ref.ly/Ps94:17), [PSA 115:17](https://ref.ly/Ps115:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 50:21; Job 34:29; Isaiah 42:14; Isaiah 57:11; Psalms 50:3; Isaiah 62:1; Isaiah 65:6-7; Psalms 28:1; Psalms 35:22; Psalms 39:12; Psalms 83:1; Psalms 109:1; Isaiah 64:12; Habakkuk 1:13; 1 Samuel 2:9; Psalms 8:2; Psalms 63:11; Psalms 107:42; Isaiah 25:5; Isaiah 47:5; Jeremiah 47:5; Jeremiah 48:2; Jeremiah 51:55; Jeremiah 22:34; Mark 3:4; Luke 20:26; Acts 4:14; Romans 3:19; Isaiah 53:7; Acts 8:32; Matthew 26:63; Mark 14:61; Matthew 27:14; Mark 15:4-5; John 19:9; Luke 23:9; Mark 1:25-26; Luke 4:35; Mark 4:39; Joshua 6:10; Genesis 24:21; Exodus 14:14-15; 2 Kings 2:3; 2 Kings 2:5; Revelation 8:1; Job 29:7-10; Job 29:21; Proverbs 10:19; Ecclesiastes 3:7; 1 Samuel 10:27; Job 13:5; Job 33:31-33; Proverbs 11:12; Proverbs 17:28; Lamentations 3:28; Psalms 39:9; Ezekiel 16:63; Psalms 4:4; Luke 9:36; Acts 15:12; 1 Corinthians 14:28; 1 Corinthians 14:30; 1 Timothy 2:11-12; 1 Corinthians 14:34-35; 1 Peter 3:1-4; 2 Kings 7:9; Psalms 40:9; Esther 4:14; Jeremiah 4:19; Jeremiah 20:9; Acts 18:9-10; Psalms 30:11-12; Luke 19:37-40; Psalms 32:3; Psalms 39:1-2; Job 7:11; Job 23:16-17; Isaiah 62:6-7; Job 31:34; Ezekiel 3:26; Mark 9:34; Luke 1:20; Psalms 31:17-18; 1 Peter 2:23; Psalms 143:12; Titus 2:8; 1 Peter 2:15; Titus 1:10-11; Psalms 94:17; Psalms 115:17

## 5951: slander (id: 5951)

False and malicious talk about others. It not only harms those against whom it is directed, but also brings ruin to those who propagate it.

Slander condemned
-----------------

### Slander is sinful

**[MRK 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:23)** parallel [MAT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19-Matt15:20) *See also* [JER 9:4–9:6](https://ref.ly/Jer9:4-Jer9:6); [EZK 22:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:9); [ROM 1:29–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:29-Rom1:31); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20); [2TI 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2-2Tim3:4)

### Slander is forbidden

**[LEV 19:16](https://ref.ly/Lev19:16)** *See also* [EXO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1); [PRO 3:30](https://ref.ly/Prov3:30), [PRO 10:18](https://ref.ly/Prov10:18), [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22); [EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31); [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8); [TIT 2:3](https://ref.ly/Titus2:3), [TIT 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1-Titus3:2); [JAS 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jas4:11); [1PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1)

### Slander is destructive

**[PRO 16:27](https://ref.ly/Prov16:27)** *See also* [PRO 11:9](https://ref.ly/Prov11:9), [PRO 26:28](https://ref.ly/Prov26:28), [PRO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Prov30:10)

Dealing with slander
--------------------

### Committing one's cause to God

**[PSA 120:1–120:2](https://ref.ly/Ps120:1-Ps120:2)** *See also* [PSA 12:1–12:7](https://ref.ly/Ps12:1-Ps12:7), [PSA 31:14–31:20](https://ref.ly/Ps31:14-Ps31:20), [PSA 64:1–64:4](https://ref.ly/Ps64:1-Ps64:4), [PSA 69:6–69:21](https://ref.ly/Ps69:6-Ps69:21), [PSA 109:1–109:5](https://ref.ly/Ps109:1-Ps109:5)

### Maintaining one's integrity

**[1PE 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:12)** *See also* [PSA 119:23](https://ref.ly/Ps119:23); [1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15), [1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9), [1PE 3:15–3:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15-1Pet3:17)

### Rejoicing in the Lord

**[MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12)** *See also* [ACT 5:41](https://ref.ly/Acts5:41)

### Not listening to slander

**[1SA 24:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:9)** *See also* [PRO 17:4](https://ref.ly/Prov17:4)

Judgment upon slanderers
------------------------

-------------------

## 5952: sorrow (id: 5952)

The human reponse to painful and distressing life situations, especially those involving loss.

Sorrow caused by the trials of life
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 90:10](https://ref.ly/Ps90:10)** *See also* [PSA 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Ps13:1-Ps13:2), [PSA 31:9](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9); [ECC 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:18), [ECC 2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:23); [JER 20:18](https://ref.ly/Jer20:18)

Sorrow caused by fulfilling God's will
--------------------------------------

**[1PE 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:6)** *See also* [MAT 26:38](https://ref.ly/Matt26:38); [PHP 2:27](https://ref.ly/Phil2:27)

Sorrow caused by illness
------------------------

**[ISA 38:2–38:3](https://ref.ly/Isa38:2-Isa38:3)** *See also* [PSA 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Ps6:1-Ps6:7)

Sorrow caused by childlessness
------------------------------

**[1SA 1:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:16)**

Sorrow caused by parting
------------------------

**[ACT 20:37](https://ref.ly/Acts20:37)** *See also* [MAT 17:23](https://ref.ly/Matt17:23) *the disciples' reaction to Jesus Christ predicting his death*; [LUK 22:45](https://ref.ly/Luke22:45); [JHN 16:6](https://ref.ly/John16:6)

Sorrow caused by bereavement
----------------------------

**[GEN 23:2](https://ref.ly/Gen23:2)**; **[JHN 20:11](https://ref.ly/John20:11)** *See also* [GEN 50:1](https://ref.ly/Gen50:1); [DEU 34:8](https://ref.ly/Deut34:8); [2SA 19:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:2); [LUK 7:12–7:14](https://ref.ly/Luke7:12-Luke7:14), [LUK 8:52](https://ref.ly/Luke8:52); [JHN 11:33–11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:33-John11:35); [ACT 8:2](https://ref.ly/Acts8:2)

Sorrow caused by national calamities
------------------------------------

**[PSA 137:1](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1)** *See also* [ISA 15:3](https://ref.ly/Isa15:3), [ISA 22:4](https://ref.ly/Isa22:4); [JER 22:10](https://ref.ly/Jer22:10), [JER 48:17](https://ref.ly/Jer48:17); [EZK 21:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:6), [EZK 27:30](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:30)

God and sorrow
--------------

### God is close to those who grieve

**[PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18)** *See also* [PSA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ps10:14); [ISA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Isa53:4); [JHN 11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:35)

### God comforts the sorrowing

**[JER 31:13](https://ref.ly/Jer31:13)** *See also* [ISA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Isa35:10); [LAM 3:32–3:33](https://ref.ly/Lam3:32-Lam3:33); [JHN 16:20](https://ref.ly/John16:20); [1TH 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:13-1Thess4:14); [REV 21:4](https://ref.ly/Rev21:4)

Sorrow in response to human sin
-------------------------------

### The result of sinning

**[PSA 16:4](https://ref.ly/Ps16:4)** *See also* [1SA 2:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:33)

### Grief over the failure of others

**[2CO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:4-2Cor2:5)** *See also* [GEN 34:7](https://ref.ly/Gen34:7); [1SA 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:34); [EZR 10:6](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:6); [EZK 9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek9:4); [ROM 9:2](https://ref.ly/Rom9:2); [1CO 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:2)

### Grief over one's own shortcomings

**[MAT 5:4](https://ref.ly/Matt5:4)** *See also* [MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75); [2CO 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:9-2Cor7:11); [JAS 4:9](https://ref.ly/Jas4:9)

Sorrow in response to God's judgment on human sin
-------------------------------------------------

**[LAM 1:5](https://ref.ly/Lam1:5)** *See also* [NEH 1:4](https://ref.ly/Neh1:4); [JER 8:21](https://ref.ly/Jer8:21), [JER 10:19](https://ref.ly/Jer10:19), [JER 13:17](https://ref.ly/Jer13:17), [JER 14:2](https://ref.ly/Jer14:2); [EZK 21:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:6), [EZK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:12); [AMO 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/Amos5:16-Amos5:17), [AMO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Amos8:10); [MIC 1:8](https://ref.ly/Mic1:8); [ZEP 1:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:15); [ZEC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Zech12:10); [MAT 8:12](https://ref.ly/Matt8:12), [MAT 24:30](https://ref.ly/Matt24:30); [REV 1:7](https://ref.ly/Rev1:7)

----------------------------------------------

## 5953: stability (id: 5953)

The state of being in a firm or unwavering situation, whether emotional, political or personal. Stability is an important element of a settled life before God.

Stability in the life of faith
------------------------------

### Stability for the believer is based on God's unchanging nature

**[DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4)**; **[PSA 103:19](https://ref.ly/Ps103:19)** *See also* [1SA 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:2); [2SA 22:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:32); [JOB 25:2](https://ref.ly/Job25:2); [PSA 62:2](https://ref.ly/Ps62:2), [PSA 89:2](https://ref.ly/Ps89:2), [PSA 93:2](https://ref.ly/Ps93:2); [ISA 26:4](https://ref.ly/Isa26:4); [MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27)

### Stability gives resistance to spiritual assault

**[1CO 16:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:13)**; **[COL 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Col1:22-Col1:23)** *See also* [PSA 20:7–20:8](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7-Ps20:8), [PSA 40:2](https://ref.ly/Ps40:2); [PRO 10:25](https://ref.ly/Prov10:25); [MAT 10:22](https://ref.ly/Matt10:22); [ROM 16:25](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25); [1CO 15:58](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:58); [2CO 1:24](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:24); [GAL 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gal5:1); [PHP 1:27](https://ref.ly/Phil1:27), [PHP 1:27](https://ref.ly/Phil1:27); [COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12); [1TH 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:8); [2TH 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:15); [HEB 6:19](https://ref.ly/Heb6:19), [HEB 10:23](https://ref.ly/Heb10:23); [JAS 1:23–1:25](https://ref.ly/Jas1:23-Jas1:25), [JAS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Jas5:8); [2PE 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:12)

### Stability depends upon firmness of faith

**[COL 2:5–2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:5-Col2:7)** *See also* [PSA 57:7](https://ref.ly/Ps57:7), [PSA 119:5](https://ref.ly/Ps119:5); [ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3); [EPH 3:17](https://ref.ly/Eph3:17); [HEB 3:14](https://ref.ly/Heb3:14); [1PE 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:9-1Pet5:10)

### Stability is an important aim of Christian ministry

**[EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)** *See also* [COL 1:28](https://ref.ly/Col1:28)

Political stability
-------------------

**[2SA 7:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:16)**; **[PRO 29:4](https://ref.ly/Prov29:4)** *See also* [1SA 13:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:13); [2SA 5:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:12); [1KI 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:12); [PRO 16:12](https://ref.ly/Prov16:12); [ISA 16:5](https://ref.ly/Isa16:5)

## 5954: strength (id: 5954)

The quality of power and might which characterises God and his relationship to his creation. Human strength can lead to rebellion against God on account of a belief in human self\-sufficiency.

* **Associated Articles:** 5955: strength, divine (ID: `5955@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5956: strength, human (ID: `5956@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5957: strength, spiritual (ID: `5957@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5955: strength, divine (id: 5955)

The strength of God is made known in creation and redemption and in his empowering of believers to live faithfully.

The revelation of the strength of God
-------------------------------------

### In creation

**[ROM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20)** *See also* [JOB 38:4](https://ref.ly/Job38:4), [JOB 38:8](https://ref.ly/Job38:8), [JOB 38:12](https://ref.ly/Job38:12), [JOB 38:31–38:33](https://ref.ly/Job38:31-Job38:33); [PSA 68:34](https://ref.ly/Ps68:34), [PSA 104:1–104:3](https://ref.ly/Ps104:1-Ps104:3), [PSA 104:32](https://ref.ly/Ps104:32); [ISA 40:12](https://ref.ly/Isa40:12), [ISA 40:25–40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:25-Isa40:26); [JER 32:17](https://ref.ly/Jer32:17); [HEB 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:2-Heb1:3)

### In providence

**[PSA 135:6](https://ref.ly/Ps135:6)** *See also* [PSA 46:8–46:10](https://ref.ly/Ps46:8-Ps46:10), [PSA 115:3](https://ref.ly/Ps115:3); [ISA 14:24](https://ref.ly/Isa14:24), [ISA 40:23](https://ref.ly/Isa40:23); [JER 27:5](https://ref.ly/Jer27:5); [DAN 4:35](https://ref.ly/Dan4:35); [ACT 17:26](https://ref.ly/Acts17:26); [EPH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Eph1:11)

### In redemption

**[EXO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Exod6:6)**; **[EPH 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Eph1:19-Eph1:20)** [EXO 3:19–3:20](https://ref.ly/Exod3:19-Exod3:20); ; [DEU 4:34](https://ref.ly/Deut4:34), [DEU 4:37](https://ref.ly/Deut4:37); ; [PSA 77:13–77:15](https://ref.ly/Ps77:13-Ps77:15); ; [ISA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Isa51:10); ; [JER 32:21](https://ref.ly/Jer32:21); [DEU 3:24](https://ref.ly/Deut3:24); ; [2SA 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:2-2Sam22:3) parallel [PSA 18:1–18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:1-Ps18:2) *David's song of praise for deliverance*; ; [PSA 98:1](https://ref.ly/Ps98:1); ; [ISA 59:1](https://ref.ly/Isa59:1), [ISA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Isa59:16)

### In human weakness

**[2CO 12:9–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9-2Cor12:10), [2CO 13:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:4)** *See also* [JDG 7:4–7:7](https://ref.ly/Judg7:4-Judg7:7); [1CO 1:25–1:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:25-1Cor1:27); [HEB 11:34](https://ref.ly/Heb11:34)

### In judgment

**[REV 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/Rev6:15-Rev6:17)** *See also* [PSA 89:10](https://ref.ly/Ps89:10); [ISA 63:5–63:6](https://ref.ly/Isa63:5-Isa63:6); [EZK 20:33–20:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:33-Ezek20:35); [HAB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Hab3:12); [2TH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:8-2Thess1:9); [2PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:7); [REV 6:12–6:14](https://ref.ly/Rev6:12-Rev6:14), [REV 18:8](https://ref.ly/Rev18:8)

God's strength brings comfort to believers
------------------------------------------

**[PSA 27:1](https://ref.ly/Ps27:1)** *See also* [PSA 27:3](https://ref.ly/Ps27:3); [JHN 10:28–10:29](https://ref.ly/John10:28-John10:29); [ROM 8:28](https://ref.ly/Rom8:28), [ROM 8:31](https://ref.ly/Rom8:31)

## 5956: strength, human (id: 5956)

All human strength derives from God. Among non\-believers, strength may become a source of arrogant self\-confidence, the oppression of others or rebellion against God.

The strength of the ungodly
---------------------------

### Physical prowess

**[1SA 17:4–17:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:4-1Sam17:10)** *See also* [NUM 13:31–13:33](https://ref.ly/Num13:31-Num13:33)

### Intelligence

**[1CO 1:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:22)** *The Gentile world relies on fallen reason rather than revelation.* *See also* [ACT 17:19–17:21](https://ref.ly/Acts17:19-Acts17:21), [ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32); [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)

### Political and military might

**[PSA 20:7](https://ref.ly/Ps20:7)** *See also* [GEN 10:8–10:12](https://ref.ly/Gen10:8-Gen10:12); [PSA 33:16](https://ref.ly/Ps33:16); [ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1), [ISA 36:18–36:20](https://ref.ly/Isa36:18-Isa36:20)

### Prosperity

**[PSA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:6)** *See also* [JER 49:4](https://ref.ly/Jer49:4); [LUK 12:18–12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:18-Luke12:19); [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)

The ultimate futility of human strength
---------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:25)** *See also* [2CH 32:7–32:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:7-2Chr32:8); [PSA 49:17](https://ref.ly/Ps49:17), [PSA 146:3](https://ref.ly/Ps146:3); [ECC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:15); [ISA 30:1–30:3](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:3); [LUK 12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20); [1CO 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:19-1Cor1:20), [1CO 2:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:6); [1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7); [JAS 1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:11)

Negative consequences of human strength
---------------------------------------

### Arrogant self\-confidence

**[GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4)** *See also* [1SA 17:41–17:44](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:41-1Sam17:44); [PSA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Ps52:1) *Boasting betrays the source of people's confidence*; [ISA 36:8–36:9](https://ref.ly/Isa36:8-Isa36:9); [DAN 4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30); [LUK 18:9–18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:12); [GAL 6:13](https://ref.ly/Gal6:13); [PHP 3:3](https://ref.ly/Phil3:3)

### Violence and oppression

**[PSA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:7)** *See also* [GEN 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/Gen4:23-Gen4:24); [EXO 1:11–1:14](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11-Exod1:14), [EXO 5:10–5:14](https://ref.ly/Exod5:10-Exod5:14); [JDG 6:2](https://ref.ly/Judg6:2), [JDG 6:6](https://ref.ly/Judg6:6); [PSA 37:14](https://ref.ly/Ps37:14), [PSA 73:3–73:8](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:8); [ISA 5:8](https://ref.ly/Isa5:8); [JAS 5:4–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4-Jas5:6)

The physical strength of some godly men
---------------------------------------

[2SA 23:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:20) parallel [1CH 11:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:22) *Benaiah*; ; [1CH 11:20–11:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:20-1Chr11:21) *Abishai*; ; [HEB 11:32–11:34](https://ref.ly/Heb11:32-Heb11:34)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 17:4-10; Numbers 13:31-33; 1 Corinthians 1:22; Acts 17:19-21; Acts 17:32; Colossians 2:8; Psalms 20:7; Genesis 10:8-12; Psalms 33:16; Isaiah 31:1; Isaiah 36:18-20; Psalms 49:6; Jeremiah 49:4; Luke 12:18-19; 1 Timothy 6:17; 1 Corinthians 1:25; 2 Chronicles 32:7-8; Psalms 49:17; Psalms 146:3; Ecclesiastes 5:15; Isaiah 30:1-3; Luke 12:20; 1 Corinthians 1:19-20; 1 Corinthians 2:6; 1 Timothy 6:7; James 1:11; Genesis 11:4; 1 Samuel 17:41-44; Psalms 52:1; Isaiah 36:8-9; Daniel 4:30; Luke 18:9-12; Galatians 6:13; Philippians 3:3; Psalms 52:7; Genesis 4:23-24; Exodus 1:11-14; Exodus 5:10-14; Judges 6:2; Judges 6:6; Psalms 37:14; Psalms 73:3-8; Isaiah 5:8; James 5:4-6; 2 Samuel 23:20; 1 Chronicles 11:22; 1 Chronicles 11:20-21; Hebrews 11:32-34
* **Associated Articles:** 5441: philosophy (ID: `5441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5457: power, human (ID: `5457@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6221: rebellion (ID: `6221@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8030: trust (ID: `8030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8203: character (ID: `8203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8219: courage (ID: `8219@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8790: oppression (ID: `8790@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8820: self-confidence (ID: `8820@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5957: strength, spiritual (id: 5957)

In contrast to human strength, God is the source and supplier of all spiritual strength through which believers can do all that God asks.

God is the source of spiritual strength
---------------------------------------

### God gives strength

**[EPH 6:10](https://ref.ly/Eph6:10)** *See also* [JDG 16:3](https://ref.ly/Judg16:3), [JDG 16:6](https://ref.ly/Judg16:6), [JDG 16:18–16:20](https://ref.ly/Judg16:18-Judg16:20); [PSA 68:35](https://ref.ly/Ps68:35); [ISA 40:29–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:29-Isa40:31); [LUK 24:49](https://ref.ly/Luke24:49); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8); [EPH 3:16](https://ref.ly/Eph3:16), [EPH 3:20](https://ref.ly/Eph3:20); [2TH 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:16-2Thess2:17); [1TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:12); [2TI 1:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:7)

### Strength comes from the promise of his presence

**[JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9)** *See also* [DEU 31:7–31:8](https://ref.ly/Deut31:7-Deut31:8); [PSA 119:28](https://ref.ly/Ps119:28); [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10); [JER 1:8](https://ref.ly/Jer1:8); [HAG 2:4](https://ref.ly/Hag2:4); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

### Strength comes from the realisation of his grace

**[2TI 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:1)** *See also* [ACT 20:32](https://ref.ly/Acts20:32); [1CO 15:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:10); [HEB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Heb13:9)

### Strength comes through the Holy Spirit

**[ZEC 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Zech4:6-Zech4:7)** *See also* [JDG 14:6](https://ref.ly/Judg14:6); [LUK 4:14](https://ref.ly/Luke4:14); [EPH 3:7](https://ref.ly/Eph3:7), [EPH 3:16](https://ref.ly/Eph3:16)

### Overcoming strength is often veiled in weakness

**[2CO 12:7–12:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7-2Cor12:10)** *See also* [PSA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Ps8:2); [ACT 14:19–14:20](https://ref.ly/Acts14:19-Acts14:20); [2CO 4:8–4:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:8-2Cor4:12); [COL 2:15](https://ref.ly/Col2:15); [HEB 11:32–11:38](https://ref.ly/Heb11:32-Heb11:38)

Aspects of spiritual strength
-----------------------------

### Profound dependence upon God

**[1SA 17:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45)** *See also* [PSA 27:1](https://ref.ly/Ps27:1), [PSA 44:4–44:8](https://ref.ly/Ps44:4-Ps44:8), [PSA 118:6](https://ref.ly/Ps118:6); [ISA 40:29–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:29-Isa40:31); [DAN 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:16-Dan3:18); [MAT 26:42](https://ref.ly/Matt26:42); [1CO 1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:31); [GAL 6:14](https://ref.ly/Gal6:14); [HEB 13:6](https://ref.ly/Heb13:6)

### Humility and gentleness

**[MAT 11:29](https://ref.ly/Matt11:29)** *See also* [NUM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Num12:3); [1TH 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:6-1Thess2:8), [1TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:11-1Thess2:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 6:10; Judges 16:3; Judges 16:6; Judges 16:18-20; Psalms 68:35; Isaiah 40:29-31; Luke 24:49; Acts 1:8; Ephesians 3:16; Ephesians 3:20; 2 Thessalonians 2:16-17; 1 Timothy 1:12; 2 Timothy 1:7; Joshua 1:9; Deuteronomy 31:7-8; Psalms 119:28; Isaiah 41:10; Jeremiah 1:8; Haggai 2:4; 2 Timothy 4:17; 2 Timothy 2:1; Acts 20:32; 1 Corinthians 15:10; Hebrews 13:9; Zechariah 4:6-7; Judges 14:6; Luke 4:14; Ephesians 3:7; 2 Corinthians 12:7-10; Psalms 8:2; Acts 14:19-20; 2 Corinthians 4:8-12; Colossians 2:15; Hebrews 11:32-38; 1 Samuel 17:45; Psalms 27:1; Psalms 44:4-8; Psalms 118:6; Daniel 3:16-18; Matthew 26:42; 1 Corinthians 1:31; Galatians 6:14; Hebrews 13:6; Matthew 11:29; Numbers 12:3; 1 Thessalonians 2:6-8; 1 Thessalonians 2:11-12
* **Associated Articles:** 2036: Jesus Christ, humility of (ID: `2036@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2066: Jesus Christ, power of (ID: `2066@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2410: cross, the (ID: `2410@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3030: Holy Spirit, power of (ID: `3030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3035: Holy Spirit, presence of (ID: `3035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6666: grace (ID: `6666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7921: fellowship (ID: `7921@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8413: edification (ID: `8413@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8482: spiritual warfare (ID: `8482@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5959: submission (id: 5959)

A humble attitude where obedience is rendered within a relationship; whether it be to God, authorities or other people at work, in the church, in marriage or in the family.

God requires submission
-----------------------

### From all people

**[PSA 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Ps2:9-Ps2:11)** *See also* [JOB 22:21](https://ref.ly/Job22:21); [1PE 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:6)

### From Christians especially

**[MAT 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Matt6:9-Matt6:10)** *See also* [HEB 12:9](https://ref.ly/Heb12:9); [JAS 4:7](https://ref.ly/Jas4:7)

The submission of Jesus Christ to his Father
--------------------------------------------

**[LUK 22:42](https://ref.ly/Luke22:42)** parallels [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39), [MRK 14:36](https://ref.ly/Mark14:36) *See also* [JHN 5:19](https://ref.ly/John5:19), [JHN 12:49–12:50](https://ref.ly/John12:49-John12:50); [1CO 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:27-1Cor15:28); [HEB 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7-Heb5:8), [HEB 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Heb10:5-Heb10:7)

The submissive spirit required in the church
--------------------------------------------

### In believers' relationships with one another

**[EPH 5:21](https://ref.ly/Eph5:21)** *See also* [PHP 2:5–2:7](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:7)

### To Jesus Christ

**[EPH 5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:24)** *See also* [EPH 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Eph1:22-Eph1:23); [COL 1:18](https://ref.ly/Col1:18) *As the body is subject to the head, so the church is subject to Christ.*

### To the Scriptures

**[JOS 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:7-Josh1:8)** *See also* [PSA 119:133](https://ref.ly/Ps119:133); [COL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Col3:15); [JAS 1:22–1:25](https://ref.ly/Jas1:22-Jas1:25); [REV 22:18–22:19](https://ref.ly/Rev22:18-Rev22:19)

### To church leaders

**[HEB 13:17](https://ref.ly/Heb13:17)** *See also* [ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1); [1CO 16:15–16:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:15-1Cor16:16); [1TH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:12-1Thess5:13)

### In prophecy

**[1CO 14:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:32)** *The implication is that individual prophecies must be submitted to the overall judgment of the church.*

The submission of all to civil government
-----------------------------------------

**[ROM 13:1](https://ref.ly/Rom13:1)** *See also* [MAT 22:21](https://ref.ly/Matt22:21) parallels [MRK 12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:17), [LUK 20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:25); [ROM 13:2–13:7](https://ref.ly/Rom13:2-Rom13:7); [TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1); [1PE 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:13-1Pet2:14)

Submission within other relationships
-------------------------------------

### Of wives to husbands

**[EPH 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Eph5:22-Eph5:24)** *See also* [COL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Col3:18); [TIT 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:4-Titus2:5); [1PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1), [1PE 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:5-1Pet3:6)

### Of children to parents

**[EPH 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Eph6:1-Eph6:3)** *See also* [EXO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12); [GAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Gal4:2); [COL 3:20](https://ref.ly/Col3:20)

### Of slaves to masters

**[EPH 6:5–6:8](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5-Eph6:8)** *See also* [COL 3:22–3:24](https://ref.ly/Col3:22-Col3:24); [TIT 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Titus2:9-Titus2:10); [1PE 2:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18)

### Of young to old

**[1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)** *See also* [LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32)

### Reciprocal attitudes are commended to safeguard against abuse

[EPH 5:25–5:33](https://ref.ly/Eph5:25-Eph5:33), [EPH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Eph6:4), [EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9); ; [1TI 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1-1Tim2:2); ; [1PE 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:1-1Pet5:4) *Those in authority have a responsibility to set an example.*

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 2:9-11; Job 22:21; 1 Peter 5:6; Matthew 6:9-10; Hebrews 12:9; James 4:7; Luke 22:42; Matthew 26:39; Mark 14:36; John 5:19; John 12:49-50; 1 Corinthians 15:27-28; Hebrews 5:7-8; Hebrews 10:5-7; Ephesians 5:21; Philippians 2:5-7; Ephesians 5:24; Ephesians 1:22-23; Colossians 1:18; Joshua 1:7-8; Psalms 119:133; Colossians 3:15; James 1:22-25; Revelation 22:18-19; Hebrews 13:17; Romans 13:1; 1 Corinthians 16:15-16; 1 Thessalonians 5:12-13; 1 Corinthians 14:32; Matthew 22:21; Mark 12:17; Luke 20:25; Romans 13:2-7; Titus 3:1; 1 Peter 2:13-14; Ephesians 5:22-24; Colossians 3:18; Titus 2:4-5; 1 Peter 3:1; 1 Peter 3:5-6; Ephesians 6:1-3; Exodus 20:12; Galatians 4:2; Colossians 3:20; Ephesians 6:5-8; Colossians 3:22-24; Titus 2:9-10; 1 Peter 2:18; 1 Peter 5:5; Leviticus 19:32; Ephesians 5:25-33; Ephesians 6:4; Ephesians 6:9; 1 Timothy 2:1-2; 1 Peter 5:1-4
* **Associated Articles:** 2057: Jesus Christ, obedience of (ID: `2057@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5218: authority, in the home (ID: `5218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5564: suffering, of Jesus Christ (ID: `5564@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5680: family (ID: `5680@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5709: marriage, purpose of (ID: `5709@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5734: relationships (ID: `5734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7446: slavery (ID: `7446@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8276: humility (ID: `8276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8342: servanthood (ID: `8342@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8351: teachableness (ID: `8351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8453: obedience (ID: `8453@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5960: success (id: 5960)

The achieving of objectives or personal fulfilment. For believers, success depends on faithfulness to God. Success can lead to dependence on one's own strength, instead of upon God.

Causes of success
-----------------

### The favour of God

[GEN 39:23](https://ref.ly/Gen39:23); ; [JOS 1:7](https://ref.ly/Josh1:7); ; [2CH 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:20), [2CH 26:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:5)

### Human skill

[ECC 10:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:10)

### Many advisers

[PRO 15:22](https://ref.ly/Prov15:22)

Nothing can succeed that is against God
---------------------------------------

**[PRO 21:30](https://ref.ly/Prov21:30)** *See also* [ACT 5:34–5:39](https://ref.ly/Acts5:34-Acts5:39)

Examples of prayers for success
-------------------------------

[GEN 24:12–24:15](https://ref.ly/Gen24:12-Gen24:15) *success in personal relationships*; [1CH 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:10); ; [2CH 14:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:11); [NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11) *success in personal achievement*; ; [ACT 4:18–4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18-Acts4:31) *success in ministry*

The dangers of success
----------------------

### Pride

**[2CH 25:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:19)** *See also* [1SA 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:3); [PRO 11:2](https://ref.ly/Prov11:2), [PRO 16:5](https://ref.ly/Prov16:5); [MIC 2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:3)

### Boasting

**[EPH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8)** *See also* [JAS 4:13–4:17](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:17); [PSA 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:3-Ps10:4); [ISA 10:15–10:16](https://ref.ly/Isa10:15-Isa10:16)

### Forgetting God

**[DEU 32:18](https://ref.ly/Deut32:18)** *See also* [DEU 8:11–8:17](https://ref.ly/Deut8:11-Deut8:17); [JDG 3:7](https://ref.ly/Judg3:7), [JDG 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33-Judg8:34)

### Lack of thankfulness

[ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21); ; [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 39:23; Joshua 1:7; 2 Chronicles 20:20; 2 Chronicles 26:5; Ecclesiastes 10:10; Proverbs 15:22; Proverbs 21:30; Acts 5:34-39; Genesis 24:12-15; 1 Chronicles 4:10; 2 Chronicles 14:11; Nehemiah 1:11; Acts 4:18-31; 2 Chronicles 25:19; 1 Samuel 2:3; Proverbs 11:2; Proverbs 16:5; Micah 2:3; Ephesians 2:8; James 4:13-17; Psalms 10:3-4; Isaiah 10:15-16; Deuteronomy 32:18; Deuteronomy 8:11-17; Judges 3:7; Judges 8:33-34; Romans 1:21; 2 Timothy 3:2
* **Associated Articles:** 5597: victory, as an act of God (ID: `5597@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5607: warfare, examples of (ID: `5607@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5776: achievement (ID: `5776@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5857: fame (ID: `5857@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5858: fat (ID: `5858@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5889: ingratitude (ID: `5889@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5917: plans (ID: `5917@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8441: goals (ID: `8441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8741: failure (ID: `8741@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 5961: superiority (id: 5961)

A sense of being of greater importance or value in comparison with others, which can lead to arrogance and boasting.

Superiority is a characteristic of the wicked person
----------------------------------------------------

### It is the product of human pride

**[PRO 21:24](https://ref.ly/Prov21:24)**; **[3JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/3John1:9)** *See also* [2CH 26:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16); [PSA 73:3–73:9](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:9); [ISA 3:16](https://ref.ly/Isa3:16), [ISA 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:9-Isa9:10); [ROM 1:30](https://ref.ly/Rom1:30); [2TI 3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:4); [2PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10)

### It involves looking down on others

**[1SA 17:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:42)**; **[LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)** *See also* [GEN 16:4–16:5](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4-Gen16:5); [1SA 10:27](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:27); [2SA 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:16) parallel [1CH 15:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:29); [PSA 31:18](https://ref.ly/Ps31:18), [PSA 101:5](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5), [PSA 123:3–123:4](https://ref.ly/Ps123:3-Ps123:4); [PRO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Prov15:20), [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22); [AMO 5:10](https://ref.ly/Amos5:10)

### It gives rise to boasting

**[EST 5:11](https://ref.ly/Esth5:11)**; **[ACT 8:9](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9)** *See also* [1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11); [ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6); [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2); [DAN 7:8](https://ref.ly/Dan7:8); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8); [MAT 26:33–26:35](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33-Matt26:35); [JAS 3:5](https://ref.ly/Jas3:5); [JUD 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16)

God hates superior behaviour and will judge it
----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Isa2:11-Isa2:12)**; **[1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5)** *See also* [1SA 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:3); [2SA 22:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:28); [JOB 20:6–20:7](https://ref.ly/Job20:6-Job20:7); [PRO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Prov3:11), [PRO 16:5](https://ref.ly/Prov16:5), [PRO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Prov16:18), [PRO 21:4](https://ref.ly/Prov21:4); [ISA 2:17](https://ref.ly/Isa2:17), [ISA 10:12](https://ref.ly/Isa10:12), [ISA 13:11](https://ref.ly/Isa13:11), [ISA 13:19](https://ref.ly/Isa13:19), [ISA 23:9](https://ref.ly/Isa23:9); [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24); [OBA 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Obad1:12-Obad1:13); [MAT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Matt18:10); [ROM 11:17–11:18](https://ref.ly/Rom11:17-Rom11:18), [ROM 14:3](https://ref.ly/Rom14:3), [ROM 14:10](https://ref.ly/Rom14:10); [1CO 1:20–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20-1Cor1:25); [1TI 3:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:6), [1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17); [REV 18:7–18:8](https://ref.ly/Rev18:7-Rev18:8)

The attitude of the godly is not superior
-----------------------------------------

### They demonstrate a proper humility

**[PSA 131:1](https://ref.ly/Ps131:1)**; **[PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3)** *See also* [ROM 12:16](https://ref.ly/Rom12:16); [1CO 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:6-1Cor4:7); [PHP 2:4–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:4-Phil2:8)

### Jesus Christ's advice to those who aspire to greatness

**[MAT 20:20–20:28](https://ref.ly/Matt20:20-Matt20:28)** parallel [MRK 10:35–10:45](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35-Mark10:45) *See also* [JHN 13:12–13:17](https://ref.ly/John13:12-John13:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 21:24; 3 John 1:9; 2 Chronicles 26:16; Psalms 73:3-9; Isaiah 3:16; Isaiah 9:9-10; Romans 1:30; 2 Timothy 3:4; 2 Peter 2:10; 1 Samuel 17:42; Luke 18:9-14; Genesis 16:4-5; 1 Samuel 10:27; 2 Samuel 6:16; 1 Chronicles 15:29; Psalms 31:18; Psalms 101:5; Psalms 123:3-4; Proverbs 15:20; Proverbs 23:22; Amos 5:10; Esther 5:11; Acts 8:9; 1 Kings 20:11; Isaiah 16:6; Ezekiel 28:2; Daniel 7:8; Hosea 12:8; Matthew 26:33-35; James 3:5; Jude 1:16; Isaiah 2:11-12; 1 Peter 5:5; 1 Samuel 2:3; 2 Samuel 22:28; Job 20:6-7; Proverbs 3:11; Proverbs 16:5; Proverbs 16:18; Proverbs 21:4; Isaiah 2:17; Isaiah 10:12; Isaiah 13:11; Isaiah 13:19; Isaiah 23:9; Jeremiah 9:23-24; Obadiah 1:12-13; Matthew 18:10; Romans 11:17-18; Romans 14:3; Romans 14:10; 1 Corinthians 1:20-25; 1 Timothy 3:6; 1 Timothy 6:17; Revelation 18:7-8; Psalms 131:1; Philippians 2:3; Romans 12:16; 1 Corinthians 4:6-7; Philippians 2:4-8; Matthew 20:20-28; Mark 10:35-45; John 13:12-17

## 5962: surprises (id: 5962)

People are often taken unawares by God's power, love and wisdom. The actions of God and Jesus Christ are often described as surprising those who witnessed them.

God's actions surprise people
-----------------------------

### Surprise at the sudden appearance of angels

**[LUK 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/Luke1:11-Luke1:12)** *See also* [DAN 8:16–8:17](https://ref.ly/Dan8:16-Dan8:17); [LUK 1:28–1:29](https://ref.ly/Luke1:28-Luke1:29), [LUK 2:9](https://ref.ly/Luke2:9); [ACT 10:3–10:4](https://ref.ly/Acts10:3-Acts10:4)

### Surprise deliverance

[DAN 3:26–3:27](https://ref.ly/Dan3:26-Dan3:27); ; [ACT 5:19–5:24](https://ref.ly/Acts5:19-Acts5:24), [ACT 12:14–12:18](https://ref.ly/Acts12:14-Acts12:18), [ACT 28:6](https://ref.ly/Acts28:6)

### Surprise at special births

**[LUK 1:29–1:34](https://ref.ly/Luke1:29-Luke1:34)** *See also* [GEN 17:17](https://ref.ly/Gen17:17); [LUK 1:18](https://ref.ly/Luke1:18)

### Surprise at the circumstances of Jesus Christ's death

**[MAT 27:54](https://ref.ly/Matt27:54)**

### Surprise at the coming of the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Acts2:6-Acts2:7)** *See also* [ACT 10:45](https://ref.ly/Acts10:45)

### Surprise at the change in people's lives

**[ACT 9:21](https://ref.ly/Acts9:21)** *See also* [MRK 5:15](https://ref.ly/Mark5:15) parallel [LUK 8:35](https://ref.ly/Luke8:35)

### Surprise at the second coming

**[MAT 24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:44)** parallel [LUK 12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:40) *See also* [MAL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Mal3:1); [MAT 24:50](https://ref.ly/Matt24:50) parallel [LUK 12:46](https://ref.ly/Luke12:46); [MAT 25:6](https://ref.ly/Matt25:6), [MAT 25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:13); [MRK 13:36](https://ref.ly/Mark13:36); [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34); [1TH 5:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:4)

### Surprise at discernment

[MAT 25:37–25:39](https://ref.ly/Matt25:37-Matt25:39), [MAT 25:44](https://ref.ly/Matt25:44); ; [LUK 5:22](https://ref.ly/Luke5:22); ; [JHN 4:19](https://ref.ly/John4:19); ; [ACT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Acts5:5), [ACT 5:11](https://ref.ly/Acts5:11)

Jesus Christ surprised people
-----------------------------

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's moral requirements

**[MAT 5:20](https://ref.ly/Matt5:20)** *See also* [MAT 5:48](https://ref.ly/Matt5:48), [MAT 18:21–18:22](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:22)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's humility

**[JHN 13:6](https://ref.ly/John13:6)**

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's control of natural elements

**[MAT 8:27](https://ref.ly/Matt8:27)** parallels [MRK 4:41](https://ref.ly/Mark4:41), [LUK 8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:25) *See also* [MAT 14:25–14:26](https://ref.ly/Matt14:25-Matt14:26) parallel [MRK 6:49–6:50](https://ref.ly/Mark6:49-Mark6:50); [MAT 21:20](https://ref.ly/Matt21:20); [LUK 5:9](https://ref.ly/Luke5:9); [JHN 2:8–2:10](https://ref.ly/John2:8-John2:10), [JHN 6:14](https://ref.ly/John6:14)

### Surprise at the company Jesus Christ kept

**[MRK 2:16](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16)** parallels [MAT 9:11](https://ref.ly/Matt9:11), [LUK 5:30](https://ref.ly/Luke5:30) *See also* [LUK 7:39](https://ref.ly/Luke7:39)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's healings

**[MRK 7:37](https://ref.ly/Mark7:37)** *See also* [MAT 9:6–9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:6-Matt9:8), [MAT 9:33](https://ref.ly/Matt9:33), [MAT 15:31](https://ref.ly/Matt15:31); [MRK 5:42](https://ref.ly/Mark5:42) parallel [LUK 8:56](https://ref.ly/Luke8:56)

### Surprise at the requirements for entry into the kingdom of heaven

[MAT 19:13–19:15](https://ref.ly/Matt19:13-Matt19:15) parallels [MRK 10:13–10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:13-Mark10:16), [LUK 18:15–18:17](https://ref.ly/Luke18:15-Luke18:17); ; [MAT 19:24–19:25](https://ref.ly/Matt19:24-Matt19:25) parallels [MRK 10:23–10:26](https://ref.ly/Mark10:23-Mark10:26), [LUK 18:25–18:26](https://ref.ly/Luke18:25-Luke18:26)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's teaching authority

**[MRK 1:22](https://ref.ly/Mark1:22)** parallel [LUK 4:32](https://ref.ly/Luke4:32) *See also* [MAT 7:28–7:29](https://ref.ly/Matt7:28-Matt7:29), [MAT 22:33](https://ref.ly/Matt22:33); [MRK 6:2–6:4](https://ref.ly/Mark6:2-Mark6:4) parallel [MAT 13:53–13:54](https://ref.ly/Matt13:53-Matt13:54); [MRK 11:18](https://ref.ly/Mark11:18); [JHN 7:15](https://ref.ly/John7:15)

### Surprise at the wisdom of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 22:22](https://ref.ly/Matt22:22)** parallels [MRK 12:17](https://ref.ly/Mark12:17), [LUK 20:26](https://ref.ly/Luke20:26)

### Surprise at the expectations of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/Matt14:16-Matt14:17)** parallels [MRK 6:37](https://ref.ly/Mark6:37), [LUK 9:13](https://ref.ly/Luke9:13), [JHN 6:7–6:9](https://ref.ly/John6:7-John6:9) *See also* [MAT 15:33–15:36](https://ref.ly/Matt15:33-Matt15:36) parallel [MRK 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/Mark8:4-Mark8:6)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's authority in the spiritual realm

**[MRK 1:27](https://ref.ly/Mark1:27)** parallel [LUK 4:36](https://ref.ly/Luke4:36) *See also* [MRK 5:15–5:17](https://ref.ly/Mark5:15-Mark5:17) parallels [MAT 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Matt8:33-Matt8:34), [LUK 8:35–8:37](https://ref.ly/Luke8:35-Luke8:37)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's disregard for Jewish customs

**[LUK 11:38](https://ref.ly/Luke11:38)** *See also* [MRK 7:5](https://ref.ly/Mark7:5) parallel [MAT 15:2](https://ref.ly/Matt15:2); [JHN 4:27](https://ref.ly/John4:27)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ's rejection of religious words and actions

[MAT 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21-Matt7:23); ; [LUK 13:26–13:27](https://ref.ly/Luke13:26-Luke13:27)

### Surprise at Jesus Christ bringing back the dead

**[LUK 7:14–7:16](https://ref.ly/Luke7:14-Luke7:16)** *See also* [MRK 5:42](https://ref.ly/Mark5:42) parallel [LUK 8:56](https://ref.ly/Luke8:56)

### Surprise at the post\-resurrection appearance of Jesus Christ

**[LUK 24:36–24:37](https://ref.ly/Luke24:36-Luke24:37)**

Jesus Christ is surprised by people's reactions
-----------------------------------------------

### By the great faith of an unlikely person

**[MAT 8:10](https://ref.ly/Matt8:10)** parallel [LUK 7:9](https://ref.ly/Luke7:9)

### By lack of faith among God's people

**[MRK 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:4-Mark6:6)** parallel [MAT 13:57–13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:57-Matt13:58)

### By the dullness of his followers

**[MAT 15:16](https://ref.ly/Matt15:16)** parallel [MRK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mark7:18) *See also* [MAT 16:9–16:11](https://ref.ly/Matt16:9-Matt16:11) parallel [MRK 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Mark8:17-Mark8:18)

### By lack of spiritual insight

**[JHN 14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:9)** *See also* [JHN 3:10](https://ref.ly/John3:10)

Surprises for believers
-----------------------

### Surprise at suffering

**[1PE 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:12)** *See also* [1JN 3:13](https://ref.ly/1John3:13)

### Surprise at divine revelation

**[LUK 24:32](https://ref.ly/Luke24:32)** *See also* [ACT 10:17](https://ref.ly/Acts10:17), [ACT 11:15](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15)

Surprises caused by believers
-----------------------------

### Surprising courage

**[ACT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Acts4:13)** *See also* [DAN 3:13](https://ref.ly/Dan3:13)

### Surprise at miracles

[ACT 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/Acts3:11-Acts3:12), [ACT 8:13](https://ref.ly/Acts8:13), [ACT 14:11](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11)

### Surprise at the teaching about Jesus Christ

[ACT 13:12](https://ref.ly/Acts13:12)

* **Associated Passages:** Luke 1:11-12; Daniel 8:16-17; Luke 1:28-29; Luke 2:9; Acts 10:3-4; Daniel 3:26-27; Acts 5:19-24; Acts 12:14-18; Acts 28:6; Luke 1:29-34; Genesis 17:17; Luke 1:18; Matthew 27:54; Acts 2:6-7; Acts 10:45; Acts 9:21; Mark 5:15; Luke 8:35; Matthew 24:44; Luke 12:40; Malachi 3:1; Matthew 24:50; Luke 12:46; Matthew 25:6; Matthew 25:13; Mark 13:36; Luke 21:34; 1 Thessalonians 5:4; Matthew 25:37-39; Matthew 25:44; Luke 5:22; John 4:19; Acts 5:5; Acts 5:11; Matthew 5:20; Matthew 5:48; Matthew 18:21-22; John 13:6; Matthew 8:27; Mark 4:41; Luke 8:25; Matthew 14:25-26; Mark 6:49-50; Matthew 21:20; Luke 5:9; John 2:8-10; John 6:14; Mark 2:16; Matthew 9:11; Luke 5:30; Luke 7:39; Mark 7:37; Matthew 9:6-8; Matthew 9:33; Matthew 15:31; Mark 5:42; Luke 8:56; Matthew 19:13-15; Mark 10:13-16; Luke 18:15-17; Matthew 19:24-25; Mark 10:23-26; Luke 18:25-26; Mark 1:22; Luke 4:32; Matthew 7:28-29; Matthew 22:33; Mark 6:2-4; Matthew 13:53-54; Mark 11:18; John 7:15; Matthew 22:22; Mark 12:17; Luke 20:26; Matthew 14:16-17; Mark 6:37; Luke 9:13; John 6:7-9; Matthew 15:33-36; Mark 8:4-6; Mark 1:27; Luke 4:36; Mark 5:15-17; Matthew 8:33-34; Luke 8:35-37; Luke 11:38; Mark 7:5; Matthew 15:2; John 4:27; Matthew 7:21-23; Luke 13:26-27; Luke 7:14-16; Luke 24:36-37; Matthew 8:10; Luke 7:9; Mark 6:4-6; Matthew 13:57-58; Matthew 15:16; Mark 7:18; Matthew 16:9-11; Mark 8:17-18; John 14:9; John 3:10; 1 Peter 4:12; 1 John 3:13; Luke 24:32; Acts 10:17; Acts 11:15; Acts 4:13; Daniel 3:13; Acts 3:11-12; Acts 8:13; Acts 14:11; Acts 13:12

## 5963: sympathy (id: 5963)

The ability to appreciate another's condition, especially through shared experience of pain or suffering. God sympathises with his people in their sufferings, as does Jesus Christ as the sympathetic high priest.

God sympathises with his people
-------------------------------

**[PSA 10:14](https://ref.ly/Ps10:14)**; **[2CO 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:3-2Cor1:4)** *See also* [EXO 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/Exod22:26-Exod22:27); [DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18); [JDG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Judg2:18); [PSA 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ps9:9), [PSA 91:15](https://ref.ly/Ps91:15); [ISA 40:1–40:2](https://ref.ly/Isa40:1-Isa40:2), [ISA 66:13](https://ref.ly/Isa66:13); [JER 8:18](https://ref.ly/Jer8:18); [2CO 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:6)

God's apparent lack of sympathy may be evidence of judgment
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 16:5–16:6](https://ref.ly/Jer16:5-Jer16:6)** *See also* [ISA 27:11](https://ref.ly/Isa27:11), [ISA 63:15](https://ref.ly/Isa63:15)

Jesus Christ sympathises with those in distress
-----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 11:35](https://ref.ly/John11:35)**; **[HEB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15)** *See also* [MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36); [MRK 1:41](https://ref.ly/Mark1:41); [LUK 7:34](https://ref.ly/Luke7:34); [HEB 2:14–2:18](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14-Heb2:18)

Sympathy as a characteristic of friendship
------------------------------------------

**[1SA 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:34)**; **[JOB 2:11](https://ref.ly/Job2:11)** *See also* [JDG 11:37](https://ref.ly/Judg11:37); [1SA 20:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:41); [2SA 10:2–10:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:2-2Sam10:3) parallel [1CH 19:2–19:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:2-1Chr19:3); [JOB 16:4–16:5](https://ref.ly/Job16:4-Job16:5), [JOB 19:21](https://ref.ly/Job19:21), [JOB 29:16](https://ref.ly/Job29:16), [JOB 30:25](https://ref.ly/Job30:25); [ECC 4:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:10); [DAN 1:9](https://ref.ly/Dan1:9); [JHN 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/John11:18-John11:19)

Sympathy expected from God's people
-----------------------------------

**[EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9)**; **[ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15)**; **[1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)** *See also* [EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21); [LEV 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33-Lev19:34); [DEU 10:19](https://ref.ly/Deut10:19); [ISA 58:6–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:7); [2CO 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:4-2Cor1:7), [2CO 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:7), [2CO 11:29](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:29); [PHP 1:7](https://ref.ly/Phil1:7), [PHP 2:1](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1), [PHP 4:14](https://ref.ly/Phil4:14); [COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12); [1TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:11-1Thess2:12); [1TI 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:9-1Tim5:10); [HEB 10:34](https://ref.ly/Heb10:34), [HEB 13:3](https://ref.ly/Heb13:3)

The absence of sympathy
-----------------------

**[PSA 69:20](https://ref.ly/Ps69:20)** *See also* [DEU 15:7](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7); [JOB 6:14–6:18](https://ref.ly/Job6:14-Job6:18); [PSA 31:11–31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:11-Ps31:12), [PSA 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ps38:11), [PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9); [JER 20:10](https://ref.ly/Jer20:10); [LAM 1:16](https://ref.ly/Lam1:16); [OBA 1:12](https://ref.ly/Obad1:12); [MAT 18:33](https://ref.ly/Matt18:33)

## 5964: temper (id: 5964)

A person's usual character or temporary frame of mind. The term often refers to a temporary state of anger, which Scripture insists must be brief and controlled.

General references to good temper
---------------------------------

**[PRO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Prov16:32)** *See also* [PRO 15:1](https://ref.ly/Prov15:1); [MAT 5:39](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39); [EPH 4:2](https://ref.ly/Eph4:2); [1TI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:3); [2TI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24); [JAS 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jas3:17)

People who kept their temper under provocation
----------------------------------------------

### Joseph

[GEN 50:15–50:21](https://ref.ly/Gen50:15-Gen50:21)

### David

[2SA 16:5–16:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:5-2Sam16:13)

### Jesus Christ

[LUK 23:33–23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:33-Luke23:34)

### Stephen

[ACT 7:59–7:60](https://ref.ly/Acts7:59-Acts7:60)

--------------------------------

**[PRO 14:17](https://ref.ly/Prov14:17)**; **[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)** *See also* [PSA 37:8](https://ref.ly/Ps37:8); [PRO 19:11](https://ref.ly/Prov19:11); [ECC 7:8–7:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:8-Eccl7:9); [GAL 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:20)

-------------------------------------------------------

## 5965: temperament (id: 5965)

A person's disposition, especially as shown in mental or emotional responses. God's people should be of an even temperament.

God's people should be of even temperament
------------------------------------------

**[PRO 16:32](https://ref.ly/Prov16:32)** *See also* [TIT 2:1–2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:1-Titus2:14)

Even temperament is a requirement for church leadership
-------------------------------------------------------

**[1TI 3:2–3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:3)** *See also* [1TI 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8-1Tim3:9), [1TI 3:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:11); [TIT 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:7-Titus1:9)

Examples of people who had an unpleasant temperament
----------------------------------------------------

### Saul

**[1SA 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:23)** *Violent mood swings and bad temper were signs of mental instability in Saul.* *See also* [1SA 16:14–16:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14-1Sam16:16), [1SA 18:6–18:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:15), [1SA 20:4–20:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:4-1Sam20:7), [1SA 20:24–20:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:24-1Sam20:33)

### Nabal the Carmelite

**[1SA 25:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:17)** *See also* [1SA 25:2–25:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:2-1Sam25:11), [1SA 25:36–25:38](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:36-1Sam25:38)

## 5966: tenderness (id: 5966)

Physically, the state of being soft or easily damaged. Tenderness in relationships is an expression of love and affection which, in the case of God, is often linked with compassion for his people.

Physical tenderness
-------------------

### Of new plant growth

**[EZK 17:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:22)** *See also* [DEU 32:2](https://ref.ly/Deut32:2); [2KI 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:26); [ISA 37:27](https://ref.ly/Isa37:27), [ISA 53:2](https://ref.ly/Isa53:2); [MAT 24:32](https://ref.ly/Matt24:32) parallel [MRK 13:28](https://ref.ly/Mark13:28)

### Of meat

[GEN 18:7](https://ref.ly/Gen18:7)

### Of the young and immature

**[PRO 4:3](https://ref.ly/Prov4:3)** *See also* [GEN 33:13](https://ref.ly/Gen33:13); [ISA 47:1](https://ref.ly/Isa47:1)

Tenderness in relationships
---------------------------

### Human love and affection

**[GEN 34:3](https://ref.ly/Gen34:3)** *See also* [2SA 1:26](https://ref.ly/2Sam1:26); [SNG 7:10](https://ref.ly/Song7:10); [LUK 15:20](https://ref.ly/Luke15:20)

### God's tenderness

**[ISA 40:1–40:2](https://ref.ly/Isa40:1-Isa40:2)**; **[LUK 1:78](https://ref.ly/Luke1:78)** *See also* [DEU 32:10–32:14](https://ref.ly/Deut32:10-Deut32:14); [ISA 30:18](https://ref.ly/Isa30:18), [ISA 54:10](https://ref.ly/Isa54:10), [ISA 63:15](https://ref.ly/Isa63:15); [JER 31:3](https://ref.ly/Jer31:3), [JER 31:20](https://ref.ly/Jer31:20); [HOS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Hos2:14), [HOS 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/Hos11:1-Hos11:4); [ZEP 3:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:17)

### The tenderness of Jesus Christ

**[MRK 10:16](https://ref.ly/Mark10:16)** *See also* [MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36), [MAT 14:14](https://ref.ly/Matt14:14), [MAT 23:37](https://ref.ly/Matt23:37); [MRK 1:41](https://ref.ly/Mark1:41); [JHN 11:33–11:36](https://ref.ly/John11:33-John11:36), [JHN 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/John19:26-John19:27)

### A mark of Christian behaviour

**[PHP 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Phil2:1-Phil2:2)** *See also* [1CO 4:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:21); [PHP 1:8](https://ref.ly/Phil1:8); [COL 3:12](https://ref.ly/Col3:12); [1PE 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:8)

## 5967: thrift (id: 5967)

Saving by the careful use of resources. In general this is encouraged in Scripture, though the dangers of meanness, materialism and anxiety are recognised.

Encouragements to thrift
------------------------

### To provide for the future

**[PRO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Prov21:20)** *See also* [PRO 6:6–6:8](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6-Prov6:8), [PRO 10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:5), [PRO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Prov12:27), [PRO 27:23–27:27](https://ref.ly/Prov27:23-Prov27:27), [PRO 30:25](https://ref.ly/Prov30:25)

### To provide for children

[PSA 17:14](https://ref.ly/Ps17:14); ; [PRO 13:22](https://ref.ly/Prov13:22); ; [2CO 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:14)

### To provide for others

[DEU 14:28–14:29](https://ref.ly/Deut14:28-Deut14:29); ; [1CO 16:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:2); ; [EPH 4:28](https://ref.ly/Eph4:28)

Examples of thrift
------------------

### Noah

**[GEN 6:21](https://ref.ly/Gen6:21)**

### Joseph

**[GEN 41:48](https://ref.ly/Gen41:48)** *See also* [GEN 41:28–41:36](https://ref.ly/Gen41:28-Gen41:36), [GEN 41:53](https://ref.ly/Gen41:53)

### Solomon

[1KI 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:17-1Kgs9:19) parallel [2CH 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:4-2Chr8:6)

### Jehoshaphat

[2CH 17:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:12)

### Hezekiah

[2CH 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:29)

### The good wife

[PRO 31:13–31:16](https://ref.ly/Prov31:13-Prov31:16)

### Jesus Christ

**[JHN 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/John6:12-John6:13)**

The dangers of thrift
---------------------

### Meanness

**[JHN 12:4–12:6](https://ref.ly/John12:4-John12:6)** parallels [MAT 26:8–26:9](https://ref.ly/Matt26:8-Matt26:9), [MRK 14:4–14:5](https://ref.ly/Mark14:4-Mark14:5) *See also* [PRO 3:27–3:28](https://ref.ly/Prov3:27-Prov3:28), [PRO 11:24](https://ref.ly/Prov11:24), [PRO 11:26](https://ref.ly/Prov11:26), [PRO 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Prov23:6-Prov23:8), [PRO 28:22](https://ref.ly/Prov28:22); [ECC 5:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:13); [2CO 9:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:6)

### Materialism

**[LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15)** *See also* [ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8); [MIC 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mic6:14); [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)

### Anxiety

**[LUK 12:22–12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:22-Luke12:31)** parallel [MAT 6:25–6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:34)

Lack of thrift
--------------

**[LUK 15:13](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13)** *See also* [PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17), [PRO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Prov21:20), [PRO 23:20–23:21](https://ref.ly/Prov23:20-Prov23:21), [PRO 29:3](https://ref.ly/Prov29:3); [LUK 16:1](https://ref.ly/Luke16:1)

Spiritual thrift
----------------

**[MAT 6:19–6:21](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19-Matt6:21)**; **[EPH 5:15–5:16](https://ref.ly/Eph5:15-Eph5:16)** *See also* [PRO 10:14](https://ref.ly/Prov10:14); [1TI 6:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:19)

### The wicked store up judgment

**[ROM 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5-Rom2:6)** *See also* [ROM 2:7–2:10](https://ref.ly/Rom2:7-Rom2:10); [REV 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Rev18:6-Rev18:7)

## 5969: treachery (id: 5969)

A betrayal of trust by a close companion, often involving a preconceived plan. Those found guilty of treachery are severely punished by God.

Treachery against God
---------------------

**[ZEP 3:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:4)** *See also* [LEV 26:40](https://ref.ly/Lev26:40); [ISA 48:8](https://ref.ly/Isa48:8), [ISA 59:13](https://ref.ly/Isa59:13)

Treachery against other people
------------------------------

**[2SA 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9-2Sam20:10)** *See also* [JDG 16:19](https://ref.ly/Judg16:19) *Delilah against Samson*; [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17) *Saul against David*; [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27) *Joab against Abner*; [2SA 4:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:6) *Recab and Baanah against Ish\-Bosheth*; [2SA 11:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:15) *David against Uriah*; [2SA 13:28–13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:28-2Sam13:29) *Absalom against Amnon*; [1KI 21:7–21:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:7-1Kgs21:13) *Jezebel against Naboth*; [NEH 6:2](https://ref.ly/Neh6:2) *Sanballat and Geshem against Nehemiah*; [EST 3:8](https://ref.ly/Esth3:8) *Haman against Mordecai*; [MAT 26:49](https://ref.ly/Matt26:49) parallels [MRK 14:45](https://ref.ly/Mark14:45), [LUK 22:47–22:48](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47-Luke22:48) *Judas against Jesus Christ*; [ACT 7:18–7:19](https://ref.ly/Acts7:18-Acts7:19) *Pharaoh against the Israelites*

Punishment of the treacherous
-----------------------------

**[ACT 1:18](https://ref.ly/Acts1:18)** *See also* [2SA 18:14–18:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:14-2Sam18:15) *Absalom*; [1KI 2:31–2:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:31-1Kgs2:34) *Joab*; [2KI 9:30–9:37](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:30-2Kgs9:37) *Jezebel*; [EST 7:10](https://ref.ly/Esth7:10) *Haman*; [PSA 25:3](https://ref.ly/Ps25:3), [PSA 59:5](https://ref.ly/Ps59:5); [ISA 33:1](https://ref.ly/Isa33:1)

Treachery in the purposes of God
--------------------------------

**[ACT 4:27–4:28](https://ref.ly/Acts4:27-Acts4:28)** *See also* [GEN 50:20](https://ref.ly/Gen50:20); [JDG 9:22–9:24](https://ref.ly/Judg9:22-Judg9:24); [2KI 9:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs9:23), [2KI 11:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:14) parallel [2CH 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr23:13)
